text: On the way to church and got run off the road by a car. Flat tire. Glad firestone was open on Sundays. Pretty quick service. In and out in about an hour. Customer service was fine.
I guess the owner must have stepped in to resolve the issue's I had with PA auto sales. I'm a fair and easy going person. I just wanted fairness from day one.

Beautiful place!! Could use more of  a "rooftop view" with the city, but the place has a nice aesthetic to it already! Drinks are made with care and make you feel classy with the service they provide. Took my manager out for his birthday and he was impressed!!! 

They are lacking one start though, because it's only honest to be fair. They happened to be out of red bull, select whiskeys, and even my go to tequila don julio while we were there. I almost ended up leaving but our server suggested I try something new and we'll....I liked it! Great spot for a laid back night with friends.

Also, pricing is literally "you pay for what you drink". Let me explain: you want the cheap stuff? Well guess what, you'll pay the cheap price LOL. You want some quality champagne? Well, you'll pay the quality price. Our double shot whiskey on the rocks came out to only $12!
One of the best DELCO staples. Amazing pizza, not the best wings. Prices are a little steep. But overall a good pizza joint. Pickle pizza is to die for!

The management changed a couple of years ago, and it's just not what it used to be. The coffee options and quality are greatly reduced (and not particularly good - Philly roaster Green Street is... average, at best) and, while it still looks good, the food remains pricy. I used to go all the time, but the coffee and sandwich that I loved are no longer served there, so I don't really make it in anymore.

Best crepes I ever tasted!!!!! While wheat and buckwheat too!!! Sooo delicious!! Breakfast crepes and sweet crepes are all amazing!!!!! Best kept secret in Wayne!!! My daughter and I love this place!!

I wanted to give a local barbecue joint a try to see how it stacks up so we tried the Philthy sampler with brisket, pulled pork and ribs per the greeter's suggestion.  Each meat selection had distinct flavors which is good.  Only drawback is I found all a bit too salty for me as I had to take sips of drink throughout my meal.  As for sides we ordered Mac and Cheese, beans, brussel sprouts and potatoes.  The Mac and Cheese was a bit bland but the beans had a nice kick of Tabasco and vinegar to it.  The brussel sprouts and potatoes were simple fare and cooked well.  The only other thing I would mention is that the sampler is supposed to feed 2-3 adults; however, it barely fed my group of 4 with three kids all under 13 and all of them not heavy eaters.

In June 2016, my realty company used Lincoln Heating and Air for the heating and AC inspection of the property I was buying.  The following month I had Lincoln return to do the over $2000 in repairs that was listed in the report needed to bring the system to a better working condition. The system managed another two years. The air died July 27, 2018, I immediately called Lincoln for repairs.  The technician at that time went over the repair costs of old unit versus a new system. The place was built in 1978, so I chose to update with a new more efficient furnace and AC system a week later.  At the same time,  I purchased and had installed a new water from them.  
Last fall after I started to use the furnace, I discovered a section of the duct work had opened up allowing the heat to go outside. I called as soon as I discovered it, and had a service man come out a couple of days later to repair.  At that time I was told the wrong tape was used during the installation. Repairs were made with different tape, supposedly the correct and stronger tape. 
Early February 2019, I am heading out my door and again notice a lot of heat on my front porch.  I open the door to the system and see a new gaping hole in the duct work that was repaired last fall. I immediately call the office and talk to Leslie. She checks Lincoln's records, talks to repair, and schedules for for two days later. I will admit, a system that is less than 6 months old and has had  a repair man out twice to fix faulty installation is really upsetting.  I sat down a wrote a letter to give to the repair man to pass along to the owner and repair manager. Letting them know my concerns.
I was very surprised when I open the door to the repair man and heard a familiar voice. I hear their commercials every morning on the radio. The owner has come to inspect my situation personally.  To his dismay, he tells me my install isn't up to his company's standards. We spent a good 20 minutes looking everything over and discussing my concerns. During this time I presented him with the letter I had written earlier.  The parts to solve my problem needed to be custom made.  Being a late Friday afternoon, he spent the time to get broken duct closed for the weekend. We made arrangements to have the third and "proper" repairs on the following Tuesday. But parts weren't ready and it was too cold outside to do the work. So we rescheduled to another Friday. I was under the impression that the owner would be at my place to personally fix my furnace correctly this time.  He came with another worker and after a while the owner left, leaving this guy to complete the job.  I also discovered the city had never inspected the original work.  Ball dropped on the end of Lincoln.  Had the inspection been done last fall, I would have known the unit was wrong. City Inspector comes out the following Friday and can't pass the heating work or the line to the AC. I had him immediately call Lincoln's owner and discuss the new issues. The owner said he would be over within the hour to see it and talk to me. Three hours later he shows up. Once again I discuss my shock and how upset I am at all the mistakes that are going on, plus the massive waste of my Fridays.  Again he asked for the opportunity to make everything correct.  Again I gave the company that chance because I have already spent over $10,000 with them in the last two years.  Yes you MUST make it perfect.
The following Friday more work was done and the inspector finally passed all the components. 
Upon the final meeting and my mounting frustrations, I request a small refund of less than 10%.  The owner agreed. One more final surprise- the bookkeeper calls a week later to tell me the refund will be divided and paid out over March and April. "Are you kidding me" was my reply. I paid my bill in full upon what I thought was completion last fall. 
 I have personally lost my confidence in Lincoln, and am extremely disappointed in my situation.  Like everything in life, don't just complain to others. Go to the source. I did just that, I talked to the owner several times before sitting down to write this review. I gave them many chances to make it right. There are many on Yelp who have great results, maybe I was a fluke.
Bottom line-
We can not be experts in everything, and we rely on professionals to do the best job for us.

The service department is a complete disaster. What's the point of having an appointment if you're going to tell me the it's going to be a few hours?! I dropped my car off at 2:30, was told to wait for a courtesy ride, waited for an hour, no one came, called an Uber. At 6:30 I had to call them to find out what was going on, was told they would call me back. No call back. WTF?! No I'm without a ride until at least the morning. And all I took it in for was an oil change and some pretty basic service. This will be last time using AUTONATION Ford.

Do not eat here if you are white!!!  I visited this restaurant with my mother last night around 930 pm and left the restaurant by approx. 935 after hearing employees conversing about spitting in white peoples food! After leaving, I still wanted steak n shake so my mother suggested we go to the location on fowler ave and I am glad I did because the experience was like night and day. Employees were primarily black at this location as well but they were all extremely nice, hard working and provided excellent service with a smile! I didn't feel welcomed from the minute I walked into the door at the Hillsborough location and that was confirmed pretty quickly but the fowler location welcomed us with open arms and treated my mother and I with the respect and care that everyone deserves!

I'm a college student, and I was worried that when I came home I wouldn't be able to find a local coffee shop that would be as good as the ones in my college town. Then I discovered Craft Kafe. I first went a week after they opened. Then, the only people there were the baristas, my mom and me, and the menu was small. I went back this past weekend with friends, and I think I've found my new coffee shop! We went for breakfast and everything was delicious. I got an egg white breakfast sandwich with avocado cream, chicken sausage, and provolone cheese. Delish. I also decided to try something different and got my caramel latte with almond milk - it was a great choice! 
I don't have a gluten intolerance, but it's good to know that I now have a place to bring my gluten intolerant friends where they don't have to worry about what they can eat. 
If you're in the area (or even if you're not), I would definitely suggest you stop by Craft Kafe!
Fun place. Really good cheap food. Just wish you could take it to go. Drinks are fairly priced.

Beware Morton's LIkes it RARE! 

Last night was the second time I've been to M's in six months, and both times my filet was just RAW on the inside. The first time I asked for Medium Rare, since I'm a fan of rare, but much of the inside was raw meat. So, this second time I ordered it Medium with a bit leaning towards Medium Rare, and the waiter confirmed my description in his own words and I agreed. Again, the filet came out with the majority of the inside raw meat.

Mister no name got his straight up medium and it was as RAW as mine was in the middle! I'm a fan of the rare, but as I did six months ago, I ate around the raw cow and left a red bullseye on my plate that i had to place at the end of the table to get it away from me until the waiter came back and took it. 

We both agreed that next time we will be ordering our meat Well Done, which will hopefully come out as a Medium!

Also, I have to comment on the outrageous drink prices there. On the low end the average of 15 bucks per glass of wine is ultra ridic. For a super fancy martini there, ok, but even then i don't think their drinks are super fancy mixology kind you'd expect to pay almost 20 bucks for. And, my 13 dollar wine glass wasn't even poured generously, as I've had at other places where the wine is expensive but you get a good glass of it.

I'm still giving it three stars because the salads are huge and the sides are ok and I don't pay for it. :)

And, how awesome is a place when the hostess pulls the giant table out for you to crawl behind in order to sit down? Well, that doesn't make it awesome in a good way, but more like 1980's power suit steak house movie scene awesome.

Finally, as to be expected, the DUDES were in FULL FORCE. BUSINESS, YEAH!

It's been awhile since I've had Korean food. Since Philly has a Chinatown, I thought this place will probably be legit... Dae Bak is located in a 2-story center and is located upstairs. For being a large facility, you would think the place would be air-conditioned... it is not. Dae Bak takes cash or card. If you pay with cash, you receive a discount. 

Just like any Korean restaurant, they provide you with an unlimited amount of side dishes to complement your meal. We started with the Seafood Scallion Pancake. I'm not sure why they would cut it like a Pizza... you can ask for them to cut it into squares. The Seafood Scallion Pancake is perfect for any seafood lover. You will find squid and other bits of seafood in your pancake.

For my entree, I ordered the Jeyuk Dolsot (Marinated Pork) Bibimbap. It was extremely flavorful and tender. If you aren't a fan of spicy, this is a fair warning-- this is a hot one. We also ordered a side of the Bulgogi. I'm not sure how you can ruin Bulgogi, but here we are. The flavor was extremely off. It was so awful, we trekked half-way across the country, only to realize, we didn't want it, even if we were starving...

This is a carry-out place. Besides the things you would expect they also sell bags of frozen pretzels that you can make at home and containers of Ted Drewe's. I've tried the sausage pretzel 'sandwiches'. I enjoyed it a year ago but when I went back this week the pretzel tasted bland. I got the impression from the older female staff member that I should have known the answers to my questions.

OMG - if I could give them 0 stars I would.  I contacted them about a Jeep that was advertised for $32000.00. I'm in California and was planning on purchasing and then flying out to pick up the Jeep. I told Tony I was ready to commit and asked him to work up pricing. 

Here's where it gets completely shady. Tony comes back to say they've added "a few" after market modifications: raised it, changed the rims and tires, added armor plating, pulse braking, and the Jeep version of lo-jack. Oh, and the price on their website reflects ALL the available rebates (so you'd have to be an active military, first responder, realtor to get the rebates. Yes, all three at once). He comes back with a final price, including tax, of $48,000.00.  Yes - FIFTEEN THOUSAND DOLLARS ABOVE THE ADVERTISED PRICE!!

I told Tony the ceiling of what I was willing to pay, and he said he would speak to his boss and get back to me. I'm still waiting to hear back...

Wonderful food,but I cannot go to the buffet, even if they still have it.  I'm sure the take away food is just as delicious as their buffet and recommend everyone who loves vegetarian and vegan food to try it. I'm sure if you order take away, you can specify vegan or vegetarian, I'm not sure.

Once a great establishment that I would go and drive out of my way for. They had the best breakfast burrito I've ever had as well as great chicken and steak traditional burritos. Management decided to make the highly questionable decision to ruin that for me and the rest of their large following when they went 'plant-based'. Just very disappointing to see and I will never step foot back in Honest Tom's ever again (until they realize that Mexican food sucks without meat and go back to their "roots")

Incredibly unique concept, kind of a food court restaurant with gorgeous decor and a wide variety of comfortable social seating. Great place to meet people. Float around among the bars and sample food from the various stands - you will get excellent service no matter what part of the venue you land in. Tons of food options (but not too many! The menu is well organized and sectioned, makes it easier to make a selection among all the food stalls). Poke bowls here are amazing, and definitely the bao buns! Craft cocktails here are as unique and fun and elegant as the atmosphere.

Tried this place today from word of mouth.Personally it wasn't that great cheesesteak wise.I wouldn't recommend anyone to go here if you're looking for a GOOD steak.the good thing was it had plenty of seating

Came here for Lunch, since it's at a great location, very close to the Wharf on state St.  I came in specifically for vegetarian food as they have lot of options for vegetarians and I believe vegans too. 

The atmosphere is casual, where you find your own seating and then order at the cash register. There was a queue of 5-6 people at the register to had to stand in line for about 15 mins to order food, which was fine.

We ordered the Buddha Burrito and the Vegetarian Tamale.  Both items were served with side salad and really good choice of dressings. But the entree were just okay. Nothing too great about the food. The Corn bread with the honey butter was delicious though and I would go back just for that.

My only problem with the food was that our food and from what I saw served around me, the plates that it was served in were way too small. The entree took up the entire plate and the side salad was somehow forced on to the plate. It was difficult to eat the food without spilling it over since there was absolutely no room. I am not sure why can't they use bigger plates or give salad on a smaller side plate.

Overall an okay place if you are looking for lot of vegetarian options at a convenient location close to the pier.
Excellent place to go have delicious food. I went for breakfast  and had the porkchop, delicious , wife had the seafood ommeltte.  5 stars .

Last November 04 I bought a Mattress with frame and everything else in the South Edmonton Store. Until then everything was perfect, everybody nice and very helpful, fast service, they even find a mattress to delivery in less then a week which they said didn't have in stock.

Less then 2 weeks after the delivered the the stitching on the bottom of the mattress starts to open, then:

I call the customer service - answer - you have to call the store.
I call the store - answer - you have to call the serta to fix the mattress.
I call the serta - answer - you have to go though the store.

After talking with the lady at the store, responsible for customer service she said she would contact serta and ask them to came at my house and fix the mattress.
Two weeks later - NOTHING. And I call her a few times between this two weeks and she even said they don't reply her emails. 
Today I spoke with her and she said they reply her (just today) and ask her for more information, but she don't know when they will reply her back or come to fix the mattress.
I sent a email to the Head Office Customer Service and they didn't even bother with a reply.

Terrible place to buy anything.

Because of it's creative international fine dining, one of the largest wine cellars in the country, phenomenal service, state-of-the-art decor, and overall elegance, Sidebern's is the best restaurant I've ever been to.

They recently changed their menu, and it's still spectacular but more diverse. They now have a complete vegetarian menu, the meat menu, plus a Mediterranean food menu. 

My suggestion is go for the tastings where you can have a six-course dinner, each dish beautifully presented and each paired with an extraordinary wine selection all the way to the dessert.

It's a bit pricey but well worth it. I will go back again and again...

@TravelPlusWine
Nothing wrong with this place. The movie selection isn't always my favorite but they make up for it by showing some awesome flicks that I do love.

Free admission before 7PM and after that it is $7-10.  This club is unique because the lap dances are done on beds, which make them more fun.  $25/song is standard prices for strip clubs but it does add up fast.

I commend the mall on having more up to date not so unhealthy choices.  But designated smoking area next to the food court next to the doors?!? Are you kidding me?!? As if the smoke wouldn't just waft right into the food court. Lame

A friend of mine who had been here before and had nothing but good things to say, brought myself and another friend of ours here on Saturday night. We had to make a reservation which is typical in Philadelphia on Saturday evenings because restaurants typically tend to get very busy. We sat down upstairs and ordered a round of drinks. Followed by another round, guacamole, empanadas and two entrees. The drinks and appetizers came out timely, however the entrees felt like they were taking forever. A manager approached our table and apologized for the wait and informed us there was a miscommunication in the kitchen regarding our order. He then proceeded to take a round of drinks off of our bill and bring us complimentary chips and salsa to hold us over while we waited. When the entrees finally came, my friends vegetable paella was underdone (assuming they were trying to make up for the lost time) and she asked to return it. Another manager came to our table apologizing, took it off the bill as well as all the appetizers we ordered. A $100+ bill ended up costing around $54! Although the service was slow and orders not correct, I am very impressed with the way it was handled by the staff here. We will most likely be returning again and hopefully I can change my review to 5 stars.

See, it's hard to yelp accurately for them because people are always leaving and they are hiring new people. They did a wonderful job with my makeup but they are so expensive.  they keep trying to sell you stuff the whole time which gets annoying.
It took over 10 minutes in the drive threw to get a smoothie and muffin. That is ridiculous for a drive threw breakfast place in the morning.

we went here for restaurant week recently.

the service was great.  very attentive, funny and charming.

the food was what you would expect for restaurant week:  safe, good, the portion sizes were a bit smaller than their normal fare.

all in all a good restaurant, but i don't know that i would go back for restaurant week again.

I've been a member at PF for about 8 months now and I absolutely enjoy it very much! The people here are very welcoming, super friendly and they're always smiling! They greet you as you enter the door and say goodbye as you leave! This gym doesn't have a ton of equipment, but it has enough for a gym. There's an elliptical, plenty of treadmills, bikes, and different types of weights. Their locker room is super clean. there's a few different sections to the gym which is really nice! they have a 30 min weigh training/session type of room that's very useful! They don't have a stair stepper. All of their cardio equipment have TV's. Only downfall is that the hours are very very very super limited!!!!!! If they extend their hours, especially on weekends, they would have more people signing up at their gym. Other than that nothing beats $10-20 a month + an annual fee each year.
I love the atmosphere and history of this place and the cheeseburger on the french breadmay be the best burger in America
I cant stress this enough... GET THE CHOC COVERED BANANAS and you will die a happy person!

This was on of the best omelets my fiance and I ever ate. I attempted to eat healthy and had the Florentine and it was delicious. Definitely not healthy. It looked like a stick of butter melted underneath it but when on vacation who cares. The biscuit was one of the better ones I had down here. Service was great. Our waiter helped us eliminate some taxis and tours by directing us to the world war II museum and areas that we could walk to and didnt need a tour guide. We live by some phenomenal omelette places that serve over 200 and every one is more delicious than the next but this may be the winner. If we didnt have a rule of not going somewhere twice on vacation I would of been back the next morning.

I'm not from the Tennessee area, so I've only ever been to a Belk one other time and it wasn't a great experience. I needed to get a couple things before headed out of town tomorrow so I decided to give them another try.

I stopped into the Mt. Juliet location on a Sunday afternoon and the store was clean and fairly organized. The clearance racks were jammed full with clothing (great for good deals, bad for actually looking through the clothing to find them), and after walking around twice, I was able to find the items I needed that fit me.

I went to check out and Kelsi was my cashier, she was quiet, but friendly. After scanning my items, she pulled a coupon out of the drawer and scanned it, giving me $20 off, score!! Such a nice action and increased my chances of coming back to Belk in the future!

Year 2 for Deadmonton Haunted House and this year Ryan Kozar stepped up his game. This year it is at the old Paramount Theatre on Jasper Avenue and the lines are treated better so you aren't standing in the cold as long. The guest are greeted with popcorn and in groups of 40 taken into the theatre by creepy ushers to watch a 5 minute short by Richard Lucas. Afterwards that 40 is lined up in groups to go through the haunt while the next 40 are being seated to watch the movie. 
The scares have also increased with many convincing, talented actors. Without giving anything away, the dread and ambience have had people screaming, shaking, crying, laughing and overall terrified. Not to be missed and I will be waiting for you inside.

I grew up in Florida and have been to many pollo tropicales and this is by far the worst one I have ever been to and clearly nobody cares after all the bad reviews they continue to do the same things you read in the comments. Service was terrible as mentioned before the guy taking the order literally rolled his eyes in front of me when I asked what is in the quesadilla wrap. My husbands chicken was not cooked all the way as mentioned in the other comments. Food was the worst tasting I've ever had from the chain. Does anyone care? I highly doubt it. Trust me take your money somewhere else that actually cares!

I love the space.  The manager is a gem.  Too bad the food just falls short.  

Service
I like a chatty waiter, but when it interferes with prompt service for us or other tables, I have to call foul.  Like some others, here, we had trouble getting more bread after the initial offering, and one in our party got to the "impatient point" waiting for a beverage refill. The waiter was hard to flag down, service was a bit slow -- and that might have affected our entrees (see below).

Salads
Endive & Arugula Salad was 95% Arugula and only 5% Endive.
The waiter brought more endive when we pointed out the issue.  

The Kale Salad with Anchovy dressing was good, but _very_ lightly dressed.  Some might want to ask for extra dressing on the side.

Pasta
Both pasta dishes came out lukewarm, dry, and congealed to solid masses -- they sat under the warming lights too long before being brought to the table.

The manager was no-nonsense and owned the problem with no hesitation. She apologized and took both dishes back to the kitchen.

Chitarra Aglio e Olio
This is a classic Italian comfort food equivalent to American noodles & butter.  Once the hot, non-congealed version arrived, it was comforting, but obviously not haute cuisine.

Bucatini Alla Carbonara
When I ordered, I told our waiter that carbonara is one of my test dishes for judging an Italian restaurant.  Tsk.  Even the fresh, hot version did not have enough liquid in it to allow the sauce to maintain its creaminess. Less than halfway through the dish, as it cooled, the sauce congealed onto the pasta.  Carbonara is thickened and flavored with egg yolk. You need to add extra liquid (cream or pasta water, usually) or it becomes like pasta in pudding rather than in a sauce. Finally, I do not know if it was the chef's spices or the spices on the guanciale, but something about the flavor (white pepper?) vaguely reminded me of KFC.  It wasn't bad, per se, but definitely not carbonara-like.

The manager comped our dessert and the dairy-free Fudgecicle (sorbet?) frozen dessert got rave reviews around the table.

As much as I like the place and manager, the mistakes are just too hard to ignore in a Pastaria. 

I might go back... 
They sell fresh uncooked pasta by the pound.  I might go back to buy fresh pasta to take home and prepare with my own sauces.
Busy spot! Food was delicious! No waiting around, which I loved that part. Hit this spot up for a great Mexican plate!
One of the best and most authentic Mexican food places around!  Very quaint, love the atmosphere.  If you have to wait, its well worth it

Camino Real is an okay shopping center and by no means a destination, unless you want to go to Costco from Santa Barbara.

Compared to other shopping centers in other cities, I consider this one bland in appearance with unremarkable stores.

There is a nice farmer's market twice a week though and I do like frequenting Costco. The Starbucks is pretty nice as well with multiple tables for outdoor seating. Other than that, Home Depot, Albertson's, Staples, Ross, and many of the other stores are just standard and most of the eateries are disappointing chains.

Oddly, "CAFE ZOMA" on the other side near Albertson's had been closed for over three years and nothing has replaced it! I look forward to seeing how this shopping center transforms. (A French Press is currently under construction across the street from the center, by Chipotle!)

I got their menu in the mail and decided to give them a try. The menu itself is very nice, I decided to try the seafood spaghetti and their wings. When I got my order I was disappointed. The spaghetti had maybe 5 small shrimps in it( they were cut in halves to look like there's more I guess) there were no crab meat at all. The entrée was 15.99 and it was totally not worth the money. The wings were decent ( they were 9.99 for 10) I guess my expectations were too high for this place, but I won't be patronizing this pats anymore.

We waited a while to get served, which is fine. The food was amazing although My rice and beans were served cold. Service was just extremely slow, no one came in to check on us/tell us that food was coming or sorry for the wait. The place looks run down and a little dirty. We got there before most of the people in our area and got our food last (even though the other groups were a larger party).

Sadly, disappointed by one of our favorites! They were out of all flatbreads. When I received my sandwich: it was thrown on to the plate; the bread wasn't even put together.
The lemon orzo soup: the lemon seeds were still in the lemon slices: no one wants to bite into a lemon seed when they are eating soup.
Panera is a great company, and we visit it all over the country. It needs to have consistent excellence in all their restaurants. This was far below the standard that we are used to.
Only 30 minutes of free wifi available.

The worst McDonald's I've ever been to.  Didn't learn the first time, and tried them for a second--I won't ever return here.  Slow.  Unfriendly.  Too many personnel, not doing anything, yet MANY people waiting in line to order, and for their orders to be provided.  Then, they aren't correct.

Absolutely DREADFUL. Overpriced, service left a lot to be desired for the price. Food is mediocre and embarrassing to be quite honest. Too much attitude for undertrained staff. Would I recommend this mostly canned food place? ABSOLUTELY NOT.

On my first visit to El Mercado San Agustin, I was a completely charmed. Yes, there were a lot of vacancies, but I had no trouble seeing where this little dining and shopping plaza was headed. Or imagining what a cool addition it will be to this still developing part of the Tucson's downtown. 

I just wish we didn't live way, way, way the heck over on the other side of Broadway! 

That first Sunday morning, a group of us ordered breakfast burritos from Taqueria El Pueblito and sat in the sun around the patch of grass in the courtyard listening to the Louisiana style ragtime tunes (accordion, washboard and all) of the Awkward Moments. It was just delightful.

The second time we visited was for Ana T.'s superb Juiceheads event. The small Thursday evening farmer's was still on and our group met in one of the commercial kitchens that can be rented by the hour by aspiring caterers and food vendors. Yep, Mercado Kitchen is a fully licensed and equipped commissary dedicated to fostering the growth of small businesses. Seriously, how cool is that? 

That night, after we were thoroughly "juiced," Debbi W. and I strolled over to drool over the cakes at Dolce Pastello. I expect my next visit to be a completely new and different experience. Seems like I heard something wonderful about pumpkin empanadas at La Estrella Bakery...

Food is good! Has super good drinks and the Mexican mule used to come in a cute little adobe pot (hoping the little pots will come back). My server Nina was very good and welcoming, also gave good recommendations. Some of the other servers I've had here were not quite as good. Really good salsa.

I ate here tonight and the experience compelled me to write a review. First, the wait staff and management are wonderfully friendly and accommodating! There wasn't a single employee who didn't have a smile on their face. 

Food service was very quick, and absolutely delicious. I gorged myself on biscuits and gravy; another notable mention is the smoked sausage. The strawberries in my strawberry lemonade were fresh and perfectly ripened. Seriously, the quality of food was awesome, far better than most chain restaurants. 

My only regret is I didn't leave room for dessert. The pie case looked amazing, and they've got ice cream shakes (which is something I've been longing for on this end of city ave for MONTHS). Looks like I've got a new Sunday breakfast spot!

walking around chinatown, we struggled to find a restaurant that could handle our group of 10, but they did so perfectly & patiently. we had shanghai pork dumplings, steamed veg dumplings, shanghai style udon, and sautéed rice noodles taiwanese style, all of which were great. prices are extremely affordable, and we would definitely come back.

For starters, I love the idea of being able to drink beer and just hang out and play games so I was probably already a little pre-sold before my first visit even started. 

Beercade is located upstairs and is right beside da-de-o's. The space, itself, is quite large and there is a lot of space to move around and mingle which I would assume is ideal on the weekend. I can see this place getting super busy (i went on a week night and it was slow). There are two bars, with the primary one being right in the middle of the venue. The outer space is surrounded by video games, everything from pool and air hockey to old school pin ball and shooting games. The bar has a decent size beer menu and their food is pretty good. We went on a Wednesday and it was 1/2 price burgers. 

I would say the only down side of beercade is that the games are a little pricey. You have to exchange cash for tokens and games are about a dollar or two depending on what you're playing. So if you're having drinks and a good time, it is super easy to rip through $20-40 on just games.

We wanted to give them a try because we live a few blocks away. The owner or operator came out to bring our dog in and was very kind. We showed a picture of what we were looking for and they did exactly that. Great job and very nice people. Will definitely be going there from now on.

My husband and I had a Wedding Rehearsal Dinner at Anthony's Cucina Fresca on July 10th for our Son and future daughter in law. I reserved the upstairs dining area for my Event. It was a party of approximately 20 people. The tables where spaced beautifully to fill the room with great social distancing. All the staff took all precautions wearing masks. The staff were very courteous and we received fantastic service. I had spoken prior with owner Danny and other staff members to ensure the evening went well. Everything was great from the spectacular appetizers at each table that we had discussed to be placed on tables when we arrived and the bread and olive& garlic dip to snack on while having a cocktail. Had time to open a few gifts and for the Bride and Groom to mingle with the quests. Then two to three servers brought all the salads to everyone. Spaced out and timed perfectly. Then again there were two to three servers to clear the tables and then to deliver our meals. We were not rushed at all and it seemed everyone were eating at the same time. I can't say enough about the wonderful staff they were checking to see if any additional cocktails were needed and refilling water glasses constantly. Everyone ordered off the menu and there were only great complements regarding how delicious all the meals were and especially the homemade pasta dishes. The serving size was plentiful that many guests had some left to take home to enjoy again. My husband and I were very pleased with Anthony's especially since the dining in had just started with the County just turning green. It was fantastic that we had such a wonderful Wedding Rehearsal Dinner Event especially during this COVID-19 Pandemic. Thanks again to Danny the owner and his wonderful staff. I would recommend having dinner or any event to all my friends and family. Sandy K.

Benchmark is awesome! 

I'll keep it short and simple this time and list the best parts about it. 

-convenient location on state street
-awesome fried chicken sandwich
-best Brussels sprouts ever 
-amazing service 
-great prices, much cheaper than places on the same level in LA 

I really have nothing bad to say, this place is great, wish I lived closer!
Great atmosphere for a touristy place! Kiwi frojito was amazing! Food was good and service was great!

In our household, Fridays are Pizza Fridays and we're not going to let a pandemic stop us! However, our usual spot was closed so we decided to try somewhere new. Chiquita's really surprised us! The pizza was pretty good - the sauce was sweet. I like my pizza to have a crispy crust so I just popped it in my toaster oven. We were depressed and ordered a bunch more food, fried Oreos (delicious!!), tiramisu (skip, it tasted like a refrigerator and nothing like tiramisu), and jalapeno poppers (good!). 

I can't wait to try their other menu items.
Went here for lunch and loved everything I ordered. A cute place, a bit pricey but it is delicious.

We just returned from a 4 night stay at Sofitel in Philadelphia and we couldn't be happier! We originally chose this establishment because we learned that they were dog friendly. When we pulled up to the hotel, we thought for sure we had made an error. The hotel was so beautiful and sophisticated, we thought we must have misunderstood their policies or walked into the wrong hotel. Sure enough, both my husband and I AND our dogs were greeted warmly and escorted to our rooms. Our pups were given treats upon arrival and they were always greeted with a smile and a kind word while entering and exiting the premises. 

The rooms were clean, modern and comfortable. The mattress and pillows were supremely comfortable (in fact, I snuck a peak at the labels and Im buying my own pillow and comforter sets for home!) Most importantly of all, each and every hotel personnel member we came into contact with, regardless of their role at the hotel, were genuinely kind and helpful, a delight to interact with. Lastly, the hotel was also very accommodating.  They allowed for an early check-in and slightly later departure and both check-in and check-out were quick and seamless.  We cant say enough great things about our stay here. We definitely intend on returning! Thank you Sofitel for making our vacation so comfortable and enjoyable!

I love love love this place. There are so many delicious pastries to choose from and I usually end up leaving with way more than I intended. It feels clean and smells so yummy as soon as you walk in the door. Highly recommend

Best beignets hands down.  We tried Cafe Beignets and Cafe Du Mond.  They each had their own take on it but my husband and I agree Loretta's takes the crown.  We tried the traditional and the chocolate filled.  Wish we would of had room for the crab Beignets.

I was so excited to come here. Our server Amy, with the thick clear glasses, appeared aloof and pretentious at the get go. We mentioned that we just moved to the neighborhood (aka potential regulars) and she appeared to care less for the following reasons: a group of 5 who came at least 30 min after us got their meal before us, and some she appeared to be friendly with, another two top got their food that came after us too. After about 50 min, when I asked when our appetizer was coming, she apparently gaslighted our table, saying "Didn't you see me talking to all those people that came in after you?!" as if we were the problem. Then when I tried to cancel the app, she said "it's coming in 2 min!" It came out about ten minutes later luke warm. Other two servers were running marathons around her. Left at around the hour mark with still no food, no asking for more drinks. Shocked. One of the worst experiences I've had in my 13 years living in Philly thanks to Amy.

I ate at Cochon after a good recommendation and seeing it on After Hours with Daniel.

We went on a Wednesday night, which was a great decision, not too busy. http://flic.kr/p/9uQPCY We started at the bar and I was indecisive so just had the cute brunette behind the bar pick a drink and she made this specialty drink with Whiskey and Orange Juice to start, kinda tasted like bad OJ at first, but after a few sips tasted All Good! I drank Napa Red Cabernet Wine through out the meal which paired well with the meat and kept the good vibes flowing.

Sampled 3 different appetizers, Fried Alligator, Rabbit Liver, and a daily special Pork with Fava Beans. All appetizers were appetizing and tasty! For the entrée I had the Louisiana Cochon with Turnips, Cabbage & Cracklins which tasted similar to a pork sandwich without the bread, topped with pork rinds. Don't let my description make it sound bad at all, it had better quality and flavors then any pork sandwich I have ever had. http://flic.kr/p/9uQQ7d

For dessert shared the Butterscotch Pudding with Pralines, WHOA!! Rich, a little sweet, and addictive!

We made friends with this French Canadian couple from Montreal who were RV'ing all over the U.S. and Mexico, and we all ate together and had a great time. http://flic.kr/p/9uQQyN

Service was attentive and food for five people came out at the right time, same time, and fast. No issues at all.

Watch After Hours with Daniel where they ate at Cochon: http://www.hulu.com/watch/22904/after-hours-with-daniel-cochon

Nobody wins here. I tagged along because my girlfriend wanted to go with relatives and the room is free. I enjoyed walking outside by the river more than this old place. Didn't gamble much because as everyone knows, casinos are there to leave you dry, money wise. Plus Reno is not as nice as Lake Tahoe, not much to see here. Even the soda vending machine puts a $3 hold on your purchase, and I got double charged when paying a split bill at an in casino restaurant. It was eventually resolved but I'd rather crawl over broken glass than come back here...
My new favorite Mexican joint! Amazing food and great service. So fresh and so clean.
The absolute worst bk i have ever been to. They burn the buns dont have food ready, food tastes really old. Its just gross. Horrible service.

My wife and I went to China Garden last night, and the food was TERRIBLE. ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE !
The General Tso's chicken was freezer burned, and hard as a rock.
The pork fried rice was SOAKED in dirty oil, and tasted mediocre , at best.
The chicken and mixed vegetables was under cooked. The little corn on the cobs were hard enough to launch out of a cannon.

The Spring rolls were the best of it.
And they are VERY SMALL and bland.

All in all, a DISGUSTING dining experience.

$31.00 down the drain.

If anyone wants to be disgusted, disappointed , and disillusioned,
this is the place for you !!!

Dan Dan was a very good experience . Service and food -excellent.
In appetizers cold cucumber is must order.
For Dinner entrees  we had Dan Dan noodles, double cooked chicken, spicy hot red pepper fish and scallion fried rice - all of them tasted good.
Bar is  well stocked. Noise level is high in dining area on upper level.

My brother wanted to check this out for his birthday.  I would never have found this place despite it being off of Rt. 30.  It is just another restaurant lost in the sea of other restaurants.  Nothing in the sign stood out for me.

For a burger place, I was not impressed.  The size of the patties were disappointing.  They reminded me of ones I could get at a fast food chain.  However, I did enjoy the milkshake.  Nothing too impressive from the restaurant's setup.

There are A TON of places selling "vintage" and/or "used" clothing, but it's rare you find a place with a selection this big for men. Lots of good designer stuff, and though you'll spend time going through the racks, it's worth it for the money you'll save. Like Nicole R's metrosexual brother, I definitely go to the Buffalo to get really sweet jeans for less than $20. I've also found some decent shoes there and, once, I bought this awesome vintage Mexico World Cup jacket for like $30 -- a third of the price they charge for the new World Cup jackets, and I've gotten 100x more compliments on it than the guys with the regular jackets. Once, in Mexico City, a taxi driver asked me if my father had been on the team. I told him he was a coach. I lied.

if you haven't experienced the Alexander Hotel, Indianapolis' swanky boutique hotel, you're missing out! I've had the pleasure of seeing it from various angles. You can't help but soak in the creativity that makes up this art hotel, as the 'Banksy' style graffiti hits you in the parking garage. Their installations are representative of what is cool and progressive in our fair city.

Plat 99, the sleek bar with gorgeous lighting that offers craft cocktails and scrumptous nibbles with a stunning view overlooking the South side of the city. Be sure to enjoy the patio when weather permits! 

Cerulean is a creative restaurant that encourages you to try new items. From the moment you walk around the spiral thatched art wall into the bright dining area, you know you're in for something unique. From Bento box lunches to the sea bass, everything I've had here has been delicious. 

Market Table is another restaurant inside the hotel that offers a beautiful breakfast and lunch buffet to order and has private space that can be reserved for small team to medium size working breakfast or lunch meetings with monitors that can be customized to your event.

I had the privilege of hosting a meeting in the Board Room on the sixth floor. This space is sure to impress your colleagues, with a stunning view of the city on three walls of windows, surrounded by a patio complete with seating. The Indiana weather was at its finest, transitioning from rain to clouds, snow and beaming sun throughout the day. I enjoyed watching the airplanes fly across the city scape towards the southwest side. The fourth wall was a fully functioning push pin board where we were able to hang flip charts and capture ideas, as we made our way through the day of strategic planning. 

The 'Nutrition hub' is a little bit of heaven on earth, seriously, I didn't select from a menu, but it was like they thought of everything. Teas, coffees, granola, yogurts, nuts, marshmallows, goldfish, fresh fruit, chocolate, and upon our return from lunch --- a GIANT bowl on Gummy Bears! Others, please take note, because what they're doing works. Assistance was merely a text away. 

I must say I was impressed with their exceptional service, style and execution of all they did for us and we didn't even stay overnight! Next time, definitely.

I met my friend at the Goleta Los Arroyos for Taco Tuesday for lunch!! I have been there several times and the food is always very good! Since it was Taco Tues and I am a light eater, I wanted a crispy chicken taco (yum!) I don't like rice or beans so wanted just a little side salad which was not listed on sides!
I usually order a one item combo with a chicken enchilada an salad in place of rice and beans for dinner. But since it was Taco Tuesday with tacos priced at $3.50..that is a good deal! But I was told I would have to order the one item plate because they don't sell side salads..hello! So I got my taco plate for $11.50 with a small portion of basic salad and my taco...which meant the taco was $3.50 and the handful of salad was $8.00...really!

From the moment you walk in Estia you feel as though you've left the city of Philadelphia and have entered a small Greek town; the energy, smell, and decor (I will revisit the decor later) of the place simply amazing. 

The greeters were very cheerful and welcoming. The barmaids were efficient, pleasant, and good mixologist.

Our waitress was very welcoming and even helped us properly pronounce the names of our meals using the native tongue, and she did so in a way that you felt the love for her culture and did not come off as belittling (which to me was very important and admirable) which helped me feel more open to try a meal out of my comfort zone. In this time I also noticed that the manager made his way to each table and greeted and thanked each guest; which to was a real class act; not staying long enough to overwhelm you, but interacting long enough to make sure you knew your visit was a valued one.

The food- Everything was amazing! As a group we ordered the Saganaki (breathe taking presentation), octopodi (done to perfection), horiatiki salata, lavraki (wonderful texture and great seasoning), lamb chops (the potatoes were taste great as well), papoutsakia (phenomenal for vegans and meat eaters alike), baklava (try to each layer at once there's a sweet taste below that brings it all together), and the olive oil cake (simply amazing); all of which I was able to enjoy and highly recommend to every guest.

The decor- Much like the restaurant it's self there a hidden well (literary) right in plan sight but can easily be missed or overlooked. There is also a staged seafood market and waterfall that you could very easily not see if you came in, had a seat and leave directly afterwards. If they could somehow space out the eating space that crowded this area everyone that enters could truly enjoy this hidden gem.

Overall- everything was great, totally worth every penny, and look forward to my next visit at Estia.

I had always wanted to go here because I had only really heard good things about this place. Our server was fantastic! She was very witty and her insults were very clever and funny. She definitely knew what she was doing. We had things thrown at us, the manager spilled napkins all over the floor, in our severs section and made her clean it all up. It was definitely entertaining, However the service would be the only thing I'd go back for. 
We tried the appetizer sampler which consisted of chicken wings, fried pickles, mozzarella sticks and chicken...bites I guess? Anyway, we had quite the tummy ache after we ate the chicken bites and the mozzarella sticks just tasted like they were defrosted and thrown into the microwave. I'm not generally a fan of fried pickles so I can't really say how they were supposed to taste or if they were good or not.  The wings weren't horrible, a little dry but that's about it. 
We each had a cocktail, they were pretty good and I'd go back there probably just to have drinks and like I said earlier, for the service!
First time trying a philly cheesesteak, expectations met and exceeded. Thanks!

Omg! Finally found my pizza place. We moved to the area dying to find real NY pizza. We've tried others and they either tasted like Pizza Hut or Papa Johns. This is by far the best pizza in Franklin. Spence is the best.

Wow, what to say about Pho Grand! The food is so fresh and tasty it is unreal. Not to mention the prices are beyond amazingly reasonable! Even though I live in the San Francisco Bay Area I have never found a Vietnamese restaurant I enjoyed this much!  The pineapple beef entree was so tasty! Even the wine prices are super reasonable. Now if I could only eat here on a regular basis I would be set!
Ryan was a great salesman and he did everything in his power to get me exactly what I wanted.

Love this place!!!  They have great food and drinks. Healthy pours!  I always get the half Cobb salad and the portion is more than I could ever eat and tastes fabulous. (Little fried shrimps  all over heaped with zucchini strings).  My husband has tried the lobster bisque and loved it. Today we had the grouper sandwich (shared) and the portion was so huge!!!  Any order served with a bag of hush puppies. Hot fresh and tasty!!!  Great atmosphere. Live music on occasion. One of our favorite places in Tarpon Springs!

I have now tried this place twice.  Both times I was incredibly disappointed by the quality of the food.  The staff was friendly and polite.  My friend sent back his cioppino because it was incredibly bland.  The fish tasted old.  He then ordered the calamari and couldn't even bite through it because it was so rubbery.  The presentation of the plate was probably the best thing about it.  Too bad it didn't taste as good as it looked.

Very disappointed drove straight in from Milwaukee to see my favorite band dude. They were to play 10-1pm
The bar manager elected to make them wait till a football game wss over. It is almost 11pm and they still haven't started 
Fi mb al score 17 to 38 
Not a vlose game they could have watched while band played 
Needless to say not my favorite bar by far.
Nice check-in. Hotel staff was pleasant. Everything else was just plain filthy. Don't stay here.
Awesome ice cream place with an extremely friendly staff and very lowfat soft serve

Himalayan yeti is one of my new favorite Indian restaurants in STL!  This place has some really good dishes and is in a great location!  They have off street parking which is super nice!  

I have ordered take out and ate in!  I have had the lamb and chicken tikka masala and both were great!  They give you big portions with lots of meat so they are worth it!  The jasmine rice is really good and fresh!  The food normally comes out pretty quick!  I also have had the garlic non!  

The reason for the three stars is because of the service!  The staff is slow and the food takes forever to come out!  Our waitress checked on us twice and said to us that our food should be out soon.  But both times she was shocked that it had not came out yet.  

I like this place and want to try the lunch buffet!  The food taste really good and the quality is great!  I am sure the buffet and all the other items are pretty good!  I will try the buffet soon and write back when I have tried it and let you all know what I think!!!! Till then go try it for yourself!!!

Stopped in for burgers and milkshakes. From the moment we ordered until we walked out the staff was awesome. 
Very friendly group of employees, definitely will be back.

This is a great kid friendly place. Always clean and lots of parking in the back. The burgers are good, or a fan of their air sled Frieda, always seem to come out Burnt. We partook in the free shakes on earth day.

The best part of this stay was the van driver . He was like a tour guide and happy to tell you all about the key points of interests. He even went beyond the range of driving to help me ! The views are great , the location is nice . Clean . God breakfast for what it is . The worst part is CNN playing all the time in the lobby . I have to wonder if CNN pays hotels and airports to air them on stop . It is really annoying .

I loved this place! It's a quick walk from the main Paso Nuevo shopping center/State Street. If you like matcha/vegetarian/health food this is for you. Had enough seating and a casual environment. I got the raw fudge bar, vegan Mac & cheese, Green Table burger and salted caramel matcha latte. All were tasty, satisfying and made with good ingredients. Just like all vegan food, be cautious and ask if you are allergic to any nuts, as this place uses a lot of nut-based ingredients. Overall, definitely happy I got to go to this place and wish there were more places like it!

I give Westworld five stars for great service to me personally, and to my dental clinic for the past 20 years. Recently we installed 12 Mac minis and an iMac at my clinic along with 24 monitors. They came in and delivered everything on time, and installed everything for us on time and on budget.  

We were having to use a Windows product for our digital X-ray so all of these computers needed Software bridges and a lot of expertise to get the Mac and Windows sides to work together.  They helped train us and were on site multiple times to hold our hands during the implementation of our new equipment and Software. 

Westworld couldn't have been more supportive and I thank them for going the extra mile.  All hard costs and labour and terms were estimated up front by Calvin Anderson our representative at Westworld, and he was not only helpful during the purchase - he was even on site during install to make sure everything went smoothly. 

I especially encourage businesses to seek out Westworld for their Mac computer and computer support needs.  They can handle everything from setting up a printer to very complicated and large networking Mac systems. 

Five stars for sure.  Give them a try.

Oh my word. This place is hog heaven. Not for hogs, of course, cuz they're getting smoked out back. But all manner of cooked hog--bacon, sausage, country ham, pulled pork--is here for you. We also spent some time talking with Eric, the pitmaster. He brought us into the smoke house and talked with us about the process, showing us pork butts, briskets and chickens in the smoker. This dude loves his job and does it well.

Back inside, they gave us a mess of biscuits to start. After we were done they gave us another mess of biscuits to go, along with the preserves. Here's a tip. Ask for the sorghum for your biscuits. And while you're at it, mix a little in with your grits. Oh my word.

The only true 5 Star Resort on the central coast. Staff is very friendly and service oriented. From the people at the valet to the house keepers, all were super attentive. Food at the Angel Oak is excellent. Everything tasted very fresh.  We had an ocean view room. It was lovely listening to the waves every morning. The beach isn't much to speak of, very rocky and warning signs of tar, but the pools make up for it. There are three pools. One is for kids, one is for adults and the large one is for everyone. The spa is a must. We got the 80 minute massage and it was amazing. They have first class facilities. You get an assigned locker with robe and sandals and have a sauna and steam room to use before and/or after your spa treatment.  For breakfast they have a buffet, which has everything from scrambled eggs to an omelet station, smoked salmon, large variety of meats, pastries, and fruit. You will not go hungry.  This is a piece of paradise on the central coast.  Come and stay at least two nights to fully enjoy what Bacara has to offer.

I always enjoy my visits to Southwest Market - The owner is always so pleasant and friendly upon entering.  In fact, all the employees are nice.  

Love the vibe of the place - little old school spot on the Hill with lots of locals and newbies too.  Great sandwich options - 3.5 inch sandwiches and up - just how hungry are you?!  

Roast beef on garlic cheese bread is so yummy! Great selection of drinks, chips, and sides. It's clean and inviting !! Go see for yourself!

Come to this spot if you're looking for an authentic experience!! Delicious food with kind, quick service. Will be back for their Pupusa and tacos!! Can't wait for covid to be over so we can enjoy their food with a corona or modelo in the dining room :)

I don't know why good service is so damn hard to find in Boise.  This place is no exception to the prior statement.  Staff tends to be inattentive whether it be in the bar or in the dining area.  We love the Happy Hour deals here.  We don't really visit when it's not happy hour.
Awesome place...great laid back atmosphere, staff is super friendly and the food is awesome.  Get the carrot cake- OMG.   Drove up from Delaware and it's def worth the ride.

Love this location! Easy to get in and out and always the nicest baristas! Their service is exquisite and they get to know their customers.  It's fun to see people called by name. 

The only thing better would be a drive thru! Definitely stop in and enjoy their relaxed atmosphere and friendly staff!!!

I work in Ybor City and have food delivered when i don't bring my own. I cannot leave work for my lunch unfortunately so I use delivery a lot of the time. I usually get the same stuff over and over again. 

Someone from The Stone Soup Company dropped off a stack of menus last week and I decided to give it a try this evening. I ordered the Fried Buffalo Bird and some Ybor chili. It didn't take long to get my food from when my order was confirmed (about 15-20 minuets). It was hot and smelled good!

I opened the tray with my Buffalo Bird sandwich thinking it would be like everything else I get delivered but was very surprised with how fresh it was. The Cuban bread was warm and soft and the insides (meat,slaw,fries) were great! I cannot put into words how tasty this sandwich was! The freshness and flavors were wonderful! I actually called them because I was so pleased with this! 

I will definitely order delivery again when I'm working and if I'm down here on one of my days off, this is a place I will go out of my way to stop by!

This Home Depot is awesome. All the associates are super helpful and go above and beyond to assist people. The garden center is pretty and well watered. Always greeted at the door. Can get in and out with all the options of checking out.

This place is a solid 4 stars!

It's located in Santa Barbara's Public Market and is right by one of the entrances. Parking can be found (if you're lucky) in their underground parking lot. Just take the elevator up and it'll spit you out right beside the market place. 

There is a little seating around the side of the restaurant and you can also find a bar of salsa's, lemons and limes. Water is also provided with little cups.

Food: I wanted something different so I tried the Tia Juana ($7.95) - Spanish Octopus, wild white shrimp, avocado and Anaheim Chile. The octopus was fresh and cooked perfectly - super tender and not chewy! And I would say the taco was a bit on the spicy side! 

My fiance wanted something normal so he got the Al Pastor ($4.95) - Roasted pork marinated in adobo with pickled habanero-pineapple salsa. The taco was spicy and the meat was tender but I preferred my octo taco!

It took maybe 10 minutes before our order was ready, which was fine because we got to walk around and look at the different restaurants (also noting the ones we wanted to try).

After shopping at the nearby Kroger several times, I finally decided to stop in here. I was a bit put off by the Yelp reviews and the generic outer appearance but was pleasantly surprised by the service and food here. First of all, they have a pretty good happy hour for drinks. $1 draft beer in a large frosted mug and $12 margarita pitchers. I haven't seen prices like that since I graduated college 10 years ago. Second, I ordered a chicken dish and was blown away when they came out with this huge plate of food that I could barely finish. Having lived in Seattle for some time, I'm not used to getting so much for ~$10.

Worst rental place on Earth! Horrible customer service, horrible wait times! Highway robbery! If this is the only rental place with a car you are better off walking! We will never use them again! Awful awful awful!

Good first visit. Good was hot, tasty, and served fast. Waiter was knowledgeable, accommodating, and very attentive without being a pest. My dish wasn't as spicy as I'd have liked or thought it should be but was good. Can't wait to visit again.
SUPERB WORK on my car friendly staff Scott is really nice will definitely be my body shop in the future if I need any work done on my vehicle!

The man is a crook! He has taken for $100s over several occasions he has worked on my vehicle. Quoting one price and always being higher when the job is done. Last time he did work he put my brake pads on backward and almost burned up my breaks and rotors, on top of that he did warranty for a bent axle and kept the money LKQ sent for the parts I purchased. Over $240 worth. Spend your money at your risk with this place.

This bar is tucked away on the 4th floor of this hotel. There is outdoor seating, which is nice when it's not too hot out. It's not rooftop seating but still nice to have. There are formal tables and some lounge chairs as well. Atmosphere is casual but felt upscale but didn't feel like that I didn't belong in my jeans. 

During happy hour, it's a happening spot! Half off of most drinks and appys. It's from 3-5p at the date that I'm writing this. We got cheese curds, olive tapenade and shrimp cocktail. All were wonderful. 

I'm giving a 4 out of 5 on this because it took about 20 minutes to get our drinks with no explanation. Our food came out before the drinks, which was super disappointing. The bar was fully staffed and there were plenty of waiter staff on board. Just wish I knew why it took so long!

A cute little cafe in Old City that is well-decorated, clean, and just all-around adorable. To be honest I've only been once and I suppose the item I ordered (a canelé) wasn't what this place is known for, but the experience was wonderful and it definitely makes my list for Old City.

The service was solid, as the cashier and the owner were friendly, approachable, and engaging without being overbearing. The decor was a delight in and of itself, especially the Simpsons' lego house, which the owner said he put together himself.

If ever in Old City again, I'll definitely stop by for the fro-yo!

Came in during an extremely busy lunch hour and was told 15 minutes before they'd have a table ready.   It wasn't even 5 before the manager herself jumped in a table, bused it, and took our drink orders.   Service was fantastic and the food was fresh and fast.

I usually get the burger from here, I ordered the double cheese burger. I was expecting it to come with lettuce, tomato and onion but nothing. The burger was okay for Lansdale. The pizza was nice, the sauce was more on the sweet end I thought. The staff was nice, the delivery guy was super nice.

First-time (non) customer - dropped in on a quiet Monday night for a quick trim. Was told it would be an hour wait as only 2 barbers working and 2 clients waiting and that they would schedule me in "next" - which was fine as I had time to kill. Came back an hour later with the previous clients already gone...and told it would be a further 25 mins and that someone else had magically been pushed up the wait list without explanation or apology. Sadly free beer and button back arm chairs don't make up for sloppy service and wasted time. Safe to say, I will not be a future customer. 

As an aside, I was willing to try this Scouts on friends recommendation and after a succession of mediocre hair cuts at the East Nashville shop. Frankly, I'm a big fan of their design concept but feel Scouts is more style than substance!
Found Ben through Yelp. All of his rekeying was perfect; smooth operation. He also fixed a safe lock and tool chest. He was friendly, reliable and professional.

I've now been to all 3 locations, and I can certify that they do awesome brunch. The center city location has the added bonus of a liquor license. I've had the tofu scramble with vegan chorizo and didn't leave a crumb. Also had the veggie benedict on a yummy brioche. And while I usually don't like too sweet of a french toast, the apple pie style was melt-in-your-mouth delicious. I will go back here any chance I can.
Yoooooo... Kay's Kitchen is legit. I ate there 2 days in a row just make sure I wasn't tripping.

This is an amazing place. If you're a woodworker there is no lack of inspiration here. It can be a little tricky walking around but the staff is very friendly and willing to help out. Can't wait to go back to take it all in again.

This was our first time purchasing a new travel trailer. Every step of the process was seamless and professional. Austin walked us through all of the gadgets on our trailer before we even sat down to start the application process. By the time we started paperwork, we knew this was the trailer for us! 
When we picked up the trailer; Jr was friendly, professional and courteous. He showed us all the in's and out's to maintaining and owning our trailer. Craig had our final loan docs prepared and we breezed right through them and we were quickly on our way to our first adventure as campers!
The entire gang at Western Skies RV were an absolute delight to work with, and we would highly recommend them to anyone!

UPDATE....So after posting my last review about Thompson Cigar Outlet a few days ago I received a call from their Social Media representative named Bianca. Bianca (I hope that's her real name) wanted to know if I had written the review on Yelp. I replied back with a yes and then she continue to ask me if I still wanted to cancel my club membership. At that point I finally felt like I was talking to someone who wanted to help me out instead of selling, pushing or locking me with something I didn't want. Bianca guaranteed me that my membership was going to be canceled and that no recurrent charges will be applied to my credit card. I was mentally ecstatic at what I was hearing and what was happening. This demonstrated to me that Thompson Cigar does care for their customers. Thompson Cigar needs more people like Bianca to help save this business reputation and keep customers happy. Thompson Cigar Outlet has a lot of my favorite cigars at an economical price. All I want is to continue to buy my favorite smokes from them but at my own pace. I'm currently running low on my favorite smokes so it time for me to place my order. Thank You, Bianca .......Another happy customer...Now let the smoking begin and be worried free....

This place is awesome! What a great find! The food rocks, ten times better than Rathskeller, and ten times cheaper two.

They lose one star for not having much seating. It's debatable whether the atmosphere is a win or lose. Has more of the feel of a store than a restaurant, but if weird dolls don't bother you, I think you'll appreciate the uniqueness of it all.

Also- try their desserts! So good!

My son loves this place and  I recently treated him to lunch here. Being a vegan I really didn't think that there would be anything for me to eat, but was  I ever wrong! They happily turned their veggie sub (no cheeses) into a salad for me, by putting it on a nice bed of spring mix salad greens. It was delicious and best of all... only 4 dollars! 
It had a nicely seasoned mix of red sweet peppers, hot peppers, onions, tomatoes, mushrooms,  broccoli rabe, summer squash and zucchini! 
I've had salads 3 or 4 times the price that didn't have the quality of ingredients much less the flavor of Frankie's. Thank you for accommodating me.  I'll be back! 
My son happily and rather quickly devoured his 9" cheesesteak and fries with no complaints! 
The staff was friendly and the facility was clean!

Oh, Coffee Cartel, how I love thee...from the Chi Frappes to the Sweet Cream lattes, you just cannot be beat. 

I have been frequenting Coffee Cartel since about 2008 and I've never ever had an issue with them. The coffee price is a bit steep but in my mind it's worth it to keep such a place in business. 

Sure you get the occasional rowdy teens and homeless folks but it's a city location so you have to keep everything in perspective. If you want a place to work by yourself at 12pm or meet with friends at 12am, get thee to this blessing of a coffee establishment.

Sorry I just don't get this place. Food was okay (sauage lentil soup, ham & cheese sandwich plus potato salad that I paid extra for), but extremely simple and uninspired. The atmosphere good old boy cheap. What can I say it can be another 8 years before I come back.

My partner and I had two amazing meals with friends at the new IndeBlue - Midtown Village. The entire experience from arrival to departure was great both times!
Tobi the Bar Manager made us feel welcome and made great drinks. We sampled specialty drinks that were innovative and delicious - He was also very interesting to speak with while waiting for friends. Maura the Manager is a "Switched" on professional who was at our table at just the right times to check on us and we really felt looked after. Our servers have been wonderful both times.  Justin really introduced us to the concept and the menu on our first visit, and Jarrod did an amazing job when six of us returned and tried the four course tasting menu -DELICIOUS and so reasonable! I could go on and on about the food because everything was so memorable. For example - the Spinach Chaat was one of the most interesting dishes I have had in an Indian cuisine restaurant. The Lamb Shank plates looked as though they were licked clean by all six of us. This restaurant has a true vibe, the minute that you walk in the front door. From styling to music, lighting to layout, it is the type of place you feel at home in immediately! I Might stop in tonight and here their live Sitar musician who I believe is there every Friday night!

Deep down I am cheap, so I am always looking for a bargain. We love going here because it's only $3 during the day and $4 at night. We dont mind waiting a little while to see movies since these arent first run. But the price of tickets and the cheaper concession prices make it worthwhile. It is a little older and could use some fixing up but I think that's part of the charm.

Even the birds turned their beaks up at these bagels. What a dissapointment. Overated and way overpriced. Bagels taste like stale irish soda bread. Hoped that they would taste better toasted. Not a chance. Bought a half dozen anticipating they would be great bagels. Line out the door. I wish them well. They said they can't keep up with the demand. No way would I go back. I would rather starve than eat one of these again.

We have a very positive experience for my son's first haircut. He's a very squirmy toddler and Gabriel was very patient. Lots of toys to play with and a TV with kids shows to help keep them entertained. So pleased with my sons adorable new haircut. He looks great. Would definitely recommend this place if you're nervous about bringing your little one in for a trim.

Hubby and I were on a road trip and decided to try out Peg's due to the number of 5 star reviews. 

We were there on May 18. Needed something other than overpriced hotel vittles (we were staying at the Grand Sierra). Arrived at around noon and it was pretty packed, as expected. The quoted wait time to be seated was about 20 minutes, but we were taken to our table in maybe 5. Lots people going in and out, which was encouraging. 

The dining area was crammed, and at first I was relieved when the hostess took us to what I thought was a back dining room. It turned out to be what looked like a repurposed storage area right by the bathrooms. It didn't smell gross or anything, but seating back there was tight and the bathroom door kept hitting the back of my chair. 

Service was really good though, despite the ambience and initial shock of being seated so close to a bathroom. Coffee cup never went empty, OJ is fresh squeezed. Hubby's Mexican omelette was enormous and my corned beef hash quite tasty, with the eggs cooked as requested (over easy). 

Good place if you're looking for something a little different (they offer Mexican and Hawaiian twists on standard run-of-the-mill American greasy spoon breakfast options). Definitely not what I would consider fancy (which is good since I'm not a snobby connoisseur of fine dining) and while the food is good, it's nothing spectacular. Average. Just don't put me near the can again, heh.
Very good food, service was quick and a calm environment. First time trying raw sushi and this place made it a great experience.
Great food at reasonable prices.  Live music without a cover charge.  Service was great too.  Another good time at Peppes

I checked this new clothing store out during the chaos of PoBoy Fest. But made it back today to score some excellent local tshirts as Christmas gifts.  They have a couple of fun non-official saints shirts, one with wild things & another that says Jesus loves the Saints.  I did not really peruse the consignment clothes as I was on a mission but will definitely be back once the holiday budgeting is over.
this is my favorite dog park near where i live! clean, abundant trash and water stations, and full of space. sushi loves it!
My friends and i go 1-3 times a week  greatest fried chicken in Florida. Wish i started my rewards points earlier because I'd be well over 10 points

I had a few recommendations from friends who are very loyal to a particular stylist at hair garage and the whole place. So I was open to purchasing a deal through living social.

My appointment was first thing in the morning on a Saturday and they were playing the hardest techno ever, super loud! Throw that in with the hair dryers and tugging of my hair made me leave with a headache for some peace and quiet!

My stylist was a total sweetheart and I did like the cut and attention she gave me. However a stylist at another chair was having major attitude with the other staff and his client. I was thankful I got my stylist to do my cut!

I don't think I would go back here, kinda disappointed me. The whole experience overall was underwhelming. Also for such a small place (the wash stations are literally right beside the front counter and couches) I'm not sure why they have gigantic vending machines in there. Totally odd! Also they don't accept debit, but they do have yet another machine for ATM right beside the desk and wash stations. If they got rid of all these machines and the obnoxious music at 10am, it could be 100% better! Clients want to relax! I personally think they have all these machines in there to make extra money. Kinda offensive.
Everything about this place was amazing. A great date-night choice for authentic Asian cuisine that provides you with elegant food and service. Interesting cocktails and amazing sushi choices!

Just ordered Fajitas Iztaccihuatl, pechuga primavera, and alambre paisano. Plus I literally just looked this place up last minute. Great find in South Philly and would definitely recommend this restaurant. No dine-ins though. Fast service.
The only way to travel to the JD Distillery from dtwn Nashville! Super easy to book. Impeccably organized and comfy. I would use their service over and over, anytime I'm in Nashville.

I'm going to start this off by saying that the food was a 5/5. I ordered Chicken Katsu and Kalua Pork with French Fries and Hawaiian Macaroni Salad. My boyfriend got Kalua Pork with Hawaiian Macaroni Salad and White Rice. It was all very delicious, wonderful portions for the price and was warm on delivery.

My problem? The delivery took 2 hours, when the location is only 10 minutes away from me. I called them after an hour and 10 minutes and they said that the delivery girl had JUST left with our order. She had apparently been out with another delivery when our order was placed. When she did show up, she was very apologetic that it had taken so long.

I do understand that things like that happen, BUT if you can't handle delivery traffic then don't deliver. I would have really appreciated a call explaining the situation, instead of having to call them myself (I gave my number with my order). 

I've had Mama's Hawaiian BBQ before on a different date where we picked it up, and the food was excellent then as well. But they need to work on their delivery service. We would really like to try ordering delivery from them again and hope that next time our experience will be better.

Business owner said they would contact me, but never have. Would love to at least be assured this wouldn't happen again if I order delivery.
