Merridee's Breadbasket is in a great location, situated within the historical downtown and near other shops.  Parking was easy.  In a cool, older building, the decor is fun, a great small town feel to it.  We really liked the look of it.  The staff was exceptionally friendly, especially the young lady who took our order.  The food itself was nothing special.  We ordered sandwiches, which were TINY and just "okay."  We both had sides, which again, just "okay."  The cost for the food we received was abnormally high, especially for this kind of place.  We may visit Franklin again, but we probably won't be adding Merridee's Breadbasket to our itinerary.

This is a great place to take the family for a good steak and even has tasty options for non steak lovers...although my family doesn't fall into that category! 
The staff is friendly and the meals are prepared very nicely. Whatever you do, don't turn down the rolls that come with your meal and are given hot when you're seated! It is a little taste of heaven served with homemade cinnamon sugar butter...oh my!

I hate to be this girl but today I'm going to be her. I dropped off a winter coat to have a zipper fixed back on January 11th....I was told it would be done by the 19th and if there were any issues they would call me...never got a call....was told the coat would be done by Saturday I called them today January 26th and guess what the coat isn't done and they don't even have it.....explain to me how it takes so long to repair a zipper and how if there are so many issues why that doesn't warrant a call to the customer? At this point I asked them if I could just get my coat back and I'll find another place...nope can't even get my coat back yet.

I'll be drowning my frustration in a bottle of wine and cursing my bad luck with zippers because this has been insane.

Tried the pork chop here which was good. A lot of things on the menu also were interesting and I was eyeing some of the dishes other people ordered. Place was fairly busy at about 12:30 and they didn't have tables available, but you can order at the bar. Only comment was that the pork was perhaps a hair over cooked, but not enough to be a major issue. I'd come back to try the tacos next time.
I just love this this shop and food and staff. I just can rarely go bc the hours are so limited. Wish you all were open late one night a week.

I'm not sure how there are people reviewing this place so high. I did not think it was very impressive.

My mom and I stopped in to Taste on a Saturday around 1pm. The line was almost to the outside. We waited for about 20 minutes to put our order in. Not too horrible, they seemed to move the line decently fast for how many people were there. It did make me wonder why they didn't have both cash registers open. 

I got the turkey and bacon on french bread and a vanilla latte. My mom got something with roast beef and their pommes fritas. We also ended up sharing a slice of chocolate cheesecake. It ended up costing about 33 dollars.

The sandwich was not all that great. It wasn't bad, but it wasn't amazing. For 8 dollars, I expected a lot more. The 4+ dollar vanilla latte had way too much foam. The cup was decently large and was over 1/2 filled with foam. I understand latte's have foam, but the foam isn't supposed to be greater than the drink. The pommes fritas were pretty good, I really liked the dip that it came with. The cheesecake was so-so, it was fluffy. I like denser cheesecake. 

The water was awful. I'm a big water drinker and their water tasted like it sat outside in the heat for an hour or two. I didn't taste any of the fruit they had in the pitcher. It just wasn't anything special.

I had asked one of the waitresses if they had any to-go boxes. She kept walking and didn't acknowledge me saying anything. So, I was about to ask someone else and she brought out a box. It was good that she got what I wanted, but I would have liked to know that she had heard me. 

I probably will only go back to Taste for the fries. I would not recommend it to friends unless they went with me to get the fries.

I'm glad people are reviewing this money-grubbing useless company. Like previous reviewers I am also charged an extra $25/month for this service so that my apartment complex can make more money off me. You'd think with the pet fee, processing fee, etc. they'd have enough extra cash but I guess not. Anyway, Valet Waste is a joke. And anyone who rents in Tampa with a complex (not an independent landlord) can probably agree. 

Yep, people don't abide by the rules and leave their trash out. Then, Valet Waste often doesn't even come as promised. Or perhaps they're just lazy since I'm on the third floor. Recently, a neighbor left his trash bag full of ribs and fried chicken outside and it was never picked up by VW as promised. But what happened next was pretty bad: a feral cat found his way up to the trash, ripped it open, munched on the food and left a trail of sticky food all over the walkway in front of my door. Of course the neighbor never cleaned it up nor did Valet Waste so I had to pick up the remainder of my neighbor's (and the cat's) dinner plus various dirty diapers, etc. on my own. How's that for a visual? The stain is still there too! Since I usually leave for work before sunrise, it's not uncommon to see cats ripping trash out of various neighbor's bags while I walk to my car.

My apartment complex, and I'm sure many others, has set ridiculous standards so that they can fine those who actually use the trash bins. Since the pickup guys usually don't come until after 9pm (or later) I often don't pick up the bin until the morning. But surprise! No empty bins allowed outside in the AM. I was fined another $25 a few months ago. 

Why must I be charged for someone to sporadically pick up my trash when the dumpster is less than 30 seconds from my door, right downstairs? Geniusly evil idea by this company to work with greedy apartment management and profit off renters. Valet WASTE is truly a great name for this company.

The food was absolutely amazing here. One of my friends got the southern fried chicken and it was so delicious. The gravy was awesome and I could tell everything was made fresh. I got the dish with ham, poach eggs, and green fried tomatoes. It was so good, wish I had the appetite to eat it all. Service was good as well. It's a cute little place to go eat brunch.

I was in the mood for Mexican food and booze (a common problem for me) and since I was in the Quarter, I decided to drop in. I have been here before, and while the margaritas were good, the queso fundido con chorizo and the el gato negro dip had been average. But when you need tequila and tacos, what are you gonna do? 

This trip definitely bumped up their rating for me. I had their house margarita, which is orange and lime, and it was love at first sip! I wanted to crawl into my big margarita glass and have a refreshing swim cuz it was a hot Nola day. 

While the people next to me were having guacamole made fresh in front of my jealous eyes, I decided to stick with my order of tacos. (I had, after all, just unhinged my jaw for the basket of chips and salsa, so I probably didn't have a ton of room left.) I ordered my tacos wrapped in a corn tortilla: chorizo, pulled pork, and fish. The chorizo and pulled pork were very tasty and the meat wasn't dried out. I still had some salsa left, which was a nice addition to the onions and peppers. Now, let me preface this with: I turn my nose up at fish tacos. I know, I know, some of you have mouths agape at this, but I just do. So, I have no idea why on earth I ordered one, but I did, and I am now a convert. It was the best taco out of the three, with the fish seasoned and cooked so perfectly. This experience even made me order fish tacos at French Quarter Fest! It's just unheard of. 

So I give them four stars now, and will reserve the fifth for my next visit when hopefully I will try one of the dinners that I saw the next table raving about.

OMG, I forgot 2 add this incredible spot! I have been a fan of Kabuto for 14 yrs! I know the date b/c I had my wedding reception here. I have eaten here 3x a week when my wife got pregnant and it's my child's favorite Asian spot! I've never had a bad meal here!

Stopped here briefly for a giant fishbowl which we had in the back patio area. It was a great drink to split among a group. We basically only went here as a pre-game because of its prime location. There is a lot of very cool artwork on the walls, hence the name. Didn't try any of the food so I can't comment on that.
Great service, good food. It's seat yourself, so head straight back to the patio if you want to sit outside. Several good beers on tap & grouper specials daily.

I was at the convention center and so chose to take a day where I ventured off the opposite direction of the typical New Orleans "flow".  The Garden District.... From the Convention Center you kind of walk down some weird streets and sidewalks and under the highway, but the neighborhood is very quaint.  Its truly old-school New Orleans and its sad to see some of the beautiful houses falling down but a delight to see the ones that they've fixed up.

I should say right up front - make sure you have cash!  No credit cards and I didn't realize until they seated me facing a sign that said "CASH ONLY - You can use our ATM"  which charges you $2.50.  BUT, as I never carry cash, I had to use their ATM as there was nothing else around.

I ordered the Omelet with avocado and the lumpcrab sauce.  I asked them to not put as much of the brie cheese in - I wish I had asked them to just keep it out.  It sounded great, but when I got to the little section where the brie was, I think the dish kind of flopped for me.  However - the crabmeat sauce was amazing and the avocado omelet with it was divine!  The omelet was not piping hot, but I've not found a lot of restaurants this time round in New Orleans where my food has been hot.  Not sure what thats about.  But - Coffee was great and hot, orange juice was fresh.

Seating was immediate and so was service.  Everyone was friendly.  A very unique atmosphere and just a cute little restaurant in a cute, unique area of the Garden District.  The only detractor would be just that I was not prepared for cash only so it took me a little by surprise.

McDonald's!

What?!?!  You can't go wrong with McDonald's.  That is, if you like McD's.  

The food there is good as usual.  Service was quick and friendly.

This one was a little different though.  I've never been to a McDonald's  where there was a large space between the cashiers' station and the kitchen/serving area.  I mean...there was enough room for a 2-way wheelchair "traffic".

Overall, good.

I'm an Allstate roadside assistance customer....and what a surprise it was to see a pop-a-lock car pull up after my dumb-dumb moment of running out of gas! Grrrrr, but anywho. I called Allstate after reading being out I gas and they informed me that it'd be like 45 minutes till someone showed up...and after using roadside assistance from State Farm it had taken that long and even longer before, I expected to wait! But no, Barnell, our pop-a-lock assistant was there in about 15-20 minutes!! Needless to say, I'm super satisfied! This guy was so cool and friendly! Pop-a-lock should be proud to have someone with his CSR skills as an employee! I'm just sayin! Lol. Great employee! Great first experience!

Great family-owned business that cares about their customers and the community.  If you need a bike, accessories, repairs....or have an old bike you would like to donate to charity this is the place!   Love the location near the Monon Trail as well. Last time I needed my tire repaired- I was in and out and back on the trail.    Highly recommend them.
Menu looked great. Poor execution. Muscles were too salty and my wife's pork could have used some salt.... it was bland. My soft shell crabs were just okay.

We waited for about 10 min to be seated which was pretty fast on a long weekend. The servers weren't exactly the happiest staff in the world. I ordered a smoked salmon eggs benedict and it was delicious as usual! I asked for my eggs poached medium so I was disappointed when I cut into my egg and nothing came out. It was cooked all the way through! Fail! The smoothie was a bit too sour. Overall, an average performance. I've been to better Coras.

The best bubble tea place in Philly! My favorite is the classic black bubble milk tea. My friends also like the honey jasmine, fire dragon, coconut milk tea, just to name a few. I'm sure everyone can find something they like here. The bubbles are a MUST with any drink! They are so soft and chewy. Yummmm. The onigiri is also good! Def recommend!

First let me say this is the FIRST review ive EVER written. PLEASE FOR THE LOVE OF GOD DO NOT RENT FROM THESE PEOPLE!!!!!!!! I honestly don't know where to begin but here are SOME of the things that happened to me while I lived there:
1. My air went out 3 times within the first month I was there. When I complained about it the fact MY SON HAS ASTHMA and cant be in the heat, the owner said "there are a lot of people without air. God doesn't do favors." I swear to God this man is a heartless, selfish prick. 
2. My son got an EMOTIONAL SUPPORT animal because of his disability and they tried to kick me out (which they couldn't do legally). 
3. The owner told me to go to hell and hung up in my face when I tried to explain to him the rules under the ADA that state emotional support animals aren't pets.
If I could give this no stars I would. Please don't rent here. I know the price is great but they could care less about their tenants. If you do decide to rent here...well...I tried to warn you.

Just what I want a brunch place to be. It's chilly outside and the tomato soup and grilled cheese special was perfect. The place has a cute little key west vibe going on, and really friendly people working there. It's on a nice small town street with antique shops tight there.  Definitely recommended.
Cheap pub food with a twist. Nothing special. Good beer and service. Not bad. Pretty good beer list and sizeable cocktail list. If you want a gastro pub this isn't it.

Johny Carino's pre pandemic use to be one of my favorite place to dine out, I visited this location after year and it was not up to my expectations. I understand all the businesses are running short on staff but quality of food is one parameter which can't be a let down. During my visit I saw some guests getting mad and leaving the place might be due to delay in serving but out server was trying really hard and I have a huge respect for all of them, it's not a easy job. We ordered Italian nachos, house salad, lemon rosemary, minestrone soup,  pasta and chicken nuggets and fries. Out of all the menu items we ordered minestrone soup was too salty for our taste and itialian nachos were also not up to the mark. Over all an ok experience.

Dianna is a life saver! We came to NOLA for a wedding and unfortunately ended up at a different salon before coming to Salon D. After the bride, her sister, and her mother came out looking like clowns and hair falling out (5 min after the do was done), we were in need of a miracle. We called Salon D and Dianna was able to help us immediately! The ladies at salon D were so accommodating and sweet. They gave us a much needed glass of wine and saved the bride! Merci beaucoup Salon D NOLA!

It's Long's Donuts...enough said. Seriously though this place is an Indianapolis landmark for donuts. They are the best to me when picked up early in the morning still warm. The place is nothing fancy on the outside and only takes cash. However the quality of the bakery items, along with the seemingly ridiculous low price, makes this one my fave. I live too far to go there alot but I do whenever I get the chance. 

Also expect a line out the door but it moves fast.

Peace

I've been eatting here awhile and on multiple occasions they've forgotten to bring me an item when I placed an order for delivery. The foods good but it's inconvenient to have a steak and no drink. I gave them a chance but they forgot again. They forgot to bring my 2 liter. The driver told me he'd be right back but never came back and I never got a call. I always leave a very generous tip. So what am I tipping for. I wanted to have a enjoyable evening but cant really have one now. My friends and I are thirsty and we didnt want to have to go out. So I wont be ordering from this place anymore and throwing out the menu from my good places in my area pile. I did give it a 2 tvstar only because the steaks are ok. Theres a ton of other places in the area where they are better. Plus I'll get everything I ordered.

FOR WEDDING RECEPTION INQUIRIES: We called Golden City Chinese restaurant hoping to find a wedding reception venue coming up and we spoke with Mary who I believe is the one who handles weddings and she said the date we wanted was still available and we can come in and check it out anytime. When we got there (we drove 50 mins to get there), we had to wait about 15 minutes or so for Mary to come out to meet with us which wasn't too bad of a wait. However, when she came out, she didn't even greet us or seem friendly at all and seemed quite rude. One of the first things she brought up already was for us to GIVE her a deposit before even letting us ask any questions. We heard her but expected her to at least be able to give us a quote on the menu we wanted and be able to answer a few simple questions since we already came out of our way to get there. She then gave us some list of their menu items for us to look at and choose. We waited another 20-30 minutes for her to come back out and when we gave her the list and wanted to ask for a quote, she told us we had to GIVE HER A DEPOSIT to get a quote and she just walked off. 

I have never met with any venue that straight up asked for the deposit before giving us much information. We only checked this place out because we knew two people who had their wedding there and I am sure the food was okay at least and the pricing was not too bad but I am glad we are not going to book this place. This was just extremely poor customer service from Mary and she wasted our time. If you are willing to give her your money before knowing much about what type of venue and the pricing of booking with them then that is on you but I just want to advise anyone interested in checking this place out that you MUST GIVE THEM A DEPOSIT before being able to ask for more information which is just STUPID and MAKES NO SENSE. We are very disappointed and hope no one has to waste their time coming here if you aren't sure you will book them.

Staff were all friendly and sweet. We got to sit up outside on the balcony. Love the view. The food was ok. Oyster was good but a few were a bit gritty. 

Not sure I will go back to this one considering there's a lot of other great ones in New Orleans

Met a couple of friends at the Hideaway last night, I'm still reeling and in shock over the way the bartender, Andrew spoke to other guests and us. Never before have I watched a show like he put on while working. Andrew was shot gunning beers, doing shots, told multiple patrons to "fuck off, it's my bar I'll do whatever the fuck I want." Told my buddies that they were disrespectful because they weren't looking at him and having conversation with him. 
We went out to be together and have conversation amonst ourselves, not watch an angry man child behind the bar have a tantrum. Andrewspoke anger and trash talk continued and he proceeded to throw his fists into the cooler tops bending them and screaming at us to leave his bar because we disrespected him by not engaging in a conversation with him. 
Having myself worked in the bar industry for many years, I have never demanded that patrons have conversation with me while they were out. The Hideaway needs to get new bar staff....won't be back there any time soon.  If I wanted to be verbally assaulted I could have stayed home for that.

For a wine bar, the wines by the glass didn't make my heart pitter patter (the way wine usually does lol). But the ambiance was great, and the food looked/smelled awesome, so I'll definitely be back to check it out food-wise. And to drink more wine.
Aaaaaaamazing views, aaaaaaamazing food, aaaaaaamazing drinks, aaaaaaamazing service!
The best damn happy hour was at this location on October 13th 2017. The work staff was very accommodating to the large crowd. I had a great time at a homecoming happy hour in this facility.

Best Enchiladas I've ever had!
I came here for lunch today and had fish enchiladas, which weren't on the menu, but they happily made them anyway, and my brother had an enormous bean burrito filled with all the expected burrito goodies, as well as fish tacos that were very flavorful. Their salsa bar has a wide variety and the salsas are delicious. Everyone was very polite, and the food was made quickly.

I would not recommend this place to anyone. I have never sent back a plate of food until yesterday. I ordered a roast beef wrap. When it came the tortilla was brown and cracking. No big deal I wasn't going to eat it anyway. But when I opened it up and the eggs were brown and looked like they were forgotten about I couldn't take it. Then when I said something the waitress came back with the bill and said she took care of it with the manager. The manager charged me $1.89, I guess for the three bites. It was disgusting!

Amazing food! Try the Cajun Sampler if you're real hungry. Doesn't look like much from the outside but our hotel concierge recommended it and I'm so glad she did! The music is good and the atmosphere is great for any occasion. We will definitely come back here in the future! Our waitress was Mary both times we went to 801 and the service was great each time! Mary made us feel at home-like eating a local restaurant in our hometown. She was honest and upfront and always attended to us in a timely manner! Already missing this place and we just left today. A big thanks is owed to Mary for making our visit so special.

We had dinner at Mad Mex on a whim and we all enjoyed the offerings.
The atmosphere is lively and fun. We opted to sit inside and our server Melissa was friendly and got us going with drinks and food orders quickly.
They've got a good selection of beers as well as cocktails that should suit most tastes.
We got the chips and three dips to start. The guacamole was good and tasted fresh. The pico de gallo was chopped fine but full of flavor and the roasted chipotle dip, which, unbeknownst to me, was cheese based, was OFF THE HOOK!
For my main course, I had the fried fish tacos, which come three to an order, accompanied with black beans and rice. The tacos were good, generous portions, however I found the black beans lacking salt. All of the other flavors were great.
My son had the kids chicken quesadilla. He's three so he's not a good indicator of the quality of food. Took three bites and then focused on his coloring page and crayons. The next day, he didn't have any adverse reactions so I'll go ahead and say that his stuff was good.
My wife ordered a burrito and subbed tofu for chicken. She go through about 1/3 before calling it quits and seemed to enjoy every second of her meal.
It hit all of the points for us and I'd love to go back.

Good solid pizza, giant slices.

Casual pizza and beer joint. Unfortunately slow service, expect a wait for your food.

Great fresh fish market. Friendly service, great selection, excellent quality and always fresh. 

I have not tried the restaurant.

Hands down, Hotel Tango is the best distillery/bar in Indiana, and I would even wager in the Midwest! When you consider the quality of their spirits, the creativity of their cocktails and the camaraderie within their culture, they're simply unmatched.

We're quickly becoming regulars here, and each time we're in the staff has been so friendly and welcoming. If you're not sure what to order (there are so many great options) they always take time to talk about your preferences and share the highlights of their seasonal menu. 

The cocktails they offer are incredibly creative. With a menu that changes seasonally, kudos to their bartenders for always finding exciting new ways to present their spirits. You'll never find cocktails like this anywhere else, guaranteed!

Also, we recently discovered they serve food! They nachos featuring the Broad Ripple Chips were surprisingly good and a perfect shareable.

Visiting from California, this is not a typical brewery we are used to. The dining room looks like a regular restaurant and you have to check in to get seated. On a Saturday night, it was an hour and a half wait, even tho we saw a couple of tables that were empty. There wasn't a crowd of people waiting to get seated either. So, we opted to just sit at the bar, but there was a wait for that too. We asked if we can order beer while we wait by the door and they said we can only have one glass (for the entire group) and we have to drink it close to the door. WOW.

Ordered their food from Pinellas Ale Works. I ordered their roast beef sandwich. It was one of the worst sandwiches I ever had. The beef was extremely dry and there was no cheese or Gouda as advertised on their menu for this sandwich. Only had a couple of slices of beef. Won't be making that mistake again....

Where to begin? Although I had made reservations, I was a little bit concerned when I saw how empty it was on a Wednesday evening. Arriving around 8 PM, we were led to a beautiful table upstairs in the corner with great views of the trees and greenery surrounding us.  Excellent detail to the server on opening the wine my companion ordered, great recommendations for dinner, and attentive but unobtrusive service.

This location is equally as adept at handling a romantic dinner or a business dinner, which is the true mark of a good restaurant.

This is my second time dinning at this Place! And booooy!!!! The food is phenomenal! Since they were short staffed, it took a minute before I got sat! But it was definitely worth the wait.. the staff are super friendly. And the place is gorgeous! Next time I shall take pics of the restaurant. You would feel like your dinning in a palace, as if like your princess Jasmine from Aladdin lol. So beautiful inside...

We had amazing food all week in Philly but the Turkey sandwich (Italian on sourdough) from The Original Turkey was hands down the best!! Dinic's Roast pork right across had the long line and was good but skip the line and get the best at The Original Turkey! Nick makes a great sandwich!

This place bums me out because it has such potential to be a blast.  The place is full of nostalgia with great games to have a good time.  I went to Savage Bingo and invited many friends because it sounded like so much fun.  It had potential - twerking, dancing, acting silly during bingo.  The problem is they took it WAY too far.  This is only meant for college age people and not all college age people either (the very immature who want to act stupid).  One big funny is to make someone spit alcohol in another person's mouth.  It was done over and over.  This is gross and I would think a huge liability but that isn't my problem.  It just got stupid.  Great potential but lost.  Other events sound like such fun but after attending that fiasco, I won't attempt it.  I don't have hope that they know how to have fun without being gross and stupid.  The food seemed bagged and the service wasn't so good.  This place has huge potential but they have the wrong people running it and working there.

I did not stay but went to this place yesterday and they are no longer serving food. They also were out of some of the draft beers. I was disappointed bc the reviews seemed to rave and I am new to the neighborhood and am still looking for a go to spot

Been going here for about 7 years now . Treat you like family. All about there work and reputation. There to make there customers happy and feel
Welcomed . Amazing people . And joe Matisa is tha man. Wouldn't recommend any other tattoo shop . A shop that isn't out there to beat u for your money but to share there art with the world .
Awesome!  Delivery was fast, fresh, and completely delicious.  Our new favorite Thai.

Although my profile might make me out to be an Italian.....I'm actually MEXICAN. Yes, it true buey (dude in Spanish)...I from di border town known as Nogaaaales. 

So, with that said, I prefer to eat my Mexican meals in the comfort of my mothers fabulous kitchen, where fresh salsa is made weekly, Machaca and Eggs for Sunday Brunch with Fresh Avocado and chilaquiles....

But anyway...this review is about Taqueria Juanitos!

I give this joint a solid 5 stars...and there are so many reasons I'll just list them:

-Kitchen is so clean you lick the floor
-Fresh Fresh Fresh Carne Asada is always rotated on the grill top
-Consistently ripe and delicious Avocados in Stock
-BEST QUESADILLA IN TOWN: (besides my own or my moms): These guys use the legit Sonoran Flour Tortilla that gets Crsipy and Flaky, Not Chewy and Flavorless like the Gringo types served at Guadalajara Grill. I prefer my quesadilla with Cheese only, but if my stomach is feeling strong I'll go for a Quesadilla with Asada in it. TASTE BUD ALTERING.***
-The CEVICHE: Simple, basic, but solid in flavor and freshness. Order it on a Tostada. Its citrusy flavors really hit the spot when you're craving something light and crisp.
-did I mention the rice and beans are always delish? I'm personally not a huge fan of Sonoran style rice and beans (the lard doesn't agree with me), but these sides always compliment the rest of the meal.
-Red house-made salsa is a 4 of ouf 5. Flavor profile is good, but doesn't have the WOW factor.
-Service is fast
-ALWAYS a great place to catch a diverse group of people dining together. I've eaten many lunches among business men, young mexican families, gangster types, men in Tiger Print Spandex. EVERYONE COMES HERE BECAUSE THE FOOD IS GOOD, CLEAN, FRESH.

VIVA JUANITO!
Best French toast, huevos rancheros, and brunch drinks ever! Plus, our service was exceptional!

This is the first time at this Carrabbas since we moved. We were very pleasantly surprised and pleased. The service was excellent and friendly as well as the food. 
I had the new lobster fra diavolo. It was exceptional. Two fairly nice size lobster tails on top of very tasty not overly spiced fra diavolo sauce with many pieces of lobster through out. Truly exceptional. I would definitely order this again. Highly recommended. I do hope they will continue to have this on the menu.

We had Glidden Construction do our kitchen remodel, and we are very happy with the result.

Lloyd guided us through the planning phases and made suggestions for adjustments that were explained in detail and ended up improving our plan with an accurate timetable. Lloyd also had good oversight of the project once construction began, and the subcontractors used were similarly top-notch, in both performance and communication. Our end result was better than we would even have anticipated.

Should any future needs to our house arise, we would be happy to use Glidden Construction again.
Only been once, but so far, so good. My family and I will return often. Comparable to our old favorite Thai place that closed down since they moved back to Thailand.

Ok y'all. I don't know if this place is tied to the Holiday Inn or not but regardless, do not let that dissuade you. The food is amazing. I loved that the entire menu is shareables. That being said, the portion size was still huge. I had the roasted broccoli, the Mac and the pork and grits. Sooooooo delicious. I joked with others at the bar I was going to have to go back for my yoga pants because I could not stop eating.  Really inexpensive too. Fabulous service. I would eat here again in a heartbeat.

I LOVED IT! Linda is great and so is her family! The salon is clean and beautiful
And the staff makes you feel like family! I am a nail biter, but kinda tackled my very short nubs of a nail and gave me a great full set! I will be returning very shortly

My boyfriend and I live right near Pieology in the Hollister Village development and come here fairly regularly. We love the crispy, thin crust and wide array of toppings to choose from for our salads and pizzas. What we really love the most is that we can have separate pizzas and nobody fights over the "pineapple on pizza" debacle! The only thing I would change about here is to have a larger beer selection on tap. Keep up the good work, guys!
They had great service, large portions and were patient and friendly with my large group. Will return soon.

it's my favorite live music venue in the world!  you have to experience it if you are in town.  the musicians are up close and the acoustics are always spot on.  the people pack it in and dance right up against the stage.  this place has excellent talent on stage night in and night out.
My waitress name was Melissa she was really good I like her if you come make sure you get her she's very polite and she do make sure you have your order right

Rode my bike out to the Rivershack along the Mississippi River Bike Trail. I have passed this place many times riding my bike and this was the first time I was able to stop in and get me something to eat. The ambiance is a 10, they a great outside porch and the inside looks like an authentic cajun restaurant out in the bayou with swamp wood, Spanish moss and all. Of course I ordered the gator sausage with sweet potato fries and both were excellent. In fact they were the best sweet potato fries I have ever had. The gator sausage poboy was very tasty with a great flavor and the bread was excellent. I will definitely be coming back.

Rude service from owner. Placed a to go order and was told it would be ready in 25 minutes. Husband went to pick up 25 minutes later and paid for order. He was told it would be ready in 5 minutes. Husband visited with other diners he knew. After 10 minutes went to check again on food. Debbie (cashier and owners wife) went to check again and said the order was next. Husband had a friendly chat with Debbie while waiting. Then 2 other customers came in and received their food. Husband said to Debbie can you check again on the order. At this time the owner, who had been having an employee issue at the counter and was frustrated,  starting yelling at my husband telling him he heard him be rude to his wife Debbie. Husband asked how was he rude, as he had been patiently waiting for 20 or more minutes for food. He replied I heard you being rude. He then went and got our order from the back. At this time Debbie apologized for her husband. When he returned with the order my husband told him he was not rude to Debbie and asked if he treated all of his customers this way. The owner then started screaming you're not my customer and said he would refund the money. My husband told him no, we wanted the food as it was a fund raiser for our church. We have been customers of this restaurant for a few years and have enjoyed the pizzas and food. We will no longer be a customer. There are too many good restaurants in the area, especially with a new Italian restaurant opening next store to That's Amore!

I had the bun bo hue.  Really good.  A friend recommended it, he said pretty close to Vietnam local flavor.  I also had the bean milk shake.  I LIKE IT.

The service is slow and can be annoying if you are in a hurry.  I had to ask for the bill more than once. 

So if you are not in a hurry, it's still a place to check out, especially, if you've already visited other the Vietnamese restaurants.
Very clean and friendly staff. Best Chorizo in Tucson, highly recommended!
New to area and Yelp recommendations got me to make an appointment. Great haircut and good customer service. Wee is friendly and very knowledgeable about her craft. Will definitely be going back

Waited months for this place to open but  the wait was not worth it. The battered fish tacos were horrible and cold. The tamales are fake Mexican tamales. Popusas are a dollars but they are the size of silver dollar pancakes. They don't have very much to choose from and their salsa bar is not fresh. The service was horrible. Sorry we wasted our time and money here.

I will start by saying I am from out of town (western new york) and I always do my own work on my cars so I am very picky about where I take my car if the situation arises. 

I came down to Clearwater for vacation with my fiancée and unfortunately my power steering rack blew a seal and started leaking. I had to drive back home in 2 days so I needed it fixed ASAP. I called a few shops in the area and found Noble. They got me in the same day at 12 o clock and had the power steering rack replaced by 3pm. I was glad they got it done quickly and at a fair price. 

As soon as I got about 10 feet out of the parking lot I noticed my car was very loud inside (the engine noise was abnormally loud). I pulled over and found that the plastic piece behind the clutch and brake pedals was missing, and the steering shaft was exposed off of the column. Honest mistake, I get it. I turned around and went back and told the Miss at the front desk that I found the piece missing. She had me speak with the technician that worked on my vehicle and he informed me that while removing the piece it had broken so he threw it away. I wish they would have told me that when I first picked up my car, and I would not have cared. I left and about 2 miles down the road my power steering pump started whining. I pulled over once again and popped the hood and found the power steering fluid reservoir was nearly empty. Perhaps the "technician" forgot to refill it and bleed the rack properly. I stopped at a local parts store and got more fluid and refilled it and found that the clamp holding the boot onto the tie rod was leaking. This tells me the "technician" either didn't test the steering system post-installing the part, or they use cheap-o parts. 

I had to buy a new clamp at the parts store and fix it myself and Now everything is well. Not my best experience at a shop ever. 2 stars.

Two words: Happy Hour. My go-to spot for 1/2 off flat breads, mussels, and drinks. The service has always been exceptional. Salu is walking distance from my house, and among all of the other restaurants around it, I have always pick Salu for easy, delicious dining.

This review is just for the restaurant.
We had breakfast, lunch and dinner.
let's break it down as it should:
-Human element: 5 solid stars. Service is spectacular, the staff is absolutely charming, attentive, educated but not stiff, upbeat (thanks Nathan!). The restaurant manager came to check how were we doing and to say hello and he was delightful and informative, classy and at the same time warm. Thanks Mario, you are a credit to the hotel and restaurant.
-Setting: excellent, five stars
-Food: BLEH. Uninspiring, boring, mediocre, traditional hotel food. No doubt many guests will be ok with this colorless menu, but you are a five-star hotel that excels in so many aspects, and some of your guests are sophisticated enough to enjoy a creative menu, don't you think? at least one or two menu choices off-the-beaten-to-death path?
-Music: we were mercilessly punished by a singer for the duration of our dinner (beware, Fri and Sat). What in heaven has this thing of the stuffy, dull past has to do now in a place like this? THE PIANIST WAS EXCELLENT. Have him play softly, that would be great. This singer that won't stop was even being broadcast through the speaker system, so in spite of asking to be seated as far away as possible, we HAD to endure him nevertheless. Suggestion: you want to listen to the singer (for whatever incomprehensible reason): go to the lobby. You want peace (that's what you were looking for at the Belmond, peace and charm) go outside... IF THEY WOULD ONLY NOT HAVE HIM ON THE SPEAKERS TOO. Easy solution. 
Our stay in the hotel, though, was solid Five Stars, and that will go in a separate review.
Amazing staff, delicious food.  Local ingredients, great chef, worldly wines.  Best steak tartar ever.  Bread was toasted to perfection, large portion of tartar, wonderful mustard vinaigrette.

We ate lunch there at 11:00 am and were sat at a dirty table. Every table in my sight was dirty and so was the floor. I asked the waitress to clean our table and she did. Service was good, food was good but with COVID going on I thought it was really bad that they weren't clean.

My 94 year old Aunt was admitted there for rehab, after being discharged from LBH.  She was fine.  Just needed to strengthen her legs to walk again.  While at Statesman, she acquired horrible bed sores (the staff had a meeting and said they didn't know why they were getting worse and didn't know what to do...but somehow St. Mary's knew what to do).
She declined so poorly they took her to St. Mary's for a feeding tube evaluation, since she could not eat, just to find that Statesman had overlooked a septic Urinary Tract Infection that nearly killed her.  UTI's in elderly cause hallucinations and dementia.  The doctors don't visit patients enough at this facility.  Now she is at Manor Care in Yardley and improving daily.  Her mind has come back finally.
The staff also continually lost her laundry at Statesman, even though it was clearly marked with her name.

We had a wonderful experience our third time back. I'm not a beer fan so I love the fact that I can get a decent wine by the glass at a fair price. The food was delicious and hot! Kennet Sq mushroom soup is fantastic! 4 of us split the tasty nachos for an appetizer and had some left- we loved the fresh jalapeño peppers on them and the guacamole was great- well worth the extra $2 or 3 bucks.
Burgers are big and come with fries or a salad. The beer lovers like the large selection. The service was the best I've had in a long time and she was extremely busy on a Friday night. Call ahead so you don't have to wait!

I ordered the Sessano special off the menu thinking it was a hot sandwich as it was in that section of the menu. I was very disappointed that it was cold, and for $16.50 I expected it to contain a lot more sustenance. It also contained balsamic vinegar which should have been described on the menu. It was a fairly decent sandwich prepared quickly, but not worth the money. Faygo is the same price as a Hank's root beer; I just don't understand that. It will be unlikely that I'll return as this is not competitive to other local lunch spots.

EDIT: $16.50 is for a full size hoagie, not a regular. Thank you John H. of King of Prussia for your feedback!

Been a client of Taylor's for years! Even moved around to different shops with her until she finally settled into her own place :) nothing but great laughs and services when you're at Meraki. If you're looking for anything eyelashes, Taylor is your girl!
I've visted twice from out of town and I have to say the food is really fresh and delicious and the employees are very friendly. I can see this as my go to place and hang out spot.

I've been here a couple times now. After moving from NYC, I realize how good I had it when it came to food selection. And I absolutely love sushi; especially anything salmon. I'd eat salmon roll almost everyday for lunch. So I ventured a few places in PA hoping that I could find many good sushi places and was sad that I can't find good salmon in any of them. I stopped here once when I was roaming the neighborhood and ordered their salmon. Firstly, their customer service is excellent. The gentleman who works there is very cordial and always greets you with a smile. Secondly, I had found good salmon. (FINALLY!) And thirdly, the place is very well organized and I love that they openly supply customers with utensils, and sauces!! (In NYC, all the sauces were behind the counter and only at request). Here you can see the different types of sauces (spicy mayo, soy sauce, some other sauces I didn't see because I only care about spicy mayo sauce) out in the open for you to take as many as you see fit.

I really like wandering in this store.  Very unique gifts and quite a selection of items to choose from.  The prices were fairly reasonable, but I wish they were open a bit later in the weekday evenings.

Heard about this place from friends.  The atmosphere was very nice and you did not have the servers approaching you at the wrong time like some of the other establishments do.  It is better to make reservations depending on the time frame of the day that you want to go there just to be on the safe side.  This is definitely  a place that I will take my wife again.

First time here both at the casino and restaurant... YAY! the purple parrot is great! good food, nice selection, good price and the service from Ana ROCKED! This was a GREAT start to my vacation (even though I got lost within the casino cause it is so big haha).... will be back tomorrow for sure for french toast I think.. bow wow!

I wish the restaurant was staffed better. I was there with a group, who planned weeks in advance. There were only 2 waitresses and 1 bus boy. Not sure how many  cooks but people waited over an hour for food. Some people ended up just leaving. The wings were good, otherwise the drinks and asparagus were average. The nachos looked delicious but I didn't eat any since we waited so long.

The space was definitely a dope concept. A bar with several tv's. And outdoor space. Also a side room that could probably be used for private parties. 

I would give it another chance, especially if the crowd was smaller.

I was very excited to try this place but unfortunately I was denied service. I had called about 7:00pm on a Sunday and asked the lady on the phone what time they closed and they told me about 9 o'clock. So I had left the house ready to try out some new yummy Thai and when I had gotten there at 8:30pm the doors were locked. They looked completely open on the inside and the sign on the window advertised that they were open until 9:30. I called the them back and they lady I had spoke to about coming in told me they had to closed the kitchen because "small town." She knew I was outside and everything and did not want the service. I was very upset about the false advertisement and would highly suggest calling this place to check if they are open before wasting the time driving there.

Not too shabby for a quick fix on Chinese fast food! I got the combo fried rice (a bit dry for me), General Tso Chicken (pretty good), and crab rangoon (just ok). Much better than Panda Express! I had a really bad experience the last time I ordered from there. Will def be back to Lulu's though!

Large, cheap slices of pizza!  The pizza was yummy and the garden salad was okay (what I would expect from a small pizza joint).  They change their special slices every day so you can always get something different.

I have never been to this dealership nor have we bought any new cars in the area. Yet somehow they have our address and are sending my girlfriend predatory letters saying they will "pay of her loan". We don't have any loans scott. Lose our damn address. 

This business seems to be predatory and I wouldn't go near it to buy a car if they are buying your data to send you falsified letters to "pay off your loan". Certainly wouldn't go through scott. He might try to sell you a car that doesn't have oil in it just to make an extra 5$. Crook

First of all I love Whataburger, secondly this is the only location close to me. It's a shame. Without fail every time I go to this location there's always something wrong either they forget some thing or the sandwich is wrong the fries are cold or my favorite a 20 minute drive through wait. Today was no exception I needed a Whataburger. I ordered it a little bit special and was seventh in line at the drive-through. It was lunchtime so I expected it to be busy 19 minutes later I get to the window. The young lady charges me and forgets to give me my change. It was only three dollars but it's my money. So after waiting for what seems forever the cashier for getting my change. I arrive home and opened up my sandwich and Guess What it's wrong again. I simply ordered a double with cheese no onion dry. The amount of mustard on the sandwich is sickening I hate mustard that's why I asked for it dry. Not only did I wait forever but I got a crappy sandwich as well that I cannot eat. I guess I can do without lunch today. Returning to that restaurant will only piss me off. When are you guys ever going to get it together? This is one of the worst hamburger places in Tucson. It's over priced and the Quality is garbage. Even McDonald's is better than this. Ridiculous service, horrible food. Strangely enough while I was waiting in the drive-through two staff members came out went to their cars smoke cigarettes and had a little break in the parking lot while all the rest of us were waiting. The management needs to get a clue. You never send employees on break during a rush. Ridiculous

We visited from chicago this week and it was amazing!! They're specials are unbelievable, .99 nigiri. 25 % off rolls under $9. The service was excellent and very fast. If i visit again I will definitely be back!

We were in Tucson for the weekend and randomly decided we would try this place. We were mostly glad we did as we enjoyed the food. The burgers are pretty big and the toppings are a lot of fun. We ordered the Sonora and the Plymouth burgers and both were fairly unique and tasty.

I would recommend sharing fries as we got so many with two orders that we had plenty of leftovers. The sweet potato fries were nasty anyways, but their regular fries are not bad.

The service is pretty good and the employees are really friendly, but one thing that I didn't really like was the strange artwork. It is not a pleasant looking place because of that, but it is clean and the seats are nice. The only other issue I have with Monkey Burger is the price. Prices here are on par with Five Guys, probably even more expensive, so that makes this sadly unaffordable even though the food is good.

We decided to go to Acme to taste some traditional dishes of New Orleans. I was very disappointed, the food was just adequate and the service was not the greatest. Our waitress was not very friendly and the place generally lacked a personality. The menu was hardly unique, and I think there are many other restaurants that have a better ambiance to try the New Orlean's dishes.
I bought some really cool terrariums for my house here! I love going into this shop when I'm in the area. They have things for everybody and the lady who owns it is always really nice when I come in.

If you want a decent nights sleep, this hotel is not for you.

We always enjoy older, unique hotels with beautiful architecture.  The Union Station has all the charm of a European hotel but without any American conveniences (such as ice and vending machines on the floor) and ONE BIG PROBLEM, the TRAIN RAILS on one side are still ACTIVE.  It just seems this hotel has had better days.

We arrived early on a quiet Sunday afternoon.  We were met by a very friendly desk clerk that thank us for being Gold members and informing us that they have reserved a deluxe room for us.  We unpacked, saw some of the city, had dinner and returned to discover that active rail lines are about 50' from our room.  We were offered another room but since we were there only one night and it would take over 30 minutes to move, we decided to tough it out.  We discovered that trains passed by our room at least every 2 hours preceded by a horn blast just to alert you that a train was coming.  Unless you love trains and don't require sleep, this place is for you, except, that was not all that was wrong with the room.

The television was terrible with wavy pictures for those stations that actually came in.  The window covering were broken allowing in bright outside lighting.  My wife's sheet were tattered and her hands keep getting caught.  The sink was very slow to drain.

The pluses:  the lobby is an architectural beauty and great credit goes to the owners for keeping the building intact.  The staff is all very friendly and attentive with great kudos to the valet staff.

This place is tucked in a small strip mall in the corner. It's nice and cozy inside not to big. Maybe 7 tables. It's a husband and wife who takes the orders and the prices are great. Lunch specials for 8$ and up. The sushi is fresh and great quality. Food is Always on point and supporting small business I love. Check them out if your in the area!!
The food here is consistently good, and you can't go wrong with anything on the menu.
Place is fun and lively. Try the ceviche, it's amazing and not your typical runny salsa you would get. Chilean sea bass and just the right spice. Plus happy hour drafts are buy 1 get 1 for 25cents.

I don't consider myself to be a food snob but when my meal came it was Sal--T. The blackened catfish tasted as it was seasoned in muddy waters.  The coleslaw was super sweet and the mashed potatoes with gravy was bland and paste like.

We sent the plate back and it wasn't taken off the check.  Until there were negotiations with the manager. They took the sides off but not the fish because it cost them $9.00.  

The restaraunt was a happening joint however, they knew we were Yankees and sat us in the dunce area ... Completely empty. When we asked why we were sitting alone they claimed it was because the restaraunt was closing in an hour.  HOWEVER, the next local patrons were seated in the happening crowd.  

My boyfriend had the chicken tenders which were small portion but passable.  The campfire potatoes or camp yard were very very dry and un-seasoned. I tried a bite and spit it out. 

The interior design is pretty neat so walk in take a picture and leave.  I recommend the cracker barrel next door.

I just don't see the hype in this restaurant. The service was impeccable, but the food was kind of average. The duck lacked flavor. Someone ordered the lamb in our party which turned out to be my favorite dish among what everyone ordered. If I were to return that is what I would order.

Good location and on weekends you can park in the back at the train station since parkings free. Food was really yummy and service was fine. The Alley fries are SOooo delicious and I had the veggie panini mmmMmM! Pricing was very reasonable and I will DEF. return!
The worst service ever!!!! There were only 3 bar tenders working. My friends and I were often skipped over. I definitely won't be back.
My go to place! They never disappoint I used to travel from north philly to come here and get my nails done thank goodness I'm close by now!

This is a great spot! We walked in close to closing time and they were so accommodating. The food was delicious. My son is very picky with his fried rice and he said it was the best he had. Delicious, delicious. Worth the drive for us.
Wonderful hotel ,clean nice rooms. Awesome pool..close location to everything. Vicky at the front desk. Is amazing. Great asset to your company. Smart, funny, nice.
Very interesting. Tour was well worth it. Wine was great.
service is not too great and the parking situation is horrible you are basically forced to valet.  Food is ok.

Not giving 5 stars for one reason only:

NO DIET COCA-COLA/PEPSI-COLA!!! 

They do have a natural suger-cane-made cola that gives me headaches.

I know it's gotto be all natural... But I really need a fresh Diet Coke with my pizza!!!!!

By the way, they have the juiciest pizzas I've every tried. I love the Sausage and Peppers... oh jeez... I just love it... but still need my real Diet Coke!!!!!!!

The food was excellent as well as fast. The service was great until a few more people came in then it was slower. We will definitely be back because the food was hot and came out in a timely manner.We will definitely go back we think they were just short staffed because of the COVID-19 restrictions.

Came through the drive in for what I thought was going to be a very fast coffee and breakfast sandwich. 15 mins later I finally got to the first window to pay. The workers literally said nothing, just put there hand out of payment.. ok? Next window, the window is open but no one even acknowledges I am there. I sit there for about 3 mins before a man out of nowhere walks up and hands me my bag. He was very pleasant and asked if I had anything else. I tell him french vanilla latte and the lady behind him hands it to me. I drive off and open my bag. The biscuits were so hard I literally had to take off on of the buns just so I could eat it. My latte did not have the french vanilla syrup. Terrible experience, will not return. Total time spent getting food was about 20 mins.. at 1000 am on a Sunday. Brutal.

Food is Taco Bell quality. All out of a can from the salsa to the pre-shredded cheese. Rice is the same as the Mexican flavor rice a roni. Carnitas are bland and tasteless. Corn tortillas could be used as book holders they're so hard. Service is slow and practically nonexistent. Didn't even want to take the leftovers to my dog.
This is the best coffee in town! The whole "slow by slow" is absolutely worth the wait and delicate treatment to your fri k. We had the most lovely baristas too, wahoo!'

Jules is an exciting pizza place that has a lot to offer when compared with traditional pizza spots.  I respectfully disagree with the gentleman who said "this is not pizza," it is without question pizza so don't let that turn you off from trying this place.  They have an extensive variety of pizzas, white, wheat, or gluten free dough, good service, and a welcoming ambience.  The atmosphere is a la chipotle with a more "stay a while" feel where bringing your own beer or wine is not abnormal and seemingly encouraged.  The food is fresh and incredibly tasty.  I recommend the eggplant pizza, out of this world.  Highly recommended.
Really enjoyed this restaurant.  Quick and attentive service.  Everything tasted fresh and generous portions.
The best wings period!!! We ordered 200 wings for the Super Bowl, 50 of each flavor and they are the best wings I've ever had!! You must try for yourself I guarantee you will be a repeat customer!!!

This is a fully stocked, properly run place in a moderately iffy part of town.  They don't steam their tortillas the way a 5 star taco bell would, but for the location and the clientele, this place is pretty solid.  The parking lot is retardedly small for a ll the seating the have inside and people sometime double/triple park to squeeze in, but things are done pretty much right.  Only beef are the the students (but thats a huge part of the customer base) and the soda fountain behind the counter (is soda really that valuable?).

In the years I've been trusting my 2015 CRV to LMH, I've always received very hospitable staff attention whose attitude makes the drop-off chore of bit more enjoyable. Today, for example, I needed some maintenance work done on my brake & coolant systems. Isaac was engaged in getting me in & out in time to make another appointment. Unfortunately, they were understaffed through no fault of theirs. Isaac was honest about the delay @ offered me transportation to & from my appointment. As it turned out he was able to work some magic and had my car ready right on time! This happy camper is once again grateful for the extra effort that everyone puts out at LMH.

If I could give it fewer than 1 star I would.  I will never spend another dime in this place.  And I am making it my mission to tell as many people as I can why.  Despite the food being only mediocre, the prices were reasonable.  But their management policies regarding their customers SUCK.

Last night (Saturday, February 14, 2015 at about 7pm) my wife and I had just finished our meal, paid up and were leaving.  At the table next to us there was a party of five, which included a young mentally disabled boy.  They had waited a long time for their food and the boy was hungry.  They asked the waitress for a piece of bread and were REFUSED!  She told them they would have to order another po boy in order for him to get the piece of bread.  The party was in disbelief, but after being assured that was the rule they got up and left.  We left at the same time.  The seven of us were standing around outside discussing how ridiculous their situation was  Another two couples came up and were studying the outside menu and overheard us.  They then left.

So far the restaurant has lost the five half-coooked meals that hey had to throw out, and the business of the four people who left after hearing from us about the incident 

And they made seven customers absolutely irate.  We will tell everyone we know how ridiculous that restaurant's stupid decision was.  My wife and I are timeshare owners of the Wyndham Avenue Plaza a half-block up the street, and we have told their management about it.  I am a member of a Wyndham Owners facebook forum where we share such information.  I will tell that story on there, and thousands of owners will read it.

I am going to guess that incident will, over time, cost that restaurant perhaps a hundred thousand dollars in lost revenue.

All because they were too cheap to give a hungry mentally challenged boy a 20 cent piece of bread.

I mentioned the exact time and date earlier, hoping that whichever idiot made the decision to withhold that 20 cent piece of bread from a hungry mentally challenged boy will find him or herself out on the street looking for a new job.  Which would be the right decision.  That kind is not needed in the "hospitality" business.
It is very good food and cozy place. Great sushi snd hibatchi style food. I will visit again

COVID-19 Review.

A little turned off that this is such a tourist trap, I was spending just a few hours in New Orleans and gave in to making the first beignet I have ever had be from Cafe Du Monde.  I'm very glad I did!  I heard mixed reviews from people I know who have come here, but fortunately I had a great experience.

The beignets were super fresh out of the frier so hot, crispy, and mounded with an unbelievable amount of powdered sugar.  I looked around to see if this was normal and not only did I see many other mountainous peeks on other's tables forming a resemblance to the Rocky Mountains, but on the ground all around the tables, it looked rather snowy so I imagine this is quite normal.   The wife and I shared a coffee which was the perfect combination.  I'm not even much of a coffee drinker, but this was so good together, I later bought a pair of socks that had beignets and coffee all over them.  I wear those proudly, by the way.

So, I have 3 kids and my middle kid does not like hot baked/fried goods and especially does not like donuts so I didn't think she'd really give this a chance and take a small bite and politely offer it to any other takers.  My middle child was the first to complete her beignet, raved about how amazing it was, and wanted another.  She didn't get it, but that's how our experience went.

Yes, do the touristy thing and try a beignet for Cafe Du Monde.  Social Distancing... not really existent here FYI, though people were mostly wearing masks.
Have been going to Lisa G. for about 5 years. Always leave with a great cut and color for a great price and friendly atmosphere!

Not sure how this place has a 4.5 star review. It was empty at lunch time -- a very awkward dining experience. Took them 5 minutes to find a plastic knife for my salad. Definitely nothing special about this place. I'd give them 3-6 months, depending on how deep daddy's checkbook is / how long the inheritance lasts before it runs out.

I had a surprise dinner for my mothers birthday last night. We called in advance and, figured this would be a great place for a special celebration since, we have had celebrated a big dinner once before at the other location.

The food was over cooked. The shrimp was rubbery and, for those that ordered anything with bacon the bacon was burnt. The server was friendly but, I had to ask so, our party could get refills the 2 times. We brought in a cake and were told if we wanted plates we were to be charged a party plate fee. Which is strange - the server came to verify we still wanted them when one of our party asked for the party plate. Our bill was well over 400. Which is fine and what we were expecting this we tipped the 18%. When we we handed the bill the cashier told us if that was all we were leaving. Out of embarrassment we left more. But, were put in a very uncomfortable situation as this is something you don't usually tell someone. While I walked over to get cash from my purse the server found out and, was visibly upset. Members of our party were able to see what was happening. Despite the food and awkward situation we were put in we still had a great time. We were cordial and, happy my mother was happy. 

I'm not sure if this is normal practice or not. But, I'm disappointed that this is how we were treated. I hope this does not happen to anyone else.

Almost too cool for me to hangout at, lol!

There's a part of me that's afraid to give it a 5 because that'll mean it'll get crowded and the already sucky parking situation will just get even worst for me!

Anyway, I feel like this is a good place for me to getaway from the crazies I often see or hear screaming down campbell!  Its a dark, lofty, woody coffee shop that I guess is a chain in Arizona.  I'll support the smaller chains and mom and pops shop as long as they have good coffee which CCL definitely does!  Their food is great too though I only had the Frittata so that's all I'm basing it on but I'll definitely be going back!  Their furniture is clean and kept which is always nice. Its a good mixture of young college kids and older eccentric folks who know how to enjoy a good cafe -- of course I was there at 10:30am on a Tuesday morning. Just be ready to share a huge table that seats like 8+ (I don't remember seeing individual/smaller tables), which I love because if no one's sitting next to you you'll have a good amount of workspace!  There's also a huge couch to share if you like.

And its always cool when you love the music mix they play :).

Tried Sip for the first time last evening and fell in love!!!  This gem of a place was super inviting and comfortable which is saying a lot when you have a 9 month old. The  food, drinks, music and staff were all wonderful. We cannot wait to go back and enjoy all they have to offer.
Replaced wipers and two filters...very expensive $150...but they keep my car in good working order.

The cutest little coffee shop with super yummy food.  We needed a place to stop on the way to the airport and this was perfect.  They had more choices than I thought - burritos, bowls, etc.   The coffee was delicious, I was thinking about taking some back with me.   The staff was friendly and love the chica-vibe.  The have a cute outside area that is perfect if you have dogs or kids.

I love breasts.  I really do enjoy a good breast over all the other parts.  When I come to Hooters, though, I always get the wings of the chicken instead of the breasts. Hooters' wings aren't amazing by any means but they're a good choice.  Especially if you like breaded wings.  

With that said, the breast strips look good too!  As does the buffalo chicken breast sandwich. Maybe next time I'll grab one of their breasts.  Both options look very appealing.

My main complaint with the wings is that they are light on the sauce.  Not that I want jugs of wing sauce poured on them.  I just wish they'd give you A Cup of extra wing sauce on the side.  Perhaps they don't have A Cup.

On Mondays, Hooters runs an all-you-can-eat wings special for like $12.  You'd be a boob if you didn't hit that up once in your life.

Interestingly, I've been to Hooters twice and I have yet to see a male server.  I can't help but wonder if Hooters is actively discriminating against men or if it's just by coincidence.

I also noticed the female staff wears rather revealing clothing.  I've felt a cold draft blowing through the dining area on both trips here.  I wonder if the waitresses would prefer to wear a shawl or jacket while working.

Very disappointing.  Besides not having the correct room for us at check in, then not delivering our luggage to the room yet telling us that they had....the hotel is either severely understaffed or it's poorly managed.  The spa pool area had left over food items on most chairs with towels scattered about, no attendant on hand.  Food trays lined the hallways as we walked back to our room and the same roll away bed that was in front of our room when we checked in still remains.  
Most sites rate this resort as a 4-5 and I'm guessing it's because of the grounds and location,which are lovely. The room itself is spacious and clean.
The best thing they have going are  the young college students who work here, they are very outgoing and friendly.
We will not return.

I loved this place and the owner was so sweet - she really made us feel welcome. I had the french dip and it was so good but so big! Half a sandwich is more than enough. Next time I'm in New Orleans I will definitely be back.

It's funny I came here when I was younger pre-gambling age, they had a mini golf course, an awesome buffet and I remembered it being really fun.  Coming back now that I am older I am a bit disappointed, first of all I remember it being much closer to the downtown Reno...  The entrance felt very old, almost geriatric.  Then same could be said for the dreary elevator and the room just felt generic.  It was a "non" smoking room but still reeked of cigarettes.  It's fine, the lobster buffet was OK, taking the price into account it would be decent.  I guess if you snowboard/ski all day and don't want to drive back home this place offers pretty cheap room rates, but to be far if you go further into Reno, you can get a similar deal with a few more creature comforts.

Amazing homemade fresh gluten free organic food! The healthy and hot fresh hot bowl was to die for!! My daughter love her homemade turkey sandwich! The smoothies and fresh juice are on point! Great customer service. The owner has a beautiful spirit and I love the fact she is so passionate and takes the time to educate you about health choices when it comes to cooking healthy. If you want to eat homemade fresh healthy food this is the spot. My daughter love this place!! You wouldn't regret it!! Definitely will be my spot to come back. The bread while chocolate pudding is too die for! Many blessings to this business. Thank you for your hospitality today. Kellin

I actually really like it here. Their massive outdoor area is pretty cool and the vibe is relaxing. I was a little pretentious about coming here because I prefer to visit local breweries but I am glad I came. This location has great food (ahem...better than Yards, sorry!), great service and most importantly, great beer. I really like the fact that they have beers specially unique to this location and some to Chicago, making each brewhouse different.

Their chargrilled oysters are amazing like everyone else said.  The only regret is that we did not order more.

Other dishes like the fried shrimp/fish plate and crawfish étouffée were good / decent for what they were.  But go for the oysters - they will blow your socks off. 

Pros:
- amazing chargrilled oysters!!! 
- efficient and friendly service 

Cons: 
- line is too long (there are other places in the French quarter that also have good chargrilled oysters, so not necessarily worth the wait)
- the bread that comes with the oysters are a little stale

Great fun drink menu! My cocktails were so delicious with the perfect amount of punch. Service could be a hair friendlier.. waitress seemed grumpy when we didn't immediately order food. I felt the urge to look around for a sign that stated we were obligated to have food in order to stay. Whatevs, we had a good time anyway. The space is efficient and they seated our group of 6 with a reasonable amount of wait time for dropping in on a Friday evening. Definitely a good spot to share a lovely evening with good company.

Horrendous customer service. Maybe the worst I've ever experienced at a restaurant. I made reservations for the upstairs venue for 12. 12 people. That's good money, right? You'd think a restaurant might be excited about us spending beaucoup bucks. Anyway, the hostesses were so rude upon arrival. So bad we thought it might be a joke. We wanted to add 4 extra people to our dinner and they told us absolutely not, and looked at us like we were nuts. I understand tables can be at a premium, so we were bummed. However, when we were finally seated (30 mins after our reservation time) the ENTIRE upstairs emptied out in 20 minutes. So there were about 40 extra seats but our extra 4 friends couldn't join us. This wasn't the end though. Drinks took centuries to come. My tartare looked like a raw hamburger patty. Oddest item I've ever been served. Our waitress was nice but overall the experience was really poor. Our 4 friends ended up eating bbq at Jack's across the street... we were seriously jealous we didn't join them. Won't go back here. Ever!

I would give them four stars for their food, I'm not a Chinese food fan.  But, from what they are working with they did a good job. Best hole in wall Chinese place in the area.  Great friendly service too.

Negative: if u didn't set up an appt., you will have to wait for about an hour, depending on how busy they are.

Positive: Brought my 7 month old son for blood work. The lady who did his bloodwork did an amazing job. My son did not cry at all. He was even smiling at her. I forgot to get her name. But she is very friendly and nice.

In summary, meh. We had 2 cocktails and the appetizer and entree samplers. We enjoyed the "lasagne" and pernil (pork), but the rest was just ok. Value-wise, it was ok with the Grope-on we used, but if we had paid full ride for the meal, it would have been disappointing. Our server was especially helpful and attentive.

We may give it another try, possibly at lunch. Or maybe not.
The gelato nearly brings me to tears. It's that good. Excellent coffee drinks and teas, delicious pastries (try the brioche!), and sweet staff. It's the best (duh).

Hoagie Town, I love you. Your my beacon in the night, welcoming me with open arms and cold pounders of lions head. I can buy Boaz cigarillos that are stale, but i will smoke them any way. Keep up the good work.
Liz was great and the Philly cheesesteak roll was amazing!!! Great atmosphere and  the cheesecake roll was great too

I'm disappointed every time I come here.  For some reason, I feel like I have ended up at this bar far too many times.  Often, Loco Pez is too crowded for happy hour, so we walk across the street to here.  The prices seem too high for the quality.  The outdoor patio is so nice.  However, I have tried at least 6 or 7 different menu options, and been disappointed each time.  It's not necessarily bad food, but not nearly as good as other restaurants in the area.  It is also overpriced for what it is.

I meet with a group of friends once a month for dinner. In February, there were eight of us and we decided to meet at Bru burger on Mass Ave. Our first mistake was meeting anywhere downtown on a Friday night. Bru Burger was standing room only. We found a small corner space to squeeze into  at the bar.  Three of us found seats at the bar, the rest of us stood, for an hour and a half. Yes, we waited for an hour and a half for a table. The hostess told us when we came in the wait would be about 15 minutes. We checked about 40 minutes later and was told that it was taking so long because our party was large. Someone in our party suggested moving a few tables together. The manager apologized and gave our table one free appetizer. We were seated about 35 minutes later. The menus were long and floppy and kept hitting the menu of the person sitting across from me. I was not impressed with the burger and onion rings. The onion rings were cold and the burger was mediocre. Two ladies did enjoy their salad and another loved the Balsamic dressing and the Vegan Burger.  I don't drink alcholic beverages but everyone else does and they ordered several drinks and enjoyed them all.  We will not be meeting at Bru Burger again.

Great place to walk around any time of the day. Great place to come on a hot summer day to cool off in the pools or lay in the grass. In the winter, walk around the lighted trees while you sip hot chocolate.

We met another couple for dinner. After our drinks, we ordered food. It seemed like a long wait for the food, as evidenced when out waiter apologized for not being in the dining area as he was prepping our food. He attributed it to being busy, but there were only about 5 tables with no groups bigger than 4. It seemed as if someone had called off or they were understaffed. 

None of this had an impact on the quality of the food. We had the fish tacos, salmon dip, and blue chips. Each was great and full of flavor we had a couple of the $5 Sunday Bloody Marys which are a must have any day of the week! Even our server Joshua was on top of things, it just seemed they were asking a lot of service staff and not properly staffed in the back of the house. 

I'd recommend stopping in and trying the food and a bloody Mary!

Once again thunderbird has come to the rescue.  I was working late tonight and needs a cheese steak fix.  Thunderbird came to the rescue!  Their rolls really make the sandwich!  Service was great to especially at 9:30pm.

Yes it's expensive, but if you take advantage of their coupon deals it's a pretty good place to shop.  

As a guy, I was a little weary of couponing to begin with because I only imagined old housewives who have a lot of time on their hands couponing.  But due to the recent economy and craze with couponing and the fact I'm still a struggling graduate student, I'd give it a try.  

I've saved between 40% to 60% on grocery visits every time they have their coupon promotions and have never looked back.  Yes, Kroger, Walmart, Target, Aldi, etc. are cheaper at face value but if you take advantage of their couponing promotions at Harris Teeter it's definitely worth the time and effort to coupon and shop here.

Needed a quick bite for lunch. Two awesome tacos (chicken & pork) for $4.50 total. They had a nice little kick. Very fresh ingredients. Super quick and friendly service. I would definitely return to try other items on their menu.
Food was very good; however service is certainly not exceptional.  Overall good place for breakfast and lunch.

I just got new eye glasses and contact lenses here and I have had nothing but professional, friendly, patient and competent service.  The Dr. worked with me on trying 3 kinds of contact lenses until I found one I liked.  I would say the prices are moderate.  I highly recommend this place.

Called early Saturday morning for an AC problem and explained that we were on a tight timeline. Sherry assured me that a service tech would be able to respond within a timely manner the same morning. Jason came within an hour and provided great cordial and courteous customer service. He diagnosed the problem and explained the work that needed to be done. Mike also came by to provide the necessary additional help for a quick repair.

Thank you 4 Seasons for the quick response and repair that brought my AC back into operation within a couple of hours.
