Best place in St Louis for the authentic Korean food! I grew up with a Korean family down from us and this is closest I've ever gotten to flying to Korea! Must go!!!
Hands down-best music venue in St. Louis!

Love this place! Went there with my children (13 & 5). This location is cozier and has a better atmosphere and design than the Milpas location but the food is excellent at both. Nice outdoor seating. You can't beat their Enchiladas. Ordered a side order of chicken which is exactly what my 5 year old likes. Great salsas as well; try them all!
delish Italian standards with a very friendly staff. This is definitely my go-to neighborhood Italian restaurant!

Great food for a great price. I always order their beef shawarmas or the kabab sandwich. Love their hummus and Baba Ghanouge. The kabsa plate is also really good, even though typically I'm not too big of a fan of lamb shanks. And it comes with an incredible amount of food, you could feed 2 people with that plate. They also have baklava which tastes pretty good, but I haven't had too much experience eating baklava so I can't judge it that much.

Bland food and bad service sums up my 2nd visit to the restaurant.  1st visit was on the line so I gave another chance but I am done now.  Whoever thinks this is the best italian food need to venture out to other areas like Philly and NYC to see what GOOD italian food tastes like.

We ordered appetizer without bacon and we received bacon.  When I told my server, she apologized but asked whether I liked it.  I repeated again that I am vegetarian.

Once we ordered food, it took good 45 minutes before it came and all this time, the server never came because she knew that it was getting late.  After receiving food, she came every 10 minutes to check whether it was OK - bad service.

Food itself was very bland.  Pasta was undercooked.  I have had much better pasta, pizza, and eggplan parm. 

Tip was added automatically for 5 people which I did not like.
These people care about your pools and spas. Take them your water to get tested and recommendations.

After my third time of trying to give that place a chance and once again getting the worst service, I have written that place off

It's a shame because they have a fun nightlife and good food!

The purchase of a grill gazebo took place on line on May 28,2018.  After the grill gazebo arrived and parts were out of the box, two poles were damaged. I proceeded to call Lowe's customer service, and they responded and stated a return call would take place within three business days. The return call never happened, so, I called again. I was told someone would call to address the issue. Never happened!! I emailed Sunjoy.Lowes, and after a day or so, I finally received a response asking for pics of receipt and damaged poles. I received a response several days later, where I was thanked for my patience. I finally received a call from Lowe's about resolving the problem, and delivery/pickup was scheduled. Delivery and pickup was to take place on Tuesday, but the truck driver was given the wrong address. Well, I had to call Lowe's customer service to reschedule. Today is Tuesday, 5 pm, and I have not gotten a call about rescheduling. This is unprofessional and a disrespect to the consumer.
Delicious food and delightful staff. We will definitely be back. The catfish and smothered chicken were great.

Food is good but a little pricey. The staff tries to push their mixed drinks a little aggressive. The staff also dressed a little too casual. Overall, I would probably not be a "frequent" visitor but I would come back if I was in the mood for their huge "heart attack burger" which included an egg, bacon, and a bunch of other toppings. The desserts looked delicious but I had no appetite left for dessert after eating the burger! There is free parking right across the street and another plus: there are a bunch of other bars and restaurants nearby!

Out of the gate, I want to say good venue, very friendly accommodating staff!  I have to think that others that give this place are not very discriminating about the actual quality of the food.    Normally, however, I would give this a 2 based on my experience.  My disappointment in part could have been based on the fact I didn't try something that  was more of a signature dish for them.  I ordered the smoked brisket Nachos.  I should have ordered just the brisket, or the ribs, but I looked at the prices and said whoa!  Da prices are too high. They don't have the barbecue street cred to charge significantly more that others in the area that have it.   $14 for a beef hamburger the call the "porker" ??   I said beef. $13 for a cheese steak?  Anyway It was partially my fault, I should have picked a signature dish, but I felt insulted by the prices.  Small portion of brisket $`16 really?   This is Pork guys not an expensive cut of beef!  So I wound up with the the smoked brisked Nachos.  So little brisket on them, I couldn't judge the quality.    The real slap on the face was that they used caned cheese on the nachos.  Given the prices, I didn't expect carnival Nachos. They were not better. Shaaaame on them.  My dining partner ordered something that required sauce.  She went to make a selection from what they leave on the table, and I was stunned by the huge bottles they use.  Sitting on the table.  In the FL heat.  Almost empty.  Ewww.  Sauce on the side is the way to go, but that was just gross.  Now as I complete my review, you might think I'm a cheap #$%  but this is the only review I've ever done that focused mostly on price.   Normally I'd give it another try, but I don't want to experiment at those prices.  Or some place that serves 7-11 nachos.

I am giving it 4 stars because I have nothing bad to say (taste wise) about the restaurant.

I have a confession to make, and that is I am not fond of Mexican food... but didn't grow up in Cali, so never got used to the taste... Our friends that just have moved to Boston missed good Mexican food, and I couldn't say no...

I have tasted Mexican food many times and only have come across few dishes that I like... definitely the seafood dishes are my favs.

So, like a good seafood fan I ordered "Los Camarones a la Diabla"... I was bold and asked if they could do it without the diabla (which is female for diablo, devil) meaning without being spicy. They said yes... but BEWARE, no Authentic Mexican understand the meaning of no spicy.

So, I wasn't able to really enjoy the dish (the shrimps were fresh)... the salad in the other hand; was incredible delicious.

My husband ordered the Molcajete with Chiken. The restaurant forgot his order, so the poor hungry bast*** had to wait longer... (and didn't come with the molcajete anyways, which is a kind of stone bowl). it was pure punishment 'cause he likes Mexican food, and it is never an option between the two of us.

It's very very good. The ambiance is nice, and service was great. The food was B+/A-, and hence four stars.

You'll need to make a reservation well in advance because the place is popular. They start you with some pickled crudités and then bring a sampling of about half a dozen little salads (my favorite was the finely chopped beats in tahini sauce-delish!) There's the Central Asian meets Eastern Europe vibe on the menu that embraces Israeli cuisine. Very fresh and the Turkish hummus and hand made bread also "to die for..."

The big disappointments were the "Mezzes" or small intermediate dishes that lived up to their name ("mezzo mezzo...) The duck hearts, Brussels sprouts, and halumi were all salty well past salty restaurant standards. This needs more finessing. 

The lamb shoulder was magnificent though, and the desserts much more hit than miss. So I recommend it highly, just make sure to stay hydrated! Still, it beats the desert (but not the dessert).

Overall their food is great especially steak tacos. Just watch out for their dessert, it was a bit of disappointment due to the lack of flavor.... Last but not least they have a great menu of alcohol beverages ( it can be a bit STRONG in taste). I love the hipster vibe they have.

All in all, it's a good experience. The restaurant itself is beautiful. A really stunning, elegant, chic space. The food is flavorful, interesting, and the presentation is grand. Service is nearly flawless, as everyone else noted. All that said, I wouldn't go back. There's something missing in the entire experience.  The restaurant is trying very hard to do it right, and they are, but neither the food concept nor the overall experience is all that warm. And this is hospitality, after all. Great job though.

I went here after a few of my friends raved about how good it was but I honestly wasn't too impressed. I ordered the orange chicken dinner, and the flavor was fine, but it was room temperature, even though there were maybe only 4 tables filled in the whole place. One of my friends ordered the fried rice, which I thought was pretty bland and honestly I think I could've gotten better fried rice frozen at the grocery store. The service was decent, but the waitress didn't understand that we wanted the check split and one of us ended up paying for the whole thing. The bathrooms were also messy and the toilet was broken in the women's. The location is convenient, as there aren't many Asian food places in downtown Indy, but I don't think I'll be returning.
Wet chicken burrito with green sauce is delicious!

When you're in Reno, you happen to be Vegan and breakfast is your favorite meal of the day, where do you go? Cafe Deluxe is your answer!

With several Vegan options to choose from (marked with an adorable death bunny) on the menu, you'll find something which appeals to you. Especially if you find yourself here on a weekend when breakfast is served all day until close, just the way it should be. 

The Vegan Deluxe Scrambler was impressive stuff. As a budding scrambler connoisseur, I have to say this is one of my favorite scramblers I've eaten anywhere. Tofu and home fries scrambled together with your choice of an additional 3 ingredients seems simple enough but most places don't get it nearly as right as they do here at Deluxe. Rather than just sticking with the three additional ingredients, I'm wondering if they can just add the whole kitchen sink of Vegan ingredients to make the ultimate scrambler for a few bucks extra?  

The breakfast burrito was basically the ingredients of the scrambler, rolled up into a burrito form. Not that there is a problem with this. It's a well known fact that wrapping things in a tortilla makes everything 10X better. It also gives you the added bonus of rolling your R's when you speak while eating it. Be sure to ask for the Vegan sour cream as it's $0.50 extra but worth it to add some moisture to your burrito. 

The drip coffee was excellent! Although if you don't prefer your coffee black, the only Vegan creamer option available to you is Almondmilk and that's not for everyone. The Almondmilk Chai that got lotsa mentions on other reviews was overly spiced, even for me. Normally you can never use too much spices but this bad boy was jampacked with more spice than your grandma's Lazy Susan. At least it has the appeal of being seriously homebrewed. 

The entire Deluxe building is a pretty cool piece of history that has been restored to a hipster paradise, replete with the friendliest hipster-centric staff this side of  SF. Check it out while you're munching on the best breakfast food in town, all at reasonable prices. 

Cafe Deluxe certainly satiated this hangry girl: http://www.yelp.com/biz/cafe-deluxe-reno-2#hrid:EPN6MN_kUn91On5Uwcrvng

Lived in Safety Harbor for 40 years.  Retired to Tallahassee 2 years ago.  Never tried Brady's when we lived here.  Visiting friends in the area this past week.  Wanted to order some take out this evening, someone suggested trying Brady's.  We perused the menu and placed a takeout order.  We picked up our order anticipating a feast.  Baby back ribs, smoked chicken, pulled chx sandwich with an array of sides.  I've eaten BBQ all over the country - Brady's falls short on all fronts.  Baby back ribs were from the biggest baby pig ever - needed another couple hours in the smoker.  These seemed to have been held in water the heated up.  The smoked chicken - dry as it could be.  Sauce couldn't help it.  
Sides - southern beans - over seasoned with too much heat. Garlic potatoes were weak.  Lot of garlic, where's the butter?  Cole slaw was decent.  Potato salad needs salt and pepper.  Corn pudding was tasty.  Not a place I would recommend.  Sorry.  Maybe a bad day.  We won't be back.

A peaceful setting...check 
Friendly staff/instructors...check (so far)
Studio with good space...check; you could fit 25 people with room to spare.
Parking availability...check;  private lot
Decent schedule...check; see the website since events change up the routine.
Good pricelist...check, it's average. To name a few: 60 minute drop-in are $13, new students 3 for $30, $50 for 5 class card (more price packages available).

I came to yoga grumpy after a crappy workday and left 3 degrees much happier. I really can't complain about the class I had and I really liked the studio space. I'll be back to get my buddhi on the mat.

My meal was amazing. I went for late lunch.  I had 2 signature dishes bbq oysters-  very good & red Rosh with crab.  I was delightful to get a green veggie on Bourbon St.  The bread was hot soft wonderful.  The house wine was very good my server Ann was free at & the manager was cordial also.  Would return.

We had dinner at Jane on the patio by the fountain and the rose bushes. They have heaters and the lighting was really nice. The tables are well spaced and we were very comfortable. 

The indoors area is an open room with an enclosed patio on the left, a bar in the middle, and another open space on the right. We got there early to sit outside and indoors was full when we left. 

We didn't order any of the main entrees, which I regret because we enjoyed everything we ordered: pepperoni pizza (maybe a little too doughy and I'm not a fan or raw onion topping applied after the pizza is cooked), chicken piccata, el macho burger, and a fried brie and apple fritters salad. All were excellent! The desserts looked great too, especially the coconut cake which I wish we'd tried! Will have to order it on our next visit.

Best salon I've ever been to, hands down. It is a full pampering experience from the moment you walk in the door. I'm incredibly picky about my hair, and I have always left the salon satisfied with my results. I've had three different stylists there do my hair, and they have all done exactly what I wanted. In my opinion, the best is Katie, but you can't go wrong with anyone. If you want quality services and a relaxing experience, this is your salon.

Nicole and Celine are great women. I recommend this shop highly.

I've been working in center city for 25 years. There are a variety of barbers and salons I can go to at any time. I've tried many shops and get board quickly.  These gals are witty and sharp. They give men's haircuts really well. 

I arrived and immediately sat down to a friendly and up-beat Nicole. She began my cut at the time I was scheduled without delay. She did a great job!!

I'll be back to this barbershop!

When this branch was newly opened, we lined up because we tried Nando's from other cities. It was good then. We tried several times and we noticed the servings are getting smaller and smaller. We tried again this weekend and the chicken portion size is smaller than what was described in the menu (attached photo is 1/4 thigh at $11.00). The Small side dish of Coleslaw and rice were not tasty either. We left still hungry and have to eat again!  What a waste of time and money!

Why is this place always packed? I just don't get it. I've been forced to go here several times and the food is always bleh. When you have to hide the mediocrity of your offerings by drowning it in cheese or some other sauce, you pretty much meet the definition of a 2 star place. Yet night after night this place is packed to the hilt. Don't get me wrong, nothing I've had here has ever been awful, its just never been very good. Truly meh.
Very good beer and food. Food has been a bit limited selection lately but the place is always very busy. PS they don't take reservations, if you get a table right away you are the lucky few.

I have to say first getting started was a bit rough. The waitress took our drink order and it seemed to take forever for her to come back with drinks and even take our order.  By the time she got back we didn't even need to look at the menu to order as we had plenty of time to memorize the entire menu. And yes this was our first time here.. 

We ordered a variety of dishes - and the flavors and combination made up for the slow start. From the pork chop, risotto, fried chicken, drinks. It was all very delisted. The Korean quail wasn't bad but seemed out of place on the menu as an odd item. Nonetheless we tried it and thought it was ok. All other dishes were stellar. 

We were stuffed but everything had been so good we needed to try dessert. Here is where we recognized we should have stopped. The dessert was mediocre. Plenty of places on the area to cap off your sweet tooth after dinner. Dessert isn't their thing. 

4.2 Star if I should say. Good 9.0 dessert 4.0 (we had 2 dishes) drinks 9.0 and service 8.0.. if you're trying to get my math to add up to 9.2 you prob shouldn't be reading my post

Beware MONDAY is trivia night so if you come here after 7 pm you won't get a seat and if you do you won't be able to hear the person next to you talk. The food was fun and there are a lot of treats to purchase. Overall I enjoyed my dinner but I was almost about to walk out due to the extremely rude bartender. I simply asked about the wines and he had no desire to give me any idea of what they even had. They don't even have a wine menu or list of any sort?! Customer service with the bartenders needs to be addressed. The attitude and unwillingness to tell me a single thing about what wines they had or even just a recommendation made me order a water and immediately leave the bar. My significant other ordered a beer and after 10 minutes we gave up and told him to cancel the beer. When I went to walk out the bartender was taking an order at a table near the door. He seen me and proceeded to stand in the way so I had to wait until he was done even though there was plenty of space to move around. I didn't care to look at his name but he was wearing a football jersey on Mar 14th. I truly hope a manger reads this and fixes his attitude immediately. They lost money on my table because we didn't order drinks at the bar due to the fact that we had no clue what to order. Thankfully our waiter was able to find me a wine and get us a beer under 5 minutes!

Anytime I'm asked what the best Mexican food is in Reno I say "Albita's" from the first time I tried it. It is also my wife's favorite place. It is family owned and run and everything is fresh and extremely delicious. The service is great and the prices are fair. I hope this place is around for years to come

Currently I give Salt Rock Tavern a three. I reallllly want to give it more, but after my one visit, it is what it {currently} is. I absolutely love the atmosphere and locale for this new restaurant in Oldsmar. I enjoyed  the decor, vibe and feel of the place. They have a HUGE bar menu and the prices aren't too bad, which surprised me because I "assumed" they would be pricey.

For eats, my sister and I shared nachos and Parmesan fries. They were both only so-so to me. The "nachos" were six individual chips topped with rotisserie chicken and other toppings; I get the whole concept but when I think of nachos, I want messy and more than six to share. The fries were fine, not ah-maze but not bad, just your run of the mill fries. For dessert, my sister, a friend and I shared the strawberry shortcake. It looked pretty and tasty...until you got to the bottom and tasted the basil aioli; it was so bad. I really hate to say that but the basil ruined the dessert completely. Sometimes less is more.

Their entree menu did look appetizing and I would go back and try lunch or dinner. Hopefully their actual meals are a bit better. Will absolutely go back for drinks, too.

My husband and I made reservations for dinner to celebrate our wedding anniversary. When we walked in there was barely anyone there. It seemed we didn't even need to make reservations. We were seated and for 10 minutes not one person came up to our table so we decided to get up and leave. Needless to say, we were glad to leave because we had the best service at another restaurant near by.

Fantastic restaurant! I had the Gnocchi, my date had the veal piccata, both were fantastic, the Gnocchi are just about the best I have ever had. Even though the weather turned and we were moved inside where they were accomidating a huge birthday party, they still kept the service friendly and attentive!!

I really didn't think the food here was worth the money it costs.  The appetizers are around $15 each and the entrees around $35 each.  I am someone who can appreciate upscale cuisine, but I'd rather go somewhere cheaper than here.  Tonight I ordered the lamb with risotto.  For me the lamb was too rare and the rissoto too runny.  I did like my husband's quail, but didn't like the mac and cheese on the side.  We skipped dessert here and went elsewhere.  I wouldn't go back.

This new restaurant is outstanding. The food is delicious. I highly recommend the grilled romaine salad, seafood pot, heirloom tomatoes, beets, pork blade steak, and the amazing blueberry pie. It's the way food should be made. The service was great and the ambience was so pleasant.  If I lived in Philly I would come here often and try everything on the menu.

You know when you walk into a place and something makes you want to walk right out? That was just the vibe I experienced when we walked in here. The staff was nice enough, they sat us upstairs on the balcony like we requested, and the attentive waitress dried off our seats that were wet from the previous rainstorm. So the place gets 2 stars for hiring decent people. On to the hookah, because that's why we're here, right? There are many different flavors and fruitheads and what-not. I suppose $15 is OK for your standard hookah but 2 people per hookah? Really?! I would understand this policy on a busy Saturday night but on a Sunday afternoon, I think it is absurd. We only got one and shared it between the group anyway. Ok, well maybe the food and drinks will make up for bad policy. Nope, it just made it worse. Overall the food was bland (although my friend enjoyed his gyro) and the fries were cold. The hummus was tasteless and definitely lacked tahini. I had two glasses of pinot noir which were good but my biggest pet peeve was committed in my friend's cocktail. People!! Coke + Sprite never ever equal Ginger Ale!! Ever! Stop doing that! The vibe was kind of clubby, with the local pop station blasting through the fake rocks in the background. This won't be my next hookah hangout. I'm sure there are people that would enjoy this place and its atmosphere but I won't be back.
Great food and experience. Hawaii was an amazing server and kept us laughing all night.
though it was on avenues 2009 must eat list IT REALLY NO LONGER IS. after gaining fame their food has gone down hill so has their service. IT IS EXPENSIVE for what you get I rather eat at an IHOP

Drove by and saw there was a new Indian place so we had to try it. The food was great and the staff is very attentive. I ordered chicken tikka masala which Is not on the menu but my "go to" Indian meal and it was outstanding. Definitely recommend this place if you want good Indian food!

Would have like to have given a better review. Our first experience was great. They were very accommodating for a party of 10. Good food. 

We decided to return and try their Sunday brunch while bike riding. We arrived and were told they would come and get us from the front waiting bench when a table was ready on the patio. After they seated the three groups that came in after us on the patio,  we came back in and they said they were finally ready to seat us, walking past the three groups that came in after us that had very nice seats in the shade. We got full sun at a table that was so close to the other tables we couldn't even sit at it. When we decided to leave, our so called waiter asked how we were and when we said not good and we were going to leave his response was a very insincere 'Sorry'. 

They might want to train their staff in customer service and how to make things right.  We have been enjoying the main restaurant for years and have always had great food and customer service. Too bad it hasn't transferred to the new location.

Just OK!
I know a lot of people enjoy this restaurant, but I truly did not!! I went on sat eve Jan 23. The peope were very friendly. I was there for take out and was waited on promptly. I ordered the haddock platter with sauted corn and Cole slaw and a veg sandwich for my wife. 
I recieved my order in about 10 minutes and was on my way home.... Very hungry! Oh if this was a review for service only I would have given a 5. 
When I got home I opened my meal to find small portions of the side items and 3 fish sticks! Fish sticks!!! They tasted like captain d's! The corn was not a flavor I would recommend and the slaw was turnin brown from age.... Yuck! The only thing good was the bread. 
The veggie sandwich was ok, my wife seemed to enjoy it. I thought there shouldve been more for the $$$. 
Over all I won't be recomendong this place to anyone.
Sorry peid piper!
If you want fish go to Boston battered, if you want great sides with normal portions go to calypso cafe!

Every cake I've bought from here has been out of the case.  I never pre-order b/c usually I forget...but I can always find something that works for the occasion I am buying for and I like that. :) 

They do amazing "present" cakes and I am particularly fond of their carrot cake.  They do all sizes and usually have a decent variety to choose from if you are looking for something last minute.

Otherwise, I do recommend ordering ahead of time.  I have never paid more than $20 for a carrot cake and I believe the present cakes are around $40.  So, they aren't what I would consider "inexpensive" but they are affordable if you are looking for a specialty cake and are in a pinch.

They also have great pastries and donuts - everything is always fresh, the bakery smells like a bakery should - of sweet flour and sugar and I always LOVE just stopping in for a whiff!  I definitely recommend them for any and all fun occasions but if you don't' want to choose quickly, I do suggest calling a week in advance to get exactly what you are looking for!

The staff is AMAZING!

Maybe more closer to 3 1/2 stars but I could still sleep fine at night with just 3 stars as well.

Lets just dive right into this one. I'll spare everyone the "heavenly bliss", "swoon by my heart" and "supremely divine" references to the food. I've long accepted that I'll never make Yelp elite status as I do not seem to know the right people. Oh well, moving on.

Crepe encrusted trout; All in all not too bad. Personally, I feel it was lacking in flavor. It seemed to be in a tad bit too much oil. The "crepe" coating was pretty good and I did enjoy it but I just feel it could have had a little more "pop".

Mussels; Probably the best mussels I've ever had. I got them in the white and spicy configuration. I know its poor etiquette to dip your bread in the broth but it was just so damn good, I couldn't help myself.

Tuna steak; The GF had this. It was actually pretty good. Whatever flavors they infused it with certainly worked.

Gnocchi; This was the dish I was most looking forward to. Honestly, it was much too dry. While it did have a good flavor to it the dryness left a bad after taste and left me running for the water glass.

Dessert; We had the mascarpone cheese cake. It was pretty good but it had the consistency of a very thick and solid jello. Most definitely far outside of my top 10 cheesecake experiences.

I would honestly say that I probably wouldn't go back here until a try about a dozen other places that are on my list first but by the time that's done, there'll be another dozen new places to try.

The menu here is extensive, and everything looks really good. I've had smoothies here and wraps, salads, and a quinoa dish and haven't been disappointed. The servers are very friendly and accommodating. So glad they came to Collingswood!

This Thai restaurant is close to my home. I love Thai food but I love only a few dishes at this restaurant. I find the waitstaff inexperienced and not very attentive. If you order tea, plan to drink only tepidly warm water. That said, the Panang Curry is excellent and they have several hot soup and noodle bowls that are quite good as well. The Pad Thai is bland and disappointing.

Been a long time since I've 5 starred a joint but it's well deserved here for sure and here are 2 great reasons to prove it. Most reviews from me start with a few bites of each item and maybe 2 trips to the table from our waitress and then I pause to write a quick review while the thoughts are fresh on my mind. Tonight my phone rested flat on our table until the last bite was taken. Reason 2 has to do with the fact that I never found it necessary to use a drop of BBQ sauce to flavor the meats that I savored. Those are some true signs that you just enjoyed a quality meal. No reductions, no aiolis, no asian wasabi slaws, no fancy chefs tricks just great smoked meats in generous portions. The thing that separates this from all the other chic BBQ shacks is that fact that they put just as much emphasis on fantastic tasting sides as they do putting together 5 star smoked meats. Most places are so hung up on differentiating the BBQ they forget the sides and their supporting role. I was fortunate enough to sample the Bananas Foster bread pudding as a side? Wow! The beans full of meat that tasted like it could be remnants of the burnt ends appetizer and the sweet potato casserole was as tasty as grandmas old favorite Now the main course tonight was the smoked prime rib, amazing smoked edges and rare juicy center section saddled with a side of creamy horseradish sauce. Perfectly cooked and thickly sliced. My second meat was a side of "burnt ends". If you are a fan of pan drippings with bits of charred brisket then this is the meal for you. All the temptations of BBQ that you enjoy in powerfully flavored scraps of joy for you. Scoop THAT on top of your Jalapeno cornbread square ! The final nail in my coffin was finding Cheerwine soda on tap as much as you can drink! Pure Heaven!  I figured that I wouldn't like the banquet style seating but my beautiful wife and I found a way to make it work for us . The "sweets bar" provided us a giant slice of coconut cake to take on the road with us.  4 Rivers Smokehouse provided us tonight the best BBQ experience we have had in Tampa in a very long time. Cowboy up, it's well worth the stop!

I already hate eating fast food restaurants so half doing things really grinds my gears. I ordered a spicy chicken sandwich from here at 1am in the morning and the only thing on my sandwich is lettuce. LITERALLY. I'm not sure who the owner of this establishment is, but you really need to speak with your managers and or employees about treating customers how they would like to be treated. If I have them this shitty patty they would not be a happy camper. I'm disappointed I wasted my time and money at this establishment.
This place was amazing!! Our waiter could have been better but other then that from the rolls they bring you to the food it was the best Italian food i have ever had

I absolutely loved my experience at Healing Waters. I got a 60 minute massage and a facial. The massage was wonderful, i wish i could remember the womans name who massaged me as she was fantastic, very calming voice and made me feel very comfortable. I've had a couple massages before at a different place, and the technician liked to talk, which took away from the experience, so i was more than happy when this woman only spoke to ask me questions pertaining to the massage.

The facial was also very nice. I wasn't too keen on the woman doing my facial however, as she seemed very plain and not very informative about the products she used. While it was nice, it seemed very short and to the point, kind of like she just wanted to get it over with. Other than that though, it was great.

I would most definitely recommend this Spa to anyone, and will most definitely be going back!

What a great place!  My wife and I just left here after a wonderful 4 night stay to celebrate our first anniversary.  Joe and Bebe were awesome and even upgraded us to a huge room since it was a special occasion.  The room was great, they had snacks all day long and they were both really helpful in terms of restaurant/site seeing advice.  I would definitely stay here again and would recommend it to anybody else.  Dont listen to the 1 star reviewer on here.  Joe explained to me what happened and it seemed very reasonable.  You won't regret booking a room here, I promise you.
The hosts we're not very happy to see or greet you, and looked like they did not know where to seat us. Food was good for the price.

I was just in New Orleans on a business trip and loved staying at the Ritz! The staff is extremely accommodating. I had giant boxes that I needed to bring up to my room and ship out some contents, and the bellman was at my service almost instantaneously. Awesome service, people, and very beautiful hotel!

Went here for an oil change.
Staff was friendly and service was good. 
Pricing seemed high. I have a 350F that holds 16 qrt  of oil. was quoted $90 over phone ended up being $120. Ford dealer charges me $100. No AC in waiting room. In AZ that is worth 1 star.

Today I recieved a call on behalf ofTunnel vision video plumbing from some low level attorney named Liz Hammett. ( sister of the owner Sharron P ) stating they had every right to dig up my property.  Guess I will see them in court.

Food was pub fare, and as to be expected it was okay.

The upstairs has an awesome atmosphere and there was a live concert going on so it was basically like being outside being able to listen to it. You felt like you were outside with all the openness of the bar. The bartenders were all very nice and friendly and talked with us. Everyone at the bar sitting near us were all out of town too and they were friendly as well. Kind of expensive, as to be expected since it was on Broadway. 

Overall, great experience and friendly, quick service.

The best chi I've had studying so far...way cooler that starbucks. Coffee is pretty good, but I only tried the latte. Will update after I sample others. Comfortable chairs, nice ambiance...music is a little loud, but not annoying at all. Many places to hang out and chat too!

I had the maw maw Cajun breakfast and it was delicious! The service was great and there's even a discount thru yelp. Definitely come back here again. It was early in the am so not too crowded but I can imagine what's its like in the pm with all the drunk revelers for Bourbon St.

JooJoo's service is amazing every time we come to this restaurant they always welcome us with smiles. The food is so fresh and full of favor. My favorite dishes are Seafood Sun Tofu and Kalbi ribs! I'm so glad that I could find genuine Korean restaurant in STL

Delicious!! Great location right on the corner. Nice outdoor seating area. Pretty small inside but they do have tables to sit. Gelato was amazing. We got blood orange and raspberry and lemon and vanilla. All of the flavors were very good. They do allow you to sample the flavors which is awesome. I would highly recommend!

Just stopped in for the first time and was very impressed. I live out of state but visit family here often. I'm happy they are open Sundays (the previous shop here was not and I could rarely go). The staff was super friendly. Biscuits and gravy may be the best I've ever had. Had a blueberry scone that was fresh and delicious. Also an excellent coffee drink. They have multiple non-dairy options too (even coconut and oat milk). This will be a regular stop on my visits to Old Hickory.

Went to the one on Gandy, but am posting here as they do not have a separate listing for that one. 

Was a little daunting for me entering (after lunch at Cafe Marti next door) as I've never been in 1 of these places before. But the staff were really friendly and approachable. 

Don't know if the prices are higher or lower than elsewhere, but everything is "but 1, get 1" year round. Not obvious that this was the "house rule" until 1 of the staff members mentioned that.

We keep trying to give this place a chance but whenever we call and order food to pick up or have delivered its ALWAYS wrong. Always. Don't get me wrong, the food is good, but there's a disconnect between the person taking the order and the people making it. "Wings, dry" the wings come with sauce in them. "We'd like the platter with french fries" no french fries. "Chicken fingers with honey mustard" no honey mustard but tons of BBQ sauce. It's ridiculous. They need to pay better attention.

I was in the city of Tucson, and heard about North. North is located in the shopping center called "La Encantada" which is a more upscale part of Tucson. The drive up to the area was scenic and beautiful and the center was amazing! North was pretty busy, but we got seated upon arrival. We were served with a loaf of bread and butter, then quickly placed our orders. As an appetizer we got the cauliflower and the margherita pizza. For the main dishes, my table ordered the bolognese, squid ink pasta, and the chicken pesto pasta. When we were waiting, the general manager came to our table to inform us that they were unable to make the cauliflower for us, so in lieu of it, they gave us the chef's board for free! The margherita pizza came out and was super good, and I even ate the crust. Finally, the pastas arrived and all of the dishes were amazing! The bolognese had a good amount of meat and the sauce hung onto the freshly made pasta. The squid ink pasta was a different taste for my palate, and had a kick to it. The chicken pesto pasta was also super flavorful, but the bolognese was my favorite out of the three. All in all, I had a wonderful experience at North and will go back again.

He's amazing, simply put. Given nothing except her name, he connected with my girl, Lucy, who passed away about a month ago.  The things she was showing him/telling him to tell me he could never have known.  The whole experience was unforgettable, and I can rest easier knowing that my girl is happy and is still with me....and I know all her best memories too! She even described last weekend as "proof" she was with me... just incredible.  All I can say is wow, and thank you so much for sharing your gift with the world.  My heart is full.  OH! A doggie from my childhood came through before Lucy did! Soooo neat! He graciously stepped aside so that we could talk to Lucy, but I know it was him because of the way he looked and the things Charles shared.  I shared the discussion with my dad, who loved this dog immeasurably, and he was so grateful and moved. Thanks again, Charles.

All I have to say is pomegranate mimosas and MIGAS.

Being from Texas, I couldn't miss breakfast tacos and migas more, believe me.  The migas here are good--not like home, but they will tide me over until I get to visit.

Brunch on the weekend tends to be a little disappointing--long waits, running out of specials, their Yelp drink deals are only good when purchased with a meal so the hour wait is with full priced drinks--but the food is good quality and the atmosphere is nice.

I've heard the wait is better on Canal, but have yet to test this.  There is some outdoor seating while you wait and parking is relatively easy on the street.

One of our favorite places to go when visiting Philly, my friends and I were looking forward to a repeat visit to 2nd Story. We were greeted by the hostess who promptly sat us at our table and got us started with a beer flight. While we were trying the beers when our server approached us and took our food order. We finished the fight and we're ready to order our chosen beers. Our server was nowhere to be found and the attentive hostess went off duty. The food runner brought our food (gourmet hot dogs and fries) and we asked him to please send over our service. Half way through our meal, my sister got up to find our server. She found her sitting and texting and when my sister tapped her on the shoulder she said she was sorry and she probably shouldn't have been texting. After a few minutes, she took our beer orders. We got our beers after finishing our food. We were ready for another beer, but our server disappeared again. Sitting at a tabl by the window, wr saw her outside chatting. Again, we asked the person who cleared our dinner plates to please get our server. When she finally came to our table, we ordered a second round of beers and dessert. We got our beers and she then came back to tell us that the "boozy sundae" that we ordered was unavailable because the ice cream was "too soft." She recommended the pound cake dessert so we have it a go. It was awful, unlike any pound cake that any of us had ever had. Of course, we had to find the server to ask for our check. Other patrons found themselves doing the same. Worst service EVER!
I love their subs but just about ever time I order it takes over an hour to get, and half the time something is left behind or the wrong thing

Tried this place after coming out of a concert in the MidFlorida Amphitheater.. It was the only place we found open late, nearby. This place is pretty isolated, there's nothing close by except a jail. Not exactly inviting late at night.

I really loved the look of the bus outside... It was fun. You walk up to order and the girl helping us was not friendly or unfriendly... Just working. We took our order to go.. And didn't take too long a wait.

We tried the preñado burrito and el plato with chicken. Nothing was particularly exceptional... Just ok. I actually really disliked the chicken. Simply not for my taste. My husband said the chipotle chicken was better. It worked out since it was open and we were hungry. Otherwise, i would skip it.

I have been to a lot of dentists. I hate the dentist generally.  I'm just not a fan.  I recently had a dental emergency, and ended up in their offices.  They were so very helpful and very accommodating.  They were honest, professional, and helpful.  I really appreciate this practice.  I plan on going back for all my dental needs!

My mother and I went there for lunch on a weekday and I was surprised the place isn't packed all the time.  I've been there twice now and the food was great both times.  The first time I had the crab cakes which were nicely crafted except I like them to be fried a little bit more so they are crunchier on the outside but the flavor profile was spot on.  The second time I had the appetizer as a meal of mussels in a garlic white wine sauce mmmmm, mmmmm, mmmmm so delicious.  I can't say enough good things about this.  First of all the portion was very generous I had a whole other meal out of the left overs.  The mussels were perfectly cooked and the broth was so yummy I couldn't  get enough of it.  My mother had the spinach lasagna which I also sampled and I don't care for the red sauce or gravy but I could have eaten this sauce by the jar full it was sooooo good.  My mother actually wanted to know if they sell the gravy by the jar because she would be a regular customer.  The lasagna consisted of a lot of ricotta and mozzarella cheese and in my book you can never have too much ooey  gooey cheese on anything.  Very much a comfort food and again the portion was more than generous.  For dessert we shared a piece of tiramisu and our server told us it had just been freshly made and indeed it was so fresh and good the only thing that it was missing was the coffee liquor which would have made it over the top fantastic.  Everything was very fresh and delicious and I will be certainly going back there more often now that I know I can have a great meal there!

based on the most recent reviews, i thought twice before i ordered from this restaurant. however, i was craving indian something fierce and this was the closest restaurant to me.

thank god i didn't heed the previous reviews! i ordered the lamb bhuna and raita online which was super easy. (i always test out the quality of my indian and pakistani restaurants by ordering lamb or goat. both meats can be difficult to cook appropriately, and if it's done right, you can count me as a future customer.)

my lamb was incredible! there were at least 8 or so chunks of perfectly cooked lamb in a rich spicy tomato sauce. it was the perfect amount of heat. my dish came with a huge order of basmati rice and three different accompaniments (a very spicy but delicious 'salsa verde', spiced onions, and i'm guessing tamarind sauce which i didn't use.) i only ate part of the rice with the lamb and used the 'salsa verde' and raita to add another layer of flavor.

based on how quickly my food was ready and the ease by which it was to place my order online, i'll definitely be back. hopefully, the previous reviews were flukes because i want several repeats of today's meal in the near future.

bonus 1: you can put specifications on each thing you order online. por exemple, i'm gluten intolerant and noted that restriction on each of my items. when you check out, you can choose delivery vs pick-up and credit card vs cash. 

bonus 2: 10% discount on my online order

If you enjoy the natural flavors of food, then this is the place for you.  This is not standard Mexican-American fare that comes pre-drenched in cheese and other gooey accouterments.  It's about the actual flavor of food cooked well.

The tongue is excellent, but the Pork Cheek (Buche) is excellent.  It's that perfect blend of fat, meat, and salt.

My husband really likes their hot sauce, but I personally find that the Buche doesn't need it or the lime.

Came with my 6 year in old in time for the KidsCard event. For a  $5 donation you get a card with 5 free kids meals on it. Kids meals are usually $6.99 so it's a great deal. You just have to purchase one regular meal each time. 

We had tasty pizza, cheese for him and spinach feta shrimp in mine. Friendly server and very clean and quiet room. Oddly hip alternative music though. 

I find BP a good standard for families with young kids. 

Can't go wrong with the kids card works out to a dollar per meal and they're valid till June 2019.
Just like everybody else says- just like what you'll find in Paris! Seriously. I would give 5 stars except that I wish they weren't cash only- that's annoying to have to hit the bank first.

Average CVS - we stopped in for passport photos but had to wait a really long time to get them because only 1 person know how to take them.

Meanwhile it had everything we needed and its own parking lot - which helps in downtown Media.

As good as this food is, and it's incredible, their service is even better. I called and asked a couple questions about the menu and was greeted with freindly and helpful answers. My food was ready faster than expected and they called to let me know. I walked in and the staff (different from the woman that called) was ridiculously friendly. 

So the food. I've had the sweet chili chicken, sweet BBQ chicken, fries, onion rings and butter egg rice. 

Sweet chili is off the charts. So good. And should be the first one tried in my opinion. 
Sweet BBQ was great. I got hot and spicy on the side. And put some of it and it was incredible. 
Fries were a pretty standard order of fries. Solid, but not something totally ground breaking. 
Onion rings were super crispy like the chicken and very good. 
The butter egg rice was simple but very good. And a very nice compliment to cool the pallet after the hot and spicy sauce. 
I'm not a super avid fried chicken eater, but this has changed me. Phenomenal stuff. And unparalleled service.

I came here on a Friday after work with one of my fellow brides to be to chirp about bridal things and what not and, most importantly, to eat pizza. We were seated at a table next to two other girls eating together who weren't really interested in making light of our extremely close proximity. They just looked at it each other and continued quietly talking to each other, but I really don't think they were even talking. Their pizza came out and they split one pizza between the two of them and I observed a conscientious effort on both their parts to out-not-eat the other. Like let me use a fork and knife to cut up this already small slice of pizza and take birdlike nibbles. 

Meanwhile, my dining partner and I killed two pizzas with our bare hands, in addition to a very womanly combo of white wine and Diet Soda. We talked and ate freely but that's just the kind of women we are. The pizza here is off the chains - we went with the four cheese and an excellent spinach pie. Both were perfectly balanced, not dry (big pizza peeve), and well-sauced. Another superb destination on the 13th Street strip.

I just got dizzy while typing this review. I need pizza, stat.

Made my first trip to 10-01 yesterday, on a cold and dreary rainy day. I was searching for soup. Specifically, vegetarian-friendly soup that wasn't secretly made with chicken broth. 10-01 delivered...in three varieties!

The tomato bisque soup was delicious, as was the wedge salad. Only complaint was that the salad was drowning in dressing - I'll have to remember to order it on the side next time. The rest of the menu looked fantastic, as did the beer list. 

The atmosphere and service was pleasant and cheerful, even with the gloomy grey skies outside. I'll be back soon for more!
The salmon here is phenomenal. Love that you have options for warm food as well. Will be coming here whenever on this side of town :)

The brunch was so good! I wish we had time to go back for a dinner, but the next time we're in Philly I would love to try it.

We ordered the salmon on toast and asparagus con truffas for brunch.  The combination of the salmon with the egg and toast was perfect.  The asparagus was also delicious.  It had some smoky bacon in it, which was SO GOOD!

I've been here a couple of times and I wasn't expecting much, just stumbled upon this place the first time around, and I liked it!  It's a little greasier than Pat's or Geno's but there is a ton of flavor so I like it!  They use typical cheesesteak bread (not very substantive) and I got mine with sauteed onions and wiz.  Yummy!  I usually need to add hot sauce to make up for the lack of flavor in typical cheesesteak sandwiches but this one definitely is fine on its own.  The meat wasn't dry and overall, it was greasy but not in a terrible or overpowering way.  

The second time I went, I didn't want to wait on the line around the block (why are there so many people wanting cheesesteaks at all times of the day??) so I called ahead and when I arrived, I got to go to the front of the line, pay for my steaks and jump right out.  The sandwiches held nicely and the flavor was all still there.  There isn't anything unusual, unique or special about this place--they just make a solid cheesesteak.  It's not the nicest of places to dine in but it's what you'd expect out of a cheesesteak joint.

An awesome and quaint gem!  
The Spanish Garden Inn was our home for 3+ weeks after evacuating due to the Thomas fire and mudslides.  The location was ideal; walking to nearby restaurants and downtown SB was great.  Our room was, like the entire SGI, a Mediterranean oasis.  Its features included:  a fireplace, lovely and tasteful Mediterranean decor, a quiet space (we never heard anyone neighbors), a balcony with seating for two, very ample space and appreciated accents such as artistic water colors (by the owner) and a French-press coffee pot.  The morning breakfast offerings (included with the room) were terrific and varied from the usual cold items (cereal, yogurt and hard boiled eggs) to well prepared quiche, a very nice cheese and meat plate, baked breads and sweets and a variety of smoothies. Oh, BT W, the weekend brought complimentary wine and cheese offerings.   SGI has a very pleasant courtyard, nicely accented with plants and a fountain.  The fitness area is modest but adequate and the pool was a nice area to just hangout.
Then there was the excellent service.  Any request was met with a "can do" attitude and from housekeeping to the dining staff to the front desk the service was flawless and friendly.  The SGI is to be complimented and not missed.
Fantastic place with great staff. Love the vibe of the place and how you can see them put together the food.
Great crab cakes but their homemade remoulade sauce makes it. The blackened shrimp wrap is great.
The coffee ice cubes make the iced coffee great and they have many different options on flavorings.

If I could take away half a star I would. Let me take you on a little trip.
8:30pm, we walk into the place from the hotel next door. It looks nice, nice patio, people smoking... But, nice patio.
 Sit down at the bar, it's small, and a guy moves aside so my co-worker and I can sit down. He starts singing their praises while the bartender tells me it's going to be a minute, and gives us a menu. 15 minutes later we get to order our first beer. I'll keep this part short and sweet, the French country ale tastes like bile, and I told her so. She told me I should have given it back, they just tapped the keg, give it 3 or 4 days. Um, I'm passing through, was hoping for a good beer tonight. 
 So we have a beer, order another, Belgian Guise, not as bad. 9:15 rolls around and the bartender asks if we're ordering dinner, because the kitchen is closing (9:15 on a Thursday night) bar and kitchen close at 10. I tell her yes, give us just a minute. So I order the the pork shank, my buddy orders the double tenderloin. 10 minutes pass, the bartender comes back and says no Pork shank. OK, I get the meatballs and smashed potatoes, with green beans. It gets brought out by I assume the GM or owner, who says she won't even let the employees have these they're so good. My buddy's sandwich is way overcooked, and my meatballs, well, it's sad, they were raw. Not even  browned, ok they were browned, but that's where it stopped. They were not even WARM inside, completely red. I asked the bartender if I could send them back, and she obliged. Same GM/owner brings my plate back out 10  minutes later (my buddy finished eating 5 minutes ago), and the meatballs are literally boiling hot, microwaved for probably 6 minutes and brought back out. She tells me her cook's thermometer wasn't calibrated,  and that's why my food was raw... Not like an actual CHEF would serve RAW meatballs. But don't worry folks, they nuked em long enough for me to burn my mouth.
 I didn't eat them, and the bartender did comp the meal, but I haven't eaten since 7am, this was dinner, and now I'm sitting in the hotel room, hungry and pissed. As soon as 10pm hit, all the staff was at the bar getting drinks, they didn't even have to wait the same 10 minutes I did.
 BOO to you Black Swan Dive.

I tried Cafe Blackbird this week for the first time for Brunch. It is located in a cute little strip mall in the Crestwood neighborhood. When I got here which was around noon, it was quite busy. There was already a line up. Usually you have to order your food, pay and then sit down. They have  a nice weekend brunch that runs from 9:00 a.m to 4:00 p.m. and their regular black bird breakfast which is basically  before 11:00 a.m. They also offer tapas and a great selection of drinks in the evenings if you chose to come here when they have live music.  

Café Blackbird is a family run coffee, wine bar and live music establishment. Although I was only here for brunch, I definitely enjoyed the vibe. You can find local art on the walls of this little cafe and they have partnerships with other local businesses in Edmonton like : Ace Coffee, Coffee Roasters, Cally'Teas, Italian Centre Shop and  Earth Water just to name a few. So if supporting local businesses is your thing, definitely come here.

I ordered the Savoury Morning Hash with eggs and substituted the bacon for tempeh and ordered their salted hot chocolate. Let's just say it was pretty yummy. I also decided to try their dairy free, gluten free cinnamon bun and OMG... I honestly think this is the best cinnamon bun I've ever tasted, no lies. If you're conscious about the  food you eat, are vegan or have gluten allergies I recommend coming here for lunch or brunch. I will definitely be coming back in the evenings to check out the drinks and live music.
Best mani/pedi I've had in years! Jen is wonderful! Took awhile to find the right fit but I'll be coming here  for the foreseeable future!

I will be honest about this business. My husband and I first started going here because we got a real good deal on groupon. The owner John is a sweetheart and really made me feel welcome. The first massage was a full body for an hour and very thorough and professional. I felt sorry for him because the building he was renting had leaky pipes and as a result he had water damage many times that left the carpet wet and a dank smell. Then it caused a fire and he had to relocate. But we had more than a handful of good experiences there and are waiting to go to the new location as soon as he gets his license. I would recommend this business to others because they give great massages and John is a people person. It is just unfortunate that they had problems with the pipes but it was not his fault.

Okay, I've been to this place three times. The first time I went, the service was okay. Our server was polite, the food was good & the noise level was just fine because there weren't many people there. The second time I went I order a side of loaded fries wings to go & the bartender tried to tell me I could not have it like that until I informed I've had it before. The manager corrected her & informed her I was correct. She didn't apologize for it & she had a nasty attitude. The third and last time I went, I order my food over the phone and when I got there, no one asked me if I needed help for about 5 minutes, so I left. The manager is really nice, but her staff is very unprofessional. New Orleans East doesn't have much in the area and I like to support black businesses. But the lack of professionalism turned me off. I haven't been back in months.

A small town family operation that is well-deserving of its Main Street store front.  One of the best spots in town for a quick bite, always quick and fresh!  The falafel pita and smoothies are my go-to!

I just had a guy come to my door, gave him about 4 minutes of my time while trying to explain to him so he doesn't waste his breath. I even got my boyfriend to the door since we live in his house. We are a family of 6 people. 4 kids under 9 years old and we left them alone to fold laundry which he knew bc our kids are loud. We told him that we weren't interested and tried to be nice, but we had to cut him off and tell him we really aren't interested. Before he left he decided to be rude and tell us, " have fun with your bug problem" 

Im glad I said no because I don't care how much cheaper they are then the competition....I'll pay extra for the customer service.

I like a drive in that has prime rib dinners on fridays and saturdays.   Unfortunately we came here for a snack, so i got a ice cream potato, and a jr hamburger.   Hamburger was good, ice cream potato was unique.

I tried my friends fries (meh), and my other friend's white chocolate huckleberry milk snake (too sweet).

I've been meaning to write this review for the experience I had two days ago. I've gone to many Vitality Bowl places, and this is by far the best and fastest service I've ever encountered! This deserves five stars solely for their friendliness, the speed of their delivery and the quality of their creation. 

My mother, my sister and I went ahead to order large Nutty Bowl. What are the odds that we share the same mood for the same bowl? Once we made the orders, 5 minutes went by, and then, a server brought in all bowls. Retrospectively speaking, the process typically took between 15 and 30 minutes before the delivery. Was it because the nutty bowls were simpler to make? I had Warrior and Superfood bowls and they took longer to finish. In any case, I had hoped that they can maintain the same speed across all the other delicious bowls across the menu. 

The granola and honey were generous, and the açaí purée with nutty substance blended in was spot on. We went outside where they had the outdoor seating and refreshed ourselves with the cold meals on that hot day. Home, sweet home.

Very nice decor and pleasant service, however the the menu is very confusing and the food was disappointing. First, as with many asian restaurants, if you order a soda, it comes in a can at $2 a pop. No biggie, but not ideal. We ordered the Summer Roll as an appetizer and it was totally disgusting. Had a very gummy texture and neither of us could eat it. I ordered the Green Curry and was surprised at how small the portion was. The flavor was pretty bland and the vegetables were mushy. I thought the prices were  too high for what you get. Back to the menu.... The last two pages had no prices on them at all and the lunch menu is marked "Entrees" I ordered the Curry under the "Entrees" for $9 and was surprised when the bill cam and it was actually $13. When I asked about the price discrepancy, I was told $9 was the lunch price which is a much smaller portion. Fair enough, but it's not clear on the menu and I couldn't imagine a smaller portion than what I got as a dinner entree. I really like Thai, but won't be returning.

Angel is a miracle worker! Between her and the assistant/receptionist (didn't catch his name) I had a great relaxing morning and left with stunning hair. Angel gave me exactly what I was looking for, and did what seemingly no one else could. I will never go anywhere else again for any of my hair needs!

As a man, when you walk into a jewelry store it can be quite intimidating because in many cases, you get absolutely stormed by sales people. I am a sales person myself, so I understand the motivation.

That being said, I came in to this location on a whim and bought a watch. They were so helpful and professional, that I brought my wife back two weeks later, and we ordered two more watches. As far as I'm concerned, they are the only jewelry store that I will do business with.

Our family, anxious to try authentic NOLA beignets, were disappointed with Cafe du Monde. This place redeemed everything we had heard about beignets. They were puffier, lighter and better tasting.  

Cafe du Monde may be the iconic place to go for beignets, but Cafe Beignet's scored much higher in our family taste test.

The food is always great here! The downside is that it can be loud with the room layouts so ifs difficult to hear others if you're with a larger group and not sitting right next to them. 

In our group-favorites are the pulled pork, brisket, and definitely the huge pork tenderloin. I tried the sweet potato this time and appreciated they bring out sides of butter and cinnamon/sugar so you can control the taste to your liking.

The fried biscuits and apple butter hit the spot every time!

They have coupons for free apps out there so keep an eye out for those!

David Martin....... worst guy to ever answer the phone or call back. You guys need to train this man again in whatever he thinks he does at this company. Horrible service will NEVER EVER recommend you guys to any of my family, family friends, my friends friends, to this company HORRIBLE. 

AVOID

I came here recently thinking I was going to like it. Wrong. If you want to get hit on by some stupid older guys wearing too strong cologne, then by all means come here. 

Not to mention, the drinks are way too expensive. $14!!!! Are you kidding me?! This is New Orleans, not Miami. I can buy 3 drinks at any other bar for the price of that one! Even though the drink was good, it wasn't worth $14. At most, I would say $9, and I've had better $9 drinks. Plus, they don't make your drink on the spot. They pre-make it and just pour it in a glass. I feel like if I'm paying $14 for a drink, I want to see it being made since I'm paying for the "craft" cocktail aspect of it.

The bartender was friendly though so I give her half a star and the atmosphere was nice so that deserves the other half star. Other than that, I will not come back to this place. New Orleans has way too many good, fun bars that are cheap and where you can have conversations with interesting people, not guys who stare at you too intently and smell like they took a bath in a bottle of cologne.

We had a 60th birthday party there.  Service was excelent.  Diane (great and very accomadating)  was our server drinks  and appetizers came fast and food to follow.  Extremely affordable and great food.  We all enjoyed ourselves.  Clean place and food was good.  What else can you ask for.  We are 100% satisfied!!!

Do not let the name of this place fool you - it's not all just from "the garden", i.e - greens. I had an amazing BLT Smash with egg. It's black pepper bacon, lettuce, tomato, and avocado mash, and I added an over-easy egg! Okay, that made it incredibly messy, but really, messy sandwiches are the best. The bread had a great crust and was nice and chewy, making the mess easier to hold onto. The fries were crispy and the perfect complement (I swapped out my greens for them). 

I also got a Hoosier Heater cold-pressed juice with it, and it was delightful and refreshing. I could taste the flavor of the jalapeno rather than the spice from it which was nice (although I do love spicy). I also can't wait to try some of their cocktails made with those fresh juices. 

They currently use Toast to allow for contact-less checkout, and I am all about that (mostly because of convenience)! I can't wait to come back and try more of their options which tend toward brunch-y! I seriously had a hard time choosing my lunch!
$5 cheese dosa was a nice snack, it feels like they are a little understaffed so it took a while to get them. It tastes like the cheese that runs off the edges of a homemade grilled cheese.

Ribs were fall off the phone tender ......
Neighborhood a little rough but should be ok.....
sides were decent but ribs ............... did i say they were tender.
little guy behind the desk acts like he doesnt speak english ... i suspect it is an act after doing it for as long as he has.
check the orders when he gives it to you ... he will not always  give u want  and then act like he doesnt understand ... yeah rrrright!!

they were out of a lot of things but it was new years eve around  4 pm ...

prices are amazingly low !!!

stop here - you will not  go wrong!!!

did i say the ribs were tender ???
Great food and drinks at the new late Happy Hour!  Well worth a try.  We'll be back - probably many times!
Worst service ever first one in and last to get food something is wrong with that picture more interested in what was going on with other employees that the customer

I will admit to being a bit of a sushi snob having been a big fan of sushi for many years.  The restaurant is very nice and the service was excellent.  I sat at the bar so I could get a feel for the quality of work of the chefs.  The first thing I noted was the haphazard approach in handling the fish, and it appeared speed was more important than skill or creativity. The result was larger than necessary cuts, making the individual pieces too large to enjoy.

I had salmon, tuna, smoked salmon and scallop nigiri and a roll.  All OK, but the presentation was lack-luster, and a little bland. The rice needs work. I asked the sushi chef to make me a roll -- something creative and they surprised me!  It was a wonderful roll with salmon, white fish seasoned with citrus zest and vinegar.

It is evident there is a large following at the restaurant since many of the customers knew the chefs by name.  So, I will reserve final judgement, and may give it another try.

I called this store to inquire about a product I saw on their website. The phone literally rang over 50 times without anyone answering to even put me on hold. I called the corporate office and let them know and there wasn't any hint of an apology for the poor service. They offered to order the item which was a small filing cabinet costing $540.00. However, they wanted to charge $199.00 just to ship it. They did not offer a store pick up. Apparently this was a flat rate if I ordered one item or twenty items. This is ridiculous policy and they have lost both my faith in the company and the potential for any future business. Epic Fail.

Meh. Wasn't bad but wasn't great. 

Ambiance: super dark to the point where I thought it was closed, and when we went back outside we were blinded, but hey I guess it's mostly a bar right?

Food: simple Italian (seemed weird for the place, I didn't expect it) menu, chicken parm, pastas, salads, sandwiches. Random brunch specials were breakfast related. Tons of vegan and veg options if you're looking for that. Boyfriend reported the pork roll sandwich was good. I got the burger medium and it was prettyyyyy rare and I was kinda turned off. My savior were the sweet potato fries which were excellent. 

Drinks: good variety all around, solid beer list and yummy cocktails with Italian connotations and aperitifs.

Service: waitress was attentive and food and drinks came quickly, no complaints.

Overall nothing major happening here, it's ok.

I have been to The Capital Grille on three occasions. The first was for a work dinner. Service was impeccable and the dinner was on point. Absolutely no complaints. This was shortly after the restaurant opened. The second visit was a Friday night dinner for two. Our server David was the best. He was attentive and extremely accommodating. We spilt the calamari, which had a great level of spice. I had the swordfish which was grilled to perfection. My boyfriend had the Oscar Filet and it was cooked perfectly. Neither of us could finish our meal or even contemplate dessert. The first two occasions definitely deserved 4-5 stars. 

Now the reason why I am giving the restaurant a 2 rating... It comes down to the most recent visit. This visit occurred Saturday March 10th at 5:00pm. I visited with my boyfriend, his parents, and his brother and sister-in-law.  Without knowing it, we each gave each other the same Christmas gift - gift cards to The Capital Grille. We decided to all go out together for a great dinner. 

The service was great as usual. The food - mostly wonderful. Everyone was crazy about their meal except me. I choose to order the special of the night - the mixed grill. It included Kona steak, lamb, and steamed lobster.  My lobster was rubbery and stuck to the shell - almost like it sat for a little too long after it was steamed. The chef was very heavy with the Kona on my steak and lamb. 

We all decided to get something for dessert. The three men and myself ordered coffee. The two women ordered a Godiva dessert coffee. I ordered a coconut creme pie to go. My bf's mom and sil had their dessert coffees. We paid the bill and left around 8pm. I had three bites of my coconut creme pie around 10pm that night. I was sick with food poisoning at 3 am. It lasted too long for my liking, but not nearly as long as the two other ladies. They were sick all night with food poisoning much worse than mine.  It was violent and included alternating between intense sweats and extreme chills. we figured out what made all three of us I'll - it was the whipped cream. Needless to say I never finished the remainder of my delicious but turned coconut creme pie.

For the price of the meal (around 650 for 6 of us and that included only 2 alcoholic beverages) I expect the level of service we received, but do not expect turned food. It was very disappointing. My bf and I are going out again tonight, but will not be returning to The Capital Grille. We are headed to Sullivan's. The next family dinner we will try Morton's. 

Again - wonderful service and knowledgable staff also.  Hopefully the food preparation procedure for the whipped cream will improve.

Nice looking space with plenty of booths and tables. Good variety of breakfast items and lunch starts at 10:30. We went for breakfast I ordered am omelette which was decent and my spouse ordered the pancake combo. One pancake is huge and it comes with two eggs, toast or home fries and a side of meat. It was ok but we both agreed First Watch pancakes are better. When our bill arrived we were charged $3.99 for home fries which according to menu were according to menu wwere included. After pointing this out the waitress she did remove them but informed us that we were confused...we again showed her menu and she finally said oh well maybe I rang it I in wrong, clearly. Not going back. Better choices in the area.
Came here for a coffee and left with a free snack. The beef pastry was one of the vest i've ever had and the coffee was great too. I'd highly recommend this place if you are in the area..Yum!!!!

If you want the details, read Jessica G's review.  It is dead on.  SImply put, the tacos are $1.50 and works of art. Truly a phenomenal taste and value.  The marinated meat tacos simply melt in your mouth.  The location is EXACTLY as Jessica describes.  The size of your bedroom, this place is 
a foodie find!  Go!  Go Now!  Run! Damn, damn, good flavor!

I almost forgot, no credit cards accepted, BUT there is an ATM at the next door liquor store.

I know how I want my hair cut. I give very specific instructions to my barbers, and expect them to follow it. I have done this for two decades.

Five seconds after telling Holly what I wanted, she said, "Well..." and then convinced me to let her do it her way.

I have rarely been so happy to be so wrong.

Her skill at picking out how to take care of my noggin, combined with her knowledge on how to achieve that goal, is impeccable.

Add to that the razor shave, and you have the closest a man can get to the relaxation of a lady's spa day.

She is now the only barber in town who I allow near my melon.

Cool hipster burger joint in a swanky up and coming part of town. I love the Pharmacy burger and the sweet potato fries. I also love that they have real fresh sodas. The only real issue is that the employees are definitely too cool for school.

I needed someone to do my makeup for my wedding because were I to have done it myself, it would have wiped and streaked off in about 30 seconds, leaving me looking like a hot, shiny mess all night long.  I looked at some makeup artists' work and felt really "meh" about the whole thing.  With the date rapidly approaching and still no one booked, I saw my hairstylist for a wedding hair trial and asked her for recommendations.  She said "Are you okay with using not-that-natural makeup for a day?" (This is something I generally commit to).  "As long as it stays looking good in photos, at this point, fine."  "Just go to MAC and have one of their artists do it--it'll look great and it'll stay put."  My only experience with MAC up until now had been in 2003 when I was a teenager and didn't have any disposable income but  wandered in 'cause I wanted to feel like I knew what was up and didn't know what to do and felt dumb and awkward and I bought a neon green eyeshadow that I wore one time ever.  But I was getting desperate at this point, so I went in before an office party event to trial wedding makeup.

Can I just say how pleasantly surprised I was by how easy and accommodating my experience was?  My expectations were totally blown out of the water.  I was immediately greeted upon entering the store by super-friendly associates.  I was seated and made up by Andie, who did an efficient and superb job on my trial run makeup, leaving me looking still like myself--not heavily made up or caked on at all--but instead enhanced and glammed up in a hyper-flawless sort of way.  I had been told by other reviews online that if an artist from the store was available to freelance, to go that route, but schedules didn't match up...Andie would be working again at the store the morning of the wedding, though, so I just returned the day of and was again treated super-cordially, so far as even the security guard took his cigarette break in order to hail me a cab 'cause it was raining and I was afraid my hair/makeup would turn into a puddly mess, and I got absolutely spectacular makeup done by Andie again while he even, without missing a beat at any point, continued to service other customers at the same time and select products for them with enthusiasm and a smile.  He repeatedly showed himself to be a total class act who takes pride in his work as a makeup artist as well as his customer service.  As far as my makeup, think false eyelashes that no one can tell are false, but still fringe and frame my eyes in a super flattering and distinctive way; airbrush-caliber smooth foundation application/overall skin texture; skin and features highlighted so glamorously so that all of the photos I've seen so far look professionally pretty (but still totally like me).  Andie did such a tremendous job and this all contributed to my giant smiles and confidence that day.

I only gave this one star because this site forces you to. It's too bad you cannot award zero stars.  James or Mitchell or whatever his name is, is the rudest, most unprofessional human being I've ever had the displeasure of meeting. Ever. No joke. I will not get into detail, but thought of contacting the local news for things I've encountered. Scamming, slander and other unmentionables. As a potential client, i wouldn't think that telling someone they can't "afford" your services would be a good thing. Do NOT go here unless you want to experience his entitled attitude firsthand. Other reviewers have had a similar experience it appears. Sad excuse for a human being. I do not usually speak so lowly of someone, but the word about this man and his "business" needs to get out there.

I have lived at 82 Flats since August 2021, but was relocated by my job and will be moving to a new city soon.  I've found that apartment reviews are usually either perfect or horrible lol.  So I decided I'd leave an honest review of 82 Flats in hopes to help someone else who is also apartment searching.  

Pros:
- Extremely safe and secure.  Each building requires its own individual key fob to enter.  I have not once heard of any instance of theft or crime.  
- Great staff.  The ladies in the main office are extremely pleasant, helpful and easy to talk to - Alex especially.  They respond quickly and seem to know the first names of most all of their residents, which I really like.  
- Location.  The complex is right in between Castleton and Keystone malls.  There are tons and tons of good food options nearby.  
- Parking.  I've had no issues with parking.  They have garages available for purchase, which we did not have.  They have an open lot and 9/10 times I am able to find a spot that is close to my unit.
