- Units.  The units are nice, clean and sleek looking - minus a few cosmetic flaws that I will get into.  
- Kitchen Sink.  This is an odd pro to have, but I've found the sink to be very convenient.  The kitchen sink has a nozzle switch that changes between a regular stream and a powerful stream.  The latter has incredible water pressure and the water gets extremely hot, which I've found to be useful when doing dishes.  

Cons:
- Appliances.  Some of the appliances in the place could stand to be updated.    It is apparent that they are pretty cheap.  My dishwasher leaks occasionally, and does not dry dishes.  I've had a couple issues with my dryer as well.  
- Cosmetic issues.  Although the apartments are generally nice.  They have a few flaws.  My unit has some floor trim that is disconnected, leaving exposed nails.  The hardwood floor in my unit has some paint stains and chips in it that make it look dirty even after I had just cleaned it.  
- Packages.  This is a bit of a knit-pick on my part.  Usually Amazon packages go to the main office, while USPS packages go directly to the door.  But the complex can be confusing if you are not familiar, so sometimes packages can end up in the wrong locations and get lost.  No issues with stolen packages in my experience though.  

All in all I have been very pleased with my experience here.  If all my future apartment renting experiences are similar to this, I'd be very happy.

Monell's wasn't that bad. We ate family style, sat with others who we didn't know. The rule is you must pass everything to the left, and no cell phones at the dinner table. Remember these rules! The waitress will call you out if you don't. If you are in a business lunch, you can step away from the table to take a call. 

The food was ok. I am a cook myself and I felt the food needed just a little more flavor.   It wasn't bad but for me it's not something I would rave about.

She is pretty aggressive; wasn't sensitive to me as a client when I went in for a filling. Previous dentists, before I moved to this city, have always told me "going to give you a shot now in your gums to numb you.."
Especially stated when they were going to start drilling! (though it's been years since I've had a cavity. Perhaps this is the new way of doing things?!) 
Dr Arias just went to town! She seemed to behave  like I wasn't even there. She talked with her hygienist about their price life. I found it very odd.
Dr. Arias also recommended I get Invisalign.
Soon, I realized she seems to be recommending Invisalign to many clients. When I talked to friends who happen to have a her also as their dentist, it's ironic how they also were told they needed aInvisalign. Since we are in our 30s & 40s, insurance won't cover this.
She actually said the same thing to all of us, "your bite isn't aligned. Invisalign will help fix those problem." 
Perhaps the clinic didn't have a lot of clients in midst of the pandemic and now they are recommending Invisalign to several clients.
$6000 I was told for this.  She is the only Dentist who has EVER told me I needed braces or any sort of treatment to straighten my teeth.
Prior dentists have ALWAYS asked me if I had braces as a child. (I didn't) i've been told my teeth are very straight and very tight and I have a great smile. Unfortunately, I found a dentist who thinks otherwise. But she's alone- because I've seen five other dentists in five other states.
Seems they are less about the client's need and more about collecting your money.
Also, I found it odd that I couldn't get my dental cleaning and exam done in the same session. (???) Then, they schedule a third appt. for a filling.  My point being, I've been there three times in the last six months. So I have a pretty good observation about what this clinic is about. It's unfortunate.

They have a beer garden! I know the reasons are because of covid but how cool to sit in your very own small garden greenhouse with heat and lights! The smoked sausage was so good with the first bite of cherry wood taste. Very good. Share the sweet potato fries because you get a lot. I want to come back and try a milkshake when covid is over.

If I could give 0 stars, I would. Ordered a quart of fried rice. I had to pay for it before it was ready and when I checked my receipt, I noticed I was charged $6.75. The tax rate is 7% which equals .42¢, NOT 75¢.

When I got home and opened my box of fried rice what I saw was $6.75 worth of yellow rice.

To top it off the cashier (young Asian girl) was annoyed that I asked for more soy sauce as there was 1 in the basket. So she put 2 more in the basket. I asked, " is there a shortage?". She was obviously, vocally mumbling as she put some more soy sauce packets in the basket. She was a snot!

Fool me once. Only!

First I just want to say that the manager responded to my first comment and said that NSW tried to call me that day. They did not. My husband and I sat in the garage for over four hours with phones in hand and never recv'd a call until I called and said the windows delivered were incorrect. I imagine someone told him they tried to call but must have misdialed.

The frustration from New South Windows continues. I feel as if this will never end. The correct windows were delivered(the second time) and installed.  One of the windows was difficult to close and I demonstrated to the installer. I was told that I was closing it wrong. I needed to put down my cane and should use two hands to firmly close the window. Of course the installer closed it that time. The county inspector was supposed to come again, (the first time the windows were wrong) soon. NSW called to schedule the inspection and I explained to the young lady that the window had become extremely difficult to close and I wanted someone to look at it. She put me on hold and came back and said, "after it passes inspection someone will come out".  Inspector came (third time) and failed the one window because it's REALLY hard to move. He sent an email to NSW while in my driveway and explained why the window was failed. Inspector said that NSW had been notified and I assumed NSW would contact me. Inspector will have to come back for a fourth time. Nothing heard from NSW and now  I can't close my window at all, it got down to 45 in my bedroom. After five days(Tuesday)  I called and spoke to lovely Loretta. I tell her my window won't close , it's 45 degrees and window failed inspection.  Of all the things I said she only heard the window failed inspection.  I had to beg her to please have someone from repairs/service or whatever it's called to please call me. It's now 6 p.m. on Thursday. I have not heard a word from NSW. Every single time I've had something that I NEEDED to talk to someone at NSW about I have to deal with Loretta. Every time I've had to deal with Loretta NOTHING is ever resolved. The ONLY time I've gotten a response and spoken to anyone except Loretta is thru social media. Loretta is starting to remind me of the troll that guards the bridge and refuses access to all travelers. Its like a border crossing and I don't have the correct passport. Sorry, not sorry, she's following her job description. Petty employees are just a side perk of NSW. I have to wonder if I'm the only person she hates or if she's just the wrong person for that job and is related to someone at NSW. 
Florida has another cold front coming in. I have my pretty, brand new, paid for window that DOESN'T work and an employee that is always ugly to me when I call. I am mentally weary and trying to just get this entire experience over with. I truly wish that I never, ever heard of this company. All I'm asking for is someone, anyone from NSW to do their job. Everyone blames another department when something goes wrong and then I have to go thru the belligerent Loretta hoping that she will actually send along my message. Btw belligerent Loretta is the nicest thing I've said about her. Would someone other than Loretta please call?

Jake was great. He found the problem right away.  What a nice yong man, customer oriented and a great company representative.  Would want Jake to be our technician next time around.

Thanks also to Tanya for setting up our appointment since it was an emergency call at night.

I have to say I was a little excited seeing a health food store in the area. I don't do my regular shopping in stores like this but it's nice to know you can get those specialty items that the regular grocery stores just don't carry. 

Upon walking in I was greeted with a hello, so that was nice. However the shelves seemed almost barren and the aisle were suffering from any true organization. I think I counted 3 different aisles with dried pasta on the shelves! So anyways I grabbed a couple items and went to the register. There was only one lane open and one customer in front of me. Should be fast, right? WRONG! The couple in front of me was taking their sweet time writing a check and conversing with no regard to me or my time. I normally wouldn't put blame on a store for this but it goes a little deeper than that. The girl that was bagging and chatting with this customer decided to open another register AFTER another customer got in line after me and told her to come over and started taking care of her order. Then there were 2-3 other customers that went through that line before my cashier was able to help me with my two little items. There was no sense of urgency or caring of my time by anyone there.

Third time's a charm. This round I found metered parking. Used the app to put 20 minutes on the clock and headed up to get some blood sucked out of my arm.

I was running about 15 min late for the appointment I made on the handy dandy website. ((Never make a Monday appt with me before 9am)) I got there and probably only waited 10ish minutes in the waiting room if that before being called back. Due Date was on the TV with Robert Downey Jr and Zach Galifianakis...interesting choice for an early Monday watch but whatevs...Remember, don't try and open the door, they'll open it for you.

I didn't get the name of the nurse but she was very nice. I told her I had made an appt online and she said that appts get preference on time though I told her I had been late so it was my own fault. Just an FYI for all you Yelpers. I didn't have my paperwork but she easily found it in their system.

Once the deed of vampiring my blood out of my arm needed to be done, she did it quick and as painlessly as possible. No ScoobyDoo bandaid but I'm sure the cotton and tape she strapped on probably applies a better pressure to my arm booboo. Again, I do bruise like a peach and a day later there is the faintest of bruises but thankfully I'm nowhere near an arm stand-in for Requiem for a Dream II. 

In and out of there in less than 20 minutes. No fainting or queasiness or admitting to the nurse that I'm a gigantic baby when it comes to getting my blood taken. Though she may know now...

This is a great neighborhood butcher shop. The prepared foods and jersey tomato selection are the best when you don't feel like cooking yourself. The soups, crab cakes, eggplant stacks, all wonderful. You really can't go wrong. Free south Philly entertainment also - the brothers are a hoot.

Food is good however couple things 

1. I have experienced/noticed that the size of Sashimi is smaller than previous size.

2. Prices list online is different than the price in store (Specifically for lunch, the prices are different when you walk in the resultant). 

 I have reported to the manager price that your charged me different than was mentioned online. Manager response was "We are sorry about prices difference" however we will be charging you the price what's list in Store. Manager didn't honor the online price even though it's their mistake by list wrong price online.

So if I could rate this and the Seabreeze restaurant that they also purchased I would go negative stars. The places are filthy the staff is rather rude or scared to death of something. We were served the most disgusting food that was over cooked. The Mac and cheese side was a shade of grey and the coconut shrimp was a color I have very seen before. I was sick all night...for the health and safety of others I hope the health department shuts them both down.

I need a -STAR! Walked in yesterday at 4:20 to get a coffee to go and the punk behind counter yelled "YOU CAN'T COME IN HERE WITHOUT A MASK!" Forget this location!!!! Oh, and I was only stopped at Dunkin 254 times last year from my app report.

This place is awesome. It was my dad and aunt and myself. We waited for fifteen minutes even though it was a thirty minute wait. But we sat down and out server was awesome he was so cool, answered all of our questions, he was fun and energetic. Now to the food, you'll need an empty stomach. The chips are to die for and the salsa was mild but had a hint of spice but not to hot that you can't handle it. We polished those off. I ordered the pollo Yucatan. I HIGHLY recommend that. It is really hot so if you have a craving for spicy this is right up your alley.

Convential Wisdom is that this is the best restuarant in Nashville, and its true.  The menu is creative and diverse, and its the only place in town with terrific seafood (beyond sushi).   Wine list is big and bit pricey.  Dark, simple, sophisticated ambiance makes it perfect for a big date or business dinner and also a casual night out with friends, and the late night menu means its a great place to see indie rockers and the women who love then after midnight.
This place is rickety, small, and old.  But they do a good job cleaning my two large dogs.  They come home smelling nice, with stupid bandanas on, and at a reasonable price.

Im satisfied with my decision to visit this place.  I almost spent $15 to go to another observation tower... without a bar... and I got to check out the view here for free. 

I loved the decor and only wish it was a wee bit bigger. The benches inside are massive and the bar is small. I can only imagine how busy a place like this gets in the summer, but in the winter it was smooth sailing.  I was checked by security and in the bar under 5 minutes. 

I happened to stumble in during happy hour, and it featured $5 for all wine. Champagne included. Hello?! Yes, please. I was able to watch my Canes win a bowl game and capture great views. The benches also featured games to play if you bring friends with you. 

Job well done!

What a great place to stay! Right across from the Mississippi and walking distance to everything in the French Quarter.  Comfortable bed. The room was a bit small, but we were only there to sleep anyway.  We were on the Decatur St side of the building, so could hear the Calliope from the river boat. I'll let you decide if that's a good or bad thing. The staff was lovely and accommodating. Would definitely stay again.

Let me start by saying if I could give this place 0 stars I would.

My coworkers and I went there at 2:30 pm on a Friday for happy hour. There were no other tables dining except for one other group of about 5 people. The service was horrible. After being asked to move our table, it took 45 minutes for our first round of drinks to arrive. When asking the hostess to send out our waitress for another round, she never came outside. We then had to go back in to ask if we could please see our waitress for another drink. Another 45 minutes later those drinks finally came. So 2 hours for two rounds of drinks. We then had to HUNT DOWN our waitress just to pay. So displeased, we called the restaurant to complain. Typically we don't do this but for two rounds of drinks our bill was $80...when we called, the manager seemed horrified and told us she would send us a gift card and to please try Tango again. The gift card never came..

Don't waste your time this place sucks.
We loved our stay in this hotel, will definitely stay again when we go back. Coffee shop at the entrance is a plus
One of the worst indian restaurants I have visited in the past year. Slow service, rude waiters, food I wouldn't serve my dog are amongst the pleasures you'll find at this local doghouse.

Long horn steakhouses are my absolute favorite of the chain steakhouses. I eat at long horns all of the Northeast/NewEngland area. I eat there all the time and have yet to ever be disappointed in anything. Top to bottom great tasting menu... you can't go wrong no matter what you order. You food will be fresh, hot, delicious and a good size portion. If you haven't eaten at s long horn do so, it is amazing. If you have eaten at a long horn? I don't need tho tell you anything. I'm sure you've been back, and became a loyal customer. What csb I say, longhorn is top notch.

This place is so good! I got the Cheeseburger. While $19 is too much.. not matter how good a burger is. I'd probably get it again. It's definitely one of the best in Nashville. I do appreciate that they use good ingredients and understand how expensive they can be.. but $19 is a lot of money for a burger. The other food looked great as it came out. I was creeping on everyone's pizza. The wine list looked good and they had locals beer. None on tap which was a bummer. I do look forward to going back though!

Prefer to read a lot! There is a plethora of information available here. Soooo much history is contained within hear hallowed walls. There's also a lot of noise and obnoxious music from the outside of the museum. 

I love the way that each level and wall panel of the museum takes you on a journey through the history or New Orleans and the Mississippi Delta region. It reminded me of history class in school and I just loved it! 

Be respectful and turn your phones off, don't make calls while in the museum (yes, this actually happened) and leave enough time for the museum (about 2 hours minimum) to respect the work that went into the museum and try to make an understanding of this history. 

Oh and the guy out front that drunkenly stumbles towards you begging for a quarter is harmless...I turned him down because I honestly didn't have one and then he called me all sorts of names and in his drunken stupor, tripped over some uneven ground.... Just another day in NOLA!

Very happy customer here. 
Although it was a small drywall repair job, Audencio was more than happy to help. In providing a quote, he was very honest in explaining how the slight damage that was appearing on the surface could have spread behind the wall. I was presented with a clear and honest assessment before any work was started. He and his crew did a wonderful job from start to finish. They are knowledgeable, efficient and friendly.
I will most definitely call on the AG Drywall team in the future.

This has been a local favorite for my family for about 15 years and must admit we have collectively dined here at least 100 times. Unfortunately, and it hurts to say this, I do not think the restaurant's recent move down the street did them any good (perhaps in revenue, though, with the much-larger dining room). 

But the quality of the cuisine has diminished than say five or six years ago; across the board. Yes, $12 salads and $20 personal pizzas are too expensive, but this is "Greenville 19807." With that said, if I dish out that much for a pizza and salad, I expect it to be well above average. And six years ago, everything was. Today, the food is sloppy and overall uneven. For instance, there are less overall ingredients used for a typical pizza than in years past, so you get more crust and less flavor.

I preferred the intimate setting and ambiance of the previous location. It had a personality of its own, yet still conveyed "Pizza Joint." The service has declined and often can't keep up with the larger space. The decor is now frilly and stuffy and simply just not attractive. 

Dear Pizza by Elizabeths, please return to your roots!

This place suffers the problem of low quality with high cost. Their food is mediocre, yet terribly overpriced and not very dynamic. They have a good bar, but so does all the other restaurants on this street. I have been twice, once for lunch and once for dinner. Their PoBoy was good for lunch, but the ribs were disappointing. Think twice before taking a seat and ordering here. The sides also seem cheap. Everything seems to be reheated from a freezer and the freshness doesn't really seem to shine through.

A popular BBQ spot highly recommended on Thrillist and Yelp. There's usually a 10-15min line during mealtime rush hours. For just under $20, the go to meals are 2-meat combos or a half rack of ribs. Extra picnic table seating in the back. 

Overall: 4.5/5 Perfectly charred and seasoned ribs and brisket. If you need a break from all the Cajun seafood cuisine, this is the bbq joint to check out. And if you get a slice of the pecan or peanut butter pie, you won't regret it. Enjoy!

This place is surely struggling. They should get some help from the other locations. 
Empty for dinner on Saturday night, don't have ingredients for "new" cocktail menu and still a 45 minute wait for 2 roles. All 5 people in here are looking at each other without food.

Dear Jimmy Johns,

Ordered a sandwich through drive thru. Proceeded to pay for sandwich and was watching the guy making the sandwhiches. He proceeds to pick up some bread that fell on the floor, throws it in the trash, then goes to make my sandwich without ever changing his gloves.... that is absolutely disgusting. I asked to talked to the manager and the manager refunded my money, never apologized for the employees reckless behavior, and was back up against the wall when I asked for the district managers number to contact her directly. He proceeds to try and give me her number by looking in his phone, then says I can't give it out.... Funny bc I worked for a major retailer for years and if there was any issue, people were allowed to contact the district manager directly.

This is a health code violation and right now you're graded "A" after tonight's incident you should be downgraded to a D. Absolutely disgusting

We went there for our 20th wedding anniversary. I should have picked somewhere else. We waited more than 10 minutes after my wife's dinner was served until I got my entree.  We were almost done when the bread was brought to our table.  It could have been dessert. 
This place has really gone down hill recently and I'm sorry I suggested going there. I'm going to recreate my wife's dinner entree the right way tomorrow to make it up to her.

This is one of the better Italian restaurants in Tucson. They put a southwestern twist on classic dishes for a truly awesome experience. I have had multiple dishes from here and they are all great but the Big Rig Pasta is easily my favorite. It usually takes forever to get a seat of you come during anything that you could consider a peak hour, so set some time aside, or arrive at an odd time. 

Enjoy!

To the negative reviewers who have posted before me, dare I ask - have any of you actually been to a real French bakery? No, not a 'French' bakery in the states, but an actual bakery in France. If you've had the pleasure of that experience before then there is no way you can dispute what they do at The Art of Bread - it is completely spot on.

The croissants are perfectly flaky and crisp on the outside, buttery and smooth on the inside leading to a melt-in-your-mouth kind of bite (even hours after having bought them, they still have that same wonderful quality about them!) The baguette has a crispy crust that breaks away to a perfectly warm and doughy interior. The danishes have the perfect ratio of filling to buttery pastry. I can't speak for the quiche, tarts, or lunch menu items for I have only indulged in the breakfast end of things, but if the lunches they make are as delicious as their bread and pastries - count me in for next time!

The staff appeared knowledgeable, albeit a bit overwhelmed, but that's to be expected during the first few opening weeks. If anything, they seemed to be a tad over-staffed and disorganized in the sense that no one behind the counter had a set job to do, so they were all scrambling around trying to do everything. They may be better off having one person running the register, one person taking care of bread orders, another taking care of pastry case orders, etc. This may alleviate some of the confusion behind the counter. But lets all relax a little and remember that they've barely even been open for a whole week - it takes a little time for everyone to find their flow in a new place.

Oh, and to the reviewer who said you might as well save your money and buy yourself a loaf of Wonder Bread... the true art of bread is meant to be eaten while it's still warm and fresh out of the oven - if you are looking for a highly preserved loaf of 'bread' that can sit out on your counter for weeks, then by all means, save your money and buy yourself some Wonder Bread. But if you're looking for a true artisan loaf that's meant to actually be enjoyed, I have yet to find a place in Merion that does it better than The Art of Bread.

Although I seem to be contradicting everyone else here on Yelp, I really believe The Art of Bread is doing exactly what they set out to do: providing real French pastries and artisan breads that are meant to be enjoyed upon being purchased. It's a lesson to all of us who seem to have forgotten what the words 'fresh' and 'quality' mean. As a French girl who grew up mostly in the states, I really appreciate what they've brought to the Main Line, and most of all I love the fact that I can walk one mile up the road and get a loaf of fresh bread in the morning - like a true European!

Really solid Bahn Mi. No complaints. I ordered the recommended #1 "QT Special", an assortment of delicious Vietnamese meats. The service is super quick and the staff is very friendly. One thing I think that makes QT stand out from other Bahn Mi is the quality of ingredients, especially the bread they use. It's not dry nor soggy, it was just right. I highly recommend QT to anyone who looking for a quick and easy place to grab some dank Vietnamese sandwiches.
Unreliable .. booked a bus for 11:40, found out they overbooked it so the bus left at 11:30 and left about 10 kids in the parking lot in the pouring rain. Trying to reach customer service is painful

This wasn't the place I was quite aiming for when I thought of pho in philly but it did the job. I've always heard that pho in philly is miles better than in new york but I just don't see that. At least not with this place. Don't get me wrong the food is fine. More than fine but it doesn't blow me away. I'd come here again if the other chicken pho is closed.

I wish I could give this a zero star. I have never had such a horrible dine in experience in my life. I was very embarrassed to take my date here. It was to be expected there was going to be a wait time being Veterans Day. We arrived at 9:45pm there was no hostess. Finally after 15 minutes someone finally took our names.  Her pants did not fit her correctly let's just say the super moon came earlier for us this weekend. Our waitress was so unprofessional she talked to us like we were old friends venting about how she gave her last table great service and they barely tipped. As we were waiting for our drinks the bus boy came to clear the table off across from us. A concerned co worker had approached him about his night he was telling her how he wanted to punch her in the face she was really pissing him the F off. Shortly after she had walked away he had shattered a glass. The manager heard it and came over to approach the boy and ask him if he was okay. The boy clearly had an attitude towards him the manager never apologized to the surrounding tables for the chaos. Our food finally came my dates dinner came out dry, the dish was suppose to come with a sauce when he had requested it the waitress came back say there was no more sauce in the restaurant. I personally would've liked to been notified of that before I had ordered the dish. because I want it the way that it is advertised. If I would of known that we probably would of ordered something else. If we weren't trying to catch a movie by a certain time I would of talked to the manager. We left at 11:10 an hour and a half later. You couldn't pay me to go back to that Applebee's.

Rachel was by far the best customer service rep! I'm extremely picky and I don't think I've ever had such great customer service as she provided! She was so helpful and thoroughly knew the product and what storage unit would be a fit for me -as I was shopping around for other storage stops. Ultimately what made me pick your spot was the amazing customer service she provided! It made me feel at ease knowing my belongings were being kept at a place that treats customers so well- thank you Rachel!

Absolutely love this joint. Completely changed the game as far as fast food teriyaki is concerned. I love the pickled ginger, jalapeños, and awesome sauces they let you load on. Great bang for your buck and is definitely best around midnight after a drink or two. I go back every time I am in town.

Blooming Grove Inn occupies the space of what was Lieggi's on Upper Ferry Road in Ewing, NJ. It is a pleasant converted mansion on the slopes of the eastern side of the Delaware River Basin.  My visits have always been at the bar for after work meetings and light socializing.  The crowd is forty and fifty-somethings; all well behaved.  The bartenders are excellent.  Noah remembered my face, if not my name, after an absence of at least six months.  Impressive.  

Last night, my margarita had the right touch of Padron and Cointreau, and my friend and fellow court gladiator enjoyed his aged malt whiskey.  Behind us at the end of the bar, a mellow guitar vocalist played gently and sang from a decent list of soft rock standards. It was just enough to fill the gaps in our conversation, but seemed to disappear when we spoke again.  

We decided to have a bite at the bar, rather than score a table.  I asked for the Meatballs I had seen brought out earlier, and Sam went for the Tapas plate. My dish was excellent.  Tender, airy meatballs in a tart, garlic tomato sauce, sprinkled with dry Italian grated cheese.  I only skewered a few pieces of cheese, greens, mushrooms off his plate, but thought everything was fine, if not enthralling. 

So, why four stars?  It has nothing to do with the quality of the house, the kitchen, nor the bar.  They were all fine.  Blooming Grove just isn't my cup of tequila.  I prefer a less well kempt, more outside the box, more diverse, kind of place.  It felt too tame.  More a place to take your aunt and uncle, or business associates, when you don't want them to know who you really are.
Mike has skills. The shop is clean, comfortable, and there's always good music on. You can't go wrong.
We loved our lunch here. Great vegetarian options. We let our server choose/recommend toppings and our veggie dogs were both delicious! Excellent service and fun Star Wars decor.

I've been to quite a few Pho spots around Florida and this place is now by far my favorite!! The staff is incredibly personable, the food is flavorful, and the portions are on point. Just had their XL bowl of Pho, and it was no joke! Last night I tried their sushi burritos and it was a delicious treat. I live in the Central Florida area, but would gladly make the trek out here to grab food again! Do yourself a favor and stop in!

My sister ordered flowers from 1800Flowers and they contracted this florist to do them. 1st they were a day late, 2nd they were delivered after 9:00pm, if I had not checked my front door the flowers would have been sitting on my porch all night. The arrangement I got wasn't even close to what my sister ordered. 
These are just disgraceful!!
Don't ever order from these people!

A nice find. Comfortable and clean, this is a true bargain. When I walked in the large, pleasant lobby, I wondered how the accommodations could be so nice at such a reasonable price. Conveniently located along Hwy 19 (which does bring traffic noise when the AC isn't running), the other side of the building is picturesque, located on the water. We walked the property, enjoying the wildlife and tropical trees. Tarpon Springs, with the sponge docks and great Greek restaurants, is a few minutes away. Breakfast is free, the staff is friendly, the wi-fi works. This is where you want to stay.
Went there for quick lunch got fried shrimp app,the best! Not heavy coating. Shrimp quesadilla awesome!

I've lived here for 2 years and never tried this place so I convinced my husband to try it. The egg rolls were ok- but not great. My husband ordered  a pork chop rice dish and ended up with little grilled pieces of meat over rice instead. The waitress messed up my order, then blamed me. She ended up bringing me the correct dish after they had brought out the wrong dish initially, but begrudgingly did it. They never brought my drink. The food was decent but the service was just atrocious.

Celebrated our 1 Year Wedding Anniversary here.   The food and atmosphere was wonderful.   Only downfall is the entrance to the restaurant & bar are not clearly labeled.... we entered through the wrong door

Day-after-wedding breakfast hosted at Urban Cafe's location -- easy access from downtown hotels and wonderful hot/cold options for 90+ guests at a reasonable price point.  Friendly, helpful cooks/servers and an overall positive experience.  Thanks, Manny!

The food is Bub's quality amazing, but the service is hit or miss and the atmosphere is a bit drab. I'm a fan of Bub's, but for whatever reason this location isn't as great. They built this establishment and whoever designed the inside wasn't thinking about customer appeal, at all! It kinda reminds me of what a factory layout looks like. A big open room two stories high, plain drab walls, a set of stairs up to the second story "office" that overlooks all the busy bee factory workers. Except their upstairs is additional seating (I've never been upstairs). It just feels so awkward to have a restaurant in this space. Because of the walls and high ceilings the acoustics are great, every sound in amplified x 100, so now that annoying family with unruly kids that's 6 tables away sounds like they're having dinner at your table. As for the service, like I said above, it's hit or miss. It seems the average age is roughly 16, and it's clear that some of them just don't know what to do. They really need to have a few more experienced staff to set the example here. Oh and if you're expecting someone to help you at the ice cream counter, you'll be waiting roughly about 20 mins. Even though plenty of staff pass you as you awkwardly look around for someone to assist you. It seems the wait staff is responsible for also manning the ice cream parlor, but with them running their tables, most of them don't care that you've been standing there for an eternity. My suggestion, go look and see what you want and then come back to your table and order from your waiter. Oh and btw, they don't have any of the ice cream parlor stuff on their menu, so you do HAVE TO get up and look for yourself. What idiot forgot that piece of the menu?! And don't expect your average age of 16 year old part-time first job waiter to have any knowledge of what's on that parlor menu, they will just look at you like "uhhhh". Now I'm rolling MY eyes, grrrrr! I think you all get the picture. But the food is fabulous, thankfully. So take a happy pill before you head over and have a couple beers and hopefully that's enough to distract you from the craziness around you and the not so great service. It's worth the 15 min drive to the Carmel one, it really is.

We went here today and it was absolutely terrific! I had coffee, it was so rich and delish. My friend ordered a mimosa it looked so good that I ordered one too! I got the salmon Benedict and hashbrowns, it was good. My two friends got the french toast. I will definitely be coming back.

Centro defines hip and happenin'.  My husband made reservations for us and two friends.  We arrived early, perhaps so we could check out the bar.  The craft cocktails are not to be missed!  I had a Smoke on the Water cocktail.  The kitchen/bar is quite small, but it's in the open so you can see the masters at work.  Each element of my cocktail was carefully assembled into a sniffer of sublime.  

I love the tapas concept.  I like to sample a couple of bites of several different dishes, and Centro does not disappoint.  We sampled duck and quail and pork belly.  I know there were more dishes, but these were the most memorable.  I don't normally go for dessert, but we decided to order four small dishes.  They did not disappoint.  My favorite was a chocolate concoction that was just the right amount of sweet.

The atmosphere is small, cozy, and inviting.  We'll definitely return here, as the menu continually changes.

Sides of Seoul is a small family owned restaurant located in a small plaza serving amazing homey Korean food!

I love their donkatsu kimbap! I can't get enough of it -- I've gotten their kimbap for takeout multiple times in the past month. All of their food is authentic and homey, and I haven't been disappointed by anything on their menu yet! They have a variety of different kimbap, lunchboxes, soups, appetizers, and ramen dishes. I would say their portions are big and reasonable for the price. They also usually have a selection of packaged sides and desserts available by the ordering station. For dining in, you order first, and then they'll bring out the food to you. The utensils and water are self-serve. The owners are always super nice and welcoming too!

During the current coronavirus situation, they have placed a table by the front where you can pick up your order. I usually call ahead, and my order is done in 20 minutes. It's super easy, so go and check out their food!

Since living in Houston I sometimes forget just how hot Nashville chicken is!!! I ordered the fish and it took me forever to eat it strictly because my mouth was on fire! But the flavor was the kind that makes it hard to stop eating because it's so damn tasty! If you're not used to spicy then you may wanna take it easy, otherwise go for it. I didn't eat inside I just grabbed it and went home so I can't vouch to much for the inside ambiance.

5 stars for such great staff. This review is for their cardiology Dept. CICU, and CCU. Our doctor, Dr. Gaynor, is highly recommended in my book.  Our daughter had to have OHS to fix her ASD.  It is a routine procedure, but ask any parent going through this, it is nerve wracking, to say the least. 

Before and after surgery, our CICU nurse Jackie, was the best nurse.  She kept us informed every hour and got us a private room at CICU.  I saw the cleaning crew come in multiple times to clean our room too.  She was so good and made our whole stay so much better and put us at ease. 

CCU was the next place for our girl to recover.  Excellent nurses and staff all around.  One caveat, the cleaning crew was a no show for the 2 days we were in CCU though. The trash and the smell from my girls diapers was getting nasty.

The food court was good too.  Salad bar, asian make your own bowl, mexican, pizza, grill (great cheesesteak!), and sandwich bar too.  Breakfast is served here too.  Good prices compare to going outside and eat. 

For parents staying for days, the parking rate is just $4 a day.  Make sure to get your parking validated.
The hotel was clean and it's right in the heart of the French Quarter. For what we paid the bed and pillows were very uncomfortable and the room is tiny.

A small rustic Italian resto tucked away in the corner of a beachfront strip in Clearwater. Don't let its humble frontage fool you, the food was outstanding. Simple, straightforward and delicious, it rivals any Michelin starred Italian restaurant we've tried charging much much more. Hubby had the Chicken parmigiana which was crisp, tasty and had a rich marinara. I had the pasta Vongole, and though I'd had enough bad clams in my life, I thought that being so close to the shore their version would be fresh. I was not disappointed. Medium sized clams, de sanded professionally and still latched onto their shells as only fresh mollusks can do . The white wine sauce was bright and smooth and had just the right touch of garlic. Our waiter James was attentive and helpful with suggestions without being intrusive. 
Would definitely return for other menu items and definitely recommend.

I think the reviews on this place are somewhat skewed. You don't go to a Chuck E. Cheese and expect an expertly executed filet mignon. My BFF and I got a couple packages from Living Social and decided to use them for my sons 12th birthday. Check in was no problem with just a brief wait at 4:15p on a Saturday. The front desk attendant was courteous and efficient even checking that everything included in the package was in the handy white envelopes he gave me. We went to our rooms on the first floor facing the indoor pool and spa. It was definitely decent and just fine for a rowdy crew of revelers. We went directly to the water park and pretty much owned the lazy river for several hours. At first I was self conscious because my 300lb black woman bulk wasn't handling the little floaties very well but then a lifeguard helpfully placed one of the larger 'adult' sized tubes near me and I gratefully took the hint and relaxed for a few more laps around. I'm one of 'those' black chicks who doesn't get her hair messed up so I steered clear of the slides but they did look fun and the kids were still playing when we called them over for the pizza included with the LS package. The pizza was not great so we decided to order Chinese (which was also not great but that's for another review) which we ate in this little cafe area which was also great for playing board games with the kids. The hotel pool was open about an hour longer than the water park and our youngest boys shut the place down. We grown ups crashed rather quickly and I have to say that the bed sucked less than I thought it would. Next morning waking at ten I thought 'oh well we missed breakfast' but nope they were open until 11a! It's kind of cafe seating with a buffet outside the restaurant proper. There was bacon, eggs, French toast and biscuits for hot food and cereal and yogurt for cold. The place was hopping and we didn't see our server for awhile but when she did appear it was with a tray of milk and juices to choose from. She was apologetic and attentive after that and again that place was jam packed. We checked out and the kids headed back to the water park and to spend the $10 arcade cards included with our package. The arcade room was not spectacular but you could play quite a few things for the price and the points went back on the card so no tickets to carry around. All in all I'd say we got a great deal. Is this a four star hotel? Definitely not. Don't come here for that big romantic night out but if you have a passel of younglings and just wanna get away for the weekend then definitely do this. The living social deal was pretty alright and I'd say well worth the money.

Lyndel was our server today. She did such a great job making sure we are happy with our food.  The tuna melt was delish and the salad was scrumptious. We will be back for sure. Thanks Lyndel for a very good service!

Very tasty Chicken Parmesan and reasonably priced. House bottle wines a bit pricey considering home brand. One of our 6 guests had 2 of 3 ingredients of her entree uncooked and had to send back (no we did not demand free meal). Manager provided 2 large portion deserts for table as apology- not expected but appreciated. 
If nice outside, do the patio if able!
Would be 4 to 5 stars if entree was cooked right the 1st time and wine prices a bit more in line.

Kakkoii, ka-koy-ee, my new favorite japanese restaurant in New Orleans. Not one dish i ordered was a disappointment. Each and every one was delectably delicious and sumptuous... Couldn't think of a third word that started with a D for that line to emphasize my enthusiasm... Decadent, decadent chocolate molten lava cake! Well if it really wasn't decadent, it still gets a 10/10 noms. The perfect amount of hot cake, a perfect portion of vanilla ice cream from Creole Creamery, 2 chocolate pocky sticks, thin slices of apple garnish, and some dobs of whipped cream.

What about that Baked Salmon? Perfect!  There are only two places so far in the city that I have found their Baked Salmon to be just right, those two places are: Kakkoii and Sekisui in the quarter.

Our other dishes?  Crab Shumai, steamed just right and presented prettily.  Beef Negi Maki,  DAMN, that teriyaki sauce is the closest to teriyaki sauce that I've had in this city, it is teriyaki sauce! The beef ribeye and scallions are awesome, and this is just one of my favorite dishes at japanese restaurants.  Oh, and Mikimoto's version of this dish doesn't even hold a candle!   Seaweed salad?  We got this dish because they had run out of the Oshitashi (steamed spinach in sesame infused soy sauce).  The Seaweed salad was also fresh, slightly crunchy, and wonderful.  Alright, and I can't forget to mention, if you go there, you must order the Coconut Shrimp.  This was pretty fantastic, the shrimp was battered and fried perfectly, every bite you can taste the shrimp, the panko, and the coconut in perfect harmony, along with the delicious mango wasabi dipping sauce that wasn't burning spicy, but just had the right sweetness and tinge of bite.

We didn't order any entrees as we ordered a bunch of starters to get a taste of a little bit of everything that we could. But we did order two, non-special rolls as to not break the bank.  These were the essential Crunchy Roll and the Maple Street Roll.  You can't go wrong with these rolls, again they were perfect.  The tuna was fresh, just the right texture, not fishy, and not slimy, like what you can get at other places.

Last but not least.  The service was awesome. The first table we were seated at the air was blowing right down on me and I got cold, so the waitress happily moved us without any hesitation or making faces.  Next we had both ordered hot green teas, our waitress not once allowed our cups to be empty and frequently bought new, full, hot cups of green tea for us, all the way to the end when we were finishing our dessert of chocolate molten lava cake.   Yes the teas were $2.50 each, but no, they did not charge us for every single cup, like some other places in town are known to do.  This is the way it should be!   Our food also came out quickly and promptly!

I am definitely coming back to Kakkoii!  I mean, I didn't even have a favorite japanese restaurant in New Orleans until I ate at Kakkoii.

Although people might say the prices are pricey, they do have a range of different dishes that range from $4.00 - $25.00, the portions are perfect and commensurate with price, and just for the quality of the food, it's well worth it.
Excellent food! Healthy, tasty, ready on time, and enough leftover for lunch tomorrow! I highly recommend!

This was my second time going to the puzzle rooms and it was as much fun as I remembered the first time. We had a group of 12, split in two rooms. I was part of the asylum room and boy oh boy was it exciting ! Not going to give away any secrets but be ready to use your brain! Our room master, Angie, was amazing as well! She was fun, energetic,  and helpful ! We will be back

Best used book store ever! Everything is $3 or less ($3 for hardback ...most are $1-$2). They have a variety of other items as well such as records, dvd's ($2), vhs, old video games (PS2, Xbox, etc.) and cd's. 

If you are looking for books for children...this place is a true blessing. You can fill up a bag full of books for only $10. I was able to pack dozens (maybe even close to a hundred) books into my bag. It was pretty much stuffed to the brim and slightly overflowing. All for $10. 

This is how the $10 bag works: Go to the counter where you pay and ask for a bag. Fill it up with the books that are found in the kiddie pools. The books on the shelves are priced according to their standard prices and not for the bags. This is where you will find really in demand books or previously popular books. No twilight or dork diaries in the pools. However, there are a lot of great books and chapter books too. I also found a few teacher books, decoding books, some coloring books and even a felt book. After you fill up your bag(s), go to the counter and pay $10 each. Then you have to struggle to carry your bag out of the mall (the only downside). 

If you are wondering, 321 is located in between the food court entrance and JC Penney. I came in the food court not knowing where it was located (their phone number also did not work). Next time, I will probably come through JC Penney. I think it is slightly closer. The less distance you have to carry those heavy bags the better. 

I will definitely be returning to 321 books to buy some more books for my students and myself.

Independent distillery is an understated Tucson hot spot. Pre prohibition bar with a way cool atmosphere.  Niel the barker is very knoledgable and friendly. Coming Back next time I'm downtown. Good job tucson!

Omg, I crave this place! While their coffee is awesome, I can't say enough about their baked goods! You can tell they put quality time and effort into their creations that you can't find just anywhere. We love their waffles, cruffins, and stuffed bagel balls. Yumm yumm yumm!

It's Christmas and I give my partner a new sweater from All Saints (A very expensive sweater at that). She literally opens the box, puts the garment on, and we walk out to the tiki bar to see the sunset. Bar back sees us and starts spraying bleach on the counter....sprays bleach all over that $178 brand new right out of the box black sweater. I showed him what he did, explained the the circumstances, and the guy passively apologized but couldn't give a damn. Said he saw us and had to clean something off the bar....who the hell sprays bleach on a bar when people are drinking nearby in the first place?...second place on their clothing?!.... Just to give a proximity reference. I handed my drink back to the bartender (whom I tipped well for Christmas) and said I was so disappointed I couldn't even drink it, but he couldn't care less either. How would you rate that service-wise? If I were the bartender I would have at the very least comped the drink. Hope the hotel makes this right.

Tried this place tonight, will not order from here again. General Tso chicken had no spice at all to it, same with the Szechuan Chicken no heat to it, it was all sweet tasting. Mixed veggies has no taste 
What so ever. Will be returning to my original Chinese restaurant, GINGER GARDEN IN SILVERDALE, this place is worth the extra time to travel to.

I seem to be out of step with the average review for 9th Bar.  My friend & I met for coffee this morning.  He ordered a regular coffee & I ordered a caramel coffee.  As he drank his he said it was not very good tasting.  As I drank mine I felt the same way.  My coffee was a reasonable $2.00.  I was surprised a shot of caramel was a dollar.  I couldn't taste the caramel at all.   That kind of added to my disappointment.  I rarely find a coffee joint that I'm disappointed in.  In addition, the place was kind of barren.  It lacked atmosphere.  Oh well.
A delicious seasonal mocha (toffee nut) paired with excellent and efficient service. I will definitely be returning.

If you stay at the Astor Crowne Plaza and purchase the breakfast,  this is pretty good option over eating out every morning .  It's a breakfast buffet with an omelet bar, sausage, bacon, ham, grits (plain and trinity), oatmeal, fruit, pastries, yogurt, juice and coffee.  There was enough variety for us for the duration of our stay.

For our last meal in New Orleans, I really stuffed myself at Eat!  I ordered the crawfish pie with fried green tomatoes and rolled myself out the door afterwards.  The tomatoes were probably the best I've ever had, light breading so you could really taste the tomato.  The crawfish pie had really great flavor, it's just a heavy gut bomb so beware! We also got the crawfish croquette things as an appetizer and those were great.  It's a cute little place and the food was solid, I recommend.
If I could give them -5 stars I would. They do not care about your pet. They have the crappiest hours of any Vet I've ever used.

This BYOB in Chester County hasn't earned a 28 in the Zagat guide for nothing.  What a fantastic place! Quaint location, beautiful setting, superb elegant food and friendly service. The menu changes daily, and the large garden in the back supplies fresh herbs and vegetables. The meal began with one of the specials, a duck confit appetizer, followed by a chilled asparagus soup with crabmeat. Delicious! The bronzino entree with shrimp and octopus was a sizable portion. . . no skimping here. But the real star of the evening was dessert. The special was a chocolate minty mousse tart that tasted exactly like the fresh mint from the garden from which it was made. The fresh mint flavor came bursting through. But the warm butterscotch cake was to die for, with a plate that contained a small bundt cake oozing with a yummy butterscotch sauce, homemade ice cream by its side, fresh strawberries, and a cookie-type baked pastry showered with powdered sugar. The restaurant is in a building surrounded by antiques, in a general store with a tiny operating post office in the same building. The post office is smaller than the one in Green Acres, and that's where you find the restrooms. It's cute as can be, and the restrooms are clean, with a tasteful decor. It's necessary to book weeks or even months in advance, particularly for a Saturday night. The restaurant is open for dinner only Wednesday through Saturday, but be aware it's CASH OR CHECKS ONLY.

The Milkshake gets a 4.5 Star...... 

But wait the name of the place is Burger Republic. It looks like that put more energy in there milk shakes then the name of this place. 

So the Burger was good. BUT!!!!! But when you order a burger Medium and cook it medium. It was solid Grey through out. 

Now the reason for the 3 star. Waitress came buy after i cut my burger in half,  I had to see if it was Medium. I took one Bite. She ask how was every thing and I told her it was not medium. She ask how was it cooked and i picked it up and ask her does this look Medium!!! She said No. Then walk off. Did she offer to have a redo, NO. Did she send a manager over and appoligize, NO!! She ignored it. 

So reason for the 3star is the Service. If you like a well done burger and Milk Shake. Hey your going to love it..

My first experience with this establishment was when I was looking to move from Cali to Reno and was looking for a job.

Needless to say that didn't pan out simply because the owner didn't specify that he wanted a face to face interview in his Craigslist ad.  Given my experience, I'm not driving five hours for a chance at a job.  You either take me at my word or just say no thanks.  After a passionate diatribe on both our parts, it stood to reason that I'd never work for this owner.

Fast forward to a recent visit to Reno.  I was curious and decided to visit this business just to see things for myself.  I was disappointed to see that the behaviors displayed by the staff members mirrored many of the previously posted reviews.  I don't know if the owner was present at the time, and I didn't really care to find out.  I spent about a half hour there (I'd say the drinks were great, but it's difficult to mess up a whiskey neat), and then picked up an Uber back to where I was staying for the weekend.

Now that I've seen this place from both sides, suffice it to say I won't give it a glance in my rear view mirror on a desperate day in hell.

Cheers.  :)

At this price range, a very nice hotel.  The front desk staff, restaurant staff and lounge staff were all friendly, helpful and attentive.  The rooms were clean and neat, very standard.  Very conveniently located for both the airport, and easy on/off to hwy 395.

The food at the restaurant was amazingly good.  Very unexpected at this type of hotel/motel.
Good coffee. Great vibe. And the use my favorite coco-almond milk. What a wonderful surprise!

HIGHLY RECOMMEND! During our stay at this hotel for a long period of time, we had the pleasure of being present for the new owner taking over. Once the new management stepped in we instantly noticed changes such as: our rooms/building was SOO clean and smelt fresh, the staff are dedicated, professional and extremely friendly. Please do not take pervious reviews to heart. I witnessed the dramatic change and this is a location you will want to go to!

My bf and I went to the movie tavern, and were pleasantly surprised with ambience. The food was really good, and the prices were quite reasonable. The seats were extremely comfortable, and there was a button you could press to get a waiter's attention. The wait staff did not take away from the moviegoing experience.

I would definitely go back there again!

It was clean and pretty fast even though it was crowded. The staff was helpful and polite. I didn't particularly care for the food though, but my daughter likes it.  It has a unique way of serving the food like getting a  burrito in a bowl without a tortilla.  Overall I haven't been back but I guess maybe I would after a long day of shopping.
I work next door at Fixit and eat Hot Wok on a daily basis.  Same great menu as all of the other "Wok" restaurants.
Best steak sandwiches you can buy.  Packed with meat and the best toppings.  They also sell beer so it's a perfect stop on the way to and from philly to avoid 76 west traffic.

I was a bit disappointed with my chicken teriyaki dish. The chicken while flavorful was a bit tough to cut and chew -- a bit over cooked.  But, you can't find a better spring roll anywhere!

Ron Campli
rc1522s@comcast.net

Very convenient location and very nice people . They took amazing care of my little Perry. It may not look like much from the outside , but they are thorough and caring to your pet. Oh and also my pet came home with a smile !

Long overdue accolades for your establishment but specifically Cici. I have been going there on occasion for years on and off. More recently in the last 4 months to the cafe or juice bar area. Cici is usually the one that attends me where I get a wrap, or beyond burger. I dunno what all she puts in there because it always tastes out of this world with her attention to detail and different from when others prep it. The others do a great job as well but Cici got it going on in the flavor department

After spending the entire week in New Orleans, on my last night there, pizza delivery was just what the doctor ordered. I'm kind of a pizza snob, but this pizza was really good. The fresh ingredients made the flavor, but the crust was a perfect hybrid of a European and New York style crusts. I'm still not sure how they were able to deliver it to my hotel in 20 minutes, but I can't remember the last time I was so pleased with a pizza delivery experience.

Not very family friendly, even though there were high chairs and a sling for the car seat. Unisex bathrooms, yuck!!! Thought this was a restaurant not a trendy bar. I guess that's why there was no baby changing station. This was the biggie for me. My son was to young to eat but spoke with another mom about the absent kids menu. So if you have children I would dine somewhere else because it's obvious they don't cater to families. Burgers ok at best. Shakes good but nothing special, mine had burnt marshmallows. Wanted to give this place a shot but I'll stick to my fave, Burger Republic.

I love a good dive bar, but this place isn't a dive bar, it's just plain dirty.

"best wings in Indianapolis" are just your normal frozen wings from Sysco covered in Frank's wing sauce. you can get the exact same wings across the street at the mousetrap. 

Their hot garlic is the same sauce with chopped garlic from a jar. Their teryaki was from a bottle, and just pure sugar.

Their blue cheese and ranch were good.


The fries were soggy -even after we sent them back the first time. The drinks were fine, but were served in plastic glasses that look like surplus Azar's cups from the 80's.

The bathrooms were absolutely appalling, the flooring was coming up, the ceiling was falling in, and there was exposed wiring.

This place just isn't worth it when there are plenty of nicer bars, with better food all around.


If you want some good, hand breaded wings try out the pawnshop pub, or head up the road to Sahm's.

I may have been looking a tad rough and not on my best food, but I had to swing by when my fellow Yelp Gladiator, Morgan F, was raving about this spot.

Good Cat or Bad Cat that is the question! Not sure about y'all but Bad Cat was calling my name. Their menu is separated by health-conscious and gluttonous dishes.

I had their blueberry pancakes which had lime, vanilla, and ricotta. HOLY COW were they amazing. Seriously were the best pancakes I've ever had. I'm not even sure how I wasn't crying tears of happiness as I was eating them. Add a side of bacon and call that perfection.

They have awesome wifi and the customer service was superb! I can't wait to come back the next time I'm in town.
I don't know how people could be crabby at a place like crabbys. The only thing I was upset about was them not knowing the secret Krabby patty recipe formula. Pinkies out

This place was mediocre at best. Poor service, understaffed. My wife and I shared the pancake. It was good, but not the best we ever had. The real exciting thing about the visit was the size of the pancake. Massive!

The prices were fair. The massive pancake is under $5. One in our party didn't receive her hot tea until about 20 mins in, and she ordered biscuits and gravy and was disgusted. If there are other options of places to eat, go there!

This is a great place in Downtown Tampa. The service is great and the food is great as well. The couple that owns this place is very personable and they will take good care of you. The Baklava is home made and the best I've had.

I recommend the lite bites tuna salad plate. The omelets are fantastic as well!

Great service, food, and amazing view! Definitely a must do in the area!

Give to crab cakes a try, and the margaritas; you won't be disappointed.

What the heck happened?

While they've never been authentic Mexican, they've never pretended to be.  What Santa Fe had going for it was options - flavors and fillings.  You want a cheeseburger burrito?  Check.  A tofu burrito? Check.  Chicken mole? Check check.  The last couple of times I've gone, though, the burritos have been fairly bland and didn't really taste all that different, in spite of the fact that they had different fillings *and* different sauces.  Everything now tastes fairly bland -- almost watered down, as if they're trying to make their sauces go further.

It used to be more like its Wynnewood cousin, but now it's just lost some of its pizzaz.  I want to support the "little guy" but if it's not as good as its larger peers?
really good gyros and chicken tacos; friendly and clean; great selection of beers; very reasonably priced

Had a bunch of appetizers including the Calamari, Lobster and a big ass meatball. Food is GOOD. Like GOODFELLAS good. The restauraunt is Sicilian owned and the restaurant is very unassuming. Located in Kenwood - what looks to be a dilapidated building is actually very well designed and delicious. Definitely give this place a try if you are italian.

FOOD POISONING ALERT!!!

I ate here last night with my son around 4:30pm and got very sick. I got the 2 item plate, with my sides being orange chicken and honey walnut shrimp. My son got just the orange chicken, and was fine, so I suspect it was the honey walnut shrimp that got me sick. 

Both my son and I noticed our food was not fresh and cold, but we ate it anyway. I am one of those people who hates sending food back, but after my experience I will never hesitate to do so in the future. 

I was extremely sick all last night and luckily being an EMT, I knew how to take care of myself. 

After this experience, I doubt I will ever set foot back into any Panda Express for that matter, and that's really sad because my son and I always loved making Panda Express our "cheat meal" 

I hope management reads this post and looks into this!

So if you like pizza.... That is good this is not the place to order. I thought it was just me but my 2yr old and 5 yr old didn't finish a slice. That says a lot. These guys if you let them will try and eat an entire medium pizza from dominos louies and the like. I waisted 25 dollars when I could have gotten a way better value and pizza  from pizza hut. I'm from Chicago this wasn't pizza this was some bull****

Tonight on Valentine Day, Elvis was in the building.  Full house.  Great show.  Once we've got seated, gave them our orders, and got our drinks immediately.  So far so good?  Not!  We waited one hour and a half wondering what happened to our foods.  And our waitress was MIA.  So I flagged down the manager, and he said it's a crazy night blah blah, and blah.  I said I understand that but at least give us the salads!!!  Ten minutes later, the salads came.  I saw three tables left, and walked out.  I was debating should I leave too?  Since I waited for almost two hours already it'd be a total waste and spoil the whole evening.  Finally, after two hours of waiting, the foods came.  BTW, Elvis left the building an hour ago.  We had the short ribs, and German pork schnitzel.  Delicious!
The foods were excellent.  I'd had give five stars, but the service was non exist.  A lot of people working there.  In this case, too many Indians, no chief.  In other words, nobody know what the &@$# they are doing.
Best sweet tea in the city!!! Try the fried donut with ice cream!!!!
Talk about a bar with really good food - dark and dingy inside - but a dive bar with great food is always a great thing.

We went to see the Rocky Horror Picture Show Live at Boiler Room Theater last night.  This was an amazing production.  We enjoyed it thoroughly.  The costumes, the music, the performances were all really amazing.  

The theater is not the easiest to find.  But, once you reach the complex you can ask anyone for directions.  It was a really great show.  Thanks

Kim is a fantastic waxer for my eyebrows! She listens to what I want, and is flexible and efficient. She is also easy to talk to. The facility is clean and relaxing, which makes me want to  get a couples massage there soon instead of having to leave so soon after waxing.

I was not able to make an appointment with Kim once, and I was less happy with the person who waxed my eyebrows that time, as they did not ask what I wanted before doing anything. Not a big complaint though.

I am so glad I went to this place. I was beginning to think there are no such thing as a decent Chinese restaurant in New Orleans. The steam dumplings were great, sweet and sour chicken was tasty, and the spicy chicken was just right. I would definitely go back there again. I only wish they have bubble tea there, but Jasmine Cafe is right next door (ish) so you could easily walk over and get it there.
I have been here twice food is always amazing.  Its a typical bar with great food. They have a decent beer selection on tap.

I was visiting from California when my mod stopped firing. I went here because they were the closest vape shop closest to me. I'm so glad I did because not only did they fix my tank quickly, they were so nice and accommodating. They also gave me tips on how to avoid the problem in the future! I definitely recommend going to this vape shop!

This place has a very hip atmosphere. We went to happy hour as a group and ordered a ton of food. It was all incredibly delicious and the servers were friendly and helpful. However, we did order the lamb meatballs, and they forgot them. Other than this though, it's a great place for some fantastic Mediterranean food!

Love this place! It's on a quiet corner , BYOB, with the best range of starter plates that you can end up just ordering all small plates! We had the bbq crab legs and andouille chips and head cheese starters. We then had the trio- delicious gumbo, and the BBQ shrimp entree. The barbecue sauce is awesome to dip the entire baguette you get with this dish in. I do wish this dish came with a side veggie or starch- it does come with a Caesar salad though. 

And to top it off we had the homemade banana cream pie- which was the best kind I've ever had. Not too sweet, kind of tangy - and just so amazing. We love the atmosphere and food here!

The space is fun and new. The staff is excited and attentive. Love that Jason is behind the bar. That always means a great drink and the best quality products! He did not let me down. I had a Michelada- it was tequila based so I little sweeter than a beer base, some delicious horchata and whiskey drink, and a Paloma. All good and worth drinking again. The food was good but just missed that full profile to give it a five star review. The menu was super accommodating for folks who don't eat gluten are vegan and/or vegetarian. I am bone of these things but I love a place I can count on to go with my friends who are. Until next time Festiva!
I had the fried macaroni and cheese and fried chicken sandwich. It definitely hit the spot!

New customer to Ybor Tire.  Called up to make sure they had my size tire.  Confirmed, so off I went.  The owner is the friendliest tire guy I've ever met.  Put used "new" tire on my old rim in about 10-15 minutes.  The owner made sure to stop me and give me directions on how to avoid the multiple construction zones in Ybor.  Will be recommending Ybor Tire to all who will listen.  New and used tires for a steal.  A+
Food quality at Chipotle establishment less than average.  Feel sick afterwards.  More responsibility should be observed.

Clean, clean, clean!  I have low standards for Reno hotels.  Most of them are pretty gross.  Whitney Peak is nice.  It's a huge improvement over the Fitzgerald's that used to be there!

It's a little difficult to find the entrance or the self-parking area.  The entrance to the hotel is not actually on Virginia Street.  You could easily drive past it a couple of times before noticing that the entrance is actually on the tiny cross-street Commercial Row.  Parking is available a kitty-corner block away (when coming from Highway 50 it's before the hotel).  Turn left off Virginia onto East Plaza Street to find it.  It's not too well marked.

The hotel decor is interesting.  The light fixtures are meant to be super cool, but they fail in their primary function:  lighting! The rooms are very dark, as other Yelpers have mentioned.  I hate turning on so many lights, but when I wanted to read, it was necessary.  Each room has an unusual looking chair or two.

Prices are good, and the furniture and fixtures are in very good condition.  Our family of 3 got a "suite."  I find the term "suite" means different things to different people.  I expected a living area with a sofabed and a separate bedroom area.  The Whitney Peak suites don't come with sofabeds (or, at least, ours didn't), and the bedroom area has a separating wall that has cutouts on either side of it.  No privacy.  Lots of space, however.

Being in a upper floor room, we got to take advantage of a continental breakfast, which was better than most "free" breakfasts I've encountered.

One thing this place is missing is a hangout area.  The Whitney Peak lobby is tiny and narrow.  There are a couple of chairs, but it's not a place you really want to hang out in while waiting for someone.

Posted checkout time is a very early 10:00 am, but when we inquired at the front desk, they told us 11 was okay.  Be sure to ask.

The best thing of all is that the entire hotel is nonsmoking!  Let Whitney Peak be your little oasis from those stinky, smoky casinos nearby.

We played phone tag trying to get in touch but they came and helped for a last minute job. Moved my entire one bedroom apartment to a new apartment 20 minutes away and stayed over what they had promised, to finish the job. Friendly, professional, and quick. Would definitely hire again.

I hadn't been to The Black Thorn Pub in over ten years. No specific reason just geography. I will say someone cleaned it up a little bit. It's not quite as dusty and dingy as my last visit. They used to have a stuffed Noid (The Domino's Pizza mascot) as decoration and I clearly remember him being caked in cobwebs and dust. My Dad and I were having dinner and he chose Black Thorn. We ordered a large House Salad (An Italian style salad, served with homemade vinaigrette) and a 14" Chicago Style Pizza with Pepperoni and Hamburger. We were prepared for a wait because we both remember waiting over an hour for food here in the past but I must say the service was speedy tonight. Their beer selection is really great and they have $2 pints of PBR if you have simple taste. The salad was fresh with ample amounts of cheese and a nice bite from the vinaigrette. I'm a fan of thin and light St. Louis style pizza but I have to say this cheesy deep dish concoction really made me sorry I hadn't been here in ten years. Their sauce is hearty and slightly spicy. I can't even fathom how many pounds of cheese they used on this one pizza or how many calories are in one slice of it. By the way I was only able to eat one full slice plus another one inch slice. This is a true South City gem. I'll be back and I won't wait another ten years.

We had a nice breakfast of bubble waffle and southwestern skillet recently on a busy Saturday morning. They have spaced tables apart and staff is wearing masks, but if you are paranoid just know that Teaspoon is very popular and gets pretty busy. I love their outdoor patio, they have heaters too this time of the year in case it gets chilly. 

The stars are for the service. The food is alright, like any other place, nothing special to say and is also a bit expensive. The servers were really nice, entertained any special requests we made and food came out fast. I have visited pre-covid and have had a dutch baby pancake which was good. 

The cream served with bubble waffle is deliciously sour and sweet, the waffle is visually interesting and appealing but tastes alright or rather has no taste of its own at all. The southwestern skillet was a very flavorful and healthy mix of veggies, fat and protein and I loved it. Hot chocolate was very good!

I found Nicck on Yelp while on the search for someone to overhaul my brows after months of neglect. The wolverine situation was real and finding some me time has been tough to come by. So, today, on whim, I reached out to Nicck after reading the glowing reviews. He responded to my message almost immediately after sending and we set an appointment for a few hours later - on a Sunday no less, people! I truly don't even know where to begin this review other than to say that I'll be a client as long as he is doing brows. 

Nicck is responsive, personable, and meticulous. He immediately went to work on the unkempt brows I was rockin and worked some major magic. The whole experience was as good as it gets when you're handing over your appearance to someone you've never met and leave feeling like you just got an extreme makeover! 

Niick understands the face and how to beautify the brow for a natural look.  I will seriously be back - and do whatever he says - to keep these brows looking  this good! Amazing!

Everyone was greeted with a big, friendly "Herro, how are you?"  The staff was so sweet the whole time.

The dumplings were great and served in a bamboo steamer basket., my spicy noodles were delicious, and my boyfriend's pork belly was tender and flavorful.

My one complaint would be that the pot of tea we ordered was made from tea bags.  (The tags were hanging out.)  I'm used to chinese restaurants where loose leaf tea is complimentary and tastier.

We'll be back!
Exetemely slow, waited for 2 hours just to get a prescription.  NOT EVEN A COVID TEST
I just ordered Uber Eats and not only was everything packaged nicely it tasted amazing! We will definitely be ordering from here again! Thank you for the amazing dinner!!

This spot is a gem! I didn't take any pictures because 1) it's pretty dark in here but 2) I was so busy enjoying my meal and didn't want to be "that girl". We came here as a large group of 13 for our friends "Last Supper" before he left and honestly, the most fun and fulfilling dinners I've had. We had great service and the staff was so genuinely excited to have us all there. He had wonderful suggestions on the menu and gave awesome pairings for wine. The bartender made very delicious libations, too! My husband got the lamb chops and the way they were prepared, I could've eaten 3 more racks it was so delightful. I got the Delmonico and it was cooked perfectly. We ordered many sides to share as a table and they complimented everyone's meal so well. Would be a great place to celebrate special occasions or treat yourselves!

After living close to the store for years I finally decided to go into it one day with time to kill. I was pleasantly surprised. The store appeared to me to be very clean and staff was very helpful in finding a particular item. The pricing for salmon beats Publix any day of the week. In the past when this location was Winn-Dixie I avoided it because the stores seem very run down and dirty. This is my next go to store. If you haven't been there recently give it a chance

First time here! We came last Friday after work. Found out about this place by traffic being redirected because of the flooding from all this recent rain. Went by and never heard of it, so made a mental note to come back by! 

Super nice place. Big open brewery / bar/ dining area. Family friendly as the Brewhouse was split with a sign directing people to the family friendly side. Nice bar too with plenty of seating. 

They offer a large amount of craft beer selections and a few options to munch on while you're there. I tried the cider option first, which was really good. Sampled a few others. Ended up getting the Ghost 1.1 after and stuck with that the rest of the night. Friends I was with tried the Ghost 2 and the blood orange option (forget the name). We all were pleased with the options we got. 

BUT LET ME TELL YOU ABOUT THAT GHOST 1.1!!!! I hope they never get rid of that. It was a mix of coconut , vanilla, cinnamon! The aroma of this beer was SO GOOD! I told my boyfriend that I wanna come back later this week and get some growlers for the weekend of that beer. YUM!  

They have a lovely little patio option with picnic style tables and soft lighting for night time. 

They offer 90's night with music games etc every Tuesday night, they have a few arcade games, and even have a few corn hole boards that you could bring out and play! 

Vibe is SUPER chill here and a great place to relax after work and not have to mess with a big crowd. I'll definitely be back soon!

Best bagels in the world! Small place but they have the best tasting bagels I've ever had! It has to be a secret ingredient! Thank you!

The place may be small and not the fanciest but the bags are Delicious!!
