I don't frequent chain restaurants but this time was a great experience. Went for lunch and all the servers were great. The food we got ( fish entree with mashed potatoes and French toast) was delicious. No cheesecake this time since we were  full due to the large portions.

Always have a good experience here when I visit with gal pals. Thursday nights are half off drinks for us ladies. Never more than a couple minutes wait time. Lots of variety for the sushi with very creative names! Typically order Cabo Wabo, Love Me, and Big Daddy. The restaurant isn't fancy, but it's good sushi! I also enjoy a big bowl of the ramen noodles - full of chunky veggies and a little on the spicy side. I've got to find one of those spoons they use to serve it.
Even when I've ordered it to go, it was still tasty. 
Casual, laid back vibe with decent service.
Parking is usually crowded in Lenox Village.
*** I'm intrigued with the Angry Banana - the name and the fact that it's sushi with banana in it. One day when I go back to visit, I'll try it!
Love the ice cream here! Prices are reasonable and the Almond Joy is my favorite! Yummy!

I've only ever been here for the lunch buffet, but I've never had a less-than-great experience. Everything is tasty, the service is good, and parking is easy. Hope Shalimar stays in Broad Ripple for years to come.

I was invited to a UYE and I thought you know if someone is nice enough to invite me I should go.    When I walked in they had a special posted $20 for a NY Strip.  Kind of did not have to read the menu after that.  With the strip steak I also ordered an appetizer of ham hocks.  These are not your mother's or grandmother's ham hocks.  Served cold with a touch of mustard, mini gherkins and cracked olives.  Perfect flavor.  On to the Gist salad. The salad for a dinner salad was huge came with a variety of greens and a balsamic dressing.  Really was a first rate salad.  The final entre was the steak.  Of all the dishes I think this was the weakest not bad just not as strong as the other two.  It was prepared to my request and had good flavor.  I did hear the manager say to another guest was he was not happy with the steaks he received and that is why it was on special.  Price value really the meal and service is outstanding.  I would put this on my to go list for sure small cozy and good food and prices.
This spot is really good. Fast service, great cocktails and tasty food. I'd like to highlight Shrimp and grits.
Great bagels and super friendly service. They even threw in a couple extras. I'll be back for more.

Went to this place due to it being across the hotel. Food was alright-not the best,not the worst. Would stray away from the Tuna Tartare. Service was not the best-had three different waiters and needed to ask a couple times to get a glass of water. I understand it's tough times, but still :/
Love this little Cafe /Restaurant People are very friendly and helpful. The service is great and the foods are very delicious.. The price of the items aren't too bad either

Really nice boutique hotel.  Located directly across from the Greyhound Station (not as dubious as it sounds).  Very conveniently located off Castillo and walking distance to State St. without catching any noise off the Avenue.  Well appointed rooms, quiet, lush, Spanish style, nice wait staff.  Can't wait to go back again.

I can't say enough Good things about this place! Audio express wanted to charge me $500 for a simple install. Stereo Pad $150! Don't even consider going anywhere else. You can trust these guys and have a peace of mind you are not getting ripped off! Quality work at a very reasonable price. 
I love to give business to Good people and these guys have earned a customer for as long as they're around.

The host at the entrance with really cool silver hair was super helpful and gave a lot of suggestions pertaining to our cravings and mood. He seemed to know everything about the menu and the night started off well.

Our waitress was a little bit less informative and in one instance was reading off of my cocktail menu (in my hand) to describe a drink my friend asked about.

We decided to share Brussels sprouts, crispy calamari, and baked macaroni and cheese. The calamari came with an excellent vinaigrette style dipping sauce and by far was the best dish. The Mac N' Cheese was quite salty but was probably because of the two part cheddar one part asiago (just a preference thing). 

I can't say I was impressed but it was A-OK. The prices gave me a feeling I would feel amazed but overall it was a solid 3.5- 4 because of the service.
Ham was bland with no seasoning.  French fries were cold.   I had the hot roast beef sandwich.  The portion was generous and hot.   Not sure I would return.

Chilly's has a pretty good location on Veterans Blvd. (next to Paretti Mazda) that subjects it to tons of drive by traffic.  Probably the best feature of this snowball stand is that they have a drive thru.  Therefore, you can pull right in, get your snowball fix and head right back down the road.

For me, Chilly's is more of a shaved ice than your prototypical snowball.  The ice is very coarse and crunchy to the taste.  I'm not a huge fan of snowballs that I have to "sink my teeth into", as I prefer the velvety smooth ice; but this is just a personal preference.  If you are a shaved ice kinda guy or girl, then you'll like this place a lot.

We opted for a cantaloupe snowball, as this is a pretty light and refreshing summertime flavor.  If I'm not mistaken, I think the syrup bottles were labeled Sno-Wizard, so perhaps they are outsourcing their syrup instead of making it from scratch.  Whatever.......the cantaloupe was flavorful but not overpowering and I would definitely recommend it.

Chilly's also serves ice cream, hot dogs and nachos, but I don't foresee trying anything other than a snowball here.

We have been going here since I was a kid.  Love the salad and bread.  The Chicken Emil is great.  Good budget Italian.  My husband and I like to go here on date nights and to the cheap theater across the street.  Good budget date night

We decided on a family style. The apps were decent.. meatballs, bruschetta and Caesar salad. We ordered shrimp fra Diavlo which was pretty good. We also decided on the salmon and chicken parmigiana and it that was a complete disappointment. The salmon was over cooked and very fishy which means it had gone bad but instead of throwing it out.. they cooked it and served it. Not cool! The chicken parmigiana was also super overcooked and tasteless. We sent back the salmon and communicated that the salmon was not fresh. There was no apology or even a comp for the two meat dishes that came out obviously over cooked and untouched by our group of 4. While the dinner started well it ended terribly. Beware. Well trained chefs should know when food has gone back and shouldn't try to send it out by chance no one would notice.

Pros: Tasty foods, especially the soups. Friendly staff. Multiple dining ambiances.

Cons: N/A

Dealt with this dealership for 4 years. I will say, they are aggressive with the phone calls when your lease is close to being up- I'd probably average a call a week from the salesman asking for me to upgrade early or buy a car that they found for me. Very unprofessional when you tell them several times that you are not ready or willing. 

Otherwise the cars I drove from here were great, never had any issues. The prices are very fair. Service has been great. 

Compared to other dealerships I've dealt with I'd say this one is definitely one of the better.

I came here with my mom on a Saturday night when the wait time at all the really good restaurants was too unbearable.  I was already a little cranky and wasn't expecting much from a place that still lets you smoke in the bar.  It was pretty busy when we got there but we were sat right away by a really friendly hostess.  I'm not really okay with the Simpsons decor (Homer Simpson watched me eat my dinner), but you forget about it eventually.

It seemed like more of a sandwich and wings kinda place, but I took a chance and ordered chicken dijonaise - grilled chicken breast and scallops in a creamy dijon sauce.  It was served with asparagus and mashed potatoes.  The meal basically completed my three favorite things (delicious sauces, asparagus, and potatoes), and I was very very pleasantly surprised!  The sauce the chicken and scallops were in was a little heavy, but very tasty.  The scallops were fresh and the right texture.  Speaking of fresh - the asparagus definitely was!  And they were obviously sauteed in garlic and oil.  I was definitely a fan.  One thing you should know is I'm Irish, and potatoes are my thing.  I mean, I live and breathe potatoes.  Especially mashed.  The only kind I eat and like are my mom's - but I have to say, for mashed potatoes, these were pretty damn tasty, and I definitely was craving them once they were all gone (which didn't take long).

The service was decent, and the waitress was nice enough.  You definitely don't get any smoke from the bar in the restaurant area.  It's kind of dimly lit, but honestly at a place called Moe's Grill I wouldn't expect them to be bulking up their electric bill just for a little more light.

Overall, had a good experience and would definitely go back.
The deli department might be the slowest in history. Several side conversations behind the counter as the line grows and grows. Don't go here on a lunch break under an hour long.

Service was slow. Limited menu and just not what I expect for a seafood place. 

My husband did like the crawfish though!

Checked out this place yesterday based on the reviews here.  It's the FREE VACUUM aspect that sold me considering my cat escaped his carrier and furred up the seats en route to his neutering, AND there were about two years worth of leaf particles and tennis ball fuzz enmeshed in my upholstery. 

I was a little confused when I drove in because there are wash-it-yourself stations and vacuums that you pay for, but the friendly attendant told me that I need to go through the auto car wash to access the free vacuums. Awesome! I got the $10 version (glad they take cards), and when the attendant saw me scrambling to tuck the side mirrors in before the water hit, he helped me close the right one in time. Phew!  

The vacuums were sucky (in a good way) as promised by the reviews here, and there were probably six or so of them. I discovered that if you park between two, it's easiest to clean both sides of your car.  There's also a free floor mat cleaning thing that didn't seem as useful as the vacuums, so I only used it for one mat.  My car was really in need of a deep vacuum, so it took me about 30 minutes to vacuum it out. However, it was well worth it, and and I was totally satisfied with the experience and cost.

PROTIP: don't wear anything too short if you're a woman (well, maybe if you're a man too, why not?). I was wearing a serviceable skirt for regular outings but not one suitable for crawling around on my seats trying to get debris off my floor. I have no doubt this is why a guy named Miguel cleaning his minivan told me I was very sexy. 

Also, don't head south down 8th during any busy traffic hours expecting to make a left off into the car wash. This area is a nightmare with that middle lane for people turning into Kroger, Walgreens, Fat Mo's -- ugh.

Do you have kids or dogs don't waist your money here!!! Ive never been more mad with a company.. We bought the goof proof plan, " it will cover any stains blahh blahh " NO it don't. I have food stains like four spots size of a quarter and they won't come out. They up sell the plan and lied. I've been in sales and could never do that to people especially young people... 

I called the goof proof and they tell me " our manager is busy they will call you back" I called multiple times and got the same thing. 
 It took me an hour to file the claim with a representative on the phone and he did not tell me it may not be covered due to multiple stains. Just waisted my time and money.

If you go on google or what ever everyone says the same thing. 

It's a year old light grey Microsoft and

Delicious pizza!! Friendly service and excellent Covid procedures . They have a take out pick up table set up , I noticed that they keep the pizza warm in a insulated bag so when you do pick it up it's still piping hot! Genius ! Annnnd if you want something to wash it down with , they do take out alcohol as well :) yay!

First let me start off .. I absolutely love Cracker Barrel biscuits but today not so much. We had 3 different plates of biscuts all cold. After the first plate I asked the  waitress for some hot biscuits she replied they have some coming out of the oven and she will bring them . About 3 minutes later she returned with the biscuits (again cold but warmer than the first plate). Waitress comes back ask if they were better .. told her no they were cold as well. She then replied " I don't know why maybe the oven is broke " .. I can bring you some sourdough. I declined the sourdough of course ‍‍. She offered to get yet another plate to see if these will be hot .. she returned with more COLD biscuits. (I give up). When she came back to check on us we gave her our feedback on the biscuits and she said well maybe the oven is on low.. so we  go from being broke to being on low .. I cannot make this stuff up . So for that reason alone I give them   .  I only came here today for some hot buttered biscuits and latte  which is another long story I will not bore you with.

I am totally crazy about this place because Italy is my favorite place on earth, and they are obviously doing all they can to offer an authentic Italian experience. BUT, they make all these amazing, high quality dishes, only to microwave them prior to serving, if you opt to dine in :(

Highly recommend these guys!  Very professional, strong communication, and excellent at their craft. Made the appointment and they were very prompt and arrived right on time for the free estimate and the actual day. Walked the house again with me and discussed exactly what I wanted to make sure we were on the same page. We scheduled 4.5 trees and they worked all day. They need to come back to finish tree #4, but the other trees look fantastic!! I am so happy with that excellent work they did, they truly understand the needs of the tree for the health& longevity of the life of the tree. Very pleased with how the trees look, shade for our house, and the clean up. They bring a machine to mulch all the debris away. Excellent clean up job & true professionalism! Highly recommend this family owned & local company. Thank you for the excellent work!!

I went here today to get my cat's claws trimmed as this is a walk in service. The groomer, gail, had no intention to take my cat from the start. I had given my phone number 6 separate times and when my account came up she asked me why i didn't go to the harahan location instead, as she was trying to leave early because she had plans. She came up with 5 excuses before she aggressively put the leash around my cat's neck and he hissed. She then told us that my very friendly cat was too hostile for her to care for and that we need to leave.

Went to buy beer and wine the other day. I had two items that I wanted rung up separately. The cashier rang up the first item and asked for ID  which I presented. He completed the order and rang up the second item and again asked for ID. The guy just saw my ID 30 seconds ago. I asked why he needed to see it again and his reply was "Just following protocol sir". 

Giant has a policy of carding everyone, even if the customer is clearly 60, 70 or 80 years old. It gets inconvenient, especially when you're a repeat customer and the cashier recognizes you, or you get carded twice in 30 seconds. It seems that Giant does not trust their cashiers to use common sense in their job.

Also, what's the deal with scanning a customer's ID? I've been buying alcohol for many years and Giant is the only place that has ever done this. Not crazy about that.

Stumbled across this little gem on a recent trip to Tucson.  We had the shredded coconut tostada.  Wow, delish!  My daughter went for the pasta with cashew sauce and I had the eggplant dish.  They use intense spices to bring out the flavor.  Loved every bite.  Definitely recommend, even if you are a carnivore.
As the name implies, very efficient service. Service person was also friendly, and he had me out in no time  Will definitely return in the future.

I, too, wish I could give more than five stars to this wonderful animal hospital. We switched over to Kingsbury about a year and a half ago, upon a recommendation from a friend after a terrible experience with our former clinic.

Everyone at Kingsbury, from the receptionists to the techs to the doctors themselves, has been wonderful. The facility is beautiful, warm, and inviting. The wooly pads on the exam tables are a prime example of the little, thoughtful touches that make Kingsbury so great. 

Each exam we'd had with our two Dachshunds and our cat has been wonderful. We've seen all three doctors at Kingsbury, and each was so knowledgeable, friendly, and compassionate. Our 10-year-old male Dachshund, Charlie, had congestive heart failure, and he had an EKG done at Kingsbury. Dr. Brammeier, Dr. Shaw, and Dr. Fry all were familiar with his case, and were so wonderful and attentive when it came to making sure he was doing well on his medicine.

Just a week and a half ago, we sat up all night with Charlie as he went from perfectly perky to constant coughing. It happened so quickly. Kingsbury told us to come immediately, even though we had no appointment, and we were taken right in. He'd had coughing spells before, but nothing like this. We put him in ICU for the day to see if oxygen and a high extra dosage of one of his meds would help, but it was his time.

The entire morning, the hospital kept in contact with us. I never once had to call for an update. When we got the call that we needed to come quickly, it was like the whole staff was there just to take are of Charlie and of us. We got there in time to say goodbye to our sweet boy. Dr. Fry, the wonderful techs, and the lovely woman at the reception desk were so focused, so compassionate. After Charlie passed, we were ushered gently to a private room. Dr. Fry wrapped him in a blanket and let us have as much time as we needed with him. We were told when we were ready, they would get him from us and we could just go home. "Don't stop by checkout; that is not a concern right now."

I know this is a small thing, but it says a lot about Kingsbury. Their services may be pricier than some hospitals, but money is not their focus. Of course we knew we had to pay, but the fact that they recognize that grieving pet parents might find some comfort in not having to think of those details at the moment speaks volumes. That afternoon, as we sat at home with our little girl Dachshund and our cat, a beautiful, cheerful bouquet of flowers was delivered to our front door--from Kingsbury.

Losing Charlie was (and is) so terribly hard, but I truly feel that the compassion, kindness, and respect shown to us by every single person at Kingsbury has made a significant difference. I can't imagine wanting to ever go anywhere else. Thank you, Kingsbury, for all you've done for us, for Charlie, and for Maggie June and Beans.

I had a gift certificate, so I had to try this place again.  Most people I know really like this place, but I'm definitely in the minority.  The 1st time I went with 2 other people, and we were all disappointed with the food.  I've heard rave reviews on the pizzas, so maybe I should have went with the pizza.  The staff was once again very friendly and helpful.  This time, I went with the Taco Paco wrap.  My other 2 friends also went the wraps.  The experience started out somewhat badly when they mixed up our orders.  The sauce that was supposed to be on the side was in the wrap, and vice-versa on the other wraps.  

Now to the food.  The food was just okay.  I really wanted to like it.  It was nothing great.  Again, my other 2 friends were in agreement.  The wraps just didn't have enough flavor.  If there is a next time, I'm definitely going with the pizza.

Went here for lunch as I wanted to try the food to see if I could buy out the restaurant for a large group party. 

Service was slow and customers after me got their order first. Their order had food that was frozen in the middle. When I Inquired about the delay in my order the manager(?) started yelling at me, accused me of having an attitude and eventually threw my bag of food at me. All the while he didn't pause to put down his vape device.

New places always have kinks to work out and I'm pretty patient but yelling at customers and accusing them of attitudes when the employees are being rude is unacceptable. I really thought maybe I was on a hidden camera. Huge loss of revenue for them and our business will be given to another restaurant. 

After sharing the story with my hotel they will NO LONGER recommend this place to dine. Kindness and respect counts. Karma people!!

A reservation was made for a large group of women on a Monday night to experience Happy Hour in their bar.  We arrived, were served water, and then the management took our group host aside and told her since she didn't have a reservation they wouldn't be able to accommodate us. They alread had another reservation for a large group.  Our host tried to explain the we did have a reservation, called it in over a week prior, left her phone number in the event they needed to get in touch with her.  Nada.  Our choice?  Take our group outside (in the 90* weather) or leave.  Our group of 20 women promptly left as we did not want to be where we were not welcome.  Not good.  I've never been turned away in a bar before I've had a chance to have even one drink!  I know the Owhyee enjoyed our company and our money.

One of the best places to get a great drink and a bite to eat! The Monsoon is delicious and packs a punch. The burger and baked potato are phenomenal. It's a chill spot away from the madness on bourbon street. The staff is super friendly plus we had the pleasure of meeting the owners daughter. She gave us some history on the bar and staff. What a great little place. Make sure to add this as a stop while in New Orleans. We went there 3 times, it was that good.

Food is amazing!  Went on a weekday during lunch hour and it was packed!  We didn't have to wait long, but a line started just after us.  Got the turkey platter with potato salad and mac and cheese.  portions for the side were on the smaller side, especially for the price.  But I will definitely be going back!  
Bonus points for having a patio, although it was packed when we went, so I'll have to go back and try again!

If I could clone this bubbly, smart, talented woman, I would!  I've been a staunchly loyal client of hers after bouncing around for a few years.  She has cut my hair when it was super short, growing out, longer, you name it.  She's methodical, thoughtful, flexible and very talented.  Lover her!!
Great experience. Nails look perfect. Didn't feel rushed not had to wait too long. nice staff.

Love Nordstrom! however, my experience here was awful. Mike Gentry the store manager was very rude and condascending and I had an Issue at the store. Will never be going back to that location and will make sure I inform friends and family members about my experience with Mr. Gentry. Thank you.

Deven always does a great job on my hair. I have been going to her for around 10 years now, and all my 'dos have come out perfect. I always get a lot of compliments, which is no easy feat considering I like to experiment with my hair. However, no matter how crazy, she always does a great job both listening and executing. Why do you think I keep on going back!

One star means NO stars. I've never been to such an unorganized, uncaring, dirty place in my life. You wait hours to see a clueless nurse practitioner that does absolutely nothing to help you, everything to upset you and then tries to give you a laundry list of unnecessary and excessive tests you you'll never need (and definitely don't want,) in the next ten lifetimes. (So the practice can bill your insurance company and make money on the patients pain and suffering.)
Why this place is still in business is beyond me. I can't believe the board of health the florida medical board hasn't shut them down....in short, FIND SOMEONE ELSE WHO IS HONEST and cares about YOU and not just how much money they can make off of your suffering and your insurance company. They especially LOVE to over-bill Medicare and Medicaid I've been told by other patients for tests that were never done. I can't believe this place is allowed to treat people. I wouldn't take my pet iguana here for treatment!!!
Such a beautiful estate, totally worth going to and the interiors are a little grander than Winterthur

We almost got to eat outside tonight, but as soon as we placed our order, it started pouring.  Our waitress and the hostess were very accomodating and got us seated inside as fast as possible (even though it was very busy). We started with an order of the Macho Nachos, which were so huge and delicious that it ruined our entrees for us. However, what i did have of the Chipotle Chicken Tacos was very impressive. My friend had the Grilled Beef Tacos and loved them. Rice and beans and a pitcher of sangria were the perfect accompaniments. I definitely plan on going back again! (maybe just for the nachos :) )

We got a pie of plain from here today as they did not sell slices. We have been used to thin crust wide slices that are often sold near college campuses in New Jersey.  That said, the pizza here was too greasy and cheesy. The extra grease does make the crust tastier but even having a slice (not too big ...comparable to pizza hut slices) is enough, and I usually have 3 slices a meal. The flavoring was slightly spicey but it was difficult to taste the tomatoes because the cheese was too thick. Maybe I am more of a sauce (than cheese) person. Anyway, it seems like this may be comforting after a night out but for a regular meal it can be too much.
food was great. the quality was very good and the food was not over seasoned. the churo desert was fantastic. All in all a very nice place to relax and have a great dinner

I was married at the Audubon Center July 1st. It was by far the best day of our lives. The venue is perfect and Kate and Shannon were wonderful to work with. The food was amazing and I've had so many people tell me how beautiful the venue was. This venue was perfect for the rustic style wedding that I was looking for. I can't say enough to express how I feel about Audubon and the staff!

Classic New Orleans! A place that I would drive by a thousand times before stopping in and then wonder why it took so long for me to try it.  Excellent craft cocktails at an affordable price.  The free food was a nice touch too on Monday.  The bartender, Anders, was outstanding.  Fresh ingredients, talented bartender, cobbler style cocktails seem to be the majority, but lots of other classics and neo classics beings served.  The crowd reminds me largely of the graduate student/academic types, although there is a good cross section as well.  I noticed lots of bicycles locked in front on the way out.  Finally, I love the buzzer to let you in.  All in all a hit for me.  As my friend Arnold said, "I'll be back!"

My car needed a new alternator and had died the night before. I hadn't had a mechanic in the St. Louis area yet and saw that Speedway Automotive had good reviews. I called them the next morning and they were able to fit me in that day to take a look at it. Their work was fast and efficient and I really appreciated how friendly the staff was! They even sent me a follow up e-mail a couple of weeks later to make sure the car was running well. Now that's good service! I would definitely recommend them :)

I didn't try the Bagels...looking forward to this week. I went the weekend they opened. The French Onion Soup was delicious. Had the Steak and White Cheddar Pannini was disappointed...maaabe 4 oz.s of meat on the whole sandwich. Thought it was over priced. Basically I was drowned in the large amount of bread in the Soup and the small amount of meat in the Pannini.

May try again this weekend before I pass final judgement.

I'm in total agreement with previous Yelpers about the kale salad. It is crazy delicious, and my boyfriend who hates salads even loved it. Apart from the kale salad, I wouldn't re-order the other items we tried. The pizza is on a dry crust and is awkward to eat. I should've read the description on the Acai Bowl, because I assumed it was like others I've tried (frozen fruit smoothie base with berries/granola). It looked more like soupt and tasted like a milky melted smoothie, which was not filling. I wouldn't come here if you're short on time either, as everything really is made to order.

Extremely pleased with Erica!  I wanted to get my back and upper arms waxed (because I'm apparently entering that stage in my life where my genetic makeup thinks I am a primate) and was asking around for someone who knew of a good place to go.  I got several responses, but I ended up taking my sister's advice (she is family after all) to see Erica.  My sister had gone to her before and was very content with her results.  I had gotten my back waxed in the past at a different place and it was this hocus-pocus session that included the waxing, along with some electricity-hair-singeing-over-gauze event that ended up leaving me with terrible bumps.  I wasn't even explained the after care steps of my session (that was my first time getting it done).  ERICA, however, not only did a freaking amazing job (painless and concise), but she also explained things to me.  I felt so comfortable with her that I mentioned I might be interested in getting a "chemical" peel (because I have pores on my face that are big enough to hide treasures...and also because I had my first facial when I was in Jordan and noticed immediate results when all of that crap was cleaned out of my craters).  But this time I wanted something a little more aggressive to actually take some of the old layers off.  So, being that this would be my first peel, I called her up the next day and asked her a million questions regarding the procedure.  She was so patient with me and more than willing to fully answer my questions honestly and in detail.  I went in for the chemical peel session... Let me first say that if you haven't gotten a peel or a facial done...do it!  I felt completely pampered!  Erica was gentle when she needed to be, and aggressive when it called for it...but the whole time she was explaining the process to me and what she was doing and what sensations I should be expecting as she went step-by-step through the procedure.  She knew that this was my first time and took it upon herself to keep me informed throughout.  I am thankful for that!   In conclusion:  Erica is a master at what she does.  She is informative, knowledgeable, and when you meet her you will feel that she is passionate about what she does and takes great pride in her work.  I will be going to Erica for all of my waxing and beautification needs! :-)

Really surprised by the positive reviews... Mediocre BBQ served by a total a-hole. Dear sir, as the first employee your customers encounter you should strive to make it positive. At least the beer was cold. Next time in town I'll take my chances with any other BBQ joint. Can't be any more disappointing.

My husband and I took a tour of the outside of the home and it was really pretty.  Very cool history that goes along with the place. I wish we could have seen the inside, but we didn't have time. Very large confederate cemetery on grounds. We both loved out visit!

We were here on a very busy Friday night in March, 2019. It was rainy-snow and you have to park in the back and walk around to the front. This is not the best way to have laid out this restaurant, considering there is a door in the rear that is at the back parking area. 

Upon entering, we were advised of a 25-minute wait. Ordinarily, I would go elsewhere with that type of wait, but we opted to stay. We were seated in about 25 minutes, in the very busy, loud, and dark restaurant. Our 25-minute wait was in a small area on a small couch we had to share with a man with a "therapy" dog. I am glad I was not seated next to the curly-haired cheerful dog for my meal. 

It was very loud, especially with what I only imagine was a speaker right above our table. It was difficult to speak to my guy. There were a lot of children and a lot of noise coming from some fairly "active" tables.

The meal starts off with "Cacia's Bakery" breads--foccacia, and a sliced roll, roasted garlic (which was incredibly good), roasted peppers and what I believe were slices of sharp provolone. The startin' pickins' were very, very good and I ate too much before my meal even came out.

I ordered the Ziti in Rosa Sauce with Chicken Cutlets on top, the Cream of Tomato Seafood soup, and my guy ordered the Salmon Berenato with a side of broccoli and a salad. 

First, the portions are huge. Here it is, Monday morning, and I am finishing my entree for lunch today after having it for lunch on Saturday too. But quality is not about portion size, although that obviously plays a part in rating.

The soup was divine. I would order this again in a heart beat. The ziti were done perfectly and the sauce was good, heavy, but good. Where it was not good was the chicken cutlet. They were done perfectly, but they had not much taste--perhaps some seasoning in the breading would help. I'm not sure the problem--because my Aunt makes the best chicken cutlets and she uses unseasoned breadcrumbs. Her's are much better than Scarpinato's. 

My guy's salmon was huge also, and he said the cranberry glaze was phenomenal--his only complaint was that he likes a firmer fish and it was a bit "mushy" inside. For $28, I would expect perfection. For my Ziti & Chicken's $22, I would expect more taste.

For dessert--which we did not need but stuffed in anyway--was a Reese's cheesecake. This was just "okay." More than anything, it was heavy--I would imagine eating a brick would feel the same. I was unimpressed with it and wished we hadn't ordered it.

But all in all, this restaurant is probably one of the better Italian ones around. I can imagine certain dishes are five star. What I find odd is it is very difficult to find a simple "spaghetti & meatballs in spaghetti gravy" on a menu. That is truly what I wanted--spaghetti gravy and meatballs. This was not on the menu. It's hard for me to imagine an Italian restaurant not carrying the one classic dish every Italian would eventually want. 

I remember going to Scarpinato's more than thirty years ago, when presumably, the father ran the business. It always had an eclectic, different menu--and I believe way back then I couldn't order spaghetti and meatballs, but I am looking forward to again visiting Scarpinato's.
Great service! I messaged them through Yelp and was able to have my old bed picked up within 2 days!
The best New York style bagels in town and my favorite bagels overall. Highly recommend.

Was happy to find an indo(Indian) fusion place in Nashville. The weight time was around an hour as we did not have a reservation. So if u are planning to go there for dinner it is better to have a reservation. The ambiance is good but is super crowded . If you want a cozy and calm place may be it is not for you. 
The food was ok. I felt the food was a little sweet and could have been a little more on the spicier side. I felt a little let down may be because I expected a little high of this place :(

I wanted to love Koco. I've just changed nail salons, and my soror recommended it. I really enjoyed the first half of my experience there, and I really liked the vibe of the salon. Work is always stressful, so it was nice to chill and be in the company of my sistas. They offered wine, and I received a fabulous pedicure that still looks nice more than a week later. I thought I'd found my new nail salon.

The problem came with the gel manicure. For the first time ever, I experienced a gel mani that consisted solely of polish removal, a little bit of trimming of cuticles, and no soaking before the gel polish was applied. Apparently, waterless/dry/soakless manicures are a growing thing, but in that case, techs are supposed to use lotions, oils, and heated treatments to clean, hydrate and soften the skin. This didn't happen. She polished and I went right under the lamp for curing. Things stung a little but I figured maybe I was sensitive.

By Day 5, my gel polish had chipped on my left index finger, and my cuticles were raggedy. No amount of cuticle oil or home care is helping my skin; only time. Here's where the other problem lies: I called for 2 days straight to get back in to fix the nail, but they were completely booked. Couldn't accommodate one nail. I understand, 'tis the season, but no time or alternative was given. They were friendly on the phone, but it didn't save my mani from being janky at Commencement this year. Even offering a couple of dollars off the next service for the inconvenience might have been nice. 

So disappointed that Koco didn't work out for me. Was really hoping it would. Back to the drawing board.

I visited this bar on a one night occasion while
Passing in the neighborhood. Unfortunately, I was displeased with the menu options but, I was able to find something that I thought I might enjoy. The food was not up to par as the two dishes that I selected were not good. And for the price of the food I expected for the food to at least be some what appetizing. I will say that although I had an issue with the food, the service from the manager was incredible. He was very professional and accommodated me properly. I will be more than willing to revisit although it will only be for the drink menu.

My husband and I had dinner there on Saturday night. We chose to dine in the Bistro and not in the main dining room. The menu was extensive and offered everything I could have wanted. I chose blackened fish tacos (3), and my hubby had beef tips. Both were plentiful and super yummy.  The price was just right, the food was great and we enjoyed the relaxing atmosphere and friendly staff.
We are happy to have found this great little gem so close to home. Give it a try!
Also, I would be remiss if I didn't mention our awesome server, Kenneth. A great, fun waiter.
Always on time and everything we've had from Brandywine has been really good. I recommend both the chicken avocado panani and the Philly traditional pizza.

Popped into Maggie Walt Design last night and browsed during a private shopping hour prior to an Elite Yelp Event.

I was super impressed by the staff, ambience, gorgeous threads!  

The shop was beautiful and while the clothing was definitely out of my price range there were many gorgeous and affordable accessories ranging from handmade jewellery to bath products.  There were even some super cute reusable bibs for wee ones (I'm a mom so I tend to gravitate to these things).

Maggie Walt was super friendly and easy to talk to!  She has a beautiful shop full of unique and one of a kind pieces of art - I would definitely refer to her clothing as works of art.  The fabrics were amazing and come to find out, she travels the world in search of the perfect pieces.

Maggie Walt is a wonderful addition to the Edmonton fashion landscape and I hope she enjoys a long and lucrative career here!  

And as an aside, she has a super secret room in the basement where she holds events and fashion shows!  AMAZING!  

http://www.maggiewalt.com/

I uploaded some pics - Check them out!
Wide variety of things, however it is hard to find someone to help you.   When I did get help, the person was knowledgeable and helpful.

This is my first time visiting this place for a facial. Although the facial was great, the cleanliness of the equipment is what scared me. I walked into a room for my facial and change and lay down. Once the facial started, my attendant used this scrubbing brush that had a faint smell to it. I originally thought it came from the lotion that she originally applied to my face. Once the attendant left the room, I removed my eye cover and peeked at the scrub brush and that brush was nasty! I couldn't believe that she actually used that brush on my face. It looked like it wasn't washed in weeks. Right after my facial was done, I noticed that she rushed another woman into the same room I walked out of less than 2 minutes ago. The equipment wasn't sterilized, and the bed wasn't remade after me.
A great casual dessert place with cool boozy drinks. There is in door and out door seating.

This was the second place I bookmarked as a fledgling Yelper. It came  as a recommendation from a longtime Philly Yelper, and her other tip proved to be one of my favorite Italian spots.  I missed going once or twice due to the restaurant's hours, but after over 2 years of waiting we finally made it for our post Vday lunch. Sometimes expectations can build and the actual experience is not able to live up to them. That was NOT the case here. 

As we walked into the low ceiling-ed dining room and were greeted with "Hai" by one of the efficient servers, I had a feeling this would be about as authentic a Japanese meal we would find in this area. There is lots of dark hardwood and minimal decoration. The lunch menu has less options than their dinner menu, still it was difficult to decide what to choose. 

We agreed on sharing 2 rolls, soft shell crab and shrimp tempura, Trish selected a chicken Donburi and I the Sukiyaki. Mine came with soup and salad, hers just soup. The miso was pretty good, maybe not the best I've had but none too shabby. The standard ginger salad was not standard here, sure, it contained the required iceberg and some other usual components, but the dressing was unlike the dayglow orange I expected. This was likely homemade with puréed pickled ginger and a touch of wasabi, it was not sweet but very savory and delicious. I overheard another table asking if they still sell it by the bottle, sadly the answer was no. Next out were the rolls, they were not sauced in anyway, just incredible rice and the main ingredient. The shrimp was also unique, these large prawns were barely( if any) coated in tempura, and had no teriyaki sauce to cover up imperfections. 2 pieces of this roll had tempura crowns and no  other distractions. The crab was equally well done, I'd order either again. Trish enjoyed her chicken and rice dish, and at under $10 a nice portion. My beef hot pot came out beautifully plated in a cast iron shallow pot. As I savored each ingredient and starting mixing them together it hit me, this is French onion soup with a Japanese aesthetic. So gooood, and a large serving that left me a great next day lunch. 

The sushi alone  would have had me coming back for more, definitely the best I've ever had, but that Sukiyaki.... I need to try everything on this menu, then start at the beginning again.

I went there end of rose season, however, still have many healthy roses to choose from. Roses are tall,  vigrous, lots of blooms and never seen like that in most nurseries across west coast. Love this nursery and back again next year. Best rose bushes available in California
Yes! First the people are so nice! We got 2 Nashville hot Chxn sandwiches and loaded nachos and they did not disappoint! Definitely will be back!

Great little bar/restaurant on Frenchmen Street that offers table service and live music! At the suggestion of the waitress, I had the roast beef po boy fully dressed. It was fantastic! Tender beef, succulent gravy, and crusty bread! The live blues band that was playing was a nice addition, though a little on the loud side, so I posted up toward the back.

We hit up Lolita during Restaurant Week and sat out side with along with our good friend Azul Tequilla.  

I have to say, this was an impressive meal.  I suppose mostly impressive because I don't hear that many people talking about the place.  Why the heck aren't you people talking about this place?!  

When going to a new restaurant, I usually half-heartedly ask the server what they recommend.  To my surprise, our super awesome waitress did a great job making some suggestions.  The food was very fresh and very well prepared.  The flavor combinations and textures were spot on.  You could see and taste the creativity their chef possesses.  

Yeah, it's been a couple weeks and I'm ready to go back for a full on meal here.  Score one for Restaurant Week and Lolita!

I wouldn't recommend sitting outside though.  There's a bunch of foot traffic on the sidewalk and if the restaurant has a wait you'll be crowded by the people waiting to get in.

We have eaten here several times and have always had a pleasant experience.  The food is great and the waiter(s)/waitress(s) are very helpful.  My son, who is an EXTREMELY picky eater loves going to Matane.

Great value and a friendly atmosphere.
Vietnam has delicious food but ALSO delicious tropical drinks. Take a date here and split the Flaming Volcano. It's much more exciting than drinks that aren't on fire.

I heard so many mixed reviews about this place but still was excited to give them a try. I called the number on their Instagram and was a bit taken aback by the rude staff member I had an encouragement with (which should've been a sign) they weren't willing to explain some of the menu options I had questions about; which surprised me because as a business trying to get its feet off the ground you should be willing to go beyond measures for any potential customer but I guess that's what you get when you hire just anybody it seems. I decided to hold off and come give this place a try on another day. Upon my arrival around 6pm I went to open the door and the lights were on, plenty of staff on in the inside but the doors were locked. Now obviously this means they were closed; but I do think it would've been appropriate for someone to come greet me at the door and let me know that as they all starred at me from the inside. I'm a bit disappointed because I definitely wanted to give this place a try as I live in the neighborhood and ride past it all the time but I can't say I feel welcomed to come back here so I'm definitely in no rush to return! May possibly update my review if I do come back (who knows)!

Service is extremely sloooooooowwwww.

But if it's late night and you're looking for some grub, you don't have very many other options.

Food was ok - had a breakfast sandwich and the biscuits with sausage gravy.

Don't waste your time coming here before closing.  Was in area and our Hungry family of three asks Siri for best place close by; Torys comes up we get there at 20 minutes to noon and were turned away.  I  supporting the small business, but this is a recipe for failure.  Can't tell how the food is;  looked generally clean. But seriously the restaurant was decently full and we know it doesn't take long to make a few egg dishes and serve coffee.  Very disappointed, we're the type that would have come back to the hidden gems.

Love coming to Murrietas for authentic food! The family who owns this restaurant is so kind, and actually Mexican compared to some places. The bartender Mona was a catch. I basically let her order for me, because being a vegetarian who doesn't like cilantro, Mexican restaurants are kinda tough to order at. But they make things special for me all the time! Try the mushroom and cheese enchiladas, omg! All I had to do was ask! Plus the raspberry marg was to die for! Great family place, be nice and tip well! They deserve it!

We picked this hotel due to the photos and the ability to walk the beach anytime we wanted. The location is great and if you're looking for something on the quieter side of the water, this is your place. 

A convenient trolley shuttle comes by the hotel and will take you to the nightlife area, but makes sure you're aware of the last stop/run or be ready to Uber back. The hotel also offers a nice restaurant to stay-in, if you decide you need to relax and also a bar if you need to get your drink on before going out. An ideal place to hold smaller conventions and work outings. 

Not too much within walking distance unless you don't mind walking over the bridge to the other side. If it's beach and parks type of vacation, this is great. If it's nightlife, it works well too. 

The place does have some downfalls for the price though. Also, John at the front desk did not welcome me as a Titanium member and I felt like I was inconveniencing him when I checked in. For staying over 100 nights a year at their hotels, Marriott and SPG should train their front desk employees better and to appreciate those that spend 1/3 of a year at their properties.

I generally like this place.  It's got a nice vibe, interesting food which is healthy and reasonable prices.  But in 2 out of 3 of my visits, they waitstaff has messed up.  Once they brought me someone else's bill.  The next time they brought me a dish I didn't order (I ate it, didn't like it, and didn't realize the error til I saw the wrong item on the bill) and they also charged for something we ordered that they didn't bring.  So the lesson is to check  your bill carefully and make sure the server is really "getting it" when you order.

Super good! We were only in New Orleans for three days and this was the first place we tried and the best place we tried on almost the whole trip! Food so good we ended up here a second time. Also nice way to beat the heat and the crowds without having to walk too far.

Fair food at a Fair price but we won't be back. Not that it's terrible but there are so many better options to spend your money on now. When there's no wait for brunch and the opposite corner of the intersection at Germantown Cafe it's 45 minute wait...it says a lot.

This is a restaurant that I really want to like. I actually prefer it's Hot Chicken over many of the traditional places you get it like Prince's, Pepperfire or Hattie B's because it's not as heavy a crust as the other places. My wife has gotten sick twice, once on a fish entree and a second on a roasted half chicken that they had at the time. This doesn't make our cut as places we would go back to.

The latest visit we ordered Bloody Marys to start our brunch and got the kids order in. Unfortunately they've taken Chicken & Waffles off the menu (which they hadn't on their website) so we were left with home fries and biscuits to keep them happy.
I'm not sure of the mix they used but there were way too much tomato pieces - like what you would get once you're down to the bottom fourth of a Zip Zap mix bottle.

We started with the deviled eggs and when we've been there in the past they've all been distinctive but this trip they all tasted relatively the same. The disappointment continued with the rest of the meal - the home fries were from a Sysco bag, the Hot Chicken was lacking on slaw to offset the heat, and the server was pretty inattentive and slow; including bringing the order of biscuits covered in gravy - which was not what we asked for her to bring the kids. We ended up getting a second order of plain biscuits for them. My wife's Shrimp n Grits was ok - it wasn't great like "Wow you gotta try this honey" type of Shrimp n Grits.

I don't know that Silo has much tenure - I know they have a solid late night crowd on the weekends that may keep it alive, but with so much coming into Germantown let alone the Gulch and SoBro I think Silo is going to find itself on the wrong end of Nashville's restaurant quality spectrum.

As a bar I would give Coop's 4 stars and as a restaurant I would rate it 1-2 stars.

It's a dive-type place in the French Quarter which I really liked. The lady running the place had a personality and a bit of an attitude, which I liked but others may not. The drinks and food are cheap compared to other places in the area.

Unfortunately the mixed drinks we ordered were not very good and the food was the most bland that I had in all of New Orleans. Everyone at our table got the Taste Plate that comes with Seafood Gumbo, Shrimp Creole, Cajun Fried Chicken, Red Beans & Rice with Sausage, and Rabbit & Sausage Jambalaya. The fried chicken was good and wished I had just ordered that alone. The red beans & rice were not good and I only ate half of them. The shrimp creole had one single piece of shrimp in it. The gumbo and jambalaya were just okay. At least the prices were good.

I would recommend coming here for the bar if you want a few drinks in a dive but not to eat dinner here - or just get the fried chicken.

A sushi spot in Armature Works. I've eaten here numerous times, and each time has been A good experience.

I haven't eaten a lot of sushi in the last six months since I cut carbs from my diet, ( I don't eat rice)...but I love sushi!  This is one of the few places that will customize your order to fit your dietary needs. They suggested wrapping the roll in seaweed instead Of rice. Awesome!!

This time I had the Shrimp Volcano roll...
Tuna, Cream Cheese, Cucumbers, Avocado, Sweet Eel Sauce, Spicy Aioili Sauce, Topped with: Tempura Shrimp, Spicy Mayo, Scallion, Masago... amazing!

Also had the miso soup... it was ok. 

Overall it's a good atmosphere, fair prices, tasty food... Definitely would recommend.
You kidding! It's a Drury. Rooms have been redone, great location to visit all the Nashville hotspots.

Hands down the best po'boy I have ever had. My boyfriend and I went there with high hopes that the hype that we heard about this place was true. We were definitely not disappointed. From the minute we walked up the stairs into the building the smell was unbearable. It smelled so good and made our mouths water and stomachs growl. We both ordered the 6" roast beef po'boy. When the sandwiches arrived they don't look like much but with that first bite we decided you can't judge a book by its cover. The meat was so juicy and tender and so amazing. Everything about the po'boy was amazing from the crusty bread to the broth it soaked up from the meat. The place was clean and not very busy (even though it was dinner time on a Sunday) but they warned us that they were short staffed so it would take a while for our food to be served. It ended up not taking very long. Our only regret about the whole experience was that we ordered a 6" instead of a 12".

I really enjoy the food from Tijuana Flats. It's a place where you put in your order, then sit down and they deliver your food. I've had burritos, tacos, and quesadillas. Everything is extremely customizable - they have low fat cheese, fat free sour cream, whole wheat tortillas, and you can have anything covered with cheese sauce, red sauce, or salsa. The food always tastes fresh and the service is fast.

I used Cherry Bomb for a networking dinner by Delta Sigma Pi at the Student Union for the first time, both as an individual and for the event. First off, they were super flexible with the price point we created. The office team was very responsive and patient with all of the changes we went through before we had the package finalized. As for the event, the team was super efficient and thorough with what they brought. As the event planner, the catering went so smoothly I barely had to think about it, which was awesome! I was impressed with the menu I got to choose the salad, sides and main entree from. We went with the sage chicken with butternut squash stuffing, mac and cheese, apple walnut salad & quinoa pilaf. It seemed like everyone loved the food! Even though there wasn't a vegetarian/vegan main entree, there were definitely options to choose from in the sides & salads. Overall, very impressed with Cherry Bomb for their execution, preparation and food.

My family and I have been going to Tony's since the early 70's.We trust Tony and his mechanics to fix/inspect the car right the first time.  We have never been disappointed.  He tells it like it is (for example, you won't have to replace those tires this year but you will next).  I trust them implicitely to do me, my budget, and my car right.  I have lived in Center City for 25 years, and I still take my car to Tony's for everything.
I've read people complain about his location.  A couple of buses stop right across the street; they can take you east or west.  If you are industrious (and I have done this in the nice weather) you can pack a bicycle and ride the bike path home.

Gordon was so good and so nice I am writing my first review on Yelp.  I  checked Yelp for a locksmith when I needed someone to come over right away.  Gordon's 5 star rating is accurate and well earned. He came on time and got everything fixed. I forgot to have my mailbox rekeyed so I called him back.  He didn't charge me for that or for spraying/fixing a sticky front door lock.  Super nice guy.  This is the guy you want!
We love this place. It's very close to our house and the food is always pretty good and affordable
I was in Santa Barbara for a day and had some of the best ceviche I've ever had here. Loved it.

They have very good selections of different things for almost all pets although depending on the item things can get very expensive. If your pet needs a new toy I would still recommend coming here. It is right off of Frank Scott Parkway.

Very professionally kept gym.  The locker room is that of a country club, with steam room, sauna, personal showers, and oak lockers (more than enough).  The entire staff is very friendly and helpful, I even got pointers on a few exercises from the staff as they were off work and just going through their own work out.  3 huge class size rooms, for anything from Spin, dance, yoga, pilates, tia chi, you name it PEAC has it.  Great all around experience with these folks for 7 months, (even call you to wish you a happy birthday, just in case everyone else forgets!).  Only setback is the price, however they do offer some specials from time to time.

So glad to see this place opened. Visited here on a chili friday night without any reservations. So didn't have a chance to direct interact with the little cuties. Still had a great time. The staffs are very welcoming and knowledgeable. I can tell they are very committed enthusiastics. They hand stamped all the cup protectors and you could leave your own art with your paws. They have special tea blends with creative names and smell really soothing.  

Fun suggestions, what about creating a unique ID profile for each individual cat? What is the breed, name? what's his/her story ? What does he /she like or dislike? Then stick them outside of the glass windows and posted on website. 
What's more? Create forum/comments column on the website to gether a community. People can share their sweet/ crazy /touching stories about their memory of cats.

Bonus, it is a fantastic place for people watching and meet new friends! Highly suggested for both cat or cat-urious people.

I frequent Panera Bread restaurants for a quick lunch from time to time, and find the food to be pretty good except at this one.  The best advice I can give you is STAY AWAY FROM THIS PLACE until they begin to appreciate your business.  The portions at this Panera bread are half the size should be.  We ordered a salmon salad, and a chicken sandwich.  The bowl of salad was half full and there wasn't enough chicken salad on my sandwich to feed a toddler!  I brought this issue to the managers attention and was told they follow stricken portions.  The fact that the Panera, 2 miles down the street, serves appropriate portions and always had a line out the door was lost on this manager.  Her comment was " their food cost is too high" !
Seated fast and our waitress got our drinks out to us fast. Food took awhile to get out which is weird cause the place wasn't even half full. Food wasn't bad but nothing great either.

Found cigarette butt in Mongolian beef and I don't smoke!  Nasty!!

Ordered also sesame chicken and shrimp with mixed vegetables

This room is small but really nice and modern. The bed is all white and super fluffy and clean looking and so comfortable.The room was listed as a city view I wouldn't call it that, but the only really disappointing thing was that there was no spa anything. No soap, no shampoo, no lotion, for the price...
Spent a little time at the bar, Fantastic bartender. Also ordered room service, never done that before, food was, well, check the review for Amuse!
If I'm ever in Philly again and can afford to stay here, I wont think twice about it.

In short, the food here is a hit and miss. I would've given it a 3 but because of exceptional service from a waiter named Ryan I decided on a 4 star. First of all, the restaurant is gorgeous. You feel like you're in a high end bar on a cruise once inside. The place is also very spacious. It was our first time there and I asked a million questions, which were all answered with upmost enthusiasm. I never had raw sushi before but because of Ryan's recommendations, I did. I was not disappointed! I had royal roll and it was amazing. The sushi were very fresh and tasty. We also ordered salmon and steak with lobster and scallop hibachi. We opted for fried rice instead of white rice. Unfortunately, the hibachi was nowhere hear as good as the sushi. The lobster, shrimp, steak and fried rice were dry, and I mean wood-dry. The fried rice were near inedible. It tasted like old frozen rice, it was the worst I've had anywhere. The lobster and shrimp were so hard they might as well be plastic. The steak was way over done. However, when I complained about the steak, the waiter was extremely polite and made sure we had another portion immediately, it was perfect the second time around. The salmon hibachi on the other hand, was one of the best I've had. The salmon is crispy delicious yet moist and fresh in the inside, very yummy. Again though, something else prevents me from truly enjoying my entree, the sautéed vegetables tasted spoiled. If anything, don't order the hibachi but then again, the restaurant is called Arirang hibachi and steak. The whole experience was good thanks to the service and atmosphere. The price and food however, made it impossible to truly recommend. We told the restaurant about their hibachi, we will give it another try. Hopefully it will be better next time.

Awesome brewery in NOLA always on my list when we are in town. I love the laid back vibe to go with the great beers. Always a nice selection of beers on tap with plenty of beers to go. I love that the have indoor and outdoor seating, I like that they have a food option form local food vendors. I also like that it's close to all the action in the city. I always make a stop when I'm in town!

Simple menu which made lunch very easy. My friend had Meatloaf sandwich which I tired and it was delicious! The Sweet Potato fries were also incredible! Crispy, moist, and flavorful. I opted for the Lime Cilantro Chicken Salad. It was also delicious. Different flavors fissioning in your mouth. We both had beers, Stella Artois and Blue Moon respectively. Overall the food was full of flavor and I was impressed considering we were dining in a department store. I would've given it four stars, but the wait service was not very impressive.

If you love paying a company money with absolutely no assistance or coverage back to your claims submitted, then Pets Best is the company for you. 

I have submitted six claims to this company and for every single claim, there is always some small nuance that excludes my pet from coverage. 

At this point, I've basically just handed them over $300 for zero coverage. 

Choose another company and stay away from this one!

Here is a trick also, when I tried calling to cancel my policy by selecting it from the phone menu option I was on hold for 30min. I called back and selected new customer and someone answered within three rings. 

They just want your money.
Pete is the best ever! Love this place, love their beer! The prickly pear is absolutely delicious!

Zoe was warm, comfortable, and professional.  I have severe arthritis and was suffering painful knees and walking difficulty. When the massage ended my pain was gone and remained gone for a good two weeks.  Amazing.  I am going to be a monthly customer.  She knows her stuff, believe me!

Dang! Took a big group and here's what we had - with reviews.  Grouper cheeks,  fish spread,  peel & eat skrimp, and conch fritters-- great x4.  Grouper sandwich(s) grilled,  blackened and fried -- all great!  Soft shelled crab blt w/ onion rings- - great/great.   Grouper reuben- great. Lobster corny dog- holy crap!  Key lime pie- yes!  Deep fried KLP- heck yes!  Cronuts-dammit boy!  Deep fried Oreos-.   Small- divey-really good!  Overall- - only because that's how many hands I have.

I had never used Wendy's Flowers previously, but I am certainly a fan, now.  On the way to visit our new grandchild at the hospital, I called Wendy's to see if they had "Baby Boy" arrangements.

They asked when I needed the arrangement.  I told them that I would be passing their business in 20 minutes.  They told me that they would have one ready with my special request of curly willows and hydrangeas.

It was in the cooler when I arrived, and it was beautiful!

Thank you, Wendy's floral designers!

Had some friends visiting from out of town and took them to the dandelion for lunch. We came before the lunch rush and were able to get a table in the back. This was the perfect place for lunch. We got sandwiches and salads and everything was reasonably priced and delicious. A great lunch spot in philly

I purchased two seats for a 5:00 show, price is too high for what you get. I was in the Cobb Theater section which is below the CineBistro. The seats are the most uncomfortable seats I have ever been in. I've been able to enjoy the full relaxation of a lazy boy type recliner and food service at another local theater for what I paid here. I won't be back, which is a shame given the location, but I'd rather drive a little further and enjoy my experience.

We ate here for the first time last week and had a great experience. I love what they've done with the decor inside. It's very fresh feeling as well as homey  with lovely green tile and exposed beams.

We had great service. Our waitress really helpful and knew her stuff. She mentioned that they all had to try the menu so they would know what they were recommending, which I think is good practice, especially when you have some things on the menu that people haven't seen before. The food was good too. Here's what we got and my thoughts:

Cauliflower lahmacun - just okay, not much flavor
Hummus - wonderful, pita bread is to die for
Cat woman (drink) - very tasty
Carrot kibbeh - very interesting dish, good with the lettuce it comes with for the crunchy texture but not the best on its own
Baby mustard Greene & cress salad - this was amazing; dreaming about this salad; photo is terrible because we ate before I remembered to photograph 
Lamb kefta - very tender and well-seasoned lamb
Spiced hanger steak - good cook but very salty
Sesame and pistachio ice cream sandwich - did not like the flavor of the cookie but the ice cream was very good 

Overall I had a great experience here and will be back.

wow, what a great meal i had. The food is of great quality and tastes great! All the food is interesting  and flavorful and is great for sharing. I love the space and find it enjoyable and just a great experience overall. I can not believe that this place has only been open about a month. This will be one of the hottest places in philly for a long time to come!!! Must have the frog legs, Banh mi and the tuna tarter!!!
Excellent event! 5 miles of closed off roads, made the biking so fun and safe. I loved how there there lots of tents with vendors and music along the way. I wish this happened more than twice a year!

Friendly, helpful staff really go out of their way for you at Gateway. They have an excellent supply of plants, trees, and tropical plants in season. For me, Gateway's pond department has been an invaluable resource. Peg and Darlene are wonderful at diagnosing and fixing any pond problems you might (or in my case will) have.

What an awesome dinner out. Of course, it could be the company I was with, but regardless, the food and drinks were awesome. 

The wait was longer than estimated by the host stand, but we were so caught up with some red wine and the fireplace that the time flew by. When we were finally seated, we ordered some appetizers -- corn bread wedge and grilled wings. Oh man, both were so delish. I could have eaten a big plate of the corn bread with the butter for my meal. 

For dinner, I opted for the RedStone burger; my friend had the rigatoni bolognese. Both were so good and so perfect. The burger was exactly the way I like it cooked and the bacon was probably the best I've ever had. I sampled the rigatoni -- it had sausage in it, which gave it a nice flavor. 

For dessert, we shared the banana cream pie. To be honest, it was so big, I took most of it home. I should have known when they brought out a serving spoon with the plates that it was going to be big. It was so creamy and tasty with some caramel drizzled over it. It will be a great snack later tonight. 

Our server was excellent and attentive. He kept our drinks full and was able to make recommendations.
Agreed with all the many reviews before, lady's are by far from nice, adequate and comprehensive. No greet no receptionist.

Since I wrote this review originally they have added CNN to the available news stations, with that change this is great gym for the price. They still do have Fox on, however, which is not appropriate considering that the the facility has a integrated customer base, and Fox regularly throws dog wristles to racists. The equipment is in good shape and usually always works. The facility is clean. The staff is friendly and helpful.

It bothers me that the only news offering on the TV is Fox, which I don't want to watch. The conservative asshole who owns the place should stop being cute and just put on CNN. I was there during the Eagles game the other day, and they actually did not change the channel on any of the TVs so the game would be on.  Unbelievable.
One of my favorite! 5th and Taylor puts a great upscale spin on southern comfort food. Some of the best fried chicken and honey sauce I've ever had.

The atmosphere at this place is homey and fun. You can grab your own drink from the fridge and sit down to "sketch" a drawing to add to the covered walls. It's a small establishment with charm and good food. I love the fries and trying new sauces to dip them in. It is cash only, which I don't mind especially as they have an ATM.

Went on a Saturday afternoon to buy an Apple product. This store was not crowded at all, and the associate was not so easy to find. Once available, the staff was helpful and knowledgeable. This place looked big and had a good selection of electronic products!

Had a delicious meal tonight inside the restaurant! This restaurant is amazing and 1 of  Oro Valley's hidden Jems! Our server was excellent and all of the people here are very welcoming! It is like home away from home! Our favorite place too have a most delicious Mexican meal! Thank you too all of the workers here for their excellent service! We will always be coming back! Our server was amazing! Maddie is amazing! Laura is amazing as well! Alejandro was our server and he went above and beyond! Thank you all for our amazing meal when times have been so tough! Theresa

Went into David's Bridal thinking we were "just looking" as I was only in town for a couple days and we didn't have an appointment. The store personnel were warm, friendly, and helpful. We were assigned the most incredible Bridal Consultant around...Dana! She listened to what my daughter wanted and with her extensive knowledge of the stores inventory of dresses was able to match Alex with the dress of her dreams. Dana's exuberance and love of what she does made for a fun, rewarding experience. Dana is a great example of what happens when people work at a job they love...they do it well! The newly hired store personnel who were shadowing her that night got a lesson in what it takes to be a great bridal consultant!

First time here sat at the bar on Sunday To watch some of the games. We ordered a few drinks all
We're good , favorite was No.62. All of their juices for the drinks are made fresh daily and all bar tenders working were very helpful. Keep the drinks and food coming as we ordered. We got there at 15 min before they opened and they let us in to sit down while They finished setting up. We ordered the Koren Fried Chicken and smoked fish dip . Chicken had good flavor And the order comes with 3 so since it was me and my wife we had them
Add another leg to the order so we would each have 2.  We dove in and did not photos of them but did get some of the Next items.It was not spicy, I would have preferred it but understand that spice isn't for everyone . Smoked dip was good and Smokey surprisingly. Comes with some crisp bread to eat it with. Next we got pappardelle lamb pasta and meatball. This pasta was the best dish and sauce was spot on and brought it together well . The meatball was good size and juicy not dry at all. Comes with a traditional spaghetti sauce on it . Overall great and saw some
Other tables get the fried chicken livers, ribs, and flat breads. All looked great, being a Tapas place would recommend going with 4 people or so so you can try more of the menu. We will be back .

Fantastic!!! Had the 3 meat combo and tried the ribs (incredible and meat fell right off the bone), brisket (juicy and flavorful) and the chef recommended the jalapeño cheddar sausage (spicy, full of cheddar and amazing). This California man is HAPPY!

The hardest choice was picking a BBQ sauce...so I tried a bit of them all!

Great place to hear up-and-comers in the country music business.  I had the pleasure of hearing Bobby Pinson and Friends on a Saturday night.  Great time, even when the band blew a fuse and the lights went out. The food is average, but the music acts are great.

We were there last evening for a small Holiday gathering and not knowing what to expect I completely fell in love instantly. As I walked through the front courtyard with lounge seating I thought this is lovely. Through the door, we went and was welcomed by the laid back atmosphere. We met our friends and as I wandered over to the dispense your own wine section (which you don't have to partake in to enjoy the extensive wine selection) Pedro came over and help me understand the concept. You are given a card to start a tab then you choose the wine, in increments of 1, 3 or 6 ounces. This allows you to sample the many varieties without committing to a full glass. The next surprise was the awesome food selection provided by the neighboring restaurant, Michael's Grill which serves you in the Shoppe at your table. To top it off, there were two same laid lounge seating areas, in addition to the bar. The music was terrific and not overbearing so you could have a conversation without shouting. If you are looking for a Happy Hour, Special Event or just a nice evening out this is your go-to place.

And did I mention Pedro shared with me they are able to cater to your needs outside of normal business hours, now that's customer service.

One can of beer away from being a perfect meal.

To the folks at adsum: don't serve any beer in a can, even if you pour it in a glass. You're much, much better than that. 

Ok, that's out of the way...on to the food.

First course:

Homemade tater tots with whiskey & bacon, served over a whipped ranch sauce. So good. More of a latke consistency than a traditional frozen tot, luckily there were only 8 on the plate or it could have gotten out of hand. Clint Eastwood would be proud.

Biscuit with cheddar cheese and apple butter. All three things come together perfectly to make a perfect appetizer. I would have eaten an entire bowl of the apple butter if it were acceptable to do so. The biscuit was legit, as well.

Second course:

The adsum burger. I'm a pretty big burger snob, it takes quite a bit to impress me. Consider me impressed. This sucker was huge, perfectly cooked, and juicy. The cheese was perfectly chosen and complimented the burger the way it should have. The burger sat on an onion and pancetta fondue that was so tasty it could be served on its own as a soup. They also gave me a huge portion of crispy, salty duck fat fries. Good stuff.

Shrimp & Grits with dirty Andouille sausage. Wonderfully cooked grits (which are somehow surprisingly easy to screw up), spicy delicious sausage, and nicely prepared, tasty plump shrimp. Nothing to complain about here.

Dessert:

Chocolate chip & black cherry bread pudding with vanilla gelato. Actually couldn't finish it, the portion was so big. Still, the flavors were great. Nice to see that it wasn't overly sweet.

Bacon & Chocolate "Snickers" bar with Mexican hot chocolate. This is one nap-inducing dessert right here. So rich, you really should have help eating it. The caramel covered nuts on the side could easily have been their own dessert, they were that good.

Service was attentive and friendly, without ever rushing. The place is very small, and gets warm when it's crowded so dress accordingly. It's also a little on the noisy side, but they're busy...whaddaya gonna do?

Overall, I'll definitely be back.

You can find me and my mom here a couple times a month. 
We go specifically go to see Lillian and ONLY Lillian. She is the kindest woman and makes your feel like you are her most important client. 
Her quality is AMAZING every single time. I have found in the past that the service of the technicians can waiver but hers NEVER does. 
We are so grateful for her kindness and enjoy our conversations with her. She always asks us about our family and she remembers details about our lives that surprise us and shows us how much she truly cares. 

I had to take a star away for the owner of the salon and the new guy they have at the front desk. 
The manager is very unfriendly and served my mom on her very first visit and was terrible. 
The guy at the front desk, Sean, has NO idea what he is doing and should NOT be in a salon. A lady had asked him how long the wait is and he had no clue how long the services take to give her an idea. I had asked for a nail polish and he told me it didn't exist. I told him I would just tell Lillian, she new exactly what I was talking about and got it for me. He told me well "I learned something today" but he should have already known that. 

Lastly, the manager was doing pedis on some young clients and they had the $30 pedicure. She instructed her team to provide LESS than what is standard and completely took advantage of them since they are young and don't know better. For what they provided I would have expected the charge to be HALF but they completely took advantage of their parents and charge them $90.00 for 3 girls. Even young girls should be treated with respect. 

Again, we will continue to come specifically for Lillian but parents should be aware.

My family and I really wanted to like this place, as it is conveniently located down the street from us. The food isn't great, but its not terrible
