The service is absolutely awful. Not sure if the servers are mostly family members of the owner, but they look so annoyed to be doing there job and serving you. And heaven forbid you need something like extra napkins. That's just not appropriate to ask, I have no idea how this place is still in business. Everyone I ask about this place hates it. And say they will never go back.

Great food! If you're looking for some good food without the Nashville prices, this is the spot. We were looking for something quick and cheap to eat near our hotel in Joelton and found this spot on Yelp. We weren't disappointed. We had just come from passing a place listed on a "Cheap Eats in Nashville" review and it was basically a hut off the side of a highway. So happy for this good meal and good service in an area that's limited in restaurants. It's a quiet Thursday afternoon, and we were the only customers when we came in, but don't let that deter you from sitting down and giving this place a shot.
Simple car wash that was efficient with great service! Will definitely come back as it beats center city prices too!

The onion rings are the best in town! And you will never eat another corn dog after eating one at BDD. My husband loves the ice cream, while I don't eat sweets- I always take at least one bite of his soft serve. The burgers are great- they are big, one feeds me two meals and the fries are amazing too. Fond memories of going here on hot summer nights as my mother's home was on 53rd and we could walk there. You don't get Nashville til you sat on the side of Charlotte eating Bobby's....

First the entrance was good atmosphere with a nearby bar.  The tables to sit and cook your meals is exciting in itself a with small portions.  Note to all:  yes you cook your own food and that's what makes Korean restaurant unique in its own way.  Loved it enjoyed it and encourage more to try it out. 

For those, yes, they have soju!

We ordered 2 regular pastas a garlic cheese bread and 2 waters and it was $39. The garlic cheese bread had a ration portion of cheese on it and not an ounce of garlic flavour. The pasta portions were quite small for the price. Overall it was good food. The price was a turn off so I won't be back.

Every time my wife and I are in Nashville, we stop by the Listening Room for dinner, drinks, and live singer songwriter music.

When in Music City... you've gotta seek out live music. Our preference is for acoustic guitar with a single voice. It is here where you find those with stars in their eyes looking for their big break hoping the song they wrote for (insert county singer name here) will be picked up and transformed from graphite in paper into esteem and fortune.

Do yourself a favor and make a reservation for a night at TLR. This will help you determine which singers you want to see, on which night, and help ensure you get a spot close to the stage. 

We always enjoy the food and the mixed drinks but the real highlight is the local talent, which is simply oozing out of every crevice of this city.

This is the second time I've been here, and there will be many more.  The service is good, the food is delicious, and the pricing comparable.  I had curried duck, while my partner had shrimp pad thai.  We shared chicken wings.  Everything was absolutely delicious.  I had to pay extra for rice (which does not come automatically with curry dishes), which I thought was strange, but it was worth it!.

Love this restaurant!  Stop by on the way home and get something so I do not have to cook at home.  I have never had a bad experience here.  Only Mexican place I go now.  Stop by and you want regret it!  Again, love this place!

The Camden Riversharks is a great night out for anyone. This is the perfect atmosphere for a family wanting to enjoy a baseball game but not spend hundreds, a great hang out place for a group of friends, or even great for a cheap and different date night. 

The stadium is small and easy to manage. But at the same time, there are plenty of food options for everyone. They have everything from classics such as popcorn, funnel cakes and hot dogs to brand staples like Crab fries and ritas. Oh, and they also sell beer. 

Tickets are cheap and you can buy them ahead of time or on game day at the box office. Anywhere you sit you'll have a great view- just watch out for flying balls! Additionally the Riversharks stadium has a beautiful view of the city skyline and the Ben Franklin Bridge. 

The Riversharks staff does a great job at engaging the crowd, especially the younger kids. Between innings they host games on the field, walk around finley, and encourage cheering and dancing by giving away free prizes. The stadium also has play areas and other activities to keep your kids busy. 

Check out the Riversharks calendar online- they run tons of specials and events. They host different charities on Monday nights, have dollar dog/beer nights, fireworks on the weekends and they even have bark in the parks where dogs are allowed to attend the game. 

Parking ranges from $5 to $6 dollars, the lots are located just steps away from the stadium. Don't be discouraged by the location, as the part of Camden that hosts the stadium is well patrolled and well lit. 

Take me out to the ball game!

We didn't eat dinner here, but we sat at the bar and had a couple glasses of good red wine. The bartenders and the nearby waitresses were very friendly, chatting with us and answering our questions. It's always nice to go into a place and have the staff take time to say hello and be friendly. I travel with a large (German Shepherd) service dog who is always well-behaved, it was nice that all of the staff came over to say hello to her. A people-friendly as well as dog-friendly place. We will be back next year during the air races, and we intend on returning and trying out the menu.
This is an amazing location to have a nice dinner. You're literally on the beach when you're waiting for a table (they don't take reservations at the moment). The food is just alright for the price
We had a great breakfast and our server Abigail was awesome! Will definitely be back!

I'm vegetarian and I come here to meet with friends very frequently. The dry pot tofu is one of the best dishes I've ever had. the Sichuan peppercorns make this dish different and the spice level is really not that bad at all. It goes well with rice and Dan Dan noodles. This is the best location in terms of food and its just a great place to hang out. The bartenders are great and all staff are friendly. I've actually not even tried many other vegetarian items on the menu because it's hard to pass up on the dry pot tofu.

Service and food is horrible!  They messed up our order several times even though we were explicitly clear on what we wanted and the manager just gave my wife the "death stare".  The pork and beef brisket were both mostly fat.  It wasn't even edible.  The ice cream cone though was delicious!  We have eaten here before and for the most part was pleased but after last night that will be the last time!  Yikes good luck if you try it.

Huge Choice of Cold beer with Good price.
Staff was kind and friendly and carry to the car.
Welcoming crowd, and great selection of bourbons and ryes. Bartenders here know how to mix a great cocktail, or help you find just what you're looking for if you're on the fence.

Home delivery not very organized or professional.  I signed up on-line for the home delivery, after a week no one had called me so I called the store and got Bobby's number whom I called, after playing phone tag we finally spoke.  We got disconnected several times, he asked for my address and didn't know if it were on his route but he would drive by and see (google maps comes to mind).  He made me feel like I might be out of his way, it might be on his route or someone else's, my next step was to go to the store and fill out an application, then he asked for my address again since he didn't write it down.  At this point I felt like poor customer service was going to be par for the course and I decided to go to another cleaners.  4 years I've been a satisfied customer.
They are ready to help you out, even on a Sunday morning. Good service communications

Grabbed a quick breakfast here on a Friday morning. Very cute coffee shop. I had the spicy egg sandwich which was delicious. The perfect hangover cure. Brought pastries back which were also very delicious!!

Filled with character, Mellow Mushroom exudes greatness. Multiple facets to enjoy: Booths, Tabletops, and a full bar w/ ample TV selections. The complex spans a large area, featuring backyard picnic tables and room for activities & games galore.

Build-your-Own, or choose from a wide variety of house specialties. You can also choose the Half-and-Half option @ no ADDITIONAL CHARGE! The pizza is terrific, flavorful; maybe the crust was puffed out slightly more than desired, but it's designed that way.

***OVERALL, great vibe. Service felt impeccable, almost overwhelming, but that's not possible. Water levels never reached the bottom of my glass. BONUS POINTS. The experience in itself captures what any customer looks for in a bite to eat***

Now it's my fault for not realizing "hibachi" was the method of cooking vs. "hibachi style gas-heated hotplates are integrated into tables around which many people (often multiple parties) can sit and eat at once." So I was disappointed they just just cooked everything in the back. Just in case anyone else thinks the same thing.
 
But their food was really great and the lunch specials have a lot of food for a good price. I saw a lot of takeout being picked up, but we ate in, and it has a really nice, elegant ambiance. I got scallops, miso soup, salad with ginger, veggies and rice for about $13. Also got a shrimp tempera roll for the table which was big and everyone could have an enjoyable portion. The scallops were so big and delicious. Service was good although they didn't bring forks at all and we had to ask (especially for the kids, they have no chopstick skills) and we didn't get enough and ended up sharing. Are people really adept at eating rice with chopsticks? I guess they don't want to set out forks that no one uses, but I imagine people use them more for them to only be available on request.

So all the food was good and reasonably priced, so I would recommend this place, but don't get your hopes up if you want them to make that little onion volcano in front of you.

Well, I did what I usually try to avoid doing, and that was write a review after one visit because I thought I had something that needed to be shared. That thing was my concern about being stopped by 3 different people asking for money in the parking lot on the same visit. Since that trip about 5 months ago, I have been back a half dozen or so times and have not had any issue at all, although that was all during the winter months, so we'll see when the weather gets nice. I'm not going to guess at that though, so I'm increasing my rating and taking back my concern.

I'll once again say that this store itself is great. They have everything you could be looking for at a supermarket. It is very spacious and has a very intuitive layout that is easy to navigate. It seems like a larger store than any that I've been to in Indy, and never seems crowded. However, the parking lot can turn into a mess if you decide to go after work or during one of the busier hours. I think that they don't have enough parking spots, and getting into and out of it can be difficult with the traffic around the area, so it always seems congested. This is one of the stores that has a gas station, and I assume that putting that in is what choked down a lot of their parking lot. That's my only complaint though.
Wonderful!  Everything tasted as good or better as it did in Spain.  The owner made the bar and it is beautiful.  We'll be back

Casino Del Sol is a five star resort. My husband is a member of their Player's Club. You do get some great perks. Every month there is free play, a meal credit, usually free rooms and more. Then there are the invitation only parties. They are nicely done. This night we were celebrating Dia de Las Muertas. There were tequila drinks, champagne, wine, beer and a delicious street taco bar with three kinds of meat, flour tortillas, salsa, chips and guacamole. Free prizes included free play and two beautiful limited edition bottles of Patron. To me every bottle of Patron is a work of art. We didn't win the Patron but Jim scored $100 of free play. Fun evening.

Wow!! Alex is amazing. My daughter had a destination wedding in Tahoe City and Alex took time to come see us a year ago when we started planning the wedding and has taken my many calls the whole year.  He was so understanding when our numbers dropped to half because of the fires and never complained. The food was amazing!! And staff were very friendly and attentive! The wedding was a great success because of all the attention to details Alex put into our event.

Not just a cute concept but also super delicious and great for casually picking up a snack if you're in the area. What really earns if 5 stars in my book was their chocolate ganache cake that one of my friends had for her birthday party. It was extremely rich, chocolatey and absolutely addictive!

I really enjoyed my visit to the Laumeier Sculpture Park. I was in town for work and found I had some extra time on my last day before heading home. Decided I would stop by here and have a look, and I am so glad I did! 

The grounds are lovely. The particular day I was there was not all that great - it was cloudy and the air was thick like it wanted to rain, but never actually did. This was no mind, though, as the park itself shines on it's own whether a nice day or not. 

There are several walking paths throughout that lead you through the grounds. I recommend grabbing a map as the park is much larger than I had anticipated. A map will ensure you don't miss anything. 

It was easy to find and parking was plentiful. Definitely recommend.

Firstly, this place is cash only. However, there's an ATM inside though, so all is well. 

This place is super dive-y. The decor is delightfully tacky and clearly hasn't  been updated since this opening in the 50s. 

I ordered an omelette named after a man. (Billy's omelette maybe?) It was packed full of sausage and mushrooms and happiness. With it, I had hash browns (kind of bland)  and toast (beware if the buttery-ness) as sides. I also tried one if their biscuits because it just felt right. That biscuit was a warm and moist situation of epic tastiness. I was so satisfied with this meal.  

Our waitress wasn't great, but the food made up for it.

My wife and I really like Crepes, Etc and visited often when we lived within walking distance.  I was a little bummed recently that they took off our favorite omelet from the menu but I tried the Croque Monsieur which was decadent and awesome.  They get really busy but don't let that turn you off, we've never had a problem getting a table well before the food was ready.

I do have to say their espresso is abysmal, however their equipment is being operated it is not by someone who actually is aware of how to brew espresso which comes out as more a strong coffee and not an espresso as if it were brewed with one of those plasticy $50 "espresso" machines. I do not thing this is the case however.

My New Year's Day started off with a pleasant surprise. Actually, I first was disappointed when the restaurant I wanted to enjoy lunch was closed for the holiday. As I drove home from Kirkwood, grumbling all the way, I tried to think of  places closer to home that would be open. As I drove past Joey B's, their busy parking lot beckoned me . 

I was greeted and seated immediately by a hostess with, attitude. It was obvious she did not want to be working a holiday.  When my server, McKenzie approached  the table to my drink order, her attitude was just the opposite. She was cheerful, fun and made my first dining out experience of the new year a good one. 

As she fetched my Pale Ale, I read the burger section, since that is what I was craving. I really wanted my usual, the Jalapeño Burger, but decided to  try their Cowboy Burger. After all, it did win best burger in St. Louis Magazine. However, I went in a totally different direction when McKenzie returned with my brew and told me the daily special. It was a House Salad and Mac-n-Cheese (Baked). She told me it was Shell pasta and a blend of two cheeses; Cheddar and Provel. I almost said, "no thanks", but thought of my Mac-n-Cheese list here on Yelp and that I needed to be more adventurous in 2014. So I said, "Yes" and am glad I didn't go the way of  the burger. 

The House Salad was huge. It was a combination of Iceberg and Romaine lettuces. Normally I am not a fan of Iceberg, but being in a mix is okay. I choose their House dressing which is a creamy Italian. Major kudos to the kitchen for tossing the salad in an appropriate amount of dressing. When my Mac-n-Cheese arrived, it had spots of crispy cheese, where you can tell it had been baked or under the broiler. For me it is a nice touch to the presentation. The cheese blend was flavorful, I didn't mind the Provel being in the mix at all. It was served with two slices of Toasted Garlic bread. The serving was large, so I was only able to enjoy half of it at the restaurant. The other half and one slice to the Garlic Toast made a great lunch for work the next day. 

Chatting more with McKenzie after eating, we both confessed our dislike of Provel. Neither of us like it on our pizza or burger, but thought it was very tasty in this Mac-n-Cheese. I enjoyed it so much, I'm going to add it to my list, even if it is made with Provel. Keep in mind, this is a frequent daily special and is not always on the menu.

My meal was perfect, the staff was delightful, and the atmosphere is modern and charming. I had the fried chicken sandwich with serrano slaw. This was yesterday and I literally just had a dream that I went back and they were discussing taking the chicken sandwich off the menu. I had a fit! It was that serious in my dream. I also had the Dirty Water cocktail. Awesome. The pastries looked too good to pass up so I had a heated chocolate croissant for dessert. So delicious! I'll be back many, many times, whether it be for pastries alone or a pleasant and satisfying meal. I might just have to go back today to make sure the chicken sandwich is still on the menu ...

Best fried chicken, fried pickles and fried okra in Saint Louis. It is world famous chicken with a southern kick.  The chicken is always crisp, hot and juicy and the sides are decent. This place is as good the ones found in Memphis. The chicken is so good that my 10 year old eats three pieces and now loves okra.

For starters, I know it's new BUT, I cannot believe I am the only one in here at 12:30 on a Monday when lots of other places are closed! Try this place. It's cute! Has inside or outside seating. Ginger Margarita, it was the special today... Came with a straw hehe. Easy consumption. And it REALLY refreshing. Totally delicious. 

I ordered the crispy calamari roll because I LOVE calamari and have never really seen this combination in a roll before so  interested. It is very fresh tasting. The calamari taste is strong. Not hidden by other ingredients. I'll be back and bring the SO, he will like this place. Try them out. Reasonably priced for fresh ingredients!

Came here for lunch with a colleague.  Was not impressed.  Like so much of the food in the French quarter, the food was ok (mediocre) and extremely overpriced.  Lunch for two- ettoufe and jambalaya with 2 glasses of waters and no apps or desserts 50 dollars after tip.  The service was great and the place is quaint.  I wouldn't tell anyone not to come...but it doesn't make the 'favorite - spots' lists.

I went into this place yesterday afternoon because i was searching for supplies to start needle felting. Loop had everything i needed and i felt totally chilled out in the space. Although i'm not super into knitting i noticed they had a lot of neat yarns(very tempting..). the staff was friendly and seemed laid back. i was excited that they gave me a cute reusable bag when i made my purchase. i'm deff going back there to get some more colors of wool!

With the caveat that I've never had anything tailored or gotten any kind of professional sewing services before, I was very satisfied with Creator's Court. I had my boyfriend's vintage leather jacket re-lined for his birthday, and they did an excellent job. The tailor chose fabric in his favorite color and even meticulously preserved details like the original tag. For a small upcharge he also polished the leather itself, which was pretty worn, and it turned out great. I don't know what the norm is, but I found the price pretty reasonable for the quality of service and quick turnaround. I'd definitely recommend them, just be warned that they won't give you even the vaguest ballpark estimate over the phone until they've seen your item.
Food consistently good...the bar area is large and very comfortable. TV's at every angle. Great happy hour specials with $5 apps.

Let me begin by saying I've  traded vinyl records with them for over the last few years and in that time the amount of credit i have been given for my vinyl has slowly dissipated. Ive  traded the  prestine vinyl in either very good or mint condition. Today   was the final straw with them i brought in Led Zeppelin remastered 180 gram vinyl in mint condition along with Princes 3rd album also 180 gram in mint condition.  The new Mud crutch album also in mint and was also 180 gram vinyl plus a few other 180 gram of other artist like spoon and Nadda Surf. They offered me $9.00 in cash or $17.00 in credit . I look at the guy  and said are you fkng kidding ? He said they just scan the item they can't  make a  manual determination anymore . Thats is bullshit i will never trade anything again with them  there a ripoff . I suggest if your plainning to trade vinyl to go to an independent record store in the area theres plenty of them around they will defiantly be more fair with there offers .
This place is as clean as a whistle! Bright lights, safe feeling, and well kept. Ability to use a credit card is a plus! Will be coming back :)

New to the city, I was looking (and still am) for "my place." I came to Mode after some reviews on the internet.  The place is small, quaint with a parking lot (not something you get when you go downtown).  The people were nice, a woman (possible a stylist) was in there with her baby, although cute at first, I feel like she was distracting some of the other stylists.  I got my haircut here, I was looking for something a bit fashion forward, I have medium/long hair and wanted something different with the cut.  I got a good cut, but it was really just a clean-up of what I had before.  Also, I could see the back area which was messy with colors everywhere, not too professional looking.  Overall, an alright experience, friendly staff, but nothing to boast about.  Good price, I believe my cut was just around $35, possible even a few dollars less.  If you looking for something simple, clean, and for a decent price, this place would be good. If you're looking to go beyond that, I may suggest looking to another place.

this place is just okay.

Lots of different wash options; opt for the lowest on the totem pole ($14) - it's the best deal. If you pay for extras then you probably won't even get them and you won't really notice until you're already down the road on your way to your next meeting and you will feel pissed off. 

Oh and there are random birds in the waiting area. I mean, they're pretty and all but it's kind of weird.

Crest Cadillac Collision has had my car for five months. A simple left rear panel repair. But, due to careless, negligence work, they caught my interior on fire while welding. COVID crested am issue with getting parts. But, the parts would not have been needed had they not burned up my interior.  It gets worse. They called and said the car was finished. My inspection easily pointed out that they had not replaced the headliner molding which was melted and warped, molding on the door panel - same story, and the new  paint had flecks of dirt imbedded. The tried  very hard to convince me to pay them and take the car while waiting for  the additional parts to be received. I refused. Three weeks passed with no word from them. Today I get a phone call from AW Collision who now owns this business.  Again, waiting on parts with no ETA.  Wonder why Crest pushed so hard to get me to take the car three weeks ago?  Did they know they were going out of business?  Judge for yourself.

Summary: Okayish drinks (possibly due to Sips event), Great apps, Good service

CC's Sips event was coming to a close, and we wanted to try some of the places that would normally be cost prohibitive to go to, so 10 Arts Lounge got the bid.

The lounge looks very nice, located in the lobby of the Ritz Carlton Philadelphia. Huge room, wonderful dome ceiling, and towering Roman columns all make this palatial room have a great ambiance. Mood lighting just sweetens the deal.

None of the drinks were that great, as everything seemed watered down and very weak. Granted, this could have been because of the huge attendance for the Sips event, but it was still a bit off-putting. Drinks were 3$ for beers, 4$ for wines (Chardonnay and Cabernet), and 5$ for cocktails (one with vodka, the other with rum).

Nevertheless, the appetizers/food more than made up for it. The oysters (from what I heard) were fantastic, and are priced at 1.50 a pop (instead of their usual 3$). The pretzels (5$ for Sips, normally 8$) were warm, buttery, and delicious! Fantastic stuff, and the dipping sauces (spicy mustard, jalapeno jam, and nacho cheddar) that went along with them really sealed the deal. The jam was a tad too sweet for my liking, but the fiery kick at the end (from the jalapeno) definitely made me appreciate it. Our order of fries (5$ for Sips, normally 8$) was predominantly crunchy and awesome, and was seasoned perfectly. Sadly, they were served with a simple ketchup.

While definitely pricey at their normal rates, the prices during Sips were a lot more manageable and we got away with our wallets relatively unscathed. Service was good as well, and while a little slow, it was certainly to be expected with the volume of people that our poor server had to deal with. If anything, my only gripe with the service was that they should add 1-2 more floor attendants to expedite the cleaning of tables as they free up.

All in all, it was a great experience, and I'll be sure to come back some time in the future to try them again (hopefully for another special event)!

Cute spot, easy walking access if you're in that part of jenkintown for any reason. My husband had to stop somewhere briefly for a call while we were out having appointments and this was perfect. A little coffee, a "groothie" the blue green smoothie was sublime, fresh pastry, great small business and local brands of yogurt available, totally made my morning. Staff was sweet and polite, a nice calm vibe overall. Definitely coming back to try some other great things.

I stayed at this location for four days and I can easily say it was not a pleasant experience.  Apparently I was lucky enough to be staying during a time when some huge pee wee hockey event was going on and my room was smack dab in the middle of this group.  Needless to say there were kids running up and down the halls at all hours of the day and night bouncing balls all over the place.  By the time I realized this I was already unpacked and didn't feel like getting my room changed.  I maned up and dealt with it.

On top of that the hotel was going through renovations and I have to say the lowest bidder truly won this bid.  My room was newly redone but when it was wired the outlets were never moved so there was cords running everywhere on the floor against the walls.  The charging station on my wall where I plugged my iPhone into was held on by two screws not even using anchors so it fell off the wall when unplugging my phone cable.  The bathroom shower was not working correctly, I needed to turn into the hulk to pull the stopper up to start the shower mode.

The biggest kicker was the room where the ice machine used to be.  The wall was totally covered with mold and I ventured in there because I saw a room off to the side and what was in that room?  Our towels!  Nothing like moldy towels to start the day off.  What is even worse two days later I walked by the mold room and noticed that the contractor painted over the obviously water damaged wall instead of replacing the sheetrock.  Insane!!!!

I would not stay here again.  Only reason I even put up with the place is that I got a good deal and it would have been too expensive to get another hotel.  The staff was nice at least and the people staying at the hotel was also very friendly.  I do enjoy the mid wast.

Side note*  My last day there the fire alarm went off and I had to grab all my stuff and head out of there.  I didn't want to chance not being able to get back in the building and missing my flight.

We orded the Potino and The vaqueiro Burger. Both of them was absolutly delicious. The chef checked on us several times and really cared about the food they were serving. We were coming home from a Special Event and that hit the spot! Thank you all the staff for this delicious food!

The waitress was sweet and knew what she was talking about. I asked her for her favorite benedict and she suggested the crawfish benedict, which I LOVED and enjoyed. My best friend got the chicken tenders with french toast bites. He is usually a picky eater, but he actually liked his food. Their iced coffee was good too.

Love everything about this gym. I felt my best when I was attending the cycling and hot yoga sessions. Although the monthly gym cost is pricey, I think it's well worth the money.  Free towels to use at the facility, amazing steam room, sauna, hot tub, free shampoo/body wash/conditioner, indoor and outdoor pools, and the locker rooms and equipment are clean! I currently do not have a membership with Lifetime due to COVID (still very cautious and would rather not be going to gyms), but plan on getting a membership once COVID resolves.

The most amazing food I've ever had - the cheesesteak bao is so incredible it makes absolutely no sense. Excellent cocktails, great service, and the ambience is so perfect for a nice night out. My new favorite restaurant. Amazing!! Thank you to Emma and Desiree for a great experience

Erica,

If you recall,  a question was asked of you regarding why the room space was unavailable if it had originally been set aside for an appt.  
We never asked for another dr there to see our dog , we asked WHO would be seeing him.  Still no response.  As new clients we had no idea how your clinic is run.  So yes, we had legitimate questions which you refused to answer!!!  Not a good sign!!!  As I explained to you, we had witnesses in the room hearing our side to the conversation.... per their recommendation!!  Never was there any "demanding" as you put it.  For you to accuse any client of that is highly inappropriate.  I did however ask repeatedly why we weren't allowed in, and got no response.  You kept changing the subject. 
Next thing...you said we were allowed inside.  The previous front desk employee said we weren't. Make up your mind!!  Are pet owners allowed in or not?!!  But please don't make up stories about clientele, new or not.  Its unprofessional to turn the story around on a client.  That does not bode well for  recommendations to others for heading to your clinic for their pets!  Again, our mistake for going there in the first place.  I certainly hope in the future you treat new pet owners who are attempting to set up appointments with more respect than you have shown us as well as  other past clients.   That's called good customer service.  We have learned we aren't the only ones who have experienced this at Clark!   
In addition, Dr. Emily was fantastic when we saw her, as were her 2 assistants that came into the room.  That day was a 180 degree turn from when we next spoke to you!  Hopefully you can iron out the problems !!  Good luck!

I love the fact that this place even exists here. Nestled next to crowded college bars and fast casual pizza/greek/indian places, exists an organic and trendy yet casual restaurant. I applaud their dedication to local and organic foods. With that said... the food is great!! 

The menu is simple and not overwhelming. Actually, I think they have more on their drink menu than food. But it's a great mix of types of food and ingredients. I've had the quinoa bowl, enchiladas, and goat cheese salad and a few of their specialty drinks. One word: chupacabra. 

The inside is a bit dark and small but it's got a great modern and simple feel. The outdoor area is great with new lounge style chairs. I was also surprised how many big screen TVs there are, they even have one outside in addition to the inside bar.

One of my favorite restaurants on University.

Ordered once through GrubHub for pick up and the foods were ready by the time we got there. Extremely fast service and pipping hot foods. 

Quality of the foods for the price is not quite there. 

Since it's our first time, we got a bunch of stuff to try to make sure there is something good for everyone, but everything lacked flavors. 1 Shanghai eggroll, 1shrimp eggroll, 1 Large Boneless spare ribs, and 1 Phad Met Muang. Everything tasted like a very highly Americanized Chinese joint. Everything is sweeter, greasier. The egg rolls are not worth $1.60/each, especially the Shanghai eggroll since it has only cabbage in it (it reminds me of the roll that Chinese restaurants usually give out for free as part of a combo meal to draw people in). Shrimp eggroll was a delicious in contrast. 

Boneless spare ribs tasted deep fried and drizzled with bbq sauce, and not the normal marinated char siu seasoning and grilled. 

The Phad met muang came out hot and at least had chillie flakes that made it spicy, which added some element to the dish. 

Overall a 2 star for quick service and pipping hot freshly prepared foods, but we won't be back.

Great Florida style restaurant on the water. Had appetizers, meals and drinks, and waiter was attentive and offered good suggestions the entire visit. Sat at a high top near the water. Lots of seating available near the back bar area even though parking and the front made it seem super busy. Strong cocktails- the waiter wasn't lying, and the Key West beer selection was good to. Nice fresh seafood.  As a visitor from the Midwest and a local- we both had a great experience.  Found a pearl in my oyster...that's pretty lucky right?

In my opinion a gas station is a gas station unless it's a QuikTrip. I definitely prefer to go to one of these if i have a choice because i know they train their staff to be really friendly, their bathrooms are always incredibly clean, and the food is good. The new QuikTrips now offer a kitchen where you can order fresh food options for lunch. It's nothing special but definitely better than what other convenience stores offer on their roller grills. This is a new location in Maplewood and is very convenient to my home. The only downside is you can't turn right onto Big Bend from the pump area you have to drive around the back of the building and wait in line to get back out.

Terrible customer service.  Our mini-fridge door was open when we checked in, flooding the floor beside our bed.  Our smoke alarm was also beeping because it needed a new battery.  When I brought up these two items before heading out for the day, we came back to the same problems.  

When you say you're going to take care of something, you do it.  One of the managers was standing right there when I brought these problems up but nothing was done.  Incredibly unprofessional.

For the price, this place is a dump.  This is a $100 or less hotel, not $190+ a night.

Weekdays or weekends, when it's lunchtime, this is my favorite place to go. I cannot speak to how fast the deliveries are, or what their whole pizzas are like, or the other stuff on the menu, but the $2.99 lunch special is pretty hard to beat. I always get the "Slice of the Week" and have never been disappointed.

The down stairs part seemed like it may have been cool for drinking and socializing, but we were recommended the second floor of this place for a place to dance. I kid you not, except for the light coming up from the stairway it was pitch black. Very creepy. The music was horrible and you need to push through the narrow dance floor to get to the bar which is in the very back. It had a very frat party vibe.

If you are in Reno, this is a most to go to restaurant. It is locally owned. Their fresh bread is a signature and the bakery is addicting. They have great breakfast/brunch menu. I'm usually there for their breakfast. I am not sure about their lunch menu though I heard it is as delicious as their breakfast. Highly recommended
I ordered pad see ew and sticky rice with mango. The food was REALLY tasty and the service was great as well. I highly recommend this restaurant!!

I bought a 4 person groupon for a 3-course menu, and dined here with my foodie family (incl. 2 teens) for an earlyish Sunday dinner.  Overall, i have to say it was a "meh, with potential"... I wish i could give it a **+ .  The baguettes and EVO were very good.  The first course- a pulsed (more substantial than puréed) green & red gazpacho-was not only beautiful looking but deliciouswith fabulous fresh flavors, topped with a dollop of basil gelato that added a creamy touch of sweetness.  (5*). The second course offered a choice of 4 pasta dishes.  We chose the spinach manicotti and the artichoke risotto.  The manicotti was 3 homemade, delicate pasta tubes lightly filled with ricotta cheese, served with a flavorful tomato squash ratatouille-like sauce... The fresh pasta was wonderful, but the chunkiness of the squash did not pair well with the delicate pasta texture, IMO.  (2 1/2*). The risotto was over salted and underdone, and the fresh artichokes were not entirely tender (pointy leaves left on), which is too bad b/c the flavors and textures  could have been great.  (2*).  The entrees we tried were also a mixed bag.  DH had the "baseball cut" steak, which was Outstanding...a home run (5*).  DD1 and I both had the fish (sea bass), pan seared with thinly sliced fresh truffle on top, sitting on puréed beets and cornmeal coated/fried tomato slices.  This could have been another great dish, but the fish was under /un-seasoned (I never ask for salt, but did for this), and the tomatoes and cornmeal were mushy.  I loved the fresh truffle, but some truffle salt was needed.  The pureed beets (which I loved alone) did not add anything except beautiful red color, to the blandness of the (albeit very fresh) fish or the mushy cornmeal coated tomatoes.  (2*).  DD2 had the roasted eggplant caponata stack....a dish with so much potential, that was simply disappointing.  The eggplant was not completely cooked (undercooked eggplant is like eating a dry sponge)- especially disappointing since we were the only diners at the time,  but the sauce was otherwise very tasty.  We didn't have time for dessert.  I think there are some kinks to work out with the menu, while some of the dishes are already clear hits.

The service was wonderful. We were greeted at the door and our server Justin was very kind. All I really need to say is, order the chargrilled oysters. They are amazing. I was worried that they would lose their brininess, but they didn't. The briny nature of the oysters with the buttery topping is delicious. Oyster lovers, I hope you enjoy !! The only thing I didn't like wasn't about the food, it was that the restaurant got pretty smokey because of the grill for the oysters, but bottom line, it didn't bother me too much. If you're easily bothered by smoke (asthma etc), ask to not be seated by the grill :)

I brought my car in to Pep Boys for an oil change as I have done many times, with several different cars in the past. Most times have been without incident. 

I got a call that my car needed brakes, rotors and one of my stress was leaking. Considering the cars history, this didn't strike me as crazy. My normal mechanic wasn't available, and the car need the work so I agreed. 

I was told a pick up time of 1pm.  At 2:45 I called and was told it would be ready in 30 minutes. I was dropped off at 3.  After some time I was told they forgot to do my alignment, and I just had to wait a bit.  After 4:30, my car was finally done.  The manager TC apologized for the wait and offered a free oil change.

The next time I drove the car, I noticed that it constantly sounded like I was bottoming out. I called first thing the next morning, and was told to bring it in. 

When I arrived, the manager Brian barely spoke a word to me as he took my keys. I called later that day after missing a call and spoke with TC who told me there was an issue and they resolved it. 

When I came by, TC wasn't there. A few other mechanics were with Brian behind the counter. They were all friendly except Brian who didn't even say a word as he gave me my keys.  No apologies, nothing for my time or money in taking a Lyft to drop off and pick up my car. 

I understand mistakes happen. But the way Brian behaved shows he's more irritated by customers rather than happy for business. 

At this point I'm not convinced the car is driving properly and I'm likely to take it another mechanic for a second look.

Edit:

A few updates:
1. Second garage did verify that PB did the work correctly.
2. Brain called me quite upset that I called him out. He claims he did apologize for the issue and he checked the tapes.  I asked to see them, but with another manager like TC. He told me they would be in touch and I have heard nothing. 
3. Brian told me he would have offered me a Lyft, they have a contact with them, I just had to ask. I had no idea this was available, so I'd have no way to ask.  Brian also offered me and my wife a free oil change.
4. I have heard nothing from PepBoys customer service or Brian when I was told I'd hear from them.

Dropping my review by a star based on this follow up. (or lack thereof)

Recently visited this coffee shop and I really enjoyed the atmosphere. I had a latte and a pastry and I have to say I was more than satisfied. The only negative thing I have to say is the wifi is sort of slow but I guess that's what you get when multiple people are using it at the same time. Over all I would definitely come back again and brig people with me.

We purchased a coupon for a regular oil change and they made my wife upgrade another $ 35.00 on top of what we paid.  She not knowing about this stuff paid it.  

THEY TOTALLY LIED TO HER AND THEY RIPPED US OFF, THIS PLACE IS A TOTAL FRAUD.

WE WILL NEVER GO BACK THERE AGAIN.

Could have gotten a regular oil change for less than $ 35.00.

I just moved here and I am looking for a good donut place. Not much of a selection when I went. They didn't even have a plain glazed donut! All I really wanted was a chocolate long john with whipped cream in the middle not the Bavarian cream middle, which was really only on the ends not all the way through it. I gave it a try, not good! Well my search will continue!

Management needs to get this place back on the rails. We had one or two decent experiences here, but the last two have been comically bad. I have found myself looking for the hidden cameras the place is such a joke.

Two visits ago, the woman working the register answered the phone while I was waiting to place my order. She proceeded to ignore me for 5-10 minutes while she took a large catering order. My family plus two other customers waited over 30 minutes for our food while the kitchen/manager decided to prioritize the catering order.

We debated not returning after this experience but decided to give the place one more chance. Today we ordered two gyros with feta. The food arrived with what was clearly bleu cheese- not feta. The topper here was that rather than admit they had made a mistake, they argued that it was feta cheese. I've been eating bleu cheese and feta cheese for 30 years...I KNOW THE DIFFERENCE. Don't insult me.

We will never be back.

Good place to look for bargains  don't really like how they throw glassware in the bins can be dangerous. . If the other goodwill stores would price their items better there wouldn't be broken china dolls with 19.99 price tags in the bins.  Everyone is very nice that works there.  Customers at times are very rude , when the bins are changed. It's every man for themself.

Came in with my family on vacation a few weeks ago. The owner's personality was as impressive as the pizza. He hooked my daughters up with some
Cake wile we talked about the incredible art inside the shop. The calzone stole the show though, a single tear dropped with each bite.
Always a warm welcome when you walk in the door. Large variety of all kinds of sporting goods.

I ran over to this place this afternoon because I was in need of getting a fed ex out before cutoff. The gentleman who helped me was AMAZING!! So kind, so helpful, and super cool. 
He took care of what I needed and also did a favor for me that he didn't need to do. I will absolutely use this place for any shipping, packing, etc going forward. It's so unusual to encounter people willing to help others and to do it with kindness. 
Thanks a million for helping me today!!
Use yelp get a salad glad I used app. Waitress is  place is clean. Hope this is an awesome end to a bad day try to edit after eating but great so far

A high-end red sauce joint that tries ( and succeeds to some extent) to reproduce what you had at home if you grew up in an Italian-American household. In this day and age where cooking seems to be a special-occasion activity most would consider it fabulous. All I can say is my Mom's sauce (and mine) is way better. But if this is what you want in Philly, you likely will do no better, so suck it up and eat! The portions are extremely generous, and you will take food home unless, like me you are a glutton. I had the Sunday Gravy which is served any day of the week don't you know and it was HUGE. I saw couples sharing an order of the spaghetti & meatballs for example. I had the Sunday gravy and the sausage was very good, as was the meatball and beef. I'm sorry to say I ate it all! (It was in the moment). My wife and our guest had the spaghetti and loved it.

One side note: There is dining on an enclosed (and more importantly-COVERED patio which is great, otherwise it's gloom and doom in the usual under-lit restaurant.
They wanted 48 dollars for a pint of gesso. Amazon had it for 18. Things I needed that they said on the phone were in stock were not in stock after spending 45 minutes just to find parking. Sad.

My wife went to No 7 and is not a fan...50 bucks for a pedi and they scheduled her with someone who it appeared had never done one before.  The person she worked with was very nice, but perhaps No 7 should of shared her inexperience with my wife in advance, especially for that cost.  She was hoping for more of a "spa" experience, but instead got more of a strip mall experience...even their soaking tubs were just basically sinks, no whirlpool/massage element at all.

We have used Action Roofing several times and find them to be excellent. Jack Martin the owner really is on top of everything.  He cares about his customers. He comes out for estimates and then checks on the work his crew has done.
High class and top notch outfit.

Andy Gault
Santa Barbara,.

This is a nice spot in the neighborhood for quality manicures and pedicures at a fair price.  The staff is friendly. Lin, who did my nails, couldn't have been any nicer. She did a wonderful job on my mani and pedi! I will definitely be back.

The other reviewers are right about the decor and prices. (poor decor and great low prices.) I went on the recommendation of a friend who has been going for 4 years. Kim did my nails. I noticed that they lasted longer than my manicures have been lasting lately. The cuticles still look good 2 weeks later after the polish is gone. I'm going back today.  I insisted on Kim and now I've read the reviews and find that that's consistent. Yan is also recommended.  I saw men there, but no one was sleazy. The kids were well behaved.  I just chalk the presence of kids to "immigrant parents:  summer camp for their kids is on the premises of their small business".

First time at The Love and will come again. Love the decor of intimate seating alcoves and framed cork board ceiling to mute the noise, and create private conversation space. The cocktails: Almost Famous and the Very Good Bourbon Drink were excellent. Also excellent were the appetizers of the cheese board, crispy duck and poke of salmon followed by spaghetti and lobster, squash, New Bedford scallops and a side of glazed Brussel sprouts, topped off with a bottle of wine. The meal was running an A+ until dessert. The chocolate slice and carrot cake were at best disappointing. Next time we will skip dessert.

Our server was awful and because of her inattentive nature created a stressful dining experience. The food was pretty good. The rolls and dipping herbs were tasty. Our appetizer was really great and we enjoyed the honey orange glaze dip for the shrimp. The salad is usually a delicious delight; however, the salad had way too much salad dressing and made the typically tasty salad a disappointment. Our food was served timely and was nice. My meal was the salmon with vegetables and asparagus, my spouse chose the steak with grilled vegetables and seasoned potatoes. The potatoes were way overloaded in seasoning and made eating them a chore, just like the salad. Our server did not check on us and we needed drink refills. The three women seated at the table beside us had a different server who was very attentive to their needs. We usually get dessert but was so aggravated by the end of our meal, we opted out of dessert. Good service and appropriate seasoning is essential in making happy patrons in any dining experience.
I have been here a couple times and have only dealt with the service department, and they are an awesome team! Travis is always helpful. The service department takes good care of us.

i've been coming to Julia at this salon for months now. She is a perfectionist, my nail shape is ALWAYS on point. She introduced me to colored acrylic; they last me over a month before i need to get a fill. i've had many compliments on my nails, they don't break or chip. Julia always asks me for ideas and we work together and make my nail ideas gorgeous. Very talented, she's done colorful marble and ombré as well as solid color sets (i don't have many pictures only videos). It's worth the money, i only get my nails done once a month. The salons atmosphere is friendly and they're very personable, the customers are diverse. Setting appointments is very easy, and they're courteous- they let you know if they're running late, always offer me beverages i.e. water or wine, and food.

First time we've eaten here. Felt like Cajun/ redolent food at a reasonable price. Choic in Nashville is limited, especially because my companion is allergic to shellfish. This place offered several choices, so we decided to try it.

Very pleasantly surprised. Service was very friendly and pleasant. Waitstaff answered questions and made suggestions when asked. Spice levels were very tolerable and still had zing. We had seafood gumbo (me) with a good dark roux, perfectly cooked shrimp and the small was a good serving size (about a full cup). We split fried okra, very nice. He had a catfish basket with extra crispy fries. Very good, crispy breading with succulent catfish inside. Nicely spiced. The slaw was too vinegary for his taste. We split a banana pudding with real whipped cream! Very tasty. I also had a lovely rum frozen drink.

All in all, a lovely meal. My only caveat is that parking is difficult, especially for someone who is handicapped. I had to negotiate stairs and walk a good distance from car to restaurant. When we left, I had my companion go and get the car while I waited in front.

Great hangout!   If you are looking for some great beers and or meades, the selection is excellent and the variety worked for those of us with very different wants.   On Sunday it was pretty packed but the service was quick and friendly.  We will definitely be back!  Great place if you are biking around Dunedin.
Great coffee. Organic free range eggs! Eggs Benedict is awesome. Customer service is okay .....always really busy and good vibe
Much improved! No smoky smell. I love the well organized shelves, including the books. Prices are higher than a typical Goodwill type store, but the merchandise is similar.

The customer service representative was nice, but he never told me there would be a $10 charge each time I changed the car's return time (on my time over my phone).  I was also never told of any online specials, promotions, coupons, aaa discounts, etc.  I was charged an extra $20 at the end of my rental because I extended it twice.  AVIS said I didn't let them know I wanted an extension, but I said to start me with a few days of service initially and I would go from there.  If you want to avoid "hidden" fees rent elsewhere, should be illegal to do a form of bait and switch after they conveniently have my credit card.  $405 for less than 5 days of driving a car is obscene.

Chaos! I went inside to order. There was one man ahead of me. After a minute or two, the fellow said "go ahead" and left. It was another 10 minutes before I was waited on. There were two employees running the drive-thru and one employee just filling orders for the walk-ins. One of the drive-thru employees was doing double duty between taking the drive-thru money and taking walk-in orders. She was so overwhelmed! Why the employee just filling walk-in orders couldn't take orders too, I don't know. I ordered a hot ham sandwich and onion rings. I waited 20 minutes for my order. The ham was very good but the onion rings were just burned scraps and only filled the container halfway up. On top of that, the employee just tossed my order on the counter and quickly left; no thank you or anything like that.

Kinda bummed. Food is tasty but...I wanted spice and this is the mildest Indian food I have ever had in my life including the ready made frozen meals. My husband is on cloud nine and loves it but he isn't big on spice anymore. I put in the special instructions to make it North Indian level 4 hot (just to try out the heat) and said hot food is comfort and I won't complain. But I am complaining a little because there was no heat and I needed the comfort. Believe me the flavors are wonderful which is why it is a 4. But I would be losing my mind if I had some kick  Maybe the special instructions didn't come through DoorDash...will try it again and see if I can get some flames

I love how only locals know about this place!!  How I miss the Merc!

I grew up in SB and never discovered this place until I asked my grad student friend where he hung out at night.  Not the easiest place to find...since it has a dull-looking door and is dimly lit in the evenings.  But man, once you step inside...the atmosphere is overwhelmingly awesome.  I have a new found appreciation for reggae and retro style decor.  The place isn't that big but it feels more like an old living room with comfy couches and board games and a pool table, which is free on Tuesdays (from what I last remember).  How great is that!  The bartenders are so friendly and easy to talk to, and recognize the regulars.  The beer is reasonably priced and they have a decent selection as well, and usually they'll pop you a bag of popcorn to munch on at the bar too.  I love how it's not so crowded, which is SUCH a relief around Goleta/SB where it just so happens that practically every decent place has been taken over by the college kids.  Best hang out spot in SB, if you don't mind the more intellectually stimulating conversations, as this place is frequented by grad students rather than undergrad (thank goodness).

My family and friends -- a group of at least 25 people - went there after my daughter's First Holy Communion. Everyone had a great time. There was plenty of bacon, sausage, turkey bacon, pancakes, waffles, grits, oatmeal, and they made your eggs anyway you wanted-omlet. Our waitress Barbara was great. We bought a cake from Night Kitchen. They provided us with clean plates and forks. They even served us water -- without asking. We sat there for at least a half a hour afterwards talking and relaxing. The prices were very reasonable. There wasn't a large selection of fruit, but everything they had was hot and good. The manager even came over to check on us to make sure we didn't need anything. I will definitely be returning. Sue Ann

Not only was the staff here on point and super friendly and polite, the food was really good! We split an order of gator tail or bites and a seafood platter. Delicious. No complaints here. This place was a 10 second walk from my hotel. Loved it.

Cute shop and honestly a fantastic drip coffee. If a shop can do something as basic as a drip coffee you know that's a testament.

It's also a cute spot from what I could see to stop in and do some work on your laptop. Seemed like 90% of the people there that day were doing just that.

After about an hour wait, our party of 6 was called yet only four of us were present. The other two were across the street at graffiti junktion grabbing a beer. The hostess, Jess, very rudely informed us we had one minute to get our party together or our table would be given away. Really? We've been sitting here for an hour - you couldn't have told us that when we put our name in? I understand if they were 10 minutes out, but they were within eyesight walking back. 

Them literally running across the street wasn't good enough for her and we almost lost the table. Luckily we didn't. After sitting next down, two other parties left after a run in with this pink haired hostess. 

Our server, John was great!! The food was great too  but we will never be coming back due to this self proclaimed boss woman, Jess, who isn't the boss. 

It's sad as it's not easy to find a great breakfast spot and unfortunately... The search continues.

Thank you Chase for such an amazing and rememberable dinner... My wife and I were visiting Santa Barbara for the day and came across this restaurant the service was superb the drinks were excellent my steak was cook perfect..our server was polite and well knowledgeable. You can expect a welcoming smile from Jack Who is the GM, and he wants to know how your night is going so he can make sure you have best possible experience pier in Santa Barbara . We will be definitely coming back.   And by the way if you can't decide on dessert you have to try the cannoli by far one of the best tasting treats I've had in my whole entire life thank you again!!

So good, fav ice cream place in town!! Double choco peanut butter + cookie dough, SUPER big scoops on a chocolate waffle cone = heaven. Busy even on the week nights, but totally worth the wait. Very personable employees as well.

Do yourself a favor and order the fried chicken. I ordered the "taste plate."  It consisted of small portions of red beans and rice, gumbo, etc. One pathetic chicken wing was the portion of chicken. But it was good! The rest was OK but nothing to write home about. I'd rather have the chicken. The waiter was rude and acted like he was doing us a favor to wait on us. Busy, yes, but really!

So I walked in without knowing they changed to appointment only, I was the only one there waiting so I thought I was just lucky! 
Turns out, I was lucky!!! Billy took me after his customer snd explained to me that I need an appointment but took me anyway! 
It was the best haircut I've had in years
Clean and fast and billy is amazing. He runs a great shop and even though his 2nd shop is closer to my new apartment, I will come back to him for sure!!

Two stars for cold beer and pleasant service. Took the servers advice and ordered the fried green tomato panini with cream of artichoke soup. The soup was made with marinated artichoke hearts which made for an odd flavor. The panini wasn't hot, which isn't good when eating fried anything and the sauce was minuscule at best. Wanted to like this place since it's down the hill from my house but my husband's lunch was also bland. Bummer for us but apparently we are in the monitory.

We had a great experience on this trip, and it did not disappoint by any means. Got to see all of the landmarks around Philly for a reasonable price, what more could you ask for? Our tour guide was friendly and fun, and the trip was a blast. If it's your first time in Philadelphia, I would definitely recommend taking this companies tour of the city! So much stuff to learn about in a great city!

Where shall I begin? Just me and the bf (kid free night) ran out of green beans at 6p, brought my salad out with my dinner, my snow crab legs were waterlogged and lukewarm at best, my bf said his shrimp tasted funny. $60 wasted and will never be back. So nice that the servers are more worried about who likes guacamole/avocados rather than tending to their guests.

How do they make such a simple dish into a scrumptious dish? When I make a turkey sandwich at home, it just isn't the same. And this is why I will be returning to Patachou! I just can't make it at home.

If you are looking for a typical breakfast like an omelet or a BLT, they have it mastered here. 

On one hand I wish the menu was a bit longer and a bit more exciting but on the other hand Patachou's sister restaurants have that covered. 

I recommend carry out- this place gets packed!! I could barely make my way to the counter to pick up my food on Sunday late morning.  But I made it up there, placed my order and was outta there in 10 minutes. Smiled and waved goodbye to all the hungry people waiting for a table!

Went there for paint nite the place is very nice it self has seating outside which I love very clean .. I got lemonade snagria which was great and also crab cakes which I loved not to much breading more meat .. Also I got the seafood bisque soup I wasn't really a fan taste like a bowl of cream to me but would definetly go back !

This place is great - wide variety on menu, good value for lunch all you can eat special, and the service is great. The owner came by multiple times to check on our lunch experience, and was very knowledgeable about the ingredients and the different items on the menu. Overall it does seem healthier than most indian restaurants in the area. Very well done, will be coming back!

If you can stand the smoke, this place is generally a great dive, if not....its an overwhelming assault.  Its that simple.  Great juke box, foos ball, and darts.  Oh...also the bathrooms are pretty much what you would expect from a dive...

The wait was short for 2. Stopped in for a snack and tried the hush puppies - it looked kinda normal but had such a good texture and taste. Great taste for a very cheap price of $2.49! Also had a dozen of oysters... it was big and fresh. 

Only tip i have for this place is that you can not bring any outside drinks in if you have any to-go cups with ya.
The problem was solved and thanks for giving the extra effort with my daughters rental.
Stopped in looking for oysters.  They were the worst oysters I have had and I couldn't finish them.   They were really dirty and sandy.   Won't be back.

Favorite Local Bar to hangout watch the game and eat some good bar food! I think they have the best 4 cheeseburger I have ever had and there wings are great. There staff is always nice and friendly I would highly recommend this place to stop in for lunch or dinner and have a few beers with your food.

If you're looking for sophisticated suds that you can take home to enjoy by your shut-in self, or bring to rescue a party with your heroic, good taste. This is the place.

Pros
-Truly amazing selection - local, domestic, international, extra-MF'in-terrestrial
-DIY Six Packs (or more, really...)

Cons
-Gets a bit pricey
-Sneaky tactics what without putting prices on the individual beers and then you're kind of on the spot when charged with a custom sixer in the $35 range. Buy hey, you've got great taste and don't want anyone to see you sweat with sticker-shock or know the real you is a cheapskate, so you pay up and The Foodery Wins (but so do you because the beer is great).

Guys I love Waffle House, but my boys and I, 4 of us were just served sour chocolate milk. The server was nice enough, but this is my own fault. The place is pretty dirty and of the 6 staff no one is taking charge. They are arguing, breaking cups, complaining and it's chaos. We waited so long that I couldn't leave out of principle and had plenty of time to observe that things in this particular Waffle House are especially bad.

The chocolate milk was inspected, confirmed sour. The other three half gallons inspected, sour. So what do we do, leave it out on the counter for 15 minutes and hope no one else is served the bad milk.

Dine at your own risk!

This was my first visit back since the remodel. What a difference! Beautifully done redo! Comfy, warm and inviting. 

We went for lunch on MLK day and the crowd was light. Service was good, but could be more attentive.

Food was good, but I do hope for a wider variety on the lunch menu.

The fried mozzarella (Mozzarella Fritti)  was very fresh and delicious! Accompanying marinara sauce was also fresh and fabulous. Totally worth $9.

I had the Luciano which is described as sautéed shrimp with shitake mushrooms, roasted tomatoes, baby spinach in a garlic white wine sauce over linguini for $10. There were only 3 jumbo shrimp. This dish was okay, but I would not get it again. I like a heavier sauce. This was nice and light. The dish came with soup or salad. I opted for the white bean soup which was flavorful and delicious.

The Hubs had the meatball sandwich with yummy house made chips for $9. He thought he was going to love this sandwich until he discovered the chunks of onion in the meatballs. The man hates onions. So, that was a big turn-off for him. It was covered in marinara sauce and mozzarella cheese. He took it home to our daughter and she loved it.

We will try again for dinner soon.
I got the cinnamon donuts fresh and hot and woah they were amazing. I am also a fan of their strawberry cinnamon donuts.

WOW! Just wow. 

I just joined Crunch last week and the crew at the South Tampa location are incredible. Specifically Vicci, who is one of the personal trainers there, is extremely knowledgeable and professional. She even went out of her way to show me how to use multiple equipment so I would have less "gym-timidation" 

I'm very happy to have this be my home gym when I'm visiting from LA. Thanks Vicci and crew!

I love Dr. Bastomski for his whole health approach and his time to go over every aspects of my health unlike my regular medical doctor's appointment were like 5 minutes and I'm just given a pill to swallow.

Holy moly! Amazing!! Delicious +  the atmosphere was fabulous. I really "judge" a place on atmosphere and The Lark delivered. We got to sit by the fire place outside and the service + music where lovely. 

We had the sun-chocks (WTF?! Amazing)... kale and butternut squash salad (holy moly!) and pork belly w/cornbread (Bury me in this dish please). 

What I loved even more than the service and food quality was ..... the fact that they do not rush you out the door! Each dish came individually- not all at once. I felt no pressure for The Lark to move me out of the restaurant ASAP to turn over the table.  This method of "feeding someone" made me feel important! No pressure to get out and having the patron enjoy their meal is valued here. It's the little things!! 

Lovely dinner! The Lark was my suggestion so it made me look good! Hence- I'll be back!!

Farm fresh food 
Valet parking + street parking 
Music
Atmosphere
Amazing food
Great for a birthday/special dinner too
This tour was exceptional. Everyone who visits Neq Orleans should do this tour.  Very educational and enlightening. The history no one talks about. Powerful.

I had the green tea with strawberry and guava, 25% sugar, and boba. It was delicious, but I did like the boba. The cashier was very accommodating as she offered my friend and I new drinks without an extra cost if we didn't like them.

Great situation with the sun setting in view. Our seating was ready & reserved outside for us-right on time. They were sensitive to my shell fish allergy. A nice breeze for the outdoor seating. Trees at the shore provided shade. The bread is warm. The extended outdoor seating caused some to think it was part of the sidewalk. They probably should put a gate up to get people to walk on the sidewalk. Wife likes the outdoor seating (and she is one tough cookie). Grandson ate his cheeseburger, and liked it. The sauce on my grooper made it taste awesome! Great service. When the meal was half way, the waiter replace full drinks that were watered down before they were empty. Swordfish was dry. The flounder was top notch.   They areMaryland crab cakes, but close. Rice was dry. Shrimp was good. Oysters were banging. The waiter paid attention to the most important person at the table-my wife. Dessert was literally the icing on the cake.
Service was truly a 5+. Food was a 4. Should come back

Had lunch with a friend here today. The food was okay, blt and grilled cheese. not much room for error. proceeded to the checkout when we were told there was a minimum amount if you wanted to use a credit card was 10$. My friend and i scrapped together just enough for our meals. Being that neither of us carry cash. After leaving, the waitress followed us out asking if there was a reason that we did not leave a tip. The waitress rolled her eyes and walked away. embarrassing my friend and I in the middle of town.

I have gone to this place off and on since they opened since it is conveniently located. Today was the last time.  They treat customers terribly.  Over the years, I've noticed that they are terse with customers, roll their eyes, and there are few smiles. Today, the guy that scrubs the front of the car didn't think I moved quickly enough into the car wash.  He started yelling at me to move up.  I told him I am waiting to put space between me and the car in front of me.  I had heard of car damage at their car wash in the past.  There was no one behind me.  He yelled over and over that I needed to move up, even after I told him not to yell at a customer.  When I called to complain, the girl on the phone made excuses for him and told me I was wrong for being upset. Don't reward this business with your business.  Plenty of other car washes available.

I would describe this place as fraudulent.  I bought a spa party Groupon for five. The space was the size of a closet and they had us sitting in lawn chairs covered by towels. They had us soak our feet in dish washing bins. We were so overwhelmed with the dissatisfactory space and experience that we declined the facial. They were pleasant enough and wanted to prove they were trained but it was not great.

This should be a single massage space at best but they are not skilled enough, in my opinion, to call themselves a spa.

Horrible. had a family emergency and went out of town. Came back and went to pay my bill, which was late and I specifically asked if there were going to be any other fees, was told NO. Went online to pay and they tacked on about $70 in "lien fees". This place is horrible, lied by omission of the truth.

You see that manager wrote "Oh we're sorry just call us and we'll help you"? I called them 4-5 times, left msg.s every time. They never got back to me. Dear(!) Lithia Reno Subaru manager, please don't bother commenting here and trying to look nice. I read other reviews since I posted mine, you didn't do any better even a tiny bit. Subaru name should be ashamed for working with people like you over there!

Noah was amazing! We went with a bachelorette party on February 9, 2020. We had originally reserved the room on Saturday night but something urgent came up; when we called, Noah easily gave us the Sunday afternoon slot and we were still able to attend. 

He was super nice and explained the rules well, especially since we had someone who had never done an escape room and someone else had completed at least 10. Noah gave us hints exactly when we needed them. The room was challenging without being so frustrating. The room we did had a 30% success rate but we made it out. I liked that he asked for feedback at the end so we could talk about puzzles we liked and the ones that were too frustrating. Thank you to Noah for a great experience!

It's my family's go to place for a steak dinner. We usually order the same appetizer ,firecracker chicken wraps.  My wife always gets the 6oz flows fillet. I either get a ribeye or a sirloin. The steak is good and service is prompt. I tried the mixed vegetables the last time we went. They tasted good but they were cut very big. I wish they were cut into smaller bite size pieces. 4 stars for good quality food and service. I don't give 5 stars to chain restaurants bc it's basically the same meal no matter what location you go to.

First time ordering delivery here though Uber Eats.  I ordered:
1. 3-cheese nachos
Was confused when I saw the order as I did not order soup. Then realized it is a very soupy nachos without the chips.
2. Hand smashed guac and chips
Guac was put in the same bag as the chips but mixed in the middle of the bag.
3. Side of salsa
4. Side of queso diablo
I asked for utensils and napkins specifically on uber eats app during check out. However, my order did not come with any utensils or napkins.

So this one is part of the Yelp has an app for that food fight. Ill be posting a review just for the app and then I'll update at a later time once I actually have a full blown meal here!.

This was stop #12 on the tour
The Onyx room was an interesting place. We went in the late afternoon and there was nobody there, but I can see how this place could be totally bad ass chock full of people eating, dancing, and having a good time. 

So the app: "That's a Wrap" it was what I call a twofer! lol it was two different styles of BBQ. The first - BBQ pork, cheese, mushrooms, onion and garlic all wrapped in lettuce was very good but the texture was off-putting it's hard to explain but it was this mushy-esque texture of all those ingredients put in a not too stable lettuce wrap thingy. Kinda weird but good. The second part of the twofer was a buffalo chicken- jack cheese and buffalo sauce in a won ton wrap served with a blue cheese sauce. This one was good too! (I'm a sucker for BBQ) The texture on this one was killer. Nice crispy wonton roll and BBQ inside, oh yeah! The blue cheese dipping sauce was an enigma all in itself. I couldn't have too much of it because it was strong but then again I could not stop dipping in it.

I gave this place 3 out of 5 only because of the weird texture.

Was told my refrigerator trim package was in after waiting a yr to get it so drove to their Nampa store where I was told it was only to have them say they know nothing.Tried to call sales person Tisha but of course she couldn't be disturbed even though I pleaded with the operator.
EXTREMELY POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE

This just isn't the place for me. Although I like the varied menu and the staff seems to be doing what they can to make everyone happy (for the most part), the wait times seem to always be long and the food just isn't all that great. 

My latest visit to BJ's Brewhouse was for a takeout order. It was a close restaurant that was open and had ribs and a BBQ chopped chicken salad on the menu. The wait time was 35 minutes but I'm not holding that against the staff. Like I said, I really think they were doing their best. But the food just consistently disappoints me and this time was no different. The ribs were definitely cooked at different times. One 1/2 rack was pretty good but the other 1/2 rack was burnt and tough and had minimal edible meat. The salad looked like it was just a bag of shredded iceberg lettuce with a sprinkle of corn, maybe 2 ounces of chopped chicken, and the strangest glob of onion I've ever seen. It was like someone started to deep fry the onion but the oil wasn't hot enough so it just kind of congealed into a ball. What we had of the chicken and dressing was good but $16.95 for a plate of lettuce is just too rich for my blood. 

I am sure that there are awesome and appropriately priced menu items that have a huge fan base. But for this Yelper, I'll stick to other establishments.

So honestly was reluctant to try this spot because I'm from out of town and was expecting a fast food like spot. I was Soooo pleasantly surprised and so happy I stopped in here. Absolutely not fast food like. Not even close. I had a chicken bowl and tried their 3 spread sampler (one was like hummus with fava, red pepper I think fava, and an eggplant one). I also got the traditional chicken bowl subbed half greens with the honey mustard and tzatziki on the side . Delicious. A bit salty for my personal taste but I think fine for everyone else. Staff was very friendly and kind. Cold brew coffee was also great!

Great mexican food.  Went with the family, and the kids loved the "little amigos" menu.  $7.95 for an entree, drink (juice or soda) and ice cream scoop at the end.  My wife and I ended up getting a few things off the happy hour menu (from 4-7 PM Mon-Fri) and a few sides.  She loved the Tortilla soup, saying it was one of the best she has ever had.  Her Cobb salad was so-so (she wished the chicken was a bit more grilled).  I had the fish taco and some pork carnitas tacos.  The fish taco was different in that there was just one big piece of fried fish instead of a few smaller pieces.  It tasted good though.  The carnitas tacos were legit and very authentic (from a Texan's point of view).  

We have been a few times for work dinners, and this was one of the first we went on our own (and first with the kids).  Definitely a nice place to eat.

Well, after reading the other reviews, I am not going to be saying anything really new or different here.  

I called initially about a dishwasher that needed to be repaired.  They came out promptly, charged me $97 and told me i needed a part, and they would call me back when they had it.  They called me back right away, and said they had the part, and could they come the next day?  Well, the next day came and went, and I was stood up---after rescheduling my entire day around them!  This lead to literally WEEKS of me calling with no return call, trying over and over to get back in touch and get the job done.  I finally went with another company.  

I would not recommend Jefferson Appliance to anyone.  They were extremely unprofessional.  It seems they just wanted their quick $97 assessment fee, and not much more.  Very frustrating experience.

Love, love, love. I had booked this hotel never staying here before. When we pulled up, I wasn't quite sure!! But let me tell you, we will be staying here again.
