I feel that this hotel is very authentic New Orleans. Loved the courtyard, the gas lit lamps, the pool, the continental breakfast, the coffee, the close proximity to everything. It was a couple blocks from Bourbon St., shopping, and Cafe Du Monde. We are a family of four, and the room was a little cramped for us, but we would do it again in a heartbeat. Rooms were clean and quiet. Only thing negative was the air condition, it could have worked better.

sold me a car said nothing wrong so i got it. seeing i had bought a car from danny before that was good i trusted him dont trust danny or joe they are liars and crooks now my car runs hot after 2 days and they refuse to fix it

Ate here around 9pm last night. While there was a line outside we were only waiting for maybe 10 minutes. I had the chargrilled oysters which were great. We also had the grilled gator, recommended by our server Izador. We told him we have only had fried gator and he said to give it a try. Glad we did! Washed it all down with the local Abita amber beer. Will definitely be back...

I was given a gift certificate for this place so my wife and I stopped in after seeing the new exhibits at the Franklin Institute. It was a nice day but we sat inside as the outside tables were full.
We found our waiter to be satisfactory, he stopped by occasionally, not especially engaging, but hey, we arent there to make new friends with them, better aloof than Outback where the wait staff practically sits on your lap.
The Van Eyke mussels were excellent as well as the Monk burger I had. Tap selection is extensive. They also have hundreds of bottles to choose from. I often wonder how they all keep the less popular bottles from expiring past the freshness date. We will be back if in the area again.

This place is everything I thought it would be and more! A friend and I drove from Orlando to check it out and it was well worth the drive! It is laid out perfectly as as we walked through the market we were intrigued by the huge selection of fresh and packaged products. There was also a great variety obit led craft beers that I had never seen. We stayed for lunch  and after much debate, I ordered the St Petersburg. It was delish! Along with lunch I enjoyed a yummy gelato and we both took home some bakery goods. I can't wait to go back and try some of the hot foods from the other stations. Not only that, we plan to bring freezer bags so we can bring back some items from the meat market. Again, this place is amazing and please open in Orlando next!

I've been to Ruth's Chris in Annapolis and DC; this was a much inferior experience.

Most importantly, the food wasn't quite up to expectations. One of the steaks was overcooked, and even the replacement wasn't quite right. The delivery of the wine was delayed and was a bit of a short pour.

Our servers were a bit Stepford-Wives-ish. The woman who delivered the bill put it in front of the one guy in our group... even though it was one of the women who was paying. (When we flagged it for us, she defended her 19th Century behavior.)

It was perfectly OK... but I'd wait and go to another one back home if you can.

My husband and I just moved to Havertown, after painting night and day, we stumbled upon brick and brew. Great food!!!! We sat at the pizza bar the first night and food was superb!!!!! I am writing a review not only about this amazing establishment, but your new server Carolyn. As a "douchey"couple next to us asked her dumb questions and were extremely rude to her, she held her composure with her bright smile on her face. Food and customer service has been superb here. Just wanted to give this gem of a newbie 5 stars!!!!!!!!

Lived on 15th for many years and came here quite a bit while bar crawling. Awesome beer selection, great fries and burgers. However, I didn't understand what a gem this place was until Dining Out For Life this past April.  I was an ambassador for the third time, first time here. The staff were unbelievably nice and welcoming. I can't explain the difference between here and other restaurants. You could just tell they all were very excited to participate in this awesome cause. I am lucky enough to work near here even though I don't live close anymore. I can't wait to go back again and again!
The place is clean. It is my first order from this place but the saag paneer that I order had more onions than spinach.
Delicious! Their panang curry is legit. And if you order it spicy they will give it to you spiiicy.

Been there twice this Mardi Gras season, the food is outstanding and the service is awesome. These people care that you come and see them. The only issue we had was the charbroiled oysters take so long, and we received ours at the end of our meal, so do a better job to get them served earlier. 

The staff seating will tell you 20-30 minutes, but if you have a party of 1 or 2, its way shorter. Great food and great staff and a great location. Tell Brenda at GW Fins that this is the kind of place that deserves tourists money.

I don't often indulge in not so healthy food, but when I do I want to make those extra calories worth the extra hard work its going to take getting rid of them. In the case of Phil-A-Mignon, I had a lot of work to do because this place was good!

A few weeks ago I was driving home and checked Yelp for something new to try along my route. I came across the review for this place and the ratings from others forced me to give it a try. I am glad I did. From the outside it is your basic pizza/steak shop. That all changes once you get inside. The people behind the counter were very kind and treated me like family. I told them it was my first time there and asked them what I should try. They asked me a few questions about what kind of food I liked and then gave me some recommendations. I told them I like spicy food, buffalo wings, and cheesesteaks. They talked me into trying their Raspberry Peppercorn wings and a specialty steak they called The Dragon with the option of having it made with filet mignon instead of the standard steak meat.

The wings were delicious. They were cooked perfectly with just the right amount of crunch and plenty of meat. The Raspberry Peppercorn sauce was delicious! It was sweet with just a little bit of spice and there was fresh pineapple cubes mixed in with it. If you want something different like this, don't waste your time going anywhere else.  These are definitely worth a try. You won't regret it!

Next was the steak. The Dragon steak is their version of spicy. It is made with ghost peppers, seeded peppers, provolone cheese and chalulu hot sauce. I love spicy food and they warned me that it was very spicy. I asked to taste some of the sauce in a spoon. They asked me if I was sure I wanted to do that and I said yes. I tried it and it was good. They were kinda surprised that it didn't bother me. I can really handle my spice and this was perfect. I told them to put extra on the steak and give me extra on the side too. The roll they used was perfect for this sandwich. Seeded with a hard outside and soft inside. The meat was delicious but might not be for everyone. If you are looking for a steak that is loaded with meat, you probably want to stick with a regular steak instead of the filet. The steak made with filet mignon has less meat than normal but it is good meat. It was cut a lot thicker and pretty much felt like I was eating a steak in a sandwich. It was good. That along with the sauce made this into a special treat. So special that I went back a few days later and ordered another one. 5 stars!

As for the heat... The steak was delicious. The Dragon sauce was not overly spicy for me but I guarantee that it would cause lots of damage to the average person. I didn't feel the effects of it until about 24 hours later. Yes, the burn arrived and made itself well known. I guess that is the norm for people like me who like to eat spicy foods. Yeah it sucks, but it's totally worth the pain.

Go try this place. If you like spicy food, try the Dragon steak. I hope you enjoy. I sure did!

I absolutely love HipCityVeg, and it warms my heart to see them expanding! I hope one day they'll consider bringing their vegan cheesesteaks to the west coast - we're in dire need of a tasty one in Los Angeles!

The past few years, I haven't had enough real estate in my stomach to grab a meal here, but I still stop by to grab the BEST HOT CHOCOLATE EVER and a cookie, usually right before walking over to the Christmas village. I am not kidding you when I say they have cracked the code for creamy, decadent, perfectly sweet but not cloying, perfectly rich but drinkable hot chocolate, complete with whipped cream. It's a good thing it's not so easy for me to get into the city, or I'd be here every day. I've never come close to replicating it at home.

Their cookies and brownies are always delicious, maybe not "best ever!" but a lovely accompaniment to my hot chocolate all the same. HipCityVeg makes walking around the city in winter so special to me, I love them so much!

We stopped by this place on a beer crawl through downtown. All I can say is we all couldn't wait to leave. The beer selection....all sours??? Out of our group of 10, sours only appeal to maybe 3 people. The projector list is cool but the music was horrible. It feels like they opened yesterday. No decorations and nothing on the walls. It feels like a cold cell. This may be a cool place for a select few but if you're looking for cool places to stop, I'd pass on this one.
Decent neighborhood Chinese place. Very reasonably priced for the area. Decent health inspections, unfortunately a rarity among some local Chinese restaurants.

There must be something about great bakeries in small spaces! You won't believe the pastries and flavors coming out of this bakery! Both owners are trained pastry chefs from the CIA. (No, not that CIA!) The Culinary Institute of America! I had the coconut cream pie and OMG! It was so good that I wanted to stick my face in it and eat it like that! It was light and fluffy, like a coconut flavored cloud! The gingersnap cookies are not as "gingery" or as "snappy" as what you are used to, but have a mild chewy texture and sublime taste. 

I like coconut, so I tried the macaroons too. These are not the typical overly dense, "try not to choke on them" macaroons. These were light and airy like a puff of air with a crispy coconut shell! Terrific! They also make salted caramels. So silky smooth, that you can taste each ingredient one at a time! Since it's Christmas time, they have Springerly cookies also. A German cookie with a mild anise flavor. They have to rise overnight, so that the dough gets the perfect amount of "spring" in it. Hence the name! They have a great variety of cookies, muffins, pastries, etc. Whatever you try, I know it will be good!

Food is always cooked to perfection I always order the chicken wings imma try the fish next the side could be better but I haven't had any bad sides yet I will continue to order from here and eat here

I don't get it. The other reviewers seemed to have enjoyed the food here, but I couldn't choke down the mostaccioli. I asked for some cheese on it, so they put a cold, square slice of provel on the top. Nice.

With a name like P'sghetti's I would have thought that my kids would like it. They loved the bread (it's very sweet), but nothing else. It's a little thing, but they didn't have any crayons like most kid-catering places.

I would have only given one star, but the place was clean, service was good, the decor was nice, and the location central. Just lousy food.

Went in around 2 pm and you could definitely tell the food has  been sitting out for hours. This is the third time my family and I have gone and each time we gave been less than impressed.  After yesterday's visit my wife refuses to go back.

Went to Sbraga for my partner's birthday dinner.  We decided from among several restaurant options on the basis of what was an intriguing and exciting looking menu.  Because of the special occasion, we also opted for the beverage pairing with each course.  

So first - overall experience across all courses, with both of our choices, was that each dish was a combination of too many strong flavors.  To the point where they competed rather than complimented.  Each course was "busy" with flavors.  Example: I had the octopus/piri piri/chickpeas/greenbeans.  The octopus was tender and spiced nicely with the piri piri, and the green beans made a nice slightly sweet balance to that, but the chick peas were spiced differently, the whole thing sat on a very strongly flavored smear of olive tapenade which was unnecessary, and the garnish was fresh dill, which has its own strong flavor that did not work with the rest.  Similarly, the pork main dish had pork belly stuffed with pork sausage, strongly bitter/spicy broccoli raab and provolone bread pudding.  The sausage/raab/pudding flavors worked fine, based on classic Italian comb, but there was also the plain pork sitting on some cabbagey stuff, seemingly a reference to a more German plate.  One or the other would have worked better than trying to do both on the same plate.   

Second- the portions / proportions.  The portions are small.  More importantly, I felt that the servings were skimpy on the proteins, which to me are usually the main event.  In the octopus dish there was precious little octopus - which was the most interesting part of the dish, and it was overpowered by the rest of the dish.  The pork, which was supposed to be a "meat course" was a very small rectangle.  Some people might be ok with that, but I found it disappointing.

Third - don't bother with the beverage pairing.  What was provided with each course was a very small (e.g. two swallows) of the paired beverage.  For $35, this was a ripoff, and frankly, I didn't think their choices paired all that well with the dishes either.  The barbacoa cocktail that was served with the steak tartar was very strong and spicy, overpowered by smoked salt on the rim, and all of it overpowered the subtle taste of the tartar.  The other beverages were less mismatched, but nothing struck me as wonderful.  And let's face it, drink prices are already hugely inflated.  Everybody knows that this is where restaurants make  a big chunk of their profits... but I expected a package deal to offer some additional value over ordering individual drinks.  That would have been true if the drink portions had been full or even 2/3 regular size.  The portions were maybe 1/2 or 1/3 of a regular drink portion - so no value here at all.

Finally - the desserts.  Again - too busy.  I got the cherries jubilee with szechuan ice cream.  The ice cream was very interesting and good, the cherries were good, but aren't cherries jubilee supposed to be brandied and set on fire?  These weren't.  Instead, the whole thing was smothered by what appeared to be ground up maraschino cherries - an unnecessary addition that swamped an otherwise decent dessert with over-sweetness.  My partner had chocolate pot de creme... which was not too sweet, but was topped with pineapple that competed rather than complimented the chocolate.

I will say that the service was quite good.  The staff was knowledgeable and courteous.  The popovers were so good we asked for seconds.  They provided a nice little birthday card and madelines to take home, and that was a nice touch.  

But overall, it fell short of our expectations.  The menu looked better on paper than the execution of each dish supported.  Too many strong flavors in combinations detracted, and kept there from being a focal flavor in each course.  Portion sizes were disappointing, especially with the beverage pairing.  Fancy restaurants are a special occasion splurge for me, and I will not spend my splurge money here a second time.

This is the only restaurant left in south Philadelphia of its kind.  EVERYTHING is made fresh; nothing is frozen.  The desserts are all made fresh and in house! The only dessert that isn't is the sorbet. The ambience is amazing and the the food is unreal and ALWAYS consistent.  The owners know all of their repeat customers by name and treat them like VIP's.  

I'm sad to see an illegitimate review from a disgruntled ex employee (Chef Michael D.) Just wow.  It's really disheartening to know that (as a small business owner myself) Yelp doesn't do a better job at weeding out obvious bogus reviews.  

Please do yourself a favor and try this place for yourself and see what an amazing restaurant it is!  
You will not be disappointed!!! 

My favorites are:
Osso Bucco (best in the city) 
Pork Chop Milanese 
Percatelli Genoese 
Lamb Stew 
Sweetbreads 
Chicken livers 
Mussels 
Lasagna 
Veal Catherine 

The absolute best!!!!!

Had an amazing meal there.  Cute restaurant.  Amazing service.   Loved the hush puppies.  Pig ears were a good experience.  Had an chef special truffle gnocchi that was delicious.  We also had a pork belly plate that was also good.  Everything was great!   Would definitely go back!

Thank you subway for staying open during this pandemic and being our Friday lunch date. We don't know what we would've done without you. The owners of this location are just so sweet and selfless. They are so thankful when we visit which has made us return each and every Friday to support local business!! You have ensured that you're following proper safety during these times and it's greatly appreciated as well. 

#ThankyouThursday

Always look for local restaurants when I travel. Found this place, WOW!!  
Great food, great atmosphere and great service!!
I would recommend Three Kings to anyone visiting the St Louis area!!

Maybe it's sacrilegious that I'm posting this review on National Pizza Day, but I wasn't wowed by the pizza here during my first visit.

I arrived to the restaurant first and was offered a communal table or the pizza counter. Decided on the table since my party could have the end portion.

I didn't order a drink, but they did have a nice selection of craft beer. Their desserts also sounded interesting, so maybe one day I'll have to head back and try something other than pizza. But as for the actual pie? Ehhh.

The quattro formaggi pizza wasn't *terrible.* It's not like it was burnt to a crisp or was some flappy dough. In fact, it appeared to be presented the way it was supposed to be -- and that's a bit unfortunate.

I'm sure the gorgonzola, mozzarella, smoked provolone, and fontina cheeses that the establishment uses for the pie are top-notch, but I wasn't a fan. Maybe I'm just used to four cheese pizzas with ricotta on 'em. I don't know. I would not get it again. Also was told that the margherita was just okay.

Kind of a bummer visit, overall.

Awesome spot for craft cocktails. This beautiful girl sitting at the bar bought me a drink! That was a nice touch and it was appreciated. Turns out she just wanted great conversation. We ended up dancing and making out in the corner. Apparently this kind of behavior is riské and usually frowned upon in speak easy's but the staff at Attaboy just seemed really happy for us. I'll be going back.
Great neighborhood spot.  The owners, Scott and Rachel, go out of their way to make you feel welcome.  Prices are fair.  Great selection of liquor.  Karaoke if you ask Scott really nice!

I loved it, no complaints.  I was expecting more mushroom dishes on the menu, or more Tunisian dishes because I heard the owner was Tunisian, but what I ate was nothing short of fantastic.  Maybe there were Tunisian influences? So good. 

It's a small place, less than 10 guests while I was there tonight, but we all were delighted with what we ate. The restaurant is small, so you can hear each other very well. And only nice things to say as everyone received their meal. 

Braised short ribs with polenta were a hit, but so was the paella, Cyprus salad. Mushroom Flatbread was the recommendation from the server and one guest, but I wanted the mushroom soup and it was very memorable,I don't regret passing on the bread. I have never had such a complex mushroom soup, really delightful. 

Mousse was so tiny, but so rich. And I recommend the Moroccan mint tea, yum! I wish I had brought wine, it's BYOB. I would return, there's lots more in the menu that looks delightful.

If you have a big group of people and don't know where to go look no further! Meat is such a great place to go with friends. The atmosphere is so exiting and alive at night. The food is also delicious and worth every penny. I'm a big meat lover so this place hits the spot for me. The side dishes they have are great too. The prices aren't too bad either and you get a lot of food. Go check out MEAT! Make sure to have a drink too.

This Nashville kiosk inside the secure area has become a must stop before/after every flight when visiting the city. They have 3-5 local craft brews from their award winning brewery along with a few souvenirs to keep the memory going.

Service is always friendly and prompt. The food court is nearby if you want to add some food to your morning, day or night. Yes, they open at 9am. Bonus!

Give them a try. I particularly like their IPA.

Super Bowl Sunday 2017, 7:30PM

The maitre d' was friendly and fast. 
The waitress was friendly, fast, and knowledgeable.
I asked if the waitress recommended the king pao chicken or the new chicken dish. 
"Kung pao! There's more flavor."
Done!
I also ordered the wok-seared brussel sprouts. 
Like always, the Kung pao chicken was amazing. There's lots of chicken, cooked perfectly, with vegetables and just enough heat. 
The steamed white rice was fresh and hot. 
The wok-seared brussel sprouts were horribly salty. I love salt, but these were several steps below edible. 
When the waitress returned to bag my leftovers, I said the wok-seared brussel sprouts were very salty. She apologized, bagged my leftovers, and departed. 
Instantly, the manager came over, introduced himself, apologized for my meal being less than spectacular, asked if he could sit down, then asked what was wrong with supper?
Wow! Epic service!

Beautiful Beach, nice pool and great location make the Hilton Clearwater Beach a good destination.  The beds are comfortable and the deck overlooking the beach is a definite plus.  Would definitely go back!

We've been coming to this family owned spot for years. Let's set the expectations so you don't walk in expecting a modern facility and write a bad review due to that. It's dated. That said, we enjoy our breakfast sandwich there when we're in the area or crave it. We always order a pork roll egg and cheese with yellow mustard on an everything bagel. We've tried their pepperoni bread and enjoyed that as well. There's seating off to the side in a separate room which again, is dated but fine to eat in. The bagels are smaller compared to some places but we like it that way. They're not dense, lighter and crispy.

These guys don't even deserve 1 star! Too bad we can't give NO stars or NEGATIVE stars! They sold me a washer and dryer and the washer broke down two years later. At that point, I was glad that I had purchased the extended warranty. A repairman came and fixed my washer the next day. Then my washer broke a second time. This was around Christmas time. RC Willey didn't give the correct contact number to the repair company, so I missed his call. Since it was the holidays, I decided to wait until after Christmas to call the repair man back. He said he couldn't get to the repair for another week or so. When the repairman arrived, it was a different person than the first time. The guy started taking apart my washer and doing his diagnostic testing. He concluded that I needed a new control board and that it would take a week for them to order the part. So I had to go yet another week without a washer.

When the repairman returned, he installed a new control board and the whole unit just died. The guy told me that the harness was bad and may have shorted out the board. So he said he would have to order another board along with a harness. That would take yet another week. On top of that, the washer filled and did not drain. So when I opened the door, water spilled everywhere. I closed the door before my laundry room flooded and had to leave it until the repair guy returned a week later.

So the repair man comes back and installs another control board and says everything is fine. I say thank you and he goes on his way. Since I had water sitting my washer for over a week, I decided to run it with some bleach to kill any mold that may have grown. When I pressed start, eager to have a working washer, I was soon disappointed. My washer was still broken.

I call RC Willey and tell them what happened. The woman I spoke with tells me that she'll try to get a different repair company and that she'll call me back. She never called back. I called her back, and whoever answered the phone said she was on another call and she'll call me back in a few minutes. She didn't call me back, so I decided to go to the store to talk to someone.

I talked to someone at the customer service window and she was trying to give me the runaround as well. Finally, she said the company that was originally contracted to do the repair needs to finish the repair. So that most likely means that I will have to wait a few more weeks just to be told that my washer is still broken.

RC Willey's extended warranty service is VERY INEFFICIENT and not worth the time and money. I've been without a washer for over a month. If I would have know that I would have to go through this, I would have already purchased a different washer.

On that note, GE washers are horrible! I don't know anyone else who was as stupid enough as I was to purchase a GE washer. All my friends and family have Whirlpools or Maytag and have NEVER had to call for service on them. So piss on that saleswoman at RC Willey who told me that GEs are top of the line and all that other sales crap that I fell for.

RC Willey + GE = FAIL

This is my neighborhood go-to for good, cheap quick pizza and italian sandiwiches. It's everything you're looking for if you want to eat, not spend a ton of money, have something filling. And when nothing else is open because you spent too much time partying or goofing around downtown, they'll still be open. Their drinks are unlimited refills.

You can get a half sandwich and drink, tell them to "make it a combo" and you can literally be out the door less than six dollars... (Make sure to tell the newbies there's a button on the screen for that special price.) And let me tell you their "half" sandwich? It would be a "whole" one across the street if you went to subway, and these are freshly made, toasted, and served with Parmesan cheese and all the fixin's. The full sandwich can easily feed two people. Can't beat it!

If you want a pizza slice, they have them. Several whole medium pizzas are for less than $10 dollars. Delivery is quick and they bring it to your door with no problems. You get to know the staff if you're a regular and they greet you by name.

Oh yes, and they have cheesecake. One time I spent an hour roaming around on a Sunday with everything else closed as far down as California St. searching for cheesecake and these guys were the only ones open that had it. It's served cold in a little plastic covered tray thingy, but it's cheesecake nonetheless.

They're also very kind to the local homeless people, which is more humanitarian than other places I've seen.

This is the second car we bought from them in the past couple of years. I love going online and shopping for a car with CarMax. There are numerous filters you can filter cars by, for example all cars under $10,000. The process was smooth and fairly quick to buy a car and they even clean it before they give it to you, which is nice.

This place had the best Gumbo that I've ever tasted. I stopped here as a part of a food tour. I wasn't a fan of their jambalaya. The jambalaya wasn't good or bad; it was just average. I loved being in such an old building. It was a great combination of old-world ambiance and good food.

We found this place a bit by accident as we were spending the night before a cruise. You know the place is going to be good when the owner greets you at the door with a big smile and says ,"You have reservation!?! No? No problem!" and then seats you immediately. The decor and staff were wonderful and the food (especially the pork chops, Turkish coffee and garlic butter pitas and bread) was exquisite. They even had a belly dancer perform which was a nice NOLA touch. Throughout the owner and wife were in constant motion - joking with the customers and asking if we needed anything. Felt like eating with family (in a good way!)

The beau and I decided to ring in 2013 by starting off the night with dinner at Scape. It was the perfect choice and I can't believe we hadn't eaten there before now. First off, the entire restaurant had festive balloons covering the ceiling and to really take care of their customers, they had a complimentary glass of Moet Chandon champagne served when you sat down. Swanky!

The food and drinks were also amazing. We started with the "Ultimate Manhattan" and the Refreshing Cucumber cocktail, along with east coast oysters.

For the main course we had the duck confit and the lobster risotto special. Incredible!! 

Now to visit and try their talked about brunch!

Disappointed... No combinations unless for less the 3-4 people. food was ok, not great, had hush puppies on the menu but not as a side... Service was ok until they realized I did not love meal.  With a HH beer for less then $3... My tab was almost $20.  If you want beer or wine they have good Happy hour prices

Super awesome gem! The food is just phenomenal. From drinks to the food. Everything was exquisite.

To drink, I order  Blanche de chambray beer. Super fan of white ale beers. Highly recommend it! Brussel sprouts is a must! Overall the whole menu and ambiance is just amazing.

I have had the worst experience with this store and it is the only Domino's that delivers to my area. They never deliver on time. A 30 minute delivery order usually takes at least an hour. The customer service is terrible when I call to inquire about the order. I was told that I should just come pick up my order if I don't want to wait on it. If I had the option to pick it up, I would have ordered it as a pick up. Obviously I should order from a different company but there are times when we just want Domino's. This time, lesson learned and will no longer order from here. It has now been 90 minutes and I just saw the driver drop off our food.

The New owner (June 2018) has made BIG changes. Firstly, he's really friendly and agreeable unlike the last owner (Mr. angry face). The lights have been upgraded with super bright LED lighting, the water is always hot and the cleaning chemicals actually clean. Alpine is better than ever.

I had just been thinking how there aren't enough restaurants that get into the soup market, and lo and behold I find somewhere with SIX soups of the day! In my sick state, all I wanted was some good soup options and I am so glad to find this spot. Everyone working there was so friendly, and I love sandwiches too so will stopping by another time when my appetite isn't so finicky. Love love love soups!
Shoutout to Paul for being an awesome cashier and making my day! He brightens the day for any customer who walks through your doors!
Great people.  Service guy Jeff is on the ball.  Only been here a month but good so far.

the pizza was okay (shared the margarita with a friend), but call me uninspired by it:  take-out pizza would've been more fulfilling and cheaper.  the only thing that did get me excited was the side salad with goat cheese.

i know that friends of mine are fans of this pizza joint and i AM a fan of the mismatched chairs and long/large dining tables, but the service was not so great.  i kept getting hit in the back of my head during dinner by the passing waitress (not once, but several times) and she, didn't even care to notice or apologize.

if i wanted simple pizza (with fancy toppings) and minimalist wait service, i'd make it myself or just call for take-out.
The Maui Tuna Salad is AMAZING!!!! Love it. Will definitely return to enjoy the wonderful food and great service!!!
I love the staff and the studio! Everybody is very welcoming and helpful. I highly recommend joining or at least trying out one session (you won't regret it!).
Excellent restaurant and very much exceeded expectations! A must spot in Tampa. Staff was very attentive and the location was very clean. Food was the high light of course!

I met my Mom for lunch here on a Thursday. I'm glad I saw the reviews about making reservations because it was very busy (thanks Yelp!).  Our service was wonderful and very attentive.  The buffalo chicken salad was good- light, and with black beans and corn which was unusual but good.  The large salad is rather pricey at $11.50, but definitnely fills you up.  FYI, You can park for free (validated up to 2 hours) on the lot behind the restaurant at Hanley & Bonhomme.

This place is great!  Very fast service, friendly and helpful staff.  Everyone raves about the mole sauce and I would definitely have to concur, it is one of the best I have had. Will definitely be back.  A great place to go to fulfill my Mexican craving.  Prices are very reasonable, especially for the quality of food you get here.
been here twice for takeout. Sandwhichs are in the Philly style if deli pick up. Big crusty chewy rolls loaded with fillings.
This is the most unprofessional and rude staff of a medical staff that I have ever encountered. Save yourself the time (3 hours wait) and see a competent organization. Would rate less than 1

11/27/2015 in town for the Turkey Day weekend. Caught the band 'Nashville Nobody's '. They were great !!! Friend ed hem on Facebook. They r the regular house band at Tequila Cowboys - downtown Nashville. They also travel a bit too. They sing a wide range from pop to southern country- and more. CHECK THEM OUT  online too  Facebook, Reverbnation too I think

I got an açaí bowl that was delicious and the portion was a good size for the price. I only took off a star because I wish there was only limited indoor seating and no outdoor seating.
EDIT *meant to say I wish there was more seating*

I hate this place for a lot of reasons but mostly because the volume during the previews is so loud I  literally have to put my fingers in my ears. It's ridiculous. And although I like  watching previews, there are way too many. It's also freeeezing.  But what are you going to do. There aren't many options in NOLA.

Alight so I had a 2 hour layover so stopped by for food because I was hungry after a 4 hour flight (and because i had Priority Pass credit so it was free). I ordered the spicy chicken club...review: all the sauces made the meal but everything else seemed unseasoned ie the chicken. I big portion but it tasted like water. On the plus side the wine I got was up to the brim. Random post but figured if you're in the St. Louis airport id let you know ‍. Go for Burger King.
Very clean.  Great Thai food. Wonderful server. Especially.  "Rung".   She is very attenitive
Amazing food, long lines but who cares, great place to go with friends. Mexican food not my 1st choice but this was amazing

Update - the Risk Manager was eventually provided my complaint. He well understands how to engage customers when things go wrong. He expressed apologies and reassured me the hotel room had undergone pest control care. He provided a weekend stay worth of points. 

I think he did an adequate job. The staff involved prior to him was totally incompetent in handling complaints.   I hope they not only had pest control provide service to the entire hotel, I hope they refurbish and update the place into the 21st century instead of the 1980's chic  currently evoked.

I only put 2 stars, mostly because they didn't accept what I brought in, but I really need to thank them.  Here's what happened:  the Tuesday before Christmas, in the early afternoon, I took a very nice down ski jacket from The North Face that was only lightly worn.  The cashier took only a brief look at it, and said she couldn't take it, then proceeded to point out what she called "stains" that actually weren't and said their lighting shows everything.  I took a damp soft cloth to the area, put it on Craigslist, and 2 weeks later, sold it for $150 cash.  I wanted to thank her for being so particular, but didn't know her name.  Lesson to learn:  if you have a designer label item that is in pretty perfect condition and you know it's worth something, you'll do better selling it yourself.  She said she would have only been able to give me about 30% of the retail value on it.  Their loss was my gain.

Lucas Park Grille has gotten a lot of press and word of mouth gossip lately...and everything has been incredibly positive. However, I took my friends there for a date night evening and we were not impressed, as the rave reviews had led us to a restaurant we could have definitely passed on.

We all ordered a cocktail, an appetizer of some sort, and a main entree.  The cocktails came out very slowly, as did our appetizers.  We had 2 salads, the lobster mac and cheese and one other appetizer.

Three steaks were served, along with the tuna steak, which was over cooked and the flavors on the fish didn't mesh well together.  The steaks however, looked like they were prepared well, but again some of the flavors didn't mix together as well as we had expected.

To be honest, the bread and butter (chive) was the best thing we ate all night long...other then the incredible social scene Lucas Park Grille has near the front of the restaurant where the bar is.

Not what I had expected, and I would definitely not go back for dinner, but for the social and drinking scene- I would probably make an appearance.

The service has gone down drastically here at White Bridge. I imagine there's been a change in management since it's never been like this before. They have no idea what "fried hard" means. When I said my wings weren't well down, the staff argued with me and then proceeded to talk about me to the cook in Spanish- not realizing I'm Latino and understood everything they said. Not cool. The staff never tended to any customers who were seated as they were too busy on their cell phones. They've lost a long time customer.

This property is not only beautiful but very welcoming. We stayed at this resort on business for 4 days and it was well worth it! The rooms were beautiful and comfortable especially after a long day in seminars. The bathtubs are amazing and big enough to really relax. 
The staff was always smiling and happy to help when we needed it.  We can't wait till next year to go back for training. This place will not disappoint you!

Santa Rita was the developer's contractor for our front yard.  For our back yard, we met with Santa Rita and two other landscapers and chose Santa Rita's proposal.  Their landscape designer, Rich, was very creative and we like what he designed.  The price was in line with our budget so we selected Santa Rita for our back yard.  

The landscaping crew was very professional, thorough and adhered to the planned schedule. In addition to landscaping plants, rock, and pavers, Santa Rita fabricated a wonderful outdoor kitchen, stucco to match the house, electrical, gas, etc.  Installed a seriously upgraded grill with a seer burner and side burners, and we are very happy with their work and the results.

At the same time we had a pergola fabricated and installed by another contractor and Santa Rita worked well with that contractor to coordinate and sequence the work so that both projects finished with quality and on time.  

We recommend Santa Rita and look forward to having them do more work for us.

Great company with excellent customer service. We have not had any bug issues since starting services over 2 years ago. Our technician Tim M. has been fantastic and takes excellent care of our home. We have a dog and Tim is great and patient with her when on the property which we sincerely appreciated. I highly recommend the team at Rise Pest Control!!

Came here on Saturday at around 5 pm. This place is so hip that i fell in love with it right away. Surprised to see something like this in Reno as this is so much like a place you'd find in Seattle!! Their drinks are so creative. Mine is called Flying Dragon, and it's amazing both in taste and visual. They also offered free street tacos when i was there, and the chicken and beef ones were both excellent. 

As far as the vibe - the place is transformed from old house. The decor was mixed the new with the old like animals' heads on the wall. Big screen showing NHL final. The band that was playing was two guys with a top hat that gave Dust in the Wind a new interpretation. Can't go wrong with this bar if you are looking for a place that's fun, relaxing yet energetic, hip and with great drink options.

I will try to be brief. Bad odors,cracked mirror near the jacuzzi, buggs, sieman stains on the comforter of bed.

Asked for another room. The bar code card for the door didn't work.

Stay home. Save your money.

The staff was extremely friendly. The hostess immediately found us seating. Our waiter was very tentative & bubbly. 

I ordered the strawberry lemonade (free refills) and it was delicious. My boyfriend had the mango lemonade. It was a little too sweet for me. 

We shared the Cheddars cheese fries which comes with cheddar cheese, bacon & ranch. They were yummy.

For lunch I had better than meatloaf with mashed potatoes and Mac & cheese. The Mac & cheese was gross but everything else was great. My boyfriend had the chicken tender parmesan. The dish is huge and very cheesy. He loved it. 

We will definitely be back.  


**Update 

Tonight my boyfriend & I came back to the greenwood location and had the best experience yet. We sat at the bar and Marty was our waiter! He was super personable. Checked on us frequently and even made us a secret flavor of lemonade that knocked us off of our feet! The food was hot, fresh and we loved every bite! (We got the same thing)
If you go here you won't be disappointed.

$3 for an iced latte on a hot summer day....so smooth and velvety, with a strong aroma of perfectly roasted espresso beans- need I say more? Not to mention its hot counterpart, the regular latte, equally smooth and with the right amount of froth. The space is beautiful, what you would expect a fancy coffee house to look like, with ornate cups & saucers and parisian looking tables & chairs. But the vibe is definitely casual and I like that it never gets too cramped. There is always a line, but it moves fast, and the baristas are always accommodating, and pleasant. I make it a point to stop here every time I visit philly, even though there are a few outposts in NYC. There's something nostalgic about the first....whether it's the city or the coffee house, it just pulls you in.

I had the Veggie Scramble.  It was really loaded on the veggies, including broccoli, squash and carrots, which I liked.  The home fries were well done but missing the onions and peppers I usually expect.

Our server was Heidi, who was the highlight of our visit.  She was quick and courteous.  I have seen servers who, were rude, and some who are faking nice, and I could tell, she wasn't faking it.

The only real negative was some minor issues with the condition of the building.  The men's room was clean, but a bit run down.

I really like the decor of this place. The bar area is really nice looking and the entire dining room is very spacious. As far as the food goes, they seem to be pretty hit or miss. Their steak and goat cheese flat bread is awesome and probably the most delicious thing on the menu. If you're going here to drink I would recommend it, otherwise more consistent food might be found elsewhere.

A must if you're visiting Tucson or looking for something new and of great quality!! Veggie options, unsure if 100% vegan. The umeboshi(veggie) was fresh and the gyu(beef) onigiri was flavorful! Currently craving their spicy miso ramen as I write this review... I'm new to Japanese cuisine but Kukai consistently holds the bar high after every visit.

I'm not a pancake person but I must admit their pancakes are delicious. It's got the right amount of sweet and saltiness. I also ordered a spinach omelettes. It was humongous. It will without a doubt last me 3 satisfying meals. I did switch up the cheese from feta to pepper jack, but to my disappointment they had overdosed on cheese and I can't really taste the ingredients besides melted pepper jack cheese which was oozing out from my omelette the whole time. I guess some people prefer that but that's just too much cheese for me 

Anyhow, I'll still come back for other dishes!
This is a great subway restaurant and very convenient to me personally. I recommend the subway because it's not very busy and it's in a great location and the people are very friendly.

Let's just say, my mother - owner of the world's biggest sweet tooth - says this is the best donut shop she's ever been to.

Delicious, fresh and CHEAP. 

Awesome staff and a great overall experience.

Overall: As much as I wish I could recommend Drake's, the mediocre food and poor service ruined anything good coming from it.

The good: There are a decent amount of beer options and the menu, while random, is solid. The outdoor games were also a plus. Haven't had a beer tower in awhile which was nice.

The bad: Service was absolutely terrible. Our waitress never got the John Daly cocktail right, even though we spelled it out for her. Food was terribly slow coming out, and the bill took equally as long. Had to basically yell to get the check, which I don't usually do. Sweet potato fries were poor.

Atmosphere: 8/10
Service: 1/10
Food: 4/10

Food here is always great. Tonight me and my friend opted for 2 sushi rolls and shrimp and scallop pan roast to share. We were greated by our waitress, Lucky, who legit is the reason why   my review is a 5 star. She was so kind and polite. Her service blew my mind, despite a busy Friday night! Unfortunately, the chief made a mistake on our order and gave us mussels instead of scallops. We ended up cancelling that order. After that mishap, Lucky felt so bad and offered us a dessert on the house. Once our bill was paid I always like to hand cash directly to our waiter/ess. Lucky, at first, refused to accept it because she felt that the service today was not worthy.(I gave way more than the suggested tip %). But I told her,nope! You busted your ass off today. You deserve it and way more!! When I find myself back here I will make sure I get seated in her section!!

My husband and I were on our way from Tahoe to Reno and happened upon Steamboat. I had never experienced natural hot springs before and he had and loved it, so he insisted on taking me to try the place out together.
We were kindly greeted by Marie at the front desk. She was warm and offered us a tour of the place and explanation of services, history of the building, and purpose of each room's color. I was fascinated!
We were the only folks there at the time and I must say it make the visit even more special. We opted to have a soak in the outdoor tub. Once provided towels and waters, we entered the enclosed area to enjoy the soak.
What a magical place! There were tiny rabbits, ground squirrels, birds and butterflies back there! I was in awe of peaceful, natural setting. We enjoyed about 40 minutes before we dried off and got back on the road.
If you appreciate natural, no-frills not springs, I would say this is a place that's worth a visit!
Great place. Amazing sushi. You get large portions for the price you pay. Love this place. Return time and time again.

Gorgeous and HUGE park! I came here more out of curiosity than anything, assuming it was a tiny place and then walked and walked and walked finding new things to do.

You can rent a canoe for $25 for the hour, fish, BBQ, picnic, play soccer or basketball or just read a book under the shade right along the creek. It really is a peaceful place. I have only come in the afternoon when it isn't too crowded and there are plenty of picnic tables open, but I imagine it gets pretty packed since they even have Overflow parking available. 

I would go as far as saying this is a great place for a cheap outdoor wedding. They even have a hidden pavilion for pictures by the creekside and a huge area to gather people under a tent. 

Get out there and enjoy mother nature folks!

Beautifully landscaped and full of native life! We loved visiting this place! Gorgeous in every glance and direction. The gift shop is also spectacular. People are extremely friendly and knowledgable. I totally want to go back. I recommend as a must see when visiting Tucson!

I was not a big fan of the PUB because the food was not really that great (ate there a year or more ago)  I love oysters and they had a buck shuck month (August) so we went tonight.  The oysters were outstanding and the oyster stew was delicious.  Our waitress, Tess was excellent and could not have been more pleasant or attentive.  All in all a great meal in for a grocery store.

Awesome experience! Talked with the owner via phone and then set up the whole thing in easy e-mails. We were given a free piano, but had to move it. A Piano's Friend handled everything. They moved it easily and efficiently. Very friendly, family-owned business. PLUS, you get a 50% discount on a tuning if you use them to move! Oh, I also priced checked "regular" movers and others and found A Piano's Friend to be the best price of the bunch!!!

Best place to buy a car!!! You don't feel rushed or pushed into buying the car. Once you do buy the car it's a pretty fast transaction. (Compared to other dealerships I've dealt with) Erik is the best (used car manager, at least I think so) He was very helpful! Will definitely recommend this place

Service and the view were the only decent things there. Yucky drinks, and dried up wings. Seriously over priced. $15 for 10 naked, dried and small wings. Way better choices around, don't go to Whiskey joes!

This has become our go to sushi restaurant lately. It's the closest to the house, the food is good and the staff is awesome. I can't ask for more than that when a sushi craving hits. Last time we were there the waitress put jasmine petals that she grew in her garden in the water our glasses. Super sweet lady. I think she has been in there every time we've stopped by. 

I still usually order the same boring rolls but they are good every time. I haven't noticed the smokey flavor I mentioned in my first review since so it must have been a fluke. One of these days I'll get more adventurous and try some of their specials!
Food was great, big portions and tasty. Only problem is the food got cold very quick.  The maracuya drink was pretty good but needed a little bit more sugar. Overall great experience

For my first review of 2019 I'll be the first to review East Passyunk Ave's very needed new bookstore.

I covered in my last bookstore review of Shakespeare & Co (https://www.yelp.com/biz/shakespeare-and-co-philadelphia-2) how despite my love of reading, new bookstores are something that I rarely find myself visiting unless they offer up an ample amount of additional features. And from bringing local Philly writers to the front of the store and also selling used books (supplied by us South Philly folk who read a lot!), to hosting events like author readings and bookclub sessions, A Novel Idea is that community bookstore that goes well beyond the norm. It's the perfect location for a fun couple who aspired to have a community events space for local artists and writers to hangout and discuss what they're reading and writing in (https://www.broadstreetreview.com/creative-economy/a-novel-idea-opens-on-east-passyunk-avenue?fbclid=IwAR3EmUFEAOo75oMF8CeyfxczGCA23988IPRRr4zmJONBn8DHQ_cWrDz8Hh4#).

The popular reading and new release books are perfectly lined up on center tables like most bookstores. But I always make a point to visit their local Philly section since there's plenty of harder to find reads and first pressings from local publishers. Someone whose predominately a used bookstore browser like me will also be content with browsing their 2 shelf section that's loaded with plenty of novels, cookbooks, nonfiction, and more. The store's Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/anovelideaphilly/) is also worth a visit so you can keep track of their scheduled author readings, workshops, and other events.

Best steak in Boise! The quality of the beef here is about as good as it gets. The atmosphere isn't like some other high end restaurants I've been to but I came here looking to find the best steak in town and left thinking I have. The rib eye was delicious and cooked to a perfect medium rare...most places I've been to in Boise ALWAYS overcook medium rare for some reason. Next time I'm trying the tenderloin!

Starwood, I was a huge proponent of you, but my last 3 visits in the past 2 months was vastly underwhelming, to say the least.  Think I'm going to go with my other hotel BFF (Hilton) for awhile.

There were no king bed rooms ready upon check-in, so I settled for a two-doubles room. It smelled strongly of smoke, even I called the front desk to confirm that not only am I on a non-smoking floor, it's a non-smoking hotel.  Well...somebody's smoking, and it's going directly into my room.  The smell got stronger throughout my stay, and of course, I was busy working so I was already unpacked, and too tired when I got back to my room to do anything about it.  I woke up the next morning with a smoker's cough, a raspy voice, and a ravaged throat.  I also asked for a quiet room...it's away from the elevator, but it's also on a low floor next to the stairwell, so street noise is pretty loud.  My Starwood elite status does pretty much nothing here, other than provide free wifi, which didn't work with my work computer for some reason.  Useless.

Ancient parking deck, mixes valet and self parkers, so that I'm going up round and round up multiple levels, in order to find a non-valet spot.

Positives? I liked the clean, simple bath/sink vanity. Nice lobby and lounge-y bar area.  It's also within walking distance of the downtown area (Broadway).

Stay here if you like:
Smoke smell, outdated hotel decor, street noise, watching people walk to their rooms (open hallways viewed via atrium), heights (elevators w/ windows), Nashville.

Do not stay here if you like: 
Working wifi, easy parking, updated hotels.
The absolute best brunch I've ever had!  The mimosas were perfection and the service was amazing.  Will definitely be back!

I've given this place three shots; unfortunately, something is just not clicking for me with Bainbridge Street Barrel House. The most recent experience was specifically for old school video game night. Friends had told me that Wednesdays are the night for this so a group of us went over. The Phillies game was on so the bartender said he would hook up the system after the game. We arrived during the sixth inning so we were there for a while. We waited about ten minutes or so post-game but the bartender was pretty busy so a few of us decided to call it a night. If the policy is that game night only happens if there isn't a game on, that's fine. But I'd rather know up front so I can decide for myself whether to stick around. 

Said bartender was responsible for the patrons at the bar plus the people sitting at the high top tables nearby. Our large group had to order drinks from the bar as there was only one server available and he was handling the rest of the restaurant and the tables outside. I wasn't in the mood for beer so I ordered the house gin infused with cucumber, with a little bit of St. Germain and some seltzer. The drink was flat. A friend said she'd had similar experiences with the club tasting flat at BSBH. 

Despite the revolving door, it was still very hot inside. The server used the side door which definitely let some hot and humid air inside, but this could have been overcome with a little increased AC. It was uncomfortable. 

On my second visit I did enjoy a burger. It was earlier in the evening and there were more servers around. I wouldn't be opposed to grabbing a bite or happy hour drink at some point, but I won't go out of my way, either.

I ordered online and went inside to pick up. She didn't even know where my order was. When she found the three pizzas I still had drinks and dessert needed. Get home and my 4 year old is all excited. Open them up...and they are burnt!!
Check your pizza before you leave the store. Otherwise this could be you.

Took my dad to this location for his birthday dinner tonight. Restaurant was fairly quiet as our reservation was for 4pm so it was nice to get there early and he able to hear each other talk. Our server was a little scatterbrained but everything made it to the table in a good amount of time and tasted great! Between the four of us at the table there was ribs, chicken and ribs, steak, and steak and seafood. Everything was delicious. Steaks were cooked perfectly and the seafood was melt in your mouth good. We opted for dessert and the brownie sundae was the perfect ending. Not super sweet and a large portion for the price. All in all it was a great dining experience. It did get quite noisy after about 5pm but that's to be expected, as it was quite busy.
Very good tacos and great choices!  Service good on a Saturday night and atmosphere the same.  Weird rave/pop music though...other than that, will definitely return the next time in town.
Went here for restaurant week with my husband. The food was good but the portions were tiny! $90 later we left hungry. Cool atmosphere and good service though.

Very good seafood. They are setting a new standard for the area. Prices are quite reasonable considering the portion size and quality. I eat at legal seafood on the east coast often and I would say the presentation and price is very similar, but the food was better.

My only regret is that I started at South St Octoberfest before I made it here!

The Pumpking beer was awesome (I'm such a sucker for pumpkin beer anyway, and this stuff was great). My friend died over her mussels. And the grilled cheese.... Was I just too many Pumpkings deep, or do they make a really, really killer grilled cheese? Anyway, it was all delicious. We had to wait about 20 minutes for our table upstairs (4 people, 9:00 on Saturday night), but it was totally worth it.

The only pizza place I order from. Some pizza places only have one thing that stands out on the menu.  I've had a variety of items from their menu and have never had a complaint. My favorite is the turkey hoagie. I also love the best quality fries, veggie pizza, and veggie Stromboli. I would even recommend the salads. FYI - you won't be able to eat the entire salad in one sitting. I usually use the dressing as a dip and eat the rest as another meal. 

I've ordered from best quality when I've had friends over and they only had good things to say.

This was Very good. I had salsa verde shrimp taco n fish taco especial and my daughter had the chicken quesadilla and the kids meal come with 2 sides - she chose applesauce and a churro.  Which she loved it all. My tacos were Delish and fresh. We will be coming back!

This was probably the worst experience I've had at a nail salon. I made an appointment to get a pedicure and a fill, and told them it would take me a bit to get there and the woman on the phone said it was fine. When I showed up, they told me they didn't have time for me. The woman at the desk gave me a coupon for 20% off so I decided I'd give them a second chance and made an appointment for the next day. So the next day I go in and they made a joke about me being on time. I brushed it off and they told me to pick a color so I picked a gel color. Then they made a fuss because I was getting a pedicure and I picked a gel color instead of a regular color. Fast forward to my manicure and they didn't even know I was getting a fill even though I specifically said that when I made the appointment. The woman doing my nails filed off my acrylics and started putting gel polish on and I told her I was supposed to be getting a fill and there was a huge fuss over that as well. I've never had such a problem and had manicurists be so careless. Needless to say I will not be going back.

Just had what could be the best fast food burger of my life. My only complaint is that I finished it and it is all gone and now I am sad. If I had a time machine, I would go back and eat it again.

#6, double meat, add onion, extra mayo. 

By the gods that was good.

Second time here, as good as the first. Ate at the counter but the server there was fantastic. Great recommendations and pleasant experience. Coffee bar with serve-yourself selections was a great idea. This is by far one of my favorite breakfast places. Highly recommended.
Stay away....stay very far away.....unless you are popping in for a beer and a game of pool don't bother.  Bathrooms are disgusting too.  Gross!!
I love the doner kebab! I'm sure the other food is great, but I always come back for that!

The worst hotel EVER at people-handling.  We stood in a line to check in, the room wasn't ready computers were down...now we are  in another two-hour line. Only a few desk clerks. Don't do it...just don't.  Would give zero stars but no option. If you value your time...don't do it!

We had take away from Han Dynasty recently.  The entire experience was less than impressive.  We were welcomed and ushered through the busy restaurant and told to wait in 2 square feet by the bar.  While getting pushed about by the servers, we had to hand our money to the host around someone using a computer at the bar.  
    After getting our change and heading home, we ate the spring rolls (good) and our meals.  My girlfriend's meal tasted like soap and chicken, while mine was chock full of the Chinese peppercorns that make your tongue numb (we own some, so I do enjoy them).  My dish had a hint of spice (billed as "very spicy"), but was very unpleasant.  We ended up throwing away both meals and cooking at home.

I really really wanted to give this five star. It was excellent. Came with a group of friends from the resort, had an absolutely amazing meal (I had the salmon), and the atmosphere was PERFECT....except for one thing. Our server (I forgot her name, the asian girl) seemed way too in our face. She seemed to check on our table way too much, and a few times I saw her butt the poor hostesses out of the way while they were doing something on the app they have to seat tables (also, very cool!). You could tell the hostesses were really uncomfortable with it. She pushed the desserts on us a bit, especially since my husband and I planned to go have room service. Overall though, Poppy's was excellent and I'll definitely be returning!

We had Guppys pointed out to us by a couple of nice ladies when visiting Indian Rocks Beach from Orlando on a birthday weekend. The Lobstercagot was a definite winner. After eating the lobster chunks we quickly asked for additional bread to soak up the rest of the garlic and herb sauce from the plate. 
My wife loved her mahi tacos and the kids Mac and cheese was delicious! My daughter enjoyed her kids burger (not a cop out lower quality kids meal like some places) and my trifecta burger was delicious. 
The only downside was that we were too stuffed for desert!
Service was very attentive and understanding of our kids, not talking down to them at all, which we appreciate. 
They kept the refills coming which is essential when you walk over from the beach!
Great experience! We hope to be back soon.

First time for us here. We were visiting from Ft. Wayne and asked a Friend where a good local place to eat was. We were using our navigation and knew we were close, so I called. Told him where we were and he so nicely directed it's. We were in the Fresh Market parking lot and he said they weren't too fond of people doing that, and directed us to street parking.

Adjectives I would use to describe Yats are: friendly, warm, funky, fun, with a Mardi Gras/NOLA atmosphere. You walk up to the bar to order. We were given a taste of everything before ordering. You get your own napkins, plastic ware, and drinks. 

Food is brought out to you on a real plate, and the waiters call your name to find you. I had the cheese crawfish etufee with the Veggie B&B; my husband had the red beans & sausage and the  veggie B&B, plus two refillable drinks for 18.00. Everything is on the same plate, over rice. Hot and delicious, it's Cajun comfort food here!

Only dislikes were the plastic ware  & Styrofoam cups, and the bathroom was pretty bare bones. While you are there, you can look at the hot water heater that is sharing space with you. That being said, the water was hot, with plenty of soap and paper towels.  

Service is fast. When you're done, you clean up your own space. We had a wonderful time and will be back!
Had an amazing sirloin, cooked perfectly.  The staff was very attentive.  Lobster Mac-n-Cheese is a MUST!!
Never had a bad meal from here, the Cheesesteak Eggrolls were delicious, and the fries are really good too.
Awesome place! We came here for Easter and everything was amazing. The staff is very nice and gracious and the food is incredible. Highly recommended!

I absolutely love this Salon! Di has been doing my hair for years and she is the only person I trust with my hair. Everyone who works here is very friendly and personable. They cleaned multiple times throughout my appointment and I felt very safe the entire time! Thank you Di!
Milkshakes were terrible... I'm giving it 3 stars because the food looked delicious. I'd probably come back to try the pimento grilled cheese but other than that the milkshakes were just plain bad

Come for the world famous beignets, they said. Hype! Waited 40 minutes in line on a Sunday morning for a bag of three beignets and coffee.  Can I just say that beignets remind me of funnel cake for breakfast...

Had lunch there today because we had a free app coupon. Free is too expensive for this place, the needed to pay me to eat there. The BBQ shrimp was salty and fishy, the blackened redfish was bland and greasy. Just keep walking because there are too many good restaurants in the area to settle for Alpine.

The brunch was good and our server was fine once we got seated.  However we came in on a Colts Game Day.  We were immediately told by the hostess who seemed to care less about her job that it would be a 25 minute wait.  We put our name down  and we waited.  In that 30 minutes not one customer was seated however I saw a lot of people exiting.  We needed to eat and get to the game so I asked when we were going to be seated.  She asked for our name and let us know that she is being seated.  We were sitting there the entire time which showed she could have cared less.  Finally our server seated us at a table that was visibly open. When we walked back to the buffet I counted at no less than 7 tables that needed bussing.  Meanwhile there were people waiting who came in after us.   I saw several parties leave which was too bad because there were seats available.  The server staffing looked fine.  There was just no bussing of tables and the hostess did really not care to get anything organized.  I am in Foodservice management and I recognize when you might be short staffed but when you are you adjust duties.

Our go-to pizza joint! Super friendly customer service, great food and always fast delivery! When we call they answer the phone like we're calling a friend, if they're busy and delivery is going to be a while they are honest and up front. The food is always on point. Can't go wrong with Carlos pizza!!
Great place , great talent , Tonys working on a cover up for me and although it's not done it already looks amazing ! Can't wait for it to be finished and get more !

Among chain restaurants, this chain is pretty good.  I have had great experiences at other Red Robin restaurants and my last trip to this one wasn't bad.  But, I think this one may be going down hill a little.

You know that sour smell you smell when a floor is mopped with a really old mop.  That's what I smelled when I walked in.  It's not all that pleasant.  There was hardly any people there and I waited a couple minutes at the front just to be greeted.  Other than this, it wasn't bad.  The food was good and the service was friendly.
Stopped in for music and lunch at the bar and loved it so much had to come back for dinner. Atmosphere is festive but chill- menu is simple, fresh and oh so delicious!

Man o man! My friends and I came to Nashville and were set on getting some good ole southern BBQ. Martin's did not disappoint! We had a lot of people recommend this place so we had to try it.

The location is huge! It didn't seem that way from the outside, but when we walked inside the whole place was transformed into a communal meeting area full of tables, people, and entertainment. The place was fairly crowded so be ready to wait for your food, but you shouldn't have a problem grabbing a table though.

We ended up getting the Big Daddy Sampler (Feeds 4-5 with  1/2 Rack Ribs, 8oz Pork, 8oz Brisket,  1/2 Chicken, 3 pints of Sides) The brisket was absolutely amazing! Literally melt in your mouth! The ribs we a little on the dry side, but I did really like them. The chicken was great too. Nice and juicy! We also got the mac & cheese, baked beans, and potato salad which were all great!

Can't wait to come back here the next time I am in Nashville. A must go!

Well I have never left a review for a restaurant like this but with that being said, 
My sister and I went there for breakfast at 10am. The place wasn't busy at all. We sat down, got our drink's and she never came back to take our orders. She walked pass us several times but never stopped. 
So we left. I went to talk to the manager and guess who the manager was!!!!!! Our no show waitress lol. And she had to say was I sorry. Not please come back and we'll make it right or anything useful. 
Now I've been to this restaurant many times and never had such horrible service. But I'm one of those people who will give you a chance to make things right but the manager didn't see fit to do so, so I won't be back and I will no longer recommend it.

This is my favorite place to eat. Must try their hermanaki wings and even if you are not a fan, their blue cheese as well. They have really good pizza and I have yet to try anything on the menu that I didn't enjoy.

Went with a group of 5 people and did the shooter room. One of the more challenging. We were able to solve it with 2 minutes to spare! Super fun. Good bonding and team work. I went with coworkers. The guide was super helpful and would give us clues when needed. You get to sign the wall at the end and take a group picture. Very fun!
Staff is what got my rating up to 3 stars. I think the only reason why people love this place is because they love Naruto. Ramen and sushi was mediocre at best.

Well, I was assaulted, physically struck, trying to have lunch here october 19th by a vagrant on panera's open patio, at one of their lawn furniture tables. Vagrants and street scum gather throughout the day on panera's patio, use their chairs and tables to sleep and annoy and unabashedly threaten customers. management could care less!

*** No response about this complaint from Panera's franchise owner, Dory Alison, or the property management of this open mall --Inland Properties of Chicago, regarding my being assaulted. ***

Beware.  While rent-a-cops stroll past off and on, this property is not actively patrolled by security.

The kids who work here can be friendly.  Turnover in employees seems  high; clean toilets but often without adequate soap. Decent coffee; high prices for frozen fast food inTucson.
The coffee, scones, cupcakes, and breakfast are all really good. But there is a need to wear hairnets in the bakery.
Excellent atmosphere!  Great staff!  Had the Bone-in Venison prime rib and was excellent.  Not your ordinary restaurant.   Great experience!
Stopped in with no appointment to get my Covid shot. Very professional in and out I'm under an hour.
Such a great shop!  3 levels, and very well curated with great prices.  I found some wonderful treasures here and would have bought more if I didn't have to worry about bringing it home on the train.

Cuddy's is the epitome of the college dive bar experience. While it's great to see everyone from school at the bar, Cuddy's is overall a disappointment. There are three floors, which are always jam-packed, except for a ten-foot radius around the front door. I am NOT impressed with the bathrooms at all. They're badly placed (near the staircase which will be mentioned later) and there is ALWAYS a line that causes foot traffic to get very confused. Unsurprisingly, they are not super-clean, due to the myriad of drunken individuals using them nonstop. 

The place is kind of maze-like, with a narrow staircase that is constantly jammed with people, and the third floor (the dance floor) is an absolute oven. They put the DJ up really loud so you can hear it downstairs, but overall the music is not that great-- too much rap for my liking and not enough pop music to overcome it. The alcohol is not overpriced, but I can't say this is ever a place I go before I'm done for the night.

The best thing about the bar is that there is no cover, so if you want to go late at night, you won't get stuck with that bill.

Also, BE CAREFUL here. Cuddy's is extremely crowded and has very narrow areas to mingle. You WILL be brushing up against people and you WILL NOT have enough room to move. I say this not out of agoraphobia, but out of commonsense security-- the crowded conditions facilitate a thieving atmosphere and more than one friend of mine has had a phone or money stolen while here (one out of her closed purse that was being held in front of her body-- he reached around and snatched it).

Evidently Sushi Brothers is on vacation.  Parking lot is locked as well as the front gate.  

I would think that a business that closes for vacation would try to communicate it to their customers.  Instead, when one calls in an order, the phone simply rings and rings.  No answer.  How difficult would it be to have an announcement "we are on vacation until . . . ". And at the front gate leading to the restaurant, a sign "we are closed for vacation."

The management of many small businesses is simply awful.

Lovely staff- super friendly and helpful. They offered me water or tea right away. 
The place is organized. I found a beautiful burberry skirt for a great price and a Lanvin suit which normally retails for $3,000 for a fraction of the price. Items are authentic and clean. The owner is adorable - super classy lady
I love the Jalapeno Crab Poppers, with mango cilantro vinaigrette. The taste is magnificent. My friend ordered the BBQ pulled pork sliders.

Hands down worst massage.

I bought a Groupon. This place is located on the 11th floor of an office building. I felt like I was entering a dentist office. I showed up 10min early, and sat in the waiting room waiting for someone to show up. I was finally taken back 15min after my appt time. 
My masseuse (Sabrina) didnt ask me any questions when she walked me into the room then didnt knock before entering the room after I undressed. She had the boniest, coldest hands and hardly used any massage oil when she started at my feet. I felt like she was rubbing me with cold leather and her fingers kept getting caught in between my toes! At least she could've warmed her hands under the sink first! She spent so much time massaging the front of my neck - it was so weird!! I agree with Young B, Sabrina must be new. She either had no pressure or way too much pressure (like on the bones of my arms). There was one point I felt her "massage" was more just grabbing the fat around my waist. I think she spent more time massaging the front of my neck then my back, which is what I had written on my questionnaire.
The room I was in was the couples room, there were two tables and a round glass table with some crappy ikea chairs. Maybe this was the lunch/conference room too?? There was some music on, but some black out shades or lighting for ambiance would go a long way. I suppose as long as the masseuse is awful, black out shades wont do much except mask the ugly.

The Pharmacy here is horrible. No matter how far in advance the prescription is called in , when you get there to pick it up the pharmacist will still be "checking it". Within one week 3 prescription and a minimum of 10hrs advance, it's been the same with no other customers waiting.  Customer service in pharmacy is none existent at best.  They know you need a prescription and just don't care or are completely understaffed.  Regardless, I would never take a prescription there.

The Aertson has been a wonderful place to live.  We have been here for almost a year.  There are two residential towers and the Aertson Kimpton hotel is attached as well.  We live in the North tower.  

Pros:
- Brand new so units are in tip-top shape, stainless steel appliances, granite countertops
- Beautiful views of downtown Nashville
- Great amenities - never-crowded gym with lots of equipment, nice pool on the 8th floor, great roof deck on the top floor
- Building staff is friendly and helpful.  I have lots of examples but most recently one staff member mailed me something I had left while out of town.  Above and beyond!
- Maintenance staff is awesome!  They will help you with anything in your unit and usually get to you in a few days.
- Fun building events - goat yoga, drinks at Le Sel, catered event on the roof
- Convenient location - lots of restaurants/bars in walking distance, close to the Gulch and downtown

Cons:
- Parking garage is a little bit annoying, tight spots, blind spots and sometimes the exits get congested
- Some growing pains as this is their first year in operation, but the staff has been responsive and develops solutions

For anyone wanting the convenience of living in the city, I'd be sure to check out the Aertson.

Fabulous tanning experience. The ladies that work here are wonderful and do a phenomenal job. My only experience has been with the tanning, and It always comes out perfect. Gorgeous color that surprisingly lasts for quite some time. They even offer touchups within a few days. Highly recommend.

3.5 stars! Cool live music! extremely $35 a plate ... expensive! Unique dishes, chicken was fall off the bone wish it had a stronger seasoning tho and veggies were amazing!!  Lasagna unique different  and tasty but not the best I have had! Cream puff desert was incredible! ... Bread and butter was lame! go if you have time to relax and enjoy yourself!
Very, very friendly staff.  Went in for a tire repair. Was taken in right away with no problems.  Would definitely recommend Millevoi Brother's Service Center.  AAA++++ service!
Excellent service. Very friendly and amazing attention  to detail.   We all laughed and chatted and it was a fun appt. I got a gel mani and pedi   I will be back

This place used to be call Lorenzos Pizza and was around my whole childhood with amazing food. Recently, "Lawerence" Pizza bought them out and cut the restaurant in half. Half pizza place, half Indiana restaurant.... 1 kitchen for both menus..
