Food is no longer the same. Service isn't the same either. I've had problems with them not answering the phone during business hours or having a delivery person. 

Wish Lorenzos never sold out to them.
Recommend the mousse and the carrot soup and their tacos. Exceptional and very nice place and help. Will be back again.
The waiting on the weekends SUCKS!  BUT, you can put your name in via Yelp, which is awesome!  The food is awesome; the vibe is great; decor is on point -- overall a great experience!

The fly is a great little spot here in New Orleans.

It's perfect for relaxing when the weather starts to warm up in the Spring. A lot of people have crawfish boils and picnics here or they just go and hang out with friends. You have a nice view of the river and you feel as if you are in another world when you are relaxing here.

I never would've thought I'd enjoy a sushi place with "inc" in the title. Especially since I have seen so many "Sushi Dot Com" type names pop up with awkward service and not-so-great sushi. Sushi Inc was not that experience. 

I was surprised by the freshness of the fish (I had the escolar) and pleased by the dumplings. The sushi and other entrees were reasonably priced and typical for the cuisine. It wasn't the best sushi I ever had, but certainly tastey and better than I expected. 

The cocktails I tried among a group of friends were a bit on the sweet side. So I recommend sticking with sake or beer.
Food is delish and there's something for everyone. The spinach eggs bene was great as were the breakfast tacos. Affordable and the staff is truly great. Super friendly and attentive. Will be back.

Steam is awesome! They have great burgers and during certain seasons "All you can eat crab legs." They have both food and drink specials every night of the week that are totally reasonable and many craft beers on draft. The building is set up really neat and resembles an old train car or something. They have a pinball machine which I always think is cool.  The staff is always really nice and friendly too!

The foods was pretty good. It gives you the street food truck full flavor but you got the sit in restaurant comforts. On top of that the portion is great relative to the price you pay. Prices are around 7-10 per person depends on your drinks and sides. 

The place is small with only one server. So you feel kinda trapped. Some people likes it some people don't, decide for yourself. On Top of that, you come in and they server you with these super spicy pickle carrots and Jalapenos.  Not a fan of that, I rather they give us salsas and chips. 

OverAll I would go back to this place, just to eat lunch not something I would be craving for.

We should have known upon arrival that this was a shit hole cause the front desk clerk made 4 phone calls while we were standing waiting to be helped told us our room would be ready in an hour and she would call us and that never happened. The night clerk is a pervert and stalked us from getting out of the cab and walking up to the room. The staff was very horrible and rude. Would never ever stay here again even if it was free. Don't recommend this shit hole. Me and my 2 girlfriends stayed here so if your around 30 dont stay here.

The breakfast here is really great. It was so good that I went ahead and ordered one of each of the three cheesesteaks, thinking they would be similarly great. Two of out three of them were a bit disappointing. The original Philly cheesesteak meat tastes like vegan bacon, I think it might actually be vegan bacon (and not good vegan bacon at that). The chicken cheesesteak was dry and looked kind of like a lobster roll. The Buffalo chicken cheesesteak was very good! Still giving 4 stars because all of the other breakfast food I tried was really really good. I would definitely go back to try the rest of the menu, but I hope they improve the cheesesteaks.

I liked Scarlett Begonia. The food was good and the environment was lovely. I would have given this a five star review except for one thing. The tables have open bowls with cubed sugar and some sort of designer-salt, both of which are designed to be taken with one's fingers.  So, riddle me this; what happens if the person who sits at the table before you uses the bathroom and doesn't wash their hands? Just a thought - maybe tongs?
Coffee house in a strip mall...not a great location. Owner was nice though.

Outstanding fusion food with wonderful flavor.  This place is casual, but the food is 5-star gourmet good.  The tostones and mofongo are awesome and easily added to any order.  Counter service is friendly, the place is immaculately clean.  If you're looking for lots of flavors this is the place to be!
I keep finding myself here throughout the week. Always a good stop for a glazed donut. Weckerly's ice cream sandwiches earn a fourth star

Beautiful and attentive ( kept clean)  beach... a jewel to visit and stay... ample parking and low beach fee ... sand is white and clean ... water can be both calm and a little wavy ... arrive early for closer parking .... there is also some fishing spots with curb parking.... opens at 8. Am

Not gonna give a business less than 5 stars during a quarantine. Thank you for being open. However was not very impressed. A little pricey for what you get and average. The collards greens were way too acidic and tasted like vinegar. The baked beans and Mac n cheese were good. The turkey was cooked well. The chicken was over cooked and dry. But I will say the variety of different BBQ sauces were very good! Also I really like the fries which were like a cross between chips and wedges, really yummy.

Great place to stop for a drink if you are visiting the Garden District.  Very nice historic hotel and very cool bar.  Also great place to hang outside and enjoy the beautiful grounds.  Did not stay at the hotel, but have been here many times for drinks.

Good place for beer, but it's really lacking in service. It took forever to get our food. We tried to get attention from the waitress but she just walked passed us and ignored us. We were very sure she could hear us but she didn't bother to stop. When we were ready for our check, she said, "there are only 2 of us working here, you have to go inside to pay your bill." What a attitude!!!! We will never come to this place again.

YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED. DO NOT ENTER

I hate this restaurant more than Melissa. 

Don't hate them because they're blind, hate them because they're unkind. The world famous Jell-O shots were "out of season" .. come on Elijah, that's not a good reason.  The oysters were good, but that's where it ended. I came came with a smile, but left hangry and offended. We were sitting there trying to get lit, waited an hour and half and didn't get sh*t.  All we can say is eat at your own risk, you'll order chicken tenders but end up with fish... the tacos were bland, the salsa was gone.. i shouldn't complain, but all the orders were wrong.  In moments of silence all we could hear was our shit talking waiter giving others our beers... we asked for the manager, he couldn't be found, he's probably hiding somewhere underground. A birthday surprise might be too much to ask, this bunch definitely wasn't up for the task.  All they do quickly is take away your stuff, but if you're not ready, it's okay- NO RUSH. And if this poem puts a smile on your face, it's more than we got from this horrible place.

DO NOT PLACE TO GO ORDERS FROM HERE!!! The company i work for orders from here every few months and they always screw up orders. Yesterday was the third and final time they screwed mine up. Ordered a hot chicken sandwich with sides. I got a chicken sandwich with nothing on it, including spice. A piece of dried chicken in a bun. No sides. And missed giving someone else their order completely. Went back to get our order right and AGAIN didnt even give my sides. Twice in one day. Im done with this place.

I love, love, love Costcos!!!!  They have sooo many great items and deals. Some of my favorite items are the cheese danishes, can't beat a tray for $4.99, they're large, flaky and delicious. The pizza is amazing and $10.73 for a 18in is a steal. The Morton's pot roast is amazing!!!! The mango salsa and jalapeño artichoke dips are wonderful. The pretzel bread with butter is delicious. The pet beds are a steal, extra large and cedar scented. I can go on and on......

At first glance this seems like a sweet spot. Although, I can only get three stars based on my unsatisfactory experience with the sandwich that I ever bought. I thought that seven dollars for a sandwich was quite reasonable, then I realized it's that low because it's small and barely had any meat. I got the artisan bread which was quite dry as artisan bread usually is. Seems like only one maybe two slices of meat. No cheese. Even with honey mustard it was still the driest sandwich I've ever eaten. Could not finish.  I'd rather pay $10 or $12 for a completed sandwich that doesn't turn into dry concrete in my mouth. I recommend you spend the extra money for the extra meat on your sandwich. Then you will have a regular amount of meat. Oh, but the interior atmosphere is a hipster daydream. So maybe that will help your decision

Don't go here for the food on the 6th floor. Overpriced for mediocre sushi. The 'boom boom shrimp' is not real boom boom, it's breaded shrimp with chili sauce. Calamari was low quality and chewy.  The 2 stars are for the amazing red velvet cake we got on the first floor. It em really was one of the best red velvet cakes, I've had - better than most in that the icing was mildly sweet - not crazy sweet like traditional recipes. The decor was fantastic and the servers were nice.

Would give no stars if possible.. Me and my husband went to this dealership looking to purchase a new car walked around for about 15-20 min not one sales rep came out and asked to assist us mind you my husband is black .. 2 white women came up as we were leaving and all the sales reps then came out asking if they need assistance while still not even acknowledging we were there.. racism at its finest.. highly disappointed.

I love Keke's! They have a lot of good options for omelets, pancakes, stuffed French toast, etc. I have never been disappointed whenever I've gone to Keke's at all locations I've been to. Service is always great. Food is always delicious. Coffee is good. I do wish they had a couple more varieties of Bennies. I also need to check out their lunch menu one of these days.

Horrendous staff! So rude and no empathy! Wait times are even worse than any emergency room in town. The "doctor" seems to be going senile - I can't believe that a facility exists anywhere else that is run worse than this welfare-scamming joke that's just shuffling as many court-ordered patients through for the most money it can bill the state for... Do not go here for medical care- it's not what they provide!!

I love trying new places to eat & went here with a friend for lunch.
The pizza was so-so but the staff was RUDE & acted like the customers were a bother to them.
Will not be back!

We came to try the "All you can eat Snow Crabs and Catfish" at Stingrays in Kenner...NOT!!!! It is DECEIVING. You get your first plate and then have to ask for the next...THEY HAVE THE NERVE TO ASK "HOW MANY pieces?" Then, if you let another in your party taste something from your plate, they call it "food sharing" and refuse your next service. "No food sharing" is NOT stated on the menu, the commercials, nor by the waitress!!
DON'T GET DUPED!!!! Save your money and time and go to Treasure Chest or Harrah's  for TRUE "All you can eat Snowcrabs!!"

The staff was very friendly and right there if you needed something. The food had a great taste, but the chips were to thick. The blue corn on the Sopes were way to thick and were still doughy. You get a good amount of food. Nice atmosphere. I really didn't like the way management makes you do a review before you can use your yelp coupon and the waitress had to take a picture if it, and show it the management.

We love Southwest Airlines!!! Flight attendants are always friendly and often funny, SWA seems to care who they hire as flight attendants. We always fly with SWA.

I know the recent news of the Engine explosion is painful, but I'd simply like to say that it's the only fatality SWA has ever had in their history
(from what I was able to find in my basic research.)

Tinto
114 S 20th St, Philadelphia, PA 19103 (215) 665-9150

While it is normally my want to display a photo of the outside of the building, this photo says everything I want to say about Tinto: sophisticated, intimate, Basque, cozy and sultry. Basque of course refers to the region of North Central Spain that straddles the area between north central Spain and south western France.  This tapas restaurant serves to reflect this fusion and Chef Jose Garces certainly does a marvelous job fusing all the best of France and Spain.  Coincidentally, Tinto is Spanish for "stained" and "red" as in red wine, so the hundreds of bottles of wine throughout the restaurant is of course no surprise.

The restaurant is an intimate setting with an open style kitchen (see back of this photo) with a luxurious bar with small rectangular wooden shelves lined with hundreds of wine bottles. It is lit with gorgeous incandescent lighting which casts a golden glow over everything--the perfect date spot.

Without further adieu, allow me to present the culinary masterpiece that is Tinto.

Since this blog is titled, Fine Food, Good Drinks, I can't help but mention the sangria I chose to accompany my meal. Sangria is a typical beverage from Spain and Portugal. It normally consists of wine, chopped fruit, a sweetener, and a small amount of added brandy. Chopped fruit can include orange, lemon, lime, apple, peach, melon, berries, pineapple, grape, kiwifruit and mango. A sweetener such as honey, sugar, syrup, or orange juice is added.

Pintxos: OLIVES House Marinated Mixed Olives.  Pintxos appears at the top of the Tinto menu, and I learned it was the Spanish word for "tapas".  The marinated olives were delicious and arguably one of the best dishes of the evening.  With so many to choose from there was not a clear winner in my book, however.

ALMONDS: House Smoked Marcona Almonds. Someone call the fire department because these almonds were smokin'. Well, not literally, but they were smoked and the flavorful sea salt, almonds and wooden smokey taste made this appetizer a delight to the palate. Marcona almonds, the "Queen of Almonds," are imported from Spain. Marcona almonds are prized for being more tender, sweet, and delicate than regular almonds, with a nice buttery flavor that is due to their high oil and moisture content.

GAZPACHO Goat Cheese Mousse, Strawberry, Rhubarb, Crispy Serrano. Jamón serrano is a type of jamón (dry-cured Spanish ham), which is generally served in thin slices, or occasionally diced.  Gazpacho is a chilled soup and this particular variety was fruity, summery tasting, sweet and creamy due to the goat cheese.  The Serrano ham was a nice accouterment with the salty cured meat balancing the sweetness of the fruit and cheese.  The rhubarb was an interesting addition to the dish, as I have never had a gazpacho served with it before. Rhubarb is a perennial vegetable, though it is generally used as a fruit in desserts and jams. You only eat the stalks, which have a rich tart flavor.

ARUGULA Serrano Ham, Mission Figs, Fried Goat Cheese, Spiced Almonds, Sevilla Orange Vinaigrette.  Probably the most interesting addition to the arugula salad here was the addition of the balls of fried goat cheese.  Absolutely delicious.  The Orange vinaigrette wasn't half bad either. Certainly cleaned the plate here.

DIVER SCALLOPS Quince, Uni Emulsion, Parsnip, Crispy Serrano.  You can see the little chunks of serrano ham mixed in with the greens.  The little yellow chunks are the quince, a yellow fruit similar to the apple. Quince is a small deciduous tree that bears a pome fruit, similar in appearance to a pear, and bright golden-yellow when mature.  The scallops can be dipped in the vanilla parsnip puree- which was amazingly creamy and sweet. 

ORGANIC CHICKEN Brussels Sprouts, Chorizo, Fingerling Potatoes, Herb Jus.  The chicken was fantastic, tender, sweet, loved the brussel sprouts.  The herb jus was rich and sweet. 

Postre (Dessert): Gateau Basque. Almond Cake, Vanilla Cream, Cherry Fig Jam, Pedro Ximenez Ice Cream.  The most interesting part of this delicious dessert was the Pedro Ximenez (sherry) flavored ice cream.  I would attest, the entire dessert could have been a giant bowel of the ice cream because that was by far the best portion of this dessert.

This was a good place for us to stay while on vacation in New Orleans. We walked everywhere and they took care of the golf clubs and arranged for my husband to play at some of the courses. It wasnt in the middle of everything but that worked out for us, we could still walk or take a cab, but we did end up walking a lot.
Dave is a very nice guy and he did a great job installing our SW and RO system! Very happy with his communication and knowledge. Happy I chose Sun Tap.

Great office, with caring doctors!  Might seem outdated, but I appreciate vintage!!! Nice staff and Dr. Caringi is top notch!! Very professional and takes his time ... I enjoyed going here better than Rothman.... something to
Be said for a small, local office. Keep up the good work!
Standard Italian fare, but _very_ good. We got a pasta with sausage and veal dish and they were both delicious. The outside patio on the water is nice. Definitely worth a stop for good Italian.
This is the beat mexican food I have had in the Clearwater area.  The atmosphere was wonderful and they had outdoor seating. Outstanding!

Loved this hotel.  Located in the center of everything on 17th and Sansom: historic stuff, shopping, and great dining!  The staff here was so courteous, friendly, and efficient.  We got some nice perks like free cocktails in the evening and coffee and tea in the morning.  Although I didn't bring a pet with me they allowed them, which I think is great.  Never heard any barking, meowing, or other domestic animal sound in my 2 night stay.  Would definitely stay here again.

I've been here a few times, mainly takeout, but I can easily say that I've had better korean fried chicken. Although the chicken is more crispy than that of Bon Chon's, the flavor is lacking. I cannot really describe it but the sauces (all of them) do not have a great aftertaste.

The bubble tea is ok but nothing amazing. It is also difficult to rate this as a bubble tea place with Tea Do, Vivi and Mr. Wish in close proximity.

The worst part of this is that I feel that anyone that takes my order has a GTFO attitude. I'm ok with rude customer service and the only thing I appreciate is that it is consistent. 

Overall: 2.5/5 small size chicken wings with great crispiness but lackluster flavor.

Officially this was the first place we ate on our Southern tour (Nashville, Memphis, New Orleans- get ready my friends).  My bf and I are staying with good friends in Nashville and they recommended this place for lunch.  There is a whole lot to choose from cold salad to hot meats and sides.  I ordered the Traditional club which had ham, turkey, and bacon with lettuce and tomatoes on 3 layers of of Texas.  Along with that I got a side of sweet potato casserole.  Man the sweet potato casserole is the real deal.  It was sweet and the whole pecans within gave it a great texture.  I could have eaten a plate full of just that and been satisfied.  My sandwich was never ending..  It was full of my and bacon was cooked just right and wasn't too crispy or soft.  My bf ordered the cranberry mango salsa pork tenderloin which was moist and flavorful.  He mentioned that he was always nervous about having fruit with his meat but felt that this place got it right.  One of our friends raved about the chicken salad and the cornbread and kept coming up with amazing flavors that he thought were in both.  Needless to say at the end of the meal we were all happy campers and I was ready for a nap.  

This place is really spacious and apparently frequently hold receptions and other events there.  If I lived here I would definitely plan an event there just to have their awesome food again!
Bad decor, horrible coffee... The food is average but they are quick and no wait on a Saturday morning.  If you need a quick breakfast, you can overlook the mediocrity of the place.

Excellent food. Very impressed on my first visit. Kids loved their grilled cheeses and Mac & Chz too. 
I'm sure we'll be back... Several times. 
Two thumbs up.

I love this place, it's so comfortable and warm and cozy! Has an old school vibe to it for sure. The pan roasts ? they're delicious ! My husband and I come here at least four or five times a year and get the same thing. we love it the servers are nice especially Elaine she's super sweet and attentive !

One of the two best steak shops in this area In my opinion when you walk out of they're door you know without a doubt there's a good sandwich in your bag! That's saying something because I have to travel through three towns to get there.

Amazing food here! Fresh and authentic  I had carpaccio, which was fresh, thinly sliced and served with arugula, high quality olive oil and grilled bread! As a main course I got seafood spaghetti - also amazing! I thinks you can't go wrong here, including good quality drinks.

Love yourself , and want to care for your body, soul, and spirit ? Six Fishes will change your life, as it has surely has improved mine !! With a combination of modalities offered including : Accupuncture , Cupping , Massage ( with CBD oil , Hot Stones , and more - oh la la : ) , Nutritional Consultations, and so much more, they utilize the best of both Eastern and Western medicinal practices to resolve your health related pain and concerns .Six Fishes will become your new holistic health home !

 Special thanks to the inspired care provided by Cara - Nutrition , Kelly - Accupunture, Zia - admin. & beyond , Rachel - life affirming massages ; & all the Six Fishes team community . Congrats Six Fishes , on your life affirming success !

Dentist area wonderful but unfortunately they are horrible with your claims. I had to pay close to $1500 out of pocket for my dental work in July of 2018 because they kept resubmitting my claims with the wrong policy number. It's been over 1 year and i finally got less than half of that back from my claims. I had to call several times last year, month to month to check on my claims. Finally i requested to get a copy of the claims so i can submitted it myself. That also took several calls. i finally did receive it, immediately i noticed  my  policy number was wrong. There is no  checks or balanced in place for denied claims, they don't hold themselves accountable for their mistakes and are really bad at responding. Actually they don't even call you back. They had the audacity to say that it's my insurance company fault when they kept submitting the wrong claims with the wrong policy number. They don't apologize for their mistakes or hold themselves accountable.  It's really unfortunate because they do have good and caring dentist. Do not let the staff fool you with their smiles because if you end up having an issue with your bill is going to be a horrific experience. Even when i tell them in going into their office to clear things up, they go to lunch and i had to wait. I highly suggest another office.
Had such an amazing experience! Highly recommend. Such a friendly staff and a beautiful space. Heidi is the BEST for massages. Im from out of town and would definitely come back.

This is a very unorganized company that has alot of evil employees and practices and there is obviously very little to no oversight or accountability from within.  Calling, or attempting to call, their corporate office is fruitless because their are many wicked employees in place who will block those efforts by making excuses about the corporate number being busy and they'll either give you another number to call or repeatedly send you back to the same number to be aggravated while you wait in vain for a very long time.  The managers at the branch where I applied (Memphis, TN) look so sloppy and gross and act so gross, ghetto and unprofessional and they seemingly overlook the applicants who show up presenting a professional appeal and favor the ones who show up looking and behaving like ratchet trash...which says a whole lot about the overall company.  The personnel that I've had contact else lie...lie...lie and whatever they say is what goes as far as employment decisions go from with that den of demons.  I can't believe that a company like this actually exists and they are really being allowed to operate this way.

The owner did an excellent job of making this place relaxing. It's in a busy strip mall on Dale Mabry but when you enter, the ambience is relaxing. Ive floated elsewhere but this was my 1st time in an enclosed pod. It is way more spacious in the pod than I expected. I was able to close it completely with no anxiety. I appreciate the ear plugs, soft towels, q-tips, body soap and shampoo provided in the rooms. After your session, there is an area in the back with body lotion, face lotion, hair dryer, etc. Highly recommend this relaxing experience!
The rabbit entree and the sweetbreads appetizer are some of my all-time NO favorites.

Fortunately, the food was worth the very long wait.  It took more than 30 minutes for the appetizer of ahi tuna on a Tuesday night, when we were the only customers to have entered in the past half hour.  The tuna was well done in a thick crust of sesame seeds with a sriracha aioli and coconut  curry cream.  All of it was good, but it was too much going on.  Just pick one of the two good sauces. 

After another 30+ minute wait, the entrees arrived.  The lamb loin was the winner.  It was well cooked, medium, served over orzo with merguez sausage and a minimal amount of feta and olives.  On the bottom was an eggplant puree.  The other entree of duck breast, was very well prepared (although it desperately needed salt), wrapped in a kale leaf and stuffed with duck confit, like a giant duck sushi roll.  It came with some berry jam, that was very good but too sweet for a dinner meal, and a wedge of terrifically crisp polenta and two little brussels sprouts.   

Also, it was good that the wine list included some good Old World styles and not just BC wines.

With a faster kitchen, this would have easily have been a four star meal.
Worst parking lot ever. You'd think that Wells Fargo has enough funds to repair the asphalt.

Pate's and porc-loving products which i discovered at the Rittenhouse Square Farmers market on Saturday AMs - but their main place is in RTM.

Pricey - but delicious.
If you don;t eat pork - try the lamb pate.  DIVINE!

Oh - and they sell pre-cooked/pre-seasoned carnitas.  Neat-o!

I've used this company twice. The first time, Jaime and Stacey came out and they were awesome! Last month I called them again because I had broken Hunter Douglas blinds that needed repair. I got John this time and it didn't go well. He arrived 20 minutes late without even an apology or acknowledgement that he was even late. He took measurements and said he'd have to place a special order. When I asked him to let me know the cost before he ordered, he looked at me like I was crazy (like no one ever requests an estimate before purchase?!!). 
After he left, I never got a call back. I tried calling a couple of weeks later and left message. Still no call back. It's almost like he didn't want the business. Think he needs to retire..... Bummed to have such a crappy experience after the first time was so great.

I love this place! This is the first time I've ever had lash extensions and was apprehensive when I called for more information. I spoke with Amber and she was outstanding. She answered all my questions and even offered up personal experiences with her own lashes. I was scheduled an appointment later that week with Alyssa. I was still a tad nervous On appointment day but Alyssa listened to what I wanted and gently and meticulously applied the most amazing lashes EVER!! I will definitely be back for refills and look forward it!

This place is huge! It's connected to the convention center and has a Starbucks and at least two bars. I was here for a quick stay and I was impressed. You don't even have to leave the place to get a workout as just walking the halls of this place is a workout in itself. The rooms are spacious and they have a fridge. The staff are friendly even when they're confused. I would definitely stay here again if I decided to go back to Philly.

Very good food! The prime rib was tender and moist, the Cole slaw and the beans were delicious. It was one of those meals that you wish would never end. Haven't had a prime rib that good in a long time. The staff was friendly and helpful, and the service was lightning quick.

We came here because we wanted to try a new all you can eat sushi place. It was pretty good. The girl who seated us was so nice and very friendly to my kids. They definitely did not have enough staff though which is why I took a star away. The sushi was really good. I also love the pizza rolls. My kids had some mochi for dessert and liked it. I would like to go back and try some ramen and the tempura ice cream.

This really wasn't that great. The service was good, and the food came out very fast, but a few things were disappointing. We ordered a "salad" which turned out to be a mountain of snowcrab slathered in mayonnaise and served with one asparagus spear and three slices of avocado on the side. The sushi we ordered was all right, but for the amount of money I spent on a dinner for two, we could have had a better time somewhere else.

I used to love this place and went there at least once a week. Previously I would have easily rated them five stars! Their coffee is delicious and I love the pumpkin bread! Unfortunatly the last two times I have been there, both in the last week, I went through the drive through and got a gal I haven't dealt with before. She has shoulder length dark redish hair and just seems to be too caught up with her conversation with the people inside. Don't get me wrong I am the first to start a conversation with a client however, you need to pay attention to the person ordering and trying to give you money. I needed to order three coffees, I ordered the first one and before I could even ask for the other two she went back to her conversation ignoring me completely. I waited for a moment before saying excuse me, can I order another two drinks? Oh yeah she replied and I gave her the rest of my order. I ordered two specialty coffees and a black coffee. Due to her not really paying attention while I tried to order she instead gave me three specialty coffees. Not what I ordered. I am really disapointed and feel they should really have someone at the window with a better ability to multitask. I also wanted to order a muffin but never got the chance. Maybe you should try going in instead of the drive thru.
By far the freshest tasting donuts on the planet. The smiles from the staff are worth the cost alone. Spudnuts + 8 pots of steaming coffee to choose from = happy mornings. Great way to start the day!

I have been here 3 times. It seems to always be a little short staffed so you won't get to order right away, this doesn't really bother me that much though. 

Staff has always been nice and courteous.

Korean bbq tacos are delicious.
They have a sauce bar for you to make your own sauces, I've never been adventurous to try to make one so I stick with the basics.

Parking can be a little hard since there are other businesses and an apartment complex next to it.

Overall, this is a great place.

Ordered the Beef & Lamb gyro on a Tuesday for delivery. Food was brought very quickly, and it was SO good. It was my first time ordering from Opa Opa, but it did not disappoint at all. Really excited to have a good place in town who can deliver great Greek food. Got the baklava on a whim as well, and that was great, though a bit pricey for how much you get ($5 for one piece). The amount of meat/toppings on the gyro made up for that though. Definitely a great place to order from

El Camino has grown on me.  My last review was pretty acurate.  El Camino is a bit kitschy, but the staff is always cool, beer rotates more frequently, and they have late night happy hour.

Veggie wings are one of the best fake meat apps around town.  Four TVs for all the bros to watch sports.  Also, this place is oddly loud.  Plenty of nice outdoor seating though for nice evenings.
Service was out standing! I loved there small sidewalk cafe. We shared some small plates among the three of us. They had several vegetarian options as well. Oh and you MUST try the home made pasta!
Absolutely delicious high end Mexican. My wife had the seafood soup, I had the Mole Negro. Hibiscus margarita tasted like more. Wonderful creative ambiance.

Standing here Father's Day and counted 36 open seats but they have 50 people waiting inside and outside for a table. They can't manage the place.  4 ladies standing up front taking names and wiping down menus instead of working.  Servers are not appearing they are in a rush for anything.  Looks fully staffed.  Why are they telling people an hour and a half wait when they all walk in and see all those empty seats?  Then we literally got a haircut next door and checked in before that.  They said there were two people ahead of us when we went there and then suddenly four and still more people standing outside than there were eating inside.  Left.  Many people walking in and hearing hour and a half and seeing all those empty tables.

Sheraton was a prime location for our rehearsal dinner at Amada (amazing) and our wedding at the Horticultural center. We booked 46 rooms and they were all very neat and clean. There are of course many fancier hotels around but this one was affordable for the majority of our guests and the staff was really pleasant and accommodating. The hotel lobby and the bar were also fun places to just get together with a rather large party.

I grew up near Chinatown in Philadelphia, loving Chinese food. I am so grateful to have No. 1 Chinese nearby with free delivery, usually within 30 minutes!!!  The egg rolls arrive steaming hot, the homemade mustard will clear your sinuses, the meats are flavorful and the vegetables are still crispy. I do miss hot tea, but their great food more than makes up for that!!
Great wine. We have been drinking wine from here for the last 6 plus years. Friendly staff and fair prices. We actually had wine we bought from there yesterday this evening!! It was terrific. Thanks

If you like white pizza, this is your destination. Toby's Little Italy has the best white pizza on the planet. For us, it is 48 miles one way from Brandon, Fl and well worth the drive.
The staff is always friendly and accommodating. The place is tidy and organized but it gets to be close quarters when the place is full. A friendly local crowd of regulars makes for easy conversation. Usually about how good the pizza is. The decor is 70's retro including the game room in the back.

This is definetly an interesting place. They have cheap cheeseburgers that are worth it and there pizza ranks pretty high if you close your eyes and you imagine your self eating somewhere that probably isnt infested which cockaroaches! XD Its a nice place to hang out with the guys.

The atmosphere in this restaurant is really nice and is great for a nicer brunch or meal. The staff are also really friendly and accommodating. The food is also delicious and there are lots of options that all sound amazing. The only thing is that the food is a little bit pricier than other options around, but this place is a great place for a nice meal! Would recommend the Eggs Benedict with Avocado :)

Saw their review and profile on Yelp and went there when I came to town.  Both the Indian and the Thai food was great.  We had some delicious lemongrass soup, naan bread, Chicken Tikka Masala, and Thai Ginger Chicken.  Great meal, a bit pricey... but worth it.  Best Thai Ginger Chicken I've had in a long time.  Good news, you can make a reservation on-line.  Bad news, they don't confirm it back to you via email.

Did I drag the family here specifically for that Rocky statue?  (I plead the fifth.)

The line was out the wahzoo and when we finally reached the cashier (30+ minutes) we realized why it was so packed - 'Pay What You Want' for the first Sunday of the month.  (You peeps only coughing up a dollar?  Shame on you!)

Countless pieces of amazing artwork?  Check.
Lots of pretentious goofballs studying the art and blocking your view?  Check.
Two eight-year-olds in tow constantly snickering and making sure you see the naked sculptures and paintings?  Check.
Being tricked to walk through the gift shop after the very first exhibit you see?!  Check!

Excellent all around.

Kalamata's is just okay.  I haven't been to the original, but I'd heard it was good so we decided to stop in to the Brentwood location.  I should have read the Yelp reviews first.  The prices are high for the portions...  $5 for pita/hummus that doesn't come with enough pita (reference photos) and $2.25 for a can of Coke.  Yes, an actual can...  It just doesn't provide value for money, in my opinion.

As for the food, it's not "authentic" Greek or anything but more of a fusion if you like Greek and Mediterranean stuff.  I think if you want a salad for lunch, this place might accomodate well.  The food was fresh, and fairly tasty, but I was surprised that the pocket sandwiches came out cold, not hot.  My husband was disappointed that his "beef and lamb" gyro pocket was just beef and not warm.  The spanakopita called "Spinach Pie" was more of a roll vs. flaky phyllo layers.  Just not what we're used to...  we love Greek Cafe in Cool Springs, and I think Taziki's is better than this Kalamatas location too.

The folks working there were very kind, and I even saw one treat a toddler guest to a free cookie, which was heartwarming.  Just wasn't enough to make up for the rest.

Really very underwhelmed, and we won't be coming back because there are better alternatives in both taste and price.

I love this place. I'm not sure how long we've been coming here but I know we started going when they were at their old location and were upset when it closed. We were so happy when we saw they re opened in a new location. Anyway, their food is great, reasonably priced and they couldn't be nicer. Every time we go in they thank us for coming in. Their burgers are the best. You ask them to cook it medium and they cook it medium (which never happens at other places). One time they made a mistake on my boyfriend's bison burger. They didn't try to be sneaky and bring it out. They came out right away and were honest about it. Unfortunately it was the last bison patty they had that day. They refunded him the full price. I felt bad as this is a small business but they insisted. It's things like this that keep us coming back. I recommend Basically Burgers to anyone.

They never make sizzles. Other then mornings every-time I order a sandwich takes forever with people just not paying attention "working" ...I had to update my review. By far the worst service wawa. I'll order a sandwich or expresso shot and just watch EVERYONE ignore it and keep on going on with their doing laughing and talking but not looking at the screen three days in a row I had to remind them about my espresso shot after five minutes of waiting and no one 's making anything,not even like their busy just oblivious of customers

I've only been here once, but liked it. Four of us shared the pretzels with beer cheese (deeeelicious!) and a flatbread. We liked both options. The beer selection on draft and to take home is much bigger than you'd expect for a small place. Pretty chill atmosphere with a few tables and decent amount of bar seating. You probably couldn't bring a huge group here, but it's good for a small group of friend for a chill evening. Beer prices not bad, but definitely double check to see the size of drink you're getting.

After bbkings was rude to our larger party we went to old spaghetti factory. I've been to one in salt lake and remembered liking it- this one was no different! Service was great, food was good, price was spot on. 
The wait staff was pleasant, very friendly and quick. I got the baked chicken and thought it was great. Just enough food. We also got a salad and spumoni. 

Consistently good food.

Great customer service.  The staff are well trained, helpful, kind and courteous. Unfortunately the one type of vehicle I wanted to rent(all-wheel drive) they did not have without ordering from a different location.

I had a really good experience at the Cup. It's been years since I've been here for food. Our group decided on brunch and someone said "Let's try the Cup." We had a fun waiter who gave me a hard time when I asked "Do you guys have mimosas?" They have an open bar, even in the morning. I enjoyed my Huevos Rancheros (for $9.50). The flavors were pretty amazing. The tortillas were just right, and the cheeses were so tasty. The hotel potatoes were cooked just right, not hard or falling apart, with a gentle seasoning on top and a few red and green bell peppers were thrown in for color and flavor. The orange juice I shared with my husband tasted fresh squeezed. 

My husband made his own omelet from the Congressional Omlete Bar (which was not a bar, it's just a name). This is his favorite thing to do, so I was hoping it'd be good. He added some sausage, bell peppers, some asadero cheese and tomatoes. He was so pleased when he had his first bite. He also got hotel potatoes and cleaned his plate. The rest of our group got food that they loved and everyone cleaned their plate. Especially the guy who got the Gunpowder (turkey chorizo, eggs asadero cheese). His was stacked in a bowl and looked huge, my friend really enjoyed the flavors.

We were treated good, had a nice time and our meals were not overly expensive, even though the Cup feels a little ritzy.

Randomly walked in this place for a late lunch during a business trip. Quite good. Fried buns have interesting stuffing: meat and mushroom and bamboo shoots. Very tasting. The soup with the noodles is great. The complimentary house made pickled bok choy is addictive and goes well with the noodles. I had a lot.

Dr. Cirka's office told my wife and I, at two separate appointments, with two separate insurance plans, that we were covered for night guards. They specifically said that they had checked our insurance plans and that we were covered. In my wife's instance, they brought a calculator into the room to show her the cost and how much she would get back.

After submitting the claims, both were denied.

Since they have our money, they don't care about the circumstance they put us in and did not attempt to correct their mistake beyond writing a two-sentence appeal letter to the insurance company.

They did not offer an office credit and denied my request for a refund. They are unwilling to answer why we were told that we were covered.

Therefore I believe Dr. Cirka's office did not actually check our insurance plans, and lied to us about our insurance coverage in order to convince us to purchase night guards for $427 each.

They are a BBB Accredited business, and so I have filed a complaint regarding their deceptive practices.

Do not patronize this dentist.

I don't understand how this place remains viable. We go about once a year hoping it has improved. Unfortunately, this last visit has proven that everything here has remained the same. 

The food is adequate, at best. The biggest detractors was the service. Incredibly slow. Two hours for a burger and a sandwich? Not acceptable. There was two servers for the Sunday dinner crowd. 

I'd go to the McDonald's that's in the shared parking lot before eating here again.
I love Bravo Pizza! The food is great and the owners are so sweet and absolutely passionate about their food.  I love the new location as well.

If you go at the right time, there is little to no wait. At least, I've been there twice, and there wasn't for me. Bravo to the fact that the vacuums are free with the wash. My car was so bad inside I vacuumed for over an hour! I cannot imagine how much that would have cost me at other car washes. The self pay kiosk is easy and quick, just roll up, follow the directions of the employees guiding you in and sit back and enjoy the show. The neon lighted soap was the best part. As far as prices, I feel they are comparable to other self-serve washes. Actually, it may be better since vacuuming is included!
Great food, good prices, good service!  Stopped in because of previous reviews and proximity to our hotel after a day of diving on ice! The Molcajete hit the spot! Not over priced for drinks either!

DanceWorks Indy is such a fun time! They have a variety of classes available to take, I was able to do a SWERK class and it was such a fun way to spice up a boring cardio routine and have fun with some amazing women. The instructor was encouraging and kept us motivated throughout the workout. I'm so excited to try out their other classes and find new ways to work out.

Whoa, madness..... this place is busy. Sounds blasting, lights flashing, music blurring. This place is A.D.D. at its finest. Memorabilia everywhere of the greatest artist to perform on this green earth. I've never stayed in the Hard Rock but I have played in the Hard Rock. I've sat at the tables a few times and busted out and also walked way with some cash. This night I did walk away with 800 bucks with an investment of 40 from one machine. I say walking away from only spending 40 bucks that night made me wrap it up and call it a night as far as gambling was concerned. Good times.

This review is only for the Pressure cooked fried chicken. I think their chicken is amazing! of course its not like mom used to make with flour and seasoning in a paper bag but damn close. The pressure cooker keeps the meat tender and juicy while cooking away the majority of fat.

Very nice restaurant within the Atlantis Casino. Excellent service, nice atmosphere. Must say the drinks are amazing and not skimpy on alcohol! Right Brain and I started our meal with well drinks, he a Martini perfectly made, I had a  Passiflora, a delightful mix of Ciroc Peach Vodka, Domaine de Canton Ginger Liqueur, Prosecco, Passionfruit Puree! Wow!
They served us a wonderful complimentary ceviche  appetiser, nom! For dinner Right Brain had the Seafood Fettucinne, excellent! I ate light with their signature creamy onion soup and a big bowl of steamed Manila clams, heaven! 
The meal ends with cotton candy. No, really, cotton candy that had a flavour I can't describe other than Yummy! We also received each, a box of homemade macaroons for snacking in the room. 
Thank you Bistro Napa for a great meal!

Came here on a spur of the moment trip as my friend and I had a couple of hours to kill.  We were warmly greeted by several of the people working here.  One of the employees offered to give us a look inside each of the rooms and a brief overview of each theme.  Given that nobody else was there at the time, we had our choice of room and opted for the Jail Break room.  Once we dressed up in orange jumpsuits and handcuffed (in the front of course - otherwise, we'd still be there!) we were placed in a locked jail cell with the objective of breaking out.  I won't go into detail about specific objectives, but you are given specifics before the clock starts.

Overall it was a great experience.  We did have an issue with one of the combination locks to the point where someone had to come into the room and open it for us.  We knew the combination, but somehow it wasn't unlocking for us.  We might've spent too many minutes on this single lock, but luckily the employee came in and saved us so we could move on.  Afterwards, they did tell us that a lot of people have issues with that same lock.  Other than that, everything went quite smooth even though challenging.  The hints were spot on and necessary at times.

There are other rooms here that I would like to try and I would definitely return.  What I especially like about this place compared to other escape rooms is that you most certainly can do it with just two people.  Other places say that you need at least four people.  I'm not sure that I would want to get paired up with strangers, but who knows, that could be fun too.

I'm a crazy cat lady / statistical programmer.  I am not cool.  The yelp reviews mentioning the resident cat influenced my decision to come to fringe.  But I am super glad that I did.  Salons often make me feel even more uncool than I actually am.  People who become stylists are generally good at hair/makeup/style (that is why they are called stylists).  I am not good at those things, so I often feel judged and uncool at salons.  Cool stylists look at me with mild disgust in my frumpy clothes.  Fringe didn't make me feel that way.  In fact, they made me feel kind of awesome.  The people were nice.  The cat was nice.  The haircut made me feel/look cool (or as cool as a statistical programmer / cat lady can).  It was all good.

I love P'unk Burger and I'm super happy they can deliver. To give you some context I ordered the "big man on campus" burger during the Eagles NFC playoff Close enough to the end of the game I wasn't sure they would make it across Broad Street to deliver my food. They certainly did, and earlier than they thought they would. The all-American burger comes with cheese injected into the beef so if you eat it hot out of the kitchen you can be burned as if someone put hot lava in your food. The delivery time gives it just enough time to cool off so it is at wolfing temperature. French fries however show up as soggy soggy noodles. So leave the gun, take the cannoli.
Yousef is new owner, true professional, fast high end service. You will not be disappointed. Forgot those mall kiosk places!

Loved it!  Great Atmosphere, incredible employees (Zack was super cool and helped me and my friends get the lay of the land) all of the staff seemed pretty great.  The prices for drinks were the best I've seen yet in Philly and we happened to be there the night before the grand opening of the new Catering folks and got to sample the exceptional fried chicken.
Fabulous place that really honors a wide variety of art.
Love and looking forward to getting to know it better.
Oh! The parking was the easiest I've seen yet in Philly as well.

I was pleasantly surprised at how delicious the food is! The building didn't look very appealing and I expected fast food quality. However, the food had great quality and great flavor! This place was pretty busy, but worth the wait. Will definitely go back again.

My true rating is 3.5. This is the most convenient location to the BB&T pavilion to grab a quick bite before a show, but I'd try to get here early because it really filled up as show time got closer. They have your typical bar food and standard beer list. We arrived and got seated right away, but shortly after we arrived there was a line. We shared the loaded waffle fries as an appetizer, they were ok and not anything too special. I had the classic cheeseburger and ordered a side salad instead of the chips - the burger was good and the salad was pretty standard. I wasn't expecting much based on the reviews I read here, but a friend recommended it to me and for all necessary pre-concert needs it certainly did the trick. Our server was very nice but we didn't see here much, she was busy but it took her forever to get us a second round of drinks. Overall I would return prior to an event at BB&T again. Bonus: we got there early and the parking lot security guy said we could leave our car parked there for free if we spent money to eat at the victors pub, by the time we were heading to the concert there was a $30 parking sign at the same lot.

Decent Mexican food. Stays open late and has a good selection of items on the menu. Parking is nonexistent since it's in the downtown area, so either Uber here or get it delivered. 

The queso and quesadilla were really good. I'd also recommend the churros and tres leches. Staff was polite and service was quick.

I don't quite understand the mediocre reviews of this place! I am here almost every time I pass through Philly. Very good sushi, noodle/ rice bowls, chill atmosphere and I have only experienced good quality every single time.  Very friendly service as well. I love it!

My dad loves this place. I don't know how far we drove to get here, but it was over the hills and trhough the woods.

We got here, there is an old waitress from New York and some really hot waitress from some place where I want to be from. Of course we go with the old waitress, no nonsense there. I got the tuna melt with the minstreone soup that was about $7, my pops got cheesebuirger with steak fries, my mom got hashbrowns and toast. Everything was good, and fast.
Minstrone tasted straight from the can, but oh well, it was good. 
Next time, I think I'll try the hot waitress.

We live pretty close to BL but really never attempted to go because of the crowds. A random Thursday morning we happens to be available so made the short walk to check out the buzz. 

Honestly can't figure out why the buzz. Nothing i ordered was memorable except the use of salt and butter (welcome to the south).  We only finished part of our meal before moving on. 

The service was good, atmosphere not to dissimilar from the multitude of other options in the area.

If you are trying to check off a must do in Nashville then yes, you should go. If you are just starving and want a satisfying meal, there are too many other options. 

Definitely not worth standing in line for an hour.

Italian Affair has  grown from their roots in the nearby strip mall. 

This is a classy place one can come to enjoy some fantastic italian cuisine. As long as you're not seated near the (normally too loud) piano player, the ambiance is great for a date or a nice night out with your husband or wife. 

If you're not up for dinner, but want to enjoy some drinks, swing by the wine bar. If you're lucky, Angelo will take care of you. He knows his wines and cocktails and will ensure you're happy. 

If you want a louder, rowdier experience, head on to the outside bar and watch a game, catch some live music, or just enjoy the fresh air. 

This place really does offer everything.
Went for Christmas, 3rd year in a row. Couldn't be better.

Good food but I got something I didn't order and the waiter insisted he didn't hear me.  Whatever, I ate what they gave me and it was good but would have preferred that the waiter agreed that he made a mistake and maybe offered to cover a dessert or coffee...

Food was good, service was ok, but the downstairs restaurant/bar has some kitchen ventilation issues. There was a visible haze at one point. And now our coats will smell like grease until we dry clean them.

I love this place. The breakfast is excellent. Everything taste really fresh and made to order. The only thing I don't like is that there isn't much space in between tables and the customer service could use an improvement.
Was not very impressed by the food. The portions are big, we order the wrap and it was terrible. Will not be going back

The menu is super fun but most drinks are quite sweet. The gimmick of the throwback bar though left us a bit disappointed. Most of the music playing was recent and the tables and decor seemed a bit low-quality. It may be our group's expectations rather than the bar itself - I think we were expecting a more immersive experience and it was certainly quite lackluster in that regard. The waitstaff were very friendly and checked on us often.

Worth the wait!!!
 We waited 1.5 hour and omg so bomb I got the chicken and waffle spicy chicken my honey had a Philly, mom had the burger and omg is all I can say. Bartender was nice and took order while we waited for our table. Great place if you come in town.... drive from Memphis and OMG

I definitely recommend this restaurant. The ambiance is lovely and you can actually hear everyone at your table. I had the filet mignon which was flavorful and cooked exactly how I wanted it prepared. The hashbrown potatoes were just evilly good. Our server Brian was fantastic and was highly knowledgeable of the menu. I will be back! Great place.

Time for an upgrade.

My last experience at 943 definitely warranted another review.  After having dined previously and enjoying the food, I had felt that perhaps the service and feel of the restaurant was a little off.  I believe these issues have been corrected and they are just catching some wind in their sails.

Early tastings from the kitchen were the grilled sweetbreads and the grilled shrimp.  If there is one thing that this place excels in, it's grilling.  The two aforementioned items came out perfect.  They had that great grill flavor and were cooked to perfection.  The sauce on the shrimp was something sweet, spicy and smokey.  There might be magic happening back there.

My entree, the Entrana with chimichurri and crispy fingerlings was beautiful.  Not only did the steak have the awesome grill flavor I spoke about earlier, but the chimichurri brought a fresh and light feel to each bite.  The potatoes were also perfect, crispy outside, soft inside.  At this price point, it is one of the better values in the city.

If the previous courses weren't enough, their house-made flan just sealed the deal for me.  I've always though of flan as kind of blah.  Not anymore.

Today we spell redemption 9-4-3.

I've been to lots of antique stores and this one is now one of my favorites.  
Just the right size: not too big and lots of variety.
Very helpful staff.  Clean everywhere including the bathroom.
Found just what I was looking for at perfect price.
Lots of clothing, housewares, records, tools, books, jewelry and lovely trinkets

I am not sure if I just have good luck with this Wendy's but I love it. Sure it can be a bit slow but if I ever see the drive up line long I go inside. I have a lactose issue so I alway let them know to leave the cheese off and they always do. 

I guess I understand if a place is busy it might take a minute to get food. I also would rather have fresh fries than old ones. 

I do miss the Wendy's salad bar from the 90's.

I enjoyed my short walk around the museum. Having been a pharmacy doctorate student in my younger days I have a special connection with this museum. It is nice to learn where and how it all began. Even more interesting was the culture where voodoo and mysticism that was also existing during these beginning pharmacy in pre modern America times.
As authentic as it gets, no frills, unpretentious atmosphere. Excellent food at excellent prices. Great tacos el pastor.

It's your standard deliciously amazing Italian restaurant done by Olive Garden. The raviolis were top notch along with the most amazing dosage of sauce. 

I regret not looking at the specials before ordering, so I do recommend always doing that. I missed out on the Buy 1, Take 1 Home deal, which absolutely gives me LIFE. 

Their menu on its own literally makes my mouth water. This location also was very large which was nice and a good spot to remember for any big dinner groups.
Waited 35 minutes for 3 salads to go. After finally asking the had been ready for 15 min and just sitting there! The only saving grace was the salads were pretty good!

If I could give zero stars I would've! This is literally the worst place in the world. This week it's because all of the freezers were down so you couldn't get any products in those aisles. At least put a sign on the front door to let your customers know instead of wasting my time and money in your d**n store.

New in town, read Yelp reviews and made an appointment (nice feature) with Haythm. He's experienced, nice guy and I got a really good haircut for a fair price. Hayrhm probably my new barber in Tucson.

So listen this was the only thing open downtown and it's this little greasy diner with the best two ladies working there. These gals served the food up and waited on the tables. The best part was when they popped out and asked if everyone was okay at some point. Like what?!  They're the best. The chili Mac was tasty. My stomach was in pain after but that's what it's supposed to do. I call it a cleanse. The price was so right too. Four meals and drinks  for $40 AND as a welcome gift to St Louis we were gifted with some oatmeal stout cookies. Holler

I love this shop! My boyfriend's main transportation is bikes, so we go into Cherokee Street Bikes every week to get new tubes, tires, etc. From my experience Jeremy has always been helpful. He will do anything within his means to help you. From airing up tires to looking things up if he doesn't have it in the shop and he will refer you to someone else or he will gladly get the part shipped to his store for you. I have yet to get a bike, but when I do (I plan on getting one this year!) I will definitely be going through Cherokee Street Bikes! Oh and don't forget to say hi to Johnny Cash--the coolest cat ever!!

I've been coming here for almost two years and this place never disappoints with the caliber of massage therapists that work here. I love how accommodating the owner Karen is to my schedule. Today, I experienced a true gem. Magdalena is AMAZING! I was a therapist for 6 years so I can always appreciate a great and thorough massage. If you are looking for someone to correct some imbalances, you must see her. Super knowledgeable and has an amazing grounded energy about her. The decor is very relaxing and the prices are very reasonable. Always book for 90 minutes! You get the most value for your dollar and quite frankly, 60 minutes is not enough. I HIGHLY recommend this place. You will not want to go anywhere else!

This byo restaurant was definitely a great pick. Probably the best dim sum Ive had in Philadelphia. The xiao long bao and chicken dumplings weren't the best, but they were pretty good. The green beans and scallion pancakes were delicious and our beef brisket soup was the perfect last dish. The decor is simple and clean. Will be coming back soon.

Don't let the name deceive you.   This is more than a deli.  The menu us quite extensive and the quality of food is amazing.  I look foreard to my visits whenever I'm in town.  The owner, Kim, is attentive to all guests and makes you feel like family.  Let's not forget the amazing bakery that is part of the deli.  It's by far the cleanest bakery I've ever seen and the selection of cakes, cookies and pastries is endless and sure to satisfy your sweet tooth.  Julie in the bakery is always so friendly and helpful.  Love this place.

The only bad thing about this place is that I went there with my ex who's a major tool but the food is great I enjoyed steam clams good glass of red wine and the view of all the beautiful people was amazing excellent customer service can't complain about anything except for my ex who is a major tool

I've been to Nashville before and somehow managed to miss this spot. My kids love to check out different candy stores so we headed here. I've seen the great reviews, so I was excited. Upon walking in, the gentleman making candy was giving out free samples (was very nice as well). I'd say 75% of the candy on the right wall was gone. Therefore, our options were immediately limited. The barrel candy options were also not amazing. The ice cream selection in the back looked great! There were some other packaged candy options around the store, which was good! When we went to checkout, we got behind a family who was holding up the line. However, a young gentleman waved us over and quickly checked us out. I thought it was great how he made it a point to keep the line from growing.

I wish yelp would give the option to hand out bonus "extra credit" stars. My family and I absolutely LOVE Dr. Michelle Caldwell, we followed her from Baring because we wouldn't trust anyone else with our pets. I have worked with Michelle previously and therefore can personally attest to the incredibly genuine attitude she exhibits along with her high integrity. She cares about animals, she wants your animal to be at its absolute best and live a long healthy life, and she wants her clients to feel well cared for. This is not something that can be faked in a profession like hers. I'm always recommending her and Desert Hills (along with new addition Dr. Sara Hogle) to anyone who ever inquires about vet care. If you are on the hunt for a new veterinarian, these are your gals! I have never seen the other veterinarians that work here but I have only heard fabulous things about them.

[566]

The first time I visited this store was a ton of years ago when their designer Shreen offered to come to my home to help pick out the perfect picture and furniture.    She mentioned that I may want to swap where my TV is and move the direction of the room around.   I'm glad she suggested it as  I LOVE it to this day.   

The sectional pull out couch is still great after years of use.  

We've stopped in a bunch of times to pick up various pieces such as my beautiful office desk.

 Stopped in to look for an ottoman but I didn't find exactly what I envision but the staff was very helpful.  They showed me tons of options and patterns I may want to choose so that it fits with my couch.
I really enjoy this no pressure shopping.

It's a little more expensive then those discount furniture stores but honestly you get what you pay for ... . 

I'll definitely be back!

I go to Bread Co. once a week and everytime I come to this location I have to look for a seat before I can order. Came to this location the other day and looked around and saw there weren't any seats and turned around and left. They let people take up more room than they need and stay for way too long. This location was just moved into a new building and they should have thought of more space to sit.
Yummy food!! The Shaved Steak Sandwich and Fried Chicken & Waffle with Crispy Bacon was perfect.  Excellent server! Will definitely eat here again!

Sonora dogs & mexican sprite!!
If you've never had a bacon wrapped hot dog with diced tomatoes & grilled onions, you haven't lived yet!.. Typing this makes me want to drive to AZ.
I can't wait to go back.

Do NOT TRUST any one behind the pharmacy counter. They will lie to you and refuse to fill Ur proscription if it is for a controlled medication  They want to make you run out, regardless of how long you and your doctor have been working on which medications and at what dosage works best for you. 

They either believe your doctor is incompetent to prescribe, or you gave them a fraudulent proscription, or you altered it. No proof for any if those but refuse to fill until get confirmation from your doctor. If you point out you are not supposed to suddenly stop taking, they refuse to comment about depriving you of medication you need!

NEVER trust any Pharmacist there?!

Only moving to St. Louis a few months ago, we have already been to Ted Drewes quite a few times as it comes highly recommended by the locals. Overall, we love it - gotta love frozen custard! Definitely hard to fight the craving for Ted Drewes.

However, a few notes:
*The menu is pretty large and can be a little hard to follow. Everything we have tried so far has tasted great. Staff has been helpful in making recommdations.
*The ordering process, line and picking up your order can be a little chaotic and unclear. Although there is always a line, we have always gotten our order pretty quickly.
*There seem to be some inconsistencies with sizing and how certain items are made. We have ordered the same thing a couple of times and have received different size cups and the end product also looked different. May need to get all the staff on the same page. 
*Really wish they had chocolate custard. Mixing chocolate fudge/syrup with vanilla custard is just not the same. (Fellow choco-holics know this to be true.)
*No seating aside from a couple benches, so if you want to sit, there is plenty of parking for you to sit in your car (and trash cans in the lot).
Cheapest gas in the north valleys that I have found yet.  You do get the odd character in there every once in a while but that is to be expected.  Overall I have never had a bad experience here.

I am so glad they changed the rules way back when to allow straight girls into this place. Sunday Funday just would not be the same without a visit to this bar.

Order a Bloody Mary. Nice and spicy and they don't skimp on the booze. 

Tim, our bartender, is a great guy and really knows his stuff. 

Not a lot of quality beers on tap, so come here for the cocktails or a bottled brew.

Cool little place sitting on Penn's campus.  Gentlemen working there were conversational and friendly. I got a great haircut for $20- a little on the high side but worth the money. 

I gave the place three stars for one reason: I didn't see a single sanitizing bin for brushes, clippers, scissors, etc. They sat in a Tupperware bin on the counter. 

Excellent cut but if you are a germaphobe, steer clear.

Excellent salad (Johnny) -- chopped, fresh, nice balance of dressing-to-greens. Husband enjoyed his short rib torta, which he said came on some of the best torta bread. I was also impressed by the housemade corn tortillas on my son's cheese quesadilla.  The margarita was just all right for $8 - not super potent or flavorful, and the guacamole was remarkably garlicky. Server Giuseppe was very friendly and patient with our three year-old, who enjoyed the blaring country music and the old Westerns on the TV over the bar. Would return for sure and stick to beer!

Been coming here for some years.  The service is super fast and friendly, the food comes out fairly quickly, but for what it is the prices are a little high.  The different choices in freshly brewed iced teas are great.  The food has always been good and pretty consistent.  It has a diner homey feel to it.  Would recommend if you're in the area and want some good food.

I thought that Five Guys was one of the best out there, but my last two experiences at Five Guys left me wanting a real good cheeseburger.  I tried Sketch Burger also in Fishtown, but the burger was WAY too big and crumbled while eating it. 

I had heard of Shake Shack for the longest time and always wanted to go to one.  On the first decent sunny day in Philly, had lunch with a friend at this one.  Ordered my double cheeseburger sans tomato and added some onions.   I forget to ask to get the bun toasted, but they are already buttered & toasted!  It's a potato bun, and I HATE potato buns, but it was good. 

Perfectly juicy, but not overly greasy, it was great.  The burgers are supposedly cooked to medium by default, but mine was way on it's way to well done, but still damn good.  Next time I'll probably ask for medium rare and maybe I'll get medium.  The shake sauce was slaughtered on and it was good!

My only ding is that it can be a little pricey, but if you went to a Five Guys, or a restaurant, it would be about the same price.

Do yourself a favor and go here. This is real New Orleans. Stop with the Bourbon nonsense. You won't find kinder friendlier people or a more chill vibe than the LPP. This is a true neighborhood spot, that's as tiny as the hearts are huge. It's been here for decades and it's not going anywhere. 
Tell Rodney Dave from NY sent you.

Everybody talked ab this place is the best the lines are big so have a little patience in summer is super hot so they have water for everyone we ordered the white meat mild , it's a little spicy but great!!!!with pimiento Mac and cheese, fries, collard greens, cole slow and banana pudding all the sides were delicious servers are nice all was good

Yum Yummy Sammys of all kinds!

The staff was friendly, the menu diverse, and no qualms about "holds" on stuff i didn't want on my sandwich.  Plus, bacon makes everything better, so don't hold that one, ok?

I stopped by because I was in the neighborhood and remembered how yummy the sample was at a previous Yelp event, so I went in for a simple dinner being out and about and not feeling like cooking that evening.

My sandwich was great, and my BF loved the soft bread and had no complaints either (except, hmmm I don't see any bacon on MY sandwich ... honey I have no idea what you are talking about wink wink)

Everything tasted fresh and good quality meats, which is a must for a good sandwich.  I didn't stay to eat, but the environment looked small and cozy.  Thanks for the pix, fellow Yelper next time I will have to see that additional seating spot they have.

Otherwise, very good sandwich!
Only walked around outside, but the building is beautiful! The architecture is incredible. City hall is also close to many other historical things like the Masonic Temple.
Very helpful in sending a sympathy basket to a house in the area. The price was fair, affordable and they didnt charge a lot to deliver locally.

This is the place where everybody knows your name. Well, not my name, but it seems like this post office must have a LOT of regular customers, which I find a little weird.  There must be an enclave of ebay sellers in the area.  They are also a lot faster than *my* neighborhood post office, which is why I prefer to come here when I can.

This Company did work that failed then they become MIA when you call about the failed work.  I had a line in my 1954 Revco Refrigerator that a line separated and caused the fridge to discharge.  Well I paid to have a new compressor installed and the line repaired.  The work was done and the lines had some moisture so they came back another day after the fridge had some time to collect the moisture in the moisture collectors.  The job worked for a just over one week then the brazing on the repair started to leak.  Now you can't get them on the phone, or get a response.  I've called every day, I guess I'll need to find someone else to fix his repair and sue for the costs.  I understand that you might get a leak just admit to the mistake and fix it.  

Also they are not good on letting you know what is going on they just show up when they feel like it and miss appointment and don't call.  Even if they are all you can find to make the repair don't use them you will regret it.
Good food, friendly staff and nice outside eating area. Come hungry because portions are great and eat the Cubano sandwich!

We had a layover here on our flight. There were several dining options in the area. Fairly clean. Friendly staff. Family friendly. Bathroom was clean. We didn't have to walk very far between flights. That was nice. It can get rather hot in the area we sat for our flight. They recovered nicely after the tornado hit. Marcy'sDinerTantrumScale 5/5- NoScreamingKids.

What do you love about movie theaters?
Popcorn!
Big screens!
Sharing the experience of a film with others!
Getting out of the house!

What do you hate about movie theaters?
Screaming kids!
Screaming adults!
Cell phones!
Sticky floors!
Huge crowds!
Expensive everything!

Why is the hi pointe so rad? It's got all of the good and none of the bad. Hey, that rhymed!

1.)Adult tickets. 8$. Student tickets. 6$. (Wehrenberg theaters are all 9 or 9.50)
2.)Candy. 2 bucks. TWO. DOLLARS. For candy at a theater, are you kidding me? It's like I"ve died and gone to sugar coma heaven.
3.)No annoying patrons. People don't drop their kids off here the way they do at various county theaters. There is no screaming and cell phones and obnoxiousness. I think this has a lot to do with the "type" of people that come here. People come here for the "experience," and thus show more appreciation for where they are.
4.)Ambiance/atmosphere/decor. I can't really describe this place. One screen. Free candy in the girl's bathroom. Signed picture of Jerry Seinfeld. 50s furniture. 
5.)Nicest theater employees in all of St Louis. Boom.
6.) IT IS NEVER CROWDED. You can always find your perfect seat.

GO HERE
GO HERE NOW
SEE ANYTHING
YOU WILL FALL IN LOVE

Please please PLEASE go to any other pet store but here. these people are uneducated and cruel. a few years ago I had a friend call and ask if she could surrender baby rats to be sold as pets. We went in to check on them and the lady said we don't sell rats as pets, only as snake food here. When she saw we started to get upset she said "oh those rats, yeah I believe they all sold". We asked to see their "rat room" that they have in the back; the associate explained that occasionally rats they sell as pets will be placed in the cages, but the food rats will be placed in the drawers, well my friend's were placed in the drawers. I also got a sick chinchilla from them that I spent $500 to try and save and it died, a flying squirrel that they didn't know the gender OR age of, and yet I STILL went back and purchased chicks from them. "Americana" is not a breed people, and any site you find online says be aware of this. I was thinking they meant "Ameraucana" which will lay blue eggs... the chick I got is actually a red sex link. I hope this place gets shut down and soon!!

Room is ok - TV is an embarrassment - they use a 3rd party provider - no national news stations, no sports just some local stations  with poor quality picture stations such as bounce decades grit ? Actually pretty funny. Guess Wyndham trying to save some pennies. They've made a 2 maybe 3 star hotel to a 1 to 2 star when they took over la Quinta brand. So sad
Oops just noticed closet missing a handle - nice :)
Can't wait for grab and go breakfast tomorrow am

I went to McDonalds because I wanted to try the Big Mac with bacon and I got my order but I didn't check it because they should have made it right so I shouldn't be responsible I checking it because it should be what I ordered. When I got to work and opened  the sandwich, much to my surprise there was NO BACON, so I was very upset. I didn't bother going back to stand there and explain my anger to a person that my son is older than, so I express my dismay on YELP. THIS PLACE SUCKS!!! and they want $15 hr LMAO.

Probably the best meal we had in New Orleans. Considering we didn't really love the Creole food in NOLA, all of our meals were very nice at Red Fish Grill. The decor is a little daggy, but the service was friendly (as was most in all of NOLA). If we were back in NOLA, I'd definitely return here.

Got here purely by chance. Yelp reviews pulled us in. From the old saying don't judge a book by it's cover should be applied here. We were blown away by our experience. 

One lamb burger: fantastic. One 12 inch ribeye: great. Sweet potato fries: hot and tasty. Insanely kind staff: wow. 

Why 4 stars: it's great doing what it does. It offers styrofoam plates and cups. It's not fine dining and that's fine. 

My suggestion: stop thinking and just get going. Grab the keys, friends, kids, and anyone else who happens to be hungry and go get happy!
Family owned and operated. Pizza is very fresh and yummy. Great chesse steaks. Best in area. Don't deliver. Staple in area

I ordered a ham, egg, and cheese croissant at the drive thru window this morning. It was early and there were no other customers in line. I opened my bag at home to find a sausage, egg, and cheese croissant.
