No big deal, or so I thought; I decided to just return to the store and have it replaced. They replaced it with a cold cheese and egg croissant- no ham. 
I will not be returning.

DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME! We just moved to Clearwater and are trying out new places, we love ethnic food and saw a coupon to try this restaurant. We walked in at 1:00 on a Saturday afternoon, the place was empty. Only three tables, Really?
We presented the coupon at that time the "owner" came out and informed us the coupon was incorrect we needed to purchase smoothies with our orders. She then went on about how she won't make any money otherwise, I found the whole conversation quite tacky and we left. Very un-polished, I feel sorry for the lady. I hope she does well in her new business venture, but I seriously doubt it. Do not make the customer pay for yours and others mistakes. The whole idea of printing coupons is to "introduce" your restaurant and hope you will tell your friends, that may have been the case if we had not been treated so unprofessionally....too bad it may have been a nice experience.

Restaurants like Martin's are the reason I travel and explore new places. From the moment I walked in, Martin treated me like family and provided far more than nutrition. After seeing the quality of the food coming out of the kitchen, I simply asked to be taken care of. The red Chile Quesadilla was perfect! An excellent combination of rich ingredients, textures, and flavor made this the best food spot on my road trip so far. This is your spot regardless of wait time!
Great food & beverage provided by the most friendly staff I've ever seen !!! Nice selection of alcoholic and non alcoholic drinks. Food options are plentiful. A very FUN & FRIENDLY atmosphere !!

I'M SO ANGRY THAT I DIDN'T DISCOVER THIS PLACE EARLIER! Cheap and so many vegetarian-friendly options. I got an avocado, egg salad, cheese, sprouts, and tomato sandwich on 7-grain bread as well as a strawberry smoothie for $9.42. It was delicious. I will be back. Often.

New brewery in New Orleans with Saints player Zach Strief as one of the owners.  I just went and had their first Nitro Stout which was very good.  It reminded me of Colorado.  This is a good brewery in town off to a good start.  Their trivia nights are fun, and you can get good food at Stokehold inside the brewery for a bite.  I enjoyed the outdoor courtyard area as well.

I work at a salon in the Plymouth Meeting Mall, not a lot of delivery options, went to Sushi Lover and got dumplings which were fried to perfection but came with no dipping sauce. I got a garden salad with extra ginger dressing. The dressing taste nothing like ginger, it was gross. I have tried their spicy tuna rolls and they are just alright.

I was a little hesitant to come here, because I wasn't sure if I would like the food.  I'm so glad that I did come because it was soooooo good!  The service was good, and servers were friendly. My husband and I ordered a variety of different things, and everything was excellent. The vareniki with potatoes, manti, and samsa were soooooo good! My husband also got a few kabobs that were excellent.

Went for Devour Summerfest. I've never been here, but I like Greek food. The service was great and the food was amazing. I wish the baklava was bigger!  it's a cute place, the server said everything is made in house. I will definitely go back.
I ordered the spanakopita.
I get my nails done on a regular basis. Honestly, this place does THE BEST pedicure I've ever had. They take their time and it truly is a great experience. Would come many more times.

Terrible service and food. We found ourselves near here this morning and stopped in for breakfast. We were seated pretty quickly at a corner table near the exit. About five minutes later, we were given a single menu for the two of us. I asked for a second menu and ordered us two cups of coffee. The second menu never arrived but our coffees did with two creamers only each. The coffee was super strong and bitter so we asked for more creamer which also didn't arrive. Hubby ordered corned beef hash and eggs with home fries. I ordered bacon and eggs with tater tots. While we waited for our food, other diners arrived, ordered, ate and left. We had to chase our waiter down to ask about our order. No one comes to refill coffee either. Hubby's hash was from a can but cooked well as were his eggs. My eggs were cooked well but arrived broken. The tater tots were so overcooked that they shattered on impact and were inedible. Not a place that we will be returning to.
This might be the best public golf course I've ever played.  Unfortunately it's $95 a round, so it's tough to play often.

The fish tacos are my go to item here. There is a self serve salsa bar. The quality of some of the stuff in the salsa could definitely be better but it's not too big of a deal. I have also tried the carne asada and the chicken tacos. They are also pretty good.
Decent fancy pizza. I heard it was good and it was. I had a half mushroom, half pepperoni. I'd certainly eat there again but I wouldn't go out of my way for it.
The food was great and everyone there, including the chef, was friendly and made me feel like family.

Really nice place, as everything in sunny Summerland. Really good coffee and eggs Benedict, although the hollandaise sauce was too mild and blend. Portions were enormous, two people could eat out of one main breakfast dish.

Chez Leon was another stop on my new-job-celebration train. I was treated to dinner here last night and really enjoyed the classic french cuisine. 

I started with the poached pear salad which is just what I needed after being in that hundred degree heat! I ordered the trout for my entree because I had heard from several people that it's an all around favorite. The serving is extremely generous and I only made it less than half way through my fish before throwing in the towel (er, napkin). The fish tasted fresh but a bit too salty for my taste and I generally love salty food. My co-diners/celebrators all ordered red meat- steak frites and the beef tenderloin and seemed to enjoy both dishes. I tasted the onion soup and thought it was great. I would definitely order that once the weather cools down! 

We all shared the chocolate souffle, which like another review states is not really a chocolate souffle. They puncture the souffle at your table and pour in a chocolate sauce. Definitely not the best I have had and you can definitely taste the eggs. Next time I would go with the creme brulee or the queen's apple.

Interior is very modern and chic for a strip-mall type restaurant. The Indian Lakes shopping center is of course more high-end, so it makes sense. 

The first odd thing was that the lighting is really bright in here. We came in for dinner and it was after dark, but the bright white walls were really popping with light. It made for kind of an odd dining experience. Like, not relaxing at all and kind of cafeteria-like, but a cafeteria for a high-end designer or something. 

I didn't get any kind of drink menu, so I have no idea if they serve alcohol. 

Boyf ordered two sushi rolls, and I ordered the beef gogigui. The sushi rolls took a bit to come out, but that can be normal for sushi. He got his sushi and it looks fantastic. Dynamite roll and Alfredo roll. Alfredo was $12 by itself, but it was huge (see photo) and tasty. 

It took even longer for my meal to come out. I figured it would since bulgogi-type dishes usually do, but the server didn't even mention this to begin with. 

He was halfway throw his food when mine finally came out, and the portions were disappointing. For the same price of his giant roll, I felt like I was missing a lot. The gogigui came with a handful of cooked beef, 3 leaves of romaine lettuce, some sauce, and my "side" item was 3 stalks of grilled asparagus. 

For $12, I felt like this was not enough food. The flavors were all pretty good, however. 

To top off the mediocre experience, we didn't get a visit from our server again after she took our food order. Didn't get drink refills or anything. We had to ask the guy who was bringing our food and removing our plates for refills and the check, then we just paid at the cash register. This guy was extremely nice and asked how our food tasted and seemed genuinely interested. And yes, the waitress was walking around taking other people's orders, but she didn't stop by our table once, not even to deliver our food. 

While the sushi was pretty good, I think I'll give someplace I haven't tried yet a go before coming back here.

I am a wedding photographer. I see oodles of weddings every year.  There is a massive, noticeable difference between brides who had their aunt/friend/sister do their makeup and a professional.  Your aunt/sister/friend may like makeup, have a big collection, and watch lots of YouTube clips but she's not a professional.  She won't know how to make makeup last all day long, she won't know how makeup reacts to various lighting conditions that a bride encounters on her wedding day, she won't know how to apply makeup so that it looks good to guests AND the camera.  The ladies at Cheekadee are professionals.  I am happy to recommend them which is really important because their professionalism reflects on me.  Emily, the head honcho of Cheekadee, is a doll.  She's kind, patient, and, most importantly, she KNOWS HER STUFF!  I have worked with her and the Cheekadee babes many times and am always thrilled by the results.  Go with these gals and you won't be disappointed.  Your photographer will back me up on this!
Best bar in Gulfport! Guaranteed good time. Usually live music on the weekends. The drinks are at a fair price, the staff is friendly and it is on the water....can't ask for much more!

Very good burger.  Nice soft hard roll and fries were done just right, not overlooked. Beer was cold.  A full bar would make this a five.  Sat outside on a nice night.  Not too noisy so conversation was doable.  Would come back again..

Visited today for first time and was not impressed. I could not find anyone to help me and when I asked a cashier to assist me a tall guy came out of back very disheveled.  He asked me what I was looking for and when I told he he said it would be on a particular isle. After I stated I couldn't find it he stated " if it's not there we don't have it" really?? I left the store with items left in cart.

I've never dined in here but I have gotten takeout from here MANY times! Most of the time my food has been good, but my most recent order contained a dish I thought was absolutely terrible. Clearly they have some dishes that are just way better than others so there is a lack of consistency. 

The good? Their noodles! I almost always get Drunken Noodles with Tofu and it is usually pretty tasty. The noodles are usually cooked just right, they aren't broken, too salty or too bland, and there is a decent variety of veggies in the dish. I've ordered the Pad See Ew once before and it was of similar quality. However, one night I was sick and desperate for Pho, and only Thai Phooket was open, so I ordered it. What a mistake. I've always told myself to never order Pho from a place that isn't a Vietnamese restaurant and I should've stuck to my rule. Forget that it wasn't an authentic bowl of pho, it was just the saltiest thing I have ever tasted in my life. I actually could not eat it. 

I will still continue to order from them but I'll just avoid the pho.

The positives:
Service was fantastic. Our waiter was always checking on us, refilling drinks and removing plates. 

Atmosphere was comfortable. They easily accommodated our party of 9. 

Some of the food was good, if it was warm....

The negatives
The fried chicken was awfully dry. 

All of the buffet items were mislabeled. Fried catfish was above the collard greens, mashed potatoes over some mystery dish, Ect. And, the correct items were either further down the line or on the other side. 

I was slightly confused of the don't waste price. Basically, if you don't waste a lot of food, you get a cheaper buffet price. I did t question it as I only take small portions anyway, but curious how they judge that. What if I eat something that's cold or I don't like? 

A lot of the food wasn't hot. The fried green tomatoes were awful compared to Selmons. There really wasn't anything that stood out. Mc and cheese was pretty good. 
---
Overall, I was disappointed, but other reviewers seemed to enjoy themselves, so give it a shot.

Wow!  Absolutely amazing fresh sushi and rolls.  Nigiri super white tuna and salmon was the best I have had including restaurants in San Francisco and New York.  The Miracle roll with Jalopeno had an amazing blend and balance of flavors and textures including citrus, savoury, crunch and heat.   Excellent meal.

My wife and I were able to get out for one of our exceptionally rare date nights without the kids.  Matoi is a completely different experience when you don't have to tell little ones not to throw stuff, to sit down in their seats and not to squeal.  The sushi is awesome compared to Tampa standards for sushi.  The fish is incredibly fresh and the Dynamite roll and Pirates roll are works of art as well as delicious.  The KimChi is a must try, and the Dynamite is a can't miss.  Service has been good both times we have dined here.  Better without kids!  We had the sizzling Korean short ribs last time and they were great too!  They are a little pricey, but worth the difference in price.  Restaurant.com and groupons help but we still spent over $60 for 2 of us... without alcohol purchases.

This place is great for what it is.  We've been a couple of times, and the food is great each time.  We've had their steak special with fries, a chicken fried po' boy, fried dill pickles, and the alcoholic rootbeer.  I was especially impressed with the seasoning on the steak, and we have gone back a couple of yes for the dill pickles.  The waitress was funny and friendly.  I wouldn't hesitate to go back for late night eats.

My husband and I came here for a late lunch. It was such a nice day we sat outside in the front patio and I love that they are furry friend friendly. Our dog got to sit with us. Our server was Jessica. My husband order Baja Cioppino and I had the garlic shrimp scamby.....The Baja Cioppino came with a large portion of sea food..large pieces of crab, calimari,  big clams and mussel. ..looked so good I had to try it too....The both was very flavorful and my husband just abiut licked the bowl clean. so enough of what my husband had....on to mine....garlic shrimp scamby which came with JUMBO shirmp and very taste....and like me my husband could help himself to stick his fork in my dish too....we were very happy with the delious and very friendly server....we will be back soon

oh and I can't for get the Molten Chocolate cake to die for..

We had termite treated by Budget Pest Control with 5 years warranty.  They stated they would inspect our premise once a year and never did follow up with their promise.  And now they sent an extended/ 2 years warranty for $80 which I don't believe them anymore.

Their initial treatment was OK. Nonetheless, we live in a gated community. Thus No one can get in without calling us first and we have never seen anyone came by for any inspection as they said they would.

this has to be about the worst place i have ever been, i would tell you to make a better vegan hoagie, but you should just get rid of it and offer nothing vegan.  it is more insulting to charge somebody ten dollars for a roll with lettuce on it, than to just have nothing to offer them at all.  the service is unfriendly and the food is horrible.  the inside looks nice, but it was an overall cold experience.  see ya around never.

WHY WOULD ANYONE, EVEN A TOURIST, EAT HERE?
My last Hard Rock $50 comp card was expiring so I ordered a lot of take-out. I was going to dine in but their 1997-ish sickly-palid, overly-pierced, mowhawked, and tatted decor/wait staff creeped me out. But the surly patrons were even sadder...

ABSOLUTELY WORST RIBS I'VE EVER HAD (SEE PIC)
Hard Rock's blasphemy to well-meaning hogs everywhere were so charred that the few shreds resembled burned pulled-jerky. It's obvious (I'm from a beef producing family) that the cheapest cuts were sourced and then doused heavily in BBQ sauce to hide their nastiness.

NON POSTED DRINK PRICES
Who orders a drink and relies upon their waitress to quote the "going rate" even on a beer!? Not a single cocktail price is posted in Hard Rock's menu (see pics).

LISTEN-UP TOURISTS! GO HERE INSTEAD:
Nearby festive bars with cheaper, way better food: Cajun Mike's, Yo Mama's, MRB's, Walk On's, Desire, Felipe's; and finer happy hours at Domenica, SoBu, Tableau, Lüke.

Husband had the T-Bone. Perfectly prepared. I had crabmeat au gratin. Delicious! Shared onion rings, creamed spinach and potatoes au gratin and wedge salad with vinaigrette... yum!!
My only disappointment was the size if the wine glass. Very small. 
We will return.

98 years old and full of old world charm!

My boyfriend and I came here twice this past weekend. We never dined at the restaurant but had drinks at the bar.

One of the oldest establishments and one of the most charming we've seen in New Orleans. Extremely well-run and the young waiters were friendly, well trained and adorable.

The person who deserves the most credit is John, the Maître D, called 'Old School' by his devoted and loving colleagues. He is a man full of knowledge and oversees everything with care and love. He is a connoisseur of old movies and we talked a lot about 1950's & 60's films of Elizabeth Taylor, to whom he is a huge fan.

Thank you John for giving us a beautiful memory at Broussard's. We will miss you deeply and we will look forward to seeing you again.

Delicious! Melt in your mouth kind of food! The Famous Ferdie is a must! Liked the space! Staff was very kind and helpful too!

Wow...what can I say. 
We, myself and three friends,  were traveling by motorcycle from Southern California to Alberta Canada.

My right fork started leaking fluid and we were staying about 10 miles from this HD Dealership.  We went in and told Tanya the problem and she found "Chuck" (please forgive me if I am not recalling the correct name--tall guy with the great hair and bod:-)
and they put their heads together and worked out a plan that got me on my bike back on my bike-- fixed the forks, saved us a few hundred dollars and even fixed the headlamp for the cost of parts.  Did I mention that they found a demo bike for me to ride while my bike was down?

Everyone there was super friendly.  We  purchased a shirt and ring...the cashier was also helpful and friendly and knowledgeable.
Thanks guys for helping to make our 3,500 mile  road trip less stressful....you rock!

I did not realize a sushi place could be this bad. My friend and I enjoy going to different sushi places and Mikimotos was the worst we have been to so far.

We started with the duck quesadilla which was WAY too cheesy and not tasty in any way.
We also both got the Supreme Sushi platter.
The salmon, tuna, and white tuna weren't bad. The eel was pretty good but it's hard to go wrong with eel.
The worst sushi piece; Cajun seared tuna... what the hell... I had to spit it out...

I would honestly rather eat grocery store sushi than Mikimotos. Their spicy tuna is just a regular tuna roll with "spicy mayo" on top? Out of the 15+ sushi places I've been to nobody did this. The reason I put that in quotes is because the mayo was not spicy at all.

Nothing to comment on about service, I don't really care about service. I just want good sushi.

Pros:
Decent ambiance & decor (lighting is very uneven... Bright lights shining down directly on tables)

Cons:
Way overpriced
Quality of food is very low

Went to have my Faux leather coat cleaned after an incident with a feline, dropped coat off, they said they couldn't dry clean because of material but would get the coat clean, $25 prepaid. So a week goes by, I called them and asked for an update, the guy said " we can't dry-clean it, I said I know, and brought up the conversation we had at the counter. He asked the woman I originally spoke with in person and said " ok, it will be done on thursday, which is another week away! Well finally the coat was cleaned, and in good shape, but the wait and poor communication was a problem.
Our waitress wasn't the greatest waitress I've ever experienced, but they have a pretty decent selection of beers on tap.

We decided to go on the weather because we were craving Mexican food. Sat outside in the bar area. Waiter was prompt and the food was a very fast to get to us. Drinking the margaritas did not taste like there is much alcohol in them. But, after two you could tell there was. This restaurant is not a place that you would want to go to for a special occasion. But, if you're craving Mexican food anyone quick fast service, I would recommend going.

I would give the food 5 stars as well as the servers when we've dined in here. I've always had a good impression of management until today. Not sure if this man is a manager or owner but we had a glimpse today of how he treats employees and it was not pretty. I can't think of any situation when a grown man should be screaming at a young girl working for him over pizza. Apparently he was upset there were not enough delivery drivers working and she had messed up an order. I can't imagine screaming fixed either of these problems. The same guy was super gracious to us on a past visit. Helped move an umbrella on the patio to shade our newborn. It was super sweet. Try to treat your employees the same way you treat your customers. You have a great product but I never want to hear someone being screamed at on the job. I'm trying to enjoy my Sunday, watch some football, and eat great pizza.
The whole family enjoyed our meal at Oceania Grill. We definitely will be back for the chargrilled oysters.

We'll, this is my Furry Baby's primary care, and for good reason too! The staff and the doctors are not only knowledgeable but caring and leaves no rock unturned. The only spot that I will take my corgi. They knew just what to do and what to say, and were greatly accommodating. Prices are also affordable and the clinic caters to my taste and expectation of style and cleanliness. You won't regret.

I had one of the best omelettes of my life here, which was a surprise since we mostly chose this place for breakfast because they were well reviewed for their biscuits. The biscuit was nice but the omelette was amazing: the crispy bacon inside and multitude of freshly sautéed vegetables was the best part. The server, Tori, went out of her way to help me locate a business that sold the type of liquor I was looking for an even called them and wrote down the address for me! The restaurant is cute and casual. I was only visiting New Orleans but would totally go there again for breakfast if I could!
Love this place, staff is amazing and food is delicious and for the most part very healthy. Great neighborhood cafe.

At the time of my visit, I am partaking in a complimentary birthday meal via the First Watch club.  This location has been open for a week.  Across the area there are 700+ locations and this location nestled into the Brentwood plaza complex has a full bar!  (See bar menu).

Decor

The traditional First Watch menu and a seasonal "Harvest the Season" menu placed at every table.  The decor is new country.  It's quaint with hanging country doors from the ceiling, floating window box planters, old tracker seats, and black cast iron skillet hanging as a curtain separating the dining area from the kitchen.

Meal

Oh the meals looked yummy.  Each breakfast which passed my eyes looked more and more scrumptious from the toast points with avocado, hash, breakfast potatoes, omelettes, and sandwiches.

I am a pancake lover so I had the pumpkin pancakes, switched from chicken sausage to Hardwood smoked bacon. There are a couple bacon options (Million Dollar Bacon - maple syrup, pepper, 530 calories and the Hardwood smoked bacon, 160 calories).
Scrambled eggs.

The pumpkin pancakes...so fluffy with the perfect Touch of pumpkin not over whelming, crispy golden edges. Served with warm maple syrup which had my mouth watering. Crispy bacon, and the eggs or scrambled hard like I like them. 

I met a young lady at the bar who'd been I'm 3 times that week!!  So, that is a real plus.

Staff

Friendly and upbeat.  This was their busiest day since opening a few days ago. They were a bit overwhelmed but everyone kept a smile on their face and very pleasant. a diverse staff.

See menu for pricing and to choose what you'd like to have before you get there. Once you look at the menu you'll want everything on the menu.

This Starbucks is by far my favorite in University City. It's not usually over crowded like the one on 34th & Walnut, and the baristas are sweet and haven't messed up an order yet. If there is a deal or promotion, they make sure to let you know about it to get the most on what you are spending for your coffee.
Really good sushi. My fav sushi place in philly. Staff is nice. Nice atomosphere and prices are decent.

True story: the latest pool tech to come out (they change just as you begin remembering their names) can't get the backwash valve to turn on my salt pool. I got a call from the company saying they'd send out a guy for $95 to find out why their tech can't get the pool in backwash mode. 
I said that if it's broken, they broke it because I haven't backwashed in more than a year (because I pay them to do that).
To which he replied, "I didn't say it was broken."
Then why are you charging me $95?
We finally agreed to say "not working" instead of "broken" to carry on the conversation. 
He agreed to send out someone (between a pool tech and a $95 guy?) to assess the situation sans charge. 
Stay tuned for where we go from here.
This is a medium sized restaurant with fairly authentic Korean food.  The staff is a little slow sometimes.  The food on the other hand is very good and properly prepared.

Talk about a hidden Gem! This place looks like nothing worth stopping for - but you couldn't be judging a book by its cover any more. The food is extremely fresh, the service was diner-rific, and the price just can't be beat. I honestly think I've found a new favorite weekend spot. They are only open for breakfast and lunch, but do serve the whole menue the entire time. Pour yourself a cup, pull up a chair, and dig in!
Just an update since leaving meenan. We went to oil express paying cash and our oil ended up costing 1/3 of what we paid using meenans plan. Best decision ever leaving then.

I don't understand the poor reviews. I've admittedly never eaten in but I've ordered pick up several times and always have been pleased.  It is a hole in the wall Chinese place and is excellent at what it is.
Went and was very disappointed. The phad thai was so salty I had to send it back. Ordered some rice with chicken and for the price it wasn't worth it. Too cramped and loud. Not very good.
Our meals were "OK," but certainly not worthy of the highline price.

great food - everything i ordered was good. 
Only thing quantity for Chicken Malai Kebab was less than expected.

I've sampled froyo from the East Coast, West Coast, and overseas in Asia where this fad started.  I even make my own!  Despite the nice concept of being able to choose from several flavors and put on your own toppings, I am not sold.  The yogurt is icy, nowhere near smooth, and the fruit is not fresh.  Not worth the money, but unfortunately there is no clear competitor in Philly (except me but I'm not selling!)... don't believe the hype!

Constant immutable fans, doors bang shut by default resulting in disrupted sleep. Refused to provide morning "grab n go" during Covid 19 so I had to drive & get breakfast elsewhere. Housekeeping conveniently skipped over my room so I had to order linens & towels, then tidy my room up myself after a long day. Typical 1 star service at a "3 star" hotel. This chain has clean rooms but scrimps on the service at every turn. 1 slowish elevator in the entire hotel, getting you closely acquainted with people you would otherwise avoid.

George is fantastic and super affordable. Come here for any of your jewelry needs! He is located inside the fashion district on the second level next to the Nike store (so 901 market street entrance).

It's hard to not notice The Siam Elephant Thai restaurant when walking the strip from the shops to the beach in Carpinteria.  We finally gave it a try for lunch and it was wonderful! We loved the wooden "house-like" eating areas outside! Super cool and unique! The staff was very friendly and on top of it! The food was excellent! We had the lunch specials so it was very reasonably priced! No complaints here! Only good things!

This place is the benchmark for others....The constantly rotating self described "strange" beers are an experience.   If you love beer, traveling here routinely is a must.  I like most but not all of the beers I have had there.  However, that is why it such a great place.  The experimentation and the passion in the brewing process is evident.   Staff is polite with a hipster edge!   The bread is great and the food is a great compliment to the beer.  They usually adhere to the seasons with  a changing menu.

While it is clearly a five star gem, there are some suggestions that would make it even better.  First, update the website with the food offerings.   I come for the beer but it is nice to scope ahead of time menu options.    Second. please announce bottle releases on Twitter.   Some of us aren't on Facebook and we would like to partake.

They have also added a hostess on the weekends.  While I understand why, I don't like it!  Just takes away from what I think it is all about......

Coming from the Western Main Line, would love to see an outpost in our direction!!!!!

Great atmosphere. Friendly server. I truly enjoyed the taco of the month and the blackened fish taco. Ordered the tableside guacamole. My boyfriend had the taco bar option. Would definitely come back again. We had a great experience.
Love this gym!!!!! Great machines and spacious workout space. The indoor track is great!

About what you'd expect from a used car dealer, if what you expect is the stereotypical dealer tactics and misinformation.  I emailed them, but they never responded.  So I called them about a car that looked good in the 20-feet-away photos on their website.  Was told only about a couple of small holes in the front seats and that it had been repainted.  I asked about something I could see was missing or broken from one picture, which he then confirmed and after some prodding he added an interior trim piece was missing too.  So I made the long trek to see the car.  As soon as I saw it I felt deceived.  Car had MANY more issues - worn out weather stripping, stains in all seats, ripped carpet, ripped headliner, gouged plastic trim, ripped steering wheel, other missing pieces, etc.  Oh, and that fairly new repaint, it was among the worst I've ever seen - no prep work done before the paint was applied (could see all the metal flaws under the new paint, plus had some paint runs).  The car seemed like a good price online and in the distant pictures (which tactically avoided the problems), but was clearly overpriced once actually seen.  Dealer had to know these issues existed, but did not disclose them.  Complete waste of my time.  They did have some other cars which appeared to be in decent condition (although I didn't really look too hard) at decent prices, which is the only reason they got one star from me.  That and they didn't harass me while I was on their lot looking.

----------------

So after posting my 100% accurate review, Donny Mills himself leaves a snide response in Yelp that I "was well aware of all the car's issues" and I "deceived myself".  I knew of only two of the issues, not the MANY others, since Donny failed to disclose them over the phone and never responded to my email.  I wouldn't have wasted my time and money if he had simply been honest.  Now he blames me?  I notice he has a response for every bad review on Yelp; guess nothing is his fault...ever.  Even more proof that you don't want to do business with this low-life dealership and his deceptive car sales tactics.  Go somewhere respectable.

I've been to Verde twice and have seen Julie and Madeline. My first visit was in April 2017 and Julie helped me achieve the cool blonde look I was going for with full highlights and a conditioning mask. My hair was also heat damaged and she recommended kevin murphy young again serum which I now swear by - I use it daily and have noticed a drastic improvement, plus the bottle lasts forever (still more than half full 3 months later). I also purchased a shampoo and conditioner from the hydrate me and blonde angel lines. The products are a little pricey (~$35) but they last me about 4 months (I have long, fine hair for reference).

I wanted to see Julie again but was informed she was taking a few weeks medical leave, so I was paired up with Madeline for my second visit in July 2017. She touched up my blonde which had inevitably warmed up due to the elements. Madeline was great and helped me re-achieve that cool blonde look that I love with partial highlights and olaplex. She also recommended a new shampoo and conditioner from the repair me line that I will start using once my other ones run out. She did comment that my hair had very little breakage and felt strong, so I will definitely be sticking with professional products from now on. 

I would visit either Julie or Madeline again and am so happy to have finally found a hair salon in south jersey!

There are times when I sigh because I know my review would be better if a business would care about business just a bit more.

It was dinner and I was ready to wolf down a dog at a new place (for me). So, BAM! I decided on Bam!Dogs. It occupies the spot right next to Honey Treat Yogurt.

Some of the positive points I like about this place. They have chicken wings, sliders, sausages, kielbasa and even a veggie dog. But, you do know what goes well with dogs? A beer. They have beer in cans and on tap. 
It's clean in there with a fresh look.

Things I noticed. One person cooking and two people out front. 
We walked in and there were two locations for two different menus. Their picture menu, located on the east wall (right) had specialty dogs. The standard fare was located on the north wall (in front).

Order time. I ordered a chili cheese dog, as did my girl (Connie), and my dad ordered a dog with chili & mustard. 
Did you get confused by reading this? We had to state the order 3 times before it was understood. We also ordered two beers, which the order had to be placed twice since the first time it didn't registered.
We started to (as customers) shut down. 
Then I made the no no. Bam!Dog is part of Perka. It's a loyaty program. Apparently, I was the first person to use it. The first person had no idea how to work with Perka and the second person, after a minute, did figure it out. 

It did not take too long for the dogs to come out, but it felt like it after the smooth customer service we had. 

Food: The dogs are good. The chili, not my favorite, but those dogs are good! They have a casing (skin) so if you like cheap dogs, do not go here. If you like a dog that snaps when you bite into them, this place is worth a visit. 
Connie's and my dogs were ordered with cheese. Alas, the cheese did the splits and even though it was mentioned on my copy of the receipt, my wiener was cheeseless. The person who brought out the dogs was kind enough to remedy that small issue quickly. 

Beer: The beer was cold and tasty. Connie had the Great Basin and I had the Pigeon Head. Both were served cold and tasted good. For $5.00, it would have tasted even better in a glass. 

Prices: Their prices are not too bad for a chili cheese dog. Right in line with other quality hot dog restaurants 7.5 miles away. Where the price differs is their Chili dog with mustard. $1.05 more than the competition.
Also, if nothing is stated, the mustard used in the kitchen is spicy brown. 


---------------
Bottom Line

I may try this place again.
*They should work on getting the order correct the first time it's stated. 
*Know how to input services, offers or gimmicks.
*I would even offer another type of chili. But, of course, that's a personal preference. 
I love the idea of offering local beers. 
More advertisements and student discounts to get people from UNR to visit. 
Dogs = A+
Buns = B
Chili = C
Beer = A- (served in glass A+)

Vernick Coffee Bar fits nicely in the gorgeous, modern, and spacious lobby of the Comcast Center. Three distinct seating areas fit personalities of all types, the bar seating for casual coffee sipping and chatting with staff, the high tops in the lobby-tasing area which are good for quick conversations over coffee and pastries, and the full-service dining area, perfect for those with a little extra time to kill and hunger to satiate.

First, as a coffee bar, Vernick doesn't hit the spot for me, but I'm a coffee snob entrenched in third wave preparation. They serve Caffe Umbria at the moment, which generally puts out more traditional, darker roasted beans. Thankfully, they pull their espresso shots pretty well, so the espresso isn't too bitter. Their milk preparation for cappuccinos is also very traditional and dry. I prefer a more balanced milk texture and temperature, but this preparation has a market. It's just not for me. Doesn't mean I wouldn't' get their coffee again, just that the aesthetic of the bar really fits in better with modern coffee shops than old Italian-Influence cafes, so don't get confused!

Food-wise, Vernick does a great job! The "eggs in purgatory" are essentially a shakshuka, and very good! Not too spicy at all, but definitely tasty! I got the green tea, ginger, white chocolate croissant, and it was quite good. If you like matcha, this is a croissant for you! For me, it would've been better standalone, but it was almost too much for me in the context of the rest offer meal. I had to try it, though. The warm 7-minute egg over English pea hummus, cherry tomato salad, and crispy pita was awesome. A really refreshing, light, yet filling dish. I would return for this, alone!

We sat in the dining area, and the service was great. Staff was really friendly, the food came out quickly, and our water was topped off constantly.

Overall, we loved Vernick Coffee Bar. The service and food were excellent, and the space was beautiful. The coffee, while not my style, wasn't bad, and will please many coffee drinkers. We'll definitely be back!

Enjoy!

What else can you say for adult entertainment. Great looking women, excellent bartenders, friendly floor staff, the drink  prices are very reasonable for a gentleman's club. The entertainers actually entertain with the multiple stages and places for them to hang and dance at the bar which is always a good time. I see people "making it rain" a lot in there which is exciting. Private room dances are some of the cheapest i have seen in the bay. They also have full bottle service that my friends and I love to do when we feel like letting loose and just relaxing. I have nothing but great experiences with this place. Thank you Gold Club

coming from a really high volume city filled with boba, I really wanted to find a place where it had good toppings and amazing flavored tea. 

Tealeaf is the only place in Reno for good boba.. try the mango green tea WITHOUT milk and lychee jelly. You'll love it!

Here's my love letter to Food+Drink. This poor guy had the terrible misfortune of opening a restaurant in midtown in the midst of all of the construction and then after only being open a few weeks the pandemic hit. I sure hope he makes it because this is seriously the best pizza in Reno! The dough is absolutely the best I've ever had. I don't usually even like pizza dough all that much. It's just there as a vehicle to get the delicious pizza toppings in my mouth. Not so with this pizza! So far, I've tried the cherry picker (my fave), the Tony's NYC, the meatball (my husband's fave), and the grasshopper. You can't go wrong with any of the pizzas - all are delicious. The salad with an orange vinaigrette is also delicious, so you can balance out your meal with some greens. Save room for the cucumber margaritas, homemade ice cream, and the magic cookies too! You won't be sorry. He's only doing takeout currently (see above re pandemic) and the ordering system is a bit wonky (he knows - watch the video on his webpage about how to order and you'll be fine) and you have to plan a little in advance to order, but it'll be worth it. I promise. The owner is also funny and talented and posts some great videos over on insta @thefoodanddrink. Get out there and support this great local business, Reno!

I am extremely disappointed. Me and my 2 year were trying to grab a cart when one of the cart attendants pushed the other end causing it to  ram into my son. If he would of just apologized, because "accidents" do happen, I wouldn't be writing this as of now. However he sarcastically said under his breath to "watch our kid" while it was obvious we were trying to put him in the cart. If I could give 0 stars I would. I'd much rather drive a little farther to a different Costco then support this location.

For four years we loved two things at this place, Fish Fridays (we take out 1- 2 times a month) and Breakfast. I've written very complimentary reviews in the past. But now, we had fish two Fridays ago and it was smelly, rubbery and all in all bad. (It was take out, which we do a lot on Fridays, so we were not able to send it back. It would have been sent back had we been dining there). We had breakfast today and they still have farm fresh eggs - kudos to that! - but the hash browns came out white (no golden brown, crispy potatoes here), the biscuit gravy had some sort of odd spice and they don't serve real butter or real cream. Must say though, coffee is still excellent. Missing your old cooking!

We stayed here during Thanksgiving week with my parents and I was very impressed with not only the facilities, but the staff was awesome as well. The GM was helping during breakfast each day and he runs a great operation. I travel a lot - 130+ nights/year - and rarely do you get this type of service. Kudos to this great team!
Great vegan options, super friendly staff, really delicious
I lied....went back....took 45 minutes for half frozen crab puffs.   Ugh.....the older lady was very unfriendly.....my husband had to walk into kitchen......we walked out.

This place is the best! The staff is awesome, so are the views, so much space and the food is so fresh, I highly recommend the calamari, I love their sushi and plum wine, try this place out and you can't beat happy hour 3-6

Tire choice on big bend in riverview. They did damage to  my rim when putting a new tire on my new 2018 Cadillac. They wanted my husband to drop  and leave the car. I would never leave my car with them or even let  them touch my car again. So if you love your car I would take your car some where else. Because they will try to blow you off after they do damage to your vehicle.

Both Dr Hubrichs are the real deal when it comes to being great dentists. While I have only seen Dr Raymond, I have heard from friends with kids that Dr Niloofar is excellent too. And their support staff is exemplary also. From Delilah in reception to Jackie taking xrays and Jolene doing the teeth cleaning, I always felt cared for, cared about and well taken care of.  Same is true for Dr Raymond --- he assured me my teeth were in good shape and set my mind at ease with his professional and knowledgeable demeanor.  All their equipment is top notch too and up to date. Even their chairs are the most comfortable I have ever experienced.

This is my favorite store to go to when it comes to buying makeup, specifically designer and luxurious brands. The associates are usually on top of their game and can handle any and all questions. I've come in here several times and left with wonderful products that were great for my skin, all because I talked to them and listened to their advice. And that means a lot coming from me, because I've always hated talking to salesmen and am a "just looking around" type of girl. But really - open up and ask questions, because this is the only store I actually have faith in their product knowledge, lol. At Ulta, you get quite a few blank stares and best guesses. But at least at Sephora, if they don't know how to help you, they'll ask another associate or at least give you other resources. That goes a long way.

Stopped in on a Saturday afternoon for an early dinner with the wife.   We both had the Drunk Monk burger,  cooked medium rare.  Add a fried egg on top for that early heart attack special.  The burger's were excellent and so was the beer paired with,  a white iron wit.  Great food,  beer,  and service is a winning combination every time,  Cheers

Sullivans is a nice change for Tucson however a bit overpriced. Ive been here a few times and the food never really impressed me much. It lacks artistic presentation and is your run of the mill overpriced steakhouse. For the prices and selection of meats, I expect nice garnishes, great tenderization and cooking to ensure texture of food is on par.

I ordered a simple Caprese salad (I believe its called the tomato mozzarella with balsamic reduction) and it was great except... a bit on the bland side and lacking a fair amount of reduction sauce on it (even on the size, even extra drizzle... nada). It was just tomato hunks and mozzarella hunks. no basil hunks either! :(

The oh so trendy to order Chilean Sea Bass was good. Mediocre for the price. It was extra salty and very bland as well. The asian inspired sauce on the side was not a great mix for this fish nor was it amazing on its own.

Our waiter Jeff did have a nice knowledge of wine as far as my weak level of wine knowledge goes to get impressed.

My friends both ordered the sliders/burgers (off menu but its a bar menu item) and were very very happy with them. They said its great to go for happy hour when its 1/2 off. Deal !

With that advice given to me, only go when its 1/2 off happy hour and order a burger at the bar. Don't get the skewers or anything else. You'll only get the quantity of a tiny nibble.


Oh. and try to sweet tea mixed drink. dont let the name fool you. its not too sweet and enough to trick you into many rounds.

I went to the long awaited Elvira's in downtown Tucson yesterday! Everything was amazing! The food, the service, the ambiance. Cannot wait to go back! And you MUST try the Hibiscus Margarita!!! So worth the wait.

Both the food and the service are always dependable and consistently good. I usually sit at the bar or in the bar area. The bartenders are very attentive and the margaritas are great! Chili's menu has lots to choose from. My favorite is probably the quesedillas but everything I've tried is good from the huge salads to the yummy burgers and sandwiches. Highly recommended.

My mom and I went together.  I had the groupon so I was getting gel manicure and pedicure and my mom was getting a full set.  My mom walked away completely unhappy.  She has multiple cuts of her fingers and the color she picked out is completely different than what was put on her nails.She has cuts from the woman putting too much glue on the tips which resulted in her skin getting stuck and the woman ripping her skin away from the glue.  She never once apologized or showed my mom that she cut her.  To be honest I think my mom has 3 cuts on one hand and 4 on the other hand.  My manicure was okay but when I went back for my pedicure I was shocked to see them take something that was just used on someone else's. foot to be used on mine without cleaning it!  I was so shocked I didn't say anything.  To say we won't be returning is an understatment!

Had to snap a picture before I tear it up, Caribbean Twist NEVER disappoints. I usually get the goat or pork chunks but they didn't have either.. The man who helped me, he is ALWAYS amazing, suggested a grilled steak and it's the best choice I could've made. Love this place

Ordered a pizza and asked for extra pepperoni. It truly had barely 2 pieces of pepperoni per slice. Let alone double pepperoni. The food was just mediocre at best. Overpriced for the quality. Also ordered a salad, and jalapeño poppers. Truly overall the food was just barely satisfactory. Definitely need to add more ingredients if someone's paying for extra.

Good prices and atmosphere, the waitress works her tail off. Beer is fine. I had a cheesesteak in lieu of the Roast Beef sandwich, and it was decent, though  I had a couple of gristly bites. Overall it was fine though. I'll be back.

Delicious food.  Way better pizza than the previous owners.  We had the white lightening and a Cesar salad.  Both were very tasty.  Not much seating but the place is open and airy and has a nice patio.  Overall very nice and we will come back again.  Service was great!

I called their office in the morning and I was able to make a same day appointment to get my brake job done, when I took my car there  Megan was very professional and polite she let me know that they would look at the car give me an estimate before performing work I was able to go off and spend the day with my daughter. They called me and let me know how much it was going to cost exactly what needed to get done and that I would actually be able to pick it up in about two hours. I was very satisfied with the work and with the pricing and I would definitely come here again.

I'm still on the hunt for my go-to Vietnamese restaurant in Tucson, but this place is pretty good and seems to be Vietnamese owned judging but the Vietnamese wait staff. Honestly the food is 3 stars but the service deserves its own star. 

Items we've tried:
- goi cuon (spring rolls) were really good and the peanut sauce was also good, would def get again 
- cha gio (egg rolls) are the kind with the bubbly wrapper instead of the smooth egg wrapper, just a personal preference thing but my husband liked them 
- bun thit nuong (vermicelli with pork) looked good, husband liked it
- bun bo hue (spicy beef noodle soup) was just okay, not a ton of flavor. probably wouldn't get it again 
- hu tieu hoanh thanh (wonton soup with rice noodles) didn't have the aromatic fried onions or classic hu tieu broth flavor I was hoping for, would not get again

I would love to say how the food, service and ambiance was, but Pluckett's decided to cancel our reservation at the last minute.  

Apparently they had forgot that they booked out the restaurant for a company event that evening.  A silly mistake, but one I can easily understand.  However when they called to cancel (at least they did that) they told me they owned several other restaurants in the area and that we could try one of them.  When I expressed interest in one they told me to "Go online and see what they have available." Really?

Sorry guys - Don't burn a customer, tout your alternate suite of restaurants then not offer to book me at the alternate restaurant of choice.

This place has some of the best pizza in the city and also some of the nicest people who work there! The thin crust and perfectly browned underside are just a few things about this pizza that make it stand out. The fresh produce, meat and cheese all perfectly combine to give you a flavor explosion in your mouth. The cheese pizza is perfectly classic and the pepperoni only takes it another notch up!

Can't wait to order here again and again!
Very happy with the services. And so convenient. Staff very friendly and accommodating. Dentist thorough and professional.
Couldn't have had a better experience.  Love my new glasses. Shout out to wonderful optition Carmen filling in at W Ashley store in SC. Had a problem.  She saw that it was fixed to my satisfaction.
Jason always does it right. Some of the best seafood I have eaten. Staff is always great and nice.  Always a good experience no complaints

Stopped in before a show with a group a few nights ago and was here for an hour and a half. In that time, I had one drink- which it took approximately 15 minutes to ORDER, and placed an order for food which was never put through. I was never offered a cocktail refill OR a water refill. My entire table just wanted to have drinks, a few appetizers, and a few entrees. Our group would have definitely had a few drinks each in the hour and a half we were there, and it's a shame it was so hard to be attended to. I had never been here before and thought the atmosphere/patio was really neat and that it would be a great place to have drinks sometimes, but I will never return here. I left that night without eating anything and felt extremely disappointed and pretty angry at the whole situation. 

Everyone working here seemed overtaxed and disgruntled. I just want to point out that this place also seems to have been operating as a restaurant for a very long time, and I feel like it's not that hard to staff appropriately for nights when you know there is a show/event at the arena. It's a shame this beautiful space is being handled so poorly.

Expensive but well worth a visit.  The lime rickey is made with fresh pressed limes.  The soda water comes from a 1906 marble fountain (complete with a lamp on top) that they've used since the place first opened.  Maybe it's the flavor of 101 years in the fountain, but the soda tastes better here.  Ice cream is good, too.  They have really interesting soda combinations with syrups like orgeat (almond) and bitters.  They have an interesting fan contraption instead of A/C.  Read the 1906 treatise against air conditioning (not sure of the authenticity, but it's still fun).

Make a point to stop in.
I went there to have a tire fix and they said it would take 2 to 3 hours. Ridiculous. They only have one man doing this.

It's a Hampton Inn and in as much - there is a certain level of comfort and cleanliness to be expected. The staff upon check in was fairly pleasant and the process was efficient. 

Hotel is located on the fringe of the Forest Park neighborhood next to the freeway - but noise was never an issue. Entrance to hotel is hard to see - so be on the lookout for a tiny signed driveway.

Room was spacious - although the decor was a bit dated. I don't know if it was my room - but for 3 nights I felt like I was sleeping on a cardboard box - sooo uncomfortable. 

The other thing which was disconcerting was a note I received slipped under my door basically stating since I've refused room service for two days - management reserves the right to 'inspect' my room. Definitely a turn-off. I never accept room service and take care of the space I'm paying for - didn't make me feel like a valued customer at all. I usually stay at the Home 2 Suites when I'm in St. Louis - will be going back there next month.

Texas style (including Shiner and Tito's) in St Pete's.  The aroma knocks you over when you walk in.  The burnt ends where great.  St Louis ribs not quite fall off the bone but good nonetheless.  Great service - food was there quick.  Right across the street from the Trop so go for the BBQ and follow it up with a game.

Three Words: 25 Cent Martinis! 

Yup it is true. On weekdays at lunch you can get 25 cent good martinis or cosmos! Such a steal! That alone is worth the trip here, but don't come dressed too shabby! Matt J. had to keep his outer jacket on when we went here since he didn't have a collared shirt on! 

Other than the weird fake birds on one of the walls here, this place is giant fancy mansion and you feel like you are eating in an old southerner's dining room. And what is even cooler, the whole space is completely open meaning you can walk through the kitchen and any area you want to tour it which I thought was pretty cool to see. 

Food wise, the one thing anyone needs to get if they come here are their shrimp and grits. MY GOD THOSE GRITS. They are incredible. I wish I had more of them. I wish I had more of them right now as I am sitting here writing this review. They infuse them with tons of goat cheese and they are honestly amazing. The shrimp of course were big and tasty, but those grits stole the show of our whole meal I'd say. I also got the turtle soup which was nicely topped with a shot of sherry, Matt J. started with their strawberry salad which was good but nothing super unique by any means, and he also got the fried chicken which was good but again, nothing in comparison to that shrimp and grits dish. Then there was the bourbon cream topped bread pudding souffle that you have to order in advance since it takes a while to make and bake to that perfect, but this is another must if you come here. So be sure to save room! Oh I also heard there is a three drink limit with those 25 cent prices :-)

Food isn`t that great.Too greasy.And certain employee`s are ignorant,cashier Jasmaine i believe is her name.Order food and Jasmaine was being rude about taking my order.Their are other plac e in the neighborhood i can spend my money at.This place lost my business because of a rude and dumb employee like her.

Indie Photo I cannot thank enough. After my Grandmother passed I found 27 rolls of film/and or cameras that had not been developed in 15-20 years ago when the photos were taken.

Not even 10 months later, my Grandfather suddenly passed away on Saturday. Wanting the photos before his viewing, I dropped all of the rolls/cameras off on a Monday and requested them to be scanned instead of printing for my convenience with all of the craziness going on.

They sent them to be WITHIN 48 HOURS! The photos, all 524 of them, they were able to develop for me and scan and put on dropbox by Wednesday morning. I cannot thank them enough for all of the hard work they put in for me for these to be done. 

Since I had so many things to develop they discounted my transaction for me and I saved a lot of money compared to any other film development studio in Philadelphia.

The staff was friendly and helpful with all of the questions I had and are even saving the negatives for me that I am allowed to pick up at my convenience. Any film you need or any development I wouldn't look anywhere else.

AMAZING!!!!
Nice ambiance, but I was slightly disappointed in my meal. The salmon was a bit  dry, and the  salad lacked any interesting flavor.  The service was also a bit rushed.

So I haven't been here in a few years aside from drinks. Quite honestly, the food was always subpar. 

A friend chose for a birthday dinner and I'm so glad! Service was great, food was great and drinks were great! Everyone loved their meal. 

I had the poached shrimp and a salad. The house made dressing was a tasty touch. Others at the table had salmon, chicken and the burger and all were very pleased. 

I can't wait to return!

Wow chicken tacos, crunchy onion, cilantro, nice quality meat. They have a salsa bar with varying levels of spice if need be. Only qualm was that chips cost extra... Other than that I'd eat here again.

The food here is good! However they need to get organized I went there with my husband today 07/22/18 and it was pretty full in there however we didn't have to wait in the line. The problem with this place is that we sat down and someone came and asked us to get up because the table was already taken, mind you that there was no sign on the table or anyone's belonging on it!!! After we started eating the server asked us to get up we waited about 5 minutes with our plates in our hands for a table while the same person served other people their drinks while we were waiting. Therefore our food got cold and we left. Not to mention the the sign in front of the the store also said to "sit yourself.." This is crazy

I wasn't expecting this store to be so huge but what a nice surprise. 

We stopped in here while we were cruising State Street and I'm so glad we stumbled in. I wanted to get something for my daughter since this was the first time I was away from her but I didn't want to get anything super cheesy that would go to waste. I'm not a minimialist parent but I do try to be conscientious on the amount of kid crap she gets. I immediately found the childrens toys (score!) and noticed their great selection of eco-friendly toys that are right up my alley. They even had a great selection of children's books too which was a nice surprise. I passed on those because she just had her 1st birthday party and her theme was around books so she now has plenty. However, she's really into things that roll right now so the pink race car that I found for $9.99 looked to the be the perfect gift. 

We had a relatively early dinner so we knew we wanted to go back to our rental/condo and sit around the firepit with some wine and snacks. And we all know that Cost Plus is perfect for that. We found a bottle of wine and we each selected snacks for the evening...how efficient is that?  I'm glad we decided to pop on in, it was fun to browse and shop a little after our half marathon race earlier in the day.

P.S. My daughter LOVES her pink little race car.

So much history here! It is conveniently located across the street from the Amtrak station. While waiting for my brother to get in town, I went here for a quick drink. It's dimly lit, huge outdoor seating area and small hotel lobby bar. Most customers at the bar were not hotel guests. 

Great service, great pricing, and cool atmosphere. There is lack of seating in lobby/bar area. Mass confusion for bar lines, check in lines, restaurant lines, etc. 

Cool place to grab a drink while waiting for the ever delayed Amtrak train.

I won't lie as a New Yorker , their pizza is good ; The service on the other hand is quite opposite . 
I spent about 20 bucks on a Large pie half topping of ham , 2 liter for my family and I which work in the plaza as well and order lunch there all the time . We asked for a side of marinara and they asked to run my card again for $0.50 !! 50 cents , I laughed and still tipped them $2.75 just to prove a point ..
Excellent food and customer service. Ask for Lamont! And be sure to have an Uptown to drink.

I'd give PJ's three and a half stars, if Yelp would let me. It's beach food in a funky setting that's better than most, but still, with plenty of room for improvement. When we stopped by two nights ago, the Chicago style oysters were just so-so.  The problem was, they were left under the broiler too long.  On the other hand,  the raw clams were really delicious (but then, what can you do to food served raw?). The blackened grouper was very tasty and nicely done, but the amberjack filet that sitting on the plate, right next to the grouper, was overcooked. The baked potato was perfect, but the cole slaw was complete forgettable. That's how it seems to be at PJ's, something done really well, served with something done not so well.  On balance, it's a dependable place, frequented by locals as well as tourists.  It could be better, but then it probably wouldn't be PJ's any longer.

Fresh? Yes. Friendly staff? Yes. Clean facilities? Yes. Quick service? Yup yup and yup. As far as I'm concerned they meet my requirements for a five star revue. 

If your looking for a good quick bite, whether you dine in or order to go, you can't beat this. I've tried several sandwich styles here with different breads during the 'lunch hour' rush and have found the ingredients to be fresh and bread to be soft. Typically i'm greeted with a smile or a hello from someone behind the counter when I walk in. The handful of times I've dined in I find the tables to be clear of debris and trash. 

Plenty of on sight parking. Handicap accessible. Haven't had any issues and plan on returning again soon. Thanks for the good work. Keep it up.

While in Clearwater this is a must stop. The servers are extremely friendly and on top of it but the food is wonderful. I recommend the Wagyu Beef appetizer because it was prepared to where the flavor was so amazing it melted in my mouth. I had the Scottish Salmon for an entree and was also very pleased with how delicious it was.

We called Tubular George about a fireplace insert. We were considering another company and also online do-it-yourself logs. They were prompt, efficient, WAY cheaper than the other alternatives -- and they installed the logs immediately instead of making us wait for weeks to place an order. We're thrilled!

Seriously?  I'm SHOCKED that more people haven't reviewed Three DDD's as their GO TO spot in New Castle!  I've lived here off an on throughout the years, and I have to admit, out of all the "Neighborhood" Cheese Steak spots, this one takes the cake when it comes to service, cooking, and GREAT TASTING food.  Thank you Three DDD's for being my New Castle HOME BASED Cheesesteak and Hoagie Shop!

GHM is the best mall in Nashville for high-end shopping.   (I didn't say best mall, I said best for high-end).  What makes it  different than other malls in Nashville:
1.  Louis Vuitton
2.  BCBG
3.  Bebe
4.  Nine West
5.  No food court
6.  Sephora
7.  Apple
8.  Horrible parking - especially with Nordstroms construction
9.  Tiffany's 


And a little birdie told me Tory Burch is coming, too.  Yay!

This place is by far the best Indian Food I have found in Indy thus far.  The butter chicken was superb and with medium level spice, actually had a really nice worth to it that I have not had before.  I think next time, I might kick it up a notch on the spice level, but I like spicy food.  

I usually judge a restaurant on my first visit by how good the "standard" or classic dish is, and this one was exceptional which holds lots of promise for other items on the menu.

Also got to have some golub jamin (sp?) which is my favorite Indian dessert, at it was piping hot, so watch out or you could burn yourself.  

Service was great and friendly, even told me of a place to find Milk Cake since they don't make it here.  I will be back!!

For those of us who miss the old downtown location of San Antonio Taco Company, especially before hockey games, Bakersfield is more than an adequate replacement. You go for the tacos--chicken mole and short rib are my favorites--but my daughter loves the tortas. Even the salad selections are solid enough. Great place to hang before and after a game, with $3 shot specials.

Not coming back! Tea was good, rude customer service! I was being shouted at upon ordering. This particular girl at the counter has an attitude as if she owns the place! Too bad i liked your milk tea.

This was my second visit and I will NOT be returning EVER! I called before I went in to see if they did gel nails, which is less abrasive and not as bulky as acrylic and I was told over the phone that they did do gel nails. So I went on my lunch break. First the nail tech that did my pedicure did a very sloppy job of painting my nails, did not want to use the toes separators and acted as if she did not understand me or speak English when I requested her to clean up her work. But more importantly I DID NOT get gel nails, I was given an acrylic overlay because once again the nail tech acted as if he did not understand me or English for that matter. I then informed him that I was told over the phone prior to coming that this service was performed at the salon. The only type of protective option was acrylic and I was not aware of this until the nail tech had BEGAN applying it. My nails were filed horribly, the nail polish was applied messy and when ever I pointed something out, I got blank stares. Then to top it all off, as a another nail tech was polishing my nails my change was brought to me but was quickly TAKEN by the owner. When I protested against it, (it was only 4 dollars which I was going to leave anyway but its the principle!) I was informed that she assumed I was giving it to the nail tech and then she quickly walked away. The techs and the owners are all about cutting corners and doing a very rushed and half done job, I will NEVER get my nails done at Elegant Nails & Spa again.

I have seen Dr. Altman, 5 stars for sure, for years and very much appreciated him seeing me soon at my primary's request.  I am, however, very disappointed in the inability of the main answering personnel to help me when I called the number on the card.  So I reached the office another way and got fabulous instant service.  It's so disappointing to see another great practice turning into a mega business that loses all personal touch.  If you can tolerate what it takes to reach this doctor and great staff, you won't be disappointed.

Very cool place! I will have to update my review when I actually eat here. Came for bottomless mimosas on a Saturday. Was very slow but also super early. Met the smoker guy Earl, who gave me a tour of the place. It's huge! There is so much room here for seating. Lots of TVs for game days. An area for live music, a boat you can sit on, an outdoor bar, a posh space for seating, a game area, as well as plenty of indoor seating and an indoor bar. Great specials each day as well. 
The staff is super friendly. I also met Ed, the owner. A very nice guy. 
The food looks fantastic. I will provide an updated review on that. 
Definitely a nice change of pace for saint Pete. Think of the chattaway meets fish tales minus the views of the water. 
The parking is very limited. I'm told you can park at Munches after their hours.
Definitely worth checking out!

Delicious! We finally stopped here on the way to the beach and we weren't disappointed! I had the caprese panini and my husband had the club med. We were both thrilled! Fresh veggies and mozzarella to die for. The market has some unique snacks and sauces. The service was great - thanks Andrew! This will be our new go to sandwich shop!
Boom boom shrimp was good.  The nachos were very underwhelming.  The service was great.  Jacquelyn was friendly and attentive!

Came highly regarded through some of my online haunts so I made it my first stop on the way into Clearwater off the airport. The good? Probably the single best draft selection I experienced during my time in the Tampa area. I got to try such luminaries as Old Rasputin, Dogfish Head World Wide Stout and the new Ten FIDY, impressive on their own and blows me away to get them at one stop. The bad? Our sandwich, while an interesting concoction of turkey, apple and brie came with rotting lettuce inside, and the service was hit and miss with pleasant staff standing beside a bartender who yells at you for ordering something they're out of on the drink menu they provide.

Don't be swayed, for a great brew at a reasonable price you're not going to go wrong in the Clearwater area. And sign up for their mailing list on their website for a little advance notice of new taps and promotions coming up each week.

Oh, and grab your home bottles at the nearby World of Beer where you'll get a 2-for-1 draft coupon to use here!

Have yet to find a burger in Indy that's keeps me coming back consistently. I realize five guys is not just an Indy establishment but nonetheless I don't go out of my way to get to it. Burger is okay nothing to really write home about. I got my burger "all the way" which has all the toppings on it. I find it better sometimes to get all the flavors on the burger and just pick off what I don't prefer. Only thing I didn't prefer on the burger was the grilled onions but again that's just a preference. Don't get me wrong they do their version of the burger well.it just wasn't as juicy as I would've wanted and the bun was super soggy after just a few minutes. I'm also picky about my fries and these just aren't it chief. Menu is reminiscent of In-n-out.

the best veggie burger I've ever had. And I've had a lot considering I don't eat meat! And the butter lettuce  !! Honestly, it's probably not a big deal to some people, but butter lettuce is the best. My boyfriend loved his regular (meat) burger so much, he got another one before we left. 

Oh, and they source all their products locally! Except for the soda. That's from Maine. But it's in the USA and that's what matters!

Can't wait to go back.

My boyfriend and I were driving around last night thinking where should we go to eat??
I had suggested Chili's, but he mentioned that the food was not worth the price and so we drove around a little more. After stopping at a couple restaurants and either hating the menu or hating the price, we went to Chili's and sucked up the price.

To our surprise, we really enjoyed our meal this time. We both ordered fajitas, he ordered the steak and I ordered the chicken. We just shared the whole thing and we were both so satisfied. Our service wasn't great or horrible, somewhere in between. 

And the place was packed with families! I guess we looked past the fact that the next day was a holiday and it was the end of spring break.

The meal ended up costing us $46 before tip. I paid with debit and the machine came up with an option of tipping 18% which I found pretty bizarre. I don't think I have ever found the service 20% worthy at Chili's and I think they should have pre programmed 15% like most places. Setting our sights a little high there Chili's.

All in all, a good meal. But my boyfriend predicted correctly : Not worth the high cost.

Wow! GREAT DEAL. Delicious and unique burgers for $6 or 2 for $10 (I think). Risotto balls for $3 (these are a bit 'too' much but good in small quantities).

The England burger is essentially a Shepherd's Pie on a bun...and wow. I'm a huge fan of shepherd's pie and this was amazing. I never would have thought to pair it on a bun...and now I want more!

I also got the Korean burger, which is burger with Kimchi and BBQ sauce. This was good, too! Different but I liked it. 

The service was also very friendly. I can't wait to find them again!

Flavors were so good! We're recommended to eat here when walking down state street on a spontaneous trip. The impossible sliders were amazing, appetizers were filling, and Dakota, our server, was great. I definitely recommend

Me and my wife used to go to this place quite often(2+ times/week) few years back for their different types of kabobs and their good menu. The food was decent and the Manager nice. 

It all stopped when we took their coupon from that current year's entertainment guide and they said they don't accept it knowing pretty well we are frequent visitors and the coupon was actually printed in that guide! After some discussion, they said they will accept it and when they brought the bill, the prices of our items were all jacked up to compensate for the coupon! Not sure how they could shamelessly do it since the actual prices were clearly listed in the menu! It's been 4 years since this happened and we never went back. 10 years back they could have gotten away with it, but there is no dearth of good Indian restaurants in the Western Suburbs. Horrible customer service. And don't let anyone tell you this was done by the old management. It was the new management-old management was worse than this!!

The staff is very friendly and I love their coffee. Unfortunately I am on a gluten free diet but I stop by every morning to get my cup of coffee. My son also loves their bagels so we come sometimes on the weekend for lunch. The Turkey Chili is very yummy too.

This grimy little place is easily my favorite watering hole in Tampa.  In summary:  cash only, $7.25 pitchers of bud, $0.25 games of pool, and everyone gets along.  I would describe it as "a hole in the wall, no bullshit... or anything else for that matter."  If you're in the mood for something else, then don't go inside.  Otherwise, it's a great place to stop in, drink, and play pool with some good competition from the regulars.  They recently upgraded their TVs to HD and have a nice new monitor and cabinet for their bowling video game, but don't let that fool you, this place is ugly.

It tends to get pretty crowded on the weekends, and it's probably smaller than your apartment.  Not for anyone who is sensitive to cigarette smoke.

Our family ate here in December and had a cool experience, but noted the service was slow.  We chalked it up to being a new place and thought they needed to get their kinks out.  Apparently, they need more time. 
We had a 5:30 reservation and were seated quickly. The restaurant wasn't crowded at all when we got there, but filled in after we were seated.  I didn't look at my watch I see how long it took the server to see us or take or drink orders, but I know it was 6:15 when I had my first sip.  45 minuets is kinda long to wait for a drink.  
Since we ordered from the survivor menu, where the food comes seared, not fully cooked, one would think we would have gotten fed fairly quickly. Nope, our food came at 7:15.  The food was good, but we were starving after being there for almost 2 hours, so we would have eaten just about anything.  
To finish off the experience, we had a quick question on our check which took 3 other employees to find our server to confirm the bill.  
The virtual experience is cool. The food is fine. It might be up your ally if you don't go expecting to eat for two hours.
Awesome bar area.  Very customer service oriented.  GREAT location.  What more could you ask for?

We decided to go to the cheese cake factory for my best friends birthday so we could have some yummy cheese cake! We went during happy hour so we enjoyed the buffalo blasts which are always amazing and since it was happy hr we got a full order for only $5 WOW! 
For our main course we both had fish and chips which were average and typical for a large chain restaurant especially one that has such a massive menu. 
The service was good and our food was out extremely fast (I'm guessing because everything we ordered was premade and just needed to be fried up for us.) The only thing that took a while was the cheese cake! It seems every time I go here it takes a good 10-20 min to get our cheesecake after we order it! It was ok though since we were already stuffed.
 And the cheese cake was amazing as always! She had the red velvet which never disappoints and I had the tiramisu that I really enjoyed! 
Will def be back for the cheesecake but next time will prob just order some lettuce wraps or the buffalo blast as my meal because it seems the entrees usually leave me wishing I hadn't spent the money.

INSTRUCTORS:
The instructors are competent and helpful when they are available.
During the day, from 12-6, the class had access to one java instructor, one .Net instructor, and zero Angular/React instructors. 
During the evening from 6-9, the class had access to one Java/Angular/React instructor, but not for the full duration of the program(except by e-mail). 
CURRICULUM:
Several of my classmates who were new to programming dropped out. They cited "insufficient mentorship" or "too fast paced" as their reasons. 
You are expected to spend the majority of your time on Google teaching yourself, which is an important skill, but not what you pay other people for. 
Do not expect more than a cursory glance at HTML/CSS here as part of the "Full-Stack Developer" training. 
JOB ASSISTANCE:
My employer sent me to this training, so I have no experience depending on them to find me work. 5 stars by default. 
OVERALL EXPERIENCE: 
I learned a great deal while I was here, by teaching myself 10x as much as I was taught. There are other programs and resources I would recommend for a fraction of the cost.
