Some of us were pressured into writing a hasty and positive review of this program. Since I don't depend on Claim Academy to find me work, I feel that I can be honest here. Take this into account when reading other reviews.
Love this place. It's usually very clean and the people that work there are always pleasant.

Had a really good experience here, Jeff is an awesome healer.  Had the deep tissue/soft tissue massage done with the chakra/aura clearing.  Price was very affordable, next time I'm in town I will book another appointment again.

Mmmmmm. That's all that's necessary. But here's your tip: Carolina BBQ sauce. IT comes in that funny vinegar bottle, and it looks all watery, but put it on your lighter meats, your pork and turkey, and its heaven. Haven't been to another BBQ place in Indy that had it. The price is good, and I brought home lots of leftovers, so, score!
Always pleased with my entrees and the draft beer selection. Service always friendly and fast. Their Mac & cheese creations are yummy and worth many return visits!

If ever there was a spot deserving of a 5-star rating, this is it. Amazing banh mi sandwiches and fantastic pho. Reasonably priced and it gives even the best Pho spots in Philly a run for their money. I love this place. So happy to have such high quality Vietnamese restaurant in the burbs.

Zayna has moved locations and while the food is the same and tasty as ever, the parking situation is much better! They have plenty of spots around the back of the new location and I think their indoor seating has also increased.

They lied and told me the pool would open July 5. Guess what? Now its July 22 for the grand opening. They wont even open the sauna because its in the same area and they think the typical member will be so stupid that they will use the pool at the same time. Same broke dick machines are still broken despite the response you may see here from corporate

Lame

Reno already has a lot of good Thai restaurants. Moo Dang has joined the crowd, offering some different takes on their dishes. I'm not going to say Moo Dang is my favorite but the food was excellent and distinctive.
I studied the menu before ordering. They offer what they call garden rolls instead of spring rolls. And they have red roasted pork on the menu. I hadn't seen that on other restaurant menus. I got that. 
The waiter took my order and I decided to flag him down and I also ordered tom kha gai (chicken coconut soup), one of my favorite Thai dishes. Each restaurant makes it a little different.
The garden rolls are aptly named. The vegetable component is pronounced. The vegetables were fresh and tasted like they were made after I ordered it. So many spring rolls taste mass produced ahead of time. 
The soup was also tasty. It had lemongrass that was edible. I can't remember having this in any chicken coconut soup before. Usually the lemongrass is thick and fibrous and too bitter to eat. This was tender and the lemongrass flavors were more delicate. I asked the waiter about this and he said that they cut away the thicker part and just used the softer, inner part.
The red roasted pork is called moo dang in Thai, the waiter said. I got it served over noodles. It was listed in the soup part of the menu. It also had slices of a meat cake, bok choy, cilantro and a soy vinaigrette. I didn't like the vinaigrette but everything else was good and worked well together.
Near the end of the dish I decided to try it with some of the fish sauce with chili. I put just a dab and mixed it with the remaining part of the dish. It turned it into a 5-star atomic scorcher. I thought I had a tolerance for spicy food but this seemed off the chart.
I look forward to trying the rest of the menu. There could be some interesting variations of other dishes.
This is in the location of what was an Indian restaurant for a long time and was an Asian restaurant for a short time.

I've gotten a few tans from a few different places, but my first tan at Shades was hands down the best tan I have ever gotten.  Elle gave me the perfect color blended perfectly with my original skin tone making it look very natural.  Scheduling the appointment was super easy and the student discount was also a plus!  Love my tan & will most definitely be coming back for to Shades for my next one!

We have only been customers of Bayshore Animal Clinic for 5 months but are treated like we have been customers for years. Great service, reasonable pricing, knowledgeable staff !! Highly recommend this vet clinic to anyone in the South Tampa vicinity.

I wanted to buy something for a colleague at work to say thank you and a friend recommended edible arrangements. Sounded interesting. So I went online and while there was a huge assortment of products available - at the end of the day it's beautiful cut fruit shaped to form a bouquet. 

Some had balloons, some were dipped in chocolate. Eventually I found one that looked good. Just a click away and the product was added to cart. You have to pay for delivery which annoyed me considering I was just about to spend 125 dollars. I found a delivery discount code which worked and I managed to avoid the 10 dollar delivery fee. I could choose which location would make the arrangement and decided on the New Orleans store. I could even pay through PayPal which was great. 1 more click and the arrangement was purchased. 

The next day the arrangement arrived. It looked like the picture except the emoji balloons were different but I didn't mind that at all. I never tasted the fruit but I will be asking my colleague about the quality and update my review. 

All in all it was a good experience. Please note that I ordered a medium size and there are small and large available to.

My boyfriend and I decided to go back for a visit to this place since the remodel. However this visit was absolutely horrible due to the bartender. After we placed our order, we watched as a bartender proceeded to mix our drinks as he tasted along the way.  The only problem, was that he kept double-dipping the same straw he was putting in his mouth.  We gently called this to his attention, only for his nasty and unprofessional reply of "there's no fucking way!"  To this reply, we got up, approached the manager and informed him of our exchange with the bartender.  The manager couldn't have cared less and at this point we left.  My boyfriend is a restaurant owner in the area (4 restaurants to be exact), and had this happened at his establishment he would have had the balls to confront the bartender and at least make good on 2 properly made drinks.  Needless to say, we won't be returning.  Should you decide to take a chance at this place, keep an eye on the bartender.

A cute place to have some really delicious desserts! The portion is pretty big and one piece of item is pretty much enough. I bought the clichy ($5.75) and the red velvet cupcake ($5). One minor thing was their slow service but it was fine.

We stayed here for a total of 7 nights. It is located right next to The Ruby Slipper which seems to have lines out the door anytime we pass by so if you've been wanting to try it then this location would be great for you.

Checking in: We walked in and were greeted by a nice young lady at the reception desk. We checked in within a few minutes hassle free. There is parking behind the hotel which you pay for $20-$30 (including tax) per night. We were informed that due to COVID room service isn't available, housekeeping is reduced to as needed, if we need towels to put ours on the floor and call for new ones, and breakfast would be in to go bags. We don't typically use room service and since going vegan there's not much in a continental breakfast I could have anyway so all of that was fine with us.

Rooms: The first nights we stayed in a queen room. This room has a normal height ceiling. It was clean and non-smoking. The room itself is a bit smaller than I had expected. Comes with a safe, fridge, iron & ironing board, marble tub & shower, hairdryer, desk, and lamps. Our room had a view of the street, we can see the cars pulling up for check in.
We decided to extend our trip and attempted to do it at the front desk but the price we were quoted wasn't what we would be happy to pay. I went online and booked a king room for what I was happy to pay. The later half of the stay was in a king room. It has a high ceiling. The room is larger also non-smoking. This time we had a view of other buildings. Albeit was a bit tough for me to reach the stick you use to slide the curtains because I'm only 5' so you just slide the curtain without the stick. Other than that no critique really.

If you are looking to book a room and debating between the queen or king just go with the king. You'll be much happier.

Our service was lacking from the start. I heard how good the burgers were and ordered a bacon cheeseburger burger no lettuce. Received the burger with lettuce and no bacon or cheese. I explained it to the waiter and he said we will have to see who's fault that was. I had scraped the lettuce an started to eat the burger because it took him so long to return. I was told ooops that it was his fault. Never really offered an apology.

I finally found coffee ice cream!!! Yay! It's a little place with big favor! The menu has so much to choose from and Ike I said before I'm definitely going to try the sweet potato fries.  This place is so much more than just ice cream and the people are so friendly and offered a frequent customer card! It's great that it's jut around the corner and will definitely be back throughout the summer months!

Great meal. Meatloaf sandwich was tasty and spicy; great to share also could never have eaten the whole thing solo. Nachos were tasty and very hot with nicely melted real cheese. Beer was very good, only suggestion would be to chill the glasses.

BRING YOUR OWN WINE.  Just wanted to get that off my chest first.  The first time colleagues and I came to Carlucci's we made the mistake of sitting and ordering before realizing they didn't have a liquor license. (Hey...red wine is half the reason I eat Italian food!)  The next time we came we went to the SuperFresh Market around the corner first so we could bring our own. That worked out well.

Otherwise the place is great. Delicious food. Great menu. Warm setting. Friendly staff. Excellent and very professional service. Prices are a bit on the high side, but both my meals here have been very enjoyable, even the one without wine.

One of the better Indian restaurants I ever visited in North America. Good food, excellent service and no feeling of being gouged by the classic Punjabi places. Good menu and tasty food. Asha gave us great service. 

Definite revisit material.

My compliments

Sidecar is a small bar that's filled with amazing bartenders and deliciously crafted cocktails. The bartenders take pride in their drinks and I always enjoy stopping here for a drink once in awhile. It's small but intimate and it's a great place to hit for a drink to focus on engaging in conversation with the people you are with, there is no television on which means no distraction. I love their patio, it's also a great place to enjoy a drink. My favorite drink is the thyme and tonic, it's so refreshing and beautiful to look at. If you ever need a quality cocktail hit this place.

This female owner has an attitude problem, and you can't eat in since they have no bathroom! It makes me wonder where the workers relieve themselves.

Too much competition in the area to waste time with a hole in the wall whose owner and delivery driver convey an attitude. This place is unprofessional to say the least.

A joke.

Tavola calda is a must. Farm fresh ingredients and so many choices. Beets were especially delicious. The vitello was tender and savory. Also recommend the squid ink fra diavolo.  Juicy and just a little spicy pasta in red sauce with tender clams, mussels and sweet shrimp. The grilled pound cake was interesting and delicious. This is authentic Roman cuisine that is superior to many places actually in Rome. 4 stars!

I come here most mornings before work. Their bagels are EXQUISITE. I get either their daily bagel special or the vegan lox and an iced coffee, and it makes my mornings brighter. The service is friendly & efficient and the vibe is chill & unpretentious. A fantastic addition to the neighborhood.

This local restaurant always exceeds expectations. The service is always exemplary. The food is always delicious (even for a non Italian food lover. And the ambiance and atmosphere is always pleasant and relaxing. A definite delight during this COVID-19 pandemic. So glad we decided to go out!

The courtyard is enchanting. It's somewhere I would sit and read and drink coffee for hours quietly- if it weren't filled with people gettin their brunch on of course. 

I was alone here and still given a nice table in the courtyard.  A prime spot for a Saturday morning rush. I didn't make it inside but imagine it's lovely as well. 

I ordered the spicy pulled pork sandwich, which goes by a much fancier name - Cochon de lait. The waitress said it's her favorite item on the brunch menu. It was good and very wet. My bottom bread got all soggy while it was still on the plate. That did not stop me from taking it back to my hotel and eating it for dinner. The salad on the side was average and the jalapeño rolls were underwhelming. 

The service was not that great. Everyone was friendly, but I was unclear initially as to who my waiter was and I think maybe they were too. Then when that seemed to be firmly established she rarely came by. I was sitting outside on a chilly morning and needed refills on my coffee and had to ask. I would think someone would be making rounds for that or check on other tables while they were at it. I also had to ask for the rolls, and was given a false excuse that they were baked late. I saw other tables with them way before me and they were completely cold when I received them.  No big deal, but bad excuse. 

I'm giving three stars because the service was friendly but really inattentive. If they'd been able to keep my coffee filled and I wasn't trying to find someone to help me each time I'd probably be happier. Also the sandwich was good but I wouldn't order it again. And the rolls were pointless in my opinion, unless they are just a conveyor for butter - which I also didn't get. 

I'd go again to enjoy the courtyard and try something new. See if they can win me over with better service next time.
Had an outstanding dinner at The Mill. The drinks were delicious and creative too. Great atmosphere and nice outside seating. Will definitely be going back soon!

This was actually a pretty clean thrift store. Didn't notice that "thrift store smell." It is well-lit, organized, and had good signage. There are some good deals on furniture. They had several cherry wood desks in really good condition for only $40! Their sofas and chairs were in good shape, too, and the upholstery was cleaned.

Prices are comparable with other thrift stores. Deseret Industries still has 'em all beat, I think.

Floor is carpeted, so that makes for a more pleasant experience. They do need more racks, I think, because many of them are so crammed full of inventory.

The food here is ok. We ordered the plain fried rice w/ bean sprouts and green peppers, crab Rangoon, and a plain St. Paul. The fried rice was pretty good. I added my usual hot pepper and soy sauce to it. The crab Rangoon was a little bit too sweet for my taste but was fried perfectly so I had no problems eating it. Now the St. Paul was mediocre at best. A St. Paul is basically a fried egg patty sandwiched between two pieces of white bread. The sandwich usually comes with mayo picked and onions. I asked them to hold the mayo and I assumed it would have the pickles and onions on it. Well it had an unmelted cheese slice a tomato and a piece of lettuce. The patty was not thick. Sorry, no pictures for this post.

Always leave here full and happy! I typically get the vegetarian pho with shrimp or tofu, the vermicelli bowl with pork and shrimp, or the shrimp vermicelli platter that comes with rice paper to make summer rolls. Portions are big and reasonably priced, especially for the area. You do have to flag down the servers if you need anything, but they're super friendly and responsive once you get their attention.

Why wait in line at ACME when you can get awesome food at Felix's across the street?? We had a large group for dinner and only waited about 15 minutes to be seated (had to be split up bu that's fine). My table ordered oysters (raw and chargrilled), crawfish etoufee, alligator bites, jambalaya, and turtle soup. The etoufee was flavorful and I enjoyed the fried crawfish pieces on top as garnish. The jambalaya was not dry like others that I've had, a plus! My friends also found the turtle soup and alligator bites tasty.

Came back again with friends and ordered an array of desserts. Service was great, but quality of the cakes not so much this time :/ everyone agreed that all 4 cakes tasted a day or two old. Flavor was there but they were all a bit tough and didn't taste fresh. I think/hope it was just one bad experience. I would come back again since my first experience was great!

Tiramisu is still my #1. Strawberry fresh cream cake was pretty stiff and everyone's least favorite pastry. Double chocolate cake was very rich, decadent and fudgy. I wanted to love the Hokkaido cheesecake because I normally love anything and everything from Hokkaido, but this wasn't a winner.

Woldenberg Park is a great hang out place. It is not that far of a walk from the French Quarter, the Aquarium of the Americas, and the Riverwalk.

This is a great place to stroll around and take in the views of the river. During Mardi Gras season, this is also a great place to go to as they have performances that are free to the public. And really, the people of New Orleans can always turn performances into a party!

Woldenberg Park is a place I love spending time in.

This review updates my previous reviews.  April Fresh Cleaning came in today and in a few hours, Stacy and her crew had our home (which was a model home) back into model home clean state - it is truly immaculate.  We are very, very, very happy.

Stacy's crew is pleasant and they worked hard to get our house clean from top to bottom.

This past Saturday, they organized my storage area in the basement, taking it from cluttered to organized and our house back to a gleaming clean state that we love.

Based on these past two experiences, we have decided to have Stacy and her crew come every two weeks to clean the house to keep it as we like it.

Thanks Stacy!!!

Very nice staff with lots of knowledge. Got a mani/pedi with gel polish. Looks great. The only reason not a 5 star is due to the chairs for the pedi. Not made for this so very u comfortable. Used a foot bucket bust since they have to fill Ana walk it over the water was not hot. No jets or anything like.

I wish I could give 0 stars - the receptionist couldn't get me through to someone in service department so I asked her if she could just walk over to service and give them a message for me and she basically said no. The receptionist station is 20 feet from service. I had a horrible experience buying my jeep from them and will never return here for service again.

I'm going to start off by stating that I am a self-proclaimed root-beer hater. I don't like the sweetness, the synthetic flavors, and the licorice-y aftertaste. It reminds me of those scratch-n-sniff stickers as a kid, only in artificial, drinkable form.

Fitz's root beer floats changed my mind.

Oh, my god. These things are huge - 3 giant scoops of ice cream are piled into a mug, and it's all served on a plate with a napkin under it to catch the inevitable spills. You don't drink this stuff, you scoop it. And oh is it delicious...melted ice cream, mixed with a root beer that tastes less synthetic than most commercial brands, is perfect for cooling down on a humid St. Louis summer day.

The burgers here are solid; they're a bit on the pricier side but are made of good quality meat, and the onion tanglers have always been perfectly crisp in my experience. Portions are large and satisfying. I wouldn't suggest anything else here, though - the wings are sticky and unappetizing, as are their pasta dishes, which have far too much sour cream in them.

The atmosphere is laid-back, you can look at the old bottling machinery and sit at the old diner-style bar if you want, too. Very fun, very St. Louis place to go. But if you take anything away from this review at all: remember to get the root beer floats!

I don't think there is a better coffee shop in Tucson on the east side! Village bakehouse on the other side of town is the only competition Le Buzz has. Baked goods are fresh, salads are so yummy and crisp, Breads amazing, creative menu items and consistantly wonderful. Staff is fantastic! Just have to wait a bit on weekend mornings but its worth it!
The food is okay. Just okay. It's super busy all the time. I would definitely say this place is a tourist trap. There are better places to get brunch around here.

Great shop. Very knowledgeable staff. Good fair pricing. Highly trustworthy. Store is clean and has a good selection of retros and the newest hottest shoes. 

You can also play the Key Master machine and have your shoes legit checked

NEVER GO HERE!!!!  My boyfriend paid $99.00 for a Valentine's Day Package on Half off depot for this spa. I was very excited as the package included a Swedish or hot stone message, a lavender aromatherapy, microdermabrasion session, and a vitamin C facial mask. Sounded like everything I would need for a relaxing day. My difficulty with this spa started when I was just trying to call and schedule my appointment.  I called twice in one week and left voicemails without ever getting a call back. I eventually got a hold of them and told them I would like to make an appointment to use my valentine's day spa package.  At the time I didn't know exactly what the package came with nor was it listed on my half off depot voucher.  I assumed that the establishment would know what they put out on half off depot and scheduling would be easy. 

Boy I was wrong. The lady at the front desk fought with me asking what services were included in the package and saying she had no idea what the package included and proceeded to laugh at me the whole conversation. She proceeded to tell me that it was no different than a friend purchasing a gift card for a store for me and me walking into the store and asking what I could buy with it. She made me feel very stupid, even though it was their responsibility to know what promotions they have and what is included in those promotions.  After about 10 minutes of arguing she told me she would have another lady call me the next day to verify the services and schedule an appointment.  I did get a phone call back and this time the lady was a bit more knowledgeable of what came my package.  Even though she knew what was on the package she stated that she would not do the lavender aromatherapy, as stated on the voucher, because it made no sense to have that with a vitamin c mask. I am not a spa person I don't know what goes with what all I know is that service was listed on my voucher and they should honor it.  After her making me feel like a complete idiot she proceeded to book my appointment.

At this point I did not feel comfortable going to this spa and decided to talk to half off depot to get my money back.  They did an investigation and called the spa to see why they were not honoring a service that they specifically included in their package.  I then got a call from half off depot saying they spoke to the spa who in turn told half off depot that I came in for my services already, was so happy with it that I booked more, and that I left a great tip!!! They flat out lied to half off depot. My appointment wasn't schedule until August 3rd how could I have come in and done these things!!  

If you want to be laughed at and mocked  then please by all means go to this spa. But if you are looking for a stress free relaxing retreat don't even think twice about this place!! It will be stressful before you even step foot inside the door.

WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER!

Bar smells like mildew. We walked in bartender said hi and walked off, never asked if she could help us, seemed annoyed that we were there. This place is gross for real, bar counters are sticky.  Alright great talk.

My wife hurt her ankle, we went to the 700 Walnut location. Sucks my wide broke  her foot, but this place and the staff are fantastic. A pleasant an experience you can have under the circumstances. Nurses super friendly and professional. Doc was great, hysterical, personable, on the money with the pre x ray diagnosis. I may go back to just hang out.
I feel really bad for my past review. I've been taken here a couple times since then and I'm convinced this is the best mexican place in the IV area. Big fan of the nachos and burritos.

I agree with another reviewer that said it looked scruffy. I think it's an old building that was never intended to be a restaurant. A bump out for a kitchen that extends into the bar area, condiments and extra beer cases stored in the open and bathrooms that were clearly an afterthought. BUT the food and service were fantastic. We went for lunch, less crowded and attentive staff. It was just me and my husband. We shared a couple appetizers and a basket of western fries. Fish tacos with blackened Mahi came with a hot salsa and cheese. My first experience with Mahi. Firm fish - perfect for the taco. We had the 8 piece shrimp appetizer fried. The cocktail sauce they serve is sweet, like a Thai sauce, a nice change of pace. Fries are seasoned and they too were fried to perfection. Crispy & hot! I'd go back anytime and have a repeat lunch.
Great food a service . The Kal Dosa was as close to authentic tic as Iev ever has in the US . Highly recommend this restaurant!!

Beautiful place just like across the street. I got the vegetarian salad and I did not like the presentation of having to cut my own huge head of lettuce. It was so much effort I just stopped half way. 
My husband got grilled oysters but once you have had them at Dragos in New Orleans (lived there for a few years) these are just eh barely on that level. 

 my salmon had this layer of crust on top but honestly that layer tasted burnt. I am not sure if that is supposed to be like that or what? I have never had salmon with a thin burnt layer on top so I am not sure if that is their thing or what but I didn't care for that burnt taste. The size of the salmon was good and it was delicious inside. 

The side of mac and cheese was very good. My husband liked his bass. 
I do not think I would go back.

Burrito Loco is by far my favorite Mexican spot. The people who work there are awesome, the freshly made hot sauce and pico de gallo are delicious. Bonus: it's a BYOB. Even though I have long since graduated from WCU, I always make it a point to come back tp BL!

2 street tacos for $6. Great carne asada! The tacos were overstuffed with meat and pico! Great fun atmosphere, family-friendly, many menu choices to choose from! It was fast service and perfect lunch size! The carne tacos with pico were mild (which I appreciated), and delicious! Highly recommended!

I checked in at 4:30pm and immediately left to go to my appointments.  We got back to the hotel at 8pm and our toilet was clogged and the sinks both had running water.  I pull down the sheets and there was not only numerous hairs and pubic hair but there is metal shards in the sheets.  The office is under construction and my shoes were destroyed by walking through the office.  The woman was rude when my husband called for a plunger she said he has to come down and get one cause she's the only one here.  Then I called again with my complaints and she says "well I can move you to another room" I explained that I wanted a refund and to get my kids to bed in a clean hotel and she said "you checked in at 4, you aren't getting a refund". I am now working with Expedia to get my refund.  Holes in the doors,  molding coming off the walls,  hair and metal construction shards in my HARD bed with stains on the sheets.  I'm disgusted and just want to get out of here.  They say there is a breakfast that I paid for with my room and if there is I'll be shocked considering the office that it is supposed to be in is under dust and piles of concrete!

I was really surprised at how reasonably priced everything was here. I thought, since the location and whatnot, that'd it be expensive, but I think my waffe cone + topping was only about 3 bucks. 
So I get that their whole deal is their toppings, which I thought a bit confusing. They take whatever topping you order and blend it into complete oblivion in the ice cream, so it's next to impossible to be able to tell what you actually ordered. There's no chunks or bites of said topping, but sort of this soft-ice-creamy, beige, sorta flavorless...thing. I suppose you could ask if they don't mind blending it so much, but I never thought to ask.
Good as it gets!  From staff to first cocktail to main coarse to desert all so very fine!!!  Been round a bit and this is just tops!

17 minutes in the drive thru for mediocre food, and it wasn't even busy!  Not anymore! We didn't even get a sorry for the wait, or thank you. 
If the food was good I would have said it was worth the wait.  I don't think I'll be back.
They made me wait 2 weeks then cancelled on me..I will never give those dum people any business

El Vez gets 4 stars for the GUAC - not for the rest of the menu - which would get 3.

The guacamole hear really is tasty - although if you've tried the guac at Xochitl - which is made tableside with cotilja cheese fresh-ground to taste - WOWZA - but that's another review (check out Xochitl and Tequila's in Philly - regional Mex fare.)

Anyhow, back to El Vez which is filled-to-the-brim with Starr-kitsch.  Kitschy or not, this is a good place to get an over-priced fruity drink and schmooze with the yupsters.

I better go again before I turn 40 and get permanently banned from the joint!

I always go out of my way to get to a Popeye's as there is really not a lot of them around thought they are growing.  Anyway sat in drive through for 20 minutes with 3 cars in front of me. Get to window and they can only accept cash which happened after my order, fine. I did not have any cash. So if it were my business I would have handed me a free meal, soda or a coupon for future free sandwich or something.  They gave me nothing for my trouble. I will not go to this location anymore.

This place is great and has evolved so much over the years. I came in today for lunch around 11:30, there was no wait and I was seated quickly. I requested a seat by an outlet to charge my computer and work, which they did not have. Although the hostess offered to charge at the hostess stand if I needed which was great! I ordered the Raw Root Vegetable Carpaccio Salas and The Special Brown Butter Carnaroli Risotto with Robiolo and Black Truffles. This was more than enough food for lunch, I had left overs. The salad was great, crunchy and tangy but rich and flavorful. They're we're many elements to it which I like! The risotto was wonderful, not over cooked. It melted in my mouth and you could smell the truffles immediately when it was brought to the table. It was on the pricey side. I'm no truffle expert but I'm sure they're expensive. I would definitely order the salad again anytime but the risotto was a splurge (a tasty one )

I would give this place more stars if it weren't for the high strung dick at the cash register (guy with spiral ear plugs). He semi taps on the counter while you are ordering to implie you are taking too long and then cuts you off before you are finished completing your sentence. The guy needs a Zanax or Prozac. He also scolded us when we were confused about which order was ours neglecting to tell us to take the state plate as the order indicator. Food was really good but service was seriously lacking.

Had a couple Hemingway  martinis and Asian chicken salad. One of the best martinis I've recently had and the salad was unique too. Everything organic & fresh from local farm. That is the piece that shines through and is highlighted by friendly staff. Excellent in everyway & made the way all food should be: local and fresh.

Why go here when you can go to Flix Brewhouse in Carmel, which is newer, nicer, and has faster service? Okay, to be fair, when I went to Flix, it was a slower evening and when I went to Studio Movie Grill it was a nearly packed showing of Trainwreck. I'm sure this place was state of the art ten years ago, but I wasn't impressed with the facility, service, or food. We ordered drinks ten minutes before the start of the movie, and it took at least 35 minutes to get the first round. My friend recommended the bacon mac n cheese, but it was, in a word, gross. Rubbery and oily, not much flavor. I also felt the dining element made it hard to focus on the movie; I did not have this issue at Flix Brewhouse - maybe the lighting at Flix is just better calibrated?

Reclining seats that go all the way down, great decors, good and attentive customer service - everything about this experience was great! There are free refills for the drinks, and people were respectful of those surrounding them. There is a button that you can press to request service, and during the movie, you can write down with a pen what you would like to order so that it doesn't get loud during the movie. The tickets are surprisingly around the same price as other theaters around Tucson. As for the food, they had an extensive menu.

Mark Twain's is a mostly very good pizza joint with a constant crowd that only becomes problematic when little league baseball is in season. Seems to be the place for team celebrating. 
One thing that's good but very annoying, is they make a wonderful roast beef po-boy but not after 5 and on weekends! Grrrr!
All the usual pizzas are good but they have one with shrimp, andouille sausages, white Alfredo sauce and crabmeat that is sublime. 
And they are very Gluten Free friendly. They bring you the ingredients and you build your own GF pizza to avoid cross contamination. 
All told, nice hang out vibe with a life size paper mache Mark Twain in the front window. Heck, its worth it to see that. 
And if you went to UNO, let Jacques the owner know. He's a very loyal alum.

This place is absolutely yummy!!!!  Trust me, I am a Mexi expert!

Salsa - yum!
Fajita - yum yum!
Cheese sauce - yummy...

Highly recommend

Best placE to host a birthday party!  The kids had the best time ever. Heard so many of them say this is the best birthday party I've ever been to. Staff was amazing, well organized, and super helpful.  Great establishment!  Everything in the arcade worked great and the laser tag area was huge, clean and tons of fun. We will be back.
Excellent experience and delicious food that you don't have to leave the table feeling guilty about ! Staff was excellent, we will definitely be back

Called to have them do an exterior maintenance spray and trap a packrat that is nesting in our backyard. For the packrat, mouse traps (the cheapest ones at Home Depot)  were set up. The mousetraps did not catch anything and the guy came back and picked them up but did not call after or let us know what other options there were. At the end of the day, spent $175 on an external spray and mouse traps and still have the initial problem we called about. Disappointed in their customer service and would not recommend. There are cheaper options that provide better service.

Love love love this place! They have a small but delicious breakfast menu. Their breakfast burritos are fresh and not overly greasy. One of my biggest complaints with breakfast places is over cooked eggs in breakfast burritos, but this place has perfectly cooked eggs. They also make great coffee- the hortchata is my favorite. Looking forward to going back next weekend and trying their tea. Their staff is also exceptionally friendly and welcoming. You can tell they really take pride in their business.

Alright...now we're talking trendy.  Uber-trendy, hip-to-the-core, pulsating late night bar scene.  If none of the preceding sounds intriguing to you, stop reading right now.  Bar Twenty 3 is the post-modern-looking, high-ceilinged bar in the Gulch nestled next to City Hall.

To be absolutely honest with you, I can't remember too much about drink prices.  From what I can recall, there are occasional two-for-ones and other typical specials.  All I can truly tell you about my three trips to Bar Twenty 3 is that the place is seems to perpetually be packed with wall-to-wall crowds of grinding hipsters--and these aren't your typical brand of hipsters.  These are the type of people that are fueled by Cosmopolitans and may or may not ever sleep at night.

The lighting seems to be exceptionally low, leading me to wonder exactly what the place looks like in daylight...not sure I want to know.  The music is typically pounding dance remixes at an unnecessarily dangerous volume.  Once I was fortunate enough to glimpse the upstairs VIP, but it seemed to be identical to the downstairs only with a small amount of seating.

If you're out on the town, perhaps celebrating a birthday or some other special occasion, Bar Twenty 3 is a perfectly reasonable stop in a long stream of barhopping exploits.  But if you're not into having to shout your drink order at the top of your lungs and promptly spilling it when you're slammed into by a gyrating shiny man in a silk shirt, I don't recommend it.
Ordered from the truck today. The finely diced carne asada was very chewy but the other ingredients seemed fresh and spices/flavors were good.

Bro this place is not it. It costed $45 for chicken and broccoli, general tsos and vegetable lomein and guess what. 2/3 of those entrees were TERRIBLE. I'm sorry but  like really bad. The general tsos chicken was soft and when cut open it was just white fluffy substance and not really chicken at all. The chicken and broccoli was extremely rubbery resembling raw chicken. It was far too expensive for the food we received. We called to complain but since it was through door dash they said there was nothing they could do. A very big disappointment :(

I see that all the reviews here are from quite a while ago. So I'll give a recent one. These people that work here are pretty much jerkoffs. They are always rude, miserable and really don't care. It shows you how much they really care in another way by keeping their store filthy.

The ice cream bar was awesome but the food was not good at all.  The steam coming from the buffet warmers stunk really bad.  You can tell the owners are trying really hard to make it a nice looking restaurant but the food was really bad.  Sorry
Great caring educated friendly helpful staff! We love it here!!

Well, I miss Frankie's a bit...  I wish they had kept the hot dogs!

This place was nicely renovated and is pretty cool inside, although a little small.

Menu has a lot of variety and great stuff for lunch.  I had the Tampa Cheesesteak, which is basically a philly cheesesteak on Cuban bread with mustard.  Pretty good.  The french fries were generic seasoned fries... I'll try a different side next time--they had a wide variety.

Service was good and the place was busy (on a Friday at noon), but not so much that you couldn't get a table.  I will be back.

Meh. There's reasons this chain isn't popular among millennials and is shutting down many locations.

Looks at my pictures: clearly I ordered the burger "pink" and they served it "not pink." My grandmother has been without power for 6 days thanks to Hurricane Irma, and her first hot meal she gets is that. Also, they didn't get a freaking side perfectly correct for the family. No cheese on that burger either or butter for the baked potato. The online ordering seemed so easy. Guess it doesn't translate well in the kitchen.

Took about 10 extra minutes to get the food when we pulled up. Why quote 15 minutes when it won't be ready in 15?

Full (Haha) racks of ribs that fit in the palm of my hand. Joke.  There's a place in the same Plaza with racks for $7 cheaper on Monday and far bigger. No excuse.

The food at least tasted good. So 1 star added to a bad experience.

Zwahlen's is hands down the best place to get ice cream in Montgomery County. If you have not been here and you live or work in Montco, you need to stop what you're doing and go. Great oasis to cool down during the summer months!

Atmosphere - Looks like a Swiss skiing lodge from the outside. Inside, hand painted family tree and wood decor is a pleasure to be around. Glass stands with all available takeaways (bosses will love fresh chocolates and seasonal items for a treat). The place smells amazing, with lots of fresh things prepared daily.

Staff - Staff at Zwahlen's is great. Every one knows how to explain the ice cream process (called low air induction), and is excited to help out. I've been with friends many times and they've even brought orders to the table. Owner also makes occasional appearances and really treats everyone with kindness and respect.

Food - Out of this world. Period. They only have a few flavors of ice cream, but they all are delicious. Monthly calendar available on website to let you know in advance (try their Mint Chocolate Chip and PB3). Have had gifts given of chocolate and my personal favorite, carmel apples and people can't believe how amazing they are. This is the best quality of dessert items I've ever found. Something on the menu for every taste.

Intangibles - Parking is at a premium in their lot, but being near a small strip mall, plenty of other parking. If lines are long, it's worth the wait, be patient. Can get quite busy on weekends or during the summer.

Overall - The finest ice cream place in the suburbs hands down. I appreciate their quality, coziness, and overall great experience. If you have not been there, make it a point next time you're in the area.
At the end of the day, this is just another Walgreens. But the employees were really friendly and helpful - which makes all the difference at a chain spot like this!

Where do I find the app with 7 stars??? We came on a Thursday night.  Our waitress was named Brittany, I think. She was friendly and charming.  We both had the short ribs and shrimp and it was delectable!! We will definitely be back and hope to have the same server.

Took a month for them to tell me I'm getting a $79 reimbursement for 400 dollars of work. Their deductible and copay fees are ridiculous. Go to a better place. Glad I didn't put my dog on this program.
Had dinner at Padavan's NY. Service was great.  Atmosphere was exciting and the pizza was delicious! I can't wait to go back.
My girlfriend and I were on our way to watch March Madness at Banker's Life and we stopped to get a drink at Coaches. The bar had great tvs all over the bar and strong drinks. Perfect sports bar!

Came in today to try their gluten free menu. I am gluten sensitive so most places I eat at lands me on the couch with a stomach cramp. 

Not today!

I ordered the biscuits and gravy with scrambled eggs. My fiance got the grass fed burger and fries. First off, those were hands down the most flavorful French fries I've ever had. I have no idea how they were cooked but they were amazing! The burger was also super flavorful. Tasted char broiled. The gravy was buttery and smooth, no lumps. 

I don't know about you but when I've tried to make stuff with gluten free flour I always get bumps. So kudos! I also ordered a side of home fries. The biscuits and eggs weren't enough on their own for me. But I like food. Lol.

We also ordered from their little pastry shelf on the side. I got a turtle cupcake, and they have huge "Twinkies". All in all was delicious and it's only 6 - 7 dollars more than a regular place. I drove all the way from Kolb rd to eat here and I wasn't disappointed. I just wish they had a shop on the east side!

Their iced tea was amazing. I know I'm weird but I judge places on their ice tea. Usually if it's good tea the food is good too. Tea is so easy to neglect and let it go stale. If they have good fresh drinks I know the rest will follow. 

And their water is filtered and enhanced with lemon! Thank you! Thank you for filtering your water and not making me drink nasty tap. Everything was great. I will be back!

We walked up & ordered our food. We stood outside for 15 minutes, watching cars drive up order their food & drive off. We were told waiting on the meat to come off the grill. Then the order was wrong. Ordered no cheese, cheese on the burger. They did remake the burger. Drinks weren't even given to us. Window service is very slow. Unfortunately this is not the first time this has happened to us. Tried to give them the benefit of the doubt before but I guess it's the way this Checker's handles their customers.

A vegan steps into a BBQ and actually finds something to eat.  And gives it 5 starts.  What's the deal?  

It's a veggie miracle.  That's what.  Not really, what I ate wasn't that exciting but my husband loooooves BBQ and I am an awesome wife so I have to go with him pretty frequently.  Normally I eat french fries and a salad.  But Pit Boss was something to actually review.  

Of course I ordered their house salad (doubled without cheese) and a side order of fries.  Same ol' same ol'.  Booooooooring.  But the salad was big with lots of fresh fresh fresh and crunchy veggies.  No brown lettuce at all, this was piled high with carrots, green and red peppers, red tomatoes, cabbage, it was just good.  House salads are a mixed bag at barbeque places.  They aren't exactly famous for thier greens.  Unless said greens are cooked in bacon.  And I wasn't having any of that.  

Next we have the french fries.  On their own, nothing to say here.  But with their sauce?  Oh. My. Lord.  Can I get Pit Boss Barbeque Sauce in a to go cup with a straw please?  I could put this on anything.  Who am I kidding.  I did.  In my salad, on my fries and on my okra.  It was so good.  Best sauce I've ever had.  I didn't check if it was vegan.  I didn't even want to know the bad news if it was.  

We came on a Friday night that was busy.  We were told a 30 minute wait and were called in by the 10 minute mark.  They didn't even care that our entire party wasn't there.  The waitress was great, really nice and the food came out quick.  The night was flawless.

Well I heard a lot about this place. I could not wait to try it. So we went and I was bummed. The brisket, pulled pork and chicken we really dry.  Ribs had no flavor but they were better then the other 3. I felt the service could have been better. Our waitress acting like we were putting her out. So I do not think I would go back.

Hennessy margarita was EVERYTHING I needed it to be. I just read a review that mentioned bad customer service; I don't know who was there at that time, but I visited this establishment today and had absolutely no issues! I even chose to return a few hours later for a second round! My drinks were amazing and I enjoyed the atmosphere. Every employee wore a mask and they were quick to turn around anyone trying to enter the building without a mask, which made me feel very safe as a customer. I will definitely be back!

This is the 3rd time within the last 6 months that I have experienced rude staff at this cafe.  I had met the store owner, Robert Szasz back when it first opened and was just Tropical Smoothie, not Nathan's, not the sandwich place, not the salad place, just Tropical Smoothie.  He is a very nice man.  However, I apparently am not the only one who is complaining about the rude staff.  When I got back to the office and mentioned to co-worker how rude the staff was, she reminded me that she too has had that same experience.  There are too many other choices of healthy restaurants in downtown Tampa to put up with this rudeness.  Therefore, that was my last trip to Tropical Smoothie.  I will get my salad and healthy food options from Nature's Table and The Secret Garden.
My oil was running low, so I made a impromptu stop at Take 5. The entire staff was knowledgeable and friendly. Brandon was extremely helpful and made my experience quick and easy!

Came in, and was told 15 minutes till fresh & hot. Worth the wait. It's been over a year since I've had a doughnut and man a fresh hot one was a perfect reintroduction! My roommates and I got an assorted dozen and added another plain for 1$ (happy birthday Krispy Kreme!) 
I am not a huge fan of the birthday one, but always my favorite raspberry or blueberry filled glazed! 
The frozen lemonade and raspberry was way too syrupy. All in all this was a good experience!

It is NEAR the beach, not on the beach. Mediocre food. 

Come for the charm, friendly staff, and nice sunny garden setting. Bring your dog.

The allure and elegance of this place is undeniable. Is it the best dinner I've ever had? No. But is it one that I will remember for a long time to come? Yes. Jacket required for guys, so definitely not a place I would take lightly. The food was on par with what you would expect from a joint of this type. The service was good. The bread pudding that takes 30 minutes to make was easily the biggest let down of the night though. Don't waste your time with that. Otherwise, you can't go wrong here.

People are so critical...dinner was great...the view was awesome...the service was on point....food was delicious.  Price was reasonable...wife and i had drinks, shared entree, appetizer, dessert, and coffee for around $60. Need more vegetarian options but would definitely recommend.

Friday September 13th, 2019. As we arrived as a party of four, early for our reservation we sat at the bar. We looked over the drink menu and ordered some professionally made cocktails. We then were seated we ordered drinks and food. The atmosphere is more dress than casual. It was comfortable for out of towners and the male server was amazing, he knew his stuff.

We proceeded to order and the bread and the New Orleans BBQ shrimp and the stuffed clams were amazing, we then our food was served, pork chop and fish, the meal and service was amazing and attentive.  We then ordered soufflés for the table and they were perfect. It is pricey but worth it, I expected to spend more so was happy.

Visiting the area and stopped here for lunch on our way back from the Sonoran Desert Museum. We purposefully tried several Mexican restaurants, all of which we enjoyed, but La Olla was our stand-out favorite. This is a small, comfortable space that has a local and welcoming feel. House margarita perfect, the salsa amazingly fresh, and tortillas delicious and plentiful.  

While they do have traditional, well-prepared entrees (I had the chicken enchiladas...the quality of meat was superb, and the sauce delicious but not overpowering), there were other super creative dishes not to overlook. My favorite part of the meal was the "Diablos Chicos" appetizer....shrimp, green onions and cheese stuffed in yellow peppers and wrapped in bacon, with amazing corn salsa, salsa crème, and anaheim crème. I'm not even sure how one would think to put all those flavors together, but it so worked and I could have licked the plate!  The service provided by everyone was exceptionally friendly - professional with a touch of fun/humor that made you feel like you were dining in a friend's house. 

All the entrees are served with calabacitas, in addition to beans and rice. This was the only establishment that did that, and having never had them before and will just say that thanks to La Olla I now love squash! No room for the daily homemade cheesecake so will probably need to start with that when we visit again!

These guys have changed their MO since they first opened. The new menu has price points and options for every palate. The happy hour menu is delightful with $5 house wines and many of the awesome new apps and pizzas on the menu. The regular price menu has been much improved, thanks to the new chef! There is something for everyone, while still keeping everything interesting! It changes seasonally, which all foodies can appreciate. The service is friendly and they are very accommodating in regards to special requests! Give this place a try!

My sister and I had attended this place for our eye exams and to purchase new glasses. Doctor Kim was very nice. She was very attentive, made sure that we felt comfortable, and overall it was a very pleasant experience in the Doctor's portion. When we purchased glasses we were attended by two different people, Kristi and Gina. Both whom were very nice. The only problem we had was when we were paying with Kristi there was a malfunction with their program, which resulted in me getting charged twice. I wasn't very happy, and the explanation that Kristi and the manager gave me were not very comforting. They explained how the charged would drop but it didn't, it actually went through. No one offered to do anything, and no one seemed very apologetic. Except Gina, she seemed genuinely sincere, but Kristi and the manager on duty didn't have a very apologetic attitude. Which is fine, but still.  No compensation offered, for the inconvience it caused me as a customer, no clear explanation as to why it happened and no solution. Disappointing. Make sure you watch how your transactions go through.

very nice concept, staff was friendly and attentive. buy bigger shrimp for the shrimp cocktail but they were still very good, suggest bar mats in the bathrooms and soap dispenser. we will be back, we had a VERY good time! thank u Brittany and tiva!!! made the visit even better!
Awesome Asian market! They have everything you need in a small store. Granted we weren't doing a primary shop and were just visiting town, however we were well impressed with everything on offer :)
Best hot dog I've ever had! Fun atmosphere and staff and great beer selection. Can't wait to come back and try more!

I flew down to FL to visit mom one day she suggested we try this spot called Wepaa. I am so glad we did, Rita was our waitress....she was amazing I loved her energy and her professionalism she knew her stuff. I ordered the shrimp mofongo (if heaven had a flavor this would be it) it was delicious, we also had pasteles, alcapurrias, flan de coco, chuletas, arroz con habichuelas and took some to go orders home for the family. We also bought some faux license plates with the names of the towns both parents are from. If you're  in the neighborhood or flying in from somewhere you should make it a point to visit this spot. It was as close to Puerto Rico as I could get. The chef absolutely gets the credit for these delicious dishes. Thank you guys I hope to visit some day soon.

I was surprised by recent reviews that said the service was poor here. My wife and I went for the "all you can eat Taco Tuesday" and it was busy.REALLY busy. but this was around 6:30 pm and they could have had a bit of a hold over from happy hour at near by office parks. apart from one hiccup-more on that later-we found the staff attentive and friendly.

to look at the place is generic chain eatery with some bright colors to look 'ethnic' but nothing too over the top, at least not in the bar. The dining room was a little more faux ethnic-I'll stick to the bar.

We ordered drinks and there was a bit of a delay but seeing the staff running around I put it down to being busy. the waiter had been careful to take time with the details of our order and there were no issues there.

There is a regular menu which looked quite good but the 'all you can eat' is very specific beef or chicken, hard corn or soft flour  wraps and you can change them up. You start with one type and when ready the next you can switch around if you like.

They were hot, tasty but nothing special. Like the stuff I'd do at home on a taco night. The one hiccup with service was at one point there was a delay with getting another set out. When the waiter asked if we'd gotten the second load yet and I said 'no' he hurried off and saw it was fixed at once.

Overall my wife and I had a good time. Next time we'll go a little later on so the happy hour can clear out and we'll try the regular menu items but there WILL be a next time.

I'm sad we didn't get the experience we needed to give this place more stars. It was dead here when we went on a Friday night. It has potential I'm sure and if you're in NOLA visiting it is off the beaten path (aka not Bourbon or Frenchman)

Ordered blackened fish tacos (Mahi). Inedible due to level of seasoned salt on the crust plus spicy aoli. Sloppily plated as well. Could not eat it after taste testing fish on the three tacos offered. Chef needs to rethink this $15 dish.

My daughter and I have visited this location in the past and everything was pleasant. So she chose this spot for her birthday lunch this past Thursday and we were thoroughly disappointed. We ordered gyozas, spring rolls, and 2 rolls. They get 2 stars because the service was good. Greeted and seated promptly but the food was subpar. The gyozas were the best part of the meal. The spring rolls were nice and crispy on the outside but a soggy mess on the inside. The ingredients in my Fire House roll were not fresh. It tasted like everything had been sitting in the fridge too long and the shrimp tempura was rubbery. The spicy Tuna on top was not appetizing looking at all. I only ate a portion of my roll and refused to try anything else. My daughter had the Lion roll and it was definitely meh. Too big, too much rice , too much cream cheese, and the yum yum sauce looked weird. It almost looked like nacho cheese sauce.
I will not be going back.
I love coming to this place when I'm in the area! The salon is very clean and the wait time is minimal. Amy the owner is awesome and very sweet.
Three of us came for lunch and got tacos tacos and empanadas. They were absolutely spectacular. You have to try the hot fried chicken taco! It's really tiny inside but cozy and friendly. We loved it!

Just place an order of enchiladas Mole, chicken was dry and luke warm.    Second order I placed, will not be back.   The first order was good that's why I placed a second order.  We live on Vine Street not far from the seven elven so we were there within minutes of placing the order.

Our 3rd cheesesteak this week at 3 different places. Shanks still rules !! Most beef in the sandwich and about par in pricing (although $15 for a sandwich with fries and drink is still outrageous in my book !) Definitely not a daily item for my budget, but we're "on vacation", so we gotta eat somewhere and take advantage of the local specialties. If I were a local, once a month is plenty. Not the healthiest, but yum for sure.

A great 2-3 hr tour.  Very informative.  Knew nothing about his life, yrs leading up to Presidency and ownership of 150 slaves.  Guided tour in mansion, self -guided with headset rest of tour.   Highly recommended.

The three stars is because the have a half decent beer list. The food is worth two. I ate it, but definitely wouldn't place the same order again. 

I got the Buffalo chicken tenders with tots. The tots were freezer burned. The tenders were fine. It is a large portion for six bucks, but I don't think worth it. 

I'd say if you go, be sure you just plan to drink.

A trip to St Louis would not be complete without a stop to Ted Drewe's Frozen custard in the historic Dogtown neighborhood. This St Louis icon is perched right next to the famed route 66 Highway. Its façade is classically retro, of the same caliber we often associate with structures that aligned this national highway. Everything about this place is nostalgic, sweet, and distinctively American. The Frozen Custard is simply perfect! The taste is so rich, pure, and creamy. With a large variety of flavors to choose from you can't go wrong. The portions are very generous. The large size is more than enough, you'll see when you get there, but nonetheless, if you are new to St Louis, or visiting, please make this a stop on your tour. We made this a destination after visiting the Botanical Gardens and it definitely hit the spot. If you love ice cream, you GOTTA COME HERE!!

This is not your typical pizza joint in a strip mall.  The menu is sophisticated and authentic, and the food is actually quite good.  That said, the service here is TERRIBLE, and they don't seem incredibly customer friendly.  Unfortunately, the servers all appear to be high school kids - maybe they're family or friends of the owners - but they really seem to have no serving experience and treat work like their own after school social hour.  Our server had us open our own wine (BYOB), got part of our order wrong, tried to take half-eaten dishes away from us and had so many other little service blunders with us and other tables that it almost became hard to watch.  Despite all this, the pizza was impressive, with chewy, homemade dough and fresh, well-composed topping combinations.  I was pleasantly surprised by my salad as well, which, though a little over-dressed, was very nice and generously portioned with orzo, arugula and creamy burrata.

In spite of the service, we decided to sign up for their mailing list and were excited to receive a coupon for our next visit.  However, when we went to use our coupon, our teenage server informed us that the coupon had been sent out by their marketing company by mistake and that they were not honoring it.  You don't have to have had years of experience in the restaurant industry to know that this is NOT good business.  In a situation like this, a restaurant should eat the loss or take action against the marketing group they hired to recoup the loss, rather than losing business and alienating the exact group of clientele who liked your restaurant enough to want to sign up for your mailing list and become repeat customers.  We still haven't been back - Philadelphia has too many other equally good options to go somewhere with sub-par service and without customer-minded policies.

Great authentic Italian food here in a classy setting with lots of tasty dishes to chose from- I ordered the Pappardelle All Boscaiola which includes my favorite ingredient: Pancetta. The wine list contained a nice variety of Chianti and Brunello di Mantalcino  Definitely call ahead for a table - especially on the weekend. The service here wasn't bad but wasn't as great as we expected it to be given the type of restaurant this is.

We almost gave this place a pass - not a lot of upfront charm. But the Yelp reviews were good so we gave it a try. Glad we did! Friendly service as soon as we walked in. Basic breakfast was hot and delicious. I had scrambled eggs (fresh and yummy) with crispy hash browns and sourdough toast. My husband had the veggie scramble (zucchini, red peppers, onion, mushrooms) all fresh and delicious, with the same sides I had. Good cup of coffee, also! We appreciate a hard-working local restaurant and will definitely come back. Four stars just because the ambiance isn't really there. Don't let that stop you from giving this place a try!

I have eaten here twice this week.  The menu rotates by the season and some items rotate daily.  On Tuesday my wife met me for lunch.  We sat outside along the Monon on a beautiful November day.  I ordered the chicken mole' and holy smokes...... it was delicious!  Today I stopped in and grabbed some lunch togo.  Chicken fingers that I dipped into Local Folks Foods BBQ sauce.  I love this place.  The people are nice and the food is OUTSTANDING!
Awesome place to check out late night First time coming to this location, it was amazing!! Quick service, delicious food, awesome Saki, great times. Ask for

The best burgers around! The customer service is great, everyone is smiling! Five Guys doesn't stand a chance compared to the quality of BurgerMonger. The owner often stops at everyone's table to make sure your order is correct, which I really appreciate!

So I was excited to eat here because I had read so many great reviews and saw so many scrumptious photos. Unfortunately, my experience wasn't so great. 

The location is cutesy. I appreciated the design, the decor and look of the place. But that was the end of my appreciation honestly. 

Our waitress, who was super cutesy in her red lip and retro look, was not the greatest. She either was having a crappy day, or she doesn't too much care for her job, so it seemed. Anyway, I ordered a breakfast sandwich and to my dismay the toast was, dare I say....under-toasted. Which would have been fine save for the wetness of the sandwich causing it to turn into a gooey mess. And not that good kind of yolk leaking yummy goo, but the soggy bread mush goo. 

As you can imagine I asked for better toast. And honestly our waiter just seemed bothered. And in an effort not to bother her any longer, my beau and I just dealt with what we had on the table and she didn't much check back on us.  

Sucks to have a first experience like that. I'm sure we will be back to St. Louis, but I doubt we will beAck here.

I was going to give this place 4 stars but then I remembered the guy who looks IDENTICAL to Bradley Cooper works there so I bumped it to 5.  Seriously though, I dig Star Bagel.  Their avocado and vine ripe tomato on honey wheat is PHENOM.  Yo Bradley Cooper, does that give away who I am yet?  Anywho, Star Bagel is full of friendly employees and good food.  Be leary of the Sunday morning Mama crowd who will squeep all of the outdoor seating with their massive strollers.  Oh and parking is awful but that's Sylvan in general. 

(Total sidenote, I do wish the lady at the register would change her gloves more often since they typically have holes in them and she handles food AND cash). EEK!

NO CUSTOMER WIFI!!! 
And that's why I'm giving them 1 star. I get there order my food, find a great working spot and bam!! No WiFi. And the cellphone signal is bad in that area so I couldn't use my phone to tether in. I asked an employee and he gave me the deer in headlights look and I just packed up and left.

Mmmmm....
I called Larry after I had an emergency zipper issue with my jeans. I was extremely pressed for time as I was traveling and leaving town in short order. 

Larry replaced the zipper, did a great job, and charged a very fair price.

His customer service skills are unusual, but what do I care? He did a fantastic job!!!!

When I arrived I was immediately greeted by a somewhat disheveled host not in appearance but ability. I don't understand why as there was only one person in line in front of me. It seem to be a problem with either his ability to handle more than one thing at a time or the iPad had connectivity issues. Because I was a party of one I was seated in the area that seems more like the bistro breakfast area instead the dining room. Not too happy with that but let's roll with it.

Initially I thought only the host seemed to be out of order or sorts however, the discombobulation continued to be the order of service throughout my encounter with the Walnut St., Café.  With several servers attending a maximum of 15 people inside and 6 outside The service did not appear to be fluid nor the workers in sync with one another. Although several people may have filled your glass, delivered your food or your check it was a quite spaced out before you received any of the services mentioned. For example my server acknowledged me ending my meal and asking for a box for my food at 10:05 I didn't receive my check until 10:25, this was after flagging down another worker.

I ordered the Pork Belly sandwich it was served on a brioche bun with Cole slaw that had way too much mayo or salad dressing. I will guess mayonnaise as it had no flavor. It was all 'cole' and no slaw! The sandwich as a whole lacked flavor or texture. How does pork Belly lack taste? Not one hint of salt? Although I am not a person who enjoys the taste of salt you do expect that in pork products.  The fries however were hot and tasty. I was impressed they served complementary sparkling water this is highly unusual but necessary as I was unimpressed with my meal and the service! The slow service deterred me from ordering more items from the bakery or a glass wine. I had to feed the parking meter, this was not a dining experience worthy of a ticket. The soft jazz and R&B music that played in the background set a nice mood, it wasn't too loud or offensive in any way. As I was leaving I learned they have live jazz every Sunday 12-3. They have live jazz and a bar, I may try it again?!

OMG, Hero!!!!  Duncan Turner for President!!!   So I had my third baby and well, things didn't quite return to normal in a certain area of my body.
Running, playing soccer, working out, my god even sneezing was followed by a quick dash to the bathroom to remove soaking underwear and reapply.  This is ridiculous I thought.  Along comes Duncan Turner, and what do you know,100% fixed lickety split.  It was my 36th birthday present to, well, me.
And it was the best birthday present I could have given myself.  Don't suffer in wet undies any longer ladies, get into Dr Turners office NOW!

In all seriousness though, my full faith is with Dr. Turner and his staff. I have been seeing Dr. Turner for roughly twenty years for various things,  however, my three child births AND this pee surgery,  have been overwhelmingly by far most impactual! Of course my children are #1. #2. #3 of utmost important,  everyone understands that, but not everyone understands what it's like to have to leave work to change their clothes in the middle of the day, or stand furthest in the back of a workout classs, so no one sees your wet pants, or play soccer only if you can remember to pack your pee pad, and the list goes on and on. My quality of life was slowly diminishing, and I couldn't stand living like that, so I did it, I saved my birthday weekend so I could enjoy a little recovery time in the solitude of my bed for a couple days. Then that was that, I had immediate results and haven't looked back, except to truly appreciate what Dr. Turner has done to make me whole again!
Bull Wash is out of business. The sign out front reports that Express Car Wash is coming soon.

When moving to Nashville, I had difficulty finding an apartment that I really loved. After searching for a few apartments I really felt like I was not going to find anything that met my needs. Moving is hard enough, but not having a home that you really love can prove to add more stress onto a transition. I went to 865 Bellevue and met with Alex who has very helpful, but felt that I would love Hillmeade more. After viewing Hillmeade, I fell in love! I adored the wood floors, high ceilings, screened-in patio and fireplace! The community can't be beat with the hillside and pond views! When I went to apply, I was so disappointed because someone else had applied for the apartment. Long story short, this application fell through and Michelle and Tanya worked very diligently to ensure that I found an apartment that worked and I got the original apartment viewed! I was impressed at how quick the move in process was and the overall community management.

Not bad in a pinch.

My kids and I stopped in this evening to have a bite. The kids ordered slices, and I got a calzone. We also ordered cheese fries (whiz on side). 

The fries were nice and crispy, however under seasoned. The cheezewhiz was not actual whiz, but the Sysco knockoff processed "cheese food". No matter, lots of places have it. I was just bummed that I was told whiz by the guy taking our order. 

The kids enjoyed their pizza. But let's be honest, my kids would eat the worst pizza imaginable off your floor if you let them. That brings me to the calzone. It was decent. It was certainly not a life changing culinary experience, but it was not terribly expensive and it did the job. Serviceable. 

The staff was super friendly. My one complaint is that all plates and utensils in the restaurant are disposable. That's perfectly OK when you're buying pizza by the slice. But if you've ever tried eating a calzone with plastic utensils on a Styrofoam plate... not so much. Honestly, I would've paid more for my calzone if I could've gotten a proper fork knife and plate to eat from. (if you're listening management, this is the difference between a two and three star review. You should heed my advice here).

So to wrap up, this place isn't perfect but if you need to fill up your kids after swimming lessons at UMLY, it does the job.

Finally went here before a show at the TLA.  We both enjoyed our samiches (which were served VERY quickly - especially for a Saturday night) and the drinks were tasty too. Nothing mind blowing here, for sure, but a solid spot. Been there forever, it seems, I hope it continues to be a pilar of South Street.

Great atmosphere and nice service staff. Sushi is good...lots of rice in my opinion, but a nice variety of flavors. Our waitress last night was very forgetful and tried telling us we received rolls that we never did, said she would refill drinks, and wasn't very attentive. Restaurant was maybe a ¼ full at the time with a full sushi line. 
Oh, if you're friends with any of the chefs, you'll get your rolls a lot faster too. One table guest said "what's up" to all the chefs...next thing you see...rolls going out left and right to that table while the rest of us waited for our half orders. 
We will return...but it might be a while before we do.
no no no no no no no no no no no no no NO NO NO. NO. NO. NO. no not good !!!!!! go to chick fil a !!!!
This place is amazing- one of the best Italian restaurants in town! Order one of the home made pasta dishes- delicious!!

There's a few places in town that I have been a regular at when I decide to get my nails done, hoarder way back in January of this year I saw the quality began to slip. 
Now,  after a nine month hiatus I decided to give this place a try. I was not disappointed. All the Covid related measures are in place: sanitizing, social distancing, wearing facemasks. Most importantly my nails look great and I felt that the person doing my nails payed attention to my concerns and didn't just blow me off. I got a Gel manicure and a spa pedicure.
All in all I'm especially happy to have found a solid nail salon In nashville!

Oh my God DO NOT come here! They should be called "Worst" Nails instead. Went here instead of my usual place which is 5 star nails in fishers because it was getting late and I was still in the mall with my son. I got a mani/pedi. I was told after my mani which by the way the tech painted my nails clear instead of the pink I asked for. I would have told him except I thought the clear was just some sort of bottom coat (He did apologize but didn't have time to fix) Once we were done he said put your shoes on, so I asked him was he sure and he says yes. I get home and polish was smeared every where so I had to change up my whole outfit and wasn't able to wear the toe out shoes I had planned to wear that evening. I went back on Sunday but they were closing at 5 instead of the same time the mall closed so I went back today (Monday) and was treated so disrespectfully as if it was my fault my nails were damaged so instead of fixing the whole thing and starting from scratch on my pedi she asked me to go find my color...keep in mind it's about 10 different polishes that look the same inside the bottle. After she put it on and with an attitude no less, after drying I realized the color was a little off plus to add insult to injury she left paint on my skin because she had a problem fixing it in the first place. I will NEVER go here again. It's looks like I painted my toenails myself, which I'm not very good at hence why I pay. Also I was skipped over several times for people spending more money then me. Spend your money elsewhere at any other nail salon in the area unless you are just ratchet and don't mind getting a lesser service for your money. Also check out their reviews on Yelp and google. It's all bad.

O-M-G. The pizza here was Ahhhh-mazing. We ordered the Big Bad Wolf Chicago style with pepperoni, ham, sausage, and bacon. Everything was perfect about it, although I have never had Chicago deep dish before, so I do not have any point of comparison. Nonetheless, this was delicious beyond words.

We also had the savory fried ravioli. I love fried ravioli and this is one of the best I have had. It wasn't too oily and even though we ordered take out, they were still crispy and tasty when we got it back to the hotel. We also ordered both the bread sticks and the spicy hot sticks. Both were good. I especially liked the spicy hot sticks; they had the perfect amount of spice to it, and it wasn't so mild that you couldn't feel the heat.

We ordered to go and ended up waiting for over an hour, even though they quoted us at 30 to 40 minutes. However, all is forgiven because it was extremely busy and once we got the food, it was so, so good. I would probably recommend just calling in the order if you know you will be eating here because it smells so amazing in the restaurant that it's such a tease!
I have to say they have some of the best lasagna I've ever had. The garlic bread was also very good. They let us sit at the bar, since we did not have reservations.

Where I come from in Wisconsin, there are no places close by that sell french macarons, so when my family and I came to New Orleans we decided to check this place out. I ordered the 15pc set for around $30, which from other reviews, seems like the price went up? They were a nice size and tasted good. If I were to compare them to other macarons I've tried though, they are not the best. still pretty good though! 

The place is very cute inside and seems to have friendly staff working there. They guy my mom and I were ordering from though seemed annoyed and wasn't very nice, but we just dealt with it. 

We didn't order anything else from there, but they do have other tasty looking treats available!

It took me too long but finally, I learn the hype is earned!  I had a late dinner after a food-focused movie at the Roxy and the location made my first trip to Vernick inescapable.  Ive been meaning to get here for over a year and to say that this city is full of competition is no exaggeration.  

The bar on the first floor is really nice, the drinks menu interesting and the staff very attentive and professional.  Later when we were seated upstairs in the dining room, we learned that that is a constant throughout the restaurant.  On the nice night, the 2nd floor windows folded in and opened the entire wall looking out onto the street.  It was such a smart idea when designing the room.

The food was all perfect.  My friend and I shared over 6 dishes from toasts (oh, get the toasts!) which were fire grilled and topped with greatness) to the medium sized dishes.  The short list of large dishes didn't really appeal to us as much, and we were more than content by the time we finished dessert.  We shared everything and kept finding our opinion of "that's my favorite so far" changing as each dish arrived at our table.  Of the parade of flavors, the Lamb Confit on summer-ripe Peaches was my favorite.  
OK perhaps my favorite part was that we experienced perfect service and food and it came out to be surprisingly affordable... like way more affordable than restaurants in its league!
