I usually don't glow so much after only one meal at a restaurant but Im pretty sure all my food-focused friends were dead-on, as they gushed over this place since it opened.  

Oh, it's hard to get into at times... maybe i should just tell you how awful it is. Yes, it was awful. (convincing tone).
Large selection of liquors, probably the biggest within walking distance of my place. Prices are reasonable and good organization too. Pretty good selection of stuff.

I've had a very difficult time finding a beverage I enjoy these days at Goleta Coffee. The cap's are just milky late's and the teas selection is dismal. Even my hot chocolate was undrinkable and the prices are HIGH!   I even question the shakers of spices and coco as being filled with product of lessor quality. Seems to be another case of raise the prices but lower the quality.  If you're going to raise prices, fine, but please also raise the quality and don't make everything so sweet! You can add sugar to drinks but you can't take it out. 

I like the ambiance and nice seating although the music volume could be turned down; it's sometimes too loud to hold a conversation and there are no quiet corners. 

I used to come here far more often than I do now.  I'd like to see this shop improve again. Haven't tried their sandwiches and such in a very long time.

I'm hovering between a 3 1/2 and a 4 . . . Maybe a 3.75.  There's something I just don't love about the atmosphere at Adsum, it feels a bit confused to me.  That said, the fried chicken and the fries with gravy, cheese curds and  lardon made for quite the week night treat.  The menu is small, and as I split my entree and appetizer, I cannot speak to the other items, but these two were quite delicious.  The menu is also quite affordable.  Every entree was under $20.

Last night I went to green lemon as a party of 3. While the place was pretty crowded and everyone is asking 100 questions to be seated. The manager Jay ran around in and out the restaurant  addressing potential customers accommodating every single person with a smile on his face. The heavy traffic did not phase his amazing customer service and attention to detail he gave each person. Even during high traffic  hours, I felt I was given very personalized service. Will be coming back regardless the wait!

I've been to the C & B in Charleston, but last night was my first time in the Nashville location.  
I will never go back.

Firstly, the space is inconveniently laid out.   I had to go to three different stations to order what I wanted, and then go to yet a fourth station to pay.  Maybe it's possible to have some communication between the serving stations and the coffee bar, so I can order coffee from the food station, and go to the coffee station to pick it up, instead of having to wait in line again to order coffee?

But that is not the reason I will not go back.
The food I had was all very good, and it's a nice-looking, clean place.
The service, however, was the most insulting and rudest and hostile I've encountered in a long time.

Many of the items in the cold prepared food section were not labeled, so I told the man behind the counter that I was a vegetarian, and wanted to make up a plate of a few items that didn't have meat, eggs, seafood, etc.     I asked him what items were vegetarian, and he growled at me and said "just tell me what you want" in an incredibly rude and hostile manner.   I assumed, thusly, that he would steer me away from any items that weren't vegetarian.   I pointed to one item, and asked if if were vegetarian.  He said "how much you want," hostile and rudely, like I was wasting his time.   I said a spoon.  He put a spoon's worth on the plate.  I pointed to another item, and asked "how about that?" and he said, again, "just tell me what you want" in such a hostile way, I was really taken aback.  He was surly and beyond rude.  He made no effort to mask a nasty and rude temperament. Every time I tried to confirm that an item was vegetarian, he would just say "tell me how much you want." I finally said to him that I thought he was being unnecessary hostile and rude and not helpful.    

I left it at that, and went to pay.  One bite into my dish, however, I tasted shrimp.  I went back to the counter, and asked him what the salad was. He said shrimp salad.  I  asked him why he would give me that after I repeatedly told him that I was a vegetarian, and he just glared at me.    

I asked to speak to the manager, who was the other man behind the food station.   I told the manager that his server was rude to me, and the manager said "I don't think he was."
WHAT?
I was stunned.  
You know what, Mr Manager? I don't care what you think.  Your job is customer service, and to find out why the customer felt he had a terrible experience.
Did you do that?
No. 
You immediately sided with the server, without having any idea what transpired between us.  The restaurant was noisy, the server's back was to the manager, and there is no way on earth that the manager could have heard what his server said to me, or the tone in which he said it. 
None.  
Yet, he sided with the server instead of finding out what happened?!?
He also did not offer to refund the cost of the shrimp salad that I asked him to remove from my plate.

Perhaps non-vegetarians might not empathize with my anger, or think I'm overreacting, but if you are a vegetarian of long standing and mistakenly eat meat because of someone else's incompetence and hostility, I think you will understand how angry I was.

I asked for the rude server's name, but the manager would not give it to me.  When he saw how angry I was, he finally tried to be friendly and very insincerely conciliatory,  but I told him it was too late.   He had a chance to find out what the problem was, but, instead, he quickly defended his worker and told me, essentially, that I was wrong.
The manager needs to learn to listen to all sides of a story, and to show some concern for a customer's complaints before telling the customer that he is wrong.  
When he finally wanted to listen, it was too little, too late.
And the server needs to understand that it is the customers' business that allows the corporation to pay his wages, and he best be polite to ensure that they come back.
I will not ever be treated that badly and rudely.

I recognize and can overlook that people are not always in the happiest mood, and I can overlook occasional surliness, but the manager's and the server's attitudes were both beyond the pale, and I can not condone a manager who told me that I was wrong, before he even knew what his server said and the manner in which he said it.
I always thought the customer was right.  Apparently, that is not a lesson they teach at C & B.
Great homemade soups and sandwiches. A Chestnut Hill institution that is not to be missed.
Love this place! I visit every time I come home. I haven't found another store that has the amount of stuff nor quality. Can't wait for the holidays to return home and visit NAP!

This was the first time my husband and I dined at Toscano. It was a Wednesday night and we didn't make a reservation but I would definitely recommend one. The only table they had available was a small table for 2 in the bar area. We didn't mind but if you'd rather not risk it a reservation would have remedied this. Even dining in the bar the service was fantastic. Our server was attentive and answered all our questions about the menu. 

In addition to the regular menu they had daily specials. The fare definitely leans steakhouse/seafood with some modern twists. We started out with 2 cocktails (aperol  spritz and a lemon drop) and go the "bacon steak" appetizer from the regular menu. My husband LOVED this. It was very meaty (big/filling) so just be prepared. We both ordered steaks and shared a side of brussel sprouts. Everything was delicious and we left stuffed! This is a great little spot for a special occasion or date night meal.
CLOSED: Sad to say, but it was average. Very slow. But it doesn't matter. They gone.

Paganini is a paga-no-no!

Pretentious, pedestrian pasta priced for the location not the palate. 

No wonder the other Italian place down the road had a waiting list while this place was wide open! So I guess, that's *good* if you're in a hurry. 

Oh well. Once was enough. 
Our waitress was very nice.
Pizzas were very tasty. Hot and fresh. More along the size of personal pan pizza. Overall very great place for pizza. There was a little bit of a wait when we arrived but pizza was worth the wait.

I just tried Shred415 for the first time. This was actually my first-ever group workout class, so I was a bit nervous when I arrived. However, I arrived about 15 minutes early to meet the instructor. She was genuinely nice and her energy was contagious - I thought she did a wonderful job leading the class for the whole hour. 

The class is broken into four 15 minute sessions, alternating between floor (strength) work and cardio. As someone who never does strength training (I know, I need to), there were different weights and resistance bands with different intensity, which really allowed me to modify my workout while still completing everything. 

The one minor thing I would change is I wish the floor moves could be explained a little more, but I also recognize perhaps I should have started with a different class. I wouldn't say this is a class specifically good for beginners, but it's definitely doable.

I only tried this place because I got tired of eating at K.G.'s Pub next door.

As far as chinese buffets, go this one is pretty subpar.  $8.50 for dinner (which is less than most chinese buffets) and a drink.  You get what you pay for.  Your basic General Tso's chicken, fried rice, lo mein, hot & sour soup, egg drop soup, won ton soup, fried dumplings, sweet & sour chicken, soft serve ice cream, etc.

Flavor was lacking on most dishes.   Edible, but not good.

Love this Pier 1 location, when we moved here we needed to buy another bar stool chair to match the one we already had. They were so helpful and nice, finding it for us online and ordering it to the store. When the chair came and it turned out I had guessed the wrong height (I said we had the tall one but I guess it was actually considered the short one), they were so awesome and sent the wrong one back. We got the new one within a week. 

The store is also very clean and organized, with plenty of parking!

The food was amazing. Very nice wait staff and the environment was pleasing. I had the Dolamades with out lemon creme sauce. simply delicious. The  vegetables that accompany the plate was yummy. Bright and very pleasing to the eye. Fun time had by all. The food looks just like the pictures that you see in the menu or online. 
Will definitely return.

Over 15 minutes in the drive thru line for a hamburger... Dealing with some potential food allergies for my child and had a very specific, simple order... SMH. 15 Minutes they had to get this right and what happened...!?

So, I checked in to Aveda today on Yelp, and this 'free sample' offer cane up on my app (I've posted a picture). I showed it to Brooke, my stylist who I adore, and she said we will show it to the manager when we leave. WARNING: DO NOT show this to the manager, she rolled her eyes at me, said 'I don't know why they do that', completely humiliated me, then said 'I dong have samples but choose one of these'. They were 2 ml bottles that did not pertain to my hair at all. In the meantime I could see, Brooke going through drawer fulls of samples but I just left with the charity I was shamed into asking for. So embarressing. It's the last picture in my photos for Aveda. Anyways, thanks for the good service per usual Brooke. 


I had an unfortunate haircut at this salon in the past and never wanted to go back but my daughter goes, and she sees a stylist named 'Brooke', so I decided to schedule a haircut with Brooke, and I will be going back. She started with a relaxing aromatherapy neck, shoulder, head massage and then she gave me the perfect cut, exactly what I asked for. She's young and friendly and she listens to what you want. She definitely made up for my bad experience with a girl who I don't even think works there anymore. 
They do training there, but they have professional stylists as the majority. You wouldn't even know there's training going on. 
Beautiful, clean, and they offer really good quality hair products that you don't find in every other salon. Plus, they're on whyte beside Lush, so you can just get your hair cut then do the whyte Ave bumming around. 
Make an appointment with Brooke, you will be satisfied, and she's very affordable.

The roasted turkey, apple, cheddar sandwich is usually great, as is Panera. However, this location has let me down a couple of times. 

The staff is usually flustered, but willing to help. There breads and desserts are dry, and most tables aren't cleaned off. The disappointment today: NO Prickly pear tea, no apple on my sandwich and some of turkey on my sandwich wasn't cooked through. BUMMER! My husband was pleased with  food & that's what saved this review today.

I'm sure I'll be back because of the proximity to my house, but as soon as I find another I won't be back. My suggestion is to check your order before leaving!

This was my first time trying this BBQ restaurant.. and I'm really glad that I did.  I tried the Hogwood sandwich and it was amazing! The pork was just perfect, and the white sauce was incredible... the whole sandwich comes together nicely and just melts in your mouth. The beans were also really good, and they had a lot of meat in them. I will definitely be back the next time I'm in town, to try more of the menu!
Outside was fine. Interior was a very poor job. Back area was not vaccummed at all. Will not go back. This was suposed to be their best wash!
best for nude rec in town love that place highly recomend.

This place became one of the Subways that became a staple for us in the morning on our trip south.  We had a hotel with a free breakfast but their set up was questionable so we would get up early and head out to the Subway.  They were always ready to go and they now have a great breakfast that you can get to go.  I had the egg white with green pepper and onions on an English muffin.  Very tasty and inexpensive.  It got us away from the scary hotel.

Love, love, love Wright's Gourmet House!! The Beef Martini sandwich is my favorite, hands down! It's melt in your mouth delicious! My taste buds (but not my hips) have thanked me many times for this treat, LOL

And of course, there's everything else on their menu, all of which is top notch affordable gourmet fare. Definitely wonderful.

This was my first time here. I ordered the pork with 2 sides (mac and cheese and baked beans.) My food was delicious. I preferred the smoky and sweet sauce with the pork. I always liked the mac and cheese which were shells with a yummy cheese...not too cheesy though. The staff were sweet and they served my food quickly. If I am ever back in the area, I would definitely eat there again.

Stay far, far away from this place. I'm relocating for work early April and told them in early March. They told me I had to pay rent through 30 days after the end of the month (so really almost two months) and THEN pay 2.5 months more rent and THEN they keep the deposit, another month worth. So in total, nearly give FIVE months rent.

Fun fact. This is illegal. Any fees collected are to cover the cost of vacancy. Fine. However, in Missouri they must attempt to mitigate damage and rerent the apt. Especially the deposit, there must be VERIFIABLE financial damage to the company or else they owe you double the deposit.

So if you broke your lease and they retained your deposit, please contact me and we'll take care of this. They prey on students and pretend their lease can subvert the law.
I enjoyed the curry chicken. The portion size is plentiful and cauliflower rice is interesting

Dr Warren is every patients NIGHTMARE-

He is HORRIBLE!  --I'm  SO sorry I ever chose him and his staff is disrespectful and not attentive ( WIFE)

The worst decision I ever made for plastic surgeon.


 I had to spend thousands more" to fix stop his "BOTCHED WORK!!!!"

There should be a law against doctors like him.


He Is not up to date with procedures and practices!

I had an issue in the middle the night and I called and had to go to the office around 3 AM and he was accompanied by his very fake wife who told me I was an inconvenience and my husband was furious.

He performed a mini facelift on someone that was 40 years old it was not a lot of work and I ended up scarred and there was still a little bit of hanging skin on my neck and he tried to say I needed , sculpting treatments after I spent over $13,000

I would've spent any amount of money to get a good job please do you not go to this doctor or practice!

I spent thousands more repairing his errors.






His work is horrible, and I know I was an easy candidate.

After D. Warren comes off like he's a good guy, but he can look at a patient and know how he messed up ,and not take accountability!!!

Just want to preface this review by saying that I'm being generous with the 3-stars for Bluefin. (It's an average from my last two visits). The first one about a month and a half ago was four stars, and my visit today only warranted two stars. 

My son and I arrived at straight up 3 PM for a late lunch, or brunch (as I was hoping for). I asked the manger, Sara, who seated us if we would be able to order off the brunch menu. I was immediately told, "No, sorry- brunch ends at 3:00." Oh well, we were given the "light plate" bar menu (and no other options), and told we could order from "this menu." 

Being that it was a sunny afternoon, my son and I got a table outside on the patio. In case you've never been to Bluefin- it's truly a great patio, with marble tables and French bistro chairs, opposite an artistic, artichoke colored fountain- in the shape of a succulent plant. Casas Adobes plaza is a beautiful and unique, rustic burnt adobe brick shopping center designed by Joesler. For all these reasons, and as far as I'm concerned, the patio at Bluefin is one of my favorite patio spots in Tucson. It echoes of its Spanish Colonial architectural past, and is reminiscent of ubiquitous sidewalk cafe's in Europe... It's a great escape, complete with views of the Catalina mountains. I normally would highly recommend Bluefin, and its patio. 

Unfortunately, all the good at Bluefin was to be found only in the setting today. The service and the food fell far too short... We were seated for about five minutes, when we were approached by the waitress, who literally did not even say hello- did not even ask how we were- did not even tell us her name... Her opening line greeting consisted of, "Are you ready to order?" Ouch!!! That really set the tone for not such a great experience. I ordered the salmon melt ($11), but I didn't want fries. What I thought was a simple request, "Can you please substitute fresh fruit for the fries," drew our waitress into a long drawn out explanation of, "Well, I can see if they can use some of the leftover fruit from brunch for you. Let me go check with the kitchen." When she returned, she said, "They said the fruit would be an up charge of $2." I said, "Well, is there anything that wouldn't be an up charge, because I'm not sure if I want to pay $2 for leftover fruit." She left again, returning two minutes later to tell me that I could choose spinach, rice, or potato. I chose spinach. My son ordered fish and chips ($15.50), and we decided to split a Caesar salad ($7). 

The Caesar arrived with sourdough bread and butter. It was a great Caesar, one of the best I've had in a long time. When the food arrived, I was surprised that the salmon melt looked like a typical (small) grilled cheese sandwich, but it had sparse quantities of broken pieces of salmon and pesto sauce. It was lackluster, at best, and my side of spinach was drowning in olive oil. Since I was so hungry, I didn't even bother to send my food back, but my son's food was another story. The fish and chips batter was literally orange! This strangely colored batter was covered with visible amounts of sea salt. I tasted it, and nearly gagged. It was soggy, salty, and sopping with oil. Perhaps they'd left the same oil in the fryer all afternoon? Whatever the case, it was clear that the brunch chef had gone home for the day. My poor son ended up eating only french fries and cole slaw, because the fried fish was literally inedible. There's nothing worse than bad fish, and to her credit, the manager offered to bring us a "few pieces of fish, or a dessert," but both my son and I were less than excited about the food at that point...We asked for the check, and the fish and chips was removed from the bill. 

My last experience at Bluefin (for Sunday brunch) was much better than today. Unfortunately, I left with a bad taste in my mouth (and a stomach ache) from Bluefin this afternoon. I'll eventually give them another try, but probably not very soon. Bluefin tends to be on the pricey side. I'd always thought the food more than made up for the prices, but not today...

One of the best burgers in Nashville!  Everything is fresh and the burgers are a good size.  The fries are amazing- hand cut and, again, fresh.  It's about a 20 minute wait for the food because they make it fresh.  Seating is all outside but it looks like they are building a new structure.

If you're craving a good burger and fries, go here!

I came here to have brunch with a few friends on Saturday around 10:50am. Service was friendly and we were seated fairly quickly. I haven't been back here for a couple of years but I'm happy that the food still tastes delicious. 

I ordered the $21 Bangers and Mash dish which included free-range organic chicken honey sausages, roasted garlic mashed potatoes, two sunny eggs, beef reduction, and caramelized onion. The beef reduction sauce is the best part of this dish since it's a perfect complement to the other ingredients. You will not be disappointed with this dish.

My friends ordered the chicken & waffles, smoked salmon & waffles and the chocolate hazelnut crepe. The two friends who ordered the waffles said their meal was great. However my friend who ordered the crepe said the portion was too small and she was still hungry after. 

Overall it's a decent place to have brunch in Edmonton but it is a bit pricey compared to other places. They don't take reservations for brunch so you may want to come earlier to get a seat.
If you want good work done without delay for a reasonable price, this is the place for you. My experience was great.

We decided to come here on a Saturday morning. There was a line out the door. They wouldn't take a reservation and we had to wait about 30 minutes. Once seated, uncomfortably close, between a table of 4 and a table of two, someone came over and offered us coffee. My boyfriend had a cup, which was a bit strong for me at a whopping $2.75. I ordered a hot chocolate with our waiter that was lumpy.  When it came time to order, I got a two eggs your favorite way that were sunny side up, with marble rye and a delicious potato pancake. The breakfast was very yummy and filling. My boyfriend got a breakfast quesadilla. It was pretty tasty. The waiter came over several times to check up on us but we couldn't find him at the end to order some soup for our friend to go. The waiter came back with the check and our to go. 
The food made up for the close quarters. I would revisit for lunch, some items looked great. I would just skip going on a Saturday and request a table in a corner or something.

Took my car in to replace a windshield. Staff was really nice , prices were cheaper than other glass places in the area as well cheaper than paying the deductible. Called on a Thursday they were able to get me in on that Monday. Great Service !

The service was less to be desired what sealed the deal on this review was the glimpse into the kitchen completely disgusting...the have failed past health department visits and it shows why...think hard before ordering delivery, the drive thru and take out...take a peek behind the doors...Gross!
I really enjoyed painting w Becky,and loved my pic,I'm going to be giving it to my nephew for his upcoming b-day,I thought it turned out awesome,and I had fun doing it!

Extremely polite staff, even at the end of the day. I appreciate the tinted windows to reduce the direct solar gain (helps the building use less AC) and additional privacy when filling out patient check in sheets. Morgan was my nurse today, she is extremely kind and funny. Makes the experience less uncomfortable. I would be happy to come again and happier to be attended by Morgan. Thanks for doing a great job and an awesome health care worker!

This is a great "do-it-yourself" Japanese BBQ spot. Each table has a flame pit in the center, where you will grill the raw meat you order and there are three dipping sauces (regular,spicy, and ponzu) to go with it. I came here to try the BBQ lunch special and it was a great deal! You can get 2 bbq items for 9.95 (comes with soup, salad, and rice) or 3 for 12.85. I ended up getting the garlic shrimp and basil chicken, both of which were great. Of the dipping sauces, I really enjoyed all three. Overall, a great spot to try for lunch!

Don't waste your time with this place.  I scheduled my first appointment, they cancelled it because they had no doctor staffing the office I was scheduled in that day (ummm, what?).  I rescheduled that appointment, they cancelled it again - same reason (why are they scheduling patients in an office that isn't staffed?).  When I went to reschedule a third time, they asked for a $25 scheduling fee.  Why would I pay them a fee when THEY can't keep their appointments?  I called and they said they would override the fee....however, as I was about to make my 3rd attempt at getting an appointment, I just gave up.  I remembered that this takes 3 appointments so to have to keep going through this is too much of an inconvenience.  I work full-time and to keep rescheduling my day to accommodate their lack of organization or concern for "patient experience", doesn't work for me.  There are plenty of other doctors in NJ that are licensed for this.  I highly suggest you find one of them and schedule an appointment.  This place is ridiculous.

I wish I could write a better review, but based strictly on the room cleanliness and the lack of communication and organization I just can't. We arrived right after 4 pm for our staycation check in. The check in was an absolute mess. They couldn't get our room keys programmed from any of the machines. I waited a little over 20 minutes in the lobby before they gave up and had the bellboy take us down to our room. They stated they would bring the keys down when they were ready. That's great, but that also meant we weren't able to get in to the pool because we didn't have our keys. We couldn't leave our room without leaving it propped open or having a bellboy come down to let us back in. 

That's not even the tip of the iceberg with the issues we experienced. There were SIX dead beetles on the floor in our room. Not in corners, not in cabinets, throughout the entire open floor area. I understand monsoon season in Tucson brings out bugs, but it also means the floor was not even partially cleaned prior to our arrival. There was also a very dirty sock located on the floor directly in front of the desk/table area. There were several bugs stuck in the curtains overlooking our porch area as well. Everything had a dirty/dingy feel to it. 

Two-ish hours later (after me calling twice) they finally brought our room keys down. We were able to get out to the pool and relax at almost 7 pm. We have two young kids (2 and 4) so it was getting closer to their bedtime, too. We get to the pool and lo and behold there are no towels anywhere. We called the front desk from the phone at the pool and they said they'd be down with towels shortly. Over an hour later, no towels. We trotted back to our room with two sopping wet kids as well as ourselves. 

Then "because of the storm clouds" the TV wasn't working. It would come in for a few seconds at a time but wouldn't hold a picture. I called the desk and they offered to send an engineer down but assured me it was because of the incoming storm. 

We took the kids back out to the pool around 8:30 am and there were still no towels. Luckily, I figured as much and brought ours from our room. 

The entire experience was a bust. I should have trusted the rest of the warnings and picked a different resort. I feel like I threw a significant amount of money in the trash for a terrible in town get away.

While you are standing in line waiting to get in the door at Pappy's (can't deny their BBQ is great to) and looking on your phone for another option, this is your place!  Their ribs are phenomenal, pit (baked) beans have a little something extra that makes them sensational, and the service is as friendly as it gets.  You will not regret the short drive to Adam's where this hidden gem will leave you never imagining of waiting in line for hours again!
Best breakfast place around but there's always a wait. They don't do reservations but they do take call aheads - definitely do that! Huge portions for a fair price and everything is delicious.

Pub style food is available on an extensive menu with lots of variety. Very accommodating to special requests. Good service including a paging system at every table to alert your server. My favourites are the Greek pizza, and the beef dip. Large servings.

Larry's steaks are very good. The steak is not too thick, seasoned well, and full of juicy flavor. They sweet peppers are fresh and the best ones I've had out of any cheesesteak spot I'm a cheese whiz fan however, they are not that generous with the whiz. 

I also ordered cheese fries and it was not what I expected. The fries and cheese were delicious. But, they literally put a little scoop of cheese on one part of the fries. Was not worth my money they could've done better with that. Also, the customer service is not the best here. Expect to wait a long time with no real organization or line as people just walk up to one window and shout their orders out :/ other than that Larry's Steaks are the best in my opinion and I will be back.

Ok. So Roundabout is the "cafeteria" restaurant at Tesla.   I work there and take advantage of ordering there often.   
The food is always good.   They offer pre packaged grab n go food as well as made to order.  The taco bar is delicious.   Tostadas, tacos, burritos...yum.   Their salads are large and loaded well with proteins and such.   Pasta bar is fantastic.  We are lucky to have such a treat available to us for lunch.   It is a bit pricey for "cafeteria" food but good.  Employees are always super nice and they keep everything crazy clean.  
Can't post a picture because we are not allowed to, but it's a cute little set up.  I'm a fan.

The lunch buffet has very limited choices - and the food display (taste, look and quality)  is poor/low/below average that you would never want to come back again. Worst ever buffet I have tried in Edmonton. Plus the place needs a good clean up.
Progressive books, quiet discussions, adequate coffee, and many places to lounge.  Positive vibes and good energy.  Cool lil merchandise as well; def stop by!

I come all the way from Philly for their sushi. The service is fast and good. At about $22 dollar the price is very reasonable. The quality of the food is good and you can choose from a wide selection of sushi, sashimi and other things. I like that it is not a buffet, instead you can order as much as you want from the menu. This concept guarantees the food is always freshly prepared.
Friendly, knowledgeable staff are also great with helping me select dog food. Great selection of American made toys and treats. Very cute little local shop.

Please please heed this warning. They lie threw their teeth. Be aware YOU ARE SIGNING a 36 month contract which is binding. READ YOUR CONTRACT. When the installer is done and you sign off, you have entered into their contract. They are crooks and shady as they come. Go elsewhere
It's cheap and many selections.  We came late for lunch and almost all buffet items were old and dry.  I think the buffet is better when you come early.

Our waitress was very friendly! The beer was great. I had a BBQ chicken salad which had a lot going on - croutons, fried onions, a lot of chicken... I actual love lettuce and would have enjoyed more in the salad. But overall a great experience - thanks!
Annoyed. Came here for something to eat and a drink. Service was terrible. 3 people behind the bar and no one paying attention to us. No menus. One drink and out.
Terrible service tonight. Cold food, wrong orders, undercooked steak. Not sure if it was the waitress, an understaffed kitchen or both but we will definitely not be returning to this establishment.
Very good food. Tasted fresh. The burritos are huge! The place is small inside with picnic style tables. I highly recommend this place. We will be back!

A co worker brought back a dozen assorted petit cupcakes from here today. I had the red velvet. It was most delicious I have ever had. I will definitely be stopping by here and it's so close to my house.

I'm surprised this is only their second review because there were lots of people there with us. 

This was my first swamp tour and I had a great time. I think everyone should do one! 

They picked us up at our house in the French Quarter. It was a shared shuttle so we went to two hotels to pick people up. The ride to the swamp is fun in itself because you get to see more of New Orleans going city to swamp. Once you're there you wait until it's your groups turn. No matter where you sit, you'll get wet. WEAR A PONCHO. If you're really a princess about it, sit next to the driver. You won't get as wet. During the entire tour, your guide will tell you stories--personal or facts. Like our guy lived in the bayou so he'd point out places he'd take his girlfriend for date night.  We saw lots of gators. Your guide will park in a couple spots and feed them marshmallows which they call gator crack; it's white so they see them and immediately swim for it. The gators get REALLY close, but none of them tried to bite. Towards the end our guy parked and brought out a bag from under his seat. He had a baby gator with us the entire time. So we all got to pet him and take pictures. They're really sweet when they're small. Our guy put his finger in between the gator's open mouth and the gator wouldn't bite down at all. 

BRING CASH AND TIP YOUR GUIDE!!!
Good beer selection, awesome staff, quick service and phenomenal cheesteaks make this my favorite Irish bar in the city. Definitely worth checking out!

This location consistently closes at 2:00 pm in the summer "due to high temperatures." What??? You are operating a restaurant in Tucson, Arizona in July. My guess is that they are relying on evaporative or "swamp" cooling, which most of Tucson has moved on from as it does't cool efficiently during the humid months of July, August and September. This Cheba Hut location needs to either invest in an air conditioning system or go out of business.

This was my first time here...I loved it. The breakfast sandwiches were fresh and delicious. The donuts were so good and a 100% better than DD. Best cup of coffee I had since I in a long time. The person who greeted me at the drive thru window was friendly. I highly recommend this shop

Loved the Jockamo Special pizza and the Goat Cheese Marinara starter was also very good. There are so many options that sounded good so I'm excited to return and try more. Pick-up was quick and easy too.

Place: Standard Japanese decor.  Strip Mall location.  Place needs overall general cleaning.
Service: Seated Promptly.  Waitress seemed distracted and not attentive.  Not good service.
Food: Did not have sushi. Sauces all seemed watered down.   Food is the non fresh frozen, Lettuce seemed old, and was not hot (like it came from a Hibachi grill)  Sat at a table not a grill.

Don't go here could be way better.
Splitting timber is soo much fun! Eric is awesome! I highly recommend requesting him to be your axpert!

This place has been bookmarked for a year and I finally made a visit. The restaurant is located next to a pilates studio, I missed it the first time circling around. Looks woody inside, reminds me of a cabin. The server recommended the ribs if I wanted an appetizer. Yup, they were good. Has an asian flare, tender, and meaty. 
Many of the pizzas sounded good but I saw a few buzzed words that led me to my default at any restaurant - "truffle" & "egg". The Nomad pizza. One of the lunch specials with the bianco pizza was also picked. The server spread the yolk around the pizza with a spoon at the table. The crust had a good chew to it and it was tasty. I wouldn't mind if it was slightly more crisp on the bottom though. Maybe I can try to request that next time. The truffle flavor was mild and a few extra mushrooms wouldn't hurt since they shrink in the oven. The side salad with the lunch special was just okay. Simple salad. The condiments of pepper flakes, cheese, and oregano came on one small plate, which I still don't understand. I have seen it served this way at another popular pizza place in town. The only way to really not put too much on the pizza is to use your hands but what if people don't like their eating partner's hand hygiene? What if it was shared with a lot of people? They do offer a moist toilette! And you can't really shake the condiments off the plate since there are three items on one plate. I tried to use utensils to push it off the plate but put a few too many pepper flakes on my pie. By the way, they use real parm cheese which is awesome! 
The pizza I had was good but I don't think I ordered the better options on the menu after reading some of the other reviews. The salted caramel affagato (gelato with espresso) was a nice yin and yang dessert too! Oh! And... they have mexican cola (cola with real sugar)! Friendly staff. They have an outdoor patio, maybe furry one can tag along next time.
Food is So Good here, Everything from the Burgers to Wings to the Spinach Salad to the Fish Tacos just Amazing, I'll Definitely be Back!!!

I've always been satisfied with the wall I get for my 2008 Jeep Grand Cherokee.  Although it is old, it sure looks good when I leave.  The vacuums are the best anywhere.  Strong suction, different hose models to get in the cracks.  I have a monthly contract, and I get in and out relatively quick and easy.

Tijuana flats is a good restaurant.  I've had a few things off the menu, but my favorite is the chicken quesadilla.  The chips and queso dip is also good, plus, it's unlimited.  Sometimes I get it to-go.  

Their menu is good.  Their ingredients are always fresh and tasty.  They have a hot sauce bar that I really love.  It starts with sweet, and ends with OMG hot.

The ceiling decoration is cool.  It looks like a bunch of individual works of art.  There is an upbeat atmosphere that puts me in a good mood.  The service has always been good.  I like the food, and I'll be back for sure.

This place used to be fun. On Saturday afternoons I used to meet a coworker on the patio which backs right up to the greenbelt and the river. As of late however their quality of food has declined while their prices have gone up.  The service, while friendly, has been spotty with each visit and they are slow to clean up the patio tables after guests leave, creating a free-for-all haven for flies, wasps and other critters.  Inside was not much better in terms of loud noise but the decor was quite interesting; who doesn't enjoy the crab T-shirts that say "Bite Me" on top?

Years ago the wait staff used to gather in the center of the meeting space inside and dance to some catchy music at the top of each hour... haven't seen that lately?  The only thing I like about this place now is the patio for brew during a bike ride but that's about it.  That vibe that used to draw excitement (for me at least) seems to have diminished.  Perhaps it's because of the Sandbar just further up the bike path.

Not sure what to make of this place. Went with 2 other friends. 2 weeks ago as pre party for late night in st, pete. Arrived around 8pm...got seated right away. Nice start. Was presented with the menus and got our drinks. Saw one of the specials was "wagu beef sushi"  I like wagu and was looking forward to an awesome experience with it. Was very disappointed with the presentation overall. As I understand it, wagu is very fatty marbled meat and needs to have some of the fat (flavor) present to appreciate the difference. Was expecting to see a nice caramelization or even "black and blue" style of cooking. ..NOT.. The dish arrives at the the table 2 pieces of very raw, grey looking beef, meaning that it was not cooked at a high enough temperature to get the fat liquid and blend in with the beef. we also ordered a crab dip on  wontons that was just  so so at best. My entree was the pork belly small dish that also missed the mark. The way I understand it, pork belly needs to be again, carmelized and crispy to appreciate the pork flavor. Mine arrived very fatty, soft texture minimal flavor but again disappointed. I may go back once more to order an item that is not sushi or small plate

Came here a few weeks ago for dinner. It was fantastic! The service was out of this world good and the food was delicious. I had the pasta with the marscapone sauce and the beef carpaccio. Both were great. I recommend making reservations for a weekend night as they can be busy!

Recently visited this run-down taco shop for the first time, and it's a gift from god. It's not your usual California taqueria fare, but more along the lines of what you'd expect to find in Mexico's interior. I had the lomito suizo which was chunks of incredibly flavorful marinated pork stuffed into a small quesadilla on corn tortillas that the lady makes right in front of you. The adobada tacos were fantastic as well. High-quality hot sauce. All but a few items are priced at roughly $3 or so, and I imagine that 2 or 3 items would be a solid enough meal for most folks. Do it.

Great food, slightly lacking service. Like any chipotke, it feels as thought employees are trying to push you out as fast as they can. Avoid going here at the peak of meal times, as there is always a massive line, snaking out the door.

Moved back to Tucson after being away for 13 years, and thought I would stop by Mama's Famous Pizza for lunch.  I arrived at 1:10, so I missed the pizza slice lunch special.  Instead, I went ahead and ordered the Meatball Hero.  After ordering, I waited... and waited.. and waited... I got caught up looking at reviews of Mama's, and before I knew it a half an hour had gone by.  Seemed kinda ridiculous for a simple meatball sandwich.  I'm not normally one to complain, but I was out of time, and I had to get back to work.  I went up and asked for my money back, and the cashier mumbled an apology, then went and got the manager.  The manager came right up, didn't talk to me except to ask if I wanted my money on the card, or in cash (since returning to the card could take a few days).  As she handed me my cash, she mumbled "sorry about that" and walked away.  No concern that after having skipped breakfast, and having a late lunch that I was now unable to eat until after work... not to mention that I had to work until 7:30PM.

I trusted them with my lunch.  Not only did they screw it up, but they made no attempt to make it right.  But more importantly, they had no concern about the impact that might have had on their customer.  None at all.

They might be good pizza (I don't know, I can't remember now), but if you are thinking about where to go on your lunch hour, consider my experience.  It could be you that ends up inadvertently fasting for the day.

Rooms are small
Location is a couple blocks from the French Quarter
Great restaurant in hotel
Fairly priced

This is where I get my micros done. The lady who does them is named Bintou. She is really nice and will help you take your braids out, shampoo, condition, and blow dry your hair before starting to braid your hair. With tip, I pay 160-170 with hair included and it lasts forever. I have even gotten it done on Sunday. I usually call to make an appointment with her. It is located in the gas station mini shopping area with the Dunkin Donuts at 52nd and Lancaster above one of the office spaces. The space is shared with Dollface Hair Studio. I haven't gotten my hair done at Dollface but the ladies are nice and they do great wash and curls and haircuts.

A wonderful addition to the downtown Dunedin culinary scene.  We ate indoors, beautiful decor, wonderful server.  All 4 of us had different dishes and we enjoyed all of them.  My linguini dish was al dente and the seafood was perfectly seasoned.  The owners were available afterwards to be certain we had a good experience.  We look forward to our next visit.

So sad because we were excited. We ordered two burritos that were different. We were given the same. Plus we asked for no guac in one because my boyfriend is allergic. And what do you know they put  guac on it. Waste of $15 because he couldn't eat it. He would die if he ate it with guac. Disappointed that they couldn't pay attention to an allergy.

Very solid BBQ place (with some great highlights) and the prices are very reasonable.

Must get:
- Brisket fries - this is an appetizer but its a ton of food. Brisket is pretty legit too.
- BBQ chicken - holy crap this place can cook chicken. I usually hate white meat (breast) but this was super juicy and flavorful.
- mac and cheese
- pecan pie - usually I'm not a fan, but this was very good.

Beer selection is great but be warned many are only in cans.

Whiskey selection is good to.

Disappointing.  As a local I can assure you there are other places that provide seafood at a better value.  For $15, the grouper tacos yielded maybe an oz of fish per each and a mess of fries.  Fairly decent tasting, but I still felt hungry after my meal. I  think the service was good; accommodating and polite, but slow....You won't get a terrible meal here, but you are paying for a convenient location more than a gourmet meal.

Great experience if you need to call a plumber. Very professional, friendly and knowledgeable. Travis the owner was in constant contact with me, taking the time to explain what was wrong and how they were going to fix the problem and the best part of all he saved me a lot of money. After having a horrible experience with a previous plumbing company it was refreshing to do business with Economy Drain Cleaning and Plumbing, I can tell they care about their customers. I highly recommend.

They tell you it's $125 plus any additional charges that you incur in treatment. I had a staph infection that the doctor looked at, but didn't clean, lance or test for anything; he just wrote me a prescription for antibiotics. I got a bill in the mail later for an additional $75. So essentially, it's impossible to get out the door without paying more than they tell you the base office visit is. This to me is false advertising and I would strongly recommend that if you can wait or have any other option, you go someplace else.

The quality of this restaurant has gone way downhill since ownership changed hands. We used to travel from lower Bucks county to come here when it was Young Bin Kwan, but have not been back here (nor will we be) since our first try under new ownership. We took a friend from out of town here, raving about this delicious Korean buffet in Jenkintown, thinking it was still YBK; yet we were extremely embarrassed with our dinner reccommendation once we left. The banchan was horrible, and there wasn't very much of it (YBK had at least 8 different banchan on the buffet). The meat was of poor quality, and the staff let some pans sit without meat in them for our entire meal (which is a shame for me because I don't eat beef, and they never refilled the chicken). It's such a shame that this place is under new ownership because it was one of the only Korean buffets around in the area.

Very good service! Wait was a little long for one person but they were apologetic for that. Food came out fast and was very good. The pancakes did taste a little bit like the Hungry Jack brand, but they were still tasty :) Menu also has a lot of choices.

I have such mixed reviews for this company. The actual movers, Joe and another guy that I can't recall, were fabulous, but Matt, the boss, has much to be desired!! I had thought everything was going so smoothly-- Matt was very quick to get back to me via email, was very helpful, polite, and gave me a great price for a move from the shore to Philly. 

But then at 8AM on moving day there was a no-show. 8:30 I called, no answer at the office despite Matt saying someone is always there. 8;45, same thing. 9AM, same thing. 9:15- joe finally calls me feeling very bad saying there was a mix-up and that Matt had told him the job was canceled, but then realized it wasn't in fact canceled! We just confirmed on Friday!! He says that it'll take them a few hours to get to us. It's not his fault so I say fine and hang up furious. I call Matt to explain I'm not Paying full price due to the move happening at 12pm not 8am, and I don't understand how this can happen?? I don't get an apology, he doesn't even sound like he feels bad- he actually sounds ANGRY! We haggle over the price, he says he'll take 5% off- $35 for my entire day wasted? No thanks! We end up with a price and he slams the phone down. 


If you go with these guys-check, recheck  and triple check they show up!!!!

The staff was not friendly at all, the ribs were dry, food was cold, macaroni and cheese was like mush, service was absolutely terrible and overpriced. I wish I wouldn't have tipped 20%. Do not waste your time or money. Worse place I've eaten at in Nashville. The best thing on the menu is salad.

When I first moved to St. Louis, I was nervous. I was a TV news personality, and if I may say without any vanity, my appearance was a critical component of my job. Hopefully you can understand I had to be picky and particular. Thankfully, I found Chelsey and I never had to worry again.

I have had numerous cuts from Chelsey and I will never go to another stylist, so long as I am in St. Louis. Grace Taylor salon is a beautiful and relaxing place to go and with Chelsey at it's helm, you absolutely could not ask for more.

Chelsey is what some would call a perfectionist  when it comes to her craft. I cannot express enough how much I admire that in a stylist. She takes her time and focuses on each cut, every angle, all to make it perfect. This is an incredibly rare trait in a stylist and it's exactly what most women want. More, she ensures that I know exactly how to replicate what she's done when we are not together. She has provided lessons to me on how to fix my hair and style it, etc. She takes extra time in the appointment to talk with me about home styling technique. She wants me to leave not only with a cut that looks perfect when she's done it, but with the confidence, based on experience, that I can do the look myself. Few other stylists would want to provide you with these skills. Any one who has interpreted her intentions as anything else is simply wrong.

Chelsey is often going to seminars and conferences to learn the very latest in styling. She is dedicated to doing the very best job and I really believe she knows exactly what's doing with every cut and color (mine is pretty darn complicated).

Though her talent for styling is really palpable, what I love most about Chelsey is her warm personality. She listens to my styling needs and concerns. She has a way of making me and others feel really good about themselves, glamorous and fabulous, when they leave her chair. I get so many compliments on my hair. I can honestly say I have given many referrals to now happy clients. I know that her number one priority is a satisfied client with the best and hottest cut and color, but she also cares that you had a good, relaxed time while you were with her.

There's a reason her business has grown so fast in such a short time. If I were you, I'd make an appointment soon, because she's been booking up lately. Hey, that's what happens when you're good.

After trying a multiple amount of Vietnamese restaurants throughout Indianapolis, (Saigon, Pho 54, Pho Tasty) I must say Sizzling Wok has the best Pho bo vein. Though our table was quite sticky...The meatballs are more tasteful and the amount of bean sprouts served was delicious. Our server was distant but the food made up for it

Is there anything more of an instant confidence boost than a cut and/or color? I would challenge you to name a quicker and easier way to feel fresh and spiffy. Salon FX has been my go-to in Nashville to take care of all my hair needs.

Salon FX is a trendy hair salon located in Music Row, across the street from Rebar. Receptionists are always friendly and make it easy to schedule appointments. They give you a text reminder and also ask you to confirm a few days before your next appointment. 

Cassandra has been my stylist the whole time I've been going here and she's great. I can vaguely describe what I want and she makes sure I'm happy with what she does. She does great highlights, cuts and styling. 

Services aren't cheap but it is worth it. The quality of the experience keeps me coming back.

Came here for lunch on a weekday...which was probably a mistake as all the reviews and amazing pics were of brunch items. I got the Oven Fried Chicken Salad with Buttermilk dressing, the sis also got a salad with tuna and minestrone soup; both salads were just underwhelming. My salad was basically romaine topped with what tasted like prepackaged Applegate chicken strips and honey mustard dressing.  Stick to brunch options on weekends and can't recommend their salads.

I've been going to this casino for ten years. Went five days ago for my fiance's birthday to spin the wheel. They dont have That anymore. Now you pick a prize on a screen and they give you a buffet ticket usually. She does this. Buffet ticket has assumably stored on her card. We go today to redeem and cant. Customer service was extremely rude and told her it printed and no one turned it in so were s.o.l.

You are a casino. Its a single buffet voucher. Just comp it.

I believe this should be a zero star or negative star if only they exist. In my experience purchasing 2 sets of tires in last 14 months on two different vehicles, both were horrible.
Young lady named Lupe seemed to know a lot of what she's doing. She did pretty much all the talking but, in a "telling" manner. She was looking at the screen, tapping the key board, said a few words that only she, can hear it. I can tell that she's an expert at what she does. I guess so experienced that her level of customer service vary from 10 to 1, depending on her mood. What's worse was after that horrible treatment from a Sears employee, you get billed $950 from their $99/tire promo.  
If you bought an item that cost 99 cent each, you get out of that store quite feeling excited you bargained until you found out it only cost a quarter. It's not the amount that I paid that disappoints me,but for getting fooled and disre


Mm
State mandated environmental & tire disposal fees per tire. mandatory tire alignment, balancing, and road hazard fee, all were  priced per tire!

Nothing special about this chiropractor except he will charge you more than most others and "tell you" that he is specially trained and has skills that no doctor or other practitioner has.  
I actually experienced more pain after going to him. Won't be returning.  Will stick with my wonderful physical therapist.

Goodness gracious. Well, I guess that's it for Face and Body Spa. I just tried to make an appointment with them and actually reached the point where I realized I would have to go get a refund from Groupon. 

First, we need your full name. Fine, no worries. Next, we need a phone number. Sure, no worries. I'll give you my land line. 

Okay, now full address with zip code is required (just to make an appointment, which is pre-paid by a Groupon, by the way). Uh, okay...fine...I gave them that. Yeah, now it is required that we have your email address. 

Uh....really? For an initial appointment for a prepaid massage? Yes. It is our policy. Uh...okay. 

Now, we need a billable credit card that we can have on file, number, expiration date and security code, given over the phone to somebody I don't know from Adam to secure any appointment because we have a 48-hour cancellation policy that bills the card automatically if you cancel. I finally said, "Wait...this is a little ridiculous, particularly for a prepaid appointment" 

She said, "Ma'am, our technicians make our LIVING this way and we can't have cancellations." 

Because, of course, my hair stylist, manicurist, current massage therapist, dentist, doctor and veterinarian must not actually make their living that way because they actually let me get in the door before asking for my credit card and authorization to bill it. 

When I told her this was unacceptable, she said, "I will not waste time justifying our policies with you." 

And she hung up. 

If the Groupon thing was to build goodwill, I must say, Face and Body Spa....epic fail.

Wow after being there for a few minuets I felt like I was in a used car lot.  The server was over selling and up selling me on everything it was so very annoying.  The food was almost average.  I almost got a gift certificate for a friend as a joke but I could not be that mean to a friend.

Yes, I am indeed addicted to Starbucks. I haven't had the pleasure to drink much coffee lately cause of my stress levels, but I do stop in sometimes for just their scones!!! The blueberry one is always the best!!! Highly recommend that one!

I really like the staff here, they do their job instead of messing around or chatting to other employees like you aren't even their. Great place all around.

I eat here for lunch at least once a week. Soy Taco meal is the way to go in my opinion. The staff is very friendly and helpful and the food is always good and relatively cheap. Of course it's not as cheap as your typical fast food places, but it's good food that's good for you.

We tried the pulled chicken, burnt ends, and the brisket. The pulled chicken and brisket were nothing to write home about. They needed more seasoning or something. Just kind of blah. The burnt ends were the best thing we had.
Skip the food here and just have a beer. Red Stallion is the best. If you're in town when they have Oktoberfest brew, that is all right as well.
This Benihana is very small and cramped. The janitorial supplies are out in the open. Poor optic. Food was good though.
One of the best burger preparations in the city every single time. If you order medium rare like I do, that's what you'll get. Highly recommended.

My friends and I went here and the servers/bartenders ignored us the whole night. We called them over, made eye contact multiple times, even ordered food that we never got. The service here is just trash. We were constantly told to "hold on," but nothing. I would not recommend anyone go there.

UPDATE

I got a call this morning from the office manager at Cory's Lawn Service so it was important to update this review from 1 to 3 stars.

1.  The billing issue was addressed and we came to an agreement on it.  
2.  My concern over the "wording" within the quote appeared to be taken seriously with potential clarity changes.
3.  Though we didn't make it to a regular mowing cadence the crews that did perform work were always friendly and performed the work they did well.

We are still discontinuing our service through Cory's because of the experiences listed in the initial review but I do appreciate the call and the fair remediation of the issue that led to the cancellation.   

***INITIAL REVIEW****

Years ago I reached out to Cory's because he was a UNR grad starting out but at the time they did not provide service in my area so, as I circled back for lawn service more recently I was happy to see they expanded to my area.   Unfortunately, that's about as good as it got.   Just getting things started was a hassle and calling the office gets you someone who seems to have more bad days than good.   But we moved forward from that rough onboarding and essentially signed up for every service they offered.  Cool right?  Wrong!  

Some services (aeration and pre-emergent) were done when they said they'd be here.  The workers were friendly and did their thing.   The pre-emergent was pushed back due to wind so that was ok.  It too was done when rescheduled.   The water system turn-on, not so great and that's where the cracks in Cory's Lawn Service started to show.   A no-show the first appointment with a "oh, sorry he was busy" e-mail.   A no-show the second appointment though I called the office this time.  Yipes.  The same "friendly" person from before tried to explain that "the technician was there, you weren't home, the backflow preventer was not there, and your garage was locked".   As I explained that I work from home, my surveillance cameras showed nobody showing up, the backflow preventer was still attached, and I left the garage man door open, I got the defensive and rude "I'm just the messenger" response and "we'll try to get back out or reschedule".   I did say that if they can't manage to meet their appointment obligations I'd probably need to look elsewhere and got the reasonable response "noted".  The technician did show up, based on my camera at 3:40pm and rang my doorbell to leave at 4:34PM (though I asked a question and they were there until 4:36PM).   So, 56 minutes, right? 

The exact quote for service states "Sprinkler Activation & Winterization: Base rate of $69.97 per service + parts *** If activation requires more than 50 minutes, service will be charged at $85.00 hourly rate."

Clearly, anything over 50 minutes will be charged at $85/hour for the entire duration of the service delivery (reasonably infers that if they are there 1.5 hours, it would be $85 x 1.5 hours per the quote)  No big deal, except they invoiced me for 2.05 hours of work for the 56 minutes of work they did.   My water turn on service  jumped from $66.97 for 50 minutes, to $178.50 for 56 minutes.   Outrageous by any stretch and makes one wonder if there is a little incentive built in to pushing just beyond the 50 minutes.   Personally, I think their time onsite was accurate and they did fix some sprinkler issues (I paid for the parts) but things that may you go hmmmmmm.

Anyway, I wrote to find out the discrepancy and only then was I told that instead of $85 per hour to deliver the service, it's actually $85 per hour for each member of the crew, a fact not really highlighted in the terms of the quote.   So, for an extra 6 minutes of work, Cory's charges a premium of $108.53.  Crazy.   Glad they didn't have a 5 person crew. 

So, I did write again, explained the terms in their quote didn't match up with their charge and asked to refund the overcharge (using their 1.05 hours, not the actual time recorded on my cameras).   Absolute crickets.   Ignored.  But hey, that was what I got from them several times so it's not a surprise.

So, in the end, I cancelled my service.  I think the actual workers would do a great job but the front office grump, lack of response, and questionable billing practices has taken me elsewhere for lawn care service.

This whole practice is awful.  I had seen Dr. Lawrence Parish on a few occasions.  He was very odd with terrible bedside manner.  I also didn't like that he accused me of doing things to my skin that I did not.  The office staff is TERRIBLE.  I went to my last appt and no one was there.  Office was closed and locked.  I was the only one standing there.  I called the emergency line as well as doctors line and left a message.  No one returned my call.  I then called the office.  The receptionist was very rude and said they tried to call but my phone was busy.  It was not.  I was not on my personal phone all day.  I rescheduled my appt.  I was then running out of my medication, so my pharmacy tried to contact them for refills but did not hear anything.  I called the office and got the same rude miserable "lady."  I told her how dissatisfied I was with the appt cancellation as well as not responding to my pharmacy request.  They tried to blame it on my pharmacy saying they have a bad track record, which they don't.  She then got foul about why the appt was canceled, took my pharmacy info, and hung up.
I immediately went on to my insurance website and located a new dermatologist and made an appt with a new doctor.  I have also canceled the appt that was rescheduled.  I will never be going back to this awful place.

Thalia was just not very interesting to me from the start. The menu was not appealing. Maybe because I thought it was an Italian restaurant and had my hopes on that. It's definitely not Italian.  

After seeing an IG post for lent fish Friday, we ended up at Thalia.  Fish was lightly coated in cornmeal. My favorite way to have fried seafood.  Served on a plate of tartar sauce and a side of German potato salad, plus hush puppies.  

The most interesting thing about this dish was the plating.  It was like a deconstructed potato salad. Potato salad sitting next to everything that is normally in a potato salad. Including hard boiled egg. It was genius. Every bite was perfectly complex yet the flavors were so familiar.  This was the best fried fish plate I've ever had.  

We shared an appetizer of unassuming "bread and butter". Labneh butter and cornmeal naan to be exact.  Wow I just wanted to eat the whole thing. I loved it so much I ordered an extra to take home for breakfast the next day with eggs. So good! 

I was so happy with my meal at Thalia. I will be back.
Some employees not wearing masks. Talking and sniffling while not wearing masks.

I hate to say this because when the word gets out this place will be packed day and night. This is our families favorite restaurant in Brentwood. The sushi is always spot on delicious. I haven't tried anything else on the menu because we just love the sushi. The service is excellent t and always with a smile. I can't recommend Hanabi highly enough. Please please try it.

This place is wonderful! This was a birthday gift/girls night with two co workers and we has a blast! The entire staff was accommodating, friendly and fun! Our art instructor was Kristy, and she was amazing. She took us step by step on how to create our little masterpieces. She was patient, told great jokes and even recognized my birthday. 

I would definitely go back again with friends, family and even my hubby! A great time was had by everyone! Thank you ladies. You helped make it a great night.

I've been here about 4-5 times. I have not had a Bartaco taco that I did not like. It also helps that I have had good guidance from friends who frequent this joint, so I know what to order. I like the order-as-you-go style wherein you can just scan the QR code for the menu, order and pay, at multiple points during the meal. We have had work gatherings wherein we order the appetizers and drinks first, then place another order when we're ready for our tacos, so that keep getting food every ten minutes or so. This system is something you have to know, though, or else you might order all your food at once and it will take a while to get to you. I'm lucky to have regulars in my corner who taught me how to navigate their system.

Sometimes service is fast, and sometimes not so much. I've only ever come at lunch or in the afternoon, so I can't say anything about dinnertime, which would likely be the busiest hours of the day. 

Bartaco is strategically placed in a plaza near cupcakes, pastries and hyppo pops, because dessert after tacos makes me happy. There is free garage parking nearby, and a bunch of spots on the street if you're lucky. 

I recommend the place for late lunch, just because that's when the ambience and lighting are best in my opinion (even though it can be warm especially outside).

Been here a few time tried to go again give benefit or the doubt . Each time the food took 45 min the bartender is taking the orders and working the bar and serving us . You wait 45 minutes for the food and it still comes out cold . Like they set them by the warmer and left them sit for who knows how long .. then when you tell them it is cold the manager comes out to argue the fact that she just made them no lady they are cold and there isn't enough staff. I don't understand it at all how this can keep happening and the manager rude and complaining that I'm raising hell for my money back when I had to have them re fired twice at that point hour later I eat and I'm pissed you come to eat as a family and don't get to . This place needs some serious help and new management

Yum! It's great to have an affordable vegan option on south street when there's constant temptation to spend too much on very unhealthy foods. I was taken back when I viewed the menu and read the prices. All of it was pretty cheap and sounded delicious! I had the eggplant and tofu burger which was delicious. It reminded me of Chinese sweet and sour tofu made into a burger. The cakes on display looked delectable but I refrained myself from those. The woman behind the counter was such a sweetheart so I'm very pleased to be giving my business here. Definitely worth a try.

Thank you for the amazing service from Denise and rest of the staff for replying to my phone calls and emails:) 

My plant arrived healthy and I highly recommend purchasing plants from this nursery! Rare and beautiful plants at a great price with amazing service:)

Park 'N Fly has the basic off site parking lot shuttle system down pat.  There are shuttles circling the lot and by the time I had parked my car and picked up my bags, one had pulled up for me to board.  On the return, there is usually either a shuttle waiting in MSY's transportation Center, or one shows up within a few minutes.

Park 'N Fly is just down the street from their main competitor (and the airport entrance).  Yet, they are a shade more expensive.  (We are arguing a quarter here.)  That said, Yelp has an offer of a 15% discount but you have to print out the coupon.  It is the same discount you get for showing your AAA card, which doesn't require you to print anything out.  Petty complaints aside, the discount knocks about $1.25 off the daily rates.  Other coupons are also available on online, not as good as the Yelp discount.

And these days, who does not have a Customer Loyalty program?   Earn one free day for every 8 days you park with Park 'N Fly.

Burger UP-grade! Went to visit last night with my roomies and was SO, SO happy with my meal.  We had a great attentive waiter, and I really enjoyed a black and bleu burger with sweet potato fries and a seasonal bevvy (who can turn down a beer named 'Snow Day?'  I think I'd like one right now (both the thing and the beer, obvie.)).  In any case, it was a happening place for a Sunday evening, and when we left circa 7:30, there were many people clambering at the door to get in.  Love that they source local ingredients whenever possible, and I was surprised to note that there are plenty of other seating options aside from the elementary school lunch table benches in the center of the space.

I went there on October 28, 2017 around 10.10am to get a cake. I am putting down the date and time so that if someone cares might be easier to find out. They should have a camera to look at it as well. All the other ladies were not old like her. The cake was for my daughter's birthday and I was at the bakery section. I wanted to make sure that her name was written correctly and was standing beside the cake shelf. An old white woman came out and told me to move away little further. I told her that I wanted to give the name to be written on the cake. She told me to move again. Seriously?? I was shocked and dump-founded! And angry too..however, I am not someone who argue immediately, so, just kept quiet. But, I was certainly concerned about the customer service from that old lady...thinking how she got that job, something she is not good at it at all. I would hate to go to a place where people are not welcomed.

Needed a nice place to study and this definitely wasn't it. My qualifications for a fantastic place to study includes 6 things: drinks other than coffee, food options, acceptable wifi, appropriate amount of outlets, clean environment, and clean bathrooms. This place hit 4 out of 6. I'm not a coffee person so I typically order some type of iced tea. Usually, iced green tea tastes great wherever I'm at. For some reason, their iced green tea was just not acceptable. I had to force myself to drink at least half so I could get my money's worth. I just couldn't get myself to drink the entire thing though. They offer food which is a plus and there were ample amounts of outlets. As for the cleanliness, it was not up to par for me. Tables were dirty, trash and crumbs were on the floor, and the bathroom was nasty. I went on a Saturday morning and they were packed, so they're doing something right that I'm just not seeing.
Since my boyfriend took me here last year, when I come back up here I try to have a meal there. It's always delicious and the waitresses was awesome!

Food is good . The spices  blended in perfectly. Butter chicken , chicken tikka masala and kadai paneer were good. Gulab Jamun is really good . Mango lassi was also good. Didn't have to wait a long time after ordering. 

Ambience was okay. Tables could have been a bit larger. It was not enough for 4 people for sure. Hospitality could have been better.

This is one of my favorite taco spots ever! The puffy taco is something that I dream about! Their guacamole is really good, their pork is seasoned well and the puffy shell melts in my mouth! Their margaritas are also really good, fresh tasting and fairly strong! The line goes quickly and food comes out pretty soon after ordering. There's plenty of seating so you can distance easily. Highly recommend this place and if you see the food truck out and about - definitely hit it up!

Decent margarita list (had the standard one and the tamarind, which was a bit too sweet if you ask me).

Cool vibe, great lightning, feels like a contemporary take on a low-lit Mexican restaurant. Great service, too, I might add.

My surf-and-turf tacos (octopus and skirt steak) were spot on. The crab and avocado salad was delicious (and bacon is always a great touch). And my Los Moles put my over-the-edge, deliciously spiced rice with three different meats (pork ribs with a lighter sauce, lamb with mole, and chicken with pablano), really dynamite, if you ask me.

Enjoyed my time. I wish the draft list, like all of Starr's restaurants, was more sensible. 

Prefer this joint to El Vez for what it's worth.

The location is beautiful. I had the pleasure of delivering a wedding cake to this venue. The only downfall is I had to walk up three flights of stairs and across the room three time to set up cake as was not permitted to use the elevator.

Worst Steak and Shake in town and that's saying something!
 Service is beyond slow and the help must be illiterate,  I'm never had a more frustrating experience.    Never again!

Had a bunch of work to do last night so I stayed in at the hotel. I don't usually write reviews on hotel restaurants cuz they're mostly just ok. but the room service I had last night was very good. black and red fish cooked perfectly not too well done. boudin balls which I had to try and were very good. Caesar salad very nicely dressed. and a great coffee creme brulee. Breakfast was okay in the restaurant but not as good as my room service last night. If you are staying at the Renaissance Marriott and don't want to go out you can trust that the room service will be good
2 screaming toddlers were riding tricycles around the deli during my visit. The staff was grumpy, probably weary of the chaos. My sandwich was fine. I ate in my car.

This is a very clean store and well organized.  The staff keep the merchandise
well stocked and your can find some REAL nice deals here.

The second I walked into that restaurant, I immediately saw a mouse run across the top of the grill. I didn't need a second more, I immediately left. The people seemed nice, and the food looked really good, but I will return to the restaurant once they improve their hygiene and eliminate their rodent issue. I'd love to go back once the problem is taken care of, but until then, I will be finding other places to eat.

Wow!!
So authentic it was insane!!
Atmosphere was great
The people were super friendly and the food was incredible!
Will definitely visit again next time I'm in Santa Barbara area!
Only thing missing was some cowboys walking around
Stopped in early Sunday morning (1/29). The butcher, Greg, was very helpful selecting a cut for steak and eggs and offered to slice it thin for us. Family loved it. Thanks Greg

I was in need of a wisdom tooth extraction sooner than later and my dentist was all booked! I was very impressed by Dr. Gardner's Yelp reviews and booked my appointment with ease. When I arrived for my appointment, he wanted to clarify/verify details regarding my insurance and he volunteered to call them right away. Unfortunately, the lines of my insurance company were down and he was unable to talk to a representative. I actually gave him the number to the insurance company & he called back so I could hear the message for myself. He volunteered to check my tooth just to ensure an extraction was necessary and gave me time to make a decision on how I wanted to move forward. Never did I feel pressured to have the procedure done before I felt comfortable, but I was ready to have the tooth removed! Once in the chair, I was very nervous but he assured me that everything would be fine and that I'd feel no pain. 24 hours later and I still haven't felt any pain. Dr. Gardner was extremely nice and comforting. Eventually, I'll need another wisdom tooth extraction and wouldn't hesitate to visit him again.

Play is great, but being one of the ONLY clubs they really need to realize that DJ'ing remixes of pop songs from the radio isn't the way to go. Expand your knowledge and dive into the techno and dance scene. It would make this place sooooo much better. WE NEED BETTER MUSIC.

I stayed here for four nights on business and can say it was enjoyable. My room was clean, front desk staff were incredibly friendly and hospitable no matter the time of day, and the complimentary breakfast was wonderful! The shower was always hot, the room heated and cooled quickly (and not loudly, I might add), the gift shop is nice (but really expensive), and it's far enough away from music row for quiet, but close enough to walk if you don't mind a few blocks. 

The mattress on the bed was extremely firm, not allowing me to sleep well. I slept in a lesser hotel in Denver on my way home with a much softer mattress. Wish they had a complimentary shuttle to the airport (there shuttle will only take you in a two mile radius) but they told me of one that was $14 one way.
