Housekeeping was in a hurry for me to leave on my last day; started knocking on my door at 9 am and then again later in the morning (I was so annoyed, I forgot to see what time it was). Was nowhere near check-out time. But they did a decent job cleaning my room while I was there.

Love this place so much. Went for lunch and got the mountain of nachos which are amazing with the fresh jalapeños and the salads are always fresh and crispy. The only downside is that my drinks usually taste watered down for whatever reason, so I choose not to go for dinner. Definitely will recommend to anyone and have already taken 3 out of town visitors there!
My boyfriend went back and had great customer service. I am assuming it was just the one employee. They fix their mistakes! Just let them know!

Just popping in here to say that Abby at the front desk is AWESOME. She remembered me from when I stayed here 2 weeks ago. I don't feel like a nameless traveller. 

Top notch hospitality and professionalism. 

She reflects very well on the Nugget and elevates its public face.

Bravo Abby.

I came across J's Seafood Dock during French quarter festival. With so many options surrounding, I decided to give them a try due to there being a display of various items they offered. The crawfish beignet was truly disappointing. The presentation was great. The food it's self was lackluster. A more accurate description would be tasteless. The beignet contained crawfish, corn, and NO SEASONING whatsoever. It was bland. Two fried pieces of dough is what I was given with a type of tartar like/ roumelade sauce on top. 

They do have an oyster bar which seemed to have lots of people. Although I didn't try it I would say maybe that should be what you opt for instead. Glad I only spent $6. 

The service was great and for that they get the additional star. Person taking my order took it with a smile. I was checked out quickly and the wait was not too long. Overall, I wouldn't give this restaurant another try. Too many places around that offer the same thing that are probably much better.

Everything I've had here has been good. Bloomsday is a favorite - we just keep coming back! In particular their veggie dishes are excellent - the salmon over a seasonal salad when they have it is a very tasty entree. Highly recommended!

We recently visited here during the last weekend in June. We order Hand Grenades and sat on the patio during the day. The drinks were good. They are $10 but if you save your cup, they will discount the refill. The woman bar tender upstairs was very helpful and friendly. I would go there just to be in her company. I highly recommend visiting this place while in town.

I love this place! They recently opened this storefront and it is where you should be on a Saturday morning! Everything they make is magical but I especially love the morning buns and 3 cheese popovers. And the churro croissants. Really, you can't go wrong with anything here. Also the people who own it are super sweet

I recently hired If Trees Could Talk, and Matt did an outstanding job. I had quite a lot of trees that needed to be removed and a lot more that needed the dead wood removed. It took 3 full days to complete, and for the amount of work that was done I think the price was very reasonable.

Matt is extremely professional, showed up when he said he would, called and communicated the status of the job each day, and cleaned everything up really well. I asked for firewood and mulch to be left in my backyard, which he did.

I've hired many tree service companies and they always seem to be fly-by-night operations with a bunch of guys with ladders and chainsaws. Matt is a professional certified arborist. What a big difference!

I highly recommend If Trees Could Talk, and I will be calling Matt for all my tree services from now on.
Super cool venue! The staff was friendly. There is a spacious concession area just off the main floor. Bathroom facilities were very nice. And, most importantly, the show was a lot of fun!

Disappointed with the food  (banh mi and french dip) @Grand Cafe -even more disapponted to learn senior discount is only available from 5 to 6 pm.  Sorry to see that change.  We were told resort has new ownership.  Both sandwiches  were made with a roll that looked like a french roll but had the texture and flavor of a wonder bread hamburger bun.  Banh mi  might of been better served on a baguette  or a bolillio roll.  The chicken was so-so with minimal  veggies and virtually  void of flavor the side  I choose was the cold slaw which was passing.  My husband had the french dip served on the same hamburger bun shaped french roll...he said the beef left a bad taste in his mouth - he thought it might have been meat that was on the shelf  a little too long...he choose the french fries which weren't any better.. 

We stayed at GSR a couple of different times in the last 3 years and were  always happy with our meals ... hopefully dinner will be better ( we usually have dinner  at Briscola and it never disappoints)

This restaurant is a treasure!! The food is amazing and the service is fantastic. Louie is the best! The whole fried snapper is by far the best I have ever had. The crispy duck is also highly recommended. We eat here at least once a week and look forward to doing so. A++++

I used to frequent this place quite often but things have changed. Now they can't seem to keep up with the baked goods. The bagel and bread options are so limited and I can't figure out why they don't keep more in stock. Also many other Paneras offer free bagel deals to reward members and yet never once have I seen this location offering this. Really disappointed in how Panera has fallen. There are much better options in the area if you're looking for high quality food.
It must be hard to make a sandwich correctly as the two times I went, they managed to do something wrong. Typical JJ's processed sandwich. Not sure why I expect more...

Well I highly recommend the specialists & hospital itself at St Lukes Boise... the ER was ok... given the ongoing COVID-19 situation... I may recommend the St Lukes Meridian ER over downtown... just because there is no valet parking & parking is quite odd ... whereas the Meridian location u may still walk but it's obvious where everything is (even at night) where's at downtown Boise ER I was quite confused... I had arrived by ambulance, but went hm via Uber... as very difficult to figure out where the driver could come get me... not fun when u r really cold, already disoriented & bonus - just in you pjs! But again I highly recommend downtown for everything else... procedures, hospital stays, & gift shop

They're reopened as of 10/27/16!!

Amazing place! Best bang for your burrito buck!! The chorizo burrito is to die for!!

As much as I hate to say it, this place didn't live up to the hype.  The toasted ravioli was meaty and hearty.  However, the roast beef sandwich on cheese garlic bread was soggy and disappointing.  It's mean more for takeout than sit-in, and there are benches to sit on outside.

Overall, good meal but nothing mind blowing.
Walls are way too thin. I heard someone next door snoring all night. It was disgusting. Avoid if you feel like having some privacy.

First time customer last weekend, loved it. Staff was super helpful and friendly, made good menu suggestions.  Prices are fair. Food was YUMMY. We had a hot pastrami and a chicken salad. The chicken salad was on the dry side, but not bad.  The place is cute and clean and they had our order ready very quickly.

So yummy! First time visiting this place, it's so cute on the outside & the inside is much cuter! The menu is awesome... They do a special buy a meal and take one home for later! It was awesome leftovers! The lasagna and ravioli were both great! 

Drinks are really pricey but yummy! 
Great service

The service at this restaurant is horrible the waitstaff is more concerned about getting back to their back booth and talking to each other than waiting on you or getting you what you need. I've had multiple waitresses give me a disgusting look or be put off by the fact that I ask for another glass of water The food is mediocre at best very Americanized Mexican but the service just totally takes away from the whole experience I'd be surprised if this place is in business a year from now.  The manager and or owner stand at the bar and get people dirty looks continuously I don't understand why they're in this business when they can't even conceptualize what customer service is.

First things first - if I could live in a museum, I totally would.

This museum campus is one of the BEST museum experiences I've ever had. If you know me, you know that's high praise indeed. The "museum" is actually a series of galleries, spread out across several buildings on the museum campus. 

I absolutely loved learning more about the campaigns leading up to WWII. The way that the curators have really humanized the story was truly impressive. WWII is much more than a chapter or two in history books, and the museum really embodies that. 

The staff were friendly and helpful, and extremely welcoming. It was touching to see veterans and their families visiting the exhibits and reliving some of their memories, good and bad, from that time. 

Thank you to the staff, volunteers, and donors for providing this awesome experience to the world.

This "neighborhood market" is a sham! The prices are dictated by whomever happens to be cashiering at the time. For a $8.99 item I've paid as much as 10.99 and that's for a single item. I'm from LA and my local Korean markets don't rob you that bad. Not to mention, they sell crack pipes. This is supposed to be a somewhat exclusive part of this town. It's sad that they think it's worth it to rob loyal customers.

We made the mistake of ordering from here on Grubhub and were seriously disappointed. I got a poke bowl and ordered two servings of salmon, one serving of scallops and one of crab. I basically got two servings of scallops and crab and barely any salmon, and the scallops and salmon were flavorless and watery. Really gross. There was also barely any rice in the bowl which was fine because the rice was overcooked. It was so dry and literally crunchy at times.
 
We understand that ramen isn't going to be as good as places in the Bay Area, but my BF got the to Shoyu Tonkotsu Ramen to try it out (we know it's best when it's fresh, but covid). The ramen was just as bad as the poke bowl though. The pork was overcooked and the broth was basically over seasoned and tasted like they just put a chunk of garlic and some pepper in there. 

The only reason I'm not rating it one star is because we got takeout, so it may be better when eating at the actual restaurant. I doubt we'll give it another try though as this was a pretty big waste of money and I'm kinda salty about it.

Let me first start of saying that Mulate's has the potential to be, and may have been, a wonderful place to visit. It was pretty empty when I went, but it was also Halloween, so people were busy on Bourbon St. 

Food: Definitely a disappointment. From what I hear this is not the norm, so I won't hold it against them. It is to my understanding that lots of people called out, so I understand how that goes.

Ambience: If you like midweek true New Orleans Zydeco (sp?) live music, this was the place to be. It is clean and open so I have no complaints about that. I enjoyed the southern feel, and it is spacious. 

Waiter: My waiter Jesse was amazing. I think overall (and I'll address this in a sec), the staff was lovely. Truly lovely.

Here is what knocked this restaurant down to a solid 1 for me. 

Imagine sitting at your table, and witnessing a male person of authority (Tim the Baldheaded Manager---his name really is Tim and he really is baldheaded.), walking out of nowhere and waving his fingers and hands in a young woman's face, who is working hard putting chairs on tables, as they close. At the same time, he is COMPLETELY dismissing her questions and walking right past her...hands flailing in her face. It really looked like a scene from the show "Pose" where one of the main characters walked by someone giving them all the shade. But this was real life. This was a grown man of authority, publicly embarrassing a young woman who was clearly doing her job. He gave her no respect. NONE!!!!  She was obviously afraid to speak up for herself, so he continued and she kept quiet.

Yes...I witnessed this...and I was HEATED!!!!!

The placed was closing, and she seemed to be doing her job putting the chairs on the tables. He apparently wasn't ready for that and completely embarrassed her with his hand gestures and dismissive attitude not even giving her the respect of telling her what she did wrong and how to correct it. He was being a COMPLETE ASS!!! I don't know their history but I own a business, and wouldn't think to do anything like that, let alone in front of customers. 

I was so pissed off witnessing this, that I expressed my feeling to another manager who seemed a bit more respectful and professional. I told him  that not only should that never happen to an employee...let alone a female employee having a male's superior's hands in her face...I shouldn't have seen it.   This manager (not Tim) said he would address it, but I really don't think it will be addressed appropriately.  I do appreciate his concern and understanding of my complaint, though.

IN NO WAY...IN NO ESTABLISHMENT...IN NO PROFESSION...SHOULD A MANAGER DEMEAN A SUBBORDINATE BY PUTTING HIS HANDS IN THE AIR AND IN HER/HIS FACE, WITHOUT EVEN GIVING HER THE RESPECT OF ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OR AN ANSWER TO WHAT SHE'S DOING WRONG AND HOW TO FIX IT!!  HE SHOULDN'T INTIMIDATE HER WITH HIS HANDS AT ALL!!

I will never refer or support this business again, as long as he is employed there. That is how embarrassed and pissed I felt for the treatment of this young woman. 

I believe her name was Donna and she was helpful, and sweet as pie. 

Yeah, this place is a no for me as long as he works there. Plus, the food wasn't great either. But again...given the day, I can give the food a pass.

Tim, on the other hand gets no pass.

Anyone who cares about the respect of women and/or minorities in a workplace should not support this location.  Anyone who believes that employees of all rank should be respected the same, should not support this business.  Please, inquire within if you have any questions. I stand by my word on this. 

Takeaways: 
Hosts, waitstaff, band, and other manager were all wonderful.  It was clean had a nice feel until....

Cons: 
Tim the Baldheaded Manager was a big enough jerk to make this place a solid no for me or anyone with any sort of respect for another human being.
Was a really fun experience. Got to bring our own booze and the activity was light and easy.

Just left this restaurant and I am so not impressed - The food came out Luke warm & not hot-This is really a deal breaker for me I think everyone should get hot food at a restaurant - The bread pudding was good and that was our highlight - They do have a love band every night so that was nice but I would not recommend this place to anyone sorry - worse restaurant we tried all of our time in new orleans:(

We found this little gem when we didn't feel like waiting for 45 minutes at Kobe's. This is a clean, relaxing atmosphere with friendly servers and staff. Service was very prompt and food was fresh!!! This is not a buffet where you load your plates with food that's been sitting under heaters for hours. They bring you your orders in very reasonable portions. The sushi is delicious. The kids enjoyed it as well. They loved the green tea ice cream. My daughter found her love for vegetable spring rolls here. Family friendly place. Look for their coupons in mailers!
Holy hole in the wall, Batman. This was a Yelp find and it did not disappoint. Service was great and the food and beer were yummy.

Easily the best Chinese food in the area. Everything is hot and delicious. The staff are all kind and attentive plus throughout Covid they were the always fast to get the food out for curbside. You can get dumplings, fried rice, and lo mein at every Chinese place but the quality at Hong Luck is above and beyond!

Refinanced a mortgage and they physically did that so that is where they get 2 stars. I've been through 3 mortgages before so I know how the process works at other companies. While I didn't need hand holding, I just felt that things seemed chaotic here. Questions were not answered, we were given conflicting information, we were told to do one thing but then asked to do it again and/or told that we didn't need to do it at all. We had a closing date scheduled only to find out it wasn't really scheduled. We worked with a revolving door of people but still struggled to get answers to our questions. I just felt considering we were a fairly easy loan customer with no changes throughout the process and no rushed timeline, it felt very chaotic on their end. Today, 3 months after closing, we got an email asking us to sign a document they forgot to have us sign, even though I am pretty sure I remember seeing it. It inspired me to write this review. I would not use them again.

If I can give this place zero stars I would. I drive by this place every day and finally decided to give it a try with my family for my son's birthday. Total disappointment from the minute we walked in. I expected a fun fifties style diner atmosphere with cool oldies music. What we experienced was a filthy smelly, loud chucky cheese. We walked into a line of people who were waiting for takeout food. Although we were seated right away. It took 15 minutes for the server to come take our order, another 15 minutes for the drinks to come, and after waiting an additional 40 minutes for our food that never came, we decided to leave. I went up to the counter and told the young girl that we been waiting 40 minutes for our food and are just paying for our drinks. I even showed her the timer. She went and got the manager and although she apologized, I blame her for doing a poor job at managing and maintaining a clean place to eat. I'm glad we didn't get to eat cause god knows we would've been disappointed again with the food. She gave me a $25 gift card that I will not use. I would never recommend this place to anyone. Department of health should stop by unexpectedly and shut this dump down.

I bring my husband work clothes here. They clean and press them. I think the price is average for our area. They are efficient and have a fast turn around.

Drop them off dirty pick them up clean. They are kind and friendly plus the location is close to where I live.

Well I give the one star for their piss poor customer service. 
I walked in to ask if they could do a cake they were not sure if what I was asking for was licensed, they told me they would get back with me and never did. Today may 13th is the day I wanted the cake and still have not received an answer. No worries though went to another place.
While she was talking to us, one of the workers came over with a phone call. She said Just take her name and number I will call her later. The worker replied with she has called 3 times today. Her response was I don't care I am not calling her back today. If they don't call you back go else because the lady running the shop has a piss poor attitude. 
There are better places with better cakes and better customer service.

For years, this has been my favorite coffee shop in Tucson. It's hard to beat its beautiful, quiet outdoor seating area with old jazz swinging in the background. A wonderful place to sit and chat with friends or to sit quietly and write. Definitely check it out!

MY favorite Indian restaurant hands down! Amazing buffet! Best service. So fresh and buffet is consistently replenished. Quality meat and best salad I've ever had on a buffet! I dream about the food here lol

Yes it is fast and close to most of the downtown hotels which are holding conferences, but the sandwich I got probably could have come from a quick-stop rather than a "deli".  The deli meat tasted like packaged oscar meyers or something like that and the cheese was about the same quality.  It actually was really hard to eat but I needed something and this was what I stuck with.  I don't know...a person in my group said "Hey, this isn't the west coast" when I asked if they had sprouts.  Nope...no sprouts.  That pretty much sums it up.  Quick, not good, and hard to eat.

I love these owners/staff! Whether it's traditional Thai or  Sushi, we have never been disappointed in our order. We've been taking our food to go since March but now have gone into the restaurant for dinner since they have CDC guidelines perfectly in place. 

The decorating is understated and COOL and the soft jazz playing in the background completes a perfect time there. 

Again, everything on the menu is DELICIOUS and we drive across town to go to Kao Thai, as nothing close by compares.

Breakfast is my go-to meal of choice for any time of the day. I was really excited that they opened up an IHOP nearby but maybe I was blinded by the excitement of yummy pancakes.

Out of the 4 times or so we've eaten here my order has been right one time. I've gotten attitude from the servers  2 of those times and since we've been seated by kitchen on more than one occasion heard the people in there arguing about it. If my eggs don't have cheese on them am I not supposed to notice? It sure shows up on the bill whether it's there or not. Also, if you're lucky the food might be warm when you get it.

It looks like I'll be skipping out on these pancakes for a while even if it's in the neighborhood.
STINGY STINGY STINGY!!!!! I hate this location .....period. The manager is rude, and half the staff has an unpleasant disposition a majority of times.

A great experience. Upon entering, I was immediately seated. As I was dining alone, I preferred a table that was out of the main traffic. It was as if the hostess read my mind and offered me the choice of 2 tables at the rear of the restaurant.
Mikayla(unsure about the spelling) was professional. Without being intrusive, she was there whenever I needed her. The best service that I've had in a while.
The food was just as I ordered. My ribeye was a perfect medium and evenly spiced. The baked potato was dressed just I requested.
Thanks for a satisfying dining experience.

If a Starbucks has a drive thru avoid it like the plague. It means that it is really busy they care less and it's just not a great experience. 
This particular location was once a great spot to have a conversation with the Barista when it was at the old spot. Now it's just person in coffee out. 
I also think the quality has gone down given the higher throughput. Try the Havertown or Newtown Square locations they are much better.

I have had work done here in the past and never had any issues. I usually get our oul changes at the cheap place a block south for 10 dollars less. This weekend I needed to get 2 of the 3 cars oul changed. I took the first one down the street and they not only ripped us off, but they lied on the checklist,  didn't check some items they said they did. So I was done, I brought the other care here and for the 10 dollars more you get real professional service.  The whole time they make you feel like the customer.  Imagine that in this day a company makes you feel like an appreciated customer. They also checked the things they said they checked...I watch closely.  They are far superior to that other operation and well worth the money.

I needed something to read for an upcoming vacation, so I stopped by Sundance yesterday afternoon. This is a great local store that carries a nice variety of books. I appreciated the suggested books section downstairs and the used section upstairs.  Pricier than big box or online, of course.

Delicious crepes and coffee! It's the perfect quaint coffee place for your week and weekend getaways!

I had the Fresh Fruit Crepe with yogurt mousse, strawberries, blueberries & basil. Absolutely delicious, I get hungry just thinking about it!!

Simply known as the best Po Boy in New Orleans, Mahoney's is tucked away in a residential neighborhood on trendy magazine street. 

So on my way to the airport, I had to hit this place. 

Ordered the signature oyster Po Boy "dressed" with fries. 

Dressed is lettuce, tomatoes, mayo 

Savored each byte of this $25 sandwich. 

It was crunchy, warm and delicious. 

Make the pilgrimage. You won't be sorry.

Yes! Check this place out! Delicious breakfast at a reasonable price! Eggs and bacon, omelets, biscuits  and gravy, it's all good. Service is awesome! Not much parking but understandable on a Saturday. Will return and definitely reccomend.

I had the Truckee Trainwreck today because I saw it on DDD.  It was a little over the top for me.  I usually eat about 6 hamburgers a year and when I splurge I usually go for a cheeseburger with onions and lettuce.  The Trainwreck beat me down!

I could have done without the chili and maybe the egg.  Regardless, I loved it.  We also shared a basket of sweet potato fries.  It was huge and enough for three to four to share.

Next time I will get a cheeseburger and keep it simple because the meat is tasty and great quality.

My friends and I came here for a somewhat early dinner (around 5PM). I was expecting this place to be packed since there were so many good reviews on Yelp, but I was wrong. When we stepped in, there was only 3 other parties. We were seated immediately upon our arrival. Their menu is pretty extensive from appetizers, dumplings, noodle, soup, and rice dishes. We shared the scallion pancake as an appetizer and I had the Shaved Noodles with Pork Soy Sauce per my friend's recommendation. The scallion pancake was delicious! They were flakey and crispy. Loved how it wasn't too greasy and the sauce that came with it. The shaved noodles with pork soy sauce was a HUGE portion. Maybe I have a small appetite, but I thought this dish can be shared between two people. For $6.50, it's worth the price! The shaved noodles are known as the wider/flatter noodles. They gave a lot of noodles. A lot more than what I'm used to in California. This noodle dish isn't dry nor is it soupy. Instead, the noodles are mixed very well with the pork soy sauce. The sauce is very well seasoned and flavorful. I wasn't too thirsty after eating it. If I was in the area again, I would definitely be back to have more of that scallion pancake as well as their other noodle dishes. Prices are very reasonable for their portion size. If you do plan to stop by, make sure to bring cash!

This hasn't always been a national park. Until you've walked the grounds, you really don't have a clear understanding of how gorgeous the entire property is. Actually until you've experienced the arch up close and personal, we don't have a clear understanding of it. Most people who haven't been to it, don't even know that you can ride up in a tram/pod to the top. I wouldn't recommend it if you're scared of small spaces, or heights. 

It's actually a bit overpriced to go up into the arch, if you ask me. It's a great "once in a lifetime experience" splurge. 

There is a cafe, and a free museum. You can literally spend all day here. Who knew?! Now you do.
Non-asian guy making sushi............ waitress with attitude... found a better place - HOT TUNA!!

Been here a few times and love it, the pizza I have tried has artichokes, ham and black olives with a thin crust, so tasty. I also tried the tiramisu and it was the best I have ever tasted, the coffee drinks are really good too, service is great! Definitely check this place out :)

Stopped by, completely unplanned, sat at the bar, had a delicious bottle of wine and watched them make the pizzas... so of course had to try those, and they were so good! reminded me of the pizzas I used to get in Europe! --Then we saw dessert pizzas... with Nutella!!!! and that was the bomb too, though a bit too much food in one meal! I will definitely go back for more.

A southern style kitchen owned by two chefs that were on Top Chef! 

Cannot say enough good things about the Deviled Eggs. I am not a fan of a hard boiled egg at all..but my fiance insists and I can usually try a deviled egg since they have more blended flavors. For these it was me who was raving up and down and seriously contemplated ordering a second order. 
We also ordered the watermelon and pickles side for an appetizer. They brought us the watermelon salad on accident but we didn't notice at first so started eating it. It was quite tasty with the fresh watermelon! 

I ordered the fish & scampi. The fish of the day was Rock Fish which is a flaky mild white fish. The sauce for the scampi was good and the fish was cooked well. 

Shrimp and Grits were bangin! The roux that was used was seasoned AMAZING and I was a bit jealous this wasn't smothered all over my meal too. 

Will be back to try the fried chicken next time!

Yim's is one of the best Asian supermarkets I've been to! The place isn't big but it isn't small either. It is clean and well organized. The workers are always so sweet and helpful. One huge plus is that it doesn't reek of fish like most Asian markets.

They have an abundance of products from various Asian countries like Korea, Japan, and China to name a few. This is definitely the place to come to for all your Asian cooking/snacking needs.

Despite almost everything being imported, the prices are still super affordable. You get quality and quantity. There are a lot of bulk items like thai tea, ramen, sauces, etc. You can find fresh produce along with Asian household items.

Their frozen section includes a variety of things like gyoza, rice cakes, meats, soups, and so much more. If you want to have hotpot or kbbq at home, they have everything you need. 

They have a fridge specially for banchan. You can find things like kimchi, fish cakes, papaya salad, anchovies, and a lot more. They also have kimbap and japchae that's made daily. Everything is always fresh and tasty. 

They also have a huge ramen selection. I always come here to pick up my favorite samyang ramen. They have most of the flavors like carbo, cheese, curry, 2x, and of course original. You might recognize those noodles from the spicy noodle challenge. They seem to be the only place in town to carry them. They also have a bunch of goodies like green tea kit-kats and some other flavors I've never seen before!

Even though Yim's is a bit far from me, it is always worth the drive!

Good selection. Good prices. But don't count on them to provide service. I encountered a slacker here...

So I came in to the store on February 25th and my shoe size wasn't there. The store worker told me to come in the following day, since they MIGHT arrive on their truck.

(WTF?)

I said "Why don't you just take my phone number and call me when they come in?"

He gets out a piece of scrap paper.

I said "Do you need the model number of those shoes?" 

No, he said.

"Okay... You're going to call?"

Yes, he said.

"I appreciate that. It will save me a lot of time."

Well, of course the call never came. I returned to the store on March 30th. And there were my shoes, on the shelf, in my size.

I hate lousy service. Nobody couldn't take 30 seconds to give me a call.

So if you ask them to do you a small favor, don't count on them to get the job done.

I'm reading some of these reviews and I guess I came here on a good day or something because I had nothing but good service. 

I came here a bit ago for my date, and I asked if I could wait a little while until he showed up, and they said it was fine. (Most places make you pay for something in order to just stand in their location.) After waiting, and he showed up, we immediately ordered our drinks and something to snack on. They lady at the register was nothing but nice, and sweet. Our drinks were not messed up and we only waited about 8 minutes, it was a but busy, so maybe about 10 minutes. 

And this is such a convenient location since it is right close to where I work.

Had a lovely lunch with my Mom here. The outward appearance of this place isn't special as it is in a strip mall, but the food is good.  
I shared fried eggplant appetizer & had lasagna with a small house salad.  All were good!
Good service & interior atmosphere with a continuous track of classic Italian music always playing. Convenient with lots of parking.  Would recommend & will return.

Came down for my first trip to Nashville. This hotel was nothing but unpleasant. It's completely ran down. We got to our room and it wasn't even the room we booked. When we went to pull the the bed out on the couch it was broke. Called the front desk they yelled at us and told us we were in the wrong for wanting a new room and not a roll away bed that they offered. I walked down to the front desk and asked why we can't have a new room and proceed to yell while I told him to lower his voice. Both front desk were yelling at me while I was standing there confused on why i couldn't even just move rooms. He kicked us out and said he'll call the cops. Simply cause I told him his services he promise was broken. He gave us a refund but that's only when I demanded I signed something and had proof of a refund. If not he was just going to have me turn in my keys and leave. But I demanded to sign something and get my receipt. If I could give this a hotel a negative star I would. And would ever recommend anyone staying there.

I recently went to Good Time Charlie's for the first time. The Karaoke bar is what drew us here. Unfortunately, it was not working all night, but that was the only disappointment for the night. For the few minutes it was working, the singers were good and the dancing was fun!

The bar is a lot of fun if you can handle to smell of smoke inside. The drinks were affordable and the service was friendly and fast. Smirnoff drinks were $3.50 all night. The bar food was also decent and affordable. We shared a pepperoni pizza and chicken fingers. Both were good and exactly what we needed after a few drinks. 

I would like to return again in the future, especially since we missed out on karaoke.

I ordered the German style sausages with spiced apples and cheddar as an omelet/scramble instead of a crepe.  Those sweet spiced apples mixed with salty sausage and cheese is one of the best things I've ever tasted.

This was a terrific place to stay. Far enough away from the crowds but within a couple of blocks of everything downtown. The bar on the 3rd floor permitted underage kids with adults which was a welcomed asset when traveling with children and needing food (and live music) after 8. It is almost impossible to find food and live music venues with children on the 5th and Broadway downtown area. A special shout out for providing me with a free laptop power cord. I was in a panic for leaving mine at home, the kiosk at the airport didn't carry anything, and I had 4 days without power and no place around to purchase one. I guess they are left behind in rooms frequently and they saved me!

I got there to order a pizza and I was never acknowledged after 5 to 6 minutes somebody came along and asked me for my order, then after taking my order I paid and was not given a receipt ass all the transaction was done cash, register was never used. After my pizza was done I was sitting waiting for it and I was never call until one of the girls came up to me and asked me if I was waiting and what my name was, my pizza I've been sitting there for like six minutes. I had never had any issues with Dominos, this is the worst Dominos in the United States as far as customer service
Friendly service.  Good food, if perhaps a bit expensive for what you get.  Recommended.

Sometimes all it takes is one visit to a business... one visit and you just know you're a fan.

This is how it was for me at Beyond Bread... 
One visit and I'm sold!

Friend and fellow yelper Allyson B (actually Allyson L but she hasn't changed it on Yelp yet), suggested Beyond Bread when I told her I was looking for a lunch spot during my visit to Tucson on Friday.  I used my handy dandy Yelp app to find my way to this location, and it was love at first bite... first bite into the extremely hearty and fresh multi-grain bread that I requested with my custom salad.  Basically, it was the Avi's Avo sandwich transformed into a salad with multi-grain bread slices on the side, and boy did it hit the spot!  Fresh turkey, avocado, lettuce, tomato, cucumber... healthy, delicious, and filling - just the way i like it!

I admired the selection of sweet treats in the pastry case but I decided to wait until next visit to splurge on one of those.  I also noticed their catering menu, specifically the pretzel bread sandwich that feeds an entire party... I wonder if they deliver to Phoenix?  Or perhaps I'll have to plan a party in Tucson just to have an excuse to order one of those!

The food was delicious and their COVID protocol is compliant. We felt very safe on the side patio in a perimeter booth. With tapas in mind, we ordered entirely too much food but the upside was that we sampled many dishes and had leftovers for future meals. The addition of grilled pineapple bits to guacamole was unique and fabulous. The pineapple touch carried over into the the very fresh kampachi ceviche. Our other favorites were the tacos de langosta, Panuchos de Cochinita Pibil (very tender & juicy pork, not overcooked; tortillas were not crunchy like a tortilla chip),  and duck taquitos.   The margarita sayulita with mezcal was a fantastic combination of smokey and not too sweet fruity mango pinapple. Really special meal and we will be back.

Fresh ingredients & delicious taste! Large vegetarian menu, and very friendly staff.
The Gobi Manchurian cauliflower is better than chicken!

I came in at 9 , they are short handed, hour wait, so I sat at bar/Courtney and servers,, great service, steak & shrimp AMAZING, as well as their signature sauce and casino butter!....such flavor and the meat was a great cut!
I had my meal in ten minutes! Love this place!

The sushi was good and my not so positive review has more to do with the bad experience after we left. One of the rolls that I had, had fish bones in one of the rolls which was strange but whatever. 

My issue is this: we went with two other people and before leaving we left our tip on the table. After we part our ways my friend gets a call from the RESTAURANT on a blocked number saying that we did not leave a tip!! What restaurant would ever call a customer over this? Either someone stole the tip or the money fell off the table but this really offended me. That's not okay to call a customer over a tip. My friend actually went back and gave them another tip. This is embarrassing to both us and on the restaurant. Not cool and won't go back here.

Simply amazing! It is hard to find great vegetarian or vegan food but Blue Island did not disappoint. I had the curry tofu with rice and peas, plantains  and cabbage. The tofu was cooked to perfection and the veggies were nice and tender. You could tell the chef took his time with it.
Pretty tasty food.  But this is sort of the Disney Land version of a Caribbean joint.
Best donuts, coffee, Boba tea and employees anyone could want! Pana is amazing and I love supporting them

Larry was one of the biggest reasons our wedding was so amazing. He both officiated our ceremony & DJ'd the reception. When it came to the ceremony, he was so specific on finding out exactly what we wanted and he helped guide us in what to do. It was almost unreal how perfect it went. The reception was wonderful too, the music was fantastic, he also did great in helping guide the entire night for our guests. We had a couple fun interactive games which helped lighten up the crowd, after that almost everyone was out on the dance floor. We received so many compliments from our guests how great he did and of course, we thought the same. Thank you SO much Larry for your amazing work. Anyone would be so lucky to have you officiate or DJ their wedding!

This spot is the most friendly and professional Blaze restaurant that I've ever been too. When I go in I'm welcomed with smiles and a hello followed by my name. Yes! They remembered my name. There's an employee there named "Lil Terri"and she's always smiling, friendly and professional. Someone better hurry up and make her employee of the month or manager. But again, I love visiting this establishment! Great employees!!!

Food is good but be prepared to wait... 

We've eaten here twice, once for dinner and once for lunch. Both times the food took a long time to come out and the place wasn't even full of customers. For dinner I don't mind waiting a little while but for lunch, waiting 20 plus minutes for a sandwich won't cut it.

The sandwich was good but the log wait just soured the taste a bit. I'd consider takeout but pass on the dine in experience unless you're not real hungry and don't have anything else to do.

I went to this place originally because it came up in the "ramen" list on my Yelp search. Unfortunately, they no longer serve ramen. However, everything else on their menu was really good. It is as other reviewers have called "no frills" regarding the menu, but it is also reasonably priced, the service is fast, and everything we got was really well done. They also have a free pineapple dessert at the end (a pineapple carved to look like a fish). I definitely plan to go back!

Great company. Very professional, knowledgeable. Replaced my screen on my Mac and gave it a tune up.

HIghly recommend them.

Ate a sumptuous dinner here with another couple. Since it is BYOB (score!) we brought wine and beer. We ordered Crispy Roll app, Masaman Curry, Thai Green Curry, Pad Thai, Evil Jungle Princess (the latter on the recommendation of the waitress), and didn't regret it.
Having been to Thailand, I can say that the quality, spices and spiciness, and the taste of the entrees was very close to authentic.

Our server was attentive, knowledgeable, and friendly. We didn't have any trouble getting a table and appreciated the decor, pace of service, and great location smack in the center of Media.

Just visited the new, reopened Yoshimatsu and had my best meal there yet!  I really loved the funky vibe of the old space, but the new spot is cozy, efficiently laid out, and retains the Yoshimatsu quirkiness. There are still cute Japanese gifts to buy, kitschy plastic food in a display case, the vintage Japanese toys are on the wall by the bathroom now, and even the toilets have been handpainted with cute designs.

The menu feels more focused but it's still ample. They have a dedicated gluten free menu now, as well. They've added a few new things like skewers, "dry" ramen bowls, and lots of fresh, unique dessert and juice options. 

I ordered the hamachi kama bento (did they have this before?) and it was a thing of beauty. Hamachi kama is the cheek of the hamachi tuna. Cooked to perfection, it was juicy, buttery, tender and crunchy on the edges. The sushi is ample and rolls came with 8 or 9 giant pieces.

There is still a sushi bar and a regular bar (like with drinks) you can sit at. They have a little table with complimentary tea, clementines, and Japanese crackers. Service was great and the hostess (owner?) came around to check on everyone. Just an overall, homey, happy dining experience!

Update: This has place has gone way down hill. They're service has been consistently bad and they have changed the ingredients on some of their dishes and it didn't work out in the dish's favor. Go back to the way things were with great food and crappy service only sometimes.


Old Review:
I give this place 4 stars because their food is amazing. 

It doesn't get 5 stars because their service is hit or miss sometimes, mostly good though. 

I also tried to get the Pad Thai with beef one time and they charge me an arbitrary $4 extra. Beef is cheaper than shrimp, come on guys!

A-

Recommendations:
Make beef pad thai an option.
Pasta is a little hard. Duke over cook. But oyster taste good. Maybe will try it again. Not like the mean picture showed on the website.

I ordered crispy egg rolls and pad thai with beef. I received a very small serving of beef with my pad thai which was sad but it was full of tofu. The overall pad thai was good and was a nice large serving (enough for the next day). Nothing to rave about though. The egg rolls had a pretty good sauce served with it but the roll itself was a little burnt. 

Service was pretty slow, in my opinion, based on the fact that I was the only person in there. After a while there were other guests, several people who were dining alone. It's sort of low key like that. The decor was clean and modern. I would return based on their proximity to my place but I wouldn't be a regular or anything.

This restaurant always remind me of the first time I met my fiancé. I met him at the Mad Mex at Penn State but every time we go here we talk about when we first met. Staff here are awesome and they have outdoor seating area. I had one experience where it was really slow but other than that it seems fine. The time we had slow service, the place was not even packed.

Great food!  Fun atmosphere.  Excellent service.  An all around win!

If you try nothing else...try the duck egg beignets with butterscotch sauce!

Mmmmmm I think I may need some more!

If they don't want to scissor cut they should say so. Told by male employee he would do my dog then a woman came out and said a buzz clip was what she was told to do. If his undercoat does not come back I will be very angry!
Great service, breakfast was amazing! Little wait but it was well worth it. Definitely will be back.

Awesome place! Very clean and friendly. I had a man do my "basic pedi" which I must add was much more involved than most basic pedicures I've had. The guy was awesome! Very thorough and made sure to take his time. I didn't feel rushed or that they were in a hurry to get me in and out. $25 for the basic pedi which included a hot stone massage! I'll definitely be back! This place is awesome, affordable, and close to my house ;)

Is this authentic Chinese food?--no. 

Is this your standard Americanized "hole in the wall" Chinese food?--yes.

Is this better than the typical Americanized "hole in the wall" Chinese food you can find in every town in America?--you betcha.

My family & I have been Happy Dragon customers for years. They do your standard Americanized Chinese food, except their quality & flavor is always consistent and delicious. You also get a TON of food--when I order dinner, 1 entree easily has leftovers for 1-2 lunches.

Another perk--they will deliver within the Plainfield area.

We went just last weekend for the first time to hear a band and do a little dancing. The place was quite crowded on the Friday night that we went, but there was ample parking. A couple of doormen explained that it was the 100th show of a local Reno band and they were accepting "donations". Although ultimately it was a cover charge, we didn't at all feel obligated to pay it. We'd enjoyed the band before and considered it $5 well spent.The bar is quite unique looking as it's in a remodeled farm building of some sort, It's set back off Virginia Street a ways, directly across from Winco. We don't understand the previous poor review, as we found the bartenders, all three attractive young ladies, to be alert, accomodating and very friendly. The only less than positive thing I could offer, is that the men's room could use a little work. It's outside of the bar "proper" on the left before you enter, and was a bit dingy, but hell, I'm a guy, give me a bush when I have to pee and I'm happy. Besides, the "wall art" brightened up the room.

5 stars! So the decor is very modern and completely updated which belies the little shopping plaza that it's in. I did get the free edamame check in which was great! She also gave us an extra miso soup and too was good. The broth was good with a decent amount of tofu and seaweed. For our entree we had the spicy California, spicy salmon, the tuna avocado roll, and the lucky roll all were wonderfully fresh with great presentation. The service was impeccable as well. I will definitely be back again for all my sushi cravings.

This has become our go-to breakfast spot now hat Zest is gone.  When it comes to brunch there are a few things other than good food that matter to me. Good coffee & an unlikely wait.  I usually get an omelette and my husband usually gets the kitchen sink.  If I had one complaint it would be that their breakfast potatoes are kinda blah.  I usually sub a fruit cup and they have one of the better quality fruit cups among similar brunch places in town.

Went back here last Friday for an eat in dinner. I had the worst quesadillas ever. I ordered Al pastor quesadillas and they came out looking fantastic! Upon tasting, they were so dry, the meat was gristle and the cheese was hard. No flavor whatsoever. I'm so disappointed because it was my go to for Mexican food in that area. Probably won't go back
First visit today and what a find!  The salon is so clean and classy!  Very upscale with affordable pricing!  Family owned too which I love to support!

What can I say?  Monon was a pleasantly surprising visit.  On a weekday night, I purposefully drove to Broad Ripple in search of coffee because Yelp told me they were one of the only coffee shops still open after 6 pm.  They close at 8 pm weekdays, by the way.  Even though I got there at like 7:50 pm the barista was super friendly and told me to take my time deciding.  We made some recommendations and I ended up going with a Sugar-free White Chocolate Breve.  They have several sugar-free syrup options available.  It was a perfect end to my night.  

Monon is definitely a hipster atmosphere!  There isn't a ton of parking available to customers unless there is hidden parking I didn't see, so finding street parking is your best bet.  They have a pretty diverse menu and options, including food and smoothies.  There isn't a ton of seating available to sit and work from a laptop, but in this instance, I was just picking up some java to go.  Free WiFi is available here.

Absolute worst. Went to get a gel. Took 20 min to get it off! Then it was streaky color so it i asked for it to be taken off. That was production...and this cost me $20! The worst customer service experience. i asked to talk to the owner...not there...yeah. Right. 

What? What? No way! Awful. Don't go there!

If I could leave zero stars I would. Have been here twice and both times have been the worst experience going out in Philadelphia. Staff was incredibly rude and kicked my friends and I out because one of THEIR workers, who was there for fun and not work, was pushing us around. We asked security to make him stop and since they knew him, we got asked to leave.

Super yummy food in a really casual but intimate space. I ordered the shaved Brussels sprout salad and the fish of the day - which was drum dusted with cornmeal, with potatoes, make and mushrooms and a beurre blanc. So tasty and a nice, lighter meal in New Orleans.

King Edwards is a nice locally owned fried chicken joint in southern Saint Louis County.  The chicken is battered rather than breaded and is made to order.  I ate here last time I was in the area and enjoyed the wings I ordered.  Beause its battered the chicken is a little messier to eat than normal.  Come dressed appropriately.

You know it must be good when the local police eat here.

Everything on the menu sounded delicious! I opted for the cheeseburger and a loaded baked potato which was tasted great. It was cooked medium to perfection with sharp cheddar cheese, lettuce, tomato, red onions and pickle coins. I would definitely recommend this choice. I look forward to visiting again. The service was also great, with very accommodating staff.

My boyfriend and I recently dined at Il Pittore. Looking over the menu before hand, I was excited for what this restaurant had to offer.  In short, the food ranged from eh-ok to delicious but our server seemed angry that we ever sat down.

Knowing that the portion sizes were smaller and the menu designed to have an appetizer, first course, and second course, we started with the beet salad and baked ricotta, which both were phenomenal.  I absolutely adore beets and this salad is one of the better beet options that I have had in Philadelphia.

For our pasta course we shared the ziti with leeks and breadcrumbs and the ravioli.  Both were tasty but extremely rich; a little too much butter/oil for me.  

The entrees were what missed for me. I ordered the stuffed trout since I don't eat meat and took three bites and was finished.  My boyfriend loved his scallops and finished my fish, but, for me, it was not a hit.  We also had a side of brussel sprouts, which were delicious. 

Unfortunately, our server seemed upset at our age from the moment we sat down; as you can see from above we ordered seven courses, four cocktails (delicious), two coffees, and dessert, so we are not cheap youngsters.  We both have worked in the industry and don't enjoy being treated poorly.  The support staff were excellent; kind, attentive, and accommodating.  I don't believe that we will be returning. Overall we spent close to $250 and didn't have the best time.
Crispy flounder was done just perfectly and paired well with jasmine rice and asparagus. Service excellent. Couldn't give it a five due to noise level. No way to carry on a conversation.

Ate lunch here with my 7 yr old. Great kid food options as well as salads and rice bowls. I had the veggie rice bowl with mojo flavoring. Really good especially for the price. Friendly staff. Clean and comfortable.

I don't go to doughnut places that often, but when I do, it's Doughboys.
What sets this place from others is the quality and cleanliness.
Not to mention, the people employed at both locations are cordial and friendly!

The first thing you notice when you walk in is the cleanliness of this location. Followed by a big screen TV. At the time of my visit, Broncos were leading. 
Their doughnuts are fresh and displayed nicely in a case that is up front so you can see those sugar coated gems up close and personal!

I was surprised this time at how many doughnuts were in the case. Perhaps it was because people buy them Sunday night for work on Monday. Perhaps it was because the Superbowl was going on. (Broncos still leading)
Regardless, there was a huge selection of those tasty morsels. 

I selected one of my favorites, a maple bar. 
What I like about Doughboys is their doughnuts are lighter and fluffier than most other doughnut houses. From the taste of the doughnut, they change their oil on a regular basis. You don't get that greasy heaviness that some other places have. In addition, the recipe they use is naturally lighter. 
All in all, their doughnuts taste great!

---------------------------
Bottom Line

Yes, this place in Reno is my number place of choice for doughnuts.
I would highly recommend that you stop at this, or their southern Reno location for your office party/snack wants and needs. 
(Broncos won)
The food was great as always but the service was really bad, the waitress was super rude and you could tell she was in a very bad mood! Not a good experience :(

One of the nicer airport restaurants/wine bars I have seen. The roasted chicken breast salad in pine nuts, dried cranberries, and herbed vinaigrette was very good. The veggies were fresh and the chicken was actually chicken (not fake airport chicken). Equally impressive is the relaxed, open air atmosphere despite being located right by the security gate. Of note, I respect any restaurant that displays the calorie, fat, and sodium composition of each option although the sodium content of most options here made me think several times before I sat down.

I think Noorish is very hyped up. I love vegan and vegetarian food, but I didn't find the food at Noorish to be very flavourful. It felt like they combined spices and flavours that don't really work together. The kale chips with cheese, for example, were awful. The cheese had a very odd spice mix added to it.

I think people come here because they like the holistic, sort of "hippy" atmosphere. It's a cool place with interesting people, but for people who just want to eat something good, I wouldn't recommend.

Customer service has always been excellent. We've been three times now. The burgers are similar to Steak n Shake but bigger and a little better quality. I highly suggest splitting a small fry if you're with someone. They give you A LOT of fries. The menu is pricey but the food is worth it in my opinion.

Probably some of the best tacos you can get on this side of town. Long time customer of the original location. Glad they opened one up on the north side.  Don't pay any mind to the negative comments on here, they're probably upset this isn't another froyo shop.  The tacos are greasy and juicy and made with real tortillas so eat them quickly or you'll have a mess. If you don't like it, go back to your tofu wraps from Whole Foods. Definitely will be coming back to this place. Recommend the Apson taco with a caramelo and a medium horchata.

If I could give negative stars I would. This place has been an absolute nightmare from the day I moved in. Do NOT fall for the shiny pretty pictures, the prices, or even the model unit. They are complete lies.
The Good: I wish I could come up with something but I really can't.
The Bad: Management is terrible. They lie, they are extremely unprofessional in how they speak to you...if they even ever answer your millions of phone calls or emails asking questions. I placed maintenance requests over a month ago and not a single one has been completed. Instead, they closed out the requests saying they had been completed when in reality they hadn't been. Good luck trying to get a hold of maintenance either. They'll never come. And management won't do anything about it.
The Ugly: I moved into this unit about a month ago and they didn't clean the unit before move-in. Rather, they lied and said the unit was inspected earlier that day. If that's so, then tell me how there was a giant spider web with a live black spider (pretty sure poisonous) spanning across the doorframe? There was dust everywhere, the ducts were filled with dust, the ground was covered in dirt. Not to mention the dead bugs everywhere. It looked like the place was abandoned for years. And the stench of cigarette smoke was overwhelming. The kitchen was falling apart essentially (garbage disposal broken, stovetop burners broken, live cut wires coming from the stove vent, stained and dented fridge that was dirty inside). The walls are painted an ugly grey color over a very crappy thick plaster. The doors were falling off their hinges. The floors were uneven and creaky. The fans squeak. You can hear the neighbors yelling and fighting in the middle of the night every night. The carpets are an ugly brown color so they can hide the bugs that are constantly crawling everywhere no matter how much insecticide you spray. They painted the drawers and doors and you can see actual hair that was painted over. Absolutely disgusting. And they refuse to let you see the unit before move-in (probably because they know if you actually saw the unit you'd run in the other direction). Needless to say, I am breaking my lease after just one month here (honestly, one month too many). Other apartments I have spoken to have heard of this place and they all say the same thing: to stay away as far as you can.

Pro TV came to my house approximately three months ago after I called them and told him that the green light was flashing on our Mitsubushi TV. They said it was the power board and they quoted me $460 to come out and install a new power board. The next day Mr. Stevenski and his son came and took the back of the TV apart called me while I was at my office and told me that there may be more problems than just the power board. He told me, "I don't know what it's going to take the fix the TV but you have to trust me." I told him to go ahead and fix the TV. When I got home he told me the TV was "like brand-new" and that they got it all straightened out. He charged me $600 for the power board alone. His bill was $1029 but he told me if I paid cash he would take the sales tax off. He also warranteed the parts and labor for 90 days but said that the TV should be good for a long time to come. Now three months later, just days before his 90 day guarantee would expire we now have the same problem. After several phone calls we got him to come to the house but he told us it may cost more to get the TV working and I told him that I did not want to hear it as the TV was under his warrantee. He changed out 7 resistors and told me that the parts were $200 and presented me with an invoice. I told him that if the TV was still working in 90 days he would get paid. He would just have to trust me as I trusted him in the first place. Later his wife called and she was quite rude on the phone. Also our cleaning lady was made very uncomfortable by him. She told us he was very foul mouthed while he sat on the couch watching his son do all the work. After he left I did a little research only to find out his $200 resistors were really only $3 each and the $600 power board was only $56. This guy is a rip-off from the word go. I wouldn't hire this guy to change a light bulb.

First these two things would make me a favorite to a restaurant. 
1) atmosphere 
2) food 

Cuba Libre has both. The menu is small but what ever I ordered I had no complaints. The appetizer bread and butter is excellent and the Mojitos are great. 

Make sure to make reservations on the weekend as it can get really busy.

I had a wrist injury in late 2017, and after a week or so of soreness was concerned that it had been broken. Went in for an x-ray which revealed that it was not broken, but the doctor suggested wearing a thumb brace to help with healing. He had one on hand that he suggested I buy. I asked about the price because I was concerned about buying it in a hospital, and he gave me a price that was reasonable. When I checked out of the office, they said they couldn't bill me because their computers didn't work, so they'd send a bill. That bill didn't show up until August 2018.  In Nov 2018, a year after the appointment, I got a $230 bill from Breg Orthopedics for the thumb brace. Nearly identical braces cost $30 at Walgreens. The doctor knew I had already had the injury well over a week and gotten around just fine.
When I brought the bill to this clinic, the receptionists were shocked at how much it cost, took my information, and said they would get back to me right away, since the bill was due soon. Three days later I hadn't heard a thing, and had to go back and demand a response again. But when I talked with the clinic manager on the phone she defended the bill, saying it must be a high quality brace, and they never quote prices to patients for these things (they absolutely did). Even if the doctor had not given me a price (again, he did), he encouraged me to walk out of the clinic with a brace that I would get charged hundreds of dollars for, knowing I didn't need to buy it there. These people absolutely don't care about you as a patient. During my conversation with the manager, she would never acknowledge that the clinic was at fault in this situation.
This clinic is disorganized, slow, and they absolutely don't care about giving you good service or standing behind the things they do.

Sad it's not the old ballyhoos I remember, Last three meals have just been so-so. So it's consistently good food just not what ballyhoos was. They should  make a name for themselves with better food then ballyhoos so they can take down that bait and switch sign that leads customers to believe they are one the previous establishment. I felt cheated when I saw the cheesy "d" glued to the sign.

Oh PHL... you disappoint me. Granted, we only spent 15 minutes here thanks to our delayed incoming flight, but it wasn't a good 15 minutes.

Dirty restrooms, long wait for the shuttle at gate F14 going to the B terminal, some of the moving walkways not functioning, etc.

I'm giving PHL two stars instead of one, only because we actually made our connecting flight back to New Orleans and didn't have to spend the night in the airport.

My husband and I showed up without a reservation on a Friday night about 6:30 and were able to be seated.  The waitress immediately approached us and read the specials which were also posted on a blackboard in the restaurant.  We were pleasantly surprised by the nice décor of this little store front BYOB right near our house. We brought a bottle of white wine and were immediately offered an ice bucket.  We started with the fried calamari which is our pet peeve as we are rarely able to find a restaurant that prepares this without being chewy and over cooked.  The calamari did not seemed cooked enough and was chewy.  My husband ordered the baby lamb chops with gorgonzola risotto.  The lamb was cooked to his liking and ample but he felt it lacked flavor.  He raved about the risotto.  I had chicken parmesan with linguini.  It was delicious with a slight garlicky flavor and the sauce was fresh and flavorful. The bread with olive oil dipping sauce was also good and we were too filled to order dessert.  The only minor complaint I had was the way the hostess was dressed.  She was wearing flip flops, cropped spandex pants and a jean jacket.  Her appearance kind of detracted from the atmosphere of the place. Service was very good.  We will definitely return.

I've been wanting to eat at this place since they opened up a few months ago, and it was well worth the wait. We started off with the Tai Pei Street Corn. It was very different, but in a good way. It tasted smoky, a bit sweet with a kick at the end. My husband ordered the Stunt Pig for his main entree and I ordered the pulled pork with fries and baked beans. We both really enjoyed each entree, and he wasn't even able to finish his whole sandwich it was so big. I'm a huge bbq fan and this pork was unreal. Juicy and cooked to perfection. I also tried all of their house-made sauces. My favorite by far was the sweet lee, but they all were good depending on what kind of sauce you like.


I'm definitely a fan and will definitely be returning!
Fantastic Fantastic......Excellent Customer Service, Clean Good Smelling environment, and Finally the Cookies, cupcakes, and Brownies are Awesome. Unfortunately I devoured most of it in one setting

Went last night, Friday evening and sat on the outdoor patio.  Our waitress was very attentive and did as good of a job as she could, however it was very busy and it was another servers first night.  This was putting a lot of pressure on our server as she was helping the new employee, too.  They should have had additional staff working.  There were three of us and a child.  We had a pizza that was very good.  A salad with chicken that was quite large and the fish and chips which was the special.  Unfortunately, the fish was over cooked and the chips were stale and tasteless.

Overall, good experience and staff.  Most of the food was good.
Server Chris was amazing! I told him about a beer I liked tgey didn't carry and he immediately recommended a beer on tap.that was exactly what I was looking for! Thank you amazing server Chris!

Every time I've tried to go to this place, the line is out the door... today I actually waited inline to get in. People were waiting outside in the snow to get in! We offered to sit at the bar so our wait wasn't too long at all. 
Sitting at the bar was great, we had a server right in front of us and had a great spot to take in all the happenings in this cute cafe by day/bar by night. The artwork hanging is an eclectic mix with a strong Dr Seuss vibe going on. It's trendy and right by the University so a hot spot for trendy students.
I ordered the Eggs Benny which is served on house-made corn bread and a side of fried potatoes. The eggs were perfectly gooey and that hollandaise sauce is sublime. SO FULL. It was a lot of food and every bite was flavourful. 
My husband ordered the omelette of the day with bacon, sun dried tomatoes and povolone, which he devoured so it must have been good. We both had a small latte each and those baristas must be damn good, because the coffees looked and tasted amazing.
I'll definitely be back and I'll come later on the day as I want to see what it's like when it's a bar. Our server told us it's all about the beers when they stop serving brunch ;)

Pros : Giovanni and Avery were on time, polite and professional. Cleaned areas I asked them to. 
Cons :  I asked them to wipe kitchen cabinetry and shelving.. found under the sink cabinet filthy. Also I asked for pet hair removal from carpet. That was not done well. They did vaccum, but there were hair still at the edges of the baseboard. I found all this after their 24 hr re-clean offer.. so even though I  called them & told them about this they said if I were to book another service, then they will address these issues. I haven't moved into my new home yet, so I didn't notice these immediately. 
All in all I had to spend few hours cleaning again the things I hired cleaning crew to do in the first place

First of all I'd like to tell that this review is written in the times of COVID era and I understand that many restaurants are understaffed and experience many challenges.  I don't know for sure if that was the case in that particular place but if so then some changes should take place. In fact, I know many understaffed restaurants do not accept poor service and that's why they don't welcome walk-ins anymore and work only by reservations. This two stars review is based mostly on horrible service and neglect of people who work at that particular location.  We came on Friday night, which is usually the busiest day of the week, which we understand and we were ready to wait for the food some extra time. Surprisingly the restaurant was not busy at all. The waitress was nice and polite, took our drinks order and disappeared.  Literally disappeared for like 10  minutes or so. Mind you the restaurant was not busy at all. Then she brought our drinks, took our main dish order and disappeared again. I ordered French onion soup and salmon, my husband ordered steak and salad. So we are waiting and waiting, our drinks are long gone, no one came to ask how's going and if we need anything?! I thought that my soup would come first and it did half an hour later, and without a spoon.  So I'm looking for my waitress to ask for a spoon and it seems like she's gone?!  So I asked another waiter if he'd be kind to bring me a spoon? Yeah of course no problem and he disappeared too!  So finally, our entire order comes, my soup is cold , still no spoon.  Ok, long story short I got my spoon but the soup was like water with a couple of pieces of onions in it. Never had French onion soup that bad. It looked like they went to grocery store across the street grabbed a boxed soup, added water into it and poured into my bowl. The rest of the food was good though. Hot and delicious, nice presentation and my husband really liked his steak and mashed potatoes. He wanted to order another drink and  a dessert but our waitress never showed up. Finally, when she did we decided it is good idea to ask for a check and get out of there than to wait forever for another drink.
Very friendly and professional. Just moved here from Georgia and was recommended from a nurse at Tucson Medical Center.

This place is my go-to neighborhood taco joint. Everything I have had here has been delicious. The grilled onions are fabulous, as are the hot sauces that come along with. Yes, it used to be a gas station, but it has a decent dining area. You're here for the food, remember?

I love record stores. Genuine record stores that are more than just 300 copies of Beyonce on CD priced at $18.

But the Record Exchange is much, much more than just a record store. Sure, they have the best, ever-changing, selection of vinyl and CDs in the Treasure Valley. But they have a great coffee/drink area next to their souvenir-esc shop. The seemingly "trendy" decor and ambiance is misleading. The staff is helpful, approachable, and knowledgeable. It is a welcoming environment whether you're the most knowledgeable about music/trends or you're new to the whole thing. They even have starter kits if you're looking to get into vinyl.

Great value, honest value, and an accepting place to browse. They also have live music regularly!

Extremely poor customer service this evening; ordered a simple happy meal and chicken nuggets, had to repeat the order and sides several times. The clerk did not appear to be paying any attention. Asked for manager- completely indifferent and apathetic. She stated she's been on the grill all night but they have new people and can't help it- she went on to say that I needed to be understanding.

Now, I've been sitting here for almost ten minutes waiting for my food at the second window and... there isn't anyone else here.

This is seriously the McShittiest McDonald's- don't come here.

Great pizza and gnocci. Pizzas were delicious thin crust was crispy and well done. Little bit greasy but delicious. The salads had a whole load of dressing which was overwhelming. The bread and garlic knots were delicious! Would definitely recommend and go again!

Yum. Visiting from Boston but I love Cajun food and this place hits the mark. Located in the back of the market. Very friendly people, fast service and really good food. Thought about jambalaya but got étouffée. Lots of shrimp and crawfish, really tasty at prices that can't be beat.

Ohh Regal Citrus Park, you desperately need a revamp. And to clean your filthy bathrooms.

The one thing I like about this theatre is the retro neon outdoor display... very beautiful... but that's about it. I've been here a few times and I'm always surprised how run-down and dirty the theater is. First of all, they have the most uncomfortable theater seats ever. Old, scratchy and hurting my back. If a 20 something feels that way, what would an elderly person think? It certainly wouldn't be a good experience for them. And the bathrooms... don't get me started! I walked down the line looking for one that wasn't covered in trash and... you know what. Then, when I found a stall with only minimal stuff on the floor and seat, there was no paper. Afterwards I checked the other stalls and realized only one or two of them had toilet paper. WTF! Plenty of employees... someone restock and clean please! Gross.

By the way, they only offer student discounts Sun-Thurs. As for me I'll only be back if there's no better option.

I have such fond memories of magpies from growing up here but sadly they have gone downhill. 
Our pizza took forever to come and was ice cold when it finally arrived. I ordered extra large and only received a large. When I called to let them know I was told that I was only charged for a large and actually undercharged for our order by $1.50 so I should be grateful. Great but that wasn't what I ordered and we were feeding 7 people so I ended up only having a few bites of my daughter's since there wasn't enough to feed everyone. But as it turned out that was find bc the crust  was soggy and pizza was pretty tasteless. What happened to my favorite spot that I used to visit when I ditched 5th period??

Overall everything was just ok. The burgers were over cooked. The fries were under cooked. There was gristle in one of the burgers. The service was fine. And the Covid precautions were good. It was the food that was just ok.

OMG! Froze' all day (or night)!  This is a wonderful space, perfect for a Sunday Funday. The bar staff are attentive and wonderful. The drinks are fantastic. Great draft selection. If I had to complain about something it would be the music. This is such a cool progressive spot and there seems to be a bit too much pop country from the 80s-90s...just seems a little at odds w the overall vibe. But seriously, great spot...highly recommend!

Yuck. The food is not good, very salty and tastes like a prepackaged microwave meal. We'd never been here and got the spicy chicken bowls. We got charged extra for "white" chicken meat and brown rice too. Won't be back.

Went to Luke for happy hour expecting to come away with a smile on my face and a belly full of delicious $0.75 oysters.  Little did I know that retribution would be inflicted upon my bowels for my gluttony and stinginess.  Food poisoning from these oysters ruined my entire Mardi Gras weekend, leaving me starving and sober during one of the most hedonistic festivals in existence.  Perhaps management felt the need to cut costs along with increasing prices by switching to cheaper oysters.  Steer clear of this place until they reevaluate their suppliers.

Beautifully decorated office, professional,  upbeat and considerate staff. The man that worked on me was very good at what he does. He was super fast and it was virtually painless. Assistants and himself had me laughing. They checked on me the next day. Best dental experience I could ask for. Thumbs up in every aspect. I am not in pain anymore thanks guys!
The sweetest desserts you can find! The cupcakes were to die for and made a wonderful birthday treat. Their pastries all looked amazing and I was tempted to get one of everything!

This place use to have such nice employees, but now all they have are people who don't want to work there and it shows!  I have been a loyal customer for several years but I have now decided to not patron this place any longer, the customer service is lacking to say the least!  You can never get a drink the way you order it even though they charge you for the "extras"! What a sham! NEVER AGAIN!

The food here is great! Hidden in an old
Motel, really a hidden gem. Haven't found a good torta since taco bus went too "chain" restaurant. My boyfriend had a great burrito that was huge! Never seen something he couldn't eat all of. The verde sauce is so good, but SPICY!

I went here for the first time last night.  I was very impressed.  It was a Wednesday night and by 8PM the place was crowded.  It's obviously popular.  We tried two glasses of their wine from their winery.  They were delicious.  I didn't have any beer but their beer selction was huge for both on tap and in bottles.  We had their homemade chips for a snack.  They were good and they only cost about $3.75.  We had Roasted Beet and Edamame wrap.  It was fantastic.  The fries were fine, but nothing special. My only complaint was that when it started to get busy the waiters got a little behind and confused with our bill, but it was not too bad.  The atmosphere was great.  The restaurant is a wide open space so there are no walls blocking the sightlines.  The look is very wooden and rustic.  I would recommend this place for anyone looking for a non-chain restaurant that's unique in West County.
Favorite Chinese place going on 15 years now! Shrimp fried rice is the best on the Westbank -- and Eastbank. Tried majority of the menu and everything is good, every time. Very friendly as well.

Went for lunch last week. It was a slow day and the nice thing was they were having a fund raiser for Special Olympics. We ordered a pizza and a calzone to split.
