It came out and looked great but as soon as we tried the calzone it was completely cold inside. The cheese wasn't even melted. Had to send it back. Tried the pizza and same thing. Soggy bottom crust and the whole thing was cold. The first basket of bread was tiny lttle hard, cold pieces but we tried again and it was like a whole different bread and pretty good. I don't know if the chef was out that day but I've never had such a bad lunch and it's not cheap!
Excellent food today, 6-11-2021! The service was outstanding as was the food. We will be back for sure!! Authentic Chinese for sure, not a thing we didn't like! Thank you

I went in to buy approximately $15k in various furniture. The salesman told me he would get me a price quote the next day. The next day he called and said it would be the next day. Two days later I called and he had clearly forgotten about me and told me it would be 3 more days to simply price the items I wanted. I told him it was unprofessional that he kept me waiting two days and that I had to track him down to get more excuses, he said I can work with someone else if I want to and hung up on me.

The next salesman I worked with, was more punctual, but also unprofessional, rude, and an incompetent salesman. 

One would STRONGLY assume that there would some incentive offered to rectify the outrageous treatment I had received from the first tough guy salesman. No. Nothing. Not a dime. It's almost like they don't want you to spend money there! 

After two bad experiences with these "salesman", I have a strong impression that apathy and/or contempt for the customers is the rule, not the exception at Sprintz. At this point, it is a matter of principle that I don't spend a dime on over-priced furniture (some of it is quite nice, but over-priced) in that den of mediocrity.

This is my second visit to Cheddar's.... my first was awesome I had the Bbq ribs and shrimp at the location in St. Petersburg . This time I chose the lighter fare meal in Tampa .....this time I got the Baja chicken it was tasteless hardly any seasoning the left off the pico receive 2 tble spoons of maybe about a tablespoon of rice mostly black beans and the chicken has absolutely no taste whatsoever the best part of my meal was the mango lemonade I wouldn't come here again I may go to cheddars but not this one I wasn't impressed.server was good... they need a new cook ... even if it's a low calories meal it shud still taste good it doesn't take much to make chicken taste good!

For what it's worth, Dear Yelpers, Umai Umai has secured a spot in my Hall Of (food) Fame.  After Doma flat-out fumbled our reservation for six on Saturday night, Umai Umai took us in for a late seating and was the savior of our Lake House reunion party.  It only seemed fitting that we get together and drink the last of our Upstate NY vino from our recent trip north.  I've been to Umai Umai several times, and I'm always surprised at the quality of the fish, the creativity of the rolls, and the congeniality of the staff.  However, this time I was blown away.  If you do not try the Eternal Sunshine Roll then you have no love for all that is delectable in life.  Just give up now and save the joyousness for the rest of us.  Get out of my sunshine.  The chef was graciously accommodating of my shellfish allergy - I loathe having to request substitutions, but the Godzilla Roll rendered me begging, even for some bastard form of it.  The chef substituted the Shrimp Tempura for Salmon Tempura.  Damn.  I surrender.  I'm sure after 10 bottles of wine, the service staff must have thought we were lunatics, but they were all smiles as we closed the restaurant down.  Two apps, three huge specialty rolls, and a well-earned tip set us back $75.00.  I've spent twice that on meals half as good.  Get to Umai Umai while there's still fish left in the ocean.  You'll see what I'm talking about.

The proportions are huge, enough for two meals. The food was decent but nothing to brag about. The person who picked up the phone was somewhat rude. He asked if I would look up their menu online because I asked if they had a few things. The garlic chicken had a lot of vegetables and not as much flavor as other places I've tried. There is take out beer which I guess offsets the mediocre service and so-so food... Or maybe not.  I will go to other local hot spots next time.

First words out of my hubby's mouth as we approached the bare boned store front of Chill, "It looks scary". The entire shop is stark white with accents of blue. From the outside as well as the inside, it would look like the place was a vacant shop if there weren't people sitting around.

We were able to sample a variety of flavors before we made our selection. Good thing too because while the flavor may say Banana...it doesn't really taste like the Banana your taste buds are thinking of. All the flavors have a tangy tartness to them, some more than others. 

I settled on dark chocolate and chocolate coconut. To top it all all I hit up the topping bar. I'd love to see these items labeled. I ended up putting chocolate caramel cups on mine thinking I was getting peanut butter cups...no harm done because I like that too. I topped it with all sorts of guilty pleasures: chocolate cookie dough bits, caramel cups, sea salt caramel sauce, peanut butter cups and health bar bits.

While eating this interesting treat, I found myself thinking, "geez this place needs some color or at least something on the walls." It's SO bare inside. The other thoughts that went through my head werehow confused my taste buds were. The frozen yogurt was just that, frozen yogurt with a tangy tartness that drastically contrasted the rich creamy sweetness of the toppings I had selected. I think I would have enjoyed the frozen delight more without the toppings so that I could just focus on the simple yogurt'y goodness, but what fun is that. 

Before leaving my husband got a kick out the bathrooms, he's never seen the extreme hand dryer than blasts your hands dry at 90 miles and hour.

It's a novelty place that I may visit again...but only if I'm craving something tangy and tart. I liked that you only pay for only as much or as little as you put in your cup.

El Maguey, why oh why did you have to go and break my heart? We had such a good relationship, we were working on our issues, and things were going a lot better...I thought we could make it for the long haul. Alas, apparently all good things must come to an end. 

Maybe it was my fault for being wasted and deciding at like 12:50am (when they close at 1am) to order delivery because I see no other explanation for how an order could get so terribly screwed up. I called in an order for 2 vegetarian burritos, fish tacos, guacamole, and some chicken soup expecting to be told that they were closed but was promised it would be delivered shortly. Over an hour later, the delivery guy shows up after getting lost and berates me for not asking for a total price when I expressed shock for it being practically $30. My bad dude, usually it's told to me before I hang up. Then, once we opened the bags to eat, we realized that the order was completely and utterly wrong. We had one vegetarian burrito, steak tacos, a weird gelatinous soup with literally a massive chicken bone in it, a cheese quesadilla, and the order of guacamole. Uh? What? Seriously, the majority of it was pretty inedible. The guacamole was good and we did eat the vegetarian burrito but stopped once we noticed bits of pork in there. It was just so awful and terrible that the next morning we woke up and called and asked for our money back, which is the only reason they have two stars instead of one. They were very helpful and returned my $29 once they verified that they had screwed up the order. 

It's such a bummer that my go-to delivery place failed me in such a ridiculous way. 

At least I have my memories of what this place once was. 

(Oh but they did redo the inside so it looks mildly less grungy now)

If I could give less than one star I would. What a rip off. They shot girls will accost you and then try to charge you 50+ dollars. If you don't pay they will call the cops. A bunch of shot-I stitutes, Asthe bouncer called them. Do not...do not, go there!!!!

This is my favorite go-to place for breakfast since it's right around the corner from my house and I always have a good meal there... Just this past weekend I had a delicious omelette which was one of their specials of the day (spinach, black olives, onions, tomatoes, and cheddar cheese)... I have to admit that I always get an omelette when I'm there so I can't really comment much on their other menu items, but I do know they have pretty amazing french toast as well... Definitely a must try!

I was super excited when this location open, because I always went to the westport location. The first week it open my boyfriend and I visited the food the chicken quesadilla was horrible, it tasted weird and soggy (Which the quesadilla is typically my fav). I gave them another try a couple of months later. Food quality is still horrible.
The gnocchi was amazing! The chicken parm was delightful. You cannot go wrong with this place. It is a little loud because it is so busy, but it's worth it!!!!

They serve you cold raw meat.

Weird.
Easy to work with. Derrick M. Made sure we had everything we needed and was available and responsive. Transparent staff. Highly recommend for a purchase or lease!

The food was horrible and the prices are unreasonably high. For example --I ordered the hamburger Po-boy (it was over cooked and hidden by lots of mayonnaise, with the a few pieces of lettuce and cut tomatoes). To my astonishment a cup of hot tea (water and a tea bag) is priced at $3.99. By pass this location!!!!!!!!! STRAIGHT GARBAGE Establishment..........
Great Italian market for wines, cheeses, and meat.  Amazing deli and great bakery.   Great handmade samwiches with deli salads and pasta dishes to go

I just had a Gaucho for the first time in over a year.  I have no idea why it took me so long to go back, that sandwich is amazing!  Do yourself a favor and call it in to avoid the lunchtime rush line.

Wonderful atmosphere and except service from Susie.
The raviolis were absolutely devine!!!  Great bread and shrimp scampi....this is a must visit restaurant if you're at St.Petes beach

We love this park as a place for a nice nature walk. There is a small playground area right at the front of the park but it is very dangerous looking and some of it has ply wood covering areas that I am guessing made it even more dangerous. So the playground area could really use a facelift. Next to that playground, there is a small dog park that is well maintained.
We enjoy walking the board walk as there are many picnic areas with shade to stop and enjoy lunch with water on one side and the forest on the other. If you venture to shelter 4 it is currently sectioned off as a mother gator has laid eggs near by. It was cool to drive by and see the mother gator sunbathing. 
The park is rather big and I would recommend for the true forest lovers to check out shelter 12 or 13 as they are deeper into the park and have lots of wild life. We have seen deer, squirrels, frogs and of course, dozens of lizards at these locations. This is also a nice area to host family get together or birthday parties as the dense trees provide shade from the strong Florida sun! Most of the shelters do have a grill present as well as plenty of picnic table
Always a great experience at this chipotle, we are so happy to have one close by and staffed by great people.

One of the best Greek restaurants in the city! They have big servings that's totally worth the money you pay for. They have my favorite traditional lemon soup! Sometimes I can finish two bowls in one sitting. They also have a big sampler plate for two. My folks couldn't even finish the sampler plate. It's probably good for three people easily. :) 

I also like the fact that this restaurant is active in the community when it comes to charitable institutions. They were one of the big sponsors for the aids walk and a variety of other charity events.

The color was pretty but the person cut WAY TOO FAR into my nail bed and the gel is peeling after 5 days! My cuticles look terrible and the skin around my nails is starting to peel and I'm surprised. I don't plan on returning here. If you do go, avoid the guy who works there. He did this and I am not satisfied at all.
The pharmacist was so awesome to take care of our needs just before closing. We wouldn't have made it through the night otherwise. Thank you so much!
Great atmosphere tucked away off Main Street. Nice outdoor dining area and always good service. Expensive, but good drinks !

Nuvo is a local gem, owned and operated by two of the friendliest guys in Nashville. Their ingredients are always fresh and healthy, and spot on. Although they're known for their burritos, their salads might be the best in town. Don't be fooled by the food court location -- this is a spot beloved by locals.

Was pretty disappointed here. We walked in around 7pm on a weekend and while there was a little bit of a line, it wasn't anything crazy looking... maybe 10 people max in front of us? Little did we know it would take them close to 30 minutes to get through those 10 people ahead of us. We had an indicator it would still be quite a wait after that when we were almost to the counter and a lady walked up and asked if they forgot about her order as she'd been sitting at her table waiting quite a while. 

They were out of both brisket and ribs. Not sure if this is a normal occurrence not to have enough for dinner time, but keep that in mind that you might want to come earlier or try it for lunch if you're wanting those items. They had a huge variety of sides so I was excited about that and the prices here seem to be very fair for what you get. After we finally ordered, we had to wait at least another half hour or so before our food was brought out to the table. Our friends that were right in front of us in line got their food about 15 mins before us and felt awkward sitting and waiting for us to get our food to start eating when we had literally ordered right behind them and ordered some of the same items at that. 

I got the pulled pork and it was good, but the sides were a massive disappointment. The cornbread was just ok. I wasn't a fan of the flavor of the potato salad at all.. but I think my biggest letdown was the mac and cheese. The menu board noted it as a popular item, but I don't know how that could be. What I got was dry and gritty and not the gooey, cheesy mac and cheese that one normally craves. My boyfriend ordered his sandwich to come without slaw and made it clear when he ordered no slaw - the lady at the counter even repeated it with no slaw. You guessed it, the sandwich came out with slaw all over it which at that point he picked off cause it was not worth the wait. 

If you're in town, I suggest trying one of the other popular bbq spots - we did not get to try martin's to compare it to that because the line was too long for our liking, but we did try peg leg and loved that compared to this place.

Mmm, delicious. Really good flavours, really great service, and speedy service. Prices are a little expensive but I get the perception they use only quality ingredients. 

If you're passing through, I would strongly suggest trying it if you feel "in the mood" for something cold and delicious.

We used to live in Chicago and loved Flat Top Grill, so BD's was a closest substitute when we moved to Indianapoils, but it never really wowed us.  For some reason BD's just never tastes as good despite both being make your own.  I think the main difference is Flat Top stir frys your rice with your vegetables to give the whole thing a balanced flavor vs. BDs which serves bland dry rice at the table.  Flat Top also cooks your meat separately (first) so as not to overcook your vegetables and sauce (which always has a nice burnt taste at BDs).  I also don't like standing by the hot, stinky grill -- prefer sitting down and having your food brought to you.  As a result, we haven't been back in close to 10 years, however now that we have kids, we thought they might like it, so wanted to try it again.  My first impression was holy crap -- this place hasn't been updated in 10 years -- fake granite laminate countertop at the cooking area rubbed off, grease hanging from the vents above the cooking area, stickers next to the spices completely rubbed off where you can't tell what they are -- the place just feels like it hasn't been thoroughly cleaned in 10 years, and the décor definetly hasn't been updated, including chintzy menu graphics, etc.  OK, so never mind they are in desperate need of a complete makeover - on to the food and service.  The beer menu was very dated -- not even microbrews, let alone craft beers.  The service was a little slow considering we were one of 3 parties in the restaurant.  Our appetizer came out after our rice for our meal.  My fried rice came out after the white rice (which was described as enough for 2 -- which it wasn't, esp. for $5 extra).  Our drinks kept running low.  We waited for our check.  We waited for our check to be picked up.  We waited for our check to be brought back.  We waited.  The bar area was not attented do.  Lots of stuff mixed up and dropped on the ice.  They ran out of sauce bowls and had dirty used sauce bowls left by customers the entire time (vs. tasting spoons at Flat Top).  It was just amateur hour, and you got the feeling it was like this every day.  The food all tasted burnt -- they really need to scrape the cooking surface better in between customers and turn down the heat.  The steak was chewy and bland -- definitely bargain basement.  Even paying $5 extra for fried rice, my stir fry didn't taste like it had been cooked together with the rice because it hadn't (see comparison to Flat Top above).  It was just underwhelming, especially for $80 for 2 adults and 2 kids.  I'd much rather go to Sapporo for that price and get high quality better steak and fried rice and more food for the money. Needless to say, we won't be back, and based on the clientele (non-existent) I don't think it will be around much longer.  It's too bad -- considering the price, they really should change their image and do a complete remodel focusing on high-end dining (clean, nicer docor, better service, better meat quality, granite counter at the grill, better beer menu, etc).
Perfect.  The burgers were delicious.  The onion rings were tasty.  The service was friendly.  The price was right on.
The breakfast is always good but felt really sorry for the only waitress working. The bacon was really undercooked today.

Absolutely love Happy hour .Prices on speciality drinks
half price as well as starter menu. Great decorating, clean, 
relaxing and friendly. It's French so don't expect diet food.

Chill bar/restaurant in No Libs. Food always pretty good and they always have a specials menu with good offerings. Happy hour is $2 off beer, but no cocktail or food specials. A little week but you can still snag a lager or yards for $2 on HH. Cocktails are good - mojito martini is one of my top favorites. Id recommend if you're in the area looking for casual affordable eats.

I had never been here before and went to join some friends for a couple drinks. Normally not a big fan of going out around happy hour because of the big crowds it attracts, and we were lucky enough to enjoy their back, outdoor seating. Super nice space in the back, would highly recommend a seat there.

I also had some of their very decadent fries which were very good, but definitely not finger food. Will definitely be back another night soon since it's so close to my Real Estate office.

Edit: Was here again last night and got some food. Just wanted to add that it was great! Pirogies & Chicken Pot Pie YUM! Also they currently have draft been from the #1 Brewery in the world! Definitely need to try.

This has the be the worst cleaners on the planet. Do not take your items there if don't want them damaged! They ruined my 300.00 dollar comforter and  would not take responsibility.  Told me that I brought the comforter in with the damage. I had only had the comforter set 4 months and took it in because there was a spot on it. They burned the comforter while pressing it and the fabric was totally scorched and now it is unusable. 
 I should have read the reviews first which were TERRIBLE! They would not reimburse me for the damage. I wanted to take the to small claims court but with the pandemic the date would have been many months  later. Never ever use this cleaners

I went here the other day, looking forward to Vietnamese food on campus.  First, half the stuff on the menu is Chinese; their selection of actual Vietnamese food is not that broad.  I had some bun, and it was kind of underwhelming.  The fish sauce was watered-down or something, and while Vietnamese food is almost never bad, this was really not that good either.  Plus it was more than it should have been, considering the fast-food quality.  Meh.
Best car experience ever.   17 C Max .  I found my family dealership.  Guys , you have a customer for life.  Ken , Que , Rob and Trevor.  Thank you gents.

Great place in a great location! Delicious options for food and awesome smoothies (without dairy)! You can do a lot of customization with their drinks which is excellent for suiting your dietary needs- they're also great at accommodating food allergies. The interior is well decorated and the staff is wonderful.

Bar Lucca is absolutely incredible. From the service to the food to the drinks, I cannot think of one negative thing to say (other than that they are too far from me in the city!) I have dined in and also ordered delivery - no matter what, the food is perfect!

Every item I have ordered has been delicious and perfectly seasoned. I highly recommend the calamari and farmhouse salad for starters, and the burrata is also a classic! I could also eat the Tartufo pizza every day, it is so unique and is a great size. I also love the Basil Drop Martini!

You cannot go wrong with anything you order at Bar Lucca!
Found this place on GroupOn! I had such a great time. The tour guide was very informative and provided a lot of history background. I would highly recommend this tour .

BUG FOUND!!! disgusting!!! zero stars! never will i ever go here again! I order a shrimp and steak hibachi and found a gross bug in my rice. I was half way through my  rice when i round this. The manager tried to justify it by saying it was from vegetables. No matter where it came from this is UNACCEPTABLE!

Went to this place for drinks and an appetizer had a cinemon apple drink from the menu was great the artichokes dip was Also Great so decided to come back the next morning for brunch was not disappointed had there eggs Benedict. Good spot must stop every time in Reno

i was so excited to find another eatery to add to my list of places to eat when i'm in town. being a vegetarian, it's challenging to eat in tampa, but i'm find it less & less so - this place being one of the reasons!  the food here is very good.  my favorite thing on the menu is the veggie ginger chik'n.  i get it with noodles instead of rice and it's perfect.  hubby loves the veggie lemongrass chick'n noodle bowl.

These little nuggets are filled with love. The dough is thin enough to not feel like all you are eating is dough which I feel is a common dumpling error and the frying makes them golden and crisp on the outside. Inside the filling is abundant, flavorful and great consistency. I tried the ancho chicken which were very flavorful but not overly spicy. The spinach was hearty and paired very well with the marinara. My favorite were the french onion, wow like a thick french onion soup with caramelized onions and flecks of thyme.

I like I like 
Quick delivery. I have ordered their Italian dinners arrived to house hot and perfect as if I sat down there to eat it. Garlic bread and an excellent salad. 
  I Love Gyros but am very picky. I love their gyros!!!!  The platter comes with a most spectacular Greek salad. Mmmmm. 
 The place is still a BYOB and has a generous dining area. Great for groups or family dinners. Something there for every1

Adorable old school ice cream shop!  Parents with strollers beware,  you aren't going to fit it through the very narrow door so make sure there is someone with you to wait outside.

There are tons of flavors,  syrups,  and you can get the traditional fudge sundae or root beer floats and the like. I opted for the vanilla bean since it was the original recipe with local cream- it was very creamy and delicious. I was skeptical of the price for a small cone but the scoops are huge!  My sister in law enjoyed her root beer float, which came with an old-fashioned cardboard straw. Nice stop on a hot day,  though you might want to opt for a cup rather than a cone because that giant scoop will start dripping immediately.
Small "hole in a bar" type of place. Quiet local crowd but not a place to have a good time.

Listen, this place has the best bang for your buck if you're looking for a good Italian meal. The chicken parm was 9 bucks and change and it came pasta, salad, and bread. My family also split an Italian hoagie. I love the way they shred the lettuce so fine. All around, great food good price.

In a world of pretentious hipster coffee houses (said by a true pretentious hipster), with snooty baristas who coudln't care less about you, it's just nice to go into a place and get kind, thoughtful, fantastic service.  They are attentive, friendly, but real.  I've been here twice and the thing I remembered about it each time was the staff, and the products they are serving are great as well.

Service was average even for the lunch hour.  I was expecting service similar to a top of the line steakhouse but maybe I overestimated.  First time experiencing Harry's and probably my last. Walking in for a lunch holiday party, it was pretty deserted. The decor was dark, bland and very uninviting. No smile from the waiter, just took our order... Food was average.  Drinks were also average. I ordered a Moscow Mule which normally comes served in a copper mug. My drink came in a regular glass and was lukewarm. I don't think anyone was trying to make our experience a great one. Maybe the dinner experience would have been different.

I went here with some friends after getting a LivingSocial deal, and it is awesome.

On the first visit, Alex taught us how to use the wheel. He was knowledgable, patient, and super helpful considering we were all trying it for the first time.  Alex was there the second time as well, and he got us set up with our glazes, explained how to do it, and was there to guide us along the way. Keith was there too, and is probably the friendliest guy ever. He had a smile on his face the whole time, and it created a really positive environment that made being creative a lot easier and more fun. He even let us hang out there close to closing time after everyone had left, since we were waiting for a ride. It was like we were hanging in his living room! 

Totally laid back vibe, partly due to the great music they had on each time. It's also a really great space - antique furniture mixed in with some more modern pieces, open layout, great lighting. Did I mention the arcade games? Pinball, pac-man, foosball. 

But all the fun bells and whistles aside, we learned a good amount with Alex, and it was an overall great experience. I'll definitely be back.
My favorite local Italian place. Usually get the ciapinno with the house (blush)sauce. Been a customer for maybe twenty years.

First of all, I'm Korean. I notice that most of the people giving this place rave reviews were not. The food is terrible! It lacks the depth and flavor that Korean food is known for. Our banchan, especially the kimchi, was garbage. I've had food in Korean cafeterias much better than this. Do yourself a favor and just drive a bit farther to the Hmart cafe down the street, there is a smaller selection, but the food is much better. 
AND PLEASE DON'T EAT RAW FISH FROM PLACES LIKE THIS. You are marginally better off getting supermarket sushi.

Formerly Poke King, re-branded as Loco Ono, And with it an expanded new menu. Aside from offering Poke Bowls, they now offer Hawaiian hot plates and Poke by the pound.  I ordered chicken katsu plate, a combo plate that included kalbi beef and teriyaki chicken, 1 spam musubi, as well as 1/2 lb. of the kimchi poke. The service was fast and the size/portions of the food for the price was just right. The food was good, although the kids did mention the chicken katsu was a bit dry and could have used a bit more seasoning/flavor. I think the stand-out item was the kimchi ahi poke. It was so fresh and had the perfect amount of umami flavor to it. I am excited to try their other poke by the 1/2 or full lb. options, so for that I will be back. So happy to have a place in town to get Hawaiian style poke.

The number of combinations they have is pretty much astounding. I had chicken/artichoke/tomato/wine sauce. Very filling and delicious. I went with girlfriends and we ordered mimosas. They were tiny and expensive. They do have a limited list of wine, it's probably better to order a bottle. Service was also very slow. Kind of weird because the place is so small the waitress should have full view of everything going on. 
Aside from the drinks and service, I'd give 4 stars for yumminess and generous portions. Next time I will order to go and walk down to the park.

I travel often and frequent large cities.  This hotel was outstanding! I enjoyed the comfy bed and big rooms! The front desk staff was very friendly and helpful! I went to the complimentary breakfast and it's not a typical cereal and yogurt bar there's an actual full cooking staff that will make you anything from an omelette to breakfast burrito! Check this place out if you're in the area! Free wifi too!

Best coffeeshop in the LGD. Good drinks, free wifi, quick service. 
Cash Only, be prepared.

Let me first preface this review with the fact that I have been to Japan and have made sushi there throughout the country. I have had sushi in Philly and at other places within the South Jersey area, and I have not found sushi as great as Sakura serves. I had the Spicy Hamachi Roll and Volcano Roll. They were big rolls, and most importantly tasted fresh and were flavorful! Hands down, the best sushi I have had in the US.

I just love this place- awesome service- actual service with a smile. And good food! Never had a bad experience at this place yet! The chic fil a sandwich with extra pickles is my go to sandwich with a sweet tea or a black and white milkshake. Staff is always on point with service and friendliness. 
Must try the breakfast to if you ever come- the chicken egg and cheese on a biscuit is so delish!
$14.99 for a full service wash (outside wash, inside wash, complementary vacuuming and much more) such an amazing job!!

Now, as I'm writing my review, I learn that Tacos El Rey is closed??!?!  What??  Guess I'll have to find a new place in SB to grub!
When I visited last, Marina took our order of Grilled Mahi taco, Pastor taco, Sea bass taco and a Cucumber lime drink.  She was very outgoing and friendly.  
All salsas are full of flavor and delicious, we couldn't decide which was our favorite!  Sad to see this place gone...  :(

I came in for brunch on Saturday. A friend and I split a few freshly baked pastries with house made jams, a duck confit hash, and the creamiest quiche I have ever enjoyed.
Everything was decadent and tasty. We went back for dinner that night and enjoyed halibut and roast duck.  

It's a cozy little restaurant that feels more like a small european neighborhood bistro. Our server both times was a cute girl with glasses who answered all of my 20 questions about the menu and kept our drinks flowing. Oh, did I mention that the cocktails are by the same people who set up Holland House? Excellent cocktail menu!

Great food, great environment, great service. I can't recommend this place to enough people. What a lovely addition to east nashville.

This is a popular park in a great neighborhood. 

It looses a star because lately it's been crowded by bench-possessive homeless folks. 

Over the past few years I've seen this park swing back and forth between a dog happy park with acrobats practicing and a park where people go to buy and sell hard drugs.

I'm giving it 4 stars because I like to think of it during its best times...

the size of this space is great, 
the coffee is brewed well, 
the bagels are actually some of the best around, 
the customers are interesting to watch, 
the staff is not the most pleasant, 
the veggie cream cheese is great.

if i'm up in fishtown i'll stop in, 
i like the overall vibe of this space.
Absolutely delicious food and great service. As much as everything we ordered was amazing, the show stopper was the Mac n Cheese!
Just finished a 3 day stay at this wonderful hotel. Clean, classic, and centrally located. Amazing staff and service! Thank you.

There is not a lot of places to eat in Zephyrhills and I have been seeing Little Ninja on Yelp with probably the highest rating out of all the places to eat in Zephyrhills. So after living here for three years and my husband recently trying Thai food for the first time in his life and LIKING it we finally came to Ninja. This place is small, on a side street and looks like a house. Only a few tables inside. We got the usuals for us, Pad Thai and Massaman Curry. The Pad Thai was yummy and the curry was probably the best I've had. This place is for sure worth all five stars.

Well I just satisfied a lifelong curiosity. When I went to Merchantville Elementary School in the early '70's, so many of my classmates would flock to Vincent's Panzarottis with two quarters in their hand at lunch time. I never did and never got the attraction. I finally decided to try it on my 53'rd birthday. It was not terrible, but it did not meet the expectations I had based on the popularity with my primary school mates. It was a little dry and too puffy. I was thinking fried Stromboli, but not quite. I think the experience could be significantly improved if it was served with a side of warm tomato dipping sauce. Anyway, your mileage may vary, but curiosity satisfied and I do not feel the need to return again. Sorry Stan, Guy, Billy, Debra, Anita, Dukie and all the others I may have missed that are devotees of the culinary form. Maybe my tastes have outgrown this sort of grub.

To Vincent's . . . I don't mean to be unkind, but it is just not my cup of tea.

My guilty pleasures in the Philadelphia and South Jersey region will remain the Philly Cheesesteak Sandwich.

We have been coming to Clearwater for five years and we love it but it has been difficult to find an exceptional steak. Thankfully this place is the solution. Fantastic service, great drinks and appetizers. Pork chop and lobster are incredible too!

Worst hotel and customer service, even by general manager Aimee Tylor. I have been a loyal Marriott customer for 5 years with an average of 120-150 nights stayed per year. I am pregnant and in my last trimester when my Dr would not let me travel due to health reasons. Staff wouldn't respond to me and when I contacted Aimee, their GM, I thought I'd be in good hands but I was sorely wrong. Aimee was not accommodating at all. Don't support this hotel. I won't ever again!

Today I had the mushroom cap panini.  The combination of ingredients is nothing short of genius and it is toasted to perfection. I'll definitely get it again.  In fact,  I am trying to get through the entire menu but I keep getting stuck with a new favorite and ordering it several times before I move on.  First it was the avocado toast, then the rainbow tacos and more recently the street spuds.  The owners are great about accommodating any request for changes or substitutions.

I don't eat much Italian so this was one of my first visits to the hill. I got Alfredo, husband-lasagna, father-manicotti, mother-seafood pasta. ALL of us enjoyed our dishes, I was really impressed with the lasagna. Not to mention the pasta portions were huge. My parents claimed the clam stuffed mushrooms appetizer were a little on the rich side, you can't eat many of them.This wasn't my first choice, but this is one of the few places  on The Hill that is open on Sunday. So glad we went.

This Panera has absolutely the worst to go order process.  For the third time, I was missing items from my order.  This last time I triple checked my order BEFORE I left and made sure I had everything I ordered. You would have thought I was putting them out by insisting I had everything I already paid for in my order.  It was just poor customer service by 1) not checking the order; 2) not doing everything to make it right. 

The last time I was missing items from the bakery and I got home and called. They said they knew they were out of those items and someone should have told me when I picked up. Amazingly, I had to insist they refund me the amount I already paid to them for these missing items. Had I not called or insisted on a refund, they would have happily pocketed my money.

Recently I went to this Denny's with some friends of mine at about 1 in the am service was ok for it being so late no complaints there but when our food came my friends pancakes had an actual live cockroach in it !!!!!! And better yet they didn't even take it off the bill they made him pay full price for some roach pancakes. I will never eat there again unless the do some serious upgrades. 
I am also calling health inspector !

We dined at Greek to Me yesterday with another couple and the food was awesome. I had the seafood stew and it far exceeded my expectations. Kaitlin and David are the new owners, but not new to It's Greek To Me, and are doing a great job,. Support this fine restaurant as it is a valuable asset in Catalina. They also have my Bombay Sapphire gin. That is a plus. As the President of IMPACT of Southern Arizona, a local nonprofit organization, we will be hosting a Dine for a Cause event on Sunday November 12th. Kaitlin and David are eager to support our community. Stay tuned for more information.

Move over Cochon, there may be a new top dog on my restaurant list in New Orleans. So, after winning $167 at the track on a $5 bet to win for the 31:1 odds "Do Dat Blues" on the last day my friend was visiting from out of town, we decided to go on an all out splurge meal, complete with cocktails, a bottle of wine, 2 appetizers, 2 entrees, a dessert and coffee. Let's just say, we didn't have any money from the winnings left but have a meal memory to last for a while to come. Both our waiter and bartender are awesome, and Chef Aaron was walking around hanging with friends (or regulars perhaps?) Farm fresh ingredients, skillful cooking techniques, unique flavor combinations elevates Patois above the usual New Orleans faire. 

Moules frites were superb, we kept the broth till every drop was soaked up by bread. We were adventurous and tried the sweetbreads for the first time - AMAZING! Awesome textural contrasts and melding of flavor. And how could I resist the ham crusted scallops - putting two of my favorite foods together (pork and shellfish)? The cauliflower puree was delish - I would have loved a big ol bowl of that stuff instead of just the smear under the dish. My friend got the rabbit special, which was yummy as well. We finished the meal with the "Snickers" dessert which was really fab - achieves that perfect balance of sweetness with a bite of saltiness. My only regret is that I don't think I can pull off winning at the racetrack every time I get a craving to eat at Patois - and my bank account can't afford me going there unless I do!

Highly suggest making a reservation - even with 9:30 reservations, we were not seated until 9:45. Worth the wait, and every penny.

Menus were really sticky at the bar, which is pretty typical... but when we were sat at our table they were gross again. We ordered the conch fritters and they were okay. Our server was really nice, but I wasn't blown away with the food.
Had a coupon, scheduling was a cinch, massage was out of this world. customer service was great.   Have gone to this location a number of times over the years.  Always been pleased.

An ice scream flight?! - Yes, please! Such a cool idea and you can try a bit of everything (trust me it's super hard to decide between all the delicious flavors).
I did love their honeycomb ice cream as well as raspberry sorbet. The whiskey one was very interesting and quite good (a bit too sweet for my taste though) but can't really go wrong with anything. 
Plenty of parking, inside and outside sitting area, and friendly staff!

The sign outside the door advertised a $7 eyebrow wax, so I made a quick stop during my lunch break. In retrospect, I should have gone to European Wax Center across the street--at least I am confident that the wax there is sanitary. At Central Nail Salon, the wax was applied using a single q-tip that she kept dipping into the large pot of wax. Gross. 

Finally, when it was time to pay, I was told that it would be $10 since I wasn't paying in cash. I would have argued, but I just wanted to get out of that place. Will not return.

The entire crew at Bernhard Automotive is top notch. Dave Bernhard, the owner, is extremely honest and will never try to overcharge you or convince you to have work done on your car that you don't need. The staff is friendly, fast and on time with their appointments. Our family takes all three of our cars there and have only had excellent experiences. I highly recommend Bernhard Automotive - Columbia Firestone.

Came here during covid 19 for some take out. We got the Thai sweet tea, pad Thai, curry (hot), and calamari. The hostess told us it would be around 15 minutes. Our food was really in about 5! Super fast service! 

The Thai sweet tea was good but on the sweeter side of what I've had. Very good flavor! The calamari came with a sweet sauce and it was delicious and just the right amount of fried. The sauce didn't make it soggy despite it being in a takeout container for some time. The calamari was probably my favorite. I loved my pad Thai. It was perfect. My fiancé's curry was very hot. He normally can handle a lot of heat but it was "hot" even for him. So be weary, maybe start with a medium if you like heat. They also have a "Thai hot" option that I imagine is crazy hot. Anyways, it was great food! Looking forward to the full dining experience when things open back up!

House of Jin (HOJ) is a real gem which seems a bit out of place in Germantown.  I lived around the corner for a few years, and even though I have since moved a few miles away, I still go back to HOJ once in a while because it has left a lasting impression on me.  It rivals a lot of the upscale downtown Asian eateries in a lot of ways.  

Food - Solid B+.  Not every dish on the menu will knock your socks off, but for the most part, they are above average, and sometimes everything is just amazing and you forget you aren't on restaurant row.  I fell in love with Phoenix rolls at HOJ, and while occasionally I'll find good ones elsewhere, HOJ makes them just right for my tastes.  I think the presentation of the dishes is one of the things that elevates the dining experience beyond what one might expect. 

Service - Always attentive.  This is one of those places where you are always compelled to tip well.  If you ever have a meal that doesn't meet your expectations, you can't say it is because the waitstaff didn't try hard enough.  Sometimes it can be a bit much when the restaurant is relatively empty and the servers are watching your every move, but if you become a regular, they are like family to you, and you don't feel like you are under the microscope all the time.  

Ambience - Surprisingly OK.  Located on the south end of Chelten Ave, the exterior fits in pretty well with the surroundings.  That is to say, while it's not run-down, it's nothing to write home about, and whitebread suburbanite metrophobes might have some anxiety as they pull into the parking lot.  Once inside, the lighting and decor are very inviting, regardless of whether you are the only patron or if it's a full house.  

Overall, I guess you could say I have a soft spot for House of Jin.  It's not perfect, and sometimes I feel as if they are trying too hard, but if you are in the area I say give it a shot--I bet you will be pleasantly surprised.  I have had to do some convincing to get friends and family from the 'burbs to give HOJ a chance, and I have no regrets whatsoever.
One of my personal favorites, some of the best fast food around Riverview but a little bit pricey. I usually get the chicken nugget combo. Cookies and cream mike shake is delicious.

Meh, if you love flavorless meat then check this place out!!!  Went across the street to Geno's and litlerally the identical cheesesteak with no flavor. Personally I've had better cheesestake in California lol.

(4) ~ very good overall service to replace an old light fixture in our laundry room with a newer LED one.  NOTES: Scheduled it the next day, took about 30 minutes.  Invoice sent via Email, paid online by credit card.
I received a thorough and professional home inspection by Core Inspection Group. I highly recommend.

It hasn't been until recently that I've started coming to this theatre more, as my apartment is within a mile of one. 

I find myself at this theatre for the following reasons:
1- location - right in the middle of the city 
2- parking - they'll reimburse you for your parking so be sure to show them your parking ticket for the mall parking garages!
3- the fancy reclining seats 
4- generally cleaner than some of the other locations, but still not the best in terms of cleanliness. 

Reasons to look elsewhere:
1- it's a pricier ticket (think $2-5 more per ticket per show)
2- you have to choose a seat. It's nice when you're going for an opening night but I hate having to choose when the theatre isn't even 50% filled. 

Overall a nice theatre!

This is by far the best taco place in town.  I cannot rave about them enough!  They make their own tortillas, their own salsas (and there are many!) and everything is absolutely fantastic.  They also do a catering service which is great too.  A friend of mine hired them for a party and the food was fantastic.  Just be sure to budget an extra hour or so for set-up I think.  I think this is better than La Super Rica (though it is a little different, so it's apples and oranges).  But by far my favorite place.  Try mixing a taco place with Rajas, Pork and Pollo.  Yumyum!!  No regrets.  Plus they have these really funky music videos in the corner that always make me laugh.

Best Tamales EVER! It was on Food Network! Chicken w/ green sauce is my favorite. The chocolate tamales are like a melting lava cake in a husk... its amazing! Ever heard of Elotes? TRY IT! $4 for a cup of it. It's corn, cheese, sour creame, mayo, paprika, chilli powder, hot sauce, cilantro... some other stuff i dont know what all but it sounds gross but it is amazing! The nachos are good, Tacos are good and the tortas are huge! A must stop in INDY!

I've been here many times and love their pizza. I like the crust and the tangy sauce. I like how they fold the crust over a bit of the sauce. It makes finishing off each slice a little fun. They have a pretty good menu and some weird desserts like the cinnamon bread that is smothered in frosting. Their Wildebread (sp) is great, too. As for parking, they do not have their own lot so you will have to take your chances on the street. The place is good for large groups and I think their pizza is superior to Giovanni's down the street. Woodstock's also has deals on pitchers some nights and a modest big screen to watch football.

This is my suburban gym for when I'm visiting family.  I mostly just go for the spin classes, but there is plenty of cardio and weight lifting equipment.  The locker room is nice.  There are separate studios for group exercise and yoga classes.  The staff is very friendly.

Too many good things to say about this place that I don't even know where to start.  To be honest, I hadn't heard about it or thought of going until really investing myself in the BLM movement and supporting our local black-owned businesses. 

I was greeted over the phone for a pickup order by the most welcoming and enthusiastic woman who was patient and kind with me ordering for the first time. Our order was cooked and ready in less than 15 minutes and reasonably priced for the value. 

The food itself was DELICIOUS!! my husband, sister-in-law, and self all ordered the famous chicken and waffles and would get them everyday if we could. The chicken is cooked perfectly and you can get wings or tenders. We opted for tenders. As for the waffles you can choose from a variety of flavors, but we stuck with the plain ones. I can't wait to try blueberry next time. There was a delectable bitter sauce and syrup included with the food which married the flavors of the chicken and waffles perfectly. 

Thank you for the amazing food and experience. So glad I decided to order and support!

Im new to Nashville and was exploring 12 South when I found this little gem off a side street. I ordered the bacon egg and cheese bfst taco and the avo cheese potato and black bean bfst taco. AWESOME! The potatoes in the tacos are CRISPY HASH BROWNS!!! YUMM! They made the perfect bite. Everything was cooked perfectly. Tortillas taste and feel homemade. All 3 salsas were spot on and the drinks... I had the honey limeade and it was fantastic! Thank you, Lady Bird Taco!  Great experience!! Ill be back!
Destination bar each time we come to NOLA. Great fun. Tradition to be seen. Great place to gather and start you visit to the Quarter

Al basha restaurant staff does not wear masks.... even though Covid 19 cases are on the rise in Indiana, it is a requirement while in public and especially for a restaurant service worker. 

If I could give a zero I would. 

I live close and used to love this place, but they are tone deaf to what's going on in our society right now. 

If they don't care enough to wear masks in their front of house... I have trouble trusting their food safety practices like hand washing, time and temperature control and other food safety protocol in the back of the house. 

It is tragic, but their recklessness will seal their failure, one lost customer at a time.

Sehr schöner kleiner Flughafen, das Gepäck wird im freien Aufgegeben und zum Einchecken reicht es 30-40 Minuten vor dem Abflug dazu sein. Einzig die Flüge dorthin sind etwas teuerer, aber es lohnt sich da man keine Verkehrsprobleme rund um Los Angeles hat.
The food was really bad. 5 of us got the flounder and none wanted to finish it the drum was also unremarkable.
Very good food, great beer selection, good service. A fantastic bar in the neighborhood!
If you want to taste heaven eat one of their Hawaiian salted dark chocolate caramels! I have eaten chocolate in France, Belgium, Italy, and Switzerland, and this place sells the best.

Up until last night, Cafe Minh was, for years, one of our favorite neighborhood haunts.

Last night, we were told about the red snapper special with oysters (I love red snapper). So we ordered the red snapper special. Then we were told they were out of red snapper, but had drum instead. We withdrew our order. Then we were told that yes, as a matter of fact, they DID have red snapper. So again, we ordered the red snapper!

We actually got drum. One of my least favorite fish. I pointed out that we really didn't receive the fish that we ordered. I even flipped my fish to show them the color (red snapper is completely white, this had a darkness, like, guess what? Drum.) We had eaten the oysters, and each had a bite of the fish. 

Management took off one of our drink orders and charged us $36 for drum(!) plus a $5 split charge. We did not ask them to split the fish. I suppose we should have made a scene but we didn't, and now are unlikely to go back. 

I just hate being lied to, especially as a regular customer. Cafe Minh will no longer be in our repertoire, and I will no longer recommend it to friends. For $41 you lost one of your biggest supporters. Shame on you.

Ok, generally love this place. Great food and in the past I have recommended this establishment.  Now, not so much. It's just not clean.  Floor hasn't been cleaned since I don't know when, tables that are vacant have not been cleaned, and the flies!
Food was amazing. Request Jessica to be your waitress. She doubles as the bartender. Our group never had so much fun and her recommendations are awesome.
Rice was UNDERCOOKED. And the girl serving my food dropped the meat on the table and put it back in my bowl. And the serving portions are way too small. Not happy!

Had heard people talking about this place so I stopped this morning for breakfast.  Not overly impressed with food or environment - had a breakfast egg bagel and coffee - there are many places better than this bakery/cafe.  Not likely to return...

I was visiting the area and looking for an outdoor covid friendly option and was happy to find LaCo.  The woman I believe to be the owner was friendly and apologized for the wait (she was allowing her staff time with family for the holidays- no apology necessary) which I didn't mind because it was a beautiful night and I was enjoying the live music.  I had tacos and my husband one of the bowls, both were enjoyable.
Very friendly and speedy service for a great rate. I plan to use this service again next weekend.

I liked this place. It is small like other reviews have said, and it is a little dark inside but the food was good. I had the miso ramen and my dad got teriyaki chicken that came with miso soup and fried rice. Next time I'll try the actual sushi. Some people have said that the owner is rude but she seemed polite enough to me. She just wasn't over the top friendly. Good place, will come back soon.
Loved the food and the atmosphere! It has the same owner of Caracaras, which is one our favorite restaurants in the area. Highly recommend!

This place is the best!! Julia has been doing my acrylics for a long time and they're always perfect. Her attention to detail is awesome and my nails always last 3+ weeks in between refills. Everyone in salon is always very welcoming.
Ordered from them online, great prices, and quick shipping, would definitely recommend and will do business with again

Great facility for the whole family, but  it's main focus is on its tennis program. My wife and I use the gym for personal training , take group class periodically. I also enrolled my son in there basketball, and PARISI program that helps your child with all aspects of sporting agility . My daughter took a beginners class for gymnastics. This place has a great atmosphere , offers personal training, has free group classes , Raquet ball , basketball court , and incredible tennis courts. Things I feel I miss is hot tub and pool. They do keep up on making sure there clients have a great experience so employees are friendly and helpful. Wish them continued success and to keep up the great work. Sorry  not mention I have been a member for over 8 years .

This track races horses who have lost multiple races before coming here...so it is what you might expect.  I gave it four stars because it is a blast!  It's still fun to bet and watch races; minimum bet is $2 and beer is affordable and cold...plus, the people watching is the very best.

My husband and I lived here through his corporate housing for a few months and it was great. 

The grounds are very clean. I always see the maintenance workers running around non stop. I put in a request for the dishwasher and they fixed it within an hour. 

The pool and BBQ area is very nice and hardly used. Gym area is great lots to choose from. 

I never had an issue w parking or people partying. I rarely saw kids but it is dog friendly. 

They also have a trash service where you put your trash outside the door and they pick it up. Very convenient. 

If we didn't buy a house we would've rented here. Great location, nice floor layouts, reasonable rent, and great property/management. 

I do wish the club house would be open before 10 am, made it hard to use the business center.

One tip for management - add black out shades in the bedrooms on the 1st floor facing the parking lot. It was so bright in there at night and when people pulled in with their headlights in it was like a spot light in our room.

This location is really easy to book appointments with, and the wait is never very long. Marisa was my stylist and she was phenomenal. She's the only one I've had in my experiences with many salons who didn't complain about how much hair I have (it's long and thick), and she knew just how to cut it so it didn't immediately frizz out on me. I'll definitely be coming back because the price is excellent and the quality is great.

I was driving by and saw "Cheba Hut Toasted Subs". Just what I was craving. You'll notice when you walk in that the toasted subs almost kind of seems like a subliminal message with sandwich sizes such as "nug" and "blunt". Also, the sides are labeled "munchies" and drinks are "cotton mouth cures". I love all the posters and things on the walls pertaining to "the ganja". I also love the menu choices because they aren't what you'd see at any ordinary sandwich shop. I chose the Pakalolo. It consists of ham, swiss, and pineapple and then whatever veggies you want to add. It was excellent.
Yummy comfort food. Pulled roast beef was the best. Staff was nice. Get there at 10:30 or prepare to wait in long line.

I've  been to Bodeli's a few times for lunch.  Every time has been a good experience.  I love the conveyor belt that goes around the restaurant carrying delicious sushi!  It is a great opportunity to try new items!  Very friendly staff also!

Ummm what happened to this place? When this hookah spot first opened, it was so much fun. There was music going, bar seating and just an overall great atmosphere. Now...there are no bar seats, so if you want to sit down to enjoy your hookah, you have to spend at minimum $100...WHAT. This is a hookah bar in philly....I guess they forget who and where they are. They charge $10 at door and I dont know why because there's NO ONE in there. Let these Yelp reviews be of service to you, change it up because you're loosing me and a bunch of customer. Back to BYBLOS I go.
Great place for a group dinner, casual dinner with good list of wine, beer, and cocktail option!

Great fresh food. Very small menu I always order a special. Nice outdoor seating. One of my favorite restaurants in St. Louis! The dessert is amazing. If you are not a big eater you can split  a meal. I usually do this here.

I stopped when they were at the Dad's Mart at Mill and Kietzke last week. I was a little disappointed in the fry bread, as it didn't taste super fresh, and had a slight taste of oil needing to be changed. I don't know if I will go back. I was really hoping for better.
Food is good but the service is absolutely terrible, doesnt justify waiting an hour and a half for a burger and fries. Especially when theres barely any customers. Wont be back and wouldnt recommend.

Ordered carryout pizza and wings on a Sunday.  Arrived to find the place pretty much being run by kids. Food matched up -  it was way below what we expect from Feola's and more like what I might have picked up at Hess. Feola's used to be our favorite pizza in West St. Pete.  No mas :(

Given our experience over the years, we'll give them one more try on a non-Sunday.  Maybe the family skips out on Sundays.  I hope?

Definitely overpriced for the quality of food. Ordered a bacon cheeseburger and a grilled chicken sandwich with 2 fries and the total came out to $18. Bacon cheeseburger came without bacon and chicken sandwich was basically a piece of rubber between two dry buns. Won't be returning anytime soon

We had seafood nachos that were excellent. The draft beer was good but cost $8 per 12 oz pour. The draft price is not on the menu!  Enchiladas were okay not bad not great. The vegetarian meal was insipid bland boring and overcooked!  Wont be back. There are better places for food an cheaper places for cigar city drafts. $100 for one app two entrees and four beers.

I was scheduled to fly to alaska the following morning. Had to reschedule the flight thanks to flu like symptoms.  I had the risotto.
My 15 month old little girl had so much fun here today. It was pretty warm but the canopies provided some shade which was lovely.

I saw other reviews on Yelp and I wanted to see what they were all about, I'm so glad I tried it.It was so indulgent and tasty. I had THE KING:Fresh Banana,Peanut Butter,Mascarpone and Mozzarella the flavor combination was incredible, sweet toasted donut,savory peanut butter, and creamy cheeses, all melted together, so good. I also tried the chili with blue cheese, super rich.Its definitely an experience and it was fun.

This is a relatively unknown food cart within the Penn scene, but they have got some great food. I've tried the banh mi, the smoked salmon sandwich (called the Nikki), and the Wistar. All were very good, and the bread is a step above any other food cart bread. They also give you a free banana and small water. The only con is that the food doesn't usually fill me up enough, though for the price, it's probably still a better deal than restaurants.

I've never been wowed by Primavera. But at the same time, I've never been disappointed. They are my go-to on the Main Line for Italian food, if I'm getting something more than take out pizza or mozzarella sticks. I've been going here for almost 4 years, and I must say, they are consistent with their good food.

The last time we went, I was beginning a gluten-free kick. I was worried, going to an Italian restaurant, that I would not find anything I could eat. Luckily, there was a dish on the menu (forgive me for forgetting the Italian name...and I can't seem to find their menu online) that was made up of grilled chicken, mashed potatoes and spinach. There was nothing remarkable about it, but the portion of chicken was decent and the flavor of the mashed potatoes was quite good. I also seem to recall their bread comes out hot and fresh, though I didn't get to try any the last time I was there.

They have a good selection of wines and make a mean cocktail as well. This is really not a bad place to take a date or your parents. The inside of the restaurant is quite beautiful; they have lots of murals or Italy and the countryside! The service never seems to be awesome, but again, it's decent. The worst is just having to wait a while to have your water glass filled.

Like I said, I am never wowed by Primavera, but the point is: I KEEP COMING BACK. So take that for what it's worth.

Went to Mai Lee last night and they are not there.  No sign stating that they've moved to another location.  Only redirection is a sign on the next window directing patrons to Flaco's Cocina a few doors down.  

Anyone know what happened to Mai Lee?  Based on previous reviews it sounded like they were on the right track.

2/8/10 Update:  Thanks to many fellow yelpers, I've been told that Mai Lee is moving to Brentwood (1467 S. Hanley.) next to the Metro-Link parking garage.  I will be sure to visit as soon as possible to update my rating.  Here is an RFT article on the change of address.  http://blogs.riverfronttimes.com/gutcheck/2010/01/foodwire_mai_lee_new_location_hanley_road_brentwood_st_louis_food_blog_restaurants_relocation_news_012510.php 

I'm not sure why there wasn't mention of the new location on their website or the front door of their old location.

This restaurant is super busy, as such they sat dining patrons at the bar. Not only was the service less than stellar (they did apologize that our drinks took 10 minutes) they must've forgotten about our food since it all came out cold. No one ever came to see how our food was so we couldn't let them know. The a la carte pancake was good, the migas were like someone dumped my breakfast on a pile of Fritos. The 2 different benedicts were what suffered the most, cold, dry chicken and very cold shrimp sauce on the other. End of story, with a little more precision and care on the employees part, this could've been a good dining experience.

Having dinned at a Yolk in Chicago not that long ago, I knew this would be a good place to grab food and go. 

Hint, for some reason the online ordering wasn't available until after 10 am for me. Not sure if that was a fluke, but wanted to share. 

I picked up a meal for myself and a coworker and Yolk provided it very quickly since I was in a rush (less than 6 or so minutes). My eggs caprese was good and I knew from last time to add avocado. I'm not a fan of their home fries. They need a punch up the favor, maybe by adding onions and peppers. 

All together for two meals, a coffee and OJ, the total was about $36.00.

This Yolk is directly connected to the Alexander hotel for anyone staying there. It's a nice break from the typical hotel breakfast buffets.
I love there empanadas. And I also love the way the place looks super clean. Also the customer service is super fast.
Fast and quick service the owner was very nice. Add glass cutters several times now. Thank you

Worst money I spent in a long time.   Sure the location is nice, right across from the stadium and bridgewalk to Broadway. But please please do yourself a favor and book the Hilton or anywhere else.   I paid almost 300 a night for this place and I've had better stays at motel 6s.   First off the shuttle service left to early or not at all.  Shuttle driver was lazy.  Second the mgt appeared to be all friend sor family and screwed off at the front desk everytime I walked past.  Could not figure out our bill correctly and had difficulties answering questions.  One guy was watching a movie on his laptop and was totally disinterested in helping any guests out at all.   My feet stuck to the floor on all the hard floor surfaces.   I don't think it was mopped in days.  The elevator stunk to high heaven.  The pool area was dirty and had visible mold on the walls and ceiling.    This place needs proper management as the current is atrocious.   Staff is unprofessional.     Disaster

Ok so over all the experience is awesome the usual drinks. The staff is super friendly and easy to get info about a monthly membership for my little one. Chances are I will get him signed up worth it forsure. Now the down side Popcorn was a little burnt the popcorn chicken was burnt as well. Great portions for the cost but over all let down on the food I purchased. A few changes and kids that can actually cook (yes kids) and this place could be 5 stars forsure!

Decided to try this place after reading yelp reviews. Was really close to the hotel i was staying at. I ordered the apple honey rosemary hand pie (which was amazing) the savory porridge with a poached egg (delish) and to drink I ordered the Wolfpack  (a little strange for my pallet). My boyfriend ordered the egg sandwich with a banana nut caramel muffin and the coffee soda. He was not too impressed with his drink but was satisfied with the sandwich. The vibe/decor was really cool. And the service was impressive. They have indoor and outdoor seating. Very chill spot, great for a relaxing brunch.

I'm obsessed with their pepperoni calzone and scamorze pie!   Their dough is ungodly fresh tasting and they don't use bottom of the barrel tasteless mozzarella and the delivery guys are always super sweet and thankful.
Delicious!!!! Will definitely be back. Had a small fried shrimp poboy. The shrimp was large and fresh, the bread was soft and crispy.

does anyone else think that if there is ever a shootout in this restaurant the headlines will be amazing??

(i've never been there, nothing about it looks even remotely appealing)

Stopped in mid-afternoon for a late lunch.  Seated immediately,  service was friendly & fast.  Portions were enormous & food was excellent.  Great Yelp find!  Only negative would be the proximity of the tables to one another & how loud it can be in there.

I won't be ordering ahead ever again from this place. They take the to go order which was just a simple omelet and tell me it will be 15 mins. I show up in 15 min and they have not even started making my food yet. No sorry we got behind or any explanation. I am still sitting here 20 min later. Don't take to go orders if you don't put them in until I get there. I run a retail shop and timing when getting food really matters.

I enjoyed the beer choices, but frankly they seemed like a generic bar. The wait staff was prompt and friendly. There was a long wait time to get in which is normal for a weekend. The only reason I give them 3 stars is the food was extremely salty. I know bar food is not the best but I swear they poured salt all over my tacos. My sister and boyfriend also were disappointed by how salty everything was. Advice, bring water in your car for afterwords.
Great coffee shop with a friends tv show vibe. Locally owned they also serve and make fresh breakfast too. Cool spot to study or get some work done  plus it is across the street from the beach
Kristen helped me out immensely over the phone. Turned out I didn't need to bring my dog in, and I thank her and whatever doctor who was on board for their help. Thank you for your kindness

Come here every year.  This year the lady that was to check us in was very rude.  I walked in, mask on due to COVID, and I proceeded to ask a question.  She rudely told me didn't I see the sign and wait there.   I have a retinal issue and no I did not clearly see the sign.   Very rude and I will never be back.  They should be happy and welcoming to their guests, especially with the pandemic and slow reopening.   Goodby Martine's!

Every time my mom and I come here, we get treated so well. We've never had to wait a long to get our nails done. They are so nice and very detailed on their work. They try their best to do any style you want. They have yet to fail. I recommend this place. It's quick, relaxing and good pricing.

Gorgeous spot (see photo) and convenient (River and Campbell). 

Also, check out their happy hour the third Tuesday of the month. DISCOUNTS, ladies!

Every person at Skinjectables has been friendly and helpful. 

Oh! And get on their text list for even deeper discounts!
Great chicken, great people, great supporters of Alberta craft beer, and great community builders here in our beautiful city!

We had an absolutely awesome experience here this morning! We arrived as a family of 11 and were able find seating for all of us without a wait. We were not able to all sit together (it's a small diner), but the owner was so accommodating in making sure that the staff knew we were all on one ticket and that our large group didn't feel like an imposition to them. All in all, I can't say enough nice things about every staff person we spoke to. They were efficient, professional, friendly and welcoming. This is a perfect example of a well run family diner that is a treasure to their community. Take that excellent staff and add wonderful "from scratch" food and this is a five star experience without a doubt! We were able to feed all of us for around  $100, which I felt was a good price for the quality and quantity of food we were served. 
I tried the breakfast burrito and thought it was good and would only request some jalapeños next time to add some kick. While talking to our server, he said they have no issues with personalizing orders which is good to know for our return visit! 
I sampled the sausage gravy on someone else's order and thought it was fantastic! It was the way I remember my mom's gravy tasting 
The group also thought the hollandaise sauce was exceptional! 
I can say with 100% confidence that the entire group was pleased with our decision to have the family breakfast here and I'm sure it will become a favorite stop whenever we are in town. I for one, can't wait to go back and try the lunch menu and the pies and shakes.

Perhaps the worst place I've been to for brunch with my family. The service was extremely poor. The food was okay. Simple things like toast was burnt. They ran out of things but they never told us at the beginning of the meal. Simple things like potatoes again. The weights are extremely long for at best okay food. I would certainly not go back again. The food is okay. The service is much below average. The prices are not fair for the what you get. I suggest looking at alternative options in the area. There are many. Would not go back here.

The dedicated and reliable service of this family run sandwich shop brings us back over and over again. We particularly like the egg sandwiches in the mornings that help kick off a long road trip with an early start. It beats out the offerings of the nearby convenience store every time.
OMG the swordfish taco was so incredibly delicious. The staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating not to mention patient. I ask a lot of questions! :)

Great TINY boutique. It is one of the only places in this city where you can buy raw milk. Great specialty items- I got some really special cheese yesterday :) Zahav's hummus- awesome. Everything sold here is special, well thought out, and very expensive. But worth it.

Went on a Thurs evening and called ahead at 8:30pm to confirm dine-in hours. Girl on the phone said restaurant closes at 10pm but as long as we're there by 9:30pm we could still dine-in. Got there at 9:15pm and was initially told dining area was already closed but after explaining I called ahead and drove 30min and was told differently we were finally able to dine-in. 

The dining area is outdated and run down. Seating is in need of repair. There was duck tape covering tears in the seat upholstery. The food was fresh and good. That's the only saving grace. Our server also forgot our drinks. 

This could be a better place if they fixed the problems with service and repaired the dining area. Better for carry out.

I got the shrimp plate with basmati rice. The shrimp was cooked to perfection - tender, juicy, and well seasoned. The Greek salad that came with it was fresh and crispy. The Greek dressing is really tasty too - right amount of acidity and sweetness. I ordered a side of tzatziki sauce and for me that was not good. It tasted too milky (like the aftertaste of cowmilk) but maybe that's how it's supposed to be? Other tzatziki sauces I've had were not like that but I suppose it all depends on your preference. Got a side of sweet tea as well; it was right amount of sweetness and no weird after taste. Highly recommend this place!

I'll start with the pros ... the Pharmacy and CVS Minute Clinic is wonderful. As for the rest of the store, I don't know what happened, but ever since they renovated this store, it has gone downhill. It used to be amazing with such friendly staff and a clean set-up. Now, not so much. Turnover is unbelievable, customer service is subpar,  and as far as setting up the store, still, months later (I think it's been close to 4 months, if not more), there are canisters out with product. At least product is starting to get to the shelves, but it's not anything to what it used to be and for this area especially, I have very high expectations. The store is a mess. I've given many chances before posting this, but perhaps with more reviews, something will be done. Whatever changed, this CVS lost a lot of great customer service people, and that's very unfortunate.

I love exploring and finding new and healthy places to eat. Being vegan and GF, it can be difficult at times. When I say clean juice is hands down one of the best places to eat, I mean it. I find myself craving their avocado toast and detoxifying one all the time. The staff is so sweet, they truly care for the customers and are knowledgeable! I find myself making trips there all the time. The energy and atmosphere is unlike any other.  best place in town!!

I hate to ruin a perfect rating for an adorable creperie in a farmers market but unfortunately I was not impressed. I wanted to like this place so much but when I finally got to try it, it did not live up to my expectations. The crepes are much thicker and oilier than they should be. The griddle is smothered with butter before cooking the crepe. Presentation is not very pretty. I had a bacon egg and Swiss crepe. I loved how they put in a lot of the stuffing but they used hard boiled eggs which just felt weird. My friend ordered the French toast crepe which was MUCH better. The juice was...nasty. I felt like I was drinking celery. The staff is so friendly though and I think this place has so much potential. I would come back and order other crepes. Maybe it was a bad day for them.

Extremely rude workers. They didn't want any more customers so they literally locked the doors and no one from the inside could get out. When we politely told them this was a fire hazard they were very rude back and told us otherwise, meanwhile they struggled with the key and the door for 10 minutes the next time a customer attempted to leave.... clearly a fire hazard. It was crowded because an Eagles game was going on, and there was some cheers and chants however no one was being rowdy. A worker starts yelling at the entire restaurant telling us he was going to turn off all the TVs and to be quiet. Absolutely ridiculous to do to paying customers. Shouldn't you be happy your restaurant is crowded probably for the first time? Also the pizza tasted like trash so there's that.
The service is exceptional. Food is very good, which is what you'd expect from the price. Ambience was nice as well. They had live music on Saturday night that was a decent touch.

What can I say... This place is awesome! The shop is owned by a husband and wife duo who really are the "Yodas" of teas & spices. You can taste all of the flavors and they will make recommendations based on what you like. Shopping in this store is really a sensory experience!

This was actually a really cute spot for weekend brunch. It was really affordable and the portions were pretty generous. The place wasn't very busy surprisingly for a Sunday (we were there around 11), and we were able to be seated right away. But when we left we saw people waiting outside.
I got a small plate of one their traditional breakfasts - an eggs and bacon situation. My friend got a larger plate and she actually couldn't finish all of it so I had some. 
My one subpar note about the spot is that they only serve coffee, no cappuccinos or anything fancy. But the mimosas were really good, so I would definitely come back for that.
This is an ideal spot if you're doing a casual brunch and don't want to break the bank!
So incredible. We went on our way to Asheville and loved it so much, we grabbed it again on the way back. Stop here if you're in Nashville; you will not be disappointed.
Chicken burger and pretzelwich are a must try. We loved them both. Can't get enough of the salted caramel latte from Tebellas!!

Have ordered take out a few times now. Each time we have not been disappointed. There's a drive through to pick up your take out which is very convenient. We have ordered the burgers, a Cuban, and the club sandwich. All have been excellent and the prices there are great! Super cheap for good food!
