I got the Spicy Garlic Stir Fry.   First, the layout of the store is great.  They have several ordering stations so a pretty good option during the busy lunch hour.  Overall, the sauce was pretty good but definitely not worth $8.  They literally give you two or three pieces of chicken, handful of vegetables and rest of the dish is all noodles.   If you're going to check out this place, perhaps go with the salad or frozen yogurt option.

Excellent experience ! I have a dog and she sheds and drools everywhere. The Boss crew got it all and had it done quickly.
Highly recommend and will return!
Thanks

Super cute little old shop. This guy did a great job repairing my husbands leather boots. He had holes in the soles of the boots and the heels were beaten up. The shoes were fixed when he said they would be (dropped off on a Monday, ready by Friday) and they look great. He even put the leather dressing on  to finish it up which I felt was a nice extra service that was included in the shoe repair. Really glad we went there and will definitely be back when the soles wear out again.

First of all my star rating should be more like 3 1/2 or 4 . Love the place right off the bat great service . The place is very upbeat and the decor is pretty sweet. The only cons I have are that it's pretty pricey for what you get food wise. I got the fried chicken it came with 1 piece and some mashed potatoes. And a small side salad. I'm a big guy so probably 1 more piece would have been what I was looking for. It had a great taste overall so it wasn't lacking there. But the cost is what gets you $16.50 for fried chicken! Yup that's what I said lol. Love the beer selection they only carry local brews and some from surrounding areas . And they don't carry Bud , Miller , none of the regular beers that everyone is brainwashed into drinking. And they will run you $5 or $6 a beer . So once a again they are on the high side in my eyes. The only thing I can think of is that it's in Franklin and only rich people live there. So that's why know one says much about price.

Great sports bar, awesome food, good drinks and very cute wait staff.

TVs line the wall so there really isn't a bad seat in the house either.

If you're vegetarian or vegan, this is the restaurant for you! The dishes are specifically catered to being fresh and either gluten free, vegan, or vegetarian. 

The best time to go would be brunch in my opinion, and then lunch, and finally dinner.

I'm not the cleanest/healthiest eater, so I wouldn't prefer dinner because of 80% of every dish having vegetables instead of some good ole' meat/pasta. 

There's a banana based smoothie that basically could suffice as a meal in itself because of how thick and delicious it is, but could also be a starter if you're super hungry. 

The flourless chocolate cake for dessert is exceptional - You could probably order two on a cheat day and not feel guilty. 

The only thing is the main courses in True Food are pretty small for their price, and I wouldn't necessarily go for the bowls/pizza/tacos because of the minimal quantity. They are all fresh, organic food I believe- but I'm definitely a girl who likes a larger/heartier portion!

Helpful and friendly staff. Very welcoming. My son is just getting into role playing  games and I know nothing. The guy was really helpful and supportive.  The prices seemed very fair too. We'll be going back for sure.

Evergreen is a wonderful company and Carlos is easy to contact and work with.  The work was exceptional and they left my yard spotless after the tree removal.  They have earned my loyalty for all future tree and landscaping needs.  If you want service that is impeccable and the job completed the correct way, hire Evergreen.  You will not be disappointed!

This restaurant/bar/winery is pretty chic and at a prime location at CWE. I have never had wine here (ironically) or sit outside in the beautiful front yard, but I think the service is great. Last time I was here, my friend and I ordered the salad for lunch, which is good but not wow. The menu is listed in the ipad and you sorta order it yourself then give the ipad to the waiter/waitress. I kind hope the menu could be more unique to match up to how unique this place is in terms of its concept (wine bar) and location.

Giving this place a one star because the service was terrible. We stood around waiting for 15 min. With no word. Mind you there were two people on the other side of the counter. Both made eye contact with me but didn't bother to greet or tell me a thing. I then asked what the procedure was, whether we sat or ordered here. The girl then mentioned we can order at the counter and to let them know where we would like to be seated. As we approached the counter to order I asked if they could make a simple egg scramble for my 5-year-old son. They mentioned they couldn't make such a plate and that only what was on the menu could be served. 

Honestly, we had decided to walk in the cafe because my son was starving, only for them to not acknowledge us and to later deny our request for a simple scrambled egg!
No one was wearing a mask inside, in the building. Guys just grabbed my phone without asking to look at reservation. He was unbelievably rude!
Super cute find! Thanks Yelp! Very small place, you can easily miss it. Only about 12 tables inside, nice sized bar with 3 large TV's. Food was great! I would come back!

This was our first time here....OMG....we will be back. Highly recommend the toasted Ravs, they are to die for. We chose the brisket & pulled pork. Can you say tender? Meat was not dried out, so didnt even need bbq sauce. Also has the mac , cheese, it's made with Ritz crackers, it was very good. We also got the bbq beans. They were different then we are used to, but they were good as well. We had awesome service, our waiter was great at explaining the items on the menue & made good recommendations and he was very attentive. Didnt have to wait to have our drinks refilled, he was there taking care of it before they were empty. Cant wait to go back & try a few other items next time.

the first time going here the food was awesome and the drinks were great every since then they're always out of the food that I order the manager is completely useless he couldn't care less very disappointing it should not take 3 hours to have dinner

Food is too expensive for the same quality, oyster is not fresh compare to the other restaurants near by. Beer comes with a smelly glass and after I ask them to change it, it still smell horrible I can't drink the beer!
Don't recommend to go for sure !
This is a great place to drink and eat. Our waitress, Paige, was incredibly friendly and helpful. Will definitely be coming back!

What a dive, and I don't mean that in a bad way! A perfect 5-star roast beef po boy and two-star ambiance = a solid four stars in my book!  

I ate in the smoky bar, not realizing that the cozy little "dining room" existed, until I ran up the steps to use the restroom.  (A restroom, I might add, that one enters through the narrowest door I've ever seen, and I spend a lot of time on boats!)

Can't wait to go back for another roast beef, and maybe, an awesome looking firecracker shrimp po boy. That one may call for two Abita Ambers to wash it down!

This was my first experience at this restaurant. Small place but very well maintained. Good decor and ambience.  Staff was good. Me and my husband were happy to see a lot of vegetarian options.  We ordered Thai iced tea, Veggie Spring rolls, Veg basil fried rice and Veg Red curry. Everything was delicious and amazingly perfect. Loved this place.

Wow, I had the smoked chicken and the Gouda Mac and cheese, delicious!! My husband got the sweet potato cornbread as a side and it was so good!! Loved this place, can't wait to come back!! Sat outside, nice staff, great food, couldn't ask for more.

I finally get to see Barns foundation myself! Amazing!! I read about this place so much from news paper and art magazine. I heard this place is legendary and was excited to see it myself. 

What to say, it is modern and beautiful. And as we heard, they have so so so many art to display. They displayed every art as Dr. Barns had them in his mansion. You feel like you are waking into gorgeous house with full of collection. I was just stunned. There were so many things to see, the time slot we had felt so short. 

Mom and dad had amazing time too. My mom did say this was the best museum she's ever been too. I can't agree more. 
For example,  Met or Louvre museums are so massive that I was already too intimidated by them and I got to walk way too much to enjoy them all. I got physically too tired to enjoy their art. 

Barns foundation was smaller and displayed so many art in one wall since they recreated exact same layout. I did not have to hop around too much and still got to see a lot. That was big plus. Now I have to schedule my next visit there to educate myself.

I think ulta needs a new hiring policy. I brought my 14 year old daughter in to have her brows done for her birthday. This lady name maggie took her in. My daughter sat in her station and she dropped a tool on her and damaged her dressed. No apologizes and then she had the nerve to tell her it was her felt for moving. Lily was uncomfortable the way Maggie had her sitting. She had to move because she was slipping. Then she was done, my daughter left and I heard this incompetent ulta worker tell her co worker that she didnt tip. And that my daughter was a f***** brat. I dont know what kind of business your running but maybe you should drug test. Or hire people with class and respect. My daughter wanted to buy some hair and nail produced in your store and I refused. I will never spend my money in your store again. I will inform my family and friends about your careless services. And rude workers. And I'll be getting ahold of the BBB. ZERO STARS FOR THIS PLACE OF BUSINESS

I never write reviews for anything but I literally signed up for yelp just to write this review. I was really scared when I found out I had to get a root canal. I've had dental anxiety for years which caused a killer toothache. I called Dr. Hodges office and by the time I got off the phone with the receptionist, I already felt calm. They even called me before my appointment to offer me a different time to see if it worked better with my schedule which shows just how caring the staff is. When i went for the root canal, I was so scared I was practically shaking in the office. I was greeted by Brittney (Sorry Britt if i spelled your name wrong!). She was incredibly sweet and friendly, making sure I had a magazine or something while I waited and she took my x-rays quickly, explaining why she was doing everything she was doing. Dr.Hodges came in and instantly put me at ease. He is so funny and walked me through every single thing he was doing. He numbed my gums with gel first and I didn't even feel the needle nor did I even see it! The actual root canal was over so quickly I felt like I was there for 20 minutes. It was totally painless!! He constantly checked on me, asking me if I was ok and explaining why he was using each tool. If you need a root canal, please do yourself a favor and go to him. I have been so afraid of dental procedures for so long and I can definitely say that because of this pleasant experience, I no longer am! Thanks Dr. Hodges!!!

Just love eating here, and drinking a Bloody Mary made at the station with all those ingredients! The menu is full of great brunch items. I had fried chicken with a biscuit and could only finish half. Saved the second half for lunch the next day. Yum! Twice!
I win there after 4 years, food is alright like usually before, we always enjoy there Long Island Ice tea, i like the chicken n fries, very crowded, we wait around 20 minutes for food,overall good.

Ho Wok is not fancy, nor a delivery place.  It is purely a neighborhood fast Chinese take out place in Wesley Chapel and New Tampa.  Really great people own thus pkace.  They are from Gyangxhou, the Southern most city in China which is kn I wn for its food and great chefs. Jack He is the owner with his brother and re l at Ives who all work there and are 100% involved.  

The food is reliably very good, nice portions. And we'll priced.  If you are looking for a sit down with tables idea seevice, this is not it. If you want fast take out with a hearty neal, for sure.  Be reasonable and patient when you call especially during the busy dinner hours. 

All these other reviews that are looking for a car delivery service - tell me which other places offer that? The answer is they don't exist.  It's is not Domino's or a pizza place.

We've been to this place just once in warm weather when we could dine outside, we love the food since it's made on sourdough bread. The biggest bummer about it is that it's only open on weekdays and closed on weekends..

I know this location is new and I think they may still be working out some kinks. I ran thru here the other day.  In the afternoon, at not a very busy time. Ordered two toasted bagels with cream cheese, one thin one regular, a latte and a smoothie. I got two regular bagels and if they were toasted it was a very light toast, but they were tasty. There was an issue getting my coffee made, it took a long time and then they made a chai latte by accident, so I had to wait for them to fix that.  All while I sat in the drive thru looking at Starbucks.  The staff were nice and apologetic.

This is by far the worst airline I have encountered! They don't even deserve one star. They charge you for every little thing, even printing your own boarding pass. 
I printed my boarding pass from home and when I got to security, it didn't even scan. They sent me all the way back to the spirit counter to have it printed. Complete waste of time and a hassle. 
Now we finally board the plane. Turns out there is "something wrong with the tail of the plane that the captain did not discover until now, 3 minutes before take off. So now the flight is delayed indefinitely. Unbelievable airline. Complete waste of money. On top of that they charge you double of other airlines, for luggage.
Pay a little extra and fly a different airline.

This place was wonderful for my fiancés birthday dinner in late October. This place is interesting, fun, and oh so cozy. We made reservations in advance and got a nice table (a corner booth on the second floor by the bar). Service was excellent, food was great, and drinks were delicious. Can't wait for an excuse to go back!

We have been to Trattoria Moma several times over the past few years. The food and servers have always been great. It's BYOB, and on Tuesday and Sunday nights there is an unbelievable 4-course dinner for just $35. And there is no much food that we always have enough left over for a whole dinner for two the next night. Two reasons why this is not a 5-star review: Sometimes the dishes are a bit slow coming out, and if the place is really crowded -- on weekend nights, usually -- the noise level can be stratospheric, and conversation is difficult. If the noise problem could be fixed, maybe with acoustic tiles, definitely 5 stars!

Len Lear, Philadelphia

This place is Cajun by name only. Basically a standard greasy spoon, not horrible by any  means, but if you want great food you can't make at home this isn't the place for you. I will probably never go back since I wanted a cajun breakfast. Maybe at least a monte Cristo, but nothing.

Ordered the panang curry and it was disappointing. Maybe I should have stuck with non curry items. After all, someone else did say that the green curry was meh.
I may be back to try one of the stir fry or noodle dishes, especially while Bangkok Thai is closed for remodeling.
Great for routine care such as vaccinations. Not so great for when your cat gets injured and needs medical attention. Direct us to Metropolitan Vet Clinic in Oaks for removal of an embedded tick.

kinda ended up here on a whim. but i have to admit im pretty glad. It was st. patricks day so the crowds were pretty thick in fishtown. we were just looking for a bite to eat and maybe a cocktail. 

the crowd was heavy but we got a seat right away, the beer selection is insane, i mean they have alot of beers, something for everyone no doubt. i started with a flight mixed with dark beers and ipas. i settled on youngs chocolate stoudt! extremely tastey. the server was knowledgable and willing to help with beer selections for my date, which was very helpful.

they were serving brunch, so the menu was restricted, but there are enough selections to choose from. the server read the specials and i was sold on the chicken and waffles. a complete belgian waffle, with a chicken and veg in a mushroom sauce! absolutely deliscious! It really hit the spot i was glad we stumbled along this joint!

The short: favorite tea is Raindrop light sweet, favorite nom is house rolls, bring your own tumbler/cup for 10% off


I LOVE Kaleisia! I used to go daily for several orders of tea. The first time you go and feel overwhelming with the tea wall and all the choices. I'd recommend a regular rain drop, light sweet with lychee or Thai tea with boba. Those are generally crowd pleasers. 

For first timers you can chose to have your tea hot or iced, then you chose a sweetness level (none, light, medium, a lot), and a "texture" (you can opt for no texture or boba, lychee jelly, passion fruit jelly, and lychee poppin boba; the last three being on the sweeter side) and size regular, large or TEAtanic (pun-tastic).

They support small businesses and have yoga, live music, and events. There's a calendar inside and on their website you can check out.  Inside is a quiet shoe-free area perfect for studying or working. 

On Tuesdays there is a lunch special called TasTEA Tuesday where you can get lunch and a tea for a discounted combo price.

Recommendations: House rolls and the turkey panini are my go to food items. For tea I like Raindrop, Winter Wonderland (seasonal), Blue Mist, Immunity (feel like you're getting sick and need a boost?), Morroccan Mint, Raspberry Champagne, Cucumber melon (light and refreshing), Sunburst (fruity and good for Tevanna lovers), Strawberry Green and Thai Tea. 

There's vegan/vegetarian opens, try the vegan cupcakes! There are also 3-4 teas of the month usually a green tea, a black tea and a rooibos/herbal tea option to choose from and they'll be slightly cheaper for that month. 

LPT: bring your own cup and get 10% off your tea order! (If you've forgotten since the beginning of this epic post.)
Amazing food, relaxed but fun atmosphere. Very laid back people!!! My father and I are making it a bimonthly tradition to come here on Sunday's!

Wow! What a hidden gem! The green noodles were so good, had that el dente texture to it. The grilled whole fish was on point, not too expensive $20 where any where else it would have easily been $35. The shrimp I got was good too including all the sauces they give! Everything at this restaurant was on point and the prices were reasonable.

I wish I still lived in Philly so I can come back more often but can't wait to visit again later this year.

Let me preface by saying that I've been eating Vietnamese pho for a few years and that this was my first time trying the Chinese equivalent. I got the basic beef noodle soup and it was good... BUT the broth was bland. I wanted to doctor-it-up (did I just say that...? I'm not even a soccer mom) and I had the choice of soy sauce (pass), sriracha (I didn't want spice but I added a little anyway and it ended up being too much) and fish oil (I added some of this and got a bit of salty flavor. I was fiending so badly for some hoison and a lime! It would've set off the whole thing!

Anyway, the meat was nice even with fatty bits on it. The noodles were surprisingly chewy (my 1st time trying hand drawn noodles), but they were cool. A teapot would've been awesome... eh but alas it wasn't a pho spot. Other than that, I just had a cup of water. Maybe a cool drink would've made things more interesting. Otherwise, the decor isn't as dreary as some other places I've seen in Chinatown. I might be back.

Don't forget a GIANT thing of water, hand sanitizer, bug spray, and wear your sunscreen. Besides that, bring cash and to go containers for storing any food you don't finish. 

It was amazing this year. Tried pepper soup from the Liberian booth, and of course meat pie and moin moin from the Nigerian booth. Tried chicken vatapa from the brazilian booth and some delicious falafel too. Everything was delicious.

So today was my first time ever gettin a Brazilian wax and i must say that my experience at the wax center was amazingly almost non painful. Amy-jo did my waxing and she was wonderful. At this boutique they use a hard blue colored wax also they do not use strips which makes this procedure just about non painful.  I'm so glad that I chose to have my first waxing experience here, and I will definitely be back for more!

I love to come here on days when I don't have time to make my lunch.  Not going to write too much because I have spent the last few years studying food nutrition, and reading all the labels.  To the point where when I talk to people about what they can do to help different goals or illness they have, that I have many of the labels memorized.  As far as quality a few of the farmers at the Franklin farmers market is the best I have found especially Zadok farms.  The meat and produce here is often pretty good, and always a better choice than McDonald's across the street.  Enjoy the delicious food and if you have a specific question feel free to send me a message happy yelping!

This place is ok but you have to order everything from the bar, if you want like a soda then they will give you a small cup and you'll get one refill for free and after that you have to pay again for more soda. this bar makes q-time which is in las cruces, nm a better bar to go to

Soooo disappointed! It's been a while since I have been here and I was really looking forward to it as we were goi g for my birthday. The chips and salsa were not great. Cold chips aren't very tasty and the salsas had much to be desired. I ordered chicken enchiladas and they were so dry! I only ate one of the three. The others in my party experienced the same thing...dry food without much flavor with the exception of the fajitas (the ones that include chorizo) and the ate was swimming in grease. I know chorizo is fatty, but they could have drained the grease. I am so sad...I used to live their food and will not go back again.

This is a must-visit on any trip to Center City. When we have friends from out of town, we definitely take them here. (And before we were residents we definitely went here too!) There's some famous stalls, and some yet to be discovered, but one thing is for sure - this is an ace food hall and there is something for everyone.

Be warned, parking is a hassle, so come mentally prepared to patiently drive around for a spot or pay for garage parking.

The last time I went to an IHOP was over 5 years ago (in the Vancouver area). It was the best waffles I have ever eaten. When I found out IHOP was opening here in Edmonton I was pretty excited. I've put off going until now (today) because with a restaurant just opening up brand new, there are always kinks to work out.
Pretty much every table was taken. It was a pretty busy night. I was seated at my table and after three minutes a man (who I'm going to assume is the supervisor) asked me if I had been asked for a drink. I said no (I didn't say I had just sat down). He took my drink order and I also gave him my food order since I was already ready. 

I ended up ordering the stuffed french toast meal (comes with hasbrowns, eggs, bacon). I didn't order the waffles I had because I didn't want to crush my memory if it turned out to be not as good as I remembered. 

I waited about 20 mins for my food to arrive (I was told 10-15 mins). Because it was so busy it's understanding to have it take a little longer. 

I found my meal quite pleasant. I really enjoyed the scrambled eggs which was very creamy. 

While eating my waitress asked me three times how everything was (which I usually find annoying.... I like to eat in peace) but I suppose it shows that they are being attentive. 

Although my experience was good, I noticed that I received, ate, and left before two tables around me even received their food (they were sitting down before I got to my table). I'm going to assume my food came much earlier because my order was taken by the supervisor and that I was a table of one while the other two tables were four.

I'd go back and eat here again.

I want to reinforce that Ayers Repairs customer service goes above and beyond.  From that standpoint, they are an excellent shop. And, ultimately, the repairs were accomplished. Therefore, I am giving them one more star.

I made a return trip to Jacques Imos this evening after having come for the first time over 6 years ago. I have to say I was majorly disappointed this time around. The wait was about an hour, which we expected. Once we were seated, the service was fine. The cornbread muffins were delish. Then things started going downhill. I ordered their drink special for the evening: a watermelon mojito. It was very underwhelming. It tasted like flat soda. I ordered the mushroom stuffed salmon, which I included a picture of. It was messy and gross. The ginger sauce + black beans + fish was just strange. The flavor combination was off. The spinach salad was supposed to come dressed in a viniagrette dressing, but it was a sweet sesame tasting dressing instead. The other sides were good, but served in TINY portions. I had the smothered cabbage and red beans and I wished I'd had more since those were the only items of my meal that were good. My sister had the smothered chicken for an entree and found it to be underseasoned. In summary, the sides and complimentary cornbread were good. The rest? Not so much. Definitely wasn't worth the hour wait.

Fast and friendly place not to much traffic so I was abel to get in and out of there with in 5 minutes. The cappuccino tasted great and it was just how I like it. The staff was great very welcoming, I've been there a couple of time and I sure want to go back for more. Keep up the good work

Delicious food, nice rich broth, and at a good price. The first time for lunch was very quick and everything was as ordered. However the second time they ran out of charsiu and the wait was a bit long but they have us some food while we waited. Overall really good!
The food is amazing and the service is top notch. All the food Sierra recommended was delicious and she is a great server. Would definitely recommend and will be coming back
I like this place!! Very good food! You should try their specials. They are not pricey and delicious. Worths your vist

Went there for drinks and appetizers. Martini selection was great. it seemed a little light on the booze but the concoctions were great. I had a spicy cucumber margatini. We sampled a flat bread pizza which was excellent and an order of chicken lollypops also delicious.

Straight away, ask for Gus - server extraordinaire.

It's food, but it's mediocre.   They were open when we checked in so I had a burger and she had a personal pizza.   
The pizza was very much frozen pizza and the burger was ok...the fries were actually the best part of the meal.  
The service and convenience were a 4 and the food was a 2...
Good for families who need to provide food ASAP.

Great People, experts at hair styling, will take care of the whole family with one stop! Check these guys out they will impress you with the quality of their work and the friendly atmosphere they do it in !

I consider myself an Italian food snob. With a 92 year old grandmother who does nothing but cook, restaurants rarely get better than what I get at home. Stogie Joes food is simply fantastic, I've eaten most of what is on the menu and I'm always impressed at the consistency of how delicious everything is. Their pizza is my favorite of all time with the most perfectly crisp square crust you'll ever come across. I always try at least one of the additions that are on the menu that day, and I'm ALWAYS blown away. Nothing too fancy just good authentic non-pretentious Italian food. It's grandmoms favorite place too, we recommend it to everybody!

BLACK OWNED, LIVE DJ,FROZEN DRINKS, IMMACULATE FOOD!! NEED I SAY MORE?? I SHALL!! Rustic modern country vibe on the outside..once you walk through those doors it's a completely different vibe.. A laid back black owned lounge/club!! Has a full bar with a full time bar tender. Frozen alcohol slushie machines on the side and a plethora of alcohol to chose from. Try their version of the Hurricane 

Now the food!! Try the spinach!! I am.an avid spinach hater, but this spinach right here....is the S**t!!! Its so full of flavor!! Can't even describe the seasonings I just know I wanted more spinach and I would never in my life thought hear myself say that!! Chicken and waffles was my entree. The chicken lemon peppered, juicy, tender and crispy. The waffle was made with vanilla and what seemed to be house made syrup that was very good. My lady had the shrimp and grits with that great spinach. Both were IMMACULATE! We will be going back many more times! My most favorite restaurant by far!!

We came here for some dessert after eating at the Sugarfire Smokehouse next door. I had a slice of the Sugarfire pie. It was good, but a bit TOO sweet. Although, I can see how someone with a real sweet tooth would love it. Might try the key lime pie next time. The employees were very nice and helpful and the place was cute. Would recommend.
Just never changes  we tried  this place again after 12 years. Cheep cheep they just want to take every dollar from you.

Guthrie, the owner, is amazing! He is so warm and friendly! There is an open kitchen so there is a connection between the staff and customers. The grits are DELICIOUS! By far the best in the city. Bottomless coffee in huge mugs! A great cafe for a healthy breakfast!

This company is on time, fast and very professional!  They made sure we did everything to our own specifications.  We were very happy with the end product and would recommend them to anyone in a heart beat!!!  Thank you for your service!!

How often do you go to a restaurant anymore and come away going "that was just so much fun!!"???

Let's start with the most important part: the food. Unbelievably good stuff. We enjoyed the watermelon bruschetta, an excellent cheese and charcuterie plate, mussels, and a ribeye we split. All of it was fantastic. Portions were very generous (we probably could've removed an item from our order and still been full). Some standouts: the espresso encrusted venison pastrami and the caramelized garlic on the charcuterie plate were special, and things we'll come back for. The blue cheese butter on our ribeye took a really really good steak and made it a great steak. 

The drink menu was a lot of fun. The old fashioned was one of the better versions of that drink I've ever had (chocolate bitters were a solid touch). We sampled several different cocktails, and thoroughly enjoyed all of them. 

The service was outstanding. Extremely friendly and knowledgeable waitstaff that never rushed us and made sure we had everything we wanted. Our waiter took the time to explain everything about our options and really seemed invested in making sure we enjoyed our time. 

The decor was a lot of fun. I mean, the sink in the men's room is an old tire with stones in it... who isn't going to get a kick out of that? 

We had high expectations going in: this was an anniversary dinner for us, and the place came highly recommended by a coworker whose opinion I trust. Everything about our experience was a homerun, and we can't wait to come back!

Unbelievable experience!!! I knew a little bit about diamonds when I was looking for an engagement ring but not a whole lot.  I went to Jewelers Row in Philly where I felt like I was getting a constant sales pitch, and the people didn't care about what I was looking for as much as they did the sale.  A friend recommended I go to Morton & Rudolph and talk to Jack & Carrie.  I, and my wife,  couldn't be happier that I took his advice and went there.  They took the time to explain everything about diamonds to me and what the different ratings means, so I fully understood what I was looking for and what I wanted.  I showed them pictures of the style of ring that my wife liked, and they were able to help find a perfect match for it.  They were very laid back and never forced me to make a decision on anything until I was 110% sure on what I wanted.  I would recommend that anyone looking for not only an engagement ring but any jewelry to go to Morton & Rudolph you won't regret it.  It is the only place that I will go to in the future.  I even had 4 friends all go there and they got engagement and wedding rings there too!!!

I liked the idea of this place and was eager to give it a try, but I'm sad to say I wasn't won over. The prices are high for the small amount of shareable food you're given. The food isn't bad, but it's not super exciting either (except for the garlic fries - they had the perfect amount of garlic). Our ID's were checked on the way in; understandable, as this is a 21+ restaurant; however, we then had to pull them out again at the table 2 minutes after we'd stashed them away. The sharing of servers is slightly annoying, as you're persistently asked by someone different if you need anything; not bad when you do, but otherwise bordering on annoyance when you're finishing up and 3 different servers have asked if you want another beer (not necessarily their fault, it's just that they don't know the status of your table since they haven't worked with you from the beginning). Ultimately left the restaurant a bit disappointed in having spent so much money and not feeling satisfied. The worst part? I distinctly recall our bill being $67; I left a $13 tip; how was I charged $86??? I didn't get a receipt so it's hard to argue, but I feel even more ripped off. Even if someone forgot to add something to the bill (although I don't know how this would happen since we received all our items from the kitchen), the final amount I signed is the final amount, period. You can't add on afterwards; that's just poor business practice. Word to all: get a copy of your receipt before you leave, and check your bank statement after. :(

Food is great, but not brunch. We ate here 2 different days during our visit. We went on a Sunday forgetting they do a brunch menu. We had gone on another Sunday (previous trip) and they did fish tacos for us anyway, but this particular Sunday they said 'no can do.' So we ordered off the brunch menu and it wasn't very good. Went back the next day and got the fish tacos and some enchiladas and both were fantastic! My recommendation for the enchiladas is to eat the beef/red sauce first as the beef is sketchy. Then eat the chicken/verde sauce (yum!) and then the shrimp...awesome. And you can't go wrong with the fish tacos. You just can't get too many of them if you are a fish taco fan.

I took a drive to Ted Drewes Frozen Custard in St. Louis, MO to get a Concrete. Their Concretes are so thick they are served upside down! It is a perfect treat on a a hot day! Ted Drewes is Celebrating 90 years in business! If you are passing through St. Louis, MO it is definitely worth the stop.

I ordered leaf springs and bushings from quadratec. My order confirmation email did not say any of my parts were out of stock and (after a week) I did not receive any emails stating that any of my parts were being shipped from the manufacturer.

I called Quadratec and was informed my parts were on back order. When I asked why I was not informed of this, I received a snarky reply and a noticeably insincere "Sorry", so I canceled my order. I give two stars instead of one because at least they never charged my card for the parts. I will not be ordering from them again.

I have never stayed at the Koa. We only go over here to get our propane tank filled, its not a bad deal and its a lot nicer then what you would get going to a large chain store in swapping out a tank. The people who work at the Koa are very nice, and so helpful.

"Tickled pink" by our stay at the Don Cesar. Fun and relaxing place for our girls' beach trip away from the Chicago cold.

***What can improve:
Value - For the price we paid, our junior suite was small. Narrow beds and no door to close off the other room. Layout of the junior suite was not the best. In room wifi was awful.
Housekeeping - Service was inconsistent. We often had to ask for more fresh towels and toilet paper because it was never enough.
Stores on site - While the stores on site had great variety, they are very pricey and the ladies working there kind of hover by you making you feel a little uncomfortable and pressured.

****The Good:
Food and drink - Delicious food and drink. Pricey, but everything we tasted on-the property, from the Rowe Bar, to the poolside bar and restaurant and the coffee and ice cream on-site were delicious. The crab cake sandwich at Rowe Bar was my favorite and so good I had to get it twice on our stay. The Un-Berrybull cocktail with fresh fruit is very refreshing.
Location - Fabulous spot. Walks on the beach, in either direction, are amazing. The sunsets here are so beautiful and the uniquely pink hotel make for a wonderful view.
Amenities - Great fitness classes and fitness center, with free weights, as part of the resort fee. Nice touch with the eucalyptus infused towels.
Pool - Two pools are heavenly and plenty of chairs to lounge in. The pool is warm and inviting. The hot tub is one of the biggest I've seen.
The fabulous service!

*****FIVE STAR STANDOUTS:
The Don should be a training ground for excellent customer service. Standouts, for me, included:
- Farahn by the pool is fabulous, friendly and very helpful.
- Lavette in service and front desk was so sweet and kind on our first couple of days checking in. Champagne at check in? Yes please and thank you.
- Susan, guest experience, was so responsive and helpful, before we even arrived. Also Gift at the front desk.
- Kylin at Rowe Bar. Phenomenal server on Superbowl Sunday. We just loved her attitude and thoughtfulness.
- Apy and the ladies at the desk in the fitness center. Loved the yoga and dance classes.

Really love the endless beautiful views here. I can't wait to return one day.
Great local business. Great people to work with. Extremely knowledgeable. Able to repair my truck when others said they couldn't do. Wont hesitate to use them in the future.

My wife and I went with a large group. We all ordered from the menu.  The service was not good. The food was horrible. Many didn't finish their meal. Not one person had a good thing to say about this restaurant. The bread pudding, served in a paper bowl, was more like a glob of glue than a dessert famous in the South. We cannot imagine how this establishment competes in a city famous for excellent cuisine.

There good. You can dine in or take out. I just think the other pizza jointi rated was i tit bit better. Not by much so there bothis is is cool u can eat in if u wish but its small seats nothjng fancy. Non the least good place for pizza
Excellent hoagies!! Fresh rolls, quality meats and cheeses. It's worth a ride out to Avondale for Earls!

Booked multiple rooms through Expedia for a very special occasion: friend's 40th bday and he was proposing to his girlfriend. We all paid the same price, rooms ranged from updated large bath with huge bedroom and sitting area to a room in an alley the size of a king size bed. Service sucked. Continental breakfast gross. Walls are thin as paper and we exchanged sorority girls for crying babies. Babies, in New Orleans? Why? This place says they have WiFi. Nopety nope nope. Sometimes, not often, there is wifi.

Kept waiting for a half hour despite having an appointment. Then a simple transaction took ridiculously long and was not actually completed due to poor customer service, poorly trained staff and absurdly inefficient systems and processes.
My wife and I enjoy Liberty Union.  No problem parking. Good food, drink and nice people.

My boyfriend and I tried this place a few weeks ago. Seriously omg its delicious! We've had pizza, stuffed pizza, chicken and cheesesteak hoagies and homemade wings (so far)! One of the best wings ive had (ill put that sauce on everything) we ordered the mild. Staff is super friendly and delivery was on point and always on time. Ahhhh a mustttt try. Finally a pizza place that we both love in NE Philly! Its football season! Trust me order here...it will not disappoint!!! You've definitely gained new regulars!
Pretty decent Chinese food. I have had better but this is the best I have found in the area. The atmosphere is nice and there is never a wait.

Nice family friendly hotel. The outside pool and water slides is really nice. Nice view. The customer service is horrific to say the least. I have seen dead roaches on the bar and tables are usually sticky. Horrible food.

just now
Went to this location maybe 10 minutes ago and the workers there were pretty angry that I had asked for my glazed donuts to be warmed up the same way I have for the past 8 years. The cashier who was a Tall African American male did not give me my total, snatched my card at the window did not give me my receipt and just stared at me angrily until my order came up and then slammed the drive thru window in my face when I attempted to ask for my receipt. Along with him was a white young female and and older white female who were loudly talking badly about having to take 30 seconds to heat up a donut in the oven. These 3 definitely need to be trained because their customer service skills were non existent. It was far too early for them to have such a bad attitude management definitely needs to fix it

I don't care what anyone says, I love IKEA. 

I know the store can be stupid busy (particularly on evening, weekends, and during holidays - try coming on a weekday morning if you can). I know the store is huge and can get you lost and confused if you've never been there before (they're also renovating, so that just makes it more confusing). I also know that their furniture isn't the most long lasting furniture and trying to assemble it with those diagram instructions and an Alan key is like trying to solve a complex algebra equation while you're blindfolded and drunk.

Still I love Ikea. I love wandering around at the display models and sitting on the chars with my friends. I also love how all the books in the display model living rooms and bedrooms are in Swedish. I love the fact I was able to pick up a travel pillow, some packing bags, and a reusable shopping bag (best travel investment I've ever made) for $7 last time I was there. Ikea now has a free membership program and there are computer kiosks upstairs where you can register and get a membership card. I love that I can pick up furniture, art frames, candles, desk lamps, kitchenware and almost anything you might need for a home/office at a reasonable price. No the products, mainly the furniture, won't last 50 years, but I have a 13 year old poang chair and it's doing fine. I also love going upstairs to the cafe and getting a Daim torte and some lingonberry juice.

I used to live close to Ikea and would go there often. I don't visit Ikea as much, and I usually try to go when it's not as busy, but when I do go to Ikea I always enjoy the experience.

Wow. This place is a dump. I try not to be high maintenance, but it's gross. Room is dirty- carpets and sheets are stained. Place also smells bad. And it was advertised as having wifi, but there is none in my room. 

They try- they act like it's a really nice place, but it was probably nice in about 1983

Cozy little mom-and-pop shop on the corner that churns out fresh mediterranean food. No frills, just heaping portions of high-quality hummus, olives, hot peppers, lamb, chicken, and falafel served on styrofoam plates.

No lie, the scratch-made baba ghanoush was some of the best I've ever had. And they sell it by the pint! If you're in the neighborhood and have a hankering for light, refreshing Greek food, do yourself a favor and stop in.
One of the best meals I've ever had, chef Robert is amazing. Ordered the chefs prefixe menu , highly recommend!!!!

Am Ufer des Mississippi unweit des Jackson Square gibt es zwei Möglichkeiten, die typischen Beignets zu verkosten - der Österreicher würde "Polsterzipf" dazu sagen - ein in Fett herausgebackener Germteig / rechteckiger Krapfen mit einer Tonne Staubzucker oben drauf. die übliche Portionsgröße sind drei, zu zweit hat man allemal genug von einer Bestellung. Eine Schlange bildet sich hier den ganzen Tag, sie ist etwas kürzer als beim nahegelegenen Tipp aller Reiseführer. Die Bestellungen gehen schnell, die freundliche Mitarbeiterin hat trotz der Hektik ein freundliches Wort für die Besucher übrig und die Beignets sind ein Traum. Ein Tipp: Nicht essen, wenn es windig ist, der Staubzucker verteilt sich überall!

Love these guys! Got my first mountain bike in over tens yrs from here and they were awesome! Particularly the older gentleman ( who's not older just compared to some of the young guys there) and the really young guy who always helps me! They are my favorites. They know there stuff and they are patient! Prices are good also, don't go in there expecting internet prices though this is a B&M pro shop, don't be that guy. Thanks for great service guys!

I always enjoy my massages from Daria.  She knows my spots that need to be kneaded.  Sometimes I'm very energized after my massage; other times I'm so relaxed and go to bed earlier and sleep like a log.  Yesterday's massage was one of those.  I love the ear massage at the end.  Clients always get a full 60 minutes (and then some).

The Original Banana Foster baby!  We came here for dinner, didn't have reservations and were dress way more casual than the formal dining room.  Luckily, they sat us in the backroom/bar!  More than thrilled!  Had a great meal and then the highly anticipated banana foster!  They made it fresh at your table, and man oh man, you'll never experience something as delicious!  You must come and try this.

This place is run by total idiots.  I called tonight asking for delivery.  I live about one mile south of Kennedy.  Some Rhodes Scholar asked if I live south of Gandy in an obvious attempt to blow me off since they do not deliver south of Gandy.  There are multiple times that items are missing from the order and no one seems to care. Everyone on the counter just laughs when you tell them how screwed up your orders have been.  It is clear that no one in this dump cares.  The good news that I heard tonight is a Tate family member will
soon be there at night. It will help. It could not get any worse

This was a really nice little restaurant, good for big groups. The food was pretty delicious and the portion sizes were huge. The best part though was the little plastic turtles they put in your drinks!

Had Chilango at Top Taco: New Orleans last night, phenomenal! Their set up defeated nearly 39 other competitors from around the city, including some pretty major New Orleans heavy weights, for Most Authentic Taco. 
The grilled Cebolla, pickled onions & carrots they offered took me back to my Southern Arizona roots, something that rarely happens here in this wonderful city. The owner was a joy, and I was proud to cast my vote for them. Thrilled they won! Fabulous food, fabulous people!

After many hours of research we chose to go with Olympus Pools and couldn't be happier that we did.  Brian Fenimore was our pool designer and consultant and did an excellent job at listening to our wants & needs and coming up with an amazing design.  What we appreciated was that he didn't push features or add ons and made sure the pool was perfect for us and within our budget.  I'd also like to mention that the management and office team has been a total pleasure to work with and have been extremely responsive to all my calls & emails regarding various items.  So far so good, they start the excavation in a few days.  Will update as we go.
I really liked the food and atmosphere here. The catfish was good. We ate around 1 pm on a Friday and there was no wait. The server was attentive. I would go again.

Me and my girlfriend found this hidden gem a little ways from the water and we are sure glad we did. The service was great from Melissa and who I think was the owner checked on us a few times making us feel very at home. I would recommend Ryan's to anyone and everyone looking for a good bite here in Clearwater. My girlfriend had the Cali Benedict and I had the rueben both looked and tasted amazing.

Centrally located historic hotel within an easy walk to the French Quarter and St. Charles trolley.  

The staff takes hospitality to heart! Everyone is friendly and helpful with an eye for details.  You can even borrow an umbrella from the concierge if there's unexpected rain.  

The King suite was roomy with comfortable living and sleeping spaces with separate restrooms.  While it did not have a tub, the multiple shower heads made for a refreshing shower.  

Sazerac and Rooftop bars are a great way to unwind with added ambiance.  

I will be back during the holidays to see the Christmas displays and try the Spa.

Love this place, never ever had a bad experience here. Food is flavorful and comforting. Staff is warm and welcoming, and really seek to meet the expectations of their patrons. I recommend hands down for an experience at this place. From the fried chicken to the meatloaf -- a vacation for your tastebuds. I have to mention, I am a born and bread southern boy, so if I like the fried chicken -- it's real!

Let me tell you, Franco & Giuseppe Cucina Italiana was truly outstanding. Superb location. Everything I've tried up until now is incredibly good. Awesome. Great service and staff. Awesome! Will return and recommend this spot always.

Celebrated a birthday dinner here. Beautiful oceanview dining with great service. We had foie gras (room temperature spread), blue point oysters( great), cobia (local fish), grouper, and signature chocolate cake for two(mousse like filling). Everything was delicious. Atmosphere was nice without being too pretentious. Free valet parking, being inside the Sand Pearl Resort. Grounds were beautiful and right on the beach.

I LOVED this place when I was traveling every week. This morning I attempted to get an Americano before my 6am flight and was scolded for interrupting a very casual conversation between employees. The young lady that I had such a GREAT experience with times and times before was being upmost rude. I normally recommend you but will walk by in the future.

Max was great! He is a Veteran with a wealth of knowledge. Be sure to ask for him!!! He made the experience over the top. Friendly and fun. He even dropped us off at a restaurant after. Only 5 stars because I cannot give more.

This is normally our go to for a mid-priced steakhouse. The steaks are seasoned well, so they are flavorful. They also are decent quality, so are pretty tender. The cooks do a good job. We have been once or twice when service was slow, but for the most part / most of the time the service is really good. And Service has always been very friendly. I enjoy the bone-in ribeye. They also have good wings on the appetizer menu.

4.5 The red beans and rice with house made hot sausage was sooo good. Also the hush puppies were great. Everyone was nice and a good vibe I would come back. They were out of jambalaya at 6pm on a Monday so that was disappointing. Boyfriend had wings and said they were good. The fries were not great.. tasted frozen and could have used some seasoning.

The service was excellent and the food was very very good. Loved the burrata, it was a perfect balance of sweet, savory, spicy and creamy. The lemon-pepper fusili was also delicious and had a generous helping of parmesan cheese on top and some delicious prawns. I loved the Black ravioli with the lemon cream sauce. So why is it 4 stars? I have to insist that modern interpretations of Italian are a great way to broaden the spectrum of the genre. It is great to see such a restaurant like this in Boise. What I can't do is put it in the same rating as a place like Asiago's that does modern Italian cuisine without trying as hard to be cutting-edge. This is of course an opinion, because both are technically variations in the same cuisine, but I just like what Asiago's is doing better.

oh man.... WHAT can i say. i love this place's pizza to death, and, i although a lover of pizza, am very picky about my favors-- and this is simply number ONE for me right now in the area for to get as close to outside-state-tasting five star mom and pop pizza as you can get. always fresh upon pickup, never skimpy on fries, and all around an awesome pizza with no skimp on the sauce which i always love to high heavens and also the cheese tastes like a DREEEEAM... i tend to order extra because i like my pizza cheesy and they always manage to make it WONDERFUL with no skimp on that ooey gooey gobble up cheese (my FAV for any pizza!) i will always come back here. staff upon entering seems often kind of conserved not going to lie, a little too self preserved, not all too social and feels like you're getting kinda a little sense of judgement and seems nearly a little murmur-y when you walk in but the oldest owner of the establishment is always a sweetheart to me when i come in so that's a major plus. love this place so so so much. five stars all the way!

Hi Yelpers!  Here goes my first Yelp...  I went to New Orleans from L.A. and had a highly recommended this fried Chicken Restaurant out of Bourbon Street.   It was only a short 10 minute cab ride to this Willie Mae's Scotch House!  
 - PARKING was pretty good... cuz we went late and they close to 3pm (I think they close early at 3pm). If you go mid day it will be busy (I witness it on my second trip).
- SERVICE./ VIBE warm and welcoming.  Even though we waited for our food for a bit because we went late and they prepared our food on the spot, which we didn't mind at all because....  Our waiter was really nice and informative about the restaurant because he was the Grandson of WIllie Mae's.
- FOOD ....  Oh MAN!  It was the best fried ANYTHING I had in my life.  This is the BEST fried chicken you can find anywhere.  I tried all the sides such as the cornbread, green beans, fries and they were alright.  But the BUTTERED BEANS were fantastic! They were rich and sweet with a great buttered finish!
   This place was so good we had to go there the next day and we only vacationed in New Orleans for 4 days.   Aside from that our concierge (local) couldn't help but ask us to bring him an order of some fried chicken.  Which we did!

This review is strictly customer service based, as I have not picked up my pooch yet.  I was told that they had visiting hours.  However,When I called to ask if I could come visit my puppy I was told that visiting hours were 9 am - 5pm. I mentioned that I would not make it by then.  I thanked them anyway, although I was bummed. Then before I could say goodbye on the phone, a voice said "would you like me to get an update on her?"
It was like music to my ears. I was pleasantly surprised to get the update....it instantly put me at ease.  I appreciated the thoughtfulness very much.

The person behind the counter was positive and very helpful.  If I was rating the place purely on customer service, I would give it 4 stars.

In terms of the food, I ordered a bacon cheeseburger accompanied by a small side of fries.  I really wanted to love this burger.  Burgers are my junk food splurge of choice.  The reality is that this burger was fine-- nothing special.  It was decently cooked with decent ingredients.  I ate every bite.  However, Butcher Burger, at least on this day, was not as good as Burger Fi (4 stars) nor even close to the burger at Garces Trading Company (5 stars).

If you're in Chinatown and want a hamburger, this place works.  Just know that there are better burgers out there.

I went to Chilly Water against my better judgement as friends have told me it was mediocre at best. My fiancee won a giftcard to here from Yelp so we decided to check it out. It was really confusing walking in as we didn't know if we were supposed to find our own seats or be seated by somebody. Another customer told us that they grabbed their own table so we did the same. Our waitress was super rude and acted like it was a burden to take our order. She was not very helpful when we asked her questions at the bar. We got hummus which was pretty decent. I wish this place was better as the location is very cool.

I have attempted to reach the office for a WEEK now to no avail. All I need is my prescription to get new eye glasses! I have left a total of 4 voicemails, have called in the morning and afternoon. I've even gone as far as to asking the Costco eye department to see if they're in office to take my call. Still NOTHING! This is utterly unacceptable! I'll be going in store today to get my one and only prescription from them. STAY AWAY!!

So disappointed! We were given a table that felt like a punishment. Everything took forever to arrive. The cream container had crusty yellow coating from never being cleaned. All of that is excusable but the food sucked too. I could see what they were tying to do with the menu but everything we had was awful. The only reason they got a star is that our waiter was very nice and tried to be helpful and the decor is interesting.

Watched employees continuously hang up on people because they were too busy/unorganized.  In store visit was so so, as they messed up our order and kind of made up what they thought we wanted.  Tried a phone in order and got to experience being hung up on repeatedly.  Not impressed.

It looks like one star is the lowest possible rating.  This reviewing system would be better served if negative numbers were available.  Oh well.

So, if the choice is between dying on the curb out in front of St. Mary's Regional Medical Center or going through their doors... go on in.  If you should chose the first option, however, your friends and family will come to understand you made a perfectly reasonable choice.

This used to be an excellent hospital  ten years ago, that was prior to the current ownership.  The doctors are probably still just fine, it's the management that's the problem.  This hospital charged my wife $9482 for a Prolia injection for bone density issues, billing it out as "chemo/radiation".  True, this is an expensive medication but it is available at seven different sources in Reno for roughly $1025, not $9482.  The shot took less than a minute, just like a flu shot at Walmart. It was not "chemo", it was not "radiation".  It was a shot in the upper arm.

Then came the bills.  The $9482 was reduced by provider discounts and insurance payments to a net cost to my wife of $1359.71  Of course we were outraged and a year ago we sent via certified mail a letter of complaint and asked for a face to face meeting. As of this date, a year later, they have not yet replied to our certified mail request for a meeting.  At one point we were directed to someone named Jamii Uboldi who said someone named Maria Rodgers would be in contact with us.  That never happened.  We called and talked with Jesse E. ext #154 who said no one of the name Maria Rodgers worked for them.  Total run around, totally wasted call...which is something you'll get used to when trying to deal with St Mary's Regional Medical Center.  In the mean time my wife has had two subsequent treatments of the same medication, Prolia, from other sources and the net cost to her is less than $100 per injection, not $1359.71.

One thing St Mary's is very good at doing after failing to respond to your certified mail is turning your account over to a collection agency.  You can dispute the claim but the agency they use, specializing in medical collections, will do nothing to check into the substance of your dispute of the billing.  I sent them  several pages of information detailing our complaint.  They received it on 3/24/2016 and replied to me the very same day that they were "unable to conclude an investigation as we have not received sufficient information".  I'm sure that was a very thorough investigation by them of the facts we sent forward.   It may have actually taken minutes.

To summarize, avoid this institution.  If you absolutely must go there, be on a first name basis with a very good attorney, you will likely need his services. These people at St Mary's are the worst of the worst and the place deserves  to go out of business.  Our community will be better served when St Mary's Regional Medical Center is simply a foot note in the Reno history book.

Dr. Sager is a true gem. I have had the most severe pulmonary issues for over eight years and had to move from Pasadena to Santa Barbara to get away from air pollution.  Finding exceptional doctors has been a real challenge.  I have a very complex and unique medical history.  Dr. Sager took the time to call my doctors in San Francisco and Los Angeles, also to research a medical trial I am on in order to give me the best care possible.  He researched drugs I am on that are off label in the manner I am using them.  He has taken a extraordinary amount of time to listen to me and hear my concerns.  Dr. Sager's CV speaks for itself in terms of his medical education and abilities.  His concern for his patients and the time he takes to learn about their unique condition as well as their symptoms is so reassuring.  I am thrilled that I have found a pulmonary super star in our little city.  Thank you Dr. Sager!

I really hate giving a Pizza Hut a low rating .... Their pizza is usually good and I am easy to please... But I am just tired of getting my delivery order and having only one two litter of Diet Pepsi ...... The delivery person always has to make a second trip to replace the missing soda pop .... And I again ordered one of their deals ( Large two topping online ordered for 7.99 ) and it again looks like they counted every crumb as a piece  ......  I would suggest if YOU go to Pizza Hut get Pick Up ( and check it there ) or eat in ....... I think it will be a while till I again forget and order delivery from them ... a shame because I got points to get free pizza ... but their deals are usually not ..... Always better when pay full price ....... Not right but way they treat you .....I should have read others reviews of this place .........
Good prices on average food.  Not many great options in the neighborhood for "better than bar-food"

This place doesn't deserve one star. Service was meh. I got fish and chips and the fish was soggy and the french fries were cold and chewy. My girlfriend got the spicy buffalo chicken sandwich and it was raw in the center and she shared my complaint about the french fries.

Needless to say, we won't be returning. If you're at the mall looking for something to eat, do yourself a favor and go to the Cheesecake Factory. CF isn't something to rave about, but at least I haven't had this bad of an experience there, yet. In short, take your money elsewhere.

Oh Blind Pelican, what a love/hate relationship I have with you. The "love" is the atmosphere and vibe of this place. I find myself retuning here when I want to have a chill going out experience where I can sit with a drink and a pretty view. Snacks help too. I especially love going during crawfish season when they are boilin' on their patio. 

However, the less than stellar part is their actual food. I have given them the benefit of the doubt and have tried a few different dishes to see if anything was better. Very mediocre. Their crawfish quesadilla appetizer used to be my go-to but the last few times it's just a greasy, unflavored mess that left everyone with a mild stomach ache. I'm not sure how one messes up chicken tenders but they managed to. Their charbroiled oysters are very tasty but my lord. Be prepared  to wait upwards of an hour for one order. I understand that the deal on them is good so they are popular but honestly by the time they come you're ready to leave. Drinks are just alright. Good if you want beer but not really up on the cocktails. Last time we came they didn't have mint or limes for any drinks-weird given that they are a tropical-ish bar. 

So I'd say atmosphere is great, food/cocktails are kinda meh.

Absolutely gorgeous hotel! Location is wonderful, you are right on State Street as well as a few steps from the beach and pier. Their complementary bike rental is ideal to explore the town, the rooftop pool it's a wonderful way to spend the day. They have a few on-site restaurants, which are all delicious. Our rooms were spacious and the entire hotel it's absolutely perfect. Our entire family had a wonderful stay, Highly recommend it!

My family has been going to the Yardley Inn for decades - the main reason why we chose it for my mother's 70th birthday dinner (older people like what they like!).  They were very accommodating setting us up with a small private room for 10 upstairs on one of the busiest holiday weekends of the year.  

Entering (and leaving) the restaurant, you must walk through the cloud of cigarette smoke as the smokers all stand directly in front of the door.  Totally disgusting.  They should have a designated smoking area AWAY from the front door.  

I started at the bar - a total mob scene.  I wanted a Calvados Slider (the bartender had no clue what Calvados was so I settled for a nice Pinot Noir).  Seriously, stick with beer, wine or easy cocktails or one of the many (and outdated) martinis on their list.  

Our server was lovely, and kept the wine flowing through our 3 hour dinner.  We never, ever felt rushed.  We started with a few dozen Cape May Salt oysters.  - the lemony aioli sauce with the vinagrette was delicious.  They also had the standard cocktail sauce.  Salads were very nice.  I had the duck breast with chestnut stuffing.  The duck was well seasoned and cooked perfectly.  The stuffing was a little light on the chestnuts and could have used a little bit more flavor.  There were no green vegetables on the plate - a small portion of asparagus or something would have been nice as I usually only have a few bites of starches and prefer a portion of vegetables.  My mother had the Ricotta Gnudi - perfect little pillows (like gnocchi) of ricotta in a yummy sauce.  It was delicious and filling, but if you are not accustomed to richer, buttery, creamy food, you may be up all night! 

The night we were there, they offered free valet parking.  The valet were quick and efficient.  However, the valet who brought my car around later in the evening either had horrid BO or he farted in my car.  I had to put the windows down in 20 degree weather for the first 3 minutes of my drive home.  What a way to end a perfectly nice evening out for my mother!  

Would I go back to the Yardly Inn?  Of course, it is where my family likes to go.  Are there better, more innovative restaurants in Bucks County?  YES there are.

We just purchased our home yesterday.   The gas company tech came to service the property and noticed that the gas bills are extremely high (in the range of heating double the sq footage of our home) and said it has been this way since 2010.  Since we had an invoice from Western Heating & Air showing the property was serviced in November of 2016, we figured an error was not caught when the technician came to service the property.  Since this service has just recently happened a few months ago, we called them to have a tech come out to see what they missed.  He found the problem (with the thermostat - that is checked when serviced) and proceeded to be told by the company to charge us the $65 for him coming out to the property.  We told him we didn't think we had to because they had just come out a few months ago. He couldn't find any records of our home being serviced.  We had to show him the invoice from the previous owners.  They didn't even have our house on file!  We paid the tech (because, honestly, he was really nice, and agreed that it was something they missed and that we shouldn't be charged) because dispatch told him to collect.

My husband calls to ask why we would be charged for something that was missed by their company in the first place.  They basically brushed him off stating any number of things could've happened between November and now  - the owner could've switched thermostats (house was empty at the beginning of November) or they could've just ran their gas really really high for the past 7 years (seriously?).  This is where I'm confused.  They basically claim that they are not responsible for anything that may have happened the past few months after their service.  What a lack of integrity.  You are honestly telling me that so many other rare things could've happened instead of maybe one of your service techs missing something during his maintenance servicing?  The previous owners paid hundreds to get the property serviced.  Instead of admitting that you guys missed something, you blame it on something/someone else. 

We're going to give our business to a company that puts their customers first.  To everyone else, be prepared to shell out cash even if you just had them work on your home. Shame on you Western Heating & Air.  You cannot be trusted!!

The new facility is nicer than the old one, except the bathrooms are still dirty. The place is still poorly managed and just because someone is good at gymnastics does not make them a good instructor.  

Their website is archaic.

Ooo ooo that smell!
Hate to give a place two stars but gotta be candid. 
I'm all for gimmicky frozen desserts but they have to taste good. The green tea and mint concoction was so overly minty that it was unpleasant. They used one or two too many drops of the extremely strong mint extract and it overpowered everything else. 
5-6 employees standing around not being friendly even though the place was empty. Holy wasted labor costs Batman!
Lastly, the outdoor area (aka alley?) in front of the store reeked of dumped oil or spoiled food.  This isn't necessarily the fault of this ice cream store, but they need to complain to their landlord or get their neighbors in line. 
If I hear the place has imoroved, I'd give it another try.

Stopped in here last week for the Enchant event.  Enchant basically took over the entire ballfield and turned it into a festival of lights, mazes, ice skating, food, Santa, and vendors selling their wares.  Lots of fun.  They even had a stroller valet, but since we left the kids at home with grandma, and they're not in strollers anymore, seeing it was more of a nostalgic novelty.  Finding the location was pretty easy using GPS, but because of the event, only one or two entrances were open.  We were guided in by the less than enthusiastic parking lot attendants with their flashlight wands to a prominent spot in the parking lot.  Why do the Tampa Bay Rays play in St Petersburg?  They're not the St Petersburg Rays, right?  Anyway, good times, loved the venue, wish I had gotten to see some actual rays, but maybe next time.

I love this place and will be sure to visit every time I am in Tucson. I only eat at the bar now. The variety of food is better than at the regular tables. Their guacamole is fantastic. We had a number of appetizers and each one was excellent and beautifully presented. . The Chipotle shrimp were delicious and a very generous portion. The view and setting are amazing. We had excellent attentive, unobtrusive service.

My husband and I had a horrible experience with this Sam Levitz and will never buy anything from there again. We special ordered a leather sectional from the store. When they delivered it the first time, they had brought the wrong color wedge piece. Our couch was a tan color, and they brought a turquoise corner wedge piece.  The delivery men took the piece back, leaving us with the four other pieces and said we'd have to talk to HQ to sort it out.  HQ told us several different stories:  one person said they didn't have the piece, another person called and said they had it in their warehouse another person said we would have to special order the new wedge in the right color. A few days later, someone found the original piece we had ordered.   
Then, they attempted to deliver it but failed four times.  they kept trying to schedule when we weren't home, and one time they tried to deliver at 11:30 at night but then said they couldn't because they couldn't process the payment since the store was closed.
Finally, they were going to deliver it but then said they had broken it. After several phone calls over a few days they finally said they had it.  However, when they brought it, the back panel was broken.  They had attempted to patch it up, but it was structurally very weak.  
We spent the next few months trying to get them to replace the piece, but they keep trying to just patch up the old wedge with a new panel.  This store is extremely unprofessional and does not hold to its word. about guarantees.  We highly recommend you not purchase anything from this store.

This place is very good! Got a few complaints but overall a solid 4.5/5 

Braised Rib: 
a bit dry but overall not so bad once dipped in the sauce. Gnocchi was my favorite! Love how cheesy that was. Not a fan of cherry tomatoes though but if u r then this is def ur dish!!

Lobster Mac n cheese: 
not a fan but that is just my personal preference. 

Parmesan Risotto: 
delicious. Soft. Buttery rice 

Truffle Fries: 
divine! The béarnaise sauce was so creamy and delicious. The egg yolk was a perfect touch. Def would get again. I cannot stop thinking about these fries. I want more!!!!!

Churro Cheesecake: 
this was absolutely EVERYTHING! I love this dessert. I am a huge snob when it comes to dessert but this exceeded my expectations. The nice fresh fruit with the warm churro and the cheese/whip cream. This sent me

This is a cute little French bakery, new to 118 Ave, and a great addition to the other places that help make the Avenue a food destination.

Very pleasant staff, almost all of whom are bilingual, samples on the counter, a wide variety of pastries as well as sandwiches and soup. The prices are extremely reasonable and the execution and presentation are amazing. 

Yum yum!
Kelly rocks! She served up one of the best meals we've had since we've been in Nola and the drinks were delicious too! We'd definitely come back!

My spouse and I were in the Carpinteria area for our anniversary getaway, and we stumbled upon this little gem one night. We're SO glad we did! Every single thing we ate, from the bread to the lobster ravioli to the exquisite calamari to my spouse's entree to the tiramisu, was EXCELLENT. Taste and presentation were both superior, and service was great too. The price was reasonable for the quality of the meal and the ambiance was cheerful, friendly, and relatively peaceful, despite a largish group dining in.
I would definitely recommend this place if you are in the area and wish it were closer home!

I'm on a quest to try all the little coffee spots in town and this one is top notch. I tired the maple espresso and it was fantastic!  While this isn't exactly s place for kids nobody acted like they were not welcome. Can't wait to try the scone I purchased for breakfast.

Great apartment complex. Very clean an the staff is really friendly. Would have given a 5 star but there seems to always be different times things get locked up (i.e. Clubhouse, pool, basketball courts,tennis courts) some nights it's 10 some it's 9 and others it's never. It really depends on who is locking everything up at night. Free wifi in the clubhouse is great and also free cable and weight room. Great place for family and also for the college kid like me.

The staff at Sierra Smiles will make you feel like a million dollars; however, it's kind of a scam. You get a great experience - meanwhile you're billed for things your insurance won't cover and somewhat unnecessary. (Like the in-depth pictures of your teeth that you HAVE to have if you decide to go to Sierra Smiles.) They consistently got my insurance policy wrong so I'd end up paying more upfront and then I would receive a credit that they could mail me a check for or used towards other treatments - kind of frustrating when you just want to pay what you owe upfront. They did end up getting it right after a few visits.You get the Paraffin wax treatment on your hands, a hot towel with lavender, and a gigantic TV with Netflix to watch. How cool, right? Maybe if you're very well-to-do. I wouldn't recommend to anyone on a budget. 

I had my teeth worked on there and had to have a lot of fillings. (When I was a teenager I absolutely did not take care of my teeth and now I pay the price.) I explained to the staff I wanted whiter teeth because of how I was embarrassed that my teeth weren't bright white - they said they thought I would be a great candidate for Zoom whitening. I should not have had this procedure done - I hadn't had all of my fillings finished yet - my teeth were in way too bad of shape. They gave me some toothpaste and mouth wash to use to try to mineralize my teeth as much as possible before hand - I was told to use it for a week or two. I went in for the zoom procedure and  had to stop because of the  zings they warn you about, it was excruciating. 

The zings weren't the issue - I did my research and expected this. It was the at home bleach trays prescribed afterward that did me in. While wearing the bleach trays one night I got EXCRUCIATING pain - more than ever before on my right side molar. It turns out it had gotten in an old filling - and it turned into a crack. YOUCH! It was a combination of things - not all of their fault. I shouldn't have gotten the procedure done, I wish they would have advised me against it instead of letting me go through with it. However, I obviously wouldn't have been in this situation to begin with if I had taken better care of my teeth as a teenager.

I just had to get part of my tooth replaced this morning. I'm sure it takes a lot of money for Sierra Smiles to provide all of the luxuries they offer to their clients. I will admit I have never felt more relaxed at a dentist but I feel like I should have been better advised or had a better filling done. Something. Thankfully, I have okay enough insurance where they covered part of what needed to be done.

The Landing was a favorite for years, not so much anymore after the way I was treated. The food is still pretty okay, but management is less accommodating, less friendly. I've been a faithful customer for years, I just ate there on this past Wednesday night. I called today with a request to reserve a table for 10 for a birthday dinner at a future date, which they reserve tables all the time, but because it falls on a "busy night", I was told No. Not even a friendly "No"- the guy was pretty rude and short with me. That's No to a guaranteed 10 customers; how does that negatively impact business? So we will have to take our business outside of our local community. Unfortunate.

Was going to lunch at another spot on Maple, when we walked passed this place and it smelled so good, we had to try it.
