Got 3 different Bacos. Gator, Oyster, and Coconut Curry Chicken. 

They were out of this world. 

Found a new favorite spot!
prices are ridiculously high.  no deals.  not worth going.  its a waste if time.  why buy here?

One of the very few places in the area that you can get a well made sandwich. The ingredients are fresh and it is reflected in their sandwiches. All of the sandwiches are named for Pure Bred Dogs.  My favorite is the Visla, a BLT that is loaded with bacon on focaccia bread. They have fresh soups and salads. You can get combinations of them if you like. They also have big, moist muffins in a variety of flavours. They would have gotten 5 stars from me, but they changed whoever they were getting their coffee from. Pure Bred had the best cup of coffee that I could find locally, until they made the switch.

Back again.  Now a favorite for several friends also.  Asked for an antipasti, no problem.  Made up on demand.  Asked for sea scallops pan seared in butter with angle hair pasta with olive oil and garlic...no problem.  Wife had stuffed mushroom, and she loved it.  Veal parm was as big as the plate, fork tender and the tomato sauce to die for.  One large glass of house Chianti.  Under 70 bucks,not including tip.
Had been talking about trying Bellini, but $6 for a side of spinach was outrageous.

Ok, so some people don't understand no menu.  We've figured it out.  Just tell him what you want and how you want it.  Got no idea?  Slow him down and ask questions.  The "old man" is the energizer bunny.  But he can be patient and listen.  If you love Italian food, you probably know what you like, so ask for it.  But we also suggest exploring!  They will do anything.

Visited Marra's during restaurant week, and was not disappointed. The service was great - friendly, responsive and helpful staff. Food was good and the portions were large - we both took food home and that is not typical for Don. From the calamari to chicken Maximo, everything was fresh, hot and tasty.
I love Pho Bistreaux! The brisket pho is great, and the egg rolls are amazing. Love the iced coffee too!

Delicious meal thanks to Holly C. Will def. come back the next time we come to New Orleans. Got the gator bites and the blackened redfish, wouldn't have thought to get that if it wasn't for Holly. So thank you. Oooo... And the Cajun cannon ball! Must have... As long as u have a dd

Maybe it was because it was Mother's Day (I am trying to optimistic here...) but the food was ...horrid. There was no taste, no spice and very bland.  My grandsons Beef Chimichanga tasted like dried up moist dog food, the chicken/steak fajitas LOOKED great, failed on the flavor. The Chicken/steak/bacon wrapped shrimp was a total bust. My husband could only eat the shrimp.  My son in laws Street Tacos (chicken, beef and pork belly) was flavorless. My daughters chicken fajitas were just as bad as the aforementioned other 2. 

Something positive: the atmosphere is relatively authentic-looking, our waiter was better than average.  The restaurant wasn't jammed pack, our wait time was about 15 minutes at best around 7pmish on a holiday evening.  

For a 90.00+ food bill with no alcohol...not worth the money. The 2 best parts of the experience?
1. Spending Mothers Day with my daughter and our family.
2. Leaving the restaurant.

Who does love brunch?!

Communists, that's who.

We tried stopping here right when we got to Tucson on a Sunday morning which is an amateur move on our parts.....they had an hour wait for two people. But hearing that only validated that we must come back so we hit up Prep and Pastry again on a Tuesday morning right when they opened so we were guaranteed to get a table. Within 15 minutes, some other tables came in an the place was bumping. 

While I would love to be a sweets for breakfast kinda person, I just can't do it, I'm savory road all the way. I ordered the pork belly breakfast sandwich with country hash and it was good. I was expecting to have a mouthgasm but there was too little sriracha sauce and the slaw wasn't too flavorful. But the pork belly was seasoned and cooked to perfection so I can't complain about that. My boo got the biscuits and gravy with cheddar biscuits and it was heavenly, I would get that if I was to ever come back while in Tucson. We got some sweets to go since we couldn't leave without one, the pistachio/mascarpone croissant redeemed P&P for the pork belly sandwich.....it gave me a mouthgasm! Like multiple ones. Mmmmmm.

I've been to multiple Planet Fitness locations, but this one is the worst. For starters, the front desk didn't have a guest log in and couldn't look up my account. The locker room was hard to find in the back. The whole gym is very dark and cramped without windows. Ventilation also seems to be a problem. There were additional machines on the second floor. I won't be going back. I prefer the Montgomeryville location.

Loved this place. The free samples were all amazing. The service was great. I got the red beans and rice, instead of white rice I got jambalaya rice and it was phenomenal. I highly recommend it. My husband tasted mine and said it tasted like Beanie Weenies; I said " heck yea, that's why I love it!"

Came here with my family and girlfriend during Christmas leave from overseas.  They do have a military rate and the Ryman is an attractive building...... Although I was sorely disappointed that we were rushed through a "self guided" tour.  The security people were very rude and pushed us through the walkway like cattle closing the doors behind us each section we barely viewed.  I understand that you may have an event to setup for but we were there well before closing time.   My money would of been better off supporting the Country Music Hall of Fame Museum.  


Congrats you get One Star!

Stop in for lunch on a freezing cold day and the food warmed me right up. I'm no BBQ aficionado, but I know what I like. The lunch special, pulled rib sandwich was excellent. The cole slaw was your standard stuff. The applesauce was killer, made with small chunks. I'll definitely be back to try some more of the menu option.

Overpriced for portions.

I went to Tres Carnales on the recommendation of some coworkers.  The staff was friendly and the pork tacos were tasty but the prices vs portion size were definitely not what I was expecting.

While I was expecting smaller tortillas, the amount received vs the cost was just not a good value.  In fact, I was left hungry still and ordered chips and guac as I left.  The guacamole went into the trash once I got back to the office and tried it as it was basically just avacado and overpowering amounts of onion.  There was no subtlety to the flavour, no spices, nothing.  It was seriously gross.  I ate the chips which ended up being much more overpriced for the fact of tossing the guac.

I will not be going back to Tres Carnales.

Couldn't talk my husband into painting my toe nails so ended up over at Hot Tips Nails. I just dropped in and was quickly greeted. I told them I just wanted my toe nail polish removed and new colour put on and was treated like an important customer even though it wasn't a big 'order.' It appeared clean and well run. They have a large selection of nail polish to choose from. As with every nail salon I've ever gone to, they tried the up sell "only five dollars more" but were respectful when the answer was no. The only reason it's four stars instead of five is because they put me in the 'toe dryer' and just left me there. After about 20 minutes or do I was able to catch someone's eye and find out that yes, I could go. My lady had already moved on to another customer and had pretty much forgotten the cheapskate in the toe cooker.

They are very unorganized here. Sat for 45 min for a pizza they said would take 6 min to make, and I had to keep coming up to ask when my pizza was going to be done and they hadn't even put in my pizza yet. I will never order a pizza from here again.
Fun atmosphere, the kitchen is open late which is hard to find on music row. The food was good, not great. The music was enjoyable.

I have always been a fan of Little Caesar's Pizza. The staff is always friendly and many times they have showed me how to save money on what I'm ordering by ordering combos or giving me a coupon in the store! Always will come back here but it does look a little dirty at times and that's why it's not a 5 star

I have loved staying here in the past. One of the reasons why we were gifted a stay here from our parents. Unfortunately we had to reschedule our arrival date a few times due to unforeseen circumstances with our newborn. So the last time we rescheduled, somehow there was a misunderstanding with our arrival date and ended up being charged as a "no show" on a date we didn't even choose (April 1st). After my fiancé spoke with a manager, we were told there was nothing they can do about it since that was a sell out date. Initially I don't know if there was a typo, but we never rescheduled for April 1st it was for the 11th... SO I called to speak with the manager myself, and he never returned my call. To say the least, Im super bummed about the overall customer service we received. Therefore i hope this feedback helps. I understand we all make mistakes, but in the customer service industry, its always smart to fix them.
One of our favorite go to spots. They have the best coffee LOVE the coffee of the day food is outstanding. I have never been disappointed. We sometimes are there a few times a week

Their crust is so fluffy and delicious. They are also very accommodating and will alter the pizza in order to fit your needs (we like our pizza without the extra spices and they are more than willing to leave them off). Have been here many times and never had a bad experience.

This place looks great from the outside, very nice decor and friendly and inviting staff but the food is so lacking.  Stopped in  for a quick afternoon bite and beverage.  The Manhattan was quite strong and tasty and decent size for $8.  However, the food left a lot to be desired...Maryland Crab soup, was more like a vegetable soup with a miniscule portion of crab and the lobster roll was a major disappointment....more like seafood salad (I am spoiled with having both Oyster House and Lukes Lobster close to my work) served on a brioche roll, which is so wrong in so many ways vs the split top buttered roll with a Maine Lobster roll.  On other note, it appears that they ran out of money for decorating the bathrooms here (Ladies room at least)...the bathroom stalls were literally falling off the wall and was desperately in need of a paint job.

Good selection of beers on tap.  Food overall is very good.  Great happy hour specials.

BBQ wings fucking rock.

3 stars as the average between 5 stars for great atmosphere, great beer and great sausages and 1 star for the rest of the food. Seems the kitchen is not allowed to cook and is restrained to reheating stale food. Except for the sausages which only require a little heat, everything else was tasteless and sad. Stick to beer and sausages for a great experience.
We came here while on our trip to Canada, i would say the pizza came out to be the best one i had so far. The people are very nice and friendly. I would highly recommend this place

I was pleasantly surprised by this hotel.  I booked on a whim (literally Saturday morning to stay that night) for a super reasonable rate and I figured it would at least be a decent place to shower and catch a nap after the On The Run concert.  

I arrived and was hoping for an early check-in (it was about 1:45) but they were booked and I was initially told I would have to wait until at least 3 if not later.  The gentleman at the counter was very gracious and offered a multitude of options for me in the interim (hang out at the pool, their business center, offered snacks, etc.).  I felt like they truly wanted to accommodate me.  The supervisor of housekeeping ( I think that's his role, I wish I caught his name) overheard my ordeal and promised to see what he could do to get me in a room soon.  I decided to step out and run some errands but sure enough, at 2:20 I received a call that they had a room for me! 

The weekend only served to surpass my expectations from there.  The front desk staff were extremely helpful and very patient with everyone checking in (literally everyone checking in around the time I did was there for this concert).  Check-In/Check-Out were quick and painless thanks to Josh and Keith (again, I'm terrible with names and can't remember which one helped when but EVERYONE at the hotel was amazing).  I left early Sunday morning but when I hit the lobby it was buzzing with folks enjoying the breakfast so I imagine that is a major plus to staying here.  

Another poster complained of dated rooms and thin walls allowing you to hear everything.  I have to respectfully disagree.  As I've mentioned several times, I was here for a concert and clearly the folks next to me were as well.  Several folks were getting in the spirit with their personal soundtracks that you could hear as you walked down the hall, however once I hit my room I could hear little more than a muffled melody from the room next door.  In regards to the amenities, this is another area where I was pleasantly surprised.  I'd estimate the hotel has been updated at least within the past 5-6 years.  There were the usual indications of wear and tear (chipped headboard, signs of repainting) but I think that's par for the course in many hotels. 

I have stayed in hotels in Center City (for much more per night) and haven't received the level of customer service I received at Hampton Inn.  It doesn't take much to ensure a pleasant experience for customers and they got it right.  I will definitely be making this my 'go-to' hotel when I visit Philly, for the price you really can't beat it!

Had to have the sewer line cleaned and inspected for the sale of my home. Shuster's guys came out and video scoped the line and certified it in "Great" shape. When the buyer called Bill Shuster to verify the report, he told them NOT TO BUY THE HOUSE because of the condition of the sewer line. He cost a lot of money and aggravation, nearly derailed the sale of the house. He doesn't stand behind his work or his plumbers. Buyer Beware!

The parking was good and the food was delish! The cheesesteak was on poi t with enough meat on a super soft roll. But, beware... any extras like pickles, onions or anything else will cost you. The fries were crispy . Definitely will return to try their breakfast.

Previously, I posted a question about the operating hours of this establishment since they have been closed during the early days of COVID. It was a popular favorite of our family so I questioned their operating hours since they had gone online at their website and changed it. I even attempted to call repeatedly yet no one was answering the phone. I even saw a previous post asking the same thing about why no one was answering the phone...SUDDENLY I RECEIVE A THREATENING WARNING FROM YELP THAT I HAD POSTED SOMETHING DEROGATORY AND THEY REMOVED MY POST.  I can get nasty and post profanity and be insulting as hell but to accuse me of something I didn't do...I'M NOT A COVID KAREN OR KEN!
Here's the Yelp post...you decide...

We're reaching out to let you know that we have removed a Question you posted for Besito Mexican Restaurant due to concerns about its tone. 
We recommend against posting Questions that others may view as inflammatory, as such Questions are unlikely to quickly receive answers that are accurate or helpful for others. If you have concerns about a business's philosophy or practices you can raise them in Yelp Talk, and you can share your personal experiences with a business in a Review. 
Although we have removed the Question you posted, we hope that you'll continue to ask Questions about this or other businesses on Yelp with our guidelines in mind.

After multiple stops at the Gelateria del Duomo in International Plaza I have decided that this is one of my least favorite gelato spots in Tampa.

This small gelato shop carries about 20 different flavors of milk-based and fruit-based flavored gelatos. I've sampled many of the flavors here and all of the gelato at Gelateria del Duomo has a funny aftertaste in my opinion. My belief is that this gelato shop is either storing their gelato at a colder temperature than recommended or they are using artificial fruit flavoring in their fruit-based gelatos.

Personally I think the gelato is better at Datz Dough and Le Macaroon.

I recently had a dermal filler and Botox procedure performed by Dr. Ho; my experience was beyond my expectations.  She took great care during the consultation phase to listen to the results that I was expecting to achieve.  After her recommendations we started the procedure.

During the procedure she was professional, enthusiastic, well-rounded and just a pleasure.  After the procedure I received a personalized text message from Dr. Ho with a direct contact.  The direct contact was in case I had any questions or concerns, this really impressed me.

As for the results...I love it; absolutely amazing!!  Everything I hoped for before celebrating a milestone next month, excited to be 40 and fabulous!  I am looking forward to discussing rhinoplasty at my 2 week follow up.

I stood in line for near 10 minutes and suddenly the person behind is served his order. He also went ahead and ordered something else. Not sure. So I left. Evidently the line means nothing to them. Slow as molasses and unorganized. Those little teens need not be left unattended to run the business. I left. Plenty of ice cream in town. If you go be prepared some one might get ahead of you even if they are behind you.

Good:
You can buy by the slice
Big slices
Bring your own booze!

Bad:
Not the fastest service
Some speciality pizza not available by the slice

I had a magnificent dinner here on a Saturday evening. This place has ocean views and is more casual than I had expected.  You will absolutely need reservations and parking could be an issue. Even though there is a big lot, parking can be tough since most people are at the beach (which is dog friendly).  

I made an 8:30 p.m. reservation (it was all they had) and we just spent the time waiting on the beach. I drank a blood orange margarita ($10) but actually preferred my boyfriend's Moscow Mule.  We shared the coconut shrimp appetizer which was delicious.  I ordered the filet mignon wrapped in bacon ($29) and my boyfriend had the sea bass. We both loved our dishes and thought they were quite reasonably priced.  For dessert we split some chocolate mud pie type dish ($8), which was alright but not memorable. 

I would definitely come back here. The views cannot be beat.  You are literally sitting on the beach and the service and food were impressive!  Next time I am calling earlier for a reservation.  Heard they are known for their brunch too!

Had my first appt. with Swan Dental this afternoon. I requested an appt on-line and received a call back first thing the next morning. The girl I spoke with was very knowledable. I was given an appt. this same week for a routine exam and cleaning. What a pleasant experience!! The staff is friendly and helpful. Always smiling. Julie, the dental hygenist is incredible. She explains every step in detail from the xrays to the polishing finish. My teeth have NEVER looked this good after a cleaning. Dr. Echols then gave me a thorough exam, again explaining and answering all questions professionally, and in a tone that expressed genuine concern. I will absolutely return to this practice. My 13 year old grand-daughter was in the waiting room and when we left, she mentioned how impressed she was with the staff. From a 13 year old, that's saying much.

It was a pleasure working with Joe and his guys. They tiled our kitchen backsplash and a few months later, we got an estimate to finish our entire basement. They did excellent work at an affordable price for our family. Always on time, courteous, and honest. I would trust Joe with any home project in the future.

This is one of my favorite sushi places in Pensacola. Any time I walk in here I'm always greeted nicely and sat at the sushi bar. They always bring out some chips and dip and are patient with my order. I like to try new things so I usually get something new every time I come here but my favorite so far is the princess roll and the Salmon Lover roll. I'd recommend always getting the Miso soup as it is amazing. This place is a little less known so if you re looking for a local sushi shop then this place is for you. Just be careful because they don't have the lunch specials on the weekend. I still haven't gotten in there in time to experience that yet.

I had Dim Sum and dim some more. My Dim Sum experience at Panda King was delightful. I loved the variety and the authenticity of the dishes. And, the atmosphere was exactly how I imagined the tea houses in Hong Kong. It is a loud and happy place. 

Our party of eight had a wonderful time. The brunch-like outting is like a weekly family reunion. Our table, like most seated around us, was represented by different generations of the family. We were seated at a large round table with a lazy susan that aides in passing the dishes around. This is the ideal way to enjoy Dim Sum, and quality family time.

I used a different strategy on this visit. I passed on the standard dim sums (Cheung Fun, Haw Gaw, Sui Mai, Sticky Rice and Chau Sui Bao), and patiently waited for my favorite carts. That was hard, Here are some the dishes I held out for, and i recommend.

Fu Jok Pork Dumpling - The texture of the bean curd skins is unique and the fillling was very tasty.
Steamed Clams in Black Bean - The clams were small and tender. The fermented black bean sauce didn't take attention away from the clams.
Pia Gwut  - The steamed pork ribs could be a choking hazzard, but the small morsels of meat are usually tender and flavorful. On this visit the ribs were tough and fatty.
Stuffed Eggplant - The Chinese eggplant was on the dry side, but the sauce helped.
Steamed Tripe - Yes this is the stomach lining of a cow, but don't think about it. The chewy texture and jalopenos slices were delicious together.
Salt Fried Squid - Squid peices were lighly dusted with flour and then quickly fried. Some of the peices were so small and random that the dish seemed like scraps.

Overall a good experience, and worth the trip to the west bank.

This is top notch food. The chicken is by far the best I have ever had. I live in Florida and wouldn't mind flying back just to have that flavorful chicken. Ive heard by many the line can be very long, but when I went (not entirely sure on the time of day, but it was in August), there was no line. Walked in and got seated immediately. Definitely worth going and definitely worth a long wait if you must.... don't pass up the best chicken you'll ever taste!

I would describe Bar Louie as the Buffalo Wild Wings of the trendy restaurant/bar world. The food and drinks are not going to make you go, "WOW!", but gosh darnit, B.L. is a perfect "go- to" place that has something for everybody.

The inside is roomy, and trendy. Good for groups, friends. Outdoor seating. Plenty of TV's. The servers are polite and attentive. Very good staff. 

The "Diva" martini was by far the best. Also good is the Clover. The Effen Good was interesting. Tasted like an alcoholic salty cucumber slice. The Double Melon is a refreshing summer drink.

This is the closet Starbucks to my house so I am usually here every morning. The layout for the parking/drive thru creates congestion when it is busy and that is so frustrating at times. 

With that being said some of the workers have taken time to remember my order which is appreciated. However for the ones who haven't, they seem to be pretty inconsistent with getting my order right. 

I mostly come here for the rewards through the Starbucks app for free drinks/food items. However this particular Starbucks is pretty average at best.

Friends and I tried La Thai Uptown for lunch.  The place was beautiful, clean, serene and quiet.   Just a great atmosphere.  The staff was prompt and professional.  The menu is not overwhelming and I like that in a restaurant.  For an appetizer we ordered the eggplant.medallions with spinach and shrimp.  Very tasty and I highly recommend them.    Then I had the Tom Kar Gai soup.  Very mild with coconut  and mushrooms.  Again very tasty and mild.  Finally we all shared Pad Thai and Spring rolls.  Everything was very tasty and professionally plated.

Crabs were a good size, full of meat and had a great spice on them that is their version of Old Bay I'd imagine, it was right up my alley.  

The oysters we had for happy hour were a buck a shuck and were from I believe Maryland.  Very delicious. 

Overall the vibe was good for a Tuesday happy hour.   I really hope these guys get some traction because the city needs a good crab house.

I would never take another vehicle there.  Mine was in the shop for a a long time and when I got it back there were streaks and areas that they somehow didn't paint.  Overall just an awful job and an injustice to me, my car and my insurance company.

You would think that a chain restaurant would have the same recipe but man was I wrong the last time I had chicken scampie I was in San Antonio it was excellent last night it was terrible dry and just plain bad   I sent it back and received a fresh plate it was even worse. Soi switched to soup and salad. So I'm finished with Olive Garden for a while

Looking for a delicious breakfast in downtown Indy?  This is a must. Located inside the Farmers Market you can watch them make your crepe and chat with the friendly owner and her staff.  The crepes are huge; more than I could finish; all ingredients is locally sourced and very fresh.  I ordered the red egg & bacon savory crepe which includes of havarti goat cheese, spinach and a red pepper sauce so yummy I would definitely get it again. The coffee is must try too.  I will visit 3 Days In Paris on my next trip to Indy !

This place is bad! If you are looking for poor service, this is the place to go. It took 45 minutes for our breakfast to come. Our waitress actually came out to tell us that the food was running late because the kitchen was too small, and one of the cooks had passed out in the bathroom. The only thing that kept me from giving Calecos one star was the good food. Do not come here.

Yesyesyesyesyesyesyes!

I need to construct a jacuzzi in my apartment, fill it with their sipping chocolate, and turn on the bubbles. Perhaps this is why they call it Naked Chocolate Cafe. Oh yes.

The sipping chocolate is NO JOKE. It's so rich and indulgent, the tiny espresso sized cup is more than enough to bring you to chocolate ecstasy.

Oh wait, I had their Chocolate Fondue too. :)

My only regret is not staying in Philadelphia long enough to try the rest of their menu! Their cupcakes, chocolate truffles, and FROZEN hot chocolate looked delightful. Next time...
at first I was scared to try this place but let me tell youuu.. The burgers were absolutely delicious and the services is great!!

Similar experience as Genc B. review from 5/14/2016 and Jessica K. on 4/20/2017.  After waiting 3 hours, they tried to sell me $1,000+ of repairs under the leverage of failing my inspection.  I called their bluff and took it elsewhere.  One hour and $35 later I was passed with no issues at a different auto center.

On top of this, the service was very unprofessional.  When I told the Service Manager that I was fine with the fail and wanted my car back, he told me it would take an hour to "put my car back together" and finish the paper work.  I suppose that was my punishment for questioning his scam.  When the car was done, he refused to come out to discuss to job because "he was eating his lunch."  There is clearly tension between the employees and their sharing of responsibilities with no attempt to hide that from the customers.

To be fair, I am sure that all of the parts and repairs would have been a good idea for the maintenance of my car, particularly in the case of brake pads.  Some of them were sound suggestions (brake pads), some were very suspect (alignment, lack of coolant), none of them were required for inspection and they used that to try to sell me $1,000+ worth of parts and service.

I don't know what it is about this Chick fil A, but they have the perfectly cooked breakfast potatoes! Usually they're undercooked at other locations, but this one they are nice and crisp. 

The line at this location is never that bad either. 

Always a good quick breakfast spot.

Continuing on my bf's quest to find the best pizza in CC, this New Yorker spotted NYPD Pizza while we were out and hunger was making it's voice heard in the pit of his stomach. I challenged him to discover if their pizza could possibly be close to NY pizza - you can't name your pizza business that and not expect customers to want that quality of pizza.

He opted for just one slice of their pepperoni, which they reheated for him, and we sat down for his afternoon snack. The pizza looked close to NYC slices, but he wasn't completely satisfied with the quality of toppings. The crust was thin, crunchy not crispy, and the cheese was good but the sauce was so-so. He said that overall it was good, but it wasn't the best in Philly and it certainly wasn't NYC pizza. The guy behind the counter certainly acted like a NY-er, barely a word or smile directed at any customer. A pity, but it just means there are many other pizza places to still try on this quest.
Delicious, definitely stop by here if you want ice cream in Reading Terminal!

I went here go see a show here tonight. I'm in a wheelchair right now, and let me tell you- this was the most accommodating place I've been to yet. 
Specifically, Dana was the staff member that made an impact on me- she checked on us multiple times throughout the show, and even escorted us out. She kept the area we were in neat- and all the times i overheard her telling someone what's what she was extremely polite, even to people who were less than polite to her.
I am extremely impressed with this venue! Great show, great staff.

This was my first time and I loved it. The place has such a cool vibe. The interior is spacious and very open. There are plenty of tables and seats. I also appreciate the trendy wall art they have there. 

The bourbon vanilla was my drink choice of the morning. I loved the combination because it was unique and the flavors were great. I didn't order anything to eat because I was crunched on time. However, from what I saw at the other tables, I was pretty bummed I didn't order anything to-go. The biscuit sandwich looked really good. 

Highly recommend. If you're looking for a place to grab breakfast and some good coffee...check this place out!

I've used many parking services over the years and this one rates an A.  The staff is professionally and work hard to make sure that your trip to and from the airport are fast and stress free.  I've used the Parking Spot several times and each time I marvel at how they exceed my expectations, even when they are very busy. 

I experienced a great example of the professionalism, when I arrived home during a torrential downpour.  Clark worked with me to make sure I stayed dry and got checked out fast.  It was after midnight and he was smiling and running to get me on my was as soon as possible.  

With an attitude like that and a commitment to customer satisfaction Clark will be successful at whatever he tries in the future.

I called for an annual service. My a/c was working fine when the tech arrived and within 5 minutes he broke my coil and all of the freon was released (huge cloud pouring out of the air handler). The tech obviously realized his mistake and kept going outside to call his company. Caldeco refused to take responsibility and wanted to charge $2,000 to replace the coil. Needless to say I used another company to replace the coil and will definitely NOT use Caldeco again!

Celebrated Son's Birthday here and we all agreed it was really good food and solid service!

Husband and I had the Ultimate Margarita - oh yeah! We ordered the guacamole - which they prepared table side. Chips and a spicy sauce are complimentary. 

We had a sausage cheese dip appetizer that comes out bubbling hot and served with warm tortillas. 

Husband ordered one of the steaks and Mr. Picky ate the whole thing!

Son and I ordered burritos which were pretty good. Mine had beef cubes and onions - I wish there had been a bit more beef. 

The service here was great! Very attentive staff! Love the atmosphere and the music. It was all Husband and I could do to not get up and dance!

We'll definitely be coming back!

Place is pretty busy and nice atmosphere. However for the food, the seafood is really lacking. It was not fresh at all. The shrimp was powdery and the crawfish was old/dead when cooked because the tail meat was all shriveled up. For a seafood place to have old seafood is a bad sign.

It's true about the prices, they aren't "outlet" prices, but the staff is great and I love Patagonia's overall mission on environmental sustainability and how they stand by their products. My husband got his puffy jacket zipper fixed on the spot and they processed another repair order for us.

Wanted to try a different sushi place in Philly  (since I have tried most of them within a two mile radius of center city lol) n gotta say i definitely  recommend kabuki.  Love their lunch special,3 rolls for like 11 bucks, can't say no to that. I get the salmon  avocado, shrimp tempura and eel avocado. For  their signature rolls I recommend the Marriott, Godzilla and pink lady . Love the place and 10/10 will keep coming back. ^_^

My boyfriend and I's favorite sushi spot. The service is impeccable and the place is super clean and cute. They have the best spicy tuna I've ever had and I've been to a million sushi places theirs is hands down the best. I also love their lobster tempura roll. Everything is great. This place is definitely worth a try. We will for sure keep coming back!

This place is a must ! I wanted a fun date idea and this was definitely the place to go. The fondue and pretzels is to die for ! I got a steak to cook on the stone and it was very enjoyable and easy. It wasn't hard to use at all and cooked the meat very nicely. I chose to cook mine one piece at a time so that the rest of it didn't burn. Go here ! So worth it.

Ate here tonight for the first time.  After finding many meals on the menu that sounded appetizing, I was excited to try their Sweet Bourbon Salmon.  I LOVE salmon and often choose it as an entrée.  It was totally disappointing though.  For a $16 entrée I was expecting something much more.  The salmon was SUPER dry and not much flavor, so I had to pair each bite with a scoop of mashed potatoes.  While the potatoes didn't look great (they looked like they had sat around for a while) they had a decent taste at least.  The grilled veggies were just how I like them (overdone and soggy) but I know not everyone would be happy with veggies of that texture. 

The patio seating was nice and I enjoyed the atmosphere.  The waiters and waitresses we encountered were all very helpful and nice.  

I had high hopes for this place, but I don't think I'll be back any time soon.

These guys are great. They work on high end to low end cars and they are the best at it. They even have a TV show thats how great they are!
I take them all my cars that need to be worked on after accidents or just dings and dents. Jerry is awesome. If you go anywhere else to get body work you didn't get the best.

Come here if you want to spend 15 bucks for a pastrami that just comes with meat and bread. I ask the guy (Jose) behind the counter what comes on a pastrami. He says "I don't know look at the menu". I say "I did look at the menu it doesn't say anything"   Ok he says "well it doesn't come with anything then". So I order the club sandwich instead. After I order (my bill was 12.50 and I give em 15$) the guy (Jose), who was very rude gives me 2 bucks change. I ask where my change is and he says something about they're a no change store but said it so rude I didn't even wanna ask again. "I got 3 pennies in my drawer you want that"? He says. WTF. I sit down at my table, wipe it off because it's got crumbs and water all over it, eat my club which was decent and bounce never to return. F this place. Go to tower if you actually want mustard and cheese on your pastrami like every other normal place in the world serves it.

Wasn't disappointed in the courses, challenging yet fun. Prices were reasonable $7 per adult, kids are free good fun for a date or just some college kids looking for something to do. Family friendly and fun staff.

It's been quite a few years since I have stopped in and I was pleasantly surprised.  The menu had a fresh update but of course had the classic lettuce wraps which are for sure a must have!!!  We shared the wraps and a Singapore Black Pepper Chicken with peppers and onions which was delicious!  Wonderful drinks as well, we had the Whiskey Sour and Mojito!
Why? Why do they have to add seasoning to a perfectly good burger? Half way through my burger my mouth was on fire. I asked specifically for no seasoning yet they still load it on. So disappointing.

My pregnant stomach can only handle super fresh food, and this was amazing and super fresh to say the least.i got the corn bisque and the salad with avocado dressing. The soup has chili oil in it and it's to die for. The salad was wonderfully salt and crunchy. The mix of savory and spicy I was looking for!

Also there is like this giant corn stuff in the bisque and it amazeballs. Thank you for reading!

EDIT: Got dessert. Fantastic.

My wife and I recently returned from an 8 Day trip to Budapest & Prague booked via Gate1 Travel.  We had an excellent time! 

We did not opt for the tours and we were on our own for the trip with the exception of the transfer to Prague.  

Budapest is an amazing city. We had never visited Hungary and this trip was a good opportunity to get a glimpse of the history, the architecture and the amazing food that Budapest has to offer. Our hotel was in a short cab ride away from downtown, breakfast at the hotel was buffet and included many local dishes (smoked meats, etc., )  We were able to get in a thermal bath at Széchenyi Baths, and during the winter months there are many holiday fares set up with food and Mulled wine. 

Prague was also incredible. The hotel was a more modern than the hotel in Budapest, also buffet breakfast walking distance to the Charles Bridge - Old Town and the Castle.  We were able to walk most places and see a concert in the Mozart Museum in the Castle. Lot of sightseeing and food - more meat - to be had in Prague. 

Thank you for such a great vacation! We look forward to our next trip with you.

Ordered through DoorDash.
They didn't put most of what I asked for on my burrito bowl. 
Then refused to fix or refund because I ordered through DoorDash... even though the order is clear on the ticket. 
Who wants to eat big chunks or brown lettuce or soupy sour cream?
Charged for Quac but none on here. No tortilla chips either.  I asked for a flour tortilla shell on the side... didn't get that. 
No utensils in the bag... so not sure how they expected me to eat my lunch at work. 
Sad really. I normally order the same thing twice a week every week for a couple of months and for the last 3 weeks service and quality have gone downhill. 
I can go somewhere else to spend my money.
I stayed at the parkside inn for 3 nights last week. I really felt like I was at home. I loved being 2 blocks from the beach. The Hyatt staff was great. I would come back.

I have visited here a few times and alway the same result, this place has a very poor inventory of anything but tired looking clothing. the furniture section is very weak and I think you might do better driving up and down the streets of UCity during bulk trash day. It's very low rent stuff here, even for Goodwill.
Yummy.  Not that expensive, nice portions.  Saturday nights they have live jazz which is nice.  The (free) focaccia bread with the dips is amazing.  I personally love the Carbonara Rigatoni.

Ordered the lemongrass beef which was very tender and flavorful. My date ordered the pho with meatballs and beef. Very flavorful broth base. Also ordered spring rolls with peanut sauce for app and it was very refreshing to start. Good service and clean. Will be back.
This place is a gem. Cheap, authentic Italian food in large portions. You can basically find anything under the sun here. All the sandwiches and desserts we got were amazing.

Ran in for a to go lunch today and was impressed. Friendly service! Super quick! Good sandwiches! 
Thanks for the effortless meal on the fly!!!!

Our FAVORITE Thai restaurant in Indianapolis. It is perfect for the fountain square area, the prices are reasonable and the food is exceptional. They are always busy which makes it tough but it's definitely worth the wait. The staff are all very outgoing and nice, young but nice. My husband and I have been going to Siam Square for over 2 years now and we travel the 30 minute drive for it at least once a month. The food is phenomenal. Pad See Ew, Drunken Noodles, Spicy Basil Fried Rice, and the Chicken Satay are our favorites on the menu. Siam Square also offers Roti Rolls which a lot of Thai places in Indy don't, these are a MUST!

I was a little disappointed to be quite honest. Everyone I've talked to just raves about this place so maybe my expectations were too high. I actually tried the sausage, egg and cheese. It wasn't bad, but it didn't have very much flavor to it. On the plus side the people working were very friendly and helpful. That being said, I will probably try it again and get something else.

Beautiful hotel, great staff, the absolute southern hospitality from waitresses at the bar to doorman.  Cross the street to all the chaos on Bourbon St. but return back to luxury at the Roosevelt.  Love the comfort.

My son loved the duck served during a late lunch on Thanksgiving Day. I ordered quiche with chicken and mushroom, and those are good. I like the flaky crust. We also ordered some pastries that tasted divine.

Food: 5 Stars
Service: 5 Stars

While my husband and I were enjoying our dinner last night, I was trying to think of a review that would describe this place. The only thing that came to mind was, "YUM" and "MORE". 
And while I normally leave lengthy reviews, I'm going to keep it simple because some times simple is better.

This place is a MUST TRY! GO! NOW!

Red Bench killed it for our weekly take out night! I've been wanting to try them for a couple months now and was thrilled when i saw they had added their own delivery. FINALLY a pizza place that not only does it right but delivers their own pizzas! I'm so tired of the middle man delivery services (yes you- Uber Eats, door dash, grubhub) that not only hog the margin from these independent businesses but sacrifice food quality for profit by picking up and delivering multiple orders at once. I hope Red Bench keeps this service up and if they do- they can count on us as loyal customers. 

Now onto more important topics- that pizza of course! Since this was my first time ordering the menu wasn't clear if the pizzas were intended as individual pies or to be shared- i assumed individual and thankfully I assumed correctly. We ordered the Spicy Pepperoni and The Big Red Bench- both were great. Fresh and quality ingredients, a perfect crust. Definitely hit the spot. I will say that we would have added on to other pies but that option wasn't available online for all of their selection- likely something they will eventually improve upon. We also ordered a couple Caesar salads (hold the olives ) and we were pleasantly surprised that the dressing was homemade! Finally a fresh tasting Caesar without the heavily mayo laced or preservative dressing! The fried capers were a nice touch too. 

Overall- stellar food, homemade and high quality goodness. With DIRECT delivery! Can't beat that.

Came here based on the yelp reviews.. I do not recommend. Very bland, over-reviewed for sure. Blood orange marg was all sugar no tequila, should've known that was just the beginning of an awful dinner.

I left my vape at home on the NS one day and had to go pick up something to get me thru the day. I took my 30 minute break from work to run over there. I got there at 11:00 which is opening time, and no one was there. I wound up waiting around until 11:20 when someone finally showed up. Had it not been for that, I would have given more stars because they were helpful and even suggested juice flavors that I wound up purchasing.

I've been wanting to try this place for such a long time. I planned a brunch for the student football recruiting club that I'm an officer in on campus. I easily made reservations for brunch on Sunday at 11 am and they were very accommodating. They even had the table all ready to go when we came in. 

They brought us drink menus (good folk I tell ya) with listings of house made, fresh drinks! I tried a sip of my friends strawberry lemonade and by god it was fantastic. I also had a sip of the grapefruit mimosa, yum!! The coffee is served via individual French press and that was a great idea in itself. I will definitely have to come back and sample all the drinks. Service was exceptional and I actually loved how the simple the menu was. There was something on the menu for everyone and all the food was artistically arranged. 

If I could give a rating just on food presentation I would give it 10 stars out of 5, that's how beautiful everything was. 

I had the brioche French toast with housemade bourbon syrup and not usually being a fan of syrup, I was surprised by how phenomenal it was. I sampled some of the sweet potato fries with chipotle aioli and once again, 5 stars. (I also love the fact that the menu utilizes aioli's)

Love love love the new location as well. The interior is gorgeous with a very trendy midtown vibe. The ceilings are exposed wood with lights lining the beams. The bar itself is beautiful and all the wood work just adds to the total environment. 

I must come back to sample everything on the menu because looking at what my party ordered, I want it all!

They were very busy. The wait was 1 hour. The service was very good. I would give a four for service. The food was all in all a 4. I had the Italian omelet with a side of a short stack of pancakes. My husband had the. Chicken fried steak with a side of hash. We also got biscuits and gravy to share. The omelet was very good. I loved it. My English muffin was slightly burned. The biscuits and gravy were very good though cold. The pancakes were very good. The two sides were larger portions than I expected. Yummy leftovers for later. My husbands chicken fried steak and hash were not very good mine was really good overall.
Amazing bagel sandwiches and coffee ! Worth the wait! We had the frittata scramble and the standard.

I've always loved tea and finally started getting into loose leaf teas! The workers here always have great advice and recommendations! I love that they let you smell all the teas and have samples! It's a little pricey, but the quality of the tea is superb and totally worth it! I'm addicted!

SPOT has been a Friday night staple and generally the high-point of my week.  I tend to start fantasizing about the Big Mick around Wednesday night, and am a little giddy by the time I order.  The cheese steaks are tremendous, the French fries are on point, and the Charlie Brown fills me with shame in the way that only a great burger can.

The staff are wonderful as well--polite, efficient, funny, and they make a point of calling you by name every single time.

Most places in Fairmount and Art Museum tend to be pretty mediocre, in part because they can be.  As long as you make food that is on par with a chain restaurant or bar food that approaches adequacy, you can probably make do with the tourists coming through.  It's so nice to see a place that takes pride in using quality ingredients to make delicious food for a reasonable price.
Fast Fair & friendly..  located inside Baldini's Casino. Not fancy but quality & quantity will not leave you hungry.

Overall it's a nice restaurant. Both the brunch and dinner menus have good options, the interior space is pretty, and they have an open kitchen that is cool to watch. The price is high for what you get though and is relatively similar to other brunch/dinner places in the area

The Village Health Market is where I go when I can't find "it" at Whole Foods. 

Don't let the nondescript exterior fool you, it's actually a fairly large grocery store tucked into a strip mall with an enormous selection of vitamins and supplements, a full deli, a cafe, a small wine department and a great frozen foods section too.  Their bulk spice department is really handy for when I have a recipe calling for a tablespoon of something I don't use on a regular basis, and they have a great selection of bulk organic and raw dried fruits, nuts, legumes and grains to choose from.  They have a nice selection of gluten-free, vegan and organic foods with a larger variety than any other local store I've visited.  

Of course, you will pay handsomely for anything you purchase here -  the same item would cost about 15-20% less at Whole Foods, in my experience, but you can negate that by shopping on the 3rd Wednesday of every month (Wild Wednesday) or the last Saturday of the month (Saturday Sale) when you get 20% off everything storewide.  

The next time you're having lunch at Pane Rustica stop by The Village Health Market just a few storefronts down, if you're even the least bit health conscious I think you'll love it!

I would give this place 0 stars if I could. I always get such bad attitude when I come.  I ordered a tire and a rim, they told me it would take a week, so I called the next week to see if it was in and they told me it was.  I drove out there and when I got there, they had me wait almost an HOUR and it was the wrong one!! So I had to wait an extra week.  On top of that, the woman I dealt with was incredibly patronizing and had a terrible attitude.  I got a free oil change for complaining and I still haven't gone back for the free oil change, not worth my time!!

Hometown Health is fine unless you need something. They "Forgot" to pay one of my Dr.'s 
and good luck trying to find someone to help with that. The people in charge NEVER take phone calls and the poor folks who answer the phones have to hear about it.. I'm on terminal hold right now for the next supervisor in charge hoping MAYBE this one will call me back. Let me repeat, it was their mistake, per a documented phone call and now it's in collections..
Attorney call in the near future.. Hope you have better luck

One of my favorite places. The music is good. The food is very good. The bar staff is courteous and Shannon is one of the best servers in the city.

In fact, my girlfriend and I met here at the bar.

I have had nice dinners, casual nights out with friends, and absolute "rip-the-paint-off-the-wall" benders with the boys at Chris' and thoroughly enjoyed them all.

This is a sorry excuse for a post office.  The staff in this place are horrible.  Today there were 15 people waiting in line and only ONE station was opened.  The elderly lady working behind the counter was rather slow but ....she's not rude like the other ones in there.  It just really seems to me that these people HATE their job.  AND a stamp machine needs to be put in there.....it rather sucks to have to wait in line for postage stamps.  Put a stamp machine in this place (on the inside inside part).......I'd love to have a job like that and they move around like they have 50 pound weights in their shoes.......come on!!!!!!

Best cheesesteak around!!!!! Ask for the sarcone roll , Get the large cheesesteak w extra cheese and Longhots , AMAZING !!! never had a bad experience w staff or timing of order, other bad reviews make no sense

I was recently invited to and Indian wedding at a Hindu temple and was in a panic to get a sari for the event. I only had about a week to do so. There aren't many stores in Philadelphia that carry saris. I stumbled upon Foreign Bazaar and decided to check it out. Chanta, the woman who works there was so helpful! We first looked at ready-made blouses and found a gold beaded blouse that I knew would match many different saris and I could wear again. Chanta adjusted the blouse so that it fit perfectly. Next I picked out a sari. They have a huge selection of beautiful saris, it took me a while to decide! Once I found "the one" Chanta draped me in it so I could get an idea of how it would look on. When I payed for everything they honored the yelp check-discount. I wouldn't say it was cheap but the service was great and worth the $100 or so that I spent. Chanta even dressed me the day of the event!  I definitely recommend Foreign Bazaar for all things Indian as well as beads.

These are the nicest people you'll ever deal with. Products are fresh and very fairly priced. A real class act. On veteran's day, they give away  cider and donuts to veterans. I just found them and I'll never go any place else. Thanks to everyone that works there and does such a great job!

Seated instantly.  Seated at a long horseshoe-type arrangement with several other families and an older couple looking as bemused as us that we couldn't be seated in one of the open 2-seat booths available. Place is LOUD and that's without any noticeable music playing. Waited 15 minutes for someone to take our order--didn't happen--so we left.

I have dealt with Micro Center before and I am a fan. This is the best one I have dealt with.

My sister was in a mad rush to get a new laptop and also had need for a printer. One stop shopping here for sure. Very helpful salespeople. Got a print/scan/fax machine for a freaking steal, like mindblowing. They were out of the megasale laptop we were seeking but they found my sister an awesome substitute in a hurry that she is quite happy with. 

Yep. If you need it and you want it cheap, come here.

Ron's is awesome. I got there all the time, mostly for lunch during the work week. Their oval's are crazy delicious. The service is always solid. The beer list is always filled with a rotating selection of craft beer and they have a pretty extensive bottle list as well.

Went for Sunday brunch... was looking forward to going, but left disappointed. 
Waited a while for a server despite very few people dining; most of the food was cold, bagels and croissants were stale, French toast was hard. Most items were not refilled until a patron tracked down someone, otherwise plates and service stations remained empty. Only reason I gave it 2 stars was for the eggs benedict- looked and tasted delicious. 
Sub par brunch buffet for the price (for 2 people, paid $80 including tip)

Pretty clean place and a short drive from Micron.  Now KFC got this grilled chicken thing, it makes me feel better when I eat their chicken.  And since this is a KFC and A&W partner, I get to drink the root beer right out of the tap.  They also have a drive thru which is pretty cool.  Now I think back, age 21 was like a barrier for me, I went from craving fried chicken every week to not wanting it that much, just feel like my body can't process that much fat and grease anymore, maybe that is a good thing.

My wife and I were vacationing in Santa Barbara and wanted to try some local Mexican food to compare to the Tex-Mex we are accustomed to in Dallas. El Torito was a block from our hotel and had received positive reviews, so we decided to give it a shot. Not until after the fact did we learn that El Torito is West Coast chain .

Even so, Mexican chains can still be good (see El Fenix in Dallas), but the staff at the Santa Barbara location seemed to have adopted the laid back personality of the local beach goers. Though the restaurant was virtually empty, it took us a couple of minutes to be set. We saw the hostess sitting at the bar, but instead of getting up to seat us herself, she made one of the servers come and seat us.

As far as food goes, El Torito is good, but nothing spectacular. I had the chicken fajitas, which were a bit sweeter than the Tex-Mex version. The salsa was also way milder than what is served in Tex-Mex. My wife ordered a margarita, which was extremely watered down.

Food gets a B-; service gets an F and atmosphere gets a C. Add it up and this El Torito gets 2 stars from me.

By far the worst looking inside and out hotel I've ever stayed in. The lady at the front desk did help me out because I arrived 3 hours early an she let me in my room two hours early, so kudos to her. Didnt leave cuz I'm on military orders and didnt wanna go through the hassle of paperwork an other things to getting another hotel AND the other hotels in the area near the airport was 150 and up. I'll  let the pictures do the talking. 97 dollars for this. Smh. Thanks PA

Went for Sips Happy Hour.   My first time to this restaurant.   It surely did not live up to its reputation.   Our waiter was VERY informal. Had to ask for water 3 times before I received it.  Drinks came, no napkins of any kind provided.  Asked if we could order  hors doerves from the regular menu, told yes if we were willing to wait until after Sips was over at 7pm for them.  Received water in a big red plastic cup.   Later I noticed a dark hair on the inside of the cup and pointed it out to our waiter.   He responded emphatically "THAT DIDN'T COME FROM HERE!!!"  (My hair is very light and short).   So if they thought that a Sips visit would encourage us to coming to the restaurant at a later date...they were much mistaken.   Too bad

This one visit seals it for me. This store's customer service is excellent. We bought two pounds of hamburger, among other things, for a family barbecue. Grabbing our sacks of groceries out of the cart and heading to the parking lot, we failed to notice that the hamburger packages had slid out of the sack, remaining in the cart. 

We arrived at home and realized we had no meat. Despairing of any recourse save buying two more pounds of hamburger, we returned to the store. 

The customer service representative listened to our tale of woe, glanced at our receipt, tracked down the clerk who checked us out, confirmed finding two unclaimed packages of hamburger and asked a bagger to fetch them for us. Happy ending!

Well done, Albertsons!

Came here again with 2 friends and opted for: a fried stuffed ravioli with ricotta and chocolate, and a chocolate covered banana with raspberry jam and an almond cookie. Both were perfect. I want more!

Fast, efficient repairs and honest, professional service.  What more can you ask for?  Brought my car in to have the air conditioning fixed, but they do a little of everything.  Oh, and offer a military discount to boot.  I'll definitely be back next time the old girl gets  sick and needs some tending to.

not only are the workers racist against white people...
It was snowing like crazy out, it took me 2 ubers to get to the theater. I had on snow boots and a change of shoes in a small bag. They claimed I could not enter with a backpack of "X" dimensions and that it was a new policy. fine. however, my bag was noticeably smaller than the allotted dimensions and they still would not let me in. "Hide your bag behind that chair in the lobby" they said. (Background: my bag is mark jacobs and is worth about $300). They let in an older black gentlemen with a backpack much larger than mine right after me. All the workers are black and I am a small white woman. I will never come back here. I left feeling victimized, humiliated and violated.

What a disappointment. There's actually a lady (Abuelita) who makes tortillas is the kitchen. However, there are NO burritos on the menu. Go figure. Also, the salsa they give you is good, but it's served in a bowl that holds about 3 tablespoons, plus we had to ask for it. The green salsa was so salty, it tasted like it had soy sauce in it. Nothing particularly good about this place other than the fact that it was a New Mexican restaurant that we were excited to try, but left very disappointed.

The bowl of soul mocha was pretty awesome, though the place was absolutely packed when we went! It wasn't all bad though, we took our coffees and walked up to the park to enjoy them. Definitely recommend checking this place out if you want a break from your usual coffee routine. Great stuff!

My husband and I just relocated to a new city, St Petersburg, and Brian Frey exceeded our expectations in every way, in particular, with his prompt attention to our home search, and impeccable guest service before, during, and following our purchase.  We would not have been as successful throughout this process without his vast expertise, local contacts, industry knowledge, and incredible customer care.  Based on my past experience with buying and selling property with relators, Brian has raised the bar and surpassed my experience with all the other agents I have worked with.  I was referred to him by dear friends in the area, and I am beyond grateful for getting matched with him.   When we first connected, he appeared to drop everything he was doing to listen in great length to and respond to our needs.  His daily follow up via phone and e mail never failed, and we were able to stick to our very intense timeline from start to finish.  His local contacts from mortgage lenders to inspection services to pest control were expert in every way, and worked seamlessly with our team.  Following the purchase of our new property, Brian was equally as attentive with unexpected issues that arose, which could not have been anticipated, and he supported us beyond expectations through resolution.  We give Brian our full support and would challenge any agent to surpass his level of excellence.

Be prepared to wait at least 45 mins total on Saturdays for your order here. My husband called in to place our order and was told it would take about 25 minutes. We had to wait an additional 20ish minutes when we got here and so did everyone else in line. 

The breakfast sandwiches were good. The light, fluffy brioche bun here sets it apart from other breakfast sandwich shops. A bit disappointed that the eggs were overcooked even though we ate the sandwiches as soon as we got them. I'm not a fan of a dry egg yolk. But overall, I did enjoy the Free Bird sandwich and my husband enjoyed the sausage, egg and cheese. Just a heads up, the sausage in the sandwich is not a breakfast sausage but their house made sausage links cut in half.

What a great place. We had the grilled oysters as starter. Shared the duck and steak as main course and ordered the caramel desert. Amazing food for a good price. And great wine. Would definitely go again and highly recommend it to others
TC and Peaches were our guides on a ghost tour.  Great information and very entertaining.  Would highly recommend to anyone, not just ghost enthusiast.

After waiting for 20 mins the couple next to us left the restaurant. We stayed there some more mins and left as well. This is ridiculous!!! 
No service at all. It's not recommendable. 
I would give no stars if possible.

This place is horrible!!!! First they have cockroaches the size of tennisballs crawling on the wall and when I got my sandwich it had mouse crap in it!!!! Grossss!  Not to mention the completely horrid service at this place. The one manager rick(?) Is a jerk. He has about as much class as he has hair (not much)!

I ate here because I had just flown in to town and couldn't get in anywhere else. There is a reason there were plenty of seats at Coterie. The food was blah. (rubbery blackened alligator and seafood gumbo that was soooooooo salty I could hardly eat it). Service was a mess despite the bartender trying to be friendly. 

Do yourself a favor and skip dinner if this is your only option for getting a seat.

Victoria's Secret is a girly girls dream and I'm a girly girl for sure.  I always have to get some Christmas jammies every year from VS, they always have such cute styles.  Upon entering the store I was greeted by a sweet girl and she even helped me pick out some P.J's and I got some free matching slippers!  Yay!  I also picked up the cutest sweater with "Angel" on the front.  I love this store, but now for the bad news. I got in line to check out and it wasn't that long, but it took forever!  I mean I was seriously starting to get grey hair!  They only had one girl at the registers and if she had moved any slower she would have started going backwards.  Then she started yelling at us to form a better line!  Seriously!  lol.   I really wanted my stuff or I probably would have left.  After a long wait, my hubby went looking to see if he maybe he could find someone else to jump on a register.  He came back saying "no luck", but then someone did come and opened up another register so things went a little faster. Overall, this is a lovely store! When you go shopping here, just bring along your patience.

OMG. Slowest and most expensive Starbucks in town, maybe in America!
My $5.94 skinny vanilla latte is $6.47 here!
And they only have 1 barista no matter how busy they are- this morning there were 12 people waiting for their coffee and another 10 in line to order/ waiting 20".
Do yourself a favor - get your caffeine before you arrive here - the SB nearby on Kennedy is cheaper and very fast!

Can you imagine a beer called mahalo? Well this is the place to go try it. 
I love wheat beer and the flavor of this belgian is to die for. It was perfectly refreshing.

I had the drunk monk burger, which has the best name for a burger ever.. I had it with no bun and salad instead of fries. The flavor of the brie cheese  and caramelized onions with the mushrroms was perfectly balanced. 

The siracha alioli and sweet potatoe fries my friend ordered and I kept eating was amazing.

This is my second time at Iron Hill  and I truly love their service, ambience, food annndd beer
Amazing french food!  Wonderful cute & quaint atmosphere.  We sat outside, the wait staff was very knowledgeable and the food just melted in my mouth.  If you love french food, this is a must go!

I came in with the my family today. The food was delicious and having been a server in restaurants for 15 years I was impressed. Our server Victoria was delightful and engaging. She told us of the uniqueness of the sauces. OMG the food the was so good. Please check this place out if you have the chance.

This location has always been pretty good to me. Today was outstanding. A friend and I went for brunch and it was remarkable. She had the chicken fried steak that was crisp and tender. I opted for the overloaded brunch platter that comes with a six ounce sirloin that I ordered medium rare and it was cooked and seasoned to perfection, two eggs, bacon, potatoes, half a waffle, and fresh fruit. It was fantastic. Add to that a couple mimosas and brunch was complete. We sat at the bar and the service was outstanding. I don't have dinner here so I cannot speak to that yet, but this brunch could become a weekly pre-game adventure.

Service is hit or miss and the food is uninspired and decent at best, however the prices are always consistent...overpriced for what you get.  The sad part is that the place should be awesome, it has a great upscale sports bar setting, but for reasons mentioned earlier it doesn't come close to living up to it's potential.  What a shame.

Short visit for Mother's Day weekend to see my sister race in Wilmington marathon.

Was not sure what to expect coming from NYC taste buds but wow, was superbly amazed by this place.

First we had reservations at 7pm but stopped in earlier to see if they could seat us.  We had an hour to order and eat at 6 and went for it (it was my husband and 7 and 11 YO girls mind you)

Started with octopus appetizer which had a delicious tomato type purée that was fantastic, cheese and charcuterie app (spreads or chutney were wonderful); husband ordered a shrimp croquette type app also delicious; entrees: husband had the steak and it was amazingly flavorful and cooked perfect at med rare. I shared with the girls the risotto with braised short ribs (risotto on point and not too heavy but wasn't too impressed w/short ribs and last had gnocchi with mushrooms w/a perfectly light cream sauce which was the best dish of all.  Gnocchi was so fresh and not too dense or felt heavy.

We finished every bit of the food and in just an hour!  Place was outstanding!
Bummed to see such bad reviews. Anthony was great! Explained everything and was very thorough! Thanks Anthony!

Went in a few months ago and just getting around to writing a review.

We walked in around 8PM on a Sunday night. There was one other couple who was finishing up their dinner. We ordered 2 hamburgers and 2 milkshakes. One burger was delivered to our table about 5 minutes before the other one was....which was sitting under the light for a bit until they realized it was ready. The milkshakes didn't make it to the table until about 1/2 of our burgers were eaten....because and I quote "We didn't have any straws". 

ALL THIS while the workers were chatting up with a friend. Will never go back.
Great prices and thumbs up to Ashley! She is such an upbeat, positive employee! Does a great job of running the store.

Loved this little spot. My husband and I were staying at this resort, and since I'm not a huge fan of seafood this was an easy decision for us. My husband ate shrimp and the steak salad. He loved both. Even I was tempted by the smell of the shrimp seasoning despite the fact I hate shrimp! I ordered the sloppy joe nachos. SO GOOD! Yes, the food is a a bit pricey, but they did not skimp on the portion sizes or quality. I also ordered a drink which was good. The view is gorgeous and ideal for a sunset meal. All and all, a lovely spot.

The Dandelion is amazing for brunch!  They have a great menu selection and it's not the typical same items you see everywhere else.  This is definitely one of my favorite breakfast spots! The food is great and so is the service. Also they are very kid-friendly and accommodating to families.

Admittedly, I stopped in after hitting up Total Wine down the street, as I ventured from downtown STL. I wanted a specific rum that they have at the large City warehouse. Alas, it was not at this location. That said, the selection on other things is decent but it's a lot of 'one bottle only'.

The store itself is a bit dated, like the liquor store you had to go to with grandma as a kid. I dunno, I'm indifferent on it. The liquor I had just bought down the street was $9 more here but I don't generally find that to be the case at Randall's.

I have been doing to Dr. Curtis for 3 years now and I have been nothing short of pleased with his service. He is the only optometrist to diagnose my astigmatism and prescribe me the proper contacts. I have another appointment to see him soon and I have the highest confidence in him. He's very polite and professional and I always look forward to my yearly visit.

Pearings earns an easy five stars for its extensive array of offerings, clean & trendy atmosphere, and friendly service. I love froyo but I always associate yogurt shops with messy toppings bars, drippy machines, bright neon colors, and quirky plastic furniture. Pearings really raised the bar by offering tasty froyo and an impressive amount of toppings but in a clean, stylish, and relaxed atmosphere that you actually enjoy hanging out in. They also have an impressive menu with something for everyone (vegans, health nuts, everyone) including smoothies, shakes, raw juice, acai, crepes, coffee, and tea.  Along with breakfast items like waffles and oatmeal, they have a lunch menu too with several savory crepes! Positioned on the cultural trail downtown, it's very easy to get to via bike. I hereby declare Pearings my new go-to meeting place for drinks or treats, and I can't wait to get back to try a crepe with nutella!

Great gelato and a really cool store... bad for crowds.  We must have walked in during the peak of a rush and it was obvious the staff was straining to handle it.  It is frustrating to watch something clunking along, even at a place that should be happy and benign like Sucre.  Lucky for me, my coconut basil sorbet was awesome, otherwise my frustration would have drained my will to live... kidding.  They just need to get better organized to handle a crush of patrons.

I would have purchased about $100 in chocolate, but could not get anyone to help me, the staff was too overwhelmed.  When I grabbed someone to help me pick some chocolates to send to a family member, I was told I needed to go online... So, I left a little frustrated.

If I could give it a 0 I would.  

Took my family out for an early dinner.  We arrived at the Wayne location.  The place was very clean with only 1-2 tables in the dining room.  So service should be great?!?  Well it was not.  The server was laughing and joking throughout our meal and less than attentive.

The food.  I ordered the stuffed pork chop special, my son ordered the sliders from the kids menu, my daughter ordered the chicken parm and my wife ordered the roast beef and cheddar sand.

My stuffed park chop was over cooked and was not stuffed.  My wife's sandwich was in the window for at least 10 mins and was dried and wilted.  My sons burgers was an adult meal with onions and also under the heat lamp for at least 10 mins.  2 meals had to go back and the 3rd sucked.  The waitress was clueless and offered no explanation for the mistakes.

So, the food was bad, the service was poor and it ruined Saturday dinner.  I would suggest anyone avoid these restaurants.  It won't be long until it's out of business and it going to be deserved.  Avoid!!!!

it's a chain Restaurant from STL
i like their RavioLi Appetizer ^.^
Wonderful restaurant. I like the food and environment. - I have to add more because this site says my review is a little too short. - good eats! Cheers!

My family chose this place because it was recommended. I'm very particular about cleanliness and I noticed that it wasn't clean, the tables were dirty, the trash was full, the counter where they prepared food was a mess. My husband and son really wanted to eat here. I ordered 2 birria tacos and a seafood taco. I'm allergic to gluten and they only had 2 corn tortillas. I was disappointed I could only have 2 tacos. My husband enjoyed the smokehouse and birria tacos he had. My son enjoyed the potato bowl. I did not enjoy my experience, but they aren't as particular as me.
Another great experience with Hickory Kitchen. This time we had a them cater a birthday party. The BBQ got rave reviews and the corn bread was unbelievable. I love this place and the staff.

On point bbq goodness. Went with the brisket and salmon. Salmon was smoked and done perfect and not too salty which I appreciated. Brisket was done well but I hoped for a bit more smokiness flavor but it was still great. Sauces were also very good and even the pasta and salad sides were fantastic. Will visit again. Only reason for the 4 stars is the price being on the steep side for what I normally see for bbq and also the long wait time. This place is busy, but for a good reason.  But wait, there is more.., next door there is a sweet surprise. You will have to come here first to find out :)

I love the tacos here and the new location is great! My favorite taco is the Macho which is 
Chopped steak stuffed in an Anaheim chile with melted cheese. The salsas are delicious and come in a great variety. The cabbage wraps are a great option for the paleo/low carb folks.

Ok WOW.... What a load of shit Dominos has become.  I remember it wasn't that long ago they were a safe bet if you wanted something good and not too expensive.

Obviously someone made the executive decision to be a greedy fuckin bastard and let the quality go absolutely straight to hell.

The delivery after a $3 tip I payed $40 fuckin dollars for an x large pizza with about 7 toppings total.  One of the items I love the most is sausage.  Ok...now for $40 fuckin dollars I expected there to be at least 4 to 5 pieces of sasuge per slice.....  What I got instead was about 1.5 pieces (OR LESS!!!) per slice and to top it off it was the shitty sliced kind....   And the tomato sauce and ranch they make you pay for all tasted like shit too....  The whole thing was fuckin bullshit.........FUCKIN BULLSHITT!!!!!!!!!

This little gem, which calls itself the "best Mexican Restaurant in town", is a fabulous find on the road between NYC and DC. I am not sure which town to which they are referring, but it does offer some hearty competition to Mex Food in both NYC and DC. 

From the outside the place does not look like much, just off a busy highway and with no visible sitting area. Step through the door and you will feel like Dr Who entering his TARDIS as you walk into a spacious eating establishment with an aura of sabrosa.

To begin with, the chips and salsa are of the best I've had North of the Rio Grande. The camarera brought two salsas- green and orange. Both are delicious and spicy in entirely different ways--but in a way that tells you they are not made for the average northeastern Gringo.

For a drink I ordered horchata-- and it came in a big class of sumptuous satisfaction.  Next came enchiladas poblano- chicken with the chocolate sauce- common to Oaxaca.  All in all, quite delicious. I am glad I stopped here- now time to stop writing so I can finish my dinner!

Went on 10/25/17, a Wednesday evening. The service was great, the staff were friendly and professional. The ambience of the interior is a little funky. It looks like Graceland met Italy. A little gaudy but it works. 
Now to the food. The mussel appetizer and the salad were excellent!  However, the Tutto Mare and the Cajun Pasta has issues. The pasta was cooked perfectly but the dishes lacked flavor. Both were bland. The Cajun Pasta has little flavor but did have a slight heat at the end of a bite. The Tutto Mare was one note. 
I will try this restaurant again.
Excellent breakfast. The omelet was delicious. Service was prompt and friendly. Would definitely eat there again.

The ultimate homebrew supply store/beer garden.  They use their own products to brew all types of beer.  And these brews (and meads, and rootber) are excellent!  They offer a relaxed, family friendly environment.  The staff are helpful and always give you tastes of anything you not quite sure about.  On Friday's and Saturday evenings they also bring in various food trucks.  Great place to hang.

Lunch Special! 

Mr B's Bistro has the best lunch special not on their food, but on the drinks!

$1.50 for a Bloody Mary or a Martini?! I am buying!!

I have to admit that I am a sloppy eater. I was impressed and a little embarrassed when our friendly waitstaff started putting a bib around my neck and prepping me for the highlight of our lunch:

MR. B'S BARBEQUED SHRIMP - A Mr. B's signature dish! Gulf shrimp barbequed New Orleans style, served in the shells with a peppery butter sauce, and French bread for dipping $18.50 

Finger licking good! The sauce definitely the highlight, I would have be happy to just eat that with French bread! Sauce was flying everywhere, now I know the need for the bib.
