PASTA JAMBALAYA - Gulf shrimp, andouille sausage, oven-roasted duck and chicken morsels, tossed with spinach $16.50

The kitchen has nicely divided our order evenly and served to us individually. Really good flavours of spiciness and smokiness. Portion size is great either for sharing or as a meal. 

We received one of the best service during our trip to NOLA here. The staff just so amazing and I felt pampered and like one of the celebrities! 

I will be back again to Mr. B's Bistro for their great Creole food and Southern hospitality!

Great atmosphere and lovely place.  We ordered delicious tuna tartare as an appetizer to accompany our cocktails.  The bartender is excellent!  

I really appreciate that they offer a gluten free lunch/dinner menu.

As for the entree I had the sea scallops and the risotto was amazing.

I would give 5 stars but we traveled in and parking was a pain.  We didn't realize they offered valet until walking up to the place.

Food and service was great!

I would have liked to give it more stars, but...I just couldn't. 

Let me just say that the service was perfect. I came on a Saturday at 12:00 and was immediately seated. The waitress was great about everything and really helpful. She refilled my Diet Coke, even when it wasn't completely empty.  

The reason it's getting 3 stars, is because of the burger. I ordered the lobster burger, because I had heard good things about it, but...well, to be honest, the flavor combinations probably worked, but it was so poorly executed, that I couldn't tell. I ordered it rare...but it came out well done. The waitress apologized a million times and had them remake it, but the second time it was cooked medium well. I mean, if 90% of your menu is burgers and you cook the same thing all day long...I would have thought you'd have perfected it. 

.

Difficult review to write!! 
Pros: View can't be beat! We were shown to a table by a friendly hostess as soon as we walked up. She took us to a table right on the dock as we requested. Hooter's provides a canopy over the sun exposed tables so it was perfect.
PROS: Drinks were cold and delivered quickly. I tried the Dragonberry Mojito which was FANTASTIC and fresh made. We also had white sangria which is always very good. 
          Buffalo shrimp is my go to favorite thing here, I order them medium, and were fantastic as always. Very crisp even after being tossed in the delicious sauce! 
          Fries: I always order some with cheese sauce to break up the seafood flavors. They do great curly fries here.
Cons: I only have one but it is a biggy. I have eaten oysters at Hooter's many time. Always steamed and always served on the half shell. We ordered a dozen and I was surprised to be brought a plate with oyster shucking knife and towel. I thought it was odd but didn't say anything. Then I got my oysters. In the shell. Closed. Umm.... They want me to shuck my own oysters? OK. I'm a local and Florida raised so no problem, I guess. But what about our visitors? Is this part of the experience? So I start shucking. We ate half a dozen perfectly steamed oysters. I mean PERFECT! This is not an easy thing to do and the kitchen should be commended. Then I got tired. And I looked down at my shirt and I was a mess from shucking. And I smelled like a dead fish. I told the waitress to take the rest back and give me half credit on the bill because no matter how good they taste I was done opening them. She shucked the rest for us and we ate them cold because she was so nice. BEYOND UNACCEPTABLE!
         SHRIMP!!  So so so very very over cooked!!! Dead as all hell. Shells so soft they were almost impossible to peel. TIP TO MANAGEMENT TO SHARE WITH THE KITCHEN: Never ever ever put peel and eat shrimp under a warming light!! That is just one of those things you can't do with seafood. The shells turn to mush in 30 seconds! These shrimp sat under a light for a few minutes. Dry as a bone and so hard to peel. The seagulls got half our order.

Excellent experience totally spoiled by a weird corporate/management decision. Why in the world would any customer want to shuck their own oysters?? My oysters are just as fresh with a guy doing them to order in a kitchen 20 feet away without me making a mess of myself at all. Not to mention the fact that I paid $12.95 for the dozen! Shame on you for that alone considering where we live but don't charge me that and make me shuck the damn things!!! I can't stress what an epic fail that is!!

 Service was fantastic from the hostess station to our waitress to the bartender who told me how to make the Dragonberry Mojito.

Expect to pay. We were 3 people. We had the 10 buffalo shrimp, a dozen steamed oysters, and a small order of steamed shrimp. Side of fries with cheese sauce. 2 beers, 1 large white sangria and 2 dragonberry mojitos. $70 before tip

Nestled away in the idyllic Berry Hill, The Loving Pie Company is a hidden Nashville gem. With a diverse menu of both the sweet and savory, this place could be any pie lover's dream. I have been working my way through the menu and have yet to be disappointed. Are they as good as grandma used to make? Pretty darn close. With an adorable location and friendly staff, this is an excellent place to grab an afternoon slice.

My only real complaint  is that "quick" is not in the dictionary of this establishment, so if you want a to-go order, be sure to call ahead. Otherwise, you'll be waiting about thirty minutes for your carry out. Some of that is due to the nature of the dish, but the LPC is also pretty laid back about getting orders out. 

Be sure to check out the daily specials, posted on Facebook!

I finally got the whisky sour I've been looking for. The place looks great and they have the worlds heaviest bar chairs lol. They got a heavy selection of whisky for your enjoyment. Now the menu looks like it has a great selection and I opted for the hot fried chicken strips. My cousin got the shrimp & grits....both kind "meh". So we move on to other foods. They said the steak is legit, so my other cousin got that and I got the short rib sliders..."meh", again. The whole food selection we got was meh actually. I'll come back for the brunch and a whisky sour with less ice. The waitress was 5 star but she's leaving for Ga.....so " meh", again! LOL

SOOOO good! I am glad that we found this place via yelp. My fiance and I went while vacationing and it was well worth the cab ride. He wanted to keep coming back here for breakfast lunch and dinner! We definitely would have if there weren't places that we also wanted to try. I would definitely recommend this place if you are looking for BBQ in the Big Easy.
Food is very good.  Atmostphere is nice.  Service is good.  Time between courses is slow, every time I've been here it has been too slow.  Quicken the pace in the kitchen and I'll give 4 or 5 stars.

I give Pizzeria Tivoli a 5 star all around! From the moment we arrived to the moment we left we had wonderful service and amazing food and drinks. 

My husband's brother was in town last weekend. He really liked pizza and I jokingly call him the Pepperoni pizza connoisseur! He likes pizza and I was challenged to find a pizza place that would "wow" him! I found it. 

My husband, his brother and I arrived around 6:15 on a Saturday and were greeted by the host by the patio. Since it was such a great night out we opted to eat on the patio. Once we sat down water was brought over and our server came by quickly to get our drink orders. We also started with the pizza plate, which was basically a bunch of pizza crust without toppings. My brother-in-law (BIL) asked for it since we had to try the crust before we tried the rest of the pizza. I also ordered the Tivoli salad as a starter for my hubby and I to share. 

Our drinks were brought out and as we were waiting for our appetizers I couldn't help but notice the music...the music station they had playing was making me smile. Every single song was a song I knew and I wanted to sign out loud. Best music station at a restaurant for real. Everything from Rihanna to Jessica Simpson and even some Michael Jackson mixed in. What a great mix! Anyway...

When our salad and pizza plate came out it all looked great. The salad was so colorful and was big enough to share. The dressing was light and the toppings on the salad were just right. The pizza plate was pretty good too. They "crust" was just crispy enough and they even gave a nice evoo dipping sauce for us to try. My BIL was very pleased with the crust. He said that it was good enough to move forward with the pizza ordering. Our server came over to check on us and at that time we ordered some pizzas. My hubby and I wanted to try their BBQ chicken pizza and the BIL ordered the pepperoni. I knew my hubby could try a slice of the pepperoni too so we had more than enough for all of us. 

While waiting for our pizzas we continued to enjoy our salad and crust and just chatted with one another. Our server came by a few times to make sure we were good and the host even stopped by to refill our waters. Nice teamwork! After about 15-20 minutes we had our brick oven pizzas in front of us and they looked so cheesy and good! The BIL was all ready to dig in and I was too! Of course, I had to make sure he got the first bite and once he was a few bites in he gave the "thumb's up". Here's how a little conversation went: 

BIL: "I am glad you didn't take me here the other night"
Me: "Why is that?"
BIL: "Because I would have begged to come back every other night"

I think that just about says it all. The pizza was crispy and flavorful. The toppings were abundant and scattered evenly. We ate our pizza and enjoyed every bite. 

I even opted to order dessert. They order their desserts from a place on the hill, but I don't know which one. I had the coconut lemon cake which was just wonderful and a great way to end the meal. 

Overall, we all had a great meal and wonderful service. I can't wait to go back again.

Good food. Good service. Good beer selection. We had the wings, the grilled cheese, and a bruschetta pizza. Everything was perfect and the staff was very nice and helpful. We will be going back. Looks like a nice patio for cooler weather.

3rd time here (in 4 days) and had a chance to really explore the menu and I have to knock off a star for pricing. If you want a burger and fries it's $16. It's enough fries for 2 but an $11 burger should come with something more than coleslaw. You'll find some other high priced items/small-ish portions on the menu as well but you'll also find all the food is quite good. We will keep coming but I hope they adjust the pricing and portions to reflect other quality bars in town.

Ribeye sandwich or even platter highly recommended. 
Good cuts. No fat. 
Fries are good too. 
Platter comes with baked potato if you're into that. 

They're busy so you might not be able to call ahead, but keep trying. It's worth it

I came for my birthday with a reservation. Very nice & super fancy. I think it is a little bit too high end to come all the time for me personally. Would come for special occasions or to impress someone (; food was good I think we just had a overcooked lobster that was fine, we didn't mention to be corrected. Highly recommend the sea bass. Brussel sprouts, potatoes, and the filet were average in my opinion.
I did have a great experience and made my night special. Ty Fleming's staff.
The food court upstairs is great, I loved their spicy chicken

Mike from Drainmen was referred to me by his brother who is an electrician.  I just purchased my first property and I had 3 plumbing issues that needed to be addressed.  
My inspector noted a slow flush in the half bathroom.  When Mike arrived on time he checked the toilet and said the pressure was good and the tank itself was good size.  
The second item on the report was a loose metal plate that covers the shower pipe coming out of the wall.  There was also space between the pipe and the drywall that needed to be filled in.  I asked him if I could just do it myself with some clear silicone and he said sure.  It worked great.
Lastly there were some exit pipes from the tub that still haven't been changed over to PVC and were showing some corrosion according to the inspector.  Mike said he didn't feel it was that corroded and certainly didn't need to be replaced right away.  That was nice to hear since it's about a $500 job.  
When he was getting ready to go I asked him how much I owed him and he said don't worry about.  He wouldn't even take $50 just for coming out from Pottstown to Eagleville.
How many plumbers or service people in any industry would tell you that you don't need any repairs when you have called them in specifically to fix something.  That's the right answer.  Not very many.  Do yourself a favor and give Drainmen a shot for your next plumbing repair.  You won't be disappointed.

The first thing we noticed was the dirty carpets.  I was expecting a nice interior from the other reviews (not sure if Essie reviewed the right restaurant - what table decorations), but it was not impressive at all and the wall decorations very bland.  It was early and we were the firsts guests, yet they had to seat the next couple of guests at the 2 tables right next to us.  Food was nothing special.  We will not go back.

I got a car but not very satisfy with my payment. The bank tells you what they want from you and there is no way you can change it or fight it. They always win. The car was over price by 500 dollars and Tyler said that their was no way they can bring the price down that they have a no hassle price. Because of my interest being so high I ended up not buying the extended warranty that I wanted to buy. Again the nu hassle deal. They don't detail the cars like they supposed to but good thing I have a wife that knows how and now looks like new in the inside. We are having some issues with the car and I understand they are taking care of these issues but the worst part on is that the driver side heater not working and with this weather it really needs it. They need to check the cars better before they sale them. One last thing the car I got has hail damage on the top and they told me that they won't fix it because it is a use car. I don't know if that was the reason why the car was 500 over priced. It was not a 3000 car it was a 25000 car so why can't they fix it. Thank you

I was so excited to try this place especially since they offer gluten free bread , unfortunately the lil girl at the counter that took my order was sooo unpleasant & just bitchy , if you are so unhappy with your customer service job maybe look into another type of occupation ...oh well guess ill be taking my business else where

Go! Join your community in a fun gathering of dancers, gigglers, relaxers, and friends. 

Music makes our soul grow. Go grow and dance.

What a disappointment -ORDER TAKEOUT AT YOUR OWN RISK!!!!
The owner is extremely rude and condescending. He is unprofessional and disrespectful to customers who complain about paying for 4 Kobe beef burgers that were overcooked to the point that they tasted like rubber. When I ordered I told the person who took my order that I did NOT want the burgers well done. In fact, I asked that the burgers be pink in the center and that we were not going to eat them until later in the evening. Well surprise, surprise!!!! Burnt burgers for my dinner guest. An order that included a couple sides and milkshakes to the tune of a wasted $125 including a 15% tip for the takeout.
The fact that the owner had the nerve to tell me that my husband was 15 minutes late to pick up the food and that I don't ever have to come back to his restaurant is why I'm writing this review to warn you that The Burger Barr is NOT customer friendly. I wasn't looking for anything but a sorry you had a bad experience. Instead I got - "You were late picking up you food so of course the burgers were burnt. So, don't come back." Wow, don't waste your money for takeout like we did.
Also, never order close to closing time. We did that before in person and the order was not correct either but at least the burgers weren't burnt.

Service is a problem here. It has been a problem for a very long time. Management has failed to introduce a culture which focuses on providing good service. Add to the mix a slow kitchen. Take out routinely flubs the orders. Corporate are you listening ?

This is an awesome post office, first one in the USA! Mail with some Historic Architecture! Cindy was very helpful with some forms and good advice to follow up on! Also, She pointed out the original floor from the build! I never saw such fit and width of the floor boards. That's my Post Office, #1 in the country!

Sooo I don't eat sushi. Like at all. Ever. I've always gone with my friends and just pushed around something tempura on a plate and then had green tea icecream at the end. A coworker recently brought me here saying that he eats here whenever he's in Reno. I was skeptical. I actually find seafood completely unpalettable no matter how many ways I try it. 

We got several rolls including Donald Duck and Micky Mouse and something with eel. I dutifully tried everything and actually was pleasently surprised. The fish wasn't half bad. Some of it was downright good! I ended up enjoying everything except the eel! If the server had been a little friendlier and more attentive, I would have easily given five stars!

My family and I had dinner here on Sunday night and it was such a pleasant experience. We had never been here prior and Torin (our server) made sure we had a wonderful evening. He was friendly, punctual and really on top of our needs. We will definitely be back. 
The food and selection was outstanding!!! Everything was fresh and delicious. 
Thank you Na'Brasa!!!

This was a really delightful meal all around.  Out of the the 18 dishes on the menu, we tried 14 between the 3 of us (a bit ridiculous it may sound but we were celebrating!).  Of those 14, 4 of them I am still dreaming out, even more of those, I would order again and only 1 that was mediocre at best.

Dream-worthy:
Raw divers scallops - it was almost a take on ranch dressing and hamachi but fancier
Pig head - served with this burnt onion tapenade which I could literally put on anything
Liver mousse - great texture and just the right portion
egg custard and cavier - probably my favorite dish; rich but light and the cavier and crisp potatoes added a dynamic texture

Repeat offenders:
Duck leg - loved the authenticity of serving it on a potato roll; takes some balls to do this
corn ravioli - this felt like Mexican street food and i loved how rustic it was
seasonal vegetables - could have eaten piles of it and just thought it was well dressed and fresh

The plating was beautiful and the service was great.  I loved how the sous chefs and line cooks were tasked with dropping plates at the table.  Seeing them float throughout the dining room during a busy service spoke to their style and how they operate in the kitchen.  

The duck breast was our last entree, served alongside the wagyu chuck flap.  The duck was just okay.  The carrots were mush and it was the only plate that went back with food on it.  The chuck was tasty but didn't blow me away.

Overall, I would go back and perhaps try the tasting menu and some of the other courses.  Oh and the dessert was BANGIN.  Loved the apple cake; talk about an ethereal twist on an American classic.  The fois gras was inventive and texture-wise, really different but ultimately it tasted like a PB&J sandwich.  Was that Serpico's intention?  Just might have been...

We took a chance on this restaurant while staying in Horsham.  We are picky about our seafood so our expectation is never very high.  We were wonderfully surprised with the great seafood dishes!  This family owned restaurant is charming with the original brickwork and wood oven. It is small so make reservations if your have several people in your party.  The desserts looked amazing but we were too full from our meals to try them.  This was a great find!

Great service and awesome oysters. Variety of oysters to choose from. The vibe was nice and relaxing. Definitely a place to visit if you're looking for a snack and great drinks. Outside seating is also available. Will be back to visit.

A great meal inside a slice of St. Louis history.

This place is a must see for those nostalgic for St. Louis history. After a conversation with The owner I learned that every framed page and photo adorning the walls and every piece of furniture has a story rooted in the STL.

The menu is has something for everyone and all inspired by the best of St. Louis cuisine. From savory Italian dishes from The Hill to pork steaks from South City. Don't forget gooey butter cake for dessert (of course!)

The vibe is clean, welcoming, and friendly to say the least. During my visit, the service was attentive and timely, and the food quality was top tier.

Make sure to allow enough time to appreciate the photos, maps, and collectibles on the walls and in the display case. Brian's collection is simply amazing.

One of the worst hospitality experiences ever!!! Saturday night watching one of the biggest college football games of the year...game being showed on the projector...owner all of a sudden turns it off in the 4th quarter without even acknowledging us or the fact that we were watching the game!!!!! FYI....we were sitting 3 ft away from the projector when he shut it off and he never once said a word.  He then went into hiding afterwards so he couldn't be confronted, without an apology, EVER!  One of the rudest acts I have ever experienced in any restaurant....don't go here to watching a sporting event, especially on the big screen, on the main drag of Indian Shores Beach!!!!

Italian done right. The meat and cheese board was a perfect selection for all who like fine Italian cured specialties. Lamb ragout and branzino fish were on point. Great place and very attentive service.

Came for restaurant week during lunch. Service was amazing and server was super friendly and helpful. The ambience is great and the decor is top notch. Food was good and very filling but a little salty, service made it a great experience though.

Sub par food and over priced.  I'm sure they tried.  However Indy has so many other amazing local brunch spots!  Within 5 miles of this place you can get cheap eats and delicious creative fresh meals.

Big portions, lacking flavor.  Most of their menu items are more than one person needs for a single meal.  However if your looking for large portions sure to make ypur belly hurt this would be your spot.

I got the chicken & waffles, the waffles were mushy, the chicken was not crispy and the syrup was not fresh (standard bottle syrup).  The gravy however did have some flavor.  The only light at the end of the commercial sub par coma was they had tinker cold brew!  Their service was pretty good as well.  For my chicken and waffles and coffee my bill was $20.  If you want good chicken and waffles check out Open Society or Maxines, way better flavor for your investment.
pretty solid zoo...I think its actually perfect in size as some zoos seem to get overwhelming.

Top notch choice for brunch! The food selection is perfection along with the drinks. Can't wait until warmer weather hits because their front and back patio ma (on the water) look pretty amazing! I hear they have live music which will really make their #brunchgame that much stronger! I'll definitely be back to try more dishes from Flatwater!

My hand is now bleeding.

The click&ship/return service was good as well as the checkout helper.

However, the storage area service was not. The worker was on her phone while assisting me and made me feel like a number.

I originally tried ordering a couch online and specifically filtered out the available couches at this particular store. Upon checking out, I was notified the item was out of stock so I opted for another couch. The dimensions when loading the sofa upon pick-up was 1-2 inches too wide to fit into the trunk so I had to go into the store and find one that was available. I marked three couches but two of the packaging boxes were too big and another was out of stock. I looked up a couch that was THE EXACT one that I originally added onto my online cart which was marked as out of stock online. Finally, I'm waiting for the couch.

If you want to order from IKEA, order online. And if something says it's out of stock, just order elsewhere unless you enjoy the torture. Ended up ripping my skin while loading the couch.

The only reason I gave this place 4 stars is because inside it smelled like dirty dish water. 
HOWEVER, the waitstaff was great and actually not overly attentive, which I prefer.
We had plain lo mein, salt and pepper tofu and salt and pepper calamari. The food is so fresh and delicious. The tofu is not a firm tofu that I am used to but it is wonderful. 
Also, we brought our own 6 pack of beer and I asked if we could drink it there and they had no problem with it. So BYOB!

So I spent about five minutes in this establishment, and those five minutes were more than enough for make me decide I am never, ever returning. 

A friend recommended this place for drinks after a movie, so we headed over. There were, as usual in Philly, people smoking right outside the entrance, so you have to fight your way through potential lung cancer just to get inside. 

We made our way upstairs to the small bar, where we found music blasting from two speakers and only a handful of guests on a Friday night. Since there weren't that many people, but the music was ridiculously loud, I politely asked the bartender, who was clearing a table, if he might turn the music down a little. His response was a curt and very rude "no." Not an explanation as to why, no "I'm sorry, we can't," just "no" said in a "you're a customer here? You put up with what we want" tone. There's also no printed list of drinks - they're all above the bar, but difficult to see because there are "decorations" hanging from the ceiling and blocking the view of half of them. With the music blaring in my ears, my imperfect eyesight, and these impediments to seeing anything, deciding what to order is pretty much an ordeal. Then, as we're choosing our drinks, we realize the place is cash only, which these days suggests either laziness or trying to avoid paying taxes, so that's also a charming addition. 

We walked right out of there and headed over to Local 44, where we had a fantastic time.

Rue Bourbon is a place everyone should experience, for some perhaps only once, for others such as myself I've been here many times. I can't say it a place I visit because I'm captivated by it's charm, but there is something nostalgic about this street in the heart of New Orleans' oldest neighborhood. The naming of this most famous street contrary to popular assumptions has absolutely nothing to do with alcohol although one would believe that not to be the case after walking the 13 blocks or so on any given evening. The French named this street after the House of Bourbon a dynasty that ruled France in the 16th Century and remained in power in some capacity for more than three hundred years.

Largely quiet during the day, Bourbon Street comes alive at night, local open container laws allow drinking alcoholic beverages on the Quarter's streets so it's common place to see inebriated individuals stumbling down the street performing sometimes entertaining but mostly annoying random acts of stupidity. One of the changes I've notice on my recent visit is the absences of the loud blaring music that once migrated out of the many clubs, now it's not complete gone by any mean but not to the extent of the past, the city's noise ordinances have really curtailed the open area festive atmosphere this city has always been known for. So if you've haven't experience Bourbon Street pre-Katrina you'll never know the the New Orleans of old, but you'll still find Bourbon Street intriguing with it's unique and alluring architecture, exhibitions of its culture through its music and food, appallingly and shocking with its deviant and sometimes disgusting displays of blasphemies and irreverent behavior. So be prepared to experience the unexpected and reserve your judgement, you're only visiting, unlike some you can leave, shower off the filth and escape the obscenity. Definitely lagniappe from the founders.

I've been going to Super Rica since I was a little girl. Definitely a locals favorite. The line down the block is 100% WORTH THE WAIT.  Order a few things off the menu, get guacamole, a side of rice, queso de cazuela, and some tacos and you'll be happy. Sometimes the tamales are on special, make sure you get one of those. 
This is not Sonora, Jalisco or Oxhacan style, No massive burritos, no crispy taco shells or yellow cheese, no baskets of chips and no huge plates of rice and beans. This is Mexico City style food, as good as you can get it.

Ginza has won me over as my favorite Japanese restaurant in Tucson.
It is on the pricey side..but all sushi is pricey here.

I ordered the combination dinner plate which comes with rice, miso soup, salad and your choice of three main courses.  I had the sashimi (variety of very fresh hamachi, salmon, and some other fish i didn't know), tempura, and teriyaki chicken.  The meal size was perfect for me so I was vey happy with the variety..but it might not be enough..for well, a male appetite.  We also ordered the spicy ahi handrolls, which btw is made of AHI CHUNKS..woohoo! I found it!  (I've been on a quest for this in Tucson for some time now).

Food was fresh and very flavorful and the service was attentive. I ordered the Saag Paneer, which I enjoyed and my wife also liked her lentil dish. We also had the Gobhi Cauliflower and the Vegie Somosa appetizers. Good food. i will definetly be back.

Perfect every single time! 
Ordered by phone for pickup. Big and custom order, everything came out just right, hot and super tasty. Quality ingredients. Great costume service and really nice staff. Highly recommend!!
The name says it all! Chan can wok, deadly good food every time. Fresh, quick, friendly, and delicious. What more can you ask for from a 50 sqft restaurant.

I've got to admit that the ambience of this place is superb.  It's not uppity like some Stephen Starr restaurants; it's actually got a chill vibe to it.  The crowd is young professionals, hipsters...anyone that just enjoys drinking.  There were no loud obnoxious..jersey shore wannabes there.  If you are looking for a new group hangout..come here, especially on a night where it's 75 degrees out.  My friends and I didn't bother grabbing food since we already ate at PYT, but there's really not that much selection at this place re: food.  In regards to beer, there's a good selection, but it's not as vast as I thought it would be.  I will be coming back again

BEWARE!!  I am writing about the misleading "methods" of ordering takeout. I pulled up Yelp today to look at reviews. I decided to order and selected to order from Yelp app. No where did it say there were other options of ordering, nor was there a disclaimer anywhere that I could see telling me that I would pay $4+ more per item. I picked up my food and when I asked about the difference in pricing from what I paid and what they had up on their menu I was told that I was charged more through Grubhub. I not only paid $16 more than I should have but picked up my own food. This seems like highway robbery. If I was a restaurant owner I would be mortified to know about this. Lesson learned. BUYER BE AWARE!!

Seems to me that I am not the only previous patient that has seen a decline in performance, customer service and comportment by Stacey Hudson and his terrible staff.

i would recommend you go to other ENT's in town if you plan on being a prolonged patient.

Stacey was a wonderful doctor at first, but overtime, with his staff's major attitude and little worry for patients with emergencies, Dr. Hudson has blindly vouched for a staff that has no respect or manners. Just people regurgitating what they have been told to keep a concerned patient away.

also, as a sidenote, if you don't have the right insurance, they will not treat you with respect or care.

Be careful!

Hope he changes his new ways and his staff.

I love Carpinteria! 22 miles from where I live in Ventura but I will make any excuse to come up to Carp. And when I do, Lucky Llama is my first stop. Very friendly staff and great coffee. I like that they always have organic and especially Ethiopian coffee (which is where the very first coffee beans came from, sorry, I am an archaeologist). The outdoor area is the very best coffee environment I can think of in my travels all over the world. Carpinteria is such a cool town because they still have non-corporate books, bikes, toys and even coffee. Luck Llama epitomizes what Carp is all about.

(3.5) ~ good food, but just (3) ok Service...not sure why but lately it's taken awhile to place our order, and then it takes a long time for the food to come out?

* Try the "Pho", which is basically a BIG bowl of Vietnamese noodles in a flavorful broth with your choice of various meats.  You can get extra meat too for carnivores like me;-)  I've also had the Red Curry Duck which is (4) very good;)

It's a dreadful thing to contemplate how you'll afford trips from DC to Tampa every two weeks, but a wondrous circumstance that prompts the thought: an impeccable full set and pedicure from an oh-so-lovely human! 

Shape, length, thickness, and design of my full set with gel polish couldn't be more perfect, nor could my gel pedi. Made me appointment via Freshy and went in an hour later, was immediately greeted by friendly front desk staff and then my delightful tech. Patient, meticulous, and kind -- what more can you ask for?

Here I am writing this review from the bathroom, in absolute agony. We went to Julio's for lunch and the food was ok; it definitely wasn't the best Mexican I've ever had. About 20 minutes after we ate and left the restaurant, my stomach began to twist and I knew I needed to find a bathroom immediately. I LOVE Mexican food and I've eaten it countless times, so the fact that my stomach reacted so poorly to the food here makes me hesitant to eat it again. I'm not sure what did it -- the queso dip, the tacos or the patiacitas. I gave it 1 stars because the food wasn't bad, but not the other 4 because it's put me in the bathroom for nearly an hour.

Great event; hope it becomes an annual one!  Parking should be free (versus $5) but I rebelled against it by parking further and walking 1mi each way via Rillito River path.  I noticed many rode/parked their bikes, which I would have loved to do if I lived closer or had a non-roadie bike.  Though there was a huge turnout of vendors, I hope that the next event includes more local restaurants.  I enjoyed my favorite Prep & Pastry doughsant and the sample-size offerings from Loews Ventana (refreshing non-alcoholic sangria, house made chips and goat cheese grilled sandwich).  Since I arrived late afternoon, some tents (like Cashew Cow and Boca Tacos) were already sold out.  I also wish I came with a friend to share samples as I was fairly full to try intriguing food from Contigo and Proper.

This place f**king sucks.  
I am extremely upset and angry at the service I received yesterday (Tuesday-5/18).  I paid $25 for a Blue Coral Clear Coat Works Wash that includes a vacuum, interior wipe down, tire shine and all.  NOTHING was done.  There was zero vacuuming, nothing was wiped down inside, and certainly nothing on the tires.  The car hardly looked washed.  I went home and had to clean things myself.  $25 for NOTHING!  Absolutely nothing! On top of that, my bank was charged TWICE.  I have a charge for $25 and a charge for $25.01. Criminals!

The food is ALWAYS slammin when I come here! Food is always hot and fresh and the energy is great. Can't go wrong with anything on the menu, to be honest! Don't forget to get some sweet sauce to dip your jerk chicken or beef patty in. That sauce is so good I almost at the little plastic cup that it came in. Much needed restaurant on this side of town!!
Still very rude and unprofessional called again to get car transported and just the way they talk to customers is appalling. I thought I would try them again. sorry I wasted my time

The food is really tasty, and priced pretty well too. Throw in the ambience and for the most part you've got a great restaurant! Only negative was the service. Food took nearly an hour to come out, and half our tables food came 5 minutes after the first half so some people were already done eating by the time I got mine. Besides that, it's a great place. I got the breakfast burrito and it was really good, had a ton of flavor!

20 for a crappy manicure. She filed my nails and every single nail is crooked. Now I have to go home and fixed. So disappointed. 
Son needs to focus on her clients instead of everything around her.

I recently injured my foot and have been on crutches, so I have only one working leg and NO functional hands!  I also have diabetes, so I need to eat "healthy" and watch my carbs.
I was introduced to BFF Café the week before I tripped, so I quickly realized that they would be my savior!!  I have been coming for lunch once weekly, then taking home a few Fuel Packs for easy, healthy dinners, which my busy, non-cooking husband REALLY appreciates.  Hubby and I finally came for dinner today, and he said the food actually tasted VERY GOOD, which not all "healthy" meals do.  We left with 6 varied Fuel Packs, with sauces that even my hubby likes!  (You can choose any of the sauces to go with a Fuel Pack).
So far, our favorites are the Salmon Zoodles and the Clucker. My husband really liked the Prawn Zoodles at the cafe tonight, but they do not come in a Fuel Pack.
He would have tried a burger, but was dubious about the falafel or cauliflower "breads"...next time!
I was confused ordering on-line, so I phoned and the gentleman on the phone was very gracious and took my order by phone.  He also pointed out that I need to scroll down to get to the "nothing extra" line, since I didn't want to add anything extra. Will try on-line next time!
It is a very good idea to order your to-go items ahead, as it can take quite a while to get your order together.  (When I ordered salmon Fuel packs at lunchtime, they were still prepping and cooking the salmon, so it took 1+ hours before they were ready, but the salmon was fabulous, perfectly grilled, the next night!)
Also, a few of the Fuel Packs are different from the sit-down menu, so the nutrition data are different.  (The Fuel pack nutrition data are on-line). Finally, a friend and I did note that some of the carb values seemed low.  It would be great if the nutrition values also included fiber.
Oh, and one last question...will you be opening a café in south Reno?
Thank you very much! My hubby and my blood sugars thank you also!

Delicious, pleasant atmosphere and reasonably priced. My family tried this out for dinner in the hopes of finding great Indian food.  We do not know Indian food very well so this was a bit experimental for us.

My husband had Chicken Vindaloo and asked it to be seasoned hot rather than mild or medium. It was flavorful and spicy. He doesn't like rice but loved it with the butter Naan.  I tried a bite. While it was a little too warm for me, it also had a fantastic smokiness to it.

My teenage daughter had the Chicken Biryani. She prefers something mild and enjoys seasoned rice. She got both. I tried it and liked it too, especially the seasoned chicken. 

I had the Chicken Tikka Masala seasoned mild. It was loaded with exotic flavor yet not too rich or overwhelming. I can't wait to have it again. 

Service is a bit slower than what I might expect at a the neighboring restaurants, but still good.  I look forward to coming back!

There was only 1 guy when my mother and I walked in. It took 15 min for our sand which to realize that the only other customer got his too at the same time. The two teen girl workers were in regular clothes. The one was texting the whole time. The other one was helpful but had her shirt risen to see her belly button ring and had on shorts that barely covered her butt. The food was good.
I live right down the street and let me tell ya, these sandwiches are the best! this guy cures his own meats and they're absolutely delicious.

Everyone should stop into this quilt shop!!!  One of the BEST and most organized stores I've ever been in
Keegan was extremely knowledgable and very helpful!! Such a great staff also Thanks for the awesome experience and making my day!!

Update to previous review: 

Quizzo started a full hour later than expected, but the waitstaff was super nice and kept the snacks and drinks flowing! First round was extremely difficult, with most teams getting less than half of the questions right, which was a lil' crazy. The trivia here is pretty much just movies and music, or it least it was the night I came. Food, again, was bomb: Calamari was crunchy and the sauce was great,  and a good portion size for sharing. The onion rings were also battered perfectly! My team shared the apps and I had the bolognese! Although it wasn't a real bolognese sauce, I enjoyed the flavor a lot, and the noodles were soft, the beef was good quality, and the buttered bread that it came with was perfect for dipping. Food is great, but trivia experience left a lot to be desired.

I called them to get my car towed because I couldn't get it to started. I didn't think it was the battery. I told them to take their time as I wasn't in a hurry. They showed up pretty quickly and instead of just hooking it up and charging me for a tow, they asked me to try and start the car so they could hear it. Well, it was the battery and so they jumped it so I could go get a new one to put in. There's a lot of companies out there that would take their time getting to you or not taking the extra effort to see if they can fix the issue, but they did. I highly recommend this company if you find yourself in a position that you need a towing company.

Had a blast on a packed Friday night. This is one of the few places to get acceptably weird on the dance floor. Eardrum busting hard rock bands throw down crazy tunes and cash only bar sports $5 pbr + whisky shot specials. Make sure you know ahead of time what show is playing because the bouncer asked us before we payed or $5 cover fee and I had no clue but my friend did. The bathrooms are covered head to toe in graffiti and theres only one stall so get your business done early. This place gets packed and people tend to rage out harder than usual. 

It does get insanely loud so prepare to have damaged eardrums for a few days.

Amazing food. Wonderful customer service. Had a great meal.  Food was delicious. Staff is well trained and very friendly and professional. Everything on the menu is excellent.  Loved this place.  Can't wait to come back.  Bravo guys !!! 

Has the burger it was awesome. The butter cake is to die for. Mixed drinks are great and the bartender was well trained and very friendly and engaging.

Years ago I ate at the location on 6th (have no idea if it is still open) and remember it fondly so I really, really wanted to love this place. I tried, but ultimately it was just okay.   

The menu garnered immediate respect when I saw calabacitas was a side option.  I promptly ordered them.  The veggies were still a bit crisp (a good thing), but it lacked that special flare I expected.  The beans were good, but again, lacking something.   The ground beef taco....let's just say I've had better.  The red sauce was okay.  My overall impression was the food tasted like the Mexican food I cook.  Good, but lacking that certain something that a white girl like me can never quite figure out.  Then again, maybe I'm jaded as my husband's Tia Mary can out cook any Mexican restaurant in this town.   Don't get me wrong, the food is not bad.  It just isn't exceptional.  Taste aside, the biggest problem was that my food was lukewarm.  On the other hand my dining partner's soup was so hot it had to cool for about 10 minutes before it could be consumed.  Kudos for the tortillas.  They were excellent.

The building is decorated nicely, and the patio looks like it will be a good place to hang while sipping on a margarita on warm summer evenings.   The wait staff was prompt and friendly.  Our drinks never emptied.  The prices were reasonable and the servings were plentiful.

I have used Arch Engraving several times for our company and for personal projects.  Each time the staff have gone out of their way to make sure I was pleased with what they made for me and were very easy to work with and offered reasonable prices.  I will definitely use them again!

Came here for a holiday party that my work was hosting. The back room where we were seated had plenty of room for all of us and never once felt like we were too crowded. 

The food provided was very tasty and the staff was friendly and very quick to make sure we had things to drink and picked up our plates, empty glasses, etc.

The only thing I didn't particularly care for were the mashed potatoes. They were kind of gummy. 

Also hung out with a couple coworkers in the bar for a drink afterward. The live music that they had sounded amazing and despite the bar being busy, our service was very quick. 

Will definitely come back with my husband.

It is very quiet. Everyone is friendly. Management is great and knows everyone by first name. Only downside is people leave their trash in front of the compactor and it piles up. Other than that. Great place to live.

First of all, I wish i didn't have to give a star at all. I am a long time Marriott business traveler but I have to say this was the most disappointing Marriott property I have ever stayed at. 

My room was dirty, there were dead bugs on the kitchen floor, thick dust over every surface and black mold in the shower and shower curtain. They did switch my room, however, it was like they were bothered by my request. No real apology or "what can we do to make your stay better?

The morning breakfast was terrible. No fresh fruit, watery coffee, powdery eggs served in a dirty dining room.

Absolutely the worst customer service imaginable.
I had a dishwasher that needed replacement and gave specific dimensions to which they recommended a specific model. They came to install it and it didn't fit. They left it there half installed. So the actual dishwasher isn't installed despite 200$+ fee.

It will now require cabinet adjustments to complete installation (which is of course in fairness my issue to resolve) but in attempting to install it, they damaged two cupboard doors and refused to even acknowledge it. I even tried speaking to the owner and he said the door broke on its own, all they did was open it! As well, the removal of the old dishwasher left water everywhere. (Just unprofessional).

If you can avoid Atlas, and based on this experience and a rather rude/rigid owner/staff, you definitely should steer clear.

I priced the same unit at Home Depot, Best Buy and the price was similar so I thought I'd stick with a locally owned business. DISAPPOINTING!

First off, read my first review.  I gave the joint a 4 star based on great pizza, good service and tasty-looking meals around the table; everyone was satisfied.  Everything was so good that I decided to drag my mom in here for her birthday a few weeks ago.  She's not really into fusion foods, or menus that get too experimental, but I figured I'd give it a shot.  I just didn't think I could eat at Outback one more time this year.

We kicked things off with the meatball app, and it was really good.  Almost good and filling enough to be a meal of it's own.  Just serve a side of pasta with that bad boy, and shazam!  I'm just sayin'.  

Next, I got the braised pork and rigatoni special.  For the love of the food gods, if they have this special, order it!  Tender, shredded pork, served with a dark red sauce over fresh pasta and topped with some fancy house-made cheese.  It was amazeballs!  I took a picture.  Look at it.  Look at it long and hard, and remember what I'm saying here.

My mom ordered a pork chop, but claimed it was "a bit" spicy.  I took a bite, and felt like she was losing her damn mind.  Maybe she's just too old and her taste buds aren't functioning as well as they used to, but there was no spice, only deliciousness.  Maybe it's because she's a woman.  Who knows.

Again, the service was great and I have no choice but to upgrade my star power to a 5.  This is one of the few St. Pete restaurants that I stand by 100%.

Loved the Spring rolls, very fresh and very tasty. The Pho servings are huge, a small bowl is 8" in diameter, & 10" for the large. The small was plentiful & you can share a large. The service was great. We will be back.

Nice BYOB.   Very impressed, with the foods quality and flavor.   I used a groupon that expired that night, and like the 50 other tables, we were all trying to get it in.   I think this is why they lost a star.  They were unprepared for the 2000 customers on the last night of the groupon and it beign a Tuesday and all.  

I tried the combo kabob and it was very good.  I was very impressed.  the babaganoosh (sp?) was very good too.  This place would be great for large groups as well.

Professional, and affordable! They were willing to show me what was wrong with my car, and explain why they made the decisions they did while fixing it. We've been going elsewhere for years, but got tired of the "$1000 list" every time we did an oil change (why are you looking st my rear rotors during an oil change?!)
The price I was given at Mitchell's was significantly less than I had been quoted elsewhere. We will be going here from now on!

I enjoyed two separate experiences here when I accompanied my husband on a recent business trip. 
I was thoroughly impressed with the food quality, cocktails and service as well as the live music on the patio (offered seasonally from April to October on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday evenings from 6:00 p.m. - 9:00 p.m.).
The Happy Hour menu offers delicious small plates and cocktails for very reasonable prices. I highly recommend the Rose Cosmo cocktail and the Crispy Brussels Sprouts.
The regular dinner menu offers a wide array of delicious options -- I can vouch for the Antipasto Slate, Crispy Buffalo Cauliflower, Fried Chicken Curlys and the Market Garden Pizza. 
If you're a dessert person, the Carnegie Cheesecake is delicious and is perfect for two -- or maybe even three-- to share.

Great noodles, very tasty. Must return. 
Great service. Tomorrow is their grand opening. I will introduce them to my co-workers.

Sometimes the counter staff is absolutely awesome...other times they are chatting behind the counter and seem to have better things to do than serve you with a smile. I like this pharmacy because it's local and will continue going there for  that reason and the few employees that are friendly. I have had several occasions where a prescription was forgotten (like today for example) as I received the automated text message alerting me that my prescription was ready AFTER closing. Additionally the young girl I think her name was Tiffany or Brittany was rude and short on the phone...no enthusiasm what so ever and she hung up very abruptly without so much as a "thank you have a nice day" my father is a local business owner and he would be horrified if one of his customers was abruptly hung up on or treated poorly. That's what local businesses do and are known for! Exceptional customer service and great personality amongst staff. Step up your game Dons please! I like shopping local don't force me away by continuing this behavior!
After finding this place on Yelp it was a must to try it. So glad I did. Will definitely stop by every time I am in town. They were super fast and the food was amazing.

Very disappointed!  I've been here at least two other times and continue to think the next time will be better.  When will I learn!  Just had appetizers and calamari. If I wanted boxed frozen seafood, I could've went to traded Joe's and bought it myself!  The appetizer was okay but what you pay doesn't reflect the quality you would expect. Save your money and head to lily's for great sushi.

We are so glad we stopped into Mike's for some ice cream and hot chocolate. They had many ice cream options as well as 9 hot chocolates from around the world. The staff was great and very patient with us as we took all the options in and made our decisions. My husband got the banana pudding ice cream which was out of this world and I got Dutch hot chocolate. It was the best hot chocolate I have ever had. It was filled with warm chocolaty goodness and topped with amazing whipped cream. We decided to enjoy our treat there sitting and taking in the music. Mike's treats definitely hit the spot and gave a break from the cold outside.

Food and staff were amazing! Tried the shrimp tacos and rockfish fish and chips. The portions were generous and worth the price. Theres also complimentary sherbert, although I dont know if that's just during dinner hours.

This is a great spot for APPETIZERS! A great date night / girls night / bros night is going there, sitting in the bar area at happy hour, and ordering off of their appetizer/happy hour menu. 

Their full menu is extensive as well, but the chef at this location is less than remarkable so I prefer the appetizers.
Ordered the pasta jambalaya for delivery on eat 24. Food was subpar at best and the portion size was ridiculously small I could've been a side dish and I was charged $20 for it.

I was craving something fruity in Chinatown and something that had fruit right in its name seemed to be the obvious choice. I've been to this place since it was Fruit Life and enjoyed it since. This hit the spot but sometimes the food is only as good as it's service. Unfortunately, the waitresses were not very attentive and I stayed there longer than I anticipated. The dessert was still exceptional but I've come to desire it less after each visit. Overall, come for the specific desire of fruity desserts.

This was the best breakfast experience I have had in the continental US. Yep, that good. The ambience is relaxing and casual but not dive-y. Great staff, led by an awesome and enthusiastic owner. We had the Parisian Za and the Berrylicious Pain Perdu. The portion sizes were perfect. Best of all, both dishes were elevated way beyond even above average breakfast places. The flavors of both dishes were perfectly balanced and everything blended well, yet nothing ever became muddled. I even devoured my little side salad in its entirety, and I am NOT a breakfast salad enthusiast. Also, unlike so many breakfast places, this place makes a stellar latte! I am in town till Wednesday, and I will definitely be back.

I booked my appointment 2 months in advance, I called 3 weeks before and the week before to make sure everything was set. I missed ONE call from all my sons while at work. When I called 2 days later, my appointment was gone becase I " did not confirm it when  called." Who does that? Cancels your slot because you didn't answer?
They had no other time to offer me except for a slot between 4 hours that I had work. Unbelievable. 

When my roommate had her appointment, they said they would come between 12pm-4pm. THey called at 5:30 pm "on their way". She canceled the appointment and they did not ofer help.  She sat around off of work getting no pay for 5 hours for nothing. 

This is NOT what you want when you have a job, a lease ending, a time limit or a life. Try someone reliable

Sotto is a quaint BYOB located under a bridge in the city. I hosted my birthday party here when they first opened and we had the whole place to ourselves, about 25 of us. The staff created a prefix menu based off of a few favorites of mine. The goat cheese and beet salad, grilled octopus, ravioli bolognese, veal saltimbocca, and grilled branzino were all selections. My guests were delighted and I think the plates were scraped clean! The prices were great and everyone was complimenting the value for the quality. Recently, I have gone back several times for lunch. I would recommend this spot for a small group of wine lovers, the staff is knowledgable ask for Julian, even though it's a BYO he will gladly share his experience while enjoying a glass. The only downside is this size of the place but that also can be a plus if you're looking for something cute.
Their daily specials are always good! My all time favorite will always be the toasted egg!
Besitos has one of the best frozen margaritas in town. You'll only need one...trust me! Love the ambience of this place as well and the staff is always super friendly.

Snob alert! 

Toucan Charlie's is a total rip-off. Reno isn't exactly the classiest city or known for it's great food... but at this price, at least try. The best part of the buffet was the soft serve ice cream... that should tell you something. Everything else was either salty or tasteless. The food was not warm.

The reason Toucan Charlie's gets an additional star is service. Our waiters and busboys were attentive and very speedy.

I have taken out one loan with OSF and applied for two others.  The first experience was ok,  my loan performed as agreed and they were paid in full.  The terms were steep but my agency was fairly new so I understood.

The second application was for a fairly small amount (approx 25% of annual revenue) secured with a 60 year old preferred personal lines book of business with top national companies.  After wasting 30-45 days jumping through various hoops they backed out as I had just purchased the agency 5 months prior.  Service and return phone calls were spotty during this underwriting period.  They advised they could do the loan after we had a 12 month track record and could demonstrate the business would stick during an ownership change.  I was disappointed but I understood.  12 months later with carrier reports and corporate tax returns in hand we showed a 13% revenue growth.  Our "loan officer" indicated there shouldn't be any issues,  sent an offer letter and collected our $500 application fee.  We uploaded all of their requested information then did not hear from her for 10 days.  After three follow up calls and multiple emails I received a call that they would only do the loan if they could cross collateralize my other insurance agency(completely different ownership structure),  get a personal guarantee and UCC filing on both businesses,  myself and my wife.  These terms were unacceptable to me.  I requested my $500 fee returned as none of these requirements were mentioned in the terms letter.  OSF declined to return my fee.  They kept my $500.  

OSF is essentially a factoring company that collects commissions from the insurance carrier directly deposited into a joint account that they control,  they take their monthly payment and redirect the excess commission back to the agent.  There are stiff fees, a 10-20 day income lag and built in buffers associated with their loans.  The high fees are somewhat acceptable to me as the money is usually only secured by a book of business.  I feel there is adequate security in place and the extra requirements (filings on other agencies and individuals) should be disclosed up front at the very least.  I would have never paid the $500 fee if those requirements were disclosed up front.  

They have every right to make loans on their terms but they should have returned my $500 fee after such a drastic change from their terms letter.  That would have been the ethical thing to do.

My advice to any agency owner is to take the time and jump through the hoops to get an SBA loan.  OSF is supposed to "understand the insurance industry" but in my experience they are not any easier to deal with than the SBA and their terms are much less attractive.

We had a difficult experience here, but I think we caught them on a bad day. Management responded to our concerns and really seems to care about their customers. On our return visit the servers were very friendly and attentive, and the food was excellent. They use high quality, fresh ingredients, which shows in the finished product. I was impressed!
Accommodations were nice and clean.  Valet service was great and front desk staff pleasant and professional.  Common areas were clean and well kept.

My first time traveling to New Orleans, I wanted to make the best choice possible in hotel. You all know how hard that is especially if you've never seen it and don't have anyone from around the area to ask. I took a big chance in Omni Royal and for the most part, they haven't disappointed. The room was pretty small - I'm not sure if there were any bigger options - but clean. I was grateful that our window didn't face into st. Louis street because this place is so pleasantly close (and just far enough away) from the madness of bourbon street. I'd definitely stay here again. BIG HINT is to become a member of their loyalty service so you can get free wi-fi. And another BIG HINT is to not to be in the position that you need to print things. It's 6.95 to use the Internet and you get charged an extra 0.98 per print job. Not cool.

Service was friendly and hospitable. 
Oysters not so good .chargrilled oysters submerged in way to much butter,cheese overwhelmed the tiny oysters . Not a good oyster and really bad bread . 
My first experience 4 years ago was fantastic . 
I've given a Drago's a chance a chance to redeem itself every year but it's never lived up to my expectations of my first experience .

This is not my first visit but may be my last. As others have noted, the concept is a nice one. Seats are comfy. Food is decent. However, the wait staff went from decent to OK to bad and now to absolutely terrible. It takes far too long for orders to be processed and things as simple as drinks served. Waited 10 minutes to order - a tea and a coke plus two entrees and an appetizer. Drinks served 10 minutes later and the rest came out in another 10 minutes... not too bad. BUT the tea was sweet, which I didn't order. When the food came out I asked for a replacement UNsweet tea. 10 minutes pass... nada. press the light... 15 minutes... flag down a young lady and explain the problem (again) and the long wait. Off she goes. 5 minutes pass... nothing, 3 more... nope, so I get up to take my plates back (she didn't take them) and get my own tea just to meet her at the bottom of the stairs. I take my tea back. Finally settled in and take a post-meal drink.

The better half gets a sweet pang and it's time to order a dessert. Not a bad wait time for the waiter and i order a refill on my $4.79 tea. Both the tea and dessert return in about 10 minutes. Could have been shorter, but there's a movie to watch so no biggie.

Then the check comes and it's wrong as it's for the dessert only. We could have bailed, but the food was decent and I decided to point out the missing $40+ worth of food. I explain the error (in the middle of the movie) and two checks finally arrive from a guy reeking HEAVILY of smoke. On the way out, dishes are piled on the side of the exit. Looks downright nasty and the management here obviously needs to address it and the poor poor service. Such a shame!

I drive for Uber and Lyft, so I put a lot of miles on my tires.  Several times, I've run over something that got stuck in a tire, and these guys always repair it quickly and at no cost.  (Be sure to get the protection plan when you buy the tires, it pays!)

Very good, fast service.
My first visit and I'm completely satisfied! Stephen is great and Tina hooked up my eyebrows! I recommend Classy Nails by Le!

We ate at Anna's many times during the year I lived in Reno. The food is tasty and the drinks are amazing as well! The carnitas dinner is my absolute favorite, but everything I've had here has been delicious. The service is also good and staff are friendly.

Pulled in today to grab a sandwich. Sat in line and watch four cars ahead of us get food. Once we were at the speaker we sat for another five minutes, only to be told thru the speaker they were short staffed and they are only doing on line orders. Mind you I'm not complaining about the wait time as I know everyone is understaffed, but you refuse to take a drive up order? Give me a break.
Love this small little brewery. I always get the fish tacos and chips which are delicious. Service is always exceptional.

Almost called one of the 24 hour companies to fix a problem.  SO glad I called Redington and took their advice to have them come early the next day! They are a family company and you can tell by the nice guys who work there. Saved a ton, professionally fixed now and I didn't have to worry about who was in my house. Wouldn't think of calling anyone else - Thanks so much!
We loved everything about this place even our server Erock! The food and surroundings were amazing! We will be back especially when weather is better to sit outside by the fireplace!

I'm a member of a Facebook group called "Kindness Matters" (please check it out!), where we do random acts of kindness in and around the Reno/Sparks area.  I saw on the page that they were going to do an event where we would create artificial floral arrangements for a couple senior centers in the area.  I was in!

Enter Create A Space for Arts and Crafts!

They asked for donations for the baskets and flowers and I happily gathered some flowers and dropped them off to Tracy the owner, who happens to also own the hair salon next door.  She is as sweet as pie.  Loved her instantly!

My 13 year old daughter came with me the day of the event.  The space is so cool!  All different mediums of art are everywhere.  Many of the pieces are actually great marketing for future events showcasing what the event is about.  For example, there were several bottles of essential oil sprays which talked about them and the date/time they were offering a class on how to make them.

We took a seat at a table by the door.  It was packed with a ton of do gooders and that's all the space they had left :)

We had a short presentation from Kindness Matters ladies then a speech from Tracy the owner and then we were off creating.  It was a lovely afternoon filled with laughter and bonding.

We have since delivered the baskets to the senior centers and they were so grateful.  It made my heart all fuzzy.

Tracy offers a class once a month to give back to the community like this one as well as local artists sharing their knowledge of their special skills.  I highly recommend Create A Space for Arts and Crafts to all!  I'll be back soon!

We stumbled across this spot on a Toys-a-Rus detour in Burlington on a drive up from DC to Northern NJ.  Being used to pretty sub-par Chinese in DC, we had low expectations.  4 stars because we were so surprised that our food was actually good!  I largely echo everything Bay S. said, especially as to the Waitress.  She was great.   I had the sweet and sour chicken with sauce on the side, pork fried rice, wonton soup and a spring roll.  The sweet and sour chicken was the best I've had in ages. Good quality chicken with just enough batter, as opposed to the crappy quality meet I've had in some places, or places that give you a ton of batter but leave you wondering where's the beef, oh I mean chicken!   My bf had the sesame chicken with white rice and hot and sour soup.  Everything was tasty especially his soup.  I almost made him switch.  Anyways, for a little spot in the middle of nowhere it was a great lunch pit stop just off the highway en route to parts north.

Yats for the win. Here's why:

- it's delicious 
- it's affordable
- you can combine 2 dishes onto one plate
- incredible, whipped peanut butter pie
- the decor makes me feel like I'm a young hippie in New Orleans
- you never have to wait long 
- great for a group 

Favorite meal: Spinach & mushroom. I've never eaten tofu in my life, but one time I even ordered that on accident and loved it. Who knew? Every meal comes with yummy buttery bread as well, so you can't go wrong!

Today I ordered on Uber eats  an el jefe burrito for my fiancée with no onions because he is allergic, 2 beef empanadas and my torta as a meal. I received one empanada that was CHICKEN. And he got onions and pico on his burrito. So I called and the employee wanted to argue and say he didn't see it and he didn't know but didn't even want to offer to remake it. I took it all to the store and had my fiancée go in. The employee told him they won't remake it, offer credit or refund even though almost the whole order was incorrect and I didn't receive my full order. I am disgusted better yet he threw a phone towards my boyfriend and was disgustingly rude. We love taco bus and go to the one in 54 and down off Hillsborough with no issues. I am officially turned off from taco bus.

So so. It's sure not Yogurtland, but not bad. I'll give it 3 stars because it's convenient and inexepensive. I'll go back, it's not bad.
The flavors are a bit limited and so are the toppings. My favorite 2 things at Yogurtland is the Red Velvet yogurt and the cheesecake bites. You MUST have cheesecake bites in order to get anything over a 3 in my book.
The roast pork is out of this world. The best I have ever had. Only available on Sundays. A container can make 4-5 sandwiches. Ask for provolone and peppers on the side.

Outstanding experience here!   Heather is an expert.   She took my clogged Dyson and like a magician had it running in under five minutes.  She is quick and efficient and knows what she is doing.   While she fixed my vacuum at the counter she answered a question for another customer and didn't miss a beat.  She was friendly, helpful,and patient.
I hope my Dyson never breaks again but if it does, I'm coming here.  I'm also recommending this business to my friends.
Thank you Heather!

So we found this place by accident, I have to say the service alone would make me give 5 stars, Elise was our server, she was great, so friendly but gave us ideas on what to order and definitely did not disappoint. The food the guacamole was amazing and the selection of tequilas was outstanding too. Definitely a stop you want to make in NOLA.

Our final Saturday stop, for drinks, darts, and dinner. 

I have to tread lightly here... 

The beers on offer are great. I had a bottle of O'Fallon Pumpkin Ale and Schlafly Octoberfest on tap. 

The food is good. More deep fried things, like cheese balls with salsa, and mushrooms with ranch dipping sauce. A good, thick, well-cooked burger (still pink), and tasty cup of chili. 

And I totally get that it's about the atmosphere and food, and not the service. But. One waiter straight up refused to serve us. The two hostesses gave us two different stories about waiting for a table late night. And -- and I'm sorry to have to tell you this -- the soap dispenser in the ladies room remained empty for the three hours I spent there... even after I notified one of the waitresses. 

The bottom line: If the Williamsburg hipsters find out you can smoke in STL WHILE giving attitude, they'll be flocking to Blue Hill!

ERF

The application process was super easy! Deziree is amazing!! Everyone in the office is super nice! These apartments isn't bug free but they have a pest control come in every Tuesday if you requested them to come. A Handful of times I have seen German roaches in my apartment in the beginning of my stay but now I see none because my apartment has been treated and stays consistently clean. The work orders do take a long turnaround time. My  work orders take around a week to a month to be fixed and the AC reviews are true my AC went out at least three times in my first year the apartment should invest in getting new AC units for every apartment I seen a person put a window AC inside their apartment. The maintenance here are super nice, they were talk to you about anything. Once I renewed my lease after a year and  my rent did go up $150 (understandable) plus a $30 valet trash which seems very overpriced! Gated apartment, nice amenities and very friendly and quiet neighborhood a lot of kids and people walking around to get exercise.

The service here is phenomenal. Natalie was amazing very knowledgeable, super friendly, piercing blue eyes that you'll never forget, she knows her beer. Service made me want to come back. Met Jaimie who works there but on injured reserve she was very kind and introduced herself to me even though she wasn't working. On tap beers were so tasty. Couldn't recommend it more. Giving suggestion on flavors of beer . Great prices good company friendly faces will continue to come back and visit.

A recently posted five star Yelp review sold me on lunch this weekend.   I've driven by this location many times and noticed a packed parking lot figuring it's an industrial area bar, however,  I was pleasantly wrong.   

Walking up to the front door, I noticed a large party with kids leaving the building, all with smiles.   Guess that's a good sign of family friendly.   Restaurant is spacious and clean with clean restrooms-another great sign!  Had a fun sports pub vibe with spacious group friendly seating. 

The staff wasn't very attentive to people walking in.  Wasn't sure if I should seat myself or just wait.   Maybe a host or something giving direction would be helpful for first time visitors.   It was a little busy and that's always a great sign so I was patient.  Finally seated, I was happy they have booths with individual TVs!  Love booth seating as I'm not an ass and elbow fan.  However, my booth seat had a tear and my TV was off as others were on.  Maybe it's broken?   Server took our drink order but hmmm.....no menus!   Well, he was gone a while and just took the paper menus from an empty table.  

Service was blah at this point until our actual server came over to greet and take orders.  No idea who the first guy was but Kyle was amazing!  Really sold the experience.   Got the TV working with a game we wanted to see.  Engaged us in friendly conversation about the weekend and what he likes on the menu.   Kyle really took his time creating a positive experience and we'll come back just because of him.

The menu is fancy pants gourmet at beer prices!  I'm impressed.  They have a killer shrimp mac n cheese and a hearty french dip fully loaded.  I'm going for the ceviche and clams next visit.  Great Basin Icky on tap for only $4!  

My new favorite Sparks restaurant!
Love the food. Biscuits and graveyard, this is the only place to get it! Wait times are horrible

Probably the worst hot and sour soup I have ever eaten, and I didn't eat all of it, consistency of half set jello and no flavor, the main course was spicy tofu, it was bland like the waitress who was more interested in talking on her iPhone than talking to customers, not a place I would recommend or go back to

I follow Nurit (the owner) wherever she goes for waxing (since I wont let ANYONE touch my eyebrows besides her), but I have to say - I'm so happy that she opened this place! It's so relaxing and nice.  I got a complimentary glass of white wine and a hot stone massage (which was pure joy!!).

The place is very clean and sterile. For $5 you can buy your own mani-pedi kit, and they keep it there for your future appts. Maybe it's just me, but knowing that no one used the same nail file and scrubber before me makes me feel way more comfortable with the place.

As far as waxing/threading - it is one of the only places I know of in Philadelphia that offers eyebrow/upper lip threading and somehow she manages to do Brazilians with almost no pain.

After much hype and anticipation we finally had a reservation on Friday 20th of May - 20:30pm. As we were really excited to savour both the reputation and recommendations from other fellow fine diners and multiple culinary sites. 

We were greeted and promptly seated upstairs at the newer wing of the restaurant which we felt looked a little dated with the 1980s style decor and from there our malodorous dining experience began. 

Our rather impersonal waitress came to give us our menus and throughout the time we were there was neither engaging nor hospitable. We ordered the appetisers which through forgivable human error had fell to the floor and we had to wait another further twenty minutes before they could cook another for us and then when that came and was finished, our entrees came straight after which were cold; I believe this was probably due to the fact that they had been cooked and were waiting but could not be served until we had finished our appetisers. 

We then proceeded to our preordered desserts which were the bread puddings but then we noticed that the table next to us was about to enjoy a Bananas Foster dessert; as preparation was been made - we then asked one of the male waiters there why this was not presented to us nor on the menu? 

This was his pompous reply, 'At the Brennan's establishments, it is "understood" that Bananas Foster is on offer for all the guests who know this of any Brennan's establishments'. Just a quick note: Banana Fosters was not created by Commanders Palace but actually in Monte Carlo in 1895; it's accurate name is Banana Flambe or French Banana Flambe.

This review will certainly be going to my other fellow diners both back home in Australia and the UK; for not only a disappointing but a rather stale taste left by both the rude and poorly trained waiting staff (with the exception of Samantha whom although was not our waitress - she did come to check on us for water refills and had the loveliest manner), the atrocious dining experience and the mediocre food. 

Sadly, Yelp only allows me to allocate one star which I feel is more then over deserving of this establishment.

I now live in Los Angeles and I visit New Orleans frequently for both work and pleasure - and I certainly would not either recommend nor patron this bourgeoisie establishment. 

Kai Tan

Disgusting.   Did not order the 24 inch blond strand of hair that came with my salad today.  Manager offered a free sandwich. Said your food is disgusting.  Outa here.  You can keep your follicles to yourselves.  Lost a loyal customer     I am so grossed out. Just kept pulling and pulling and pulling.   Where is the end of thishair strand?  Frigging GROSS.   You can close and makeTucson a better place.

A month has passed and I am still grossed out about the long blond hair in my salad.  Do yourself a a favor and go to Senae or Nook for terrific food.

Now a year has passed and I am still grossed out by the memory of the hair that was in my salad.

I have seen this restaurant in the food court at the Willow Grove Mall (PA) and recently decided to give it a try. I have noticed that it usually does not have a line and I was willing to give it a try. Simple order of a burger, fries and draft root beer. For the burger i simply asked for ketchup, pickles, lettuce and extra onions. Given that I was the only person in line, I was surprised when I had to wait as long as i did for my burger. The line cook then had me repeat everything I wanted on my burger. When he finally said the burger was ready, I asked about my fries and draft root beer. He said "what fries" and then looked at the ticket and said that it would be another wait while he cooked the fires....I guess my burger is supposed to get cold while I continue to wait? I am by no means a tough customer and my order was simple.
