I even reached out to management with my complaint as I said I had not posted anything to social media and wanted to directly approach them first. I did not ask for anything and expected nothing other than a simple "apology" which never came. Just be aware that this location is not well run, management could care less about reaching out to customers who contact them directly and there is an obvious reason why they never have a long line in a busy food court. This was unfortunate as I love to support good burger places and with all of the burger competition out there, they are only hurting themselves.

When Horizons closed in 2011 I was devastated. It was not only the best vegan restaurant in the country, it also served the best food I'd ever eaten, plain and simple. But when Vedge opened, with the same chef-owner team, I was ecstatic and eager to see what new things they had in store.

First, Vedge is a beautiful space: it's housed in a gorgeous, old brownstone with much fine original woodwork throughout. The bar up front manages to be both upscale and unpretentious, which is a rare feat. Actually, I think Vedge has a better look and feel than Horizons.

Onto the food: we started off with some cocktails and a "Dirt List" small plate. My "sherry temple" ($10) was an excellent cocktail, mixed to perfection, consisting of absinthe, sherry and a couple other delicious things. The "Irish stew mushroom" ($9) dish we got was overly basic, however---it was a simple potato-carrot stew with a great variety of soft, succulent mushroom (whose name I've forgotten). The ingredients were all great, but the taste was pedestrian.

For the mains, it's suggested that you order two or three dishes per person, as the portions are fairly small. We got the "wood-roasted sweet potato puree" ($8), the "grilled gochujang tofu" ($13), the "fresh hearts of palm with garbanzo crepe" ($13), the "roasted maitake mushroom" ($15) and the "steak spice seared tofu" ($14).

The sweet potato puree was tasty; it was basically a smoky, sweet spread that you put over some pieces of small, toasted white baguette. I liked this dish, but was not blown away by it and I do wish they'd offered whole wheat instead.

The gochujang tofu was superb and the best dish of the night, in my opinion: a piece of grilled tofu with a delectable piece of fried yuba (tofu skin), a smoked miso broth, and a savory edamame puree. This dish reminded me of Horizons at its finest.

The hearts of palm and garbanzo crepe was akin to an Indian dosa filled with lentils and hearts of palm. I liked this dish and I think the green harissa added a big dose of flavor. Kudos to Vedge for this fusion offering.

The roasted maitake mushroom dish was mixed. I loved the maitake mushroom, which has a really great texture (a little soft, a little crunch), and the fried celery fritter also tasted great (and I normally hate celery). I didn't like the truffle mustard, however, which hurt the dish considerably, in my mind.

Lastly, the steak spice seared tofu was basically an English dish consisting of grilled tofu with peas and mushrooms. The beech mushrooms had a strong briny flavor and the dish on a whole was strong of salt; it lacked the subtlety of the gochujang tofu.

For dessert we had the cheesecake ($9) and the turnovers ($8). I liked the cheesecake a lot, as the mix of meyer lemon, crumbly-sugary crust and neutral tofu base created a nice mix of flavors. My friend, however, though it tasted too much of tofu. The turnovers, both of us agreed, were bland: fried pastries filled with sweet potato and served with cream. It sounds great, but the dish had no real sweetness and just didn't come together.

The service was friendly, attentive and knowledgeable.

Vedge is one of the finest vegan dining experiences in the country. I'd argue that it's the best vegan experience in the US, easily beating places like Millennium in San Francisco and Candle 79 in New York City. All that said, it's not quite as good as its predecessor Horizons, which never let me down on any dish, and had dishes with a stronger flavor profile than Vedge. I gave Horizons five stars (one of very few places to get five stars in my book), and I think Vedge falls short of perfection, which is why I can only give it four stars. However, I will be happy to go back to Vedge and it is a wonderful place on a whole.

Hole in the wall with great home cooked meals. I had the shredded beef burro. Wife had chicken fajitas. 2 year old daughter looked at a small hand made taco and ate some sour cream, beans and rice. Good stuff. 

The mild salsa was mild enough for my 2 year old. The hot was enough to burn my lips, but not so hot that I needed to mix the two. Both were flavorful and fresh. 

Beans are outstanding. 

Décor: Clean painted cement block walls with cheesy paintings. Cheep tables, but they did not rock back and forth. 

I saw one fly. Not bad considering.
Love the food, this one is actually one of my favorite places on the westbank, the only bad thing it was not very good  parking

Shout out to Mack for being a total gem and saving my life today. I came in to pick up my pull and didn't even bother checking what was in it. I headed off to dinner, and while checking out my sweet haul, realized that I was missing two key books. I ran back over right as the poor guy was closing up shop and he let me bulldoze him and grab the comics I so impatiently insisted on having right that moment. Just another reason to love this place.

My Husband and I had a wonderful breakfast after church. The food was fresh and HOT! Absolutely delicious. The staff was wonderful and even helped me decide between the Popeye Skillet and the Grande Huevos Rancheros, I enjoyed the Huevos Rancheros. This will be our NEW after church breakfast place.

This is going to be short and sweet. From beginning to the end I loved everything about. The service was impeccable. Our was waiter (Dae) was OUTSTANDING I could not have asked for better service. He was very helpful with our food choices and helping my boyfriend and I find the right drink. After test tasting some of their wines I didn't really like any of them so he went and told "maybe the drink manager" idk that we could not find something and she made something that was absolutely perfect and fit the taste that we were looking for. The food was delicious and it was just a great experience. I would say this is the best restaurant that I have visited with outstanding food, service and atmosphere.

This place was great and the food was amazing! 
My husband had the ribeye it was cooked perfectly and the bone marrow butter was delicious! 
He also loved the tortilla soup!
Definitely tasted like homemade! 
I had the burger and fries and everything was exceptional.

Atmosphere -A+
Service- A+
Food - A+

EH....it's not a mall really anymore. It's a selection of shops and medical building. I sometimes come by here to poke my head in at TJ Maxx, but honestly I don't like this TJ Maxx as much as the one I frequent in Rivergate. Is there really anything else to say about this place? Oh yeah avoid on Saturdays...just like any other mall.
Absolutely fantastic food, the Gyro and Tex Mex are especially good. The smoothies are to die for and left me filled and satisfied. I would give it more stars if I could

Great food, even better service.  My wife and I had a great dinner there before the Smerconish event last night.  Here's what I'm going to say - the entire staff - THE ENTIRE STAFF - was amazing.

1) We were initially seated at a teeny tiny table.  Upon request we were promptly moved to a largely more comfortable table.  Thanks to the front-of-house staff for the move.

2)  Perfect waitress/wait staff.  Great recommendations and conversation, friendly, polite, not overbearing, not absent either.  Perfect level of attention.  

3)  Our favorite part of the evening - Kayla who refilled our water glasses and bussed our plated.  She is a polite and charming young woman that really rounded on the night.  We were sure to ask her name and promised to leave her a great yelp review because she was so charming.  

Kudos to everyone at the Washington House - and Kudos to Kayla!

This place is useless, no one answers the phone even when Fedex customer service tries to call them.  I paid extra for overnight delivery, still no package, no update on when items will arrive.  Customer service is dysfunctional and a waste of time.  Absolutely the worst experience ever this place should be shut down.

I can't say enough good things about this business! I'd been getting my eyebrows threaded at the same salon for almost 10 years back in my home state, and when I moved out here a handful months ago I was hesitant to let anyone new touch them. I took a chance on a & a and I am so incredibly lucky I did. Not only do these woman do an incredible job threading (keep in mind I'm quite picky and these women *exceeded* my standards!!), but they are great people as well :)  it's always a pleasure to see them for my monthly threading and I highly recommend them to anyone looking to get their brows beautified :)!

This is a truly exceptional restaurant. We wound up eating here twice in our weeklong stay in New Orleans because the food was so incredibly good. They also make a really good Sazerac. I'm a really good cook and at this restaurant I felt that I learned new approaches new ingredients and new dishes that I hope to copy once I get home.

A great find! Accidentally found this place while trying to Google Map a different place in Philadelphia. We decided to give this a try also.

The environment was really cool. Just a simple place with wooden tables, a great lady (chef? owner?) behind the counter who also was the waitress, and Post Its and cards and notes from satisfied customers on the board. There was a family in there eating as well as us, and a bunch of people came and went ordering take-out while we were there.

My wife & I split 5 things. I shall tell you about them...

Pineapple Banana Smoothie: Awesome. She put jasmine tea in it and it really gave is an awesome flavor, simple, but unlike any smoothie I've ever had.

Vegan Cheesesteak. Very very very good. I was nervous, but a simple Instagram search for Vegan Tree put my mind at ease and I was very happy we got it.

Chow Mein. Good. The spinach noodles were really tasty and had a great texture. Tofu was yellow and looked like egg and was really good. Tempeh would've knocked this outta the park.

Flaxseed cake. Also really good. Not dry at all, not overly sweet. Really good to split at the end at it wasn't expensive ($4.25).

Avocado spring rolls. They were what they were. Tasted fresh, peanut sauce was really good, but they were plain and we'd try something else next time. Not the restaurant's fault. They were literally romaine, avocado and rice paper. 

I recommend this place highly. Very cute, very friendly, and the repeat customers say a lot about them. And it wasn't expensive at all. I'm from NY, where all this stuff would've been $60, but here I think it was $31.

This is a fabulous yoga studio. Katherine is an excellent  teacher. Her instructions  are clear, and she is very personable. During the hot yoga class, she used a wonderful blend of essential  oils to refresh her students. The smart yoga class helped me understand some concepts i didnt learn previously.  The facility  is spacious and clean and best of all smells wonderful.  Bombay's prices are reasonable and you can easily walk, take public transportation  or park to get there. Online scheduling is available so you can easily sort out your life in and outside the studio.

My hubby and I had gone to this place 4 years ago for lunch before heading home from our anniversary trip. We had the walnut chicken and mongolian beef last time. We remembered it being pretty good so we went tonight for dinner but what a difference! We paid $13 per person for the dinner special. The wonton soup was bland and the wonton was not good. The fried rice was the worst I've ever had! Just plain sticky rice with some soy sauce splattered on it without even a carrot! The egg rolls were gross, the fried wonton was overcooked and yucky and the almond chicken almost made me sick! The only dish that was ok out of all of the stuff we tried was the Kung Pao Chicken. We ended up asking the waitress to switch our Almond Chicken for another Kung Pao. But it was only ok. The only redeeming thing was that the service was great! Not recommended at all for the food or the decor! I won't ever go back!

Hells yes this is good food. We love eating here. The only gripe is all the damned tourists. There should be 30 minutes or so at every local restaurant for each meal locals can jump to the front of the line. Anyway delicious Mexican food great price.

Firefly is my favorite place to go for dinner or a drink at night. The food is always different and delicious. Their desserts are to die for. The waiters and bartenders are always courteous and helpful. I can't say enough nice things about firefly Grill.
I buy all my groceries here! Top quality produce, fresh baked bread, the most unique  and natural products at a reasonable price, plus amazing coffee next door! Can't go wrong with the Italian Centre

My family and I decided to stay here for one night. We were celebrating my mom's birthday by going to a Pacers game. Home2 Suites was perfect. Great location just walking distance to the Bankers Life Fieldhouse, the Circle Center Mall, the Soldiers and Sailors Monument, and more. It is right downtown. Check-in and check-out were smooth and simple. They have valet-parking only ($30 a night), but that is common. Service was professional. The room was spacious. Had a kitchen area with sink, fridge, and microwave. Beds were comfy. Clean. I liked how there was fresh coffee in the lobby (makes things cozy). And my mom enjoyed the free breakfast which had pretty good options. My brother liked that they had a small pool and workout facility. My dad liked the free wifi. For me, the number one thing was the location and price. If you are gonna splurge a little for one night, especially to go to a Pacers game, then this is a solid hotel to do that.

I'm writing about the two play place they have in the mall.  Didn't find much info about this.  My 3 and 6 years old enjoyed it. 
Mall has two buildings: the court ( smaller) and the Plaza.  
One playplace located at the court 1/F by the food court area.  
Another one at the Plaza mid level next to H &M.   
Two buildings are connected via Macy's.  Outdoor crossway at 2nd level and indoor on 3 rd level.  
Free nice soft play areas.  The one at Plaza building seems to be a Bit bigger and more to play.

More like an "inconvenient store" ... the last few times we have gone it's taken so long to get gas.  One time none of their pumps were working but they hadn't put up the yellow tape so I went to each one in disappointment.  Then today the people in front of us are waiting 10-15 minutes to pump their gas... what on earth?!  It's the closest gas station to our house but bc of the disorder I'll just go to QT instead

There have always been reasons for avoiding the Chipotle chain. It was subsidized by the McDonald's corporation, the mass e coli outbreaks that severely and rightly damaged their reputation and, now, these ridiculously priced "lifestyle bowls." Like many others, I stopped going there for a while. When I heard about the their new "Lifestyle Bowls," I decided to give it another go. Your regular bowl there costs roughly 6.50 to 7.50 bucks. These new menu items' prices are hidden to start with, (not listed on menu) and they are outrageously priced for what you get which you don't find out until they ring you up.  Example is the "Keto Bowl":  a small to moderate amount of lettuce, carnitas meat, red salsa, cheese (if you want it but no thanks from this paleo person) and a dollop of guacamole. Almost 11 bucks. Screw you, Chipotle.
Go there often for quick meal. All freshly made. Sushi is also pretty good. Great eats fast and budget friendly.

Waited over thirty minutes and waitresses just ignored. Horrible service on a moderate busy day. Pay attention ruin to your job
And your customers. Many other better options; ignore if you value service

THE WORST!!!!! They rushed us through our mani-pedi's and my mother's and my nail polish both bubbled AND they left nail polish around my nails and my mom's nails. A second grader would have done a better job painting my nails! Not to mention the owner was rude and "didn't see the bubbles" on my nail polish. I'm never going again.

Today was my first time at this little breakfast place in Arnold - pretty good food, good service...I'm sure I'll take the kids and my husband there to get their opinion.  Tables were clean, our waitress was patient with us as we sat and chatted for a while after breakfast was done, and the coffee cups didn't get below the halfway point (big thing for a coffee drinker like me!)  I had the bananas foster pancake and enjoyed it- just one because it was the size of the plate!  One of my friends had the pumpkin spice pancakes (good) and my other friend had a breakfast quesadilla (okay).  The only thing I wasn't too pleased with was the cold smoked sausage that was ordered with my breakfast.  Next time I'll skip the side ($2.00 for a half order was a little steep).

The people were so friendly. The area inside and outside where the seating for eating there was clean. The food was fresh, hot, and delicious. Being from out of town and my brother being new to the location it was a great find. My brother got the All Meats and I got the All Veggies so we both got try a little bit of everything. Definitely recommend this place!

This was one of the best hotels I've ever experienced! With the homey feeling of the lobby and friendly, accommodating staff made me love it more. Being so close to State St. and the beach , this hotel's complimentary beach cruisers are perfect for a nice sunny day in SB. 

This was my 3rd time staying here and I am excited for the next one!!! :)

The front desk is so helpful for any recommendations or ideas of things to do in SB! 

Make this your first choice if staying in Santa Barbara!!!

Played "The Higlands" at Forest Park for the second time in 2 weeks last night.  

It really is a great little course with a reasonable price.

Great driving/chipping range and putting green with a stunning "unlimited range balls for $44.95 per month" deal.  Are you kidding me?  I'm not a golf fanatic (anymore) but that seems like one helluva great deal.  Otherwise it is like $12 for a large bucket (140 balls); $8 for medium (70) or $4 for small (35).  For the monthly deal you must sign up for a minimum of 3 months, but so what!

The course itself is nicely maintained with very little water and generous rough.  As previously commented, there are fairways that are bounded by public streets so if you slice or hook you may do some damage...makes it even more fun.  I have not played the back 9 yet, but the front 9 is fairly short, even from the yellow tees.  Only one Par 5 on the front.  Average hole length on the Par 4s seemed to be around 350 from the yellow.

Clubhouse does have a really nice selection of clothing and logo-wear, balls, gloves, etc.

My favorite thing about the course is that you can pretty much guarantee yourself a walk-on tee time on a weekday.  During working hours you can go out as a single and never be bothered.  After 4 p.m. it tends to get a little busy, but still no problem walking right on with a threesome and no delays.

Cart + 9 holes = $25.50 weekday.  I'm a fan!

Easy access from Kingshighway or Hampton.

Their Midnight Cuban sandwich and frozen pineapple drink were great. the delivery time was pretty good but the food was cold. Could have been the smoothie cooling the sandwich though, not sure. in any even I was pleased!

I arranged for this mover to move a tenant from a 1BR apartment to a shared house located one block away. As the facts were related to me by the tenant, it was a highly unsatisfactory experience. The movers showed up 2 hours late. Although the move involved transporting a 1 bedroom-apartment's worth of furniture to a location just 4 blocks away, the move took 13 hours. The movers were rude and unprofessional, at one point screaming at the tenant. The personal belongings were not handled with care, and some went missing, though sometime after the police were called they reappeared under a tree nearby. When some large furniture could not fit into the new location, it was kept on the truck overnight. After failing to show up twice to drop off the furniture as promised, it was finally delivered a week later.

We made the Uber voyage from south philly to the Yachtsman on Saturday night for my birthday.  My experience was ok.  I ordered the piña colada for two.  I shared it with my brother's girlfriend.  It came in a pineapple which was super authentic.  It was not strong and quite the contrary to what I read on yelp.  

The bar had a chill vibe.  It looks like a tiki hut right out of a beach in Hawaii.  The service was slow and the place itself was very small with limited seating.  They have no outside area at this time.  

I will probably not return unless I am in the area.
I will make this quick. If you need an electrician, call Paul first. Fast,friendly, reliable and reasonably priced. Will use in the future without a doubt.
Love getting coffees and tea at Satsuma cafe when in the Bywater. They have every variety of milk - which was helpful for our group.

Great open air back porch and bar area. The brisket was really bad. Fortunately my friends shared their meals - the catfish is good (a little bland) but the chicken fried chicken was the best. Overall it's fine, they take reservations and seem to have plenty of space for large groups. Just don't get the brisket.

The wrapped potatoes - loaded - to die for!  Best salmon i ever had.  Ribs are moist and fabulous.  Service is great too!  I highly recommend taking a date here - one having the salmon and one having the ribs - both with the loaded potatoes.  Then switching 1/2 way through - thats what my husband and i do.  Highly recommend this place!
Great service, great food. Avoid "after school" times! The kids there have to be corralled by the staff to keep them in line.
They were honest and prompt.  Came to fix a problem under warranty on a weekend.  Could not have more pleased.  I would say that their technician (Mark)  was excellent.

Made a return visit for all you can eat crab legs last Thurs. It was a total disappointment. I had to ask for orders & then wait a very long time for pathetic snow crab legs to be served. It wasn't until I complained that I got a somewhat decent order to come from the kitchen. What a waste of time & money. No way was this meal worth what I spent.

Great cheesesteaks in the area, BUT the service is poor Takeout attitude makes me feel as if I am inconveniencing them. Packaging soup is a major challenge for them since the container explodes in a paper bag in my car. If the food weren't so good, I would never go back. I get takeout since the hard fastfood seating is awful. Go for the steaks and take them home!

Food was excellent! First time I've ever been there, but also the last. The manager was really rude and called the group I was with ignorant and a bunch of bums. When we called back to complain, the manager who made these comments was very rude on the phone and when we asked to speak to the manager she said was the manager, then when asking to speak to the district manager, she hung up on us. Will be choosing somewhere else for lunch from now on. To many other businesses in the area that treats everyone fairly.

Rude service, and trashy accommodations. It's as if meth opened a hotel. It wasnt the actual garbage in our room upon check in, it wasnt even the pubic hairs in the shower. Nope, it was the blatent uncaring attitude of the staff. There are lots of other places to stay in Reno, next time we'll seek them out.

Veggies were perfect al dente & chicken was perfect, not greasy or oversauced. The food server smiled and gave me a great serving of vegetables and chicken, she did not skimp on my mixed vegetable side as was done by a past server, so I was very happy and so was my stomach.

They really stink.  The appraiser was highly untrained.  I am not an appraiser, but have worked around the business for 14 years (family).  I understand the process and how it is supposed to be data-driven.  I provided comps, the appraiser chose the ones he wanted and ignored the once he didn't with no rhyme or reason.  I provided specific sales comparison data to properly correct for subdivision and location and it was ignored.  They wouldn't listen to reason even though I had data and they had nothing.  They price the value of our land at a 40-fold differential between the cost and sales comparsion approach - i provided a differential lot size comparable to help them correct this and they ignored it.

There were many other mistakes made that were frankly embarassing and if it were a commercial appraisal it would have been one of the easiest ever to file a suit against the company for bad practices - however this is the dark and gloomy world of residential appraisals where everything defies logic and there is no due process.

Overall i got the sense that they basically wanted the refinance to fail - after all it probably means more business for them when i try to refinance again next week...

My bad review goes entirely to the girl that was bartending. Two friends and I walked into what appeared to be a relaxed small bar. The music was unnecessarily loud for early evening small crowd. We politely order beers. She was pushy and uninterested in serving us as she drank and socialized with her regulars. In a bar with about 8 people in it we waited about ten minutes to get her attention for your bill where she then asked who we were. ( us and one table were the only people in the bar she wasn't on a first name basis with) then ignores us a little while longer as I tell her I have cash to pay and runs my card a anyway. Good bartenders make our break or break those places, so if you want to treat newcomers poorly no wonder that place was dead and every other bar around was standing room only.

The beer at the Ram is pretty good. But the food is pretty mediocre. There are a couple servers at this location that are top notch, and we're usually lucky enough to get them since we pretty much always come in  on the same nights. 
We have had a different server a couple times, and she was a bit slow and forgetful. 

It's good for burgers, appetizers. The Alaskan BLTA is alright. The beer is pretty good. Tuesday night pint night is pretty tight ;) 

It's usually freezing cold in here, too.
to cheap to put silverware don't appreciate costumers

I wish I could give this place five stars, because this chicken is one of the best I've had this side of the state line. But the sides are so bland and lame that I could just stamp this review with a three star rating. However, because of the very efficient service and the hostess attentiveness, it is worthy of a three and a half star rating, or four stars. 

Like I said, the fried chicken is really good. It is very well cooked, moist and tasty, and with lots of crunchy skin. It has a very light taste and it's covered in a very light batter. Also, for fried chicken, I think it's the least greasy I've had in a while. The sides, on the other hand, are not so great. I had ordered green beans and they tasted like if they were fresh out of a can. The side of corn bread was just flavorless, and another side of red beans and rice was just bleh.

I am not the only one who feels this way though. My companion also felt that the chicken was excellent, but the sides were terrible, fried green tomatoes in this case. We also overheard the table next to us with a similar complain regarding the fried green tomatoes. 

With that said, I must mention the x factor in this equation: the bottom dollar. Yes, this place is not expensive at all for the amount of food served. In my case, I got a side of french fries (yes, those were good), half a chicken, three more sides and a drink, and it was under $15. That is crazy cheap.

And that is why this place is packed with retired couples, seniors with their families and elderly people. This is not the hang out after going to church, this is just the place people on a tight budget come to eat. PoFolks is not the trendy place, it may as well pass for a retirement home.

But none of that bothers me. It all actually gives me a sense of fulfillment. I enjoyed sitting with seniors, seeing them savor their meals and eat with no hurry, or worry. PoFolks certainly gives this community a chance to have a decent meal at a decent place, and for that I give them a tip of my hat. Good job. In other words, if lame sides are what keeps all these people able to sit, with me, in a dinning room, so be it. I'll stick to the chicken only. And the french fries.

So there you have it. If you are ready for an existentialist crisis, or are not afraid of old age, and if you can stick to fried chicken with a double side of french fries, PoFolks may just be okay with you. It is great chicken, and they are alright people too.

The first time I went here, the experience was great. They did a great job on pedicure & my eyebrows were even BETTER! My second visit was today and it was HORRIBLE. The woman n charge of front desk needs customer service training or to be fired. She is so rude. I asked a simple question about what comes with the pedicures, we clearly weren't understanding each other so I said it was okay forget I asked. But for SOME odd reason she just keep going trying to argue with me. IT WAS GHETTO! and as a black woman, I FUCKING hate when ppl put me ina place to be rude or aggressive because than im the aggressive mean black lady. I understand nail salon clerks get rude customers all the time so if they give you the energy match it. But EYE work in customer service myself so i would NEVER talk to somebody crazy unprovoked. She's the front desk lady that wears glasses and speaks fluent english. I would NEVER go there again. And if you are black, search for a black nail tech instead. We need to support our ppl instead of these Asian ppl putting up all these businesses in out communities and treating us like second class citizens. My money is the same color as everybody else.

This used to be my favorite brunch spot.  Went there this morning. Many item were lacked flavor.  The dessert and many breads were from Panera.  It seemed like a run of the mill hotel buffet.  It is a shame to see it degrade.  Last straw was that I got sick.  Bristol's you can d

Manager did reach out and promised to improve. I will try again in the future. Thanks Mitch

Perfect bar for drinks over the summer. It's a cute set up with lots of seating areas and, of course, giant jenga. This bar is popular, so it becomes crowded on the weekend and you might have to wait for a place to sit. 

In order to get in you need to register for a card, but no biggie if you forget because you can always get a new one

Dog friendly

The location is good with nice outside seating. The food is average at best, but  unauthentic. The real problem was the price, in my opinion it is outrageous. I have been a few times hoping that it would get better to justify the price. The staff is hit or miss. I have had a very bad interaction, mediocre interaction, as well as excellent service. There is so much potential for the location and I truly hope they turn this place into something special.

Came for dinner with a very large and loud party of 15 and Samantha took care of us without any issues, she was on point, gave us the best service and treated us like Family. I can't wait till our next trip to New Orleans to come back to Copelands!

So I started this service back in Sept. The technician I initally spoke with was really nice however when I called as instructed to confirm an appt the person I spoke with was extremely rude. 

A month  later in October I called in because I had requested that the same person service my yard monthly to which they agreed but the people services the yard was not the one I had requested. Also the service had not even been rendered but I had a bill which is what prompted the call, I was advised by the manager that the te h I requested no longer worked for the company but the the following month they would have the new service tech knock on my door and introduce himself prior to service. Not only did that not happen but the tech never showed up the day they said he would however I received an invoice on the door the next morning. 

Again I call in and spoke with Trinity (such poor taste in names for someone of this person caliber and lack of professionalism). She was rude and uninformed, she offered no explanation as to how I received a bill the day after my service date before 7 am. Was my lawn serviced? I think not. She stated she would send him back out (again that never happened). About a week and a half later I received a voicemail and then we played phone tag for abother week. Finally I get her on the phone yesterday and she was nice enough to apologize for what had happened thus far and that she would look into it and speak with Trinity. I asked for the owner to give me a call back because I was concerned with the appearance of my grass and was advised that because it was after 2 pm and they close at 4 pm the call would more than likely take place the following day. 

Today I get a call from someone who I believe said her name was Marie (The owners wife) and she allegedly was to go over my concerns but she was unable to keep her cool on the line and started yelling because I advised her that she was misinformed about certain information that she presented to me. Seriously, this company is a joke, I can see why the employees are rude because the owner is just as rude. Beware of doing business with these people because they have no problem sending an invoice but are neglectful when it comes to service. I have green grass on the boardwalk but my front yard looks terrible. I sent them pics but of course they are unable explain it. I can: You didn't service my yard! Oh and Trinity was proud enough to tell me that they could service my yard in 5 minutes or less.I am appalled at the behavior displayed in interaction with this company. DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH THESE PEOPLE THEY ARE CONCERNED WITH GETTING YOUR MONEY BUT NOT YOUR GRASS AND DEFINITELY NOT PROFESSIONALISM! Picrures attached are the boardwalk and the second picture gives the appearance of my front yard. How is that possible if you are treating all of my yard?

Me, my girlfriend, and my friend all went here for brunch one sunny spring morning. We all ordered our drinks first, but they forgot my cup of coffee. The first sign of a bad waitress. Then we ordered our food. 1 hour later and we still did not have any of our food. There was an old couple next to us who were seated at the same time as us, who already received their food. Then there was a couple who showed an hour after us and received their cocktails when we still did not have have ours, which we ordered at the beginning of the meal. My girlfriend was so upset she called the bartender right there. WE waited so long for the food that  i went in and questioned about it, and they said my order was the next one up. As I went back to my seat outside, the manager showed up at the table with our friends dish, lox and bagel, which obviously they took 2 minutes to prepare. really sloppy plating. Then she told us that me and my girlfriends dish were not even prepared yet. What really pissed me off is that she was trying to force us to say its OK to cook the chicken in a separate way to make it cook faster. Which means they just completely forgot the order. It was like we weren't even there. Then we asked them why they never got our order, and the manager just said she didn't want to ask the waitress because she was overwhelmed and upset. The Manger didn't even ask her what is going on with our table. By far the worst service I have ever experienced in a restaurant. The waitresses can't remember orders, the manager can't accept responsibility, and the bartender can't remember drinks. After this we just couldn't take it and left, they tried to get us to stay, but the food is just not that worth it.

Night and Day

I wandered aimlessly downtown in search of "something different".  I ordered the Chicken Queso and added onion. It was good and hot, cooked to order. The cashier was pretty nice and offered samples. 

I was impressed so I visited again this week. Unfortunaely the nice cashier was no where to be found. Instead I was "ungreeted" by a distracted woman with blonde hair pulled back into a ponytail. She was very busy arguing on the phone she didn't  have time to take my order so the cooked asked what I would like. 

I ordered the same thing. Just this time it was a container of greasy, unorganized tortillas thrown around.

UPDATE: A friend of mine sent me a news story that they are not going to be closing after all! YAY! 

I used to go here all the time when I lived in St. Charles. I'm so incredibly sad because I read a story that they will be closing its doors after Labor Day. :-( My mom's first job was at this theater after she bugged the manager enough and he finally hired her. LOL (She really wanted to work there.) 

I will remember the fun nights of Rocky Horror Picture Show as well as the organ that would play before the movies back in the day. 

I just wanted the owners to know I am so sad to hear the news and thank you for keeping it going all these years.

I have been going to Lyse at Healing Hands for about 2 years, she gives really relaxing massages, and even though she works out all of the kinks, her massages always feel great, they are never painful (which can happen if you are as tense as I am).  I am always extremely tense around the neck and shoulders and leave feeling great every time.  I also get migraines, and Lyse's massages help more than any other massage therapist I've been to.  She is in high demand (for good reason), so you definitely want to schedule a bit ahead of time.  Having said that, if I'm dealing with a "migraine emergency", even if she is not scheduled for that day, the schedulers will contact her and ask her if she is able to accommodate you on her day off, and oftentimes, she is.   I feel very lucky to have found her when I was looking to leave the local massage "chain" store.  I'd never leave her in a million years!

Bottom line: Awesome food and service.

So my wife and I were going to be on a business trip in Philadelphia on Valentine's Day, which happened to be on a Saturday this year, and we decided to make reservations early.  I checked the menu and made reservations at Opa as soon as I could.  I was disappointed by the fact that only a fraction of the menu was available and instead, they were pushing a more expensive multi-course menu.  That is the end of my complaining.

We found plenty to eat on the more limited menu and since we had a big lunch earlier, we had no interest in the bigger menu.  The food was excellent.  The wine was excellent.  The service was excellent.  I think you understand where I'm going with this.  We didn't have to wait to be seated and our waiter Zach was probably the best server we had on our trip.

As for ambiance, it was a bit louder than I'd like and too dark to read the menu, but don't let this stop you from going because whatever you order will be delicious.  Go, eat, enjoy, and save room for the baklava.  And then when you're finished, go next door to Time for some absinthe. :)

Buyer Beware,  Double charged for labor,  job poorly completed

Timeline and Discoveries for 2009 Nissan Murano Repair.


1.  Saturday 7am AAA Called for tow
2. Taken to recommended Service Center Pit Stop Auto Body before 8am.  Pit Stop is not Open on Saturday,  this was a AAA error by the dispatcher.  ( Already addressed by AAA)
3. Monday morning, called to give better phone contact #.
4.  Call from Ray about 9am
 a. We diagnosed your car needs:
  1.  Alternator
  2.  Alternator Belt
  3.    Reported No Oil on Dipstick,  Oil change
  4.    100,000+ miles needs Cooling System Flush
  5.  100,000+ miles needs Fuel System Flush
 b. Cost $1500.00.   I told him to hold the keys I would pick up the car
5.  1pm Monday went to Pit Stop and spoke with Ray and he told me he had returned my call and had got the labor down price was now $1230.00
I asked about just the alternator and belt and was quoted $825.00, this was within reason,  I was at the desk and expected to sign the estimate,  Ray asked me for half for the parts and when I asked why he told me we were good, the part was in stock and they would have the work done for Tuesday.
6.  Tuesday 4:30 pm, I called to see if I could pick up my car, after a few minutes was told the technician would have to call me back.  I waited until 5:30, no answer at the shop and I went home.
7.  Wednesday 9am still no call from the shop.  I called and was told they came in early to work on it and I could pick it up later.
8.  Wednesday 4:15 pm, call from Ray could I pick up my car today and please be there by 5pm.
9.  Wednesday 4:40 pm, paid Cashier for bill as listed $879.00 and asked for warranty paperwork.  She called Ray and he told me that this receipt was good for a warranty. I asked what if I was not here how would someone know this was a NAPA or Product under Warranty.  He told me they could call and ask.  I then pointed out that the mileage on the car was about 119 and the receipt says 179,000.  I am not sure how the 12,000 mile warranty would work with this.  He told me he would initial this and it would suffice.
10.  Disgusted by the answers I went outside to retrieve the car.  I thought I would resolve my issues with an email to the owner and NAPA to get the proper paperwork for the job and not deal with someone too busy to do things properly.
11.  Technician delivered my car to the front of the building.
12.  Place my personal items in the back seat and this is where the fun begins.
(Items below are documented with photos attached.)
13.  Upon opening the Drivers side door.
 a. Greasy hand prints inside door and on the seat .  Deep gouge in the plastic on the back of the door.  Floor Mat filthy.
14.  While the gentleman who delivered the car had a big smirk on his face about cleaning the filth from the inside of a VERY clean car (detailed 7/1) I wanted to check the oil since I was told I had none.
15.  Opened the hood and pulled the dipstick gave it a wipe down and checked the oil.  While dirty there was oil in the car.
16.  While the hood was open I found the following (very visible) incomplete work by the technician.
 a. Missing high pressure cap on Air Conditioning
 b. Missing low pressure cap on Air Conditioning 
 c. Air Box Bolt Left Side Missing
 d. Air Box bolt Right Side Not Attached
 e. Battery Tie down right side Not Attached Battery Loose in Pan
 f. Engine Cover Bolt not tightened (Not visible until Air Box Removed to fix battery tie down)
 g.  Positive Battery Terminal only on half way
 h.  Positive Battery terminal Cover Broken thrown in next to battery

After over 3 weeks and complaints to BBB AAA and NAPA I have received a refund with no apology or acknowledgement of the poor work or service.

Buyer beware

I finally got the chance to stop by early today! Made by Lia did not disappoint! The outside and inside are just too cute. 

I tried their vanilla latte, chocolate chip cookie sandwich, macarons, and raspberry lemon cake. Not only did they all look amazing, they tasted amazing as well! The filling inside the cookie...melts in your mouth! I highly recommend supporting this local business!

Decided to walk the downtown area and found this fun little joint. The menu looks fantastic, however, we just had the French fries with Gouda and pulled pork. Really good! My boyfriend & I decided to just eat something "light" so we can try another restaurant. But then there is this "Christmas beer". If you like the peppermints made by Ghirardehelli you'll like this beer. It's not overwhelming nor lingering but perfect. The beer is called frosty nibs.

After many hours of joy here, it's time for me to give back and write a review. This store is just awesome! I used to order from their catalog, which was dangerous enough, but browsing this store is a must for a foodie! The spices and herbs are always fresh. They have plenty of varieties within spice families. There has to be five kinds of cinnamon here!  And don't get me started on the peppercorns and oreganos!  Dozens of great spice blends, too. They have many sizes so I can get larger quantities of those herbs and spices I use regularly and smaller amounts of those I use only for holiday baking. They also provide recipes for inspiration and easy ways to broaden your knowledge and repertoire of international dishes.  The employees have always been friendly and knowledgeable and the store is always clean. The only thing I wish they sold was spice shelves to fit the 60 or so jars I have falling out of my cabinet onto my toes!  Please, Penzey's!!! Pretty please!

I had promised to visit Jacquettes for a while...and finally got there a few months ago. I couldn't write a review because I was hacked big time and had to close all my accounts...now I'm back and the delay was worth it. This bakery is everything a neighborhood bakery should be...great product, especially the cakes, convenient, and friendly!
I am sooooo in live with this place! Even tho there is always a long line during the lunch hour it's worth the wait! Try their grilled steak salad. It's to die for!!!
This is my go to local pizza place.  I always order the pizza with thin crust and it's perfect that way, for me. I enjoy their Stromboli too and their mega fries are soooo good!!

Man, I LOVE THIS PLACE!

The fake chicken is some of the best and most flavourful you'll find in the city. I've sampled a number of dishes, and can honestly say that the only dish that didn't appeal to me (or either of the compadres at the table) was the shrimp. The texture isn't especially pleasant, but every other "meat" was delightful.

Next time in, we plan to sample every appetizer, since it seems to be the only page we haven't pursued. Thanks fellow Yelpers for the suggestions!  :)
We come here often for special occasions. Doesn't matter if it's a chain because the food is so good every time!

Had the cinnamon pecan bun (something like that) and grapefruit pistachio donuts. Both were really good! First one was like a fancy coffee cake and the second one was sweet n tart with a spiced cake base - yum! They're a little pricey, especially when compared to Beilers which is my go to donut spot...but I guess that's specialty donuts in the city for ya.

Holy wow the foie gras was AMAZING, that combo of savory, sour and sweet was DIVINE. The Caesar salad was pretty solid, a nice mid dish. The steak was mediocre, way not worth the price. The dry aged taste was way too mild and the shallots were over powering as presented. The suggested wine with the dish was pretty nice but the steak was so off I couldn't tell if it paired well or not. The appetizer was pretty nice too, really great olives and garlic. The staff were super nice and great at explaining the dishes as well as staying attentive. The schlep to the bathroom is less then ideal but you know. This place real deserves 3 stars but the staff and that foie gras was just so dang good.

Hi, My name is Linda and I'm a sushi addict.... 
Seriously, I have been to every sushi bar in town and have never experienced the artistry and beauty associated with this type of food anywhere outside of Japan that compares to the Ichiban experience. 
The service is always excellent, but they do see ma all the time so I don't know how much of that is preferential treatment, but every item on the menu has been incredible. 
the crazy roll is a must and the seaweed salad and crab salads are to die for. The artists behind the sushi bar are skilled, engaging and charming to watch as they work. absolutely masterful....

Yummy!  Since there isn't a Whole Foods nearby, this is a great place to pick up organic veggies, meats, and other local yummy food like Zsa's ice cream (the salted caramel is AMAZING!).  We picked up some Green Mountain tortilla chips after trying a sample.
The people are pretty nice; one guy ran across the street (they have another building for soaps, etc) to grab the soap I wanted.  The check out was a little confusing, so that was annoying, and I was charged $.15 for a bag though I ended up not taking one.

Several people in NOLA recommended them, esp. for oysters. Their charbroiled oysters and  bowl of gumbo were tasty. But the overall experience was eh:  the restaurant has the atmosphere of an oversized Holiday Inn lobby by the interstate. Think impersonal dull airport restaurant. No ambiance, interesting decor, lighting. The host staff seemed indifferent to helping us get a table or show us which bar had seating: it wound up there are 2 bars, but only one offers seafood. We did sit at the serving bar, but floor and counter rather dirty. We were told wait time for a table: 40 minutes. Granted they were busy. For some reason their indoor oyster broiling station was down and they were having to grill oysters in the driveway outside. The "5-7 minute" wait to get some was 25 minutes.

I love how crispy their wings are... but the last couple of visits have been.. sub par. We usually come here after dance practice on Saturday and it's not too packed. Usually we are greeted and seated quickly on this trip it was just the Hubs & I and we kind of stood around and then decided to just find a seat. We waited about 10 mins to see if anyone was going to pop up in the dining room area but that didn't happen so I just helped myself to grab the menus. As SOON as I did that low and behold a person appeared. 

This place is family friendly and divey all at the same time. That's the charm that keeps bringing us back in. The crispy crispy wings are a plus but this time my chicken drummettes were literally a "insert Lollipop kid voice" drummette i mean like where is the chicken? I had half regular size and then the other half were these teeny tiny little things lol I would have taken a picture but I was starving so I do apologize. 

We'll be back and hopefully the service and wing sizes are back to normal.

The food is good, however the portion size and quality seems to have changed from when they were a smaller pizza establishment across the street.  The personal touch is not the same. My opinion of course..others may not agree.

I'd give it 3.5 stars given the option, but I had to round down based on all the rave reviews.  

I found their beers to be good, bordering on ok.  The pomme frites were nice, and all their sauces were fun, but service and time of delivery were pretty bad.  I ordered a French onion soup (or was it Belgian?) that the waiter promptly forgot to put in.  After my dining party was 1/3 way through their meals, I asked again about it.   "Oh, I didn't know you wanted it."  So he goes back and has the cookstaff make a fresh fucking soup.  I know how it's done properly and if it's already kept hot, that should bang out in 3-5 minutes.  If they were out, I would have gladly ordered something else.  Point being, you don't make someone wait an extra 20 minutes to halfway carmelize onions.  Eff that.  The beer's ok, but beyond that, this place can go without my patronage.

Yum! This is an excellent quick and easy lunch spot with something for everyone! I have had their baked goods before, but this was my first dining experience and it is a new favorite. Inexpensive, high quality food. All their breads are amazing, but nods to pretzel bread and miller's five grain. I will be back multiple times, because there are too many delicious menu items left to try! 

Four stars because I don't like the new interior. I miss the cozy corner bakery feel.

I decided to give Char a second chance and I'm glad I did. Everything was awesome. The service was great. I ordered the crab claws two ways and they were delicious.  My husband ordered the seafood carbonara and it was good. I ordered the filet and it was cooked perfectly.  It was tender and flavorful.  The baked potato and mushrooms were also delicious.   The one thing that I did not like was the blueberry bread pudding.  I tried it before and still did not like it.  Overall, my experience at Char was stellar.  I would recommend you try this restaurant.  I'm ready to go again to try other dishes.

DO NOT rent from FOX...the wait took an hour to get a vehicle....staff was okay... I reserved a full size SUV which showed a newer model Ford Explorer (or similar)...when I got there they said they only had a Toyota 4Runner.. A Toyota is not similar to a Ford and the vehicle had 40,000 miles on it, and did not drive well. The brakes in the vehicle were shot, and the vehicle smelled like wet dog and dog food. There was also dents and scratches all over the outside of the vehicle. This vehicle is not worth $100/ day. This vehicle should not have cost more than $30/ day. 

I have never rented a vehicle from Fox, but I have rented from several other companies. I have never received a Toyota, or Honda (which are models that tend to be cheaper and not as nice). Every vehicle I've received from a different company has looked and drove like a newer car. 

If you want a nice car worth the money you're spending rent from Sixt or Enterprise.

Came here for lunch, good fast service. Tasty sandwich in an attractive setting.
Will come back for dinner.

Dining at Panchita's is an uplifting and casual affair where you can expect extremely fresh ingredients and house made everything. I tried the queso fiesta w/chips and the Jarocha (I think it was called) that was delicious and filling. Portion size was great and the queso fresco was fantastic. 

I tried their mango juice and it was thick and sweet, I haven't mango juice like that since I was in Ecuador. If you have traveled in Mexico and enjoyed the food, you will not be disappointed with the national dishes and truly authentic ingredients.

This review is 100% based on the bar and bartenders. The bartenders are all excellent and they know their craft beers, which they have a huge assortment. 
The bar does a huge take out business so the entire bar area is lined with cases and cases of beer.
They have great happy hour and a diversely crowd is always there. I love the happy hour here.
The food 
Full disclosure: I have not eaten here in a long , long time. I hear they have a new chef and the food does look really good recently, but I just don't eat here.
In the past the food has always been inconsistent , one time I had the best extra hot wings ever. The next week , completely different experience. Different sauce and just not good.
To be fair , this was many , many years ago and the food does look better. 
If I want to gamble, I'll go to Atlantic City. If I want great beer & bartenders I'll go to the blue dog.
Truly wish it was better fit to it being one of the closest bars to my home.

As one of the most important graduations of my life was coming (Graduate School/Teaching Credential), Mayra was flexible with not only her time, but my many ideas for pictures. She was very professional, giving me ideas of where to take pictures, but also allowing me to be creative. It was because of this, that I felt valued while we were capturing my special moments! Highly recommend! 

Sincerely,

1st year 4th Grade teacher

The Frog Pond was quite a value!

The dishes that we ordered (Veggie Frittata) were giant!  The menu seemed like it had over a dozen different ways of having Eggs Benedict.

The standard cup-of-coffee was very good.  The service was friendly and prompt.

Loved it!

The best selection of fish anywhere around.  The prices are a little higher than what you'd find at a Petsmart or Petco, but the selection and health of the fish is SO much better.  I have also purchased a tank setup here and the pricing was very fair.  They have some awesome display tanks and my daughter loved the Nemo and Dory tank they had set up.  I only wish they had more pond fish and supplies, but like the name says....they specialize in tropical pets.  I don't know much about reptiles, but they seem to have a large selection of those as well.

3rd trip to the Yard House.

Went to the Yard House Saturday night.  Every time we go it just gets better and better.  Last night we split a gorgonzola burger and the mac and cheese.  The flavors were rewarding and enjoyable.  Great meal.  In fact, the food is so good it almost deserves a little more respect.  It's a bit loud and hard to really enjoy but that is a preference thing and it won't prevent me from coming back.

The beer was great and the choices are fantastic.  My only gripe is that if the beer has a higher alcohol % they won't sell you a real pint.  They charge you like its a pint but when it comes it's like 2/3 of what you are expecting.  The server said it was because of the alcohol %.  I'm a big boy and I promise I'll regulate my drinking I don't need your help with it!

Having said that, this is one of my favorite places to eat.  The happy hour appetizers are a bargain and are all delicious!  Go to this place and eat!  You won't be disappointed.
Yes! This place is awesome. Wonderful food, fun atmosphere, fresh ingredients and great service.

We had the breakfast special with sausage with the ferdi sandwich and shared both to ge a taste of breakfast and lunch. Both were very delicious and hit the spot. The biscuit would melt in your mouth and the sandwich had a perfect combo of ham, roast beef and coleslaw. There was a little of a wait to get in but definitely worth it  (if it wasn't good, there wouldn't be a wait).

As a Venezuelan visiting Florida from Oregon, I was very happy I found this place! It reminds me of my childhood when we used to visit COlombia. The coffee we order was really good. They add sugar to it so if you want it without sugar, you should tell them. They did not have a vegetarian sandwich on the menu but they were happy to costumized a sandwich for me. The cheese bread is HUGE and absolutely delicious. It was only $3. I should have order more than one. The costumer service was excellent. Many Spanish speakers were at the bakery when I went and they almost make feel at home. The person who takes the orders speaker perfect English. I highly recommend this place. I wish we had something like this in Oregon.

Great options for breakfast and brunch. And great for families. Was staying at the Renaissance which made this our go-to every morning we were here. Beignet bites were a great treat with coffee every morning.
I brought my dog here for grooming and the shops dog peed on me! All I was offered is a paper towel! I had to ask for a disinfectant wipe for my shoes. I doubt I'll go back

This is a Santa Barbara go to. Breakfast is amazing. I recommend getting the salmon Benedict and a blueberry scone. obviously the lines get pretty crowded in the mornings but definitely well worth the wait.

The only thing I liked about this stadium was that it made me happy that we got what we got in Marlins Park in Miami. This field doesn't need to be renovated it needs to be torn down. No scoreboard for other games, very little information about anything happening in baseball, the announcer barely speaks, no music playing almost never. The list goes on and on it feels so dry in there it hurts. The field is destroyed looks real ugly, and even the phone customer service is horrible. I made a mistake ordering tickets and the phone person told me you should have payed attention to what you were doing and hung up! Unacceptable! Went once now never again!

Loved this restaurant.  Food was great, staff extremely friendly and helpful

For appetizers we had great meatballs, amazing dumplings and some of the best hummus we've ever had

Main courses were equally good.  Excellent steak kebab and a special roasted chicken, both served with rice and salad, neither of which were run of the mill.  You could tell everything was made to order and cooked perfectly.

Portions were large

To top it off, staff could not have made it easier to dine with a 16 month old!

Will definitely be back.  Great addition to the neighborhood.  No knock on Zahav, but overall a better meal and experience

Pretty sure there Bbq and ranch sauce has crack in it... food was so good. They accommodate indoor seating; where areas are sectioned off for social distancing. I had the something blue cheese burger and my bff had the monthly special. We were blown away, fries were on point and the service was good. The place had a good amount of people throughout our meal. Would come back here for sure.
Decor and paint are a little worn, food very good, service excellent. They took the time to plate items nicely and make it attractive. Good job!

We hired starving artists to move to a new home. They wore masks and were personable and friendly. Compared to other companies we contacted they were responsive and scheduled with us right away (instead of telling us they'd check their schedules and get back to us, as others did). They were quick and came prepared and on time. Their prices were extremely reasonable. Loved their positive attitudes!

Was so stoked to see LemonShark Poke coming soon, almost couldn't wait for it to open...but how would the poke and experience be?
Super clean and bright (a must for raw fish, right?). Employees were very chill, cheery and friendly and walked me through the process of choosing what I wanted. 
Ahi Tuna, Hawaiian Ahi and Scallops were delish as well as all the available toppings.  The bowl was perfectly sized as were the portions.  Enjoyed every bite down to the last grain of black rice (white and brown available, too). 
Always dicey eating fish when we're so far from the ocean, but everything tasted great (and fresh!). Can't wait to make this my lunch spot. Hit em up

This is specific to the Marana location. Service was fast and friendly, as we've come to expect. I ordered the gluten-free crust and it was not cooked through -- the edge was done (about 2") but the rest of crust was white and raw. When I showed this to the person manning the oven, all I got was round eyes and a shrug.

This may be in part to this location just opening, and a lack of proper training -- both in customer service and cooking. For now, we'll be sticking with the North Oracle location.

Hello Yelpers,

if you decide to consider these apartments, please, please, read the contract VERY carefully!!  It is not the standard lease contract.  This complex is owned by AIMCO, a very large Apartment Investment and Management Company, so taking legal action after the fact is difficult and they behave like bullies.

Also beware that when you send them your deposit, you will most likely not see it again and in addition, they will send you an itemized bill of additional items they claim you must cover, over and above your deposit.  I have rented from many different landlords in many different cities and have never been denied my deposits for the items they are stating are owed.

Please consider our advice to proceed with caution and read the contract.

Without re-hashing what Gerry D. said in a previous post on 11/11/2012 basically the same thing happened to us.  After not hearing back from Sherwood Park Kia for several days I called several times asking for my deposit back.  I was told it would be ready on Friday, then Saturday, then Monday, then Wednesday.  On Monday I had enough and asked to speak with the owner/manager.  Mark called me back and was extremely belligerent and rude.  I said I simply wanted my money back and would move on.  I did file complaints with the regulatory agencies involved and was told by Mark that I "couldn't get him in trouble" so what was I doing.  Simply have the right to post my displeasure with his dealership.  I was also told that he "talked to staff" and that I should "be ashamed" of myself for the way I treated them.  I was always polite and direct.  I would recommend anyone looking at Sherwood Park Kia look at their "F" rating at the Better Business Bureau as well as other online rating systems.  They are rude, unprofessional, and will abuse you if you complain.
Delicious food. I had the house special with the red snapper. The crab cakes were definitely a run for your money. Good food and good staff. Marvin was a great host to us.
Mckenzie was awesome and super helpful!  Her patience was amazing.  I really appreciate the time and attention to details in solving my problems.

A great place if you're looking for gluten free pizza!  My grandson requests GF cheese pizza with broccoli every time he visits which is usually once a week.  Great regular food also.  Waitstaff is very good and very knowledgable.  We've even had the gluten free pizza delivered!

What an amazing find. The food is fresh. The service is spot on. You cannot go wrong here. 

Blanco is a west coast style Mexican joint with a flare of openness. The joint patio/dining area is well thought out. Decor is refreshing. 

Food... Exceptional. I finally found a place that uses Mahimahi for their fish tacos! Seriously folks, grouper is NOT a good choice, hence other taco joints. The black beans and rice, great. Again, everything is fresh. 

Come relax and enjoy the outdoor breeze!

Their desserts are as cute as they are yummy! The portions are perfect for a late night snack, or if you wanted to try multiple items. The macarons are really yummy and their drinks are delicious! I couldn't resist taking a bunch of photos :)

Without question, this is the best Chinese in South Jersey, and blows away most of Chinatowndestinations.

I always start with a giant bowl of roast pork and dumpling noodle soup.  Salt and Pepper shrimp is just killer as well.  I also have enjoyed the spicy eggplant on several occasions.

Some "typical" dishes never disappoint as well.

Joe's is a fantastic family destination also, and the staff is super friendly and helpful.

I wish I could leave negative stars. This place SUCKS for what they charge for their food. 60$ for a 20$ meal. I WILL NEVER order from these thieves again!!! I feel just like I got robbed. I'm far from cheap, I just know the difference between a $60 meal and a $20 meal. Not a place for locals!

Still my go-to chinese joint. The chinese pancakes are great. The pork pies are awesome reheated for breakfast, lunch, or dinner. The noodles with pork bean sauce is my favorite.  The cold black fungus (mushroom) salad is tasty.  It is a special place
Great and delicious food. Nice ambience and comfy booths. Great service from a very friendly staff. A must eat!!!!

As I'm staring at the menu, the cashier pops her head out and says everything is good!  I know, that's why I can't decide what I want.  I went with a milkshake which was so thick, I had to enjoy it with a spoon.  I had crushed Reece's peanut butter cups in a chocolate/vanilla mix.  The kids had cones with sprinkles (rainbow and chocolate).  Nice old time feel with great staff!

Ever since I went on my first cruise, I found the experience so delightful, so fulfilling, so, well, for lack of better descriptors, so experientially orgasmic, that I occasionally have a very vivid dream that I'm on a cruise.  I mean I live through the whole seven day experience. I dream up new friends, crazy foreign waiters, foreign outings, drunken karaoke, injuries, the whole bit.  Like after having dreams of lost loves or finally bagging Ryan Gosling, I tend to wake up sad as I face the cold reality of my workaday, hamster wheel life.  

Ever since I ate at Bayona, I have had new dreams... dreams of tangy, perfectly cooked sweetbreads, kinky rabbit three ways,  exploding fonts of delicious spicy Spanish wines, perfect service, and basically the best of everything epicurean... you get the picture. 

Just like I was "happy with my life" before I went on my first cruise, I thought I had tasted some of New Orleans' best cuisine before I ate at Bayona.  Not so!  If you have not been, get thee to Bayona.  You will not regret it.  In fact, you'll never be the same...

Love the waiters and waitresses here. Always very friendly and welcoming. The guacamole has improved exponentially since they first opened. Desserts are great. But the best part of Besito is happy hour, and the happy hour specials. Frozen (la casa) margaritas for $5, giant plate of nachos (that is only available during happy hour) for $5, tacos for $2.50, and several other $2.50-$5 drinks and small plates. Only complaint is the drinks seem a little watered down on the weekends. Reservations during happy hour on a Friday is a must.

Godzilla roll is a game changer. I've been here a couple times, and have never skipped on the Godzilla roll. A light tempura batter wraps this ginormous roll that's slathered in a sweet and spicy sauce. The veggie gyoza are also other-worldly. Pan fried on one side, they are well flavored and complimented nicely by a light soy based dipping sauce. There are a number of specialty rolls here that are also very good. The interior is dark and modern. Service is friendly and there is never a wait. What complaints do I have? First, none of the other rolls have stood out to me as a "must order again." Second, I had one mildly negative experience- server was friendly, but offered two specialty rolls that were several dollars more than anything else on the menu (over $20 each). I was surprised, to say the least, when the check came. Even still, I will come back again and again because this place is one of the best.

Food:
It's great! But it can also be better. I like the unlimited toppings bar they advertise (they aren't too skimpy on that) and the price definitely matches what you get. In my experience, Poke is usually pretty expensive and Poke Loa is slightly cheaper than what I am used to paying. The first time I went there, I felt that they gave way too much rice and were pretty skimpy on the fish. I ordered the three scoop poke bowl and was still hungry afterwards. The second time I went there, I ordered the two scoop poke bowl and was very full. (I got the same toppings both time - everything). 

My only comment about the taste of the food is that I wish they had a sushi rice option (even if it's more expensive). I've had poke bowls with regular jasmine rice, sushi rice, and brown rice before. I definitely favor the sushi rice over the other options. 

Service:
Good service, great decor. I think the store could improve by focusing a bit more on plating the food. It's definitely pretty difficult considering this poke place is Chipotle style (and not a poke place where you check boxes on a sheet and then the workers make it for you). One of the great things about poke is how colorful the toppings are and I think paying a bit more attention to how those colors are presented on top of the bowl could go a long way. Outside of that, I wish they offered a better drink selection. Other than the water, I don't feel like they have many simple drink options. 

Background: 
Poke is from Hawaii/West Coast and Poke Loa is the first (other than Costco) to bring it to New Orleans. It's described Poke- eh, not poke - ee. I've described it as Chipotle for sushi to most of my friends so they're prepared for the experience. Poke Loa is located at a great spot on Magazine St. There's paid street parking (until 7pm) on Magazine Street, but you can usually find parking on a side street or on Louisiana. Since it opened, I've gone twice, once the first week it opened and once ~four weeks later. Bathrooms are in the back and they also have outside seating.

Judy's is our go to place for a low-key meal. The food is always good and prices can't be beat. Our favorites are the daybreak burger and the chilli cheese fries. We can't wait for their bakery to open.

Absolutely loved our NYE dinner here. Don't understand the negative reviews at all. Service was great. Proper cocktails perfectly prepared and the food was tremendous.  Great ahi , sea bass and loved the deviled eggs and seafood stuffed peppers.  We will eat here again soon.

Great place in Franklin, good food and atmosphere! Right in the heart of downtown and super convenient. Ask for Gramps or the OG as your server, got a great attitude and spectacular service. Would give 6 stars if we could!!

We were recommended the place by the hotel concierge. It was average at best.  The place is super dark inside makes you wonder what they are hiding. the staff were either all having a bad day or just plain rude. Everyone from the hostess to the bartender were like I was bothering them.  The filets seemed like good cuts of meat but were way undercooked. We ordered medium well and got maybe medium which we can't stand. We ordered take out so not like we could send back. The onion rings were super dry and the coating was literally brittle breaking into crumbs. The mushroom onion mix is slimy and dripping in whatever they cooked it in.  They charge a premium but offer sub par. Stick with Ruths Chris or Mortons. Don't waste the trip there.

I really wanted to enjoy a traditional Louisiana meal with my husband.  I'm a vegetarian so when I found a places that offer a vegetarian dish i was very happy. My husband got rhe snow crab dish  which came with two big legs and some sides.
I ordered the hush puppies and the vegetarian jambalaya, the hush puppies where good and the jambalaya was good too. the jambalaya is about $22 but it's a big portion. they have a pasta option if you don't feel like a eating that entree. The atmosphere was great our server was super nice .

Nice retro decor. I did not try the rooftop, though. I just wish the music was a little less loud. Staff was friendly, but a little slow to show up, even though the room wasn't half full. Our bench had crumbs, my husband started to clean to sit and she didn't even bother to clean it herself. 

We had the hummus, the tofu tempura and spinach raviolli, it was all wonderfull, but the size of the dishes were small (not the hummus, though). The deserts were excellent also, very very tasty. We had the banana wonton and the chocolate cupcake, they are also small, but satisfying size for me, not fory husband, though. The price is fare for the quality of the food. I'll try the brunch next time :)
Cute little cafe in Old City.  Nice decor and good coffee
This pizza was ordered & provided during a pickleball event and was very good.  The cheese pizza was great! I would eat this again!

The owner of this restaurant is consistently one of the most insulting individuals we have ever encountered.  He came to our table and argued that the food was prepared according to "policy" and that policy includes not serving bread with salads, but he will charge a $1 for a stale roll and refuse to take it off the bill becuse we shold have known the "policy".   The food is average at best but the service and customer treatment is the worst you might ever experience.

I drive over an hour to get to Precision Diamonds. The owner and staff know their industry, diamond quality and cuts, and ring styles. Their repairs are very reasonable and fast. Moses is a true artist! Super friendly and efficient.
Best pizza in Tucson so far! Attracted by the name at first! Creative names for pizzas. Love the dough! Give it a try, you won't regret it!

Outstanding pastries! Made on site. Their chocolate croissants, on this day, were as good as we got in Paris at one of the "top 10" rated (in the local newspaper) croissants.  

They also put a lot of effort into my coffee, but my cappuccino today was kind of eh, much better than average, but not up to the level of Hub Roasters, Verve, Four Barrel et al.

First experience must've been a fluke. I've been back 3 more times since the first, and the employees have been super chill and attentive. Food has been great every time.

The harissa is highly recommended if you like and can tolerate spicy food. And the garlic hummus is great.

Willy Wonka opens a cafe is essentially what you are walking into. I don't have a crazy sweet tooth so I was impartial about trying the food here but I don't have anything bad to say. A lot of the items on the menu seem strange since there is cocoa in the ingredient list but I promise its good. Decor is a big factor here since each branch is made to look like a chocolate factory with its own front counter for treats to go. 
Get the:
Mac and cheese 
Best BALT Sandwich
Wings
Cocoa spiced fries
Milkshakes are FIRE
DO NOT LEAVE without getting a dessert pizza!
My favorite place with the best margaritas! Love Tarrah, super sweet! I recommend ordering the strawberry margarita and a tequila shot!

The only reason they don't get a 5 from me is the price. If they weren't so expensive, I would eat here on the regular. The mushroom alfredo is the best alfredo I have ever had and the breadsticks are like little clouds. Sooooo good!

OK, so full disclosure, I know the owner, and seeing the mixed reviews reminded me that I've been meaning to try this out and see for myself. So my review might be slightly biased, but objectively? This food is delicious!

It's not cheap, but I feel like they have reasonably priced options. There's sliders with a side for $7.50 and reasonably priced sandwiches. I, however, decided to treat myself to the pulled pork platter, which, at $15 dollars is not what I'd normally spend on lunch.  But this was an early dinner for me, and I got tons of food, so I feel like it was fine. They're committed to sustainable sourcing, which is important to me, and I'm OK with paying a little extra for that. 

The pulled pork was done perfectly, a juicy, tasty, huge pile of meat with some big chunks thrown in, which I love. The sides were awesome too. I had the warm potato salad, which is less traditional potato salad and more tasty side dish of warm red potatoes, capers, and red onions in a tangy sour cream sort of sauce with tarragon, I think? It wasn't over-sauced, just enough, so yay for that. I also had the mac n' cheese which is solid and definitely NOT like Kraft. The dill pickles were tasty and crispy and I got two, score! My only disappointments were the bread and the sauce, but that requires some explanation. Due to customer requests, they recently changed their bread to a softer bun type deal, which I know is traditional and popular, but I always skip that, at any BBQ joint. I just don't personally like that sort of bread, and was sad that I missed out on the corn bread they were previously serving, but each to their own. Now, the sauces. I only tried one sauce, which was the blackberry ancho, and it was very good and different. However, apparently they offer four sauces, but only two were sitting out on the self-serve station during my visit. (The other was a mustard sauce, which I opted to skip.) I figured they were refilling the other options and was going to ask about them, but then got an important work call and had to leave. So...I didn't actually take the time to let them help me with my problem. Next time!
