This was the easiest tire buying experience I've ever had.
First, you can shop online easily and select what you need and set your appointment all ahead of time.
The check in process once you get there is easy.
The wait time was very low considering how busy they were, the work they did was fast and efficient, service was friendly, and it sounded like honest answers to all of my questions as far as I could tell.
Good company, high-quality materials and tools. One of the few places in Santa Barbara area that is open on the weekend for landscape materials!
This may be my new favorite ramen place. Strong execution and bold flavors had me dreaming of ramen for the next couple of days. I'll be back.

I went back because a friend was adamant she wanted their buffalo ckn salad, so I reluctantly went but wasn't going to order because of past experiences, however, the mngr Teresa came over and remembered my last experience and persuaded me to order and I did. It was much better and she even took care of my order. Better experience this time around.

My family and I have come to Island Way Grill for the last 3 years and it has been one of the places we have looked forward to eating the most when we visit. Unfortunately this most recent trip will be the last. I will note that we have always gone for Sunday brunch which is a buffet which they are highly regarded for in the Clearwater area. The food in the past has typically been well above average for a buffet but this most recent time it was a bit of a let down. The biggest issue I have with this place is there set up for the buffet...it's an absolute nightmare. There are no clear lines and where they have the buffet set up is a main thoroughfare for the restaurant which clusters up that entire area. I wanted to point out that we only go to the Sunday brunch because I believe this is the only buffet setup they have so this may not be an issue except for on Sundays. The restaurant itself is really nice and love sitting outside on their deck. I wouldn't say don't eat here but I would suggest maybe avoiding their Sunday brunch buffet.

Carol was amazing! She is so friendly and knowledgeable. As an active duty military member we run into numerous issues when it comes to our vehicles each time we move. I was grateful to find this location and have such a wonderful experience. She did everything! Made my move less stressful and saved me so much time from having to deal with the DMV.

The first thing I noticed about this place is that the stylish decor inside is completely different from the cheesy neon lights outside - so definitely don't judge a book by it's cover! They have a great selection of nail polishes. 

I got the manicure and spa pedicure, and thoroughly enjoyed my experience. I especially loved the scrubs and hot stone massage on my legs and feet. The technician was friendly and spoke fluent, flawless English. We chatted quite a bit and he was very pleasant. I especially enjoyed the chair massage as my nails dried, a very nice touch. 

Mon through Wed is 20% off, so be sure to get in on that if you can. One note - tips must be in cash. I paid for my service by card, but had to run out to an atm.

The owner Jackie has been cutting my hair ever since Hair Kutz Studio opened. Hands down the best barber to cut my hair. I am fairly picky because having been in the service I like a "High and Tight" and while every barber will tell you they can do it, very few can do it well. Especially when away from a military base or community. Also on a few occasions Chelsea has cut my hair too, she is also a skilled barber and does a great job.

Possibly the best thing about Hair Kutz Studio is the environment. It is very man friendly with a large flat screen TV always set to ESPN and a great selection of male oriented magazines available(you won't find Cosmo or vogue here guys). I cannot recommend Hair Kutz Studio enough. Great hair cuts, great environment and 2 beautiful ladies with tons of skill and personality. It is more if an experience than a hair cut.

Do not believe the other reviews! My family and I wanted to have a sit down lunch one day and decided on this place based on its reviews, and wow this must be the most unclean place I have been in. There are no places to Sit, they don't have heat, and there is marker marks all over the walls. I'm not even positive this is a legitimate business because a place that dirty couldn't even pull off a C. We ate the food anyways, my mother puked it up later, and my sister had to go directly to the restroom. I used to be in the Navy and because of this, I have an iron gut, and even I got an upset stomach. This place is shady and only accepts cash.

My boyfriend and I dropped in after grabbing coffee. We weren't expecting much, we simply just stopped in because we were next door and hungry. 
Now this is my boyfriends FAVORITE gyro spot. He simply won't stop raving about it.
It's a, "mom and pap," shop. The cashier was the grandson. They were a very welcoming family and helped us order, as we're very indecisive. 

We'll definitely be back soon!
Excellent service and food! Never a bad meal here. I like the French toast and hash browns. The lox sandwich is amazing too.
Everything was fine! I got my order taken care of quickly. But then I come to look at my drink and it has Bugs in it!! Disgusting!!

Today was my first (and last) visit. An older lady did my nails, and she took SO LONG. It was the longest gel manicure of my life- it took almost 2 hours! She repeatedly messed up the paint on my nails and had to redo them, making it go on even longer. Her manager came over and started arguing with her in another language, making things really uncomfortable. Then her manager apologized to me, saying "Sorry, she's so slow!!" Then once the manager walked away my manicurist was rolling her eyes and making faces and muttering under her breath. The manager came back for a while and just stood there watching. The whole situation just made me super uncomfortable. (then when she left again the muttering under the breath started again..) Also the "massage" was awful- she just dumped a ton of cuticle oil on my arms, barely rubbed it in and then called it quits. My nails don't even look that good, not worth the price... I don't think I'm coming back when there's other places that probably act more professional and don't take 2 hours just  for a gel manicure.

Horrible. Staffed by the laziest, indifferent idiots. In 3 years living here Stratford has never once responded to an email. Never. It took 5 months to get tags for the entry gate. Finally had to drive to their office, where the assorted lazy slobs were highly indignant that we were using old forms. They took no responsibility to provide realtors with their new forms. But oops, after idly seaching around, gosh, they seemed to be out of gate tags. But they'd mail them to us when they got reordered!
By a miracle, they did. Of course one was defective. In all it took 5 months to just get gate tags.
Recently emailed needing a link to HOA approved paint schemes as we're having the house repainted. Dead silence--of course. Fortunately the painters directed us to a source. Stratford? Please, they just cash their checks.

Taking into account the decent food reputation of this restaurant, my friends and I were thoroughly excited for our saturday night reservations, but from the staff, service, to food, we were utterly disappointed.

Table: 
We were seated at a 4 seater table right next to the front door, host counter and bar, which not only kept getting blasted with cold air every 10 seconds, but guests were bumping into my friend's chair throughout the night, physically interrupting his meal, and staff using his chair as a hand rail as they brushed by our table. 

We noticed when we first walked in, that we would undoubtedly be seated at this table by the door, and when we asked the hostess if we could have another table and wait for it, we received an unapologetic response that they were booked solid and that is the table for us. As someone who works in a client service profession, the response should never be no, but what can I do to resolve this, even if it means agreeing to an unconventional solution to accommodate the customer/client's concern. 

Staff/Service: 
After being seated at our table, almost 15 mins went by without a single person coming by to greet us, take drinks orders or even apologize for the delay in service. It wasn't until the hostess noticed us looking around that she called someone over to take our drink orders. Our waiter, as apologetic as he was about the unfortunate table and staff, couldn't make up for the poor experience we had throughout the night. His comped prosecco and cheese plate service was appreciated but not everyone at my table enjoyed the prosecco so it was a bit of a wasted gesture. As my friend said, it would have been more helpful if he asked us first what he could do to improve our situation instead of attempting to resolve it in his own way, but we appreciated his gesture and concern, as he was the only staff that night that seemed keen on attempting to fix our broken experience.

Food: 
Expected to be blown away by the courses to make up for the bad table and service but each course was just decent, nothing truly exceptional, except for the key lime dessert. My friend even had the unfortunate incident of having something inedible on his surf & turf plate, but besides that, all the other courses were just fine. Had better tasting menus at lesser known restaurants, so again, based on their reputation, our meal experience did not live up to it.

Net, my friends and I felt that the restaurant was just trying to get things done haphazardly and crammed way too many tables and reservations in that Saturday night, without any real concern for providing excellent customer service. 

From the distracted, aloof staff, poor table placements to less than mind-blowing food, I would not recommend this place. If anything, just grab a seat by the bar or order the key lime dessert as take out if you can.

My husband and I used to dine at Matoi Sushi all the time in college. They used to store customer's personal chopsticks at the restaurant which was pretty cool. My husband still owns his flashy purple chopsticks with his customer number on the case.

Last night we were craving sushi and we didn't want to drive out of our way so we decided to order take-out from Matoi. I can't believe we haven't ate here in so long. The sushi is even better than I remembered! 

We ordered these dishes:

Vegetable Tempura Appetizer: 5/5 I love how light and sweet their tempura breading is! The onion rings were the best of the vegetables.

Sashimi Salad: 5/5 The salad included a generous portion of fresh sashimi.

Sexy Mama Roll: 5/5 This roll was loaded with tuna! Definitely one of the best specialty sushi rolls I have had in a long time.

Banzai Roll with soft shell crab: 4/5: Good, but I prefer my sushi rolls raw. My husband selected this sushi roll since he loves deep fried soft shell crab.

Will definitely be back soon!

I've been a full-time resident in the Madeira Beach area since 2009.  I've always considered Brown Boxer to be one of my favorite hang out spots after they first opened in this area.  I was surprised at the complete transformation this establishment underwent over the past year.  The lack of customer service, the filthy state of the restaurant, and the shockingly rude manager on duty are the only reasons that I need to never return.  Restaurants that solely rely on seasonal tourists are doomed after they have pissed off their year-round clientele.  Proof of this is all along the beach.

I found Dr Perkins and Natural Med Therapies on Yelp after experiencing a week of severe back pain. Being new to the Tampa Bay Area, I turned to Yelp for guidance on an acupuncturist and found stellar reviews for Natural Med Therapies. Luckily when I called I was able to get in the same day which was a huge relief. After a consultation, I was treated for my back and other concerns. After 20 minutes Dr Perkins checked in to see how I was doing. I was so relaxed and the heat lamp felt great on my back. She added another 10 minutes to my session and then applied some essential oil to my lower back. I was definitely feeling relief from the pain and will be back in a few days for another treatment. I'm so grateful to have found Natural Med Therapies, as I needed a great Naturopathic doctor that I trust. Machelle is kind, patient and professional. Plus her dog Rusty is a cute addition to welcome patients to the office. I even booked my husband for an appointment too!!

I just discovered this great place!  I had a small project - well, not so small actually - a manual of 160 pages.  I wanted it in color, bound for a small price all in about 24 hours.  They came back to me with a price that was in my budget and even though the time frame was tight they could do it.  Now what can I say about their customer service?  AWESOME!  Oh my gosh -- what good fellows these folks are!  They listened to my needs and came back with a quote very quickly.  They were courteous and friendly.  I felt like I was talking to an old friend.  These folks want your business, and your return business and they are working hard to get it.  Ya gotta check them out.  You won't be sorry.  I'm not.

What a jewel of a surprise!  We decided to celebrate Cinco de Mayo with some takeout from this new restaurant, and it is now our New Mexican favorite in Nashville!  We enjoyed the children's tacos, the California burrito, the taco salad, and a couple of veggie tacos accompanied by beans and rice.  The beans and rice were as outstanding as the rest of our selections.  Very often, bean and rice sides at other restaurants can tend to be bland and almost afterthoughts.  We could have eaten just the beans and rice as we were floored by their delightful authentic flavors.  ¡Viva Mexico!

Cassie, was a friendly and inviting game master. Very outstanding experience. I screamed a lot in Apartment 13 and it was a lot of fun. Highly recommend coming here and asking for Cassie as your Games Master

this is the first place I ever tried sushi and i've been addicted ever since, the staff is polite, the food is great, and the happy hour prices can't be beat. I would highly recommend this place to new and seasoned sushi eaters.

Great coffee and a cool interior. The architecture of this Riverbend coffee house on St. Charles is worth a trip. The sculpted bats drinking coffee are a hidden treat.

Food choices include pastries and bagel sandwiches. Both look great and the bagel sandwiches have interesting combinations. 

The coffee, the reason fur our visit, was some of the best we had in NOLA. We had macchiatos and the espresso was great with notes of citrus. 

The interior has a balcony and large open feel. There is outside seating and friendly and competent baristas.

Had a tour with Josh and we couldn't be more happy. Awesome way to see the city and learn the history. Definitely recommend anyone visiting the city to do this tour. Josh was extremely safe and went at a great pace, plus stopped enough for bathroom breaks and history talk. You don't have to be in great shape because Philly is so flat. A must do....ask for Josh!

MM have upgraded their online client portal and it's better than ever!  Stephanie, our trusted dog walker, now uploads a quick message in real time after each scheduled mid-day walk. 

Now I can see if my little monster has peed or pooped while I'm at work - which takes the guess work out of deciding what time I need to get home to relieve him.  It's a tiny new feature that makes a big difference & sets them apart from other dog walking businesses in the area.

1. Waited in line for our reservation requesting 2 connected rooms. When we get to the desk we are told that the room was in the process of being cleaned and would be ready soon. We huddled at Starbucks with our suitcases for the 30-45 minutes wait with no text. Went back to the desk and were told by another (unfriendly) employee that the people checked out late and that the rooms were not even started yet and that it would be another 45 minutes. They had no explanation, of course for the previous misinformation. Went back to the desk at 45 minutes. Room not ready and I'm starting to lose it. No 2 rooms available on the same floor even. I'm here to help my 78 and 80 year old unwell parents enjoy a couple of days in Reno and we've just spent 2 hours sitting with suitcases in Starbucks based on some stupid system that led us to believe we would be sitting there for 30 minutes. My mom really needed to lay down by now. I did ask about a suite but they would not adjust the price to meet our budget. My husband finally talked to a manager but coincidentally I received the "room ready" text. We got a small credit and went to the room. 
2.  That evening Elks party at the end of our hall. Elks are loud apparently. 
3.  Grand Cafe: parents ordered a milkshake, coffee, burger, toast. Time it takes to receive this snack; 1 hour 5 minutes. Explanation; early cook forgot to tell next cook to make it. Parents paid. Bad server decision. Should have been refunded. 
4.  Hot tubs are closed. 
5.  Second night, another group in the "hospitality room" at the end of the hall. Yelling. This is not hospitality. This is a stupid idea. 
What could have been a fun and rewarding getaway was a total disappointment.

Disappointing...... poor service. It took over 30 minutes just to get seated. The host continued to say she didn't see me however I gave her my first and last name. It took another 20 minutes to get frozen rolls brought to the table. I ordered fettuccine Alfredo with shrimp let's just say go to Olive Garden you will save money and time. The quality is the same or equal to Olive Garden. I only came here because Benedetto is no longer offering dine in service....... This was probably one of the worse Friday night experiences I had in Lutz area. The place has potential but obviously they just don't have what it takes to make good Italian food. First and last time coming .
Best nail salon in Tampa! Beautiful work, very clean and friendly. Have been coming here 10 years. Wonderful experience!
Great drink and food select. This bar would be nothing without Marty. Just kidding everyone was great. But Marty was the icing on the cake

This place is really good. Love the re model of it I came in for my birthday brunch and our server was very sweet she sang happy birthday and sounded awesome. The food was reasonably priced and very flavorful. I got steak and eggs and it is really hard for me to eat eggs somewhere because they normally make my eggs wrong. THESE eggs though.... they were perfectly cooked middle runny and white cooked thoroughly (it's like magic)   Definitely will come back on my next visit to New Orleans.

The croissants and breads are outstanding.  They make the best french bread in Philly by far and the closest to ones baked in Paris.  The croissants are big bites of super flaky and buttery goodness.  My favorite ones are with cream cheese and fruit (blueberry, cherry, strawberry, etc.).  The tiramisu was rich and extremely creamy.  The offerings here are made with high quality ingredients in a very skilled manner.  There are also wonderful savory breakfast items such as guiches and cheese/bacon croissants.  The owners are most friendly, kind, and warm.

I thought I would never find a better falafel than Mama's in Center City. I wasn't even that enthused for food when I chanced upon this food truck. I had my heart set on Ben and Jerry's but thought just perhaps this one time I should have food before dessert instead of the other way around. O:)

Well, Marrakesh definitely wins for best falafel of Philly for me. I really cant' even describe why. Maybe the texture, size, sauce. I don't know. It was amazing though. Maybe the $4 price tag made my tastebuds more open to new company? Regardless, it was so good...must go back. 

Moroccan fries are worth that extra $1 too. Just potato wedges essentially but well done with an excellent spice. I need to steal that spice combination and put it on everything I cook at home. 

Just a warning..this food will be highly addictive.

all you need to know: 
al pastor tacos + free margs

seriously, that's it. 
the al pastor tacos are the best i've ever dad. i also ordered the chicken tinga and a steak taco, but they didn't come close to as good as the al pastor. the pork wasn't dry, it was well seasoned, and the pineapple was just right - not overly sweet, not cold out of a jar... it was perfection. note to self: next time, order 3. 

i came on a weekend afternoon with my mom and sister after apartment hunting in the area (yes, conshy - i'll be in- or near- you soon!). first surprise: FREE MARGARITA. effing brilliant. love it already. the menu was pretty straight forward, the usual suspects when it comes to mexican dining were present and accounted for. all very reasonably priced. sister had a steak and cheese quesadilla that she scarfed down before i could snap a pic, and mom had the grilled chicken fajitas (i think?)... honestly, i was so into my tacos - specifically the al pastor. did i mention it was the best? it was. really. 

we split the tres leches cake for dessert - a nice sweet bite, if you're into the texture of tres-leches cake. i'm mehhh on that, but it was still tasty. 

parking is all on-street, which is something i'll have to get used to - seems normal for the area. the resto is nothing fancy, but no need to be - the food and margaritas are all you need to worry about. and they're open super late, which is cool for a 1:30am taco fix. 

i'm a fan, and would like to say i'll try something else (like the mole) next time... but nah, i'll stick with the al pastor tacos, let's be real.

The folks at Bayer are super helpful and resourceful when it comes to picking out virtually anything that grows or that they sell.  We live nearby and always go to Bayer when it comes to buying basically anything plant-able.  Our fave recommendation has to be the purple shamrocks.  We were struggling to find the perfect plant for the cement planter we got from Bayer last year (Yay clearance!).  After explaining the location/lighting - they strongly suggested the shamrocks, and they look absolutely wonderful!

We bought several other items and all are basically doing fine and dandy so no issue with quality there.  They have a nice variety and also sell accessories such as vases, planters, and other yard goodies.  We will always go to Bayer first and foremost for our floral and yard needs.  They are affordable, dangerously close, and extremely good at what they do.

This place is great- the mechanic (Herb is the best!) was super friendly and brought me out twice to show me different things on my car and explained everything they did. Scott at the desk was also super friendly and nice. Recommend this place highly!

The perfect place to pick up a quick meal. Lamb is great; falafel is one of the best I have had around here. I like to get flat bread here from the grocery section. I stopped in last week for tahini, as the one they carry is my preferred brand from Lebanon, but they have drastically reduced the grocery offering (including my tahini!) Oh well, its still good for a cheap, quick lunch or piece of baklava, if not for the groceries.

Quality hair cut from friendly stylist.  Not a high end salon and not priced like one.  In cut and out.  Much better than the city ave location which is more culturally diverse.   Need a quick cut, this is the place in Drexel hill.

Arguably the most popular brewery in the Nashville area, we drank our fair share of Yazoo beer while here. Yazoo Gerst became a go-to beer us. Upon wearing that one out, I also drank a decent amount of Dos Perros. We got adventurous during one of our visits to the brewery and tried some type of Red Wine Sour, that was very difficult to drink. 
For more brewery info visit: travelptswithacraftbeerproblem.wordpress.com

Frontier Airlines is terrible. My fault, I knew better, but I thought I was saving money when in fact I was not! Wouldn't give a refund for a bag I didn't have, check in online takes forever because there are 5-6 steps with each step asking you to pay more money, baggage fees are non-refundable,  and by the time you check out to purchase your tickets the price that you started with has doubled. Ridiculous. And drinks and snacks cost extra too! So frustrating....feel like I was just throwing money in the trash. Based on the service, doesn't look like they take very good care of their employees.

This is a great little gem of a breakfast café. An older house turned into a café very creative. Service was immediate and friendly. Food is affordable and delicious I would recommend this place whether your new or a local. They do serve lunch. I love how you can sit in the front yard to eat.
This place is amazing. Get there early or get there somewhere after 12:30 or you'll be waiting a while but it's well worth the wait. The food is delicious the service is great.

Super delicious pizzas and I really love their ricotta with the flatbread. Wait staff was super friendly and attentive. Only thing I didn't like was that the table they had outside was awkwardly placed and pretty wobbly.

If you go, especially your first time, sit at the bar. There has not been a time when I haven't struck up a conversation with the bartender or fellow patron. The location of the pint room is definitely in their favor. They are in the heart of Carmel. Whenever I visit Indiana, this is always a stop. It's a good place to catch up on central Indiana's ever-changing beer scene. 

Service has improved over time. Good customer service will keep me coming back.
The menu is solid. They have something to meet all your food moods. The staff is as well-versed with the food menu as they are with the beer.
Delicious gourmet burger and Belgian frits.

The absolute best take out restaurant in the delco area. Seriously, I've haven't had better Chinese takeout than this. The food always tastes fresh and the owners are super nice. When I was picking up food for myself, I noticed the owner running out to give a previous customer a can of soda. They go above and beyond to get your order right.

Very friendly staff and awesome food! It helps that I can walk half a block from my home to eat here. Biscuits and gravy are my favorite and they have really good Bloody Mary's and don't forget to ask for a piece of thick cut bacon to stir it with!

I love Blaze I do! But I visited this one today and the girl was condescending and just not customer service friendly. I will go back but on her day OFF. I only wanted a salad and there wasn't a listed price for a "build your own". I waited 6 minutes at the register to be rung up. My cousin said NO cheese on her pizza then they got mad when he put Parmesan and my cousin asked him to remove it.

Continental is one of my favorite martini places in the city and the restaurant has a retro feel.  There are multiple levels (three in total) which means that there is plenty of space!  The first two levels are the restaurant and both floors are equipped with a bar.  The second floor has these cool swinging chairs you can sit on and dine.  The third floor is the lounge which also has a rooftop area with table and chairs and heaters during the winter time.  As a word of caution is does get rather crowded in the summer time on the rooftop area and the lounge.  The restaurant is designed tapas style so don't expect large portions and dishes are a little on the pricey side.  For the lounge, my favorite martinis are the Astronaut, Sourpuss and Twizzle.  Martinis are creative and strong.  They also have all your usual cocktails as well for those that are less daring.

Best wings in town!
Huge space with a ton of tv's. Great place to watch a game. Get there early on a Sunday afternoon! Fills up for Colts games for sure

I needed to have fixtures installed. BrightLife Electric checked off all the boxes in what I needed in an electrician. Installation was professionally completed in a timely manner and pricing was reasonable. They are committed to customer satisfaction and are easy to reach even after the job is completed.

Would have given zero stars but you have to give at least one on this review. Waited half an hour for 2 sushi rolls which, they got wrong...Venue only had maybe 5 people dining at the time  (for mostly pizza). NOT how we planned to spend our evening. Read the other reviews before going 4/5 stars, don't believe everything you read...

I haven't reviewed a place in a long time now but if any of you walks into this place, you will leave with the need to tell all the World about it, too.  The second one walks through the doors of this true Mom & Pop operation you feel the positive vibes; you quickly understand that this place is NOTHING BUT positive.  Go ahead....I dare you to even think of one negative thing about it!  Look at all the posts on this blog.  Who can say anything bad?  When you walk up to the counter, employees (and you'll immediately love them) warmly welcomes you and get you situated.  However, the first thing they warn us about is that they're out of small thin crusts so all they have left is large size pizzas (Ah, FINALLY, something negative I can say).  Problem is, it's just the wife and me.  Well, we really want to try this pizza so we order the large with intention of bringing some home for later.  Anyway, you're part of the phenomenon as soon as you walk in.  It's not hard to tell that this place is pizza with a cause.  I could go on forever about this part of the business but, well, I did come here for some pizza.

Ah yes, the pizza.  You can smell the authenticity from outside the restaurant and then you enter and hear them yelling orders and statuses back and forth in the tiny kitchen space.  You can see the perfectly browned crust of their pies as they come out of the oven and anticipate that you've made the right decision to give this place a shot.  Yeah, they do crust right.  Perfectly thin & crispy on the outside, little bit of chew once you take that first bite.  On their pizza oven, there must be a setting labeled "perfect".  Don't know how else to describe it.  You see them pouring sauce on the pie from the bucket as they make it and wonder, "What's the secret to that part?"  Then you taste it and realize....there may be no secret except for the use of great canned tomatoes.  Not sure if they added a darned thing to it but somehow, it's in perfect harmony with everything else on the pie.  I could say the same for the amount of cheese and all of the 4 toppings I ordered on ours.  By the way, I recommend that you make sure sausage is one of the toppings on yours.  It's made in-house and delicious.

Oh, and about that "out of small pizza" problem.  Turns out my petit 5'2" wife and I polished off the whole large pie and left nothing for my teenage son at home despite that we promised him we were coming home with leftovers.  We ended up having to get him In-N-Out.  It was that good.

Overall, this place is a small, hidden dive that serves great, authentic, New York style pizza, arguably the best.  Service is friendly, prompt and sincere.  I encourage you all to eat in and take in the art, the employees and the whole vibe to truly understand what this great business is all about.  Its name is appropriate:  When you leave, you'll be smiling because you just ate the best pizza in the Truckee Meadows.  You'll be Smiling with Hope because you've just been reminded that there are still good people left in this World!
Horrible sushi! It tasted like it was going bad and it was hurriedly put together. Very disappointing to spend $67 and throw half of it away because the after taste is so horrible.

We came here after learning of the hour long wait at Amelie, the larger sister restaurant next door. The hostess promised us we would get the same menu at this smaller cafe and it didn't disappoint. I got shrimp and grits and a fresh juice, and let me tell you, I have never tasted anything better. The shrimp are hearty, the sausage was tender, and the grits were seasoned to perfection. All the while my boyfriend and I sat next to the window and people watched. Serene. I will be back.

We love this place!! 
We always order the same thing - Wings, Garlic Parm & Hot (sauces mixed together) and steak fingers which come with a side of fries. A waitress actually suggested the sauce combination for the wings and we have never thought twice about it. The wings are always a great consistency - not too saucy, but not too dry. There is always plenty of meat on the bone but not enough to raise an eyebrow. We have ordered steak fingers around town but none have been as good as this. 

They have a decent beer selection. The typical craft beers one can rely on. 

The staff is always accommodating and friendly! Even when it gets insanely busy during games, they are always on their A game.

I have been going to Armen's Custom Tailor since 2004 and would recommend to anyone.  His prices are great but his quality is amazing.  He also now carries a very large selection of quality, well made and VERY affordable suits.  Anything from pants, shirts, sweaters, coats.  You name it, he can custom tailor it to your needs.  If anyone ever asks, I tell them to look no further.  And if something isnt just right, he will fix it for you free of charge.  Great emphasis on quality all around.

I wish I had taken a picture of this place because it is always hoppin! Business people. Book clubs. Bible study groups. I've never tried the food or pastries, but they look delicious. Everyone is going to The Well! 

The coffee and drinks are great, but I need to bring my teenage daughter back for the bracelets and necklaces. 

I think part of the money goes to help villages in Africa. So now you are helping your fellow man and woman.

I needed to get help from a surveyor for a simple but important project to merge some parcels of land together. Apparently, every land surveyor is busy beyond belief, and after the first surveyor I contacted led me on for over a month and finally bailed on me, I had to find somebody else.

I called Mike and he answered his phone; took my information; and set realistic expectations with me that he too was busy but that he should be able to help with my project. We needed help finding a map to complete the project, which he was able to do, and he wrapped the project up in a timely fashion with no drama. 

Mike's services were timely, professional, and completely reasonable given the fact that I contacted him on short notice and that I'm sure he had other better-paying jobs in front of mine. He made it easy for us and I appreciate that!

Really good I was surprised. Service pretty good. Enchiladas with cheese sauce real good. Steak burrito was $7.50. Margaritas a little steep at $10 we are not at the Vinoy for brunch. Recommend over all.

Life is short, start with dessert!  The butterscotch cheesecake was the best cheesecake I have had in years ~  if not ever.  Our table ordered one to share so I only got 2 bites but I savored them greatly.  

Our table tried the lasagna, the primavera pesto penne, the roasted vegetable lasagna, and the chicken marsala.  Every dish was delicious, fresh, and served piping hot which is how we all like our food.  I would order any of these dishes again (we all shared bites with on another) but my favorite was the chicken marsala.  The chicken was incredibly tender and melted in your mouth.

We also ordered a side of meatballs as that is one of the items we use as a barometer for Italian restaurants.  They were delicious.  Not too salty like many places.  Subtly flavored in a way that you could taste the meats in them versus all the seasonings and sauce. True Italian cooking is about subtle flavors and simple ingredients.  Many places in the U.S. over season/salt Italian food and nothing that I tasted at Dominick's fell into that category.

Our server, April was friendly and attentive.  She made an extra strong coffee for one of the people in our party.  She brought garlic knots (for the third time)  and my husband sopped up his sauce with them.        

Given that it is located in a strip mall, my expectations for atmosphere were  not too high.  So I was pleasantly surprised to find real table cloths and pleasant decor.  I was seated facing a wall with cool, old photos which I enjoyed looking at throughout the evening.  My friend and I actually both commented on how we would love to make a wall of photos like that in our homes.   

The only negative thing I have to say about the experience was that there were 2 televisions on in the main dinging area.  I only like to see televisions with basketball (or any game) on in sports bars or bars in general.  That is the reason why I am not giving 5 stars.

The host staff completely let a couple cut in front of us. 

Not pleased with the service at all.

Jackie has been doing my hair for 20 years!! No one better!  The whole team is wonderful. I have been to other salons and no one has been able to style my hair like Jackie! Jimmy is the best for shampooing and coloring! Love these people! Tina for nails!

Hoosiers vs. boilers aside, Upland has good beer. 

We always stop at Upland on trips through Bloomington but it's nice to have the tap room just down the street as a relaxed place to grab a great beer. 

Dragonfly IPA is a great go to as is Bad Elmer if they have it on tap. Tonight I had a delightful coffee brown called RevUp sooooooo delicious. 

The place is quaint with decorative walls, mismatched tables and chairs and character out the wazoo. They also have bike racks out front, made of bike frames, so you can grab a beer on your journey. 

We were just there with live music and it was a blast. Craft beer and folk-esque music is my thing, though.  They also allow dogs inside; we love they allow dogs inside!

There is not food here, but they fill growlers and there are food spots nearby.

Husband texted them to get an estimate for storm damage and some trimming. They texted back the next day to say the'd be out sometime in the week beginning 10 days later. Husband asked them to give us a heads-up when they planned to come. Never contacted us and never came out. After the week passed, I used their website to see what they planned to do but have never gotten a response.

Well I have been looking for about a year for good pizza. I did find them on yelp and gave them a try. Was very impressed with what is going on right now with easy of service my only not a complaint but wish there was a little more char on the pie

Always enjoy coming here. Never have had a bad experience which keeps me coming back. Kristen was so attentive and pleasant made everything sound phenomenal. For her first official night I'm gonna say she is winning on her service and drinks. She even recommended a drink that was off the menu and nailed my gin needs! The mussels are to die for and of course I am a "satisfied woman" after the drinks she made us! All day happy hour on Sunday is where its at. Thanks again and a cheers! This place is a hidden gem and a must try!
Sooo good. Waiter suggested items & we tried them, glad we did. We also ordered shrimp & chicken gumbo. The shrimp was okay. We did not like the chicken, it was mediocre at best.

So I currently live in ny, but used to live in Maryland right outside of DC and when I lived out there I was obsessed with Zoe's. My first time having it my job at the time had it catered for a staff meeting and I was so in love. I had the piadina before with spinach and mushroom which was so good it's like a quesadilla on a pita bread, I've also tried all their skewers and sauces. I went to visit a friend of mine in Jersey and I was craving some Boston market and gps'd myself to get there and when I pulled into the parking lot and saw Zoe's I said forget Boston market lol. I got the mix and match skewers one salmon and one steak along with turmeric rice and my fave from there their white beans. The skewers per usual were amazing. I liked the tzatziki sauce on the salmon and the shkug sauce on the steak it was like a spicy chimichurri sooo good. I also ordered an extra pita on the side. My boyfriend ordered a steak pita sandwich with roasted corn as his side and he said it was absolutely amazing. I'm so sad there are none closer to nyc because I would highly recommend. It's like a really bomb chipotle style/fast food take out Mediterranean restaurant.

The New Years Eve Event was a disaster! As our fellow reviewers have stated the event was nothing like the advertisement .. to begin with we researched this party in November .. we specifically asked what was being served and they replied in November that a delicious cajun buffet would be offered .. He bragged about how wonderful the food would be.. 
what we experienced was no food .. no forks .. no napkins .. waited 1hr and 40 minutes (9 pm to 1040) to be offered a pan that has 5 brats and 2hotdogs? The only other item on buffet was a cold mac and cheese with no serving piece .. women in front of me were eating the cold max and cheese with their fingers ! We paid over $170 for two gold tickets ! Where was the Cajun ? Where was any food ? 
Also in November we were told the Squirrel Nut Zippers would perform .. after researching  this group on you tube we were very excited to be able to hear their music 
At event they did not perform as stated in advertisement but management never addresses in an email to the gold level purchasers about the band change 
Impossible wait even get a drink since it took over 40 minutes to be served 
Since food line connected to entry area it was mass confusion 
Finally they now offer a future event discount to come back? Who would want to come back at a 33 percent discount after spending over $ 170 on a disaster? 
We had no choice but to contact our charge card to file a dispute for misrepresentation of event .. 
Sadly I spent over 2 hours away from my partner waiting in lines ! In 40 years it was the worst NyEve these seniors ever experienced ! 
Next issue
This place makes me really miss living in Philly.  Very nice staff, and the scenery is great! Our food was amazing,  and we were stuffed! !!!

Horrible experience with this company. We were in town for the weekend from about five hours away and I booked this venue about a month ago. 
With Covid restrictions I emailed them a week from our reservation and didn't receive any response. I emailed them again on Thursday about our Saturday reservation and still received no message. I tried to contact them numerous times on the phone and they directed me to the email address that I had no luck with. I showed up with five other friends for my Saturday appointment to find the doors locked and nobody around. I totally understand the closure due to the virus but the communication was awful we won't be returning

Thinking that this place would be pricy because it's on the pier and has amazing views, it's only $10. The bottle prices were also reasonable. My fiance and I ended up buying a bottle of wine for $20. Sat down at their outside table for an hour an half enjoying the wine and and chatting with a few other folks there. Would of stayed longer if we didn't get hungry. Will definitely come back next time I'm in SB.

While not as famous as Cafe Du Monde - the beignets are equally as good.  If not side by side, I'd be hard pressed to difference from either store except that Du Monde gives you 2 pounds of extra powdered sugar while Cafe Beignet only gives you 1 extra pound.

While both Beignet & Du Monde offer outdoor seating (much easier to get here)...Cafe Beignet also has a house band (Steamboat Willie & His New Orelans Jazz Band) was playing and made the overall experience that much better.  Warm beignets a hot beverage as we escaped from the craziness of Bourbon Street.

Fisrt time here Sunday is pretty chill only one waiter but she is excellent she took care of the whole restaurant. We ordered mini parrillada and shrimp fettuccine and it was filled with so much flavor this place is a must visit if u are in Ybor

We had sooooo much fun on our Pedal Tavern.  We had a group of friends coming to Nashville to visit and thought this would be a fun way to break up the day and take our day drinking outside for a change of scenery.  I'm so glad we did this.

We were in the area early and did not want to drag around our beverages and the Pedal Tavern people at the office were kind enough to hold and chill our drinks until our reservation time while we stopped in a few bars along Demunbreun Hill.  

Our Pedal Tavern "host" was Tonya.  TONYA!  She was the best part of the tour.  Absolutely hilarious, so high energy.  She made the party happen.  Frankly, she was just great entertainment herself.  We were a party of six and we were joined by another group.  She did a good job of getting us all to know each other and interact.  

So pedaling down a busy Nashville road while drinking is not exactly the safest way to spend a Saturday afternoon but it was by far the most entertaining.  Don't lean backwards, hang on to the pub and just keep pedaling!

We made two stops along the way.  The first was a quick 15 minute stop and then a few blocks later we stopped for about 30 minutes.  So the two hour tour is not really two hours.  The first 15 minutes were spent going over the rules.  Pedal.  Stop at bar number 1.  Pedal.  Stop at bar number 2.  Pedal.  Done.

A lot of the local bars offer discounts for people wearing the Pedal Tavern bracelets, so there you go.  More cheap drinking.

Driving in a car behind a Pedal Tavern could be the most annoying thing ever.  Being on the Pedal Tavern though is the most fun.  Do it.

I've either owned or leased 10 Lexus' over the last 28 years. Just leased our 11th from Lexus of Wesley Chapel and they were right up there as the best. First class in every area. If you can ask for Amanda in internet sales, she is the best.

This place is.... fine. It wasn't great but it wasn't awful. My husband is Korean and cooks a lot of Asian food at home, so maybe we aren't the "ideal customer" for this place...

The bulgogi wasn't real bulgogi, it was basically just teriyaki beef (if you know what real bulgogi is, you know what I mean). The wontons were very dry, the Pad Thai was nothing special, but the Kimchi Rice was pretty good.

Our server gave off a "too cool for you" vibe--the kitchen forgot our chicken dumplings and he didn't even apologize for it when they came out 20 minutes after our entrees. That was really surprising behavior.

Like I said, it was fine. I think we have higher standards for Asian food so if you're looking for an authentic experience, this isn't it. But a cool spot to hang out? Sure, go ahead. We won't be back.

Unfortunately not a fan of this place but maybe after it gets experience it will work out the kinks of the restaurant. Had a burrito and a taco of Carne Asada and there was no flavor. I've had better.

I only get sushi once in a while, but it sure is a treat when I get it. Mike's did not let me down. We were given complimentary cold green beans in a really yummy sesame oil marinade. That doesn't sound nearly as good as it tasted! 

We had the eel and tuna nagiri (2 pieces each), a tuna roll, and the tempura roll. The tempura roll was the star of the show, with a lightly fried tempura batter, drizzled with 2 kinds of sauce. So good!

The sushi chefs were quick, friendly, and personable and we had excellent service.

I'll definitely be back!

I was really excited to try Tredici Enoteca, but I was very disappointed. I came here with a friend to get some drinks and appetizers and we were surprised at how underwhelmed we were. We each ordered a glass of Pinot Grigio, which tasted like watered down wine. The menu said it was $6, but we were each charged $12 because the waitress claimed she gave us more than a typical glass. For food, we got the bacon wrapped dates and Israeli couscous. They were delicious, but definitely not worth the money we paid for them. I had a lot of high hopes for Tredici Enoteca, so you could imagine how upset I was when I was not happy with my drinks or meal.

Today I decided that I wanna get in shape in a fun way. I googled up pole dancing classes and came across "A sensual you" I left a message and Barb ( the instructor) encouraged me to come in. I ABSOLUTELY loved the class. She has a lot to offer. If you are not interested in a good workout this class is NOT for you!!!! She plays no games!!!! She is very energetic and she wants you to work!!!! Make sure you bring water because you will need it!!!! I love the music selections as well!!! Although my body hurts I'm soooo looking forward to my class tomorrow. I had a ball!!!! I know with her intense workout I should be seeing results real soon!!!

Excellent bar-b-que. My son and his friends frequent here. They absolutely love it and on occasion he has brought over different things for me to try. Have to say I'm partial to the brisket,  especially when an end is available. It's seasoned perfectly and tender enough that u could cut it with a fork. If any of u know about brisket,  the only way to get it tender is to cook it for hours and hours. Sweet Lucy has perfected that.

My 8 year old daughter has been a patient at Castor Pediatrics since her one week follow up. I absolutely love them here! Dr. Sey is a very competent doctor and he genuinely cares about his patients. He is great with babies and can always put a smile on their faces even when they have shots. Any time I've had to call the office because of my daughter being ill, I've always had a call back no later than a half hour after I call. and they can always fit you in, no matter how busy they are. The staff of nurses are so kind and so helpful. the office just had a revamp in the past year so the waiting area is looking extra spiffy. Love it here!

Ever since the owner switched this food has been trashed. Today was the last straw, I ordered a cheese steak with light mayo got a cheesesteak drenched in mayo with nothing else I had asked for on it. Disgusting.

We had a nice time last night.  Brought one of the dogs in their out door beer garden.  Husband liked the Woodchuck on draft and the pub style snacks.  I just likes the beer garden style seating.   A very pleasant experience.

Service was great the lobster tail was on the small side but tasted great, steak was tender and juicy, good for the price. The crowd was a good mix and we were able to carry on a conversation with ease.

Purchased a high end water filter system in 2014. It leaked multiple times, they fixed it but it was inconvenient, then annual service this year ran over $600, due primarily to over priced parts ($132 UV light bulb and 1/2 cube Media Cor Corosex $145 - both retail for less than half of that), portal-to-portal charges added $179 to the cost. Last straw was the salesman, Bryce, did not return any of our calls, we made multiple attempts to discuss the service and left numerous VMs.  I also gave Bryce multiple opportunities to call before reporting to Yelp and Home Advisor. Very poor follow up and customer service. Office staff has a bad attitude when you attempt to ask questions. Stay away, there are much, much more honest, responsive, and well-priced providers out there.
We have went here a few times and each time has been phenomenal! They have a great outdoor area that has some shade if it's super sunny out! My two favorites so far are The Duke and The Fatty B!

Hell No. Bad kitchen staff making terrible takes on asian inspired food. I ordered a watery beer and chicken wings that were half cooked. Replete of flavor, the food and this eatery. Once and never again.

BEST Buffet in Nashville!  My boyfriend is a huge fan of Indian Cuisine and we have hit up about 3 or 4 Indian buffets in Nashville so far.  Every new one we try becomes better and better but this time I will look no further!  For location, it is super convenient for franklin/cool springs residents or business people which I'm thrilled about since that's my side of town and parking was easy.  Upon entering I thought how spacious and beautiful the place was.  I was greeted by the sweet hostess and directed promptly to a nice booth that had a window looking out onto their patio (can't wait for that during warmer weather!).   Then I spotted the buffet!!  The largest I've seen, and the most spotless!  I wouldn't say previous places were unsanitary or anything like that I just felt like the food temps were spot on at Bombay, and there was no food spilling out into other containers or on the buffet line etc.  I found Bombay Bistro to have the most variety on their buffet as well.  I was excited to try new items I've never had before and also that there was other choices besides vegetarian items and chicken cooked different ways, Bombay offered goat on their buffet which was another awesome selling point!  Fresh naan was brought to the table, the dessert was delicious and they had great sauces!  Overall I was VERY impressed and will GLADLY return!   Buffet price was $10.99 and I went on a Sunday.
Terrific date night out. Service was great and the drinks and pizza were on point. Highly recommend!

If you are looking for a work of art on your body this is the place. I see Lara and she is an amazing artist. She did both my forearms... love it! I also have a friend who sees Meghan and wow shes amazing as well. She has done two cover ups on her and they are phenom. There are 4 tattoo artist and it's by apt. They do all custom work, better then some Miami Ink. Call for a consult.... you will love it!

They also do art shows in the studio!

Very nice orchards. But it is rather small. The shop is small and the areas to pick are quite small compared to other orchards. However, it is still a nice orchard that allows you to pick fresh fruits and buy fresh vegetables. I like to support all orchards.

Came here on a Sunday morning and the bagel selection was a little low. My friend and I got 2 of the last sesame bagels, and I felt bad when the person behind us asked for one. The bagel sandwiches themselves were super tasty and filling; however, I would not recommend the iced coffee. My friend and I both ordered iced coffee and found it to be super watery. This new location has a good amount of seating with outlets scattered around, but be prepared to smell like bagel sandwiches for the rest of the day.

I recently had a working lunch at Rose Tattoo Cafe. The last time I had been there was about thirteen years ago with my college beau. At the time, Rose Tattoo felt romantic and the menu seemed exciting. On my recent visit, I was shocked by how little the place had changed, which made it feel and look extremely dated. 

The menu didn't provide many exciting options. I had a crab cake sandwich with fries that was fine but nothing memorable. Our server, while nice, was slow and had several mixups. Rose Tattoo was better as a memory.
I had the Pho Tai, and it was a bit bland. I enjoy pho with a bit more spice and flavor. The soup was pretty bland and didn't taste like much when I ate it as a whole with the noodles and meat.

I walked in yesterday and got a full set from Alex, who I believe is the owner.  The service was really bad.  The tips that he put on were way too narrow for my natural nail, which made them look gaudy and cheap.  He also did not fill the acrylic to base of the nail, making it look like my nails were already due for a refill.  I asked for square tips but he did rounded, which I didn't realize until he was a few nails in.  

What topped it off was that he was constantly yelling and snapping at customers that it was close to closing time and his employees wouldn't have time to do whatever they were requesting (closing time was still actually an hour away).  He told me that he absolutely would not be paying his employees overtime.  All around a bad experience being a customer having to sit and listen to it.  I was surprised because I had read many positive reviews from other websites but I won't be returning!
Clean, great employees and food was fabulous. Finally a fast food location east nashville/ Inglewood that is fast! Will be a regular customer of this location.

I love, love, love Carmelita's! I've been getting the bean and cheese burrito from here since I was a little girl and now at 30 it's still all I ever order. Chips and salsa both spicy and mild come complimentary. The chips are always fresh and salsa, I think it's homemade is always delicious. The house sangria is also a family favorite! We order it by the pitcher. We always have great wait staff and on occasion, especially at the Park location you can expect to be serenaded at dinner time by a wonderful trio! The chimichangas and quesadillas are also super yummy! Every time I visit St. Pete Carmelita's is a must on my itinerary!

Last night was my first time at the Broadway Bean. I went for a show, but arrived early so I decided to have a coffee. I didn't just have a coffee, though, I had the most awesome Irish coffee in the history of Irish coffees. I would drive all the way downtown just for another one of those. 

The show was great, too, and it's so nice to have a small venue for independent local theater. We had a great time - the place was packed. The staff handled the crowd easily. 

We loved the tons of selections in coffee and other drinks, and will definitely be back!

Didn't expect to rate them a 5 star but I sat here asking myself why they were not a 5 star restaurant; I could find no reason. Firstly, I only order take-out so as far as serving I'm not qualified to speak on that. However, I often call ahead to place a crunchy roll order and unlike picking up your local chain pizza who say 30 minutes upwards, these restauranteurs have the roll ready to pick up in only 10 minutes oftentimes 5 minutes from the time I hang up the phone. I show up, pick up my order, pay, then I'm gone leaving a tip on occasion. Shame on me I know. The staff is very friendly and smile at me. 

Food: The crunchy roll comes with 8 sushi measuring nearly 2 inches in height a piece. I am given a to-go container with the commonplace wasabi, ginger, chopsticks, and two packets of soy sauce. Very good!
We've eaten here a number of times fantastic food. Great service Yesterday I ordered takeout and the food was excellent- well prepared and exactly what I wanted!

I got the Classic Benny for takeout from here recently. The restaurant has a nice setup for the current situation: Spacing for pick up and the abundance of hand sanitizer made it feel quite safe. The food was really well packaged, with everything for the benedict in its own separate container and the box was sturdy enough when cutting the english muffin. In terms of food, the eggs benedict tasted quite good once everything was put together. One very unfortunate thing was that the yolk of one of the eggs broke in the container. Aside from that, the potatoes were really good with pepper and onion.
Adorable classic American spot with a good food. I look forward to going back the next time I'm through Nashville.

Sushi was fair but service was not the best.  This is one of those times when the owners should just stay in the kitchen and let the chefs do their thing.  The lady owner was so abrupt and loud. To start all of the sushi rolls on their menu have crab or shrimp or both so if you have a shellfish allergy don't ask for a modified roll because she will shout "we don't have it."  they have a role that has torched salmon on top but yet when we asked for torch salmon nigiri she said "so sorry we don't have it" now how difficult is it to torch salmon!  When we  asked the chef to substitute an item in the role he would look her way for approval.  The chef was great but the lady owner cost them a 4 star rating.

The staffs are amazing. I like how they go to each table and give out FRESH biscuit to each plate. The appetizers are heaven in your mouth! We got the grill oysters(5stars)&the blue crab pop stickers(a must get). Entrees we got the scalibut  ,just wonderful. The redfish is also just as wonder. Over all great food&the price is worth the meal! Most definitely will
Come back!!
I took my special needs son in to get a hair cut and they were AMAZING with him. I will definitely be going there from now on! Thank you to everyone at the Barber shop!

All the waitstaff are talented singers but we came to talk and visit with one another and were seated near the piano and under a booming speaker. Conversation was extremely limited. If I ever returned to this restaurant, I would request a table far from the music. My companions thought the food excellent but I had penne pesto which was supposed to be topped with roasted tomato and broccoli florets. There only a trace amount of either so it was mostly just pasta.
Fantastic experience! Food kicked ass as did the service. If you are at the Big Bend location ask for Brett, he will take good care of you. Cheers...

As a part- Mexican woman who has eaten an abundance of great Mexican food throughout my life, I approve of these tacos. They were absolutely delicious!

This place sets the standard of a good taco. I had the adobada tacos. The meat was crispy & juicy. These taco sauces were amazing. & the service was fast & the workers extremely kind. 

If you happen to be in the Santa Barbara area & actually want to see what a good taco actually taste like, this is a must. God bless!

Is the water organic, too!?
This place is over priced and a hipster nightmare. The food is mediocre. The cornmeal crust is a nice change but the rest leaves something to be desired. The mozzarella garlic was awesome. We only ate here because we had a gift card. Our total bill for a large pizza (which is a medium any where else), the bread and one margarita was $44 before tip. Not worth the atmosphere the slow service.  Go to Fitzs instead!

Why only two? Well, the coffee was fine. Yes there was some pretty latte art, but besides that it was nothing especially remarkable. The ambiance is neat - sort of a rustic meets industrial vibe. The place was packed; my friends and I managed to find some space at the long table, and those stools are definitely the antithesis of comfortable. The choice was style over comfort. And the parking situation? Really crappy. Oof.

Now, the real reason I'm not loving BB that much - the service. The lumberjack-esque man who served our coffees was fine, but the hipster d-bag we ordered from was a total turn-off. He wasn't friendly to begin with, and then when there was some joking over spelling my friend's name, he got sort of offended. Questions were also clearly not welcome. Guess you have to be a regular and know exactly what you want to be on this guy's good side. All I could do the entire time was think of Chris Hardwick's bit during the transaction, "Hipsters, please don't work in the service industry. You don't want to serve us, we don't want to be served by you." 

I was cursing the recommendation to hit this spot as a meeting point; I'm not sure I would ever go back without someone asking to meet me there.

I've been in East Nashville 15 years plus. This was my go to until I realized Mo was high all the time and prices were different.  I decided tonight after the tornado ect to give him business.  I chose Coors Light Seltzer 12 pack.  He says 22 bucks. I said no way he fights with me trying to justify the price.  I get in my car he motions me to come back in. I've known this shady fuck for a long time. He over charges every one.  Some one else should run that business. Not his fucked up ass.

This is the worst place that I have ever come in contact with Nubody Concepts Franklin. Customer service is terrible, there is no follow up provided after a procedure the only reason I gave it one star is because I had to in order to leave a comment. I should have know something was wrong because the never took my weight, measurements, body mass, nothing! 
After my procedure in 2012 I was  told to wait at least 4 to 6 months to see real results. 6months later I'm still working out but depressed in tears constantly because I see no results; not to mention over the loss of the 4200.00 I'm out of just for aqua lipo to the belly area only.  I finally decided to contact the doctor to complain. The Doctor presented very attentive and asked why I had not called him sooner because in his words "yes your still fat I should have used more laser". He agreed that I could have the procedure redone NO CHARGE. The fact he agreed to redo it is proof it was a bad job to begin with. 4weeks later the morning I took off work for my appointment I get at call at 730am(appointments at 8) The receptionist calls to tell me the Doctor is sick and had to go to the ER and not to come in. Well I was outside because I like to be early I see one client walk in so I go in and see another client already waiting. I walk up to the counter and was greeted by receptionist asking my name. When I told her my name two other staff came from the back she stated she could rescheduled me the doctor wasnt in.  I asked who i could speak with inregards to getting some money back. They told me no one was their I could speak with. She would have someone call me.  I am saddened  by the money lost(depressed by the sight of myself)by what I have experienced with this place and the way the procedure has changed the shape of my belly. I will finally file a report with the state medical review board. Please beware of this place. I know first hand this doctor decided not to follow through with my procedure a SECOND time because he took someone that was paying which is so greedy and unethical. This has bothered me  and stresses me out for the last 10months. It is my fault I'm so passive and did not research more places before going to Nubody Concepts Franklin,TN.
This review is based solely on my opinion of the way I was treated at this facility and should be recognized as only my opinion when considering whether or not to let an establishment with such in my opinion bad customer service and ethics do medical procedures on your body. I appreciate yelp for letting me voice my 1st amendment right in a forum such as this.

Menieos is a favorite spot for casual night out for wings and/or pizza.  My boyfriend and I absolutely LOVE their wings.  He also loves their pizza.  I personally haven't tried it.  We have had mixed results with the service we have received there, but overall it has been great.  The one really poor service experience we had was immediately addressed by management when we emailed them to let them know of our experience there.

If you love pizza and have taste buds than this place is for you! 
Grimaldi's specializes in pizza and you can taste it! The pizzas are all made to order with fresh ingredients that you can see and taste. The pizza is light and not at all the typical greasy mess, pizza usually is. DELICIOUS!
My favorite dishes to order are the Margarita pizza  and the lemon arugula salad. The lemon dressing is mouth watering and fresh. It complements the pizza so perfectly. 
There is a bar area, a patio, and a main dinning room. There are usually plenary of seats during the weekdays, however, it can be packed on the weekend. If you have a large party, I would recommend calling to set up a large table. 
If you are not interested in dinning in and would rather eat pizza in your pjs at home, then you are in luck. Grimaldi's makes To-Go orders so easy. You can call in or order online for pickup. You pay once you arrive and pick up your food. The hostesses always check your order, in front of you, to make sure everything is perfect. 
Next time you are close, stop in and grab a take out menu. There is a $5 coupon that you can apply to your next order.

The lack of knowledge displayed by two different Horizon Services techs was honestly shocking. We were having an issue with our AC not kicking on and called Horizon to diagnose the problem. The first tech who came out was nice but seemed totally tired and confused. He looked at the unit and got it to kick back on but didn't give a reason for the issue other than offering for horizon to do a separate tune up because "the unit was kicking our air that was 'too cold'". We paid the fee for him to come out and he left. Later that evening it shut off again and stopped working. We called horizon to come back out to try and fix the issue. The second tech was even more inept than the first. He jump started the unit again and then offered to get us a quote on a new AC system.  But was not able to diagnose a problem other than the unit was older. 

We called another local HVAC business that was recommended to us and they were able to diagnose a problem with the blower in about 5 minutes of LOOKING AT OUR UNIT!!!! It was nothing to do with the AC at all but the Horizon Service techs couldn't be bothered to even look. I don't know how they hire and train their staff but it's shocking the lack of knowledge or drive to fix things.  Incredibly disappointing service and a ridiculous lack of knowledge displayed by Horizon Services.  I will never trust them in my home again.
Great job! Came in with a cracked surface pro and was able to get it fixed right away.

I ordered a pie to bring for Easter dinner last year.  It was the Tennis Ave pie ( from website:  "chocolate peanut butter pretzel- chocolate pastry cream and creamy peanut butter mousse layered in a buttery graham crust. Topped with peanuts, PB candy and pretzels").  The pies have a reputation for being expensive but they are worth every penny.  Bring one of these to the party and you will be the hero, for sure.  It was truly amazing.  

It was probably the best dessert I've ever eaten.  Everyone was raving about it, we have family about a half hour away that now want to come to Ambler just to go to this shop.  When the dinner hostess was packing up left overs, people were asking if there was any pie left, and the hostess was jokingly trying to hide the pie in the fridge.

If memory serves  there was an optional charge (like maybe $1) for slicing the pie. This is because the crusts are so thick, it's probably a good idea to do so you don't need to struggle with it later.  I didn't get it pre-sliced do it b/c I didn't know how large the pieces would be and if there would be enough for everyone.  Next time I'll see if they can double cut it and pay the fee!

I seem to always be on a diet so I wouldn't typically go in and order pie for the heck of it, but I have taken friends visiting Ambler, and they have gotten slices of pie to go.  Just know they don't always have all pie options available.  You just sort of pick from what they have that day.  

They also have actually non-dessert food options as well.

First time to Palais De Jade and see why this is a neighborhood gem!

We were greeted warmly by the staff immediately upon entering which was really appreciated. You can tell the restaurant caters to their neighborhood crowd where the owner seems to always know your name. The restaurant is well appointed with booths - which I really liked adorned with white linen table cloths.

The menu is full of typical Chinese cuisine but the full dinner offering for $21.00 is a sensational deal and the one we went after!

The appetizer was a generous feast including a good sized spare rib, vegetable spring roll, cream cheese filled wonton and a chicken dumpling. For dinner the generous portions of sesame chicken and chicken chow mien was really flavorful and certainly enough to share or to get two meals out of one dinner!

Service was attentive and friendly too!
My favorite take-out breakfast place!  They cook bacon perfectly and their prices are great.  I'm always a fan.

Being from the West Coast, our choices for great barbecue are limited. That being said, I was disappointed to find out that there isn't much good barbecue here in Gallatin, Tennessee just outside of Nashville. Thanks to Yelp I discovered the BBQ place in White House, about a  30 minute drive from Gallatin, and worth the drive.  I was able to try their pulled pork and pulled brisket. It's nice that they serve them with 3 sauces  on the side so you can make your choice and actually taste the meat before you sauce it up. The potato salad and turnip greens were also excellent. Never having had Chess pie before I had to give it a shot, I'm definitely going to try to make it when I get back home in California. 
Thank you BBQ place! You made this West Coaster very happy!

This is the second time in a row that I've gone to rally house and had horrible service. The first time I went into mid rivers and every associate was on their phone and didn't acknowledge me or my husband the entire time we were in the store. I've worked retail for 9 years and every store I worked in first rule was to greet the customer when they came in. The second time went in they didn't have the product I was looking for. Okay no big deal, it's the Stanley cup finals. While I was in the mid rivers location I called the other st. Louis stores to check availability. Chesterfield answered I asked if they had a hat, she said no (rudely) then hung up. Next I called Fenton they were nice and said unfortunately they are sold out same with Brentwood. Finally I called Des Peres, first they hung up on me, then when I called back, I could hear someone answered so I said "hello?" And I get the response "yeah what's up?" I thought to myself did I dial the wrong number? Nope! I asked if this was rally house and she said "YUP" and when I asked if they had the hat i was looking for she replied with "NOPE! BYE!" Then hangs up. If you hate working retail that much then find another job!

Loved this place. Great atmosphere in a redeveloping part of town. Everyone was courteous and attentive. Outstanding service. I had the poutine which was cleverly prepared with jalapeños and a sweet/savory sauce loaded with deliciousness. The chopped salad looked very good as did all the items I saw around me. Only drawback to the poutine was it was a bit salty but other very very good. I will be back!

I was on my own and I was wearing shabby shorts so it was 'take out' to my room for me! I ordered simple soup and sandwich plus dessert which hit the spot perfectly. The food was nicely prepared and then packed to go so I could easily transport the packages. The roasted turkey is great, just be aware there are three slices of bread plus greasy French fries that come with it! And I ordered the apple strudel because after the Sound of Music I always order strudel when I see it on a menu. With real whipped creme on the side and ice cream when requested, I was pleased as can be!! The o ly thing is, there's enough for two dinners here! So either order less or bring a buddy! Please see the photos which always tell it like it is. Also, the servers in the bar were happy to get me a glass of very good wine as I waited, which I appreciated. Bon Appetit.

we have been going here for years and are consistently happy with the food and value. I always look forward to the left overs the next day - serving sizes are generous. They always cook the calamari perfectly and the pasta as requested. Sauce is tasty and servers are warm and efficient.

This is a cool little store with lots of unique clothes, jewelery and various "stuff," mostly being gag gifts, and things no one really needs, but are fun to look at and get for a unique or kitchy gift.  Everything has a hippie feel, from the merchandise, to the light smell of patchouli when you walk in the door.  This is not the type of store I shop in very often, but as I am frequently in Haddonfield, I make a point to stop in occasionally just to see what they have.  

They gain a star for being a foster home for Furrever Friends Rescue, a cat rescue.  They always have a cat who lives at the store until it is adopted. It is a ritual that our running group, who meets in Haddonfield on Sunday mornings, runs by Jamaican Me Crazy as we start out our run to see the latest cat, usually sunbathing in the store window.
Just great sandwiches. Need I say more. Yummy.

Very good food, but service quite lacking. Went in on a Sunday afternoon for lunch, hardly anyone there and just having the server come to the table to bring chips and water took more than 5 minutes. Another 5 or 10 minutes until our order was taken. They would not honor the Spanish Springs athletic coupon card saying that it didn't apply to the lunch specials, appetizers or desserts, even though the card says it can be used for "any meal" which we did purchase two meals, but were on the lunch special list.....Seemed sketchy. Finally, server took our check and never return it to the table, had to go up the register after 10 minutes.

If this place didn't overload with you with food for next to nothing, I probably wouldn't rate it so high...but this has got to be one of the best deals in St. Louis.  The unlimited pizza, salad and pasta buffet is always under $5.  I mean, it's not exactly stellar pizza.  It's really not that great of pasta or salad either.  It's exactly what you would expect to get for $5 when you are on an all you can eat binge in a strip mall in south county.  

There's about 20 different types of pizza at this place, so even though it's not that delicious everyone will be able to at least find a type of pizza they like.  Another drawback...it's always crawling with children.  I mean, this place is packed with families with young kids.  I can't say I blame families, it's expensive to feed a family and find a place that will make everyone happy.  I think that might just be the void that this place fills.

6/2/20.
Now that we aren't being denied medical care I figured I would schedule my routine mammogram. Jamel at the radiology desk was excellent as was my tech. I have no gripes about that.

I think it's ridiculous that I waited for thirty minutes to get checked in at the front desk. There were three people waiting in the lobby- one lady was being checked in. It took forever. There is zero sense of urgency. They are as slow as molasses. Terrible!  By the grace of God another person was helping register patients otherwise I would probably still be waiting. It made me more than 20 minutes late for my appointment. The registrant was having a sidebar conversation with a coworker and was distracted which added even more unnecessary wait time. The front desk wasn't friendly and security at the entrance was unfriendly too. The new thing is getting a temperature scan and signing in and telling the security desk why you are there.

If you're me, and lived in the tundra north until your 30's (and never had true bbq) this place is not too bad. My co workers who live in Saint Louis, Kansas City, and Texas were not fans at all. The meat was fatty and did not have much seasoning.  The bbq sauces were too sweet for my taste. I'm sure are better places to go for bbq. The mango habenaro cider was amazing!

Very tasty. The place isn't much to look at, but you aren't there for the decor, so who cares. I ordered take out, and I'm an Indian food novice, which is important because I barely know what I'm ordering and can't pronounce anything. The staff was very nice, and there seem to be quite a few people working. They offered suggestions, and were very understanding trying to explain things to me.
