I tried the Chicken Biryani for the first time, and it's probably just something with the dish but beware of bones if you've never had that before-I wasn't expecting them and it was a unpleasant surprise. It was very good, though, as were the vegetarian dishes I got. Very reasonably priced and large portions. Will definitely be back.

Amazing Brunch! So glad to find a brunch that goes until 3. All the great brunch favorite drinks and a great beer selection. The shredded pork biscuit benedict is a great idea, messy as hell, and delicious. A crunchy sturdy biscuit sopped up all the delicious hollandaise and bbq sauce, a real hangover cure (not that I needed one). We also ordered the bread pudding french toast - they were firing on all cylinders with delicious combos, because this was once again, impeccable, sweet, moist, everything you want from bread pudding and french toast. And, I can enjoy a nice beer with it. A good place if you're a fan of strong 'rich' flavors. Our server (Amy?) was really sweet. They ran out of creamer for our Irish Coffee, so she gave us a dollop of ice cream instead - seriously, love this place.

I have two favorite places for a getaway north of Los Angeles.  This is the first one.  It's located right of the freeway snugging the shoreline with a golf course adjacent to the resort.  This is the perfect place.  The swimming pool is filled with heated sea water, leisure stroll around the properties especially the hill climb that runs up overlooking the Pacific is release of stress in one mini-hike.  The spa is excellent.  The food is up to par.  The room is a bit small compare to many of the newly built luxury hotels but it's ample for a few day getaway that can melt away hedge stone on your shoulder.  Lovely place... it always remind me of the soap opera 'Santa Barbara' when I drive into its drive way.  Glamour and glitz.

The Owners are extremely friendly. The food is delicious! I suggest the hot Italian sausage and the chicken Parmesan sausage. The house sauce goes great with their French fries. The two beers I suggest are the Nelson IPA and the coffee stout!

This place is an awesome addition to downtown Indianapolis! Prices are great, kids are welcome and there's such great nostalgia going on with all of the pinball machines. The ONLY reason I'm not all in for 5 stars is the beer list. It could be better and I'm sure that it will get better over time. We'll definitely be heading there again soon!

Amazing!! Sat at the bar with franky G, kartrell, and mike and talk about amazing hospitality and amazing food, this is the place. I was there for hours and the staff made this the perfect experience. I will definitely return before I head back home.

We visited Brewster's most recently on a Friday evening around 7PM, after having eaten here many times before. I used to work in the area and would come here for lunch once a week. We've also had dinner here about once a month for the past two years. Oh, the good old days. 

This last visit was on a busy night, and there was a long wait for a table. Only a handful of people waiting for a table were ever acknowledged by the staff, I presume they were regulars, despite a large and growing group of people gathered by the door. 

After 35 minutes, we were finally seated. And then we continued to wait. Waiting was trending that night, if only we'd checked Twitter beforehand we would've known what we were in for. 

Several tables seated after us got a drink order in, while we waited for a server to even come by our table. After  25 minutes, a waitress finally came by our table. No apology for the wait, no acknowledgement of it even. She must've learned crisis response from Bill Cosby's PR team. 

Typically guests are given peanuts to snack on while they wait for their food. We're still waiting for ours. Good thing our food came fast... Oh, wait. We were finally able to put in our food order after another 15 minute wait. At this point, it was a grudge match between us and time. Time is a cruel prick. How long was it going to take to receive a hamburger? I felt like the waitress may have whispered, "May the odds be ever in your favor," after she said she put our order into the kitchen. 

At this point, we could've been served peanut butter crackers for dinner and we would've happily thrown twenties at our server out of sheer gratitude. Instead we got lukewarm burgers with half melted shredded cheese on top. Needless to say, worth the wait.

We actually stumbled onto Soy Teriyaki Bistro one evening and decided to give it a try.
WOW!!! Were we glad we did!!! My little 4 yr. old girl loves the chicken Yakitori to death. She could probably eat two orders if we let her. Her mom and I love the tacos! We usually get 3 each. One chicken, beef and shrimp. We get light sauce with them because we kinda have wimpy taste buds, but the full throttle sauce is great too! We'll get the special of the week sometimes which has never disappointed, but we always want to come back and have Chris's fantastic Dumplings. They are the best in Middle Tn. by far!! Glad Soy is here in Brentwood. Customer for life.... :)

I wish I'd read the other reviews before going. I took the advice of a native, but she hasn't lived here for years so I should have doublechecked. Arrived 10 minutes after my reservation (for one person) but the table wasn't ready. Then instead of seating me at a table next to another woman eating alone, they brought me to another brightly lit room next to a large group of men. And I had a  view of the swinging door into the kitchen where lots of mopping was going on. I wasn't approached by anyone for a solid 15 minutes. Twice I was asked if I was dining alone. I thought that was a no-no for all restaurants, but in this case, I had a reservation for one, so no need to ask! Then a host came and inquired if I'd been helped - when I said no, nothing, no water, no nothing - he sent someone right away. I ordered a drink and a meal. Two minutes later someone else came to take my drink order. I said I'd given it already. Three minutes later someone else came to take my food order. I said I'd given it already. Then someone who seemed a manager came to apologize for the delays and said that my drink was on the house. The food was lukewarm and too salty, the seafood rubbery. But the service was warm and friendly. And guess what, the drink was on the bill, not on the house. I wasn't going to insist so I left. What a disappointment and a wasted evening in New Orleans. You can be sure I will never be back.

So this place is good. It's a perfect date place, and the restaurant is hidden away in an older building with ivy creeping down the side. The inside ambiance is fantastic too, it's a smart casual place with walls covered with unique photographs and artwork and candlelit tables. I got the duck bolognese which was great! I would have given this place a 5, except for the fact that there were a few pieces of undercooked pasta. I could have let one slide, but there were more than a couple. Despite this, I'll definitely be back.

An overall nice place with great burgers, friendly staff, and a cool atmosphere. The only draw back is the high prices, and they are only a tad high.  Basically its center city prices in the suburbs.

Theres a wide variety of different types of burgers to try.  The Ghost Burger is great if you're into spice and some "heat".    

If you don't mind spending a couple extra dollars, you will enjoy this place.
Super fresh. Super fast. And super good prices. Friendly service and free discounts and birthday stuff if you join their club.
Excellent little BYOB.  Every dish at the table was superb, including desserts.  service is relaxed but thorough.  i will most definitely be back.

When you turn 30 you tell yourself nothing will change and age ain't nothin' but a number (Aaliyah). Then St. Patrick's day rolls around and while the city collectively gets hammered you find yourself alone sitting at your desk doing economics homework ignoring drunk texts from your friends. So when boo poked her head in the office and asked if I wanted to do brunch the next day I said absolutely, I used to go to brunch all the time when I was 29!!

After looking through the deck of city dining cards we zeroed in on Kennett and after taking to Yelp and reading the glowing review from my man Vinny P. I knew it was the one. 

We arrived around 11:45 and were welcomed by the bartender and a young man playing guitar, first impression, it looks like a mini Royal Tavern but brighter. Everyone must have been hungover because we were the only ones in there until around 12:10. It's cool though after reading the menu I figured if the food was half as good as it sounded we'd made the right decision.

Starting off we ordered some cocktails off their very imaginative  drink menu. Everything sounded amazing but I went with old faithful and got the Bloody, boo ordered some sort of French drink that came in a carafe and had champagne, St. Germaine, and soda water. Both were awesome.

Flipping the menu over and reading the options I knew Kennett was the one the Lord chose. All the beef was grass-fed, the eggs and chicken were free range and antibiotic free, and the pork was local and sustainably raised which means the pigs aren't fed corn which is uber uber importante. Whenever I'm out and I ask the waitress where the meat on the menu is sourced from and I feel a bit yuppyish so this was a major unexpected surprise.

Boo got the Italian breakfast which was crispy gnocchi (made with organic flour) topped with short ribs and eggs. That's right short ribs, eggs, and gnocchi. I opted for the English breakfast as it was a bit more protein heavy. It was grilled pork loin, butter beans, homemade blood sausage (amazing), and eggs which I added an additional one instead of the sourdough toast. Once the plates were served all conversation ended and nods of overwhelming satisfaction began. 

Taste-wise everything was out of this world but the importance of eating "clean food" can't be understated. Beyond the food the vibe of Kennett was very friendly and welcoming and I am looking forward to returning soon.

I was expecting a Longhorn type steakhouse but landed in a very dated, very loud, very big, very western Applebee's.  I'm not an Applebee's fan!  Decent salad bar is as far as I'd go.  Draft beer was not ice cold as it should be. Service was ok...no complaints...friendly. Definitely not my kind of place.

I LOVE the food but the young staff REALLY need training and maybe even some basic life skills because everytime I've been in there it's facepalm city. I usually order from doordash if I'm craving this cause it's truly not worth having to deal with the staff.

Nice local restaurant with tons of local beers that they serve in these unique chemistry glass type containers. I guess they are going with the old time pharmacy theme?  

The staff was friendly and helpful to make a beer selection, brining countless samples. The place was clean and parking was abundant. 

The food, well the food was not quite what I expected. If you look at the pictures here on Yelp you will see a very unique menu with oysters and bone marrow, etc. a foodies dream. They drastically changed their menu 4 months ago or so. Now it is more elevated bar food. Burgers, smoked meat platter, flatbreads. The food was tasty, but nothing special nor memorable.  The prices though were really cheap. 4 of us each ordered a dinner and we had about 12 beers among us and the total was around $120. Not bad at all. 

Would I go back?  Probably not. There wasn't much to draw me there. Like I said the food was tasty, and the prices were good, but nothing stood out to me. I'd likely hit the Rooster & Till or Mermaid Tavern for some off the Wall great food.

I have no idea why people think this place is so great. Service is ok, cleanliness is ok and the food is not very tasty. Greasy. Not impressed. Where is the good chinese food in this area. Best I know of is P.F. Changs in downtown Boise. If you want cheap, quick eats, this is the place.

Our first time staying here but not the last!  Very nice staff, room upgrade, reasonable price, nice linens, clean room, comfortable bed, nice breakfast, nice pool, free wifi, quiet, nice room.  I don't think you could ask for more, and the ocean is across the street!!!  Loving this mini vaycay!

I can't give it zero stars, so it gets one. The bartender with the very clearly artificially plumped lips basically threw my "medium" burger at me. Burger was over cooked, lettuce was wilted, and half my tomatoes rolled off my plate as it was tossed at me. I don't expect much from airport food and service but this place literally made me nauseas. Ew.

The service is lousy, our server was more intrested in playing on her phone than waiting on us. The food was terrible, our fish was under cooked and slimy, and my scallops were still pink, and when we told server she went to kitchen and came back and said " you can order something else because it was done and it will just come out like that again " Which leads me to believe that their cook needs to get another job or get retrained because I am a cook by trade and the food was not cooked.

Fad workouts come and go and so do chain health clubs. Iron Sport's been around since 1995 and it's stronger than ever. The owner made his bones as a bodybuilder, powerlifter, pro strongman and Highland Games athlete so he knows his stuff. No fluff, no fashion show. Whether you're training for high school field hockey or NFL football, this place is where you need to be.

I really was excited to order from the the pizza and portobello sandwich sounded great. Ordered at 6 it arrived 752 on a Saturday evening! Wasn't pleased the food was ok I was starving. Maybe I'll pick it up next time.

This is a great local restaurant with amazing food. It's located in a strip mall but has has a very cozy atmosphere. The Cajun shrimp were a great appetizer and all of the food was great. We had the shrimp tacos, grouper tacos, grouper Ruben, and grouper bites and finished with the homemade rum cake, all was amazing. The service was great and very helpful with any questions we had. I would recommend this restaurant to anyone.

The Front Porch has a very good ambiance to it. The drinks are cheap, but still pretty good. The food was average but the atmosphere makes up for it. We had a chicken sandwich and mushroom Swiss burger. The portion size was healthy however you cannot substitute sides. We will be visiting again soon!

I visited this establishment on a Friday evening.  My son and I walked in and they were able to fit both of us in.  He had a foot massage while I had the same plus a detox.  It was so peaceful.  I could actually feel my body relax while lying down waiting for my turn and listening to the music.  My massage therapist has magic hands.  She was excellent.  The detox was really interesting.  I really enjoyed my time there and have told so many people about it.  Prices are great too!  I'll definitely be back!

Having previously eaten at three other locations including two in Las Vegas and Downtown Disney, I can say that the Earl of Sandwich has not lost any of it's charm. I have been eagerly awaiting the opening of this location, and after finishing my excellent sandwich, I can assure you that I will be visiting again in the near future.
Fast food style BBQ , ,order at.counter and take food to table.  Good cheap food.  10 bucks got me brisket sandwich fries and coke.  Not best BBQ I ever had, but pretty good for a cheap eat.

While visiting Reno, I had a lot of extra time to kill. Seizing this opportunity to relax, I thought I would pamper myself. I yelped nail salons and a whole list popped up. I had called the first few but they all closed early, didn't have time, were busy with other clients, or needed to schedule appointments ahead of time. Scrolling through the lists, I found this one with already one review. Hmmm, it seemed like a good idea to try the salon out. I called and a friendly lady answered the phone. It was getting late in the day and thought that they might be closing soon and was just about to give up on my luck. To my surprise, they were willing to accommodate me. 

As far as customer service, it was one of the best I had experienced. I forget the name of the one who helped me (Sheri? Shirley?...Sorry!!!!) but she was very nice and helpful. She was very gentle with my nails and the manicure didn't hurt at all like some of the places I had been to.  She was a very pleasant person to talk to. Unlike other places I've been, she actually keeps her attention to you instead of constantly talking to other co-workers. 

Overall, I had a good impression of the place. The people were extremely nice and very helpful. I had came in later in the day and was still accommodated. If you need a quick mani/pedi, this is a good place to go for walk-ins (and they are probably the only salon who closes later). The lady who helped me stayed later and was the last one out the door. If you get the chance to go, I highly recommend her =)

City greens is my go to place when I come down to New Orleans for work for half the month. Yes it might seem  pricy, but it's fresh lettuce nothing old and gross and since when is salad synonymous with cheap, it shouldn't be. You want fresh stuff. Anyways, their salads and wraps will fill you up.  You can get any salad as a wrap and vice versa. So go nuts!

Thai wrap is excellent and quinoa and vegetable salad is filling. Check specials everyday on Facebook.

I would like so see a frequent buyer card as I eat there like 8 days straight. 

Tip for the wise:
When it's cold soup sells out fast so go early.
When crab is involved as the special it sells out fast so go early

And finally, be nice, the guys working there are great!

We drove all the way from New York to South Philly Barbacoa to try its food. It was an amazing experience, staff is friendly, the energy of the place is great. They have all fresh food, homemade tortillas are the best and coffee taste delicious. 

We're coming back!!

Was beyond disappointed by the service or shall we say lack of service. Told we needed to wait even though had two large tables open. When we sat at the bar, we were totally ignored. Will not visit this restaurant again.

Nashville classic, must go, blah blah blah.

Freaking awesome burgers, second-to-none milkshakes, etc. etc.

Original 80s Galaga arcade game, childhood memories and all that jazz.

JUST EAT HERE ALREADY, your stomach and taste buds will thank you.
The ceviche is nice.  Great chips to go with it.  The crispy chicken tacos are killer.  Home made shells (like the chips).  Friendly staff and cold horchata.

Place was ok. The music wasn't great. We had the crab cake appetizer looked absolutely delicious. Bit into it and it was a potato cake & crab cake sauce was a mayo and Tabasco with a little cilantro. Was disgusting and made me sick. People were nice and friendly and the staff very attentive .

First and foremost, the food is great!  We love their sushi and Thai and really everything we've tried has been delicious.
The ownership and staff are friendly.  My daughter loves the colorful sodas and after a few visits, the owner started to recognize us and snuck an extra soda for her in to our order.

And did I mention the food is great ??

Went here for dinner solo on a Tuesday evening around 6pm in may. Sat inside since the wait for outside was 40+ minutes which is to be expected with the beautiful view and weather outside. I specifically came here because I was told to try a grouper sandwich and was recommended this place. Tried the "she crab" soup which was hearty and creamy and flavorful, and a grouper sandwich which came in 3 sizes (I tried the med of the 3) with the norm side of chips. Very tasty. Only thing it needed was a lemon wedge for the Extra pizzazz but all in all I was not disappointed on my first try of grouper. Would I come here again? Yes. Would I bring the fam (husband, 10 yr old and 7 yr old) absolutely.

I want to love Dutch Bros but I just can't.
I can't complain about anything free for me, even if it's now restricted to a small that they didn't even have to give me apparently, because I don't get most apps so they can't glean ALL my personal info for maybe a free drink once a year.
& I choose not to make a deal out of being young & foolish & making inappropriate jokes (he'll learn over time & I don't feel the need to parent anyone else today),

but my daughter paid $3.75 for a small iced caramelizer that used to cost $3 & it was 3/4 ice today!
$3.75 for a few ounces of coffee drink.
No one asked if she wanted it iced or blended either, or if either of us wanted whipped cream, & it's partly her own fault for not advocating for herself because she did think of it, so hopefully it will help her speak up next time.

But such things are part of the reason why I don't  frequent overpriced coffee/drink stands of any brand, in general. Besides my caffeine oversensitivity lol. But there are plenty of low or no caffeine options & I still go rarely (once a year or maybe twice) because it's just not worth it.
I value living a basic life where I don't need or even want much besides God's agape love through Christ, prayerfully the unmerited love of others, and basic food & shelter & clothing.
I am abundantly blessed so I'm being gracious with 3 stars.
Drinks great, apps great, meal great.  Can't go wrong here. Thanks Isa for the amazing service!!

This place is owned by a man who knows barbeque.

We had the brisket and dry rub short ribs. Both were epic. I truly mean it - as good as it gets. Yes, it's true, they do run out of food. We ordered baby back, but they were already gone for the day. The thing is,weI don't feel slighted in the least.

The sides are pretty much what you've seen at every barbeque joint - fries, green beans, mac   'n cheese hushpuppies, etc. Nothing groundbreaking here, but great execution. There's something so perfect about a freshly cut and fried potato. And oh man, that hoecake.

Be sure to try all the sauces. They're all great, and the "best match" is always a matter of personal preference. Their spicy sauce is legit spicy.
Far below average in quality of foods in grocery and deli. Very old school. Service was slow and unsatisfactory, especially in the deli. Worth driving into Santa Barbara for a decent grocery store.

My hubby and I have been fans since Eddie opened this pizzeria in the 1990s. We have been loyal customers since his "grand opening". We moved to Chicago for ten years and would come visit friends and family in Tampa and bring home a pie on the plane ride back. The quality and taste have remained fairly consistent. I do remember it tasting better. The side crust doesn't seem as light and airy as we recall. It's still the best "NY style" pizza in the bay area. We'll still frequent it (more so now since we're back in the bay area). However, it's a bit sad it's not as good as it used to be.

Here is our video review:
https://youtu.be/u-dayc94ZWA

Trying out some donuts here in Reno, Nevada. A friend of mine from elementary school to high school. Galen Schmidt, very proud of his venture as an entrepreneur. We wanted a taste of some donuts and try them out.

I think the donuts are very funny and unique! The designs are clever. And plus donuts taste great. I use to own my own donut shop myself in Mexico City. I can tell the taste and the freshness.

Thanks again, we will definitely be coming back.
Excellent place especially for Cheese Steak Sandwich, but really huge selection of almost everything   And souvenirs in the second floor. Food is great and Mimmi is a great person

I came here for a relaxing day at the spa.  I'm new to the area and was looking for a nail spa to call home.  I wanted to give this place a 5 star as I have heard great things.  The decor was awesome and the atmosphere was pleasant. With that being said...I won't be back.  I had an appointment with Salina for a manicure with dip powder and the Olivia pedicure.  I was looking for Salina, but was  introduced to Kate instead.  I was confused because the appointment was scheduled with Salina.  

Don't get me wrong Kate was AWESOME!! She paid close attention to detail, asked me if I was comfortable more than once, provided a great massage, and did not make me feel rushed.  Kate definitely deserves 5 stars!!! 

Salina, however, made the experience not so great.  She rushed my nail service as if I didn't have an appointment with her, I asked ahead of time which colors were dip. I chose a color that was described to me as dip, but was later told it was chrome.  I asked to choose a different color instead, as I did not want chrome.  Salina told me she would put gel on top of my already clear dipped nails instead of proceeding with a pink dip powder.  I informed her that I asked for dip and was paying for Dip, so my expectation was Dip, but instead she continued on with gel and said this was the same as dip, but a different way.  I am in no way new to dip/gel polish and I'm aware of the difference.  I was highly upset by this time and couldn't even formulate a word to say to her.  She still expected me to pay dip price for a gel polish.  No... it is not too much more than a gel polish, but it's the principle.  I thought maybe it was routine to have both mani/pedi done at the same time at this shop, but the lady she rushed to after me received 5 star treatment.  She did not receive a rush job, was seated to get her mani and then seated to get her pedicure.  After my service was near complete.  Salina stopped by to tell me she would discount the gel overlay and charge for Dip instead.  By this time I'm fed up!!! You are not doing me a favor Salina.  I DID NOT ASK FOR A GEL OVERLAY!!!!! and STOP CALLING ME GIRL!!!!!

To top it off...the tall skinny guy was RUDE AF didn't say thank you, have a great day, or anything.

Company work party the food was delicious the venue was incredible all the stuff was really very very nice, the one downfall where is the drinks were expensive and very weak so I had to go next door to the saloon to buy my drinks

I called here the other day to see about having an alteration done on a formal gown. After searching locally for a formal gown the only one I found that I liked was too small. It was a size 4 and I am a size 6, not a huge stretch. It fit everywhere except the chest area so I wanted to have a corset placed in the back to give me the extra 2 inches I needed. The lady I spoke to on the phone was very rude and told me that it couldn't be done. She hadn't even seen the dress! I have a general knowledge of clothing construction because I do sew, I knew it could be done. Then when I explained that there was a seem in the back and asked if they could rip the seem and place the corset she said "go buy a bigger dress and we can take it in". Unbelievably rude! I explained that wasn't an option and she said that there was nothing they could do. Clearly they didn't want my business. I took it elsewhere and it is currently getting the corset back that I requested at another tailor that said it was "easily done". I am sure this place will be out of business soon enough turning away a hundred dollar job without even seeing the dress.

Fast friendly service and delicious flavorful food. It's a fun relaxed BYOB restaurant. I don't mind that it's cash only because I'm happy to give an independent restaurant my business without the fees, especially since they have an ATM inside. The food is interesting and the portions are huge. I'm a pescatarian and there was plenty for me to eat on the menu. I would recommend highly over some of the other less authentic restaurants in Fishtown and Northern Liberties.
Was a great find . We were looking for houses and wanted a place to eat lunch . Wow ! Amazing food and service . We will definitely go back for this gem

I wanted to love this place for their unique and delicious rolls and the fact that they were all you can eat. BUT our 2 servers were not the business! Our party also ordered bentos and they never came even after the rolls came and even after asking 20 minutes later. Supposedly she put the order in and after waiting another 15 minutes nothing came out, we summoned someone else who was NOT the friendliest by any means. We saw groups come in after us and leave before those bentos even hit the table.

To Whom it May Concern:
The purpose of this letter is to formally and publicly commend Tony Ray Baker for excellent service he provided to Brian and I.  Throughout our home search after moving from Michigan to Arizona, Tony has been a wealth of information, kindness, and true expertise during our home search.
I have never before written a letter like this on an entirely unsolicited basis.  However, in this case I was so impressed by the support and service provided by Mr. Baker and his team of realestate professionals that I felt compelled to go on record with our praise.  These people truly deserve it.  In an era where exceptional one-to-one customer service excellence has virtually disappeared, the work Mr. Tony Baker provided should be held as a shining example for others to emulate. 
In closing, I believe Tony and his team truly deserve to be congratulated and rewarded for providing exceptional service and support well beyond the expectations of myself and Brian.  We wish your company and Tony many blessings in business in the future.
Best Wishes,
Joseph L. Berry
Brian C. Hayes

I called Mitey because I got a flier from them in the mail. I asked the man who answered about pricing for a transmission flush (my dealership said I needed it at 30,000 miles). He asked how many miles my car had and for more info on the car. He looked it up and told me that I actually didn't need this service for a long time, and that the dealership was probably trying to get more $$. He told me to check the manufacturer's book but said according to them I didn't need this service until a lot later. He also gave me some great advice about what I would probably need to get done in the next year so I would be aware.'s 

This man could have told me to come in,  done the service and taken my $, as I didn't ask if I needed it, I just asked for a price. Instead he went out of his way to not only make sure I needed it but also was honest with me that I didn't. 

It is rare to find someone in this industry that would give up money in the interest of honesty and they have just gained a loyal customer, when I actually do need service on my car :)

I ended up going to have a new tire put on my car, it was a fast, and painless experience.  They have a really nice waiting area, flat screen tv's, and as many 12oz cans of Soda you can drink.. FREE.. I recomend going here to anyone who has had bad experiences with auto repair shops in the past.  They are really 5 star.

Indy Yogis, UNITE! 

Honestly, this event was amazing. I love mostly everything holistic, so this event was right up my alley. I love the variety of classes offered that lead up to the main event, so if you're looking to try something out, fret not, it's free and you should definitely give it a try! 

I loved the variety of vendors as well, so it was great to look around and learn about a few new businesses I had never heard of. 

A HUGE shout out and thank you to Invoke for organizing this all and making this such a successful, five-star event. Getting thousands of people downtown without a major flaw is a success in my book, and they went above and beyond. 

The best part of the event was the main vinyasa yoga. I loved being surrounded by like-minded yogis that are there to give it their all, sweat it out, and leave the stress on the bricks surrounding Monument Circle. I loved that they switched up instructors as well. There's nothing worse than going to a class and not connecting well with the instructor so you're stuck with them for an hour and aren't really feeling it. They all transitioned into each others' moves very well and it worked out nicely to get a variety of genders and voices. 

I can't wait for next year and hope to continue to see more and different vendors!

Great place to eat outside and enjoy live music. I had the shrimp po boy with fries that was very delicious! After lunch I finished with a White Russian blended frozen drink that was AMAZING! Definitely recommend coming here to eat if you visit Nola

My friend and I were in New Orleans for a few days and decided to rent a car to visit Alabama and Mississippi. Due to the flooding incidents in Baton Rogue all the major car rentals (Herz, Enterprise, etc...) were sold out so we ended up renting from A Priority One...and that was the best idea ever!

James and his family run the business and they are all super friendly. Their prices are pretty competitive and we even got a discount in the mileage.

I would definitely recommend to anyone specially if you feel like helping a local business.

Amazing food and great service.  My wife felt like her pork chop was the best pork chop she had ever had.   Lots of staff and constant attention to the simple things like keeping the water glasses full.  Excellent waitress.

We were traveling from NoCal to SoCal and needed a stop with good wifi. Found this place but were disappointed when we arrived. They closed early because of spring break, so we didn't get to sample anything yummy. Hours on yelp said it would be open til midnight but they closed at 2. Revised hours weren't posted on yelp :(

Beautiful Venue that hosts live Swing band every other Friday. Perfect for dates and birthday parties, and there is a beginning lesson too! The instructors are super nice, and everyone just has a great time, all for a measly $10! could not ask for more.

It can get a little hot though
They steaks are awesome. The service was excellent and the waiter was fun. The only reason they are not getting five stars was because the hostess was rather unpleasant
I Luv this place. Great brats & potato salad. Awesome cakes & pastries. & to top it off, a couple little hot waitress running around. Keep up the good work & I'll keep coming back!

Uhm. Not worth the wait in our opinion. Wait was  very very long but we expected that given the high reviews and recommendations. Food was nice. Looked more appealing than it tastes though. My pics as example. That looks great, right? We had 4 different Benedicts. The best were the salmon and pulled pork. Spent 2 hours here. Only 15 minutes of it eating. Wouldn't go back.
i gave the wendys on little rd @ hudson a 1 star, really bad, lousy burger; however, the one at us 19 @ 52 was a 4 star chicken sandwich and fries were pretty fresh.

I don't understand the 1 star reviews on here.  I live in the neighborhood and am in Ippolito's a few times a month.  I have never had anything less than fresh fish here.  I tend to buy from the market side and cook my own food.  That being said, I have had cooked dishes here that were not amazing, but never anything I would not eat again.  On the fresh side, I have never bought anything that wasn't first grade when I got it home.  If I'm out and walking the dog, even if I had not planned on food shopping, I stop in Ippolito's just to see what the special are and what's for sale.

Okay so I went to egg today, my first time visiting there.

 

I may go back, however, I was not impressed!


You can park on the side of the building on the street, which I don't recommend.  You may prefer to park in the side lot which is preferred.  However, there are designated spots for you to park at.


When you see the building from the outside it'll look like it's a warehouse, Egg does not look like a regular restaurant that one would be accustomed to.


It is a restaurant with another one connected to it, Spare No Rib.


It didn't appear to have a lot of dinning space, so it appears to me that on any given day, it could fill up fast.

 I believe it can accommodate a nice size group of up to 20 people.


I had the French toast with a topping of blackberries and mandarin fruit.  You can tell they use real fruit and it was not that can syrup fruit, tasted very healthy.


Egg has something for everyone, they have your meat protein's, pesceterian dish,  vegetarian dish and even vegans are included.


They do offer a full bar. There is at least one TV that I can see.


I just had the French toast and water.  It was good, not the best, but not the worst either.  I enjoyed it and if there would try it again.  You get a nice serving and real fruit however, at $12 it was a bit rich for my pockets to make this my go to breakfast place.


When I come back here I will have to try some of the other dishes.


They have so many more items I would like to try.


I will say my plate was chipped and restaurants shouldn't serve food on chipped plates, especially if they are trying to be upscale and the glass that they brought my water in was a dirty or fingerprints all over it that were not mines,it was a very unappealing glass of water, how do you mess that up?


Will go back to try more of the food they serve and may even try the one next to them as well.
This is one of my favorite places for a delicious sweet treat!! The mango is my absolute favorite, although I finally tried the blueberry cheesecake and thought it was pretty amazing!

Great Staff! I tried to return a prepaid PayPal Card here last night and Debbie John and Calil did all they could do to help me! Great Employees and Management. The other Rite Aid didn't want to go out on a limb for me. Warning do not purchase anything from PayPal involving a prepay type purchase, as you probably won't see your money again.

This place has really good seafood. They have three different levels of oysters (from the cheapest to the most expensive), I recommend trying all of them. The beef tartare comes in a toast and it's really good. We had to wait for the fish but it's worth it, it's really good. It's small but the taste is excellent. You need to reserve before you come but I was lucky enough to get a place there at 5.30 pm on a Friday. It gets more crowded at night. The server are very friendly and patient. This place is quite pricey and it's worth it. You can spend around $25-$30 per person here.

Their service department seems to be over worked as they are the only Nissan dealer on the east bank. I haven't been there for service in a while but it was a real pain to deal with them when I needed too. Based on the my experience with their parts and sales departments I would imagine that the service department still lags.

Their parts department people are borderline rude and while they did help find the parts and accessories that I needed they weren't very friendly. 

I was shopping for a car last December ('09) and test drove an SUV here. I told the sales guy that I was dealing with that I was just starting to shop and wanted to visit other manufacturers. He brought over another guy to help close the sale. This guy was horribly rude and informed me that my method of car shopping was ridiculous because I didn't narrow down certain makes and models before visiting the showroom. Needless to say I didn't choose Premier.

awesome margaritas! 
The plantains are mildly orgasmic! They are sweet plantains drizzled in sour cream with chopped green onions and finely shred mild cheese. beyond superb!

Oh and the portions are HUGE! I ordered the plantains as an app. and a small oder of vegan beef nachos and I wasn't able to even touch the nachos. The portions are that big. 
The only downside is that when they are busy the waitress goes MIA for a bit.
Other than that its amazing :)

Don't fill out a review drunk just bc you're sad they switched the softball game to something else that was already playing on another TV so that when you ask them to change it back, they put it on a different TV and by that time,you've missed the ending of the game. That's my words of wisdom
I'm new to the area and have been seaching for a place to get farm fresh produce, egg, and dairy. They have a great slection and nice people working there.

In my neighborhood and couldn't be more excited! I've been there twice, both on week nights, and they have been busy both times but I've never had an issue getting a table. Great food, drinks and atmosphere. One tiny note.. It can be a bit loud. Still love it here though!

We stopped here after just getting into town. Such a wonderful experience. Everything that my party had was delicious. We had the blue crab corn, the queso dip, bbq shrimp and green enchiladas. All were absolutely amazing. We each had a different margarita that were each on point. Bread pudding was amazing, ask for the dessert menu, you won't regret it! Service was friendly and accommodating.
Our very fave in Reno!!  Best food ever!! Always consistent and amazing. Best tacos enchiladas everything.GO!!

Always clean, always helpful, always cool stuff! REI appeals to me because the store is consistently well-organized and spotlessly clean. And I know that the employees will help me find what I'm looking for and will be knowledgeable about the products.

My favorite yoga studio - hands down ‍!  The owners are caring instructors that want to share the transformative practice of yoga with any and all that come through the doors. Move from thinking to feeling and focus on your breath, as you move from one position to the next.  Hot enough, but not too hot for a good sweat.

I'm astounded at the amazing reviews for this place.  You guys thing this is good Mexican?  The only reason I bumped it up to two stars is for the guacamole, which was excellent.  The salsa is mild, I ordered tacos and got taquitos and then was told to "eat it" because "I'll like it."  It was, however, close in proximity to where we were working (the convention center) and cheap - $5.50 a plate for the weekday lunch special.  

The next day I read in the paper that they had failed their cleanliness inspection two visits in a row and have a 10 day probation until they are shut down for health inspection violations.  That made my meal seem especially tasty in hindsight.

Next time I visit Tucson I'll be skipping El Minuto.

I get it.  Its the power lunch.  But is it awful I don't want to be powerful if it means I have to sit on my neighbors lap in this crazy packed restaurant where they are so interested in moving folks thru the doors that there is nowhere to breath or stand if you aren't sitting?  And if you are sitting, you could be paying $50 for lunch for two only to be unsure what the person across the table is talking about because its so loud?  
The food is really, really good.  I'm not talking 5 diamond, but better than Hillsboro fare and if you want to see someone 'of importance', you will surely be in luck.  I got a scallop salad, which was amazing, but we came when everyone and their mother did - 11:30 am - and our server was inattentive at best and having a spell of ADD at worst.  
I'd vote for a cheaper alternative or an off prime time booking to get your money's worth here.

Absolutely delish! As a chicken parmigiana lover, I tried the pork parmigiana and it was absolutely perfect! It came with fresh light salad and was a perfect compliment to the hearty pork !  My boyfriend got the Rigatoni and it was so good I keep stealing bites of the perfectly cooked sauced noodles!   The cocktails are so good and the staff is delightful!  GO! Start at bar for a lovely drink and ease into dinner.  The space is so inviting you will want to stay!!

Had a chill night, tipped the waiter and band trying to support this place I care about coming back. Leaving though, I passed through the Karaoke bar, nothing blocking it off, no signs or anything. Turned out it was closed and the way out was locked. Thats annoying and kind of lazy that they didn't block it off to save me the hassle, but no big deal. Before I could leave and find a different exit though two bouncers came in, and one immediately started screaming at me and cursing at me. That is insane! I know sometimes things get out of hand and the bouncers need to escalate to handle that, but this was 8:30pm, on a Sunday, after 3 drinks and saying "oh gotcha, lead the way". More cursing and threats continued as I followed them doing exactly what they requested/demanded. This is just pathetic. I am the one always telling my friends that Broadway is for locals too and trying to get them to come out. But this is disgusting, it doesn't just piss me off the way I was treated, it makes me ashamed of our city that this is how we treat people. Especially now when we need to revive things. That turned a quiet peaceful night of just appreciating that we're back and running, to a shit night that makes not interested in ever coming back and makes Luke Bryan himself seem worse. His concert was cancelled because of the pandemic, but after this kind of vibe do I even want to see his concert anymore? This is his place and this is how he's running it. I am so disappointed.

The neighborhood needed a decent pet store soooo badly and Baltimore Pet Shoppe fits the bill nicely. They have a great selection of dog and cat foods, from high-end boutique organic brands to high-quality but less expensive major brands. They also have a great selection of small pet supplies (guinea pigs, rabbits, hamsters, etc).  The toys and other accessories they carry are great too. All that in this small shop and the prices are better than most pet stores in the city.
And if it's possible that you can't find Fido's favorite food, they will gladly order it for you.
Absolutely delicious coffee, and a good place to sit down and get some work done. Would definitely come back in the warmer months for gelato!

Got a couple tattoos done by both Chris P and Tony and I couldnt be any happier.  These guys are professional, highly entertaining as well as very talented....Will def be back for more!!!! :-) Thanks guys!!

The food is good here with a nice menu..just make sure you eat at restaurant and Do Not do take out
 They never can get the order correct
Great food and awesome servers! I would recommend biscuits and gravy and the country fried chicken and eggs. Perfect hangover breakfast.

Called today to get pricing on FIOS in my location. The rep did not listen to a word I said, and kept asking the same questions. I explained to him I wasn't interested in package deals, just internet, as this was for work. He came back and started offering package deals. He then stated he could NOT tell me starting prices for 1GB internet, because he needed to pull credit first. I explained I wasn't about to have credit pulled to get a price on what their service would cost. He then claimed he would lose his job. 
So off to the internet I went. Found the cost for 1GB, but then decided to google yelp reviews and between my experience and the other reviews, not a snowball's chance I will be getting their service. Sad because years ago I had Verizon FIOS and it was great service.
Never received my food from DoorDash I didn't know who to contact about getting my order

Amazing place to live, the team is incredibly friendly and helpful with any request. The apartment managers plan fun events for the residents on a regular basis so it is very easy to get to know people if you're new to town like I was. The noise level is very quiet between apartment units and the free parking garage so close to midtown is definitely a plus. Service requests were completed the day of every time I asked. The place is beautiful and the rooftop pool and grill can't be beat. With all of the luxury apartments springing up around Nashville, look no further if you want the best. 

-
Jordan B.

Couldn't wait to return to Rodizio after avoiding eating out for so long.  Knowing the servers make continuous visits to each diner, who will be unmasked while eating, I decided to call and check their policy on staff wearing masks. 

I am speechless on finding out that not only are masks NOT required for the staff, but on the night I called, I was actually told that "no servers are wearing masks right now." I was also informed that the restaurant "will not disclose the vaccination status" of any staff. 

So beware: STAFF MAY NOT BE VACCINATED, NOR WILL THEY BE WEARING MASKS!!!! THESE SERVERS STOP AT EACH UNMASKED DINER AND THEN SERVE THEM. I could not be more disgusted upon hearing this and with the disrespect this venue shows to the public. 

PROCEED AT YOUR OWN RISK!!!!
First time there, amazing customer service and one of the best kale Caesar salads I've ever had.

1st off I waited for this restaurant to open because of the good reviews the original had. 
First thing I was raised in AZ. and have had Mexican and Spanish food all my life, and I also consider myself a good judge ON the different styles of Mexican food.  Our 1st visit was just plain horrible, I had a chili relleno with no cheese in the chili and my wife had carnitas
 which had no flavor at all, but I chalked this up to new store opening etc.
  Today my wife Scheduled a party of 20 gals for lunch, I say SCHEDULED because they waited 40min just to get their orders taken and then received cold food.
TO MANY GOOD MEXICAN FOOD RESTAURANTS IN THIS AREA TO WASTE YOUR TIME HERE
.

Finally a good pizza for our area. It can almost pass for a pizza from pizzeria Vetri. Great flavors, thin nice crust, and it doesn't hurt portions are great and it's priced well. Now, can this quality be sustained??

Dope. Dope.  Dope.  Dope.  Dope. 

In an old Ms. Winner's joint and converted to kinda tiki lounge. 

Prices are good and atmosphere is great.

Not large selection of offering so be prepared to like beer, bourbon or mezcal.

I love this place!!!! It's so cute inside and I love the ambience of the restaurant. You have to try the taco platter!!' It was sooooooo delicious. We also got macho nachos, which was also delicious. The food here always has a clean, fresh taste, which is why we love coming here.

Came for breakfast. I had the Parisian eggs Benedict and it was AMAZING. I usually don't like eggs Benedict but this was soooo good. My boyfriend had the French breakfast and he loved it. I would have given them a 5 but they didn't let him substitute fruit for the potatoes, which was annoying.

Yay! I'm a fan. -as my 4 star rating shows. We eat here often. Really often. I enjoy the food and it's one restaurant the kids will agree on. So, we find ourselves here OFTEN (have I said OFTEN enough yet?), all times of the day/week. I personally enjoy stopping in for lunch during the week. Never a wait and the servers always seem on top of their game. Evenings and weekends the service is always a bit off, but that's probably due to the increased number of patrons. Anyway, our dinner last night was a train wreck from start to finish. I do not like to get the manager involved but unfortunately I had no choice but to ask someone, anyone working to get the manager. The manager's name was Kelsey and she seemed to be rather new at the manager role. However she was very friendly and eager to correct the issue. She comped our entire check, which was WAY unnecessary. I insisted several times that I only wanted the wrong charges corrected on my bill and that I will pay for our food... but she insisted that it was on the house. It was way above expectations. She said she was doing it so we continued to frequent the restaurant but I assured her, we would be back, comped meal or not.

We ordered some of their Oven Baked specials and found both dishes to be very tasty. The bread was amazing, as usual and the soup was just okay. They must have changed recipes over the last year or so because it just tastes different. Our server was new and we were all being patient, until she just disappeared and our bill was completely wrong. After being unable to locate her for some time, we finally asked for the manager's assistance. Thanks again Kelsey. As I said last night, we'll be back.

The guys in my family love this place.  I should just get the salad bar, because I always over eat and the salad bar has a TON of choices.  Good service, but it's been crowded both times we've visited.  Reservations recommended so you don't have to wait.  The free dinner (with purchase of another) for your birthday is awesome.

This place has THE best seafood & chicken...and, that's sayin aLOT bein in NOLA! To add to the pleasure of doing business here, the prices are SUPER reasonable, the wings are WHOLE wings, & the staff treat you with kindness & respect! You KNO your business is appreciated here! Try it! I GA-RAN-TEE you'll like it, & stomp your foot if u miss their closing time, like I do!

My 4 year old son loves going to 'Lil Ninja class at Gracie Tampa West.  His instructor, Tony, is so patient and good with the kids.  My son learns something new at every class but also works on the fundamentals as well.  It keeps him interested! 

I'll just say:
If you want an air conditioned, fancy, everyone gets a black belt type of place-go somewhere else.  This is a real MMA gym, with real fighters, putting in real effort to earn real respect in their sport.  They know what they are doing and I cannot wait to watch my son flourish amongst them.

Worn, tired place. Dumpy, trashy. Two star or less, If you want a budget hotel fine, but this one is not for me.  But staff is gracious, friendly and helpful. Would not stay here again. Sad, because several years ago I stayed and thought it was good but time changes things.

Amazing! The building is unique and fun. The staff was knowledgable and fun. Ribeye was perfect! Grouper slightly overcooked but the flavors were delightful. Spaghetti was delicious. What needs some help... Kale Caesar had way too much cheese and lacked flavor. King crab leg appetizer was good but not worth it.
Beautiful and original arrangement at a reasonable price. My wife loved it! Highly recommend.

As far as texture goes these cookies are rocking. They are soft, and chewy just like a good cookie should be. Where they are lacking, however, is flavor. They have a very mild taste to them. I was expecting so much more, but I was let down. I tried chocolate chip, white chocolate almond, and a bite of my husbands oatmeal. I will say the oatmeal had a strong flavor of cinnamon, but it still wasn't up to par in taste. My husband felt the same way. Personally, I think Christie Cookie has them beat. I would still recommend you try them out because it wasn't as if they weren't edible. I'd eat them again, I just would not purchase them. Tollhouse cookies had a better flavor in my opinion.

I'm not happy with the used car sold to me. At the time it was sold to me in 2012 there was a known defect with the transmission. Now five years later I'm stuck with a car I can't drive and still have to make payments on.

3/30/21 Just received plants ordered 2/10/21. Zone 10
Condition: DOA! I'll try CPR and place them in ICU, but don't hold out much hope for survival.

It's bad enough that they claim they would ship March 8 or so, actually shipped 3/25/21 but according to UPS tracking information, plants sat at UPS facility in Ontario CA since 3/26. I'm not sure if this is the fault of UPS or the fact that Burpee shipped 3 Day Select, but a problem just the same.

When you Pay $10 a plant (including shipping & tax) you would expect to receive something better than you would find in the trash at a Doller Store. 

I was really looking forward to getting some special plants for my garden, very disappointed

I came here this past weekend while on a visit to Philadelphia and I must say what a great brunch spot to find! The food was good the service was great and we didn't have to wait too long. I'll stop in again when I'm in the area.

Everyone believes his or her regular mechanic is the best. 
But Lang Motors really is the best in SB. 
In business 40 years, though rents have forced the husband/wife team to lower rent areas, you always know you're getting the truth about your car. 
Today I went in for new low and high beams. I also got an oil change (synthetic only for the 2007 Saab). 
I walked to Los Agaves for lunch and then cruised up for a car wash to the only SB wash I go. 
Due entirely to the excellent work of the Milpas Community Association, Milpas is a pleasant though not affluent place to get your nails done by the terrific people at C and J, grab the best steak tostada in SB at Los Agaves, and then get a fast, high-quality wash. (Montecito Vons receipts have coupons.) 

I will weep when Lang Motors is no more (because the wonderful couple retires after decades of providing spectacular service to Santa Barbarans).

Amazing place to try almost any scotch, bourbon, vodka or rum you could imagine. Very pricey but worth the visit. If you don't like cigars you obviously shouldn't visit. But if you like (or don't mind) cigars, it's definitely a great place in a great location. Be sure to dress nice or you run the risk of not getting in.  I would also recommend visiting Holts on the first floor if you want to shop for a good, well priced cigar before going upstairs.

Carmen Fichera, the locals sales Rep. (Tucson)  spent nearly 3 hours doing in-depth estimates to add more tile (or re-carpet some) floors in our home in Oro Valley.  He spent a lot of extra time advising of what our choices are and how much they will cost.  Don't know if we will use Express, quite yet but his input was much appreciated!
A genuinely nice Guy (and a VET too).

This was by far one of the most pricey restaurants in Doylestown.  It's a tapas restaurant and the food is delicious. The portions are ridiculously small and extremely pricey. One beef rib was $26. Granted it came with a handful of potato's.   The waitstaff was very efficient and pleasant. I had the mushroom quesadilla that was delicious.  It was a good portion for a small eater but not enough food for a normal person. 

The ambiance was wonderful. It's a very pretty restaurant. 

Sorry honey, I don't think the portion size equals the price

This place has been around for decades. You can tell that the quality of service in this place has never wavered throughout its entirety. Staff is very helpful and humble. Food is phenomenal. Some of the best cheesesteaks in the Philly area. Family-owned and operated. Very homey feel and customers are all great people. Come to this place if you're looking for the best.

If I could give them zero stars I would... Bear Services came to inspect my HVAC system as I reported it wasn't working. A technician came and spent no more than 5-8 minutes in my basement "looking" at the issue. Apparently he wrote in the paperwork that there were paper towels in the blower but never told me this was the issue. He explained my unit worked fine and there was nothing he could do. Fast forward--my husband smelled a burning smell the other day and found a wad of 15-20 paper towels shoved inside the blower motor. They were beginning to char/burn. Thank goodness he was home or this would have started a fire in my house. The technician from Bear Service was incredibly reckless and didn't take the time to ensure my system was safe. Upon contacting the owner he explained there was nothing he could do. This company showed me they truly do NOT care about the safety of the customer. When I spoke to the owner on the phone he told me they have had complaints to the BBB in the past and they couldn't help every customer that complained about something. What an awful thing to tell a paying customer. I honestly would Youtube and DIY my issues before EVER contacting Bear Services again.

I am a dark skinned African American woman with course hair. I have looked every where for a laser treatment specifically for darker skinned women. My main concern was scarring. So many salons told me that I could not be treated. However, I now know they simply did not have the proper equipment.

The technicians at Bare Skin are great. They are well educated and are always able to answer any question I have. The results are great. All of my ingrown hairs have been eliminated. I started with a Brazilian and I saw results immediately. I have since moved on to my underarms and stomach.

I was under the impression that laser was extremely painful, however, I find it less painful than being waxed. I would highly recommend Bare Skin Laser for all women of color. I have no scarring and the results continue to improve after each session

Lousy.  Have been passing this place for months, finally gave it a shot.  A completely mediocre brunch.  Worse than most diners I've been in.  Potatoes cold. Pancakes dense and cakey. Crappy maple flavored table syrup.  Greasy fatty bacon as thin as paper. Everything was pretty much the quality you'd get at a crummy hotel breakfast.

So I began to wonder why the food was so bad.  Then I realized there were as many wait staff present as patrons.  And that perhaps the reason the owner isn't investing in better food is because he or she is paying a large staff to stand around.

Whatever the reason, I won't be back.  Not alone.  Not with friends.  Not ever.

Wow. Unbelievably slow service.  Waitress took 20 min. to take our order and that's only after she stopped and asked if we needed anything. We had to remind her we didn't order.  She forgot our water and we have to flag down another server to get utensils.  The food took about 50 minuets and it wasn't that crowded inside.  The fish was blah and didn't even finish it. The turkey croissant was ok.  We asked for our bill when the food came knowing it would take her forever to come back. Paid in cash because we were not comfortable with her being gone for so long each time.

Went to Amy's today because I've been wanting to try this place for some time now. The donuts were pretty tasty! I enjoyed this glazed donut with strawberries. The donuts were fresh and soft. I do think that the prices are a little high but in the end I enjoyed the donuts!!  Customer service was not all that!

This joint was an oasis!  The place was so clean, which for me is most for a nail salon. Clayton Nail Spa did my nails for my wedding. I was impressed by how my nails turned out for my wedding. I showed an image of a natural ombre dip nail style and they hit it out of the park. My nails were the top of conversation at my wedding. I live in 30 minutes and I make sure that I make an appointment with Clayton Nail Spa! These ladies know the art of nails and they are all so wonderful! Next time I am getting my nails and pedicure  done.

Delicious lazy breakfast here on a steaming hot late Sunday morning. Tasty feta & spinach omelette with really good, hot, crisp home fries, a lemon iced scone to die for, and good iced tea. A croissant the size of your face. We walked away with a strawberry & peach pie that was bulging with ripe fruit and weighed about a ton, whose crust was made of angels' wings. The kitchen is on view, and a city of industry. The women who work here put on a terrific service and work very hard, aprons bustling everywhere. Very glad we happened to stop here, we will be ordering more of those pies.

Our visit was to the Buerger Center for Advanced Pediatric Care, to see a specialist, so my review is for our experience in this facility, specifically. My son was not inpatient, nor emergency, nor surgery, etc., so our experience is office visit only, not hospital departments.

Luckily, this was not a grievous visit for us. It afforded me the luxury of taking so many photos of this beautiful zillion dollar hospital health complex, & writing about it! (actually $1.5B)

CHoP downtown is so big, comprising so many buildings & specialty centers, that it seems inappropriate to have only one Yelp listing (aside from their separate cafeteria listing). Then again, with only 57 reviews on this one, it seems that the topic of your child's healthcare is a relatively private matter, not a first choice to share on Yelp.

At Buerger, security is a serious matter, without being oppressive. In the lobby, reception gives various ID check questions. They provide each adult a temporary badge, to be worn while visiting. This high-tech badge later turns red, & your name is replaced with "VISITOR" when it expires (see my badge photo, taken the following day). Pretty fancy, & darn secure!

From the entry level, you head to your floor for yet another check-in, this time to confirm all health details, insurance, etc. The metallic sparkle check-in counter was another fascinating touch. There are EIGHT elevators, each with all-glass shafts, reminded me of "Charlie & the Choco Factory." In the elevator, a different child's voice announces each floor. Very cute. In the parking garage, they color code the floors, & pair each level with clip art of a kid's ride-on toy (we parked in the blue tricycle level).

The waiting rooms are large & colorful, with tons of windows, & spectacular views of the city. There is a kids' handprint design on the lower panels of the windows, which casts shadows of the same on the floor nearby. (Knowing it would inevitably be touched by little hands, they surely added this to hide smudges.)

When I encountered one over-the-top kid-friendly detail after another, at first I felt like it was all material, and just intended to distract people on a shallow level, to make the image of a haven for kids. But the staff dynamic felt earnest to me, and made me meditate a little on the word hospitable.

Obviously, your doctoring experience will depend on who you see, and how the medical particulars manifest. Our visit went smoothly, and our follow-up will be more convenient at the KoP location nearer our home. I do appreciate having visited the downtown location, though. I feel at ease in this place both due to the impressive investment in the facility itself, and also the apparent mission of quality care, that comes through in the attitudes of the fixed staffers throughout the center. It certainly gave me confidence to return for other needs if they arise. 

The dual check-in system is organized & calm. It made me feel safer in a place where anxiety and unknowns are omnipresent. Everyone was positive, smiling at my son, welcoming us on our first experience at the downtown CHoP, hoping to be useful, etc. This attitude filtered through the other patient families there, and people were on their best behavior, acting totally different than they might a few blocks away on the street. It was an upbeat, friendly experience, which takes energy, & a choice to give that to people, & this is appreciated. It is clear to see administration has invested in human nurture, which is hoped for in any setting, but especially in a hospital, and especially for kids. 

-----------------
Grammar Note: No one follows this, including CHoP themselves, but any grammar book or manual of style will tell you that when creating an initialism, you don't capitalize adjectives such as of! Most acronyms drop adjectives out alltogether, so they don't end up in caps there either. It should be CHoP (little o), like RHoNY or KoP. Little o, okay? Drives me nuts!

*My husband told me this review isn't complete until we get the bill... so if CHoP's billing is anywhere near as bad as Main Line Health's billing, I may be posting a necessary update.

This review is for Shop Rite of Marlton.  I have been shopping in this store for over thirty years.  The store has many products, which is a good thing, but what it also has is rude employees!  I do not think the management trains people properly or they don't make it clear at hiring what is unacceptable behavior, or maybe they just don't care.  I've had a cashier comment with a derogatory term when I had a very full cart of food and asked how many people it was for.  I said the owner should be glad I'm such a good customer.  When I complained, another employee said she likes to joke around.  I asked if she was defending the cashier's boorish behavior.  She then passed me on to an assistant manager who said she had high blood pressure from her job and offered me five dollars in credit.  Another time, as I was bending down to find a product shelved on the lowest shelf, an employee came over and said why don't you just throw them on the floor.  I have no idea what that meant.  Another time, when an employee couldn't find a product I asked for, I heard her call me a nasty name when I walked back to my cart.  Then, when I asked her name, she hid her name tag with her sweater.  The manager said he would speak to her, and I hope he did.  My daughter has stopped shopping here because of the rudeness.  It's not enough to have a lot of products.  The store (management) needs to make sure all their employees are polite.  They need to take a lesson from Trader Joe's or Chick Fil A.   One other thing, many times when I need a product on the top shelf and there aren't many left, I have to find some one to help me or find an employee.  They should take a page from Trader Joe's book and have an employee check for this and move the products forward.
Great and convenient gas station if you are driving down the 101 and forgot to get gas and you are below E! Very clean and not too busy.

This is a decent work lunch spot. Quick and attentive service. lunch specials with soup/salad, plus sushi or ramen. Sushi was decent and filling! Ramen was surprisingly satisfying. My group really liked the veggie ramen esp the spinach noodles. Also tried the shoyu and tonkatsu ramens. I personally preferred the shoyu for it's flavor but tonkatsu was also good.

Wow, I followed all the other yelp reviews looking for some delicious authentic Mexican food in Philly and they did guide me wrong! Mole Poblano is awesome! My wife got a delicious trio of tacos with different proteins and I got a skirt steak special that was topped with a jalapeno salad and served with warm tortillas, beans, and chips. So delicious! And the complimentary chips to start and rice pudding to finish were perfect. I would absolutely come back here.

The batter on my fish in my fish and chips was tough, the fish was dry and chewy -- altogether unappetizing and barely edible. (I ate only 1/4 of it.) The cole slaw was nice and attractively served in a red cabbage leaf.
DELICIOUS! I would recommend this place for anyone who is having a crave for quality Chinese food. The service was also great!

Sola was a fine dining experience but I personally would not provide this establishment with a five star rating for the following: a very pricey bill disproportionate to my satiety. I was still starving following our New Year's Eve meal. The portion sizes were underwhelming considering the price tag. 

All in all, the food and service was above average. I simply cannot justify the price for such a skimpy meal, though.

Love this place. I live in St Pete but make the drive over for happy hour and sporting events whenever I can. My friends who live in Tampa are there all the time. The food and drinks are top notch and the servers are fun. The ones I can remember are Janelle, Amanda, and Caisey. 

I'll be back soon.

Went there for Customer Appreciation Day, first time goer too. I was very surprised at how well the place looked as a grocery store. Very neat and simple, yet distinct and retro.  

The place felt very welcoming. The entire family was there doing their part, so cheerful! 

My wife and I also tried their new restaurant. I had the breaded chicken with potatoes, sauerkraut, and leek salad. She had the potato pancakes. I gotta tell ya, that was the best chicken cutlet I ever had, so juicy and crunchy. And the potato pancakes was amazing with their homemade sour cream. So simple but delicious.  

The grocery store had a lot of very interesting foods, so we bought'em! Their sour dough bread was perfect. Krakus were giving out samples of their kielbasa. It was so delicious I ended up buying 2 pounds worth! 

When you decide to go, don't forget to try their killer filled donuts. Krispy Kreme has nothing on them. 

Everything is so fresh it's crazy!

The worst service have had in along time. So slow. Started off watching them wipe crumbs off table onto booth seats then saying sit here in the crumbs. Ask if I was ready to place order then said be back needed to get order pad. Came back 15 minutes later. Brought tea with no sweetener so had to wait for that. Then brought meal with no utensils. Had to track down someone to get those then wait 10 minutes to get them. Meanwhile 8 of the servers are standing by drink machine by bar talking and laughing. 

If I ever go back it will be for the games only. Food is not fresh or very good plus you have to pay at least $10 for parking.

Grants Farm is located on 281 acres of the original piece of land gifted to Ulysses S. Grant by his father-in-law Frederick Dent. It is now owned by the Busch family, who owned the Anheuser-Busch Brewing Company for many years until it was sold to InBev in 2008.  
At the turn of the 20th century, August Busch, Sr. bought part of the original plot of land and used the property as a summer/weekend retreat. The "Big House," where the Busch family lived for many years, was completed in 1912.
In 1907, "Hardscrabble," a 4-room log cabin Grant built, was moved to the property. It originally was located a mile northeast of Grant's Farm. "Hardscrabble" is the only remaining structure that was hand-built by a U.S. president prior to assuming office.The cabin was displayed at the 1904 World's Fair. August Busch, Sr. bought it and moved it to what's now known as "Grant's Farm."
Dwight is the man. He took great care of me when I stopped in at the last minute. I will always come back.

You know it's good pho when you see a restaurant completely packed with customers waiting to get a table. Customer service is A+, food is A+, broth tasted authentic and flavorful and the whole restaurant was filled with the deep pho broth aroma. Soup noodles were filled with fresh herbs. Summer rolls were also very fresh and they nailed it with the peanut sauce.
The price is a bit higher here than the restaurants back in NYC where there is a lot more competition restaurants but still very reasonable prices. Definitely coming back

I came here to get my Samsung Galaxy S5's outer glass screen replaced. Before I got there, I had inquired about the price match guarantee and was told they could match the price of a competitor in Apollo. They reluctantly agreed to do the repair when I got there. I was quoted as it taking 4-6 hours to fix, which I didn't mind because I know screen repairs for this model are a bit more time consuming than just replacing, let's say, a iPad screen.(which is cake) I came back 7 HOURS later, and they told me that they had broken the LCD and needed to order a new one which would take about a day. So instead of calling me, they just had me come up at my estimated time so they could tell me of their failure. In return, since I'm a pretty easy going guy, I told them to keep it. They gave me a phone to use while I waited, I supposedly just needed to activate it. When I called my carrier to do so, they said the phone's ID code didn't exist anymore and it was likely stolen. I came back the following AFTERNOON and the repairs were still not done. I patiently waited 3 days for my phone to be repaired, and when I finally got it back, the screen didn't register my touch as smoothly, and the LED lights on my display no longer worked, and the screen protector had been taken but not replaced. I took it back to tell them and they acted like I was stupid enough to have not checked my settings. After that mild insult, they took my phone again. A few hours later I was told I would need to come back to get my phone when they had reassembled it and bring it back in 3-5 days to get it fixed. I couldn't do it because of some personal issues, but now I'm left with a water resistant phone than can't touch liquid, no leds, and a shitty touch sensor.

Fat sandwiches do not compare to the grease trucks.  Most of them seemed to be the same sandwich with different sauces.  I was hoping that this place would have the equivalent of a fat moon, but no such luck. The sandwiches might not have been so bd if my expectations weren't so high, but if you are looking for the disgusting goodness that comes from New Brunswick's grease trucks, you'll be disappointed.

Purple Heart Park has been expanding since we moved here 14 years ago.  There are lots place for walking, walking your dog and plenty of fields for all kinds of sports.  They even have a skate park for those that enjoy that.  Plenty of picnic areas and a couple of Ramadas.

Worth the wait! In line about 25 minutes - took the opportunity to chat with other guests. I had the red beans & rice. Very well flavored without being spicy-hot. Husband had gumbo - and as a Louisiana native, he really knows what he likes. Said this one stood up as one of the best he's ever had! Treated ourselves to bread pudding - so warm, rich and sweet. Try it!
i think the same dopes that operate the arena run this airport. talk about missed potential. can the trenton area PLEASE get it's act together?!!!!!!!!!
Real great authentic Mexican food! If you are looking for that local spot to get what you want and have it hand made, this is it!!! SO GOOD!!!!

WWOZ has the most diverse playlist of any station anywhere; and they serve as inspiration to other stations to strive for non-conformity.

When I was a little girl, my Dad's friend (a construction worker) had a radio show on WWOZ - back when it was on the 2nd floor of Tipitina's.  His show was all about 50s NO R&B; they're HS years.  The Duka Paduca was barely understandable to non-New Orleanians his yat accent was so thick.  It would have been funny if there had been streaming radio back then.

I ordered the mushroom cheesesteak and added onions and peppers! It was delicious! Bread was soft and filling had a great taste. Also ordered a small potato salad which was okay. It had a strong egg flavor. Definitely worth checking out the cheesesteaks if you haven't yet!

My 20-yo stepdaughter was taking a trip to Philly from Virginia and the car broke down on the way. We live in Virginia so the car has Virginia plates. We also only had AAA local towing so we had to have the car towed to a garage within in 5 miles of the breakdown. AAA recommended Bob Wark's Liberty. As one could imagine, this was not an ideal scenario and all I could think was that any garage will see this out-of-state car, realize the desperate situation we are in - that the car had to be fixed in order to get it back to Virginia - and see dollar signs. I could not physically be there to asses the car and we couldn't have it towed anywhere else because it would have been too expensive. To add one more layer, the car broke down late on a Saturday afternoon so the car could not be looked at until Monday. I spent many hours that weekend stressing out about what was going to happen when I called the garage Monday.

None of those fears materialized as Bob Wark's Liberty were absolute car angels. That is the only fitting analogy that comes to mind. When I called from Virginia to ask about the car, they explained that the diagnostics returned two possible issues - a failed sensor (a repair cost of $~300) or the timing belt (a repair cost of ~$1000). They asked if I had ever changed the timing belt (the car is a 2004 and has 180K miles). When I said that I thought I did but wasn't 100% sure, they did not push replacing the timing belt any further and recommended fixing the sensor first. I authorized the replacement of the sensor.
