They then called me back and offered to check the condition of the timing belt at no extra cost (since I wasn't 100% sure) because they had realized that they had to access that part of the car to fix the sensor anyway. That efficiency saved me $100 in labor. 

I cannot express my gratitude enough to Bob Wark's Liberty. We were in a vulnerable position and they could have easily pushed the more expensive repair on us. In the end, they found the timing belt was ok and it was only the sensor that needed replacing. 

I want to particularly mention that I treated with absolute kindness and respect over the course of coordinating the repairs while I was in Virginia. They also took the time to explain how the timing belt works, why the timing belt is so important, and how the sensor that failed works. This is knowledge that I will have now for this car and future cars. 

Bob Wark's Liberty went above and beyond in terms of customer service - especially given that I am not from the area and will likely never have the opportunity to come back. To me, that is the mark of truly outstanding service and integrity. Thank you for helping our family!

My husband and I had breakfast here. The portions were good size for the money. The mimosa was a little on the strong side. The siracha sausage omelet was really good but could have used a little more filling. My husband cleared his plate of the toast bacon eggs covered with maple syrup.

Compere Lapin on paper is everything an exceptional restaurant should be.  Excellent design, trendy interior, thoughtful cocktail list, well dressed servers, small well groomed menu, great location.  

Waitress was nice but not attentive.  Drinks took too long to reach the table for the price they were.   The complimentary biscuits were very hard on the outside, soft on the inside and paired with two delicious butters.  The pig ears were drastically over seasoned and the croquettes were decent but nothing out of this world.  The tuna tartare was the best of the appetizers.   Entrees:  The curry goat was a single note texture and the drum was beautiful but more beautiful than tasty.  The seafood pepper pot was highly anticipated but disappointing.  For dessert, the chocolate bar was incredibly small as well as dense and not luxurious.  It was topped with a tart whip that did not pair well.  And unfortunate end to the meal.  While nothing I ate was offensive, nothing I ate did I say....I have got to go tell my friends about this place. 

I don't live here and I could give you 10 restaurants easily that you'd great food start to finish at.  I'm hoping my experience here was one off based on the reviews

Oh lord, PLEASE STAY AWAY!!!  I came to this place for lunch with another employee. He ordered the salad bar with an almost empty restaurant. More people arrived after we ordered and I. Ever received my lunch. The other employee got up to get his salad from the bar about 10 minutes after we ordered and several people were seated about 10 minutes after we ordered and fed within 10 minutes of them ordering. They appeared to have burgers, sandwiches and I ordered a chicken Ceaser salad. The other employee finished his large salad from the bar and the other folks were half way they their meals when I called our waitress over and asked about my salad. Frustratingly she responded that the restuarant is full and she's so sorry. 

Nonsense! We ordered before the place was full so therefore I should have been first not the others near me. We proceeded to ask for the check, we waited and waited about 5 minutes to see our waitress to then tell her we need the check. At that point here goes another 5 minutes and finally she bring it. I spoke to the manager (Female) who alarmist seemed that it didn't matter. She did take out check but com'm, it's a salad bar cost of $5.99. Will never be back again to this place and also sending this same comments to their district manager, they need to uphold service and be more attentive to the customers. 

HORRIBLE HORRIBLE!!!

Some of the best Ethiopian I've ever had. 

Heads up: If you're vegetarian, make sure to mention it. I don't know if this is how they always do it, but we didn't get a menu - they just seated us and a little while later brought out a giant platter with basically everything on it. All of the food was delicious, so I was into it. But there was a healthy serving of spiced meat included. I'm sure they'll swap something else out if you just tell them you're veggie.

Went to this place for the first time this past Saturday night for my brother's birthday and I will never go back. As everyone else has already said; its way over-packed, drinks take a year and a day to get (but only 2 minutes to be spilled by one of the many obnoxious, stumbling, frat boys who make up most of the crowd), and the music is just bad. 

However, my real problem was with the coat check operation and the disgusting way in which the staff handled it's mishaps. Our group of 9 was trying to leave. We hand them our coat check tickets and proceed to wait for 25 minutes as they get 7 coats. My friend and I were the last two left of the group waiting, and waiting, and waiting some more....for a total of 45 minutes. At this point, we are starting to get pretty annoyed by the whole thing.

A girl, named Ashley, who claimed to be the manager was sitting next to the coat check and watching the progression of the situation in between breaks of playing on her cell phone. As my brother started to express our understandable frustration to the coat checkers, Ashley decided now was the time to step-up and get involved by getting aggressive with him and yelling in his face. I tried to calm the both of them down and politely explained that we just wanted to get our coats and leave. Her response was (in a tone not even appropriate for a stuck-up teenager and certainly not OK for a "manager"), "Uhhh do you see the other 800 people in here, your just gonna have to wait". This floored me. I run a company myself, and was appalled by the complete lack of customer service. All she needed to say was. "I'm sorry for the delay, we appreciate your patience. Let me see what I can do to help speed up the process as there is no reason you should have to wait almost an hour to get your coat." But nope, once again manager-of-the-year Ashley went with a different approach and now put her hand in my face and told me to chill out or she would kick me out. The irony in this whole thing is that she looked like the one who needed to be kicked out as she aggressively yelled in my face.

Ugh, grow up and learn some management skills Ashley, Your behavior was a disgrace and caused your bar to loose nine good, paying customers who will never return.

I've been to Dandelion a few times and have enjoyed each time. I like to consider myself a fish and chips connoisseur and these are some of the best I've had. The tartar sauce is delicious! I like to rose mimosa as well.

Been living in Fishtown for a few years now and while there are so many good places to eat, only one or two have been as consistent, welcoming, and a no-brainer destination for me as Stock. I come here often for lunch when I work from home. Reasonably priced, friendly staff, and bonkers delicious food. Highly recommend the cold noodle dishes, particularly the kao soi and burmese street noodles (with prahok katee added) will blow your mind. But you can't go wrong with the chicken or mushroom pho, the banh mi, or the khao poon.

Must try the pork belly sandwich three mini buns of BLISS!
Always re-creating these at home for Movie night.

Epic is the best pizza in Soulard, can't speak to the other foods they have. 

Personal favorite is the chicken pesto pizza, but these guys run some amazing specials also. Never had a disappointing special. 

The pizza itself is New York style, so it's pretty large and the crust is thinner, not quite Imo's thin, though. The cheese is the best part as its plentiful and delicious. 

Epic is adjacent to iTap which adds a huge convenience factor the the joint. Ordering pizza to go is a huge plus. They'll deliver it straight to you as well. 

Fairly priced at about $20 for a large. It is certainly a good amount of food for the cost.

Staff is friendly, and the pizzas are out in between 15-40 minutes depending on how packed it is. 

Overall your best bet for local pizza in the area.

Brad and Bre, along with their charismatic staff made us feel SO welcome and comfortable as we enjoyed they're amazing BBQ.  Throw in their marquee VEGAN cornbread, and OMG!!

Will definitely be back!!

Tell Michael hello when you see him!

I may be a bit biased towards the aquarium.  I am an animal lover and looking into conservation education as a possible career choice.  However, I still think this place is a stand-out beyond that.  I've been to a lot of aquariums, including some pretty crappy ones. (I'm looking at you Brooklyn...)

I understand why people might be upset at having to spend $24 to enter the aquarium, but do those complaining really understand how much it costs to run an aquarium?  It's quite a lot.  It's not like the owners are sitting in their mansions hoarding the profits.  It's pretty on par with other aquariums around the country in terms of ticketing.

One thing I actually DON'T enjoy about AA is the hippo exhibit.  I'm not sure what all the fuss is about there.  There are hippos 15 minutes away at the Philly zoo, so I have no clue what they were thinking when they put hippos at AA.  They have nothing to do with aquariums really, and aren't that interesting.  Why not get something unique that will be a bigger draw?  I'm guessing that would be more expensive, but alas.

I also agree with some other reviewers saying that this place can get scary during busy times.  I've been here during times when there are so many people that I'm sure it must be a fire hazard (not exaggerating).  However, go during a weekday or the off-season, and it's blissful.

I love this place.  I am a member and I go whenever I can.  I am planning to do the swim with a shark/stingray thing one of these days as well, and I can't wait.  The shark tunnel and the stingray/shark touch areas are something I have yet to see duplicated.  By the way, I saw someone say you had to pay extra for those... I'm not sure who told you that, but that's the furthest thing from the truth.  Those are free, and they are awesome.

BEWARE! Do not shop at this store!

I typically shop at Pet Smart but was in the south Philadelphia area on Sunday and needed cat food. This location was near my church. Below are the reasons why I'll never return and why you should not risk it either.

1) The staff, though friendly, are all 16 and remedial. NO one had a clue what product they had in inventory or the location/placement within the store. I was told the brand i was looking for was not there. After spending time looking for a similar brand, I ended up finding the exact item I initially asked for in the back.
2) The store reeks! It smells horrible as if they don't clean it frequently enough.
3) Against my better judgment I still purchased the food items I needed. Well when I got home to open the bag I noticed the top of the bag was taped with thick clear packaging tape! I buy this brand from Petco all the time so i know this tape job was not done by the manufacturer! I cannot believe this store repackaged pet food!! Would you sell repackaged food cereal or yogurt from a grocery store? NO! So why risk the health of an animal by doing this.
4)Since i do not live in Philadelphia, I called the store manager to inform her that there was a problem with the product i purchased and potentially others. However, she would not get on the phone. Repeatedly I told the person who answered that I had no problem waiting on the line for the store manager since it was important. I was told to "call back in an hour or two". Now really irritated, i said, no thanks I'll just call the district manager instead.

I've been to plenty of car washes before. In the reviews it says friendly service. Not so much. I went through in neutral. Then the machine just stopped. For a good minute. So I drove out. The guy at the end started yelling at me, you gotta put it in neutral. I said I was in neutral and the machine just stopped! He said go around again. So I went around again and waited again. The the guy at the beginning said, what happened?! I said that the machine stopped. He said, it has to be in neutral! I said, I didn't touch anything, I didn't get the brake, the machine just stopped. He said well it was only you. You're the problem. I really didn't do anything or touch anything.
Great food great service. Luv this place.  It's a busy place but all for good reason.
By far the best hookah lounge/bar in Nashville if not Tennessee! Great atmosphere, AMAZING FOOD, the hookahs are absolutely phenomenal! Must Check them out! Tangerine Dream is my flavor!
Great place filled with great people and amazing food! Truly scrumptious. Food options were spectacular.
I had a great chili cheese burger and chili cheese fries.  I was in one of those moods and it really hit "The Spot".  See what I did there?  :)  Anyway, I am a fan and will keep coming back to it.

We came here because the line in front of Acme was too long but we were pleasantly surprised
. The only downside was that the service is a little slow and our waiter was a little rude. We had the seafood sampler, crabcake, bbq oysters, swordfish and banana fosters. My fav was the bbq oysters but everything was great. My niece ordered the bla salad but they were out. The only reason I am giving 4 stars is the service.

Quite possibly the most stunning popular restaurant I've ever eaten at.  It is clear that the people of Willow Grove have numbed palates because this place is atrocious.   The menu is like a pizza joint, and the food is laughably bland.  Oh, and don't order the pork  chop unless you want it totally dried out.  A chef would serve that??? Nope. 

Honestly, this place is really awful with slow service to boot
This is the new Cafe V-dal, with so much more flare! We love the changes they made and the food is still sooo delicious!

The hotel itself is very new and nice. The guest room was comfortable and very clean. The front desk staff was horrid on both occasions I interacted with them. Very disappointing and with so many other choices I'll never return.

A refreshing Negroni on ice with prosecco? Yes please. (They call it the Zissou Zissou Spritz, by the way.  Every time a bell rings, I want another!)

The funky little bar with the friendly and fast and furiously attentive bartenders serving up drinks like this and local craft beers- should be enough to lure to this joint. Oh yeah- and there's good food. 
 
I'm told brunch is da bomb here - of the hearty chicken and waffle Soul Food persuasion- but our party of 4 came for a dinner on a Saturday night.. There is an outdoor area that looks very inviting but it was hot as Schwety's balls outside so we ate inside. We sat at the bar to avoid any wait. Reservations are a good idea. Friday nights are especially bust  in this up and coming Seminole Heights neighborhood- or so I hear. FYI

The menu is classic American fare with a creative twist - and some creative names -  from pizza to meat loaf  and "rasta fara fries" along with some international dishes Americans love like curry (Sulu's Curry, that is). 

The ahi tuna 'cracka stacka' app was a perfect light entree.  Too many Zissou Zissou's for me to remember what everyone ordered. I know my bf ate a whole meaty pizza and his brother made fun of him. Most of the entrees are heavier fare, which is a good thing considering all of the serious drinking you can do here. I do know we all enjoyed what we ate and drank - and drank. 

A go- to spot when in Tampa for sure!

I have rented cars from Dolan for years. Not only is all of the staff friendly during busy times but most of them always have a smile to share, especially Brandon. 

Because I've been a long-term renter and travel for business, when I was rear ended, I needed a reliable winter car while my car is in the shop. All other car rental stores that insurance would cover  in Reno were sold out of AWD/4WD so I decided to pay out of pocket and go to Toyota where I've always had great service and can trust the integrity. 

Jazmin has been beyond kind, helpful and has gone the extra mile over the phone, email and in person. She has been the only one to call me back and even checked in with me when there were lines crossed internally because she could sense a miscommunication. She was able to facilitate resolution for me by having me come in and meet with Jill. 

I've been in a Dolan rental for almost 2 months and have had to have 3 different contracts due to circumstances and extensions. Jazmin has worked hard to make the process as smooth as possible every time. 

Jazmin appears extremely busy, getting pulled in multiple directions, yet she's been very thorough with me every time. She has made a lengthy process waiting for my car back from the shop, the smoothest she can and obviously strives for genuine customer satisfaction.

I do think the rental department needs more staff, but Jazmin handles the challenges with poise and kindness. If it wasn't for her, this process for me would be a bigger headache - it really makes a difference to have kind, caring people helping you during situations such as mine.

I highly recommend Dolan Toyota for car rentals (I have been for years). A special "THANK YOU" to Jazmin for going above and beyond to assist me. This girl deserves a raise and an assistant!

I have read that thi restaurant is perfect for a first date, Well it is definitely true! The re is a lot of specials  and the food is just delicious. I had for the asparagus and shrimp in a sort of basalmic sauce which blended perfectly. For the entree I had the lanb which was absolutely amazing! The atmosphere is really intimate and the service is great (a little hard to understand the italian accent!). Had a great time !

Couldn't have been more disappointed after patronizing Angelo's based on Yelp yesterday. 

Called 45 min in advance to order 3 grilled chicken breasts. As I'm on the road all day, I bring my own sides and am looking to eat healthy. So all I needed was the protein to add to my lunch meal. When I got to Angelo's to pick up my order, I was charged 21$ for 3 grilled chicken breasts. Nothing on the side, just plain chicken breast. As I order this same meal at restaurants all over the area, I was shocked at this price tag when I came to pick up. I've never paid more than 12$ anywhere else for the same order. Flabbergasted is an understatement when I opened the container to see 5"x2" grilled chicken fingers. For 21$. 

Angelo's, you've got to be kidding me. 21$ for three chicken breasts!? Hope you enjoyed taking advantage because there's not a situation in this world that would bring me back to patronize your establishment.

This is one of the cutest and tastiest bake shops in Philly. The owners are so down to earth and they take pride in their ingredients and their products. The flavors of their desserts are some of the best. I definitely recommend stopping by and trying some of their desserts

I'm liking the new digs. I miss the old bar but hey that's progress. Guys can you throw us a bone and carry a crappy beer like PBR tho...? As much as I like your fancy pants, high falootin beers why don't you put a crappy one on tap just for comparisons sake?

We have had great luck with Dr Chernoff. He has amazing skills and has provided us great results. We have used him in both IN and his CA office and every time we have had fantastic, pain-free results. When I see other reviews that say the best plastic surgeon in the Indy area I have to say yes for sure. Dr Chernoff was recommended to us a few years back by a friend and nurse and we have never looked back. He and his staff have taken care of us each and every time and I count my lucky stars that I have found a trustworthy doctor.

Came here for a beer during a Mardi Gras morning (shhhhhh!). 

I seek the unique, and had hoped to find a place with house-made brew on tap. Instead I got beer that was made locally, but a convoluted story that didn't explain why they didn't have their own beer. I didn't feel their tap was extensive and two or three of the drafts were out (again, it was Mardi Gras, we let these those things go). 

The people inside were really kind and helped with ideas of things to do, but ultimately we ordered one beer and left.

It has been a while since my visit, but I was browsing yelp and remembered I had been meaning to write a review. The food here was delicious. I remember having some mac and cheese and a couple of flatbreads that really impressed me. They also had a few "trios" on the menu, which some people in my party of six really enjoyed. I will not forget my dad's comment,  "I like that she said the price of the specials." Every little detail helps. The servers were very friendly as well. I will be back very soon! It really is a shame they have a strange location off the main drag, hopefully the good reviews will bring them more attention.

great service!! server went out of the way to make our nite a special one. appetizers and desserts were great. though i was a a little disappointed with my  main course. overall it was a  great experience. will def. go back to try more dishes on their menu.

Person taking my order was retarded or on drugs  She got everything wrong  .I  had to  go back 4 times to get it right.I cant imagine management putting up with such in competence and lack of   awareness.
@ other persons behind my wife and I had to return to the counter to correct their order.
When they first opened they were on the ball.Not  anymore .
I will contact headquarters to complain.It was a bad experience to say the least.

Wanted to try out a new breakfast place in town, a friend suggested Los Chilaquiles. I highly suggest this place if you want a great meal that hits the spot. We started off with the guac and chips, which was fresh. I had the Tex Mex which comes with carne asada and chorizo. I added fried eggs to the dish as well. It was delicious. The sauce I got was green and red, since I wanted to try both. Upon tasting both, I'll be getting the red sauce now on. Will be back again!

We love the Tailhook.  We live in North Jersey, but my best friend lives in Mayfair.  Even though we can't get down there all the time, Marie always remembers who we are and greets us with a hug, that's how well she knows her customers!  It doesn't get any better than the Tailhook, perfect little dive bar that feels like home.

Fantastic black tea!! Their teas have a lot of flavor (I.e. Mango, Passion Fruit etc) but still tastes like tea! Not a big fan of milk teas in general but had the taro and was hooked!! Highly recommended.

This is the second time I have used Home Hero: the first was to replace a bathroom vanity and sink and this time was to build a closet in my finished basement, construct a wood covering for my electrical panel, and paint the entire basement space, including the ceiling.  Troy and Bruce did a fantastic job, asking me questions and resolving tricky issues along the way.  They were prompt, courteous, and showed up each of the five consecutive days they were scheduled.  Bruce is an amazing carpenter and Pete, the owner, is always accessible and willing to discuss any matters of concern.  I highly recommend and will no doubt use them again for my next project.

We went here for my boyfriend's birthday with a group of six. I made reservations and, although we ran a little late, our table was still waiting and ready to go. We had the calamari, bruschetta and mussels and they were all fantastic. For dinner, I had the lobster ravioli (on special) in a beurre blanc sauce and they were amazing. My boyfriend and one friend ordered the New York Steak with bleu cheese mashed potatoes (another smash hit) while another had the filet but I couldn't get a good feel since she ordered it well (I know, I know). We also tried patron and limoncello oyster shooters which are great if you like oysters (and alcohol). After a shot of vodka (on the house from the Exec. Chef) for my bf and a delicious creme brulee, all done up with a candle, I officially decided this is a must-eat-again location and I'm dying for a second shot at devouring everything on their menu!

Awesome! The feijoada is perfect and there is vegan version. It is not cheap but you should go there! Caipirinha is very taste. The service is amazing. If you are Brazilian like me , you will have a good attending by Graziela.

Really wanted some BBQ today so my wife and I went to Jim n'Nicks after her appointment. We got the BBQ chips that are home made and not to bad. I also got an Arnold palmer. It was just ok, but my wife's peach tea with the bits of peach on the bottoms was amazing! We both got the piggy Potato with the pork on it. This was a potato the size of a football with chives, sour cream, bacon, butter and shredded cheddar. This thing was killer. We both left stuffed and full for the day at 245. This is an Alabama based business that yet again proves that the state of Alabama is the king of BBQ. (And yes, I have had BBQ from Memphis to Texas and all the Bama BBQ is still better imo) Make sure you ask for the white sauce for any BBQ you have.

Got the job done quickly! After calling around and receiving quotes I found this company on Yelp. They replied quickly and were up front about pricing. I also needed to push my time back on Football Sunday and they still arrived. I would recommend them to friends and family.

I've heard the food has gone downhill but I really wanted to experience their brunch. YIKES! It was bad. My burger patty was raw and the waitress and manager tried telling me it was supposed to look like that. I asked for a new burger and when I got the check my server said I was only charged for one burger since the first one was cooked incorrectly. So did they try to talk me into eating the raw burger to avoid cooking another one?
I'm so disappointed with the amount of food given at take out. I ordered a salad that cost $15 and barely anything is on it. I won't be returning again.
Food is NOT the same. Found out owners sold Si Amigos & moved to Casa Grande off Robb Drive. I found them yay! Love the food!

Keep their number in your phone. Honest, reliable and fair pricing. I've used them to tow our box truck, get gas out to our stranded employee, and perform a lock out on a couple of customers' cars. I would highly recommend them.

When this place opened, I could only rave about the service and food quality. Now the prices are up and the portions have gone down. Sometimes the food is good and sometimes just fair. They have the same attitude as other establishments throughout Tucson, which is.. Great place for about a year or 2, then up prices, lower quality, and serve less to save. They think since they have a customer base people will keep coming. Some will stop in, but the originals will eventually stop. Costs them more money in the long run when people catch on and go elsewhere. Hard to get customers back once they figure them out. Nevertheless, it's the Tucson mentality I guess. I have stopped going and know others who have said the same thing about Crispy's.
Boba Tea, Egg Rolls, Pho Kim Chi. Pho Tasty delivers warm service and delicious dishes.

The People/Staff are worth 5 Stars. The location is worth 5 Stars, but The Sheraton BS is worth ZERO star. I'm paying $250/night plus $45/night parking- I should not have to 'request room service on a daily basis'.

They have this 'Green Policy" that states that they will not clean your room unless you request it to be cleaned. They have this lame offer of a few $'s of dining credit ($5) for every 3 days you go w/o having your room cleaned!!! Are they NUTS!!!????!!! I'm paying almost $300/night and you think I want to use dirty towels and sleep on dirty sheets for a measly $5 food voucher??? Hell No. 

I LOVE the Staff!!! The location is amazing, but the Sheraton Corp is full of BS. I will never stay here again.

We picked up pizza to go so I can't comment on the atmosphere, service, etc. The pizza was good though. A little bit overpriced ($20 for the smallest pizza they had) but we were satisfied. They also do pizza by the slice.

If I could give this place a zero I would. So the incident im about to explain is now the second time that a bartender at this restaurant has shown poor judgement resulting in my husband being made to feel he had to leave the restaurant. I have worked in the restaurant business for over 15 years as a server, chef, and bartender...and I would never tell a person who has clearly already ordered that they were eating it in the wrong section.  
Point one- if a person walks into your restaurant and makes a purchase they should be able to sit where there is an open table. or seat at the bar.
Point two- the customer is always right. 
Both of these corner stone concepts to proper customer service are apparently lost on the bartender with the hair that resembles 'Mo' from famous comedy Brothers. She walked up to my husband who was clearly seated in front of a pizza he had just ordered and was about to eat...rudely asked him if he needed a menu..when he clearly did not.  Then she took it upon herself to be furthur annoying by standing there and telling him that for next time...'the bar is for full service only.'
We will NEVER eat here again. We will tell all of our friends who live in South tampa..and we know a lot of people...about this incident. 
Just a word of advice to the management there....get a new bar tender.
One of my favorite places to stop in New Orleans! The store is so cute and they have an excellent selection of hard to find plants
Best Korean food in the STL area
Solid business. Got 4 hard to find tires for my Subaru in 24 hours....thank you Heather for your follow-up & professionalism! Definitely recommend over Costco where we typically get tires etc al.

Quaint, intimate, yet casual dining experience. attentive wait staff- exceptional waitress, Linda. 

We got the manicotti aka vodka, chicken and eggplant parm. Absolutely delicious!

I believe this was something Tampa was missing. This city has so much potential. 
Armature Works is a huge food, bar, and coffee lounge that caters to foodies and pets alike. The only downside is that it is always very busy so there are usually "long" lines to order food- I waited about 7-10 minutes to order then another 10min to receive my food. I didnt think that was too bad. I stopped at the bar to grab a drink before I ordered and I walked around. It a fun atmosphere to gran drinks or coffee with friends or to come with family on a Sunday. 
Also, every Sunday they have an indie market until 3pm were local vendors sell their cool soaps, chapsticks and funky hippie attire. I loved it. Great vibe!

The girls and I came here for BRUNCH on a Saturday morning, and it wasn't packed at all.  There were probably only 2 other tables occupied. 

The decor here is similar to many of the newer, trendy restaurants that give off that "hipster" vibe.  

Anyways, we were seated right away, and we ordered some cocktails right away.  I'm personally not a fan of Bloody Marys, but I wish I was, because they looked pretty bomb here.  I love all the pickled garnishes they use in the bloody marys, but I just cant down the drink, lol.  They have a bloody mary bar, which I thought was cool. 

I went with the Mimosa, which was $9, and only $1 more for each refill.  You can pick between a few flavors: Orange, Grapefruit, or Peach.  I had both the Grapefruit and Peach Mimosas, and they were good.  Nothing out of the ordinary, though. 

I loved that the menu offers organic, and healthy ingredients.  The eggs are all organic, and their dishes seem pretty healthy overall.   I went with the SHORT RIB & SWEET POTATO HASH.  The roasted sweet potatoes were cubed, topped with tender, marinated short rib, topped with two sunny side up eggs, and served with chimichurri and horseradish.  The sweet potatoes were cooked perfectly, and paired deliciously with the short rib.  The short rib was fork tender, and tasted great with the chimicurri and horseradish.  The first few bites were tasty, but it got boring after the 8th bite.  I would suggest sharing a couple items with a friend.  I would rate this dish a 7/10, and I think $18 is a bit steep for this dish. 

I tried some of my friend's ROASTED PORK BELLY & EGG.  Can't go wrong with pork belly!  Poached eggs, served on top of toasted bread, with smashed avocado, and topped with chipotle hollandaise.  I love toast and avocado, and adding the pork belly and poached eggs were a huge plus!  A bit on the fattier side, but it was delicious!

I also tried some of the SEARED AHI TUNA dish that my other friend ordered.  The presentation of this plate was beautiful-- bright colors, and the tuna looked super fresh!  Taste-wise, this dish was pretty basic.. The tuna didn't have much flavor, and the other ingredients on the plate didn't seem to go well with the tuna-- plain slices of cucumbers and quinoa.  It would have been nice if the tuna was sliced thinner, and drizzled with a light sauce.

Overall, the service was great, and the ambiance was nice.  The food was so-so, which is why I'm rating this a 3 star.

I love this place and it had been 20 years and still awesome... you just can not get fish sandwiches like this in Texas and I get it this is not the best seafood I have had I  my life but it is really a great sandwich and after 20 years still taste like I remember ... yes on the beach, a lot of fun and great food and drink what is not to love.

Came here on a Saturday around 6:30pm. Meh. Full points for decor, choose-your-own taco assortments, and beer list. But we were rather underwhelmed.

I was surprised that both the fish and shrimp tacos were fried... and not even "Yum, tasty! This is SO worth eating fried food!" kind of fried. But just a bland batter. 

$7.50 for a tiny bowl of guacamole (that I highly suspect was cut with yogurt or cream) was pretty steep. Otherwise prices were fair.

Worst part: SERVICE. Our server barely checked on us; we had to flag her down whenever we wanted anything. And it didn't seem that busy, either. I probably would have gotten a couple drinks over the course of the meal, but with the lack of attention I ended up just sucking on leftover ice from one cocktail the whole time.

The tacos themselves. Okay. Nothing to rave about. Meats were a little dry. Nothing special about the toppings. I could make better at home.

Service left a lot to be desired, food was fine.

We made a brunch reservation for 12 several weeks in advance.  The 3 course prix fixe brunch took over well 3 hours due to extremely slow service, which seemed odd since the restaurant was less than half full.  The drink order was brought a few drinks at a time and took over a half hour.  Once it was finished, we finally put in our orders and the experience extended from there.

The food was ok, but there was just no sense of timing and the server was not attentive to the progression of our meal.  I'm not sure if he was overwhelmed and needed help (which didn't seem to be the case), or if he legitimately thought we wanted to spend our entire day there.

To top it off, our check included the customary "included gratuity."  However, this amount was rounded up from 18% to 20%.  It may only be a 2% difference, however, 18% is the standard "middle ground."  It allows for errors in service and accounts for the difficulty of serving a large group.  After living through the frustration of a never-ending brunch, it was a bit much to know that we had tipped the equivalent of "good service" at any other restaurant.

I decided to go in because the drive-thru line was horrendous. I walk in and nobody was in line. I wait a bit at the counter, the workers are rummaging around. Finally someone came up to the counter to take my order. I asked for a large caramel frozen coffee with a turbo shot, and whip cream.  I use my app to pay. I didn't even look at my receipt, and when my number was called I told the gentleman I didn't order a small. The lady at the register yelled, "she used a coupon!" And I explained that I did not use a coupon, and it wasn't what I ordered. Ridiculous.

We came to this restaurant for dinner following several recommendations. The decor is sorely outdated and a bit dingy. But let's face it, most often you don't come to a steakhouse for the decor. So let's get to it... The ribeye was tender and tasty, the filet was also very good. The T-bone was tough even while being cooked (by my grill master husband) perfectly pink in the middle. The sides and salad bar were okay, but not great. They did have a good selection of wine and beer on tap though which is always a plus! One kid ate the chicken fingers while the other did not. It's an experience for sure coming to this place (cooking your own food and what not) but not sure we'll experience it again.
Delicious, the pancita is a most, the coffee was really good & horchata, it is busy but food is really good.

I am of two minds of this place. First of all, I think it is overhyped, and overpriced. In fact, I find that this place is worse than Starbucks, which at least has indoor seating with booths and Wifi, so you can hangout and write Yelp reviews about how bad it is. 

I'm not a fan of their coffee either, but the great thing about this place is that there's a large, very solid selection of alternative drinks on the menu. When you add in their great tasting pastries, and a solid customer service experience, it is bound to become a must stop for coffee and pastry lovers. 

I know people who will drive a half hour, just to visit to this location, and although I worry about their health and their wallet, I will concede that they have a great point in coming here, as it is a premium experience.

Still, it's still too expensive for what you get, and personally, I am not a fan of the coffee. But I do like their hot chocolate, and service, so there's that.
You must try the waffle and fried chicken it is the best in Tucson. Love this restaurant! Great customer service. Every time I eat here with friends we are always happy.

Understanding that we are currently in the midst of a pandemic I still have to be honest and speak on my experience with this location on Mother's Day. We tried to order food on the website which was overwhelmed with a lot of traffic. When we were finally able to order our family placed an order for 6 people. Once the order was placed the website would not allow us to pay via credit card online, so we were ok with paying once we arrived for pick up. The order was placed at 5pm, and the email confirmation advised that our order would be ready at 5:40pm, but we allowed a few extra minutes and arrived around 5:50pm. 

Once we arrived the parking lot was chaotic but again we understood based on the current events surrounding Covid19. The email confirmation told us to call the restaurant to inform we had arrived which I did, and was asked to provide our name for the order and the color/make of the vehicle that we were driving. I gave that information to the restaurant worker who answered the phone, and her response was that she would give this to the people working outside and we will receive our order shortly. As we pulled into a parking space we were greeted by another female employee who asked us the same information and we gave it to her again. The female worker advised us that someone would come out to our car and bring our order. I mentioned to her that we were not able to pay for the order on the website and she said to me that's fine you can just pay with your credit card when the server comes out that's no problem. So we pulled into a parking space and proceeded to wait but while waiting we notice the parking lot had more cars continuing to pull in, and the restaurant parking lot began to look like it was at capacity with people waiting for orders. We can see restaurant servers coming out looking for vehicles basically walking throughout the entire parking lot before they were able to find who an order belong to. After waiting for about 30 minutes and no one coming to our car my husband got out and walked up to the front entrance were several people were waiting for orders. There was one server standing outside with a clipboard and she went on to inform that they were at about two hours behind on filling orders and she had no way of knowing where everyone was in the process. At this time people began to get into their cars and leave the restaurant without their orders some even begin to argue with the server who was out there by herself. I was frustrated and concerned because the restaurant when ordering on the website never told us that they were behind two hours if we had known they were behind we would not have placed the order. But at this point our family was hungry and ready to eat so we decided to be patient as best as we could and wait it out. Needless to say we did not receive our food until about 6:45 PM. The young man who brought the food to our car was very nice however he never even once offered an apology for the lateness of the order. When the food was brought out we were not given an opportunity to confirm that all of the correct items were inside of the bags and luckily upon checking at home they were.

Again I totally understand that we are in the middle of this pandemic, and it was Mother's Day which is typically a busy holiday for the food industry in general.  I wish that the restaurant would have done something to update their website in real time to inform their consumers that they were over two hours behind on filling orders. Had we had this information we could've made a more informed choice about where we were going to eat, or we could've been patient and not left home for an hour and a half so that we did not have to sit in that parking lot to wait for food that was promised earlier. When we got our food home you could tell the restaurant was overwhelmed. Our steaks and shrimp were overcooked and somewhat burnt so it kind of almost made waiting for the food very disappointing. We've had both good food and service with this restaurant in the past.  We've eaten there frequently and never had troubles with our orders.  I just think that they were overwhelmed on Mother's Day. 

Going forward I would like to see this location attempting to communicate with their patrons a little bit more effectively. For example if Mother's Day was overwhelming given the current situation maybe they should've closed their website down to taking orders which would've allowed them to provide more timely service and not be two hours behind. I think my family will continue to patronize this location going forward but. But I will honestly say if we have another situation like this again where the restaurant it seems was more concerned about making money then providing good service we might not.

I started seeing Dr. Dubin when he was on JFK Blvd. because it was close to work and I could go over and get a cleaning in the middle of a work day.  The office then was calm and comfortable. Dr. Dubin always saw me and did not pressure me to add any sevices, which was good with me since I was going here between my regular dental visits and paying for it on my own. 

Since that time, Dr. Dubin moved to Walnut street further from my office, and I have decided to have invisalign to correct some crooked teeth.  I have selected Dr. Dubin to do the work, although I know many other providers who could potentially do it.  Dr. Dubin's new office, like the other one, is calm and comfortable. It is the one place where I can imaging getting treatment that I expect is going to take a long haul.  

Dr. Dubin remember me and some of my particular concerns. Dr. Dubin's staff treated me professionally and like Dr. Dubin answered all of my questions.  The office is clean well organized, and scheduling and payment issues have been a breeze. 

I'll keep you posted on the dental work, but for now, I am glad I am making this choice.
Hours are not as advertised drove.  Call before going.  Wasted our time and they let us in but didn't serve us only to tell us the hours posted in their door, Yelp, Google are all no longer accurate.

I've had the same experience as Justin and Klaus. I've never been in direct contact with VUI, yet I receive job postings from them via email. I wouldn't mind, but they've never been applicable to my experience, and every one of them has been outside of my geographical region. Their unsubscribe function is useless, so all of their email now goes into my spam folder...where it belongs.

I have a theory that VUI is spamming people in an attempt to generate numbers for their clients, so they can tell a client, "Look, we've reached out to 250 candidates," without, of course, explaining that those candidates weren't qualified for the position. Their customers should be more outraged that the job seekers they spam.

We took my parents for moms birthday and were very pleased. We started with clams casino and fried calamari. Enough to share and piping hot.  Dinners were delicious, lamb chops, branzino and 2 stuffed pork chops. Best part was dessert. We don't usually order dessert, but these looked fantastic and tasted even better. Rum cake, bread pudding, and a chocolate dessert with ice cream. We will definitely be back.

I've been here twice since they opened last week and both times were delicious!

I've had the Pho Beef and O'Miso Spicy Ramen. The pho was yummy and the beef was tender just the way I like it (: and the O'Miso Spicy had a delicious broth and great noodles, but I thought it could've been spicier. However, it wasn't a problem because they have a ton of spicy sauces in the condiments section so I could reach the spiciness level I was going for. 

They also have boozy slushies. During my first visit I tried both the Green Tea Margarita and Hi-C slushie. Both were boozy and sweet-- would recommend. 

I definitely will be coming back for food and drinks~~

The ambience is pretty chill and super trendy. It's pretty convenient for me since I live next door and can drop down for a quick bite if I need one. The first time I went on opening day it was packed, but today I went around 3pm and it wasn't too busy. Both times I was so shocked how fast the food came out.

The service seems pretty consistent and the staff there is super friendly, they even have high fives for free on the menu :)

Soda Parlor, & I say "Ice cream Parlor" instead + soda. 

Lots of ice cream and drinks options in a case, milk shakes. 

Lots of seating, cool games & t-shirts and stuff to buy while there. Friday night was fairly packed. I liked how they were opened later than most. 

Service was good. Young guys working & fast service. 

Clean! Clean. Is what I noticed & liked.

It has the best smell when you walk in, your hypnotized! 

Zombie ice cream!

Had a great lunch today at Bangkok Siam Thai.  Went with two coworkers and we liked not only the food, but the owner's nice conversation as well.  Pad Thai, Panang Curry and Pork Fried Rice were our selections and everything was delicious.  The peanut sauce, as others have mentioned, was fantastic.  The owner went out of his way to make us feel welcome and went through all spice levels with us in a funny joking way which we enjoyed.  He invited us to their party on Saturday night and kidded with us the entire lunch.  He even mentioned other good Thai in the area in case we haven't tried them - find another restaurant that refers to competitors in a friendly way!  He made some return customers today with his service and friendliness as our whole office will be back!
Overall the food is delicious we had the catfish, hush puppies, red beans and rice. The stand out for me was the red beans and rice, literally the best I've ever had

I feel like regardless of what nail salon I try, I never have a great experience. Either the specialist is too rough or my nails end up warped or falling off a week later. I'm happy to say that my bad luck streak is over.

Stupidly, I never thought to try the salon that's almost literally behind my house because 1. that would make too much sense, and 2. nothing good is ever close to my house so why should this place be any different? I walked in on a Tuesday afternoon and the place was empty. One thing I noticed right off was that it smelled amazing. The woman who did my nails was friendly, quick, and gentle. When she was finished, my nails looked perfect. This was three weeks ago. I actually want my nails to fall off or come loose enough for me to pry them off at this point, but they're still on as solid as ever. I imagine this is a good thing for most women.

The cost was average, I was extremely happy with the service, and I don't think I'll be trying another nail salon any time soon.
Johnny is the man if you want your beard shaped up! The place is a little hard to find but definitely worth it!

Stillwaters Tavern was a bit of a let down for us as far as service. I ordered a cocktail, the first one had a bug in it and completely melted ice... which makes sense since about twenty minutes passed between ordering it and receiving it. They remade it obviously, but yeah still gross. Another 25 minutes passed between ordering our food and receiving it. If you are looking for a casual, slow atmosphere, this is it. 

The actual food itself is to die for. The mac and cheese with added pulled pork is legitimately breathtaking. It's a bit spicy though. The tavern burger was also really good. Really juicy and delicious. The cocktail (#60) was just okay.

We had a 5:30 dinner reservation for two on Saturday 1.4.20. Our server was very friendly and attentive.  We had a Riesling and a Montepulciano that was offered on the bar board as a special. For apps we had scallops wrapped in bacon with a horseradish mustard sauce and a cup of the seafood bisque with sherry. I do not eat pork but the scallops looked gorgeous. My girlfriend said they were the best bacon wrapped scallops she had ever had and that the side sauce was delicious. They were gone in about 45 seconds. We had the Centeron Salads which is a favorite of mine. There is nothing like a cold, chopped, cheesy, garlicky salad. The flavor is great and they do not skimp on the cheese  I had been debating on whether to get the Crab Imperial or the Lobster Newburg. You do not see Newburg on menus often. She ordered the Crab Imperial with a side of sweet mashed and I ordered the lobster with asparagus on the side. The Crab Imperial was generous and delicious. My Lobster Newburg was in a thin brown sauce that appeared to be broken. It spread out all over the plate since it was in a thin sauce on a large plate. I was confused. I thought Newburg was a white cream sauce with either sherry or cognac. I thought maybe my memory was failing so I did not question the waiter. I even google imaged Newburg when I got home to confirm, yes, it is a white creamy sauce. I am an experienced cook but I started to doubt myself while at the restaurant. I ate it. Lobster was cooked perfectly and was tender. Taste of the dish was fine. The presentation would have benefited by the main being placed in a smaller dish set upon the larger dish with maybe some fresh chive on top.  Since it was not, and the sauce was thin, it spread everywhere and the color was not appealing. The end result is sloppy. My dinner tasted fine it simply was not what I was expecting or in the mood for. I also wondered how the sauce was made because I do no eat pork, beef, or veal. I also sometimes get an allergic reaction to beef. For this reason, I was a bit worried for a few hours after dinner. I won't order it again. The menu is quite large and varied so I have plenty other options. All the food that floated by us looked great. The atmosphere is colonial and roughly hewn. Personally, I love it. It has a lot of character due to its age.  Are there things that can be done such as painting the exterior? Sure. But, it doesn't turn me off as long as things appear clean and the food doesn't suffer. It is unique. So, four stars because my Newburg was "confusing". We will be back.

When we went to the hurricane restaurant we were sadly disappointed. The food was terrible, it took a long time, and was extremely overpriced. When we asked to speak to the owner, he hid in his office. The waiter stated he was "reprimanding a waitress".... For 90 minutes come on... And this to him was more important than addressing his customers. I also found out other disturbing facts about the hurricane from that same waiter. For example, the cooks are provided by the Pinellas County jail inmate work release program. We found this to be awkward and highly unethical. We have small children, and without knowing the criminal background of these people the hurricane was exposing my family to potential harm and charging us a pretty penny for. When we told the waiter we had read good reviews about this place, he stated the owner pays a lot for that..... Another thing we found to be unsavory about the hurricane restaurant.
Pass a grill is such a lovely beach community, and one of our favorite in the Tampa area, it's a sin that this restaurant has been gouging people for decades and tricking visitors into paying big bucks for jailhouse cuisine.

I had a wonderful experience at the Green Hills Mattress Firm. I was buying new pillows for my mom and Autumn helped me out tremendously. I got the most comfortable lavender scented pillows, and she loves them! I will be returning soon to buy a mattress and look forward to Autumn's assistance. Highly recommended!

I would not recommend this moving business to anyone. I had a couple of movers from this company help me move some bedroom furniture from my apartment to a new house on July 25th and the movers were very unprofessional and dropped multiple pieces of my bed frame and they also knocked off a 6 inch piece of wood from my headboard. The company contacted me the next day and asked if they could come out and check out what happened, which I agreed to and they followed through. I thought they were very genuine and professional and they agreed to pay back half of the moving cost, which amounted to $70. It has been almost 4 weeks and I have had to contact them on two different occasions now asking for a refund and still nothing. I could have asked them to replace my furniture completely, which would have been hundreds of dollars, but I was very understanding. I will never hire them for moving help again and do not recommend them to anyone else.

We stopped in for a quick snack since we had dinner planned in a few hours. We had Pimento Caprese which was pimento cheese over fried green tomatoes and arugula. Awesome! Next we had buffalo oysters which is how they sound, served with blue cheese. Excellent.
My wife had the Bloody Mahoney which is their version of a Bloody Mary using infused bacon vodka and garnished with candied bacon.
She loved it. We noticed the other food being served and noted we would try one of the Po-Boys and the thin cut onion rings on our next visit to NOLA.

What we had: 

- Polenta fries
Crispy on the outside, gooey on the inside. Served piping hot with marinara sauce. Pro tip: ask for the Green Goddess sauce, sooo good! Not that the marinara sauce isn't good, it's one of the best I've had. 

- Scotch eggs
Hands down best scotch eggs, well seasoned. The paired honey mustard sauce tastes amazing. 

- Prime rib dip
Beef is pulled instead of thinly sliced, which is a surprise but it's sooo good! The bread is toasted to perfection, and stays in shape after soaked in au jus. The fried shallots and horseradish sauce take the sandwich to the gourmet level. 

- Beef and polenta stew 
Ultimate comfort food! 

Overall, everything is delicious, each item has a unique twist and the quality is comparable to any other high end fine dining restaurants! I just can't wrap my head around how good the foods are in this place. Honestly, how is this happening? 

Bring your date or friends to this place and they will finally know how cool you are!
Great place never had bad service yet good pizza, with that being said "what the hell is even that"

Bad service from management, probably why the place is dead all the time!  The food is good though so hats off to the chef.  Forget about your "rewards" card if you're a regular there.  The points expire and when you ask the server about it they respond with "who did you speak with?".  Like I remember who I ordered my lunch from a month ago???  I specifically went there to pick up 2 expensive cobb salads for lunch so I could use my rewards points and that didn't happen.  They told me they expired, even when I chose to save them less than a month ago while I was there!  If I would've known at that time I would've used them.  I should've gone to J. Alexander's!  You can never go wrong there!

Hands down best reuben sandwich in Nashville. I'm from New York and honestly miss my Jewish food something fierce. Moving here six years ago I found this place and it made my move a bit easier.

Their Reuben is kind of perfect, satisfies the soul. Thank you Mitchell's deli, you beautiful sandwich slinging, bread boasting, meat mincing, condiment casting, sexy sandwich super store.

I went here for lunch about a week ago. Their service was astounding, friendly, and knowledgeable about their sources of food and menu items. I ordered the cold cut (or I should say Italian) and my date ordered the tuna melt and Caesar half and half. The tuna was fantastic, bell peppers always add to a tuna melt, and they nailed the proportions. The Caesar was underwhelming, with bottle dressing and boxed croutons. The sad show of the day was the cold cut. Sweet Dill relish has no place on an Italian or cold cut sandwich. Especially one with no spice (where are the pepperoncinis??). The cheese was over-powered by the sauce and the prosciutto was lost in the plethora of ham slices abundant within my crunchy soft French roll. I was going to drop a three star on this place and be done with it, but the owner brought us out a sample of their tomato bisque. Hands down, the best tomato basil bisque I have had in Santa Barbara. It was smooth, yet textured enough to know you were enjoying fresh tomato. Seasoned perfectly, and the only thing missing was a loaf of parm ciabatta. I will go here again, if only for the soup and astounding service. Also, check out the dill flavored popcorn. A simply elegant treat that will excited your tastebuds and entice your appetite.

I like this place ALOT !! The entree and everything that came with the entree was Good, Good, Good !! Baby Churrasco - A delicious 8 oz skirt steak, grilled to perfection, served with rice, plantains, a fried egg, French fries and salad. The only reason I can't give 5 stars is that they don't make their own tres leches in house. I will be a repeat visitor when in Ybor City.

I want so badly to love this place. This was at one point one of my favorite places in all of Nashville. My biggest issue with this spot is portion size. Not a fan of leaving and not feeling full. I just spend over $15 on the side sampler and cashed out my two free sides (rewards program) so really got about a $22.50 meal here. That's what it takes to get me full here $20-25. No thanks. My weekly visits will turn into semiannual.

I was visiting family from out of town and this Y was the closest gym to where I was staying. Since YMCAs now have nationwide reciprocity, I came here and used my home membership to allow access.

I swam laps in the main pool on two different days and the aquatics facility is GREAT. There are nice, clean locker rooms adjacent to the pools and everything is in very good shape.

I didn't spend much time in the main gym but from what I saw it looked nice enough (I took a walk around just to see what kind of courts, classrooms, etc. they had).

I didn't give this facility five stars for a few reasons. First, the main locker room is pretty small- the pool locker room is actually much bigger, but it's in a separate building. Second, the parking lot could use more spaces- there was a basketball tournament going on the first day I visited and the lot was almost entirely full. Third, they don't offer towels. These are pretty minor quibbles, though- I was very impressed with the facility.

This company was recommended by most in my neighborhood and I too will now recommend them as well. They replaced my old tank and gave me a quote to replace and fill the new tank. I had a leak in my old furnace and had to replace it. They were very helpful in finally getting the old tank from another company off my property. They were very patient while we found a replacement  furnace. Communication was great with them when the new furnace was finally installed. Shawn and Don were so great, helpful and just great guys in coordinating the fueling, testing and making sure all was right before they left! The quote they gave me never changed and I feel confident that they were the right choice.

Jeff & Audrey S.

Amazing service and Burgers to Match. Have been here a couple times now and have been to the other one downtown even more. I ordered burgers from here for my coworkers and I at the airport firehall and they missed a burger and fries, I called them about it and they drove out the missing burger and fries in a person vehicle to make it right, they went about and beyond to provide good service and they will have my business again and again.

The BBQ here is no better than what you can get at Famous Dave's, BBQ Rush, Holy Smoking Butts or Mr. K's (which is awesome). Weird thing is, there's a woodpile next to the building, as you'd see in the South, but the meat here tastes like it came out of a chip smoker instead. You're basically paying a premium for high-end, old wood ambiance and cold water in a bottle, not awesome BBQ. They do have a good beer selection. When they have music, the acts come on late. I attended the Sunday Jam today, which is advertised on Tucson Happenings as starting 3:00, but actually began at 3:30. The first band was nonchalantly putting their stuff together when we walked in at three. Never a good sign and actually pretty disrespectful to the business and its customers.

Incredibly mediocre experience. I have had many facials in the past, and I consider this to be one of the worst facials I have ever had. 

Jess, my esthetician was nice and courteous, but did not explain anything to me about my facial. She did not tell me a single product she was using on my face. But she suggested I pay an extra $40 for microdermabrasion to clear up congestion in my skin and assured me it would help. Her technique was terrible, and did not remove a single blackhead or congestion. I should have stayed at home and washed my face myself. My facial only lasted  40 minutes when I paid for an hour. What an unfortunate waste of money. I do not recommend this spa, and will not be returning for any future services.
Hot Sauce is an asset to this establishment. I've traveled over 12 hours just to see her. Ace was extremely professional with my requests.

This place is so delicious and totally hits the spot when you want something out of the norm. My husband and I have been here twice now, and both times left stuffed and happy. The staff is all really nice and helpful when suggesting things to try. The baked hummus is amazing, definitely don't miss that. We also ordered the Bayshore mixed grill and it was all excellent quality meat, and the pickled cabbage and yogurt sauce with it made it perfect. We also got the Beyti and man that stuff is amazing, we never skip ordering it. But if you get one thing while you're here get the baklava! It's so buttery and delicious! Excellent meal, I can't wait to go back!

Great service, friendly, trustworthy.  I am glad to have found this place to service my Oreck.  It's so close and better than my personal experience at the Oreck service center that tells me a need a new $50 part on my new vacuum after only a year!  Thank you Vac & Sew! I plan to purchase bags here as well as any new machines I add to my home.
The bathrooms are always dirty. The staff here are rude. I hate going to this target. We will be driving the extra 20 minutes to go to a different target.
Best sushi in the area. The parking is a little bit of a hassle especially when it's busy, but it's totally worth it. My favorite is the donut roll.

Tapped is a unique take on a self service beer hall. With what I would say was around 30 beers on tap. The system is easy, tap the wrist band to the machine pour your beer, and repeat. The fact that there is also wine on tap is a great feature. Food was good bar food. I recommended the toasted ravioli or one of the pizzas.

Excellent service, ambient and food!!! Definitely 100% recommended. A must if you come to New Orleans. Great value and pricing for the food. Jorge helped us out to on our selection of dinner. He was very kind. Truly we enjoyed it. Coming back tomorrow.
Excellent food. The portions are generous and delicious. The outdoor patio is beautiful.

So I ordered a burger and a hotdog. The hot dog was great, but my burger was undercooked in the middle. Undercooked to the point where I could have gotten sick probably. If you get a burger here, make sure you ask for it well done.
1 word. awesome. Great service and great food. I will be eating again for sure. Quick service and perfect food and awesome communication. A++++++++++

Tried Pharmacy for the first time on a Tuesday evening around 6pm and was pleasantly surprised that we were seated immediately.  I'm sooo glad I got there when I did bc by the time we got our food, which didn't take that long at all, the line to get in was down the sidewalk! 
The burgers are tasty and cooked which ever way you request. The bread is super soft and toasted the way I like it! The tater tots were crisp and if you get a side of beer cheese to dip them and your burger in you won't be disappointed. I also had the black bean and corn salad and that was fresh and delicious too. Lastly, they have glass bottled Cokes and that's always a treat bc you know it's gonna be the best Coke ever every time! All in all a cute little place that stays busy bc they have good food. I could stand for a little more seasoning on my burger so that's why a 4/5. Will be back.

From the ambience, to the impeccable service, and to of course the flavorful meal - this spot has been a memorable place for my boyfriend and I on our trip to New Orleans. We are lucky enough to be staying at the Old 77 Hotel where this restaurant is housed and have been popping into the bar for a few cocktails and tonight finally a meal. The pig ears and the buttermilk Caesar salad with Brussels and fried chicken skin were hands down my favorite. The drinks are also affordably priced for the quality. Craft cocktails served to you by unpretentious and cool bartenders. They even had a couple blended daiquiris for Mardi Gras that sounded refreshing and un-artificial. Can't wait to eat here more during our stay! Thanks to William the bartender for the great service in addition to the support staff.

Finally checked out Redwood Rotisserie + Grille!

We got there early enough for Happy Hour and sampled two it the apps. One was the Thai Chicken Wings and the other was the Tachos!  The chicks wings were the usual challenge for a beardo like me but they were tasty. And that Tachos were a cool twist on nachos substituting tater tots for the tortillas. I would have preferred real melted cheese to the nacho cheese sauce, but they were still tasty. For dinner I had the quarter pound of tri tip with a side of tots and Mac n cheese. The tri tip was perfection and I throughly enjoyed it with the chimichuri sauce.  The hot sauce was good as well. One of my dining companions had the tri tip and chicken combo and mentioned that the chicken was juicy and tasty. Another of the dining companions had the tacos and said that they were tasty too and the final dining companion had the catfish sandwich, but requested it deconstructed. It  was huge and she thoroughly enjoyed it as well. My only complaint was the size of the side of Mac and Cheese which the other dining companion that had it as well confirmed. All in all we enjoyed our dinner and will be returning in the future!

Might be the best Thai food we've ever had, and we've had a lot... Great gem in a wonderful little town. Dishes were all fantastic beyond compare. Highly recommended. Visually, aromatically and taste-wise wins on every angle.

Five stars for breakfast on the ground level at Hurricane's! Let me say however, if you are agitated by "island time" service, we recommend you go elsewhere. Otherwise where the heck can you get 2 eggs, bacon, & toast for $4.25???!!! That are actually decent quality....Across the street from the beach!!! I say decent because we do organic eggs and homemade bread at home, so we obviously adjust our standard when eating out. My husband got the sausage gravy and biscuits for $4.99 and added an egg. Another nice touch that is rare these days, is that the gentleman making your breakfast always comes out to say hello and check-in on everything! We will definitely be hitting this place up again. Our server Tina was very nice and made our visit with our kiddo a success...she brought the waffle as soon as it was ready! Without us asking!

Had a great meal here. Server did inform us that they were a testing restaurant and that they don't have items listed in the menu but still offer them. What? Ordered the chicken with bacon and avocado sandwich minus bacon, which was delicious. Food came fast and no complaints, this time.

First off, I have to say the wall art is amazing. It's very aesthetically pleasing. The man who took our order was very kind, patient and informative. Food came out pretty quick once we ordered. My boyfriend got the Braised Beef with Rice Noodles Extra Spicy and I got the Rice Noodles In Chili Oil Spicy. It was sooooo good!! The chili oil made the soup so flavorful and the noodles were slurpworthy. We will definitely be back. Next time I will try some of the desserts, they look so good! Sadly, we forgot to take pictures :(

I wish they had this place in Erie, PA. It was wonderful and its how country eating should always be. This is one of the very best places I have visited in a very long time. The staff was excellent and gave me a tour of what makes this place tick. They are well known and have awards to prove how good they are. 

Make a list and make sure this place is on it.
Excellent salmon dinner. I sub'd the red beans and rice for the spinach and it was excellent. And when they JUMBO shrimp they aren't kidding.
We are local and eat here often. Good food and fun spot at the marina. We had visit that had some hiccups recently but the manager Ray graciously took care of us.

Loud not just from people noise but also the game commercials and all blaring at high volume. Ordered a hamburger on a gluten free bun and waitress doesn't have the sense to mention fries are breaded in flour. Burger was mediocre, my husbands rueben was not good, fries fair. Will not be returning. It was crowded when we got there so some must like that whole atmosphere where you can't hear the waitress or the person at your table.
Came in for a burger and a beer. It was you standard bar burger. Nice selection of drafts. It was right across the street from my hotel so it was convenient. Service was excellent.

Pretty good sandwich wasn't what I was expecting, the first bite of the salad was good after a couple it seemed a bit too much, wasn't a big fan of the ravioli and seemed a bit overpriced  over all great place and staff personal are really nice and have good vibes

Oddly enough I have never been here before, it is not the part of town I travel through often. The south Reno store is the one we frequent.

SO we did stop in for lunch the other day. 

The look of the place is old fashion Mexican restaurant or in other words, dated. The seating is good mix of chairs and booths.

We were greeted and seated all while being asked what we would like to drink.. We each opted for the iced tea. It came with a dish of chips and salsa, a variety of two and both were good as were the chips, very good.

The main dish for me was an enchilada/cheese and a beef burrito with large chunks of very tender beef and of course cheese over the whole thing along with rice and beans all of which was very good. All the food by the others was received very well.

The wait person and sub waitperson were very attentive and friendly. We never had to ask for anything as it was all supplied before we even knew we needed it.

Yeah if you are in this part of town come on in and have a good meal, hell even if you have to drive out of your way it is a good bet.

do not buy the groupon!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

groupon states that it is a 60 dollar value.. let me tell you that is a lie. rodizio is 20 dollars per person, drink is 1.75 per person, and the dessert is 3 dollars per person.. therefore the total cost without a groupon is 24.75 cent per person... at THAT PRICE... the building and service reflects the meal correctly. the building looks like a converted Laundromat. But never judge a book by its cover, so we went in, and redeemed the groupon to give it a fair share. we sat there for 1 hour and 23 minutes (timed it before we couldn't take it anymore) and only had 3 meat selections brought to our table.. literally the worst service ever.. the last time I had to get up, go to the host desk and ask them why there were not serving up. while the meat selections were tasty (especially the garlic chicken) a restaurant gets reviewed on more aspects then just the taste of the food. and because of the ambiance, service, lies about the value of the groupon, I have to give this place a failing grade. and for the other reviewers. there are plenty of rodizio style places that offer great value in pricing while not compromising on service or ambiance.

sorry for the various misspellings.
We had the best experience today with Jacob! Very knowledgeable, kind and explained things well to my daughter and I.  We hope to see him again soon

My salesman David J was very nice, but he had an easy sale, I knew what I wanted prior to coming in. However, that's when it went all downhill. 2 weeks to pay off my car since the new finance company kept requesting info that I had already given and I was getting harassed from my previous company, because I was never late with payments and I had a payment due in the middle of that 2 week time frame. They would not speak to my previous company, the general manager told me to deal with it. The management in that dealership is a joke and they are extremely rude all the time.

Never disappointed at this location!

This City BBQ location distinguishes itself with consistent high quality in food and service, something that many chain restos struggle with. Not this place! Food is always wonder and served quickly, which is very nice when dining in with a hungry toddler :)

I would even go so far as to say the quality of food has gotten better over my five visits. We even opted to have our boy's 1st birthday party catered by City BBQ, and it was a hit. Pulled Pork and Ribs were of course a hit. The kids inhaled the Mac & Cheese, the adults appreciated the Crisp Salad, Cole Slaw and delicious Baked Beans. But the winner was the Corn Pudding! I'm craving it as I type...my in-laws were in town for the birthday and they're from Canada. They don't have Corn Pudding in that strange country to the north, months and months later I still hear about how good it was. The mother-in-law even told me she looked up the recipe online to make this exotic foreign dish, lol.

Dine in, pick up to catered...food and service is wonderful!

One tip...they're normal slaw is a vinegar-based slaw. If you want creamy slaw, you need to specify that. Both are very good.

This time ordered takeout, Salmon with mashed Potatoes and Parmesan cheese on side for potatoes, with a salad.  Ordered the same before, several times and it just keeps getting worse and worse.  Salmon is just too dry, not overcooked as I can tell, but just dry, poor flavor.  The mashed potatoes were ho hum but the Parmesan cheese product, well it was awful.  It was, to the best of my ability to discern, that cellulose Parmesan you find in the grocery on a tub.  It is mostly cellulose (read paper) and almost no taste.  The lettuce was like rubber, dried out, old but refrigerated so it didn't spoil. No taste or flavor in the lettuce or other stuff mixed in with the greenery. PS: am a member of the rewards program, so go to OSI brands often but am seriously reconsidering, it just keeps getting worse and worse.

Kids at home - Friday night this week 3/20/15 - M80 wings were great (extra crispy - perfect!). But both wife and daughter sandwich was just ok. Next day we had coupon for fast foot and my daughter said her sandwich was better from fast food restaurant. (She is 28 yrs old married with her own children) Fire side has a great place and perfect opportunity to reach others. I feel they need to consider food price and quality of food. I want it to be good.

I was debating on whether I should write this review, and how I should rate Brio. This applies to ownership, not the manicurist. 
My wife and myself have been a regular customer of Brio since the first week that they opened. We remember that there were not many customers in the beginning, but that has quickly changed. We usually make our reservations for several weeks in advance, as my manicurist only works on weekends. We currently have ours booked out until mid February. 
On our last visit, I was told that men's manicures went up from $15 to $20. I was told this during my manicure, not before, or in advance. However, women's remained the same, with no increase. I do understand that prices to rise, but it seemed like I was being singled out for this increase of over 30%. Let me also let you know that we are always punctual for our appointments and tip very well. I have even supplied my own hand cream and nail polish! 
I guess they no longer want our business. I will stop by later this week to pick up my items. It will be the last time that they see us!

NY Richie's is now under new ownership.  I am new to town so I cannot speak on the restaurant in the past, but the reviews make is sound like terrible service.  I can ASSURE you, this is no longer the case.  The new owner, Raj, is incredibly friendly.  He was working to install more Giant flat screen tvs when I walked in.  We struck up a conservation, which had to be cut short once I was served some of the Most Delicious NY style pizza I've had outside of NY.  

$3 for a giant slice of exactly what you are craving.  So good.  The staff is very friendly and promptly brought the food out.  It was piping hot and I couldn't stop eating the pizza.  

I also tried a calzone that was around $6 and packed with four toppings of your choice.  The calzone was beastly in terms of size.

Great selection of beers on tap.  

The place has a pool table, dart boards, arcade games, and 6 or so giant tvs with sports always on.  

A+++ to the new owner and his transformation of the place.
