I've always been a fan of their pizzas and salads. In fact, their pizza crust is incredible. This morning, we came by to check out the new breakfast menu. Wow! I had the morning carbonara, so freaking good!!! My wife had the strawberry french toast. She could not say enough about it. She was smiling the whole time she was eating! Breakfast, lunch, or dinner, you will enjoy the food and service. Yum!

Having grown up in the Bay and San Diego, I really enjoy wharfs, piers, and boardwalks.

Stearn's is by far the most underwhelming for the town of Santa Barbara, which hails many visitors. The most entertaining thing really is the games that the local bums set up along the sand to collect spare change.

Other than than, the water is beautiful, but that has little to do with this pier. It could be a lot better if there were better eateries and other attractions, such as Trish's Mini Donuts at Fisherman's Wharf in San Francisco. Now that would be worth a visit.

I will NEVER order from here again. Lets see. They screwed up my order, and refused reimbursement or correction. The food i did eat was either bland, or on the verge of expiration, and some of it wasn't fully cooked. Hands down worst chinese food/customer service I've ever had. My order was dinner for two, bbg duck, and 10 chicken wings. Each order came to 1.00 more than advertised online, they over taxed me, refused me free delivery (though i intentionally spent that amount to get it as they advertised), didnt get 4 free cans of pop for the dollar amount as advertised. Completely screwed me. To top it off, my 10 wings arrived as 6 pieces of deep fried chicken instead, and they refused my lawfully protected right to a refund. I Will NEVER order from here again.

Cute place, but certainly understaffed and undertrained. Took about 10 minutes for anyone to come by after we were seated, an additional 40 minutes to get three plates, and 20 minutes to pay the bill. The Brussels sprouts were quite undercooked and inedible and the waffles were cold. No one ever came by to ask how the food was. Will not be coming back
I have been there 3 times now & each has been a positive experience. Tony does an outstanding job on dip nails. Love them every time!

This is the same team from Woodlands in downtown Nashville. Great food - vegetarian and real South Indian taste. 
Location, decor, service is better then other location.

This place has really good food! My husband had The Skillet which he thought was delish. I had the Monte Cristo which was good and then some of the, if not THE, best French fries I've ever had. They had a thin batter on them and were fried to perfection. We also got two potato pancakes which were soooo good! We'll be back for sure.  The service and our waitress were superb!
Great sandwich, soup and potato place.  I love the portobello wrapini and their muffalettas are amazing!  We eat here quite often as my husband works at USF and it is so close to the university.

The food is nothing short of spectacular. Everything and I do mean everything cause I've had the whole menu is great. The staff is super chill and very pleasant. Adequate parking available. It is BYO and the setting is small and intimate. Only reason I took star off is the pricing seems very high and there was just 1 tv without cable (not good during a NBA playoff game). But otherwise the food makes up for the minor miscues.

Excellent boutique bakery with hand crafted homemade selections plus coffee and tea. Tried some macaroons and 2 slices of the famous coffee cake.  We met Paige and yes she was sweet.  The macaroon cookies were amazingly dee-lish, and the 2 slices of coffee cake ended up as our breakfast and lunch filling up a good full sized 'to-go' cake box by themselves (see pic)

Long wait to get in, even when the line wasn't that long. Just kind of a sad zoo, in that there are not many animals. Most of the animals were sleeping when we came around 1pm.

I wouldn't waste my time coming back here.

Honestly - won't be back here. I'm a very forgiving patron, but after today's awful experience I can't help but comment. We received our beverages quickly, ordered and waited. We were sat in an area upstairs without any other tables. We slowly had 4 other tables join us. Before we received our food, 2 tables had eaten and cashed out. We still hadn't seen our server to let us know where our food was. Finally we flagged down an employee to inquire about our food. Our food came out and wasn't bad - a little on the cold side. The Creole rice was awesome. It took another 20 minutes for our server to come check on us. For 4 people to eat and pay it took over 2 hours. So disappointed. Also - no manager came by to talk with us about our bad experience..
All the reviews are correct. The snacks are amazing too. Miss this place already. Super friendly employees and cute concept.

Yesss blue corn! Eat your heart out folks...if your heart is made of guac, tacos, alambres, etc; JUST DO IT! If Mari is your bartender than BONUS points for you! Strong but balanced drinks, great food, open clean kitchen, very attentive staff, great happy hour...A+ all day!! Cash only ps...they deserve your cash trust that

This was our first ghost/ haunted tour and it was AMAZING! Our tour guide was a real sweetheart; he was was entertaining and had a great personality from the start to the end of the tour. He had a great grasp of historical knowledge and helped to debunk myth from fact about a lot of the more commonly known New Orleans lore. He spoke on sensitive subjects such as events surrounding hurricane Katrina and horrendous acts ending in many deaths at the locations we visited with tact and consideration. The info about Delphine Lalaurie and Marie Laveau is NOT to be missed!

I had yet to be to a quality frozen yogurt place in Delaware until I came here.  Scrumptious has about 8 different rotating flavors and all the toppings a girl could dream of.  Also, the staff is incredibly nice.  Oh yeah, they also serve Indian food and a variety of breakfast options.  It's totally eclectic and AWESOME.

Check in: very good except a piece of plastic skirting under the bumper was damaged and went unnoticed during initial inspection.
Check out: got stuck waiting to validate damage report for above mentioned issue.  Agent insisted on reporting as damage to "bumper".

We recently hosted our wedding reception nearby and blocked a hall of rooms for our guests here. The hotel's front desk was helpful in handing out our wedding welcome bags upon check-in -- along with welcome bags for 10 other weddings taking place that same night!!! As the hotel was so close to our reception, we were able to utilize the hotel's shuttle service to/from the hotel and the reception for our guests, which was great. We then came back to The Fox and Hound bar after our reception for the after party -- I met 4 other brides this evening -- obviously a popular spot! We then hosted a delicious brunch buffet for our guests the next morning in The Hunt Room.  All of our guests loved this brunch, as it was more extravagant than your typical hotel continental breakfast -- a little pricey but definitely yummy! Our bridal suite was awesome and it was certainly a great place to get ready for the big day. The staff was very helpful when we were planning our wedding and arranging our guest accommodations.  This hotel is a very classy and upgraded place to stay in Chester County.

My only complaint:
I also stayed here the night before our wedding and got little sleep -- part nerves, mostly part yelling/loud guests for other weddings that night.  I called the front desk a few times to complain but the noisy guests continued until the early a.m. -- that's what happens when the hotel is a popular spot for weddings! Despite that, our experience here was great!

This is the dive bar you have been looking for with some of the best food around. The fried clams are huge and a basket of fries is a basket of fried, not a side. The people were great and diverse. I saw a biker sharing stories with a grandmother who was knitting at the table.

We booked a room here last minute while traveling through St. Louis, I just have to say we were impressed... for the price and the amenities it was well worth it. We were impressed with the cleanliness and space of the suite, after traveling across the country and staying at many different chains this hotel was the nicest. I would recommend again and again friendly staff, great breakfast and to me you really cannot beat the price! I would give fives starts but our toilet ran constantly and the neighbors upstairs sounded like they were doing jumping jacks for part of the evening but nothing too crazy and we never called about the toilet either.

This isn't just a ups store--It's your friendly, neighborhood office. The staff is amazing-Lori, JB, Doe and everyone else are so amazingly friendly and helpful. They are attentive and give amazing customer service. And they're always so helpful and have the most cost-efficient ideas for shipping, copying, etc. 

They have a ton in stock-tape, packing materials, fun stickers, and funny cards. They also have envelopes and all the office supplies you could ever want! And they're always well-stocked. 

There's also rarely a line unless it's move in/out season at Tulane so it's super efficient. And even when there is a line, they move you through it very quickly. They're also really patient with the spoiled school brats and unappreciative business patrons. So more power to them!

They go out of their way to help you too. They taught me how to fax (sorry I was born in the 90s and didn't know how)--didn't make fun of me or anything but walked me through the process and waited with me. They created my graduation invites for me (which took several tries on card stock and computer sizing trials). 

I once forgot the address of something I had to ship at home and they let me email them the address and shipped it for me so I didn't have to come back into the store. 

They also just have a nice conversation with you instead of rushing you out the door and waving you to the window like the post office. 

If you need to ship, fax, copy or just have a laugh, THIS is the store for you.

My only complaint is the parking lot is super tedious-way too many cars packed in small spaces and no one ever drives the right way in or out!

We come here to buy supplies and throws,plain and simple ,we can see the staff are busy but helpful Valerie,Brianne, Jessica,and the rest of crew simply wonderful and friendly. We highly recommend this place its not a glamorous place, but the product is the best in the region ,our regular sales girl Valerie is got to be one of New Orleans gifted when it comes to ideas and craft and talent .

Absolutely delicious!! Beer Braised Brisket sandwich is a must try. The brisket is unbelievably tender and the spread they use has a bit of a kick. Delicious bread, too. The three-cheese grilled cheese is great, too. In terms of sides, homemade waffle chips, fries and jalapeño hush puppies are all super yummy! A great find!!

Really cool place. Yes, the decor is really committed to the automotive theme, even the restrooms. But, the burgers are really good too. We had the fried pickles (good, but the pickle taste was pretty mild), the Firestone shrimp (great flavor and perfectly cooked) and several different burgers. All the burgers were fantastic: just the right portion and very juicy. 

I'd definitely go to Ford's again.

You must check out Bradford Woods, we moved in here after a terrible experience at The Preserve at Brentwood (which is false advertisement because they are not located anywhere near Brentwood.) the first person we met was the Assistant Manager Amy. She was so polite and delightful. We met the Leasing Agents Caroline and Yesenia they were equally delightful. The Manager Cheryl was so proactive in getting us approved that it felt like we were apart of a family.
The apartments are a little older but they do offer great incentives and the best thing for us was that they offer 4 bedroom apartments. The apartment rooms do not feel so compact, and on top of each other from the 2 to 4 bedroom. The 3 bedroom offers a regular porch and a small one off the master bedroom. (While waiting for the 4 bedroom apartment we had to live in a 2 and 3 so this is why we know about the others.) The one thing that they do best is they are on it when it comes to any problem in the apartment. 
This apartment staff does a great job of making sure that you feel comfortable and heard. I can not be any happier about living here. I absolutely suggest that you come by and check out Bradford Woods apartments.

Recently purchased a suit for my significant other at the Langhorne, pa store. They had to order a pair of pants for the jacket, which was in stock. The pants came in quickly and we went in a week later for fitting the alterations needed. We were given a pick up date three weeks later; with a ticket that indicated I could pick up at "6pm" on Oct 9. I called that afternoon to confirm the pick up time- hoping maybe ready a tad earlier in day. 
Nope- I was told it was "on the rack" for the single tailor in the store--- and it would not be ready until "7:30-8:30 pm" and they closed at 9pm. The young woman i spoke with made it sound like this was no big inconvenience; without checking if I could go along with a new pick up time I had no control over anyway.

I chose to wait and pick the suit up when the store opened Sunday morning; annoyed as can be.  Annoyed that the store gives you no option but to ignore the p/u time promised three weeks ago!  For God's sake, hire more help and honor the time frames you give out in black and white!!!!
To make matters worse, in the middle of the event my guy wore the suit to, we noticed two pale white chalk marks on the jacket-- obvious leftovers from the fitting!

Wow!! Never again. $400... they don't honor the time you counted on to get your garments. Then they let ya go to a formal event wearing chalk!  Lovely. Next time--Macy's and the tailor who altered my gown.

Mind-altering amaze-balls crazy trip through the beautiful brain of Bob Cassilly! Wow! Words can't do it justice and neither can pictures. You have to go . . . and I have to go back. We ran around for hours and barely scratched the surface. If I lived anywhere near St. Louis, I'd be there all the time. There's really no point in detailing the sights I saw, the stairs I climbed, the slides I slid, or the tunnels I slithered because your experience will be your own and it'll be even better if it's a surprise. Now go!

Bad reputation company that could ruin everything. No one disagree that internet is one of the most important things in the life. Especially one is happily working at office building (where IT provides solid network support), and suddenly the internet is down at home (where is supposed to have the relax and family time). Probably it is unfair to complain one company. Comparing to other developing countries, they used to be behind the technologies and have bad service quality. Now ISPs in the US are going lower and lower to the bottom. Predict in near future, new revolution will change the market and kick them out. Because people won't stand that long.

Expensive for outrageously bad decor, good service and mediocre food. 

Penang is a much better choice if you enjoy your food, but Buddakan is good for groups, or gawkers who enjoy staring and taking pictures of giant Buddhas.

It's a high-end restaurant where I felt the atmosphere (super dim ceiling lighting with very high tables, counter-height chairs, tables with lights inside, etc) was more suited to be a hangout place for young professionals looking to impress or college kids with money. I would not go back when there are many other places that are either cheaper with better food, or the same price with MUCH better food.

Definitely the best grocery in the area. Great prices, family friendly folks, and a great selection. They just recently put in a healthy whole/organic foods aisle, that really provides a ton of healthier options for the uber health conscious shopper! And just recently they put out a stand with free fruit as a snack for your kid while you shop! So very cool!

They have amazing food! The crawfish boil was fantastic and the oysters are to die for but the service is terrible... Do not go if you are starving. You will wait at least 30 minutes for anything including a drink! Over all it is great but service sucks!

My tattoo looks fine but the artist fought tooth and nail with me every step of the way. He was super rude and belittling and made fun of me with his co-workers. Jason was not accommodating with all my wants/ideas, was clearly tired and just ready to peace out, and laughed at me with all his coworkers because i'm getting my second tattoo and i don't want it bigger than a quarter..This felt like a really judgmental environment. Im never returning to this establishment, this isn't the kind of treatment you want when you are worried about permanently marking your body!

The ferry is a very convenient way to travel as a pedestrian from the Eastbank to the Westbank or the opposite.  The ferry has also saved me many times when I've been working on the Westbank and the bridge gets shut down for some reason and it has allowed me to escape the Wank to get back home without waiting for hours on end of backed up traffic on the Expressway for the same $1 price as the toll on the bridge would be.  The main thing I don't like about the ferry is that it stops running relatively early (by NOLA standards) so you can make a run to happy hour in Algiers but you can't stay out very late at all or at midnight you will turn into a pumpkin and have to take an expensive taxi ride to return to the Eastbank.  There are a few nifty bars to see in Algiers and it can be a relaxing trip to take in the Mississippi River.  You get some great views of the area that are impossible to get any other way during the ride.  For the price (FREE for pedestrians and $1 for vehicles) it certainly is one of the best values of anything you can do in all of NOLA.

Pros
Authenticity
Additives
Taste

Cons
Messy
Space

My first time in Philadelphia and I knew it was a life or death matter in finding an authentic Philly Cheese-stake. I got more than enough of the experience upon coming into this place, it had an old 1950s restaurant type feel. The theme was similar to the Johnny Rocket franchise or Steak N Shake, but the food was 100% Philly. The additives were nice there was more than 20 ways to customize your sandwich. After getting it, there was a chance to add more banana peppers and tomatoes and more. The taste was extremely aggressive, depending on how the sandwich was customized there was a mixture of flavors from the additives alone. A mixture of tangy, sweet and pickled flavors could confuse and amaze your taste buds.
Being a somewhat picky but simple eater, one of the things I didn't like about the food was how messy it was. The running melted cheese and mixture of juices from the peppers and other additives could make the bread soggy and the entire sandwich runny. You would definitely need extra napkins to make sure your hands were clean after a meal. As with most things up north, there was little to no room for having personal space. The chairs and tables were easy to bump into while navigating through the restaurant. Bumping into patrons is very common here, but hey maybe that's the Philly experience too. Overall though, the location was decent, the food was good and it gave me my first ever actual cheese-stake, check it out!

I've eaten at this location twice now and it was great both times.  The portions are large.  The regular burger is two patties and the small fries are enough to share (I had as many fries in my bag as in the serving cup, which was full!)  A little more expensive than the regular drive through, but a good value and a great burger.  It was crowded at lunch, but the line moves quickly.
Best Alfredo and prosciutto Mac n cheese I've had in a while. Make sure to try the sangria of the day. It's delicious!

The Gaylord Opryland Hotel is a world class resort hotel with an amazing history, unfortunately run by untrained, incompetent idiots.
My girlfriend and I had both been guests there in the past, and in need of an escape from back-to-back to back family get-togethers, we stopped in Nashville for a quick holiday getaway.

Let's start with the good: the hotel is beautiful, the shows (A Country Christmas and The Radio City Rockettes) were excellent, and the bell hop staff was top notch.  

However, that's where the praise ends.  From beginning to end, our stay at Gaylord Opryland a nonstop shit show.

It all started when we pulled onto the property at 2:00 in the afternoon on a Monday, only to sit in the car for 40 minutes waiting to get to the valet station.  When I inquired (rather good naturedly) why it took so long to valet the car, the valet quite smugly laughed in my face and said, "this is a 3,000 room hotel - 40 minutes is a short wait around here."

He was right, because over the course of my stay we waited in line an average of 55 minutes each time we attempted to valet the car.  And since valet was the only handicapped parking option, and the self-park parking lots were a zoo from their ICE and SNOW exhibits, this colossal waste of time remained our only parking option.

But the lines didn't stop at the valet station: 25 minutes of sitting at the bar at The Falls to get the attention of the attitude-encumbered bartender, who didn't know what a Hot Toddy was, didn't know bourbon was whiskey, and when another patron asked if the bar carried limoncello, made a face as if someone had broken wind and said, "I've never heard of THAT."

And there was that time a maid, without waiting for a response to her brief knock, walked into our room at a MOST inopportune time.  You'd think she would apologize, turn around and leave immediately, but instead she WALKED INTO THE BATHROOM over our shouts of disapproval to do lord-knows-what before finally leaving (sans apology).

One evening we had the valet pull our car around but after 25 minutes of waiting we went back inside, so we weren't there when the valet arrived with the car.  So he locked the doors, put the keys in his pocket, and promptly walked off.  We stood in front of our locked car for over half an hour while the valet staff scrambled to find out keys.
There was also the time we waited in line, outside in the cold, for 45 minutes to board a shuttle for the Rockettes that was supposed to start running at 6:30, but we didn't step foot onboard until 7:15 (for a 7:30 show).  

Or our final morning, when our wakeup call was a 'fire drill' demanding we evacuate the building, followed by a 30 minute 'all clear' alarm.

But the icing on the cake was when we were ready to checkout.  We called down to the valet for our car, only to be told, "We're not accepting phone requests for vehicles, you have to stand in line curbside with your luggage in hand before we'll get your car. CLICK."  So we finished packing our bags, lugged them downstairs ourselves, and I joined the back of the valet counter line (which stretched halfway across the hotel lobby).  Fifteen minutes later my girlfriend returned with a super friendly manager from the front desk, Brittney.  We explained the wait times, the lost keys, and the morning's phone call, and she offered to find a valet manager for us.  It took her ten minutes, but I was still only halfway through the line when she returned with the valet manager (Matt).  I explained again the wait times, lost keys, etc.  He looked annoyed, but under the eye of Brittney, took my valet ticket and offered to personally expedite our car.  He disappeared, but 45 minutes later there was still no sign of him, or our car.

So I tracked down a second valet manager, who chuckled at my inquiry and non-chalantly replied, "yeah, Matt was looking for a white Lincoln Navigator.  I guess you're driving, like, a black car or something.  He's pulling up now, he'll be there in 30 seconds."  He must have missed the valet tag on the key chain which clearly identified our car as a black Charger (which looks nothing like a white Navigator).   Five minutes later, my car finally arrived.  Matt's response: no apology, no reason why he hadn't come to find my over the last hour to explain the delay, just an attempt to justify the situation by giving me the whole "looking for a white Lincoln" story.

In the end, Brittany ended up reimbursing us for two nights and our valet charges.  However, the problems at Gaylord Opryland Hotel run far deeper than one person's experience.  Every other guest I spoke to in the lobby that morning felt the same way, and I heard stories from other guests that were far worse than mine.  The general consensus among the staff I spoke with was that this was "just the way things are," and unavoidable for a hotel this large.  If that's going to be the case, I would suggest that Gaylord Opryland itself should be avoided.
My kids love the place. The remodel makes it much more enjoyable. The outdoor patio is comfortable and a great way to chill out at the end of the day.

Another bad experience. 

25 minutes of waiting for the shuttle. Wow, only one person working. This worked the first time, but not the third time in 3 months.  Get it together. Rental counter, umm, they'd prefer you call with the kiosk. This takes 3 times as long, and these guys have no authority. Downgraded cars give, have to get a sup to override so that you may get the class of car you reserved. Again, get it together.

It is very hard to be a good chain restaurant, because you are almost set up for Yelp failure as soon as you open.  This is simply because it's almost like you're a sellout for not being a mom & pop place. Well, I am here to tell you, that for a chain, Red Robin as really won my vote despite all that. 

Pros: 
1. Reasonable prices for a full meal of burger and fries (around $10)
2. Bottomless Steak fries - uhh where else does this happen?
3. A variety of fun burgers and chicken sandwiches
4. Reasonably priced drinks
5. Short wait time during busy times

Cons:
1. It's a chain.

Ok, now with that out of the way, I just want to say that living in Bensalem, you can't avoid chain restaurants, and not the terrible kind either, considering in this same mall there is a Crackle Barrel and a Chik fila, which for a North Jersey girl - these are high commodities. As for Red Robin, we came to have a cheap and quick meal after spending $18 on a burger and fries in a local micro brewery - which was delicious, but just way too much for a meal that consisted of a burger and fries. So here are my three favorite burgers that will make you beleive you are NOT in a chain restaurant. 

1. Bonzai Burger if you like something sweet and tangy. A little more on the adventurous side since it does have pineapple slices in it, but completely delicious and will make you wish you always put pineapple in your burgers. 

2. Guacmole Burger - this is messy and delicious, and I am a bit bias, because you could put guacomole on roadkill and I would probably love it.

3. Chili Cheeseburger - this is my favorite burger, it is a disaster to eat - get utensils! The chili is very tasty and it is one hearty meal, so make sure you come in starved.

As for sides, I'm not much of a steak fries girl, but with their fancy dipping sauces I am! I like the spicy ketchup and some fancy creamy bbq sauce one they have for free. But most of the time I go for the onion rings, because these are some of the BEST onion rings I've ever had. They are crispy and flavorful, I only wish these too were bottomless! Overall, I would definitely come back, they are also very family friend, they give balloons to the kids, they have milkshakes (and adult versions too) and the decor inside is actually  really cute.

Zero stars if I could. Do not purchase the Groupon or at least don't come to this location if you. The Groupon deal I purchased didn't apply to my car apparently and of course right . I paid the same price with the Groupon or without. The guy calls me over to my car and starts telling me the mass load of things wrong with my car, struts, shocks, roters breaks, calipers....my car is 3 years old....... i literally couldn't believe what the guy was telling me like I might as well go total my 3 year old car...I will never come here again, they try to up sell you and rip you off

Worst restaurant I've ever been to.  Not sure how anyone can give them even a decent review.  I grew up in South Philadelphia eating good italian food and our experience was not even mediocre.  Dinner was atleast two hours long, no exaggeration.  Our table was practically on top of the table next to us.  I ordered a pasta dish for my entree and could not even eat it.  I like my pasta al dente, yet this dish was completely raw.  when i brought this to our waiter's attention he did absolutely nothing.  I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone who is looking for a good italian meal, or even just a good meal at all.  The best part of my dinner was the bottle of wine that we brought with us.  If I could give them a 1/2 star i would.

I really liked the ambiance of Time. There is a dining room, an absinthe lounge, and whiskey bar. All great concepts, but perhaps too many? For people like me who like variety, it could be a great place.

I was definitely intrigued to find a nice space so close to where I live. The whiskey bar's set-up is very neat and clean with its polished wood bar, tables, stools, and seating.

I knocked down some stars because I wasn't really wowed by the place. The bottle display in the whiskey bar is cool. But I got confused as to whether they would honor $2 drinks because I kept receiving e-mails about parties that would happen at Time. No $2 drinks for me and now those parties are no longer happening at Time :(

If you haven't tried a class at this new Barre3 studio in Philadelphia, then I don't know what you're waiting for. 

The new studio is absolutely *flawless.* it's got two huge studios, cubbies, plenty of room to actually put your shoes on (unlike Pure Barre Philly, which if you've been to the studio you know the struggle of even trying to get your jacket on when classes are switching), and beautiful bathrooms with 2 showers. Staff is constantly cleaning and trying to keep this new studio in tip top shape. The staff is also so friendly and accommodating. 

The classes are always challenging. Every time I take a class, I've got muscles aching in places I never knew of. The biggest difference between Barre3 and Pure Barre is that you actually get cardio in your classes and your heart rate up, so you're burning more calories and in the long run, getting a better workout. Also, for all of us who are $truggling, this studio is CONSTANTLY offering community free classes! SO TRY IT. 

The best part about this studio though are the owners and staff. Sandy and Stephanie are always finding ways to make their customers happy to be a Barre3. They've developed a whole community of people that are so welcoming and excited to be at Barre3, both instructors and clients. And the instructors are amazing, always willing to stop during class and give you a modification if a move doesn't feel right for you. The people are what make this studio 5 stars!

Walked in before 3 PM on a Sunday only two people working in a shop with several stations. There were 2 people ahead of me. I was not greeted and signed a list on the counter saying I wanted a cut and my hair blown out. A girl told me it would be about a 25 minute wait after I had already been waiting for 15 mins. It took them over an hour to get to me. While watching the haircuts leaving and waiting so long I decided to opt out of the haircut and just get my bangs trimmed. They aren't good. The girl working at the first station compensated me by not charging me for the bang trim and it's worth less than that. I would not recommend the place due to waits and less than mediocrity.

The best shrimp and grits in Delaware! The grits were so creamy and cheesy (and I don't even like grits). The shrimp had a sweet barbecue and savory mix. The bacon (optional) added to the delightfulness of the dish. 

If they were open for dinner I would have gone back for dinner and ordered more! Delicious!

The server could have been a little friendlier but I guess we are all entitled to a bad day.

They have a great outdoor bar and i would love to be able to hangout and chill on a more frequent basis, but I cannot afford the $6.00 for a Yeungling. Average price for a Yeungling draft in St pete is around $3.50.....

Super, super tiny shop and has ceilings so low it can be dangerous if you're over about 5'5".  When I told my 6'6" husband to be very careful, the clerk snarkily said, "Well, this IS a cellar." Alrighty then! Needless to say the tone of our visit was set before we had even properly gotten in the door!  I did see some cute samples and some gorgeous yarns, but nothing that jumped out at me.  The clerk spent most of her time on her phone. Rosie's was my third Philly yarn shop of the day and, quite frankly, the worst.

I got married here and I've always loved this place. But I'm sorry to see on social media that they are being irresponsible during covid and endangering the community. I won't be back for a really long time.

Great pizza, but only giving 4 stars. The woman ahead of us was taking all of the pizza maker's time, and no other employee came around to take the order for my family. I had to wait until this woman was finished deciding every little thing. Ironically, when her pizza was ready, she didn't hear her number called. I told her hers was ready when ours got called. Service for those a few clicks off training? Might be a thing.
Good guys. We've been customers since 2001 (with four different cars over the years, foreign and domestic, crappy and solid). They've always been great.

The place wasn't bad. All the hotels in the area were pretty expensive, so I had the expectation going in of it being a little overpriced. The lobby was pretty and the rooms were clean and comfortable. The "view" is a joke so we didn't open our blinds at all, lest we see into the neighboring hotel. Also, they charge way too much for parking. But, again, I knew this going in. The person who checked us in was friendly, but the gentleman who checked us out was not. So that's hit or miss. And it sounded like I inconvenienced the staff when I had a stupid question, so I just stopped asking. Given the cost of the hotels downtown, I'd still stay here again.

This is the by-far worst Japanese Steak House I've ever been too. Our stove would not work, so he decided instead of move us to a table that was working, he would cook it on the other tables already hot stove - so we missed the entire show.

We all ordered our steak medium, it was practically tar-tar.  They forgot our ice cream too.

Iced vanilla latte = 4.5 stars
Service = 5 stars

Seating = plenty indoors and out
Parking = 75 minutes free parking in lot behind cafe
Tried pastries and coffee at breakfast. The pastries were fresh and very good. The coffee -- not an espresso drink -- was just ok. If you want strong coffee, order an espresso drink.
If you're looking for the best seafood in the Palm Harbar. Always fresh fish great menu fabulous wine and drinks
My experience here was great! Friendly staff, fast, efficient, and reasonable. I am so grateful to them for fixing my phone!

Always a great time grabbing a drink/bite to eat and taking a look at the wide range of books that are offered to read/skim.  I'm hoping that with the digital age upon us, stores like this do not go out of business.  Keep up the book sales and author visits.

This location is very much open for business despite (as of this date) Yelpers reporting it closed. I tried to tell the Yelp Review Board but they didn't read through my message... Anyway...

I swore off Steak 'n Shake last year but the temptation of "Free Fries" was just too much. You had to order through the drive-thru, but since it's Covid-time, that's no big deal. It was a wait in line, then I was asked to pull forward to further wait for food, after they just gave me my water. I had asked for a cup of water and it tasted funny. Shortly, someone comes out to give me my bag of food: bacon cheeseburger and fries plus another bag of free fries! You need to specifically ask for the free fries.) Lo and behold, no free fries. Luckily, there was a direct number listed on my receipt. The guy tells me I can get back in line to get my missing fries. I say NO! I'm parked out front, please bring them out to me. It doesn't take too long but GEEEZ. The burger was actually ok and the fries were good, as long as they're hot. Once they cool off, yuck! Better options out there for burgers. Boo since they couldn't even get my small order correct.

I came here with my family while we were visiting Santa Barbara. We needed to stop somewhere for breakfast before we headed back to LA. We decided to come to Sambo's and I'm glad we did!

The parking isn't very great. There's a tiny lot right next to the restaurant where you might be able to snag a spot. Otherwise it's street parking. We didn't have to wait at all when we came in. We let them know how many people were in our party and they seated us right away. They have a very nice outdoor patio that has a great view of the beach and pier, but that was full and we didn't want to wait since we were so hungry. We were seated in a booth next to the window so we still were able to get that view, it just wasn't outside. Definitely try to snag an outdoor spot if you can! The restaurant is located right on Cabrillo so you are basically right near the water.

I ordered the Crab Cakes Benedict which was super tasty! I love crab and all seafood, so I wanted to try this spin on the classic Benedict. They also serve you blueberry and chocolate chip mini muffins on the house! That was a nice treat and it was really hard to not devour the whole basket!

If you're looking for good food with a view, definitely check out Sambo's! If you want to sit outside, it's probably best to make a reservation beforehand.

Fantastic Thai restaurant with a pleasant ambience! The lunch specials are a great deal ($9.95 for a soup, salad and entree), and dinner is yummy, too.

I came here today for the lunch special and thoroughly enjoyed the red curry and lemongrass soup. The soup had a tang to it, and a good mix of onions, mushroom, chicken and other veggies. The red curry was fantastic; the sauce was full of flavor and there was lots of broccoli, chicken, bamboo and beans cooked perfectly and portioned well. I've had the pad Thai here previously, and it's also great. The price is a great deal for the top-notch food.

The entrance boasts plants and a small waterfall, giving the restaurant a serene and relaxed feel. The whole restaurant is elephant themed, playing off of the name: it's fun and tastefully decorated! Elephants appear as statues in the entrance ways, the centerpiece of paintings on the walls, the table clothes and even the bathroom mirror. You can also hear soft, soothing traditional Thai music.

Overall, I'd highly recommend!

It would have five stars if they had more than fried chicken wings to go with their combo meals. The chicken tastes great but many people prefer other parts of the bird. Sweet potato pie, my favorite was in the oven so I had to pass up. Food is the best Southern and barbeque in Northern Nevada. Try their fried okra, best I ever had. Let's go for better selection of chicken. My wife had chicken and waffles and had chicken wings and a so, so waffle. It can easily earn that 5th star as I believe they are on the cusp of earning it. Very friendly, warm, staff. I will be back for more.
This is the BEST sandwich shop in Saint Louis hands down!!! We drive from Illinois just to come and get a legend club... I recommend everyone I know to check this place out!!!

Just-- don't.

The first sentence should be sufficient, but in case it's not, just know that this is by far the worst customer service I have ever dealt with in a company before.  This failure to not make me want to slap people across the face is across the board, by the way.  Every time I have ever had to call to speak with anyone at Comcast, I always am told "well I don't know who told you that/gave you that information."  So I started writing down exactly who told me "that" and their Comcast ID number, and exactly what was disucssed.  And guess what?  They still "don't know who told [me] that."  

I have dealt with Comcast at different apartments in Philly over the years and the customer service is always terrible.  Not to mention, I find their billing fairly confusing, and there seem to be hidden charges.  I finally decided to switch to Direct TV because I couldn't deal with it anymore, so I made an appointment for Comcast to pick up their equipment at my apartment.  So I had to set my alarm for 7am this past Saturday because they gave me a 7am-11am window.  And guess who didn't show up?  They did send someone out to terminate my service, though- so someone stood right outside and shut off my internet, but didn't come in to pick up their stuff.  Now I just had to make another appointment for a Saturday 2 weeks from now and again set my alarm for 7am.  The real kicker is that I'm being charged for the equipment for every day after they terminated my service that I haven't returned the equipment!  I will allegedly get a refund for these charges once the equipment is returned (although last time I returned everything and was charged for a modem that they say they never got back- not true).

I seriously cannot say enough bad things about this company.  The actual service was OK in terms of the cable not going out and internet being pretty consistent.  But customer service?  I mean, words don't really cover it.  I would basically have to interpretive dance my hatred for this company's customer service.
Horrible customer service. No regard for people and mediocre food. I haven't been here in years and know I remember why.

Latin food options lined pretty much on every corner of the walls once you walk in. Walk in and sit at their small cafe where you can watch them prepare authentic Latin food. When I visit Florida, I always stop by for their cafe con leche con pan cubano which is always on point.

My mom took me there to celebrate  my birthday . It was both our first visit there . I ordered a appetizer calamari perfectly cooked and had a lobster garlic noodles and strawberry martini . At the end of the meal I received creme Brule a complimentary birthday dessert . Best thing to describe this place a high end sports bar it was a little loud but had no trouble talking to my mother . Great atmosphere very energetic

I loved this oasis of creativity. I'd often hear Philly being regarded as a place of art so as I walked into this 'garden' I felt elated. There is so much to look at- details in every stroke, every bottle. Isaiah Zagar went through many phases and it was mesmerizing to see that all throughout his piece of the garden. I enjoyed the work inside, but the mosaic outside just felt more surreal. The shape of women, confidence of men, the questions of being, Latin culture, the repurposing of  items otherwise discarded, color fusions, unity, recycling, reusing, reinventing.. all ideas that came into mind. Do yourself a favor and stop by- even if it's a little farther from city hall or center city. Make up your own deductions of his art. I think you'll have no trouble.

Best veggie burger I have ever tasted! I'm thinking about relocating so I can be closer to that burger . That's how delicious it is. Sweat onion jelly , savory aioli, and melted Diaya cheese tops the patty which  has a hearty texture that holds up. No mushy veggie burger here. The acorn squash made me sad. Bland , bland ,bland .  
Great atmosphere . The bartenders are very nice and attentive. I wish they had a server because it's awkward waiting at the bar to order food especially when it's busy . 
If you're vegan then you must try out the veggie burger . Just stay clear of the acorn squash .

One of the best all around dining experiences ever. Very neat and eclectic place. That's reflected in the menu. I was admittedly a high maintenance customer and asked for a lot of changes and the staff was really friendly and even recommended some other additions they thought might be good.  You could tell they just want you to have a good time. Excellent food.  Highly recommend.

The atmosphere is fantastic and the view is spectacular! NARAL had a celebratory dinner last night and they treated everyone like qweens. The wine selection and champagne list was very diverse. All the details and service was exceptional and we were so happy that our hard work was noticed by the staff. Thank you all.

BEWARE!!  My family and I decided to try this restaurant based on reviews posted here.  Being only 1 of 2 tables in the restaurant when we arrived, the service seemed to be very good.  Food was delivered to our table in a timely manner, but half of the order was forgotten and had to be made after 2 of the meals already came out.  After finishing our food, we gave our waitress a restaurant.com certificate we had purchased.  The certificate cost $10 and was good for $25 toward your purchase.  When the waitress saw the certificate, her attitude completely changed.  She took a long time to bring us our check and boxes for our food, no big deal to most, but I was with my 2 young children who were definitely ready to go.  When we finally got our check, I noticed an 18% gratuity added.  Now, the checks at this place consist of a calculator tape and that is it.  You would never know how much anything costs by looking at your check.  The 18% gratuity was added inconspicuously after I gave her the certificate.  My party consisted of 4 people, myself, my husband, and our 2 children under 4 years old.  I couldn't believe a tip was added to our check.  My husband had dined her before, and there was no tip added, so clearly it was not a restaurant policy, it was at the waitresses discretion.  Also, there was a sign outside of the restaurant when we walked in that stated that fajitas were the daily special at $6.99.  On our check, there was nothing for that price, so when I questioned the waitress, she said that was a lunch special only.  THEN TAKE THE SIGN DOWN!!  I will not be back to this restaurant based on service alone, but on top of that, the food was only average...try Dos Margaritas in Hendersonville, much better service and much better prices!!
Convention Chinese and sushi buffet serving with plastic utensils and foam plates. Plenty parking spaces. Good for quick lunch and dinner for your family.

Never again.  The only reason I gave one star is because the food was hot.  Beyond that, there's no reason to ever visit the place again.  The madness starts with signs that dictate customer behavior.  "We don't care if other places make it better.  If you don't like it, NO REFUNDS, NO BUTS."  No sense in repeating the other obnoxious signs.  As for the food itself, there should be a picture of this joint next to the word "bland" in the dictionary.  And what should be customary in the South--"thank you" was never uttered.  The prices are higher, on average, than similar places in New York City.  Try that one on for size...

Clearly, this place gets enough business from people who must live close by and see only the convenience.  Because quality, professionalism, passion for the craft, and appreciation for the customer are nonexistent.  I honestly believe they don't really want any more customers.  Despite the fact they never even said "thank you," since I was "raised right" I will go ahead and say "you're welcome."
Barbara is nice to work with and is flexible with hours. She hemmed 100 miles of wedding skirt for me and did a great job at a fair price.

Interesting menu that changes frequently. They have a large bar with a decent draft list and a great selection of whisky. Bartenders and servers are professional and friendly.  Try the chicken sandwich or the burger; both are good.
Scummy location that fails at putting on good live performances. Picture quality is crap and has the service to match. Go else where for food and watch a movie at home.

Wonderful place to buy secondhand toys in great condition, at great prices from great people. The owner Antoinnette and her sister are great people to deal with. The rest of their staff as well are very knowledgeable and very polite. I personally collect vintage toys and Lego mini figures. I really thought I was the most knowledgeable person on what I collected until I met Antoinnette. She will go out of her way to help you find what you're looking for as well as the rest of her staff and give you a great price as well. I try to stop in there as often as I can. They do consignment but you must call and make an appointment because the store is always busy and loaded with people. I highly recommend the store for collectors or people with children looking for that special toy that may cost too much brand-new at Toys "R" Us. Always a great experience....

Today as I was hungry when I went in I was grumpy and tried ordering off SB menus as the menus was upsetting me so I called pizza Meetze directly and they gave me 50% off for the hassle the only downside was I had a drive-in but as I put in my order they got something wrong I ordered a chicken tortilla soup and I ended up getting a minestrone soup for my girlfriend she flashed at me on Valentine's Day because I ruined her valentines night and all she wanted was a chicken tortilla soup now I'm stuck with the minestrone and i'm mad girlfriend thanks a lot pizza Meetze for everything tonight as far as my stomach goes but I'm not getting laid.

Wow can not recommend this company enough! Jose answered immediately, was so professional but also friendly. He worked with our schedules, was very quick and SO EFFICIENT. our carpets looked and smelled brand new in one hour and all the pet stains and dark spots were just gone. He insured us that he goes the extra mile to deliver a quality job and he wasn't lying! It smells so fresh and is a completely different color! It feels like we have a brand new carpet. He even reached out the next day to see how they were... just an all star experience and definitely will be my go to. Awesome job!!

Classic bait-and-switch fraud
I booked Sonic Auto transportation services a couple of months prior to the move date. They kept confirming until the day of the scheduled pick-up. The day before they told me the driver had canceled and I had an option to pay $500 more and they would be able to find someone else. I kept saying that we have a contract that they will transport my car, on the agreed date and for the agreed price but in the end I was left we no choice but to pay since it was a day before the scheduled pick-up. If I was to look for alternative options, I would have to pay a premium. This is a classic bait-and-switch fraud, and I would like to make other potential customers aware, so they don't do business with them!

In a nutshell: Edible, but not worth the wait. 

After reading reviews, we were excited to try this place. We got there and waited 30 odd minutes, not insane but not quick either. While our hunger was satiated, my desire for flavor was left wanting. I got a breakfast "cuban," I suppose what makes it a Cuban is that it's kind of a panini? Lacking flavor, with pretty unappealing bread, the sandwich was blasé. The bread was basically heel pieces, too thin for me to consider a Cuban. Also, totally lacking flavor. Please get some seasoning! I ordered sides of rosemary potatoes and bacon, both of which were good. The potatoes were well cooked and seasoned deliciously. The bacon was sweet with the right amount of crunch. My companions ordered a frittata and a breakfast wrap. Again, both stated there was a lack of flavor. The ambiance was fun, people watching was entertaining, and I liked the self serve coffee.

Being a Blues fan, I had to come to STL from Chicago to catch the Blues/Blackhawks game. Although the Hawks won in overtime, we had a great time. We parked about 3 blocks from Scott Trade Center (formerly known as Savvis Center)for only 5 bucks! That's unheard of in Chi-town. The ticket scanners were efficient and the line moved along quickly. However, we were a little sticker shocked when we saw that beers costs $9/each. Really? For cheapy beer at that. Although that didn't stop us from drinking our little hearts out like good hockey fans, that's a little much. For the seats, they were pretty good. The venue is not super huge, so it looks like all the seats would have a fairly good view. Of course we stayed until the end of the game, and everyone hustled out without following the hockey players lead of pushing or punching. All in all, a clean, overexpensive sports venue.

The staff and management were fairly friendly very attentive. I got the filling that this place is a low key single spot. There were a lot of us there alone. Maybe because the prices are high not. I gave no complaints on the food i ordered but I've definitely had better for the money. I didn't order a entree because i actually wasn't supposed to be eating . On a liquid fast and only wanted to try the onion soup my friend had raved about. I don't think it was any different from any one else's onion soup. However i didn't feel like i could of just ordered that and nothing else so i ordered the crabcakes, asparagus and a cocktail  just these three items added up to 64$ which is insane for food that wasn't spectacular. At any rate i tipped my server tried to finish that drink and left. My take on this restaurant it's designed to keep whatever they think riffraff out of the establishment. Im definitely a secret shopper and had been requested by the company i work for to shop b this restaurant i just have not been able because they want two people and i don't have many that does things with me. I'm the person that does things with others. However i would of love to do this shop.  So unofficially i actually got a chance.

I love Kimberton Whole Foods!  They offer a wide selection of organic foods, teas, herbal supplements, and raw products.  The staff is always kind and willing to assist, as well.  The store is always spotless.  I love their organic pet and cleaning supplies!

A few of The mail carriers have to be the worse. They are lazy and just stuff mail in the boxes. They even placed my package on the ground next to the box....in the rain. Can't wait to move out of voorhees nj. I also get other people's mail and damnit answer the phone. Terrible terrible....never had a problem with other city's mail carriers.

Wanna know a secret? This is the place where just about every small restaurant shops for a lot of great products, AND, it's open to the public, no membership required! 

Yep. I know a lot of people that have never set foot in this store because they think it's another club like Costco or Sam's, and I'm here to dispel the myth! When you want or need larger quantities for a big party or event and don't want to deal with the longer lines and massive square footage of the other guys, this is your one stop shop! 

Extra large bottles of Sriracha? Giant jar of mayo? Whole briskets or pork shoulders for the smoker? Harder to find dairy like buttermilk? Perfect paper goods and plasticware? Fresh produce from organic spring mix, to radishes, romaine, and more? Chaffing dishes and other cooking and catering equipment? They've got it all! 

I learned about the store from friends in the industry when I was managing Z-Pie and it's been my ace in the hole ever since. The first time you come, the store can be a bit daunting, but the staff are incredibly helpful and will guide you toward any product you need. The only downside is that because restaurant owners shop here, there can be times when certain items are out of stock. If you visit a few times, you'll quickly learn what that looks like, and the staff will even tell you when deliveries happen so you can plan around it. Better yet, if you need something for a special party, Cash and Carry will even take orders! 

Now you're not going to find local meat, a huge selection of organic products, and the seafood they sell, if any, is frozen, but you'll find a whole lot of the staples available at great prices in an easy to shop atmosphere. 

I love Cash and Carry! There, I said it! Go check it out for yourself!
Excellent service, great atmosphere, and reasonably priced--all tucked away in a quaint neighborhood. Lots of Italian dishes to choose from--the pasta con pesce was excellent.

I would give 0 star if I could. The accountant had a horrible attitude and was extremely impatient. He refused to provide any advice or answer any questions until "I'm ready to pay him". Whenever I asked a question, the response I got was "I will look into it once you pay me () amount of $." I understand H&R Block is a business and therefore needs to generate profits. But clearly they put their interest first instead of its customers'. I have been a customer for four years, and will no longer be one. Btw, try to avoid Paul Raison. He clearly doesn't want to help you (a long-term customer) and intentionally withholds basic information once he knows that you are not filing ur return with him during the appointment. Paul even gave false information to make my situation sound seriously bad so that I would pay them. Do not trust him.

Great for large groups! Came here for a second time with our basketball team members and their families in between games. The helpful staff was able to quickly set up seating for our 30 members, take our orders and get our food out fast. The menu has lots of yummy options from pho to egg noodles to chow mein/birds nest style noodles. Everyone enjoyed their dishes and it was only $10 per person after our Yelp check-in 10% discount!  Sweet deal all around!
Wayne was very knowledgeable and great work. Highly recommend to anyone. Gave great advice.

Kotobuki Japanese Market and Video Store is a delightful little Asian market located along Sheldon Road, just two blocks north of Hillsborough Avenue. Kotobuki is tucked into a small L-shaped strip mall right next to Philly Phlava restaurant. Watch for the Twisteee Treat ice cream stand with it's 20-foot high swirly soft serve ice cream cone, and turn into that little shopping center.

A very nice and helpful lady named Kim runs Kotobuki, and she spent some time explaining that her store focuses on Japanese food products (packaged, canned, and frozen), along with a variety of Asian teas, spices, sauces, noodles and soups. She said that her selection is limited because she imports primarily Japanese products, and does not try to compete with the larger Asian markets.

I told her that I'd like to try making my own miso soup after watching an episode of Alton Brown's "Good Eats", and was in search of the ingredients to make the dashi soup stock, and then the silken tofu to add later in the process. Since I was the only one in the store she walked down the aisles with me and helped me to select the kombu (dried kelp sheets) and the dried bonito tuna flakes. After that she showed me a number of different styles of tofu, and ultimately helped me to pick out a block of silken tofu. She very kindly explained her personal recipe for making the dashi stock, which turned out perfectly!

I picked up some Nori, rice wine vinegar, sliced ginger, fresh wasabi, and sesame seeds for making my own sushi. I also found a bottle of fish sauce, and some fresh udon noodles for making a different type of soup with the remaining dashi stock. Finally, with Kim's help I located a bag of very tasty Japanese hard candies.

In addition to the Japanese grocery market, Kim also has a big library of Asian movies, TV shows, music videos, anime and manga for rental or purchase. I'm not really into big ol' tentacled creatures chasing after animated Japanese school girls, but if that's your thing then you'll really enjoy looking through this part of her store.

Kotobuki is a great little ethnic grocery store if you are searching for specific Japanese items that you can't find in a larger Asian market.
The prices have gone up significantly, and the portions are half the size they used to be, but the food is good and the people are nice. My fav: fish platter...

Was there yesterday and it was the WORST service ever.. Our food was cold, we waited an extreme long time for our meal , waitress was not on top of serving our table and bought us lukewarm water and no tea bag for tea refills, for the price we paid, it was so totally a waste  with the food not  great ! Ourselves as well as our family will NOT return to this establishment..
I said I would come down after I returned from Puerto Rico and try the food that my friend Shana recommended, all I can say is I will be back.

Ate lunch here on a weekend afternoon and sat at the outside bar. The service wasn't bad or anything, but definitely could have been better as the bartender spent a lot of time talking to some people at the end of the bar. On the one hand it wasn't a big deal as we never had to wait that long for everything, but on the other hand he was only tending to 7-8 people so there was no reason for my beer to be empty before another one was on the way.

I ordered the buffalo chicken wrap and it was good enough, but nothing special. I wasn't a huge fan of the fries as they could have used more seasoning. My friend got a burger and he seemed to have the same "meh" reaction to his food as I did to mine.

The best thing this place has going for it is its location right on Central in the heart of downtown St. Pete. It has a fair bit of outdoor seating and when we were there on a Sunday afternoon there was live music, which is a good thing. So while my first experience here wasn't memorable or anything, I'd be more than happy to come back.
Fantastic!  A great alternative to the sloppy, greasy, blah Mexican you're used to.  Great flavors and variety with good beer and margaritas!

Last night at the Prime Stache I enjoyed easily one of the best drinks I've ever had, a strawberry and basil infused Tito's lemonade. Yum. It was like tasting summer in a glass and apparently is the Bar Manager, Kevin's own invention. My next drink was a blueberry mojito. Also incredible. 

One of the great things about Prime Stache is that it is an American Bar in that every fork, chair, decoration, and all the alcohol was made in the US. I give management (including owner Brent Celek) a lot of credit for pulling that off. 

The staff is friendly and accommodating, the drinks are awesome, and I can't wait to try the food. Without a doubt this is my new favorite place in Old City. I highly recommend this to anyone.

While I didn't love any of the wines that we tried (we had 8 samples) I really enjoyed the Jalapeno wine (I could see it being great for cooking) and the Gewürztraminer.  I find that sometimes smaller wineries just have a lot of very sweet wines and this was not the case here -- they had a variety of wines from dry to sweet.  So the two-star rating is not because of the wine -- to each his own.  I admire what the guy is doing -- he offers a lot of variety for the size of the operation.

My biggest gripe about the place (and the reason for the low rating) was the tasting situation.  First, tasting is a dollar per sample.  I think that for amount of wine you get, that price is a little steep.  You get about a shot's worth (or less) in a little plastic shot "glass." I also think the fact that the wine is delivered in a plastic shot glass kind of ruins the experience.  I would much prefer my wine poured into a glass -- even if it is still the same small tasting amount.  It might be nice to offer a price for a flight of some sort -- even if it's preset grouping.  And maybe you could have a flight of four with two "you pick" options afterward.  For a flat fee of $5, maybe?  I think if they reworked their tasting format it could really add to the experience there.

If you're in Nashville, you can't miss Mas Tacos. The menu is small but their options are amazing. I was blown away by their tacos, specifically the fried avocado (MUST try). Beans and plantains are good and horchatas are yummy. The atmosphere was so lax and open, we loved the music. Looking forward to coming back

The latest location of the Indy-based mini-chain of breakfast/brunch spots known as Hotcakes Emporium, this spot is located next to Whiskey Business at the corner of Pendleton Pike and Oaklandon on the corner of a strip center that has always caused me a bit of angst. 

This strip center, for whatever reason, has a serious lack of wheelchair accessibility despite actually having defined accessible parking spots. It's the dumbest thing ever. Okay, maybe not the dumbest thing ever but it is seriously dumb. As I noted in my review of Whiskey Business, my visit to that business actually involved having someone help me up a step. In this case, the good news is that it appears that Hotcakes Emporium has actually had a wheelchair ramp installed up to the sidewalk. 

The bad news? Are you ready? It's smack dab in the middle of a parking spot. Seriously. It's smack dab in the middle of a functioning parking spot that sort of dangles over two parking spots and, thus, it can easily be parked over and is not adjacent to accessible parking. 

In other words, it was just a lazy, cheap, and stupid way to address a very real concern. . 

It actually took me a few minutes to see it. I was on the verge of leaving as I couldn't find a way to access the restaurant, but I finally looked and thought "They didn't?" Yep, they did. So, be aware that you could easily show up and not be able to access the restaurant. 

Inside, you encounter a small hosting area surrounded by an open concept dining room with some outside dining also available. I was seated by a friendly hostess at a 4-top table and my server quickly took my drink order. 

I browsed the extensive menu. Hotcakes Emporium serves up breakfast/lunch/dinner options, though it's actually only open until about 3pm daily. 

As I was in the mood for breakfast despite it being after 11am, I opted for french toast and sausage along with my usual unsweetened iced tea. The entire bill came in at just over $11 without tip. The iced tea was delivered promptly and one refill was received prior to meal's end. I will admit I would have preferred for an additional refill, but I also didn't ask for it. 

The french toast included three pieces - it was solid, though not spectacular. It had no real distinct flavor, but it was good and filling. The sausage included two patties - they were pretty close to abysmal. They were under-cooked, bland and kind of, well, chewy. I would definitely not order the sausage again. 

I have really wrestled with whether to call this a 2 or 3-star rating. I initially called it a 2 - primarily because I don't intend to return and when it comes down to it the difference between a 2 and 3-star for me is often the simple "Will I go back?" Between the poor accessibility, the disrespectful effort at installing a ramp, the average food and the middle-of-the-road experience, there's simply too many better options around to settle for less than what makes me happy. However, I was happy with my server and the overall atmosphere, outside accessibility issues, is warm and pleasant. I'm also somewhat swayed by the other positive reviews, with one notable exception, and realizing that my own experience was not equal to that notable exception. So, a 2-star ultimately feels inaccurate. So, having slept on this review overnight I decided to amend my rating from a 2-star to a 3-star while also expressing that those who have a guest in a wheelchair might want to consider more accessible options or plan ahead.

Where do I begin? First of all, I think it is imperative that all business travelers try a little bit like a bed-and-breakfast Wolder on work. Not only is it near and more loving to stay in establishment where the caretaker has that did interest in your happiness while you're there but hotels like Hilton and Marriott see you as a number.

I stayed in the Josephine apartment for about seven full days and one of these weekends included Valentine's Day and my birthday. Not only were the Praxis form a style and can be adjusted your dietary needs their unique every single day and full of love. Stacy the owner at caretaker of me in spoke to me every single day with text message for John for my well-being but also explained to me interesting fact about napoleon, Josephine and the entire bed-and-breakfast.

The suite had a kitchen bathroom bedroom living room and fireplace. If you stay longer than two nights I definitely recommend this suite. I had an extraordinary time and the prices were extremely affordable for anyone. I can recommend that was meant as a place to not only see inform romantic weekend and also business. I cannot see enough great things about Stacy and Napoleon's retreat! 

You will not be disappointed by your stay and will be a true believer in b&bs after you come here!

I had Amanda as my pa, and she was beyond amazing. Super nice and caring! This is the best experience I have ever had in any e.r. The rest of the staff was amazing too! Everyone cared, and tried to make my visit as comfortable as possible.

This place is awesome!!! We played some golf then settled back for some brews. Ashly G. Did an amazing job and served us promptly.  She had a great personality and didn't get angry when I asked her 100 times which BS beer I was drinking. Highly recommend the course and the club house. Thank you to Ashly for making it great !

Went here on Saturday for a girls luncheon to celebrate my birthday.  Derek went out of his way to make this a wonderful lunch!  The food was delicious and the service fantastic!  The mimosas were wonderful.  We will for sure add Caffe Torino to our list of places for girls lunch/dinner.

Sabrina's has built a little empire for a good reason. Delicious food at reasonable prices! My only quip is that they use marinara as their "red sauce" on Mexican dishes like Huevos rancheros.

If the fish tacos are on the specials menu, get them! Delicioso!

Took Boyfriend here on his birthday with his family.  The food was delicious, the service was laid back and wonderful and the ambience was just right.  I recommend having your next date night here if you don't mind stuffing your tummy - if you have to look good naked later, pass on this for the night.
Unfriendly service from Ali. The man making cheese plates was not wearing gloves and touched a lot of surfaces while making the food. The music is also very off it was rock.
Dozens of beers to choose from. If you get the soft pretzels, order them with caramel along with the other dipping sauces. You won't regret it.

Delicious salsa and chips!! The staff was fantastic and let us try all the salsas. The pico de gallo and guacamole with their homemade chips are the best! 
We're from the west coast and were spoiled with good Mexican food. This place does not disappoint!

Wow it was my second time in like ten years having a philly cheese steak and honestly it was so yummy I ate the whole entire sandwich mind you I barely eat. And it wasn't pricey. I put hot sauce ketchup and mayo because I'm Spanish but it was so so good didn't even taste the American cheese which I despise.  Def recommend when in philly for the first time

EXCITED that they built a location near me.

1st visit, ordered online, paid through app, and picked up.  At that time I was told they were out of queso but to comp me they gave me a LARGE tortilla chip!  I thought OH BOY...more chips to eat without any queso??
Got home to New Palestine and opened everything up ($37 worth) and no black beans rather white rice.  Was eating my chicken quesadilla and I had a odd feeling on my tongue...Reached in my mouth and pulled out an 8 inch curly hair.  I about vomited.

Not a good visit ...will not return...I was concerned about its location being in Beechgrove.  : /

Going to my regular location down on us highway 31 in Greenwood, IN.

We own a pizza shop in our hometown so we like to seek out pizza shops and try them out.   This seems to be the only one in downtown so we went there!  
I was a little worried as there wasnt alot of people in there.  All other places we had gone to were pretty full but we decided to try it.  
He got 2 slices - they were huge!!!!!! One pepperoni and one plain.  The dough was homemade and tasty.  He wasnt a big fan of the cheese tho.   But liked the sauce and pepperoni.  
I just got spaghetti and meatballs.   It was a big serving with 2 big meatballs. The sauce was good ... not alot but I like it that way.  The meatballs were homemade and pretty good.   
Server was really nice.   So if youre in the mood to take a break from all the southern food... go here!

I can't believe it has taken me so long to finally try Zavino. Just thinking about the years I wasted not eating this delicious food makes me want to shed a tear. 

We split the meat and cheese plate (with something called the "Baby Jesus") and the Diavolo pizza. The meats were incredible and truly worthy of the Baby Jesus name. The pizza was spicy and cheesy. The outdoor seating was perfect for people-watching. Love love love it here!

Heard it was a great beer bar and being a Quizzo fan we decided to try it out for quizzo.  Usually they get a nice crowd for quizzo but there was only a dozen or so people upstairs.  The person running the quizzo was nice and we still played, but not for prizes.  A bit lame, but OK.  Beer selection was nice.  My friends enjoyed their food.  I had the burger and it wasn't quite right.  Cooked nicely but they seemed to add some spices into the meat, maybe oregano?  Which actually seemed like it should work, but it just didn't.  Anyway.  The beer selection and atmosphere were enough to check it out again and try some other food.

On a previous visit I had Le Pig Mac, which is pretty much everything a lover of pork would want in a sammich. This trip I had the muffuletta, and as one would expect from a butcher shop, it really let the house meats take the spotlight, with the olive mix & mustard coming through subtly. Pro tip: I got there at noon on a Wednesday & it was already filling up; by 12:30 it was packed--so go early if you can.

Based on other reviews on this site, I gave this restaurant a try. It was a close walk to my hotel, and was well worth the trip. I was by myself, and they have a few cozy tables inside that they could accommodate a few singles with no wait. I was thrown off a bit because the salmon item I had seen on the online menu was not on the menu in the restaurant. The waiter was quick to mention they had been trying to get the owner to update the website for 2 years! However, not to worry, the Salmon that was on the menu was indeed with the orange glaze, so I felt much better knowing the item I came in for was what I was ordering.

Everything about this place was top-notch. The ambiance was quite pleasant, and although I was alone I didn't feel awkward as I waited for my food. The waiter was terrific and extremely attentive. And the food - from the house salad to the very tasty flatbread with chipotle humus to the well-portioned salmon with spinach and rice - all magnificent. Add in an iced tea and the bill was $26 and that was not a bad price at all. I highly recommend a stop at this restaurant.

I ordered a number of dishes, about 8.   I am a big dim sum fan and in town for several days so I planned to take it back to my room and have it for lunch the next couple days.

I ate one piece of each dish and could not eat any more.   Each dish was so salty, I actually could not have more than a bite of any single piece.    

I drank two bottles of water after leaving and still felt like I had a mouthful of sand.   

What made it a bit worse, is that when they asked me if everything was good and I told them that I was not able to eat it, they really didnt care.   I spent $40 for nothing...   

I really wanted to like it, so I am super disappointed and will not return.

We had dinner there last night and every plate was amazing! The food is served tapas style and there was something there for everyone. Not only was the food stellar, the service and attention to detail was outstanding as well. Highly recommend and will definitely be back soon.

What a great way to start off a trip.   It was a pretty bad weather out so we stopped in for a quick meal.  This was a perfect way to brighten a rainy day.   We got a roast beef, Cuban, chicken and buffalo chicken sandwich.  All fantastic and HUGE.  It is not worth your time stopping by any fast food restaurant when you have this place so close by, very reasonably priced, pretty quick.

We had went to 6 other places including Chapman where my daughter got her car and we were told sorry we cant help. My daughters window fell out but did not break. No one would tell us how to fix it or why it fell out. My daughter saw the yellow sign and we went to check it out. The worst they could tell us was no again. We met Jeff. He not only told us that he could help but it would not cost her a fortune to fix. Jeff went about fixing it and he helped her get the goop off the seats. Jeff saves the day. He was professional,  kind, and helpful. I will recommend this place to anyone who needs a windshield or window repair. Thank you so much Jeff. You are an angel.

Awesome....a fantastic throw-back ice-cream shop and grill.  

Bobbie's is such a treasure.  There are options for the whole family, including the vegetarians (black bean burgers!).  Plenty of transitional, fun ice cream treats, sundaes, and a huge variety of shakes.  

Picnic benches outside, limited parking.  But prices are affordable, fair.  Service is always prompt and friendly.  

It's a SWEET Nashville tradition.
Experience was awesome!!! Rabbit was on point, ribeye was to die for, appetizers were perfect!   Staff kinda slow but friendly.
Just got a nice-looking cut by Brandon today. These guys are good. Definitely going back.

Horrible service!!! They came up to our house three times to fix are sewer system and were extremely rude and never fixed the problem. I would not recommend using Leo's if you are having a sewer problem!!

So I went there again and it was better the second time. I avoided the greasy sandwiches and got a 4.5 star French onion soup (chef's got a thing for oil) with a house salad. It's clean bar/restaurant and is quiet during lunch. The people are just so darn friendly here.

The room was ready and cleaned nicely when we arrived.  Room was large and well supplied except short on TP.  Had to request more.  Parking fee is too high but normal for NO.  So much noise from the street we had difficulty getting to sleep.  And we were on the 17th floor.  Noise subsided about 
1:30 a.m. Bell service was excellent.

Room very musky.  The bar closed at 8 on a Sunday night even though the hotel said it closed at 10PM and there wasn't even a cook so no food.  Asked for specials, they said 2 dollar tap, 3 dollar bottles.  Asked for a Bud, Tap.  Then I was told I don't want that cause it's "been acting funny" but still had to pay $3 for a bottle.
