I had just drivin 12 hours and I was really hungry so maybe I had more than an attitude than I should have.  The two main reasons I chose that hotel was for the smoking rooms and the Champs connected.
I checked in on Sunday, checking out on Saturday and they hit my card on Tuesday for the full amount due.  Found that odd since I told them my company would be calling to put the room on their card.  
If I had to go back to Pottstown, I might stay there again...but I doubt it.

Visited on a Tuesday night and there was plenty of seating. The space itself is very modern, clean, and welcoming. The staff were all very inviting and our server was great--very knowledgeable about the food, friendly, and efficient. I ordered the mechada arepa (slow cooked skirt steak, maduros, sofrito, and mozarella cheese). The arepa was wonderful (soft and warm) and the meat was perfectly cooked and seasoned; the maduros and sofrito were also exceptional. Although I like cheese, I felt that there was a little too much of it in the arepa that somewhat took away from the other flavors of the dish. They don't do substitutions, but if you order anything with cheese on it, I would ask to make it light. All arepas come with plantain chips which were also yummy! Good experience overall!

My mother always said: "After every storm, there is a rainbow."
Why this is truly applicable since through all this Covid chaos, several new businesses have still managed to open up in town. Backstage Kitchen & Bar is one of these examples. Previously the M8trix Dance Club, Backstage now has a large outdoor space that opens up onto State Street. They are offering a new menu with bar bites, craft cocktails and local/craft beers. Whether its wings, tater tots, Mexican street corn, quesadillas, burgers, or bacon jalapeño mac 'n cheese, they got you covered.

I came here with co-workers for an outdoor Happy Hour on their first day of opening. They were offering Happy Hour deals on drinks and food. We ordered the Backstage Snack Sampler (street corn, grilled cheese, choice of chicken bites or wings, served with fries) and it was a crowd-pleaser. I had their version of a Moscow Mule called the Lucky Mule (vodka, ginger beer, mint, lime). The staff was extremely attentive--plus I even got a tour of the inside. I'm looking forward to the day when we can dine and dance inside again because Backstage will be a hotspot!

Very disappointing Mother' Day experience.  They did not seem prepared for a crowd yet they obviously had numerous reservations.  The food was okay at best, what there was of it.  Before being seated we asked if the empty platters would be refilled and we were told they were constantly replenishing.  We saw perhaps two new trays come out in an hour's time and they dismantled the omelet station leaving behind only the mess.  I felt sorry for people arriving after 1:00 thinking they were bringing mom for a nice meal.  The manager-type in a suit was pretty arrogant and obviously didn't care that chafing dishes were empty since he did nothing when they were brought to his attention by many people.  Bottom line, it seemed like they only had a brunch to cash in on the opportunity and they were ill prepared for it.  They had to have let a lot of people down.  I would never recommend this place based on this visit.

I've driven by many times and always wanted to see what this place was like.  Stopped here for dinner with my son on the way to a concert.  We weren't looking for a big meal and we found exactly what we were looking for.  I got the Tortelloni Sfatto.  A nice dish of what appeared to be fresh made tortelloni covered with braised pork.  It was very tasty and a nice portion, not the huge dish of pasta you often get which is way too much.  My son got the The Wise Guy
pizza.  A white pizza with gorgonzola, spicy salami, fresh tomato, baby arugulai.  Thin crisp crust with tasty toppings.  He enjoyed it.  While we were waiting for our meals, the waiter brought out a basket of fresh bread and gave us a saucer of olive oil and fresh ground pepper.  For 5:45pm on a Thursday, we were surprised that we were the only ones in the dining room, from start to finish of our meal.  The waiter was attentive and checked on us as expected.  Public parking lot next door costing .25/15 minutes is convenient, we lucked out and found a spot that already had 30 minutes on the meter.  I would come back, nice place.

YUM!

This place is incredibly popular. Located in the old Sushi Garden location on Broadway and Alvernon, Azian is the first AYCE Korean BBQ joint in town. I think it is quite a deal because you almost always spend about 20 dollars on yourself for food going to any other Korean place, but here you get AYCE! 

My husband and I came in on a Friday night. It was a full house. They seated us after about 5 minutes and gave us menus. I informed them we would be doing AYCE KBBQ. And they directed us to the buffet. There is a serious assortment of meat here. I recommend the korean short ribs, spicy pork, korean bacon, ribeye, and korean steak. I think I would have gotten the shrimp if Aaron G didn't have an aversion to seafood. Don't forget to grab a couple pads of butter before you head back to your BBQ!

Next to the meat station, all the little Korean appetizers are out. Kimchi, radish, sauteed spinach, potstickers, cucumbers.... etc. I should have taken pictures! There are lettuce wraps, chap chae, and rice as well. There is also a section with desserts. Picture a Chinese buffet dessert section.

We loaded up out plates and proceeded to cook, be merry, then moved on to self loathing for having consumed so much! The service wasn't the greatest. It really didn't need to be. I wasn't ordering sushi or drinks. It seemed like they were able to keep my water full. So that is a plus for them. We definitely ate our money's worth. I really want to go back with a big group of friends. Eat, drink, watch football, talk, and laugh for hours. 

In summation, give this place a shot. Don't bother eating  a la carte.... I saw the waiters get all the food from the buffet anyway. Sushi however, is always made fresh and is very low priced!

Love this place.
sandwiches are great, really all of them. The Bistro, Chicken Cesare, Jersey and Sicilian are the best. They give you a frequent customer card that adds up to a free sandwich at the end. Plus they're always selling next day bread for basically nothing. I would go here almost every day for lunch.
Healthy, great selection, and easy on the wallet.

Our visit here, unfortunately, was a visit to the ER. My wife had gotten an allergic reaction, couldn't stop coughing, and her throat was swollen to the point she couldn't breathe. Holy Redeemer was the closest ER to us so we made the trip at 2am. 

As soon as we walked in we told the front desk that my wife was having an allergic reaction and couldn't breathe. She was standing right there gasping for breath. Rather than phoning an RN right away, the receptionist made us stand there while she gathered all of our info - name, numbers, insurance, etc. before she finally got on the radio and asked a nurse to come out. 

Inside the rooms, everyone was very nice and helpful. Lots of new tech hooked up to monitor patients and doctors were knowledgeable. There was a long wait, but it's the ER, so that's expected. It's not first come first served, but whoever's condition is worse gets seen first. So no complaints there. 

Just so disappointing on the check-in as I've had better treatment from an urgent care facility during a crisis. Last time this happened, urgent care (Patient First down on Street Road) took my wife in and got her triaged immediately while I took care of paperwork. The amount of time I had to wait (and the pain my wife was in) just waiting for someone to type her data into a computer was beyond me.

We were driving through town and stopped here for a bite to eat.  We were trying to decide if we get it to go or eat in.  When we got there we were very impressed with the measures they had taken to protect both staff and patrons.  They had ample outdoor space that was well spaced, provided mask to anyone that did not have one and used single use menus, and had hand sanitizer.  Staff was friendly and service was fast and efficient.  Food and drinks were excellent (we had the brisket and ribs). Wish we lived closer because we would make this one of our regular places.  Glad we decided to give it a try and dine in!!

For the money, Taco Bus is the best taco place  in tampa. I always get the Cochinita Pibil taco because it is the best I have had in the US. This time I had the shrimp taco, which was perfectly cooked and delicious, the sauce on the shrimp is just right too. Most places add too much of a mayo based sauce, not here. 

Speaking of sauce I highly recommend the red hot sauce (available on demand). It's not too spicy but goes really well with most of the dishes. 

One note, this location does not have a liquor license, but they have Mexican coke, horchata, and agua de piña, Which are all worth  the swap.

We have been coming here for years to listen to music. We heard some old country, it was ok and the band was pretty good. Beer is cheap and the people are friendly..

stayed for a little while--was part of our bar hop..

Thanks to this amazing woman-owned business, my days waiting in line at the post office are pretty much over.

I found them when I needed a notary public, but discovered that they have a lot more to offer with a lot less hassle.  They ship by USPS and Fed Ex, have  post office boxes, all kinds of packing materials, and do copying, faxing, etc. And while you're waiting (usually not long), there are racks of greeting and post cards to look at. Everyone in the store is friendly and knowledgeable, and they keep the line moving. You have to pay to park on Magazine, but I always find free parking on the side streets, and the neighborhood is cute. I love this place!

For 6 years running went there for my birthday. If you reserve for your birthday, they print your name on the menu and take a photo. Great filet and the sides are all good! Service has gone down so I don't go as much. Beautiful restaurant and nice wine selection.

We've been twice in the last month and have really liked it both times. Texas and Southwest style food, very casual atmosphere, outdoor dining and quite reasonable prices. I've had the barbecued seitan and the shrimp burritos with pineapple salsa. The s'mores bread pudding seemed like it still needs a bit of work to become a really great dish, but tonight we tried their peach cobbler, which was very nice, served up in a hot skillet. Service outside was a bit slow, but it was a great place to hang out and watch the world go by on a beautiful night.

Sat at the bar at this relatively new mexican spot in a very different fairmount ave than that was there just two years ago.  Paralleling the theme of most new restaurants on fairmount, the decor is hipster-esque and overall very appealing.  We sat at the intentionally dented copper topped bar, however, the restaurant area seemed pretty nice from far, adorned with many people with inverse relationships between them and their eyeglass rim thickness.  None the less, a chill enough vibe to randomly stop in while being nice enough for a date with a girl who isn't in the business of digging for gold. 

On to the drinks and food.  The beer menu was ehh.  The tequila selection and margarita list seemed extensive.  We started off with the nachos.  Though they don't come with chicken, the chef was kind enough to throw some in.  While nachos are nachos, their amazing open fire oven giving the nachos a smoky flavor made regular nachos pretty spectacular.  Plus, the portion is huge.  If you have $20, you have dinner for 2 any night of the week splitting the nachos with a couple beers.  

On another visit we shared the hamachi ceviche, which was spectacular. Super fresh yellowtail, mixed with fresh avacado, tomato, and not overpowered with citrus, served on a crispy fried cracker of some sort that made eating ceviche so much easier. The boneless ribs were satisfactory, but I wouldn't get them again, I like my ribs traditional style. The Elote loco or grilled corn with a lime mayo, cotija cheese etc was outstanding. Don't let the mayo throw you off, it's not weird. And before I forget, the chili margarita is banging.

Looking forward to sampling the rest of the menu.

In my opinion, this place used to be a lot better than it is now. I mean, there IS a reason for it's noteiriety. I remember coming here for a dinner with an ex-boyfriend's HUGE Italian family many years ago, and they simply loved this place. And they were all 100 percent Italian...and it was really delicious. 

But, yeah, any recent trip and the food isn't quite as flavorful, the staff isn't quite as friendly...it's rather sad. They are going to have to kick it up a notch to keep their good name.

Yeap 3 stars! 

I've been dying to go here for a while. Last nite, we finally did. Love the bakery, super cute inside. Love the decor, very inviting. Not cheap but understandable since it's in Soho. The only thing I didn't like was how uninviting the baristas were, there were no hellos or welcome to our shop. They will however, answer any questions you have about their menu.

Some things to try: 

* Affogato: despite mispronouncing the word, this expresso drink was AMAZING! Expresso topped with your choice of ice cream, I picked salted caramel. Highly recommended. $4
* Orange blossom tea: didn't get a chance to try it but heard it was delicious. They make it hot and iced. $3.50
* Ice cream float: the ice creams are amazing here, they make it fresh every day. Not cheap - $8. It's good but not worth the price.
* Salted caramel cupcake: fluffy cupcake with mound of frosting, drizzled with caramel. Good but not my favorite. $4
* Pineapple upside down cake: the cake was good but I wish they didn't add so much frosting in between the layers. It was too sweet. $5
* Chocolate Pi: buttery crust with vanilla custard, chocolate and bananas. I am not a big banana fan but this was a home run for me. Not super sweet. Loved the different textures. $6.28 (I didn't understand why they charged 6.28 until I got home, drank lots of water, got rid of the excess sugar in my system. Pi + Pi = 6.28). You're welcome

This place is cute, but small. Very small. Has a front and side patio and a very small bar that maybe seats 7-10? Very intimate seating inside. Luckily we got a table at the front, by the door and greater, which we didn't want because of all the traffic but worked out since that part of the restaurant seemed to have more space.

Booze. Their Bloody Mary is very good. $9.00). Rich tomato base, spicy and boozy. They are known for their champagne cocktails but also have local craft beer on draft. Ask the server what's on tap for the menu doesn't always reflect changes. When we were there 3 Floyds Yum Yum was on draft, but where wasn't it that weekend....

Regular coffee is self serve. Think Panera's setup. It's a little cumbersome since it's right by the door but seems to works.

Food. For a starter we got a small Duck Fat Pommes Frites ($4.95 fries fried in duck fat.) This is a French restaurant so you might have to Google a few things to see what they are. hey were surprisingly just okay. I had potato chips fried in duck fat in Wooster, Ohio and they. were. amazing. These just tasted like french fries-good but meh. A small is a perfect size for two to share.

The Smothered Fried Chicken ($14.95 toasted english muffin served open-faced with mushroom duxelle and a PERFECTLY COOKED [thank you!] sunny side up egg) was a lot of food. You're getting close to two chicken breasts here which is a lot of food after an app and a few drinks. If you like mushrooms you'll love this rich, well done duxelle.

The seasonal Caprese salad ($9.95) was perfectly stacked fresh mozzerella, tomatoes and basil, drizzled with olive oil and basalmic. Very nice and fresh. Small, but I was still able to share it.

The Benedict Crepé ($13.50 ham, gruyère cheese, sunny side up egg and bernaise sauce) was VERY good. Fluffy and rich and just the right amount of cheese and sauce.

This place is a must try but definitely call in reservations or use OpenTable. If you don't they'll take your number and text you when your table's ready, so you can browse some of the neighboring shops.

Hard to find. It's in a newer shopping center off 267, but we caught the banner in back that made us pull in and try it. 

The decor is more of a modern American diner look rather than the typical bright Mexican places I'm used too, but it's ok. Many TVs on the wall playing sports, which kind of gives it more of the bar-feel, but still ok for families.

We tried the Beef burrito, Enchilada Suizas and Cheese Enchiladas and Taco. Very good. Plates came out hot.

No complaints. Nice service. Not bad but not exceptional either.

The worst Round Table ever, which is horrible bcs I am a RT fundy and its the only one close to my house. Its always dirty, disorganized and they skimp on toppings so bad I have literally received pizzas where slices were bare. 

I used to blame bad employees. But considering its always like this I can only blame the management for the consistent cheapness. Everybody in the area avoids this place for the same reasons ... but instead of fixing any of this they raised their prices.

I got owen for acrylics and never have i ever gotten someone that has dedicated so much time and effort into perfecting my nails.  They lasted for so much longer than any other salon i've been to in the past.  Very pleased!

20 vendors market, small but with great produce. Most of vendors grow their own produce.  I am vendor at Broadripple farmers market and Carmel farmers market , but I really like this market; friendly people and great variety of fresh produce. Parking may be a little hard.

Fascinating subject matter here if you're interested in the human body and medical abnormalities. The museum is small, but I'd give the content and the thoughtful curation 5 stars. Our visit was on a Sunday, and maybe it was busier than normal, but it was almost intolerably crowded. There's no obvious "right" way to proceed through the exhibits, so people were moving in all directions. Many of the items on display require reading information posted next to them or looking closely at small items-- almost impossible with what felt like hundreds of others jostling to read and look at the same things. It made for a frustrating experience-- we gave up after about an hour.
They stole my friends shoes. Claimed they donated them but wouldn't provide proof. Lots of consignment shops around town. Go elsewhere Buyer Beware!!!!!!!!

Average.

Hotel has a great location. Center City, close to lots of attractions and transportation.

The hotel itself is in a beautiful old building.  That being said, it's an old building.  Elevators can be slow, rooms can be cramped and noisy.  Some overhauls wouldn't hurt.

The rooms are fairly standard RI city rooms.  

The staff is friendly and helpful.  However, they don't appreciate your gold or platinum status much here.  

The breakfast is less than average.  Yes, they have the same old runny eggs, meat, and assorted bread products. Meow ever, the bread options are less than appealing (would it hurt to spend a few extra bucks to get fresh whole grain bread).  No vegetables to be found.  The evening meals are not worth attending.  Again, less than appeal "salad" and things they called burgers but did not appear to be any sort of meat I recognize.  The best thing on the spread was the potato chips.  I don't know why Marriott can't make a little more effort in their RI meals.  It would go a long way to appealing to frequent travelers considering a switch to a healthier chain like Westin.

Cleaning the rooms seems to be hit or miss here.  Half the time I don't get my room cleaned- I honestly thought maybe it was their thing not to clean rooms...

If I have to I stay here, but I do think it's time to explore more options in Philly.
Every time I am here in Reno this is my go to spot for milk tea. Their Assam Black Milk Tea is my favorite and it's a cute little spot.

The service is mediocre.  Only one girl is professional- young, short blond Caucasian with glasses.  I pray you are lucky enough to order when she's working.  Excellent person.  The others have a lot to be desired.  Unfortunately- after my last visit on 9/10/19 I found a strand of hair in my food.   Very upset and was disgusted!   Disappointed at this location.   I just pray management tighten up on the cleanliness code with respect to hairnets.

Amazing prices and super fresh crawfish and shrimp at this little local gem.  
Boiled crawfish 4$ a lb, nice and spicy.
Huge boiled gulf shrimp, ours were chilled. Truly amazing, best shrimp we've ever had.
The fried green tomatos were hot and crispy and I bet the fried oysters would be great with that crust.

Definitely recommend this place, our favorite restaurant we ate at in New Orleana.

Save your time and money. This business provides mediocre, over priced service. There sales person will promise anything and not deliver. The owner has no sense of customer service whatsoever. Their work crew is one dimensional in that they only high pressure blast scale and do not offer chemical application with brushing to do an acceptable job of removing scale on pebble tech. The grout lines around the tile still has scale. A much better job was promised and not delivered. Beware ! Sorry I could rate them lower.

So, it's a brewery; concrete floor & aluminum kegs. But, it's also a gathering place for friends & families with fooze ball, giant Jenga, corn hole, yard size Connect Four, chess, checkers, darts, shuffleboard, dominos, and numerous board games. Thee beers are delicious and the beertender service is good. Perfect place to chill with your friends for some first class beers and casual competitive sport.

I rented from this place recently to ski mount rose. These guys could not have been nicer! incredibly friendly, super helpful.  They operate their business the old fashion way. Incredibly nice, great customer service. Nice rental equipment. 
These guys are AWESOME. will never go anywhere else for ski rentals & purchase equipment.

I looked for months for a florist in Santa Barbara who would do a bridal bouquet and two boutonnieres for me for my small little wedding---no luck.  Other florists did not "do small events".  My photographer suggested Kaleidoscope Flowers which I had not tried because I assumed they would also turn me down.  Felipe is AMAZING.  He responded quickly and without any hesitation took my order and sent me a VERY detailed email of each and every flower he was going to use, how they would be arranged, and information on the size of the bouquet etc.  The price was very reasonable as I had been quoted for far higher for the same bouquet by another florist in the area.  The bouquet was breathtaking.  It was bright, the flowers were beautiful and it was put together exactly the way I wanted it (no greenery showing).  Thank you thank you thank you!!!!!!!

I was really excited to check this place out, but the attitude I got from our server was not the most comforting.

I am totally and completely a cheese/charcuterie newbie. I have no idea how cheese and meats and oils go together, and what matches with what. And when I told our server, he seemed less than willing to share even a little bit of information. Maybe I would enjoy Garces more if I didn't feel constantly judged throughout our entire meal. :(

With that said, our food was good. Not amazing. But nothing especially terrible either.

Things to try:
-Housemade Mozzarella: though I'm not an expert, this is the real deal!
-Funghi Flatbread: I am a sucker for mushroom pizzas! Supposedly made with duck fat. Though I pride myself in being able to taste most food details, this just tasted like regular flatbread to me..

I liked this place a lot ! They had an interesting tea, honey hibiscus . The place was kind of industrial looking on the inside I liked it was very simple and clean . They give you bread and oil for the table that was amazing the bread was like freshly baked and the oil had an interesting flavor to it . We got for appetizer the fried eggplant which was really really good and came wit a lot , I got the Chicken parm with a side of fettuccine omg the pasta was amazing freshly made you could tell , the people who say that this is not a good place and compare to what ever 2 star places they had mentioned in their reviews are nuts and just don't know flavor . This by far was the best pasta i ever had . I also got My brother a to go order and he said the same thing it was the best Italian and pasta he's had and he's a pasta snob lol

Worst experience ever! I attended the Sunday, July 10th pool party as a guest of my friend- who is a member. The wait staff ignored us when we sat at the table for about half an hour. Then a waitress walked by and smiled and waved, but continue to walk by and not wait on us! We finally left to go eat at a place down the street. We came back (since we already paid the cover charge) and the horrible service continued... I tried to get a drink at the bar... The bartender ignored me for about 20 minutes, then finally someone took my order (RUDELY!) and charged my card.. but did not make the drinks...after 10 minutes  I told a second bartender what I wanted and he too didnt make the drinks..and a third .... finally after complaining-the bartender who I originally took my order made my drinks. he was very rude.. as if I was bothering him.. (I heard him talk to another person the same way).. he then spilled part of a drink on me and seemed a lil drunk , annoyed and frusturated. After all the agrivation  the mojito was horrible and not worth it.... Also, the clientele was not the best....I saw a women lift off another womens top and basically do things I cant even post here but have pics to prove it happened...  this is my first time leaving a review for a place. It was so bad I had too!! Don't bother wasting your money on this place!

The service is generally great at the bar. The rolls are really hit and miss though- a $14 roll consisting of 8 small pieces is annoying. Never know how much to order depending on who is cutting the fish or preparing the dish.

Chef Cook's food is amazing (Oyster and Caviar poboy gets 2 thumbs up!) and the location, atmosphere and service were outstanding. We will definitely be back soon! Locals and tourist will both be impressed. Can't say enough good things about Gris-Gris.

I love art house cinemas - at all Ritzes you can get coffee and scone, in addition to typical movie house fare. When Landmark bought the Ritzes in Philly (Five, Bourse and East) they didn't change anything or raise prices. Still a great place to see art house films. I never hear noisy kids or people chattering through movie like at other area theatres since this crowd is sophisticated and film snobs into movies. I like all 3 Ritzes  and never have issues. I have come for the film festivals as well.

It was pretty good, homemade tortillas and good food overall. The delivery itself was also rather quick, no complaints here. The rice seemed a bit bland to me, could have used some more spices. The rice seemed to be the instant Spanish yellow rice you buy in bags but everything else was good.

It's an OK hotel: super convenient location, very comfortable beds, but minor housekeeping issues that were-- to the hotel's credit-- quickly resolved. It would have been nice, though, not to have to ask to have towels replenished (if all the towels are dirty and removed, then please give me new ones!). Or, when I checked in, to have shampoo, conditioner, and other stuff in the bathroom. But a call to guest services fixed this.

The best part of the room: the extremely comfortable bed and ample pillows. 

Rooms have flat screen TVs, but most channels (none of them?) are HD, so the picture quality leaves something to be desired. There is Showtime.

Wifi is $3.95 for one hour for $9.95 for a day (until 3pm the next day, regardless of when you start). There is, however, free internet at the computers in the Sheraton Link area in the lobby. You can also print boarding passes and the like there. 

There is a fitness center with various cardio machines and weights, and a pool, but no sauna or whirlpool. Some of the public spaces look a bit tired. It looked less modern than many newly renovated Sheratons I've stayed at: a bit 1988 all over.

I booked it off of Priceline (it's an option under 3 1/2*), and for the money I paid, I was satisfied with my stay.  It's not the nicest Sheraton I have ever stayed at, but a decent option.

I love the atmosphere here. I went to check this place out after a really long day at work and I was exited as soon as I smelled the smoked meats. I got brisket, pulled pork, fries and mac and cheese. 

The pulled pork was the star of the plate. It was flavorful, smokey, tender and not soggy. The brisket had a decent smoke ring and was pretty tasty. The fries were alright, but I wasn't into the mac and cheese. It seemed a little strange and soggy to me. I'm pretty picky about my mac and cheese though, so who knows. It could just be me. 

I thought I saw Jack White while we were eating, but I cleaned off my glasses and it turned out to just be se dude who really wanted to look like Jack White...

Anyway... I really enjoyed the atmosphere of the place. They, evidently, have some thing where they only make so much meat per day and when the meat is gone it's gone. I didn't experience this, but I don't think it's THAT weird. It's kind of a cool concept. 

The place was crazy busy when we went, but the staff was super friendly and attentive. We had great service. I would go back for sure. I just won't get the Mac and cheese next time ;)
Needed brakes redone.  They were helpful, quick, and inexpensive - my three criteria for service!
Delicious food. Great price. All the paneer dishes are so good.  Staff is awesome who provided great service. I'll be coming back for some more paneer.

What a gift it is to have this place in our community.  They are super knowledgable and friendly and they sell very high quality products.  I took my kids to four shoe stores today and three wanted to sell me flimsy overpriced junk.  While BM running co is not cheap it is a decent value given the quality.  Both my kids run around A LOT and I trust their advice 100%.  I consider myself a running company fan forever.

Excellent excellent bread, foremost. It takes a regular sandwich to the next level. It John's Roast Pork Is the Husband, Carangi's is the loving wife. The perfect spine on the break sandwiches never bust open down the middle. 

Lets talk tomato pie. I've just had my favorite. It was hands down excellent. I got a slice also with cheese which was fantastic. It was quite unique. I wish I had a place like this near me.

Good for one time experience. One the high side for so called ice cream.
Hard to taste ice cream.

Purchased season passes. While its understandable the experience is not the same due to COVID, you guys need to represent what is being offered and not mislead your customers. Example #1....Dine with Elmo. We went on Monday and purchased a autograph book thinking that while the characters may not interact with the kids(which you're website does not say that they have to be 6' from the characters but again due to COVID I understand)I did think that at least my daughter could have the characters sign her book which you are selling in your shops. After being told they don't do that anymore we spoke to the entertainment manager who reached out to the souvenir shop and said to take them down as they should not be selling them. He was nice and took the book to the back and had them sign. But those autograph books should have never been for sale to begin with.

Also when we first arrived as season pass holders we reserved for both the morning and afternoon sessions. After the morning session we left and waited in the car for the afternoon session to start. When we were at the ticket checker she scanned the afternoon session and season passes and said we were there earlier in the day. She called a manager and she said "for future reference you cannot attend the morning session and then come to the afternoon as well." I then told her my family has season passes and I reserved both sessions. She then said "oh ok then you're good". I didn't appreciate the manager implying that I was trying to sneak back in the park again without making a reservation when I had it right on my phone and the ticket taker scanned the reservations!! Train your staff better and stop hiring so many young kids who have no clue how to do their jobs!! 

There were only 3 rides open and not many activities going on at the park. I sincerely hope this spring and summer they have more things going on for the amount of money I paid for season passes for myself and my family as well as other people.
Nice place but way over priced. Xmas cookies double the price of most places and not even real Xmas decorated cookies. Just regular ones.

I had heard good things about the Royal Tavern for a long time but hadn't made the trip there.  I finally pulled the trigger and stopped in for dinner and a beer.  First off, this place is definitely  an older bar that hasn't seen a lot of renovations, but that is the charm of it.  

The beer list is solid with a host of crafts on tap.  The menu is a pub food with a modern twist.    I had the Vegan Sloppy Joe sandwich, which was good - not great, but good.  I was actually fairly disappointed in the sandwich.  Mostly, when i hear about Royal Tavern I hear how good the food is, but I was not that impressed.  

The service was good and the prices were fair.  I will probably come back and try some other food options since I have always heard such good things.
Sooo good! Got the flank pho with round eye, and a thai ice tea with boba. Both were amazing. Definitely going back. The staff was really nice too. :)
Just got delivery from them. Excellent food. Great prices and they could not have been nicer on the phone and in person with delivery. Highly recommend.

Had a long week that culminated with a $90 trip to Randall's Wine and Spirits last night. This St. Louis liquor paradise has just about everything you need to get your party started. 

Located right off Jefferson, getting in and out of Randall's is pretty easy. They have a vast parking lot. Be warned that if you go around Halloween or St. Patty's day, the parking lot can be pretty full with people going to Johnnie Brock's for costumes and party supplies.

Walking into Randall's can feel overwhelming due to the vast space that is packed with alcohol. They have a great selection that can seem daunting. How will you find what you're looking for?! The employees of Randall's are ALWAYS willing to help. I find their staff to be very friendly and ready to give expert suggestions. 

I really love the huge selection of craft beer Randall's has in their store. They do a great job of stocking local craft brews so that you can grab all of your favorites in one stop. There are also really awesome beers from around the country that you won't find at any supermarket. 

I'm not giving them five stars because their credit card machines require you to sign using your finger, and then there is not hand sanitizer for you to use afterwards. Other than this small COVID-era mis-step, they are handling precautions well with staff wearing masks and protective barriers between you and the cashier.

Every time I go in there you can cut the tension with a knife, I hate it! First of all they try to rush you no matter which location you're at. I know it's sort of a fast food place but I don't like it when I get looks (the stink-eye) along with attitude when they aren't happy with your "speed ordering skills". The last time I went in there I took charge when the lady tried to manage the ordering process because they do the same thing every time - they talk too fast, ask too many questions and confuse between which sandwich they're talking about when you have two or more types on the order. The sandwiches aren't bad but I hate how they portion some of them out. Like with the tuna salad, I watch them scoop it out but then take a knife and level off the spoonful. Maybe that's just a peeve, I hate not getting my money's worth so I love it when a restaurant piles the stuff on.  Anywho, by the time you're at the checkout their smile/smiles are gone and they're more smug than a peeved Paris Hilton. I only go there because others I'm close to want to get food from there and my situation dictates that I go there to get it for them.

After being chased homeward by a snowstorm, my bf and I realized we were starving and lost on a tangle of farmy backroads so the closest town with a good variety was going to be Phoenixville. Taste of the Tropics popped up on yelp and though neither of us have ever had Filipino food before, it sounded warm and very inviting. 

It was nearly 3pm on a Saturday when we arrived and so it was no surprise no one was eating there. They appeared to be setting up for an evening event, so some tables were still not cleaned up, while others were being covered in fresh tablecloths. There is a dancefloor up front, karaoke machine and the tv was showing some very festive event/parade. The woman, who I think is the owner, seated us and gave us water and menus.  

She suggested a few different dishes, which was nice, especially as it was such a big menu with items I was not familiar at all with. The one chef came out and answered some questions too, as I wondered what a "red sauce" was on the shrimp pancit palabok. We settled on sharing a sisig appetizer, I got pancit palabok and my bf ordered a spicy lamb coconut curry. 

The sisig came out really quickly, and it was a huge plate, sizzling and smelling completely intoxicating. The pork belly was cut into small chunks and cooked to crispy-spicy-caramelized perfection in the vinegar sauce with onions. We agreed it was insanely rich, completely heavenly and if it came with some rice we probably could have split is and both been full on that alone. 

Before we were even halfway through it, my giant entree came out. A mountain of thin rice noodles surrounded by a moat of thick, creamy reddish-orange achuete  sauce, topped with sliced hard-boiled egg, shrimp, crumbled pork rinds and scallions. It tasted really amazing, I had asked for it to be extra spicy. It had a great heat, and the flavor of the shrimp paste in the sauce was really smoky, and I had the sense of there being cheese and/or cream in it when actually there is no dairy at all. 

My bf's entree came out shortly after mine. The slow-cooked lamb shank was glazed in a thick dark sauce that tasted almost as much curry as almost a mole. It was tender and delicious, served with rice and sauteed vegetables. He loved it. 

We were both stuffed to the gills, for the price the portions were enormous. Now that I have done some more reading on Filipino food I really want to go back and try some different things, probably something a with a little more veggies, not something so rich. Service was extremely attentive and friendly and clearly they took great pride in their culinary work. As well they should. 

They mentioned there was Sunday breakfast/brunch which piqued my interest!

Not my favorite BBQ in St Pete but definitely worth a try or two. You can tell from your first step in the door that this is a family owned restaurant. Brothers working behind the counter, younger kids, what appears to be the owners son laughing about going out to buy more beer? Ok...I don't need your family drama in the background while I eat but regardless it made for some entertainment. 

I've had the BBQ brisket sandwich (great) and what was supposed to be a BBQ chicken wrap. The Chicken was good however there was absolutely no taste of anything BBQ. It was smothered in ranch and cheese? 

Service is quick, order at the counter and they bring it to your seat. A lot of people seem to come in and out for takeout. There's plenty of articles covering the walls from previous newspaper articles raging about the place but I wonder if it's only there for a boost of confidence in a product that may have gone down hill over the years.

It's worth a try, but i'd much prefer Rib City on the other side of town.

May not in the best location with not many parking options without taking a walk. But their food is great!! Great tacos!! I liked their nachos better than tacos I think. When I'm near downtown Tucson and want something to eat, I'd come here again for sure!
I stand with you! Do not be intimidated by cowards that crawl out in the darkness or cloak themselves in white sheets.

Walmart Saga:

Sunday order groceries to be delivered Tuesday.

No groceries on Tuesday.  We reach out to the store and are told the driver left with half the order, they called him back because they were still shopping the order, and we should get groceries sometime tonight.  A follow up call resulted in being put on hold 15 minutes and being hung up on three times.

We reached out online.  First, I contacted the "wrong" customer service.  Second, I contacted the "right" customer service.  Our issue was escalated and we should have heard something last night or today... Nope!

I called the store today about the yet to be delivered groceries.  I was transferred and no one picked up.  I called again and was transferred to management.  She was going to find out what was going on and call me back...  Nope! 

I called the numbers I was given the previous evening.  Surprise!!!  They are the wrong phone numbers.  I do get transferred to someone that can help.  Only she can't help.  I am told I have to call or go up to the store for my refund.  They don't answer the phone at this Walmart.  So I go up to the store.

I wait in line at customer service.  I am referred to another person that cannot help.  She gets the manager.  I am told this is a problem they are having because Door Dash does their deliveries and steal orders from time to time.  I am assured that once a complaint is filed with Door Dash, our order will be refunded.  We will receive an email shortly...  Nope!

I call to follow up and am told I can call in the morning to speak to the manager.

All we want is our money back.

A very big hotel, but the staffs in the front desk if definity not enough. The room is small than the picture. The worst part for us is, as their diamond member, we did not get the free breakfast buffet in the morning.

Went on a whim and reservations are *highly* recommended.  We got lucky when someone didn't show up.

The food is certainly very good and you get what you pay for...seating is tight and makes for a crowded experience.
Great for solo dining! They have a dine in counter around the corner. Came here twice while visiting Philadelphia cause the food was that good. Waitstaff was helpful and the food was delicious.

Well.... I have been a customer for almost a year now. I go back every two weeks without fail.... Until yesterday. I called, make an "appt" at 4:15. Showed up at 4:13 and my nail tech was just starting on a new customer. I was sat in the pedicure chair. Waited there for 45 min..... And got up and left. 
I understand that I am a loyal customer so maybe they thought that i wouldn't complain and just wait, they took advantage. I wish I could go back, but at this point I feel like my time was disrespected so I will be finding a new salon in Tampa. 
Every single one of the techs here is great. They do an amazing job. Never had any issues in the past, but yesterday was too much.

Thank you to Mark and Larry's Shell Station! Absentmindedly I lost my bank card when I was pumping gas yesterday. When I drove over this morning it had been safely put away. What a relief. Neighborhood businesses are why Tucson is a great town. Thank you!

I actually met the owner Brian Flowers before he opened Tabletop Café, as he was advertising himself at a POGOB boardgame meetup event. I'm pleased to find that the TTC is well-spaced, adequately lit, if a bit overly warm and lacking temperature control (if you're a thin guy like me that spews BTUs, you'll die in the summer months!). Spacious hardwood tables greet you as you walk in, with reservations available. You sign up at the counter and are free to float between tables, with your entry fee and all consumables paid for at the end of your visit.

The most attractive feature of TTC is the large boardgame collection as well as the flat $5/day for all-day gaming ($2.50/hr at rival Hexagon Café on Whyte). The customer service is efficient and polite, but not necessarily friendly. Orders may not be taken from tables generally. You must make your way up to the counter and interrupt play (or hope there isn't a queue after taking your turn in your game). 

Long hours (up to 1am on Friday and Saturday night) are quite a delight as you may choose whenever is convenient for you to go solo or with a group of friends. Despite my belief that this out of the way location would be an issue for most gamers (as many are not vehicle owners), I was pleasantly surprised that the location is only a 5 minute walk from a #8 stop. 

As with most boardgame cafés, TTC suffers from more emphasis on the boardgame and not enough on the café. Overpriced and undersized portions for all food (as well as lacking diversity), though I found it of good quality and quite edible (of course, outside drinks and food are not permitted. Just like movie concession, this is where they make their money!) 

All in all, a fun hangout place at great prices, just eat before you go or head out for a meal and come back to more gaming afterwards!

This place has grown ever more popular, as evidenced by the continuous line of customers I encountered on a recent weekday morning.  I felt a little sorry for the lady in front of me who ordered a iced soy milk latte, and got the last of the carton, not quite enough for a full serving. Running out of soy milk at 8:00 on a Thursday seems like a circumstance that would be fairly easy to prevent. But perhaps there was an unexpected surge in demand. The service was friendly, though it took about ten minutes to work through three parties in front of me, and the line was even longer when I turned to leave. I enjoyed the "blueberry pocket" pastry and sipped my way slowly through a good latte.  The best part: I went home with a pound of beautifully roasted beans.  Notice that Goshen Coffee Co. embosses the "roasted on" date on the top seal of the bag.

Mans asked me if I wanted rice "on the side," proceeded to charge me 7$, and gave me so much attitude when I politely brought it to his attention. The charge was resolved but it toutally soured our night. In addition, I told the hostess it was my friend's birthday, and it was disregarded completely. We would not recommend virago nor dine here again. Feel free to come if you particularly like being disrespected for the duration of the meal!

My early dinner at Morimoto was fantastic, and the service alone would've justified the price. Managed to get a table for 2 at 5 PM on a weekday without a reservation and were taken care of from the minute we sat down. Got a great run through of the menu, attentive water service, and consistent check ins.

I ordered a spicy salmon roll and tobiko sashimi, splitting an asparagus side with my friend. The sushi was room temperature instead of refrigerator cold, an excellent sign of quality. Rice was flavorful and the cuts of salmon were nice and thick. Tobiko sashimi came on a spoon rather than wrapped in nori stacked on rice, but made for a fine presentation. Asparagus side was excellent, definitely one of the best preparations of grilled asparagus I'd ever had, just wish the portion was bigger for the price. I wouldn't recommend splitting it.

Great visit, would make for a perfect date night location.

2 experiences in 1 week one good one bad...so 1st I like this place, the sushi was really good could have been a lil bigger but aside from that it was good.  Within my 2 visits I've had their Samson roll, vic special roll, fried sweet potato roll, philly roll, eel, and California roll...my absolute favorite out of everything on this list was the Samson roll and next would be the Vic roll...only thing is I think the Vic roll might have made me sick . So they offer a 3 roll for 11 buck special and on that day I selected 3 rolls and ordered the Samson roll this was a Tuesday...everything is fine! I go back Friday after work order the Vic special and the sweet potato roll...the next morning and all weekend me and my toilet became really close!  My personal thoughts about it was that it was the Vic roll because it's topped with lobster salad...maybe the salad was sitting out too long or it wasn't cold enough in the area where they keep the ingredients but that's the only thing I can think of.  If anything would make me sick I thought it would be the Samson roll because that has fresh salmon and fresh tuna on it but nope I was fine and an entire 3 days passed with no symptoms from Tuesday's visit.  I understand sometimes food just doesn't agree with us at times but 2.5 days of having gas, bloating and making brown stew in the toilet kinda makes you think twice before you decide to go back. I don't think I'm scarred for life from the place, but I'm scared to order the Vic special roll again...now that the scary stuff is out the way other things to know is that the place is small so no huge groups at all but perfect for maybe 3 people in a group at most, it's a byob spot and the area it's located is very nice!

Terrible customer service. My husband went to this car wash a few weeks ago and witnessed some racist behavior by a staff member of the Auto Spa at Swedes Plaza. As my husband was vacuuming his car, an employee (older white man) went up to my husband and said "look at those two over there" pointing to two black men vacuuming their car. "They are animals, disgusting. Look at all the trash they're leaving behind. And they want equal rights?!" My husband looked over and said just a few things were on the ground, nothing out of the ordinary. My husband started arguing with him about his behavior and the employee just threw his hands up and walked away all frustrated. We've tried contacting the owner, but the phone just rings. Maybe the owner will see this review and do something about their employee and his behavior.

One of the coolest little bars. The restaurant side was closed when I arrived, but went into the bar side and the full kitchen was still open.. Pizza was bomb, and atmosphere really felt like an old NYC bar.. will def return.

They are wonderful there. They seem to get a "bad reputation" but they provide excellent veterinary services at a reasonable cost. I came in last week for my puppy's appointment and I got the date wrong, it's actually for the next week. It was about an hour before they were closing but still took us for the visit! They were extremely accommodating and I appreciate the staff there.

I was not very crazy for my sandwich.
Restroom was very unsanitary,  dining area was not very clean either I will never return..

I have been here at least a hundred times for dinner and/or drinks over the past 5 years. I feel as though I have watched it fluctuate as far as it's food quality is concerned. It's a hit or miss, sometimes it's fantastic and other times, I'm let down. 

Because of dietary restrictions I feel the menu is quite limiting, wish there were more veg/vegan options, not a place I can frequent as much as I'd like but it definitely hits the spot when my stomach growls and I'm not feeling cooking and washing dishes!

Positives: great coffee, dog friendly with dog bowls, super helpful employees, and better food than Java. Negatives: $0.50 refills vs free (see District Coffee), internet speed is slow and horrible and cuts out every hour (hopefully they'll fix this soon), and no patio.

Average service, mediocre food and drink, and - as far as I could tell - virtually no COVID prevention. 

My wife and I visited this location on 8/21. We arrived shortly after they opened, and upon walking in, noticed that almost none of the staff were wearing masks. In hindsight we should have left right then. I told myself they'd just opened and maybe just hadn't put them on. 

When our server Jason came over to the table, in spite of the fact that he'd just watched us walk in with masks and sanitize our hands after touching the door, he came to the table unmasked and took our drink order. My wife commented about being uncomfortable, but I tried - yet again - to give Jason the benefit of a doubt, saying, "Maybe he just forgot. They just opened."

Overall, Jason was an OK server. Not good, but checked all of the necessary boxes, I guess. Never did put on a mask, and none of the other servers or staff I saw seemed to even be aware that COVID or masking is a thing. 

The food was pretty disappointing. I've been to this Cantina Laredo many times, although not in the past few months. Something has changed. The margarita I had seemed watered down, and the tacos and salad we had were just dull. Definitely not worth the money or the increased COVID risk. 

I definitely can't recommend this place to anyone anymore. Plenty of other good restaurants in the area where you can get a better quality, safer dining experience.
Bernie and Mike were awesome.  Very knowledgeable and willing to help.  Cannot recommend highly enough.

I have passed this French bakery quite a few times. I'm so glad I stopped in. 
There was soft french music playing which I adored. This is a quaint, somewhat out of date little bakery, but the treats make up for it. 

I had the apple strudel and apple juice for a pre-dinner appetizer before going out to eat with my fiancé. The strudel had powdered sugar on it and the strudel was warm on a flakey croissant. Perfect!
I will be coming back to try the lunch specials and to try to the chocolate croissant!
Smaller than expected and very expensive. 5 bucks for one dragonfruit!! Cheese selection is good though.

The white  wine from the tap was fine, reasonably priced, and fruity. Husband had funky Buddha beer it was fine. We tried the mango, fig goat cheese baklava appetizer. It was good. I love goat cheese so it's hard to not like it. The ciabatta bread w/tomato-oil pesto was nice. Our main dishes lacked flavor short rib with mashed potatoes. It was advertised as having a flavorful reduction sauce. It didn't. My sacred pepper raviolis were just OK. Not anything I'd ever want again, but it was good to try. I should've tried the crab and lobster rotol. Did anyone else notice the weird prices? $11.90, $9.90...just round it up! Honestly expected more for the price! Our server was very good.

Food is good and fresh my first time in the store I usually order through Uber eats . But I saw the workers don't wear hair nets .seeing the girls swing their hair around while handling food  or near it is disgusting .... !  I won't be going back I've seen enough .

baklava is gr8!! v. friendly service as well, Alan is super nice :) we came in about 40 minute before closing (the close at 10pm) and they were still very friendly w. us 
we got 2 plates, 3 baklavas, and a tea and it came out to only $35

Our new favorite restaurant in Philadelphia

Right up there with Zahav, Vernick, High Street on Market, Brigantessa.  Our party of four had everything on the menu except one main and one second as well as the two specials (crudo and stuffed zucchini blossoms).  Chef George Sabatino has an amazingly creative and  thoughtful menu, with many vegetarian options.  He makes his own bread, as well as all dairy products including delicious cultured butter with salt flakes.  He also supports area farmers (Three Spring Farms, etc) and is committed to local vegetable, dairy and meat producers.  

Standout dishes:  special crudo with sea scallops with picked vegetables and sprouts (amazing!), charred onion salads, roasted carrots, grilled zuccini, grilled pork  belly (using a heritage Hereford pig, which I didn't even know existed raised by Jennifer Sabatino's father) and pork sausages.  The main dish, fazzoletti (fresh pasta hankerchiefs with duck prosciutto) was one of the best pasta dishes we have had anywhere (in a city of standout pasta dishes with Le Virtu, Brigantessa, Il PIttore).  I usually never like panna cotta (too bland), but Aldine's version is a standout with two types of cherries (pickled Rainer and sweet cherry preserves) and almonds. 

Finally, wonderful beer list (two great drafts by Tired Hands made especially for Aldine), reasonably priced wine list and terrific service (Alex our waiter was terrific).  

We will definitely be back and bring all our friends.

My husband and I usually go to the bar and have no issue, however the past two times were were sitting outside with our kids we have had issues! The first time we left after 20 minutes of no one came over to us acknowledged us. The second time we waited onver and hour forna chicken wrap and quesadilla. When we asked someone about thebfood, thebresponse was... what do you expect on burger night by the manager.  

Ridiculous to say the least!

Woohoo for Confederacy of Cruisers!! 

Whenever I travel, I look for bike tours hoping for a mixture of fun, interesting people and good way to learn about the area I'm visiting ...i love bikes! So I've done a lot of them at this point....and THIS WAS MY FAVORITE of them all:) 

I grew up in New Orleans but have lived out of state for several years. I'm a history nut and know a lot about the city so i was really happy to learn stories i had never heard before. Cassady was our guide....AWESOME! He's hysterical.....and took us to parts of the city I hadn't been to before. Cass can definitely cater to your group too. We were in the mood to drink (it was the holidays!)....so we hit some good local bars....3 of them. We had yummy cocktails/beers, met randoms and got to know one another. My favorite was when Cass was telling us stories while we sat back in sidewalk chairs watching the locals go by. You didn't feel like you were on a 'tour'.....So I guess what I'm saying is, whether you're visiting, or a local, you'll have a great time, you'll meet some fab folks and learn lots about an amazing city....so you should do it:)

I would also recommend doing the tour closer to the beginning of your trip if you're visiting so Cass can give you recommendations on other things to do while in the city. YAY for Bikes! 

(oh! and book early!! it fills up)

I had never had coffee here, although I stopped in the market occasionally. What a surprise when I was served the coffee someone had been saving in the bottom of the pot all day. It was disgusting. It doesn't get strong, which I like, it gets to tasting like garbage. I should have asked for my money back, but I didn't want to deal with people who would do that.
Very clean updated and efficient process we were in and out in less than 45 minutes to get to new drivers license and a new title registration for a vehicle

East Town Donuts staff always welcome you with a smile and are very insightful with any questions you may have. They offer a large variety of donuts even fun decorated donuts. Their donuts are always fresh and wherever I take their donuts too people are always saying "wow these are great! Where'd you get these?" I always enjoy my visits there and of course the amazing sweet treats are the cherry on top!

Restaurant has gone downhill.   Maybe they get enough takeout that they don't need to worry about diners who choose to eat in.

1) Restaurant was filthy.  They haven't swept or mopped in days.  The floor was so dirty that there were fries and bread on the floor that had dirt on them.  None of the tables were wiped down.  We took the least dirty table and wiped down ourselves with napkins and water.

2) Food was so so, but definitely not what we remembered.  Pizza was very greasy.  Appetizer was acceptable but not outstanding.

3) Service was lackluster.   No one was rude or mean, but they were bunched up at the the front counter socializing.   Place was not at all busy, only 3 tables.  They were not tending to any of the customers who were trying to get their attention.  One older woman walked up to them to ask them to take her table's order.

4) Forget COVID precautions.  None of the staff, including the cooks in the back were wearing masks or anything.  Staff was drinking out of their own soda glass one second and delivering our food to our table immediately after.

This place used to be amazing.    The founders would be sad to know how let down we loyal customers feel.   Other reviews have shared similar sentiments.   Current owners are operating it like a cash cow and so be it.   We will chalk it up to a lost cause and find a new favorite.
Great selection of food and very tasty.  Competitive prices for a buffet.  Lunch selection was quite vast.  Lots to choose from.

I have tried a few other dry cleaners, but none can compare to Deluxe. From the warm welcome offered by everyone behind the counter (especially "Miss Mary", a local icon who has managed to remember my different phone numbers over the last 30 years) to the top flight service (they repaired a shirt that they didn't damage at no charge), this is one business that truly lives up to its name.

The rolls are delish. Could use a little more variety on menu. Be sure to check the wipe board for more choices. The poke salad was a bit much. Came with lots of raw fish large cut with tentacles. Service was lacking!

Now that we have been to The Matador a few times, I thought I could give them a fair review.

The service can be very slow on busy nights, but forgive them, they are still pretty new and I'm sure, are working out the service bugs. The guys at the bar though seem to be on point at all times. Quick to fill up your water or Diet Coke, even though most bartenders may not (thinking they will not get tipped as much).

The decor is upscale Southwest style with Longhorns, horseshoes, and iron with initimate low lighting and comfortable booths. I'm a huge fan of waiting for the seating around the indoor fire-like pit! People have compared it to Barbacoa, but Barbacoa, in my opinion, was more.... plush? You go to Barbacoa for more of the entire sensory experience. I could be comfortable going into Matador dressed up OR in jeans, since it is right in the midst of 8th street which is super eclectic.

The food is decent with a great spread of tequila of course. Chips and salsa are free as long as you are ordering drinks, and I'm definitely a fan of free fresh chips and salsa! I believe they are making their own salsa because it's been a little different each time we've gone. I like this. It's not CHEAP food, but it's not expensive. 

We've ordered quite a few of the starters with the goat cheese stuffed jalapenos wrapped in bacon being my favorite. The Texas style nachos are good as well, but I would stay away from the marianted button mushrooms - not a real bang for your buck. 

Alas, we will be back. It's new and fun and always lively, which I cannot complain about!

I wanted to take a minute and write a review about this barbershop!  I decided after over 20 years of going to the same salon, I would give Sals a shot since my normal guy was on vacation.  With that said, I am very picky seeing twenty years with the salon and person I had a comfort level beyond to say.  Walking into Sal's I was greeted right away by Sal and the other barbers!  The shop was great and beyond very accommodating with food, soda, and water.  I sat in Sal's chair told him what I wanted and he did an amazing job to where I said to myself it was exactly how I wanted it.  Fast forward I started using Sal's barber shop for my hair and even went there right before my wedding and it was definitely top notch. This place is by far the best barbershop hands down in Montgomery county.
Great food and ambiance! Indoor seating is open now so it's nice and cozy inside. I got the salmon quinoa bowl and it was delicious! Service is great price is pretty normal for that area
Amazing food & amazing service.  Owner is great.  Portions are huge.

Bill is Best in Town! I had Reno Convertible Tops & Trim replace the Canvas Rag Top on my 1997 BMW Z3 Convertible and The Quality Of Work Cannot Be Beaten! My New Top Looks Amazing and I Feel Like A Millionaire when getting into my Ride! This should be the Only place in Town to have your Convertible Top Replaced Or Repaired and If you are getting Ready For Hot August Night's Bill is Your Guy !!

Best place to get gas. They always have paper towels for those of us that don't want to wrap our bare hands around the pump handle. They also have that clip on the handle so you don't have to keep a squeeze on the handle to pump the gas. The inside of their stores are very clean and their coffee is pretty good. Highly recommend.

I live near this place, so I eat here often. The table-side salsa is not just a gimmic, it's delicious--don't be shy, ask for just exactly the extras or leave-outs you want from that cart! They serve a decent michelada and margarita (la flaca), make their tortillas fresh on site, have a decent happy hour, and a lovely little outdoor area when the weather is right for it. I recommend the Mexican paella. It is freaking delicious, and is enough for several meals or a perfect sharing dish.

So... I had this place bookmarked for a while now. Almost a year. Finally decided that I needed some Pho in my life, and stopped in. I agree with my fellow yelper in that the restaurant looks like a run down old Pizza Hut. They are definitely in need of updates. The tables were shorter than the booth seats, there were holes in the booths, the curtains were dingy and there were ants crawling on the wall. Also, I had to ask for a new soup ladle because the ones on the table were dusty.

Based off of the taste of our Pho, I've had better in other states so that is why I rate them a 2.5/3. We had the create your own and also the spicy bowl. The portions are large and price is reasonable, however I feel as though they weren't that flavorful. The spicy bowl was definitely lacking spice (as a fellow reviewer mentioned).

I am not in a hurry to go back. Probably will never return. If this is the best Pho that Brandon has, I guess I will be looking for some in a nearby town.

Bad quality. Not at all fresh during the week days. I have a feeling this is one of those very good Indian restaurants that went bad. I see a lot of veterans from de praise the food. The food is heavy on cream and lacks quality ingredients. This is not original indian food.

Would never stay here again. Customer service was horrible. I had a room booked and I got there at 6pm and had to wait until 9pm to get the room. I was then charged twice for the room and it's been a month and they still haven't refunded me.

Little slice of heaven smackdab in the middle of Bristol borough!
Clearly the front runner.
Any other Italian hoagie is a distant second and so not part of conversation.
A serious blessing to the community.

Consistently fantastic pizza. The cheese pizza is great, the pepperoni is great, the buffalo chicken in great, the BBQ chicken is great, the veggie is great! Super friendly table service, quick to pick up, no hassles. We love Tony and Joe's! Their strongest appeal is their wings. Extra crunchy deep fried with the wing sauce and bleu cheese/ranch on the side so they're never soggy. Put the whole wing in your mouth and pull out the bones, they're that tender. Free refills on soda when you dine in, credit accepted.

Excellent food and excellent service! My boyfriend got the sausage and pepper pizza and I got the short rib pizza, which came with a delicious fig sauce! We had the fried risotto as an appetizer which was amazing, as well as complimentary biscotti and tomato bisque shooters which were adorable! Plus, the biscotti was super fresh. Our server was very friendly and attentive, which really put the cherry on top of our experience. We will definitely be back here!

My only complaint is that I didn't quite understand the serving sizes, and I had to ask.

Thought about doing a review just on the Kaldi's/Benito's aspect, but it seemed like overkill.   So, I'll just add (and to get it in the keywords) that Culinaria has a Kaldi's Coffee Bar/Benito's Gelato.  The coffee is Kaldi's quality that you expect, and if you really want to go into diabetic shock, get a gelato shake.  Freakin' delicious!

Went there for a late lunch and was not disappointed. Firstly, all the staff was wearing masks and gloves. There was seating available outside, but we sat inside. Other customers were socially distanced from our table. I order a vegetarian dish "Indian hot". It was perfectly spiced, just the way I like it. The vegetable biryani we ordered was awesome. Definitely will order that again. The service was impeccable. Appetizers came out early and the main dishes came as soon as apps were done. Billie, our server, did an amazing job. I was really impressed with how they are handling the COVID-19 protocols and will definitely go back soon.

The only thing I eat when I go here is the cheesesteaks (with cheese wiz and onions, of course). I am on a cheese steak tour during my tour of the Philadelphia area and the one here is good. (Not the best, but far from the worst, top 30% for sure). If you get the giant sized sandwich, I recommend taking the other half home, or ordering fries and splitting it with a friend. Do NOT try to finish it all in one sitting, or you will be in a food coma - like I was. 

Pros: Quick Service, Close to the side of the Villanova campus that I study and work at, Friendly staff (they honored my Yelp coupon that they thought was taken down), Tasty Food

Cons: I would visit here more often if they had free Wi-Fi and cheaper prices. 

Recommend giving it a try. I'll be returning again (but only if my time on campus is unexpectedly extended past lunch/dinner)

I love bringing my dog here, and my dog loves coming here . The staff does such a good job and they are so friendly !  I've messaged them about last minute appointments and they have always been able to fit me in. I love that they text you with updates; and that you can book an appointment via text as well . They also take such cute photos of your dog during the appointment! 

If you want a groomer that will love and spoil your dog as much as you do, this is the place to go !
Best Salon great mix of beds love the King Bed go if you want quick tan I can tell they change their bulbs , love the new Versa Spa sunless booth they got

Amazing !! staff and great atmosphere. They had a nice outdoor seating where you could relax and do your work. Love their cafe Aui lait with medium house roast beans. Over all good place.

Tip: They do offer cupcakes for a  $1 on fridays.
Best pizza in town! Coal-fired, fresh ingredients... and very friendly staff. Restaurant is clean and contemporary. Terrific for business lunch or entertaining.
Fanstastic small and well organized record store.  Super nice staff. Lots of hidden gems in their bins.

This place popped about 1 year ago the hours are 9am to 5 pm most day and I am always at work. I finally had a day off and was able to try their stuffed beignets.  What a flavor bomb, it was a great idea to put meat vegetables or dessert inside a beignet. I enjoyed the steak and cheese and added hot sausage. I also ordered the pecan pie beignets these had a the classic pecan  pie flavor,  I enjoyed it a lot. They were out of strawberry so that must be a favorite flavor. Just a note each order is 2 beignets and one is pretty filling.  This is fresh made to order. You will wait about 10-15 minutes, but you can have a beer or cocktail while you wait. You pick a base flavor beignets and you can add or subtract ingredients. I really liked this food and the idea behind it. They also have regular burgers. #thankyouthursday

Stay away. To start they did not show up when promised. When I spoke to the owner he told me it was my fault for not allowing a couple of days of flexibility and I need to plan better! We moved from out of state and gave them our credit card to guarantee they would be at our new house. My son and I unloaded two trucks on our own because we were in a jam on time. I used them for 4.5 hours and they charged me for 6.5. One hour drive time to my house and one hour drive time back to their shop. The owner is a CROOK. Stay away. The worst part was the supervisor worked hard along with my son and me. The two other movers were on permanent break. The supervisor kept getting frustrated with them, but said it was his last week working for EZ Move and he didn't care because he was quitting. YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED.

You need to train your people better.  I am a gluten-free vegetarian, not some pariah.  I do not need to be talked down to re:how hard it is to keep a gluten free atmosphere.   I choose to be gluten free and am not gluten intolerant, but your employees are obviously trained to be combative re: gluten and have not been educated re: why someone may have to avoid gluten.  This will end up biting you in the butt, I imagine.
And your vegetarian options?  The Quinoa Vegetable soup was all broth...really.  Two, maybe three spoonful of vegetables in the bottom of a Large Soup.  And the Kale Quinoa Salad?  Tasted good but, hey I know how much Quinoa and Kale cost...what a rip-off.
Oh and to illustrate the total lack of education regarding food allergies, there was the (2) rolls packaged with my soup and salad. 
This is a management issue and I would imagine it is franchise wide.  Needless to say, I will not be back and I will not recommend Café Zupas to any of my friends, no matter what there eating habits are.
I will recommend Chipotle next door, however.

I've lived in Nashville for about 9 months and have heard about Mas Tacos MULTIPLE times. I finally decided to hunt down the truck on 12 south street with a friend today. 

WOW.

I ordered two tacos, the spicy carne molida and the quinoa sweet potato. The quinoa sweet potato taco was amazing. I mean, amazing. The tomatillo sauce, red cabbage, cilantro, and crema tasted soooooooo delicious a top the sweet potato and quinoa. I'm not saying the spicy carne taco was bad, but I would order like 5 thousand more of the quinoa sweet potato tacos. 

From now on Mas Tacos will be my go to taco truck.

Do NOT go here! I ordered coconut shrimp tonight and am now lying in agony with the most intense stomach cramps I can remember having. Though the service was friendly and wonderful and the food arrival timing was what we expected... food poisoning is a deal breaker. Disappointed, Red Lobster was always a guilty pleasure of mine.

Hadn't been to BJs in a while and was happy to know they have remained one of the best restaurants in the area.  The BBQ is yummy - choice of sauces, mild to hot.  Their BBQ Salad was fresh and plentiful.  The pulled pork sandwich and fries were great.  I really love their breakfast the best and will have to go back soon for their awesome breakfast menu.

Anthony expertly transformed my aggressive mastiff to an obedient pup. Before his efforts, the stress caused in my home and anytime I tried walking my dog was palpable and I wasn't sure I could keep her. After a month at doggie boot camp staying with Anthony, she returned to me a different, manageable dog (so long as I kept up my end of the training). I highly recommend Anthony for any issues you're having with your pooch causing you stress. He delivers!
Fantastic thin slice pizza. Best spot in Rittenhouse for sure.

Cash is great don't forget :) they only take cash so be prepared. Did the full body massage really great. Got all the knots out of my back. Feel very refreshed. I wasn't used to the open feel as I've only done private room however it was wonderful and very relaxing.

These guys are awesome!! I felt like a child in a toy store when stepping foot in the waiting area. They had VMR + BBS LM wheels up for display, motors and other various BMW tuning parts. Out in the yard sits a few dozen rare old school BMW's.

I brought my e46 330 there for a new expansion tank + thermostat. They priced me at around $300.00 for parts and labor. A bit pricey I understand but totally worth it! 

The service there was very friendly and honest! Something every customer should be expecting from a mechanic! Would definitely be patronizing this business again in the future!

Great place to hang out! Loved the chill atmosphere with lots to do, drink, and eat. Definitely on the busier side,  as with most new places in Nashville. The taco shop was amazingly delicious! The drinks were also amazing, as well as the beer. Taking off points for the wait. I understand that its busy, but only 3 bartenders for a huge area? Waiting 20 minutes in a line to get a drink was annoying. Might be nice to have separate bars, one for just moonshine and the other just for beer. Other than we had a great time and will probably be back!

After our initial review we had moved on and forgotten the regrettable encounter with Lucca. Now, 8 months after our review, at 4:18 am Mark (Melissa's husband) posted a malicious, hated filled and defamatory rampage filled with lies attacking me personally (which was eventually removed). I am deeply disgusted by their behaviour and cannot believe a company would act so unprofessionally. In my opinion I would urge anyone considering hiring Lucca Truck to keep FAR FAR away!!!!!

Simply outstanding every time we've eaten here. Drinks, staff, service and food are excellent;
Nothing more to say. except you must eat here.

Today I ordered the medium sized Pho with rare steak and brisket.  The food was absolutely delicious.   I'm a lover of Pho, and this is a restaurant I will be returning to again and again!  I even have enough left over for lunch tomorrow (I had to force myself not to overeat because it was so good)!  Also, the cashier was extremely nice and respectful.  The restaurant was small, but clean.  I hope I continue to have a wonderful experience, then they'll always have me as a faithful customer.

I WOULD NEVER SHOP THERE
Very Very poor quality clothes
I bought a sweater 3 weeks ago, ripped the first time, first time it happens to me.
I went to the store at the west Edmonton mall, the staff are very rude, lady simply said we can't change it, more than 2 weeks, claiming she was the manager, then my husband interfered asking for a manager, turned out to be another person, who came so confident after 10 min chatting with a group of 3 girls, asking us, what is the problem, "after 10 min conference"
Anyway, showed her the defect which was clearly manufacture defect, which she admitted, she still says, it has been more than 2 weeks and labels are off.
How I would wear a sweater with a label on.
Anyway, if she admitted it is a defect, they should have replaced it.
I WOULD NEVER SHOP THERE

Excellent oysters! Staff was great and very helpful also. 
The Bloody Marys were awesome also!  A must have drink with the oysters and they put pickled green beans, along with a bunch of other "goodness" to garnish the Bloody Mary with. 
We didn't order any other menu items, but the Po' Boy sandwiches looked amazing there too. Next time, that's what I'm going to order.
