Absolutely love the food and the service. The coffee bar and bakery is great as well but my absolute favorite is the gelato.

This place falls under the category, "You pay for what you get." It is cheap as hell, but the food is on the verge of being inedible. Everything was greasy and burnt. If you like spending time on the crapper, you should definitely come here.

My wife had given me a gift card for these guys that I finally got the chance to use today - and I am glad she did. Terrific Haircut & Shave today provided by Justin - nice relaxing atmosphere and quality treatment made for an awesome experience.    Would definitely recommend !!!

So... I've ate here before and it's alright. It's not horrible, but it's definitely not grade A. The view is gorgeous so it makes it doable. However, we own a unit at the Inn right by this restaurant. I go and clean it every time a tenant checks out. I always park in the same spot, I've been for years. I park right by the restaurant, on the side. One day, I'm taking my cleaning supplies out and this woman walks up to me and asks me, with a strong tone, if I'm a cleaning service. I said 'something like that'. She was speaking to me very rudely and almost as if she were talking down at me and claimed that the parking was the restaurant's parking. Turns out, she's the 'owner' of the restaurant. Don't get me wrong..I can respect that it's there property, I understand that. I just wasn't aware of this. Listen, if I had been approached and spoken to in a civilized manner and a respectful tone, I wouldn't of been so offended and here now writing a review. However, that was not the case. I even mentioned to her that I was also a resident and I'm a 8 minute walk from the Inn. She told me then I can park at my home and walk from there. I carry a huge and heavy cleaning bag, huge backpack, big mop and bucket, portable vacuum and this woman really told me that I could walk in 95 degree weather with all of this in my hand instead of parking at the Inn. She claimed as well that they have a big problem with the cleaning services parking there, I could understand that and respect that it's there property. But we also have tenants that park there all the time. I can't imagine how she speaks to them, if it's anything like she spoke to me. There's no signs that say it's Hooks parking, so it's not anyone's fault that we don't know. The hotel just had everything paved and painted so it all looks the same. So I found it very disrespectful that she spoke to me in the manner she did when I just wasn't aware. I was never told this. I find it very disappointing and shameful that the OWNER of the restaurant spoke to me the way she did and I just had to write a review about it because if you're the owner, you represent your restaurant still. You are still the face of your business. It doesn't give you the right to speak to someone disrespectfully though, especially when we just don't know. I'm glad it was me she only spoke to like that, because if we had heard it from one of our tenants..well the situation would of been a lot worse. I can definitely say now that the restaurant is as bad as her attitude. If you have such a problem with people at the Inn in your parking, then take the time to get some signs..not using that time to attack anyone and everyone that parks there.

The owners were very accommodating and reassuring.  They went the extra mile to make the bouquet stunning for my friend.  Her car was ruined by Hurricane Ida.  She sent me a picture of the seasonal flower bouquet.  I know it lifted her spirits.
Always delicious we have an amazing day playing at the beach with all the dogs love it after swimming a fantastic dinner love this restaurant if there would be more stars I will give them more

We stopped in for our first trip tonight.  I had the #1 on Texas toast with an added patty and fries, both were really good.  My wife had a mushroom and swiss with an added patty and grilled onions on a bun with onion rings.  She enjoyed both, but the bun got a little soggy and the onion rings were way over cooked, very dark.  The staff wasn't very friendly, but service was quick.  Overall, a good experience, we will definitely give them another shot!

Actually embarrassed!  Need to meet up with some friends slash business associates somewhere, and I suggested Red Robin.  Have eaten at the other local Red Robins, and everything has always been pretty good.  I was actually embarrassed when we walked in this place with them.  The floor had trash all over it.  We went and sat down, and ordered some drinks and appetizers.  My wife asked for water, and we were told the water was broken.  Got the appetizers, and they were fine, other than the fact that we had to ask for napkins and silverware, and one of the appetizer plates had something on it. 
 .  Both my wife and I ordered burgers, and our friends ordered salads.  Our food came out, and the salads they ordered looked fine.  Both of our burgers were luke warm at best, and the fries were hard, stale, and cold.  Probably didn't see the waitress again for 1/2 hour.  We were there about 2 hours because we were discussing business.  When the gal brought the bill, we still had an entire onion ring tower sitting there untouched.  Never asked if we wanted a to go box or anything.  Just asked if I could run the kiosk, and then left.  The place wasn't particularly busy, and after the 2 hours we were there, all the same garbage was still all over the  floor, and my wife never did get any water.  I will eat at Red Robin again, but I will never set foot in this one again.  Just plain embarrassed to have invited my friends there.

Took the husband here for his birthday dinner at his request. He is a big fan of deep dish pizza.  We were properly advised that there would be a wait to cook the pizza.  The service on a week night was very good and no wait to be seated.  The atmosphere is casual with a bit of an industrial design to the dining room.  The beer menu was okay but could be larger.  The pizza was served nicely with the server plating our first slices. The flavor and quality of the pizza were good.  We had enough for left overs that heated up well.  For us this location is a bit of a drive. I'm more than happy to continue to go to Union Jack Pub in  Broad Ripple for deep dish (tastes better IMO).  I would come back here but it won't be frequently.

Five Guys is always great for fast food style burgers and delicious hand-cut fries.

The free peanuts are great to snack on while your order is prepared. Since everything is made to order you usually wait about 5 minutes. Just enough time to pick a table, fill your cup with soda, get any condiments you like and eat some peanuts.

Basic toppings (i.e., veggies and sauces) are included in the price. Personally, I usually get grilled onions, L, T, mayo, and ketchup on a cheeseburger. Now when 5 guys says cheeseburger they actually mean double cheeseburger. If you want a single (and why would you) you have to order the little cheeseburger. Love it.

The fries are probably the best part. Hand cut and golden brown. They pour so many in the bag that 1 order is plenty for two people.

The place is generally crowded, but they do a decent enough job of making sure everything stays clean. There's usually 1 person walking around and wiping down tables and cleaning up. Don't be surprised if the place is a bit messy when it's packed though.

Overall, one of the best fast food style burgers in Philly and definitely a direct competitor to Nifty Fifty's burgers.

The food  and service were both a hit and miss. Crab meat cheesecake, andouille crusted gulf fish (sheep's head on our visit), white chocolate creme brûlée, bananas foster, and the root beer marinated pork chops were excellent. Server (food) was nice, server (drinks) was not knowledgeable about the drinks ( the NOLA beer said IPA on the can but she claimed there were 'no IPA's') she also forgot about the glass of wine and only seemed interested in helping our table after one couple ordered a bottle of wine. It would seem that if you are the drink server that you should be assisting with the drink order by being knowledgeable about your products, delivering the drinks that were ordered and refilling wine glasses when available.

I don't care what they tell you: Magnolia pralines at this shop are the best in the quarter. Good snap, no grainy feel, melty and full of rich flavor. We tried every other praline today and came back for these. Pick up a box!

Awesome bread, great price, and tastes delicious.  This place is just a bit further down Washington Ave from Ba Le.  Trust me it's worth the extra couple blocks.  $4 for a tofu banh mi, can't beat that.  The bread is enough reason to come here alone.  I prefer the bread here than Ba Le.  It tastes fresher.  My order was only for 2 banh mi, not sure why the order took a while to come out.  We waited about 5+ minutes.  I tried their viet iced coffee with boba and it was $4.  The boba was fresh and coffee was strong.  I'd def come here for the tofu banh mi over Ba Le.

I used this Enterprise when my car was in the body shop.  I had a fantastic experience.  They picked me up immedately, and got me in a car and out the door.  They were a pleasure to deal with.  I wouldn't have found it on my own, as there's poor signage, but I will use them again.

I had a lady practicality put her face in my groin while I was eating lunch to clean the frames of the walls. She just couldn't wait to clean the spot I was practically sitting in. Things get done here, I like the determination. 4 stars. Minus 1 star for the practical hug. Even cleans the booth as I'm sitting here.

I really wanted to like this place...

It's actually not that I disliked it, it's just that I paid $12 for a drink that tasted HORRIBLE. Stay away from the Long Island Iced Tea (no alcohol at all.. and tasted really weird). The crab rangoon was way too sweet and our fried chicken was bloody!!!!!. Everything on the menu was deep fried or just sounded yucky. 

They allow people to smoke in restaurants in Philly??? We reeked of cigarette smoke after our night ended.

The highlight of it all was that my fam and I pre-gamed and were already pretty loose by the time we got there.. enough where we could actually enjoy the karaoke which was pretty fun. I just wish the drink prices were cheaper, the food tastier and actually done, and no cigarette poisoning.

Lunch- Friendly service and nice atmosphere- the gourmet mac and cheese is a wonderful surprise! It is truly gourmet and excellent. We also had the frozen cheesecake balls which were a unique twist on cheesecake and the perfect ending to a great meal. Highly recommend this place .

Unfriendly service. WAY OVERPRICED!! A refill was $42 ! The work was mediocre !! I could tell that the nail tech didn't want to do anything. I had to ask her to file my nails straight, and to remove the access polish on my skin. Do yourself a favor and skip this salon. The only reason for the extra star is because they do a good job on waxing eyebrows.

I wanted to love this place.  I love the location.  I love Garces' stuff.  I went with another couple and none of our food was very good.  The cauliflower and burrata were decent appetizers.  The tuna pasta was sent back.  I got a salad which was ok and my date got the rabbit dish which was pretty small.  Nobody was excited about their food.  Will try again but disappointed to say the least....

We had the absolute best pizza here ever. But the pizza didn't compare to the fantastic service we had by our waiter, Jimmy. He was one of the best waiters I've ever had. I don't know what he's being paid, but he deserves double that. He was so on point to everything that was going on in the store and made sure we were happy. We came all the way from Maryland and never had service quite like this. Great place with great food and great service!!!

Fishtails Seafood Market
Great for fresh seafood and great frozen items such as lobster tails, breaded shrimp and much more.  Also, fabulous homemade crab cakes and chowders!!!  Won't be disappointed.

The two gentlemen I usually deal with at this store are both very knowledgeable.  Pricing is very reasonable and much better than Wal-Mart or Future Shop.  Even though I live in Stony Plain, Battery World in Edmonton is now my first choice for batteries.

I've been several times and was never really impressed but last Saturday's visit was probably the worst. While the pizza had a good taste the crust was very thick, soft and doughy. The Italian Sub was a mess, the bread was tough and the tomatoes weren't ripe (come on, really, a bad tomato in New Jersey!). I'm not sure why every one raves about their Texas Weiners because I think they are the worst. The hot dogs are usually soft (like a cheap brand) and they're very skimpy on the sauce.

My husband and I are so pleased with our new counters. We had a great experience from beginning to end. 
Juan showed up for install and was extremely personable and professional, along with his partner Renee. We had some issues with our cabinets being level, which had me concerned. He assured me he would not leave until the job was completed and we were happy. 
The counters were finished and look gorgeous!! I will recommend Stoneworks to everyone I know who maybe looking for counters. I can't thank you and Juan enough for a phenomenal experience!
Sincerely,
Cassie & Jeff Eckert

Had the chance to visit wax the city this evening for the yelp Wine and Wax event. Salon is very clean and the staff was extremely nice and helpful. I also think the location is very convenient and easy to find. I chose to have my brows waxed. 
I laid down on the table and the technician gave me a mirror so we could discuss how I wanted my brows to look. I really appreciated this step because it was nice to have a plan before she started waxing away. This has not always a norm at other salons I have visited. I also liked that they used hard wax which was relatively painless compared to the soft wax and muslin method I am used to. She did each area twice and plucked any remaining hairs. Overall my brows look awesome but as a girl who loves a deal, I felt $21 is a little expensive otherwise I would have given a 5 star review.
I would definitely encourage anyone (men and women) to give Wax the City a try!

**Also- they are not associated with Sun Tan City- I asked

I heard so many good things about this place that I had to try it out. I was happy to see that there is a parking lot behind the business that they will validate for you so you don't have to find street parking. However, after waiting an hour (about 3 people ahead of me), I was finally acknowledged for the first time, and was told to go upstairs. I mistakenly thought this meant someone would be upstairs to give me the gel manicure I had waited an hour for at this point. That would have been too much to ask. There was one manicurist upstairs working on another girl's nails, and after I waited 20 additional minutes, she started her pedicure. Didn't say a word to me. So, after an hour and a half I walked myself back downstairs, asked for a parking validation, and essentially can never get that hour and a half of my life back. I'll find another place to go for a manicure where I'm treated like a paying customer.

First time and really enjoyed it.  The tofu soup was excellent, tasty with yummy tofu and vegetables.  We didn't have reservations but they found a place for us anyway ... Thank goodness because we loved the food and we will be back again.

I got the hottest chicken breast sandwich. The flavors are great and it made me sweat and my mouth was on fire lol (and I'm Korean!). I only gave three stars though because the chicken wasn't as juicy and tender as it should be, in my opinion. I've had better tasting hot chicken in Houston.

We enjoy this location very much, it's my husband's favorite chain resturant. We came on father's day with a large party. It was busy but we were seated in 20 minutes.  A typhoon shrimp was ordered for the table, we all agreed they were seasoned well and not too heavily breaded and all loved the black and white sesame seeds. I had the Cajun ribeye rare warm center and grilled zucchini and spaghetti squash! The steak was out of this world, perfectly rested and juicy!  Last steak place I enjoyed the steak as much was Smith and Wollensky. I really couldn't get over the quailty. It obviously wasn't a super thick bone-in, ha, but I also wasn't paying 85.00.  19.95, celebrate! The viggies were prepared and seasoned like I do at home, yummy. Anyway, they have flavored lemonade, 4 types. Free refills, my husband has them all. So refreshing during the summer. The new flavor ribs, bacon wrapped and Texas dry rub were reported to me seasoned well and tender tender!  
Our service was flawless and she even tried to think in advance before we asked. Drinks mantianed and food was all out together despite 3 separate tickets.

I'm not a big fan of fast food chains but subway is the only exception. This is probably the only subway I go to and they always take care of my order and ask how my day is going. Very good customer service.

Staff is extremely nice and helpful! They also have a lot of stuff you wouldn't expect, including school supplies, shoelaces and birthday cards. Prices probably cheaper than the big competitors as well.

Many thanks to Tonisha!! She spent much time on my consult with no charge. Left my appointment so happy!! Hair went from damaged to spectacular! Highlights color condition tone and style. All are beyond my wildest expectations! Salon is fantastic!!  Very friendly and fun atmosphere! Tonisha is an artist!! I have an allergy to most products and I am so thrilled not to react to any of the products used. Wish there were 10 stars!!!  Thank you again!! Can't recommend a visit to The Parlour Room enough!!!! I've found my new hair home!!!
Huge portions of great tasting food make this place worth every penny.

Service was ok, the food was something else. I ordered the steak nachos but it seems they forgot to put the steak, Since there was so little of it. I also order a beef chimichanga, Taco Bell would've been better. so disappointed with the food. I know better now.
Always really good, affordable and fast Mexican food. I ordered a variety of tacos and enjoyed them all.
The breakfast burritos exceeded our expectations at a very reasonable price! Salad bar was exceptionally fresh and we loved the self serve chips! Great spot for some great Mexican food!

Second visit to this location and again the service was SLOW ! First visit was dinner time and we thought it might have been slow due to a big dinner crowd! Today we went during lunch hour and there we not many cars in the parking lot or inside, we were greeted quickly by the wait person and told them we needed to be out in 45 minutes, we were told that would not be a problem, however it took 25 minutes to get our food! Had crab dip snd chicken cheesesteak! Wait staff kept saying it will be out in a minute which turned in 15 minutes! Food quality was good but the service leaves a lot to be desired!  Our thoughts were that the kitchen staff was probably chaotic or just unorganized!

For some reason or another I've always gone to Geno's.  I finally gave Pat's the old college try, and I liked it more then Geno's. Seemed a little bigger and more flavorful. Only 3 stars because of the price. For Christ sake they're cranking them things out all day yet the smaller mom and pop shops charge less and in some cases give you more meat.

Definitely the MOST fresh nigiri I had in Reno! My favorite would be the salmon and yellowtail. Also I definitely recommend trying the Priscilla roll not fried! It's so tasty that I am craving one right now. 

The three chefs there are very friendly and funny! I'm surprise how quick they can make their rolls when the place is pack. If you have no idea what to order, don't hesitate to ask them! 

Definitely must try at least once!!

This is not a diner. This is a family restaurant that you just don't want to bring your family.

I have never, nor will I ever  go to the new location. I don't frequent ex-Chinese buffets, even if they're in their afterlife. I used to go to the old Philly Diner because the coffee was decent and the wall of windows was good. It was bright, the music was terrible, and they made an okay burger.

Over the years I learned that the food is pretty bad. All the meat comes pre-stamped, as in freezer-block city. The french fries are terrible (the onion rings are okay). The food here was just pretty comparable to Denny's. I did always like the service, which never lasted long. Still... as this are continues to get more franchised, even my independent-business-loving self sees little purpose in accepting mediocrity.

In Feb 2017 I had AZ Awnings and Canvas build a custom privacy screen for me, and it came out really nice. The price was decent and the service was good. Now in April 2018, I wanted them to do an additional panel for me. I sent them all the info, including a scaled drawing and their previous invoice, and asked for an estimate. It's been a couple of weeks, and no estimate. I did get an excuse via email about how "busy" they are. I guess I'm too much trouble and my $800 project is not worth their while. The good news is there are other companies that do the same thing here in Tucson, so it was easy to find someone else that was happy to have my business. Tucson Awning & Screen came out and measured and sent me a quote the very same day, and I recommend their responsive service and can-do attitude. I wonder why a company like Arizona Awnings & Canvas would ever think it's a good idea to treat an existing customer this way? They took what was a five star rating from a satisfied customer and totally destroyed the relationship. Not too smart.

This is my first time dining at Flemings.  All I can say is ..."WOW!"  This is a nice place to dress up for a birthday, anniversary, or any other special occasion.  The service is spectacular.  
First, when making the reservation, they ask if it's a special occasion (for us, it was our 20th anniversary).  When we got there, everyone wished us Happy Anniversary.  Also, our server was wonderful.  Everything was served with perfect timing, glasses are never empty, and nothing is rushed.  You will have time to enjoy your wine and salad or appetizer before your entree is brought out.  Portions are perfect, the food is delicious (I had the filet mignon), and my wife and I loved the bread with the two different spreads.  (just be careful to not fill up on bread).  Our reservation was  at 615pm, and we didn't leave until 930pm..  They don't rush you.  
Also, the wine list is on an iPad, and if you like your wine, you can email the name of the wine to yourself during dinner.   Just understand, everything is priced separately.  However, two people can share a side, so order two different sides if you are going as a couple.  
Finally, it is a little pricey.   If you are going as a couple, plan on spending between $150-$200.  However, it is well worth it.  If its a special occasion, they even bring you a signed card from all of the staff, and a nice gift.   Thank you to the staff for making our evening wonderful.  
One more thing, .....order the lava cake for dessert!!!!
Really don't like their pizza at all. I would go out of my way 2 go somewhere else even though this place is right around the corner.

Great spa day!
Ask for Sara
Very professional!
Went out of her way to make sure my spa day was a pleasant experience!!!
Highly recommend!
Awesome food and some of the best beers I have had in awhile. Rye bread IPA and spicy red muscles are favorites.

So today was our wedding anniversary, I got hung up until the late afternoon with work. I called Rosas minutes before closing and explained my situation. They came through big time, preparing a wildflower like bouquet. The best yet actually. And stuck around past closing. 
The preparation, floral quality, attention to detail and KINDness gave me renewed faith in people. 
My wife has had some personal tragedies this year bringing much sadness. But,tonight she was smiling :) and I didn't have to sleep on the couch. I don't post much so I hope you can sense the level of gratitude I have. Thanks ROSA's!!

I know this makes me an impulsive jerk, but I saw this place from the St. Charles streetcar and immediately thought, "that's where I'm having lunch!" and proceeded to push my way through gobs of overweight tourists to jump off the street car. 

The menu was overwhelming, but an awesome member of the helpful staff sorted it out for me and I went with his muffuletta recommendation. Yum. Cheesy, salty, gooey, crunchy - just as he described. 

Also, the Abita rootbeer is amazing. I'd say it pays to be an impulsive jerk.
Disappointed that they were out of breakfast sandwiches, and all the brioche and croissants.  Quiche was okay.

I've been eating here since I was a kid and I've never had a bad meal. The service is always excellent as well as the food and atmosphere. I recommend the gumbo yaya. It is a must try. They also have a great dessert selection.

Also I recommend asking for either Stephen or J.C. (both great waiters)

This Pizza Hut doesn't deliver unless your on a curb. If your apartment is in a building unit do not expect deliver from this place. Order from Savvas they understand the concept of delivery -"FYI the definition is the act of taking goods to a person or a place" NOT THE STREET! 

Thank you! Now I will check my bank since you couldn't full fill a simple contract!

They only accept cash and when you make your way across the street to get some, they take things from your cart. There is no telephone number listed and you can't call them. As the cashier said it is bad for you but good for us. Cheap food should not equal bad customer service. 

Added, if you went and ran your card and they said it didn't go through. Watch your bank account you may have been charged twice or three times for your groceries. Looks like a good way to boost their sales by stealing your money.

It's so funny how many, "I don't feel safe" comments there are, I have been to this mall TONS of times and never felt unsafe unless I was around certain people who tend to do mass shootingS at public establishments. This mall has everything all the basic stores I need in a mall, and a good selection too. I usually base my mall experiences off of when I have a event and I need to go to the mall and I can't find a thing. LOL Again great mall and centrally located.

Tried this place numerous times for both dinner and dessert or just dessert itself. It's a rather small bistro with friendly service. Always seems to be filled with people whenever I go but can always find a seat. Perfect for few friends looking for good dinner and dessert. 

Although they are known for their dessert, they have quite a few really good entrees on their menu, what sticks out in my mind is their Shepards Pie and Beef Brisket. As for dessert, since I'm a fan of fruit pies, their blueberry and sweet cherry pies are a perfect choice. They also have a delicious peanut butter pie!

It's become a go to place when I'm craving dessert. Definitely check this place out!

I saved this place a while back when I found out my sister in law had a gluten allergy and have been waiting to take her. Well, today was that day! If I had known it would've been this amazing, I would've gone alone when I saved it onto my yelp! Everything was amazing. I understand Covid is a big deal, but there is no need for an individual to be rude when they made the conscious decision to leave their home, especially when its obviously busy. Either way, the ladies never strayed from their professionalism. Anyways, from the moment you walk through the door, the atmosphere is welcoming, Destiny and Jennifer were personable, and the artwork around the area is gorgeous. We had the Pumpkin Seed Bacon Waffle, Mozzarella Tots, &  the Pulled Pork Sandwich. So good! I ordered another waffle for my Wife to have at home! As a dessert junkie, I definitely wasn't planning on leaving without a cupcake. We took four to go and plan to be back.

10/10 would recommend.

Huge awesome burritos and chimichangas, semi-fast place order and come pick it up style.  Watch out though, it can be expensive.  If you get any kind of modifications on your burrito or chimichanga, dollars are getting added.  The worst example was something like $3.75 added on for a few standard toppings like cheese, sour cream, etc.  I'd say get it without anything added on top, since they seem to put cheese and lots of other good stuff inside anyway, and you really don't want to feel like you just paid almost $10 for a burrito.

Management is non responsive and arrogant. I pointed out that management was parking in the handicapped van loading area. I was told they were just running in and out however I had seen them parked there for as long as 4 hours. I was told I complained to much and told not to come back. I have been playing golf at this course for 20 years. When I tried talking with them I was told this is private property and they can do what they want. They owe my husband and myself f two months of season pass and cart pass. The pro shop manager refuses to call back.

East Falls is definitely one area that probably gets overlooked for awesome food. However, there's one outpost in particular that is truly, epically, wonderful: In Riva. 

In Riva's mastermind, Arthur Cavaliere, was at Amada, El Vez, and Parc before heading to East Falls and opening his own gem, which is half Philadelphia greatness and half Naopli-inspired deliciousness. The pizzas here are out of control delicious--flavorful crusts and wonderful toppings combine to make excellent pies. 

The cocktails are really refreshing and change seasonally. The antipasti offerings are also great--the whipped ricotta is a guaranteed crowd-pleaser thanks to caramelized onions, sweet ricotta, and buttered crusty bread. The spring pea soup is also incredible! A great place for elevated pizza take out (it reheats beautifully) and an even better place to go with friends and/or family. Casual atmosphere with exceptional offerings!!
The best we've found so far in the Riverview area. Nothing to rave about but a decent meal. The sorrel is really good.

Our go-to Chinese take out place.  The staff is very friendly and easily accommodates special requests.  The food is always hot and fresh.  Mainly takeout and delivery - there's a small table there in case you want to stay and eat but I've never actually seen anyone eat there.

I was highly disappointed by Sichuan. It came highly recommended to me and I was looking forward to a great Chinese meal. My date and I had combination Lo Mein, Pork Friend Rice, Crab Wontons, and Egg Drop Soup. Everything was fairly "Meh' and was bland. I know that our selections were fairly mainstream as americanized Chinese food goes, but I hold my americanized food to a high regard.

I would have given them another try, however we found egg shells in our fried rice; that's unforgivable.

When my boyfriend suggested we head up to the Cherry Hill mall to get sushi for lunch, I thought he had to be joking! I mean, come on, how on Earth could mall sushi even be edible?! Well, I'm now a believer!  Yanagi has really fresh sushi, and the rolls are huge.  Not only is the sushi amazing, but it's cheap too!  This place has just about the best sushi I've had since I moved here from CA.  I highly recommend the Deep Sea Roll or the American Dream Roll.  There is always a line, but it's worth the wait, I promise.  Only downside is trying to find a table in the food court.

I used this office for my children's basic cleaning for almost three years.  The experiences I had with the staff based on this alone were fine.  However, Dr. Snodgrass recommended that my daughter have a phrenectomy (spelling?) and also have an implant placed in the roof of her mouth to stop her thumb sucking and adjust her mouth.  He indicated that parents are not allowed in the room while children (my child is 4) are having procedures done.  This is unacceptable to me! There is no way I'm sending my fully conscious child back with multiple strangers alone.  I simply wanted to be back there with her until she was put under.  

Prior to having the recommended work done I obtained a second, third, and fourth opinion.  Both dentists and the orthodontist I consulted were appalled that these procedures were recommended.  They all went on to tell me that many patients experience these types of recommendations from all of the Snodgrass facilities.  Further i was told that these procedures are strictly done for profit and may cause serious damage.  Multiple complaints were filed against this dentist in a Nashville dental association. Subsequently Snodgrass left the association to avoid the scrutiny.

Needless to say, we are no longer patients there.  PLEASE get multiple opinions before subjecting your children (or yourself) to any procedure!!
Tasty pizza, great gelato and quality sandwiches make Nola Mia a superior choice when looking for great opportunities to eat in NOLA.
Usually get JPs chicken, steak sandwich, big ass salad, mudd pie & chicken wings. Take out is super fast

This is how I enjoyed breakfast prior to leaving my car,  #FrannyTheGetLostMobile, at the shop. 

And it was almost just like the picture!!  Note: order a plain pancake and it comes plain (no fancy whip). 

#Getlostindiana 
#GiftCard

Probably the worst cheesesteak I've had in Philly over the years. Overpriced, dry and skimpy with the meat. The bread is cardboard.

There are better convenient, nearby cheesesteaks if you check my reviews.

Best lobster roll I've had in a while! Buttery and so good. Initially was disappointed by the size but ended up being quite filling! If you are starving they have a Jumbo size of every roll. There are 4 seating spots, 3 inside really far part, one out door seating. Will definitely be back
Stopped in for a Big Gulp and a donut. They were great. The store could use a good clean and like most a little update but the employees were friendly. Thanks

Not a huge fan of the steak burgers, but it is a pretty cool place. It does the trick when you are in need of a fast and easy lunch! I hope you can give this place a try. The only reason I gave three stars was because of the burgers.

Great service and very convenient! I had an HP laptop I had gotten my son for Christmas that worked ok for a little and then he said didn't work at all. When my own laptop starting acting funny one day I decided to take both in. Wise Guy arranged to have both picked up from my office which was a huge help, then ran some tests and let me know the one had a software issue and the other seemed ok. They resolved the software issue and continued running tests on the other one but said it seemed fine so no charge for that one since there was nothing to fix, even though they invested some time in it. They also gave me the option of an inexpensive upgrade for my son's laptop which has worked great! All in, a few weeks later everything is working great. Would definitely use them again!

I almost passed on the Boyler Room, but am so glad I did not. They have a beautiful old wooden bar with intricate detail. The bartender was knowledgeable and made sure I found just the right beer on tap. He was dressed to impress: dress shirt, khakis, and a tie. He was happy to help the group next to me when they needed a charge for their phone. I passed on the food specials: $1 hot dogs and tacos, but only because I'd just eaten. Their selection of liquor was impressive, but I came to Philly for some great beer that's not available out West. 

The Boyler Room has 21 beers on tap. Their selection was mostly craft beers from across the country, 10 being East Coast beers. I had the Rivertowne Hala Kahiki (pineapple beer) and the Victory Summer Love (blonde ale).  They do samples so I knew both beers we're going to be perfect for me before committing to a full pint. Providing  excellent service is obviously a key part of the business model here!

Pretty freakin awesome pedicure. Paid extra for extended massage. Was worth it. Polish was perfect! Only complaint is that the nail tech didn't speak English. As in none at all... I asked for a Diet Coke twice and told her how beautiful her own nails looked, but all I got was a tilted head, some mumbling, and a smile. Man, I really wanted a beverage...

So happy to finally have a great restaurant on this side of town. It's beautiful Inside and so far I've always been able to walk in to get a table. We have been two nights in a row now because we wanted the chance to try something else on the menu after loving our food on night one. Plus it's family owned and operated! Highly recommend the drunken noodles, panang curry and coconut shrimp. Oh and don't forget the sticky rice with mango, you won't regret it!

Summary of my stay: beyond disappointing.
I'm a frontline doctor and needed a weekend getaway to clear my head and rest. Having had a great stay here with my fiancée June 2019 I came with high hopes for a restful weekend. This was actually at the top of our potential wedding venue list. I was clearly wrong.

We got to the hotel and the immediate response we got from Stan at the front desk was chilly and rude (at best). I asked if dogs were allowed on the beach. He said he hadn't seen that many cops on the beach = never really answered the question at all....

We settled in, and decided on a late dinner. Dining options are extremely restricted due to COVID19 (understandably) so at 10 pm, we decided to get a late dinner on DoorDash since all hotel options end at 9 pm. The reception desk person told us that the DoorDash person could come to our door, or the concierge/bellhop would bring to us. 11 pm we are in bed in PJs and good old Stan said we had to come to the lobby to get our order. DoorDash person texted me that they aren't allowed past lobby. So we amble there for our late dinner and 2 staff on the way say "oh no for sure we can bring it to your room." Then my fiancée asks for change to tip and Stan, again, says no he can't do that. Stan is clearly the limiting factor and quite difficult.

Next morning, round 2. I call the front desk BEFORE ordering food again to find out if they can come to our door or we have to pickup our food at the lobby. Reception person again says "we can for sure get it to your door." So I order breakfast. It arrived, DoorDash person texts me they will not let them past the lobby to drop my food, and different reception guy says "no you have to come get it yourself."

So my one star is only because the property is beautifully landscaped and close to the ocean. But the thing people keep reporting about how they are disappointed by $1000 per night for no service at a Ritz-Carlton, I agree. Maybe it's because I look normal and my fiancée is a minority which is super sad, but if I can handle daily protocol/policy changes at my hospital as a COVID19 doctor, you'd think the Ritz of all places could at least streamline their protocols and properly educate their staff to at least be on the same page....

Not staying at the Ritz-Carlton Bacara again. Bummer, thought it'd be a great wedding venue next spring....

Well, very sad situation.  Made a reservation to dine there last weekend.  No mention was made of the fact that the hallowed Vesper Club has closed its doors and moved across the street to the basement (yes - the basement) of the Racket Club.  Enjoyed a drink at the bar with the very personable bartender, Joe.  But our experience went downhill in the dining room.  Prices very expensive and menu limited (no more flounder stuffed with crabmeat)  The snapper soup was over $7. and was barely warm.  It was straight out of a can as were the vegetables on the side.  The entree was also served warm with processed mashed potatoes.  I would never return - and this is a place that was a favorite of our family for decades.
This has been my favorite Italian Restaurant since I was a child. The lasagne is the best! My brother loves the chicken piccata also. Could not think of a better spot for my 50th Birthday!

I live in the city of Florissant. I have often had the displeasure of making an error in judgment. This has led me to having take out from this trash business yet again. The service is terrible. Everyone has attitude. No one knows how to do the job and the wait is forever and a day. The food is never correct, it's over cooked and the taste.. Why did I decide to spend money here? The grounds are disgusting. Trash out side. Trash inside. In my experience the bathrooms have been disgusting and smell awful. Which is ironic because the employees seem to have the same issue. So from service to food. From smell to taste and from wait to actually getting food in hand this place is a complete disaster. Management doesn't exist. They can't fix any issues and the owner?? Or franchisee? Never to be found. Can't lodge a complaint if he or she can't be found. I'll happily forward my issue to the corporate office. Please just shut this place down and displace all these "hard-working" people. Florissant will be better off with out this joke of a restaurant. Zero stars

I enjoy Arby's food & have been a patron since I was in high school. a very long time ago. I haven't found any other fast food chain that offers a "jamocha" shake,  which is delicious. My 1 star review is not for the food, not the young man (Michael) who handled my order. The receipt that was handed to me along w/my credit card was itemized & accurate. However, the receipt in the bag was not itemized & was for $8.10; the accurate receipt was for $6.49. It took 4 phone calls b/4 someone answered the phone & I was told if I came back w/both receipts I 'might' get a refund. Because of a sprained LCL I was unable to drive back there. I called the bank associated w/my cc & only the $8.10 was posted. I also called Arby's 800 customer service line & gave the rep detailed info, per his request, about the check & order #, etc. He told me that my cc acct would be credited. Today, Thurs, I rec'd a call from a mgr named Jimmy (wouldn't give me his last name) who wanted proof of the 2 receipts, either in person-or-by mail. This was about a difference of $1.61!  He, I & Cap One bank had a 3 way call & he had the audacity to tell "us" that he would have to consult the franchise owner to rectify this debacle. This purchase was on Monday, 11/4. I will drive to Tucson b/4 I'll return to my local Arby's!!

King Cakes, Kringle Cakes, Wedding Cakes, Birthday Cakes and all sorts of other cakes and pies are made in abundance at this dessert wonderland.  Cookies, cupcakes, petit fours, fudge, cannolis and pastries are all available in abundance along with a fantastic selection of chocolate candies.  They also have a variety of other New Orleans themed gifts and food items.  This is a fantastic NOLA place to visit if you're in the mood for sweets and need to get your dessert fix.
Had the chicken fried steak. It was average. The bartender was nice. Their lot is tiny so you will be parking on the street.
Moved to this location and still just as great quality. Loved my cut and color from Brianna & highly recommend this & other location salons!
Everyone that works here is a prick. I've never had a good customer relation with this bar. Great location but awful bar .

This business is not about customer service and making their customers happy. You must pre-order if you want more than 4 of the same cupcake. I wanted six of the same cupcakes, but they only would supply me with 4 so I had to have my husband get out of his car to come in to order me two more. Which is completely RIDICULOUS!!!! The owner said her cupcakes are popular and they need to have cupcakes for others who come in. My response is SUPPLY YOUR DEMAND, Inventory Control!! Her response was,"Well if I make too many I will waste ingredients and have to throw them out at the end of the day". If I'm a paying customer do I care? NO! Shouldn't customer service always be first? I as a customer should not have to worry if you have enough cupcakes for the next person. In my experience, as a black woman, this is a problem I have with some Black Owned Businesses They don't have good customer service. CUSTOMERS COME FIRST!!! The manager  (the daughter of the owner) was also very rude.Just because you're the manager doesn't make you a good one!!!!!! If you looking to be a respect paying customer go somewhere else, this is not the place.

Love this Lil inn! Rooms r super clean, walking distance from Bevmo, Gelsons market. Ralph's & trader joes is a short drive away. Lil market/deli @ the corner (by the gas station) had amazing Tri-Tip; gotta stop by & try it out.

I really love eating at Kibrom's. Everytime I come I try a new dish and fall in love all over again. The lamb dishes are my favorite. I usually come with a friend and we split the family size three or four dish items. I will be bringing my parents here next and hope that they love it as much as I do! Again, I highly recommend the lamb!

My first visit was for lunch. Their lunch menu ranged from $8-$10 but included salad and fresh garlic knots. 
Few restaurants provide anything more than their entree. Extra points earned. 

I ordered basic spaghetti and meatball. Pasta cooked to order. I enjoyed a hearty meatball and JoJo's homemade red pasta sauce tasted fresh like grandma would make. In addition I enjoyed a cappuccino. 
Bill was a reasonable $15. Cute place with cloth tables. Unpretentious with a very friendly server.

An easy experience from purchase to install. One set price and no surprises. I will definitely give them my  business again in the future. 


A second purchase of carpet for 3 rooms March 2019,  was equally as easy, no haggle price, great installation within a quick time frame. This will always be my go to store for carpet!

Bought the ultimate craftsman package. A BEER WITH YOUR HAIRCUT! Where have you been my whole life. I normally get a haircut once every two to three months. Now, I will get one every two weeks! I will try out all the different stylists and find who I like the most. I went in as a walk in and they got me in. They take your time with you and make sure you are satisfied!

We had a group of 10 from work enjoy lunch here today.  Our server was Brandi, & she did an amazing job handling us.  The decor inside was fun, & the menu was fun as well.  My only complaint was that it was freezing inside LBC, & they had monster ceiling fans making it even worse.  I'm pretty sure the wind chill was 60 degrees inside, lol.  I ordered off a lunch menu.  Sadly, we had to ask for a lunch menu, otherwise we wouldn't have known they had one, as the table started off with only having more expensive dinner menus on it.  I ordered a pairing of Asian Chicken Salad & the Queen Cleopatra flatbread.  It was a lot of food for under $10 & was a good deal.  The salad was tasty with ample chicken & good tasting dressing.  The flatbread was very dry but very flavorful.  I'd return & be willing to try some other pairings.  Next time I'll bring my Autumn coat though to stay warm inside.
By far the best Mexican restaurant in Tampa Bay! Make sure you try the cheese dip! And the pollo fundido is awesome

The equipment is not the best, but I will say this one is much better than the one in reno! It is also open 24/7 and has superior equipment to the downtown one. 1 power rack, 2 squat rack type things, 2 flat benches, 2 incline benches, deadlift platform, and a bunch of weight loaded machines. They also have a sled, two different sized tires, and a punching bag. As for extras of the gym they only have a sauna.

It was a coworkers birthday so I brought in a strawberry shortcake birthday cake in from Traubs.  It was delicious...the cake was of perfect moisture and sweetness and the frosting was fantastic.  There were strawberrys in the layers of cake.  Everyone at work loved it!  Perfect strawberry shortcake!!!!!

If I could give it a zero I would. I made an appt to get my dog groomed online. I called several times to make sure they had my dogs medical records with no call back. Since I had taken him here before I knew they had them but wanted to confirm. We took my dog in for grooming and they would not take him as his rabies expired yesterday. I understand the rules - the rudeness they exhibited was uncalled for (maybe because they are in Eagle and think they are above it all???) We have 2 dogs and 3 cats and will never shop or groom there again. Chewy.com has my business forever

Mmmmmmmm .... well let me tell you all that the manager, the place and the view saved this review... when we walked through we thought it was the perfect place for a bottle of wine, appetizers, food and family time. 
So a waitress came and we ordered a cheese board, time went by and the two groups of people that came way after us (50 minutes) were served. We asked three times to our waitress about our cheese board and we never got it ... so we had to cancel our order, when we notified our decision to her she rolled her eyes, and came back with our bill ( bottle of wine and 2 fountain drinks ) and just a few words, nothing that could make us feel like staying or coming back.
The manager came and apologized in an outstanding way... we also were reached by him downstairs to get another apology and offered a better experience in the future... we liked the placed we are still thinking to going back, cause of him this place deserves a second chance.

STAY AWAY FROM THE "BRUNCH"!!  I don't know what was "Brazilian" about it - maybe the rude guy making the omelettes????  We nicknamed him Mr. Snarky!!  Otherwise - it was like every breakfast buffet in every hotel in the US...and NOT CHEAP.  Other than getting to be with my family - I would have just as soon had a sausage biscuit somewhere.

Second time was not the charm. 

Rewind back to my first visit at this location, the tomato soup (bisque) was barely luke warm and the service was no better. The cashier was new, as she shared with us as I ordered, all of which is completely fine. Unfortunately for her, she had to seek assistance from a superior (general manager or possibly owner) who seemed very preoccupied and bothered by her need which, in turn, made us as guests feel awkward and unwelcomed. 

On the second visit, the same manager was at the store and taking orders at the front counter. I was greeted with "I'll be with you in a moment," - not the most pleasant but not terrible, in my opinion, until I got ordering. As I approached the counter, I kept looking up at the menu, for soup, and mistook the WEEKLY SPECIALS menu for WEEKLY SOUPS, to which I mentioned "oh, no chicken soup today," to which he said nothing. Upon turning to face him, I noticed an entirely separate menu titled DAILY SOUPS, where I noticed chicken soup was listed and to which I said "Ah! You do have chicken soup." - to which I got a glare from under his eyebrows and a "mhhhm". The remainder of the interaction was cold, unfriendly and sprinkled with one word responses to questions like "cup?    Yes". Interestingly, though, the manager perked up shortly thereafter, checking in on guests dining and sparking some short-lived small talk with a couple at another table. His check-in with me was, again, awkward and obviously not sincere - though he might have noticed me typing away at this review.

We've lived in Alton for almost 2 years now and were just able to try this place out.  Now I'm sad it's taken me this long!  Everything was so good, fresh, cheap with plenty of food!  Pancakes tasted ridiculously good, even a little crispness on the edges from the grill.  Eggs, hash browns, bacon, toast...everything so good!  And the atmosphere is great, definitely reminds you of old school diners.  The owner is also the cook, waiter, and cashier, for his age that's awesome.  They were all really friendly and seeing the people that come in and the owner knowing them by first name just puts a smile to my face :-).

My husband and I first ventured into Bourbon on a Friday night during the Rib Cook Off.  The place is small.  Very small.  I understand that a lot of it is still under construction and am told that they might open a second floor (I also heard that the second floor is occupied by a printing business that the owner of this casino uses to market all of his other casinos) so I have no clue what is supposed to be going on with this place. 

The entire floor is covered with slot machines.  There are only two bars, one has about 10 stools and the other is right in front of a live stage so you can forget about conversation with friends if you are sitting at that one.  The one bar that we did sit at had rude bar tenders which was upsetting considering they just opened. They do have cheap drink prices, we paid $2.00 each for beer and $3.50 for a shot of Jack Daniels.  Those prices might have been promotional for the cook off though.

The staff here is so ghetto!  I saw maybe two employees that were not either tattooed, dirty or missing teeth.  It appears that everyone who went to the job fair was hired regardless of background checks or appearances.

What really got on my nerves was the next day when I went back. I didn't go back because I wanted to, but rather that I had left the previous night without cashing out my slot machine vouchers.  So the next day my husband and I took the kids to the rib cook off and my husband waited outside with them while I ran into the casino to cash them out.

The place only has two cash out machines.  Just two.  The first one is in the middle of the casino so you have to walk pretty far and it had an out of order sign on it!  So I walked all over looking for the other one.  I found it in the back of the casino with two people standing in front of it.  I walked on over to it to stand in line and see that the people standing in front of it are a security guard and a manager.  They turn around and tell me the machine is broken and I have to go stand in guest services.  I turn around and see the line with about 15 people in it.  I say "that line?", the manager says "yeah - that line.  Don't worry, It'll go fast."

The line didn't go fast.  I stood there for way to long and then the security guard came over and pulled me out of line and told me that they think they fixed it and asked me to try it.  I pulled out one of my vouchers which was folded once in the middle and they told me that wasn't going to work and pulled another lady out of line.  Her ticket didn't work either so they told us to get back in line.  I told them I just lost my spot so the security guard proceeded to walk me to the line and then tell some big scary biker guy that I'm in front of him.  The big scary biker guy was super mad and didn't say a word to him or me even after I apologized.

I wont be back to this place.  It's too small, everything is broken and the employees are scary.

Told the wait was longer to sit rather then take out.

Sitting 45 minutes for a pizza to go. Line is gone. No one waiting for a table.

Tell your people about honest line management.

Very disappointed right now.

Get your shit together.

I LOVE THIS PLACE! 

This is a true Jersey inspired dinner. I believe the owners are from Philly. The decor, clinging dish sounds, and little candy machines in the door way are so Jersey. It makes me so happy!

They have a full, delicious menu with breakfast all the time and even a little Greek section. My boyfriend loves the chicken souvlaki and I usually go for breakfast or a club sandwich. 

Open 24/7. Yes!!!

Parkway has the best poboy selection in the city, hands down.  Even my ultra picky chef father likes it, and he doesn't like any restaurant.  I always go seafood or BBQ beef, lots of Crystal  - or plain lettuce and tomato if what I really want is a reason to eat a footlong loaf of NOLA bread while basking in a balmy November day.  I don't like fries but theirs are amazingly good.  Their bloody mary is also wonderful - ask for extra pickled okra.

Most importantly, I have never failed to come away from Parkway without a food coma AND a story.   From James Carville to the old lady in the bathroom who informed me the paper towels were "so nice because Obama is a Communist" (how do you even respond to that??), it's always a NOLA experience.
Place hit the spot. Great clam chowder, really good burger, and some good veggie choices.

The best cell phone repair ever! 
I took my otterbox off my iPhone and.......

Yep dropped it. The glass smashed. 
Ian had me back in business in 1 hour
I heard about an additional glass protection, I mentioned it a yes! Dual protection with a life time guaranteed. 
A week late I broke that and lo and behold, true to their word , fixed in less then ten!

Really good beer! Really good pizza!  Just visiting St. Louis but really wish we lived closer to this place as we would be here all the time! Already talking about making sure we stop here even if we are just driving through St. Louis. You will not be disappointed if you stop.

Fantastic breakfast food.  Wonderful atmosphere and friendly staff.  I wish I had time to try their lunch menu too.  It is very crowded, so be prepared to wait for a table, and keep in mind they close at 2.
Awesome BYOB that I can walk to, and has live music every night....

I was traveling for work and decided to call ahead and set up a reservation. They told me since I was alone, they can't take reservation over the phone and I should stop by since it wouldn't be busy on Thursday 2/15. I decided to go and when I got there I was told there's about 45 Minutes wait and I can sit at the bar and wait. I decided to wait and couldn't help but notice a lot of empty tables for 2. About 30 Minutes of waiting, I asked how much longer and I was told another hour. I mentioned the open tables and were told that their manager Ernesto said to leave those seats up. I proceed to walk out since this is not ok. I took by business to
Roadhouse steak house where I was treated with respect as a consumer: I would advise to take your business somewhere else. This place is terrible and I advised my coworkers who travel to Tucson often to take their business elsewhere.
GREAT roast pork sandwiches!  Limited hours and, unlike Tony Lukes, no  "broccalarobe".  The spinach is almost as good though.

Looooove this place! If you want honest competent service. This is the place to go hands down. 
I ll give you a quick example. I wanted to get my handguard replaced on my AR15. Took it to a shop I will not mention. All he did was talk of how difficult it would be. I said no problem. Took it to this shop, not only did they not complain. But he took it to the back while I waited even though the store was busy. Of course he had help but the point is they could have easily said leave it and they would do it later. This is the type of service that they have. And like I said honest also!!

I've really tried to like frescos. The beautiful location makes it so enticing. It sits right out on he marina with ok views of the water. 
The thing that makes this place somewhat unenjoyable is the patrons. Each time I come here there's a group of older, obnoxious gentlemen who make snarky comments about people being on their phones. I work from home so I sometimes just want to get outside and check email. Gimme a freaking break. Or, there are about 3-4 people smoking and blowing their smoke directly in your face. Yuck! They should really consider banning smoking from their outdoor deck, it turns people off and I can't even imagine sitting through a meal getting constant whiffs of dirty cigarettes.
The service is usually really good though. And the food is really good. I had the salmon salad and it was grilled to perfection and awesome.
This place has potential. I really think banning smoking would improve this place greatly as it would also reduce the amount of rude patrons here as well. 
Nice place to get a happy hour drink and sit outside if you can handle the smoke.

The little Candy Kitchen has been here for a long time and has all the charm that you could imagine with old and new candies along with malts, shakes and floats. They have a lot of ice cream flavors to chose from. I ended up getting a rum raisin flavor milkshake, though I don't recommend that you do because I like raisins more than most. I also got a handful of bit-o-honey at ten cents a pop. Jesus, am I an 80 year old man!?!?

We love this company! They are so quick and efficient. We've scheduled them quarterly to keep bugs out of the house!! Very responsive and discussed the product with me in regards to kid and pet safety.

I think the traditional Thai flavor is completely evident in all of their dishes at Bangkok Cuisine. The thought and detail into every dish is beyond words. 

From the spring rolls to the yellow curry the flavors are well developed. I seriously think their yellow curry sauce is the best in Reno and I'll go one step further to say it's the best, richest I've had. My ONLY negative is the chicken in the dish. I like a story fry kind of thinner sliced chicken and this is too bulky and almost like a potato. It throws off the texture for me and I'm a super "texture eater." But... that yellow curry sauce is *chefs kiss*. I'll have to try the shrimp next time because I'm sure it compliments it so well. 

You can tell there is so much attention to detail and that those cooking care about flavor and development. It's awesome!

One of the best happy hours in town - they do a double happy hour, the first from 3-7, and the later 11 to close (I may be off a bit on those hours . . . ) The first is your typical, and the second is trying to draw in some of the service industry folks.

Anyway, great atmosphere in the bar - modern, with dark, rich tones.  Bar appetizers are half off (8 wings for $3 and beef strips were $3.50), and $3 drafts . . .
The grilled cheese is phenomenal and the French fries are so crispy and it is always relatively fast service. A lot of the time it is packed, but other than that this place is the best.

Great drinks! Came here with my bf for a quick weekend Philly trip from NYC. We stopped by for some drinks after we ate at Pat's and Geno's so we were too full to order food but from what I saw the food looked delicious so i'd definitely love to come back to try it again someday. We came for drinks happy hour and I had 2 bloody marys ($5/each) which were delicious, heavy on the horseradish which I like (so if that's not your thing you may not love it), my bf had a couple Beer & Shot combos that were $5/each which he was also a fan of.

We sat outside because the weather was lovely but we sat at this isolated table by the trash and the staircase so that was kind of awkward but the privacy was nice I guess. 

Will definitely come again!
Pastrami was great but just a little more expensive then it should have been. Nonetheless certainly worth trying.

Absolutely the worst experience ever. It isn't so much that we have had to wait three months, and we are still waiting, for our furniture, however every time we called ( six times) they lied about what has come in and when it would be delivered. When we purchased the furniture the worst case was a three month wait. Ok COVID etc we understood. Just got off the phone with them and they promised December. And that was a tentative promise.  Absolutely the worst customer service. They have never called us to update anything, just unbelievable.

You have donuts and then you have REAL donuts.  

Doughboy is the real MVP.  Doughboys doesn't have any crazy, off the wall or super unique donuts, but they need to because their traditional amazingness surpasses the need for crazy gimmicks. 

Each and every donut they offer has the perfect texture and the glaze is always the perfect amount of sugar & sweet.  

The store is always clean, the employees are always smiling and they offer free samples every day, 7 days a week.

At $11.95 for a dozen donuts you really can't go wrong.

Great staycation! Clean room, had everything we needed. The front desk staff were the best part though and I feel bad for not remembering their names... but they were over-accommodating and really made us feel like royalty!!!

Great prices, fast delivery!!

I do wish they had a little bit larger sized wings and more sauce but they were perfectly cooked!!

I've only gone to Mr. of Rittenhouse on Sundays, but I plan on keeping up my normal routine because i've had awesome experiences with the girl who cuts on Sundays, Libby. She always gives me a cut exactly in line with what I tell her I want. She's really cool to talk to which is important to me because awkward haircuts are well.....awkward. Would totally recommend Libby and the barber shop.
The atmosphere was. They chips and salsa were yummy. The fajitas were absolutely delicious.  The wait staff were phenomenal.

Good service, Kristine was our server. She asked if we were first timers there and explain the menu. 

We started out with mimosas (lemon berry & black cherry), they were about 7$ each. A pitcher with 4 drinks was 26$.

My mom ordered the Mardi Gras Omelet (crawfish,shrimp, and andouille sausage), she stated that the andouille sausage had all the flavor while the shrimp and crawfish didn't really have flavor. She also ordered the tomato hollandaise on the side, so she didn't have that additional flavor there. She was really pleased with the grits, she wasn't let down at all.

I ordered the seasonal Strawberry Pound Cake French Toast. Strawberries were amazingly fresh and was also topped with whipped cream. I sent this dish back because the pound cake's texture seemed like the pound cake was previously frozen and thawed, maybe grilled for grill marks. Not pleasingly at all.

The Beignet Biscuits were amazing, comes with honey marmalade with orange zest.

I am willing to try this place again, maybe order something that is required to be made fresh.
Fantastic place for a pedicure. Try the hot stone massage option. It's heavenly!
Food ok. Bar food and " new age menu"everything with beets , blue cheese or eggs. Non of which I like on my hamburger.

BarBQue Shrimp PoBoy. Amazing!!!
Purchased the 11" sandwich, didn't think I could eat the whole sandwich, couldn't stop myself...
Hubby got the 11" roast beef, he loved it.
Onion rings were crispy and awesome as well. Do I need to say it, yes we ate all of those also.
Stuffed yes, but so worth every bite.
I got won ton soup from this place one time, I opened it up and it smelled like cabbage so I threw  it out. Not a fan.
Best bagels in town! Seriously good bagels and cream cheese, best prices around and friendly employees. Will never go to blue sky bagels again!

I'm currently finishing my Teri Chicken and Teri Beef plate with rice and mac salad, and it is by far the best food truck meal I've ever had. They were quick and friendly. They made conversation with every customer coming to order before just asking what they wanted. Great experience with amazing food!

Loved the atmosphere,  food,  music and our server Cristen with a "C". She was very attentive. She answered every question thoroughly. Very sweet individual. She made our first experience a memorable one. The wings were amazing. They had the grouper on special and it was delicious! The fish tacos had a twist, instead of cabbage they use a cheese. The weather was perfect to be outside and the views are beautiful. The drinks were also very good and reasonably priced. I will definitely be back!

Went here for dinner, a much needed break from studying.  Nothing bad to say for this place.  I had the shrimp with watercress dumplings and clams with ground pork in black bean sauce.  The dumpling was good but nothing special.  The clams again was good but not memorable.  I wouldn't mind going again but I would not gush and tell everyone about it.

Real New York pizza.  I tried a slice of pepperoni and supreme.  The pepperoni was excellent but not as large as I had expected. The supreme was a bit disappointing as the toppings appeared meager. I highly recommend the pepperoni. Decent amount of off street parking. Service was good.

Super friendly shop. I was visiting from out of state and needed some minor repairs to my Ibis Mojo 3. It was only 90 minutes before they closed so I called first to see if they could get me in.. They said to come on in and they would at least take a look. They made all the necessary tweaks with 30 minutes to go before closing. Friendly, knowledgeable & fair prices. Definitely recommend.
I love the pomodoro sandwich, I am from the land of pesto and their pesto is amazing. You have to try it.

Being a previous employee at this salon I would caution you to reconsider your patronage of this establishment. During my brief employment my baby and I both were diagnosed with the hand foot mouth virus. I clearly explained to Tony the severity of this virus, provided documentation and information on the condition from my Doctor, and immediately left work at the first symptoms. Tony continued to show no regard for client safety by continually asking me to return before my doctors clearance. He even harrassed my husband threatening to fire me if I did not return to work. For those of you not familiar with HFMD I have attached a link. It is a virus very common among children yet highly contagious for approximately 7 to 10 days.

https://www.cdc.gov/features/handfootmouthdisease/index.html

A MUST GO for anyone visiting (or living in) the Tampa Bay area!

Dining at Bern's Steakhouse is an experience you won't soon forget. Although it's known for its legendary (DELICIOUS!) steak, beef-lovers, fish eaters, and vegetarians alike will have no problem finding options here. 

Upon entering Bern's, the dim lights and red decor immediately suggest you've entered into restaurant greatness. Once you give your name to the host or hostess, you may have to wait a bit for your table to be ready (even if you have reservations, which I highly recommend). Don't worry...because once you are seated, any wait will be totally worth it!

I have dined at Bern's twice, and both times, the waiter or waitress has been extremely friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable about the menu and food options. I don't typically eat steak, but I couldn't resist at Bern's, and it was AMAZING! All steak dinners (and boy, do they have PLENTY of steak options!) come with onion soup, salad, and plenty of sides, so you'll certainly be satisfied! The wine list is extensive, as well, so you can find the perfect wine to pair with your meal.

Once dinner is finished, you'll be given the option of taking a tour of the kitchen and wine cellar, and/or enjoying dessert in their legendary Harry Waugh Dessert Room. Even though I have the biggest sweet tooth, I have yet to enjoy dessert at Bern's, sadly. Still...I have no doubt I will be back, and perhaps next time, I will go straight for the dessert!! :)
It's always good and fresh. And it doesn't take to long to get it. The chicken cheese steak is the best   The Stromboli's are awesome. Five thumbs up

Excellent food; we had the crevette appetizer, veal chop, filet and creme brûlée which was all delicious.  The filet dish came with a grilled onion, mashed potatoes, horseradish cream and a wonderful sauce - it was possibly the best steak I've had at a restaurant!  Nice ambiance.

My boyfriend and I arrived for brunch on Saturday, excited to try the food at our new neighborhood joint. We arrived at peak brunch time (~10:30am) and were seated right away. Once the waiter took our order, however, it took close to 30 minutes for the food to arrive. And once it finally did, we realized that he'd misunderstood us and brought out the entree portion of the pancakes instead of the short stack we'd ordered. When we brought it to his attention, it became a bit awkward because he insisted we'd ordered the entree and left it on the table. Fortunately, the food was quite good. Later on towards the end of the meal, however, we tried to flag down another waitress for a coffee refill while our own waiter was grabbing the check, and she gave us a really dismissive look and pointed to our waiter as if to say "he's taking care of it". When I tried to point to my coffee cup and signal we wanted a refill and not the check, she threw me an even dirtier look. Needless to say, I gave up on the coffee. The waiter did adjust the check to reflect the error, but we still left disappointed by the service.

I haven't washed my car in a LONG, LONG, LONG time and it was really dirty.  So I started out at one of the regular do it yourself car washes.  I spent $6 for the high pressure soap, foam brush, high pressure and spotless rinses.  

Then I came to Champion Car Wash and paid $10 for their mid grade wash.  After completing the car wash I parked at one of the vacuum stations and started wiping down my car.  This is when I noticed that the hood, roof & back of my car still had some visible dirt caked on.  I sort of expected this since, again, I haven't washed my car in ages & not to mention it's a black car...so you can see EVERYTHING!  I showed the attendant the dirt, I even told him it was because I hadn't washed my car in a longtime and asked for a recommendation as to how and what to use to get the dirt off.  I was surprised by his reply, he said, "I recommend that you come back through after you finish vacuuming and I'll spot clean it with a brush and put you through the car wash again, no charge."  That was really unexpected, he went above and beyond.  I was honestly expecting him to recommend a product of some sort.  

Anyway, this didn't necessarily fix my issue but it was noticeably better.  I'll more than likely have to get it professionally detailed to really get the grime off the car, but I will definitely come back to and recommend this car wash due to the level of customer service.

Brisket, ribs, pulled chicken, pulled pork, turkey...I've had them all. They're all delicious. I'd say my favorite is probably the brisket, but don't ignore the turkey. Really good. 

With it's close proximity to SLU, Pappy's is a great place to go before an event at SLU.

Bridge and Tap is a great date night location with an extensive beer and wine menu. If you enjoy cheese plates, this is definitely the spot for you. I enjoyed the fact that it is easy to create an a la carte customizable cheese board, and if you choose this option, make sure you get the Foccacia and clover honey! 
For dinner, I had the steak sandwich and it was absolutely delicious. I left feeling absolutely stuffed. My only knock is the tiny size of the tables. The menus are on these large clipboards, and in between plates and drinks, there isn't a lot of room and everything ends up feeling fairly crowded. 
Overall, it was a great experience, and my partner and I are excited to come back again soon!

I'm giving this establishment a big negative my friends. My bf and I tried to book a trip to Howl-O-Scream on a budget, and this was the LAST place we should have cut corners on. Good price?? Heck yeah!! ($30) But, they say you get what you pay for... And did we ever! Filthy carpets, smarmy people swarming outside, the bathroom was gross, teeny ants were crawling all over the sink... And my favorite part?? The police officer that posted up in the parking lot, all.. Night... LONG!! I may not know much about the 5-0's but I know they don't overnight park at the Hyatt. Long story short... Don't let this review fool you; we have stayed at many a Motel 6 on our budget trips, and I can say that not all Motel 6's are created equal... But the one on East Fowler in Tampa?! Do yourself a favor, and spend the extra money to keep yourself outta this place!
Literally the best brunch ever and I travel a good deal! Go go go and you won't regret it!

Rooms can use a definite update.  For $200+ the window had a broken lock.  No in room guest safe, made more concerning since they still using standard key locks vs swipe cards for entry TV had a VHS (yes VHS player!!! )   Minimal furnishings were in poor condition - none of the basics that one might expect for that price point.

World of Beer glows enticingly as you emerge from the 4th Avenue underpass, but that is where the enticements end. Why couldn't we have gotten a cooler bar/restaurant in that space? I think the other reviewers on Yelp have WOB pretty well nailed down: it's soulless. Calling it vanilla gives vanilla a bad name. If you're stuck going to some corporate douche-pub in the bottom of a glorified dorm, at least it should be cheap and fast. WOB is neither, but that won't be a problem if you live upstairs and your trust fund is overflowing.

I guess you could go to World of Beer. You could also go to Phoenix.

I've been to this location a few times over the last year or so & never had a problem. If you like JJ's sandwiches that's exactly what you'll get here. There was no line at around 2 PM on a Saturday I was in & out super quick. Sandwich was made super quick & correctly. Still wish JJ's offered salt & pepper, but I just added it at home like always.

This is place makes great food. Their prices are reasonable and the pizza slices are unbelievably large. They also make great cheesesteaks and french fries. Sometimes  
Its a great neighborhood food spot.

I took two white blouses in that were getting a little yellow and needed some help. They did turn out on the whole much better, but, when I received them back, on one of them the first button was missing and the second had clearly been yanked off (also ripping a hole in the fabric) and then very sloppily sewed back on to try to hide the hole that was now there... So much for trying to save that blouse...

First off, let me say that the only reason this is getting ONE star is because the area and grounds are very beautiful; full of trees, grass, two pools, and dog stations throughout. And that is the ONLY reason this is getting one star.

Let's first start out by saying that the "staff", if that's what'd you call them, are super unfriendly and unhelpful when it comes to anything that they aren't used to hearing. Oh, you have trouble with your apartment? I'll put you on a list for this Saturday when the maintenance men come out. But the guy won't actually show up until Monday, but then you'll have to come back for another appointment because the guy did it wrong the first time. The staff is a mash of lazy, ignorant, unintelligent women. And if you call the manager, Veronica, a "manager", that's a joke. Her experience and intelligence level suggests a job at the local Taco Bell/KFC down the road is more suitable for her. 

The lease is literally a prison sentence. READ THE FINE PRINT, because that "staff" in there doesn't go over the lease with you. Like how on page one it states that rent is due the first and if you're X amount of days late, you'll get charged a penalty fee, when on the next page it says a totally different number of days...can you say contradictory? I sure can. Oh! And not to mention that your lease may end October 31 but you need to let them know 60 days prior to vacating or be charged $1300/month. Be prepared for a whole lot of this plus some when it comes to their lease. 

Gated community? That's a joke within itself. There are three sets of gates that are controlled by a remote control, that may or may not work on any given day.
