If you don't have a remote, well you can forget about getting in the gate that comes from the Fry's parking lot. And the two entering by the leasing office? Oh, there's a pin box there that you can punch in your 4-digit code...let's say 6-7 times before the gates even open! And if you go in and let the "staff" know, they let you know in a very rude way that, "We know"...and that's it. And they sure don't work on it, trust me. The gate I mentioned before, leading from the Fry's parking lot? It's either never working, or ALWAYS open. So let me ask you, does that sound like a secure gated community? One of my neighbours had the tires off his car stolen because of this! And when he went to tell the "staff"? "We're working on it". Like they have been for months? And the wall right by that gate is set up like a set of stairs, so anyone can climb right on up and jump the wall to this "secure" environment. Let's not forget to mention the one gate you can go out of with a key that also leads into the Fry's parking lot doesn't work...WHATSOEVER. A teenage kid showed me how he opens it: with a STICK!! Oh, and let's go into the office and let the "staff" know, what do you get? Another rude response, "Well what key did you use?" They give you three keys, mind you, one super small one for your mailbox, a regular sized key for the lock in your apartment and a HUGE key for the gate. I'm sorry, I'm not that ignorant to use the wrong key. 

The irrigation system that runs through this place? Oh, yeah, you mean the huge waterfalls that erupt from the "sprinklers"? Or the huge holes that are dug around the sprinklers that are left unattended or fixed for weeks on end, while children are running around this place and the holes are about the size of my head and about two feet deep? 

Let's talk about the lawn people who come twice a week for a second. Who in their right mind sets up a lawn service twice a week when this grass does NOT at all need that much maintenance. Especially by a bunch of guys for, literally, 6-7 hours on Thursdays and Tuesdays. Literally going around with their leaf blowers, blowing what I might ask, for hours. No joke. I sat and watched a guy through my window standing in the same spot with his leaf blower for ten minutes not doing jack squat. Seriously. Then he saw me sitting there and started to swivel his blower blowing at the nonexistent leaves on the ground. I'm not kidding, it is the most ANNOYING thing I have EVER experienced in my life and I'm from Detroit. 

Dogs are welcome here, which is great, if you're a responsible owner. The "staff" sends out a monthly newsletter to tell you what's going on during that month and there was notice that you will be asked to leave the apartment complex if you do not clean up after your pet...sure isn't enforced. I'll say that. I see piles of dog crap literally about twenty feet from the dog poop station. So, I may ask, is there any surveillance by the "staff"? I'm guessing not. 

That theatre they advertise? Oh, it hasn't been working for 6 months. Just so you know. And the gym they provide? The key works, sometimes. The times it does, you literally have to kick down the door to get in. 

So in conclusion, these are not "luxury" apartments as they advertise. They are just a sad excuse for a living arrangement

This place needs work! I waited 10 mins in line, drive threw and I still haven't given them my order, and when I finally gave them my order after being told we will be right with you, 2 times, I still waited another 10 mins before I was handed my food. And there was ONE other person in front of us, and I didn't see anyone in line, because I had a friend that was with me. Who went inside no one in there eating or standing in line. He even said when he got back to the car he touched his chicken fingers, and immediately said " oh no, I'm not accepting this, these are ice cold." Went back inside and waited 4 mins for fresh hot food. And then, When I got home, my food was ice cold. I hate this burger king, food is always cold,( and they were not understaffed cause there were 3 ppl, walking around taking their time with my meal, talking and laughing with one another) so that is out of the question, they were not rude, but not friendly either! HEY CORPERT FIX THIS MESS! I have been hoping they will finally fix this place, but nothing has changed for years!
Sincerely a concerned customer that would love to come here more often for good food, that I pay for. And when I eat it, its not worth it in the end.
Amazing food! The grilled blue points are amazing, sushi is always fresh, and you can't get better bread pudding anywhere!

I left my two dogs with Joshua for a weekend.  I am thrilled with the care they received while they were with him.  This was my first time leaving my dog here, prior to their stay I brought my dogs over to meet Joshua and his dogs.  I was impressed with the care that Joshua took to make sure that his home and yard were safe and pet friendly.  He also made sure my dogs were up to date on their vaccinations.  He is very knowledgeable about dogs and has a genuine love for animals.  When I returned my dogs were happy and it was obvious that they were well cared for while I was away.  Joshua sent me a few updates along with pictures while I was gone which was comforting.  I have to say that being able to leave my dogs in a home setting so much better than having to kennel them.
They have a great Shrimp Po Boy at lunch that is always good and done the right way!!

The burger was ultra greasy and had a wild taste to it I was not a fan the fries were good. The price was average but since I could not eat the burger seemed expensive 13 dollars for fries (burger was included in price ).  The person who took the order was friendly did really wait long but if the store had more people in it I could see it taking longer to get food. I can't say that I would not recommend this Resturant some people maybe would like the flavor of the burger.

I was in Manayunk on a warmish evening in February with my husband, and the mood of the neighborhood was not bustling. We passed a number of restaurants and looked over menus, finally settling on Il Tartufo. 

We had a total of four servers serve us in a half-filled room. Our meal started with the hostess, followed by the waitress and the food-server, and finished with a bus boy to clear our foods away. Needless to say, the place has impeccable service. I don't have impeccable wine-choosing skills and I relied on our waitress to guide me to a glass of their Montepulciano which was quite nice and a good portion for $7.50. 

The bread at the tables was wonderful; it was real crusty Italian bread along with some salty flat breads. For an appetizer, I had the warm mozzarella which paired with the bread well. It wasn't the best mozzarella I'd ever had; I think I prefer it on the cold side. For my main meal, I ordered the gnocchi with red sauce. The portion was gigantic and I had enough left over for an entire second meal. The gnocchi was delicate and delightful and the red sauce was just right. This place does Italian well. Next time, I might skip the appetizer, and save my money and room in my stomach for a giant bowl of pasta.

Watch your bill when you check out.  Apparently there are new owners and they have raised the fees!  Picked up meds for my pet, the price went up 110%, yes 110% from last month.  MORE THAN DOUBLED over last month.

Food is ok and bit pricey, but the lady at the counter has some attitude problem. If the Owner is reading this, check who was working at the night shift on 02/19/2012. I know it was one year back but my draft review was sitting for so long. She has no knowledge abt the menu and no customer facing skills. If the place is still surviving I only hope she must hv left it which is a big favor to the owner.

I'm going to give it one star bcuz the food was ok. But there was this old lady which I believe was Tara and she was the rudest old lady I have ever met. I heard the people who worked there was rude but I still gave it a shot, not even 1 minute in the shop n she slammed her hand on the counter n was extremely rude. I live 40 minutes away n drove to her for the kind of attitude, screw her n her run down shop.

We were looking for a new place to try this weekend and decided to go to The Tavern. Set in the basement with low ceiling, it has a warm and cozy feel. Dress code is casual too, which is cool.
They have a good selection of cocktails and wines. My husband ordered one of the tequila based cocktails and I got the house Cabernet. We got kale chips with the "secret sauce", deviled eggs and roasted veggies-mushroom with poached egg for appetizers, sliders with edamame-mushroom-black bean burger for entrees. Everything was great!!! I must add that the house wine was impressive. We think the secret sauce is mayo+sriracha+garlic, whatever it is it went perfectly well with those crispy kale chips..yumm! I am a vegetarian and it is pretty hard to find a GOOD veggie burger right but the slider with black bean burger here is probably the BEST I have had so far.
Will definitely go back for more with friends and family! :)

Seven stars. The ambience is dark, intimate, and not too loud. The service is beyond superlative--the staff radiated genuine concern for our dining experience. At one point my wife dropped her napkin and a waiter immediately appeared out of nowhere with a clean napkin. That was a first. We had the stroganoff, and the celery root. Amazing flavors. I can't recommend the experience enough. Well done Tinker Street.

I expect to wait a while to get seated in a busy restaurant but we were promptly greeted by George the head server that made every effort to get us a table. Once seated we decided to go with the $35 mezze which was absolutely outstanding. At the end of our meal we were treated to a complimentary baklava for our wait before we were seated. The best service i had ever recieved!!!

El Agave follows the norm for most Mexican Restaurants in St. Louis, but it stands out with:
1.  The food is always decent.  I never had a bad dish here.  They have an interesting dish on their specialties that I've never seen anywhere else.  I forget the name of it, but it comes in this big coal chalice and contains cactus, big chunks of cheese, and other miscellaneous fun.  Try it! 
2.  The server is very good.  Everyone's friendly and they check up on you many times
3.  The place is spic and span.  
4.  Their 4 dollar margaritas at lunch time are huge...and not just 98% sweet and sour mix.
5.  They always have a ton of specials listed on their markerboard up front.
I ordered the sesame chicken. There was plenty of chicken and it was very tasty. The restaurant itself was a little grungy. But all in all a good lunch

Food is good but the service is really slow.  We have been here several times because it is close to home and the food is good but the service is always slow.  Slow to take your order and bring your food

This is a vibrant gem on this dingy strip of East Washington. Located next to the Englewood library, or East Washington street library, is this little multi-colored house. Inside, a tiny, but clean grab and go Mexican restaurant featuring various tamales and tacos. I ordered the lunch special of 3 tacos and a side of rice. I had to wait a few minutes to place my order but the man cooking and cashiering was very nice, and very thorough. It's not handicap accessible, there is no wheelchair ramp, which is unfortunate, but it's really, really yummy. I had the beef tacos with pico de gallo and I thought about it the rest of the day. I almost went back for dinner, but they close by 6.

First things first...... Imitation Crab Meat!! YUCK!!!
I expect my SIT DOWN RESTAURANT food to not be take-out style! 
This food was nasty! I'm not a complainer but I was not happy with the wait or the taste of the food!
Good burger. Lots of options, which is sweet. I like the condiment choices too. Friendly, helpful staff. Good stuff. A friend said the "impossible" (vegan burger) was good.

Great patio, food and desserts. 
My wife and I are from Houston.  We came to this place thanks to other yelp reviews. Everything looked good, but the bacon wrapped mac and cheese was the main reason why we came to this place. Unfortunately they didn't have it. We sat on the patio and Miss Susan was our waitress. She did a phenomenal job. She is very kind and helpful recommending places to go to. 
We were here on Martini Monday, so they have martinis for $5. We ordered two of them and they were good. 
FOR DINNER we ordered:
- Jalapeño and cream cheese stuffed dumplings with raspberry sauce. This was great. Never tried something like it in houston.
- Crab meat nachos. They were great, but they definitely need to be a little bit more expensive, so they can add more crab meat. They are only $10, so we understand why there's so little crab. But everything else on it was fabulous, the pineapple, onions, mango sauce, and cheese was great. They would make them great if they make them more expensive and add more crab meat.
- Grouper Sandwich. It had a great flavor, and the jalapeño slaw that came with it was delicious, but the bun was a little too much bread. They can do better by making a lighter bread.
FOR DESSERT we ordered:
- Canolli. It was huge and full of flavor, just as the one we had the night before in a Italian's home. This place definitely made it taste italian, too. It was only $3.50.
- Chocolate cake with white cream layers. I don't remember the name, but you can come in and find it displayed as soon as you come inside the restaurant. This cake was delicious,  it was enormous (2 of my fists together), surprisingly moist for a high cake, and balanced -my wife and I love chocolate but we hate a cloying chocolate cake.
I love this place!!!! Natalie is fantastic. I have had scads of facials but the facial I got here was by far the best ever.  I will not go anywhere else ever.  My skin is looking amazing.

Definitely a local and family gathering place. On nice days the place is always packed inside and out. People bring their kids, dogs, their own food and lawn chairs and chill outside.

The beer lineup is fantastic. Had some nice and interesting beer there. Never been disappointed. They have some nice collaboration with neighboring breweries, as well as guest brews.

They don't serve food but you can order from many nearby restaurants or bring your own.

Service is basically non-existent. You go up to the counter/bar and order drinks and clean up after yourself.

5 stars for their beer, 1 star off for nothing else is offered.

LOOOOOOOOVE WAX WERKS!! Thank you girls for everything. You girls are the best at what you do and you don't make anything or anyone feel awkward. Its a really friendly, clean and professional environment. I have gone more than 5 times and every time its a great experience.

I cannot rave enough about Dr. Walters and the quality of care she gives to me and my family.  I've been seeing her for over a year and my husband and my kids also receive regular treatment from her.  She takes the time to fully asses you and then offers treatment that for us has brought immediate improvement.  My kids feel comfortable seeing her for aches and pains as well.  She will offer at home exercises if that is the best treatment.  I feel like I get a great value at each appointment and am not being rushed through nor being tried to use up all my insurance benefits like at some chiropractors I've been to previously.  The office is clean and quiet.  All the staff is friendly and courteous.   Put away that bottle of Advil and go get some lasting relief from this excellent chiropractor.

Read the reviews before coming here. 
Straight to the food. I ordered 4 oysters from the Happy Hour menu which were dollar oysters( oysters, clams and shrimp were a dollar from the happy hour menu). They were subpar at best. Nothing special about them.
Happy Hour Mussels were 7$ and on menu it would be 12$. Flavor was Coconut Thai Red Curry. I wish they added more of the broth and less salty. Some of the mussels had a distinct taste which they were not fresh.
I tried the small plate - Scallops - Truffle Almond Vinaigrette. Worst dish I ate here. just oily and had no flavor at all. They cut up the scallops in half. I wanted the whole pieces as seen in photos. Disappoint for a 16.25$ dish. Skip it or try the Ceviche next time.
If you like cider apple try the Celt cider beer. Pretty tasty and yummy.
Noise level...it can get loud and the ambience is unique.

Service was good & friendly staff. Just disappoint on the food. Maybe come back to try another dish.

Every time I've gone to Nashville, we always come here. I don't even plan on coming we just do because it is so good. I suggest making a reservation because it does get busy but we were leaving the Saints vs Titans game and so happened to be that there was no wait. I will say we have gone here before and asked how long the wait it and it is usually pretty long. We are seated and they bring you popcorn. We ordered the spinach dip to share and I got the grilled chicken sandwich. TWAS AMAZING! I honestly didn't have high expectations for a grilled chicken sandwich but this one was perfect! I also got a side of tater tots that is fried in duck fat with a side of smoked ketchup aka bbq sauce.

Our Valentines dinner went exactly as hoped. Seated as soon as we walked in (had 6pm resv) prompt service from hostess and wait staff. In and out in an hour. Lobster bisque never disappoints! And the valentines-special entre I had was off the wall good! We were very satisfied as usual. Which isn't surprising, I don't think I've ever had a complaint in the 20+ years I have been coming here. And the new owners are keeping it great. Keep up the good work, we'll be back again soon!

I got Hairy Mexican and Crazy Tuna.  I wish my Crazy Tuna had a little more avocado on it but it was still really good.  My husband got Chicken Teriyaki which he liked and it came with amazing but spicy green beans.  We got popped beans for an appy, which is really just edamame with soy sauce, and we each ordered a dessert (hey, it was my birthday!).  I got  Tempura Brownie and he got Peanut Butter Panic (peanut butter cup sundae).  Both were alright, they were huge though so feel free to share.  

Overall I'd recommend this place for out of town visitors and people within walking distance.  Parking there is a nightmare.

This is THE most charming hotel I've ever stayed in!!

I knew when I walked through the door that this was my kinda place...a grand old house turned boutique hotel. They did not come in, renovate to modern standards and subsequently rip the soul out like most places do here. If they've made improvements, which I'm sure they have, you'd never know it. The historical integrity has been successfully left completely intact. Full of everything you'd expect from an old New Orleans home, right down to the antiques that decorate the entire hotel. I'm sure they came with the place.

AND there's a lobby cat!!! He's supposed to be protecting the joint, but he was asleep on the job! Lol

I rode up to my room in the world's tiniest elevator. So adorable. There was even a sign that cautioned against ballroom dancing lol My mouth dropped open as I walked through the door to my room. It's like they knew me! A step back in time. My pictures do not do it justice. I have a thing for exposed brick. There was plenty of that! I was raised in a home built in 1902 and I felt right at home here with the familiar ambiance. And the cutest balcony!!! I slept with the door to the balcony wide open to hear the sounds of the French Quarter. My room faced Toulouse Street and it was surprisingly quiet considering how close it is to Bourbon Street!

The building itself has twists and turns and many hidden courtyards. I spent an hour just exploring the place and taking in the history. Its fun to imagine what it must have been like back in the day.

I am completely charmed by this place. I will definitely stay here again. I will also suggest it to friends who want a true New Orleans experience!!!

This hotel was crap. Look, if you're easy to persuade with a warm, melty chocolate chip cookie -- then this is the best hotel ever.

For those of you that would like to be considered as a unique guest, and not inconvenienced by a hotel with no elevators, a complex that is 'complex' to navigate -- then, go somewhere else. As a business traveler, I do ask for convenience in where my room is, relative to the gym/spa & any elevator. The front desk staff only "sounded nice", because that's the DoubleTree standard - to sound nice. Sound aside, the front desk put me in a room that was towards the back of the property, on the 2nd floor, and about 3min from the nearest staircase. I did arrive late, and my patience was diminished -- but in reality, the inconvenience of the property amenities, did not help.

Go somewhere else. Or, if this is your only option --- make sure to tell the front desk to put you somewhere CONVENIENT. They were at less than 50% occupancy on my visit, and was placed where the social outcasts are sent!

(Side Note: This visit made me look at Starwood properties [www.spg.com]. I signed up, and feel a lot better).

2 Stars because the bed was clean & the pillows soft.

I don't frequent the bars downtown due to most being tourist traps but I've been to Acme a couple of times for work events.  It is a huge place and I've only been to the bar on the second floor.  The atmosphere is nice, music is everywhere and they usually have live music on at least one floor.  They also have pool tables and a couple of other game tables.  I have not experienced this establishment on a weekend night but I'm sure it can be quite crowded.  It's not so bad during the week from my experience.

The food is good and the drinks are strong.  I've tried the BBQ nachos and hot chicken, both of which were seasoned well and nice portion sizes.  Acme is a great spot for group events and to take out-of-towners.  One downside is parking but that is the case anyplace downtown these days.  If you park in the Acme lot, you will pay about $20.  Better off taking Lyft or Uber.

It was pretty packed so we ended up eating outside. This was to be expected on a Saturday night. It was cold outside but they had heaters to help. The food came out pretty quick! The catfish and the Cajun shepherds pie was great! The portions were great so it filled us up!The red beans and rice were average. Our waitress was very attentive in the beginning then disappeared. We didn't get any alcohol. No one in the group likes Hurricanes so we just skipped it. With it being so cold outside the food got cold pretty fast but that is what happens when you sit outside. Overall would go again and try out more things!
They got rid of the receipt checking, and they have a decent selection. In the upper half of Guitar Centers I've seen.
Consistently great food no matter the day or time and reasonable prices.  Really love this place!

The brisket was good, but the pulled pork was outstanding.  Mac and cheese could have used a few less breadcrumbs, but the flavor was still good.  The beans were awesome.  Prices were average.  Still, gotta give it five stars just for the pulled pork awesomeness.

We were so glad to finally go out to dinner after being quarantined for so long. Made a reservation at 6:30 for party of 4. Tables were spaces apart in accordance with city orders. Walked in & wasn't sure where to check in. Walked to the back & someone finally approached us. Three of us ordered steaks & 2 came out like they were requested. 1 ribeye was ordered medium rare but delivered well done. Sent that back then 4 minutes later waiter brought another steak but immediately came back before I cut into it and took it away saying it was a strip steak and brought that to another table. 5 minutes later another steak appeared & when it was cut it was so rare almost raw! The very large "manager" was so disrespectful & non apologetic after we told the poor waitress to just take it back & to take it off the bill. Everyone had already finished eating by then. Came home & made a sandwich . The Balogna family needs to get rid of this man & get the kitchen to step it up.

Let me start by stating that I am a big fan of micro brewery restaurants (ex. Iron Hill, Victory, and McKenzie's to name a few).  After skimming through some Yelp reviews we went to Barren Hill with mediocre expectations but certainly hoped for the best.  The reviews for this establishment are really all over the board.  Unfortunately I walked away knowing that a 2 star Yelp rating with the description of: "Meh.  I've experienced better" is a more than fair assessment of BHTB.

We had 7:30 reservations and arrived at about 7:20.  The hostesses (tandem) confirmed that we were a little early and were welcome to wait in the bar area.  I actually took this as a good sign, perhaps the place was full of happy diners.  But upon entering the bar area and taking a peek around it was extremely noticeable that that there were many open tables.  The majority of the visible restaurant staff seemed to move in a frantic fashion and gave the impression of being a little overwhelmed.  As 7:40 approached, we went back to the lobby area and waited for one of the two hostesses to reappear.  As one did, I asked if it would be possible to be seated in the bar area.  The hostess gave us an emphatic yes and showed us to a table.  The other dining areas further away from the bar seemed to have overly excessive lighting, as the rooms were obnoxiously bright and didn't jive with the darker setting of the core of the "tavern".

BHTB has a fairly expansive beer menu.  Unfortunately though many of the selections seem undrinkable.  I am under the belief that they most likely keep brewing until they find a winner as the rejects remain in the kegs...  In this case they are probably still concocting new brews.  Fourteen, that is right 14, draft beers from BHTB are currently available on tap.   Their pints run between $5 and $6 and many are on the heavier side of ABV (alcohol by volume).  We gave our waitress the opportunity to endorse some of the house brews, she failed to do so.

Our server, although friendly, seemed to be a little aloof.  We waited almost fifteen minutes for our beverages to arrive.  Fortunately or unfortunately, both of our selections were not worth the wait.  This seemed to be the state of pace for the rest of the evening as we ended up spending close to two hours at BHTB.

For starters, I went with the wedge salad and the wife went for the roasted beet salad.  The wedge was perfectly acceptable.  The roasted beet salad however seemed to be missing the roasted aspect.  The beets appeared to resemble the straight out of the can variety.  See White Dog Cafe for how this is properly done.

For dinner, I went for the steak frites, due to the fact that they were out of the scallops.  My wife opted for the quesadilla.  Both selections were fair, nothing out of this world but perfectly acceptable.  I probably should not have ordered the most expensive option($25) on the menu given how everything was playing out up until that point but the steak was fine.  My fries were served w ian accompaniment of BHTB's very own spicy ketchup.  They may need to go back to the drawing board as I found it awful and there was no condiments readily available on the table to substitute with.  

Again, we have definitely experienced better and after dropping a little over $70 here, we walked away feeling much less than impressed.  With many great options out there within the micro-brew category, we most likely will not be heading back to BHTB.

Unique atmosphere.  A Harley Shop revamped.  Full bar.  Friendly staff.  Today was a soft opening.  Looking forward to the full opening and  full menu in a couple weeks. 

I recommended the pulled pork and the spicy slaw.  Just enough heat to keep the taste buds tingling.

If you ask an usher at the Wilma if your seats are good seats, they will tell you that there are no bad seats. And it's true. The Wilma is a very small theatre, and arranged so that pretty much wherever you are sitting is a good view. Every production we've seen has had a nice set that is easy to get a glimpse of the action from all sides. The seats are really comfortable and sitting in them for the ~2 hours of the play is a breeze. While a slightly expanded concession stand would be nice, that's my only qualm with the Wilma. It's pretty fantastic otherwise.

The quality of the plays and actors have always been great; so far, we've seen three shows there and all of them have been very impressive, even when one of the lead actresses was sick and another woman had to read her lines from the sidestage! The theatre seems to pick very modern, on topic shows with a good variety of subjects. Tonight's production of Becky Shaw had me laughing out loud and clapping, something I have never, ever done before. 

But my favorite thing about the Wilma? Student season subscriptions, four plays for $32. Which is an incredible steal considering how nice the theatre is, and the quality that the productions seem to be.

I went to Annie's for the first time while visiting family .  I live in Los Angeles where food is taken very seriously and I must say that Annie's food was the best cuisine I have had in a very long time!I My taste buds were blown away!!  I ordered the spinach and tomato quiche along with potato salad.  My family ordered the Beefy Brit, the Palermo and the grilled Chicken sandwiches.  In a matter of minutes we were oohing and ahhing over the food.  We immediately started sharing with each other.  The staff is amazing as well!  Please open an Annie's in LA!!!!

This is my second year here and this time, sucks! First I'm on the first floor and the screen on the window is bent and the other window isn't able to lock! I used one of the hangers to stop the window from being opened, from the outside. The night shift is ignorant. The back parking lot didn't know how to open the gate, no directions on how to access, they need directions on how to insert your room key to open the gate. Now here is the big issue. I'm paying over $150.00 for this room and the TV isn't HD? Y'all need to upgrade your TV SYSTEM and why is there no volume on Fox 29? Get you stuff fixed! Trust me I will never stay in this dump again.

I'm into all kinds of music but I really like traditional country music. I moved to Nashville from NYC a little over a year ago and made the rounds looking for a bar that had decent food, a good booze selection and good music (without being a dump full of townies, bros, and drunk bachelorettes)... So that pretty much rules out any place on Lower Broadway... I find the staff at The Local to be attentive and polite, (I don't know, maybe because I'm polite to them?)... Maybe there's a difference in the patrons on different nights, so I can only vouch for the vibe on nights where they feature classic honky tonk music, usually Friday, Saturday and Sunday. On those nights The Local attracts a nice mix of regulars and some out of towners of all ages. The sound guy is great and you will be hearing world class musicians do their thing (it is Nashville, after all)... I'm not young anymore, but I quickly discovered a local scene of mostly young people who are devoted to keeping the look, sound and spirit of traditional country music alive. The crowd on these nights is really great... some older Nashville folks, some tourists, and young people all tatted up and pierced, in vintage western wear all dancing and having fun together. Good country music and good country folks! ... it's quite endearing actually... I only drink bourbon and beer and the selection is good on those... I haven't tried everything on the menu but so far what I have ordered has far been superior to run of the mill bar food... 2 thumbs waaaaay up!

Such a let down. We decided to stop in, checked out the decor, pretty cool, however the establishment was not. We ordered the house margarita, thought I was drinking water with lime juice...so sour and so disappointing. What drew us in was the live music however, we stayed for a few songs, pretty good actually, a nice off-set from the overpriced drinks we had purchased. The atmosphere was nice a few people, just enough of a crowd to make it fun and interesting. The lead singer kept asking for song titles and requests. We all responded and things were going well, except for the end of the set, the gal informed us to make sure we tip the band, which would had been perfectly acceptable, however when one of the couples from the bar looked like they left, the band member lost her composure at that point and let us know how pissed off she was that they just took off without tipping her, not to her knowledge,  they just retreated for a bathroom break but heard all she had said and felt as if they needed tip tip her since she made such a stink about it. We were so appalled at that point that we decided to leave and no we did not tip the band, nor will we ever go back!! Don't waste your time your money and your tips!!!!

As my vacation to st Petersburg came to a close, we decided to go downtown and visit one more restaurant. El Metate caught our eye as it was located in a beautiful plaza and had outdoor seating. Upon being seated, we were greeted with complimentary fresh chips and salsa. We ordered a side of guacamole which was a bit pricey, but well worth it. The portion came with at least two avocados worth, and it was absolutely delicious. The main courses were great because the combos were affordable and allowed you to try multiple things. I got a bean burrito and a beef taco. Since it was a combo, it came with a side of beans and rice, which tasted out of this world!! The burrito was covered in a mild tomato salsa, which made it more like an enchilada, but it was delicious. The tacos had good quality meat ,however I personally  wasn't the biggest fan of the spicing. The crispy taco shells were unfortunately a bit soggy, so the tacos overall weren't a home run for me. However, the chips, salsa, guac, rice, beans, and service were fantastic. Once again I'd like to point out that although the guacamole cost a bit much, there was enough of it that I could eat it with my burrito and rice and beans. The place just has such great food and atmosphere. I highly recommend it.I would have given them five stars of not for the soggy taco shells.

Waited about 40 minutes in line on a Saturday at 1 PM. Walk in and order and someone will bring your food. Big Nasty was amazing. Biscuits are to die for. Princess was delicious but hard to eat without a fork. Shrimp and grits were delicious. Very accommodating to vegan in our group and made substitutes and changes without an issues. MUST TRY THE BONUTS!!!! super cute inside and perfect brunch spot!
This is the best place for breakfast, alligator sausage, eggs benedict, spicy bloody mary's and the best breakfast grits in tow.

If you are wise enough to book a room at the New Orleans Marriott, I hope you are lucky enough to encounter the friendliness and hospitality of Victor at the front desk. From his sincere smile, to his "on-point" recommendations and directions, you will not be disappointed. I utilized the on-site restaraunts and swimming pool on my 5 day trip, The room was clean and the hotel staff could not have been more accommodating. The location was located within walking distance to the heart of the French Quarter, and across the Street from Harrah's Casino.

Currently waiting for a table... was told it would be 15mins or more... not exactly sure why when I see 4 empty tables and 3 people not being helpful. Not off to a good start .. 
ok, table for 3 and we arrived at 707p. It was definitely busy for a Weds. 715p after having 3 employees walk by and not acknowledge the group at the door, I asked if we seat ourselves and was told a table would be ready in 15min... 727p we sit 733p we get water 743p we place our order to go cause we've waited too long. 745p my bill arrives. 747p I ask Giavanna (who made sure to spell her name for this review) tells me it will be 20min before my food is ready!?!?! What.. in.. the.. world. When I asked who was the manager (Henry) he turned and walked away. G' we'll call her for short, proceeded to spew her frustration towards me.. the customer! Needless to say, customer service is non existent. Go to the Exton location... your eating 10mins after walking in the door!

I always enjoy the food, it's the atmosphere that needs work. They keep the restaurant open to the patio and that allows flies to swarm when you are trying to eat. I think spraying may help keep them away. Sometimes when I eat there in the evening, it's too warm and humid. Also Sunday brunch would be a lot better if they staffed more than one waitress for the whole restaurant.
Had an excellent burger here but the fries were over cooked and over salted.  They are definitely consistent with the hamburger quality though.

My family has loved BBQ for the longest time! 
Even during the rib cook offs, we come to Famous Dave's BBQ booth. As pictured, I throughly enjoy their dinner platter for two people, just enough to share with someone or a group of people. Cornbread is so moist, along with their tasty meats. 
They have daily deals, that you cannot just pass up. They also offer discounts during the Reno Rodeo coming up next week, just bring your tickets! 

Don't forget to ring the bell on your way out, to show staff how much you enjoyed your food!

I keep hoping that they will get better, but they continue to be the worlds worst McDonald's. Nothing tastes quite right. The beef is rubbery, the toppings are hap-hazard in placement, and something is always broken. One day I was in there and they didn't have straws. Please McDonald's corporate, Fix this location for chestnut hill fast food lovers.

Save Your Car - Avoid Murray's Tire & Automotive

My husband's family has used Murray's, a small auto shop, for several years. They have purchased tires, gotten cars inspected, had oil changed, and some other work, as well.

Recently, I brought my Jeep Wrangler to Murray's for a state inspection in March. The inspection revealed that I needed new brakes, so our bill was over $600 for the inspection and new brakes.

A few weeks later, in April, I noticed that the "check engine" light was illuminated when I started up the car. Once we started moving, the car was sputtering, as well, so we took it back to Murray's. The verdict: only 5 of the 6 cylinders were firing. They notified me that they were replacing the spark plugs, starter and putting in a new engine coil. We paid over $500 on April 17th for all this additional work.

Two weeks later May 2nd, we took the Jeep out for a drive, since it was such a nice day. About 10 miles from home, the car broke down and just stopped working as we were rolling up to a traffic light. Luckily, we were able to push it to the side of the road. AAA arrived and jumped the car. The AAA technician stated that said my starter "looked really old." My starter should have looked brand new, as Murray's supposedly replaced it just two weeks prior.

We got the Jeep to Murrays, and on the morning of May 4th (two days later), the owner Mike Murray told me that I needed a new engine, as there was no oil in the jeep. I told him what AAA told me about the starter, and he said "those guys are always wrong," referring to AAA. I then proceeded to ask about why there wasn't any oil in the jeep, as the Jeep had been in the shop multiple times in the month and a half leading up to the current issue. Mike Murray told me that "it's not [his] job to check the oil unless [he is] doing an oil change." I was not due for an oil change for another 1,000 miles. I also hadn't driven more than 50 miles since I had gotten it out of the shop, so where did the oil go? It does not add up.

My husband got on the phone with Mike to ask more questions, and Mike said he would get back to us with an estimate. This was on Monday, May 4th. Because we still had not heard from him, my husband went to Murray's to check in on Thursday, May 7th. When my husband got there, Mike Murray said he was waiting to hear from us about what he wanted to do. I am not sure why he would be sitting around waiting for us to call him when we were told he would call us with an estimate a few days prior.

My husband asked more questions about why the oil wasn't checked, and how it couldn't have been gone or leaking since there are no oil leak spots in our parking lot. How could all the oil disappear when we have barely driven the car. Mike Murray then says, "You teach right? Your wife is like a high school student, they don't listen well." My husband says, "Are you saying my wife can't listen, because I was actually on the phone when you spoke with her." Mike Murray says, "Well then, you both can't listen."

We subsequently took the car to another mechanic, who miraculously found oil in the Jeep. He thinks this shop added oil after to the fact to cover up their mistakes. Our new mechanic, also let us know that this isn't the first time, he's seen cars messed up by Murrays Automotive. They've had several new customers due to the mistakes Murrays makes, and will never own up to.

Overall, this experience with Murray's Automotive and especially with owner Mike Murray has been wholly negative, frustrating, and overly expensive. The repair history that he claims to have provided does not add up given the current issues. Moreover, his lack of professionalism was exhausting to deal with and is just unacceptable. He attacked my husband and I's intelligence and listening skills, which is obviously no way to speak to a customer. Our interactions have been honestly ridiculou. We will never use Murray's Automotive again.

Really lovely atmosphere and great food! Super family friendly. Definitely going back soon. 
Had the arugula salad, tuna tricolor salad, eggplant Parmesan and a pizza, all were really great!
This place is amazing! I've tried the keto and cauliflower dough here with a few different toppings and it's always very good

Don't think you can get a decent bagel in Nashville? Proper Bagel is here to prove you wrong. 

I come here every other week to get a sesame bagel with rosemary garlic cream cheese. I'm not a sweet cream cheese person, but their savory cream cheeses are incredible. And unlike some other places, they don't skimp on the cream cheese. The drip coffee is also unexpectedly fantastic, and all of the baked goods that I have tried (black & white cookie, doughnuts) are as great as their bagels. 

I've been coming here since before COVID began, and their service was always consistently good. During the pandemic, they have done only takeout with no entry allowed. While customers sometimes harass the employees about having to order online and wait outside, I truly appreciate that Proper is one of the only Nashville businesses that has taken it seriously the entire time. Good for them for not putting their staff and customers at risk. The employees have continued to give great service even in this new service setup. Just make sure to place your order in advance, as there can be quite a long wait -- don't blame the employees for your failure to plan ahead!

I have also ordered from their special Jewish high holy day menus, and I've never been disappointed. Their challah, matzah ball soup, and rugelach were all as good as their bagels, and the owner was unbelievably kind & helpful throughout the ordering & pickup process. At a time when l couldn't be home for the holidays, Proper made things just a little bit more bearable.

Erica was amazing!!! I came to the salon while I was in New Orleans for a wedding and my expectations were surpassed. I was met with outstanding customer service and Erica went above and beyond to make the photos of the hair styles I wanted come to life. I would highly recommend this salon.

Great food and wine!  My husband took my daughter and I for Valentine's Day 2016 and we had a great time!  Food was delicious and had to come back again to take a girlfriend for her bday just to have the Diablo tortellini again,  so so good!!!
Amazing food at a great price.  This is a place you can go to everyday because there is always something different to eat.  Great neighborhood as well.

I just moved from California and needed my Depo shot ASAP. The man at the front desk was very kind and helpful. They were able to get me in right away. No complaints. Clean facility...kind and helpful staff. 10/10
The small yogurt can feed an army. They offer seasonal flavors as well. But I'm a sucker for the plain original tart. Reminds me of the Pinkberry I used to love.

The ONLY reason I shop here is because of the proximity and the exercise I get in walking here versus driving somewhere. The lines are ALWAYS long. The cashiers are always having issues with their registers, sometimes it's human and other times it's system error. (Man Vs. machine) The shelves always seem to be under stocked and missing important items. Whenever you need assistance you have to run a relay to find an associate to help. Most times they'll watch you looking for something and won't offer assistance or sometimes seem to dodge or hide from you.

Nice little Breakfast brunch place. Just go to bed to you has looks like he likes it. I got the biscuits and gravy, not my favorite gravy.  Food OK large coffee mugs.  They do have great scrambled eggs. Freezing by the windows.  Nice place

My first experience at Paul's Chicago Pizza was at a Yelp Event they hosted in 2013.  They were lovely hosts and provided a sampling of pizza, pasta, and a few of their other specialties like wings and it was a great chance to try a lot of things.  Fast forward to early 2014 and from somewhere in the depths of my brain, a memory arose that Paul's offers a buffet at lunch.  A quick car ride later and I was greeted by three or four pizzas, baked pasta with homemade meatballs on the side, garlic and cinnamon bread knots and a small salad bar.  JACKPOT!!  

The pizzas on offer are all thin crust, which I prefer.  Their sauce is a nice balance between tart and sweet with a rich tomato flavor and they replenish the pizzas often.  Even living over towards Citrus Park, we've been a number of times and taken family members.  Everyone has enjoyed it - no complaints.  The buffet is a little limited but when you are hungry for a few different kinds of pizza and perhaps a spot of salad or pasta - it's perfect!

I have never really had a good customer experience at Cracker Barrel. This was my visit to this location and I was there to order some take out. I approached the counter where there were 2 staff members. They did not greet me even though they looked at me, instead, they continue working on what they were doing. I decided to stand there and be understanding and patient because I wanted to give them a chance to "redeem" themselves. LOL. Literally a male customer lined up behind me and second later the male staff member looked at him and asked him how he could help him. Now this irritated me and made me wonder why he did that. I still remained patient, but then I got upset when the female staff member looked at me and kept doing what she was doing. After the guy was finished helping the man behind me, I stepped closer to the counter and asked him where I can order takeout. Wow, he mumbled something and started to walk away. I was beside myself thinking these people are having a bad day. I know that many businesses are struggling due to low staffing because of COVID and so I tried to take that into account. Eventually a different employee came up to me and helped me. I was able to place my order (only reason I didn't leave is because I was using a gift card. I wouldn't have used my own money at this place). So I decided to look around at all the fun stuff in their store while  I was on video chat with my daughter. They had some of the greatest gifts with such great prices. Let me back up, before I started looking through the store, there were other patrons shopping hassle free. But when I began to look around, immediately I noticed that the guy and the girl employee came with in the area I was shopping and pretended to "straighten" the shelves. I was like this is mighty strange but I understand that in retail it is important to have floor awareness. So I continued to navigate around the store and of course I was watching the employees in the corner of my eyes and literally they were following me. Not one time did they ask if they could help me find something. I was wondering if they were following me because I had a big purse or if they were racially profiling me. Yes, I hate to say it but I began to feel targeted because the other shoppers who weren't my race weren't being followed around the store. Even the manager did a walk through while I was shopping and I was like what the heck. Coming from a management background, I know how vital it is to engage with your customers and make them feel welcome as well as asking them how you can help them. Zero of that occurred. As a matter of fact one of the patrons that was leaving, loudly told the employees that this particular location needs a new manager because he is rude as heck.
Whether you are black, white, Hispanic, Asian etc, I would not recommend this place EVER. Why, because their service is way below the standards and I don't want anyone to experience being followed around the store while they shop. I gave them one star because the steak I ordered was cooked as I requested it and their fries were yummy.

The four of us want there for dinner, and we were seated with a party of six tired people that were not interested in the show and got our cook to lose intrest in the show.
it is expensive dinner and I felt that we didn't got the full Ichiban effect.
The food was good, but next time I will go I will make sure our party fill the table.

I'd give Emily an A+ for making me feel comfortable and knowing exactly what was going to happen. Very warm welcoming, and I always knew I'd feel comfortable there. I got laser hair removal for underarms and for the bikini area. For some reason I didn't notice a huge difference in my underarms, unfortunately. 
I'd give the laser a 1 out ot 5 just because it didn't seem like it was strong enough. I don't know how to tell what laser is more efficient or not, but this one definitely didn't suit my personal needs. I used a groupon, but I wouldn't come back for more treatments.
Great pizza, service, and food. Fun atmosphere and location in Irvington. I will definitely return.

I went to el limon for the second time last week. Both visits have been for lunch, and both times the food and the service was good. 
This past time I was asked to leave because I was already done eating, and they wanted to use my table for another person. I was in the middle of a conversation with a friend, and was forced to finish the conversation in my car, before we parted ways. 

Needless to say, I will not be in a hurry to come back for lunch service.

Apathetic customer service. 
Unwanted goofy music.
Don't ask them anything, or they high pressure try to sell you an upgraded plan.
What a great practice!  Doctor Savochka is very professional. The showroom is clean as well as the exam rooms.  State of the art equipment.  I will be going there for all my future visits.

Visiting Santa Barbara, this style of seafood is always fun to eat. Service was very sparse, it was hard to get more water in the waitresses attention. Other Cajun seafood places I've gone to let you combine the seafood in the bag which is much easier to share and you get a better variety. Drunken crab, all of your seafood comes in separate bags which makes sharing more tedious.

The seafood is good, but The crawfish may have been over cooked and I have better options in the bay area.
Good food, good service. Nice neighborhood place. The Eggs Benedict with lox were the perfect break in our day!

I just had an all day experience at the spa and it was much needed and overall a wondrous thing! The fact that I got to spend it with my best friend just made it that much better!

Checking in was a bit confusing because the stairwell I took that said Spa from the parking lot leads to the towel counter, not overall check-in. There wasn't alot of direction for someone coming in as a newbie and spending hundreds of dollars, which was a bit underwhelming for my initial impression. Then, there needs to be a map if they aren't going to help because it's like a cavern of hallways that take some getting used to, some things are South-like the dressing rooms, but then there's an East or West Sanctuary that is unclear on where you're supposed to be for which service you've signed up for. All of these things are what stopped me from giving a 5. The lack of treating the guests like...guests, I guess?? 

I started with a sea salt scrub at 8:30am. Yep, started my day with a vigorous scrub that felt wonderful and got my mind moving!! I've done this before and got to stay on the table at other spas with a magical electric shower that scans over you, but here you had to get up halfway through, shower and get back on the table for the oil. Not a deal breaker as long as you're careful because the table slides across the floor-an accident waiting to happen...

I had some time for hanging at the pool with my friend, then we did Water Zumba...her idea, not mine, but Wow! I was shocked at the energy of the instructor and how much of a workout dancing in water can be!! I loved it and wish I could do it everyday! That's a workout I could get accustomed to!

Now, massage time with Ian. Amazing! He gave tips, helped my aching shoulders and said it happens to people who are on the computer or knit alot... Is he psychic??? I will definitely be back for his massage help! 

Time for lunch and it's raining out so we head upstairs to the hotel restaurant in our robes...SUCH an indulgent feeling!! Lunch was amazing and the only snafu was that at checkin the girl told us we didn't need to carry our wallets around, that all charges would be together at the end of the day...nope. So, one of us ran downstairs to the lockers to get payment. No dishwashing for us!

More pool time with a side of drinks (needed that pesky credit card again) because the sun is out and the pool bar is open!! Yippee!! Careful tho, the tapper for corona light is hooked up to the wrong beer (I'm an avid corona drinker) so we got ours in a can...whew!

Almost the end of the day...sadness...and a pedicure by Natalia. She was one of the best I've had for being thorough and very kind! She made the end of our day perfect!

Time to get ready to meet the husbands! Nice shower heads and soap, shampoo & conditioner, but I was a little bummed that they didn't have hair dryers...such an easy addition for a full service spa. 

Yes, we had a few drawbacks, but overall I would return tomorrow and do it all over again! There are very few places like this in our area, so everyone should trek to Safety Harbor and check it out! Look into their daily specials, also! Great ones that aren't in the booklet!

Great staff, hotel was clean and safe zone. Parking spaces where good. The breakfast was ok and did the job. They could upgrade the TV in the room since it was small. The bathroom was nice and the shower was awesome since it had a lot of pressure and always have a lot of hot watee.water.  will recommend.

I been wanting crawfish all day, and saw this place C&A seafood and I was happy. I brought 2lbs of crawfish and got home and start eating good. I'm sure I ate about half of it, and something in my head told me to look down in my bag, and OMG!! I COULD HAVE DIED!!!!! a big a** bug in the bag. I instantly got sick!! Puking everywhere, now I feel like I need to get my stomach pump. :( never eat there, please I'm warning you all, never eat there. Check out the pic if you think I'm kidding...
Best Chinese restaurant in the Nashville area. I'm pretty sure the staff are also the owners and they are great. Very attentive, great suggestions, fast service, and most importantly great food!!!

Ordered The Greek salad with the Shawarma chicken along with the lentil soup and a side of pita bread on Uber eats. My order was a little messy when it arrived. The dressing was spilled all over the bag however not sure if that was from my delivery person or the restaurant. That being said the salad was really good and the lentil soup was also very fresh and tasty. I would give it another go

Ended up leaving after 30 minutes and decided to go out of my way to go elsewhere on my next day off.

I made an appointment for my chevy that I had bought the month before brand new out of state. The screen had frozen the day before so it was probably something electrical. Of course, it works just fine when I arrive at the dealership. I understood that it may take some time to figure out what might be wrong with it since they couldn't see the screen freeze with their own eyes but I had also booked an appointment and felt that waiting three hours for my car to even be seen was a bit ridiculous. At the very least they could give me courtesy car so I could go to work, right? Wrong. 

I bought a new car so that I wouldn't have to worry about getting the car serviced and, if I did, that I'd have a courtesy car available to me. But of course, Banner Chevrolet is out of courtesy vehicles (indefinitely for the foreseeable future) at least, for me as I did not buy MY Chevrolet from a Banner Chevrolet. They made it quite clear I was at the bottom of their priority list because of it. They even said to take it back to the dealership I bought it from... in Texas. 

And, as silly as it seems, the coffee they had in the "lounge" added insult to injury. How do you make coffee so weak yet so bitter? Every other dealership I've been to at least had a halfway decent coffee machine. Tastes like how this experience felt.

Spent Christmas Eve with the family there. It was my 1st time. The staff was very accommodating and friendly. BEN was an amazing server. It was the 1st Christmas without my mom, AUGUst made it a lil easier. That wasn't even the food. The PJ oysters were amazing, as was the turtle soup. They brought out lil tasteties like this lil egg custard (see pic) The fish entree was epic. The desserts-went to fast to get a pic-were Devine. It was good to be somewhere cozy and to be together.

Okay, so the delivery wasn't super speedy as it took an hour and 5 minutes. But all my food was hot still like I had just ordered. And it all tasted so good! The crab ragoons, fantastic!!!! how they should be made. I have been let down by 5 happiness, jungs and more by there ragoons, and I love crab ragoons! The steamed dumplings where also yum! but, they seem small and don't lok like any I have every had. The flavor though is really good. I will be coming back for my delivery American Chinese food needs.

I can't believe I am writing a review for car dealer! lol But this one was 5-star experience. Mo and Massoud did fantastic job in helping us purchase Tacoma. We have been shopping around for a while and did our research so we knew what we wanted and they were very easy to work with. Mo worked through the purchase and trade in deal with no BS, can't tell you how much I appreciated that! We had went to another dealer in Pasco and they were just awful and shady as h@ll. Glad we came to Clearwater dealership, love my new truck and looking forward to the continued service relationship. And Dan was efficient in working through the finance paperwork, very knowledgeable and answered all our questions! Great job guys all around! Fantastic team Mo!

This is my go to Chinese delivery when I am at work. The deliver always under 30 minutes and the food is always piping hot and fresh. It's your typical Chinese takeout food which makes you feel kind of gross after eating it...but that's to be expected. Yummy, good prices, fast delivery.

Lovely place. Nice sports bar type feel. Bartender Jennifer is awesome, friendly and attentive. Had the baked ziti , super delicious fresh tasting sauce and smothered in real mozzarella   and other cheeses. Had to order one to go!

After years of seeing their advertising for beer and wine, we decided to stop by and check them out. Deer lord, they are atrocious for beer and wine. We asked about wine, but were directed to their beer taps. Ok, fine we'll just have beer. The 1st beer I selected sputtered and had to choose a 2nd. That one didn't even pour anything out. The last one I selected was so foamy that I only got 1/2 a glass. The server apologized because this was her first time pouring beer. Oh and it was 2:45 and they close at 3:00 on a Friday! Never again.

I had a Kenwood touchscreen unit installed in my Audi by an Audio Express in Las Vegas about a year ago, and it randomly stopped working the other day. Today I brought it in to this location in Reno, and they took everything apart, found the problem, fixed everything, cleanly reinstalled it, and sent me on my way with no charge in only about an hour.

While I was waiting for my car, a sales associate was teaching me about different speaker systems that would work well for my car down the road. When I do go down that road, I'll definitely be doing it with this store specifically.

Very kind, knowledgeable, patient, and helpful staff.

This place was really neat. Very laid back vibe with a great concept. It was very dark and loud but there was a good amount of bar space and the tap list was awesome. They have a mystery keg that we didn't get to try but we loved that they had a mystery one. What a nice way to bring excitement to a bar. We didn't get food but we saw other people's, and it looked really good from afar!
Great food, ambience and service!  The pizza is amazing.  The perfect thin crust and just the right balance of toppings.  There  Caesar salad is delicious and the rest of the menu looks amazing!
They accommodated us right before closing. Food was delicious and service was awesome.

Not sure why it's listed as Master Bait, upon arriving the sign said "TBBT"/Blue Crab. That being said, a very friendly and courteous young man helped us out with the frozen bait we were looking for. The store is small & we drove a bit to get to it but it had exactly what we needed in terms of bait, frozen and a well stocked variety of live bait. The small shop was clean and it doesn't have very much tackle, more of a bait shop but that's we wanted.

I am so disappointed in what was my first and last visit to the "spa" at Ulta. The only reason I was there was because the BEST brow girl, Amanda, resigned from the Benefit Brow Bar in the store and they had no one working it. So, I took a brow appointment at the spa. Spa is such a bad word for the two, dimly lit, plain, and somewhat dirty rooms in the back of the store. It did not feel like a spa-they waxed my brows at a freaking hair washing sink with a plastic tub full of supplies they pulled out of a cabinet. Nothing was set up for waxing, it felt like I was getting a wax out of the trunk of a car. On top of that, hey charged two dollars more then the Benefit bar. The girl, Rebecca, was sweet. However, I told her exactly what I wanted and I mentioned multiple times that I thought she was taking too much off and what did she do? Take too much off. Not happy with the outcome at all. Won't be back and will go out of my way to not recommend this place to others. When I walked out, I passed the Benefit brow bar and shed a tear.

About three years ago, I went into Lee Read in Meridian to look at their tank.  I own a saltwater aquarium and many of friends in the boise area continued to tell me that I needed to go see their aquarium.  I went there one day at around 2 P.M. and when I walked in I was on the phone with a friend.  I went straight to the tank to look.  After about 5 minutes, the owner of Lee Read came out and introduced himself as the owner and asked me if I was going to buy something.  I said, "perhaps...but I really wanted to see your tank."  He got really upset with me and said, "Well this IS a jewelry store and the tank is here for our CUSTOMERS enjoyment."  He told me that it was extremely rude of me to go into the store and look at the tank and also said that i could not take any pictures of the tank.  He said he thought I was from a tank cleaning company and that i would the pictures to show people that I clean his tank, and he made it very clear that he maintains his own tank.  CONGRATULATIONS!  I decided to leave and he walked me out of his store and I go into my car.  I drove away in my brand new BMW M3 Convertible and looked at his face as I drove by...I could tell he felt bad for being so rude to me.  Every time I have driven past the store I look up at the sign that invite people to go see the aquarium.  But I'm warning you, they don't want ppl to go in without buying something.  So if you want to see the aquarium, make sure you bring your wallet.  The Owner is a Jerk, and I have told this story to countless ppl and will forever do so.

For a museum grill, the food is impressive.  Large portions, great taste, quick delivery of food.  Ice cream is very good as well.  Prices are quite reasonable as well.

Stop here and cool off after exploring outside

A little shop next to Geno's Steaks and it's worth a trip! Ordered 2 mini cannolis (one that was dipped half milk chocolate and half white chocolate, and the second chocolate dipped in nuts) both were delicious! And we also ordered 2 little pastries filled with cannoli cream and it was good but I don't think it was the same exact cream as the open cannoli. Prices aren't that bad. And they also have gelato and an area to make hot chocolate with a variety of different chocolates. So if you're in the area, no need to hesitate on walking it!

This event used to be HUGE pre-Katrina! Now....it has died down ALOT. After last year's teenager problem, my thoughts about Celebration in the Oaks have hindered quite a bit. I love all of the lights especially around the Pavillion of Two Sisters Courtyard. The lights are beautiful.The trains are fabulous to bring back. The different school's Christmas Trees in the very front is a tradition. I was very disappointed with the train. Half of the park was pitch black. I overheard a 6 year old last night saying..."Mommy where are all the cool lights?" That is a shame that this event used to be a big money maker back in the day and is now hard for people to come to meaning there are barely any lights.

I used to love this restaurant however after tonight I'm never setting foot inside this place again. I placed an order for pick up over the phone at about 6:20 they said it would be 15-20 mins. We got there about 6:40 and they said it would only be a couple minutes. 

We waited there for 30 min while many other people came and went with picked up orders. Many of the people who came in after we did were seated and were served their food in the time I was there. 

Frustrated I just asked for my money back which was a hassle in itself. The lady stated she didn't know if she could and was very reluctant to give me my own money back.

I have never received such horrible service and will not be going back

This place was terrible! Not that I expect a ton or great service from a fast food place but this was bad. Rude employees, cold food, limp fries, etc. And let's not forget they don't have a kids menu so they recommend some adult garnished burger that was giant for a child!

Love this place!!! I eat here once or twice a week. Staff is always nice and service good. They are  always fast and food is A+. The chili is unique and some may not like the style of a sweeter chili. Me personally I think it's great on everything they have. The CHEESE!!! They pile on it also is like no other shredded cheese it's fresh shredded in house.

Walked in and it took a minute to even be greeted. When I told them I didn't have an appointment she looked pretty annoyed. Any other dress shop I've been in doesn't mind no appointments so I figured it was the same way. Nope. She told me to go ahead and look around and I found nothing in my price range or styles I liked. I decided to leave and as I passed TWO girls at the counter neither of them bothered to look up. I walked out and never looked back. I ended up finding my gorgeous, backless and trumpet style dress at Marchele's.

Some of the best Indian I have ever eaten. Chicken tikka masala is my fav Indian dish and they do it so well. It's byob so that's a another plus. The servers are very good. I usually don't have to wait long to be seated. This place is a must try.

Ambience was amazing. The interior of this restaurant is so inviting. My friend and I opted to sit where the live jazz music was - it happened to be open mic night.  Very amazing talent with both singers and players of various instruments all age ranges. 

They had a great deal - 3 courses for $25 where you pick one of three starters, brisket entree with potato salad and cole slaw, and choice of two desserts. My friend ordered this and I got the stuffed red fish. The brisket was incredibly tender, the perfect amount of fatty. It maybe had too much sauce. The sauce was sweet but truly complimented the brisket and especially was delicious with the potato salad. Being a true southerner from NC - this was definitely southern comfort food. The red fish was just ok. It didn't really taste like much and a stuffed fish you expect to be tender and full of rich creamy stuffed flavor. It just wasn't. The side was also just "meh". We did have the deviled eggs to start and I would eat just those as my meal. Also tried the gumbo of the day - turkey and andouille sausage. For dessert we chose the pineapple bread pudding. It was divine. I also ordered the cucumber jalapeño Margarita. This was SPICY. I mean like very spicy. Smoke sat in the back of your throat spicy. I would have liked a bit more sweet to compensate and offset. I ordered it because of the jalapeño I just didn't think it would be all spice.
Food was decent, but $11+ for Frito Pie? It's 1/2 bag of Fritos and chili....waaaayyyy too expensive.

Had a great time here last night. Awesome food, great beer selection, and nice outdoor seating. Between three of us we tried a lot of items from the menu- highlights were the short rib egg rolls (with an absolutely delicious Gorgonzola sauce that we loved so much we saved to dip fries in), the New Yorker burger (cooked perfectly medium with fontina cheese), and the Parmesan truffle fries. Really, everything was good but I'm still thinking about those... The beer sampler was perfect, I wish more bars did this so you can try new beers you've never had before. We will definitely be back!

We've been boating to this restaurant for at least 6 years.
We always loved the peel and eat shrimp, the conch fitters were to die for and probably the best margarita in the Tampa st Pete area.  Apparently the new owners felt they needed to fix what wasn't broken.
The food is bland (fancy names though) and the margaritas suck!  It might explain why the place is empty on a gorgeous Sunday afternoon.

With all the great reviews, I had to try this place out and so I did.  However, I am going to say this right away, I was very disappointed.  First of all, the service was a little less than average at best.  The waitress who was apparently also the bartender was very slow with the drink orders and seemed constantly in a hurry for the most part (which was understandable if she really was bar-tending at the same time).  I can take busy for an excuse to a certain extent.  

When the orders were placed, the person in the kitchen completely overlooked my order and I ended up sitting on the table with nothing but my friend didn't want to eat the crab cakes in front of me.  So, the waitress took the food back and zapped it a little I suppose until mine was ready.  The crab cakes were pretty tiny, we started calling it baby-sized crab cakes but was told by my friend that it tasted fine.  I ordered the baby New Zealand lamb chops and they weren't joking when they said "baby" because they really were infant-sized (perhaps one big bite or two if you cut it) and additionally, they had forgotten all about my Dolmas that were supposed to come with it and instead replaced it with a very small portion of sauteed vegetables on the corner of the plate without asking or informing me.  It tasted okay and since it took a while to get my order to begin with, we didn't bother telling them that it was messed up again.  We ended up ordering a 13" pizza because we were still hungry and got more sangria.  The sangria were very refreshing though but again, we had to wait for a while to get another round.

Not sure if I'll come back.  I have a friend who seems to really like this place so I might eventually end up here but I would prefer not to.
Awesome. Great environment, and wonderful indoor activity for kids in the winter. The only issue is the wait list integration with Yelp.

My favorite cupcake spot! I've literally tried everything here and I have no complaints. The sweet burger is amazing and I love how they have vegan and gluten free food. This place is amazing they also sell art as well great laid back atmosphere.

We ordered Liang pi, biang biang noodle (don't know why it calls this name because the Chinese name is nothing close to this): pretty authentic place for their noodles. 

Crispy rice: I feel the sauce is a bit too americanized. Feels like those you got in any American Chinese restaurant. But the crispy rice is good. 

ma po tofu: don't order this dish. It's Sichuan dish and they completely screwed it up the "Ma". 

Small place, pretty local that no one speaks English.

We felt the food here was just okay.  The servers were very friendly, but the service wasn't great.  With very few people in the restaurant, they forgot to take our order and bring us items that we asked for without prompting.  This place has no AC, or if they do, it was broken.  So don't go on a very hot day like we did!

I'll give this place another go, because I really had high hopes for it. Unfortunately the eggplant cannelloni was uber disappointing. The service and ambience was fabulous and everyone else did enjoy their dish including the salmon and scallops. So def try those, but not the eggplant dish.

In any city, there exist places where everyone has probably been one time or another.  It might be a park, restaurant, bar, or theater.  Despite the place, there probably isn't anything outwardly special about it - other than the fact that it's the only one.  Under the surface, however, the place might hold a little place in the history of that city; its ceasing to exist would create a true loss of cherished local flavor.  If you were born and bred in (or are at least a longtime resident of) Indy, Moe & Johnny's is one of those places.

As a teenager, I was a regular at Cornerstone Coffee.  I always thought that once I turned 21, Moe & Johnny's would be one of my regular "adult" hangouts.  It appeared to be unique, cool, and a place where my friends' older siblings went.  When my time came to join the ranks of its customers, I was pretty impressed.  I thought they had tasty food and cheap pitchers of beer.  It was fun; I was one of the "older guys."  As time progressed, I discovered new places that had more interesting food and beverage offerings.  I was a little perturbed at the way that they ruined Cornerstone Coffee when they acquired and renovated it.  While I continued to enjoy Cornerstone's new concept (which I have detailed in another review), I couldn't help but harbor some resentment for their drastically altering my favorite coffee shop of all time.  In short, I stopped patronizing Moe and Johnny's, albeit not purposefully.

When it was recently suggested that I eat dinner at Moe & Johnny's, I neither protested nor became overly excited.  I knew that it would be an above-mediocre meal.  The food at Moe & Johnny's is just fine.  A mediocre meal can be found at Applebees; a meal here is much better than that.  Over the years, I've eaten burgers, chicken sandwiches, and various salads.  The last item I ordered was the Cajun Chicken Sandwich with a side of French fries.  It was just fine, and I had no complaints.  Had I not written this review, I doubt that I would remember it by this time next year.  I will remember the friendly, neighborhood bar-like service and atmosphere.  

If this were 2003, I would tell you that Moe & Johnny's had a great beer selection, and I was interested in watching a ball game with a cheap pitcher of beer.  More than a decade has passed, craft beers are the rage, and European beers are my preference.  Moe & Johnny's beer menu is boring, and they could afford to spruce up their selection.

Broad Ripple has taken a dive, and I fear that its adjoining neighborhoods to the south will suffer a similar fate.  Moe & Johnny's is the most average place where I will certainly return.  Meridian-Kessler would be a lesser place without it.
Great place. Quiet, beautiful views. Good food, wonderful service. And I got 25% off for checking in on Yelp! Will definitely be back!

The view of the harbor with mountains in the background was so beautiful and relaxing. The food was excellent. We ordered the Oysters Rockefeller as a starter and they were great. My wife ordered Halibut and I ordered the Sea Bass (with a Cajun seasoning). May I suggestion go for the Sea Bass and dip the Sea Bass in that butter sauce and enjoy. OMG really really good.

Management was very unresponsive when we asked about gluten free food


Having a gluten sensitivities means the restaurant should really understand the food they are serving

The waitstaff and particularly the manager did not take any responsibility and a simple I'm "sorry but served you the wrong food "would've gone a long way management made it the customers fault for being gluten-free

Take responsibility for your screw ups

This is a humble family business that practices kindness and great service. They remember your orders and your name making you a regular from day one. Their prices are reasonable and the taste is heavenly. Service turnaround is quick and satisfactory. Love to order from them anytime we have celebrations and special events too.

This was our first time here and we were very impressed by the service. Maddi served us & she went above and beyond.  We ordered two Earl of chicken sandwiches and a ceaser salad. We are very pleased with our purchases & will be back soon. Hands down a must visit place.

Came after a trip to aquarium for some lunch...excellent spicy fries,  and victor nachos. I had the victor cheesteak with the long hot. Subbed chicken for regular steak, awesome! Wish the beer selection was a little better, but the waitress was good, food was solid and cheap. Been here a fee times before but first time I ate here,  would def come back for a bite.

Great find ! Family owned Turkish restaurant tucked away at the end of a street in New Hope. 
Everything we ordered was delicious and authentic!

Tim and Sharon Osborne are the best and most professional people that our company has been privileged to have as our a companies long term professional transport drivers. They are professional and friendly with an impeccable timely transport!
