It seriously dawned on me a few days ago that this place was really just filling up our waters half way because its called half and half.  Neat.

I had the liver and eggs and potatoes or something like that, it was pretty good, if you can stomach liver.  The doughnuts we had before the meal were nice, light and dissipate in your mouth, lovely!!

Service was spotty but it was busy, and I can see why.  I can only imagine how mobbed this place was when Josh Galliano did his mobile resturant thing.  Still so pissed I missed that.

Thoroughly enjoyed the fried chicken. The greens were excellent. Great service. We ordered too much good and fortunately they had run out if a couple things during the day service because we had more than enough food with just the entrees.

My husband and I ordered from her last night, and the food was very good. We were grateful they did decide to stay open because so many restaurants have closed. We were also happy to be able to support a local business.

However, when I picked up our order the staff was not wearing any protective gear such as gloves or masks. And there was no suggestion or requirement that customers should to enter with caution by wearing protective gear themselves.

As a healthcare worker I would caution patrons as what I witnessed last night is a potential major hazard for spreading COVID-19. 

Please be smart and safe by wearing gloves, masks, and keeping distance. When ordering take-out be aware of the establishments that are following CDC recommendations as you will be less exposed, and therefore keep our community safe. 

At this time I would not put Toni Roni's in Havertown on my list of safer establishments.
This is one of the best restaurants in Carmel. They serve genuine Jamaican food for a great price in a casual atmosphere. Love the location right on the Monon Trail.

The most trustworthy and caring auto repair shop around. My husband and I were in a financial rut with lots of vehicle issues, Kiwi's does free inspections and were able to quote us as soon as we brought our car to the shop. After explaining our situation the owner wanted to help us get out car running no matter what it took and by noon same day our car was up and running at half the cost anyone else could have done!! They genuinely care about the greater good and safety of their customers. We hung out in shop while our car was being worked on and got to know the owner and his employees who all had a good sense of humor with huge hearts for others. I never though we'd have such a great day at a repair shop. As long as we are in Santa Barbara there will be no where else I'd take my families car.

We were excited to find this place and attended one of the concert nights. The entertainment was fun and in addition to the musicians they had belly dancers, which really got the crowd up and dancing. The grounds were nice, but the staff was an *unbelievable* disappointment. For a Friday night they close at 9 p.m., however no one told us this when we bought tickets or when they sold our table 4 bottles of wine at 8:30. 

Also at 9:15 staff will proceed to start breaking down your table and pushing other tables at you and your guests, making purposefully loud noises and exclaiming to each other loudly and passive aggressively "we just want to go home" as you try and say goodbye to the people you invited to this sorry place. 

We had a party of about 15, all had never been here before and let's just say this was not a good first impression. The "service" was so bad it even became a joke. I was embarrassed because everyone from our party made it clear they would never come back and I was very sorry I had chosen this place. 

Four of us that didn't head home were in the parking lot deciding where to go next and two bouncers came over and even more rudely (as if that was possible at this point) told us we had to leave immediately as "they wanted to go home." I want to remind you this is 9:30 p.m. on a Friday evening! It was clear nobody on staff cared one iota about giving visitors a good time or any customer service at all. I haven't been treated this rudely in a very long time. Needless to say, we will never go back to this place and we do not recommend it to anyone that wants to have a good time. On top of it all most of the wines we tried were very very sweet although a few were okay at best.

Most of the employees were quite young, I'm pretty confident the owner does not know about his employee's disregard for customers, as these people clearly cared more about the rest of their Friday night than their jobs or making repeat customers.

One of my guests called me this morning to say "he had never felt that unwelcome at a place." Never again.
Went back twice recently and was impressed. Staff were attentive and food was great ! Still quite pricey but I have no problem paying when it's good like this !

My personal mass ave favorite at the moment, the classic gyro is my go-to with rice or fries. The rice is full of flavor and pairs perfectly with the gyro sandwich for a full Mediterranean vibe but if you're going for Mediterranean-American mood, 100% go for the fries. The fries were crisp, fresh and perfectly seasoned. I cannot recommend this restaurant enough. My search for the "best gyro in the city" has been found so far.

Fun place to be during CMA Fest. Went for drinks and to hang out on the rooftop bar. It was very nice. Loved that they have self serve water. I heard there was a bathroom attendant which is cool. We played cornhole up on the roof. Wish we tried some food.

As a storyteller, I find warped words and twisted tales from heckling haters often lead to muddied misunderstandings. I may not know the true origin story of Café Capello, but I want to tell my part of its many chronicles with complete honesty. 

Everyone thriving in the modern age of the interweb is adept to navigate the world via posts, tweets, and reviews, seeking out conversations and searching for stories. So naturally, my first steps into Café Capello was not through the wood-framed glass door, singly-stickered with its humble name, but rather through social media where the small business is already decorated with descriptions and details. Coming across the name "Café Capello" was both a familiar and an estranged feeling; the letters imprint in my mind a typical place that sells hot caffeinated beverages, yet the syllables stir up memories of an old and distant friend.

Most of us are well-versed with our thumbs swiping though an electronic universe, yet our mouth melts, lips linger, tongue tumbles, and throat tickles at any attempt of a real, physical interaction. During a recent trek home, I made the decision to visit the establishment again, not through tempered-glass protected screen of my mobile device, but this time, by being there physically to quench my caffeine thirst and to satisfy my curiosity. As I stood inside Café Capello awkwardly acknowledging Mr. Capello at the register, he held no reservation in welcoming me with warmth and a hint pleasant surprise. Immediately, words trickled, sentences rained, and stories poured as faded memories are filled with new colors.

After the short-lived exchange, my father and I situated ourselves by the large window pane brightly lit with the rays of the winter sun, and I continued to relive the Capello tales over our hot drinks, both caffeinated to the rim and handled by Mr. Capello himself. I reminisced about when Café Capello was daily boisterous bus rides to school; when "where do you source your coffee beans from?" was "where did you get those answers for the AP Calculus homework"; and when break time was once a coveted thing, always shorted by higher education, but it is now personal rewards we gift to ourselves. These stories echo through the spacious room, reverberating from the rustic red-bricked wall and weathered wood floor, harmonizing with the occasional kitchen tinkers and register rings. Only then did I realize that these walls belonged to a former business, and I was glad that it now belongs to someone whose hard-work made it a home.

Taking the final sip with reluctance, I made for the exit still wanting another well-crafted cup of coffee and another chance to hang out, but just like this lore is bleeding beyond its character limit, my bladder was full and heart was overflowing. "There will be more opportunities," I mused, brimming with hope, and embraced the chills on West 1st Street.

I wish Café Capello a lasting saga and its owner a lifetime of success. It was great: the bitter beans in a fresh pour-over and the sweet scent of friendship.

For what I paid it was good value, the meat is comparable to what you buy in the grocery store. My card did not go thru the first time so it was ran twice accidentally the owner corrected this immediately once discovered. 
I will probably buy again in the future.
Delicious food and coffee. Adorable and modern atmosphere. We ate at the bar and had a lovely time. Everything has been carefully thought out and designed

Extremely not pleased with this salon. Went there for a routine eyebrow wax and left not happy. The lady who did my brows managed to take off too much and ruined the shape and made it short and thin after telling her to keep the shape and leave it thick. On top of that she was very pushy and kept offering me other services that I declined several times. After having my brows ruined, I will never go back there nor would I recommend anyone to go there for a wax! If you're looking for a place to have your brows done, this is not the one!

Didn't even get to eat here because the girl on the phone put me on phone for 15 minutes called back she hung up. Finally got to place my order and she wouldn't let me pay with a credit card. My girlfriend was going to pick up the food because I was sick, she started arguing with me saying I couldn't pay till I picked it up, and then I had to replace my order because she had rang it in under my name but said she couldn't give my girlfriend the food because her name wouldn't be mike gallagher.... worst and weirdest service ever canceled my order never going here. Regardless of how good the food might be wont change my mind about customer service.
The media has been suspiciously silent about the fact that Dunkin' Donuts has only had the large straws this summer.

The food was not good and they charged $2.50 just to add sour cream and cheese and the baked potato. The burger was bland and the restaurant is dimmed in the inside. The monsoon was yummy but if you're in the mood for a good burger I would recommend 5 Guys instead.

First restaurant I dine in since the pandemic began. The staff was really nice especially our waiter wish I remembered his name he was bilingual lol if that's any help. We wore our masks to order to show consideration for the staff as well. The lobster was super good and my family was happy with everything they ordered too. If you're in the area should definitely try this spot :)

Love the Italian! Stopped in for lunch today and John was behind the cash register, super pleasant and efficient without being pushy to get me through the line. You guys are doing a great job, I'll definitely be back soon!

I usually have a great time here and have been coming for over 20 years. However, last night the staff was all incredibly rude. The piano player scoffed at every request and when he decided he could submit himself to such "lowly" songs for 4 minutes the entire bar was singing. Next, the first bartender I had (with an order of 3 drinks) would make half a drink, walk to his friends in the corner and chat for 5 minutes, and then come back to making a simple gin and tonic. Lastly, the second bartender I had was condescending about my beer order. It was as if they didn't want a single tip. I will definitely come back here because I love the general atmosphere and have never had an issue but the specific staff I encountered needs to get rid of the high and mighty attitude.

Used to come here all the time. Went there for convenience tonight the place was out of almost everything. All kinds of things left at registers from ppl changing their minds. The floors were filthy.  Just going downhill

A very fine restaurant with a unique simple elegant ambience and very proper service. I was somehow not so happy with the food and felt that most of the items that we ordered was high on salt. I went there on my birthday and we were 4 people. So, we did order a lot of food and atleast 3 items were high on salt. I loved the mushroom entree though.!

Authentic Italian food and a great atmosphere. Staff is always on point. my wife and I drive a good distance to come here when we are in the mood for Italian. price and portions are more than generous. i reccomend it for all families

THEY DELIVER with no fee! +1 star

The green salad with gorgonzola cheese and almonds was the BEST side salad with a delivery meal I've EVER HAD!!  Ask for the extra of the dressing!  +1 star

BBQ Shrimp Ravioli was very creative, and executed perfectly.  The flavor was pefect.  I have also tried the mussels figaro-one of the best mussel dishes I've ever had.  +1 star

Tiramisu was among the top 3 I've ever had.  +1 star

Delivery was fast and friendly, and their food is packaged to travel well (again, with no fee) +1 star

New Orleans needs more places like this.

I bought a used Ford Escape from this location and I am very happy with the vehicle. The staff member who helped us look at the cars was very conscientious of our budget and didn't show us anything above the budget we had which was nice. I didn't feel like I was being upsold on features I didn't want. I believe we were told they work on commission, but based on number of sales rather than total cost of sale which makes it a nice no pressure environment.


I would give this place 5 stars, but they try so hard to sell you their in house warranty which personally is a big turn off for me. It isn't that good of a deal for the warranty and I had to say more than 5 times that I wasn't interested in the warranty. But this is an issue with CarMax itself, not this particular location. Just be wary you will have to stand your ground for a very long time in order to not have the warranty program forced upon you.


Perhaps the nicest part about the whole process is they take care of ordering the plates for your vehicles for you and you go directly to CarMax to pick them up. Anyone who has been to the New Jersey MVC knows that any way to avoid that place is a blessing.


Overall I really enjoyed the purchasing experience from CarMax, but they need to lay off pushing the warranty program.

HAPPY MOM'S DAY to all.Just in from lunch at Cafe Fresca,everything on the menu looks good but my fav is the soup/sandwich combo.Got the chicken salad and spinach-artichoke soup,always a winner.Nine bucks,can't beat it.
Like that herbal tea,too.Service is great,fast and friendly!

Great atmosphere, great food. Only thing I didn't like is that while I was on a date they charged me full price for the sangrias even though it was happy hour. Didn't want to say anything because I was with a date but usually I would of.

Being frequent customers of Chabaa Thai, my friends and I decided to give the owner's new restaurant, Mango Moon, a shot.  Mango Moon definitely has a completely different feel.  

Along with Mango Moon's full bar, comes a very modern, trendy, and nightlife oriented feel.  The inside atmosphere is great if you like Thai chic, but the mood music seems out of place.

As for the food, it was varied and very tasty.  The menu features more "small plates" than "medium plates," so it's a great place to grab a few appetizers and make them into a full meal.  I had the Thai jerky with jasmine rice and the grilled pork skewers - both were something a little different and came with dipping sauces (as do most of the plates).  Complementary nuts and herbal tea come while you wait and the staff is very gracious and friendly.  Ms. Moon herself came around while we ate to make sure everything was fine.

Chabaa is more my gear, but Mango Moon will suit others and is definitely a great place to spend an evening
Not the best experience while trying coffee. Might come back again to get another impression of coffee and breakfast.

Very quick and Friendly service for Xrays. Amy was knowledgeable and didn't rush me (Icould not walk) for my foot pics. She also asked me if Iwanted my CD without me asking and had it ready in under 10 mins. Front desk was efficient and a clean nice waiting area. ALAYNA has great customer service skills.

Fantastic food and service!!!  Great atmosphere, great menu selection and friendly staff. Everyone in our group had a great time!  One of my best dinning experiences!  And definitely kid friendly!  Our servers have been very accommodating to my son :).

This cannot be real life.  I came here almost a year ago with my wife, and fellow yelper, and everyone was run out of the Casino under the guise of a threat.  She swore she would never return, since the employees were wiping down the machines as we were shuttled out.  I convinced my wife to come here tonight for her birthday, and it's closed from a power failure 5 hours earlier.  What?????  Closed after a cards game ends?  How can a casino be profitable doing business like that???  Sooooo disappointed.   Never again.  Hollywood or River City from here on out.

After an unsuccessful & unpleasant visit to a different vet (see previous not so great review), I decided to give Mobley a try, mostly because it's close to my house and they had an appointment available the same day. Good thing because I was more than pleased with the service and care I got from Dr. Brannon and the Mobley staff. They were all friendly and warm and made me feel like they truly cared about my pooch and her upset tummy. Dr. Brannon asked a lot of questions and didn't jump to a diagnosis (again, reference previous review for contrast). He offered a couple potential remedies and asked me to bring my girl back if the problem persisted. Fortunately, Dr. Brannon's recommendations did the trick and I have a very happy, digestively sound pup again! I also got a nice card in mail from the office a few days later, thanking me for allowing them to care for my girl and asking me to keep them updated. I will certainly be bringing my pup back should she need some TLC in the future.

What a night with an incredible cast and crew. From the producer to the team managing tickets. Enjoyed ourselves tremendously and would recommend the show to anyone - wether your popping your burlesque cherry or have seen a few! The MC, GoGo is perfect for this crew. This is not for the easily offended and get ready for outrageous funny nasty dirty entertainment! Perfect for Nee Orleans visit!

To be fair, we did go on opening night. That being said, after the nearly one hour wait between ordering and waiting for food, it was horrible. The chicken breast was half the size of the bun. That would have been ok if it weren't burnt and dry as a bone. The pimento cheese and sauce wasn't enough to make the sandwich edible. I hope the get it together. It's a great concept. But I doubt I'll be back with the current reviews.

Their pizza = greatness. I don't like pizza with a lot of marinara because usually that ish ain't good. So less is better. This place does it perfect (for me) with the cheese to marinara ratio. Anything else though is a no go.

Absolutely delicious!!!!

We visited this restaurant after a special Christmas concert with my son's band.  The hostess addressed my son (who was in a tux), and told him she knew it was a special night for him and guessed band.  He was impressed as he's also worn a tux for his choral concerts. 

We all shared the Ben Dip.  Holy crap, that was soooo good! I was being made fun of because I couldn't stop eating it.   It honestly reminded me of the crab dip I've had along the North Carolina coastline. 

I had a filet, my son had chicken fettuccine alfredo (American style), and my friend had veal.   We were all super impressed.   My friend said it was the best veal he's had in ages. 

I was not overly impressed wth the house salad.   I loved the dressing (and want to buy some), but I'm not overly enamored with greens other than spinach/kale, so it seemed like a bunch of weeds/sprouts to me.   I'm positive it's my lack of culinary adventures/experience with greens, but I would've preferred something a bit simpler.   Just my taste. 

We shared tiramisu and molten lava cake for dessert.   Both were phenomenal. 

If you're from the Midwest or up North, this is the kind of Italian place you're used to.   Close tables, lively conversation, dark wood, Christmas lights, tons of pictures.... you get it.  If you've been to The Hill in St Louis or anywhere that is remotely Italian oriented...this is that place. 

I will most definitely be back!!!

I've only been for brunch a handful of times, but it never disappoints. The upstairs patio is gorgeous, great view & the fans keep you somewhat cool in the FL heat. Brunch choices are limited, but excellent, I've had both the chicken & waffles and the crab cakes Benedict, and both were great!

As a career nurse, I no longer affiliate with hospitals as a clinician. Doesn't mean I want my CPR to expire.

Enter CPR Nashville. They rock! Best class I ever took for BLS.

Go see them.

This place is such a lifesaver ! It's a great variety of King Cake items from various bakeries and restaurants. This location did require social distancing and limited entry. I went for the Brennan's Strawberry Cheesecake Kong Cake! Oh it was delicious and worth every penny and the short time in line !

Nice comfy bed. Clean room. Cool TV. HD channels. However, this place is situated about two miles from downtown. Just far enough where it'd be a hassle to walk. There was pee in the toilet when we arrived. Walls are paper thin. Could hear the couple getting busy next door. Luckily it didn't last long:) Finally, the breakfast was terrible. Everything was stale.
Fantastic food and service! My wife and o split the fajita and it was fantastic! We come after we had moved here and were told to come here. Each night they have fantastic specials!

I love Vietnamese food. It's more than just Pho and spring rolls!!! Having traveled there a few years ago, I was struck by the diversity of cuisine which is influenced by the countries location on the coast and squeezed to it's north by China. Seafood, fish sauce, dumplings, all things I love. Gabriella's takes me back to those days while also adding a bit of flare and updating of styles! BYO restaurant is happy to supply you with glasses and bottle openers, as well as recommendations and a whole lot of hospitality.

Our server recommended a few small dishes and large dishes for a group of 3 hungry men, and after some deliberation...we ordered EVERYTHING she suggested...and wow....am I glad we did. EVERYTHING was a winner! We started out with the Hue Tamal, thin Vietnamese style rice masa imbedded with seafood and steamed in a banana leaf, they were so delicate and fresh with the salty pungent fish sauce and hot peppers. Fantastic. We then had the Water Fern Dumplings which come in tiny saucers stacked on top of each other to resemble a lotus flower. These open-face dumplings resemble little tacos and again displayed a playfulness, bright and fresh confluence of flavors that is typical of Vietnam. We were really working our chop stick skills to dowse then fold and eat these tiny treasures. The three main dishes came out and continued to blow our minds. The Emperor's Fried Rice was bursting with veggies and giant prawns and spices. Succulent and not overly salted, this fried rice ranks in my top 3 EVER! It worked perfectly as a base for the Shaken Beef that was so tender it barely held together inside an unctuous brown sauce! This was a home run!! The piece du resistance was the whole Branzino with rice paper wraps and shredded greens. I love tactile dishes, and anything hands-on is a great experience to share with friends, especially when every roll looks like a disaster and we can laugh with each other. The fish was perfectly poached, delicate and juicy making each roll an elevated version of what you get from your local delivery. 

Don't walk, RUN here! Eat EVERYTHING! What a fantastic Vietnamese restaurant. What a neighborhood treasure!

Happened upon this truck while working downtown earlier this week - it was parked on Market at 8th street (I think.)

Ordered a regular gyro with no onions, and a bag of chips.  The gyro was tasty - carved off the spit with the right amount of tomatoes, parsley, tzatziki(sp?) sauce, and feta to bring out the flavor of the meat.  The chips were...well, chips.  

The sandwich was manageable, but was served in a box.  I found this a bit odd for a food truck. Kinda thought the idea was to walk with your food, but whatever.  I ate most of the sandwich, which means that most of the guys that I work with might have thought it was small.

It was also $10 for a sandwich and bag of chips.  This seems really pricey to me for a place that has very little overhead.  

Service was friendly and pretty fast.  

Warning - the following anecdote is unrelated to the review:

While I was standing there waiting for my gyros, the traffic light on the corner next to the truck just fell out of the sky!  No one hit it, there was no wind, etc it just randomly gave out.  There was a lady crossing the street about three feet from it who needs to either go play the lottery or take it as a warning and change her evil ways.  A little reminder to live each day like it could be your last...

We had our daughter's wedding dinner at Stella Mares last Friday night, and words just can't express what a wonderful time we had there.  Our long table, set elegantly with flowers and white tablecloth, was centered in a long, intimate, private dining room with tall windows and large pocket doors.  Our guests really enjoyed ordering speciality drinks from the bar and then ordering whatever they liked from Stella Mare's very impressive menu--even though we were a fairly large group.  After placing our dinner orders, we closed the double doors for a short program about the bride and groom, but as soon as we were finished, the doors flew open, and a group of waiters brought our in dinners all at once.  I don't know who the chef is at Stella Mares, but the food was ABSOLUTELY INCREDIBLE!  Every meal was perfectly prepared and beautifully presented.  After dinner, they presented the wedding cake for the bride and groom to cut the first piece before they plated it for the guests, and we finished off the evening with champagne toasts and wedding gifts.  Our waiter, Nelson, handled everything, including our special requests, expertly, flawlessly, and unobtrusively.  We live 1,500 miles from Santa Barbara, so when we made the reservations for a wedding dinner at Stella Mares, we didn't really know what to expect.  I can honestly say that the setting, the food, and the service exceeded all our expectations, and we are so glad to have chosen Stella Mares!

Well designed, efficient, easy to access airport. Part of that, of course, is the relatively small population of Indianapolis. If this airport were in Chicago it would be a completely different story.

Yelp says: "You've checked in here 15 times! What keeps you coming back?" Maybe because I have to fly out sometimes?

These guys are the greatest! This is not a PT mill.  At City Park Physical Therapy you will have an individualized and hands on treatment approach with a licensed physical therapist.  Randy, Adrienne are both immensely knowledgeable professionals who genuinely care about their patients.  I have no hesitation recommending City Park Physical Therapy to anyone in need of competent and innovative physical therapy in the New Orleans area.

This unassuming side-alley Mexican joint is definitely what I was looking for. An array of apps, the street corn of which was my favorite. Yummy tacos, though I would have loved to have a corn tortilla instead of flour. Especially since the fish came with a gluten free breading, which is nice except that was rendered useless by the flour tortilla. 

I skipped on cocktails because, though their descriptions sounded delicious, they were all priced $13 or more and I was in the mood for a flavorful meal on a budget. 

I loved the vibe of the place. Beautiful artwork and artifacts with fun music playing. The reason for only getting 4 stars is that the food was good but rather basic and the service wasn't the best. We had to request napkins and silverware 3 times before getting it. And our waiter did not seem super happy to be working there. All in all, I would definitely go back for the tacos again.
Called for a quote and said he would call me back to let me know if he could do the services the next day and didn't call back. A little frustrating when you're trying to get work done on your home.
Great wontons, I also like the roast pork and duck rice platter and shrimp with lobster sauce.

Craving for burger and this place hit that spot.

We got wings as starters. It was well marinated and flavored that I didn't need any sauce for it.

Main courses were fried chicken burger and fast joe. Former is very crispy and tasty. Latter was also very juicy and tender. Can't go wrong on both and each are very filling.

Cool place to have a bite and the have outdoor seating. Ample parking at the back. 

No papertowel in the restroom. Must be a Fountain Square thing.

Food is always very delicious, staff not the friendliest but its an order at the counter type place so you don't need to deal with them much. Food is best around though and always fresh. They have an extensive coffee and smoothie menu as well : ) This is my favorite place to grab lunch!

Cocktails with seasonal, local ingredients and homemade cola are so good. The 4500 is my favorite drink. The fried chicken is awesome, but it is a special. The mac and cheese is probably my favorite and I never want to share it with anyone. With that said, this is where I take the out-of-towners (I just make them order their own mac). High Hat's burger with manchego and fried egg = superb. Almost forgot, the BBQ shrimp are SO delicious. Everything's pretty rich but wonderful.

We have been here a couple times now and we love it! The drinks are strong, the food is on point and delish, the service is awesome and the views here are incredible. If you have t stumbled across this hidden gem your missing out!

I went in for an oil change today. They got me in line quickly. The workers here let me know that my oil was fine and didn't need an oil change. They're really honest with you with their suggestions on maintenance. I'll definitely be back.
The food is fresh and amazing!! Service was great and fast. We will definitely be back!
Great falafel. Love the chili sauce and the garlic paste too. Nice family business but always have to check to make sure they got our order right.

We had been to their original location on the river and appreciated their variety of plants, succulents and planters. My wife also really liked the fact she could plant right there at the succulent bar. 

We hadn't realized they now have a new location across the street from the original. My wife had reached out to the business owner in advance and asked if they were comfortable with her bringing in some existing pots and succulents that needed some refreshing and they were gracious and said "absolutely - bring them in". 

We arrived about 1pm on a cold Sunday afternoon and were impressed with the interior and offerings. They had a great selection of plants, succulents, pots and gift items. 

My wife quickly chose several new plants and got to work!  In a matter of minutes they looked great. She also found a cool air plant as well!  If you are looking for great quality plants at reasonable prices make sure you stop in. Their hours are 11am-5pm Friday's through Sundays.
Eagle Eyecare is the Best of the Best. Everyone in the office treats my children and I with the greatest care.  Very happy with this Eyecare Facility.

Let's start with the food: average to below average at best it seem like most things were either frozen freeze-dried or came out of a can. We sat on the deck which I usually like to do when I'm in Florida, not a great idea here though. Most places have string or wire to keep the seagulls and other birds away, this place did not have that. The service was not very quick or complementary, it seemed as if the put people cleaning up were not that motivated.  Overall, just not a very good morning experience.
To visit STL and not go to Ted Drewes is to stick a fork in your eye! It is THE best. Ask for any kind of concrete with hot fudge shot down the center.  Oh crimany, it's orgasmic!

With the increasing number of unique & quality eateries in St. Louis, of which the Fountain on Locust is one, differentiation becomes necessary. But how? On our visit for my Mother's Birthday on March 1, 2014, the Fountain was slammed. Mardi Gras and the eve of a winter storm had folks out and about. We were seated after a 10 minute wait. We ordered soon after that, and we waited... and waited... and waited for our food.

40 minutes after ordering, the manager and the server came to explain that our ticket had been punched as fulfilled. It hadn't been. Apparently, our original food order sat there and got old.

So what they did they do? They comped the entire lunch, they made fresh food for us, and they took great care to ensure our satisfaction.

The food is great. The cocktails are fun. The ice cream and candies turn jaded adults into kids again, and the kids into smiling beacons of pure joy. Decor alone makes this place worth a visit, and the food, drinks and desserts are worthy of anyone's time and money. But the service will bring you back, as it will my family and me.
Love every dish they have. Not going to lie, it's kind of pricey. But I only go there once in awhile.

They have an extensive selection of liquor and wine! The prices are the best in town. The staff is friendly and VERY helpful when a customer asks for recommendations. They don't hover, but really take into consideration what taste/event you are buying for. the beer section next door also houses a pleasant selection of local brews as well as almost all of the big name brands. 

The take away: good prices, good selection. Great for the budget conscious and not picky.

This is such a disappointing review to write. I followed Atelier Beauty Bar on Instagram for months, gaining confidence that this was going to become my go-to salon for both nails and haircuts. Turns out, I was unfortunately wrong.

I had three gel manicures with three different gals over the last couple months, and two of them just ended up being unacceptable. The first time I went in, I wanted glitter polish on rounded nails. Getting my desired shape was a struggle, and after trying to get it right three times, I gave up. As soon as I left my appointment, I filed my nails in my car to the appropriate shape, which should have been a huge red flag. As for the polish itself? BRUH. Glitter polish takes extra coats to be glittery since it's a clear base, and every other salon I have been to has put a coat on, cured it in the UV light, and repeated two or three times to get the desired affect. This gal literally globbed the polish onto my nails, all in one go, which made it thick, uneven, and allowed it to slide around and cure funky. Sometimes it didn't even coat the entirety of my nail. I was so dejected at this point, I just sucked it up and left without comment. 

The second manicure was fine, and gave me more confidence in coming back. The coats of polish were thin, but I hoped the third time would be the charm and I'd find my go-to gal for future appointments. The third appointment went much better overall with a friendly, chatty girl providing the service, but I once again had to file my nails in the car afterward to fix the ragged edges. My polish disappointment didn't set in until I'd been home for a bit, when I noticed my thumb nail felt like there was a bubble in it. I pushed on it, and POP! Out came wet nail polish! The nails hadn't been properly cured, so wet polish was trapped between dry layers, which resulted in an opening that ripped the top layer as it snagged on my hair or clothes. I eventually had to file the raggedness away to stop the snagging and ripping, and have had the same experience on two more nails as of now. Such a bummer for a brand new manicure!

Unfortunately, I just can't keep giving Atelier Beauty Bar my money. Three strikes and you're out, which sucks. Any other recommendations for a nail salon?

I really liked that place till last visit the meal was good but for the price they really skimped out.We were rushed out cause we never had a reservation and the waitress avoided us so had to deal with the old bag running the show there she didn't like what I said about the meal that I was going to Mac Donald's to get full cause the salmon I had there was so small wasn't worth eating.wont be going back there

First time getting waxed, Taylor did such a fantastic job! She was quick, thorough, and super personable! The shop was clean and neat. I was able to schedule a last minute wax on a Sunday late afternoon. I took advantage of the bogo deal for first time customers, and I am so looking forward to my next visit!
This is now a must stop Place one in the St. Louis area!  I'm so lucky to live close by.  The macaroni and cheese is to die for.  I highly recommend getting a combo plate with the brisket and chicken

Delicious and Fun! I've never had a snoball taste sooo good. Awesome selection and a lil fun for everyone. Snoballs, Ice cream and adult popsicle too oh my! Yes, make your way over to try something unique in a very relaxing atmosphere that lets you feel like a kid again.

Super convenient and not fast food. Not five star thai food but it is pretty tasty and the quality is great. The fresh rolls and my go to. The panang curry is by far my fav. I have only got take out so far but they have a few tables outside that you can eat at.

This place is so much better than the one in university city. The portions are larger and the food is cheaper. Double win! As a bonus you get free a mini dessert after your meal! The dessert is usually 3 small scoops of 3 different flavors of ice cream.

I was a little put off with the fact that it was in a mall but after seeing the selection of beer, I forgot all about that.  The cook is doing something right.  The menu alone is impressive.  I ordered the empenadas, which had great flavor but I had the feeling they were just reheated and not made fresh since the meat filling wasn't piping hot or maybe they just sat too long but the flavor was there, just not the temperature.  For the main course I had a bison burger, which was cooked perfectly and had great flavor.  The spicy mayo they make in house was perfect to accompany the bison.  Overall, this place is worth going just for the beer alone but having great food is always a bonus

This is a beautiful place and I really wanted to like it.

The choice of draft beers was lovely and the menu looked delicious. We were able to order a couple of pints after only a short wait--nothing worse than would be expected when sitting at the bar during happy hour. We took our time looking over choices for food and waited for service. Tried to make eye contact with bartenders, but no luck. Toward the end of our slowly sipped beers we decided it was time to eat elsewhere. Still took some effort to track down the check. 

So, while it's not a complete review in that we didn't get to eat anything, all the food around us looked delicious, the draft selection was lovely and it was a nice ambience in which to be stuck sitting around with a beer.

I hate to leave a below average rating but I have had 2 bad experiences at this hotel and I won't be returning. I travel for work which brings me to Tucson occasionally. I stayed the the JW approximately 18 months ago and when I returned from my dinner meeting, there was sewage backed up into the bathtub. Although i didn't plan to use the tub, I needed to change rooms in case it would impact the other plumbing. Unfortunately this change had to happen at 11 pm at night. I did not write a review at that time as I assumed it was a 'one off'. I decided to give it a try again since I was working close to downtown Tucson. 

When I arrived at my room (~5pm), I was impressed with the suite but it was warm in the room (temp set at 72) and it was 105 outside. In addition, there were a few flying bugs near the patio door. I killed them with my shoe and then jumped in the shower to get ready for my dinner meeting. 
As I got out of the shower, there was a brief knock at the door followed by a young lady entering my room with some truffles. I asked if I could help her, she said... they told me this room was unoccupied. Ugh, although I appreciate the water/truffles, I was taken aback at them entering my room without waiting for a response. 

I returned from my dinner and the room was still warm. I called the front desk and they brought me a fan assuring me that the ac would soon cool the room down. Unfortunately, at 10:00pm, I am trying to fall asleep since I had a 6:30 am flight... and a bug was crawling across my face. I killed it, and called the operator. They offered to send someone up to identify the bug (dead on the bedspread) and ensure there weren't any others. 

At this point, I was low on sleep and patience. I packed up my stuff and headed down to get a room change. They offered me complimentary resort fee or $50 credit which, in my mind was too little, too late. I wish I could say nicer things but my experience was just not good.
Take out is very slow and they always run out of something. Order is usually incorrect. Eating in is a much better experience. Great outdoor area.
75% and 95% higher than two other quotes from reputable local painters (they had done work on my block as well so I saw how well they did job).  I didn't go with them based on price only.

Arrived at about 8 pm on a Saturday night. Lots of tables open and we were asked by a snotty hostess if we had a reservation. Drinks were overpriced (expected) but they were full of flavor. Food was sub par. Husband ordered a Cuban sandwich (supposed to be one of the best in St.  Louis) not impressed at all. I ordered Cajun chicken Alfredo which had no flavor or kick at all. For $14 it was an average size serving but not impressive at all. The atmostphere is cute but service wasn't great. Not impressed and probably won't be back .

I came across Maryanne's on Yelp while searching for a new breakfast spot a few weeks back.  It's in a strip mall and is easy to pass without noticing.  During our first visit on a Saturday morning the place was packed and had a 30+ minute wait so we left.  We tried again on a Friday morning assuming it would be a little easier to get a table during the week.  It was still crowded but luckily we were able to get a table right away.

We were hungry so we over ordered slightly.  We ordered the breakfast sandwich with home fries, loaded potatoes (ham, cheese, spinach, prosciutto and more) and the stuffed French toast.  The food was delish especially the potatoes!  My only complaint is that the French toast is not actually stuffed its layered with cream cheese.

I would like to visit again to try some of their other options.

After months of trying to get a loan figured out, I finally decided to switch over to a branch on the other side of town (Sparks, NV). I met with Bryan (don't know last name) very friendly, and very very helpful. He got everything figured out and called multiple people to get this situation resolved. It took him about an hour to do what people couldn't do in 3-4 months! I would and will definitely be recommending people to go down and talk with Bryan and also I will drive the extra 20miles just to work with him again! Great guy all around! Definitely promote this guy!

Another really good dinner at J. Alexander's.

My new favorite dish? The smoked salmon appetizer. It's hard to get good seafood in Nashville, but J. Alexander's can hook you up.

This place was great! We loved our food - I had the red snapper and hubby had the lobster roll. Food was really good, drinks were really good. View was amazing of the marina. The service was just ok... we walked up and hostesses seemed put out and our server was also just ok. We will be back, just hope service is better next time.

The Citygarden was the highlight of our STL weekend getaway for me. The art is varied in style, medium, and size which made it a lot of fun to tour. There's a celebrity narrated audio tour available on iOS devices which gives a lot of interesting background on the pieces and the artists. It's a comfortable park to relax in or walk through.

I dream about Hellas bakery. They have so much to choose from. Everything always looks and tastes amazing. It's a pleasure coming here when I'm in town. The rice pudding, as simple as it sounds, was my favorite. I've tried a little bit of everything over the years and nothing disappoints.
Just went back here recently and thought I'd update.  Simply put...nice people, good food, but the drive thru is consistently incredibly slow.

Love love love this wonderful place.  So family friendly.   Great food and great value.  The owners are so customer friendly.  They will do everything in their power to make your experience a pleasant one.  They have been in the business for a long time.  They want you to come back again and again.   Would highly recommend this for your casual dinning or a family celebration or just a quiet dinner for two.

***UPDATED REVIEW 3 MONTHS LATER*** 

My first review still stands. The place has the best price to replace broken/cracked screens. My only problem 3 months down the line is...my phone is randomly glitching. I haven't dropped or even have a crack for the screen to go hay wire. It'll be on the table untouched and my phone starts to "click" randomly! At times when I do open my screen to nagivate what I want to use it'll start going bazar that I can't even use my screen. I'll have to lock my screen and when that doesn't work I restart only for it to happen a few days later again...so I'll have to bump my rating from a 5 to a 3 unless the store has some type of replacement or discount to fix this issue 

-------------------------------------
Thisplace is the best! Just got my iPhone XS  max screen replaced today. My screen cracked since  November and I didn't want to spend the money fixing it as everywhere wanted around $280-$300. Just last week my phone screen completely stopped working. My boyfriend have found PHONE REPAIR PHILLY on google. We live in south philly so this place pop up for us.

They have two different prices. $150 for adverage then $250 for a premium screen. I came in and asked what's the difference he said no much unless you're a photographer and what that extra crisp. I'm a mother to a toddler who can careless! So I went for the cheaper one. 

There was a guy ahead of me when I walked in. While waiting it took him 45mins to fix the person ahead of me and my phone! 

After he was done he also put on a screen protector . Paid and left! 

Honestly there isn't a difference from the original Apple screen and this. Only downfall now my phone isn't  water proof which is fine. 

I'm so gonna recommend everyone I know here!! 

Will update  review in a few months to let everyone know how the screen hel

They have great wings and a great drink/beer menu. I love that they have Rugby games on TV often and have fun outdoor seating too. Overall, a great place for watching a game and enjoying a beer and some quality bar food.
Service is very poor, difficult to eat dishes, serving very slow, we recommend you do not go！！！

I probably should have read the reviews before eating here!  But I didn't, so here's my review....The pizza tasted good if you were one of the first ones to grab a slice after they put it out on the buffet bar.  And the salad bar had a decent variety of toppings, the area was just a little messy.  I did think that the restaurant could've been cleaner. The guy going around wiping down all the tables was just using a rag to push all the crumbs on the floor.  At least he used some kind of cleaning agent on the table afterwards. 
 I think it'd be nice if buffet places had hand sanitizer available for customers to use.  Especially if they serve food that is primarily eaten with your hands.  
I didn't go in the bathroom, so I can't give my opinion on that.  I probably won't go back again.  If I'm ever craving pizza in the future, I think I'll go elsewhere.

Had a poor breakfast experience due to service. Over 30 minutes to get two egg dishes ; never got a requested extra item; server rarely seen. A shame since the food when we got it was good (the baked eggs and an omelet).

Waited about an hour to be sat, which is not unusual for the pier. The service was great though, recommended some dishes/drink. I did order this watermelon mojito and I will say that was pretty bad, tasted like artificial watermelon syrup but otherwise everything else was great. I replaced that with a strawberry margarita and it was yummy. Order calamari and Buffalo wings for app - super delicious. My boyfriend ordered the chicken sandwich and he loved it. I got the blackened grouper and it was amazing. It was a little pricey but would definitely come back for the great food and service!
Quality coffee, I enjoyed the cortado and scone

It's been quite some time since my last review so it must mean this place is worthy of reviving my critique whether it be good or bad, right?

Yup.  And this establishment is worthy of a 5-star rating.

Admittedly, I stumbled upon this place after my first choice was closed.  But being such a big believer in nothing happens by chance, I was meant to come to this salon.  I called in advance to see if he had an opening for a color and cut, which he did so I arrived within an hour of calling.  Jeff greeted me upon my arrival and went straight to business.  I always know what I want in a haircut but when it comes to color, I must admit I can be a little indecisive, but Jeff was incredibly patient and really took his time to guide me and understand what I was looking for.  He had an impressive breadth of knowledge of hair coloring and how those colors would fade to over time - certainly a topic you'd think every hair stylist would know but in my experience, this is certainly not the case.  I opted for balayage highlights, which takes a lot of intention with where to place the color - Jeff was certainly very mindful, even in the midst of having a full-on conversation with me haha and his hair-cutting skills are just as impressive.  He is very well-versed in Asian hair so the way he layered and trimmed my hair was nothing short of excellent.  High marks everywhere!!

But perhaps what really impresses me about Jeff is not just his hair styling and coloring skills, but his pursuit of the American dream.  As a child of immigrant parents, I empathized with his immigrant story.  From what he has shared, after years of working for various salons, he ventured to open his own which is this salon now.  He has been open for just about 2 years now but the with the current pandemic, his brick-and-mortar establishment has taken a financial hit - a narrative we all know too well by now.  If not for his impressive hair skills (and good conversations - Jeff is a great conversationalist!), please help in changing the narrative of small businesses suffering during these times.  Get your hair pampered at JJ Salon!

I stopped in here real late not wanting to really eat another drunken cheeseburger. Charlie is a sandwich GOD. My two am craving for a soft soft breaded blt was lit alive due to the fantastic customer service I received. Not drunk but definitely satisfied. Ok drunk too

Overall this would have been a great experience if our waiter wasn't such a snooty asshole. Both myself and 2 other people in my party ordered fish. I ordered the special which was black grouper - I've only been able to find this at one other restaurant and generally order grouper whenever I can. The other 2 ordered halibut. I could tell almost immediately that my meal had been switched with one of the halibut meals. Unfortunately the person with my meal had already started to five right in and couldn't say enough about how great it was. When I brought this to the attention of the waiter he practically argued with me. I did not want to create a scene but wow what a terrible experience. I don't understand why servers can't ever admit that they're wrong.

This is the worst service. They tell you 20 min. wait, it's 45 min. wait. After you sit down you wait another 30 minutes to order. Breakfast took almost 2 hours, the food was not good. The hollandaise sauce is a cream sauce, not hollandaise. The omelet was horrible. The waitresses are rude and busy talking to their friends so you have to get up yourself to get condiments. There are so many other good places to eat where at least the staff treats you nice and the food is good. Not here!

Went here for lunch, love the space & atmosphere. The outdoor space is really nice as well. Menu selection is decent, I got the small spinach salad with chicken and sweet potato fries. Everything was good, thought it was a bit overpriced for the sizes/quality but nonetheless not bad. I think I need to go back and try out a few other things.

Have not been to this place and a long long time so Friends wanted to go so why not give it a another try. Like I thought still really OVERRATED!!!! Food is just okay nothing exciting Salsa not good and they serve it in what looks like a plastic cup you would get if you asked for extra sour cream Lol chips okay . I do Have to say the Draft Beer was excellent nice and cold like it should be Dessert was pretty good . Service just okay as I thought it is the same as it was 15 years ago just okay maybe the HYPE is because of the outside seating area . I would pass if You are looking for Mexican good try somewhere else  but just my opinion ..

Amazing!!! Great hot chicken! Recommend you order online and take out to avoid long lines. Our order was ready the minute it said it would be. We were able to grab a table, but it's not really the normal operation.

Good Grief Hertz....get a clue!!  Nashville hosted NCAA Final Four which means thousands of visitors to this city (which everyone should be very thankful for).  Common sense would dictate that management would have additional staff available to help process transactions.  I rented a full size vehicle through Priceline (got a great deal) and paid for the insurance online.  Arrived at the Hertz counter to find 18 people in front of me at 12:00 noon which was absurd since there were two -- yes, two -- agents at the counter, a manager walking around like he was overwhelmed, and a young lady behind the counter in training. I had my paper confirmation in hand and presented it to the blonde lady with glasses and she asked for my last name and did not look at my confirmation number.  THAT WAS HER FIRST MISTSAKE.  She typed on the computer and then requested my driver's license and credit card.  That was where the "upselling" began as she desperately tried to get me to pay for an upgrade and I indicated I paid for a full size vehicle and that is what I wanted.  She was supposedly training a young girl and was more interested in talking with her than assisting customers.  She then said she could give me a great deal on an SUV and I told her again that I was not paying for anything other than a full size vehicle which I had already paid for.  I was in a hurry and just wanted to be on my way.  Then she asked how many in my party, wanted to know where I was traveling, and if I wanted to upgrade (again).  She swiped my credit card (I suppose that was for the $200 "hold" required deposit).  I was getting frustrated because I had waited in line 1 hour because of the lack of employees and I told her I simply wanted a full size vehicle with good gas mileage.  She tried to get me to purchase insurance (which I already had through Priceline) and she tried to sell it to me anyway.  She swiped my card and said I was all set and the car should be returned around the same time 14 days later.  I said "WHAT"?  I only rented the car for two days. She then typed on the computer and said there is another person with your same last name in the computer, so she had to start all over.  I have not received my statement, yet, but I better not be billed for anything.  I just don't understand all the pressure (unless she works on commission).  I am from Nashville and this experience really made me mad and is a total embarrassment to anyone visiting the town as we are supposed to be very friendly.  Oh, and my full size car turned out to be a Dodge Avenger.  SHAME ON YOU HERTZ!  I will use one of the other companies next time since they did not have a one hour wait time.

The Doylestown Inn has been converted into a funky spot with fabulous interior design, attentive staff and a fun American style menu. The space is fun with hats as light shades, chandeliers hanging everywhere (that are for sale), exposed stone walls, chalk boards and furniture made of funky recycled pieces. Check out the bar downstairs for more fun.

The menu for lunch was easy, no GF options however I ordered the burger (upgraded to Kobe beef) without the bun and served with large fries in a trendy stainless steel basket. Presentation of the food was great and the service by our waiter Bob was excellent. I will be back to try the dinner menu too.

Doylestown has been in desperate need for a more funky space, this is it. Check it out.

If you are in Indianapolis YOU HAVE TO GO HERE.  It is so insane, you will see what I mean.  Average food, and weak Gin and Tonic, I may have just built up my expectations too high, given that I drove 16 hours straight to get there!

I go to Kimmswick at least once a year and I always have my lunch at the Dough Depot.  Their prices are very reasonable and I love their pretzel bread.  The chips aren't very tasty.  I would recommend upgrading to a soup or salad.  I never eat at the Blue Owl.  Their prices are double the Dough Depot and you get the same kind of food, basically!!!  They only have 2 bathrooms, so you might have to wait till one opens up to use.  The people that work there are very friendly.

Was looking for a new brunch place to check out nearby and not wanting to drive all the way to tampa. Happen to walk by this place while trying to figure out where to go. Service  was great! They had pretty decent menu as well as extensive drinks to choose from! I ordered shrimp and grits. Let me preface that I normally am not a fan of grits but I thoroughly enjoyed and devoured this dish! 

Highly recommend!

Treat yourself to an foodie experience.  Dining solo, I opted to sit at the bar.  The bar scene was vibrant and full.  The staff was working their tails off.  I gotta say, sitting at the bar let's you watch how everyone interacts and gives you a good sense how they treat others---even when slammed.

Asking for steak recommendations, I was actually surprised my server did not upsell me.  His recommendation of the 14 oz. NY Strip was spot on.  Was so filling, had to get a doggy bag.  More on the bag.

I enjoyed a clossal shrimp cocktail to start the evening----I thought a little over-priced, especially for only 3.  With that said, the quality and size more than made up for the sticker shock.  

After this meal, I could not look at the dessert menu.  Just a nice black coffee.

I really enjoyed the experience.  Dining solo away from home is never fun; however, when you get to enjoy the energy watching many of these young people deliver----it is really refreshing and makes being away from home bearable.

A great place to dine as a group or as a couple.  Famously quoting: "I shall return." 

Oh...about the doggy bag: It is a bloody full-size reusable shopping bag. Brilliant advertising when I am at home in NC. #Tony'sGoneGreen. 

Again, many thanks.
This is my absolute favorite spot in NW philly to get breakfast. You don't need to decide between bacon and sausage when you order the breakfast combo, so extra points for that.

I rented a Uhaul, directly from Uhaul it was a nightmare. Reserved a week in advance, no truck when we went to p/up. They sent me somewhere else, Uhaul app wasn't working, 40 minutes on hold (paying movers to stand around) waiting for customer service to get a code to get truck key, no go. Finally 2 hours later went to Goleta Motors, in & out 5 minutes they are WONDERFUL. Do not go thru Uhaul just call Goleta Motors direct, moving is stressful enough. Also, I had a neice buy a car from them and they were great and she was very happy.
Loved everything about this coffee shop! Friendly staff and great atmosphere, coffee was also amazing!

Awesome salon, great staff, meticulous with hair!  i moved here from Denver, CO and it took my a long time to find a local stylist that i loved.  AMY JOHNSON is a color magician and is the sweetest and kindest person EVER!.  I have blonde highlights and was previously getting 7 different colors and she transformed my hair with only 2 colors, lots of patience, and got it super healthy again.  The prices are very reasonable and the service is outstanding.  AMY always has assistants on hand to help with shampoo etc so she can focus on her top talent:  HAIR COLOR and cuts.  Also i have long hair and am.. well... pretty high maintenance when it comes to my hair.  She asked me to come in for a consult before she ever did anything to my hair to make sure we would be on the same page and i have never been happier with my hair style or hair color.  also STACEY at the front desk is a gorgeous red head with a glowing smile and always confirms every appointment in advance.  Amy  has never run late in 4 years for me.  SHAYLA is the best assistant - sweet, great help to Amy - and ensures Amy runs efficiently and on time.  Love this place - wish i could give them 10 stars.  Not sure why anyone would give them a 1 star - hard to believe that person was actually here.
The corn chowder was delightful. The crab cakes yummy. Very nice venue. Friendly service too!!

This group is excellent. We took our SUV here to get the two front windows tinted and they went above and beyond in making the experience great. They were patient, fast, and addressed any concerns we had quickly. I would definitely recommend them.

First time to Philadelphia we had to have a philly cheese steak sandwich. Most people said to go to Pats or Geno's but we were told to go Jim's. The line was almost out the door but it moved fast. Our sandwiches were the best we've ever had and would recommend it to everyone. Can't wait to go back it was a good experience.

I've eaten here and had drinks and friends and I got a few things and split it. The mac and cheese was phenomenal. Beer selections are always good. Upstairs and downstairs bar but upstairs is small and I think downstairs has a better vibe. Tough to get a bar spot but there are some booths along the side. Always packed.
Avoid this place like the plague. They scratched my entire vehicle and left a few large dents. I believe this was a malicious act.

Dr. Howard is by far the best dentist I have ever dealt with. I feel their prices are very good. The office staff is always nice and so helpful. They take care of my whole family. They all try very hard to make sure they build a good rapport. I give them 5 stars.

My friends and I visit this PJs fairly often and we love Rachel as our server! She's friendly, attentive, and provides great food/drink recommendations. We usually just pick on some apps or wings while watching a game, but we have ordered some of the burgers and sandwiches and those have been good. Nothing out of this world, but for sports bar food - it's good! The atmosphere is fun and the games are easy to see no matter where you're sat.

Went to see Dr. Crawford for the second time today (my daughter and I both had appointments), and was very impressed with the office and the doctor.  Did not have to wait months and months for an appointment like other dermatologist offices.  Received both phone call and email reminders of appointments.  Office was pleasant and extremely efficient and we were taken back to the exam rooms almost exactly at our appointment times and seen by the doctor very quickly.  NO LONG WAITING - worth our drive into Philly from NJ.  Dr. Crawford was very professional and thorough.  Offered great advice without trying to sell products (even told us the things he recommended could be bought on Amazon).  Would highly recommend this practice.

The food is average, the drinks are decent, the prices are steep, and the service is generally nonexistent. I used to go here for some of the specials (because it's not worth the prices they regularly charge), but they recently got rid of those specials despite continuing to advertise them. If you can make it for happy hour, it might be worth it. Otherwise, don't waste your time.

The food, the atmosphere, and the very nice staff that work here make this restaurant a must go to restaurant! I sat upstairs where they offer balcony dining and an incredible view, especially when the sun sets. I ordered a french onion soup, a deep fried eggplant appetizer (which was more like an entree), and the blackened shrimp jambalaya. All three dishes were amazing and so filling. The only reason I gave this 4 stars is because we waited a little longer than usual to be greeted, and even longer to receive our 2 glasses of wine we had ordered. I would still definitely return to dine here again because of the food and the niceness of the staff and management.

Stopped by for lunch during the week and thought it was closed since there wasn't a single customer.  Needless to say, we were greeted and served quickly.

The interior?  Well it's in a strip mall.  Nuf said. When I go to restaurants I often think "how could this place be improved?"  The décor & location mainly.

The menu was quite extensive with a lot of vegetarian choices.  I ordered the ravioli's and my meal was quite good and reasonably priced.  

I would definitely go again...
This cafe located in Fishtown has a super cute theme filled with vinyls and music. I had a chai tea latte and it was decent. Definitely worth a visit!

I love this place. It was first place I ate at when I was in New Orleans. The food and service were incredible. I had the  grits and shrimp which were each flavored deliciously. I also had the rosemary  and champagne  drink.

There is not too much more to say but what an outstanding  place. Happy to give 5 stars.
Purported to be the "original" - I must say it was a phenomenal cheesesteak even cold when eaten at home 2 hours later!!

Fast service but very low quality and the staff is not very friendly. I had a mani/pedi done and I was thrilled that they would do both at the same time so I could get back to work but the polish on my hands chipped in several spots in within 24 hours and within a few days looked awful. My toes weren't much different except the polish seemed to hang on a bit longer for 3 days before it started chipping. They accept credit cards but run your card separately for each service you get and they only accept cash tips which they don't tell you beforehand and do not have it posted anywhere in their store. The foot massage was great but if you are looking for nice nails that will last beyond 24 hours, go somewhere else.
Great service, will return! Nothing bad to say about this location, I love the coupons that come in the mail as well.  Plus, if you look online you can get them before you go :)

Ok...I hate to diss anyone... I had been here when they first opened so I cut some slack... I was downtown NPR last weekend & saw their doormen looking all sad & lonely... everywhere else...popping... This weekend I went for the blues fest... Which was AWESOME I might add.. afterwards strolled all the local bars...including Dulcet... Once again..looking all lonely...only now 2 DOORMAN... U know I had to see what was up.Ok first mistake 1:15 am....wanting a 5$ cover!!!! GTFOH YOUR IN NEW PORT RICHEY...5 OTHER BARS WITHIN Walking.... staggering distance with NO COVER...NO BAR IN NPR CHARGES A COVER...WHY DO U THINK U ARE BETTER??? NOT! NOT TO MENTION THE ATTITUDE BEEOTCH WITH BAD DREGS THAT RUDELY STOPPED ME AT THE DOOR TO TELL ME THAT. That's why your failing.... From what I remember it's very nice inside...food was Ok... How bout you blast some Al Green.... Keep the very sweet doorman...get rid of her & drop the cover...you will be much better off.

I have been to amphitheaters all over the US and while Ascend Amphitheater is not in beautiful scenery, it is very well done and I am thrilled to have it so close! It is right there in downtown Nashville and small enough to make every seat a treat! It also made it quick and easy to get an uber ride to and from the venue which no doubt was the cost or less than parking.
We went to see Kings of Leon and had seats towards the back which made for quick access to the bathrooms and beer carts but one look at the lawn and I wouldn't have minded it either. Very nice looking lawn that was sloped only enough to make viewing good for all and not a dangerous slope as we have experienced at other amphitheaters. Seats were padded & comfortable with enough leg room that exiting the row was not a bother to those you had to pass. Right behind us they had tables and chairs which we will look into our next show there, very nice to sit at a table and have a plce to set your drink. (only complaint about our seating was no place for a drink other than the ground)
We will return as often as possible! Ascend is a wonderful venue that makes a concert feel almost intimate.

I went to VP Square today at lunch to have what would have been my first bowl of Ramen ever, but weirdly, they had no Ramen - delivery problems, they said. OK, so I had a Lemongrass bánh mì sandwich instead.  I was not disappointed in that at all!

I did ask for no cilantro, and my wish was granted (thank you), but after eating half the sandwich I noticed there were no jalapeños on it either. It turned out that was a good thing, because when they brought me some in a little container for the second half of my sandwich that I took home, I was told to be careful because the jalapeños were "ferocious", lol. Not into ferocious jalapeños, so I was glad they didn't get put on there to begin with!!  I loved the pickled carrots, radishes, cucumbers on top of the sandwich, they were slightly sweet and salty, pickle-y and really good. Oh, and there was plenty of chicken on it.

I was very happy with my sandwich, overall, but I was more happy with my Taro milk tea. It was thick, creamy, delicious. I will say that I thought I was going to get bubble tea, but realized that when I ordered, I just said "tea", and not "bubble tea" - note to myself for next time!

Nice decor, plenty of tables, bathroom was clean, and I enjoyed my quiet lunch.

This place was exceptionally adequate. The food was good enough quality for the price but I found it rather disappointing in the taste department. The hot sauce bar was good, but unfortunately, necessary to add flavor to the food. 
     The atmosphere is fun, and the music playing was considerably better than the standard chain restaurant soundtrack. The staff was quite friendly and was on point with the service. I went there for lunch, and could see this as a decent spot to hit around 2:00am after a night out. All in all I probably won't return, but that is more because I'm only in town a short time. I would try it again if I were here for a longer stay.
Standard Subway, nothing special. Fast, friendly service. They have gotten rid of the Monterey Cheddar bread though, so that sucks.

For a place that has WINGS in their name, they probably have the worst wings I've had in this area. Their wings are dry hard and cold. You have to literally chew aggressively most parts of these wings to eat them cause their texture  is so overdone. Glad I tried this place finally and won't have to be spending any of my money there ever again.

The museum needs to step up its game. There are is not enough art or exhibits to justify the cost of admission. It takes at most 2 hours to go through the museum as it is small.  I have been going to art museums throughout my life and this is one unimpressive museum. I wish they had more to offer than just local artists and the Roman/Greek sections. 

The museum is at a good location and gives guests access to the riverwalk nearby and Curtis Hixon park. There is also parking in the William Poe lot which is a very short walk to the museum. There is also a cafe that guests can buy a coffee and sit while having river views! 

The location is on par! The art needs some updating, there needs to be more exciting exhibits, and the price of the museum should reflect what is there.

This is hands done the best natural nail salon in Delaware. I have been going here full time for over 2 years now and I have never been unsatisfied with their work. The inside of the salon is very nice and clean. The service is usually very quick, I have only had a wait twice (without an appointment) only two times. I am consistently happy with their service and level of professionalism.

Worst club in Reno.

The scene is trashy, the place is disgusting.

It's WAY too overcrowded. It is way too small for the amount of people they squeeze in there. I often feel like I'm going to pass out with all the smoke and cramped bodies in the room.

The music is awful, and never changes.

This club would scare most people away on the first night.

I suggest people think twice about going here.

Had lunch at the Cafe.  The food was amazing!!!  The Alchemy Arts Center is a very unique place.  I toured the spa and yoga rooms - they were beautiful.  I dream about this place and can't wait to come back.

Would not recommend this establishment to eat at when there is so many other restaurants in the French Quarter that serve good food, are clean and have competent cooks and servers. Had breakfast, toast cold, server acted like she was in a coma, fried eggs looked like they had been cooked by an amateur and had black specks in them probably from a dirty grill. A few days later stopped in later in the evening and had the worst grilled cheese sandwich at the bar. Bar condiments were dirty and whole restaurant felt sloppy and not very well tended after. After trying this establishment two times found other great places reasonably priced just a few blocks away close to Jackson Square.

Why I never wrote a review of Bill's the entire time I worked in the same building as them is beyond me--I would go there ALL THE TIME on lunch breaks.  Prices are reasonable, and while it's not fancy, the food is great.  If I were in the mood for breakfast, I would usually get a mushroom, egg, and cheese sandwich with ketchup on a croissant or a bagel, and if I were in the mood for lunch, I would often get a burger or an American hoagie.  It tends to get a bit crowded during the lunch hour, though they are very efficient and serve with a smile, so it is worth it.  As is stated in their name, they are only open for breakfast and lunch, so don't expect to be served after 3pm! I would definitely return for a bite next time I am in the neighborhood.
I have been back a few times to have the boils and they are just as delicious today as they were when they opened. I love this place the food is absolutely amazing
Really liked this place. Good ambiance. The food was delicious. I had the hamachi and the octupus. Both were amazing! Will def come back here again.

Bit good at all the service  could be better 
The food sigh  not good. No taste what's so ever 
Next time maybe will go just for some wine or so

I've been going to EWC Rittenhouse for about a year and a half. I've had two different estheticians. Seems as soon as get used to one, she leaves. So, I recently was paired with April. What a wonderful experience, as far as waxing experiences can be. She was personable (but not over the top), quick and thorough, not a scraggly hair to be found. I was so pleased and I pray she stays for awhile.

I had placed an order with them and they said it was delivered on August 14th.   I never received it.  They sent it to a different address.   I called customer service and they said it couldn't be replaced. Now I'm out of money.  Don't shop here or order from them. Don't waste your time or money.  I'm very unsatisfied.

We came here after a terrible experience across the street that caused us to leave. We were starving and just walked across and we were thrilled they were able to seat us within 15 minutes. Our server was Michael and he was super friendly and quick. He talked to us more in the first 10 minutes than our waitress at Loulou's did in an hour and a half. Our food came out quickly. The food was ok but the service was great!

Mug 'n Bun is nostalgic Americana at its finest. This is a drive-up hamburger stand that has been in the same spot for over forty years. When you pull in to the lot you can either have the waitresses come to you (unfortunately not on roller-skates) or you can walk up to the window and eat at the picnic tables on location. Everything I've tried here is fantastic and they brew their own Root Beer which is worth the drive from Indianapolis alone. The first time that I realized I loved this place was when I ordered a double cheeseburger. Not only is it tasty, but there is a slice of white bread that separates the two patties. One piece of white bread in the middle of the sandwich. How old school is that??

It is definitely divey, but in a fun way. Don't look at the floor, just watch the guy trying to jump up onto the bar with both feet at once. OK pour of Guinness. The hamburger was fine, cooked just right, messy with BBQ sauce as expected. The fries were fantastic, no grease detected, hand cut, not crispy but not limp, inside done just right. The cook said they had a new kitchen manager that thinks "bar food" should not be mediocre, and the cook seems happy to be there. The bartender was friendly and attentive. The denizens sported beanies, backpacks, ear gauges and tats, and all very nice. Kathy C.'s post said a quality earring experience, I agree! There is outdoor seating in front, which is the usual for downtown Boise.

Long time, first time. 

Have always loved me some Cafe Desta. It's my, aaah, top destAnation (Laffy Taffy status). 

The Shorba soup and the bread that goes straight to my thighs is wonderful. Add a mint tea or glass of mango juice and I'm all warm and fuzzy.

Skip the hipster haven next door and hit up Desta!
Very cute place for a cappuccino and a treat. Chicks love this place. Bring your girl
Got a flight of red wines (Pinot Noir, Cabernet and a Meritage.)  Not too impressive.  They are new so I will go back.  Right near the Speedway.

I was beyond excited to try northeast sandwich co. I was not disappointed! We ordered the mushroom cheese steak, pork belly Ruben, falafel burger (not featured) and the plain Mac n cheese. My favorite was the pork belly Ruben. This is honestly the reason I would come back to Northeast Sandwich co.
