The cheesesteak was good but would have been better on a different roll that was perhaps more sturdy. Flavor was incredible as well as the vegan cheese sauce. The burger was ‍ could have done with that. And the Mac and cheese, was Mac n cheese. Not the best but not the worst I have ever had. With all that being said There are still a ton of delicious sounding options I can't wait to try!

I chose this practice because they are open on some Saturdays. This is a major perk for me because I hate having to take time off work for so many appointments. You do have to schedule far in advance for a Saturday spot, but it's worth it for me. They also have a few locations all around town. I go to the Carmel location. 

I can't comment on how long my waits have been, because I am always browsing their optical selection and trying on glasses while I wait. They have a GREAT selection of glasses from all of my favorite brands. They are also continuously updating their stock of glasses so they always have the latest styles. 

I have seen two separate docs at the Carmel location, and both have been extremely pleasant and nice. I would recommend this place!

This place is a total dump. Don't even waste your time. Chicken sandwich was nearly raw, I'm currently sick. And I was surprised after I sent the food back, the waitress had the audacity to charge us 6.00$ for the two waters. Never coming back here again and recommend you don't either. What an embarrassment.

This was a wonderful find. The place is small but the food is absolutely delicious. The special today was Crawfish Monica and it did not disappoint. The lump crab and corn soup was equally as delicious. Well actually better than any other that I've found. You need to try this place. And the people who served us were so kind and helpful.

It was ok, but it left me wanting more.  As in more meat on my wrap and more hummus.  I should have taken a picture of the "side" hummus for $1.99.  It was a small plastic cup you would put some free salsa in at a Mexican restaurant.  I expected a 1/4 cup of hummus not a large tablespoon.  Pita, an extra $1.  That is pretty standard.  

What did I learn today?  Good flavor. Portion management in favor of profitability.

Five stars doesn't seem like enough. It's a pricey establishment but worth it to splurge. A great local spot for special occasions. And if you're trying to budget - you can't beat their burger!


Cool esthetic, great for a date

As we pulled into the parking lot, I had second thoughts about visiting this place. It's located in the back corner of a parking lot dominated by a less-than-savory-looking bar. However, once I stepped out of my car, I could smell the hickory smoke and I knew that any BBQ place still cooking at 5:00pm was worth my time.

Stepping inside, the seediness of the parking lot and the bar next door fade away. The inside is warm and inviting with family photos on the wall and an over-the-top friendly staff behind the counter. The owner--Papa Turney himself-- took our order and talked with me for a few minutes about my military service. He made some recommendations to my wife (including the off-menu combination plate of any three of their smoked meats) and offered us free desert since they were out of brisket (my wife's favorite).

We sat at one of their four tables and my five-year-old son talked to/played with the owner's grandson, who was a couple years younger. They brought our drinks over (the sweet tea is good and surprisingly lemony) as we waited only a few more minutes for our food.

Oh, the food.

Now, I consider myself to be a barbecue aficionado and will turn my nose up at fake 'cue. This stuff was pretty damn good. I took my first bite of rib without sauce (as one should) to really get a feel for how they do things. It was good. My wife, taking her first bite of her pulled pork sandwich, made a face generally reserved for the best BBQ places in STL or the bi-monthly cooks on the smoker in my backyard.

The meats are served without sauce, allowing you to actually enjoy the taste of the smoked meat, but bottles of a pretty tasty, peppery BBQ sauce are on the tables. We tried--and enjoyed--the ribs, pulled pork, chicken, and catfish. All were delicious.

The sides were amazing, too. The baked beans were delicious, as was the grilled cabbage. The cole slaw was nothing to write home about, but was still quite tasty and, while I didn't try it myself, the wife said the same thing about the potato salad. 
We didn't have room for desert, so I can't comment on it, but I heard good things.

In summary, delicious barbecue, yummy sides, kid-friendly, and don't let the outside fool you. I'd drive down the 45-60 minutes from Clarksville just to come eat here again.

If your looking for a salon to get your hair blown out and styled, this is the place to go! I'm a hairstylist and I would highly recommend this salon. They were so friendly and welcoming. The salon was so cute inside and she offered me champagne which was AWESOME!!! I had Heather style my hair and she was wonderful and very creative. If I ever come back to Nashville I will 100% be coming back to Heather :)

This is a solid member of the Sheraton chain, and a great value. Attention is paid to the right things here: Big comfortable rooms, very solid Club Lounge, salt water pool, super pet friendly.

I have spent over 30 nights in various SPG hotels this year and have 40+ the previous few years, so I have seen a lot of Sheratons - this one is my favorite.

A few friends and I came out to Park & Rec on the 4th of July in hopes of a few games and maybe some drinks but that didn't happen due to us wearing "slides"... The place was empty, it was the 4th of July, and I'm sorry but I don't feel like I need to dress to the 9s to go play some freaking corn hole! Like really? Not to mention as soon as we were shunned away a group of girls (who were dressed less than casual, AND WITH SLIDES) were let in with no issues. I didn't want to really come here in the first place but we've come before plenty of times and have had great fun. Sadly, due to this scenario we will not return and neither will our friends.

This business runs with great efficiency! I absolutely appreciate how Mike helps and guides patients when choosing the perfect frame. This is a huge selling point for me. The doctors are also calm, cool and collected and do a fantastic job of explaining everything! Awesome work, Lakeview Vision!!!

Based on a Yelp rave of the "best friends chicken in SB" my sister and I came in to give it a try and we really liked it. The dark meat especially since is was so Tender and Juicy!   Not a huge rosemary fan but it was super delicious with fresh veggies and mashed potatoes and gravy!  Huge portions. We also got pie! Pecan and Apple pies a la mode!  Kristina was a wonderful waitress! Very kind. Fast service with a smile. We would definitely come back another day!

I had Boyle's rescreen my back porch. They did a wonderful job.  They set up a meeting for giving me an estimate, were ontime and professional.  They set up a date to return to do the work and appeared promptly and and did great professional work.  I'm very pleased with my results and highly recommend them

Amazing sauces and salsas and smoked meats but with delicious seasonings. All that and a super cool decor and pretty tasty drinks. Love when restaurants take the time to make tortillas from scratch. Can't go wrong! My only recommendation is go early, it's busy on the weekends but well worth the wait.

Tried this salon based on reviews. Requested highlights (something I have had for 15 years). I always get this done with bleach and it gives the bright blonde that I like and expect. The stylist Dawn insisted that the non-bleach color they use would achieve the look I desired and match the grown out highlights I currently had. Even though I pushed back saying that one time another salon tried this and I hated my hair, she insisted. So I said ok but explained I hate yellow or golden color and like beige blonde with a lot of my natural color coming through. I explained that I want a natural look. The end result is exactly what I didn't want! Yellow and gold in tone and almost my entire head. No natural color shines through. Then she cut my hair and it is completely butchered and looks way worse then it started off. It's uneven and not a reverse bob like I wanted. I will not be going back and would not recommend Dawn. She literally didn't listen to anything I said and just did whatever she wanted. Such a disappointment and a waste of money. I still tipped 20% and didn't tell her I hated my hair because I was too embarrassed and I'm sure she needs the money. The place was completely empty.

If you're vegan and a person who likes to eat a lot, do yourselves the favor and don't eat here. Their vegan portions are SMALL compared to the non vegan entrees, and they're just as pricey. The vegan ceviche was actually pretty depressing in regards to taste and size. The vegan entree I had was tasty but depressing in its size. I'll definitely be exploring better options next time. 

Service was okay. We got our glasses of water refilled a couple of times. Waiter never asked how our food was because had he I would have been that guest that tells him the truth.

Ridiculous on how they say "you cant shop here because you are not 18", it's legal to shop at age 14 15 or 16. This is the Ridiculous ShopRite I've ever seen. Children who are 14 or 16 can shop, and there is no Policy on ages to shop.

Sunday brunch today was very good.  Our table tried the farm burger, French toast, country egg strata and shrimp and grits.  Everyone was pleased with the food.  It's a small restaurant and our server, Scottie, was energetic, courteous, and efficient and answered our many questions.  Local laws prohibited alcohol being served before noon, but the champagne was brought exactly at 12:00pm.  No complaints here!
Really good vegan donuts! And this location is right next to 2 vegan restaurants! Free parking in the lot or street.

Con Murphy's accomodated our fairly large (maybe 50 people??) private event with a semi-open bar (soft drinks and selected beers and wines) and butlered hors d'ouvres.  The ladies were really young and cute and friendly as they offered platters of "goat cheese & Truffles flatbread" and sliders and mini crab cakes.  The (obviously frozen) veggie potstickers were the best of the lot IMHO.

It was a convivial setting with MANY big-screen TVs broadcasting the Phils game. 

It's a bar.  It's conveniently located.  It had 2 clean unisex restrooms.

Will I rush right back here?  Nah.  But it's a great option for the nabe.

Ice Cream is super premium quality and I love the variety and seasonal flavors offered. I usually gravitate towards the nice flavors (toffee butter brickle and sweet corn are my favorites) but today I tried the pink champagne sorbet from the naughty list and, it was so good I felt I needed to write about it. Not too sweet and just the perfect hue of pink. 

Staff is always friendly and happy to let you taste to find a flavor or flavor combination that you will love. 

If you're looking for pints to take home (and you should be) make sure you look closely/ask about the flavors. They often still have pints of flavors that they are no longer scooping.

My party and I came here during Restaurant Week and boy were they packed for a Tuesday night. Two people from our group got there a bit early and got seated first for our reservation and the rest of us spent 20 minutes looking for parking so we came in a bit late. Upon arrival, we let the host know what our reservation was under and was going to lead us to our table but instead our waiter seated us! How nice of him! We were poured water and wine very quickly and served fresh bread. Our waiter took our orders and our food was well on it's way out. We had our appetizer (all of us got salads, either the caesar or house) and my salad was lovely. But I mean, you can't mess up a salad very easily. We all ordered the prime rib for our entree. Being at a restaurant called the prime rib, it would be silly to order anything else. We all ordered our prime rib to our own liking and when my dish came out it had a little pool of blood on top. I thought it might be a bit too red to be a medium/medium-well but I just sopped it up in the au jus and was pretty pleased. The prime rib really was outstanding. So juicy and tender and all the flavors meld together perfectly. The best I've had in awhile! Definitely worth the drive from the burbs. The mashed potatoes were just standard buttery potatoes and the green beans were sauteed with almonds. For family size vegetables it was somewhat of a small portion but for our table of five, we couldn't even finish the small portion. Our server was very friendly and attentive and was always quick to make suggestions and talk to us about other great eats around the town. Our dessert orders were split between the creme brulees and chocolate mousse but our server convinced my cousin to try the key lime pie. I had a bite of everything and it was pretty exceptional. I've never had key lime pie before but it's amazingly silky and smooth. If I wasn't so stuffed silly I would've tried to eat all of his pie! The chocolate mousse was very rich but equally delish and my fave was the creme brulee. Mmm, just makes my mouth water thinking about it. Overall this was an outstanding experience at a lovely restaurant. The food is awesome and the environment is relaxing. Would come back the next time I visit PA again!

Also, our waiter was fantastic! His name is Douglas and even though we were late, he was extremely patient with us. Two of the people in our party had already waited nearly half an hour for the rest of us and already knew what they had wanted and the rest of us glanced at our menus and knew what we wanted also. He is very accommodating because my cousin got the house salad and hates cheese. The salad dressing was a parmesan based dresssing and he offered to get him something else but my cousin actually enjoyed the salad with some fresh cracked pepper. He's a jokester and really made our table feel comfortable eating a higher scale restaurant. He's very friendly with many suggestions for other restaurants. You can tell that he is very knowledgeable and takes pride in his work. It definitely shines through in his work ethic and he knows how to have fun. AND I mentioned the yelp deal at the end of our meal (after the kitchen had closed) just to joke around because I had completely forgot about it and he said it had been a tad bit too late for the free appetizer. He grabbed us our check and gave us our side order of au gratin potatoes for free! How sweet! We weren't expecting anything. And he also offered to take a group picture for us. He also was not quick to kick us out either because they had been closed probably for a good half an hour before we were on our way out. We were the only ones left! I see so many people with mixed reviews about service and even a huge complaint about our waiter in particular and I cannot disagree more with these people. This man is an amazing waiter and knows what he is doing for sure! I'll be back as soon as I can! Best experience I could have ever wished for! Thank you Douglas! :)

My wife and I ate dinner here recently. This is our new favorite local restaurant!!! The food is delicious (a little spicy, but that is Korean food), the quantity is more than enough, and the owners are very nice and friendly. We really like this place and will definitely go back!

I have had pretty bad luck with massages in Boise. But Spa Urbana has restored my faith! Story was absolutely amazing. I was so impressed with all of her massage techniques and she did amazing work releasing severe muscle tension in my neck, back and hips. I had a baby a year ago and am still feeling the physical effects. Story saved me! I am looking forward to going back and will recommend Spa  Urbana and Story to everyone I know. I feel like I have found an absolute gem!

My husband and I just wrapped up a weekend at Wedgewood Inn. We loved everything about this place. The Inn is gorgeous from top to bottom and the outside yard has many little areas to sit and enjoy the landscaping and quiet. Everyone was amazing. They were all approachable, friendly, down to Earth and we especially connected with Carrie. We look forward to returning.

Well aware this is a chain, however this is my first time eating here. I'm sure any of these reviews could apply to any cpk in the Unites States. Our waiter surprised me with a little snack of chips and special Guacamole dip as a first time customer. Our food was fresh. I got the BBQ chicken salad, which I liked. I hate when salads are bland and have no nutrition to them. This salad was loaded with greens, tomatoes and avocados. Tried my wife's pizza. Yummy. All four of us dining together split the strawberry shortcake and oh my gosh it was incredible. A biscuit split in half a la mode with fresh berries. We devoured it. 

Each entree I would say is large enough for two people and the desert was plenty for four of us. Seems like serving sizes keep getting bigger and bigger these days. 

Awesome place. I could tell the ingredients are fresh and I walked away feeling healthy about what I ate. Will go again.

This place is completely unprofessional. I first heard about them through a Mom's group and they offered a free class and childcare. Very cool. So tried to sign up online, I was unsuccessful 3 times. Called them up and they set me right up. Well yesterday I got a email message saying that my class was canceled. Then another email saying I was bump up to early class without a phone call to confirm that I was okay with that. Then later on an apology email saying not enough riders so we are combining 2 classes and now its at 10:30. Way too many emails. If you want to introduce a free class and potentially get new clients don't keep switch classes and canceling classes. Stay consistent!! I will not be giving this business a second chance. You lost my potential business.

Went in to cancel/downgrade service and ended up with a better plan for a better price. That's the way to do it! Great customer service and very friendly. Definitely will go to the store over calling Comcast Customer Service because of this experience.

Out of the 3 tasting rooms we went to in Santa Barbara, I liked this one the most.  I really like the different atmosphere it had since it looked like a re purposed gas station with the tasting room inside.  I really enjoyed their wines, especially the Pinot and the Malbec.  Maybe we caught them on a good day, but the woman who poured for us was very knowledgeable and introduced each wine with background and food pairing ideas.

The food was amazing! 

However, the guy at the front who greeted us wasn't the best. We came in off the street without a reservation, and we are young (23). When we were greeted we were told we could only sit at the bar, and no tables in the dining room were open at all, so we left. The part that was frustrating was when we opened the OpenTable app and saw there was an open reservation available in 10 minutes. So, we walked out of the door at 5:05, came back in 10 minutes for the reservation that we immediately booked online. 

It would have been nice for the person at the front to inform us that all we needed was to make a reservation quickly online, or check to see if there was any openings. It was also frustrating because the dining room was empty the entire time we were there. I honestly feel it was because we looked young that the host didn't want to serve us. 

TL;DR always make a reservation because they won't be accommodating if you do not, even if it's slow.

​We were headed back from the beach, and one of our friends suggested Mexican, and Grand Hacienda was close by and had great Yelp reviews.

It was mid-day on a Sunday, and there were a few spaces open in their parking lot, which is HUGE along this stretch of beach. 

The place is really cute with colorful 3-D metal murals on the outside of the building. Inside has cool carved wooden chairs with some carved table tops. There is a mix of booths and tables, and thankfully they took the four of us (good sized guys) to a large round table in the corner.

The host was awesome. He seated us, told us our server would be right with us to get drinks and immediately brought us two molcajetes of their flavorful chunky homemade salsa and two baskets of warm fried corn tortilla chips.

There were three of four kinds of hot sauces on the center of the table, so we were able to add more spice as we liked.

Our server came over and took a drink order and gave us time to check out the menu, we was ok, just not very friendly.

Our one friend is a fairly plain eater and got the Trio of Tacos with ground beef, with just meat and cheese with the rice and beans; which came out exactly how he ordered them.

His partner ordered the Beef Chimichanga, which is a house special, while it was very good it is listed as having beef fajita meat and was made with ground beef.

My husband ordered the Spinach and Cheese Enchiladas with a poblano pepper cream. It was a nice size portion but lacked flavor not only in the spinach filling but the poblano cream sauce; it tasted like they forgot the peppers.

I opted for the Chicken Fajita Taco Salad. It was a nice sized fried flour taco bowl; it had very little lettuce but a good portion of the grilled chicken breast and grilled peppers and onions. It had some creamy refried beans, shredded cheese, tomato and sour cream. It was flavorful and enough for a late lunch.

Overall it was OK, nothing great, the fajita meat missing from the chimichanga and lack of flavor in the enchilada was a pretty big disappointment.

Too many other good local places, don't know that we'd rush back.

I guess I never head down 16th Street much, because I didn't even realize this place existed 'til yesterday - and I was pleasantly surprised, for sure.

I'm not huge on La Colombe, but the owner sure knows how to make his drinks.  Wow. Perfect cappuccino foam that I could only dream of making.  And the chicken panini I ordered, with mozzarella, roasted red pepper, pesto - YUM.  The panini was perfectly toasted.

I dig the atmosphere, too - big windows, pretty green walls, and comfy chairs.  Plenty of seating between the front and back room.  And the music - a strange but amazing mix of international and jazz tunes.  

Definitely a cute little spot.  No wi-fi, but it was nice to sit down in a cafe the old-fashioned way - with a book!  Imagine that!

Just as delicious as always! One star knocked off only for rude, chaotic service at the deli line during COVID. It was PACKED in there and social distancing wasn't well enforced. That said, the food gets the same usual 5 stars. It was so good that when I left with my takeout initially and grumbled to myself that I would not be back during COVID, I changed my tune after eating the Bubbe style matzo ball soup + salami + bagel and the super delicate, wonderfully made Mexican wedding cookies & apricot rugelach. 

To sum up - 5 stars for food. Get takeout. Expect your anxiety to tick up to high alert while inside. Drown your frustrations in authentic Jewish deli cuisine. Repeat.

This place is always crowded. The elliptical's and treadmills are the selling feature of the gym. I enjoy watching TV while doing cardio here. The rest of the equipment is cheap stationary equipment. It would be nice if they had better row equipment. I would say this gym is for people looking for a quick workout. Restrooms are poorly maintained.
Really good donuts. I mean seriously good. Lots of medical folks lining up and really great service. $$$ for donuts

It's so unfortunate, I really wanted to like this place. The ambiance and inside are pretty cool, much better than I expected. The potatoes are great. It's rare that I find a breakfast place that makes good potatoes. They got those right. But that's it. The food is VERY bland. I got regular pancakes, side of potatoes and tried the red velvet pancakes. My friend got chicken and waffles with scrambled eggs. Very basic stuff. We basically left a full plate of food. They do give you large portions so that's an additional star.

Hot Box is the best!!! For everything that do they do it well. Always a fun atmosphere and there are no better deals in town. Always great specials for customers from the Hot Box here at The Sun Bar not only on tanning, but aqua massage and foot detox!!

What an incredible meal! Our only regret on the drive home was not ordering more so we could try more off the menu. For the first time ever at a restaurant, everything we ordered was delicious. The funghi went the fastest, my favorite was probably the baba (cashew cheese-- and I'm not even vegan), and the kids were huge fans of the margherita. This is my absolute perfect pizza. We will be coming back very soon, and bringing everyone we know.

PEP Bowl is a gem. I went one day after work by the recommendation of a good friend who lived in the area. I never would have found the place without him. The vibe is very chill, and it is a really good time down there. They leave you be, and you can bring your own food and beverages as well. If you are looking for a neat dive of a bowling alley this is where you want to go with some friends to hang out. I will definitely be going back soon for another afternoon out with friends.

We were walking around after deciding not to wait 45 minutes for Village Whiskey, and after finding out we both like sushi (even if he doesn't like fish!) we stopped here. It was a little concerning that there was no one else in the restaurant, but after we sat down and ordered, another person walked in, and then a couple. 

I have to admit, I'm living in the suburbs, and so haven't really explored all of the food places Philly has to offer, but I was pleasantly surprised by Numa. I recently moved here from Seattle, and there are so many sushi choices there, I was a little frightened I wouldn't find anything good here, but this was definitely acceptable. The waitress was attentive (we kept needing more water after walking around the city all day) but not intrusive. The sushi plate ordered to share was a fine selection, and the eel was pretty tasty.

The decor is nice, and modern, as well as clean. Admittedly, I don't have know all that Philly has to offer for sushi, but I enjoyed myself, and the food.

I follow IHS on Facebook and have seen all the amazing things to help all kinds of animals find their forever home. They go above and beyond to care for them! I also encourage everyone to donate what they can to this amazing organization. Whether it's $1 or $100 - I can tell you it'll be 1,000% worth it to the animals in their care.
 
I adopted my shih tzu and it was the best decision I've ever made. IHS made it so easy and was definitely very helpful walking me through the process. Because of IHS, I have a new member to my little family.

Excellent service that provides produce, meat/fish (frozen), dairy and pantry products from local farms/producers.   Delivers to you porch for a fee (or free if over a certain dollar amount).   

The quality is better than grocery stores and some farm markets.   Order online and get delivery to your door.   

Prices are higher than the typical grocery store but in line with farm markets.

The name "Life Line" could not be a more fitting name for this business.  Over the last 2 years I have taken three different phones into the Green Hills location for diagnosis and repair.  Each time  I have been impressed. Unfortunately , my husband's phone was beyond repair but I felt like the technician went above and beyond to see if  his older model phone could be revived to save his pictures. 

Deciding to opt out of a contract phone, I bought one of my younger teens a used phone here and it is still going strong.  If you have a cracked screen or your phone becomes possessed with odd behavior this is the place to get help. A business that seems concerned about customer service and satisfaction is not easy to find.

I always come to this location to get my oil changed! Excellent service always. Lupe and the team were great and were quick and professional! They are always knowledgeable, quick, friendly, and professional. I will always come here to get my oil changed!
I have always liked their food. Flavorful, Huge portions, low prices. The bread sticks and garlic bread are soft on the inside and lightly crunchy on the outside. Big selection of food choices

I can't even begin to put into words how much I love it here - but I will try my best anyway! My sister had told me about this gem and how I needed to try it - particularly the salted caramel! I went 4 or 5 times in my trip down to visit family, and everytime I went I had salted caramel mixed with one of their other flavors - a win with everything! 
My first adventure was salted caramel with praline cream - the salted caramel was the star, soooo amazing, but the praline cream was a little lost in it. So I tried again the next day with Salted Caramel and Butter Pecan - that's the winner! These perfectly compliment. The NY Cheesecake flavor is also lovely.
Servers here are friendly, and Sunday is Karaoke - always a fun and delicious stop!

Hired Fence Pro to do a small job at my new house.  They responded quickly, gave me a great estimate and the job was done promptly.  I would highly recommend this company and will use them again should I need more work done.  Quickly work and no issues what so ever!!!

Classy, fun place; perfect stop for a meal and cocktail before seeing a show at the Straz.  We stopped by early on Saturday night. I had tried to get a reservation earlier in the week, but they were booked, so we just figured we would take our chances at the bar.  As luck would have it, we were seated at a high top in between the bar and dining room, and it was perfect.

Our waiter was spot on, with great drink recommendations, and was able to knowledgeably answer questions about the food. We started with a cheese tray - it was an assortment of three, served with crackers, dried apricots, nuts, and a small piece of honeycomb. For my meal I had the catch of the day, mahi mahi. It was perfectly cooked, very flavorful, and served on a bed of asparagus and mashed potatoes. Very good, although a bit expensive. I feel like the menu does need some tweaking; there weren't enough items for just a light meal. I'm still so sad I didn't have room for dessert, because they had a dessert cart that kept traveling by with some amazing looking options on it.

I only had one type of cocktail, but it was so good I ordered it twice.  It was called the Clover Club, made with gin, raspberry syrup, lemon, and egg white froth. It was phenomenal.  Don't let the name of the place fool you, though - they had an extremely extensive list of every alcohol imaginable, including some rare options in every category.

The interior is gorgeous, and I feel like they paid a lot of attention to fine details which made the place unlike most in Tampa: a formally dressed doorman opened the door and greeted us, there was a pianist playing in the lounge, stunning chandeliers were hanging everywhere, and they even had a live grow wall of different greens for salads.  I can't wait to return!

I'm from Puerto Rico, used to dance when I just younger, took 3 different Salsa Classes and owned a Dancing and Modeling School in the island and I have to say that I NEVER experienced a class like this ones, Erica is very detail, knowledgeable, funny and very nice to work with... the studio is very organize and LOVE how they run this business... Im already telling everything about the classes so hurry up and take the classes... Congratulations and see you next Monday

Lets talk about how I was here with my friends and family this past Saturday night. We purchased two premium bottles and were in the mood to simply have a good time. What became the headache ? The fact that my friends ID card never got returned to her and now she is unable to fly for a trip that we have coming up because it will take longer to receive her replacement card. The upsetting part is the fact that she had asked for her ID back two times after being settled at our table. For whatever reason the security guard wouldn't give it back because we were not yet "settled". I work on the industry so when my God damn table is sat and you have my payment already, THAT IS CONSIDERED BEING SETTLED. Hell ya we are upset that this is going to affect our girls trip. How many managers does it take to ask the security guards working that night about some damn ID card that was never returned ? Definitely left a sour taste in our mouths. Don't go here. Customer service is some type of wack. We gotta suffer for not having had her ID given back...

Really enjoyed our meal here. We had samosas, red curry, lamb korma, and chick peas. Everything was delicious and a little unique. Service was fast and friendly. I really wish I didn't live hundreds of miles away.

My boyfriend had raved about this place to me before, but I was a little hesitant to get my haircut here, as it looked as if they mostly did men's haircuts. However, I've been here a couple of times now and think they do a pretty decent job! 

BJ's is not too far from UNR, with parking spots available directly in front of their shop. I highly recommend making a reservation, because a few times they have told me they accept walk-ins, but upon doing a walk-in they always tell me they are booked for the day. The inside of BJ's is very mellow; they have a couch, and a few other seats you can wait at, as well as a mini fridge stocked with complimentary water and soda options. They also have this 'style bar' for lack of a better term, which is for the men to style their hair with gels and whatnot after their haircut is finished. 

My most recent stylist was Mimi, and I think she did a great job with my hair! My hair was shampooed and washed before being asked how I wanted it styled. After Mimi was done cutting it, she also gave my hair a quick blowout, making it feel light and soft. She was really kind, and engaged in light conversation. 

I believe the prices between men's and women's haircuts are different (?). My boyfriend has said he gets charged around $20, while I get charged around $25. I suppose it's not a bad price, at least for a women's haircut, since you can either get a low quality, but cheap priced cut, or spend about $45-50 (still just for a wash and cut) at a higher end salon. Overall, I recommend trying this place out!

Things are not what they used to be... I confirm the other reviews. Dilworthtown Inn was known for its fine and creative cuisine, the atmosphere, its amazing wine list and the great service. They are all gone...
We got married at Dilworthtown Inn many years ago and we had the pleasure to come back every or almost every year to celebrate our anniversary. 
This time, we found the flavor combination surprising and not refined.  The tables are cramped and the service was poor for a nice restaurant. We were asked to choose our drinks before we got a chance to look at the menu. We had to ask for water refill and bread. We never had the chance to look at the famous wine list... I'm not used to give bad reviews but that was a disappointment.

By far the best ice cream in New York. It's so thick and creamy and amazing. I would get one everyday if I could afford the calories. You can find their trucks by most parks. My favorite is the vanilla cone with rainbow sprinkles.

If you click on my user profile, you'll see I leave a combo of positive, negative, and ambivalent reviews. I am not one of those people impossible to make happy.

That said - avoid this place like the plague. We had a lovely apartment, yes. Great location, yes. But every possible chance management got to screw us over or ignore us, they took.

We are law abiding members of society - I'm a teacher for God's sake. It's not like we were bad tenants. We had several problems pop up (like ones that were several hundred dollars worth of charges) that we had IN WRITING responses about. Actual emails telling us not to worry, we wouldn't be charged, wasn't our fault, etc. Yet when the eventual bill came and we forwarded all communication, what we got was the equivalent of a shrug. Any time we would say, "Please just acknowledge that what we sent you is a confirmation from your team that we will not be charged," they would ignore it. We felt like we were being gaslighted.

Eventually we went over everyone's head to try and get a legitimate response and you know what we got? An accusation of harassment. This was one of the occasions where the person in the right just had to suck it up and leave without any kind of win.

The most ridiculous thing was when some lunatic went around the complex putting yellow flags in dog poop that hadn't been picked up. Sometimes they'd write obscene messages on them. We're talking, "PICK UP YOUR F***ING S*** YOU WASTES OF SPACE!" So, clearly, a psychopath. What did Jefferson do? Send out several passive aggressive emails telling he to make sure we picked up our dog poop because someone had TAKEN VIDEO of residents not doing so. So! Rather than address the fact that we had the Zodiac living with us and threatening us, Jefferson decided to try and, you know, make nice with the psychopath. 

Anyway - keep looking, you'll find an apartment not run by grifters.

Dollarama is always well stocked with a great variety of always changing items, this inventory is always presented in an organized fashion of being front, faced, and free of dust and dirt. The store is mapped out and categorized well, which makes locating what you need easy within the wide aisles. 

Sadly the staff always seem depressed, there is noticeably low morel, though a simple task, smiling and greeting their guests seems to be to much to ask. You will never hear a "thank you" or "have a great day" after making a transaction but instead have your bag pushed at you and ushered on your way out the door.

This is a Lao place.  Not a Thai place.  I love Lao food. It's a shame that Lao people have some sort of inferiority complex and pretend to be Thai.  When you get the papaya salad, make sure you ask them to make it Lao style.  Otherwise, they whitewash it and leave all the good spices out, and call it Thai salad ;)

This is by far my favorite place for Raspados and more!  I tried all kinds of place in Tucson and this place beats them hands down..... the elote(corn) is freaking awesome and the raspados are so fresh tasting and ice cream is delicious.  I'm glad I found it, we will definitely be back,

Overpriced Steak and Shake burgers with not as good as fries. The "funnel cake" is not like you'd get at the State Fair. It should be called funnel fries. We get to go out to eat once a week and choose here. We regret that decision.

Great hot dog. Tough buns!  The bun was fresh but not even the knife they supplied would cut through the outer side. It was way too chewy for my teeth. Also, I wish they had fountain soda with ice. I despise drinking soda from a can while dining in. If they'd just change the bun they'd have a great product. I enjoy hot dogs from QuikTrip much more. I won't be going back.

Priceline.com FALSELY ADVERTISES this as a 4-star hotel.  It is overpriced and desperately in need of a complete renovation.  

- Sent us to the 3rd floor, but the main elevator can only go up to the 2nd floor.  We had to walk to the end of the hall to take another old, small, and spooky elevator to reach the 3rd floor.  It's almost like a scene from a horror movie. 
- The room smells moldy and clearly have been vacant for quite some time.
- TV is placed on the left side of the bed.  Seriously, whats the point of having a TV here?
- Air conditioner sounds like a Boeing Streamliner taking off, and fan speed cannot be adjusted.
- There was a beeping alarming sound kept going since 7:30 a.m. in the morning. We called and they said they'll send someone to turn it off.  It is now 8:50 a.m. as I type this review and it's still beeping.  What a great way to start a morning.
- The hotel charges $35 for parking.  

I've had better experience at Motel 6.  Do yourself a favor and book else where.

Girl's going back to college tomorrow and Liberty was her call despite our recs for Russos, oh well, whatever my baby wants.... Artisanal pizzas are very good, but dude, $17 bones for a personal pizza?  We got 3, one apiece. BYO, that's very cool.  Excellent service, and a very nice room. Salads are pedestrian, underseasoned and no salt on the table.

You've gotta like a place that has good music, good coffee and tea and friendly counter people. (Their chai mix is right on the button with a tasty mix of spices. Mmmm!) But you really have to love it when you ask if they'll consider adding gluten-free menu items and they do so immediately! 

Tasty selections from Amaranth Gluten-Free Bakery include: gluten-free raisin bread (2 slices with butter & cinnamon sugar for $3) and trail mix cookies (4 for $3). Also to come: gluten-free coconutty granola with pepitas (just sweet enough and pretty filling) and Mexican Wedding cookies (a crumbly, almond-y, powdered-sugary delight).

I'm so thrilled to have a cafe right around the corner that is willing to accommodate me with gluten-free items. If you're out in University City, stroll on down to Locust and 43rd and eat, sip and sit in comfort and deliciousness!

Great building, super attentive staff, and a pretty decent brunch.  My partner loved his sweet role and the omelet. The non-stop chilled water was so welcome after a night of drinking in the quarter.  My black-eyed peas and greens soup, I just can't say enough.  It brought back some nice nostalgia from being a boy living in backwoods Louisiana.

The Eggs in Hell were a miss when compared to everything else.  Primarily the lack of seasoning.  It's just such a mild dish, for a main dish.  The poached (?) eggs were under seasoned, the ricotta is very mild, and the tomatoes lacked some "punch"when being advertised as spicy.  Not a disaster by any means, but not something I would hurry to order again.  

The handsome waiter was great (tall, slim, glasses), and we loved the music.  Will recommend, absolutely.   And I just have to come back for more of the soup.  Thanks for the wonderful new memories.

I would rather keep this place a secret cause I don't want the people in Acme's long line to come over, but I got to give props to this place. It doesn't look as fancy as Acme's but who's going for the looks? I eat for taste and the charbroiled oysters are amazing!

Tried out a new food spot in Nashville, at least new to me. 450 reviews and 4 stars on Yelp generally doesn't steer me wrong. My only chance to eat out for a nice meal while I've been here this week. BBQ rub salmon on a sweet mash with bbq strawberry ring on the plate, and a Castle Rock Pinot Noir to help with my cold a bit. Superb.    :)

Beautiful hotel.  Building was built in the 1930's and has been a bank and office spaces, but recently became a hotel.  Wonderful old touches were retained such as the old fashioned floor indicator over the lobby elevators, and the mail tubes built into the walls.  The bathroom door in our room looked like an old fashioned office door with frosted glass window like the door in a vintage private detective movie.  Control panels operate the lights and shades.  Safe and fridge in the room.  Our room was clean, neatly appointed, and had plenty of space for us.  This is an expensive hotel in a city known for expensive hotels, but we received a great deal through priceline.  By booking an unnamed hotel online we got our room for $140.00 for the night. Staff was friendly, efficient and competent.  The only quibble we had with this hotel was the outside noise.  You would think a hotel of this caliber would have better sound proofing.  We were on the 8th floor yet could hear car doors, back up beepers, trucks, voices and other street noises.  We will definitely stay here again if we get another great deal.

Bar service lacks, seems as though bartender is uninterested in serving guest , did not feel welcomed. Hostess was very pleasant.  Although our server was very nice, she was somewhat slow. Food was just okay, if it weren't for the decor and ambiance this is just another mediocre eatery.

I order the same food every time I'm here.  Coastal harvest salad was delicious as usual.  The bacon wrapped, goat cheese stuffed cherry peppers were back on the menu & they were delicious!   The short rib cheese fries are a must.  However they changed to crinkle cut fries and I don't like them as much as the original shoestring fries.

Good size dogs, but pretty pricy for a dog. I ordered the reuben dog and it was good, but after eating it and paying almost $6 bucks, I was still hungry. Two dogs and a drink for $15, ouch! Airport food is crazy about their prices. Pack your meal if you can.
If you order the cheese curds for $8 you'll get about 10.     Super sketch.   The service was pretty good.

Great food, but TOO pricey!
Tonight was my second visit to this Amma's restaurant. I ordered take-out again. Friday night... the restaurant appeared very busy. All tables were occupied. My take-out order was ready when I got there in about 40-45 minutes after placing the order.
I absolutely love their dosas, so I ordered a plain dosa crepe. It was awesome, came with two dipping cups with chutneys and a watery curry with rich spices.
I also ordered "Nattukozhi Ney Roast" appetizer, little pieces of bone-in chicken in a delicious thick masala paste. I had never heard of this item before, and didn't even know how to pronounce it, but the menu has these little chef's hat icons next to chef's recommendations, so I went by that, and was not disappointed. However, I wish it was boneless and white meat, especially for $13.95!
I also ordered the fish fry, which again was amazingly delicious. The fish was perfectly cooked, tender, and spiced just right. However, the portion size was a huge disappointment. I thought I was going to get an entire fish for $12.95, instead, I got only three little pieces. That makes it well over $4 a piece. Ridiculously high for little 4 inch pieces of fish.
I had seen a photo here of someone's fish fry that showed an entire fish, which was totally deceptive, because I only got three little pieces.
The food at this restaurant is really awesome, unique, flavorful and authentic. The restaurant is clean, service is great, and according to their website, the food is prepared without any artificial flavoring. But, the high prices will keep me from coming back too often, and for that reason, I'm withholding one star..

I really hope the new management can get their responsibilities in order.  Having the elevator being broken for over 2 months is a disgrace. Please rent somewhere else, it is not worth it to live Here.

I never frequent on this side of town and was very skeptical on trying this establishment...we had arrived here on a Monday afternoon. Nobody was there except for works. The restaurant is clean (with an exception of their food odor) inside and the staff were super friendly. We decided to eat here since we figured by the time we get home our food would be soggy. Well there was a good and a bad to it. We spent less than 30 mins inside the restaurant and we came home and had to take a shower right away. The smell of the restaurant was very strong...it stained our clothing lol. We smelled like Tasty's and it wasn't a good smell. Just felt so dirty!  Anyways, on with the food. We order...

Italian beef: does not taste like Chicago style Italian beef at all! and I've experience many Italian beef sandwiches while living in Chicago. The seasoning was very bland! The au jus tasted like watered down fat...had no flavor what so ever. The girl that took our order asked if we wanted spicy or sweet peppers. We replied with sweet and was charged 55 cents extra. Italian beef sandwiches come with peppers in general so why charge the additional 55 cents? Especially for a few pieces. 

Philly cheesesteak: this sandwich was average. Way better than the Italian beef...that's for sure! When I think of a Philly cheesesteak I think of onions, beef, and cheese. Well they lacked in the onions and cheese which is fine because the meat had some flavor but a little on the salty side. 

Chili cheese fries: OMG.....it's not fresh chili. Chili looked like it was a few days old. It was very dark and chunky. Not the soupy chunky but the chunky because it's old. The fries were average, probably bought from SAMs club along with the non flavored processed cheese. Fries were very hard. I know some complained about them being soggy but man the ones we had were super hard. 

One sign that just made me laugh. Condiments are free but extra condiments aren't....I understand it's a new establishment but to me the owner sounds super cheap. Charging for all the unnecessary extras. 

Overall, staff was nice, everything else not so much. We will most likely not return. 

PS: the menu is super small to read...even with my 4 eyes. I had to stare at it. And they do not have to go menus. Luckily I was able to glance at a menu via yelp.

This place is amazing! Great atmosphere, good and simple beer plus the inside is gorgeous. You can tell they spared no expense in getting it all remodeled. 

I can't wait to see some live bands soon!

This is one of my favorite places. Yes, it's loud and busy, but that's what I like about it. I was just there last night and had a really good steak. The only dissapointment weas my daughter's caesar salad. The lettuce was soggy.

I hosted a birthday celebration at Olio after visiting several times & loving the menu & atmosphere. We were able to reserve the private space near the wine room & it was perfect. The ambiance was excellent & the staff ensured all of our needs were met & exceeded. The food was delicious & I truly appreciate the Olio team going above & beyond for our celebration!

Got there about an hour before closing and there was still fresh hot food and naan being brought out to the buffet.  $11.95 for all you can eat (and all the mango juice you can drink) is a great deal made better by the variety and quality of the dishes out there!

Nothing was yucky, a couple things were forgettable, chicken tikka masala was standard.  But channa masala and tandoori chicken drumsticks were fantastic, and there were some kind of chicken wings that were lovely as well.  Vegetable samosas were huge and packed full.

Chutneys (we tried mango/mint/onion) were good too!

This place is a winner!  Bring your student ID for discount!

I knew before we went here that the fare was on the expensive side and that is fine. I don't mind paying but the food has got to be outstanding. Well, outstanding it was not. The bill was $115.00 for two and we each had two drinks. I got the branzino and the wife got a crab and shrimp salad. At $32 the branzino was just okay. I've gotten much better branzino for less (Argana Tree for example). My wife's salad at $20 something was a joke! Honest to god, if you're going to charge me that much for a salad please don't make it all lettuce. The crab and shrimp were hard to find and the dressing was bland. We will not go back.
It's actually really good. Yes I highly recommend when you're in the are. Here are my picture and ratings

I felt transported.  The food is absolutely authentic. We had the Zaalouk, hummus, Tajine Marrakesh, Tajine Fes and mint tea. All were delicious.  The staff is delightful and I loved that the owner's wife chided me for having the tajine with preserved lemons since I know how to make it, you. should try something else. I responded, not as good as yours and I will next time! Even the humble hummus had a special something and texture that was a cut above. Do bring your own spirits if you wish - as is traditional, no alcohol is served by the owners.  The decor is warm and cozy with lots of pillows on the banquettes and traditionally inspired lanterns throughout.  I will definitely be back.

Just tried on a whim for the first time tonight. We were served quickly and chose to do the walk up as it was a gorgeous night.

I had a nerds tornado which made me happy as I've been mad at DQ for a decade now since they got rid of the Nerds blizzard. My husband got a chocolate tornado with Andes mint. Both were good, nothing outstanding, but good. Custard alone would be 3 stars but the quick service and great ambience with outdoor seating got an extra star. They also have large portions!

We will go back to try again. Not sure it will be a staple, but it's close and exactly what I expect custard to be, nothing more, nothing less.

I came to New Horizon fresh from a hideous experience with a local storage business in which their absence of security led to a burglary I suffered. It made me really appreciate how much New Horizon is doing right. For the same money I paid before, I have a highly secure, clean storage setup with more space than I ever had. Easy to access, I feel very safe here, and I have enough room to move things when I have to.

This company installed a $37,000 roof several months back.  The roof has leaked four times.  They sub out their work and are totally unengaged with their business. They talk a good game but when it comes to being a professional organization they do not understand customer service. I would never ever use them again

I've never been a big rum fan but I LOVE THIS BAR! Not only was Enzo, the owner/bartender, knowledgeable about any kind of rum you can think of, he was willing to share that knowledge and really make you appreciate good rum. They have all different kinds of flights that cater to your taste. The drinks are also amazing as well! I personally like the scorpion, maybe because it's in fire, I don't know but it's a drink best shared with someone. Have to also give a shout out to the wall paper in the bathrooms! Overall a great place for a cocta

Pecan pie, napoleons, cannolis, ice cream, cookies, pralines....this place has it all and they do it well.  I had a stark raving amaretto ice cream that made me want to never leave.  The cannolis are fantastic and so are the spumonis.  This is a dessert wonderland.

We LOVED this place. What a great little gem! We were looking for a patio space on a Friday night that wasn't super crowded, I'm not sure how this place wasn't, but I wasn't going to complain.

Their whole indoor section is off limits and they're doing an awesome job of social distancing. Lucky for us, they had a Friday night tapping (I guess this is a regular thing!) so we got to try a Strawberry Kiwi beer which was awesome, and their other options were great as well. They had a wide variety to choose from which was perfect for us, I love sours so was stoked to see one on their menu.

The food was also delicious, I liked their small menu, but very specialized. You can see the guys making the food, it's extremely fresh. You HAVE to try the fried pretzel because I'm literally still dreaming about it.

This will definitely be a regular spot for us, affordable, great atmosphere, good beer - highly recommend.
Is it a pretentious "craft beer" Mecca or a neighborhood gathering spot? Probably both. At least it isn't AZ Beer House.

When a bartender opens with "I can't right now, there's going to be to wait" in reference to finishing a drink, you know it's not going to be a customer friendly environment. Either poor management, bad customer service, or both. That's just te bartender

Out server, Wasim (sorry if I misspelled!) nothing but friendly and encouraging. Knowledgeable up and down the menu.

The food here, is a marathon. You aren't here for an hour. For the full four course meal, which is delicious, it's about 3 hours. No exaggeration. 

The seating for large groups, is uncomfortable. There was a large divider that split our group and made it awkward for the one group of four to reach over to the other tops. Oh well.

The food, definitely is good. You cook it yourself of course, which is a bit of a pain, but understandable. Would I come here again? Maybe. It won't be my first choice, but I won't say no if invited

Amis has become my destination whenever I want to impress a guest or for celebration. The food here is always good and consistent, I've never been disappointed during my many visits. 

Last night for Dining Out For Life, my companion and I came here for a meal and the place was virtually packed at 7:00. I sat at the bar and had an amazing prosecco cocktail called Alto. It's amazing because it's mixed right and also the orange peel they put in there make the whole drink fizzes up like a fountain and perfumed the whole drink with its fragrance. One of the best drinks I have for a long while.

Amis is the place that introduced me to the combination of cheese and honey. Other Italian food and wine place have similar appetizers now but I will always come back here for their baked pecorino. It's like french toast but so much better. I just wish they offer more seeded toast for me to spread it on. I love their veal tongue, well, I wish it was a chewier cow tongue but the pepper mostarda was awesome, even the bed of spring onion that it was served in were great.

The "explore the menu" function is not as updated as often and when I fully intended to order the chestnut fettucine with duck ragu or the oxtail canneloni, they have already switched those items out. the spaghetti with pork jowl is quite awesome. The pork jowl was really tender. The pasta perfectly al dente. I have never had pasta this perfectly cooked before. NEVER.

Every restaurant has their own brussel sprout dish now and Amis' fried brussel sprouts is really a good addition. They are crispy like potato chips, well seasoned  and really, really addictive. I don't know if they are good for you but I'm telling myself that they are. We had to thank the very friendly waitress that recommended it to us. We also ordered a monkfish dish that was ok.

All the food and drinks were quite good and I imagine I'll be back for more again sometime soon.

Pleasant little shop hidden away in the farthest corner in a shopping strip off the State street. Frequented by the locals. Delicious European style pastry at reasonable price. Get an organic coffee to go with it.

Restaurant look clean and nice.
The food is just ok. The Exceptional hot pot was little blank. I expected little more flavor in the broth. Hopefully they will change next time.

Excellent service, purchased a healthy parrot  from them in 2018.  I go there for Grooming, excellent service. Would recommend this place to anyone. Owner is very knowledgeable . Bird prices are high! The owner will do all he can to help you pick out the right bird for you.

I had the fish and chips for dinner and found it very good. Light on the chips but very generous with the fish. Service was very good. The gal was attentive and passed my number one test. The beer delivery test. Had our drinks to us super fast. My only gripe which can't change is the noise level. Almost as bad as the Friendly Tavern.
We eat here frequently and EVERYTHING is delicious. We have never been disappointed. The help is friendly. The few times we've gotten take out, the order has been correct.

Beautiful location in sunny, SB! Sit outside on the patio if you can.  If you have to wait, take a stroll and enjoy the scenery! 

So, I wish I would have tried the breakfast, 'cause the burgers weren't all that.  Got the Santa Barbara burger...since when is guacamole spicy?  Sambo's - you could have warned me (i.e. write it on the menu description). Their burgers get an "ehhh".  You should probably stick to breakfast here.

They do give you chocolate chip & blueberry muffins - great way to keep one from scarfing down their food (i.e. me).  Not too shabby!

This is among the best Indian restaurants in Indianapolis, if not the best. 

I always come away feeling quite full from the lunch buffets, even after skipping the meat dishes -- meat-eaters should have even more of a feast.

What I like about India Sizzling is the (dare I say authentic) taste of the dishes. I had Moong Dhall Payasam today, a dessert which is a comfort food for me.

I recommend this restaurant highly.

The customer service was awesome and the people were friendly.  Family fence had the best price in my area out of all the companies we called.  They were super quick in putting up the fence and the employees were very nice.

My boyfriend dragged me here for Sunday brunch. I wanted to like it based off of the awesome interior and decent variety on the menu. The service was a little slow but we didn't mind too much since we were savoring our time together before a hectic week. I (hesitantly) ordered the finals breakfast sandwich. I'm not the biggest breakfast person so this was a big step for me. Our food didn't take too long, and everything tasted decent until I PICKED A HAIR OUT OF MY FOOD. And no, it was DEFINETLY not mine OR my boyfriends. So I probably will not be returning. Nice try peacock, but it's just not for me.

Had a bacon jalapeño burger and it was pretty good. Fries were just right. Chocolate shake was not your average shake. It was surprisingly delicious!!!! My parents were really happy with their food too. Waitress was nice.

My only complaint is potato skins were too mushy. But it could have just been a fluke. Will definitely return here. Loved the decor.

Everyone is so friendly and helpful, they got me right in, talked to me about my injury extensively, did some X-rays, gave me some medication for the pain and sent me off with a referral to an orthopedist and physical therapist!

I love the Piazza but  I understand why many people do not though.  A lot fear that it's taking away from Northern Liberties' original charm and I can understand that.

I like the Piazza because of the galleries, boutiques, restaurants, and open space.  It really is a nice setting.  Now with the new farmers market I love it even more.

I live in one of the apartment buildings and pay a really, really decent price for what I get.  I love being able to walk out my door and into the Piazza- especially in the summer.  There's always something going on.
Love their bah min! Grilled pork is how i order it. They also have a these light spring rolls. Clean eating all the way.

Clean & fast .. My daughter ordered the ham & Swiss & changed out the regular ranch sauce for just mustard & it was done correctly & efficiently. We also each got a small frozen lemonade, she got raspberry & I got the original for $1. They also have a tv above the fireplace.. Definitely cozier in here than before. We will frequent this location again for sure.

I was grateful that a fresh pizza was made for me in no-time-flat when an accidental topping was added. I appreciate the help I was given in successfully navigating a pizza that was safe for my #nightshade intolerance. Thanks for reading labels for me and your delicious pizzas!
Good service but the food was a let down. All the food was just mediocre at best. The crab cakes were horrible. But the atmosphere was nice.

I live in Nashville and go here all the time....definitely my favorite place around. I've never had a roll that beats the Fire Crunch. Great drinks too. 1/2 price certain rolls on Mondays....happy hour 5-7pm. The waitresses are never the happiest people around but that's easy enough to deal with considering the delicious food!

Me and my wife love this spot. Can honestly say we haven't had a bad meal, they have an amazing selection of beers, and their cocktails are can't-miss.

Highlights: Wings, fries (angry or sexy), and all of their fish entrees (salmon, swordfish, mahi mahi, etc.)

Great service, talkative bartenders, and never too long of a wait, no complains here!

The food...absolutely amazing. We started with the slab of bacon and it was fantastic. My boyfriend and I each tried a sausage and meatball dish, and split the mac and cheese. I have to say - my one disappointment was the portion size, but I realize you are paying for quality and not quantity.

Definitely save room for the warm cookie and ice cream, it was awesome!

I'm only giving this a two because they do nails decently. But I will not come back here because the PRICES CONSISTENTLY CHANGE. I was charged $90 for a basic gel polish (no tips, just my regular nail) and a basic pedicure (not gel polish, regular polish). They also charged me $10 for removing polish on my feet. I was fine with this, because they told me ahead of time I'd be charged for that. BUT the prices on their website and in the store are different than what they charge. They don't even tell you the total... they just ring your card. When I complained that the price was wrong based on their in store listed prices and online prices, they told me that they forgot to update their new prices and basically too bad.

I love Pizza Plus. We usually don't eat at this location but my wife's softball team is having an end of season get together over here. The food is fantastic as always. We ordered combo #2 which includes a large 2-topping pizza, half bucket of garlic Parmesan wings with the traditional carrots, celery, and steak fries. We also ordered a small gourmet veggie pizza. I could have finished all of the food if I didn't have to keep telling myself not to become a glutton!

So even after all of the good food, why a 1 star rating, you ask? Originally I wasn't going to include this in the review, but it happened twice on the same visit. I needed to use the restroom but somebody was in it, so I waited. An employee came out and went back to the kitchen. I did my thing and started washing my hands. As soon as I went to get soap, the dispenser was empty. It was so empty that not even a drop came out. I thought to myself "Hmmmm, that's kinda gross" and wanted to tell a person in charge. I wasn't able to talk to somebody without having to cause a scene, so I mistakenly brush it off. 

It's been over an hour now and I wanted to wash my chicken wing sauce fingers off, but again, the restroom was occupied. I waited almost 7 minutes until they finally came out, so I know they did their doodie, I mean duty. Again, it ended up being an employee. I went in as fast as I could to see if anybody even refilled the soap dispenser but it was still empty. I did a quick rinse and went out immediately to see if that employee went to the sink that they had in the back, but he never went. When I went to get a to-go box, I mentioned that there was no soap in the mens room.......

It's been 20 minutes since I've told the employee that there was no room and guess what???? Still no soap!!!! I don't know, maybe that's why the food tastes so good??? Beats me.

Incredibly classy place in the heart of Chinatown. The restaurant is bright, spacious and clean, and the service is excellent. The tables have heating pads on them, in case you order any of the shareable hot pot kinds of meals. I went for the clay pot Bulgogi, which had lots of beef and glass noodles, and was in a broth that was rich and had flavour points that reminded me of plum sauce. Meals are served with four sides: potato noodles, seaweed, kimchi, and bean sprouts. The menu is very details (indicating spicy, gluten-free, and such) and prices are on the higher side, but reasonable. I'm already looking forward to my next visit.
Consistently good food. Great prices, elegant surroundings. Tom yum soup, fresh spring rolls & the curries followed by green tea ice cream are our staples.

My family has been coming to Jack's for breakfast for as long as I can remember! We came again this weekend to celebrate after graduation, and Jack's was yet again delicious. 
Almost everyone got biscuits and gravy (a classic in my family), and they were SO GOOD! 
Service was great! The women were so sweet and the food was fast!!

I love my masseur, Andre. I have been going to see him for almost year now, coming up this summer, and together we have improved my severe, chronic back pain. He's simply the best! He asks questions about my needs at the start of every visit and always fulfills them. The establishment itself is a chain, and pretty middle-of-the-road. It's much less haughty than Hand and Stone feels to me, and the employees are always warm-- which I have NOT found at other chains. They've never been late on an appointment for me and are always willing to do any add ons I want on a whim! I hopped places for a long time before I found this particular group, where I have a membership and pay $60 a month for a top notch massage from a talented and super friendly guy. If he leaves this place, I will follow him if I can (he just works the best for me) but I saw others before him at this business and ALL of them were attentive and talented. It's a great hidden gem!

Update: Andre has now LEFT this location and I had a new massage with a woman named Dawn who was AWESOME and took care of me fully to suit my problems. She handled way more on a first visit than I even hoped for and I absolutely rebooked to see her again. I now give this spot 5 Stars for the professional way they handled my old Masseur leaving and hooking me up with the perfect new one!

Went on Christmas Eve 2009.  Got there early, which was good, as it filled up 30 minutes after we got there.

Clean, orderly place.  Neighborhood is a bit sketchy, so be careful walking, particularly on side streets.

Service was friendly and efficient.

Food was excellent.  I got spring rolls - fantastic.  Unlike any I've had elsewhere.

I got the vermicilli noodle bowl with various barbecue meat toppings.  Absolutely delicious.  Meat barbecue flavors I'd never tasted, plus some crisp and cold vegetables thrown in for contrast.  

Sorry, can't remember what my dining partner got, but he thought his was excellent as well.  

Very inexpensive, for what you get.  Again, very clean, which I really like in a restaurant.

Only thing I wasn't crazed about was the Vietnamese iced coffee.  It was expensive, for a small glass with no refills, and it was really no different than many iced coffees I've had elsewhere.

I would definitely go back.

Thank God for movie theaters, right? The little things I took for granted before 2020. Any way, I had my first couple of movie experiences here and overall it was perfectly fine!

The parking is a little confusing for someone new to the area like myself, but once I figured out where the lot is for the theater it wasn't too bad. Plus it's reasonable too- I think i paid $2 or $3 to park while I saw a movie. Free would be better, but the pricing could be a lot worse.

As some who before COVID didn't like interacting with people, I purchase my ticket(s) online to avoid the line, but here even if you buy online you still have to go to the main area to get checked in , which I didn't like. There is no usher standing near to rip your ticket or check you in. Once you walk in you have to go through one standard line which if it's busy could take awhile.

I don't know if it's a product of the pandemic, but I thought the tickets were really ,really cheap.

Of course I got my popcorn and soda. And free refills on my drink? I'm sold.

The theater was clean. I loved the chairs. I've been here two and one time the chair didn't recline. Oh, and btw, you have to pick your seat when you buy the ticket- it's reserved seating. The second time I was here I realized the seats were also heated and it reclined so double win. All I needed was a blanket and I'd have been fast asleep.

I still had to deal with noisy people sadly and a few folks who coughed so much I really thought they maybe they should have stayed home, but all in all, I was more than happy to be at the movies again.

i've experienced many tickets from my time in philadelphia. the signs are purposefully hard to read in order to make more profit. red light cameras and parking tickets are handed out like candy because this is a business.

Definitely a must! Tonight was their grand opening and there were no reservations available. Husband and I really wanted to check this place out so decided to take a chance with walking in. Figured we could hang at the bar. There was a wait but we enjoyed ourselves at the bar. Lycee mojito was the bomb along with apps such as the tuna nachos! The staff was able to get us a table. The bbq was ah-mazing! Service was ah-mazing! Price is really good too! I can't rave about it enough! Such a fun experience! Do yourself a favor and go!

This place sucks. On a busibess trip, bringing in 40 people on a conference and a party taking place beneath our rooms from 7p-12am impossible to sleep, front desk oblivious , police notified....wake up call 6am...  I want a REFUNd!!!outrageoys

4/5 great, friendly place. really like that patio!

i like the space alot. it's a bit of an escape and once i'm in the patio i don't even notice that it's right by the expressway. the folks who run the place seem really nice, and it's big enough that all the different folks can have their fun without it feeling like i'm being crowded. the drinks aren't bad (standard stuff), and i could happily spend a friday evening here after work.

Food was pretty gross, but I was so hungry I ate a bit of it anyway... I'd call that an act of desperation. 

Ordered thru grub hub, delivery time was accurate (about and hour at 9pm on a Friday), but that's the only positive thing I have to say.

General tso chicken was just slimy/mushy but somehow had very little sauce. They have those triangle shaped crab rangoons that were kind of stale and soggy (although the filling had a decent flavor), Probably the absolute worst wonton soup i've ever had. Shrimp lo mein was waaaay overcooked and the tiny shrimp were dry and chewy.

The food tastes fresh and not frozen.  I have been here twice and have liked everything we have  ordered. The dishes that have fried meats are not just fried batter but big hunks of meat so you actually taste the meat not just the batter. The portions are big enough to share and srill have some left overs.  The wait staff is also super friendly and quick.  One of the downsides is that they dont have a soda fountain but do have many different types of can and a couple different bottled beers to choose from.  The food is also very reasonablely priced.

This is my favorite Mexican restaurant.  The food is wonderful,  the restaurant is immaculate and the service is prompt and friendly (shout out to Vera). I am so happy to have finally found a great place to satisfy my need for authentic Mexican food.

A group of us came for dinner before our paint nite.  They sat us outside of the lounge, and it was 82 degrees outside!  Yes, they had ceiling fans, and a floor fan, but neither offered any relief to the heat and humidity.  I had to go move my car, as finding the valet in traffic was a non-starter.  By the time I got back up to the group, it was about 7-10mins, and my group had not even been offered water!  WHAT!?!  
Because it was so hot, we tried to find light food that we could still enjoy.  We had the Calamari Fritti $13, Antipasto Toscana $16, Melon and Prosciutto $10 and Valentino's Flatbread $12.   ...the Calamari was pretty decent, I could have that again.  The antipasto for me, the best was the herb goat cheese, pretty disappointing considering the price. The melon & prosciutto, that was disappointing - the rind wasn't cut off all the way, it was 4 small pieces of melon and lets not wrap the prosciutto actually around the entire melon, but just in one place.  The flatbread was pretty good.   Our sever was the bartender and it showed.  While we were doing the paint nite, towards the end we ordered dessert...they did not have lemon sorbet, they did not have Creme brulet ..what kind of Italian restaurant doesn't have creme brulet ready?   And of course I was not notified until the other desserts were brought out - so when I decided I would have the Tiramisu I was given some grief and it never arrived.

We will not be back for the food....just for the paint nite.

My kids absolutely LOVE being able to pick their flavor and how much they want.  And kids (and we adults too!) have a blast picking our toppings.  It's always a contest in our family who can come up with the best combos!  Definitely a fun choice for a family night out!

Slowest service ever, EVER! Ridiculous. Sub par sushi and lackluster everything. Portions are tiny and the fish tasted cheap and not fresh. 
There are much better sushi restaurants near by.
Great customer service. Michel Johnson and Walter couldn't have been anymore helpful. This was a successful 12th bday for my daughter bc of them. Thanks you so much!
Complete rudeness. I was the only customer in the place and asked for a menu. The guy pointed to a picture of a sandwich and said there's your f menu. Are you kidding? Totally uncalled for.

I went in earlier to pick up dinner. We ordered the pad Thai, appetizer sampler and another noodle dish. I was almost halfway through eating and saw a dead BUG in my pad Thai!!! Looks like a baby roach. Needless to say, I am absolutely disgusted and will never be ordering from here again. I will also be calling the health department.

Banko Overhead Doors installed the garage doors in the inventory home in Dec 2010 that we bought in 2011. So I called them on Thursday night to see about a service call being I could not get my car out of the garage. They told me it would be much more expensive as an emergency call and could have someone out in the morning. Like everyone else's house in Florida, frequent use and humid conditions cause the springs to wear out prematurely and break.

The next morning, I received a call from James letting me know that he was 30 minutes away. When he arrived He explained that it looked like the warranty had expired but was going to call his office and see what he could do to at least get the parts covered because The warranty had expired if you go by the date of install, but being it was a new home, they honored the warranty based on the occupancy date like some of the other companies did on our new house. He said that they did not last as long as they should.  He was very courteous and quick. Cleaned everything up. I will definitely use them again.

This is a very reliable place. Both their Greek and Italian cuisine  are delicious. It is also an exceptional value for the money and that you receive a very large quantity. Some items I have had are
chicken marsala 
spaghetti with Bolognese
Their Greek salad. 
All are fresh and delicious. 

For those who are tomato sauce snobs, I will say that their sauce is definitely homemade and has plenty of flavor. It is a bit salty and just a little bit sweet with very nice garlic overtones.  Definitely not out of a can!
If you are eating in just note that this is a decent place but not fancy in any way.  That never bothers me as long as the place is clean which this is. You may have noted from my tip that I noticed that the kitchen staff was not wearing mask and they were not wearing gloves when handling food. I did notice this on my last visit and that does upset me. However all my other review points remain.
The place is a mess. Food and trash on the floors. Most of the tables were dirty. We were told the pizza would take 15-20 minutes. It took over 45 minutes. This place sucks monkey balls
