A friend of my husband's raved about this place when he heard we were visiting NOLA. We ordered the spinach and artichoke dip as an appetizer and it was delicious. I ordered the gumbo and shrimp poboy. The gumbo was very good but my shrimp came out with a foul odor and an even worse taste. I could not eat it. I'm hoping this was just an off day as the restaurant seemed to be packed with happy patrons.

AYCE sushi is a good deal
 
service was excellent 

between us we ordered many different things:
- miso soup, seeweed salad, edamame
- nigiri sushi - tuna, octopus, sakura sake, hanabi, tobiko with quail egg 
- rolls - KitzMo, godzilla, yoshi, orange blossom

- MOST FAVORITE was the sakura sake 
- LEAST FAVORITE - octupus and tobiko with quail egg - both had too much rice 

will come back if in Reno

I must have caught these people on a bad night. I went for their $1 taco Tuesday and boy was it a bad experience. Started with horrible service. Waiter took forever to even replenish our chips and salsa and had an attitude most of the night. The tacos took forever to arrive and when they did arrive, they were cold. I was starving so I ate anyway, but the tacos were just bad. Didn't taste good and the meat was sub par. I will never return to this place. A waste of $6 and I felt like I was being robbed giving the rude waiter a tip.

What a strange experience! Lol I've been here twice and both times I felt pretty uncomfortable. I thought maybe I was being paranoid the first time but then it was even more uncomfortable the second time  maybe it was because I got there at 9 and they close at 10? When I walked in, there was a lady standing there that decided to just stare at me instead of greeting me or saying anything. Just stared. Since I dont like being stared at I walked over to the bar, mind you, they weren't slammed so I don't know what that was about. Anyway, i ordered food to-go after asking for the menu, and they have like 10 menus for whatever reason. Food was over priced, but I thought maybe it was worth it. It wasn't. The blackened fish was super bland. I mean how? Maybe this place is just for really rich ppl because I don't get it  Best of luck to this place!
Incredible food, great vegan options, one of the best veggie burgers I've ever had. The vibe of the place is also pretty unbeatable with the lights and live music.
Yummy cupcakes and great vegetarian fare. A little pricey but worth the splurge for what it is.

This is in the same plaza as Nekter Juice Bar where I got a Turmeric Smoothie, chased it down with a Ginger/Lemon shot & followed it with a pastry next door at La Segunda - not a bad lunch if you ask me!

CINNAMON ROLL (4.5/5): I stuffed my face with lots of pillowy bites - love, love the cinnamon in it. It's not too big or small, just the right size. It really hits the spot when I badly want a Cinnabon, but doesn't hit my wallet as hard for $3.95!
APPLE TURNOVER (4/5): It's not as sweet as I wanted it, a good thing because I need to cut back on sugar (Def. easier said than done!). There was plenty of minced apple & loved the golden top.
GUAVA PASTRY (4/5): It was messy with guava filling oozing out, so make sure you grab plenty of napkins.

SERVICE (4/5): Fast but after I ordered, at least 6 people showed up after me, so try to get here before the lunch rush! 

I washed it down w/a Jupina - I love the sparkling pineapple soda, especially paired with any pastry!

Cute cafe but needs improvement. No 3d coffee art and a lot of the drinks are just too sugary for my taste. We made a reservation to sit with the cats which was nice but it was towards the end of the day when many of them were tired or irritated with the kids playing with them.  I wish the person moderating the room would have better control over that.  They apparently bring in cats throughout the day which is stressful for cats which was also worrisome but they are still pretty new.... great idea and glad they're able to help cats find homes.
Ran into a couple issues where a couple things weren't put back like vacuum lines, missing parts, bolts not bolted down.  Etc. I'd write a list but the pictures I have says it all.

We recently visited Fine Palate twice during a,10 day period and have come to the unanimous conclusion that both meals were our favorites in 12 years of dining out in Philadelphia. From personal greetings by the chef, and his care and interest in providing an individual dining experience, to the friendly and attentive service and on to the food which was so lovingly and creatively prepared, Fine Palate pleases on every level. We will return often, and we urge others to give this relatively young hidden gem a try.

Wes recently helped me sell my home. He was very informative about the whole process, helpful in answering my questions, and strategic about getting through the sales process. I found his guidance to be essential, as he prevented me from making expensive mistakes, which is why I recommend him so highly. He could save you a lot of money!
I love Ulta..they have everything I need..This is truely a "girl" store..I love the atmosphere. .

I've lived in Tucson almost my whole life and how have I not known about this place? Weird I know.... but better late than never. Family and I have been here twice in the last two weeks to keep trying new stuff, nothing has disappointed us! Chili Rellenos are delicious! Love the sopapillas! Will be back again and again!
I came in to get 4 new tires for my car and was greeted by David. He took my information and got me back out on the road in less than an hour. The customer service is AWESOME!

Ive been taking my puppy here since i got her. They always take good care of my puppy. I will say trying to get through on the phone sometimes is terrible and they don't always acknowledge you when you walk in the door, just a simple "hello, we will be right with you" would be nice. But they take of my dog and thats what matters.

Our favorite Mexican restaraunt in town.  They have great salsa & chips (get the extra, hot salsa to add to it).  I like that they have whole pinto beans as an option to refried and the corn is an awesome side.  Echiladas are great ,as are the chimis.  This might be a little out of the way but definitely worth the drive to west side St.Pete. Plus huge portions, which normally I am not a proponent of in favor of quality but this is good food and warms up well.

The staff here isn't super attentive so if you want to order your food and then be left in peace to scarf it down, this is the place for you. Although it does get irritating when they forget to bring you the beverages you ask for. 

The sushi isn't the greatest quality in the world, but I'm not very picky about it since there is an all you can eat option and, in the case of quelling my sushi cravings, quantity definitely beats quality. There's a fairly decent sized menu for the all you can eat option and it includes a variety of cooked foods in addition to the sushi. If you choose all you can eat, there's a 2 hour limit. (My friends and I may have been very assertively notified by staff once our time limit was up, oops.)

I wouldn't go here just to buy a couple of rolls. It's very much an all you can eat experience and you should eat your money's worth.

Mostly bun, not much dog. I was excited to try this place since I love hot dogs. However I was pretty let down. The one guy working was gossiping with his friends and seemed almost annoyed when my co-workers and I came in. 

Because he was the only worker it took awhile to get our food. I got a buffalo dog, and was surprised at how small it was. My meal consisted mostly of the bun and seasonings. 

Additionally, the food wasn't that cheap. I don't think I'll be coming back here.
Not sure why they only had one bartender working when we went... but it was still cool to see the original spot. We're all huge fans of the movie :)
Yummy little dinner type restaurant. We had brunch and it was delish. The staff was super friendly and helpful. Lovely little downtown gem.

Franklin Abbey for drinks & a small bite to eat. It's nice English pub. Well set up. The bar staff was on point. Great selection of beer. Menu appears to have nice English fair. Just looking for something small, so we tried the thiggy pig skins. The worst ever.  The skins were freezer burnt, the bacon was like rocks, and the pork was very dry. The cheese was the best part. We will only go back for drinks.
My daughter is currently enrolled and we love it here. The teachers are amazing and the staff and families are great!

This place was so good! Have been here already twice since opening. Their O.J. is the best and the Vietnamese coffee was delicious. Was the first time I had ever tasted it. Staff was super helpful and always checking in on us to make sure we had everything we needed. In a great location, can work out then grab something healthy and delicious to eat! Am definatly coming back.

Another joint near Cheltenham/Olney that is as good as it gets besides Kim's BBQ, both places are fairly close and requires around 20 minutes drive from downtown Philly. Korean fried chicken, not to be confused with KFC of kentucky, is simply fried chicken wings coated in different sauces.

What's not simple about these chicken wings are its flavors. Although located in an inconvenient neighborhood to get to and slow service, we should still give it thumbs up for the comfort food Cafe Soho has to offer. 

Getting back to the food part, here are the recommendations after more than 10 visits...

Recommendations:
1. All the chicken wings! 
(Soy-flavored (original) are recommended to everyone. The spicy-flavored are pretty spicy and hot, so beware if you're not a spicy food person. Four season flavored are marked with white sesame on top and they are as good!)
2. The radish that goes with the chicken 
(These pickled radish cubes are most suitable while you have your chicken wings, totally relieves the grease and resets your taste buds)

Above-Pars:
1. Stir-fry Seafood Udon
2. Yogurt Soju (Calpis + Soju takes off the heat in all the fried food)
3. Seafood Pancake
4. Stir-fry Spicy Rice Cake

Sub-Pars:
1. Fried Squid and Fries (The squid had a weird texture although the fries were pretty good overall)    

The juicy, fresh, crunchy fried chicken wings are best when served hot and in store. To-go is an option but not very recommended since you'll be expecting a long wait anyways, why not eat it right away? Prices are a little bit on the high-end for fried chicken, usually around $25 if you only drink water with it.

In all, amazing chicken wings. Enough said, just go for the chicken and you won't be disappointed!

Family of 6 ate here on a Sat in Dec.  The restaurant was busy, but not to the point of overcrowding and the service was prompt.  The food was excellent!  Four had seafood, two had beef, and all raved.  Brussel sprouts were excellent too.  High praise for the desserts as well.  Will definitely go back

Have been there many times over the years. Had been disappointed in the service, acted like they were doing you a favor to serve you. Had lunch there today & the service couldn't have been better, so glad to see it. Food is always great

We were in SB for a weekend getaway.  On our roadtrip back home we wanted a good breakfast burrito. Read a couple yelp suggestions and decided on this place. WINNER :)

Location:  away from downtown and all the touristy places.  A true hole in the wall. 
As we entered we saw older Hispanic folks just drinking coffee and reading the newspaper.   Looks traditional and authentic to me :)

We got a chorizo, egg and potato breakfast burrito.  It was BIG. I split it w/ my hubby. 
It was DELICIOUS. NOT as oily as typical chorizo burritos.  The SALSA was great.  I got a  mild and a hot one.  It was a mix of salsa and pico de gallo.
I would have to say this is the BEST chorizo breakfast burrito I've ever had to date.  Good job :)

About $5.80 for a burrito- enough for 2 people for sure.

Orsini's Auto Body Repair Shop is excellent. I cannot recommend them more highly.
They are honest, trustworthy, reasonably priced, and do a great jonb. Len Orsini (owner) is a pleasure to work with and really knows the business in terms of price and timing. My repairs were done on time and on budget, which was hundreds of dollars less than a local competitor.
We have used Orsini for years and have never been disappointed. The Enterprise Rental a Car shop is right next door which makes it very convenient if you need a rental.

Unfortunately, when an owner is nasty, that means the business has extremely poor practices. I came in for a pedicure and overlay for my hands. The pedicure was fine, but the overlay became a problem. The owner (as well as one of the technicians) caught an atrocious attitude when I asked for clarification about the service and pricing. Moreover, the shop is just DIRTY. Apparently the owner is a Physician's Assistant also. And if that's true, I'm not sure how she could keep such a dirty shop, seeing that she would know that you could get a systemic infection from dirty tools. The salon would have so much potential if it weren't for her attitude and the lack of cleanliness in the shop. Long story short, spent your money elsewhere, because you're not respected at this salon. Furthermore, who would post a sign about tips? If the service is good, reminding patrons to tip isn't warranted. Boy does this woman have poor business practices, either way, I wish her luck and hopefully she'll read this comment and look at it as a way to improve.
The staff of the theater was wonderful.The ticket seller was friendly, attentive and answered all my questions. Watching the movie was comfortable.

Only giving 1 star because there is no option for 0 stars.
DO NOT go to the teen psych unit here. I was an inpatient there for 3 weeks back in 2012, and I'm not even from Edmonton, me and my dad had to fly there from Yellowknife and my dad had to stay at a friends house while i was admitted there. They treat most of the teens as if were all just bad kids with no legitimate problems, I felt as if i was being treated like an inmate in prison. When i was finally released, my diagnoses was that I was "provocative". Um excuse me? I wasted nearly a month of my life, away from my mom, brother, friends and pets, so you could tell me i am provocative? Just a few days ago I went for a psych evaluation at the mood and anxiety clinic in Calgary, and they diagnosed me with ADHD, moderate recurring social anxiety, past trauma, and major depression which was already diagnosed before going to Royal Alex  The doctors who just diagnosed me with this at the clinic said they don't understand how the ADHD could have been missed, and a lot of problems could have been avoided if it was treated a long time ago. If you are need of psych help I would strongly suggest avoiding this hospital at all costs, i would even say its worth the trip to the mood and anxiety clinic in Calgary for a much quicker and much more accurate diagnoses.

It sounded good on paper - a vintage theater laying dormant on N Broad getting refurbished and open for concerts again, with pit space and lots of seats. And yet it's hard to say The Met in its current incarnation has been anything other than a disappointment. I've been there 4 times - 3 seated and one in the pit, and while the venue has never prevented me from enjoying an otherwise good concert, my seated experiences have been less than ideal.

The big issue is that if you're not willing to shell out for the primo seats, you're going to be squeezed into small seats with no elbow room. There's not much to elaborate on that with - it's cramped, plain and simple. The stairs you have to climb to get to these little mini seats are steep and annoying, though I don't mind that so much on its own. The entrance/merchandise area feels similarly tight and narrow, making it easy to feel overly crowded and hard to navigate despite its small size. 

My experience with GA pit tickets thankfully avoided the problems I have with the seats. No real complaints there, but nothing great or memorable to distinguish it from GA concert experiences I've had at other venues in the city. Another key area I have no complaints about is the sound, which is fine but again not the best the city has to offer. 

Overall, I won't avoid an artist I want to see because they're at The Met alone - but I'm starting to get to a point where it does at least influence my decisions. Khruangbin is playing there next year and I find the tickets overpriced for the band, especially now that the show has sold well and most of the good tickets are at jacked up resale prices. I wouldn't have let that even cause me to hesitate if they were playing The Fillmore, with its spacious interior and crystalline sound quality. But to see a band designed to be stood up for and grooved to in a venue I dominated by cramped seating, where the highest Mezzanine seats are the price I think is generally appropriate for the band? I'm thinking twice...and three times....

If you'd have told me a few years ago that Philly would be getting a corporate venue trying to cash in on the name of two classic venues (Fillmore East & West) and that we'd also have a refurbished old theater, I'd have expected to easily prefer the latter. But here we are, years into both of their existences, and I think The Fillmore slays, eagerly awaiting bands I like to announce shows there. And The Met? It makes me sigh and mutter "alright, I guess..." when it gets similar announcements made.

5-1-18 Lunch for 3, 11:30

Parking - Excellent 
Ambiance - Excellent 
Staff - Excellent 
Quality of food - Excellent 
Ordered:
Angel Hair Pasta w/garlic & shrimp - Better than Excellent......it was perfect in every way. 
House Salad - Excellent 
White fish - Good

The best foot and back massage in Nashville. I get massages regularly, and I have paid $200 for an hours worth of service at other places and and not felt nearly this refreshed. I got 30 min foot reflexology and 30 min chair massage. Alice is truly skilled. Highly recommend!

Very helpful. I had a bad cough and mucus. Had gone through half a bottle of Mucinex and wasn't getting results. Dr. Gu gave me 11 different herbs to steep into a tea that I was asked to drink, for five days in a row. (11 herbs, five days' rations for each). The consultation was $20, and the herbs were $35. My cough and mucus are gone. 

Xie xie, Dr. Gu. :)

- Mills
I visit New Orleans two or three times a year. Acme Oyster House is one of my favorite restaurants to eat at. Great food, large variety and a great atmosphere.

This is such a fun concept for a business. Peanut butter and jelly sandwiches that you can make the way you want. Picking the type of bread, the type of jelly's, toppings, it really isn't just a peanut butter and jelly sandwich we used to take with us in our school lunch boxes. These are peanut butter and jelly 2.0 the new and improved version. There Instagram drew me in with the fluffernutter pictures. I chose the Texas toast, Reese's peanut butter, marshmallow fluff, and I think they drizzle chocolate on top. Why not right? It's all in the flair! Oh, they aren't done yet though! They then add it to the panini maker so the marshmallow fluff and peanut butter melt and it becomes a match made in heaven!! The side I chose was the strawberry smoothie bowl. Oh, that was so refreshing straight from the blender. It was really good! Give it a try if you're in The AMP! :-)

Alright........put on your cole haans, safari shorts and a THICK louis vuitton wallet....and you'll feel right at home at this place.  

Now, I love to cook - and I have been known to download a recipe or two from the 'Top Chef' website.   The products here are top quality and hard to find elsewhere.  I got telagio cheese, fresh pizza dough and black truffle oil with no problem.  For that I am very grateful.   I also respect the fact that so much is organic.  Having 'tried' to grow organic before, it is a very hard, long (I think it takes at least 3-4 years) and tedious process. 

But be prepared at the checkout.  I have never left this place with a bill less than $50, even if I'm just 'grabbing' a couple things.

I walked into the shop and Corey said, "What's up Josh?"  I hadn't been there in 7 months.  I walk in and get a personalized greeting?  
There are literally shops everywhere here.  Most want you in and out. If they see you aren't sure about buying, once that is made in their mind, they go about their business and tell you, "let me know if you need anything."  Corey in my few trips here has outshined every employee on this "row of jewelers."  

As someone who lives in the burbs but comes to the city fairly often, customer service in the city is secondary to curb appeal.  Look who we are takes precedent to how can we help you.  Here, this is not the case.  I had someone I'd only met 3 times ask how my wife was, how everything was going in general...

If you own a shop in the city and your staff is sub-par, send them here.  Let them shadow Corey for a week.  See the finished product.  

Praise aside, UCD should be your first and only stop down jewelers row.

I'm going to go out and say it. McGilin's is definitely a 5 star establishment.... Am I just going on a 5 star binge. That's a NO.  Here is why.

1) They have been open since 1860. 
2) They allow you to be crazy, but kick out people that just need to leave
3) they allow flip cup and other outrageousness
4) they had a hurricane party for Hurricane Irene in 2011
5) the drinks are cheap
6) i always feel like the people in the bar are LOCALS and there are 0 tourists or people from out of town (jersey) here.
7) great place to watch sports games
8) upstairs is my favorite
9) they do pitchers and they are cheap
10) they tweet back to you when you are there. LOVE.
11) great for big groups

the reasons go on... and so does this bar.

it's always there. and it's always fun.

Wow! We were sent here by a local and it was a great recommendation! The BBQ was amazing, as were the desserts. Super homey and awesome servers complete the package. I really loved the parsley potatoes, a must try. My husband is a hard one to please, especially when it comes to BBQ and he was over the moon. He gave lunch a grade A and then said the chocolate cake was in the top 5 for lifetime best cake. Try this place, you'll be delighted!

First Watch rarely fails. We love this place. 
The only thing I can fault them for is that my eggs were a little undercooked for my liking.
Everything else was on point. 
Huge props to our server, Ashley. This young lady deserves a raise. She was kind, helpful, and attentive. If I was rating this experience solely on her service, it would be 10 stars. I wish all servers had her wonderful disposition at 9:30 in the morning!
This will be a go-to from now on when we are in town.

This used to be my favorite place to hang out in Tucson.  But, the last 3 times I've been there to sit outside on the rear patio, the service has been less than average.  We were there for happy hour last night, and were served one drink and one appetizer.  The appetizer was served before the cocktails showed up (no big deal) but the wait was still long. Both items were good, but the waitress never came by again, even when we tried to get her attention.  Zero eye contact. Had she just popped over to tell us to go to the bar and order our own drinks, we would have done it.  By the time she did come over to our table, happy hour was over. Our friend that was visiting from out of town was very disappointed, since we spent most of our time trying to wave someone down to order more food/drinks. We complained to the manager on the way out, who said they "were working on it" meaning they were trying to get more help.  Neither our young server nor the manager, while apologetic, offered to comp us a drink or appetizer for our almost hour-long wait.  Sadly, we won't be going back to Hotel Congress when there's so many other good bars in the downtown area.
Pizza is a little sloppy and there's a random pepper slice on it but it tastes good, was delivered fast, and is hot, which is what really matters.

Lovely little spot just steps from French Quarter. Our group of 5 had a very tasty breakfast with lots of Italian-cuisine touches and was able to cater to a Celiac / Gluten-Free diner with ease. The red sauce is very good and don't hesitate use it on your egg-based breakfast!

I've been travelling with Gate one since 2006, and every trip has been a wonderful experience. With gate one you have the option to tour on your own or stay with the group. Gate One has every detail covered, from hotels in the best areas to explore, airport pickup to excursions.
My latest trip was the 16 day Thailand with Phuket and it was the best trip by far. Our tour guide Tuk made our trip memorable, she was so hands on with the entire group and she made sure we were all taken care of, she stayed up late nights and was up early in the morning with us each day.  
I couldn't ask for a better trip.
Dirty.ripped seats small movie theater.  Popcorn was good.  The movie had been spliced so much. Horrible acoustics.  Hard to walk up to theater.

Came In for pedi. Beautiful space. Missed having a massage chair. Young tech was very chatty with employees and talking about social life.  A lot of chatter amongst all young nail techs. Not a spa experience. Will go back to my regular place. Glad it was 20% off the service. Wouldn't recommend if you are looking for a relaxing experience.

The first thing you notice on entering the restaurant is the decor, it is amazing. From the basket weave ceiling to the grain light covers in front of the kitchen area, the effect is wonderful. Our food was also very good. We had drum fish with crawfish étouffée and lobster dumplings along with mac & cheese with greens on the side. Everything tasted wonderful. The only thing preventing me from giving five stars was the not great service. Our server appeared to be out of sorts and gave barely adequate service. I don't know if this is typical, but except for that, I highly recommend Emeril's.

I love Noble Hops for their beer selection. They usually have twenty something taps of great craft beer, some of which are hard to find elsewhere in the area.  Their wait staff are always friendly and helpful.  Service can sometime get things wrong.  We've had some incorrectly prepared food and mixed up drink orders.  But they're always quick to fix and hiccups and are eager to please.

Outstanding! My wife made reservations for us to celebrate my birthday with a nice kids-free dinner together. We arrived 15 minutes early and were promptly seated and greeted by hostess and waitress with birthday well wishes and a birthday sign. 

We shared Maryland crab cakes and crab wontons as appetizers and both were excellent. For the main course I had wild blackened grouper with sweet corn risotto that were also excellent. My wife ordered the blue cheese stuffed lamb burger medium that is served with fries and 2 sauces. She raved about both! And enjoyed her samples of the grouper and risotto from my plate!

We were provided a complimentary dessert for my birthday so I had the Ghiradelli Brownie Sundae and my wife had the creme brûlée. Absolutely delicious! 

The atmosphere was great, beautiful, fun decor, I'm a large guy and the tables felt spacious. Service was excellent. We enjoyed the upscale fine dining with more of a casual feel. Food and service delivered without the stuffiness of some upscale higher end restaurants. 

Keep up the great work! We will be back to explore the rest of the great menu!

To me, there are three things that make a restaurant great, the food quality, the service, and atmosphere. This restaurant has two of the three things, great service and wonderful atmosphere. 

The food was merely average, however, the service and atmosphere made up for the lack of deliciousness. We ordered a few appetizers, which were actually better than the entrees. 

I ordered the chicken and it was ok, it tasted a bit dry and not fresh. I actually had to season it with black pepper and salt.

Nice atmosphere. Arrived for 6:45 reservation and sent to bar to wait. After 10 minutes of being ignored, waved my hands and got service. Another 5 or 10 minutes go by and table is ready but no drinks or bartender in sight. Assistant waiter brought water to table immediately and waiter appeared with drinks. After almost 30 minutes of no waiter appearing we flag down a waiter who apologized and who graciously took our orders although he was not our waiter. Two of us are vegan and ordered the only vegetarian entree which was soba noodles and very very disappointing. One of us ordered filet which seemed okay but overcooked. Between the terrible service and nonplus food, I cannot recommend this place to anyone. It has received lots of hype and some great reviews but suffice it to say the service and food are variable. I suggest vegetarians, especially vegans, avoid it altogether. And no one should go there who has any other plans after dinner as service can be very slow.

This place took us in at the last minute and we had superb service! We told our waiter it was my Birthday and  he surprised us with an upgraded sirloin steak, instead of 8oz he gave us 12oz Not sure if they do that for everyone, but if it's your birthday, it doesn't hurt to let them know The food was to die for!! Seriously, the pasta was literally melt in your mouth foodgasm good. Make sure to come hungry and with someone to share! You'll want to try as much as you can! Everything here blew us out of the park. The scallops, sirloin and mushroom butter sauce on the side was so good that I still have dreams about it.

We just had lunch here and it was great.

The customer service was outstanding! They had plenty of people on hand to handle the lunch rush and the manager came by to get our input and took genuine interest in our suggestions.

Our group had the lean brisket and jalapeño cheddar sausage. I won't repeat what the men in our group said about the sausage, but will say they really enjoyed it (a lot)! The green beans were really good, as was the potato salad. The cornbread had a good flavor, but was dry. The baked beans were also tasty, but being from Texas I would have preferred pinto beans. They could also offer pickles and onions as sides (again, traditional in Texas).

Overall, would definitely recommend!

We tried Chihuahuas for the first time after a coworker recommended it. I will definitely go back. The food is amazing, the service is very good. Definitely the best Mexican food in the Reno area as far as I'm concerned.
Great Hookah bar!!! Food is amazing! Do the appetizer sampler platter. Try the apricot coconut mint hookah. Val is an amazing server. Def try this place!

Saw plenty of mixed reviews, but this place was close by and decided to check it out.

Prices: decent, low prices

Overall cleanliness: dirty...

Overall service: staff wasn't really personable. They greeted you as any nail salon would but that was pretty much it. And they pretty much rushed my daughter and I out the door as soon as they were finished..

Pedicure: did not receive a good pedicure

Manicure: did not receive a good manicure, nail paint chipped/came off the next day
The pupusas were fantastic. As good as any I've eaten anywhere. Definitely the real deal.
So far, so good. Simple selection in a clean and crisp environment. Great service at the bar. The burger is great! Nice job.

This review is for their Pharmacy. I had to take a minute to rave about their service and communication. Two weeks ago I had an awful experience at the Walgreens in Wyncote. This Target/CVS could not be more opposite. The pharmacist, Frank, was incredibly kind, smart, knowledgeable, and funny. His communication was spectacular. He called me to let me know my prescription was out of stock, called the pharmacist to make a correction for me, told me when it would be in, called me when it came in (ahead of schedule!) and when I picked it up, I was in and out in less than 2 minutes. The medication was also substantially less expensive than it was at Walgreens. I can't speak highly enough about their operation, and Frank's customer service!

Charbroiled Oysters...Charbroiled Oysters...CHARBROILED OYSTERS! Do NOT order anything else 

The end.

p.s. We weren't that hungry but we wanted to try the charbroiled oysters we heard so much about so we decided to order a dozen. It was soooo good that we ordered another dozen and stuffed ourselves to maximum satisfaction 

Ok now really the end.

Fantastic service, everyone was fun and friendly.  I had a great time and my nails are awesome!! 
I was most impressed by the amazing care they took with a veteran recovering from foot surgery...that was the true reflection of the wonderful humans that work there.
This is the type of small business that makes our country such a great place!  Good people providing a great service with empathy and compassion.
Everything was excellent! Good food great atmosphere, good service.  Most meals are over $20 and dress a bit more than casual.

Kinda cool with parking on the first level, then take an escalator up to start shopping or have a meal.  I've only been here to have lunch so far so can't comment on much else.  I would definitely recommend the Swedish meatballs; they let me sub mac-n-cheese to replace mashed potatoes.
Stayed in the Hilton right across the street and ate here every morning! Good food and always busy. Try the coconut syrup!

I once unconsciously patted my belly after dinner here and didn't realize until my husband made fun of me. Here's why. Fatty brisket (ask for fatty and ideally 1/2 lb per person) + white cheddar cracker mac and/or beef fat fries + popover + their mustard sauce and their spicy sauce = my go-to meal at salt and smoke. I've got the formula down pat. 

If the above math or if fatty meat isn't appealing to you, then you really shouldn't read my reviews. Salt and smoke is known for their brisket. Unexpected for St. Louis, right?? But so thankful we have access to incredible ribs AND brisket in St. Louis! When people are between meat selection here, I'll always steer them towards the brisket. Or just try them all. Their bestie combo is great for first timers who will undoubtedly have a great meal and get to try various meats and sides. As for me, I stick with my formula now and I leave so happy each time.

Service is always friendly and fast. Bar service is equally great.
Fun restraint with good food and ambiance. Great lunch place and even better at happy hour.
Best Cajun in town! I always get the pulled chicken wrap. It's so good, but massive and way too much to eat for lunch.

Oh man, this place oozes with class.

West Philly does not have many destinations for dining with a stylish, hip decor - particularly along the Baltimore Avenue corridor - so the Vietnam Cafe is a welcome addition to the scene.

I've always been a regular at Fuh Wah for their tofu hoagies and other assorted goodies, due in part to the proximity to my office - so I knew any restaurant affiliated with the same family had to be good.

And that it was.  The spring rolls were delicious - they even cut them into smaller pieces that were easier for sharing.  The summer rolls, a personal favorite of mine, were also nothing short of fresh and tasty - my only complaint would be that they were a bit on the small side, in contrast to the gigantic summer rolls you can get at any of the Green Line Cafes from O'Sandwiches.

We all ordered a variation of the vermicelli dish, chicken for me, pork and beef for the others.  The meat was prepared deliciously - the chicken not too dry, the pork crispy, and the beef plain old yummy.  Piled on a big bowl of vermicelli and lettuce, it was complemented perfectly with the dressing.

Attentive service - yummy food - and a sexy ambiance - this place is perfect for a date or girls' night out.

We tried the lunch buffet here and the food was below average. I expected good food as it is one of the main Indian food restaurants in Reno. The service is good and has pleasant ambience but the food is not quite appealing.

I have been a patient in this office multiple times over many years for different procedures and always had great experiences.  The office is tidy, clean and inviting with convenient, plentiful parking. I have never had Dr. Mackay as my service provider but the various physician assistants who have performed my treatments are very knowledgable, friendly and efficient. Front office staff are friendly and I have never had more than a few minute wait for my appointments. I would definitely recommend this practice to friends & family!

Used to come here when it was Huck Finn, been here twice this week, and they still can't bring you a soda.  They say it's the carbonate, and the guy fixed it once already.  My question, how do they serve mix drinks at the bar, without soda.

Probably the best chinese food we have had in Tampa.  Never have tried the original Yummy House on Waters, but have been to the new one on Hillsborough across from Sanwa.  Very classy, upscale decor and napery, surprisingly located in an industrial, low-income neighborhood.  Far different from street-front cramped to-go shops, or acres of mediocre buffet.   Very attentive teamed table service, with slight language challenges, but note a high percentage of Chinese/Asian customers as testimonial of authenticity.  As noted by other reviewers, the salt & pepper apps are excellent, as are the other standard dishes.  Went first time with our son, who is now hooked on their food to go.

5 stars for the food, 3 for the service. I figure 3 is a good compromise.

I thoroughly enjoyed my food. All of the food, especially the oysters, were delicious. There will probably be a line, and that's okay. Wait in the line--it's worth it! I had both raw and grilled oysters. Fresh, cleanly shucked, delicious. Dining partner's Etouffee was flavorful and I almost regretted not ordering it for myself.

Our waiter did his best with what he had to work with--a bartender who ignored him multiple times. I watched the waiter demand our drink orders throughout the meal, and the bartender would wave him off and would go talk to people or otherwise occupy himself. Our meal was all but through when we canceled our drinks. The waiter went behind the bar and made them himself and brought them to us, complimentary. It wasn't necessary, but the gesture was very much appreciated. No manager was visible to address the situation.

Restaurant is loud, but just know you're walking into that! Very clean facility as well!
Amazing atmosphere (mom and pop).  Food is excellent, the coffee is amazing, and the personnel are extremely courteous.  We're just moving here, and I think we've found our favorite lunch stop!

THIS PLACE IS TERRIBLE! The customer service was the worst part about it, they ruined my order, then yelled at me when i complained because my food was ice cold and completely wrong, they are immature and unprofessional and should not be legally aloud to run a restaurant.  NEVER GO HERE!!!

While visiting New Orleans, my friends and I came here for breakfast twice! Let's just say, if I lived in NOLA, I would come here at least a couple times a week. This place is a true neighborhood gem and I highly recommend it!

This place is absolutely delicious! I had French toast and a biscuit the first day (see pictures) and scrambled eggs, home potatoes and a biscuit the second time. Everything was delicious and cooked just right! The biscuits were to dye for and my mouth is watering as I reflect on them! My two girlfriends that I came on this trip with also loved their food! I understand I am from one of the most expensive cities to live in (San Francisco), but the prices here were unreal! $6 for 2 eggs, tons of potatoes and a biscuit?! Unreal! Their coffee here and bloody Mary's were amazing! I loved how the waiter gave us coffee to go, without charging extra or any hesitation - great service! 

The decor here is very down to earth. I love how they have tons of local art all over the walls. I also love how although they were trying to play jazzy music in the restaurant itself, you can hear Drake blasting from the kitchen.

This review might be TMI: So, I shop here often as their marketing campaign "free panties" coupon really works! At least once to twice a month they send something in the mail that tells you to come in to choose your free panty.  So I come in and always get lured into purchasing something even though you don't have to to use your coupon.  As many times as I've come in and purchased something at the other stores they let me go on my way. For the 2nd time ever I was asked if I wanted to be fitted so they can get me into a comfortable size.  I was just measured about 6 months previous at a different location but I figured what the heck It couldn't hurt to get a second opinion. She put me in a different size very different to the previous specialist and I've never been so comfortable! YAH for 2nd opinions and someone who knows what they are doing.  

To get to this store park by Johnny Rockets at the main entrance and you're almost there. No need to walk around the whole mall if you don't need anything else.

We looked forward to a special dinner here, but were greatly disappointed. From the moment the host greeted us on the 2nd floor reception area our experience went downhill.  The host was not friendly or even courteous, followed by our server not being knowledgeable of the menu or good at making suggestions or being attentive, and the food was unremarkable.  The crab dishes were uninteresting and the bread pudding dessert which is a specialty was almost inedible because the bread was too tough to chew. The room was extremely noisy to the point of not being able to hear each other at our table. We had much better experiences at other restaurants. Years ago we had a great dinner here, but obviously they have lost interest in being a fine dining experience.
Good food made even better with Zydeco music night's at 7. Really fun place. A big, roomy restaurant. Should be $$$, however, but I'm not complainin'.
$5 tickets! That's a great deal. Movie theater is large (20 screens) and has the reclining chairs that are more comfortable. Also, this place is well decorated. I loved my experience here.

The Brewhouse is a real micro brewery that serves up AWESOME food.  Not just 'pub grub" that you're probably used to, but real food, uber-grind.  They always have 6-8 beers they made right there along with a solid selection of imports.  Pete, the Brewmaster does not mess around.

Some favorites dishes of mine include the Gorgonzola Salad with candied walnuts, the Mixed Grill with lamb, pork, sausage, filet mignon, chicken and shrimp (oh yea), the Pork Chop with an apricot chipotle glaze, and the Salmon with a raspberry habanero sauce.  

Their dinner specials are great to, Prime Rib Mondays ($17), All You Can Eat Pasta and salad Tuesdays ($12), and the "mother of all mothers" - All You Can Eat Ribs on Wednesdays ($15).  Starts at 5pm and they WILL run out of ribs so don't come late.

Did I mention that they usually have live music on the weekends?  No, I haven't yet.  Well they do, and it's usually pretty good.  

I love the Brewhouse.

Auf.
I love this place.  We got our fur baby there.  Employees and Volunteers are very nice and knowledgeable.

These guys were awesome.  They moved me from Rittenhouse to South Philly and I had to reschedule; they made it easy.  They called me the day before to confirm and then again that morning with an ETA.  They were 40 minutes late, but obviously I wasn't charged for that time.  I had a few scattered items I hadn't had time to pack but I had some boxes in the corner.  They packed my items for me so I didn't have to make another trip!  They were so fast.  I cannot believe how fast.  All polite, friendly and professional.  One box that had been turned on its side leaked a bit but I probably could have packed it better.  They give you a 5% discount if you pay in cash.  Won't ever use any other company again!

Shady Brook is a mixed bag, steadily rolling downhill. There's a greenhouse/nursery, upscale farmer's market/gift shop, surrounded by playgrounds and fields. Everything seems overpriced, except for maybe the basic, seasonal produce items. If you're looking for specialty foods such as handcrafted jams and sauces, you'll find a wide selection here, but be prepared to shell out for it. There's lots of interesting items to browse and Uncle Dave's pumpkin ice cream is excellent.

Many weekday customers work in the numerous office buildings within walking distance. The deli counter ought to realize they are on their lunch breaks and step 
up service. I've seen people walk away and just grab a pre-made sandwich because their order is taking too long. The cashiers look like they're barely out of high school and really have an attitude. There wasn't enough salad dressing left and the guy basically told to me to ask somebody else. He could've at least called the back room for me. Another young-looking girl asked me what she should name her band. Yet another teen complained to me that someone else told him he was too slow. "Do you think I'm slow?" he kept asking me in an intimidating manner. Just ring my food up please, preferably with a smile.

I don't go to any of the events/hayrides, lightshow, etc. because they are too expensive.

Based on my last few experiences there, I wouldn't write this place off entirely, but I certainly won't be back soon.

La Va is a great little neighborhood spot with comfy couches and lots of tables to sit at.  The couches have large tables with magazines on them.  Decor is pretty in that old world kinda way.

I have been here several times for coffee, they have La Colombe so I know what to expect.  Today I tried the Mexican Spiced Mocha, which is still to this day one of my favorite froo-froo coffee drinks.  I tried it over ice and it was delicious.

When I was a kid visiting Germany, I used to order the Wienersnitzel even though I didn't even really know what it was.  All I knew is it was tasty and had a cool name.  For my first time since Germany (like 15 years!) I saw Snitzel in the States, at La Va.  They had two options, a platter or a sandwich.  I opted for the sandwich, which had garlic ranch, feta, spinach and the beloved breaded chicken from my childhood.  It was pressed on a paninni grill and served with a mediterranean salad of cucumbers, tomatoes, lettuce, olives, and a light vinaigrette.  Perfect lunch and a relaxing atmosphere to enjoy it in.

This was her first experience at Brennans and I have to say it was the best experience we had at New Orleans the staff was amazing and the environment was amazing but none of that holds a candle to the food we were able to have at our table!! We ordered coffee and breakfast or brunch items in each one was simply amazing at some point we thought we would play chair until we realize we were willing to give ours up to anyone else and after our main course then came dessert first time for That dessert banana fosters that originated here at Brennan's they bring out a portable cooking table and they create the banana fosters right in front of you most amazing the other desserts were equally as good this one is just their trademark the banana foster is the trademark

Okay guys and gals.  The old chef is back from what I hear.  My fiance, myself and 2 other couples stopped by.  We were not disappointed.

The decor and music are very North African.  You really don't feel like you're in the States when you're here.  The staff is always smiling, very friendly, and willing to explain anything to you if you have questions.

Our friends enjoyed the Sultan's Feast.  My fiance and I ordered from the a la carte.  Everything was great!  The Sultan's Feast is a LOT of food.  Make sure you come with an appetite if you go this route.  It's priced nicely as well.

Their drinks are tasty and the fact that this is a BYO with a beer store and liquor store in the same strip makes it very convenient.  Check it out and enjoy it!
great comedy show, I went to a show in September at Verde Restaurant and really enjoyed it.  The show was not dirty like some of the shows out there.  Highly recommended!
They cut my dogs hair perfectly. I'm a very picky dog mom and it looked awesome!! Here's a picture for evidence. I will definitely be returning!!

What a night! A perfect venue for a lively family supper. Our out-of-town cousins had a blast, as did we, at this festive, fun filled restaurant. Plenty of delicious food, tasty year, hilarious music/entertainment, and fantastic service.  Our server, Jamie, totally rocks! Couldn't ask for more efficient, friendlier service.  She kept the beers coming, made sure our food came out hot off the stove, and even led us in dance moves.  Our party of 10 (ages 14 to 57) had a wide variety of dishes, starting with the Sausage Tower, then entrees of chicken salad, veal, pork, beef, hamburger. Everything was great. Best potato salad ever!!

DE-licious.

great atmosphere, surprisingly good acoustics, sublime food. 

the huevos rancheros were ridiculously good, esp. with the red pepper (aioli?) sauce. i must note, however, that although i love roasted red peppers packed in olive oil as much as the next gal, a whole container's worth under my slightly undercooked eggs (not my preference, specifically asked for over medium), is waaay too much. (another side note: black beans are not available, but refried beans or a white bean puree are options). no less, it was a fantastic dish, great flavors and beautifully presented. 

btw, you're going to love the homefries, so order a side in advance as the dishes only come with (literally) 5-6 pieces. 

my girlfriend (and far less critical dining partner) really loved her vegetable(?) frittata. a lot. she claimed to dislike frittatas before our visit; she's now a convert, thanks, cafe lift!

oh! i should include the really friendly folks next to us who allowed me to taste their hollandaise sauce, which was a real winner. he ordered the eggs benedict whereas i will (in the soon-to-be future) order the florentine because the super salty ham undercut the flavor of the hollandaise sauce significantly. nonetheless, he was awfully nice to share!

the 3 star rating rather than the 4 was because of service. we had a travel mug of coffee with us because we cannot and do not leave the house without it. since i had reached my pot and a half coffee allowance for the day, i proceeded to order water. my girlfriend did the same. our waitress asked that we order coffee as opposed to drinking out of the mug. ok, fair enough, makes sense.

the annoying part was that she never returned to fill it! several attempts were made to get her attention from out of the clouds and into our empty mug, sitting ever so sadly on the edge of the table, and yet nothing. she stood blindly against the wall, staring into the middle of the dining room. this was really aggravating for me. again, my less critical gf was much more understanding and easy going about it even though she became a little irritated after (again, literally) 15mins of no coffee.

the poor service (esp. because of the time she spent standing against the wall rather than checking on her tables), will only have me ask for a different server if sat in her section. otherwise, we're going back soon!

I have been going to Dr. Larson for years. He and his staff are amazing. The front desk is always helpful and works hard to find time to get you in when it best fits your schedule. If you are a long time resident or new to the area, I highly recommend Dr. Larson and staff to take care of your teeth.

We love this place.  We eat here weekly.  The menu is plentiful with comfort food and healthy options.  The weekend specials are my favorite.  The drink menu is fun and delicious.  Serving sizes are generous.  The staff is amazing. Friendly, knowledgeable and attentive.  The owner greets everyone and when busy it is all hands on deck.  They hustle.  We have never had a bad meal and family friendly!!!  It's why we keep coming back.

So much better than a hotel! I needed a conference space for organization's small annual conference of 150 people. I decided to try the Hub (Commerce Square) and I'm so glad I did. The staff are incredible--professional, yes, but also happy and helpful. The all-day beverage bar made my attendees so happy, and is a good deal if you are there all day. I was very impressed by the wifi--the first time we've been anywhere where it hasn't failed.

The Commerce Square site is probably best for groups of 10-100; at 150 on our biggest day we were pushing the seams a bit--that said, it's a great place to create excitement--to make a small conference seem like a big one.
Best Bourbon Chicken, Mac and Cheese with Shrimp is a must and mashed potatoes... Wow delicious

I had the crab cake eggs benedict with soft shell crab. The food was good but very stingy on the hollandaise sauce.

Services very poor. I waited over 10 minutes with empty glass of water and coffee. Service staff, cashier and busboys passing by and standing at cash register.  No one offered refills. The waitress came three times. She did give us one refill of coffee after 10 minutes of dry cups.  I had dry cups again when she handed the bill. Not asking if I needed anything else.


Raven was are server.  She said she had not tasted the menu.  That she only likes one thing.  She liked one other because of the name.  She said she had not had it.


.

Wow!  Loved this place!  The dining was comfortable, had a great vibe, open and busy.. great for people watching.  The service was fast and our waiter was attentive.  The food was outstanding!  Fresh and delicious!  I can't wait to go back!!!

Had an amazing time here. The service was excellent. Staff were amazing. Owner was hands down the best. Every course was well prepared and thought out. It was a really fun evening. If you want a to do something special for that special someone make sure you come here. She/he will be impressed. Will be coming back each month.
favorite place to  grab a drink .  be sure to ask for bartender Jed . he  has a heavy hand and more importantley , gives free tickles

This review is not for the food truck only but for the Wicked Wiches catering service.

I hired Bryan to cater my wedding and he was very flexible and creative in creating a delicious menu for us. They don't just make the food the truck offers, they can do much more. 

He prepared a custom appetizer for us as well as some food stations. For the sit down dinner portion we did the food family style (entrees like and there was ample food for all to share. Over a month after the wedding we still have friends telling us how good the food was. 

Definitely look to Wicked Wiches to help you for your next event! :)

Rescue spa comes to rescue...or not... I've been to Rescue Spa a few times getting various different services(waxing/message/mani/pedi). I think it was worth the money for the Swedish or hot stone message but the rest was about average. The spa atmosphere is good, they do really make their space comfy and inviting. Some of the products (esp. candles) they sell are amazing. However, none of that ever makes me feel.. completely satisfied... I can't put a finger on what but it always leaves me feeling....something missing. You won't get bad service, it's just that it won't blow your mind either. Maybe it's just me...

Just recently opened this location so we took my in-laws for an early dinner @4:30pm on a Thursday. We were greeted by a hostess that was anything but friendly. She pointed to the table we were to sit at and walked away. We went to the buffet only to find that 80% of the food was dried out as if it had been there since lunch and the fried rice was gone and by the time we finished our meals it still had not been replenished. 
Definitely will not be back!

Good variety of brews from pilsner to boch to super hoppy.   Great "bar" food with congenial servers who aim to please.  If you haven't been to this venue, you are missing a treat.  Indoor and outdoor seating.

This is my favorite bar in the city. They always have good beers on draft and the food is always solid. I've been coming here for about 3 years and I've never had a bad experience. This place is good for groups, and also for a date. If you like beer and bar food then check this spot out.

Not impressed. In Tucson for a work week. Looked on Yelp to find good seafood after having Mexican and steak houses.  I read the reviews and we drove across the city to get here. The outside didn't look too promising. After all, the reviews said it was "upscale".  Since when are vinyl booths upscale?  Yuck!  
The waiter told us the specials and remarked they only had couple of one left. After he ran around and ignored us for about 10 minutes, I ordered and was told they were now out. I ordered a grilled shrimp salad. It was okay.  However, the kitchen had already cut up the shrimp into little pieces?  I like to see my seafood and cut my own food.  Weird?
My companion ordered Ahi, medium.  His order came cooked all the way through. He sent it back and was given an order cooked medium, but not seasoned. It was awful. He was so hungry that he just ate it. I also had steamed clams. They served it in a bowl with plastic wrap over it.  It looked and tasted like it just came out of the microwave.  I have friends that just moved to Tucsan and others moving back. I told them not to bother eating here.

The online ordering is such a great option to make take out easy and efficient. Ordered online, paid online, and food was ready for pick up in 15 minutes! The sushi was amazing and yakisoba one of the best I've tried in Tucson.

We all hear about Central Grocery as being a store where you can pick up speciality goods. While you can get some interesting local and remote goods, most come for the delectable and one of a kind Muffuletta. I will admit I double dipped, picked up a few local goods and a tasty Muffuletta. 

I heard the lines can be crazy during the day, so I opted to visit right when they opened, which turned out to be a great decision after seeing the lines later in the day. They offer two sizes of the Muffuletta, a "Half or full", I was of course hungry, but was suggested to go with the smaller portion. Good thing I ordered the smaller portion as it was more than enough for two people. Onto the sandwich itself, bread was amazing, as it soaked up all the juices and was not stale in the least. As for the spread, veggies, and meat, they all blended into an sensational flavor that I still dream about to this day! I did not get any extras to go with the sandwich because honestly enjoying the one thing that made Central Grocery famous is what I desired! As for seating, good luck, they have a small counter, but can get quite crowded, I'd suggest ordering and running to a nearby bench and or pier. The pricing is not cheap, but it is so worth it for you foodies at there! Easily one of my highlights during my New Orleans visit.

First I'd like to start off by saying that I've never had any problem going to dentists or doctors, but like any other person there's always a few what if's in the back of my mind. This particular appointment, which also happened to be my first, was a consultation for my future wisdom teeth removal. I got lost on the way to the office, called the front desk, and miss Mackenzie answered was very kind and understanding. As soon as I got there I hurried to do my paperwork and was called back as soon as I was done. I had x-rays taken and then was brought to a room where I waited for Dr. Gita I was asked if I'd like to have the wisdom teeth extracted that same day. At first I laughed thinking that it was a joke, but after the assistant had explained to me that today would be a great day to remove them, I agreed to go forward and have them extracted. Dr. Gita and the kind assistant that stayed by my side were so kind and attentive to me. They made sure to explain everything before and during the procedure, which was so important for me to hear. They made sure I was comfortable and regularly asked if I was doing alright or if I needed anything. I can confidently say that this was one of the most relaxing dentist appointments I've had...and that's saying quite a bit considering I had my wisdom teeth removed. After such a wonderful experience, I can't see myself going anywhere other than Lakewood family Dental for future dental work. Thank you again to Dr. Gita , miss Mackenzie,Caitlin and Laura to make this a great dental clinic.
KEEP UP THE GOOD WORK
Oysters were great, PoBoys were delicious.Crab cake not so much. And the Rockefeller soup is something to pass on. Gave it a 4 because the oysters were perfect. Everything else was good not great.

Very poor quality work. I had a windshield replaced at my site. The installer called me when he arrived, I went out to talk to him about the install, but he didnt want anyone talking to him so he said to leave. Once the install was done, the installer left without saying anything about the windshield, he also did not leave any paperwork so I assume there is no warranty.
I noticed the black glue/sealer they use for the windshield was everywhere, it was on the hood of my car, it was dripping my the side mirrors, it was coming out from the sides of the windshield on the inside of the car. I took it back to their shop and one of the installers there said it was impossible for that mess to not happen. Well its not impossible for them to clean it up after the install, which they did not do.
All around it was a terrible experience and very rude and sloppy service.

Kuma's is the perfect burger joint for Fountain Square. This heavy metal bar is like nothing I've ever walked into before. 

Their burgers are bar none some of the best I've ever had. The ABSU and Goatsnake are nothing short of complete tastebud satisfaction. The beer list is also incredible, always filled with hard to local craft brews on tap and in the bottle, like Zombie Dust to name drop one.

If you're not feeling the heavy music vibe, don't let that deter you from coming in. They have a great outside patio and they are pet friendly!

I'd recommend this place to all of my friends.

If you want super fresh flavour, veggies as well as tender meat offerings and prices that will make your wallet do a happy dance....this is the place.  How many restaurants do you frequent that have you sighing a big mmm and trying new dishes on the menu each visit.  Rice Paper is wonderful..price point, service and the thing we all go for- the food

I frequented the downtown Greenleaf location and during Covid I learned of the Greenleaf location just a few minutes away from my home! With online ordering, text notification when your order is ready, and contactless pickup, they make it easy to have a healthy lunch. I love all acai bowls but I crave Greenleaf's Soma bowl. It is full of fresh healthy ingredients and you can taste the difference. It is not frozen berry sorbet like some places (I hate when it's mostly ice!) The toppings are generous as well as the size, 24oz! It could easily be split in two. I've even eaten the second half the next day. Acai will oxidize when exposed to air for a long time and turn brown like an avocado but it does not affect the taste. I also live for their Morning Berries Oatmeal bowl.
I've eaten at Seasons 52 a handful of times and a my experiences have been great. Food is on point, delicious. Service is excellent. Lots of great choices on the menu. Will definitely go back

This is definitely an exceptional unique breakfast spot. Their food tastes very fresh. Long live the simple eggs and bacon (which they do serve too), however, it's nice to have a twist on your "typical" breakfast. It seems to be family owned which its always great to support the locals.

We came in for brunch. 
Liberty was our server she did great. Our coffee was never empty. We order early for our kid it takes him longer to eat. His chicken strips and fruit bowl where great. 
My husband order the steak and eggs his food was great. 
I order the French toast and hashbrowns and over hard eggs.
When my food arrived by an other server, my eggs weren't over hard more on the medium side no issue I can eat them like that. I did let her know that  I order hashbrowns not country potatoes she oh okay and left came back with an empty plate and a plate with hashbrowns.  
Than she started to literally scrape the country potatoes off my plate with a steak knife.  I just watched while this was happening.  After that she said "Sorry about that " I was like okay that just happened.  So I started to eat my French toast which by the way it was soft and fluffy but... it did taste like green bell peppers and onions.  I didn't mention to our server I didn't want to make a big deal out it. I eat my food and it was good. Our bill was under $40 for 3 meals not bad.

The food is good, the service is not quick, but the music is freaking fantastic!! My husband and son joined me to take advantage of a Groupon that I purchased for dinner at  Blues Ship Cafe. It's located in Ybor City right on 7th Ave. Our first impression was not great. There was no one there! It seems things don't get "cookin" until 8:30. We found a table and as we ordered and were served a few more people came in. By the time we got our food, and it did take awhile, there were 4 or 5 tables. When the singer, Bobby G Summers, started all heads turned and the rest of the night was like a dream. We felt like we would have payed $50-$100 for the performance. Bobby it seems has worked with Al Green and many other famous singers. Bobby is so talented we listened as he turned a Pink Floyd song into a blues rock song. It was amazing. I asked our very busy waitress and Bobby is there 3 out of 4 weekends. Look him up on YouTube and see him singing here at the Clearwater Jazz Festival with Earth, Wind and Fire.
I ordered the Southern Style Ribs with Cabbage and Collards. The ribs had just enough sauce and were tender and tasty. The Cabbage was by far the best thing on the table I think. The collards were also good, but not the best I've had. When I go back, I will probably get the same thing, except the corn bread. I have a weakness for corn bread and  I am not too picky, but the corn bread was not good.
My husband got Ribs and Shrimp with Red Beans and Rice and my son got the Chicken and Pork combo with fries and Red Beans and Rice. Both really liked their meals, but the red beans and rice was a bit odd. Like it had to much cumin, not bad, but different.
To top off our meal, and to delay leaving so we could listen to Bobby, we got their Key Lime Cake with ice cream. I would give it a 3 out of 5.
Over all our service was a bit slow. There was only one waitress and she was working hard, but it was a bit much for one person to handle. She was very nice and tried hard.
So, by all means go to Blues Ship Cafe, don't go to early or you'll be bored, have a good dinner and if you are lucky Bobby will take the stage and entertain you.

First off I'm not a starving/earthy student so this restaurant isn't geared toward me. I only came in for dessert, but it was really good and I'd still go again. 
The whole vibe though is not somewhere I fit in though. It's young, earthy students working on laptops or hanging out with friends eating vegan natural foods.
The restaurant is shabby chic and somewhat small for how popular it is so sometimes it can be hard to find a place to sit.
It does have free wifi which is great if you're not the Starbucks type.
Only bad thing is the time the cashier takes because he or she also gets your food so it's a long wait. Change that and I'd give it a higher rating.

This place was absolutely amazing! Excellent food, friendly people, exceptional service, and a sweet waitress/manager named Katrina. 

I will be going back there again soon. Like, tomorrow...

We are regulars. Its one of our places for sushi rolls. Very clean place. I think they need to change up the menu a little more often. I personally dont care for much of the hot foods. Just dont like the sauce. Soups are GREAT. I would tell you to go.

This is my neighbourhood mall.  Great selection of stores, and they are always keeping things new and fresh.

There is lots of free parking located all around the mall.  The food court also has a lot of awesome choices. 

The mall in general is great about providing events and supports local charities. 

My only complaint is that there is no grocery store.  The old Target space is currently being renovated though, so a girl can hope right?

I love this place. $30 for a regular pedicure but they rub you legs and feet with hot rocks and it's amazing. I walked in on Sunday to get a pedicure with my friend and we were sat together and everything even though the were busy the owner came and started on me so the one girl could finish her lunch break. That's the other thing I like everyone seems pretty happy, the owner went around and made sure everyone could take a break to eat and said she would take over since it was so busy. But everyone is so hardworking that they all wanted to finish what they started. They do beautiful work there and they end it by messaging your back while your toes dry. Everyone is really nice and the decor makes it feel very cozy.!I highly recommend them!
Bad, bad,bad.  Go elsewhere and save yourself much aggravation.  Service vehicle ces on in first five miles,difficulty exchanging, and long wait for every transaction.

I was recommended trying their Trenton/Chambersburg style pie, and was not disappointed. Ordered square pie half original Trenton/ half calabrese; captures great balance of crispy, chewy thin crust, sauce and mozzarella. The broccoli rabe and sausage on the calabrese has fresh but not overpowering flavors. Well worth it all in all.-

My wife and I stopped by to ask them to check out a loud revving noise. They hoisted the car up to check it out and soon saw that the muffler was rocking. They recommended a muffler place, didn't charge us anything, and sent us on our way. They were quick and truly helpful, not just I-want-your-money helpful. Steve worked with us and was very nice.
Chargrilled oysters!!! Honestly, I think that may taste better than acme's. Something special about how they make them here. Delicious!

This is a beautiful restaurant inside and the place was packed on Saturday! We actually didn't know how packed they were but luckily we got a table in 15 minutes. My wife got a steak fajitas things it was huge, she couldn't eat it all. I went big with the volcano mocajeto bowl! Trust me this thing and everything in it, shrimp, chicken, steak and the sauce, you could dip a shoe in it and it would be a tasty shoe! Over all the service, cooks, the manager even smiled and said it's pretty good? As both gave thumbs up with our mouths full! Authentic Mexican musicians filled the air with musical ambience.  If you're ever in Tucson this is a must try. Now go!

Best Deli in Town!!!

It brings me back to Canters back home in LA.  The meats and breads are super fresh and you can tell a difference!!  Not the place you need to go if just visiting NOLA for a couple of days but it's worth it!!

Kathy is so efficient. The shutters are Hunter Douglas- great quality. The price is the best out of 5 comparables. Shutters for the entire house, 3300 sq feet.  Kathy came to measure.  We had no window covers at all. I asked Kathy if she can make it happen in very short time. All window shutters are installed in 2 weeks. No problems at all. So efficient and such grear quality.  Thank you, Kathy.

Cleanness: 3/5
Food: 4/5
Service: 3/5

I used to visited here for lunch whenever I want to eat some Japanese food. As a lunch menu, I just ordered the Chirashi Bowl and the bowl was plentiful. The Sashimi on the top of the rice was also fresh enough. Overall, I could enjoy my lunch and I want to taste another foods on the menu.

I have been here 4 times now and although it's a cool looking place I can't justify $75 for breakfast for 4.  It is certainly not worth the price tag.  The food is overly spicy or bland.  Huevos were wet and sloppy and no one finished them.  Greek omelette was pretty flavorless. Chicken fried steak was good as was the gravy. Sopapillas are very good. But for $25 we could have all eaten at Courtesy and been happier.

Food was yummy. The waitress was what really did it in for us. Couldn't answer questions about the menu. Her family was dining, and so was she while waiting on us. Had to finally stand up to get a check. I don't think we will ever be back, just wasn't that yummy. Did have great portion sizes and they can defiantly cook shrimp. No alcohol, so no margaritas.
Amazing food! Cheap margaritas! Don't pass this place up. 99 cent margaritas food all around $10 and well worth it!

Do I like Moe's? Heck yeah! Yesterday I ate it twice! My favorite is the Burrito Bowl. It's tasty, not messy to eat (while driving) and I'm not filled up by the burrito wrap. My favorite of the meats is the steak. Some of the items I always get in my bowl are rice, fresh jalapenos, guacamole, corn, lettuce, cilantro and sometimes sour cream. I love love love the variety of salsas Moe's offers. I especially love the hot kind. Don't tell them, but I get a few scoops on top of my bowl and about 6 more containers to go. My only complaint about Moe's, all locations, is that the rice always tastes like it's under-cooked. Is it a Moe's thing?

This location, as with all locations, are clean and everybody who work here are welcoming.
My husband and I love this place - best Italian in Boise.  The owner is fabulous as well - if Bev is there spend some time with her because she's a hoot.

Really terrible experience. I have a lot of experience in the medical field and went thinking I would support the solo practitioner. While I knew exactly what was going on, she still thought I was a "mystery." She didn't have a certain mediation in stock and had me go buy it for myself at Rite Aid and then come back to the office!! I landed in the ER after a procedure, even though I saw her earlier that day to try to address what was going on.  I followed up at Jefferson Women's Center where they took great care of me. I wasn't that "mysterious" to them at all.

The food is actually really good however the staff seems unprofessional. I went in to order and a woman in the back was literally screaming at another employee and said "I'm going to f-ing quit!" Not the friendliest environment. I'd go back for the food though

Booked on a whim since I was in town for the day.  Turned out to be one of the best deep tissue massages I've ever had.  Have no idea how Mie-Mie was able to be so consistent for 90mins of a deep tissue without getting exhausted herself.  Seriously.  Give her a medal. Highly recommend.

I would like to start by saying the servers and employees bussing tables were lovely and this review has nothing to do with them, they are extremely under staffed and based on the lack of management I'm not surprised. If I could leave no stars I would. We were a large party who ended up split due to different flight times. My half was about an hour behind. And we texted the other part of our group and they still didn't have food and their food order was just being taken once we got there. We added our order along and then proceeded to watch multiple parties who came after us either get their food or leave due to how long service was taken. Once the food was finally coming out some was cold and had to be sent back. My food was the absolute last to arrive and was so late most of my party had finished eating. Every one felt the food was sub par based on what we heard. Extremely under seasoned and Luke warm. The worst part was we asked to speak to a manager and he refused to come out and sent another employee who just comped the meal. That is a disgrace. You should never set your servers up for failure in that way, which from talking to the servers the manager frequently does this.

THIS PLACE IS OUT OF THIS WORLD. I'm not even kidding, the food is to DIE FOR.
 
First thing I ordered, two appetizers: the deviled eggs and the sundried tomato goat cheese spread...

The deviled eggs were two pairings, two were maple bacon bleu cheese and the others were sundried tomato pesto. The flavors were INCREDIBLEEEEE.

The subdried tomato goat cheese spread was AMAZING. It had capers in it too and the flavors were SOOOO in point.

My pain dish was the sweet potato Thai noodles with blacked ahi tuna..... the vegetables in this dish had a beautiful char grilled taste and I LOVEDDD the texture of the noodles with the teriyaki flare. The ahi was dynamite. 

This is definitely not a cheap restaurant, but it's well worth it and I have NO regrets. I can't wait to go back.

The family and I definitely enjoyed our time. The service was very friendly, the environment was nice and clean. The drinks are great and better priced than the average bar and grill. We shared an assortment of food, the sliders, jalapeño poppers, etc which was all fantastic. I ordered the club/chef salad and it was freaking huge and delicious. I'll definitely be going back.

Love the company of my husband but awful dining experience. Food was mediocre, and service absolutely none existent. Will never be back. I Was hoping for something better for my 60th birthday. Trying to get the check so we can go buy something to drink, I asked for a tea refill 30 minutes ago, doesn't look like it's going  to happen

Crowd seems nice but we waited for about twenty minutes before a server waited on us. Also, while we were waiting, the bartender was sitting socializing with another table.  It wasn't very full and we felt like we were being ignored.  Also, when we ordered, the server seemed really annoyed after I ordered a drink she had never heard of before and didn't seem very apologetic for skipping over us to wait on several other tables that came in after us.

Fantastic free tour and the crowd was courteous too.  Normally, I'm pretty wary of free tours and groups of annoying tourists, but our tour group was great and most of the kiddies were very well behave.

I've been here three times, and I really can't rave about it enough. Except it's GD PRICEY.

Whether you're in the starlit inner dome with an amazing ambience and great acoustics, or out on the lawn with extra screens and a blanket, the feeling is incredible.

Downside of lawn: bugsinyourface.

Buying food and beverage here is absurd, no doubt, and you get checked for so much as a bottle of water going in so don't try to smuggle, you'll lose.

I think there are summer time family picnic competitions though that you can bring your own food for free and stay for the concert. I once saw the Philadelphia Orchestra tribute Star Wars this way. Fabulous. I think it's called Picnic Jamboree.

They have a lot of really great bands come out, of all genres. I was once even scheduled to see Peter, Paul, and Mary! Then Mary died. :(

Wonderful summertime fun, not far from home.

Great food great service great vibe. New York City quality restaurant. 
Pasta was fantastic and the starters are amazing, I wish they had an espresso but other than that it was really good! Highly recommended (PS walk ins are really fine!)

Much better than I anticipated!
