- Rooms are (relatively) small, but clean and well laid out.
- EXCELLENT bed 
- Smallish desk, but workable. Free Wifi was faster than average for hotels
- Room service is speedy, and reasonably (for room service) priced
- Everyone insists on greeting you in French

I would definitely stay here again if I was back in Philadelphia.

Where the fries are king and the burgers are sides. Seven kinda of potatoes, nine kinds of salt, nine kinds of dipping sauces. Need I say more? (They even had one combination that tasted like cinnamon donuts. Mind. Blown.)
First experience with Beignets!  I absolutely could not get enough.  They were made fresh to order.  The staff was friendly and the atmosphere was pleasing.  Definitely a must if you're visiting.

I won't bother getting into the whole "is Midway Pizza really deep dish?" or "Midway pizza isn't Chicago-style pizza" debates. Personally, neither of those really matters to me. What matters, is GOOD PIZZA!

Two friends and I swung by this joint one evening for dinner. Parking was easy; score! The door was opened for us, we were greeted and quickly seated. Our server came by for drink orders, gave us time to look over the menu (we needed it... lots of it... no one could decide on what to get cuz it all sounded so DEELISH), and he answered our questions so that we could ultimately (finally!) decide on pizzas. Great service in my book!

I chose the FD2: chorizo sausage, green chiles, red onion, cotija cheese and green onions. My friends ordered the Money Pie: spinach cheese, pepperoni, meatballs, roasted garlic, grape tomato, basil;  and the King Leon: meatballs, pepperoni, mushrooms, onions and extra sauce.

Yes, there is a lot of crust. I dont know it that makes it a real deep dish or chicago style (the King Leon DID have extra sauce?!) but one thing is for sure... it was ALL so tasty! Warm gooey cheese, fresh toppings and yes, a thick crust. The smaller size pizzas (10'') are definitely big enough to fill a hungry tummy AND take some home.

For the vegetarians: apparently the salads are phenomenal and there are lots of options for meatless pizzas.

My friend Sara L. is always talking about this place, and it's around the corner from her office. I had been traveling and had not seen my girls in a bit. So, we decided to have lunch. However, we were having lunch with Little Miss PITA so I was worried. I wanted a nice sit-down lunch where we could all order something. I recalled Sara saying she orders the mushroom hoagie a lot, even though she is a carnivore. 

So, I was surprised when my suggestion of Matyson was not balked at. We all managed to get there about the same time, which was great, because it was cold out and there isn't much room to wait in the restaurant. It's small and cosy, yet very warm and vibrant. Seriously it feels and looks like autumn in there. Like you are inside a leaf. Which is amazing, because the view outside the window is of Famous 4th street and a parking garage! 

The clientele is definitely office workers having business lunches, but you don't feel out of place and anyway I get dressed up to the nines to take the trash out. 

Our waitress was great and continuously refilled my iced tea without asking. The evil bread basket visited our table a few times. Delicious bread and butter.....my weakness. I had steak frites and they were pretty delicious and not ridiculous. The steak frites at Parc are great, but nearly 30 bucks kinda rip off territory. My fave place for steak frites was La Terasse in University City, but RIP La Terasse.  The skirt steak was done perfectly medium. The hotel butter and steak juices were wonderful to drag my fries in. 

I was in heaven, I don't even remember what the girls had....one of them had something vegetarian and was happy....which coming from Miss PITA thats practically awarding the restaurant two michelin stars. Sara also remarked hers was tasty. I was on planet steak, population keep your fork away from my plate. 

We did peruse the desserts, but PITA and I were heading to a coffee shop afterwards to do some work so I knew I might want something elsewhere. 

I would definitely return, and I would even feel comfortable going here alone to savor my lunch in peace. I also would love to come for dinner!

I had an opportunity to virtually plan a friend's wedding.  My first trip to Philly, I met with Nancy who helped me order everything I needed for the wedding.  As the wedding approached, I thought of other things I would need.  Whenever I called, the staff was very helpful.  This was important to me as I was not able to simply walk in to their office.  I needed pipe draping and they created a solution for my needs.  They delivered the requested items promptly.  There was a change needed with the table runners.  I sent a photo of what I was expecting and had the correct color delivered in a short turnaround time.  Thank you for helping me make my friend's wedding special!

I spent over an hour looking at each booth here.  
Cute and original things in many of the booths here but i found it to be wildly expensive.

Don't get the beef wellington...

Let me say the service is top shelf. The drinks are great, the apps are amazing... 
The beef wellington needs to come off the menu. It's boring and horrible

I'm a fan of other Fray's stores and this one holds onto the Fray's charm... ehhh... mostly.

FOOD
Oh gosh--how can you complain about anything? The donuts are God's poop on earth (and that's a heavenly good thing). Melt-in-your-mouth and warm while the sweetness is not overwhelming. Although small, the coffee mixes well with the donut sweetness but is good on its own as well. 

SERVICE
The employees seem a bit too comfortable here. They only come out when a customer is waiting and one time an employee was eating a sandwich practically behind the counter! On the plus side, once they know you're friendly (and I am FREAKIN' FRIENDLY) then nothing but smiles ooze out.
Decided to skip Friday night pizza and try something new.  Wow....wonderful little spot with great food!  As others have noted jerk chicken is very spicy but it should be. Will definitely be back.
A must see if in Nee Orleans. The museum is done with modern tech but has that old feel to it.

Our experience was good. I feel the over-all experience deserves not more than 3 stars.

The Music is just too darn loud for dinner. It was near impossible to hold a good conversation with the other guests at the table.
The other downer was the price of Liquor. Holy Cow talking about being price raped!
Way way too much for a cocktail!

Now onto the food. Try the 29 dollar special all you wish small plates. It offers a decent selection which encourages those of us who are not familiar with the tastes of Cuba to experience many flavors. The Good dishes were terrific which you can taste the many layers of seasonings.

The stellar choices was the following;
Pulpo con Berenjenas - Octopus, tender and succulent
Skirt Steak, Tender and well seasoned
Black Bean Soup, one of the better choices
Guava BBQ Ribs, very good
Sopa Levanta Muert - good but the soup needed more depth. Just a tad bit more to this soup and it had the makings of something special 

Chicharrones de la Cas- Decent but the chicken and pork were dry
El Chinito Cubano - A good rendition of the Cuban Sandwich made into a Springroll
Albondigas Camagüey - good meatballs
Copitas de Tostone - the stuffing in the cups were good but the plantains cups were dry and mealy
Cóctel de Camarones  - Shrimp Cocktail - Good taste but the shrimp were way over cooked, dense almost hard. Total miss
Pato Ahumado-  Smoked Duck total miss and just not good
Carne de Cangrejo Ceviche  - Total miss almost as bad as the Duck. Too finely chopped up seafood, lacked Lime, just terrible.

You can skip the desserts, nothing to write home about.

So folks it was a hit or miss. Service was good. For 4 people including the over priced cocktails, and tip,  total was 270.

Not exactly what I would expect from a southern take on deep dish but boy, it hits the spot. What I took away most was the salad portion size and their flexibility! They were so accommodating with vegetarian toppings and letting us make two pizzas into one (half and half to get a little of both worlds). Definitely a win.

Worst experience! Went to the men's dept to get my son a suit. Two people at the counter in the center of the men's dept said that they couldn't help us and referred us to the empty suit dept. We tracked down 2 employees in men's shoes helping 1 customer.  We waited for an opening and asked employee, David, if he could help us. His response was that he was leaving soon, but maybe could help. I said, "Never mind" and started to leave. He shouted, "That will get you far!". I turned and said, "That will get you fired!". His response to that was, "Go for it!" I was astonished that an employee would treat a customer that way. I was so shaken by this. What an ass! We did report the incident to a manager. Doubt it went anywhere. But rest assured that we will NEVER go to Macy's Moorestown again. And will tell anyone that will listen to not frequent that store!

This is a hidden gem of a restaurant.  The food is very high quality, and authentic, better than what is found in most cities.  I ordered a Pad Thai, noodles were firm, flavors were spot on. My friend ordered a Szechuan beef, which looked like it could be from a magazine photo.  Food was well worth the price.  If the food is hit or miss, find out who the head chef is.  

Service is lacking which is common in most Asian restaurants.  Get used to it.

With all of the good pasta/pizza places in south philly, I am not sure why this one is still in business. Pizza was flimsy and bland (we tried the sausage one and the prosciutto) pasta was WAY undercooked (we had the tagliatelle) and the gnocchi tasted like glue. Also, this place isn't BYOB which in itself is a downfall. I really wanted to like this place...we will not be returning.

You might want to bus your outside a little more. Our time sitting here has been shared with a couple of kids who not only bought no drinks but also smoked cigarettes non-stop and openly drank booze from a half pint. Classy.

Came to NOLA for my bachelorette and this was the first place I wanted to go! 
There was a long line but we got through very quickly - i would say maybe 15 minutes for ten girls! I immediately ordered the chargrilled oysters, DELICIOUS! So so good, I'm thinking about them now! 

Then I ordered a fried shrimp basket which was yummy. My friends ordered the fried catfish and the bbq shrimp and loved it. 

I'll need to come back for those oysters! mmmm
First time ever , the food was okay. The wings were OK, I had better. if you like sports this is your place lol. Every game on and the staff was nice.

Shocking! It is 11:38 am  June 24, 2013, I had a 11:00 am appointment to have my windows tinted. I just moved into Nashville from out of state and although I left with plenty of time unfortunately I got lost (even using my GPS), as a result I didn't arrive to the appointment until 11:15. I called ahead of time at 10:45 to let them know I was arriving just a few minutes late. When I walked in the door and announced who I was Landon C. shouted over his shoulder that I was 20 minutes late (really less than 15) and that he might have to reschedule me to the next day. 

I have been a small (and not so small) business owner for over 20 years and I understand schedules and there importance, but the way Landon continued to handle the situation was so completely unprofessional that for the first time EVER I am writing (yelping) a negative review. 

I asked to be allowed to call the dealer that sold me the car and arranged for the window tinting from Landon's office. I was handed a phone that didn't work and told that no one knew the phone number. When I went back outside to retrieve my mobile phone I found out Landon was on the phone already with my contact at the dealer. When we were able to talk he was rude, aggressive and hostile. My opinion is that they over booked themselves and saw me as an easy way to lessen the load.  

In ANY event they were rude and extremely unprofessional. STAY AWAY FROM THIS BUSINESS.

DO NOT GO to this hotel! 

I have been in the hospitality industry for over 10 years and I've never seen such a terrible hotel in my entire life do not spend your money here. 

Town Park Valet service is terrible!

Krauter Photography (Now, Love Leon) was the first thing we booked after we set the wedding date. Our friends used them for their wedding and we were obsessed at how perfectly they captured their love that we had to have them! They didn't disappoint. I've never seen more beautiful pictures IN MY LIFE. And we are probably biased because they are pictures of us and all of the people that we love, but they truly captured every detail, every kiss, every laugh, every moment that we could've possibly wanted. The image that we had of our wedding day in our heads was completely blown out of the water compared to the beautiful pictures they captured. The photos are ethereal, whimsical, forrest-y. The photos embodied us. We couldn't have felt anymore like a little woodland fairy couple just sprinkling our love all over nature (cheesy but it's appropriate). Lauren and Casey, thank you for being our little fairy godparents on that day letting us love each other openly, freely and in nature. This explanation truly doesn't even do it justice what our hearts are bursting to say to you both. So.. We will just say THANK YOU from the bottom of our hearts for capturing our love and spirit in every-single-photo!

Another must for me during my visit to STL.
The Moolah not only has the cheapest drinks in town, but one of the best bartenders and crowds. 
It was a Hatewatch night at the bar and it was a blast. I got a Manhattan to drink and that was able to last me the night. 
This is one of the places that I think is a must for any visitors to the area who are looking for things to do that aren't touristy. 
Tip: Get a Dogtown pizza from the concession counter and take it into the bar with you. Nothing better than a pizza and a drink!

This is one of my favorite restaurants in town for a number of reasons, but it does have a few drawbacks that keep it from getting 5 stars from me.  Basically, though, I'll preface my remarks by saying that the "main event" foods are WONDERFUL, and if that's all you care about, you are in for an absolute treat.

The GOOD:
1. It's the only game in town, and even though they might be spurred to improve a bit with some competition, they acquit themselves quite well.

2. If you want spice, you can get spice.  This is one of the few places in town where I can tell the waiter that I want my food spicy, and it comes legitimately well-seasoned, and not what I would call "white person spicy."

3. They have the best injera bread I have ever had.  I can only conclude that the previous reviewer who said the bread was tasteless was a) a smoker, b) in the midst of a cold,  or c) missing some taste buds.  The bread is flavorful, tangy, and full of spongy goodness.

4. Portion sizing is perfect.  Am I the only one who thinks it's ironic (bordering on the distasteful) when people complain about portion sizing in an ETHIOPIAN restaurant?  In a place where you can spend $10 on a unique entree that comfortably serves 2 people, can one really complain.  Are Americans really so out of touch with how big their food needs to be?

5. The meat is perfectly seasoned, and the lentils, beans and vegetables are tender and succulent.  I have never eaten an entree here that failed to impress.

6. BYOB, with no corking fee.

The NOT-SO-GOOD:

1. Service is slooooooooow.  Even on weekday nights, when there are few patrons, food takes an agonizingly long time arriving at the table.  The last time my girlfriend and I went, we waited 40 minutes for our order of injera and entree to arrive.

2. Salad is bad.  There's no other way to say it.  Basically for $3.50, you get some bagged, sometimes partially frozen, iceberg lettuce, an onion ring, a couple of cherry tomatoes, and a mildly flavorful vinaigrette.  Just pass on the salad.  That's not why you came, anyway.

3. The restaurant is in a renovated home, which feels cramped and which gives the dining experience a "rat-trapped-in-a-maze" feel.


All-in-all, though, the negatives are MINOR quibbles for me.  The food is the thing, and THE THING IS GOOD!  At the very least, where else do you get to go out and eat with your hands?!  At the end of the day, Zemam's is a gem in Tucson, and I heartily recommend it.

What a gem!! The staff was great the service was exceptional and the food was outstanding! I simply cannot say enough we were so impressed and will definitely be back soon. A little crowded but not worth taking off a star this place is perfect otherwise.

My HVAC system stopped working, and I called Velocity as they were the ones that installed it 2 years ago. The tech's name is Richie, and he did a great job and was courteous an helpful. The system needed a new capacitor, but he also spent time cleaning things out and providing advice. The only reason I'm not giving it 5 stars is the fact that the AC broke down in the first place given it was only 2 years old. Parts were covered under warranty but labor wasn't, which is somewhat frustrating given I had just had Velocity do a tune up a few months earlier (obviously not Richie's fault).
Found a large roach in my chips, the waiter didn't seem to care. The service and food have gone down hill big time! We used to frequent this place, and now I cringe when some one suggests it!

Did not enjoy the food here one bit! Personally, rarely do I ever go to these franchise-type of diners, but since my cousin insisted us to go to try out the burgers, I was like why not. We started off with the white spinach cheese dip with our chips, and to be bluntly, it tasted awful! Barely any spinach at all with one single piece of asparagus hidden somewhere in the cheese. The guacamole was apparently a single slice of avocado that wasn't even mixed well with the pico de gallo, not to mention there was barely any pico de gallo and guac. The cheese itself was all liquid and not creamy at all. The complimentary salsa tasted better than this spinach cheese dip that nearly cost us $7.

We then got the sweet and smoky burger and the southern style hamburger with a side of onion rings for one of them, which at first glance looked tasty but was not the case at all. Each burger was super dry, like one of the driest burgers I have ever tasted and was borderline burnt. The fries and onion rings had no flavor to them whatsoever, and it was like the cook just put them in the fryer. Like really? I can just go to Walmart, buy some fries and onion rings, and do that myself for much cheaper. 

At the end, our bill was almost $37 for two burgers with a side of onion rings for one of them ($1 extra), white spinach cheese dip, and a coke; which is one RIDICULOUS bill that should have been under $30 for the quality of food we were getting. On the plus side, our server was very at tentative and nice.
Very busy and slow service. Food is ok. I'm not a big fan of Mexican food but it is not the best place I e been to for Mexican.

Staff was very friendly and super sweet toward my toddler. My mom and I got smoothies and they were pretty disappointing. But, I got my son the acai bowl and it was super tasty and the fruit was fantastic. I would come back to try some other items on the menu.

Ok, so I have not been to a ton of airports in my life, I am definitely not considered a jet-setter, but I really have enjoyed my experiences in BNA more so than a lot of the other airports that I have been in. Its big, but not so big that moving around becomes a task, you wont have to take a train to the other side of the airport. It has been renovated to be very updated and modern inside. The gates are very comfortable and include plenty of outlets that allow you to plug in and recharge. Overall I am fan.
Always playing games, crackhead games, like closing super early and saying they ran out of pizza. Pizza is really good though.

I decided to give this place a try as I had never heard of it and was in the neighborhood. I ordered the seafood platter so I could try a variety of menu items. Wow! It was really great. I like spicy so I Tel Derek's it "hot". I found the spice level to be great. Enough kick to let you know it is there but not so spicy that it is all you can taste. The shrimp was not over-battered and had great flavor. The catfish was cooked perfectly and not greasy at all. Then... there was the lobster tail... I've never heard of Nashville Hot Lobster tail... and I'm a native. It was awesome. Cooked to perfection. Crispy and spicy on the outside and tender and buttery lobster meat on the inside. Not to be overlooked, the fried okra was outstanding in flavor and texture. All around, a great meal for a very reasonable price for what I got. Give it a go!

I don't know what all the hype is about when it comes to Calriottis. I've tried it twice now, and both times the sandwiches were completely mediocre. You'll get a better sandwich at Jersey Mikes or Penn Station!

Ordered two pounds of steamed crawfish and ate every last one. Absolutely delicious. The perfect amount of spice and creole seasoning. The dueling pianos went great with the food and drinks. The pianist were very friendly and I was impressed by their talent. They also have a great (and surprinsingly cheap) happy hour here. I would definitely recommend this restaurant.

Great time! We did the four hour tour and saw a ton of horses. Patrick was our guide. He is very knowledge about the area and his passion for the horses became evident very quickly. He was out of the car and taking photos alongside us. Patrick did an excellent job giving us the best viewing angles and getting us close to the horses while not intruding upon them and still giving them their space.

I didn't know what to expect going into the tour, but was blown away. I had the misconception that we would be off the beaten path and deep in the Virginia Range. Although we ventured off the paved road a few times, the horses were extremely close to the roads and we didn't have to go far to see them. If you do this, you'll see your first few within a few minutes of the tour starting.

This was an excellent time and I definitely recommend it to others! Support a small, local business and call Sonny Boys Tours.
Absolutely love this place. Staff is super friendly, place is very clean, and haircut comes out great everytime. I use Patrick and have always been happy with his service!

Finally got to enjoy the ambiance and food at this place.  Loved the look, very intimate and comfortable.   Loved the Lil bar area in the front.  Being a vegetarian, we tried the following dishes: 

Grilled Artichokes - was ok, but didn't have much of a texture.  But good flavors.
Patatas bravas - was the best dish of the night.  Very good flavor. 
salsa de berenjena - loved the flavors in this salsa.  Wished it came with more pita, and worth eating every bit.  
Berenjena napoleon - was the most disappointing.  Perhaps becuz it was a cold dish, but when i added a Lil salt, it made the flavor the eggplant more intriguing.  But it left the most to be desired. 

All in all, i would go back as the ambiance the wonderful wine menu would make this a great place to enjoy a night out, but i have had better tapas in the city.

When I walked into this place, I felt like I warped to a small bar located in the Mexican countryside. Festive skulls painted on the walls, alongside other extravagant paintings, the décor was unbelievable. The bar is very open and there are plenty of tables for sitting. We drank $1 tequila shots and $2.50 PBRs. It was a good deal for a weekday evening. I believe they change their specials up, so it could vary from day-to-day. I really enjoyed the time I spent here and would very much like to go back sometime, preferably on a weekend, and when their signature Revolution lager is in stock, as it is advertised for only $1, which is great. Much to my disappointment, they were out when we went, but I guess you win some and you lose some. Hopefully I'll return soon enough to try their cerveza barata!

Great service! I brought in 3 pairs of shoes, one of which I needed just the heel taps replaced but a pretty quick turn around. The owner was very friendly and willing to get it done on time while being transparent that the other 2 would be a bit of a wait (which I assumed ahead of time). Definitely will be going back! Before & after pics of the heel taps.
I ordered an arrangement online very last minute and they got it out the same day! It was exactly as pictured and my mom loved it. Very happy with the service and would definitely order from again.

The ambiance is busy during the night and it is difficult to find a place to sit just for 2 it's a challenge for a bigger group of people. Cute decor and great pizza. The pizza is more of an individual size. Id go back but because it's so crowded with no were to sit I say a solid 4 stars. Unfortunately they don't have beer on tap and the pizza price is kind of up there. Over all good experience

Just left with a squeller of the coffee stout and let me say this "OH MAN" this place is super good. I'm from of of town and was looking for a brewery, found this one on yelp and I'm so glad I did. It is superb. Only bad thing is that they don't have growlers only squellers but besides that the beer is some of the best I've had

It was my birthday and my boyfriend ordered a gluten free and vegan cake from them. They weren't able to make a gluten free and vegan cake but didn't tell him. They then made 2 cakes, 1 huge gluten free one, and 1 tiny after thought vegan one. They also, through text, had a message to say "happy birth week" but instead ignored that, and they both said, happy birthday. So not only did they not make a cake I could eat but he still paid for it?!? I did try the frosting on the vegan one, it had the texture and consistency and mouthfeel of vegetable shortening with confectioners sugar.. it wasn't appealing ...If you can't make a cake don't tell people you can!! And then charge them full price for something that no one can eat! Easily could have been cancelled or could have let my bf know.

This review is for the Sunday Breakfast Buffet

We'll start by getting the price out of the way. The Sunday breakfast buffet is $75 per person. All you can eat, drink, etc. Yes, it's expensive. Not something to do often, unless of course you're loaded, in which case you're not reading this anyway (that's what minions are for).

So, clearly, the Biltmore Sunday brunch is a special affair kind of deal -- and boy, is it ever!

I have had the luck to have been twice this year, thanks to the ever considerate (and beautiful) Wiff. Once for Mother's Day, and another time for Father's Day. 

I won't go into details on the service or setting. Both are seriously stellar. Top-notch all the way, as you would expect from the Biltmore.

Special occasion or not, $75 is a good chunk of change for eggs and toast, so we made sure we did our best in 'leveling' the field a bit.

Multiple mimosas to get things started, and then off to the races. 

First round: prime rib, salmon, poached egg, a few oysters, stone crab claws, and since years of waiting tables has enabled me to balance well, a good half dozen freshly made blinis topped precariously with ridiculous amounts of caviar.

Round Two: A small omelet (made to order), bacon, mini bagel with cream cheese and lox, a waffle, fruit, and a small spattering of very tasty lobster puff pastries.

Round Three: This one was purely for the win. A entire plate dedicated to oysters and caviar. Sheer unadulterated heaven.

Round Four: Barely able to move by now, so pacing myself time wise. A nice cappuccino, and off to the 'deserts' section. A sample of each and every cake and/or pie -- all conveniently proportioned to be bite sized. A few freshly made cookies, and of course, some fruit on a stick, totally covered from a few well placed swirls in the chocolate fountain.

And so, 90 minutes later, we all sat outside on the shady garden terrace, enjoying a final espresso.

But for that once a year extravagance, the brunch at the Biltmore is very hard to beat, and most certainly a 5-star experience all the way. 

Was is worth it? Dollar for dollar? Was I able to get my money's worth? 

Hard to say.

Let's put it this way -- I'm certainly willing to give it another shot.

I love the grilled chicken and broken platter. I also ordered the salt and pepper calamari and VN rolls. I've been here 3 times since their grand opening and everything I've ordered has been delicious and perfect.

Took a ridiculous amount of time for our food to come. After 40 minutes, an emergency came up so we requested our food to-go instead - which took an ADDITIONAL 15 minutes for them to put our food in a box. I had high expectations for this First Watch. I love their other locations, especially the one in Villanova, PA. This one is definitely not up to par.
Best beignets in New Orleans! Light not greasy, perfect amount of powdered sugar.  coffee was great too.

I used to love Superstore--adore it even, but lately the Clareview location has been falling apart, and it's totally turning me off.

I'm not sure if the roof leaking problem has been totally fixed, but how would you like your magazines soaked in roof water? For some reason, the staff did not seem concerned that the water was dripping all over the magazines by the checkouts. 

Superstore is always busy, but the line-ups used to be not bad, even when the place was crawling with people on any given evening. But lately, the line-ups are ridiculous, and you will have to work your way through one of the drenching-wet magazines to keep you occupied. They do not have enough cashiers, but that's probably the labour shortage. 

Also, this Superstore doesn't seem to be very organized or clean anymore; not to mention, the shelves are often quite empty. It's really bad sometimes, and the produce usually looks fairly unappealing. 

I hope they remodel this location, like they did with the one in Skyview, and put in some self-checkouts. I often find myself going to Save-On or Safeway, even if it costs an extra $5, just for the quiet, pleasant, and non-stressful shopping experience. Still, no one can beat Superstore's prices though, so it still gets three stars out of five.
The service was so terrible and the bartender was extremely rude... it doesn't matter if you have the best food in the world if you don't have good customer service.

It's so hard to find a good restaurant that you can take the kids to. One where everyone will have a good time, and there's something to eat that everyone enjoys. I am one of those parents that doesn't take their children to sit-down restaurants because I don't want to ruin other people's meals. (You're welcome.) If a child is sitting down at a restaurant with me, it's because I know that this is a place where children are welcome.

At Thr3e Wise Men, children are welcome.

First, there's free popcorn. You know it's going to be a good restaurant visit for the whole family when you get a big bucket of free popcorn. From my experience, children are always starving. They also don't understand the concept of time. They don't get why you have to wait for food, or why its taking so long, or why they can't get up and run around. Providing them with something that they like eating (popcorn) while they wait is an excellent idea. This lets the adults at the table BREATHE for a moment. Everyone is happy and crunching, not destroying things or complaining.

And the drinks. Child beverages are served in an appropriately sized cup, with a lid and a bendy straw. Yes, this is how it should be done.

There is an open treasure chest in the middle of the dining room that is filled with the kind of cheap little toys that kids love. Tops, gummy hands, dinosaur tattoos ... take your pick, kids. It's all for you!

So Thr3e Wise Men is kid-friendly. There were lots of families eating and enjoying themselves. Families won't stick out here, nor will they just be tolerated. We felt welcomed.

For all the raving about family-friendliness, Thr3e Wise Men isn't a kid place. They serve beer here. They MAKE beer here. There is an entire area of the restaurant dedicated to gigantic silver vats that have beer in them. All of the dozen or so TVs in the restaurant are tuned to sports games. Half the menu features beer and alcoholic beverages. This ain't no Chick fil-A. That's what makes the restaurant so great. Adults can be adults and drink adult beverages, right next to children who can enjoy children things. Everyone co-exists in one happy place.

This kumbaya feeling extends to the food, too. I haven't enjoyed a chef's salad as much as I have enjoyed the one I ate at Thr3e Wise Men. Lettuce, hard-boiled eggs, sliced ham, avocado, buttermilk dressing ... yep, that's a salad, but everything was fresh and in perfect proportion. I ate the off out of that salad. It was so good that I almost ignored my meatball sandwich, which would have been a shame. The meatballs are housemade, and the marinara sauce is, too. It, too, was perfect and flavorful and delicious.

We didn't get to try the beer this time. Young children in tow and all that. We are already planning our next visit to Thr3e Wise Men, though. It includes a visit without children, but a couple of glasses of beer and a soft pretzel or two.

Unfortunately this is the Rouse's closest to my house so it's the one that I come to the most. I've resisted writing this review because while they do have some typically bad employees they also have some spectacular ones. My problem with the store is with whoever is making the decisions above them. I'm leaving the store today without everything that I needed and going to another store to get the rest. The selection seems much more limited than at the other Rouse's in the area. I know that the store isn't as large as some of the other ones but  when it was a Winn Dixie I never had this problem. There seems to be acres of wasted space in the store that if it were utilized would remedy the situation. The back of the store on the right is usually a huge display of non grocery items. The  front left is a large area of baked goods, etc on tables. And there are many rows of shelves that could be extended for more stock. While the meat department has all of the standard items, beyond that good luck. I understand the designer's thought that wide aisles and open spaces are attractive to the customer but if I can't get everything that I need on most of my trips there then I will just have to start shopping elsewhere.
Tried 3 times and struck out 3 times! Wait staff incompetent and snippy. Asked for a HOT corned beef and each time it was cold. Pickles are tasteless. Save your $$$&
Extremely talented stylists! Amazing makeovers! They can make miracles happen!
& pizza's food is absolutely amazing with an outstanding staff to match. I will continue to visit at least twice a week!!

Came to town on a work related trip. Was pretty tired and not super hungry, but knew full well not eating dinner was not an option. After consulting Yelp reviews, I landed on Drunken Fish as it was less than a mile away from my hotel. I was seated immediately and a few moments later, my server arrived to get my drink order. While I perused the drink menu, I went ahead and ordered water as well as an order of edamame. Server repeats my order. Server returns with my beverage to take my order. I opted for a Starburst Roll as well as some mango sake. Maybe five minutes later, my roll and sake arrive. The roll is beautifully presented and is absolutely delicious! It is unlike any other roll I've eaten in my many years of existence. However...still no edamame. After consuming my roll, the waiter approaches once again. I ask about my edamame and he said he hadn't heard me so it was never put it in. Honest mistake, even though he repeated it initially when I ordered my water. Not a dealbreaker so I still have them 6-stars. I just requested it to go so I could eat in my hotel. I would definitely try this restaurant again when I'm actually hungry! Added bonus (and I would've given more stars for this if I could) - THEY WERE PLAYING DISNEYS BEAUTY & THE BEAST AT THE BAR! My fave Disney movie!!!

Come comfy, hungry, and ready to walk !!

Today was my first time going to IKEA and I fell in love at the entrance. The store is set up with mini showcase areas that feature specific styles of how you can decorate your home. 
I loved the setup of the store because you get to see how you can decorate your home in case you aren't great in putting things together that would look good. And with that being said, I thought that IKEA would be INSANELY expensive, but everything in the store is definitely affordable which is hard to believe because.... it's IKEA. 
Aside from the showrooms, I was so eager to try their Swedish meatballs that everyone talks about. I tried the meatball meal and it was very decent and the chocolate cake was very good (I love chocolate cake). The cake tasted like it was made with coffee in it and I assume that it brings the chocolate out. 
All in all I loved IKEA and I will be going back very very soon!! If any of you decide to go, definitely wear comfy clothes, shoes, and have plenty of energy!!!

Amazing food great staff love the fact of the vintage dinnerware the collard green grilled cheese was phenomenal great take on a grill cheese sandwich hats off to the inventor of that sandwich and this restaurant was seen on Diners, Drive in and dives.

If you're actually Irish or English, then the most important thing to know about McNamara's is that they have a real breakfast, a real "full Irish," complete with black and white pudding, rashers, and Irish sausages. As far as I know, they are the only place in Nashville that makes this possible. It's not the best Irish breakfast I've ever had, and the sausage could use improvement (you can find much better than this on the internets), but it's lovely to go and be able to have a little taste of home (and a possible heart attack) on a plate. 

McNamara's is an establishment with a split personality. There's the almost kitschy downstairs bar and restaurant, where you will often hear live diddle-y-de music (always fun), and the upstairs bar, which has big screen televisions and dart boards and has more of a bar atmosphere. I usually prefer the upstairs, but if I'm going to eat, or there's good music, I'll sit downstairs.

Things to try: 

The Breakfast (If you're not of British Isles descent, then don't give me that rubbish about "blood" pudding and how disgusting you think it is. Try it. I promise it's less disgusting than what's in a hot dog, or what's in a fast food burger, and it's tastier too, but don't take my word for it if you don't want to - that means there's more for me).

Kettle chips - They come with ranch or curry sauce. This might sound odd, but culinary choices coupled with beer often are: get both sauces and alternate chip dippings. Really, really good stuff. In fact, I am writing this review because I currently crave these chips, but am too lazy to drive to fetch them. 

Coleslaw- they have amazing coleslaw. It actually tastes like Irish coleslaw.

I really enjoyed my time here. I came here solo and appreciated the fact that they checked vaccination status and or negative test results before you were allowed to come in. I definitely came during Cowboys Saints game and cowboys are tired but their staff was more than gracious to me. My server Damon was incredible and he provided excellent recommendations before I settled on the seafood omelette and a few mimosas. If I'm ever back in the French quarter, which I probably will be in the coming month, I definitely wouldn't mind coming back here for a quick bite and some beverages.

You get a really good value here.  The rooms are cheap but they are real nice.

The hotel staff was very friendly, easy and fast check-in.  But what do we all want in a hotel???  We want clean.  This place is super clean.  I didn't do a black light or anything, but over I was impressed.  The rooms were very modern and comfortable.  

They had a pretty good breakfast bar spread in the morning.    It is close to the airport, downtown and BSU.

Microwave and mini fridge in the room.

You can't beat this place for value.

I've been to this beautiful establishment a few times now and am never disappointed.  The property stunning and the view just as fantastic.  The menu is filled with great selections and their drink menu has some fun, tasty, eclectic choices for your tastebuds.  The place is popular and busy, and can be a bit on the noisy side if you sit indoors.  For a quieter experience you may ask to be seated outside along the river.  The staff is very knowledgeable and accommodating.  You can also get their via the water taxis along the Riverfront.  Enjoy!

I attended a birthday dinner here last night. The day pictures of this establishment do it no justice. At night this place lights up and you cant help but to notice it through its large windows as you drive by. The restaurant was simple, modern, and chic. The waiter served us water that was infused with pineapple and cucumber - yum! We all ordered different items on the menu and were pleased with everything. The meals are a good portion size, are incredibly tasty, and served gorgeously. You could not order a bad thing from the menu...everything was delicious. Our party had 3 big meat eaters who were all surprisingly pleased with their "high protein" choices. 
We were a group of six and ordered about 10 dishes amongst ourselves - spending a good amount of money. It was a birthday and our waiter was aware of that - I do think it would have been nice for the restaurant to have done something special or at least brought out some kind of small dessert for the birthday girl but this did not happen. Although not absolutely necessary, this would have been nice. 
The service was excellent otherwise. Also, the price of the meals was very fair and reasonable.

By far the best bit about Nashville! Our valet at our hotel recommended we use Joyride to get from the hotel to downtown areas. We're sure glad we found out about Joyride because it made for such a pleasant way to get back & forth from our hotel! Drivers work for tips and you can either flag one down when you see them on the street or give Joyride a call and the next available driver will pick up. It's just as easy as that! We rode Joyride probably 4-5 times over our long weekend. Why, oh why would you take a hot stuffy cab or a boring shuttle when you can hop in a golf cart and whiz to your destination? Important to note that Joyride can't use freeways (it's golf cart, remember) but instead focus on the 2-3 mile radius around the downtown area.

Far and away the best sushi in the area.  I've tried other places along 202 and in West Chester, and Blue Fin wins for freshness, variety, and overall value, hands down.  My wife and I order takeout from here about every other week.  Our usual order consists of an Alaska Roll, Yellowtail Scallion Roll, Spicy Tuna or Yellowtail Roll, Shrimp Tempura Roll, and some kind of specialty roll (love the Sweet Heart Roll).

Blue Fin, if you're listening, please put your specials on your online menu so we can order them sometimes!

over priced, and awful.  i was a charged both a service charge and a delivery charge.  i was told my shrimp po'boy would be dressed on the side. it wasn't.  it also only had 8 shrimp on it.  and diced tomatoes.  who puts diced tomatoes on a po' boy? unheard of.  was led to believe fries or onion rings came with my po'boy.  they don't.  there's an up charge.  I've lived here 12 years, and it was hands down the worst shrimp po'boy I've ever been subjected too.  really bad.  better you walk across town to the riverbend area and go to cooter browns. or parkway in mid city.  or yangs.  basically any other place.  don't even get me started on the onion rings. boo daisy dukes.  boooooooo.

Love the addition to Butler's campus! The food is delicious and it's a fun atmosphere! Unfortunately the service was very slow which is why I am giving it a 4/5. We sat down right away, however people that got there later had a much longer wait on a weekend night. I ordered the grilled chicken club sandwich with waffle fries and it was delicious. The sandwich had a lot of avocado which was delicious since restaurant usually skimp on this. Overall it has pretty standard bar & grill food with some specialties.
I was not a big fan of this place. The food or the drinks weren't the greatest. I was a little disappointed.

Ok forget all the hype, this place is not good. People clearly don't know good barbecue anymore. go to the old north st. Louis that's where the good barbecue is this new style of so called barbecue is not good at all and neither are the sides. i hate to say it but its true. i want (Barbecue) and they have non. they don't have barbecue they have grilled meat with sauce on the side and yes folks there is a difference!!!!!!!
Good service and food. Space needs updating, but the food makes up for it.

My husband and I along with my parents ate dinner here lastnight. The atmosphere was great! You can tell that the business invested a lot of money into art work and the beauty of the restaurant. The food was amazing; however, the waitress "Erica" made the experience regrettable. She had so many missed opportunities, not explaining the dishes and what comes with what, she was totally transactional and overall gave us the hurry up and order and stop asking question so I can move on to the next table feeling. It was awful! No personalization, rushed through the order, even though we told her that this was our first time there. Then, after all of that this took the cake. Both couples ordered 2 appetizers however, once the bill arrived we couldn't help but notice that she SPLIT all the appetizers between both bills, despite us telling her that we wanted two separate checks. When Mom asked her why she was charged for half of an appetizer that she didn't order, Erica smirked and stated, "because you ate some too, didn't you eat some Ma"am"? In disbelief, my Mom looked at her and said, this is not to hurt you honey, this is to help you my dear, you can't charge someone for something that they didn't order, even if I had eaten all of it. Eric said "well I do it all of the time and no one has said anything to me yet". Wow! I said well we are saying something to you now. She then smiled and said, well that's fine and walked away. She still didn't ask to remove it. She missed the whole point and the chance to humble herself. We tried to give her feedback with love and support but she was not receptive. I probably won't be going back there, I just have a bitter taste in my mouth from the entire experience.

I've tried the lunch buffet (11am - 2:30pm Tues - Fri) a few times now and it's been very consistently tasty.  A few dishes rotate so it's never exactly the same, but there is plenty of variety. I am a total heat wimp, so I am basically chugging water (thankfully they are prompt with the refills!) and eating bread to help me get through, but it's still delicious. I wish there were a few mild options on the buffet, but that's my problem, not theirs. 

It's vegetarian, but you don't really miss the meat - I agree with their "best place to trick carnivores into eating vegetarian" distinction. 

The service is always quick, and since it's a buffet you can be out of there pretty quickly if you're on a tight lunch break. Due to the somewhat odd location, parking can be a little tricky when it's busy, but there's free valet if needed.

Under $10 for a quick, filling, tasty meal. Yes, please.

Dr.Blake is an excellent chiropractor that helped me with my shoulder and neck pain.  This was the 2nd Chiropractor's office that I had been to and so I was not sure if they could help me with my pain.  I am so glad I decided to come here.  After 1 adjustment, both my neck and shoulder are so much better.  I am going back every time I need an adjustment to see Dr, Blake.  I didn't have Chiroprator care coverage  in my heath insurance so coming here was a blessing as it is very affordable too.
The food is delicious. And despite waiting on a packed house the service is good.

This was my first time doing hot pot. I didn't really enjoy the experience but I don't think that was the restaurants fault. In fact everyone was really nice there. Its like 23 dollars for all you can eat and the drinks are reasonably priced. 

I got the mushroom/rib broth in one and the hot and spicy Szechuan broth in the other. We also had pork belly, angus beef, mushrooms, spinach, shrimp, udon noodles, ramen noodles, & spinach in the pot. Having mushrooms with the mushroom broth was too mushroomy for me. Everything else was pretty good, especially the udon noodles. 

We were idots for thinking we could and the hot and spicy broth. We like spicy but we forget Szechuan food does not fuck around. Not the restaurants fault, but we couldn't eat it. It was spicy and boiling hot so it almost seemed poisonous because it burned your eyes too trying to drink it. I had some go down the wrong pipe and that basically ruined the rest of the meal lol.

Ummm.... Awesome would describe it!

Nok is super nice, super talented, and the beds are super comfy (heated beds...ahhh!).

Anyways if you are in the area and want an absolutely awesome Thai massage check this place out. You will not be disappointed!
It's 24/7 so it's very convenient for me I go workout around 2 a.m I've never had issues there, solid gym overall.

We have been here twice and our last experience was subpar... Went for breakfast after church and found out they only serve pancakes until noon, the waitress suggested French toast which was disgusting. My husband had the Mexican omelette with overly gritty grits.

We went to this place on a Saturday afternoon, the court yard had the ambiance of a frat party, packed with drunk people looking too party.  We ordered the famous Hurricane drink which came in a plastic cup and was $9.00.  I am familiar with the recipe and I have had a "real" Hurricane.  This one was literally Koolaid and Everclear grain alcohol, it did have a slice of orange and a cherry though !!! So much for a signature cocktail.  LOL !!!  That being said, I would not even think of ordering food at this place.  If you like a tourist trap, this is the place to be.

I've been to a few different people for extensions and I've never experienced someone as professional and passionate as Tami.  She is extremely knowledgeable about what she does and leaves you with no questions. She's personable and so sweet so getting your hair done with her just feels like your hanging out with a friend.  Not to mention she can style the hair and make you feel like a queen! If you are looking for anyone to do your extensions I recommend that you look no further Tami is the best in the Santa Barbara/ Orcutt area and will not leave you disappointed. You'll never want to go to anyone else. 15/10!

Consider this place if the carnivore in you needs to be heard and your budget is ~$70.00 per person. Ribeye was great as well as the the Top Sirloin. Chicken?? Forget about it. The salad bar was uninspiring. Ask for Chimmichurri Sauce and thank me later.

As the star-ratings here say, Ekta is indeed "as good as it gets".  Don't be scared if you go there to pick up your food.  It's definitely understated (which is an understatement...  or something).  There is nothing about it that says it will be good.  You might even be a bit frightened.  However, I tell you that it's completely worth the trip (I say this as I've never gotten delivery).  The food is excellent, the prices are completely affordable (for those of us with less-than-normal paychecks) and the people there are very nice.  I thought the food was far superior to Tiffin and the prices were definitely easier to handle!
Not impressed.  Food was seemed to be marked up because of the prime location. Food came out quick but wasn't anything to call home about.  We won't be back.

We stopped by for lunch after an amazing boat road from the marina (the Little Toot!! Check it out too!!) and were so pleasantly surprised. The food was delicious and prepared perfectly. I ordered fish tacos. The fish was so fresh and delicious. I typically only pick at my fries and I ate every last one. My husband had the fried oyster po-boy. I tasted and even loved the fried oysters. Our waiter, Danny was great- friendly and personable. He even made dinner recommendations for that evening!!

The hostess staff were excellent and very friendly! Our waitress, Ashley, was on top of things and took very good care of us. Since this was Father's Day It was a bit crowded but this close to the beach it most likely is crowded all the time. They had live entertainment, a single guy playing a guitar singing his version of karaoke. We were sitting in front oh him so it was too loud for conversation but throughout the rest of the restaurant I imagine it was tolerable. The music wasn't bad, just loud. The ambiance is normal for this type of restaurant. Since we live close this may be a normal spot for me to escape to.

Had such a positive experience here. My husband and I went in to purchase tiles for our backsplash. First we had to return some tiles, and the gentleman at the counter handled it with courtesy and a smile. When we went to purchase our new tiles, Amry took great care of us. She told us how much we needed and put it right in our cart, then helped us pick out accessories. When we noticed a discrepancy in the price, she called a manager who came right over to help us. Chuck was able to resolve our problem right on the spot! Amry then proceeded to help us through checkout and even helped us out to our car. Definitely a great place to shop!

the atmosphere was perfect. it definitely worth going. the presentation on the food was great and the service was exellenct. 

I do have a fairly high standard and I would've given 5 starts but i feel like that would discredit some other really really good restaurant in the same area. it is actually a 4.5 (if only there is .5 in yelp). 

but definitely worth going. great for dates or business meeting.

their wine selection and menu is very outstanding. if you are in the area you should definitely pay a visit.

Pros: The Trader's Point Fromage Blanc appetizer was excellent. They use ingredients from local farms/shops. Menu is creative. Food was pretty good. The 
raspberry Italian soda was delicious. 

Cons: Space is limited in this location, felt kind of cramped and very loud. Like the other reviews said, the glasses they serve water in are ridiculously small and the waitress was not very attentive in refilling the glass vases you use to pour yourself a drink (although the vases look really cool). The waitress was not very attentive at all, we had to keep catching her attention to request refills, boxes for leftovers, and the check. 

As other reviews said, I think Napolese was okay. I would come back and would like to try the downtown location but I wouldn't plan on being a regular.

Nothing but love! So many games, some of which I didn't even realize I had forgotten. Great and attentive staff. I am excited to go back and check out new games and beers as they rotate new ones in! This place is definitely a must!

My friend and I decided to go for a massage since we were celebrating her birthday.  I found this place by searching google since we don't have a favorite place.  It is a small business located on the 2nd floor of the stripmall in Hawkstone plaza.  The parking lot is large and they are open late to accommodate those who work during the day.

I booked our appointments one week in advance and was told they needed at least 6 business hours in case we needed to cancel or change the appointment.  When we arrived, the placed smelled like mint, strong enough to clear your sinuses.  Since I am a little sensitive to strong smells, I requested unscented massage oil.  I had an one hour massage for $89.25 which I think is reasonable for this amount of time.  My massage therapist Kristie, asked if I had any special areas to work on.  I wanted a full body massage.  She was excellent, asking if there was enough or too much pressure, how I was doing, etc.

Once we were done our massages, they offered us a glass of cool water with an orange slice in it.  They also gave us a bag of epsom salts and suggested we take a warm bath for 20 minutes before going to bed.  The ladies working here were helpful and friendly.  This may be my regular place for massages from now on!

Best beer bar in the city, as far as I'm concerned. Sure, you could go to one of those mega-tap bars that keep popping up (where you get the same beers you can find at any bar or liquor store in the city), but for my money, a well-curated small tap-list is much better. So many good beers, the servers are awesome, and they allow food from the vendors downstairs!
First time eating here. I've popped in before for a quick drink, but I'm happy that I tried the food. I'm coming all the time now!! Perfect little Monday night dinner. Compliments to the chef!!!

I have been to Seminole Sea Hags twice since their opening. We have had nothing but excellent service & the food was prepared well. I had the Grouper both times and I have to say it was perfectly grilled. Grouper is not the easiest fish to cook with out overcooking and drying out. I have not experienced this problem at Sea Hags. Plan on going again soon!

Sondra Morton is the antithesis of her mission statement, she is verbally and mentally abusive to children. My 9 year old granddaughter was shamed and humiliated by being ordered to stand in a corner by Morton because she asked if she could have a sequin tee and sparkly skirt costume like the other girls instead of the boys vest and plastic pants she was given. When her mom arrived to pick her up, she was crying so hard, her face reddened and eyes swollen from the cruel bullying by this woman. When her Mom questioned Morton as to why she had ordered her daughter to stand in a corner and be humiliated, Morton raised her finger and yelled, "That's where she belongs"! 
Upon telling Morton they would not be returning and leaving, her mother was asked by 3 parents in the lobby if Morton were the one who "yells at the kids", obviously reported by their children. 
"Instill self esteem" on her mission statement is a joke, this bully Sondra Morton should NOT be working with children. My granddaughter was so hurt and traumatized she had counseling at school the following day.
She loves theater and I have been taking her to the Franklin Theater for plays for 7 years but NO MORE! 
SHAME ON CHILD BULLY SONDRA MORTON!

Long after this review gets buried in the hundreds to follow, I'll still remember the place and the exact moment the enormity of what this museum represents struck me.

The WWII museum is divided between the European and Pacific theaters.  There are exhibits, many interactive, detailing events happening at various times, both in battle, and back at home.  Several places throughout invite you to sit down, push a button underneath a picture and brief biography to hear a first person story of some small event.  If I closed my eyes to focus on the story, I could hear the inflection as he or she recounted his vignette:  The marine landing on Omaha beach who heard his lifelong friend stumble in the water behind him and when he looked back, saw the water turned red; the woman here who saw the military guard come to her door and that of nearly all of her neighbors that one summer with the dreaded telegram; the young man telling his mom she should let him enlist or he'd run away from home. . . . and on and on.  

The film, Beyond All Boundaries is a near perfect way to understand the magnitude of the period covered by the museum exhibits.  The film brings it all together.  The bubbles falling from the ceiling mimicking the snow falling on the holographic screen, the flashing lights timed with the sounds of bombs and gunfire, the seats shaking and vibrating as the planes fly overhead and the tanks appear over the hilltop on the screen, all bring the audience into the story.

Then there's the living history available here: four WWII vets.  As I talked to them, watching their expressions as they relived their firsthand battle experiences, I tried to find in their eyes the young men they were when they made the decisions they did without a reason other than that it was the right thing to do.  

There are no regrets in their voices, but rather a tinge of sorrow and respect for the friends and comrades left behind. The awe, or perhaps regret, in the faces of the people they speak with, including mine, were those for time lost with elderly relatives, teachers, coworkers, or neighbors, who remained humble and reticence to speak about their own experiences.  

There's not a lot of politicizing here, and what comes out is nothing more than the vocalized thoughts of people from that time. There's no revisionist story lines; simply the stories as written at that time and events reported and explained as though they were today, from the perspective of those who fought, lived, and wrote.  This museum is not to be missed if for no other reason than the opportunity to speak with someone who lived it, while you still can do so.
Gorgeous girls, wonderful entertainment, professional managers, attentive staff, great music, amazing lighting. What else can be said.. Hands down the #1 club in Philly!!!

I really enjoy this restaurant.

I have only been here twice and have utterly enjoyed my meals here.  

I try to always come hungry, since I somehow always decide to order a small plate, a large plate and a dessert to share with my friends.  For the small plate, I really like their duck confit.  It is succulent and flavorful.  Highly recommend it.  As for the main course, I've tried the mussels (great!) and the fish.  I forget what kind of fish it was, but it was impressive.

Try to make reservations, I think they fill up quickly!

In a word, awesome.  Brandon brought a huge amount of knowledge and, importantly, entertainment to the tour. We had a great time.  This was a fun, way to get to see the city and learn the history behind it. We especially enjoyed Brandon's knowledge and enthusiasm about the history of jazz, or should I say Jass.  Big thanks to Brandon and Erin!

Delicious truffle fries and chicken sandwich. Great ambiance with the layout & lighting. Music could be switched to a different station in my opinion to get 5 stars for ambiance . Food was delicious and the cocktails were to die for

This is HQ for auto shopping bait-and-switch. This dealership advertises that it carries pre-owned cars from multiple manufacturers: Acura, Audi, BMW, Mercedes, and so on. We called to verify specifically that they had Acura cars on the lot that we could look at. "Yes, we have several Acuras I can show you. just ask for me, Richard, when you get here!"  So we made the very long trip up Dale Mabry and found Richard waiting for us. But not a single Acura on the lot. 
"Could i interest you in a Chevy Blazer?" 
where are the Acuras?
"What about a Honda CR-V?" 
Where are the Acuras? 
"One of the guys here has an Acura but he just left." 
Your ad says you sell Acuras, and you said on the phone that you had several Acuras to show us. Where are they?
"Well I thought you would be flexible. We don't have any Acuras." 
We went back to our car to leave, but I decided to share my thoughts about this with the manager while my wife waited outside in the car. Little 20-something Richard actually followed me into the sales office and cut right in front of me to get to the manager's office first, going inside and closing the door. That was more than enough BS for me. Good bye Ferman.
Very good, lots of variety and very tasty. Great start to the morning with these bowls. I purchased the coca-cado bowl and was better than I thought it would be.
Great place for Happy Hour. We've never had the food but it looks great as they bring it out to the tables.

Finley's is cool, the food is good and the atmosphere is good. It can be dead at times which I think might be due in part to its location in a strip mall. But inside it has the feel of an authentic Irish bar, with maps, flags, the whole bit. WHen it's dead the staff is kind of slow, but where is that not the case? We lived nearby and I thought Finleys was one of the better restaurants in Largo, if not the best located.

Great place to watch any sports game. Lot's of tv's and plenty of waitstaff to take care of you. No one rushed us out while we watched an entire football game even though we only ordered one round of drinks and dinner. Food was decent and they have a large selection. 

Will probably come back during baseball season and try out some wings.

AWESOME burger joint! Huge space, would be great for groups. Staff was attentive and professional (not on their phones or talking loudly with each other). Our waiter was energetic and helpful, even bringing us a few dollups of his favorite sauces for us to try. 

I loved the ordering system! It's definitely overwhelming with all the options. I got the lagniappe (2/3 angus +1/3 sausage) on white and my fiance got the alligator on french bread - not a crumb leftover! I especially enjoyed my side of cole slaw - which had a lovely vinaigrette dressing instead of the traditional mayo-based. 

I'm so happy this place is in walking distance of home and we will definitely be coming back.

Came here on lunch during a holiday which is a plus and the atmosphere was nice.  I liked my sangria with the blend of flavors.  The harvest bowl was an excellent choice on a hot day.  The deserts fell flat.  I did not care for the s'mores, the flavors all blended together and tasted like a chocolate goop with burnt marshmallows on top.  The butterscotch cake was ok, should have more cake in it.  They had a standard kids menu, but that is way overpriced for $13 and four ravioli.  Some other menu items are also overpriced for the amount of food that it is associated with it.  They also forgot to bring out the bread basket for our table.

Cucina Maria is a unique pleasure in Philadelphia's Bella Vista neighborhood, which we could have lost were it not for owner/chef Juana Franco. It was previously known as Cucina Forte, until the previous owner closed during Covid. Luckily, Juana took it over, and more luckily, she was the previous chef for decades. That means the menu we've come to know and love will continue. 
As Italian Americans, my wife and I attest to the quality and authenticity of the fare. The pastas are particularly excellent, and Juana offers a variety. All are prepared exquisitely, with a good mouth feel and suitable sauces. the agnolotti alla pesto, a crescent-shaped ravioli filled with pesto in a tomato basil cream sauce, was exquisite in taste, preparation and presentation.
Non-pasta offerings are also excellent. I had the calamari appetizer , and it was grilled to perfection. The Veal Saltimbocca, sautéed in a wine and sage cream sauce, was generous and flavorful, tasting as though it was prepared seconds before reaching my table. Others had the grilled salmon served over a bed of spinach, which was equally tasty.
Vegetables are cooked crispy and served healthfully. 
Prices are all reasonable for the quality, and being a BYOB, makes Cucina Maria especially well priced. This restaurant is an example of the small eateries that make the Philly scene so great and fun. I encourage folks to come out and enjoy this treat. Help keep them in business.

There is nothing better than eating a fat burrito which stays together til the very end. Food Coma. 

I've only been to Santa Barbara 4 times, so I made it a point to revisit Freebirds, and this time (considering I was done with college, and not drunk) I wanted to really get a taste of what I was eating and remember it. 

I came here with a couple of other friends, per recommendation, and was not disappointed. I had a Super Burrito with chicken, which came with rice, salsa, guacamole, and sour cream. Now, it seems basic, but I mowed through this burrito like Clowney on Michigan (http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UalKtKcldvE). 

Get some chips and guacamole on the side, and call it a night when you're done :) You won't be awake much longer after.

This place is more of a 3.5 but since there aren't half stars, I decided to go with a solid 3 only because it's a chain.
First of all, some must-eat appetizers are the lettuce wraps and/or spring rolls, which come in three flavors that are all equally delicious!
Secondly, their name is "pizza kitchen" so yes, their pizza is pretty good. I wouldn't say it's anything super unique or crazy good because I know some other smaller (non-chain) pizza places do what CPK does and make "gourmet" pizzas, but they are pretty decent and worth it because the prices are decent!
Another favorite of mine is their flavored iced teas. Nothing special because I think they just pump syrup into fresh iced tea, but it tastes so good!! (Sometimes they make it too sweet, but just add some water to suit your taste.)
Overall, it's a great affordable restaurant that you won't be disappointed with!

I have eaten here several times and have never been disappointed.  The sesame encrusted salmon with mustard aioli is delicious, as is the sirloin prepared with the blue cheese mushroom sauce.  I have had this twice and it has been cooked to perfection.  Sides area also first-rate.

Saw this place on Yelp and decided to give it a try. I've mixed experience with celebrity chef joints, but it sounded good enough. 
I had the Ledbetter and a lemonade. It was a really nice taste and texture experience from the first bite. The ginger teriyaki was sweet and tangy and melded well with the pineapple salsa. The creaminess of the avocado was nice as well. Brioche is one of the better burger choices, IMO, as it doesn't compete taste-wise.  The patty itself was tender, but not mushy. Though it didn't have a strong taste, at least it didn't dominate over the other flavours. 
The only disappointment, for me, was the fries.  Not that they are bad, they weren't.  But that they were skinny. I much prefer medium to thick fries as I prefer the taste of potato the the taste of fry. 
Next time I go, I think I will order a plain cheeseburger, as that is the best way to judge the burger itself. But as a whole, I really liked the Ledbetter.

My daughter was stranded very early Saturday morning with a flat tire. Alex, the angel of a manager, went above and beyond by taking her in so she could continue her journey up to Monterey. Purchased tire and back on the road....thank you so much. We recommend this establishment because the customer first!

I decided to treat my sisters and my nephews wife to lunch hoping for a good experience. Because I live out of state I see my sisters maybe once or twice a year. The service was horrible my sisters did not receive what they asked for and when I brought it the the waitress attention she started to argue. The service was extremely slow. Had it not been because I understand the financial struggle of these waiters and waitresses I would not have left a tip as it was I only left 10%. The meal was a C plus. I won't return to this red lobster next time I'm in town.

I purchased a used car there February 2016.  We finally negotiated a decently fair price, and they (Watson chevrolet) was going to include window tint for me as part of that negotiation.  I had to leave the car there for 2 days, and never got the window tint I wanted.  The general manager was no help, and wouldn't provide the product I wanted, and frankly, deserved for buying a car from them.  I offered other options of something I would want in lieu of the tint I originally wanted, and they refused.  This is no way to treat a customer, it should have been a very simple process, but it turned into them having a disappointed customer, who will never purchase from them again, nor would I recommend anyone reading this to buy from them.  There are too many other dealerships around to have to deal with getting shorted on an agreement.

I thought it was pretty cool to visit a Milkshake place, I've never seen one and I don't have one in my home state (RI). I got one called "Rolling in The Cookie Dough," I split it with my girlfriend and it was BANGING. My only few complaints are about the line, it took maybe 20-25 to order but it was absolutely booming in there. The walls were a bit empty, you think it would've been decorated better for a better atmosphere.. and last but not least, it took probably 30 minutes to get what I ordered. I was there on a Saturday so it wasn't entirely surprising to see how busy it was. I think it was worth it regardless off wait times but I wouldn't go in expecting speedy service.

I had someone who knows food tell me about this place. I have my cheat day on Thursdays . I love donuts! How they are made and how they taste. I went directly after work. I got there a little after six o'clock .Space was clean and staff was nice but their selection was not good. Only had 3 kinds of donuts. One chocolate and the other two had cream in it. I was very disappointed  because I settled for the chocolate donut. They had cup cakes that looked great but my heart was set on a donut. It was  good but no different than ACME donuts. I will give it one more time  to see if it is as  great as my buddy said it was assuming there was less selections because it was late in the day.

A religious experience. 

The burritos, the molcajetes, the mole, the a la carte menu, the salsa bar - all amazing. My wife and I discovered this place a year or two ago while enjoying a romantic getaway. The line was out the door and down the sidewalk, but we waited and determined this would become our "spot" as we love Santa Barbara.

Yesterday, we came back for lunch after a short outing. The line wasn't as long, but it was just as crowded. The bustle of people moving around makes for a loud experience, but it doesn't matter as you're nom, nom, nomming yourself into a trance as you enjoy your meal. There were no left overs. 

The lines move quickly as does the service of your food. The wait staff is courteous and gladly answers menu questions before you order.

Truly authentic flavors that will keep you coming back again and again. I was glad to learn there is a location in Westlake Village. I'm going to gain 30 pounds....

I was pleased with the response of the rental company and their willingness to deliver for a small rental order although we had to pay $86 for delivery. Once the tables and chairs we rented arrived I was absolutely shocked at the condition of the tables and chairs. The tables were in awful shape. Thankfully we had decent table clothes for them because they looked like they had been jimmy rigged and half chewed up. The chairs we paid extra for looked dirty. We tried to clean them off but they are just beat up from being used so much there was no getting rid of the staining on them. It was definitely not what I expected when I rented the tables and chair but we made it work. I guess you shouldn't expect things to be in good condition when renting but I'm just not sure. Overall they were easy to deal with but the quality of the rental equipment was much lower than I had expected.

I'm not a huge fan of frozen yogurt, but I must say that Yogurtland really delivers where it counts (at least what is most important to me).  Good selection of flavors.  Good taste.  Texture is not too soft.  Good variety of toppings.  Decent syrups.  And very reasonably priced ($0.30 per ounce).

This Yogurtland, located on State Street in Santa Barbara, is not much different than other Yogurtlands out there.  I tried their pistachio flavor, which was good.  I also tried their lychee flavor--I definitely could taste the lychee; decent but maybe not for me.

They have a lot of toppings and I went with some Fruity Pebbles, Cap'n Crunch and bananas.  They had a lot of other stuff you would come to expect such as other kinds of fresh fruit, candy bars, cheesecake bites, chocolate chips, and so on.  They had nice big Ghirardelli syrup dispensers.  I tried the caramel and it was good.

I went a little bigger than I usually do and my total was in the $3 range.  Usually, I'm in the $2.50 range at Yogurtland.  Service was pretty quick.  There was a little bit of a line, which is often the case at Yogurtland shops.  It can be a little frustrating because I worry that my frozen yogurt is going to melt.

Of the self-serve frozen yogurt places I've tried, I would have to put Yogurtland at the top (though I could not really tell you what makes one frozen yogurt better than another one so the affordability of Yogurtland is weighing heavily here).  Cherry On Top has a lot of toppings, but their frozen yogurt is too soft.  Menchies has a lot of toppings too and their frozen yogurt has a nice texture, but they are too expensive.  There are other frozen yogurt places out there, but for me at least they don't seem to provide a significant advantage over Yogurtland.

Parking is available on the street (though hard to find); parking is also available at a nearby lot that is free for the first 75 minutes.
Great place to spend a Saturday.  Wicked fun time. Everything is nice and clean and the wine is very good.

This store is terrible I had my eight year old son with me and the man from bjs would not let me bring my son into the ladies room with me.  Made a huge scene and embrassed my son.  Said what you think he will be messed with there is men's room right there.  Anyone who is a mom of a son knows this day and age women bring their sons in with them.. Disgraceful will not be shopping there ever again.  How sad so ignorant.

This is such a great find!  Was so tired of eating so-so Chinese food so I looked on Yelp for some inspiration. This place had good reviews and we decided to try our luck. The Mongolian Beef was amazing!! We also ordered the Orange Chicken which was delish and some Chicken Chow Mein. It is the best Chinese food we've had in SB. For reference we usually go to China Palace. This will be our new spot for Chinese food going forward.

The name says it all - just good ol' fashioned home cooking! 

The chicken tenders are the right combo of juicy and crispy. They don't have too much batter, and they're not oily. 

The sweet potato casserole has a crumble on top and they're definitely from a can. I would recommend them to any sweet potato connoisseur.

Now, I'm not a creamed corn fan, but I feel like at places like Barbara's the server knows best. So at her recommendation, I ordered the creamed corn. I was not disappointed at all. Sprinkled with a little hot sauce, and I ate em up!

The pork chops are so flavorful and the roast beef had a peppery flavor. 

Top it off with some homemade pecan pie, and you've got yourself an invite into a real southern home kitchen.

I go to Leslie's for pool supplies, as they are cheaper than Patio Pools and tend to have everything that I need.  That said, there is a manager (or employee who is mostly always there) that works at this location and his demeanor could not be more insulting.
