He does not smile, does not engage customers and makes no recommendations at all.  I have a friend that is building a pool and I told her that there was a Leslie's across the street from where we work, as she was looking for several items for her new pool.  She had the exact same experience.  Being a new pool owner she had several questions and this guy, as usual, simply had nothing to offer in the way of advice or a personality.  There is another location in Oro Valley that is completely opposite.  Helpful, polite, nice and they will actually smile once in awhile and make you feel welcome.  We have both agreed that the few extra miles to deal with those people is worth it.  Also, there is a Patio Pools directly across the street from this guy!  It's like he's trying to drive people away?  Customer service is a skill and leaving in the hands of someone like this is just a great way to kill business.  It killed mine and it took no time at all to kill my friends.  This guy is one of the only employees I've ever seen working there and, perhaps, that's why he's such a grumpy jerk every time I go in.  If you decide to go there, try to make him smile, or interact with you, or have a personality. It won't happen.  It is so convenient to shop there because it's so, so close.  I've had pools for many years and know what I want, so the personality thing isn't that big of a deal.  I just knew he didn't have one.  When it affects someone that needs advice and help, that changes things.  No thanks!
Not impressed! The pool looks old, no free wi-fi and bedroom very basic. Would not go back to this hotel. Employees were very nice, though.

We've lived in Nashville for a little over a year now, and have been TRYING to find a decent Chinese food joint. This place is so amazing, from the decor, to the AWESOME service, to the fooooooood. MMmmmmmmmmmm GET IN MY BELLY

Let's say you walk into a burger joint and you say you want a cheeseburger but they're out of beef patties. Okay... how about a chicken sandwich?? Out of chicken as well. So you settle on a fried fish sandwich cuz it's the only thing left. 

That's exactly what happens here 2/3 times I come to Pokemee. This time they were out of tuna (seriously?), yellow tail, and shrimp. So I'm stuck getting salmon and albacore. This happens way too often to just brush it away as a one off situation. 

Fish quality is okay. Nothing special but not bad. The bowl itself is just run of the mill mediocre. That's not a bad thing but then you see what the establishment does to you. 

They upcharge you for everything. Extra masago (first scoop is the tiniest spoon you've ever seen), extra seaweed salad (not available from time to time), imitation crab, and avocado are all extra cost. Who charges extra for imitation crab at a poke place???

Now I get it, SB/Goleta may have higher rent rates than Orange County and lower foot traffic than LA, but they already start at $10.50 for the small bowl. That's a full $2-$4 more than you'd pay for a small bowl down south. Adding all the extra charges in addition to the overpriced bowl just pushes it past the edge. 

Customer service is hit or miss, and the spotty availability of very expected menu items at a poke shop merits a 2 star review in my book. I keep coming back since it's the only poke shop near work, but my visits are getting less and less frequent.

I really think that Borgne is one of the most underrated restaurants in the city. The service is always great and the food is even better. The wine list is short, but full of great options. That said, there is the option to bring your own bottle for a $20 corkage fee. I've never had any of the cocktails here, but I've heard wonderful things from friends. On my most recent trip we had some of the Caminada Bay oysters and they were delicious! So fresh; I seriously could have just had those for my entree. We also had the duck poppers and split the whole stuffed flounder. The kitchen was kind enough to prepare the split entree on two separate plates so we didn't have to do the dirty work of splitting an entire fish on our own. By the way, high recommend the flounder. Lightly fried and so flavorful. To be honest, everything I've had has been great here. I enjoy the casual environment and the music they play. I'd recommend Borgne to anyone looking for fresh and delicious seafood in New Orleans.
Excellent food. Shrimp curry was wonderful. Mojito was fantastic. Overall wait staff was very attentive.

I've been to Sauce in Tucson and Phx for years. I'm smitten with the fresh tasty salads- crisp healthy simplicity! Finally convinced the folks to tag along. They prefer pizza leaning more toward the sicilian 'sigi' style than thin or hand-tossed, but they obliged and Sauce won! 
We ordered two salads, a cup of soup, and two pizzas to share. Shortly after ordering, the manager, Erin, greeted us, tableside, to inform us that the salmon was actually unavailable - as it was cooking- and would not be ready- so she apologized and asked if she could offer a different entree; we switched for a different salad... i jokingly mentioned it was their first time to sauce and she was surprised... 
needless to say, the folks evidently made a big impression (as always) and for the remainder of our dining experience we felt as if we were the only table in the restaurant- royal treatment! Checking in, refilling drinks, saying goodbye, even holding the door when leaving.... it's those little things, that only take a minute... that matter...  Great food, Great Service! Kudos, to Erin and the staff, you definitely know how to get repeat customers!

We LOVE Sit Means Sit! Thanks to Elena, we are able to take our dogs anywhere!! She had taught us how to truly connect with and train our dogs in the safest and most appropriate way. Our pups LOVE her as well. We always look forward to bringing them to group training twice a week. It's our own SMS family. Elena is wonderful and so knowledgeable and we trust her greatly with our dogs and the process of training. We'd be quite a different pack without Elena and Sit Means Sit.

Even wrecking balls can't kill the spectrum.

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DO5frWtpqWU

Your typical Greek pizza place with a super huge menu. So much so it's probably hard to be great at a couple things. But delivery and take out places these days have huge menus. 

Their food is average for take. Nothing real special. But they have a couple things going for it. 

First you can buy dough by the pound. Second delivery is always fast. Third you can walk in and by pizza by the slice. 

Most places these days don't sell pizza by the slice anymore. 

I think most takeout/delivery places would get 2 stars the things I mention bring it to 3 stars.

The people here are really compassionate I really care about the work they do. I went in there about two and a half years ago to get herbs for my mother who had cancer. While I would wait for them to fill I could just smell all the wonderful herbs and I ordered a really strong tea that was delicious. Whether it is recreation or serious health issues this is the place to go! Awesome group of people can't say enough about them!

this place is so freaking good!! i'm not really a meat person and i'm very picky about meat when i eat it and this place did not disappoint. the meat is so soft and tender and the bread is so fluffy. the only thing is the sizing. i'm pretty small so a small sandwich normally fills me but for anyone else i know a small would not fill them up. it's small for the price but totally worth it cause it's so delicious
Nothing out of ordinary a lot of their grilled items have very high sodium content. I you don't work at Angie's list this should NOT be on your bucket list to visit.
Food is terrible but great venue for live music. Be aware, if you don't specify the vodka you want, they will charge you for the most expensive one they have.  What a rip off.
It's every thing they promise! Perfectly seasoned wings, delicious sauce lots of meat on the bone. Perfect French fries, really awesome donuts and Carmel sauce. This place is the bomb!

The little Tokyo on Clearview actually made me sick when it First opened, so I was hesitant to try another location. I found the location on Uber eats and ordered the hibachi shrimp. Everything was cooked perfect and tasty. Will be be back

This is a Kohl's that I always forget about, because it is pushed all the way back in the corner of a shopping center off of 86th Street. It is in a weird place, but that means it's not very crowded. At least, it wasn't when I visited on a weekday. 

The aisles were clean and organized, and the whole store was very pleasant to visit. I don't usually like buying things at Kohl's, but I find myself in one at least once a month because of coupons and returning merchandise and whatnot. 

There is a huge selection of Columbia gear in the men's section.

Ordered a seafood platter, gumbo and shrimp and oysters platter for takeout.  Everything was delicious and hot and the portions were huge.  We made 2 separate meals with a group of five!   Highly recommend

Casa Molina is pretty typical for a Mexican restaurant. They have a nice bull and matador statue outside...hahaha. A Tucson landmark pretty much. 
The food is okay. I should have ordered something else. My friend really loves the cheese. The service is slow, but that's okay because it's very relaxed. I would come back again!

Our favorite taco, Taco Shop, is not open on Sunday and we are so disappointed. We decided to try Tacos Tijiana and I am sad to say that we were not happy with the experience or food. We called in the order and when picked up, there was a lot of chaos going on which was a delay, but not the end of the world. The carnitas tacos were average and my husband said the Lengua was "ok". The asada tacos though, were almost not edible as the asada was so burnt and sooo dry. The guac was also awful, it was a thin green sauce that had zero flavor. I usually think you should try a place twice, in case it was a one off but just not sure this will be one of them.

So disappointed in our lunch today. The service was extremely slow. The salad I ordered arrived missing dressing and 3 ingredients listed  in the menu description. When I asked for the missing ingredients they said they were out of them. Probably should have mentioned that when I ordered. I waited 15 minutes for the dressing to arrive.
My husband ordered a desert (apple, raspberry crisp)  and jokingly asked if they had all the ingredients. The waiter checked, came back and said actually they were out of raspberries. Waited another 20 minutes to get our bill.
We will not be back.

Let me start by saying that I moved here from a city that had almost as many indian restaurants as american, so I realize I'm polarized for having that luxury.

I ordered for two to-go.  I ordered spicy (~7-8/10) madras curry and my friend ordered spicy korma (also 8/10), and they were really underwhelming.  We asked the waitress what she thought of the spice scale and she said to her, something incredibly spicy would be a 9, so we thought we'd go just slightly below that to make sure we were on the same page.

They weren't spicy, were a little watery and the portions were disappointing.  For $40, we ordered two entrees (with rice), peshwari naan and kheer and the portions were so small, we were still hungry.  

I wasn't impressed enough to go back.

I went there on dec 29, they were having a deep cleaning and the store was closed, but I did not see anything from Yelp. Better calm them before you go. 

The Indian restaurant was delicious. Anyway, surrounded by good cafes and very mix cultures.

I give it an average rating because it is just like any bar food joint around. They all get their frozen heated up or fried food from the same suppliers.  When I want a meal like this I get it out of the freezer and warm it myself for a fraction of the costs. If you like this kind of fare and don't like eating at home it is a good option because it is a clean, friendly alternative to many of the local bars. It seems to be well managed by folks that like what they do. My daughter and G-kids like the place so I guess it is just a generational thing. To put my comments into perspective: When I go to a bar, I go for the cold beer and social atmosphere not the food.
Employee that was working the   coffee station picked her nose & flicked it on the floor  just prior to restocking the splenda.  For now on I'm going to wawa
Easily the best pad thai in Boise. The staff is friendly and the service was quick. There is usually a bit of a wait to be seated but that's to be expected at a place this popular.

I am a big fan of fresh fish and OysterCatchers gets an A+ for food and service.  The fish entrées can be prepared several different ways accompanied by your choice of side sauce.  There are a few non-fish items on the menu but the focus is definitely fish.  They also have a great dessert menu which features a nice cigar selection for smoking out on their bayside deck.  If you are staying at the Hyatt hotel you will need to take the 100+ yard boardwalk or the hotel shuttle to get to the restaurant.

Nice hotel with a beautiful view of the ocean. My room wasn't ready when I was checking in, so I was upgraded to an ocean view room. It was amazing. The ocean view rooms are $700+ so keep that in mind when booking a room. 

Hotel staff was very nice and the rooms are clean and big. I would stay here again if I win the lottery or have some unknown great aunt die and leave me her estate.

Recently received my ccw renewal and was very pleased with the staff and facility.  Jerome was an excellent instructor who introduced hypothetical situations that made you think outside the box.  His practical knowledge enabled explanations that were easy to comprehend.  The virtual shooting portion of the training was exciting and informative.

Wow. All the other reviewers were dead on. These guys suck. The sales rep seemed to promise a lot , but they never seem to deliver. My lawn progressively got worse over the last 8 months. I got fungus in my lawn and they failed to treat it even after several requests. I finally demanded a supervisor to show up and never got a call or visit. I found a sign in my yard after I fired them, but no one even called to speak with me.  Several of my neighbors have experienced the same problems I have and will be firing them.

End result is that my lawn is horrible and dying.

Horrible customer service.

My husband and I had a very enjoyable experience at Grapeseed last night for dinner! I used the nowait app, so we only had to wait about 10 minutes, we had a drink at the bar, the brown derby and macklind mule... Both delicious. We of course had the turkey nachos because they came so highly recommended, they are very good. We split the burger and the pork chop entree, the burger was good, but the pork chop... Hands down, one of the best things I eaten in a long time, I don't usually order a pork chop out, so glad I did! My only complaint about the restaurant is the noise level, I guess it comes with the big open design of the restaurant which is very pretty, we'll definitely be back, there was so much more on the menu I would like to try.

Decent bar food with a nice rotating craft list. Pitchers is a locals bar that has nice bartenders and darts in the back.  Great bar for a chill Sunday. Weekend nights can get a little packed and the layout of the bar makes it feel a little cramped when you don't have a seat. Overall a bar I frequent  to just grab a beer.

You can order delivery from here on Yelp.  I heard its a very college kids kinda pizza and sandwhich joint after I placed my order.  Was happy with the pizza we got and how quickly it was delivered.  Will avoid the other items I have heard negative things about but this place was good for what we needed!
Coffe and French pastry... how can you go wrong. Delicious. Employees are very friendly and attentive. This is a great weekend quick-stop if a full breakfast isn't what your looking for.

A really pretty place, with great service.

The only issue is the price.
We checked online and the Google result said rooms start at $89.
The website said $109 is normal.
When we checked in, rooms were $160 before any other add-ins and tax???
Heck no.

But the manager registrar asked us what we their website quoted, and said they'd honor that quote.
However, our card was then charged $300 something for two nights.

Hopefully, they'll amend the issue tonight before we leave, because this place is so pretty and pleasant!
Lattes are yummy and so are the muffins. Service is good. It's a go to place for coffee when I'm in SB.

While the better half and I speculated that El Hornito Bakery was "The Horny Bakery" because Mexican Bakeries makes us Horny, it's actually The Little Oven.

The rules are simple. Show up, Take a tray, go from display to display, choosing all the delicious baked goods you want, go up to the counter pay for your goodies, head home at eat all your goodies. 

There are a lot of varieties and the looks can sometimes be deceptive to the taste. But the prices are low enough that you won't be saddened by any amount of experimentation. 

If you haven't been here, you should absolutely give it a try.

Acero is outstanding. It is exactly what I wanted I Fratellini to be. The food was wonderful, the service was very friendly, the dining room was not claustrophobic and there wasn't a hint of pretension to be found.

I opted for all three specials of the day: root vegetable and lentil soup with sausage, bolognese over creamy polenta, and a skirt steak with mashed potatoes and roasted cipollini onions.

I could have eaten a bucket of the soup--it was incredibly flavorful. In fact, I'm going to attempt to recreate it this week, and if I achieve 50% of the flavor I will be very happy with myself.

The polenta dish was also very flavorful. The bolognese was perfect in my opinion but the dish generally lacked texture. A little crostini would have put this one over the top.

The skirt steak, again, had great flavor. I couldn't tell if it had a marinade or not but I noticed a hint of balsamic. The mashed potatoes were solid but under seasoned, nothing a little table salt and cracked pepper couldn't fix though. My only gripe with the entree is that it came to the table at room temperature. You know when a medium rare steak sits at room temperature for a while and it gets that certain texture and hue? It had that going on. I was, however, not deterred and ate the entire thing.

In an effort to achieve total food dominance, I had dessert too, in the form of delicious bread pudding. It was remarkable in that I noticed overt hits of lemon throughout, something I was not expecting at all.

I give Acero four stars because it is an outstanding restaurant overall. The price point matches the food quality, service and general experience. Acero is joining my rotation of restaurants, especially restaurant recommendations.

Hands down, the best Italian subs in all of Santa Barbara.

I love coming here for their Super Deluxe sandwich. It's a wonderfully fresh and tasty true Italian submarine that just tastes awesome. I had one for dinner tonight, and the bread tasted so fresh. It was heavenly. I know that they have other sandwiches as well, but I always stick to my tried and true. 

This is also a nice place to come to if you're making an Italian dish and can't find that one authentic item  on the recipe at the grocery store. Tino's probably has it!

My grandmother has memories of coming here as a SBHS student in the late 1940s. The location may have changed, but the sandwiches and family service are as solid as ever. 

The new location is a vast improvement over the old. It's bright, modern, and fresh, while peppered with old family photos. Tino's legacy will forever live on here. 

I look forward to coming back soon!

Hands down the best hair cut experience I've had...just by looking at their website you get good vibes, and a sense of professionalism on behalf of the staff. Oswaldo is awesome - he cut my hair; my brother also goes here and has had both Oswaldo and Eric, both of which style hair with care and precision. For someone like myself who is extremely particular when it comes to getting a haircut, I have no worries at Philly Bloke...very cool place for sure. It is a relief to have found a new spot after losing a stylist I was going to for nearly ten years!!

Came here just for a bowl of chowder after enjoying the chowder at the City Walk location in Orlando. I am sure this is the same recipe but it just didn't seem as good. I am thinking it was because in Orlando my granddaughter mooched most of it.  Wished they did not have tables so close to each other. Actually had waiter bump me twice and step on me once waiting on the table next to us.

I love a good burger joint and this one is great. I had an intense craving for a Frostop burger and root beer and I satisfied it today for lunch.

The burgers are made to order, so be a little bit patient. It will take just a few minutes, but the wait will be worth it. Juicy meat, good pickles (this is a MUST), and crinkle-cut fries that are perfectly seasoned. 

Don't forget the root bear, which is excellent. Be sure to put it in a frozen mug, which is right next to the fountain machine. 

I need to go back more often. Maybe for dinner tonight...?

P.S. Bonus points for old-school pinball machines!

We loved loved the jjajangmyun. It was exactly what we were looking for. It is so hard to find a place that can do the sauce right. Tangsuyok was also amazing! It was light and crispy and tender all at the same time. I highly recommend this place for Koreanized Chinese food!

Best Tom yum soup I've ever had. Veggies were fresh and all the favors were right there. I've been all over trying to find something comparable. I'm giving a 5 stars just for the soup but everything else is just as good too

I'm a big gym goer (I know... not a word). At different times, I've wanted different things from my gym. At this point in time, I need a place I can go in between sales calls to make sure I get a workout in. This is the Planet Fitness I frequent the most. The staff is friendly, they have tons of cardio equipment, and offer all of the fabulous black card amenities. 


I'd not recommend this gym for competitive lifters, those looking for the best tanning beds ever, or those looking to create gym friends. All of which I've wanted in a gym at different times of my exercising life. 

This is a great gym at a great price for the get in and get out work out.

Seriously the worst pizza place on the planet. Never gets anything right. We've been nice and tried this place twice. How hard is it to only put pepperoni on half of a pizza? And how difficult is it to remember to throw a ranch in the box? Don't waste your money. A $7 pizza isn't even cheap enough to deal with the incompetence here. Thanks for nothing. I'd rather eat a frozen pizza, at least then I'll actually get what I pay for.

If I could eat pizza every day, I would be at Square Pie all the time.  Thick Pies are right up my alley...Service is great and the small restaurant is enjoyable.  I promise this pizza is unlike anything you had before.

This park is great with walking paths, grassy fields, and playgrounds for the kids! On Tuesday's in the summer, the farmers market is awesome. There are food trucks, local vendors, and farm stands for fresh veggies. So many young families visit this park and it's a great place to relax, walk around, or jog. There is also a community center that has descent gym offerings. Living in the area, I am so appreciative to have a park like this close by!

Ok so we have been coming here for a couple of years now.  
Always it has been a great experience: food,service. 
Tonight. Not so much. Service was slow. Had to get up to ask for more water and to get the bill. 

On to the food.  
Menu has changed. 
In my opinion not for the better. The new cheese dish is just brie with some cranberries. Nothing impressive i can make that, so can you. 
Next, we had the mac n cheese, again not that impressive. Really bland, really small. Totally overpriced. 
Smoked shrooms amazing.  I wish i would have just ordered 3 and not wasted my money on the brie or the mac n cheese. 
Ordered the tacos also, they are still good. Not the same, but still they were good. 

Miss the bread and pickles. 

Really torn on weather or not we will go back. 
It was a favorite

I should actually give this two scores.  For food and coffee I'm definitely a fan.  Both are great so 4 stars.

On the other hand, I tend to do a lot of work at coffee shops and my one complaint about Fido's is that it's practically impossible to find a good seat that has a power outlet.  The only ones are really along the front corner window where there are a few bar height seats, but you get nailed by the sun there.  For this reason I don't actually go there very much.  That gives it 2 stars for it's comfort/conduciveness to a good coffee shop for working.  

Hence, my 3 star review.  So if you're just in it for the food and drinks (or if your laptop has a fantastic battery) I'd recommend it.  If you're looking for a place to spend some longer working hours I would suggest Edgehill Cafe around the corner.

Okay, let me start this off by saying that I love airports. The hustle plus the bustle, it's fantastic. Many others have commented on the decor and atmosphere of Phila Intl. I don't get it, who is looking at walls when they are leaving on a jet plane? Not me, that's for damn sure. I'm too busy looking at people, and there is no better place to look at people than an international airport. I can't help but chuckle when I see people engrossed in their laptops or magazines. Nothing could be more interesting than what's going on around them, but instead, they choose to be reading reviews on yelp.com or catch up on what Brangolenia are doing in Africa (other than adopting babies) in US Weekly. 

Don't get me wrong, I'm all for reading (my mom's a librarian so its in my blood I guess), but there's a time and place for everything and the terminal at Phila Intl is not it. The Intl airport is like a hilarious sociological experiment. There are so many variables; are flights delayed, overbooked, on time, etc? So many factors that affect traveler's moods; luggage getting lost, babies crying, drug sniffing K-9s patrolling. Show up a few hours early and take some time to look around. I promise you won't be disappointed.

When were on vacation visiting Philly, we went to this restaurant after almost every place in the area had a waitlist and we were quite hungry and in the mood for Indian food. 
There were four of us and decided to share the pav bhaji, bhel puri and fries. 
The bhel was sweet but okay in flavor, the bhaji in the pav bhaji isn't the traditional bhaji that's normally served. The flavor was off and the fries were actually spoilt. The potatoes had a very bad aftertaste and when I pointed this out to the guy there, most probably the owner, he was quite rude and asked what I wanted him to do about it and that I was the first complaint that day! We ended up getting the paratha and paneer curry but again the paneer was horrible and I guess it was some frozen curry he just thawed and served us.
The two stars are for the bhel which was okay despite being on the sweet side and the fact that he didn't charge us for fries. We left almost everything on our plates.
Bad food and bad attitude!

Been here a few times and never reviewed... Cruised in here for lunch the other day. Landed right in the middle of the lunch specials from 12-2. Most of the options (6 total) landed around $7 or $8 each.

I had the Chicken Tenders lunch special w/ fries. Very small chicken tenders. Luckily I didn't start out very hungry because if I had been my normal hungry self this never would have done it for me.
My girl had the 12 wings with a salad ($0.99 upgrade charge). She did 6 w/ medium sauce and 6 w/ garlic parm. She said the medium was excellent but the garlic parm was too covered in sauce. She said it felt overloaded.

I was also instructed by her to include that the bathroom has Bad paper towels and foaming soap. Neither the soap nor paper towels allowed her to get her hands as clean and she wanted post wing demolition.

For a wing place you def. need to step up your bathrooms and maybe even throw in some wetnaps table side.
Very good enchilada omelette!!  Good coffee and a family atmosphere. The service is excellent and I believe I even saw the Owner bussing tables!!!

Dr. Fish has been my eye doctor for years, as well as my family's. He is caring, has a state of the art eye practice. His staff is very knowledgeable, and accommodating. I recently had cataract surgery, with a procedure shortly after called a Yag. Was dealing with some discomfort in my eye, and Donna scheduled me in the same day I called. This is a wonderful practice, if you are searching for a great Doctor and  staff, search no more.
The food and drinks were amazing here! The staff was very friendly. Great vibe. If I was a local this would be my go to place

This place is amazing! I have been here about five times and every time the service has been amazing and the food even better. The Duck Rolls are delicious. The shrimp hibachi is amazing. What I had tonight is a side of fried rice, side of hibachi vegetable, and the amazing gyoza. Mike is such a sweetheart. Definitely give this place a chance, so great!

Awful service! I was expected to cancel with 60 days notice on a month to month contract after the initial year on my contract was up. In the end I paid for a quarter of pest control I didn't even need or receive.  Avoid these guys...awful way to treat a customer of 2 years.

I was extremely impressed by the professionalism and cleanliness of the entire staff and the quality of work that I recently had done with Steven. In light of the current COVID situation. I felt comfortable and safe with the policies that they have put in place  in order to continue to provide services while being mindful of everyone's overall health. I highly recommend Steven! I LOVE my new artwork and I will be back to have more work done soon!

Great openness to the street.

Food was ok. 

One item on the sampler plate was inedible. The server subbed an order of bread pudding.

Very nice.

We would eat here again.
We first ate here in 1981 , when we moved to Tucson. In town visiting and had to stop. Still outstanding. Had the tacos with a cup of Gazpacho soup. Delicious.

Ive eaten here twice and both times the orders were screwed up. The first time I called for a to go order and ordered a pulled pork sandwich. I get there and had to wait 10 min almost making me late for work. The barmaid goofed up my name rhyming with another saying ah, those names are all alike whats the difference??                       
I said nothing but thought, its people like me who are the reason , you schmucks have a job!!!! Got a dry , burnt ends sandwich. Yum  
2nd time called up and ordered almost $200 of food for the next day for a group at work. Again I had to wait 20 extra min and again almost late for wrk. I was told its because they were finishing up wings. You had 18 hr notice? How much time you need to plan ahead???!!
Pulled pork was a bit better this time  but average at best. Service wasnt too great considering I dropped almost 2 hondo. They charged me for plastic utensils and paper plates and we ordered buffalo wings 
and the wings were good except they were plain wings! My buddy called and talked to them and placed an order for the following night and we actually received a bunch of free buffalo wings. Supposedly this order was over 100 dollars. I did eat the wings but nothing else. But with this kind of cash forked over, the service received was dissapointing. Yes they made restitution but too little too late. I cannot recommend them. I have moved on. I find them to be way overrated, & have spotty customer service. Two stars cause they weren't rude, and eventually tried to make a little bit right. Good Luck should you chose to go there.

This place has great food and service! We walked over for a late lunch after seeing the WWII museum, and were delighted by the experience. Our food was delicious (especially the fried gator!) And our server was super friendly. She even have us a great recommendation beignets to avoid the craziness of Cafe Du Monde!
Wow you really have to for eat here before you leave New Orleans it's a must. Just go you won't regret it. Food is AWESOME!!!

If I could give El Charro 10 stars, I would. This is the best Mexican food in the entire world. My favorite is the carne seca, there is no other restaurant in the US with anything quite like it. 

Try anything and everything on the menu, you won't be sorry!

Green Curry w chicken  on a bed of rice! 
It was average, could be better, almost could not taste any blend of Spices, the chicken was frozen and ready sliced which anyone can find it in the frozen section of any grocery store, Green and red  were fresh, could barely taste any Wild Ginger, or Lemongrass, or Galangal, or Kaffir lime which is supposed to give a kick of awesomeness flavor and aroma! Wish they would use local poultry instead of frozen

It's true that tours can be hit or miss depending on what guide you get. Today we got the infamous Dave from previous yelp reviews. This guy was excellent! He knew dates, names, side stories, and had a great personality. I noticed some other guides while we were walking around the cemetery and they were clearly not as knowledgable or enthusiastic as Dave was. He made it worth well more than $20.

All-around excellent! Interesting flavors (including flavors that are only available to those over 21 years old), delicious ice cream and combinations (they have an ice cream sandwich that is 2 scoops nestled between brownies) and a fun atmosphere!!!

The staff and owner are also very friendly and helpful. 

The location is odd - when you find the shopping center, you need to drive around to the back.

I'm a bit underwhelmed. Having had a disorder in law from Thai land that owned a local restaurant I like to say I'm well versed in Thai food and the depths of the flavors. I always order yellow curry. It's my go to dish.  The flavors were good. Not as spicy and I though. I like spicy and can usually handle a Thai 10. The curry base was thinner than I'm used to and separated immediately upon arriving home. I'd still order it again. The garlic and pepper beef was disappointing. It had subtle sweet hint which is typical of Thai food but the flavors were very bland. Again no spice, no depth. It's was like eating boiled beef with three pieces of broccoli.  The garlic sauce wasn't a sauce but more a stock. Very thin and watery.  The portions were however good for the price. No here comes the good part. The mango and sweet rice. This is an absolute must for me. And while I do know it's season the mango was perfectly ripe and the rice was good.  I was longing for a side of coconut cream that usually accompanies this dish. I know restaurants have off days so I'll probably give this place another shot.

We had a hot water heater go out on us. They came out from the Duke Energy program we had. They called within 5 minutes after getting our appt. They were so courteous, professional, knowledgeable. They replaced the old water heater and it was cleaned up too. I would definitely recommend and will use them again.

Osteria is by far the most sophisticated and a true stand out from all the typical boring South Tampa restaurants and with such a beautiful design. 

From the immediate time you walk in, the outside bar and seating is unlike any other place in the area. From back of house to front, service is impeccable. The staff is fully knowledgeable of all dishes and give great recommendations. 

I took a client from out of town here and he was amazed. The fresh mozzarella appetizer is amazing! (A must get) For our entrees I went with the Kobe meatball with fresh home made pasta and my guest got the rigatoni in the jar, WOW!! The deserts were incredible and drink service was on their A game, Allen and Kate are incredible. This beautiful restaurant just upped the game in south Florida and we're so lucky that we came across this gem. Bravo' to the staff and ownership that blessed us with this heavenly spot. Cheers and good luck, this is a for sure winner!

Went on the opening night and it filled up pretty quickly. Found a seat at the bar and within minutes I received a menu and great service from the bartenders. Everyone was very friendly and the atmosphere was very welcoming. Decor is beautiful, but it is very tight. When we were finally seated it felt very cramped and it was like everyone was on top of each other. Overall the drinks were creative and tasty and if you like sweet cider go for the blackberry one. Food wise mostly finger food selections. The loaded fries are a must, but I wish we had passed on the beef kabobs the toast/chip/bread was teeth breaking and bland. Overall it was a nice experience, I'll definitely go back for drinks with the girls.

When we arrived the host said our table was set up in the bar area at a high top table for 8- I actually called them directly to verify reservations that morning and also advised it's for 10 people.  Yelp only allowed me to select 8, but I did add a note saying we had 10. I advised high top will not work since we had two elderly people in our group. The host was very rude and advised me that anytime they get reservations through yelp that's where they put you. There were other tables and she didn't offer to put tables together or anything. So I said well we will split the group up and take these 2 booths. She said she had to go ask someone if that would be ok-really.  Both tables ordered a pitcher of Sangria - it looked like they just pour wine into a pitcher. There was no fruit in it at all and the glass pitcher was so freaking heavy- not that that matters I guess- just wanted to add that. The devil crab was wonderful. Fried green tomatoes were not. Looked and tasted like they dipped the tomato slices into unseasoned flour and fried. Also one persons order had to be returned because it was so salty he couldn't eat it, but the black beans and rice had no salt. I've eaten at Carmine's often for lunch. Never dinner and never will again. Have to say our waitress was very nice.
Super friendly and helpful staff! Very safe social distancing setup as well.

Tonight was our first time at this restaurant. It definitely will not be our last. I rarely rate any establishment a Five Star but in this instance it was totally deserved.
Hubby and I came with another couple about 5 and we were promptly seated. It was not busy which made me a little nervous I confess. We were warmly greeted and our waitress took our drink orders which gave us some time to review the menu. Actually we had pulled up the menu on their website so had a heads up. The waitress was very helpful as I had some questions about the preparation of some items. Two of us  chose the liver and onions, one the chopped steak and one the open faced roast beef platter. None of us were disappointed..portions quite ample, real mashed potatoes, large side salads and the salad dressings tasted homemade.
Believe me when I say...go . You will be so glad you did.

Eh, I would give this place 2.5 stars if I could. But I am a glass half full kind of girl so I rounded up. The concept is ice cream with no guilt. I have read over their nutritional info and this does seem to be true. It's lower in calories, fat, and carbs than frozen yogurt, significantly so. If you don't load up with a sprinkled chocolate waffled come and candy toppings like I do it's a pretty guiltless treaty. (I am not using the word healthy because I am sure it's also full of chemicals). Some of the flavors are actually quite tasty. The oreo flavor I had the other day was actually quite tasty.

The problem is the consistency. I don't know if the mix they use varies a lot depending on who makes it or what. But sometimes I go there and the ice cream is totally tasteless. It's like eating cold nothing! Then, other days ts great. There doesn't seem to be any rhyme or reason to what's good and not either. I have had the same flavor on different days with different results. 

The other day I decided to capitalize on the good Oreo day and get a quart to go. When the cashier told me my total I thought she must have made a mistake. It was $10. For 4 servings of take home ice cream. Which I could purchase fo about a quarter of that price at Publix. I won't be making that mistake again.

You are taking a gamble if you go hear, but at least it won't effect your waistline!
What a wonderful experience and dinner. Knowledgeable staff, wonderful food, great atmosphere.  Truly something to treasure and enjoy in Nashville.

Ambiance: 3/5
Food: 4.5/5
Service: 3/5
Value: 2/5

AWESOME little bar. Huge huge selection of beers to drink there, fill a growler, or buy by the bottle. It's just beer, so you might not plan your whole evening around it, but you have to check this out.

It is a really small shop, so maybe don't bring the whole crew, but like I said, you can fill up a growler and quench a lot of thirsty friends with that.

I can't give a review of this exact store location of My.Suit, but I can tell you that I have a suit from this company (measured at one of the New York CIty locations), and I cannot praise its quality and craftsmanship enough. I have several custom, made-to-measure suits from some of the other top-listed companies in Philadelphia, and my navy My.Suit suit is the best of the lot. I get more compliments when wearing this suit than any of the others. It has held up after a few years of constant use.  The suit fit perfectly upon receipt--unlike my experience at the other shops in town, which required multiple sets of alterations. And the price point cannot be beat. 

I am looking forward to adding another My.Suit suit to my wardrobe soon.

Came with a group of 4, all ordered pho. There were small bugs in my food. I thought it was a random freak thing but two out of my three dining mates subsequently found the tiny bugs in their food as well. Could almost have been mistaken for large pepper flakes. Were not. The restaurant was apologetic, offered to redo the dishes and certainly did not charge us, but needless to say I would never going there again. I would NOT feel safe eating there.
Peter is a great masseuse. He can sense my sore spots and really helps to alleviate the pain. I recommend Spa Haven

I received a gift card from my husband for my birthday.  I stopped in the day before and scheduled a 30 min massage with Chris and also asked adding hot stones and was told that was fine.  I arrived at the appointment on the day of my standard 10 minutes early, filled in the paperwork and they asked about add ons.  I told them I already added on the hot stone.  They called back to the masseur to let him know I wanted hot stones.  I checked my phone before going in and it was 12:08.  I got called back, did the standard change and got on the table.  Chris came in and completed a nice massage but it felt like it only lasted a minute.  At the end of the massage I looked up and saw the clock read 12:30 to the second.  My 30 minute massage was actually less then 20 minutes.  
I complained at the front desk and they took 10 dollars off the price, so I paid $49 for a 20 min hot stone massage, it was no bargain.  I still have some gift card left over from my husband so will be back to finish that off and then not again.  And I will definitely be using another masseur in the future.  Hope my other massages go better then my birthday one.
So yummy! And the nicest staff. We love this place. Just make sure to order early, they run out fast!

It is a shame I felt the massage I did receive there was nice and that was a five star experience. Unfortunately that prompted me to get a monthly package and I am beyond disappointed in how that was handled. Cancellation was difficult and never confirmed (I called to confirm myself, they had to do it via email as their system was down), and again a minor issue in the scheme of things but annoying. I was basically never going so I decided to cancel and had wracked up several massages. To use them up I decided to give one as a gift for a $10 fee (seems like a stiff charge for handing me a gift card, but frankly I just didn't want to waste it). During my call a month later I placed to confirm cancellation I was told the gift card recipient had 60 days to use the card or the massage would expire, which I was NOT told at time of purchase of the gift card. I was very upset to give a gift that has such a short expiration on it. I'm waiting at this time to hear if management will make it right and crossing my fingers they don't also try to charge me again....

My experience here is strictly for the haunted house review, not the general visit. I came here the halloween haunted house 2 weeks before halloween. Without trying to ruining much of the surprises, I'll make a few commentaries.

1. it's worth money, especially it's your first time
2. get the fast pass: it'll save at least 30-45 min, which is even more important if it's cold outside
3. wear warm clothing, as each of the room is separated by a 5-10 minute wait outside

I am not a big fan of haunted houses, but this is definitely worth the trip! All of the actors are incredibly passionate about this event and really get into the mood and will scare the bejebus out of y'all. They even have a special event where you can op-in in a 'full-contact' experience, where they may separate you from your friends and take you to a special area of the haunted house.

Alright, enough spoilers. Point of the story is that this was a great haunted house experience, and when the weather warms up, I'd like to revisit this historical site and learn more about its history in the light of day.

Not our first visit! Oh maybe our 24th.

I gave it 5 stars because I couldn't do 6, or 8, or 10.

The food is always amazing. The roasted artichoke is to die for.

I am a vegan and it is never a problem. the gnocchi and eggplant was amazing.

The service is second to none.

If you are in striking distance...just go!

I drove around the maze for 8 minutes looking for a parking space. I gave up because my 8 year old was nauseous. It took another 6 minutes to find my way out. They charged me $1 to get out and the "lady" was VERY nasty. Rip off rules

Very disappointing experience.  I have been going there for years and loved Teagan and "yoga hour."  The owner told me that Teagan was like family and that she was keeping the "yoga hours" so I paid for a full year.  Two weeks later the owner got rid of all "yoga hour" classes and Teagan is no longer teaching there (not by choice I am told).  The owner flat out lied to me and knew there was no way I would have paid almost a thousand dollars upfront for the year if she told me the truth which she knew when she took my money.  
The good news is that Teagan is now teaching a few classes at Sanctuary at 13 and Locust!

I hate giving this restaurant one star because I love Applebees. The food is good and the servers are friendly but this location refuses to turn the heat on in the winter to any level that would make indoor dining comfortable. I'm sitting here right now in my ski jacket, hat, and gloves. It sucks to have to eat with your gloves on inside. I recently ate at the Applebees in South Lake Tahoe on a frigid night but it was so nice and warm and comfortable in the restaurant. I made the mistake of coming back to this Reno location just because I was craving the food but it was a huge mistake. There is ice cold air blowing on my food and I don't understand why because it's 50 degrees outside. I will make the hour drive to SLT rather than returning to this location.
Hot bar ridiculously over-priced. I paid $14 for some chicken and fried rice that would have cost about $7-8 dollars at low quality Chinese restaurant which is what this food was.
Staff was lovely.  My caprese salad was to die for!  The purple haze beer was delicious.
This was the best escape room experience ever! We had a blast! West was awesome!!! He was very helpful and friendly! Would love to come back!!

Upon moving in I found there was carpet in my unit, while advertising only mentioned concrete floors. I found so much pet hair I had to have someone disinfect it. There have been many let downs- the hallways always smell like weed, there is almost always dog poop in the hallways, there is zero notice about your packages being delivered to the front office. My unit had always felt very damp despite there being two dehumidifiers running at all times. The air conditioning was never as cold as I wanted. Some pairs of my shoes had been completely ruined by the humidity in my apartment causing them to grow mold.
Just have to say, best ice creams I have ever tried. Flavors were amazing and texture was perfect. If you stop by Santa Barbara you shouldn't leave without trying these delicious ice creams!

Horrific!
About five years ago a relative had brought my small pickup truck here for service. They removed & never put back two screws, which was causing my vehicle to make a rattling noise. The dedicated muffler/exhaust system shop in Marana over on west Ina & Thornydale discovered this.

Had a great time dining and drinking at the outside bar last night at Rumfish Grill. Atmosphere was fun and lively. Bartender Eric was attentive, helpful with drink suggestions and helped us have fun celebrating my husband's 40th birthday. We tried the MaiTai, which was a great drink, although it sneaks up on you, and sunset ale, as well as other drinks! All good! Also had the coconut shrimp, and shared a cheeseburger. Both were exceptional! Will go back for sure! Thanks Eric!!
The linguine marinara with shrimp is to die for. The seafood gumbo is pretty good, too. I highly recommend you eat here. Tastes like Jersey and Brooklyn.

THE WAITLIST ON YELP IS A HOAX.  We have been to Arpeggio many times and decided to go for dinner on a Saturday night.  We signed up on the Yelp waitlist which indicated a 70 - 100 minute wait.  We moved from 32 to 8 at a pace which would made us believe the estimate was accurate.  When we arrived at the restaurant we were number 7 on the Yelp waitlist and the next table for 2.  The hostess still told us it would be 30 - 40 minutes.  Although surprised by the time quoted considering we were the next 2 (at least according to Yelp), we decided to wait.  While waiting we saw THREE parties of 2 being seated (which should have been impossible since we were the next 2 on the waitlist).  After not being seated after an hour from when we arrived and spoke with the hostess, we left.  On the way out we confronted the hostess about keeping TWO LISTS, one for Yelp and one for walk in customers. She did not deny that the restaurant kept multiple lists.  I am not sure if Yelp maintains standards for restaurants who use their waitlist function -- if it does, Arpeggio should be removed.

This is an okay Nordstrom Rack, I think it's slightly a step below the selection in the one in Brentwood, but that's ok. 

Here's the lowdown on this Rack. It recently opened in the giant shopping complex that houses Wal-Mart, Ulta, Carter's and Sam's Club just east of the highway and across the street from Uncle BIll's Pancake House in West County. The size of the store is bigger, and it has more space to spread its merchandise around - it also gives more space to the men's section (always the afterthought in everyone's mind for department stores), so that's a plus. 

All this being said, I don't think the selection is quite as good as other Racks I've visited, but honestly, it seems to change every time I'm at the Rack. Sometimes it's a treasure trove of good finds, other times everything's a miss. This one looked okay at time of visit.

But that's beside the point as the first time I came here I came on a mission: I'd received a pair of sunglasses from Nordstrom Rack as a birthday present, but they turned out to be crooked and no amount of adjustments could get them to sit straight (believe me, I tried). So, I was on a mission to find the perfect replacements, and lucky for me, this Rack had a good selection to choose from. After much debate, I found the right ones and made an even swap. Nice deal.

With no push back at all, the staff took back the glasses and swapped them out for the new ones. Nice! Good job Rack, I may come back to visit the next time I'm in town.

Went with my daughter, her mom and my wife. It was my daughter's birthday. Arrived about 15 minutes before others arrived and with the huge crowd lining up on a Wed. night decided to put our name in for a 4 top. The wait was only 15 min. Service was fast and good- wait staff is well trained- even with times out for line dancing and doing the birthday sadle on saw horse routine. Foods were all very good and drinks as well. I'd recommend a visit as long as you understand it's quite loud and is best if you make a reservation. I'll never get used to throwing peanut shells on the floor of a restaurant, but the waitress would laugh and scoop the shells I put in a pile on the table, off on the floor. This is a fun place so go enjoy.

Very bad experience!  even after we explain that we are waiting for the food for over an hour, The manager came out apologize and explain and ask us to stay, after we said we don't want the food anymore because we were waiting for too long, he got mad at us. This is not a way to run a business

This place was delicious! Super friendly staff, authentic menu, phenomenal execution. 

5 Yum Stars.
This is fantastic food and great service! It's very nice on the inside and wonderful Italian music. All around phenomenal place!

This was a very nice spot to come to for drinks. It is small and intimate and a great spot to come to and get away from all the craziness on bourbon st.

The bartenders and service are great. They always seem to make sure you are doing good and ask if you enjoy what they are making you. 

Only drawback is the martinis that we got were pretty tiny compared to most places you go. Normally not a huge deal but considering the price tag you pay here it definitely is a factor.
Pizza taste old and they constantly forget to put the crust flavoring on. You can try to call them but it doesn't help

The quality of sushi, presentation, and service are great here.  I think the menu is a little pricey for being located in a strip mall, but the quality more than makes up for the added cost.  If you are looking for a quality experience, you'll find it here.

I went to Hungry A on a whim and riding up to the place I didn't know what to expect but I have to admit I was pleasantly surprised the owner was very welcoming and the bacon egg and cheese on an English muffin was great. 1 flaw no home fries but that can be overlooked. A really nice place

We fancy ourselves snobs of Mediterranean food having lived in the Middle East. So amazed at the great take we had at this new place in Drexel hill. Terrific falafel with Lafa bread, not the usual pita ! Schwarma, cauliflower, Greek salad, Loved it all. So glad we finally have our own place here in town.
Pizza took more than 1 hour to appear after order. Arrived cold and with wrong ingredients. Nasty. Never again

At first I wasn't very eager to eat here as I'm not a HUGE fan of Mexican food. After my 1st trip to Los Caballitos, I am now sold! The cocktail list had several intriguing options. We decided to order the prickly pear mohito and the blood orange margarita (extra salt!) The former was really good however the margarita was the favorite choice on successive rounds. I strongly suggest you to try the margaritas if you do decide to eat here (on the rocks and opt for the whole pitcher...it will save you $$$ because you will want to order another after you have the 1st-it's THAT good!). I can't leave out my favorite part of the drink which was the neon straw that came with it (yes, I get excited by neon straws... super fun! LOL)

The place is very clean and has a wonderful vibe with the jukebox and the neon lights on the ceiling. Very hipster-ISH. You have both outside and inside seating options which is great, especially on a nice day like it was today! It is indeed quite loud inside, but I found it fun. Our waiter was very friendly and offered up recommendations (I'm sorry I didn't get his name so I could give him a 'yelp' shout out). 

Overall: Highly recommend to everyone (especially if you are trying to have a foodie adventure!!)
The hype is right! Super clean and nice-but still casual and comfortable.  All salon techs were professional and super sweet.  Great prices and services!

Fantastic value, very cool property. Staff is very friendly and accomodating. Great location, just a short walk or ride to all the action of the French Quarter and downtown. Had a very nice stay, no complaints and will be back again.

If you're in the mood for simple, no-frills, home-cooked Italian, this is the place to go. The menu is full of choices but no explanation of what each one is (you should know). Marinara, meat and alfredo sauces are rich and flavorful and the pasta selection is good. The meatballs are huge and worth adding on the side if they don't come with your main dish. The wine list is simple (basically red or white) but there is a full bar. Oh, and finish your meal, or you might get sass from your waitress, especially if you try to order dessert without cleaning your plate. Yeah, it's that homey.

Excellent food! My new husband and I ate here not once but TWICE while we were in Santa Barbara for our honeymoon (we were only there for 2 days before continuing our road trip, so we did breakfast at Dawn Patrol on both days). Make sure to try the fresh orange juice!

Front desk staff courteous and that is the only good thing I can say that is good.   2 days no room service in a 3 day stay.  AC broke 2 times.   No dish soap, dish detergent left in the room.  Had to ask for towels and sheets to make my own bed.  Bugs in bathroom.  No manager on site in evenings to escalate issues too.

Melograno is a very good and authentic Italian restaurant run by an Italian (from Italy) and his partner.  Their focus seems to be on simple but quality ingredients served in a comfortable environment.  The kitchen seems to run on autopilot as it was a pleasure to observe the chefs create their dishes without rush or confusion.  The service is very good and the prices are a bit high but worth it because of the quality. Again, it's simple but made with a focus on the quality.  For example, we ordered the paccari (like rigatoni without ridges) with the arrabiata sauce.  Arrabiata is nothing more than fresh sauce with hot pepper flakes and therefore relatively easy to make.  Melograno's version was made with meaty tomatoes and the pasta itself was cooked perfectly and of high quality (as noted by the al dente and its ability to take up the sauce).  The dish was delicious and straightforward which seems to be the differentiator of Melograno and a welcome one.  Buon appetito!

The place is great but the lady that was enforcing the door was a complete bitch first she started freaking out that only 3 people in when I was with somebody I said ok face the other person money then she said you have to have a mask when we were cover up with shirts I took off my shirt to put around other person BUT THE FUNNY THING IS SHE WAS WEARING HER MASK HALF OFF MAKING FOOD!! Her nose was exposed like where the fuck do they hire these morons and did not see her wash her hands inbetween glove changes and change gloves as frequent as should be

This place always is pretty clean and has fast service and some friendly smiles behind the counters.

The chocolate custard is miles above any DQ product. The burgers and fries are fine as well. Overall, I would say that it's a decent experience for casual, fast food dining.

I would highly advise potential patrons to avoid Freddy's right after the adjacent charter school (https://www.basised.com/oro-valley/) let's out. The parking lot is jammed with children who have little awareness of traffic, and the restaurant itself can become loud.

This is the worst "Irish" bar in Tampa Bay. Terrible Bar. Even worse than O'brien's in Wesley Chapel. Too bright always. High ceilings. Open concept, not intimate like an Irish Pub should feel. The staff  has no idea about Ireland or Irish Customs. St. Paddy's Day and the "DJ" is playing House and Latin R&B. Never again...
Had Omoni today for lunch and it was delicious as usual. The customer service is outstanding, Kimberly is a keeper!!!

Its a friday night I expected there to be await but it was only an hour which was great  I was starving!! I ordered the sampler because I just couldn't decide what I wanted. I got the chicken tenders, onion rings and wings, best decision I made that night! We bowled afterwards so that was great after our food settled. It was a great night to start my weekend!
That place has great food, I promise you the seafood gumbo is the best i have every eaten. and hospitality our server Guy was awesome!!!

Not a good experience. Host told us to sit wherever and  dropped the silverware on the table and walked away. Waited about 10 minutes for our order to be taken. They were out of 4 menu item. So far not a great experience

Absolutely HORRIBLE. I ordered the General Tsao's Chicken and when it came...whatever it was...was UNIDENTIFIABLE. (HONEST)...The employees said it was white meat, but it was something mushy and very ugly. They would not take it back and I ended up throwing the entire plate away, including the sticky cold pork fried rice. The word YELP! does not even come close to rating this establishment...It would be more like..CHOKE AND DIE. I actually put a picture of "whatever this was" on Facebook and got TONS OF YUCKS!!! ...What is that??? Don't be fooled by the positive ratings..don't take a chance on this place...I PROMISE YOU, YOU WILL BE SILENTLY THANKING THIS YELP REVIEW.....

I was really craving a fruit smoothie with my dinner and Banh Mi Cali was one of the closest places where I could get both, so I finally stopped in for the first time.

First, the strawberry bubble tea was amazing. The bubbles were soft and on the smaller side so they were actually suspended in the drink, and not at all rubbery or slimy, which are always my biggest fears when agreeing to bubbles. The smoothie itself was made with fresh strawberries, milk, and a little ice, so it was more akin to a lighter, healthier milkshake than the smoothies you can get elsewhere.

I also got a bbq pork banh mi, and the bread was easily the best part. A perfectly toasted baguette that was crispy on the outside and still fluffy on the inside. Someone needs to teach me this toasting wizardry, because while the bread itself was still very good by the end of the meal, it was no longer magical. But yes, eat this immediately while it's fresh! The pork itself, well, compared to a good American bbq or the cha shao (char siu) of Chinese places, the pork here was average, but I look forward to coming back and trying a different filling. :)

Cash only, listed price on the wall is tax inclusive. This is not a fast food joint, plan for a ten minute wait even if the shop isn't busy.
Don't waste your time here, they are slow and clueless. Not helpful at all. Sat around for a half hour only for them to be unable to perform a simple eye exam.

Most  of the Thai food we had at this hole-in-the-wall restaurant was really good. I particularly loved the ginger salad and the chicken wings. The Green Lava house special was extraordinary. The summer rolls were quite good. Only the egg rolls were disappointing. The ambiance is not inviting. Had we not arranged to meet friends there, the smell might have been an excuse for not staying, which would have been a shame. This was good eating. Nothing overly spicy but properly seasoned. The staff are not fluent English speakers, so we might do even better when we go back since we'll have clearer expectations with which to work. The prices were reasonable and the servings good-sized in not remarkable.

Just opened today, 3/12/11 and the store front is very cute and whimsical. Located on the main street of Collingswood the location is easy to find. Selection varies from day to day according to the owner. The variety ranges from Coconut, Smores, Apple Cinnamon, Vanilla Bean, Red Velvet and more. The prices are extremely reasonable, with mini cupcakes at $1.99 and large ( over sized compared to many other shops) at $2.99. 

The cupcakes are very eye catching, and the combinations offered interesting. We picked up a coconut and an apple cinnamon. I would say these cupcakes rate middle of the road, they would be great for a children's party because children loves cupcakes and especially fun toppings. I think adults may find that while the cake is soft and fluffy the flavor does not get incorporated into the cake. The coconut was a toasted coconut flakes on top of icing on top of yellow cake.

Similar the cinnamon apple was a yellow cake with white icicng and apple filling with cinnamon sugar on top.

By no means am I saying this place had bad cupcake, I just think they are not as flavorful as some of the others I have had in the past.
One of the worst nail salons in Nashville. Do NOT listen to customers request, especially when they have special events coming up. I would not recommend this business for women of color
Kwok's Bistro is a fine example of why Reno's culinary reputation is continuing to develop and evolve into a food destination!
Had to update this after multiple times of receiving bad service. Some examples: Never picks up phone/calls goes straight to voicemail; food takes longer than expected ALWAYS

Let me tell you a story about 2720.

On the day of the underwhelming Snowpocalypse, the Pageant cancelled its scheduled show.  One of the bands on the bill was a 7-member funk/soul group from LA called Orgone.  Seizing the opportunity, Josh from 2720 Cherokee contacted the band to see if they wanted to put on a show at 2720.  They said yes.  At around 6pm, 2720 announced on its Facebook page that Orgone was putting on a FREE show that night.  Being cooped up inside all day, I decided to go.  Sure, the roads weren't that great, so I drove slow.  Orgone took the stage around 11:30 and put on an amazing show for the 30-or-so people who ventured out that night.  They normally play to thousands, so getting to experience them that night at 2720 was a real special treat.

2720 has brought so many acts to St Louis that otherwise would have never stopped here.  The space is just so cool and well decorated and the sound is top-notch.  While waiting for a band, you can grab a beer and head upstairs and check out the Art Dimensions gallery. It's always been non-smoking. And there are always artists painting when musicians are performing, making some very cool looking art.

This is a very special place run by people who care about music and the community.  They bring a lot of electronic artists and DJs, as well as reggae groups and rock groups.  Check out their website at http://www.2720cherokee.com to see what's coming up.

Fourth time here and not going to count anymore.   Went light for new treats with my companion, marinara pizza and  broccolini.  Best thing, didn't taste like a vegetable!!! Was awesome.  Laura treated us like royalty.
$32 for gel mani. Lots of color choices. Large clean place. Amy was sweet and got my mangled hands looking better in about 20-30 minutes. Plus I was a walk in.

This is a fun Egyptian store in the heart of the French Quarter, the owner is Egyptian and that's why the theme. And it's fun. The lady that was behind the desk is a good salesman. I like something but started that i'd probably tear it up because it was fragile and she asked me I'd I'd have fun tearing it up. It's just a fun store to check out

We decided to try the Aptive Environmental for one year.  We tried two days in a row to call to schedule our next appointment only to be put on hold for over 12 minutes each day.  We only had the original application which needed to be reapplied within the first 30 days per the contract terms.  We don't like dealing with companies we cannot get in touch with.  Would not recommend any company that doesn't answer your calls.

Kens had been on my list for a while and I'm glad I finally tried it! I dined here with a large group that ordered a bunch of different things so we all got to taste a bit of everything.

I ordered the pulled chicken, ribs and chose mac n cheese and collard greens as sides. It was also served with cornbread. The pulled chicken was my favorite! It has just the right amount of smokiness and moisture for my taste. The ribs are good- but less smokey. So if that what you're looking for, I'd say they are heavier on the dry rub and not really saucey if that's what you're looking for. They are tender and cooked well.   

Crowd favorites were the catfish, pulled chicken, fried okra and the sweet potato fries! The sweet potato fries are delicious! They're perfectly crispy with a light cinnamon sugar coating. They has more spice from the cinnamon than sugar, which is what I loved. They are a must try! Every table is set with paper towels, which will be put to good use! Food is served on trays, as BBQ should be! And is also served with a side of BBQ sauce, but they have other sauces on a condiment bar available as well. 

The service is super friendly and the staff checked in with us several times throughout the evening. If you like bbq, check this  place out.

Good food, however won't ever eat inside again. The frog (cigarette) sounding counter lady smokes at the front door while it's open which stinks up the place. On top of that she doesn't wash her hands when she comes back in.

If you're a vegetarian and want fast food, this place is paradise! Seriously, for just over $5, I get a black bean burger on toasted whole wheat with super sloppy toppings (the way I like it).  Today I added hummus, caramelized onions,  peppers, pickles, mustard, lite mayo, tzatziki sauce, oregano, and probably more that I can't remember.  Yes, quite a mix but I was in heaven.  It does get busy at lunch, but they're fast, and people eat fast too so not a problem for me.  Oh and it doesn't hurt that my wich was free. They sent me a coupon for my birthday. I'll be back to pay full price asap.
Great atmosphere. Serve large portion. Food was excellent, fresh and tasty. Portions are large enough to share. Meats are good quality.

There are not words to describe how awful my experience with Dr. Lee was. If you want someone to get you in and out of the office quickly and not care about your health at all then she is fine. If you want a doctor with any compassion or willingness to explain things to you DO NOT go to this office. I left the office in tears (despite the fact that I am a completely healthy person) because she was so awful. In addition, her technique is much more painful than other GYNs I have been to. Office staff are almost friendly. Birth control refills ran out in less than a year. Trust me, if something goes wrong with your lady business you do not want to be crying in her office, she won't care about your tears.

I do not understand the Covid practices of this mall. Entries to the mall and bathrooms were shut down. Everyone entering in the mall is funneling in the same doors instead of spreading out the people through multiple entrances. Same goes for the bathrooms, when I finally found an open bathroom many of the stalls were out of order (not due to Covid) and the entire hallway had a foul odor from the bathroom down the hall to the mall.

I have nothing but good things to say about core life eatery! It is always so good and so fresh. Their aesthetic is so vibrant when you walk into the restaurant. It almost is like a chipotle style way to order. Therefore you can kind of scope out what you want. 

I got a bowl filled with quinoa, arugula, spicy broccoli, siracha sprouts, chicken, corn, and much more. Their jalapeño lime dressing is too die for. Perfect amount of spice and lime flavor. 

I also also get the chicken bone broth to sip on and it's perfection. It's a must when you're feeling under the weather or just want to warm up!

I've also gotten the buffalo chicken tacos and OH MY WORD, insane! I don't have a picture because i literally inhaled it in two seconds. They put it on a pita taco and it has changed my life forever. Dramatic, i know.

Overall, you can definitely find something for everyone here, kids included. Healthy yet so delicious!

Nashville. Man, did it change!!!
I don't event know how to rank this place because part of me like this posh, hip, trendy place but at the end, it's just a biscuits with fancy stuff on it. 
Wait on a weekend was insane. Reminds of my time waiting to be seated at the pancake pantry. But that's what you did because you ran into locals during the wait. This place seem bit tourist trap. Either case, cool scene, decent food, and matches growing Nashville. 3.5 star but since I can. 3 star.

Be aware of the wait. We waited 50 minutes for our entree to come out. They didn't give bread or anything while we were waiting. The waitress admitted they were understaffed. Even though there was no wait at the door, they are deceptively slow inside. The food was ok. We complained to the manager and he comped us our entree.
By far one of the best menus during my trip. Delicious food out of the gross touristy area. Chicken and Waffles. Full stop. Great atmosphere and staff. Bartender was great.

Excellent espresso and lattes, nice homemade pastries and a great atmosphere. Perfect for a lazy Sunday morning if you'd like to read a paper, have a bite to eat and relax. I'd highly recommend the crepes. I had the spinach and swiss, which is quite large by the way, along with a light mix of spring greens and house vinaigrette. Simply delicious!

This is the best salon I've ever been too, a Friend of mine referred me and I'm so grateful. The owners are absolutely awesome and so is the rest of the staff . I highly recommend this place! Beautiful decor and great vibes.

Compared to the other mexican spots in IV, Cantina is definitely one of the better ones. Both cashier and cooks are friendly and the prices are affordable. They give big portions but their salsa bar is limited to just the basics. Their guac is very good and don't skimp on giving you a big spoonful, unlike other places. 
I recommend the chicken tostada!

Went in to get my front two windows tinted. They did a horrible job. Creases and the tint scratches so easily that it was ruined within a few weeks. Service was so bad I will not go back to have it fixed. I am just having it removed by a different company.

Great service. Food was really good, except for my buffalo wings that were under cooked. I brought it to my server's attention and she said that they get "THAT" complaint all the time. And that it was just the pink vein that's in chicken. Not only was the meat itself pink, but the meat tasted a little raw (bloody taste). The meat was rubbery (uncooked) near the bone.  She brought out a new order of wings, that were properly cooked. Don't think I'll be going back.

This place is the worst. Dave the manager is a jerk. Puts phone down when you are talking to him and is very rude and does no try to help anyone. And dont get me started on Lynn the post master. She talks over you, lies to get you off the phone and does not do what she says she is going to do. The post offices in the sparks area need a new post master and managers. I have never in my life been treated this bad at a post office. I will be going all the way to the top to have the post office replace these bad apples. Even their own employees are saying that they dont care. The post master never even goes to any other locations. Just stays in the vista location. Its bad when your own employees want you gone. I will be escalating this myself to the top. These people need to learn how to treat people. They act without consequence. Well here we go!!!!!

Started with the bloom petals which are always good!  Then did a lunch combo with a side salad and fish tacos. The sauce they serve with the tacos is the perfect touch of flavor. All in all it was a great lunch and Crystal was a great and friendly server.

This cafe and caterer is located by the university in Edmonton.  Great for lunches.  Hearty chicken salad sandwich with real cheddar and and multigrain bread.  The potato salad was excellent.  Also enjoyed the fruit crisp.  Has very healthy choices.   Prices very reasonable and good decor.  It's really a 5 star place for people on a budget.  Includes a selection of Canadian brewed beers and a selection of wines.  Parking can be challenging.  The prime rib and mashed potatoes were mouth watering!  Ok I'm salivating here.

Oh my gosh the food here was so good. I ordered way more than I could eat but had no regrets. The venue is really nice, the service terrific, and overall it's probably my favorite Korean place in Philly.

First of all, every other El Limon restaurant I've ever been in is exceptional. However, I recently had a negative experience at this specific location. I ordered a burrito with chipotle sauce, and it came out with mole sauce. I informed the waiter and he took the burrito away and put it on a counter. Minutes later the manager came up to our table and asked what was wrong, we informed her the burrito was a mistake. She responded by saying it was too busy to remake the burrito and unreasonable to do so  on a weekend (the restaurant was less than half full at the time) and it would take up to an hour to remake. Her tone was off putting and she said the only option was to take the burrito back. I was disappointed, we would have waited for a new burrito or at least liked to order a less complicated dish in its place that we would actually enjoy. No option was given so she handed the burrito back from the counter. We were charged full price for the meal although it wasn't what I ordered. Great food, disappointing management.

My brother ordered Sesame garlic and I ordered red coconut curry. Both were stir fry. Our orders were made in a timely manner, however I am giving this location two stars due to the flavors of our foods today. I have been to the Honey grow in Center City and was impressed so I wanted to try it again, but today's dishes were totally not what I wanted. First, the sesame garlic stirfry sauce tasted burnt as if they did not clean the stirfry pans or really overcooked the foods and really salty. The red coconut curry didn't even taste like curry and was super bland. Both were really hard to eat... I will not be returning to a Honey Grow anytime soon and do not recommend this place to anyone else

mmmm yummmm..... the menu here is unbelievable with something for everyone. the presentation is fun and colorful and the taste is really special. they say is latin american fusion but yo its just GOOD. I think its most similar to cuban based on my limited experience, but its more gourmet than the places here in LA which are more mom and pop.
