Both are good, in different ways. the ambience of mixto is really great too though as the whole place is lively looking but not in a loud or obnoxious way. its great for families as well as people on dates, and they have nice cocktail menu as well. overall really great.

I purchased a Groupon for Power of touch massage. I scheduled an appointment online as requested on the Groupon. A day before my first appointment I got an email saying my appt was cancelled. I tried calling to reschedule but no one answered and the voicemail was full, but said to text so I tried that. After getting no text or call back I rescheduled online. When I showed up for my rescheduled appointment (I got no cancellation email) the door was locked and lights off. From what I could see thru the window there's not much in the office. The really ridiculous thing is that another woman was there at the same time and said she was friends with the guy and he said she could come in for an appt at the same time as me. So not only did he double book himself, he also no-showed on 2 people one of which is a friend. There's countless reviews on multiple sites with similar stories to me, I often feel reviews don't tell the whole story and thought it was just people making mistakes or a misunderstanding and so I tried it anyways, but now I can guarantee this is a scam and waste of money.
We had a Sunday lunch here. Great burgers and homemade chips. Very nice place and great staff. We will go for dinner soon.

While driving to Three Palms if you think you are lost you are probably in the right place. Tucked back in an industrial area off of Falkenburg road, this unassuming little brewery makes some very solid beers. It is a cool little joint with lots of seating, board games, and a TV. Bartender was very friendly and knew everything about the beers (her husband is the brewer). They had a wide variety of styles but if they have the Coconut Porter you have to get it. It was one of the best porters I've ever had. There are a lot of breweries in Tampa so add this to the list of ones you need to visit.

I had to try the place that was runner up for "Best Thai Restaurant".

I ordered some take out from Erawan on a Sunday night. It was pretty packed and busy, but they still had my food ready to go at the promised time.

I got the seafood drunken noodle. It was good. It was like any standard drunken noodle you would get at any decent Thai restuarant, except it was not particularly spicy and it could have used a little more kick. One word of advice, I would not get the seafood version again. The squid was a bit fishy tasting, as if it was not quite fresh.

I got a cabbage salad on the side which was tasty as well. No complaints here. 

Overall I thought the food was a little pricey for what I got. It was good, but didn't blow me away. To explain the best Thai restaurant runner up status, I saw the offer of a $15 gift certificate if you voted. They could persuade me to vote for just $2. 

Still, with the average food, it still hit the spot. I would be curious enough to try the dine in 3 courses meals next time.
Fun place...seedy neighborhood..great drink specials
LOVE their sandwiches and salads!  Try the Tri Tip and Salsa on an Italian roll.  To. Die. For.

First time here for a 60 minute massage and my therapist was AMAZING! Her name was Megan and she was so knowledgeable about problem areas, solutions, and techniques. Plus, her hands worked miracles! Requesting her every time now.
I'm still dreaming of my crispy, cinnamony warm waffle and creamy, rich, TO DIE FOR ice cream! Just go there. You won't regret it!

My wife and I went out to dinner and decided to try a new place, we came here and already in the first 5 minutes we felt very out of place, but we tried it anyway, the food was very good, but the service was horrendous, our waitress Jo Ann, was very neglectful, and seemed to be very uninterested that we were there, she brought me my salad and didn't bring any silverware to the table, just walked away, it took over almost 10 minutes to come get an empty plate, and even then failed to see that my wife's glass that was right next to the empty plate, was empty, we had to flag down another waitress to get our silverware and our plates, and also to ask her to find our waitress for the check, upon giving us our check, Jo Ann came to us with a snarky attitude and proceeded to tell us she didnt see how she didnt do anything that would make us feel neglected but, she sat down with the OLDER couple in front of us, and had a very nice conversation, and after that disappeared to the back until she came back out and watched some of the game on TV, and horsed around with the immature bus boys that had been playing around and dumping most of the work on one of the other bus boys. Mind you there was three bus boys total. The waitress that came to our aid was Dee, who was very nice, and friendly who seemed genuinely concerned when I asked if there was a manager on duty, which there wasn't do to the fact that "she hadn't had a day off in 21 straight days." however, Dee was helpful and very nice, would return to have her as a waitress, but would be hesitant due to out first experience.... Seems to be a 35 and up crowd, we are an interracial young couple in our 20's and seems like we were being treated like total dirt because we were the youngest people there... Dee was the only person that seemed to not care about our age or color...

I drove past this place for so long and didn't stop in, because I thought the sign looked cheesy. A friend invited me here last year and now it's one of my favorite spots. I love that they have all AZ beers on tap with some rotating handles. One time they had Kilt Lifter aged in Four Roses Barrels! Wow that was an amazing beer! I usually get a burger or the wings (cuatro cinco style) and holy cow their fries are my favorite of any where in town!
Of the dozens of times I have been here I only had one mediocre burger and nacho lacking in sauce and toppings. I told my waiter and he corrected it right away!
A culinary experience to rival only the best of establishments.

I used to go to the Valence Church across the street. This place closes by 7:30pm so go early. 

However bad it looks from the outside this is one of my favorite take-outs (the only way you can eat here-no tables). 

I have tried the po-boys-great bread-the chinese food, the fried fish, it's ALL very good and cheap. Only in New Orleans baby!

Whoa! Didn't someone get stabbed here back in 1999? Well, the "Funk Zone" sure has changed this part of SB, that's for sure. We couldn't get a rezzie, but we managed to mosie up to the standing-room only table just in the nick of time.

The four of us shared everything but the standouts were the beets and the pork shank. The wine list is extensive and they accommodate you if you want to try a few different things. It was all wonderful and I hope to return soon.

Our little piece de resistance was running into "Elaine" from Sienfeld, and unfortunately, out friend isn't above going to talk with her. Sorry Elaine; for your sake, I hope we don't run into each other again.

Auf.

GREAT LOCATION JUST NEEDED TO EXECUTE  nothing I mean nothing gets under my skin more than a top notch Sports bar serving GRADE 3 wings ! Don't know the  difference you say ? It's the size for GOD SAKES CHARGE ANOTHER BUCK MANNING and get grade 1 or select please. I'm not a complainer but why in the hell don't you have auto paper twl  dispensers in the Can ? Ok on to the service FLEMMING was our waiter he was thorough when he got to us it must just be the PACE OF THE BIG EASY  I ordered beers by the 2 's cuz I'm not a smart man but I also don't want to not have a beer while sucking on those Pigeon wings! So here it is go ,see it  throw back a few then go eat dinner somewhere else good places just a screen pass away ! Show me your LIGHTING BOLTS

I love good Mexican food! This place was a very pleasant surprise. It's kind of tucked away but nice, clean and bright decor celebrating Mexican heritage.  Perhaps the decor was a bit over the top but, it was still fun. We actually got warm tortilla chips and what tasted like fresh salsa that was great! Beware though-salsa was spicy-which I liked.  I ordered 2 burritos-one steak and one pork that were served hot with green sauce (could have had more cheese on top) and rice and beans-all very good and that's from someone who is quite picky when it comes to Mexican food. Only downside was queso dip that was bland and served in a very small bowl and not a good value. Overall, pretty great food and an enjoyable atmosphere and a good find.

Showed up around midnight; took more than 30 minutes for the staff to process check-ins in front of me. There was a lady and a gentleman working the front desk. The gentleman seemed to be a shift manager. He would periodically wander off, leaving the other girl working alone visibly upset.

Upon FINALLY getting to my room, I see the TV is broken. I call the front desk to inform them; they say they can send maintenance up (at 12:30 a.m.?). I decline and ask that they come in the morning, anytime after 6 a.m

I also say that I want it noted that the TV was already broken, and I don't want to be charged for it.

Return the next night after 10, TV was never repaired or replaced. Call the front desk AGAIN. I'm asked if they want me to have maintenance sent up. I said no; I just wanted to be sure that I wasn't going to be charged for their broken TV. Spoke to the same lady with a thick accent. Very rude both times; she acted like she did not care and that I was taking up HER time.

Long story short, check my credit card statement, and the hotel charged me $202 for the broken TV. General Manager refunded the money after I went off on the phone, even though, apparently, no one remembers speaking to me (TWICE!) about the TV being broken. G.M. was very dismissive of me, never offered an apology for the inconvenience, and acted as if I was a nuisance. Absolutely some of the worst customer service I have ever experienced.

Travel a lot to Indy for business, but never staying here again.
Went opening night and service and food was on point! Everything on the expanded menu was amazing - and Tired Hands on draft! Excellent design as well; this will be our new Cheu spot.

I went here to celebrate Easter with my family and I was content with the quality of food at this buffet. I'm not a huge fan of Chinese food but I really enjoyed the amount of options present and how often the dishes were replaced! It was a good mix of Western and Asian cuisine! I'm not a huge fan of buffets either because I am not a huge eater but I would definitely come back here again!

I really enjoyed their ice cream selection (just wish it was labeled!), their sushi, and salt pepper calamari.

I sponsored a business card exchange here this week and it was awesome! Great job on catering with nice deserts included. I couldn't wait to try the blue cheese pie , it sounded so interesting and I love blue cheese... to be honest I was concerned if it was going to be a hit at the party.It was even better then I imagined, it was kind of like a Brie consistency, kind of like a quiche and served on an assortment of crackers. It was a mixture of blue cheese, cream cheese with a hint of bacon and it wasn't an overwhelming blue cheese taste which was unexpected and nice. It wasn't a big hit immediately, but when I had a few people try it ( after I had my fill ) they loved it and everyone devoured it.
I tried the Richmond ice cream and saw some customers order sundaes , they looked great!
Super cute place and I also had the gluten free oatmeal bars , really good . They have some gluten free items and they seem to be popular.
This would be a great place to bring the family and you will also get to see a train pass bye.

This place is taking care of business as far as I'm concerned. 2 stars might've been the reality of yesteryear, but honestly I feel at home at BK. Granted it's not as swanky as the Hampton remodel in South City, but this place really has it going on. The soda machines are space age. I just got a raspberry Coca Cola from the touch screen pour lever!
Also, the service was top notch. They are well staffed and an energetic bunch behind the counter. Granted it's not the middle of the summer and I came a bit after the lunch rush, but these guys are efficient in shop and drive thru. Nobody has idled longer than five minutes. McDonald's would be a different story. Glad Burger King is a staple of the neighborhood. Long live BK Kingshighway and Delmar!
Also worth mentioning I was about to fill up my drink when my order was called up. Nothing big, nothing fancy, and I don't care if it was premade (which it tasted fresh and wonderfully flame broiled), but just damn, I can't remember a time when I was handed my burger so quickly. Like ever!
Thanks you wonderful people of BK. Thanks. Can't wait to dine with you again outside of the coupon book.
The only confusing aspect of this restaurant was the ketchup dispenser. There oughta be a sticker annotating where the sauce falls...

Great food, good prices. This is a nice place to take a date. I liked the atmosphere and the food. I had the turkey chili and my date had the Lamb stew. We shared the hummus appetizer. And the bill was not much more then going out for burritos. Absolutely a place I will be returning to.

I've been to Oak and Stone a few times. Each time has been mediocre. The space itself is really nice. There is ample room for seating. It is located right downtown on Central Avenue near everything. There are both indoor and outdoor seating options. And plentiful bar seating. I came in recently for brunch on Sunday. They have a bottomless mimosa special for $15. Which sounded intriguing. They also have a decent menu of pub food. 
Parking was difficult from the get go. Finally found a spot near 1st. All paid parking. It's only a short walk to the restaurant. Once inside, I saddled up to the bar. There was no warm welcome. The bartender didn't appear for about 10 minutes. And seemed to be rushed to do side/opening work. There were about four other people at the bar. The service was very slow. I Ordered one mimosa and waited another 10 minutes after finishing for her to come around to request another. And then another 10 minutes to order food. 
Some yelpers said the wings were amazing, so I wanted to try them. I was hesitant because they were $14 for only eight wings. I ordered them anyway. Out came 8 decent size good looking wings very quickly. I dug in and then also realized they were close to being raw. See pics for bloody wings. That is just downright dangerous! I know they bake maybe brine them overnight which begs the question of how they could even be that raw. I ate a few until biting into raw dog and then asked for a box to take them home and put in the air fryer. Not a huge deal but I didn't want to get sick. The sauce is supposed to be the bomb but tasted like nothing, like having Covid taste buds. They are served with another tangy side sauce which is also meh.
Bartender who was there at that time had slipped away and there was a stronger bartender aboard, yay! Mimosas then flowed freely. Extra points at this point. I had also met a few new friends by then. The patrons are the best part of this place. Come here to meet new friends!
All in all I don't think I'll come back. The customer service needs some real work. The menu is great but the kitchen crew needs to abide by cooking times to keep patrons from getting ill. I didn't see a manager the whole 3 hours. (I'm not a Karen and would never ask for one, but the support aspect). The specials are great. The space is great. They could definitely make this place awesome if they try. 
I really don't enjoy leaving less than positive feedback. But I feel it important that the team here knows how customers are experiencing their visits. This place COULD be great!

Super friendly staff. They do an amazing job with nail art at a super decent price. I showed them a picture and we talked to figure out exactly what I wanted. They worked fast and made me feel welcome the entire time.

I absolutely love Beverley's selection and variety in baked goods! This is just what Pottstown needs: a fresh, friendly, and chic place to get yummy foods...I am so floored that they have also incorporated my fur babies by providing all natural, homemade doggie biscuits! Keep being fabulous!

Large bourbon / whiskey inventory that is all INSANELY over priced, including all of their easily accessible items / lower shelf brands.  Specialty whiskey case has good items that are all 3-4 times over the MSRP they should be.  I'm an acid whiskey collector living in Nashville and I refuse to shop there because of this.

I visited Star Nails to get my dip polish taken off. It was a relatively quick but definitely painful process. After soaking my nails for a bit, the technician complained that the polish was hard to get off (I got them done here by a different technician). She soaked my nails some more and then began filing and grinding the polish off. The technician was pretty rough on my nails while trying to remove the polish. She was so rough, in fact, that she ended up taking off several layers of my nail. I left with sore and painful nails. The weirdest part was that some nails were painful after having layers taken off and others still had polish left. I've had dip polish removed somewhere else and never had this happen. I will not be returning.

OMG! This might be the cutest shop in the Quarter and maybe even the city!

I was strolling down Royal when my eye caught some really adorable collectibles in the window. Limoges! Limoges of all types! My mom used to love Limoges and she had quite a few and I had never seen so many in one spot, so of course I stopped in. They pretty much have any type of Limgoes box that you could ever imagine- they also had small ones in the shape of red beans and bigger ones in the shape of bananas and alligators. If you are a collector, this is your dream shop.

I picked up a couple "New Orleans" themed boxes to ship to some friends out of town, as they are always on the hunt for unusual New Orleans souvenirs. The staff is helpful and will help you track down the perfect gift. Seriously, if you are visiting, skip the tacky tshirt shops and stop in here instead.

They also sell chocolate and other fun things here, but for me, it was all about those neat little boxes.

Note: There's a review on this business listing that is not for this business. I flagged it and nothing happened, I just wanted to mention that this store is an antique shop that doesn't serve food, so that one star is unwarranted.

I could not rate with a minus so I had to give  them one star more than they deserve for service. Apparently they are lacking business since the snowbirds are gone. I have been coming for months for their Wednesday wing special and I do not drink any thing other than water, now they are saying in order to get the special I would need to order a drink , it is not posted in their establishment anywhere and I have ordered the same for months. I will not return to this place again.

Food was so yummy.  I had a red pepper gouda soup,  it's hard to find a good vegetarian soup sometimes and Fresco nailed it. 

My friend had a shrimp pesto pita sandwich, the shrimp wasn't the freshest we've had in NOLA, but it was still great especially with that pesto and artichoke.  
Their appetizers are dope, the sampler dips with pita was yummy and the spinach triangles (which are not triangles) were fantastic. 

Service was friendly and non intrusive but not aloof. Overall,  totally dig this place.

It's been a year since I've been back. I said I never would. I shouldn't have. Between listening to the servers talking about quitting the owners Who were right they're cared nothing about what was going . It was this Sunday early afternoon. Not busy at all. Service was horrible food. Waited forever. Almost laughed when I saw what they brought me. I sent things back. And nothing was better.I had enough and went to talk to the owner the woman was on the phone I said I'll wait I need to talk to you. With an attitude she puts the phone to her side. I tell her they should be embarrassed for the amount of money they're charging me for the excuse me shit they put on a plate. And charge like we are in Manhattan. Then I handed her my credit card she says they don't take credit cards. I heard another table talking about that too as I watch them pocket the money and put one or two tickets through. So not only do they have a bad food bad service. They are pocketing money not paying taxes on everything from what we could see. If you come to this country to make a living you better damn well pay your half. Stop screwing Americans that are keeping you in business somehow. Very disgusted and unhappy customer. Unfortunately they don't care

Literally best taco place in Nashville. Best margaritas, great price,$8. They're served in mason jars. Strong but they also have great flavor! You can't go wrong with any of the tacos... highly recommend the Al Pastor and Baja Shrimp. Homemade tortillas is what really sets this place apart. The starter sampler that comes with two salsas and guac is so worth it! Margaritas by the pitcher for $36 (they are huge pitchers). Have tried other taco places that are similar in the area and they just don't compare!! If you're looking for a fancier style taco place this is the place to go! Also MUCH shorter wait than any of the other similar taco places in the area because they have excellent and fast service! Walking distance from Broadway.

I would give zero.   Sorry ass service.  I knew the food wasnt good wasn't good but I t was convenient location. ..
Please go somewhere else....horri5.

Total scammers. Was trying to charge me over $1000 for brake change and $500 to shave rotors. Didn't not want to do any of the services and service advisor was very aggressive with me and wouldn't let me leave without getting something done. Ended up paying $200 to shave rotors, which at any other shop would've been less $100. Please avoid this place at all costs, they will rob you. Took an automotive course after to make sure I will never be taken advantage of again.

What a truly hidden gem! Hubs and I were in the mood for fried chicken and fried catfish. The reviews and option of both are what drew us to Fred's and I must say... WOW! We went in on a Saturday evening, around an hour before closing and there was still a 10 minute wait. After being seated we were greeted by our server Dustin who explained the menu options. Hubs and I both got the full buffet so we could get a good tasting of everything and boy does Fred's deliver!!! Every item we ate from the true sweet tea to the: fried chicken, fried catfish, baked tilapia, baked chicken, pot roast, pork ribs, pulled pork, collard greens, flat green beans, corn casserole, mashed potatoes w/gravy, broccoli salad, pasta salad, brownie with vanilla ice cream and pecan pie. Sadly they ran out of peach cobbler. :(((( I digress, EVERY single item was spectacular!!! The flavor is a true representation of down home southern comfort food and we left fully satisfied for the price, $36 for two with one sweet tea. And already planning a return trip for their breakfast. Eat here, you will not leave disappointed! Namaste!

Not sure what is going on, but I have dined at the a few Maggiano's locations (Las Vegas, Scottsdale, San Jose) and this Philadelphia location was so-so. I usually order the same dish no matter where I am. Here in Philly it was lackluster, needed seasoning, and just was bland. With many Philly dining options in the area, I won't likely be heading to this one.  But then again, maybe staff was having an off night?
My wife and I visited O'skis on July 4th and was greeted promptly. The waiters were very friendly and the food was great. We will be back soon.

I went to Suraya yesterday for brunch and ate in the garden. It was absolutely beautiful back there. The phone ordering system was a bit confusing (or maybe unnecessary) since a waitress occasionally came over to ask how we were doing and take orders for smaller things. Her service was great too. Other than that, everything was fantastic. The Rosewater lemonade was super fresh. I liked the prix fixe brunch menu a lot. The Mezze plate was fantastic and perfect for two people to share. The pita was warm and fresh and the waitress also let us know that they could bring our cucumbers and lettuce cups as well so we got those to add a bit of variety. I ordered the Halabi Kebab which was delicious and the perfect size considering how much hummus I had eaten. We finished off brunch by sharing a rose pistachio cruller which was the perfect amount of sweet. I definitely plan on returning for brunch ASAP!

I've been here multiple time and feel like they were adequate in providing my care and comfort. I had a baby in 2008, another in 2010, and twins in 2012. My twins had a NICU stay because of prematurity and have nothing but wonderful things to say about these ladies that work in there. 

The bad points? You'll be brushed off in the ER.
There were no extra pillows. Really? Pillows?? 
Lab result wait can be long. 
Some of the ER rooms are filthy.
Amazing food that is always on point! We usually order once a month and everything on the menu is great. The house hot mustard is worth a try, and amazing.

I've never had scrapple, I decided to try it here! The waitress thought I may not like it, as it's mostly a Pennsylvania thing. It did not change my mind as I am opened to most foods. Hey how can you know unless you try it! Most eat it with ketchup and syrup, I tried it not bad. It kinda reminds me of spam, but mushy! It was OK, not something I will order regularly but glad I tried it! My breakfast consisted of eggs, toast, home fries, scrapple, and I added grits. All of it was tasty, big portions too. The staff is super friendly, they have a punch card...buy 10 get one free breakfast. I believe the owner is French? She had an accent and greeted all guest with "bonjour" I thought that was cute
Love this place, whether it's breakfast, happy hour or whatever, you get great service, food, and amazing views every time! Lobster mac and cheese for the win.

I took my daughter and nephew to this establishment today and let's just say we won't be returning. For starters the parking situation is TERRIBLE! It took us a good 20mins to get a parking spot. That should have been our queue to leave, but we didn't. ‍


The price wasn't bad however forcing parents to purchase Kids Planet socks in order to supervise kids only to find out you can't go beyond the first floor was a big turn off and yet another sign to leave, but once again we didn't. ‍‍

The customer service was beyond TERRIBLE! None of the staff I encountered seemed to be equipped w/minimal customer service skills such as speaking when spoken to, smiling at patrons, doing their job, and etc. None of the promotions they were running were offered to us. ‍‍‍

Lastly, I felt discriminated against while inside of the playground area. I purchased the children Gatorade from the concession stand (terrible concession might I add) and was approached by a staff member who felt the need to inform me about the no food or drinks in the playground area policy. My issue was that this lady walked by AT LEAST 3 people w/drinks inside and didn't say anything to them. When I brought that to her attention she made the excuse that water is allowed. So I once again showed her multiple non-black people who were drinking juice and tea in the area only for her to argue with me about my Gatorade. This was the last and final straw for me and this establishment. I'm sad I wasted $26 ‍‍‍‍

Happy that JCPenney went back to sales and coupons!  The Greenwood store is a nice size and conveniently located in the mall.  The move for some of the locations to be satellite just seems to make it more inconvenient to stop there to shop, and the Greenwood location seems to have a broader selection.

This was hands down my favorite coffee from saint petes. Quality coffee. Amazing baristas. Friendly people. I got a latte with almond milk and it was perfect! I found myself wanting a second cup because it was that good. Really liked the ambiance and I loved the eco friendly products they sold there also. (Reusable metal straws, canvas totes, toiletries, etc.) it's amazing to me when a company promotes sustainability while serving amazing coffee!

Decided on this place because I was looking for a nice spicy indian curry and I was so disappointed! I ordered the Paneer and it was so horrible. I had 2 bites and sent the rest back. The spinach tasted old and the paneer cheese tasted like eating rubber bands with salt ( not that I've eaten rubber bands with salt, but I imagine that is what it would taste like). The food lacked flavor / spice/ freshness. 
The samoosas ordered was average. It was edible I guess. The reason for the 2 stars and not 1 star is because the dessert was nice. I ordered the chocolate cake which was nice. 

Another disappointing fact is that the waitress was terrible. Asking her questions about the menu , she kept telling me that it was only her 3rd day and she didn't know. I think that they need to train their waitresses on the menu before they deal with customers. It was extremely frustrating having such a terrible waitress. She never checked up on us and we had to call her over everytime. She couldnt work the POS system and we waited forever for our bill. 

I wont be going back at all! Its such a shame.

Not that impressed. Furniture was sub par and cheap.  Even the expensive furniture was built cheap.  We were looking for items to bring to our cabin in pine top.  Salesman didn't know anything about the furniture or how it was built, they just wanted to sell their stuff get our money and get us out of there.  I don't think we will go back

I walked in here, not really knowing what to expect. My girlfriend has been eying this place for some time now, so we decided to try it for lunch. 

We both ordered the house salad which was good. Anything with blue cheese is a winner in my book. For my main dish, I ordered the Cuban Skirt Steak! It was cooked perfectly. Just like the Cuban steak I use to get down in WPB. My girlfriend ordered the Ropa Vieja. I had never had this dish before, but she kept talking about how delicious it usually is. I was not disappointed. The brisket was very tender. She said that the sauce was a little too sweet for her, but I thought it was really tasty. 

From what I have seen on-line the dinner menu look a little expensive, but I would for sure return for lunch.

I know this is a great restaurant but I must have hit it at a bad time (Tue. night in Nov. 08).  For starters, we got the "new guy" waiter and his sarcastic humor was a spoiler from the start.  I had to lug my sports coat to New Orleans and we end up getting a waiter whose humor was way out of line for a pricy, classy meal.

And then several times when he realized dinner was not going the way it should, he volunteered way too much information on why things were so bad.  Myself and my date were sharing plates and we had broiled fish and a sirloin strip.  The strip was ordered med. rare but arrived almost well done.

We asked for certain condements and they were always late as was the waiter who then blamed his faux pauxes on someone else.  And mind you we really weren't complaining.  He simple arrove late and began making excuses.

We were starving so we ate and quietly left.  And if it weren't for the coat requirement, I'd probably give them a second shot to redeem themselves but I'm not hauling a sports coat to New Orleans again to give them another try.

There are still too many places in New Orleans left to try so Galatoire's is off my list.  What a waste of $105 (5% tip not included).

A little dive bar off of Jefferson Highway.

Service and food are ok but not great.

Parking can be a little tough - the lot is SMALL.

Wow, I must have gone here on a full moon b/c my experience rates this chain as probably the worst that I have been to. The beers were probably the only thing I could remember being good about this place.

The blackberry mojito is fresh and tart. I had the green fried tomato blt which was good. The bread could have been better. The food took a while and the waitress informed us the kitchen lost the ticket; however she did not make much of an effort to make the long wait for our food easier.

I am 100% confident the STL Post Dispatch is not capable of providing service to paying customers. I've had a Sunday paper subscription for a month and have not received a single paper. I've emailed every Sunday and they keep telling me they will contact my carrier. 
At this point, I have requested a refund and cancellation...let's see how much effort I have to put into actually getting that done.

Delicious. I had been driving for 10 hours and wanted a decent dinner before crashing for the night. Excellent service. The shrimp scampi was perfect buttery, garlic goodness with the right pasta and white wine sauce. Server Joy was terrific. Great non-casino dining in Reno.

What's not to love about this place?
well I've never stepped foot here but they deliver my pizza every time on time Just the way i like it.

Great value for the location, right off Canal street and in walking distance to tons of things (e.g., good restaurants, Jackson Square, waterfront, Cafe Du Monde). I would also agree with some other reviews that the rooms were charming, as was the feel of most of the hotel. I would also agree that the staff were quite slow and in no hurry to check us in when we arrived.  I was also disappointed to learn upon arrival that parking was valet only and $30 a night, especially since the person who I  booked the room through specifically said there was non-valet parking when I asked. But overall the hotel was in a great place and worth the cost, I would stay here again.

BEWARE!!!! Yes, the place is new and clean, but the service and quality of care is nothing to be impressed with. You will spend about 10 min. with a doctor, probably get a script, and then be billed out the rear end!

I have insurance and they accepted the $88 my insurance company (AETNA) gave them. I also paid a $30 co-pay. A month later, I got a bill in the mail for $100! They charge you DOUBLE what a regular practitioner would charge you for a sick visit. 

They will tell you that they take insurance and will work with you - but they do not tell you how much they will charge the insurance company and that you will most likely get a bill in the mail for even more. 

RIP OFF!!!!

One of the BEST Happy Hours in town!

I'm a fan of The Virtuoso Martine, Crab Cakes, Popcorn Shrimp, Calamari, and their Sliders.

Third of fourth time there for Happy Hour and we'll be back!

This is such a great spot to see a show!  A friend and I decided this afternoon to go to the show tonight.  It was at 8pm which was the perfect start time!
I love that there is a parking garage right next store!  So convenient!
Enjoyed a great show with lots of laughs (perfect for a comedy show! Lol).  And to be honest, the food and drinks are good too!  I had the chicken fingers and fries and my friend got the chicken Caesar salad.  I didn't drink alcohol tonight but had their drinks before.  And the staff is always friendly and helpful.
Wanted to add that they are now adding 20% gratuity to all checks.  I was not aware of this and tipped 20% on top of the bill that already had that amount on it.  I emailed the club when I got home because I couldn't figure out why my bill was as high as it was.  The woman who responded to my email was very nice, we chatted on the phone and she told me about the gratuity being added to all checks.  I too 20% all the time but would have never tipped on top of that.  Was disappointed but I've leaned my lesson-ALWAYS look at your itemized bill, even if it's in the dark at the end of a comedy show.

Came into this place on a Friday night. Was seated immediately. There were multiple TVs with sports on each one. I made a request to change a channel and they took care of it for me. The food was delicious and the burgers are huge. We had the onion strings as an appetizer and they are some of the best I have had. Overall a great experience right off Bourbon

This is definitely a go-to spot for dinner, brunch....not so much.

Good: 

1. Bread basket and strawberry preserves 
2. SHRIMP AND GRITS - hands down, some of the best in the area
3. Pork (anything) - the tenderloin will change your life.  I usually am not a fan of pork chops, but this was one of the best things I've tasted in awhile.  
4. Banana pudding  pie - different, in a good way.
5. GRITS - amazing....seriously, if you don't get any other side, get the grits at dinner

Not So Great:

1. Brunch. The menu left much to be desired and so did the taste.  It seemed like everything was under-seasoned, which was surprising to us as dinner everything was perfectly seasoned.
Wish they had more vegetarian/fish options. Ok, but not the best.

Let me preface this with...the food is ALWAYS very good...but the service has been terrible.  The last 3 times we have ordered from there the order was wrong and they will tell you 20 mins but you will get there and still have to wait 20-25 mins.  We love the food there but you never know if your order will be right and how long you have to wait for the food.  So if you go there always go a few mins later than they say and ALWAYS check your order before you leave!!

Pleasant staff, reasonable wait time and good sandwiches. Nothing amazing, but nothing bad either. Thought the menu board was a little confusing--would prefer to have hot options separate from cold options and subs separate from sandwiches.

Love love the new location and the menu is not the typical they put a Cajun flare to this location.  The staff are all nice but our favorite is Ana (the Gypsy) Sophia Vega we have followed her from another restaurant and just love her. Their margaritas are made from fresh squeezed juice not bottled 3$ during happy hour.

I love this place.  It's the perfect neighborhood spot, and is worth the trip if you're not lucky enough to live a hop skip and a jump away.  The food is always delicious, and the beer menu is impressive.  My favorites include: the brussels sprouts (melt in your mouth), mac and cheese (amazing), duck confit wings (tangy, delicious sauce), the veggie burger (homemade, mooist, and delicious - I'm not even a vegetarian), the warm turkey and brie sandwich, and the lamb lettuce wraps.  Everything is great and always a little unexpected - cucumbers on the veggie burger, not your average wings, etc.  

The waitstaff are friendly and knowledgeable about the beer and food selections.

My only complaint is that the beer list doesn't have prices, and they're not cheap (about $6-7 per beer).

We LOVE this place.  It is by far our favorite restaurant in the area.  Would highly recommend to anyone who wants great food at a very reasonable price along with outstanding service.  Keep up the great service guys!! See you tomorrow night!

Best ever!  About 4 years ago I had my car done at Mission Car Wash.  It was over 200.00 and the inside was still a mess.  This scared me off from auto detailing.

Finally knowing it was time for a good thorough cleaning I began my search.  Thankfully I found High Desert Mobile Auto Detailing!!!  Based on positive reviews I took a chance, and I could not agree more.  I have no idea where the previous poster came up with one star, because this was not my experience.

From my initial phone call things went well.  Nick was friendly on the phone and gave me an estimate.  He stuck with it all the way through.  When our original meet place didn't work, he was quick to accomodate a drive to Truckee.  He kept his appointment with me, calling in progress on a slight delay to my home, and stayed until the job was done.

Wow!!  What a job!!  He and his helper both worked non stop.  About 5 hours with two guys, and I can't stop smiling at how great my car looks!!  

Literally, better than when we bought it as a new used vehicle from the dealer!!  I feel like I have a new car again, and that is amazing when a car goes through harsh Truckee winters with no garage, and many kids in and out of it.

Thank you Nick!!  I will be spreading the good word and definitely call you again.

This tidy and super clean neighborhood Persian restaurant was so worth the drive for us. The owner brought out a complimentary bowl of soup with a basket of bread. We had hummus and a barg and kobideh kebab - Yum!!! So delish and flavorful.

No complaints about the food, but the staff is lacking in professionalism.  I once ate here on a less than busy Sunday and was shocked to find one of the girls who worked the cash register painting her toes at one of the tables in between customers.  That was a first for me in any restaurant I have been too and makes me question the controls in place regarding the cleanliness of this place. I will look elsewhere for places to eat.

Came here with a friend for lunch on a Sunday. She ordered a sandwich (I can't remember which one) and I got the Fish and Chips (yummMmm!) Our waitress was really nice and attentive even though it was busy. I definitely will come back to try things on their dinner menu.

We visited here on vacation and stopped by with a toddle.  First of all I love a brew place that can accommodate a toddler.  The waitress was very nice and was able to adjust the grilled cheese sandwich to not include the preserves.  We got the charcuterie board with the beer bread, it was so good I could have eaten in all day.  I also got a beer and burrata salad...you had me at burrata.  This was amazing and had great flavors.  We got the beer flight, our favorite was the Southern Wit!  I would go back here again if I could!

This place is a little pricey but if I bring my 9 year old (12 and under are free), it balances out. I am allergic to cats but because I am a saint, I took allergy medicine and brought my daughter. I love cats, but had to wash my hands after petting them. Even with that, I was sneezing but able to hold off the whole hour. 

Your admission covers an hour of playing with cats. There are so many toys to play with the cats with. Lots of obstacles, stands for them to climb over. You also get a free coffee, hot cider, or hot chocolate. Plenty of places to sit. 

Despite my allergies, I enjoyed myself and my daughter keeps begging to go back.

Best mexican food around!  When you try to find them look for a buiding that looks like Pizza Hut. They seat you and you are served some warm chips and salsa, a great start while you wait for the food. The wait after your order is not long and soon you are able to dive into Yum City. Food selection is big so it may take a few minutes to narrow it down.

BYOB if you please, bring beer or wine. The cost of the meals are very resonable. Parking can get tight and expect a wait for a table during peak dining times.

We got the very excellent chicken kabobs and lamb kabobs, all good. But the highlight was the naan, made in-house and delicious. Don't forget to order a mango lassi.
Yes, go eat here. Right now!

This is the place you go for convenience because you're at the gardens and not for the food. The food is expensive for what it is and all of the food was pretty bland.  The spinach artichoke grilled cheese wasn't really toasted had lots of raw spinach and little cheese. They probably try to push stuff out quickly and it was quick and easy-- order at a counter and get food brought to table. Nothing was bad but it also wasn't good.  The staff were friendly.  If you have out of town visitors and have some time you'd be better going to one of the many options close by.

I recently had my hair cut and styled by Philana at Pure.  She was amazing (with a name that cool how could she not be?!) Here was my experience...
- The location is unique. You can grab a coffee or shop at the local boutiques nearby in Marathon Village. 
- The receptionist was friendly and greeted me when I walked in. 
- Appointment started on time... can I get an amen?!
- Before the cape was even put on, Philana took time to listen to what I really wanted, and helped me decide what to get done. Game plan... check.
- Shampoo Bowl was as comfy as they come... and I generally hate the bowl.  The shampoo and scalp massage was super relaxing. 
- This is the first organic salon I've ever been to. Philana did a great job of telling me all about the products she used on my hair. It felt educational, not "salsey". 
- Most importantly... I felt truly pampered, left with a gorgeous cut and style, and will absolutely be returning.

Good for young college kids with fake ID's.  I'm in my younger 20's and feel like I should be baby sitting rather then enjoying a beer.  Usually crowded with chicks falling all over themselves and "Bro's" with too much testosterone.  This place is only good if I only have to stay for a short time, or unless I'm dragged there.  This is the night life view, I haven't ever given the place a chance to eat there so I gave the review an extra star, or two.

Having visited nearly every section of this stadium there is not a bad seat to watch a game. The sell out games need to be experienced first hand. There is an energy that can't easily be put into a few words. It is palpable. 
The food is great. Choices from healthy to heart attack on a roll. Eat responsibly!
Enjoy.

Amazing food truck! The customer service is great plus the food is delicious and perfect! Great value for a food truck as well :) food is severed  quick and hot! Don't forget your napkins--you will need them

Way over priced food.  Burger King onion rings and they must buy their crinkle fries  at Audis. I guess the food is overpriced to keep out the rifraf... the food tastes like it was leftover from a fleamarrket food truck.

We got here at around 3 PM on a Thursday afternoon and we were one of the 1st customers to rent a surrey. We wanted to try the surrey out before renting it because we weren't sure if we would be able to pedal. James was incredibly helpful and patient with all of our questions. He helped us with our helmets, and took some amazing pictures of us in the surrey. It was harder than it looks but we had lots of fun.  We will definitely be back to try out the other large bikes!
It's pretty good, better than a place that's closer to.my house.  They were really busy the night we went so service wasn't great, but not horrible.
The food was expertly spiced and flavored and beyond compare. I am an Asian food snob and this was excellent. The service was only tough because of 1 girl everyone else made up for it.

I've been eating at Cesare's pizza for over 2 decades. This pizza is better than crack. Try the spinach pizza for a real treat. They also serve whatever Silvana (the owner) decides to cook up that day. Lasagna, pasta, eggplant parmisan etc... with top quality ingredients.
Consistently good gyros, hummus, tabouli, kabobs, etc. priced right and friendly service. Really tasty pitas. Definitely one of the best food options in Fishers.

This review is long overdue. These guys are great! I was in a bind a few months back and needed help moving some fitness equipment in the Tampa area. I googled local movers and must have called a few places with no availability or needing 24 hour advance notice. 
These guys showed up in 35 minutes and got the job DONE! Appreciate the work. Hard working and very fair pricing. Thanks again

Had our Christmas dinner here. Arrived a tiny bit early but thankfully they let us in as it was cold and snowing. The staff was extremely friendly and helpful. The food was outstanding. I have never seen potstickers that big and they were delicious. Love their different sauces that came with the appetizers. 
General pork was my pick. Love general chicken and have never had the pork version. Again...outstanding. Pork was cooked just right and the sauce was great. Can't wait to come back. Reservations needed.

Never been here before, won't be returning.

They serve red wine at refrigerated temperature. Thought I'd order something to nibble on while defrosting my mouth from the cold red wine, and got the pub pretzels. The "pub mustard" is literally straight-up horse radish. Thank goodness I had a cold glass of red wine to wash it down. Also got beer cheese to dip these pretzels in, which came out with the consistency of butter... butter from the fridge. 

So I came here to have a quick glass of red wine and a snack with my friends. Both of these missions were a failure. The server was nice and pretty attentive considering I joined my friends later. A heads up about the wine being served cold would be nice, and a heads up about the mustard would probably save someone from having to use an epi-pen.

Probably not the best place to go for lunch, I understand that finding staff is an issue right now, but when you have an hour lunch break and the wait on a sandwich and fries is well over that, maybe you should have the server let the customer know. Even though they might not be able to eat at the restaurant that day, it comes off as courteous and entices the customer to come back.
Now about the food. It wasn't great. Greek fries were soggy and over cooked and I could barely bite through my chicken sandwich. It's almost as if the cook took it directly out of the freezer and put it on the grill. Like eating a piece of rubber.
The only reason this place didn't get one star is because the server was very friendly and engaging.

Really like it here. Good Mexican, good with kids.  Quick service two times out of three (once we were unintentionally ignored due to there being a big party next to us).  You can almost always find a groupon or coupon if you're cost conscious.  Gets crowded, and it's "seat yourself".  Go for carne asada.

The atmosphere was cute plus they had friendly staff. However, the restaurant website says they aren't serving authentic food, and they are right. It is "asian" cuisine, which means is a uninformed combination of elements of different cuisines within Asia. It highlights the owners' shallow understanding of non-western cultures and ignorant belief that they can appropriate Asian ingredients without learning anything about the culinary heritage of the countries they are referencing.

I can't say enough good things about my visit with this car dealership. Chelsea Burr was the one who helped me into my new car, and folks, she was as sweet and genuine as it gets. I've heard horror stories about spending the day at dealerships, but finalizing & signing for my car with Chelsea felt like hanging out with an old friend. Not once did I feel like she was trying to up-sell me or sneak in any extra costs. She was true blue.

Worth mentioning: when I walked outside the doors of the building, I S#!& YOU NOT, there were about ten associates chanting my name, cheering, and blowing airhorns for me as I walked to my new car, which was pulled up in front... They also had filled my trunk with balloons that came pouring out as soon as I opened the doors. LAST but NOT least, an Italian ice truck (ten flavors!) seemed to pop up out of thin air right before I was about to leave, as if the man knew that I had an incredible need for a delicious frozen treat. I'll never forget any of this for my whole life. What a crazy wonderful experience!

Have been here several times.  It is a great place for a beer and a meal.  The food was above average and the beer was great.  Highly suggest ordering a flight of beers to see what fits your palette. Stick with more traditional items from the menu to avoid disappointment.
Closed when we went.  Appears to be under construction or something. Hopefully will re open soon.

Lorraine bar should have a slogan "The Neighborhood bar" since it is a staple on Ridge and 15th.  It is the perfect spot to meet up and grab a drink with your neighbors and meet new friends over a cold one.  A dive bar for certain which is not a bad thing.

Once you enter there are usually a few people at the bar chatting in the low lighting atmosphere, with the smell of hops in the air.  The drink options are hand written on the wall across from the bar and the bartender is usually ready for the next order.  They have good beer options but if you're looking for fancy cocktails or an extensive wine list you are in the wrong place.  The prices are good so that's a definite thumps up.

Once you grab your drink you can sit at the bar, go towards the back where there are some games, or head out back to the outside seating area.  I normally sit at the bar or go out back because there is a slight stench in the back seating area by the bathrooms.

My rating is based solely on the fact that, if you are looking for a dive bar in the area this is it.  But if you are looking for wine and cocktail options, nice decor, and ambiance...this is not it.

The food here was excellent!  We arrived shortly after they opened and was quickly seated and accommodated.  I loved the "Mile High" French Toast.  It was recommended and I definitely enjoyed it.  I also had a breakfast burrito prepared exactly the way that I liked it!  The bathrooms were clean as well.  This plan is cute and quaint and I could tell that it is definitely a place that the locals frequent daily!  I'm happy that I was able to check out this gem of a restaurant tucked away in the middle of a neighborhood!
I love this place, Vicki and Kim are the best. Probably the best pedicure I've ever had and Vicki does an amazing job with my nails
It has been years since we were in SteinMart. They had a good selection of big men's clothing. Everything was very well organized, decent prices and a big parking lot.

The east-end of Boise Springhill suites.  Quiet business neighborhood with several restaurants all closely nearby.  Easy direct access to downtown.

Amenities included Paul Mitchell shampoo and conditioner.  Full-sized hair dryer !!  not some on-wall miniature.

We had a king bed in our room, and there is a partial wall that separates the bed from the rest of the living room and kitchenette and desk area. The TV is visible from both the bed area and the living area.  It is a great setup for a 'home-base' approach if you will be in town especially more than 1 night.

The breakfast they offer is very extensive, there is something for everyone here!
Hard-boiled eggs, sliced meats/cheese, yogurt, scrambled eggs, hot meat item that rotates daily (like bacon or sausage), fruits, and of course the usual muffins/wafflemaker/cereals/toast/etc.

Excellent deal for this hotel, we obtained on Priceline several times (name your price bid).  I presume because it is not as popular as the other location, and incredible deals are available this way!  Yes, we will stay here again the next time we are in Boise.

Open seating less than 5 minute wait. Ivan and Maria were my servers and they gave great recommendations from the menu. Muffaleta sandwich was tasty. Not too heavy but satisfying. Fall harvest cocktail was amazing. Milk punch was so good. Definitely can get you in trouble.

Love this place. Spinach wrap with steak or pita with steak are great. Steak can be a little tough sometimes but has great flavor. Love the tzatziki sauce, so good. Greek salad is awesome, great Greek vinaigrette dressing.

Just got a job in Goleta and have been scoping out the local lunch spots with my coworker.

This place is a good place to get rolls in Goleta.  Food was good and service was great.  They got our order wrong and brought out a different roll.  The owner came back and gave us that roll and the one we ordered.  She was very friendly and so was the waitress.

Fish was good quality.  Had the yellowtail roll, albacore roll, and golden California roll.  They put a lot of "tobiko" or tiny orange fish eggs on the golden California rolls.  Kind of surprised because most places only put a little.  

Definitely going to be making a return trip here.  :)
Cute little place with fast service. Super friendly staff, quick to get your food. Yummy steak bites

This is my 2nd time for coming 2 weeks in a row. Is 23.50 for both hotpot and sushi AYCE. Great selection menu. Nice and Friendly Waitresses. Good Services! Would recommend to my friends and families.

I'll be honest, I have never eaten at a "Vegan" restaurant, I am your basic meat and potatoes kind of girl, but I loved my meal at Leaf. I had their noodle bowl, which had gluten free noodles. I chose their curry and it was delicious. The price was so incredibly affordable too, it was the least I had spent on lunch downtown in recent memory and it certainly didn't look or taste like it! 

The atmosphere was also amazing, small and cozy and not overcrowded. It felt like a secret get away right in the middle of downtown. Will definitely be adding Leaf to my rotation of downtown lunch places.

We had a vehicle breakdown just outside of Reno while traveling to Utah for Christmas vacation.  Luckily, Roy Fosters was open on a Saturday and took the car in.  We got a rental car to complete the trip and left the car at the repair shop.  They tried to fix our 1997 Expedition for the infamous spark plug blowout that occurs with the 5.4L engine, however, since it had occurred once before on the same cylinder, the HeliCoil repair would not hold.  They gave me reasonable quotes on a cylinder head replacement and an engine replacement.  But, because the cost was about as much as the vehicle is worth, I decided to try to sell the vehicle or tow it home and try to sell it for parts.  The folks at Roy Foster's actually looked for potential buyers for me and checked the wrecking yards to get a quote on what they would pay for the vehicle.  They then let me leave the vehicle in their parking lot until I could arrange a tow.  Super service all the way around.

Thank you Nathan for your amazing pictures!! --

We had a small elopement reception where there was nothing more than a simple hall , our guests, and our floral decorations . Since scenery was scarce , I asked Nathan to take close ups of our guests and their shenanigans . I can honestly say I can look through my album and completely be back in that beautiful time again!!! I have pictures that my friends and family can display of themselves proudly . He used several diffrent lens - the quality of the photos are amazing!! He always responded back promptly with answers to my questions. Was on time and professional. Nathan completely blended into our party without distracting our guests from having a good time . Thank you thank you ! Highly recommended!!
I had a great experience! Loved how the outside looked and the vibe of the restaurant. The food was delicious and I would definitely dine here again!

I have mixed feelings about Rick's Boatyard.   The location is really nice with a great view of the resevoir and it was fun to watch the sailboats on the water.     Went with a bunch of co-workers and we had a bunch of different appetizers.     The goat cheese was really good as was the calamari.     Everything else was "ok" but not great.    Their pizza oven was down so we couldn't try the wood fired pizzas that all looked good on the menu.
Best oysters in the state.  Very good fresh food.  A taste of home.

So I thought this place was pretty cool to park my car. You pull in. Swipe your card and it mechanically takes ya car inside for you. But when I needed sown thing out of my car I was told that I had to pay for it to come back out and then pay to put it back in. Smh. Now what type of mess is that. Luckily the attendant turned out to be a nice guy after going back and forth with him lol. I was able to get what I needed out with out paying again. When I came back to get my car it did take a while but overall I like the concept.

I discovered Element Tanning online when searching for a new place during the construction at Kietzke and Moana. I knew getting in and out over there would be even more miserable than usual and dreaded it. Imagine my surprised to find Element Tanning right next door to Kinders BBQ on Warren and Moana, and much more convenient for me. The staff is friendly and the place is large and clean. There are wipes, deodorant and body spray in each room and eyewear is provided. Even ice cold bottled water--free. One of the best things is that you aren't mercilessly hounded to buy product or specials. They do however have a nice selection of lotion if you need any. Plenty of high pressure beds and no waiting. I will be staying at Element after the construction is over.

Very cute, hole in the wall place. I had no idea this place existed but I was walking by with a group of friends and we (okay, I convinced them) decided to go in. 
Wide variety of unique flavors. I got the Graham central station and it was very good. I was debating between this kind and the raspberry chocolate truffle and the server recommended the Graham Central but offered a small scoop of the raspberry on top so I could try it. Both were delicious and unique. Portions are huge too! 
The server told me they are a newer business that started not long before COVID and when COVID hit, the business took a hit but they are hoping for a good summer! I told them I would tell my friends about them so... here you go friends! If you're in Fountain Square, make sure to check out Square Scoops! Perfect place for ice cream lovers and if you don't like ice cream, they also have candies and sodas for sale too!

This is one of my favorite breweries!  They brew up  some of my favorite ipas in the area and the food is always great and the service is awesome!    Highly recommend this brewery to anyone who is a craft beer drinker like myself!

Everyone talks about the wings here, and yes, they are excellent.  The Tony Bruno sauce is different yet probably one of the best wing sauces I've ever had.  

But come on people, I had the BBQ ribs during my last visit and all I can say is "Wow, just wow"!!!  Who would have thought you'd find BBQ this good in a Jersey bar?  Seasoned to perfection, just the right amount of of BBQ sauce and tons of meat that LITERALLY falls right off the bone.  I've had my fair share of BBQ after living down south for almost a decade, and I can say that the Jug Handle's ribs can give many of those southern boys a run for their money!

Convenient location, cheap stuff, almost always end up leaving with more stuff then I intended to purchase. $10 minimum required for card. 

Only issue I had is that the second time I came into this store (I was coming from class so I still had my backpack on), the worker  at the jewelry counter told me to put my bag down. I explained to him that I had my laptop in my bag and did not feel comfortable leaving it there. He proceeded to follow me around the store (not so subtle) as if I was going to steal something. I was very offended. There were other people in the store that had bags on and they were not accosted. So I can only assume I was being racially profiled.

I ended up calling the store after I left and talking to the owner. She apologized profusely and I was satisfied with her apology.

First I'd like to say, my personal barber is amazing and I absolutely love that the universe brought us together because he makes me look great so that I feel great. I am 42 and I've moved around this country a lot. By far my barber At WestSide is the best barber I've ever had. The ability to actually style a mans look is his talent and I am grateful. Now this is an overall assessment of the WestSide Barber Shop experience. So, with that in mind, I will share. 
Upon entering the showroom, you are always greeted and made to feel welcomed. The vibe is completely chill and relaxed. Each and every barber shows love and consideration for everyone, even if you are not their personal client. The funny barbershop banter is light hearted and entertaining.
Barbers clean up hair for other barbers so that the shop stays clean and it is always organized. There are over 12 barbers there every time I am there for my appointment, and walk ins are welcomed with a smile and open arms. 
Don't search by trial and error like I did when I first moved here from California. Go to WestSide which has proven to be the best side, and find your barber .

This gym and the staff made my life change super easy.  From meal prep at the cafe to the incredible work outs with the training staff.   This place has tons of equipment and I have never had an issue trying to get on a machine.

We hired True Friends to help our across town move. There were several positives and negatives. Chris was very prompt and accommodating to our requests of overnight holds. Unfortunately they did not come out to do an on site estimate which I would recommend making them do. They underestimated and had to get a second truck which delayed the move several hours in the evening. Additionally the movers were unprepared and did not bring packing paper and asked me to pick some up, this wasn't possible because we were closing on a home so they moved forward packing without it. As a result some of our items were broken, damaged, soiled or ripped. There was also a hole in the truck and several boxes were water logged which stained items. On move in to our new home, the movers were careless bringing in items damaging some of the walls and door frames. They also kept pressuring us to leave boxes in the garage to "save us money on time", but would keep saying they had another move to get to. We had to have the house touched up quite a bit to repair the damages. Chris was alerted of these issues and he came on site to see them. However it took 6 weeks and some reminding to see the items broken reimbursed. Overall it was a negative experience, but they did follow through with rectifying some of the issues.
Will be back good coifee drinks - counter needs aa bit of work in hearing/taking/translatting ordeRs - our seas a bit mixed up but still worth the splurge - but not yet as good as Coat Check imho

Very good, somethings to work out. Girls are the owner's daughters. Still learning . Miso soup needed a little stronger miso. Yellowtail was stringy. All the others were delicious, fresh and well executed. Price was comparable to others around town. I hope they do well.

This may very well be the type of place that everyone else says it is. This may be a wonderful Indian place to go to. I'll never know. Wife and I arrived at about 6:30pm and the placed was deserted. Another couple just beat us to the door so now there were four of us in this lonely restaurant. The phone was ringing when we entered but there was nobody behind the counter to answer it. And on it rang. And on we waited. After a great long while a woman finally appeared and answered the phone. She barely acknowledged us and had a great long conversation with somebody on the other side. She began to take their order and explain many of the food items. So here are four living, breathing customers in this great big lonely restaurant ready to be seated and order some food and she's fussing with somebody on the phone who may or may not be placing an order. After 10 minutes of standing there and staring at her (and probably a little bit longer than that) my wife and I couldn't take it anymore and we walked out. If this is how you treat your customers when they're right in front of your face, I don't want to know how you're treating them when you're behind the wall in the kitchen where I can't see you. Very bad business move. You lost two customers and earned a bad review on Yelp.

Make sure to wear comfortable clothing because the meals are big, the food is hearty, and you'll be leaving a few pounds heavier. We visited Chris' on a Sunday morning around 10AM with a party of 7. This would normally mean a 30 minute minimum, but we were seated within 15 minutes of putting our name in. And that's with a full waiting room.

The food is great, it's nothing ground breaking, but they're definitely not aiming for that mark.  They have all your essential American breakfast fare. Make sure you try the pancakes, it is their namesake after all. Corned beef hash was fantastic.

Did I mention how affordable this place is? For myself and my girlfriend, we had a very large meal (with left overs) and were out the door for less than $25 with tax and tip. That's impressive.

Overall, Chris' is a great breakfast spot.

I took my Mom for a hot stone massage to celebrate her birthday while we were staying in St. Petersburg.  We had such a great experience - an excellent, relaxing massage, great masseuse and really nice facility.  My mom had her massage done by the owner and had a great experience.  I think she was ready to go back for another one the next day too!  The price was extremely reasonable and we definitely got our money's worth.

This McDonalds is one of the most incompetent fast food drive thru I have ever experienced. I have had multiple orders (mobile/drive thru/ in person) missing items or having wrong items. 

it is also one of the SLOWEST McDonalds I have ever experienced. I have spent 20 minutes in the drive thru REGULARLY. Just sitting waiting for them to get my order wrong.  then when i get my wrong order the food is the poorest quality fast food 

Why do i keep going back? answer : it is in a very convenient location right off City Ave. HOWEVER, at this point i have had so many infuriating experiences at this mcdonalds that I now travel 15 minutes out of my way to stop by a different mcdonalds to get my food without the annoyance. 

Recommend to everyone to not waste their time waiting to get food from this location until management is changed to be more efficient.

Your typical Saladworks, but SERVICE MOVES AT A GLACIAL PACE during lunchtime, when the line reaches the door.

The lady at the register is nice, but everything leading up to it...hearing employees complaining about wanting to go home, inefficient assembly line...kills me. 

You have been warned.

The ramen broth was delicious but the noodles weren't as fresh as they should be for the price. 

Server, Kait, was very knowledgeable and attentive. 

Long wait so make sure you have reservations! 

Overall, ambiance was good but it's just a little overpriced for the quality of the food.

I discovered LOFT.com a few months ago (they ship to Canada) and instantly loved it. The clothing has a clean look that kind of reminds me of Banana Republic but with lower prices, and pretty good quality. I was thrilled to find out that a brick-and-mortar store had just opened at West Edmonton Mall.

The store is clean (literally, very clean, and clothing was folded/hung up nicely, unlike a Winners store) and modern, though I found it on the empty side. I noticed that there were lots of the same items that were displayed in different parts of the stores (for example, one type of camisole on more than one display shelf) which I think could just be to fill up space as a new store. The variety is obviously not as good as the online store (they do not carry the Zoe fit pant at all, which is strange) but they do carry a decent Petite section. Prices appear to be consistent with the online shop (which is displayed in Canadian funds). They also have a nice accessories section (I find their costume jewelry to be great quality).
We were seated quickly. The service was excellent and Celeste was a great waitress! I would highly recommend!

The lunch that we had at this restaurant was entirely disappointing. We ate late in the afternoon so it was clear they had already had their lunch rush, but they had not cleaned up any of the tables and there was food in various places on the floor. The salsa was watery and had no flavor, the steak in the tacos also had no flavor and while the refried beans seemed like they were homemade they were also lacking in flavor and the rice was bland as well. The tables all appeared to be slightly dirty and were very sticky. This place is billed as serving the food of Mexico City and I have a hard time believing that this is what people eat when they go out in Mexico City. In my opinion, there is no excuse for serving food without flavor. In this instance, salt and properly grilled or cooked meat would do a lot to give flavor to the dish that I had. Again, this was very disappointing and I felt like I had wasted my money.

As a vegan you must avoid this place.  I really tried.  I picked up vegan shawarma today for lunch.  It had white sauce all over it. Dairy is not vegan...I guess they do not know this?
So they redelivered another meal to me with no white sauce.  I appreciated the effort until I stumble upon what looks like a small piece of fried meat...
I immediately threw my food away and now have been feeling sick to my stomach all day.
Their vegan food is not vegan at all.

I have never been treated so poorly by restaurant employees in my life. After a wedding, the wedding party, friends and family came to the Concordville Inn Restaurant to have a few drinks before calling it a night and retiring at the hotel portion of this establishment. Upon entering the bar area the female bartender proceeds to roll her eyes and treat us very rudely. She copped a rude attitude when asking us what we would like to drink. The drinks she made for us were disgusting and over priced. I proceeded to send it back of course because I was not paying $7 for something that did not taste right. Then the man working the door that night intervened and made my drink. Instead of asking me what was wrong with the drink, he proceeded to remake the drink without saying a word to me. It still tasted awful and when I told him it still did not taste right he literally yelled at me in a very forceful argumentative nature and said "You ordered a vodka drink, you get bar vodka...HERE..." And literally shoved the drink at me spilling some of it. Many people were witness to this. It was extremely embarrassing and I will never return there again. 

Upon questioning friends and family if their experience was any different they said no. The staff was extremely rude and they too would never return.

On a lighter note, the staff at the hotel portion of the concordville inn were extremely accommodating & friendly.

Great food, great service. Family owned business. The specials were the way to go. Good prices more food than we could eat. The bartender was also your table server. She covered both well.   Plenty of big screen TV 's to watch the Phillies game.

Ahh the Chop Shop...the best in the West.   

I moved to SB a little over a year ago, and my first concern was finding a barber.  I tried a few around town before finding this place, and there's some pretty good Barber's in SB, but none have been able to stand up to Frankie and the Chop Shop.  Price is extremely fair and the cut is always on point. 

Overall, one word comes to mind when I think of this place.. QUALITY.

Call ahead though, Frankie is an in-demand man for good reason, and walk-in's will be really lucky if they can get in right away.

We been clients for ten years and sushi shefs did not want to attend us at 930 when they close at 1000 will not be coming here anymore asian older lady in front did not attend us either their was a older gentlemen older guy who was a waiter tried to helped us and try to get us food but the shefs denied its a far drive but the goleta location is better anyways the sushi sheff their is on point

I have to say the food is pretty good. Pizza always fresh and wings are ways great but the speed os very slow. They will tell you 15 minutes and then you are really waiting about 45 min on a weekday with no one in there. Also the owners girlfriend wife or whatever works there and is very rude. She often is yelling at her toddler kids and very poor attitude. Other than that great food so thats why we keep going back.

To be honest, I was disappointed. My dining companion and I both felt the same: ho-hum food, nothing to write home about, and a little overpriced for what it is. I appreciate that they have allergy information on the menu, so I'll give them that, and the atmosphere isn't bad, but when it comes to the food, it's not a place I'd revisit. I had the salmon (which was overcooked and underwhelming), and my crony in eating crime had the pork chop, which was more fat than chop. Also, it's a serious understatement when they say "wilted" greens (they were dead).

Studio 49 is the real deal when it comes to burning hella calories! The CRT method is undeniably a great way to sculpt and tone. The basis of the class is centered around these bungee like TRX chords that zero in on particular muscle groups. The Butts & Guts class is a real killer. I wore my Fitbit to class it counted over 5,000 steps! 

The owner has the most energy you've ever seen and she is absolutely tremendous! Beautiful studio, wonderful staff, and cardio blasted classes. You'll love it!

So who would have thought i would spend $15 on Popsicles for me and my daughters. Buuuut they're delicious, I don't feel guilty giving my girls "real food" treats, and they're fun. I love that they're just fruit along with milk or ice or whatever base you choose. I also loved that there's no artificial flavours and colours (we try to remove those from our kids diets). We all loved our choices- lemon, cantaloupe and coconut. I didn't dip them in chocolate or sprinkles (again, prefer things as natural as possible) but my kids didnt seem to mind. I think my cantaloupe bar was like 50 calories. Perfect treat with no calorie guilt. We may not get them every time we go to the mall, but great for a special date.

We visited philly for out vacation and this was one of the places I bookmarked to try. we got the Noah's ark for 2. It came with 2 sodas, 2 pickles, 6 lobster claws, 2 1/2 lobster, shrimp and crab rolls and your of 2 chips or 2 coleslaws. I had the Mexican cola which was good, I enjoyed the sweetness. My sister had the blueberry soda it was fine but I would not order it again. I found the flavor off putting. The chips and pickles were good. I found the lobster claws to be tough and could have done without them. Now for the rolls the lobster is the star. There is no mayo on This roll but it's not needed the buyer and flavor was so good. I did get butter on the side after reading a prior review that suggested it needed more. I would agree, but it just needed a little more than was on there. The shrimp roll tasted fresh and I loved the light seasoning. With the butter and the crispy bread they perfectly complimented one another. The crab roll was okay I could have done without. It lacked flavor even with the extra butter. I wish I lived here, I would be a frequent visitor. As others have mentioned it is a little pricy, if I had one roll I doubt it would be filling, but with the Noah's ark I was stuffed.
Amazing food. Had the grilled salmon with spinach. Light and flavorful. Definitely recommend this place

After visiting Acme on my first trip to New Orleans, I had to come again. The charbroiled oysters here live up to the hype. They are so good, I could probably eat a dozen by myself! I also had the half shrimp po boy and seafood gumbo, both were delicious. But by far the star of the show are those charbroiled oysters.
