I came during lunch time on a Tuesday and sat at the bar so there was no wait for me. However there seemed to be a short wait for those wanting tables. The service was wonderful. Curtis, the bartender did a wonderful job. I already have plans to return before I leave.

I'm really disappointed after coming here. I got a mani pedi spa and was over charged. They tried to charge me the price of a regular mani on top of the mani pedi I was getting. My girlfriends also got a pedi and we were all confused about what the hell we were paying for. The pricing on their website is not true. And a couple of the staff couldn't explain to us what was included in the different pedicures. They have no menu of what services they provided, I asked! 

Overall my nails do look nice and the staff was friendly, but they overcharged me according to their website and that definitely bothers me.  Won't be coming here again.

The food was good but overpriced and of course you have a great view....The service was HORRIFIC and not up to par for a restaurant that we paid over $200 for a dinner. This could be one of the best places to go in Indy for the uniqueness but they don't measure up

Very nice restaurant. Open for take out during Covid-19 pandemic. Very clean inside. Parking lot. Good sized portions. Tasty food, like homemade. Very nice employees. No one spoke fluent English, but one person spoke some English. I recommend this restaurant if you want good Latin food.

A local brewery that has opened a couple store fronts. They've got a lot of beer on tap, when I went this time I didn't have food, just a cider, but it was good! The reason for 4 stars is parking isn't ideal because of the location.

If you are looking for excellent food and a great atmosphere then this is the place. My team of 8 business  professionals shared 5 different appetizers which were all top shelf quality.  When dinner arrived it was outstanding... I had a lobster stuffed ravioli and it exceeded expectations. My teammates were equally pleased with their meals and we decided to order dessert. We shared multiple dessert and would highly recommend Victor's if only for the food.  However, there more... throughout the night the extremely professional staff would break in to opera songs worthy of broadway. What a great experience all round! Highly recommended!

When I was growing up in middle Tennessee, Surf & Turf meant hot dogs and tunafish.  Now, if you check any restaurant's seafood section, it is catfish.  Fortunately, my wife is a Boston girl who help educate me about real seafood many years ago,  So when we heard about Cousins Main Lobster, we were nervous about getting dinner from a food truck, but the promise of real lobster rolls was too much to pass up.  And yeah, it was every bit as good as we were hoping for.  Cousins can come back to Sumner County any time they want.

My sister and I actually "stumbled onto" O'Keefe's Tavern... first we tried a restaurant near the beach, server went to get us water and never came back so we walked. Then we were going to try a pizza joint right near O'Keefe's but before we turned in, we spotted this Tavern and decided to give it a try instead. We came in around 8pm on a Saturday night and it was DEAD here. (Kind of nice to get away from the busy beach crowd). 

O'Keefe's is an Irish Pub... but the menu is all over the place. It's quite extensive and has enough options to suit everyone's taste... But because the menu is SO BIG, we had a hard time deciding. The waitress came over a few times before we actually knew what we wanted.

I went with the French Onion Soup and a side salad. My sister had the chicken souvlaki with a side salad. My soup was good BUT I didn't think there was enough ooey gooey melted cheese on it. I love when you get to peel all that melted, crispy cheese off the sides of the crock. Nonetheless, the soup was really good but lacking in the cheese. The salad was average. My sister LOVED her chicken souvlaki. She said she had never had it warm in the crispy wrap like it's served here. 

Our server was nice. It's kind of ironic that she came over a number of times when we weren't ready but when we were ready to order, it was a bit of a wait for her to come back.

Prices were fairly reasonable. I think it was like $25ish for the 2 of us.

We went on a Thursday afternoon to give it a try for lunch. When we arrived we were seated right away and it was not busy.
Once our server arrived we were asked if we wanted anything to drink and we told here we wanted to look at the list. Never saw her again for about ten min. We ordered our meals and the server wasted to know if we wanted protein with the Niçoise salad.The Niçoise salad already has tuna in it .
We asked if we could have bread with the salad and was told that they don't have bread.  When asked about the flat bread or bread for sandwich we were told they only have enough for what's on the menu.The only thing they had was pita and we were charged for that.
I wonder how long this place will last?

Solid food and drinks. 

The gumbo was very good. I also had the gator sausage po-boy, it was also good. All washed down with some Louisiana micro brews.

The only thing keeping me from giving it the 5th star is the smoking. The place is small and they have fans pushing out the smoke, but still was annoying as a no smoker. That is the price you pay to get good food at a pub.

I came into the St Roch Market looking for something lite & I found it in Elysian Seafood. Lots of choices, but as I said ' something lite' was the goal... And ELYSIAN SEAFOOD fit the bill. 

Perusing the menu, I opted for the CEVICHE, signage said $9; but then my happy little eyes landed on a $5 - 4p to 6p Happy Hour sign that brought me more JOY. 

The portion size was juuuuusssssstttttt right for my appetite & for my pocket as well. 

I was able to place my order, scour the scene for more tasty treats while I waited for my food to be packaged. 

Checking out was simple & swift. #stickandmove 

The ambiance of the Market was very laissez-faire; making me want to do some laissez-fairing of my own... But I couldn't. #nexttimethough

Thank the Lord, I was favored with a parking spot as soon as I drove up; street parking is scarce, but don't be dismayed, there's a lot adjacent to the market to catch the overflow. 

I'll be back to try ELYSIAN SEAFOOD's other yummi-goodnesses.

Grateful for the owner Trevor to have reached out to me to make the previous review and its situation right. He would offer assurance that his staff would inspect the food prior to serving it. I want this Noble Pie location to continue to be a favorite for everyone in the Reno community and I look forward to that next slice!
Great food - good atmosphere.  Had a Caesar salad with chicken and ate every bite.

I paid the Bourbon Orleans Hotel 757.10 deposit in March of 2020 for a stay over the holidays in December, 2020. Due to the pandemic the Hotel was closed. They have not and apparently will not refund my deposit. They say it takes up to 120 days. I suspect they do not have enough funds and are in financial straits. Why else would they take 120 days to reverse a credit card charge. This conduct is outrageous. Buyer beware. Where there is smoke there is fire. You have been warned. Do business with these people at your own peril.

caveat emptor
[kavēät em(p)tôr]
NOUN
the principle that the buyer alone is responsible for checking the quality and suitability of goods before a purchase is made.

lets see where do  i start we schedule  an appointment with this gentleman rob richlin for Saturday we pull up to the house me and my wife  and see this man sitting in a jeep we ask if he was rob he says why do he owe me  money we say no he gets out of the jeep he looks at us with a look of discusse because my wife was black and im disabled we walk to the front of the house and we go to inside the outside steps were covered with outrageous amount of bird poop on the front steps as soon as you open the door it smells like death the floors are painted black and are  scuffed they   had a broken 2x4 railings going up stairs  and broken electric heaters in all the rooms  walk in to the kitchen and the stove was covered with grease and roaches crawling every the so called laundry room had the pipes haning on the floor and the back yard was an Amazon forrest we open the hatch to go in basement and it smells like death and the floors had dog poop everywhere we go upstairs and there is a bunch of dead birds and bird  poop everywhere we come down stairs agent rob richlin says yeah this house is a mess but thats how they keep rents low for people like you  and what would.i pay per month and to call him tuesday morning i call him and tell him that i would pay 750 he said that the office said no way and that they dont want to do business with people like me and my wife  i said oh my god and told him that not only is that discriminatory practices but wrong and  that he was rude and for me to hear this comment two times WE DONT DO BUSINESS WITH PEOPLE LIKE YOU OR THIS IS HOW WE KEEP RENTS DOWN FOR PEOPLE LIKE YOU  SHOWS ME THAT THEY HAVE DISCRIMINATORY PRACTICES AND DONT DO BUSINESS WITH THEM SAD VERY SAD RASICIST AND DISCRIMINATION AGAINST DISABLED PEOPLE AND PEOPLE OF DIFFERENT RACES

Owner is rude and unorganized. It looked like he was running the place all alone and didn't want to hire employees to help out. The food here might be good though, but I wouldn't know because I walked out after the owner/managers attitude

My nail stylist, Nhan Ho (Lina), did an excellent job with my nails by giving me exactly what I asked for. Lina was attentive, precise, and meticulous.  Brought in a picture for her to reference to, which always helps. 

I will be returning to Virtue Salon because of the excellent service I received.

BRO, DO YOU EVEN LIFT?

We came in after the Eagles Parade, unbeknownst to us it was one hour before closing. There were a few tables open, and we were seated immediately. 

They have what appeared to be unlimited Rival Bros hot coffee. It was really really good.

I had the sausage sandwich and also tasted the french toast. Both were great. I'd come back if I were in the area.

My only "complaint" (it's really not that big of a deal, though) is there's only one unisex restroom which is weird compared to the size of the restaurant.

Mastry's is a nice addition to the St Pete Beach scene. They brew their own beer and also do collaboration beers (the lemonade beer was awesome) as well as guest selections. They do $2 tastings, and I like that you can order as many as you want for the same price, instead of typical 4-5 pour flights. My favorites of theirs were the salt and coriander sour as well as the kolsch. Plus, they have a great outside area with tables and games (giant jenga, connect 4, and corn hole), so it's the perfect evening spot.
Terrible staff. Both times I've been here the service has been subpar. The food is okay at best.  I wish Sam's would come back.
Amazing customer service! Come on down to relax or party with the chillers people and live entertainment the quarter has to offer.

If you're looking for really good food and tasty drinks at a reasonable price in NE Philly, I definitely recommend checking out Burger Bar. The honey soy salmon and the crispy chicken sandwich are favorites of mine. It's a pretty casual spot. They have an indoor space that is connected to Gallo's as well as a small outdoor patio for dining. Since the pandemic, they've also added extra outside seating under a large tented area.

I'm very surprised by these negative reviews! Aside from the extremely long wait I had, the service and treatment were fantastic! I requested a shellac manicure & pedicure for my best friend's wedding this weekend. They did an amazing job - no fungus reactions or post infections as other reviewers mentioned. I even waited a few days before writing this review just to ensure I didn't jinxs myself. Haha. Overall great place, excellent work and definitely recommend!

Oct 2015
It seems that the review I wrote was rather premature. I ended up with an infected fingernail that hurt for a week! I've never ever ever had this happen to me at a nail salon so I am very disappointed that I ended up with a swollen finger.

Holy greasy spoon Batman.  They have taken their grease to whole new levels - coating the floor, chairs and windows with the stuff.  The place is just downright N.A.S.T.Y.  And I have had my share of Jersey diner experiences, but at least they were fast, descent and a reasonable value.  This place is an overpriced pit where they stack you up and had you your check with your food just to let you know that you should, in fact, flee as quickly as you can.  For those looking for 'a Nashville original' jump in your car and head to the Loveless Cafe or Pancake Pantry 'cuz this place is gross, loud,  and you need to runaway!

I dont mind popeyes as a whole and I could care less about fast food service. But the window girl forgot to give me my credit card back and I was so exhausted it didn't dawn on me that she didn't give it back. I immediately tried to call the place, but their phone is unavailable. I locked the card immediately and was going to go back the next day to retrieve it but I had personal issues that overcame my day. Then today, one of their workers is clearly a THIEF and tried to use my card for purchases. Beware of this location, they clearly have some unethical workers in their mist.
I have been here one time before but took the Mrs.  Here for our anniversary. Took the 'Ultimate' 4 course meal which has been great. Loved every bit of it.

I absolutely love Ulta.  They have almost all of the name brand products as well as the drugstore products.  They offer salon services as well as waxing services.  This is my go to place for beauty/spa products!

Expanded hours.  Friendly people.  Sparking clean if awkward space in lousy strip mall. Good food even if not the best for us.  Moderate prices.   Order at counter and carry your food to table service format.  We are wed to another local full service barbecue spot but there was a lot to like here for some folks even if not our thing for repeat visit. That's the nutshell view.  Detail follows.

A TIP - THE HOURS:  Originally open only for lunch Tuesday through Friday; recently added dinner on Friday and Saturdays.

THE DRILL:  The service format of order at the counter, food served on disposable paper ware, pick up your eats when ready and carry to your table, is not our thing for eat in.  We don't do fast food for similar reasons.  Different service format strokes for different diners. 

SKEPTICS WON OVER:  We walked in with skeptics' view given our hearts and taste buds belong to another local BBQ place.  But we were won over by warm greeting as we were offered a rib to sample while we decided on what to choose from the big chalkboard menu over the order counter.  Aroma of barbecue as you enter is winning too.  We liked the unpretentious vibe.  We liked the absence of loud music; one could carry on a conversation.  We liked that nearly all of the seating was standard height tables and chairs that works for everyone, no need to perch on a bar stool here.  And we appreciated that the huge and awkward space (reclaimed from Mickey Ray's) is clean.  All in all, pride of ownership shows.  Still, the location in a woeful strip mall and space layout is uncomfortable and that is what that is.

THE EATS:  Almost all is a la carte.  Be aware of that when ordering for a group.  I notice some reviews not flattering for Rib Shack that seem fueled by diners not understanding the a la carte set up.  Sides do not "come with".  

Sides are offered in a few size options like 1/3 pound and 1/2 pound.

Meats come by half pound, pound and other options I think (like maybe a rack for ribs).

We appreciated the menu has both rib ends (pork) and pork ends among their offerings. (Also burnt ends for beef eaters, which we are not.)  They also offer baby back ribs and another cut which I'd say is like a St. Louis style.

We chose spicy red potato salad, coleslaw, rib ends and  baby back ribs.  They have over a dozen different house made bbq sauces.  They are all available to sample and all good enough.

BARBECUE AND SIDES ARE LIKE BUYING JEANS: Even if you wear the same size as the guy or girl next to you, different styles probably fit each of you best.  So for the food here as fit...

...we like that serve meats dry rubbed with sauces to add as you choose and each of the three sauces we tried fit us for being not too sweet, vinegar forward, and lots of chiles in the mix.  

...pork rib ends were great.

...baby backs had a LOT more fat than we prefer; we would not order them again.  

...spicy red potato salad was for us perhaps the best potato salad we've had at any barbecue place in Idaho.  

...coleslaw was fresh and crisp with celery seed (yahoo) if too sweet for us.

THE DAMAGE:  Prices are competitive.  We were full to capacity on that meal and it was just about twelve bucks a head without beverages.

THIS N THAT:

1) LOCATION:  This is one of the most poorly maintained strip malls in the this part of the Treasure Valley, imho.  Not the Rib Shack owner's issue for sure. I hear the mall owner is a commercial builder.  Shame on him.  He knows better. 

2) WHEELCHAIR ACCESS: The parking in this strip mall is woefully in non-compliant with Federal ADA civil rights law and Idaho Building codes for mere minimums for accessible parking. The law and code is 26 years old.  The mall owner has had plenty of time to fix this.  I imagine it will only get changed if the mall owner gets sued.  Sadly but seriously.  But once you get in the door at Rib Shack it looks like the restaurant owner has his piece right;  all on one floor with ample aisle space and several standard height table options.  

3)  OUTDOOR SEATING:  Patio in the front of Rib Shack with tables set up for good weather dining.

Shopping for wheel covers, did my research online and found it no surprise there. Ahaha, they carried my item in stock. I advise you to do the same if your looking for specific items. This location is well done, as in they are prepared with the season decorations both halloween and Christmas. Makes me wanna watch the Nightmare Before Christmas movie. Anyways, this place has auto parts all the way to snow blowers and so forth. I'm surprised they even have all the kitchen and house cleaning supplies. You can't go wrong in this place if you love to fix things at home. Obviously gardening department is closed due to this current season but supplies are still available. Staff is friendly but sometimes you gotta chase them down if your looking for certain stuff, if not just browse or window-shop. Parking is large, I believe they have washrooms somewhere, but haven't scoped it out.
Recommended for people looking for household to auto and so on... Make sure you see online if they have stock of your desired item.

Why have I lived here for 10 years and not tried this pizza?!

The crust is so flavorful.  The sauce is sweet and savory.  

I had the Greek salad, no olives.  It was fresh and crisp.  I asked for some extra veggies and they did not disappoint.  The dressing is Marzetti's and it's very good.  

I tried the wings, which are my friends FAVORITE and they were good, but nothing extraordinary.  He gets them plain.  Maybe I'll try that next time.  Buffalo sauce was tangy.  

My husband had the Hawaiian and I stole a bite.  Wow.  I hate Hawaiian pizza and I could have smashed that entire pie. 

I'm trying to watch my carbs and I was spying their crustless pizza bakes, which look fab.  I settled on the meatball bake under the appetizer section and added mushrooms. 

Wow.  It was unctuous and cheesy.  Also, huge.  It says it serves two and it certainly will.  (Let's face it, normally, that's lies.). 

We will definitely be back.

Great cookies and ice cream! This is the perfect place to get dessert! You choose your cookie, ice cream, and toppings, and they create a custom ice cream sandwich for you. They also let you get ice cream cones or bowls and cookies by themselves. I had the funfetti and chocolate chip cookies with the cookie dough ice cream.

The muffaletta was just okay. I realy liked the mac+cheese though. The gumbo was fine. Note: there are two different affiliated establishments side by side. One was a sit down restaurant and one is a deli. They serve completely different foods. The muffaletta and mac come from the deli. The gumbo is from the restaurant.

The location is in a small strip mall , and it is a hidden "gem"!! The atmosphere is lovely, the staff are all friendly and helpful. The owners are on the premises greeting the customer with smiles and  preparing the food. 
The food is excellent!! As good as any Italian restaurant We have ever eaten at. And their pizza is aces!! 
We get take outs quite often, and they are the same great quality as eating in the restaurant!  
A&B

We went here for take out on a Sunday night. Nan Zhou is located in Chinatown and has a large menu to choose from. Everything from dry noodles, rice dishes, and noodle soups. I ordered the sliced braised beef noodle soup and added broccoli for $1. I didn't eat the beef but the broth was really good. It had a lot of spices in it and was spicy but not overly hot. It was definitely authentic and it came with spinach and daikon (yum). I will definitely try other dishes if I go back to Philly!

This place is amazing. We sat at the bar, which is always lively and very friendly. The service is simply exceptional. Everything we ordered was no less than award worthy. I genuinely can't wait to go back.

My family has a pretty wide range of needs and tastes.  Finally we have found a place where everyone's dietary needs... including the vegetarians... to those who want sushi can all be happy.  When the whole family on the east coast gets together... this is where we go out.

The food is decent, but it's totally  over priced.
The tomatoes were obviously not fresh, nor were they from Jersey. The server was nice, and attentive, but the "pizza", which was basically crackers with cheese and tomatoes dumped on top, took sooo long to come out. We've decided that the next time we want sushi and pizza, we'll go to Tokyo Mandarin and Nick's instead.

This is one chill hotel to stay in (literally) when in Tucson on business during the pandemic, July 2020. The hotel is close to the airport and Green Valley area (south on Interstate 19), and within reasonable distance from downtown Tucson by Interstate 10. The hotel is somewhat tucked away in between an industrial business area and other commercial properties, and supee quiet. Some of the local shopping is somewhat within walking distance. But really, do you really want to walk anywhere when its 115 degrees outside during the summer months?

I was upgraded to a suite by the sales manager, the very fabulous Patti Bracamonte. The room was updated, very clean, and comfortable- everything that you would expect from an HGI property. There appears to be a good amount of parking all around the building as well.

The restaurant is actually open, yet with limitations. I was able to receive a better than expected breakfast (see pictures), yet not the smorgasbord of deliciousness that is usually offered pre-pandemic. Nonetheless, I was satisfied. 

I greatly appreciated talking to the GM (Robb is his name, I think) on getting good tips on where to golf in Tucson. Ms. Bracamonte was also forthcoming in her city insight, as well as helping me print a ton of legal documents on the printer that was in the nearby lobby.

You'll love staying here.

We went on opening night. It was quiet but there was at least another party that came in to eat. The whole staff was very kind and checked in often for drinks, our food ticket, etc. 

We tried a good portion (especially the appetizers). All were so good! My favorite being the Shu Mai. It was like a potsticker in dumpling form but also had shrimp on top of it. Loved it. 
The rolls were great but I would love to see lemon in a roll or two personally. The Hawaii roll was very good (I like the fruity stuff though) and I enjoyed the volcano roll too. 

Overall great opening experience and I hope they get some good business! Give them a try and support!! Would go back!

Sigh...It is with a heavy heart that I do what needs to be done. Pan Tiffin. 

Tiffin has been a staple in our lives. But the last few times we ordered have been horrendous (I think four times counts as a representative sample, no?). Tonight we waited over 2 hours for our food. Mirroring our last delivery time frame. And when the rice is the best part of the meal, it is time to move on. The chana masala needed SALT. And the aloo gobi was just greasy and tasteless. And despite calling back and making sure that we requested "hot," our food was neither hot nor really seasoned. Again, sigh. I can't write anymore. The pain is just to great. 

It is time to move on...

No, don't try and stop me, Tiffin. It's you not me.

Why haven't I eaten here before? To celebrate kids going back to school and surviving the summer, grabbed breakfast here with other moms. Anyway, I had to chuckle when a reviewer judged other moms drinking here because when I went and saw that you can bring your own champagne and have mimosas there, I was jealous! (BTW, just because moms eat here in the mornings, they just might happen to work later in the afternoons/evenings and therefore aren't really stay-at-home moms.) Service was good. Place was nice and clean and cute. LOVED my corn bread french toast, the refillable iced coffee and the skillet burrito (no I did not order this but shared this with another mom who also works at nights). ;)

I live very close to Hattie B's.  I have taken several clients and friends to Hattie B's.  I would have to say that they are the best chicken in Nashville.  Price and food quality are very reasonable.  I am not a spicy chicken fan, but they made me a believer.  Great job and keep it up.
Food is Uh-Mazing!! Authentic Italian Food. Couldn't be more pleased. Great entertainment too! This place is a real find

Brilliant, Old school, No Nonsense and Focused - these people are all about the art of the Po-Boy - and they have it figured out. The walls testify to the history and tradition of this place. Can't believe I've never been here before! Roast Beef Po-Boy was generously portioned, well dressed, juicy, sloppy and DELICIOUS.....wow. Others had the Oyster, Hot Sausage and Shrimp - home runs all the way around......

I finally made it to this spot yesterday. I went mid-day Saturday so it was kinda quiet. The bartender was extremely friendly and welcoming, and you could tell he really loved his job. He asked what I like to drink, tequila, and offered immediately to make a custom drink for me. My bf and I each had one cocktail, but the beer selection was fantastic, the atmosphere was nice, the stools were SERIOUSLY comfortable but the best part is that they allow dogs INSIDE in the air conditioning. So on a hot summer day, get out of the house and bring your pup!!
Food was great today and our waitress was jam up! I was overly impressed with how fresh the food was and the staff was overall impeccable! We will certainly return!

Ok so I'm really writing this for the boyfriend. I'll be honest, I'll go to a car wash place and get it done, no need to get myself dirty! Lol but since he's into cars, he's always washing them! 

This is THE only self serviced car wash place he will go to.  His car is extremely low, therefore, his selection is limited.  It's very spacious and clean.  Thank goodness, I've been to some where I don't want to step foot out of my car.  The car ports are wide enough for all types of cars.  I have seen a small RV park in a port to be washed as well.  The set up is nice.  The washing stations are all to the left and the vacuuming and drying is to the right.  It makes more sense to me.  There are places where the drying station is right in front of it, so when you're done you pull up, while another car can pull up behind.  This ones neat because  people aren't staring at you from behind, watching what you are doing.  Instead it's more private.  

Anywho, it was just taken over, and I believe the owners are there most of them time and take the time to know their customers, even though it is a self service wash.
Great hotel clean and walking distance to all the bars. David during the complementary breakfast is kind, courteous and very helpful.

We absolutely loved this place for brunch. They had everything from the basics to an awesome waffle station, plus you can order items from the menu (all included).  

The Bloody Mary bar was phenomenal and the mimosas were plentiful. Our server was kind enough to tell us all about other places to explore or visit. He even took the time to write down a list for us. 

The ambiance here was phenomenal as well. Even though the place was slammed, we didn't feel like we were crammed in and the server was highly attentive. The next time I'm in Nashville, I'll definitely be back to this place.

This is the best cigar emporium in Arizona and possibly the US! Mike is extremely helpful when you call and make orders for delivery. He is able to give you deep discounts and the cigars are in great condition. I have bought full boxes as well as 20-25 assorted  cigar shipments and they always have given me a great deal. Shipping is quick in AZ, like one day and when I moved to Hawaii it takes only 2-3 days! Highly recommended!! Only place I buy bulk cigars.
The gluten free waffles are the best. They don't get soggy and they don't fall apart. I'm am so happy the chef took the time to figure out how to make food the gluten free community can enjoy.

still just as delicious as I remember from my undergrad days.  Nothing better than the berries plus as a delicious treat to sneak into the movies:). For those who complain about the styrofoam cups, they offer a discount if you bring your own, as well as sell reusable cups on site.  Calm down.

Absolutely a five. I love this place. Mike who tends bar is the best and just makes it look easy. The wait staff is awesome. Great vibe,great music and great draft beer selection. The pulled pork sandwich with long hots is legit the best. If I lived in the area I'd be here all the time. Hoping this review stays on the down low cause I wouldn't want to not be able to get a seat at the bar next time I aI am 100% in on the PIG.

I really enjoyed my multiple visits to Hogan's beach rest. I absolutely loved their Grouper sandwich...better than the one at Frenchy's near by in Clear Water beach. I absolutely loved their relaxed atmosphere, music was ok...can do better. But the place is really nice, lay back. If you want to have good drinks and a nice time I recommend it...the sunset views from here are Spectacular...never seen better ones. Will go back again every time I'm around the area.

Honestly it's very expensive for what you get. The food is about on par with cafeteria food in schools, which is not saying a lot. It's all fairly bland. The only thing I'd recommend at all is the dessert bar and only because o the chocolate fountain - the rest of the desserts are basically on par with what you would get out of a boxed mix. You will end up spending nearly $20 per person to eat here. The staff seems rude like you are an inconvenience to them, I've seen food taken away right as a guest is reaching for something, and food that's honestly cold when it should at minimum be warm.
Inexpensive hotel with some cleanliness issues. We found We were finishing up before checkout when housekeeping opened the door to our room.

Summer House was interesting, but my experience with the help was anything but friendly.  A tall blond not only showed up late to open the front door, but she was exceptionally rude.  I left without spending my cash, and I am sure the vendors would not appreciate her rudeness toward a client or her work ethic of arriving late while a customer waits for her to open the store.

Terrible experience. This goes as a first for the many times I've been to in n out. They only had a few people in the restaurant and a couple people in the drive thru. They took over 20 minutes to see me at the window, then said they never input my order, then asked me to pay and took another 15 minutes. Terrible management, poor training. The employees didn't appear to know what they were doing. They offered me a free shake, but all I really wanted was my food and maybe a store credit. Bad job.
Great broth and loved the option for adding extra veggies! I had enough pho for 2 meals. I will definitely be ordering from here again.

Update: Received an email from Ashley stating "I have logged your feedback as an official corporate complaint and have shared your comments with our Regional Vice President and management team for this location so that they are aware of the situation. They would like to reach out to you personally to speak with you about your experience.  Our team does the best they can to get back to all of our guests in a timely manner and you can expect to hear from someone within the next 2-3 business days."  That was Jan. 14th.

This Saloon is wonderful for drinking and meeting strangers who accept you as a regular. Open late and from time to time a nice dog is around looking for petting. Outdoor seating is great in good weather. Very nice neighborhood watering hole.

There is food available but it is not the reason to visit. 

Location is great for meeting people and for going to for a drink before or after events in the Warehouse District. There is some free parking adjacent to the bar. And there is street and paid lot parking in the area. 

I really like the place and do plan on spending more time there. Once again the people who frequent this place are friendly. So don't hesitate, if alone, to start up a conversation.

During my stay I had access to the exec lounge which made my stay great. Breakfast, snacks, drinks and evening hor d'oeurves really go a long way. Although the hotel is older, it was clean. I liked the robe and slippers touch. The breakfast is very basic. Extremely basic in fact but it beats nothing.

I'm sad to give such a low review because we had a pretty good, affordable meal a couple of weeks ago. HOWEVER we were going to eat at Benchmark last night but when we walked up to the restaurant I saw a server run, full speed ahead, after one of her tables that had just left. I assumed they had left their credit card or something of the like. But, she stopped them and asked them if "everything was alright?" They replied yes, they just closed out their tab and were obviously going home. To which she replied, with a snarky attitude "Oh, cuz I was wondering why you only left me a $2 tip." OH MY GOD PEOPLE. Do servers really do this? I thought I was crazy for witnessing what happened. We immediately turned around and went to Milk & Honey, which was amazing. I have worked in customer service/gratuity based industry for years and have never witnessed something like this nor would I ever in a million years think it appropriate to do so.

When critiquing a cheesesteak I look at:
1) Meat (amount and flavor)
2) Bread
3) Drip factor (GREASE)

Cleavers:
1) Flavor and amount are decent
2) Good bread
3) Not enough drip

There's something about a clean establishment that makes you miss the grunge factor in South Philly. Cleavers has such an extensive list of various versions of the sandwich, it's a great place to please many pallettes. And you can actually sit down indoors and enjoy your sandwich, with a beer too!

I think this hotel is great for an overnight or weekend getaway. I arrived right at check in time and was greeted by friendly valet staff. The people working the day shift on the desk were a bit less friendly than the valet staff but still helpful. 

Check in was quick and easy. I was placed in a room that's in a second building with no view of anything worth looking at. I was only there for the night so I didn't make a huge fuss about it. 

The reason the review is only 4 stars is solely due to the quality of the room. There were ants in the bathroom which no one wants to see in their room. We also woke up to a spider crawling across the wall. I noticed that when I look at the door it wasn't sealed well at the bottom and that's likely part of the reason there were critters in the room. Also, this is small pet peeve of mine but a thing I don't expect to be changed, the bathroom lights are outside the bathroom door and on the opposite side from where the door opens. The placement is just odd. My final gripe is that the WiFi was slow. 

I happened to lock myself out of room and while waiting for new keys and ride back, Rebecca was very nice and friendly. She not only asked us about our evening and gave additional suggestions, but she offered additional toiletries and gave us snacks as we waited near the fire place. She's really a great team member, the kind of person you want to
be greeted by. All of the valet staff we encountered were nice and worked quickly whenever a request was made. I really appreciated them having a golf cart to shuttle us between their buildings.

Went to get a coffee and decided to treat myself and got a pumpkin spice latte instead - it was great! I've had their regular drip coffee before as well & have enjoyed it. Cute place, a nice amount of seating & some tables outside too. The next time I go I definitely want to try one of their baked goods!

5 stars for bartending and bar back staff but less than one star for whoever was in charge. We decided to spend an evening here because it was new and seemed to have an impressive bar and upstairs patio. We went on a Sat night around 7pm. It was quite busy and we never got to see the upstairs patio, which was fine. What we did see (while sitting at the indoor downstairs bar) was a guy, wearing a headpiece, like a walkie talkie, berating one of the female staff members behind the bar. He referred to her as a "bitch" on one occasion and was being super rude and belittling to her repeatedly. Honestly, I'm surprised she didn't walk out crying. I've bartended for 10 years and there's no way I would have let this guy (Chris is what I heard him say his name was) talk like that to me or anyone else behind my bar. It was SUPER uncomfortable and I feel incredibly embarrassed and hurt for the girl he was berating. He definitely shouldn't be in a management position. Everything else about the place seemed cool. We didn't try the food. We mostly just wanted to finish our beers and leave before things got worse

I want to like this place but I have never been.  The first time I showed up within their operational hours that are posted online, no one was there and there was no sign indicated why they weren't open during their hours.  A neighboring business owner said "sometimes they are there, sometimes they aren't."  

Since then I have called multiple times to try and schedule an appointment to redeem my groupon.  I always get the voicemail.  They have called me back once, but unfortunately that phone call did not result in a visit because of extreme weather.  

I just want someone from this place to get in touch with me to let me know what their hours are and whether I can drop in to use the sauna.

Absolutely horrible service.  I got a delivery order from DoorDash from these guys based on the reviews on DoorDash.  They had decent reviews on there so I thought I would give them a try.   We ordered 3 things.  One of them being their vegetable and tofu soup.  DoorDash delivered the food and we were confused because there was no soup. I double checked my order just to make sure I actually ordered the soup and not something else by mistake.  But of course it says right at the top, "Vegetable and Tofu Soup".   I then thought I'd call the location to see what they may be able to do and when I called I just asked for the manager hoping to have a simple discussion of the mishap and get it corrected.  Well, let's just say it was anything but that.   The manager proceeded to tell me that it wasn't his fault that they didn't make the soup even though he confirmed that the order was there.   Then I told him all I wanted was to get what I ordered and he said that I would have to pay for it again.  What?!  So I order something, pay for it, you forget to give to me and you want me to pay for it again?   This is when I start to get upset.  As I'm getting angrier the manager cuts me off and says exactly this, "why you mad bro?"  Seriously?  What manager calls their customers "bro" and then proceeds to ask why they are mad when I already explained why.   Then he proceeds to tell me that a new girl wrote down the order wrong and it's not his fault.  I told him I didn't care who fault it was I just wanted what I ordered.   Anyways, you get the picture.   A manager that, no matter how hard I tried, interrupted and spoke over me constantly,  told me it was my fault they messed up, and then asked me to pay for an item twice is not exactly a good experience.   I would recommend you save yourself the headache of dealing with this place and order elsewhere.  DoorDash ultimately refunded me for the item and I thank them for that.  

Oh, and their food sucks.
Sandwiches are always yummy, staff is very friendly and the place is kept very clean. I eat lunch here 1-3 times per week and have always had a great experience!

So my wife's been trying to get me to go with her to this restaurant for some time now and today's our anniversary so I figured why not. I was not disappointed the wine is great!! A must have. The service was outstanding, Gerri S was very attentive and helpful. The food was fresh well made we will definitely return!

I would give this a 2.5 rating. It is sub standard compared to a Chinese take out place and about the same as grocery store sushi. It is above average compared to many buffets. Very good selection but it's standard Chinese buffet fare.
Excellent tacos, quick service,  great beer, and a nice patio for people watching.   We love the Thai inspired Thaicos, but even the "Simple" taco has a delicious flavor.
Food was good.  pork belly sandwich was excellent although kind of small. muffaleta was a pretty sizable portion, and it was good, but kind of salty.  brussel sprouts were good.

Delicious chicken, apps, and drinks.  Very similar to Hattie Bs.  Good service and not expensive.

Only negative- parking. You must valet or you will be towed.  They don't have a parking lot and not enough valet guys to handle the crowd.

I have to say Fushia was amazing, I have never ever tasted better Chinese food. I went in since the place is near my house and I was still a little cynical at first thinking I was going to get food that is similar to take out. I have to say I was pleasantly surprised. The waitstaff was so kind and constantly checked on us, the cook came out to ask if we like the food, and we even met the owners of the restaurant Sharon, who is super sweet and really an all around awesome person. I was honestly surprised at how much food I got, we got complimentary spring rolls (that comes with your meal) and an option of soup that also comes with your meal. I ordered Miso soup thinking that I was going to get some seaweed at the bottom of a pan but it was so good, one of the best Miso Soups I've ever had (Even though I have dinned in several Japanese restaurants in the past). My main course was the Orange Chicken. It was so tasty and awesome, I really couldn't believe how good it was. I have been telling all my friends about this place and am actually going to head over once I'm done typing this review. I recommend this restaurant for families, couples, solo, and friends to go to. They also just opened a Karaoke joint right next door where you can get low-cost Karaoke rooms and have amazing food all at once! 

I also just noticed they are now offering free discounts if you like them on Facebook!!!
https://www.facebook.com/pages/Fushia-Karaoke/

Currently taking the weekend classes. Lesley our mixologist teacher is great! But we've got 18 people in the class, only 3 to 4 people can really fit behind the bar to practice. So we practice in groups and have to share a lot of the bottles. So you're not able to practice efficiently and learn the count of your pours correctly and timing.  Our teacher Lesley did extend our class time an extra hour so we could get a little more time behind the bar to learn. But it was still very minimal.  Very frustrating with the money that I have paid and the little time that I get to practice.

I saw a Yelp photo of their awesome rooftop patio and couldn't wait to stop in. We made a reservation on a night with beautiful weather and are glad we did, as all of the restaurants in The Yard had almost two hour waits. When we arrived for our reservation, we were immediately seated. They have an indoor bar, two floors of indoor seating, and the upstairs, outdoor patio. It's very dim and swanky inside, so is ideal for a date, celebration, or business meal. 

Our server was super friendly, personable, and quick. She was the epitome of an ideal server, so they must have impressive training here. While you pay for the bread, it does come with a fancy bourbon maple butter that sounded delicious. We decided to instead start with the pimento cheese fritters. They come with a chipotle ranch aioli for dipping. They were a bit spicy for my taste, as pimento isn't my favorite cheese, but visually they're so appetizing and you can't go wrong with fried cheese. 

I then got the shrimp dumplings and grilled Caesar salad with chicken as my main meal (because I can't choose one thing, haha). The dumplings were okay. The sauce was super ginger-y and I bit into ginger a few times, which was a lot on the palate. The Caesar dressing was really good, although in general, I would prefer a cold Caesar as opposed to grilled. My husband got 
 the burger and fries, which was also pretty good, but we couldn't finish the fries because of how salty they were. 

HC Tavern is in the same restaurant group as the famous St. Elmo's Steakhouse, so you know they can make a mean steak and shrimp cocktail. That's what we'll be ordering next time!

I never write reviews but I just have to for Paul Revere!! It is an Amazing place! The White pizza is by far the most amazing pizza in town. The wings are out of this world.  They even called my phone one time to tell me I get a free soda with my purchase, since I order from GrubHub I wouldn't have had any idea, A+ customer service. Definitely worth the money.
Just a good old bar where people take full advantage of the smoking policy- which IS allowed. Nice bartenders - very friendly.

Visiting Philly for the day, and it started pouring rain. Went into this place to wait it out at the bar. Service was great, had a spicy margarita and some guacamole.  I'll definitely come back next time I'm in Philadelphia. Like the vibe of this place. People next to me had tacos, and they looked awesome.

This is a terrific neighborhood BYOB with home cooked Italian food as good or better than anything in South Philly.  They also offer an early bird special M- Th before 5:30 for $10.95 - Soup or salad and dinner entree.  The wait staff is very attentive to all the customers.  HIghly recommend

The 90-minute wait...outside in 40° was worth it for me. From what I was told, this is the Original Hattie B's Chicken so a lot of hype and history at this place. I had the Tender Plate (two Hot tenders, one Damn Hot tender w/ fries and coleslaw). I was a little worried about the spice levels but Hot was a little underwhelming and Damn Hot felt like it was right on the money of being spicy (caused a little sweat) without the misery. The chicken itself was so juicy! Fries and coleslaw were on point and was the perfect accompaniment to the tenders. Also ordered the peach cobbler to end the meal but it was pretty much a "gloopy" consistency and had a burnt taste. 

This location will always have a line of people so please make sure you show up at least an 1.5 - 2hrs before closing.

Found this place through Groupon and could not have been happier with the experience!  Our group was full of family and friends that all had to drive over an hour to the event.  Traffic hit us and we didn't get there until a half an hour late.  Frederick was wonderful and accommodated us despite that.  The experience itself is worth more than the price it costs, so go do it!!

Martail was the manager and bartender that helped us. The place was clean and busy. We popped in for a drink to
Check this black owned establishment out. It was a bit warm in the place because the air wasn't on. Martail quickly turned on the air for us which was great. 
We started our night with bourbon drinks so we kept it dark at ph Craft with whiskey drinks. I had the one winged Angel and my sister had the Guidance counselor. Shocked that when these were served to us in coupe type glasses, you know like a smaller sized margarita glass? We asked that they bring us a old fashioned glass with one whiskey cube so that we could pour our drinks into the old fashioned glasses. After that it tasted so much better! Many people like whiskey or bourbon neat but not if it's a mixed drink! An old fashioned is mixed and essentially that's what our drinks were! Hopefully they will consider changing the glass for other patrons permanently. We had a good time, the crowd was a mixed group of everyone. We were happy to come in and support a BOB....
Went here for lunch and was very friendly atmosphere. Had myself a cucumber drink and it was delicious.  Also has many tvs to watch games on.

Back in the 70s, when my dad was in undergrad at the University of Pennsylvania, he would come to Smokes (what everyone calls the place) to kill a few brain cells. Thirty years later, my best friend goes to UPenn, and whenever I visit we go here. It's definitely a place to end your night (at least, before you hit up a late night food place). You can sit in a booth and talk over a few pitchers, or dance your cute little butt off. Their bathroom situation is a little dismal considering how crowded it can get, but between Kweeder, Quizzo, and just seeing lots of friends,  this place is just as fun for my father's daughter as it was thirty years ago.

Went here for my 21st birthday weekend. Gave it 3 stars because it wasn't everything I wanted it to be. Very upscale club. Has many rooms with different music, which is pretty cool but I went on a Friday night and paid a $20 cover charge-not worth it. Drinks were expensive. I didn't think it was a very diverse group of people. Didn't think the DJ's were all that great even with rooms, I think each room is pretty small. I heard Saturday's are better but I won't be going back. Roxxy is better!

The food here was awesome. There was no wait to be seated and everything smelled delicious.  It had a nice vibe going on here.

The menu was a little confusing at first because we had no idea what was what, but the waiter was very patient with us and everything we ordered turned out so good!  I got one of the dishes with chicken and it had so much flavor.  I also recommend getting a flatbread appetizer, it was amazing!
These artists are great!  J9 does all my tattoos now. Shes very talented and is a genius when it comes to ideas. They always come out better than I imagine. I'm truly a lifer

We stayed at the Holiday Inn across the street. We were about to cross over the busy 5 lanes of traffic when a gentleman rolled down his window and offered to give us a ride. Scary, right? But it was the manager dropping off menus at the hotel. He even offered to bring us back. The French Dip was out of this world. Burnt end brisket bites were a little fatty and too sweet. On a side note, being from the south, stop messing with classic Cajun dishes. Pork in jambalaya was odd. Jaylon was attentive and friendly.

Update: yeah!  a restaurant with edible food in Aston!  We've now been here quite a few times since it opened earlier in the year, and it still definitely passes muster.  Menu is huge, and we've liked almost everything we've tried.  I still have no idea how they manage to provide such fast service at a packed restaurant with a huge menu, but they do.

We've found the gyros and cheesesteak to be fine, not best-in-class, but probably better than anything else in the immediate vicinity (although that's a very low bar). The French onion soup is good, and we've also had good luck with salads and breakfast foods.   Prices are reasonable, too.

Seriously the hoagie was good...but as a bread lover...dang that hoagie roll was delish!

They did an excellent job wrapping my three large Italian hoagies to go. I was going on a flight back to San Francisco. On my way down 95 I yelped a Deli nearby and voile...Jack's is what came up first. It was SO easy to get to. I was already pulled over so I only had to get back on 95 and take the Route 73 exit. make a couple of turns and there she was!

The wait was maybe a total of 18 minutes from order to walking out the door with my aromatic delectable treats! I also ordered a meatball hoagie, which was DELICIOUSNESS. I had them add mozzarella. The meatballs were seasoned just right for me and they add just enough mozzarella without it being a gooey mess. It was about a foot and a half long...and y'know what...I ate the whole dang thing by myself and didn't even feel guilty as the other passengers on the plane kept watching or turning their heads to see who had the most amazing meal on the flight!....Yup it was Moi!

They packed the sweet peppers and onions separately and honestly I don't know how they did it but the hoagies were not soggy when I arrived home more than 10 hours later. I have ordered from Primo's and Lee's and many other local shops but nobody has wrapped a hoagie to go like Jack's. They get 5 stars just for that alone!

Also their rolls...are amazing...I love the bread...and bread loves me...but truly their rolls are soft inside with just the right crispness of the crust. Also who still makes hoagies with sharp provolone?!! Well Jack's does and it's really tasty!

Overall I think Jack's deserves 10 stars because they're OUTSTANDING...like a farmer in his field! :)

A little pricey but overall a great environment and experience. Clean and relaxing. Good music. Staff is friendly and helpful. Wide selection of pieces and paints to work with. The mugs we painted (all amateurs) came out better than we expected.

My boyfriend & myself go to rubys every Sunday.  Today for some reason the place was not running smooth at all. As every Sunday I ordered my really big breakfast, I asked for cheese on my eggs. I got my food along with my boyfriends food, I didn't have any cheese on my eggs so I told my waitress, she took my plate. 30 minutes went by and still I did not get my food back & there was no sign of it coming anytime soon, now at this point I don't want the food anymore. My waitress then came and told me she took my meal off the bill, which was obvious because I didn't get the food to begin with. I work in the food business & when something like that were to happen a manager would come and apologize for the inconvenience & offer the entire bill to be paid, a free meal in the future or something along those lines, But nothing at all. So for that reason today would be the last time I would be stepping foot in rubys diner. They need customer service training classes.

Cheap (~2 per kolache), the dough is delicious, very filling treat. I've never had kolache before so I'm really excited about this place. So far I have tried the cherry cream, blueberry, cream cheese, spinach/mushroom/pesto, and the St. Louis slinger. Spinach and mushroom was delicious, as were all of the sweet varieties. I especially recommend the cherry cream cheese. The only ingredient that didn't work for me was the eggs, probably because I went at lunchtime and reheated eggs can only do so well.

I recommend going for breakfast - much of their menu sells out by lunch!
Nice joint. Casey did us solid at the bar. Enjoyed the wings and pizza during happy hour. Would return.

I called and placed an order for delivery, not only was it cheaply put together while getting charged about a 100.00 I asked for it to be Anonymous but apparently the delivery person didn't care and announced who it was from etc. called and complain and after 20 min arguing with the owner I was offered some credit back to me which I never received. If anyone is considering doing something special for someone don't go with this place, you'll get nothing but disappointment

This salon is formerly known as Lucky Dog. One of the Lucky Dog groomers purchased the business and created Suds and Swagger. So if you loved Lucky Dog, fear not because Suds and Swagger is still the same wonderful groomers. 

I love the girls who work here. I'm very picky about who grooms my dogs and have had some bad experiences in the past. My dogs always look great when they are done here and most importantly they don't seem stressed out.

I feel like I just have to leave another review - it's been two years since I wrote my last review where I claimed I was so impressed that I felt we would be loyal customers, and guess what! Here I am two years later still raving about this groomer! We are indeed loyal customers and always have the most pleasurable experience, and our dogs do too. They are literally smiling when I come to pick them up! The owners are truly wonderful people who are passionate about their work and adore all fur babies that enter. The grooming is top notch and the prices are the best around. I just can't say enough good things!!

OMG! I finally made it here.  I was meaning to come here for over a month and when I unknowingly discovered them online looking for Lou Malnati's in Chicago (weird right?).  Lou Malnati's ships their pizzas and I was looking to have one shipped because I just knew Louisiana, of all places wouldn't know anything about deep dish pies.  I was wrong, "Thats Amore Pizzeria" popped up on my search feed and I called to make sure Google wasn't tripping lol.  

I have to say, I was a bit skeptical; however, this place has nailed a Chicago's classic.  The sauce is very tasty, vibrant and thick. The cheese is cheesy but doesn't appear to taste or look the same as super fresh mozzarella.  That's fine because the darn ole thang was nothing but scrumptious!  

We ordered the 14" deep dish, half meat lovers and half supreme.  We paid $36 and it was well worth it.  We could only eat once slice each but have plans to annihilate it later on tonight or tomorrow.  

As far as service, the folks here are extremely
friendly and accommodating and they're offering curbside service only during this pandemic and it goes fairly smoothly. They do come outside often to give you an update of your order and that's alright by me.  

The restaurant's operation hours are modified a bit:  Tuesday - Sunday (closed Mother's Day) from 11am - 8pm.   I would certainly recommend them for their deep dish pie because it is really, really good!

I ordered two iced coffees on Sunday, one with cream and one without.  The iced coffee without cream was a few shades lighter than tea.  It tasted like coffee flavored water, and the other one tasted like coffee and cream flavored water.  I'd say their iced coffee needs some work.

7 days ago, paid $30 for gel manicure. It started to chip the following Wednesday. I went in today to ask about repairing it and Tony the tech who did the work did not apologize for the bad manicure and try to make it right, but instead he inferred somehow I caused this to happen by stating that that lots of people coming in after the holiday with chips and then he grinned about it. I requested either the repair to be made or my money back. The manager sat in her chair and did nothing. This is my first Yelp review and it saddens me that it is negative. The customer's experience should be the first priority. This nail salon has alot to learn about business and customer service. I will never go back. Should have waited for someone at VN Nails.

I purchased a Groupon as well. I thought it was ridiculous to have to pay extra tax and tip. 

I have been riding for many years and ride competitively on my college's equestrian team, so I'm not a beginner. 

The indoor arena had so much crap in it that it was hard to navigate and very small. 

The horse I was riding was fine, but had some serious confirmation faults (that impacted his gaits)  and refused/ran out on almost every (tiny) jump we did. 

The lesson itself was fine, but not worth $40 some dollars after you add in the "tax" and tip. 

As someone else said, after my lesson I was told to sweep the aisle-ways.  Yeah no. I have absolutely no problem cleaning up after myself but you can't ask your customers to clean your barn! I also got yelled at for walking through the "lounge" to get into the barn even though nothing was labelled, I had never been there before, and no one was there to like greet or tell me. 

I live very near to this barn and I see their student program at shows and my normal trainer and I joke about how much of a mess their riders are.

Had a nice couples massage here today. The massage tables are super comfortable. They are heated which is a nice relaxing touch. I was also asked if I wanted to add essential oil at no extra charge. Why yes I would, thank you! The eucalyptus smelled great. The message itself was good. I think I fell asleep for a few minutes. The circle massage technique they used on both of us felt great. 

Initially they had us fill out a paper with areas we'd like focused on and areas to avoid. I circled avoid massaging my scalp. It just doesn't feel good to me. Well, she ended with a scalp massage. Not the worst thing to happen but I thought why ask if you aren't going to read the paper? Overall, nice place and it was clean. I would go back.

This place came recommend but I really don't get why. I don't recommend. We waited in line outside for about 20 minutes which was fine since we had heard great things. Then once we ordered we waited another 45 minutes. When we asked the waiter what the deal was he said "didn't the host tell you it would be awhile". Since I wasn't feeling up to par it allowed me to drink my Sprite and chill but under normal circumstances I wouldn't have been happy. Since I wasn't up for eggs I ordered the crab cake burger. Bad choice: it had a hard crispy shell, a bready mushy inside, a hard dry bun and came with cold fries. The omelet hubby had was overcooked, the eggs Benedict had cheddar on top which was very strange, one egg was hard boiled. I'm a lover of the benny but I don't care for how they do it in New Orleans. I didn't taste the eggs Benedict but it didn't look too appealing.

I get the #6 Vegi. I love it. NEVER have had anything bad to say about a Jimmy John's and I have been eating there for 4 yrs. 

Order ... eat... love.

From start to finish Mercedes treated me like a princess.  She gave me helpful advice, did beautiful work to transform my dress to the exact detail that I wanted, stayed exactly at the quoted price despite re-doing certain parts, made me a lovely hairpiece, and even gifted me a pair of wedding heels!  She altered 3 bridesmaid dresses + flower girl dress just days before the wedding (please don't do that to her like we did!).     There is a great collection of jewelry at her shop - I bought my wedding earrings from her.  Visiting her shop is a fun, relaxing experience that reduces wedding related stress and allows you feel extra special and one-of-a-kind shopping and getting fitted in her sweet boutique.  The day of my wedding when I went to pick up my dress, Mercedes gave me her stash of ginger candy to calm my nerves & my tummy!  She is fabulous!

Do yourself a favor - order a Twin Turbo burger then come here and write your review.  It was a phenomenal burger that can go toe-to-toe with any local burger joint.  Very nice staff as well.  Give it a shot!

I bought a brand new suv gle Mercedes and it's horrible. It Shakes and rattles when its idling, I took it back and they said there was nothing wrong, it's embarrassing when I have anybody in the car. The remote start hardly ever starts the car and u have to pay a monthly fee for mbrace to b able to start the car, which they never tell u about. You should not be charged extra for mbrace . Between the rattling and shaking , it sure doesn't feel like a Mercedes, more like a vega. Very disappointed with Mercedes and fort Washington Mercedes.
Rude, dishonest place of business.  Was quoted one price and then they wanted $30.00 more because they mistakenly had me down for a right window instead of a left.  Don't trust them.

Somewhere well beyond the $5/30oz standard cold brew from Starbucks and the $9/32oz Fair Trade certified private label brew from Whole Foods lies Blueprint's sublime $13/32oz growler refill.

This is coffee with character.

Blueprint offers tasting notes for most of their products online and the cold brew is no exception. I  agree that chocolate is the most distinctive undertone, but would be remiss to not mention the floral hints in the mix. That the drink has layers of flavor that cascade, a pleasant mouthfeel free of wateriness and a finish like a slow, even fade is what really sets the drink apart.

Whether in whole bean form or premade, I can't help but trust the direction Blueprint is taking their Blue blend.

Pre-ordering online (Square-enabled) was simple and pickup was just as easy. The COVID processes for this location are about as seamless as they come, with clear signs outside guiding the direction of the queue towards the open garage door to the right, with several tables clearly designated and spaced apart.

If your looking for a quiet, candle lit, romantic restaurant then this is the place to go! My hubby and I LOVE it here. The food was amazing and had so many flavors! I've never tasted food this wonderful before. Please don't hesitate to eat here!

I read the reviews on Boat and Motor Superstores and I should have known better than take my boat there. I would not recommend anyone take their boat to this place as it appears to be a Bait AND Switch operation. I took my boat there for repairs they told me on the phone if I dropped it off on Friday I would have it back by the following Tuesday at the latest. Dropped it off the service rep said he would call me next week..DID NOT HAPPEN.. So I waited NO CALL..the Friday after I dropped the boat off I called...oh we are 3 to 4 weeks out he tells me...of course the service guy claims I would never tell anyone we would have your boat ready that soon next week as we are 3 - 4 weeks out. WELL if you had told me the truth I would have NEVER left my boat THERE. We are buying a new boat in January it will be Suzuki motor BUT I would never recommend this place to anyone. I begin thinking if they are being dishonest on the phone in an effort to get my boat there then I CAN NOT TRUST them to work on my boat especially after reading where they did not change the plugs when they did a 300 hour tune up on customer's boat. So today I went and picked up my boat after a month of not hearing one word and calling repeatedly..DON'T BUY FROM THIS PLACE OR TAKE YOUR BOAT IN FOR REPAIRS..read the reviews the positive ones are probably employees or something....BAD NEWS!!
fantastic food great atmosphere perfect for Family Dining the bar is really fun a lot of cool cocktails. Would definitely come back

I have been to Rat's before and always enjoyed it...but never made it to brunch. I'm glad I finally did. The food was amazing! Totally worth the price! I love brunch and have been to many around me in Bucks County and in Philly (and in Vegas)...this is one of the absolute best!
All I have to say is... juicy,  spicey , satisfying!! If you have or have not tried crawfish, this is the place for you!!

Cholita blew our minds Being from Texas, we consider ourselves taco snobs and Cholita satisfied every craving we had and more! 

 We started the meal with the Queso Fundido appetizer, you HAVE to order this. The bubbling cheese and chiles rajas had us scrapping every crispy bit off the skillet 

Like Mexican Street corn? Well Cholita has it with an awesome twist! Covered in flaming hot Cheetos and cotija cheese this is definitely one of the best variations we had. Don't forget to squeeze some like on top  

TACOS:

Al Pastor: marinated and fire roasted pork, salsa roja, pineapple, guacamole taquero

LA Style Mexican Hot Fried Chicken: cactus paddle salsa, California white sauce, cabbage, Mexican Buffalo sauce, lime 

Crunchy California Avocado: fried avocado, cabbage, Baja sauce, pickled veggies, pico de gallo

Charred Brussel Sprouts: melted cheese, caramelized onions, chili cream, pickled veggies (they do their brussels so unique and delicious we thought it was meat!)

Barbe'Q: 24 hour brisket, crispy chicharron, tamarind mustard BBQ, chipotle aioli, cabbage

We also need to talk about dessert because the Pumpkin Spiced Flan was the perfect end to our meal. This fall twist on the Mexican classic featured maple caramel, pepita granola, and whipped cream. Need I say more? 

We can't wait to come back to try to Sunday unlimited Brunch!

Instagram: @siblingsthateat

I always love a solid, hearty meal of Mexican food at reasonable prices. The chips and salsa are delicious, but don't like that you only get one basket regardless of how many people are in your party before you have to pay to get more. I've only been here twice, but both times the food has been great with extremely filling portions to the point where you're taking food home. The lunch specials are are a great value and are offered 7 days a week!

This place is like an "invasion of the body snatchers" restaurant.

It has all the requisite material to be considered a dining establishment, but it's bereft of substance, has no soul, both the food and the facility are woeful.  This was evident in a nearly empty dining room at 6PM.

By luck of the draw our server was very pleasant, upbeat, and prompt.  

The food is profoundly unremarkable.  There were five in our party, each entree was cooked properly and presented well but it was bland and generous in sauce and carbs, the cheap ingredients.  It appears meticulously prepared to preserve gross margin, meats were sparse.

The floors were filthy, as was the first table our hostess approached, when she saw it she moved to the next booth.  The custom booths are poorly made unless you like sitting on the edge of a 1x4 piece of trim in your cushion. 

On a good note the piped in house music is fabulous; only Drake's can compete with this soundtrack.  And the tea is good.  Otherwise you'd be better served with a microwave breakfast at a microtel.  Unless the lunch trade is really really brisk watch for 'For Lease' signs coming soon.

Great food and superb bar. Has a really wonderful ambiance. Feels like you're in a club from the early 1900s about to set off on some adventure.

I was pleased that there are vegan options on the menu. While I'm not vegan my wife is, and many times I prefer the vegan dishes for something lighter. On this visit they had a special carrot soup that was both vegan and amazingly savory. 

Cocktails are great. This is a somewhat upscale restaurant. However the staff are both super attentive and down to earth about helping you with menu choices. When not in the mood for a sitdown meal we like to sip and eat at their impressive bar. My only real complaint is now that Summer is almost here they don't have any outdoor seating. But truthfully the attention to 1910 style decor and furniture inside makes you want to soak up the atmosphere.
Huge Paesano's fan; welcome to the shore. Lunch will never be the same. Worth the trip to bring them back to the beach. Best sandwiches in town.

Poor experience here today. I took my friend for lunch, we both had the half sandwich/soup. She couldn't eat her tomato soup, said it tasted like eating marina, not tomato soup. My turkey, apple, cheddar sandwich was tasteless..the turkey was mushy. Hardly recognizable as turkey. Very disappointing.

Was craving shrimp and grits, so made it to Twisted Tail for Sunday brunch. Loved the ambiance and the server's attention. The shrimp and grits were good (not amazing, but respectably good). We started with a cheese selection. Would have liked if the server could have explained the cheeses better. We picked 3 with no information and ended up hating 2 of them. Overall, pleased with the experience.

A few friends and I stopped by for dinner. The patio and the Audubon bar is open. We ordered the pork belly bun, Caesar salad and  Vichyssoise soup. The pork belly buns come 3 to an order. The pork was juicy and the skin was crispy. The bun was nice and soft, very good. The Caesar salad came with anchovies and croutons, it was a good size. Loved the vichyssoise soup. It was served cold sitting on ice. Great flavor. Service was attentive. The manager, Kurtis stopped by a few times to check on us, great service. We sat in the library after dinner with the large Christmas tree and roaring fire. It was nice to relax with friends.

I'm only giving 2 stars because I actually love the groceries here. I would give 0 to the deli section. I've been coming here consistently for the past year. I'm vegan and had asked someone working in the deli section if the breads were vegan and he said all of the breads were vegan, usually I'd double check but he seemed very confident. I had been ordering the build your own veggie sandwiches almost every week. Until one time, I made the realization that the breads they used were the same ones out on the floor and almost all of them said eggs milk or honey. I had asked to see the ingredients list only to find that the bread I had been ordering for the past 6 months was NOT vegan. I expressed my concern, no one apologized. 

Today was the last straw. They had a new sandwich promoted called the ultimate veggie sandwich with broccoli sprouts and all sorts of things. I asked a deli person about the ciabatta bread, they looked and said the ciabatta bread didn't have milk so I had nothing to worry about. I came back to pick it up after some shopping and they informed me that the bread said it may contain traces of milk and I said no worries I'm vegan not allergic, she smiled and handed me my sandwich. I got home and unwrapped it only to find freaking cheese on the sandwich, no broccoli sprouts, no almond spread. Was this girl delusional? She made sure there was no milk in the bread but then she puts cheese on my sandwich?! The deli people are very snarky at times and uneducated on allergens and diets. It makes me so angry that somewhere like Sprouts doesn't train their staff better. I really like this grocery store but I can't keep getting my orders messed up. What if someone was terribly allergic to something?

THIS PLACE IS AWESOME! My husband and I came here for "Motown Monday's" and danced all night. It was hard to leave! Drinks are good, music was awesome, interior is a bit small but nice and clean, and the staff is friendly. Don't miss this local spot if you're in the Nashville area!

We have a spider and a wasp problem and we've tried to take care of it ourselves for the last two years. But after both our kids got stung by wasps last summer, we knew we needed to get some professionals. 

I was referred to Vertex by a friend and then of course checked the Yelp reviews. It was a pretty easy choice! We opted for the quarterly service which includes a first, thorough treatment.  Because we were referred, we got 50% off! So this first one was $85. After that it will be $99 a quarter. 

They sprayed the foundation and eaves, put granules in the grass, dewebbed and denested all over the house and treated all the areas where they found nests. If we see any nests or extra pests, they will come out every time to take care of it free of charge! 

I can't yet speak to the long term quality of the service, but I was really impressed by our first appointment. Matt was our technician and he was amazing. Efficient and very knowledgeable. He patiently answered all our questions (while maintaining a 6 ft distance). 

Yay for no more wasps!

I love this Korean place, and it's one of my favorites! 

Service: Their service is okay, but sometimes they can forget about you! The more I've been, the more they don't check up on me which should be the other way around haha.

Atmosphere: Its a nice place to just eat; nothing fancy since it's just a typically Asian place to dine into. Everyone is enjoying their food and seems satisfied.

Pricing: Its decent for the amount your getting...Korean food is generally pricy in my opinion. Lately I feel like their portions have gotten smaller? At least in the galbi which what my boyfriend says. It's still okay, a lot of places are more pricier.

Food: We always get the Galbi! And a big soup. The Galbi is perfectly flavorful and the soup is soooo good. Although we tried a different kind of soup with the beef in it and I didn't like it since it was way too sweet. I prefer the spicy seafood!! Knocked off one star because they haven't been consistent with their food lately and it makes me sad because I really liked them a lot when I went their for a couple times, but I feel like a lot has changed. 

Other than that, food is great. Everything is good so I'll always be back anyways!

The cashier was maybe going through an existential crisis which slightly gave me a non friendly experience however the food was amazing I had the brie and avocado bagel and their espresso was good too.

This is the best Chinese Vietnamese food in all of Delaware county. Eat in is BYOB. 
 Forget delivery and pick up

Love this place!! Had so much fun with my kiddos and got amazing photos of them. They also have some great vegetables at very reasonable prices, especially the zucchini it was a great size to make stuffed zucchini with. Had food and drinks available as well.

The staff at this store are fantastic. Nick was very helpful and answered all my questions. He helped me find a better tire for my car than what I was previously looking at and at an even better price. Every single person in there had a smile on their face, thanked you for coming in their store, and were extremely pleasant. Overall great customer service and good prices. I have found my new tire store!

Looks can be deceiving. It doesn't look like much from the location and from outside but I received a great shrimp Caesar salad. Definitely will be going back. Just an FYI- the bad reviews all seem to all be regarding delivery services and how long it took to receive the food or bad communication about it.

This park is a hidden gem! If you have a kayak, they have a nice launching point. I ran into 2 park rangers, who were both very kind and told me the best route to take on my trip and answered a lot of my random animal questions. If you bike, there's a long paved trail you can enter before the parking lot. There are also a lot of people who bring pets here for walking- just make sure you bring a leash! I saw someone's dog run straight into the water which is filled with gators- luckily it was fine this time.

I've been doing business with these fine folks at Eddie's Guitars for years.
