Whether it's high end, or something small, they have consistently shown outstanding time and support towards my needs as a musician. The entire staff has been so supportive. They carry affordable and/or super cool custom equipment from the best brands. I really appreciate this 'personal attention' and I look forward to doing business with Eddie's Guitars for years to come.

SUCH a rockin experience. We had a great time. They give extensive information and each tourist guide gives a different perspective. you can use it to go around town as a taxi, but free, getting off at all the major stops like the zoo, museums, barnes, etc! get a citi pass and use it up! 

we had Meg who you will recognize because she can do back bends to avoid the branches. ok branches you ask? well if you get the super tall bus, which i highly recommend, you have to duck here and there to  avoid the branches. Hey it just makes it that more entertaining. So meg can do back bends to avoid them... skill I am telling you. She is also the most informative tour guide.. then there is a younger, blonde haired woman. you will recognize her.... doesn't matter, she is great too. they run (the buses not the tour guides) very often, so you can wait around and talk to people if that is your thing and then hop on the bus.
Reaaaaalllly.good.  calamari appetizer was the best ive had in a long time.. friendly and responsive waitstaff.  Definitely a place to go again

Fantastic experience! The fried green beans are the bomb. It is a must get if you are there. 

Cool staff and fun patrons.

Root is one of the best new restaurants of Philly, and seeing as it's tapas style (I love a little taste of everything), I had to try it as an out of towner. I went during happy hour, which was an amazing surprise and win cuz I got the sangria for $7, famous and super delicious chicken liver toast for $5, croquetas for $5, garbanzo beans for $5,  and salumi and cheese board for $7. Such a deal!!! I also got the gnudi ($14). I wish I could've tried their burger, the lamb chops, and more toasts.... 

..btw, Philly's high-end restaurant scene is really into toast! Everyone offers their own version or specialty.

Anyway, the star dish was the croquetas. They were crunchy, creamy, and complex with tarragon, parsley, and Parmesan notes. It made my eyes go like WOW.

 The chicken liver toast was dang tasty too, and something I'd like to eat again and again... 

Pass on the chickpeas and gnudi. 

Decor is retro modern. Very interesting. I hope to return with a bunch of friends so I can try more dishes.  Now go!!! Before they get too crowded!!

A few nights ago I was pulled into this bar by a friend because they had to have a special drink called a "HARRY POTTER". 

Its a blue shot , with a lemon slice hat, sugar on top that is set on fire with some other alcohol. As its on fire they spike it with cinnamon which creates a really cool camp fire magic effect and is definitely a showstopper. The fire also warms the lemon and caramelizes the sugar and when blown out this becomes your shot chaser. Too much sugar can lead to mixed reviews on this as it alters the experience.  It is a magical experience and a great way to impress on a fun night out. 

The inside bar has well dimmed lighting and lends it self to that old fog atmosphere of Ireland. There are interesting artifacts on the walls and ceiling which make it well suited to create that old tavern ambiance.

The restrooms are really far in the back down a hallway that feel like two separate buildings. I wish they could dress it up in the future to keep the experience harmonized.

The smoking porch out front is a fun place to post and watch passerby's on this bust street and witness cops doing their job, whilst having strong feelings of schadenfreuden as you see people get pulled over.

Its a great pub and one i will be giving more patronage in the future.

I got the cheesesteak (10inch) with mushrooms. They have a 16 inch too but the smallest one was 10 inch. The cheesesteak by itself is 7.25 but with mushrooms came out to 8.25 and then I got a coke which was 2 bucks.

The cheesesteak was pretty good- probably would be super dry without the cheese. I wish I had mayo to put on it. I think for wilmington standards this place is pretty decent. It closes at 7pm on weekdays so we got there right before it closed and had to bring our food back to the hotel to eat. It's basically a hole in the wall place, it's in the shopping center towards the back side 

When I was eating the sandwich I liked it but afterwards when I went out it didn't really sit well...so not sure what happened there. 

I'm from philly so I'm probably biased when it comes to cheesesteaks.
A must see bar in STL!! The views are breathtaking and the ambiance is perfect! It's indoor/outdoor so if the weather becomes unruly you have a place to get warm or dry! So beautiful!

YUM! Do yourself a favor and GO HERE ASAP! The owners are super friendly and helpful while ordering. They use fresh ingredients, fresh bread, unique side options and super delicious banana pudding. The philly and meatball sub are so good but we're looking forward to trying the brisket on our next visit. Kudos to local businesses doing it right!

Worst salad place of my life. Normally a salad place has green lettuce, but the lettuce was mostly white and it looked like it had been left out for a while, despite going shortly after they opened. They had many options to add to their salads, but it seemed random, not very cohesive. They also lacked many vegan items, despite being a salad place. I asked for a vegan dressing and the staff was not sure which ones were vegan. I was very disappointed.

It just doesn't get any better if you're an extreme carnivore like I am.  I've come here a few times with very large crowds (15+ people) and the staff was great.  You can roll your own coolers in full of beer and wine and proceed to get pissy drunk while you bbq your own food @ the table.   Shrimp, bulgogi, flank steak, pork belly are some of my favorites.  The meat platters come w/ unlimited side dishes of Kimchi, veggies and my favorite, seaweed salad, which will help all that meat exit pleasantly the day after.

After dinner you can go downstairs and rent karaoke rooms, this place is a blast and has quickly become one of my favorite spots in Philly.  A must go for bbq lovers!

After dinner on a Saturday night, we walked to Acme Feed & Seed on Broadway. Initially, I had ignored this bar because it seemed touristy. But, it kept popping up in suggestions, so I thought it might be worth checking out. I should have stuck with my first instinct. 

Everyone said that the three floors all had different vibes. Lies. The main vibe was casual, loud, college, tourist. The first floor apparently has live music. The second is a sushi bar. When reading about that, I imagined a more refined, quiet vibe -- wrong. It was like a grocery-store sushi vibe. And it looked like they were still serving burgers and your standard bar food on the second floor. The third floor is reserved for private events, and the fourth is a rooftop bar with an indoor area. Good view or not, it was packed like all the other floors, so not worth it. 

We decided we'd go get a drink on the sushi floor, but when we saw the long line and that they were serving drinks out of plastic cups, we were out. No craft cocktails here.

I've ordered from here a few times now and I am very very pleased with the quality of seafood +freshness . Their boils are amazing and generous portions . One thing that really impressed me is that when ordering through the Uber eats app my orders are usually very specific and picky lol . They have never messed a single thing up , kudos to that .  You don't have to worry about liquid or anything leaking , they secure the boils in plastic bags . Delivery time is very reasonable ! I recommend anyone who loves seafood tries this at least once . You'll be hooked ;) 10/10

I can't believe this car wash has the nerve to be in business. My car is two weeks old with just a light dust of dirt on it. I paid $10 for the wash, went through, and twenty minutes later when I looked out my rear view mirror I could still see dirt on the window. Upon further inspection I could still see the exact same dirt on the car that I had when I went in the wash. In fact, I could still see the handprints left in the dust. The car looked completely unwashed. The windows were covered in spots worse than before. I had to drive out to quakertown for a proper wash, and ended up spending another $13 to get it done correctly. What a scam, don't waste your money.

This place is a super cute spot you must try.  The side salad alone is off the charts.  The Madeira chicken is incredible and scratch made mashed was perfect. Everything is made with love. The tropical nachos were a new flavor profile, and the beet salad was a rave!!!
awesome food! The waitress seemed to be having a rough day accepting her life decisions...but, who hasn't at times?

Jen Grillo was my stylist. I was a walk-in. I requested a trim and color and was able to be attended to quickly for a walk-in. Jen was attentive, polite, and knowledgeable of what she was doing. The time I spent in the salon was a great experience. I definitely recommend Ms Grillo. My hair turned out beautifully!

Extremely friendly staff, quick service and a variety of pizzas. The Kitty pizza was my favorite, jalapeños and Nashville hot chicken. The crust was crunchier than I normally like it but if you like thin pizza with a good size crust this is a good place to go!

Enjoyed breakfast here last Sunday Oct 3. We went early around 8:30 a small line, but when we left about an hour later it was much longer. There were the BEST biscuits that we tried in all of Tennessee and we went to Memphis too! I ended up buying the jam, it was so good! I didn't care for the eggs, sausage as much. Must try!

Bought a Louisiana pellet grill last week. Price lower than MSRP - anywhere!
Elderly helped get the grill from the storage area, and two young men loaded it in my car. Flawless service, full customer satisfaction and I will return to this store as a regular customer. The elderly gentleman made sure I received the proper cover for the grill - he made a point of it. Real professionals here.

Wow, the owner knows his sake. He gave us a quick lesson before we ordered, and he was really nice and polite. The happy hour was great! We must have ordered five different things for a very reasonable price. I'm a fan.

The main reason I don't frequent Olive Garden is the price vs the quality of food. Gave it another try and had same experience. Unlimited salad was poorly made due to limited dressing.  Portion sizes were small. Food was average at best. 1/2 of customers jammed into a few small sections so felt crowded even though much of restaurant was almost empty. Server was friendly but odds were stacked against the poor guy. Always hope for that one store in a chain that's a standout. This isn't it.

Mama Dawn was so welcoming she made you feel like you were family explaining everything from the menuto the history of Goody Goody.  She gave us some good recommendations as well. Emily our server was awesome as she kept checking on us to see if we needed anything. The royal with cheese was a homerun. Our family will beck to check out the breakfast!!!

The food was wonderful! The woman behind the counter was wonderful and warm and friendly. It took a little while, but they make everything to order, and it's worth it. Make sure to bring cash, it's cash only!

Update 12/30/2015
The collection agency hired by RR have declined to pursue their claim against me.

I have offered on several occasions to pay for a mediator to have our concerns heard, and if they rule in RR's favor, I will gladly pay them. To date, RR have refused to accept that offer.

RR corporation don't want to get involved because they say, the Santa Barbara company is a franchise, therefore they can't comment on it.

I have heard some horror stories about RR since my bad experience, and will be posting them once I have confirmed their accuracy.

12/10/2015
Roto-Rooter tried to clear a block drain. What followed, ended up costing us $4,495.00.
After the work was done, I discovered that most of what they did was totally unnecessary. They have put our account into collections with a company that has a stellar reputation for collecting, mainly by one person in particular who I'm not sure I can mention his name yet.
His tactic is to try and intimidate and threaten you. I'm coming up to 70, but I still think I can hold my own especially when it deals with the likes of this guy. I'm in discussions with another representative, of this collection company, a woman who appears to have a little more tact. Depending on how this is finally resolved, will depend on how in depth I want to explain what happened.
In brief detail though, the house we bought had had the septic tank empty a few weeks prior to us moving in. This apparently is the law in California, yet the Rooter team leader that came to my house, talked me into doing all this extra work because he said it needed it. I'm no sewer expert, and if my doctor says my pipes need cleaning I believe him, the last thing I want is house full of you know what.
For now, I'm going to hold off commenting any further until I hear back from the new representative to see if this can be resolved amicably. I have told her that I refuse to pay anymore unless we can have an independent mediator or go to court and have a judge decide if we were ripped off or taken advantage of because of our age. Here is a link to an interesting article about elder abuse.
Elder Financial Abuse.pdf https://www.wiserwomen.org/images/imagefiles/Elder%20Financial%20Abuse_Undue%20Influence.pdf

Plump and juicy melons. Large and firm bananas. Thick and engorged meats. The selection and quality is evident, and you'll detect the Earthy aromas as soon as you walk in. What the staff lacks in hygiene, they make up for in personality. Hit the fridge section early in the day if you want the organic chicken thighs. They fly.

My wife and I came to New Orleans for our honeymoon over Christmas. We got one of the Corner rooms at the Ace Hotel, and our stay was fantastic. The room was beautiful - lots of windows, great design, unique furniture, beautiful bathroom. The staff were all incredibly friendly and helpful. The restaurants and coffee shops downstairs are great. Location is safe and close to everything. Five stars!
The veggie tikka masala and the lamb tikka masala are both really good. I always order delivery from this place and is always on time and the food is hot when it arrives.

Maybe they should change the name to Joey's Cheesesteaks and Pizza.  Leading solely with pizza highlights (to be honest) their worst offering.  The crust is way overdone and tasteless.  Everything on top is good but just like a sandwich, the bread is everything.

Before coming to Saint Louis I looked up some of the best restaurants and almost every site I went to mentioned Pappy's. I don't really eat that much BBQ, but this was definitely some of the best I've had. I went with the pulled pork which was done well and served in generous amounts. They don't put any sauce on the pork, you get to choose from one of the four sauces they have on all the tables. 

I tried two which honestly tasted the same to me and weren't really very spicy, but they were both good nonetheless. Food is served super fast and the restaurant is very clean. It was pretty packed when we went on a Monday around 12ish, so I would definitely recommend getting there before the lunch rush if you don't have time to wait in line. It moves quickly, but this is not the place to go if you're in a rush.

Some of the other reviews site bad service for not giving 5 stars. Yes, the service is hit or miss, but for the most part, it's never been a huge issue for us. But because we ALWAYS love the food, BJs gets 5 stars from me! Yummy.

This is the worst restaurant that I have been to in a long time. I Sat there for an hour while the waitress kept walking by back and front, it has been a long day and all I wanted to get a Meal and relax before retiring for the night they need to get rid of the bad waitress.

This was my first birthday cake, meaning year 1.  I can say that between me and my family, we have probably ordered cakes from Viking Pastries 50 times over.  For my recent 30th, my mom asked me what I wanted for my birthday.  I didn't care what I got for presents and I didn't care what we ate for dinner...all I wanted was a birthday cake from Viking.

In the end, since it was President's Day, they happened to be closed even though they told my mom they were open.  I didn't get the cake (apparently they are going to make good on it later) but my birthday still was good.  I should deduct a star for that, but I won't based on the fact that their cakes are amazing.  

The fam favorite is chocolate chip cake on the inside that is so moist, I could eat it sans icing.  But why would I when their icing is the best I have ever eaten.  It is also surrounded by large chocolate shavings that are easily picked at by kids (and maybe some adults) before we get out the second line of Happy Birthday.

They are not open every day (as we just learned) so be careful.  Also, recently I had their cream filled doughnut and it was amazing.  Amazing.  Did I write that enough?  No....Amazing!

I've had mixed experiences thus far, never anything worse than okay and being that it's i one of my favorite neighborhoods, I'll certainly keep coming back.

We'll start with lunch.  In my opinion, the Blue Corn Fried Oyster Baby Spinach Salad is one of the best salads in the city.  It's such a great blend of flavors and somehow, the fried oysters don't make it a heavy salad to digest.  It's filling, but won't send you headlong into a food coma.  The Cuban sandwich is a good bet too, but I'd shy away from the hotdog.  It may be hyped by the posters on the walls, but my boyfriend and I have been thus far unimpressed.  

On to breakfast (or brunch...depending what time you roll out of bed).  Bottom line?  I was underwhelmed.  Nothing on the menu jumped out at me, so I stuck with the basics:  eggs, bacon, grits, and a biscuit.  It was perfectly fine, but nothing I couldn't get elsewhere for less.  The Boyfriend went with the biscuits and gravy.  Again, perfectly good, but nothing particularly mind-boggling.  If you get there early enough that there's no wait, go ahead and have breakfast, but it's not necessarily worth the 30-40 minute wait you have to look forward to if you get there after 10 (unless you're in the mood for lunch...then stick around!)
Highly recommend " The Local Burger" with sweet potatoes. As a Nashville native one of my fav burgers in town. I'd say I've had most of them.

I was treated to brunch at Iron Hill on a freezing Sunday afternoon, and was delighted with almost everything about the outing.

Their brunch menu was, in a word, impressive.  When I lived in Boston, I was a brunch addict-- I mean every week, sometimes twice a week-- so you can definitely take my word for it when I say impressive.  I closed my menu with something in mind, though pretty much established that I could have closed my eyes and pointed to any option and been perfectly content.

The food was great (I had eggs benedict), and the service was friendly and attentive: she kept the water coming; something VERY important to me and always worth a full star.  Oh!  They also bring you cups of fresh fruit and delicious muffins!

My only complaint is that things got a little awkward when my boyfriend and I got there.  The host and a few other employees just kind of stared at us when we entered, and then seemed very unsure about themselves when giving us a table.  It made me feel like I was trespassing.  Later, when our friends arrived, the host mentioned that half of their party was there, but they had no idea where they (we) were seated.  Our friends just kind of wandered around the restaurant looking for us.  Ha.

So they get a C in first impressions, and an A+ in everything else.  Not bad.

Absolute favorite place to hangout in donelson. It is my local pub. Live music almost every night. The selection of Irish whiskeys is unmatched. Don't forget there is a bar upstairs too with  darts. Take a friend, take a date, or take the family.  You will have a great time.
Service was good. Beer was food. Portobello burger was super tasty and huge. Definitely filled me up.

This place is better than Geno's, but worse than Jim's. I like the way their chop their onions smaller here, but they don't mix the cheese in as well and their rolls are usually a little bit more stale than Jim's.
The rack of lamb was amazing! I was there for about 30 minutes which was great and quick service. The frog legs were also a cool experience. Great choice in STL

This place is a good spot to eat, the food is great and the location is in the cool, hip location of the gulch. This restaurant has you line up and order before you sit down, which is different and in my opinion is effective.

I would recommend coming on a weekday rather than a weekend, I've tried to come on a Saturday/Sunday are out the door, so try to arrive early if you're up to the challenge. 

The food is great! The Bonuts are amazing to share and the chicken sandwiches are great!

An amazing café in University City! The food and drinks at this café are delicious and the portion sizes are quite large. Would recommend the Philly and the apple cider and hot chocolate. The staff are also very friendly and accommodating! The only thing is that the café is always busy (for good reason), and so before you go, make sure to join the waitlist online around half an hour before, as they don't take reservations. This is such a nice café for a weekend brunch!

Horrible Service in Clarksville, TN!! Work they started in April 2017 that I was told would take no more than 2-3 weeks max to finish is still incomplete and already being paid for through SMAC since Terminix initiated it in April-may 2017!! DO NOT USE THEM!! Broken promises, inconsistent stories, terrible communication, and Service and Branch Managers do not get involved to follow-up on delinquent and incomplete jobs. Where is the QA/QC? I have been EXTREMELY PATIENT with the Goodlettsville and Nashville Branchs for almost 5 months!!!  No more...
Employees were outstanding and I was in and out quickly. I will be a repeat customer. Sorry I thought I hit 5

The service was great - but the sandwich was incredibly overpriced for what you receive. I ordered the "unforgettable baggins" - definitely not memorable. Still searching for a great deli in Tucson ...

Visited 8/2/2014 during my bday weekend festivities.
It was ok.  Not great.  Not bad.  Just ok.

SERVICE
Average at best.  The place was not busy when we went (3 or 4 tables) but somehow our waiter was gone for long periods.  When the waiter was with us, he was nice though.  The wait between courses was a little long considering the lack of customers.

FOOD
N.O. BBQ Lobster: amazing!!!
Buttermilk Poached Chicken: a little dry to me (I prefer dark meat) so I requested more lobster sauce and the waiter made it out like it may be a problem to get the chef to give me more stating "I'll see if the chef will give you more".  Ummm ... I'm the paying customer.  The chef should be giving me whatever I want seeing that I'M PAYING FOR IT.  With more sauce, the dish was much better.  Great flavors.  
Peanut Butter Stuffed Chocolate Tart: pretty good / different but good

OVERALL
I'm not rushing to get back there, but if a group was celebrating something there I'd definitely go!

I don't think people, or even Yelp! understands how much of a help this app is for me when friends (mostly visitors) are constantly asking for places to go and things to do around town. I rely on my bookmarks a lot, it's great for knocking items off that checklist and exploring new places myself. It's not always i get to be a tourist in my own city, and this app is more so my tour guide.

Anyway... all that to say.... had a visitor and we were in dire need for nourishment nearby. It was one of the top choices in the area from the bookmarks, and one that's been on my mind a lot so it was meant to be for paying a visit.

We got there around 5:30 ish, no reservations, but apparently we got there just in time because when we walked in, there were only a couple tables occupied, but by the time the first glasses of wine hit our table, the place had just about filled up.

I ordered the combination plate of the crab imperial and crab au gratin and sweet baby Jesus! I was not ready for all that goodness bestowed upon me. Everything was divine but that crab au gratin: Prescious! I felt bad because my friend wanted more of it when I let her try but I learned from her review that I was being stingy with the c.a.g. (you would be too).

Also helped ourselves to the bread pudding dessert with pecan ice cream on top. It was a crime how good that bread pudding was. Like its not even fair. If they don't have one of the best bread puddings around, someone please tell me who's better!

This cozy place is tucked away on a small side road but once you turn off the highway, it's pretty easy to find. We stopped by on our way home from Reno.

I ordered the sandwich and soup lunch special, which was plenty enough to fill me up. The soup was flavorful and fulfilling (forgot what flavor exactly but it was creamy and tomato based). I also enjoyed dipping the sandwich in the soup, since the consistency was relatively thick. The sandwich was nice and crispy on the outside and I enjoyed it as well. My friends ordered the prime dip and they loved it. It was a lot of food but they finished all of it.

The service was great. They only had one waitress when we were there, but she was welcoming and attentive the whole time.

Came here after a very long day and not in the best of moods, but the food and amazing service from our waitress Kelsey definitely brightened all of our days. We shared dishes and my favorites were the fried chicken and the plantains foster split.

Yum, every time I visit Tucson I go here. Let me state for the record that I am not a vegan, or even a vegetarian. It doesn't matter- I love the hearty comfort food and the cozy atmosphere this place has. The woman who owns the place is super interesting and sweet. The restaurant itself is a great place to relax and enjoy yourself.

Chicken dishes, burgers, fries, chili, and soups have all been very good. It's a bit on the expensive side, especially for the Zone, but easily forgiven once you've had a bit to eat. My friend (who is vegan) says the dinners here are even better.. so if you have a chance you should probably visit then.

I had a really great experience at Opa a few weeks ago.  My boyfriend and I were looking for somewhere to have dinner on a Thursday, and because we both have packed schedules it happened to be at 9:30 pm.  Most places weren't open, but we decided to try Opa.  We were warmly welcomed, and seated promptly.  Upon ordering we discovered that they closed at 9, but that they had cleaning up to do, so we should enjoy our meal and not feel rushed.  

The service the rest of the evening echoed the initial encounter.  The food was good and the portions were gigantic, leaving plenty of leftovers.  But, as I've said previously the service was really the star of the night.  I know I'll go back to Opa again for that reason alone.

Quiet little coffee house off the beaten path...the blueberry muffin was delicious, moist and full of blueberries with a crumble crown. Would have given five stars but the staff weren't as inviting as I thought this small community coffee shop would have (average service)--still a must during your walk down Magazine St in NOLA.

Where to start the customer service was on top of there game. They sat us quickly and were extremely nice the waiter came in a timely manner. The food was soo good everything was fresh and it was very much enjoyed by all. I had the  strawberry margarita it was very good. I will definitely come back

Mediocre. What some DO's don't get is that people want the best quality care. When they fall short, it is a serious turn-off. My impression was that it's all about billing as much as possible with all the testing that they do. I felt like it was more of a standardized visit, not a personal visit. I recieved the same run down, routinized process. It was this, that and done. Pleasantries were exchanged but that was the depth the interactions. 

I recieved all the allergy testing (prict and intradermal) which I thought was beneficial in order to find out what I was allergic to. I was surprised by the results. The only thing I do not like with the intradermal testing is the potential for leaving marks. The MA that administered it wasn't gentle either--very abrasive.

You'll leave with medicine necessary, especially if you're an asthmatic or have chronic allergies. The visit is also long--about 2 hrs.

I'd hope to find a specialist that listens, is analytical, and will personalize the visit.

Was it the fact that Carlos Ruiz won the game for us last night? Was it the fact that I was with my best guy? Was it the beautiful weather? Was it my fantastic Bull's BBQ sando? Or was it the fact that every time I go to "The Bank," I have an awesome time. Whether we buy last minute standing room only tickets or are lucky enough to scam season tickets from my mother, I have never had a bad experience at CBP. I loved the Vet, but I can abandon nostalgia to admit how freaking awesome CBP is. From the t-shirt selection in the Alley Store, to the veggie food options now available, to the relative cleanliness and overall good spirits of all guests and staff, this place can't be beat! Insider tip: STANDING ROOM ONLY TICKETS ARE A STEAL!

Came here for lunch last week, as it was a nice but not so humid day on the beach. I was craving some seafood and a beer. The place was relatively steady when we arrived and we were seated promptly. There is both indoor and outdoor seating. Bars inside and outside. Very old Florida vibe. Waitress was very friendly. However, when we placed our order, we waited over ;0 minutes for our food. The place slowly started to clear out as time went on, but no sign of our food for such a long time. I got the blackened grouper sandwich with fries. It was cooked very well-fresh and flavorful. But again, the wait time almost made it not worth it. It seemed the staff were stretched too thin. I'd be willing to try the place again if they get some more hands on deck.

This is good food. This one star is because the order taker told me not to treat her like she was STUPID! I was at the takeout order counter waiting to order and the phone rings. She picks up the phone and takes an order right in front of me. Then takes my order, I ask her to make sure my order goes in first. Thats when she said the other one already already went in and mine was almost at the same time and don't treat me like I'm stupid....I did no such thing...she did it herself by not putting phone order on hold and taking present customer order first. Her name was Angel...she is no Angel. Additional watching her prepare the orders in the kitchen she had a long pony tail she wore in front over her shoulder that dipped into the food as she spooned it into the plates. And there was a spillon the floor she wiped it up with towel and her foot then threw the towel into the kitchen which landed on the order up counter...now I'm glad my order is not up!!! Lol...not coming back until she is gone.

Looking for a quick delicious lunch? This is the place to try.

Had never been here before, and I saw the menu on the wall outside while I was walking to my vehicle. Since I needed to get something to eat, I figured I'd go ahead and try this place.

I ordered the Lunch special: whole sandwich + half bowl soup or salad.
I had the pesto chicken sandwich with clam chowder. It also came with a small roll and a chocolate covered strawberry.

The chowder was flavorful without rubbery pieces of clam in in. The pesto sandwich was tasteful as well, but I forgot to order without tomato like I usually do. It just made the sandwich a little watery.

I DO recommend this place to eat. It's on par with Panera.

This place is top notch!!  Have dined all over the world and have never had such service.   You have 5 people at least waiting on you hand and foot.  They do everything but eat for you...thank god because the food is fabulous.   they have a soup tasting trio, one of which is turtle which is a must try.   But then again everything we ordered was amazing!!!

Dr. Berg at North Hills Chiropractic and his staff are awesome! When I have problems with my back, wrists, or knees, he is always very helpful. I don't have to make treatment plans for a year. With one adjustment, he has me back on my feet feeling great. I will continue to go see Dr. Berg and recommend that all my friends go see him as well!

Customer service person on the phone seemed so uninterested in helping. She mumbled so I could barely hear. Their online appointment schedule system for servicing does not match how they actually schedule and charge for services. Because of that phone conversation I am taking my business elsewhere.
Their pizza is so good, it is easily my go-to pizza place in indianapolis. I highly recommend!

not sure what happened over the course of 6 months that i have been there.. but today we went for lunch. I had liberty burger with no bun and no chips, with a side salad. $17! I love Mark, I do love what he brings to Reno and to local scene from local farmers.. but  $17 for a burger?? come on

This place is interesting... when you walk in it looks really nice then you realize half of the people sitting down are junkies... while I was getting my drink one of them grabbed my hair and sniffed it. The staff refused to do anything about it. If you dare try to sit and eat you will be approached and asked for money, don't try to offer them food either because they will scream at you that they asked for money. Once again the staff did nothing. Real family environment for the family!!

If there was an option for zero stars this location would get it. The fact this location is by the highway absolves it from actually hiring quality employees, because it's going to get business no matter what. Why would you charge your paying customers for condiments? That's really low, and on top of that the rude employees enforce it with joy on their faces. Fries are always old and cold and I ordered two quarter pounders which were under cooked. I like dipping my fries in ranch. The sauces shouldn't be just for the chicken nuggets. Whoever runs this franchise doesn't give a damn about his or her business. Just as long as the money is rolling in, who really cares? Avoid this place.

Needed a quick places to fix the tire. My light would show that my right tire kept running out of air. After refilling with air over 30x. I took it the problem to Monty & Miguel at Discount Tire Town. Mad humble, caring, and genuine Vibes is all I got from the two. They literally did the job in seconds while we conversed about the dumb tire. In seconds in and out they sealed the hole that was in it. And has us put in less then 5 minutes. $8 bucks for the patch. Thanks again, Monty & Miguel.

The shame in this is that it will end up being a waste of time because if you want to see a band bad enough I guess you will give in and go. Much was the case on May 12th when my wife and I made our way to see Greta Van Fleet. What a shame that such an amazing band has to be associated with such a horrible experience. I will start by saying the bartending staff did a nice job and were polite. However the rest? Yep! Welcome low life thanks for buying a ticket now deal with our dictatorship! The security staff is pretty much worried about their precious "lanes" that they don't want you standing in. Hey that's fine, but to wave my wife over and berate her like a CHILD because we were standing CLOSE to a certain area is ridiculous! It's a concert venue! The view point from this piss poor facility is bad enough as it is, then to be cut off from all types of areas is that much worse! Another security gem told us we weren't allowed to go to the area up top where people were seated in bleacher style set up because all the seats were full! Meanwhile at LEAST 50 people were standing along the rails and on the steps. Not only that but as we were making our way, two different girls walked up and said yeah we were here earlier and with not so much as a peep he let them walk right on by. Don't dare take your time trying to get an ID out or walk through a crowd, someone is certain to be yelling at you! Oh and then the poor guy who said "are you sure you don't have an extra stool, my wife just had hip surgery" and was told "if you want a stool you'll have to get it off of someone already sitting in one" mean while an empty section near the top with like 20 stools was not being used! It was filled 2 hours into the show i guess for some sort of VIPs? Yeah no special sections or VIP stuff was made for sale when the tickets opened. The band sold out in minutes, the EF made their money and then proceeded to treat concert goers like shit! Way to ruin a moment I waited 7 months for!!

If you need a touristy activity to take friends to, especially if it's a cold or rainy day, this place works great. Without being as stuffy and expensive as NYC's Eately, it's a fun experience with lots of great food options. There are some Amish shops that are fun to look through, and they do a great job around the holidays.

Terrible service, food sub par. Took an hour to get in and over and hour to get our food and the waiter had half the orders wrong or missing. Save yourself the trouble and walk around till you get any where else

Typical Philly course. On par with the other muni's in the area.

Course is short. Mostly only Par-3's and from what I remember only had 1 par-5.

Clubhouse guys were nice.
Chicken and beef brisket are both tasty, variety of sauces, great sides. I visit at lunchtime & feel it's a tad pricy for lunches.
The crepe was DELICIOUS! The only reason they didn't get 5 stars was because they regularly get backed up and the wait can be long.

Bufad is perfect for grabbing a super high quality pizza. Go on Sunday, half of the pizzas are buy one get one free, so you end up with 2 pizzas for around $15. Also, it's BYO, so bring something to drink unless you're fine with tap water.

Fantastic dive bar with lots of character and friendly bartenders. Went here several times during my first visit to NOLA based on a friend's recommendation and will definitely be back whenever I'm in town in the future. The place itself doesn't serve food but there's a separate business in the back (Killer Po' Boys) which has out-of-this-world-delicious po' boys and an amazing Jameson grilled cheese sandwich. I whole heartedly recommend this place to anyone who likes good food and a strong drink.

My husband and I have been using Happy Tails frequently for the past several months, and we are so pleased! After our initial meeting with our sitter Lisa, she has been able to make last minute accommodations when our work schedule fluctuates - and of course our pups love her! 

In addition to taking care of our pups and playing with them, Happy Tails offers trash-takeout, plant care, and various other household tasks to ensure our home seems like it's being "lived in" even in our absence. 

I would highly recommend to busy families who want peace of mind for their pets while they're away for vacation or work.

This is one you will have to try.  What a fabulous surprise.   I ordered  for apps. Seared scallops in a truffle cream sauce.  I then ordered a seafood salad. For  the  Entree'  veal chop with porcini mushroom sauce.  After such a delicious meal I was taunted to ordered a fresh fruit Napoleon .  Outstanding. Outstanding, Outstanding.  I observed the freshness and sweetness of the scallops, seared just right, and not drowned in the sauce. Cooked to perfection. The salad was so fresh and again, not overwhelming with dressing.  Just drizzled just right. Wonderful salad.  The veal chop was cooked to perfection, and as always nice and tender. Great sauce.  I don't know what I liked more, the beginning on this meal, or the ending. The desert was the Napoleon, made to order. It's one of the best I have ever had. My meal will have me coming back forever.  I just hope the line won.t get too long. after the word is out.   Nothing but quality here.

The rooms were nice, very spacious, clean, and had two nice TV's. 

But... I don't think I'd stay here again.  The guy at the check-in desk was not very friendly.  I was expecting some southern hospitality.  Parking was off site, about three blocks away.  Normally this wouldn't bother me, but it was COLD, temps in the 30's and I didn't want to walk.  We could have used the valet service for $20 a day, but it doesn't run between midnight and 6 am, we had a flight at 7 am and needed to drop off a rental car.  

If I were to return to Nashville, I would have stayed one of the hotels closer to Broadway or come back in the summer.  The parking in Nashville is ample, so staying a little outside of downtown would work too.   The night before we left, we parked in a lot next to the hotel for $5 and we were able to get the car.  Another issue with the offsite parking garage is that it also closes between midnight and 6 am.  

So, if you don' t having your car locked up for the night then you'll probably enjoy this hotel.  Otherwise explore other options.

This place is great. Once you enter this beautiful space you forget that you are in a strip mall. The menu is vast and diverse. The hummus was great with a citrus kick that I prefer. The Turkish tea was hot and delicious.  I really enjoyed my lamb stew. It was stuffed with veggies and the lamb was tasty and tender. The rice wasn't bland and complimented the stew perfectly. The service was friendly and efficient. A great place for a delicious Mediterranean dinner.
Open from 5am to 8pm, conveniently located, with plenty of parking and a good staff. I have come to this location a number of times and have not been disappointed once.

This used to be one of my favorite pizza spots until yesterday.  The cleanliness standard of the place has gone down hill unfortunately.  I used the rest room prior to eating and looked like it wasn't serviced in days and was disgusting.  After ordering my pizza although it was decent there was not one clean table.  The staff was yucking it up while folding pizza boxes and not one of them cleaning any of the tables for the patrons.  Keep in mind there were pizza boxes stacked to ceiling.  The visit was at 3:15 yesterday.  I hope this was a one time thing but I fear management isn't as active as it needs to be.

I was contacted by the owner of Heritage and he personally apologized for the problems we encountered during our visit. He mentioned that the seating issue has been resolved and that he spoke with the service/kitchen staff about timing. I found his email to be sincere, so we may give them another shot.

Hot Dogs on Main has the best hot dogs I have ever had. The Chili Cheese Dog was great. The best hot dog I've ever had was the Dixie Dog, it's a chili dog with, wait for it...coleslaw, legendary. 

The hot dogs are amazing, but even the toppings that go on the hot dogs are fresh and put these hot dogs over the top. Definitely will go back again for lunch.
 
They are very reasonable priced, the only bad thing is choosing which hot dog to get. I was too full to get some ice cream, but it looked good, and plan on getting some next time.

I had two TVs that needed mounting on the wall and I found "Tucson Tech Guy" on Yelp. I called Mark at 11am and him and his assistant were here at 2pm to mount the TVs. So happy I didn't have to wait until the following week! Mark has great customer service! Him and his assistant wore a mask during the installation and were super friendly and careful during the process. He installed a 70" and a 55". Both are mounted solidly on the walls. Highly recommend the company!!!!

A local gave us a helpful tip of parking our car right outside of the French Quarter ($10 all day parking!). The only downside is walking in the heat to get to the French Quarter.

**TOURIST REVIEW**

Having never been to New Orleans I wanted to try all the foods that are famous in NOLA. While walking down the French Quarter, I saw this restaurant with balcony seating and a menu posted that showed a New Orleans sampler (chicken & andouille gumbo, crawfish etouffee, red beans & rice, and Cajun jambalaya) all for $21. They were in small cups and was enough for me since I don't eat much. My favorite was the crawfish etouffee and the rest were mehh.

Service was great, ambience was great, but from what I ordered, I really only enjoyed the etouffee.

When we ate there in December 2019 it was quite an experience. Everything the server (female, white, 35) said was at double the normal speaking volume. HI HOW ARE YOU WHAT CAN I GET YOU TO DRINK. When she brought our food: OK CLEAR SOME ROOM HERE'S YOUR FOOD. Clear some room? For us this was a pricey meal but we were treated like toddlers.

My SO got the fried-everything platter, and it was fine. I got a surf-n-turf deal, small steak, small salmon, and added the small lobster tail, whatever it is called. Steak was ordered medium-rare, it came medium-well. The small lobster tail was literally 2 tablespoons of overcooked pulp.

On our previous visit I got a whole lobster, part of which was overcooked, the other part almost raw. SO got crab legs, the knuckles of which were still icy.

So if you want a platter of fried stuff, this is the place.

Edited 1/22/20 to add that my SO went there with a small group last week. The food was good, but it took just under an hour to come out.

Amazing food. Fun atmosphere. Good value (especially for all you can eat tacos on Tuesday!). The biggest downside is they typical long wait to be seated, though it's a sign of a healthy and thriving restaurant so they're obviously doing something right. It's worth going just to have the "Ranch Water" margarita!
John, I would love to buy your coffee beans again! Are you still at Oro Valley Hospital?  By far, the best coffee I have ever purchased .

I love this place!  It's not often that I crave the atmosphere as much as the food, but this place is just fun to go to.  The staff is all smiles, joking with each other, singing out loud some times to the rock-star 80s soundtrack playing overhead.  Great, great music just puts you in a good mood, and when met with a laid-back, smiling server, it's perfect!

I like the 2-egg simple breakfast and the biscuits and gravy (without the green onions) so far!  Menu does tap into "Southwest" and delivers some spicy options, but my tongue can't handle those.  :(  My spicy-lovin' friends say they're delish though.

Highly recommended.

My husband and I love this place. The food is always consistently delicious. The last time we went, they even brought our order out to the car so I didn't have to unload my infant to go inside. Great people, great food!

A truly lovely, sprawling apartment complex.

My aunt and uncle had lived here for going on two decades.  They decided to relocate out of state, so my brother and I went to help them move.  I see why they stayed here so long:  this is a nice place to live.

Apartments are situated on meandering, tree-lined streets.  There is ample parking for each section in front of each building.  There is an abundance of greenery and green space here.  Parks.  A dog park.  Walking trails.  It really is pretty.

The apartment itself was spacious and well maintained.  The grounds around it were also in pristine condition.  The staff here had always been kind and courteous to my aunt and uncle, and I experienced the same.  They were wonderful to work with during the move and for the final checkout and walk through.  I got a glimpse of the large clubhouse/rec center when I went to surrender the keys.  Very nice.

Although this is an older complex, if you are in the market for an apartment in this section of St. Louis, I would highly recommend that you investigate this property.  Close to many retail stores, restaurants and medical facilities, it truly is an attractive, picturesque, and relatively quiet place to reside.  Also convenient if you commute on 270, as it's about a mile from access to that thoroughfare.

I was impressed with my first visit to Peninsula! However I'm reserving full 5 star status until I've had an opportunity to sample more food. 
The space is open and I love the decor. The kitchen is located in the corner of the restaurant and we were able to secure a spot on a Tuesday. 
This is a tapas restaurant, so come here ready to share! The drink menu is very gin focused which didn't do much for me, but my companions were very excited about the cocktail menu! I had the sangria, and it was not as wine heavy as I would have liked but not sweet which is a plus. However, there was some kind of spice that wasn't my favorite, and I probably wouldn't order it again. 
We ordered a sheep cheese (not the blue cheese), the tomato toast with pate, chicken thighs, and rabbit. The chicken and rabbit were a highlight; the rabbit was in a fabulous rich broth and the thighs were on a bed of grits with fermented blackberries that was divine!! We didn't love the tomato pate toast; it had a smoky flavor that left a weird aftertaste. Pate is also hit or miss for me so that probably didn't help. The cheese was good and overall, and we all left happy and full splitting these dishes amongst 3 people.
I think this restaurant would be ideal for a date night for 2 people and will be back to try more of the menu!

This place is a regular on my Goleta lunch rotation.  Do not go here without getting the onion pancakes and put some of that dip (if any is left after the pancakes are gone) on your rice and in the wonton soup.  A solid place to have Chinese food and the woman that runs it (Sue) is usually welcoming and gives you a hug when you leave.  I like the salt and pepper fish for lunch.

I'm picky and I was impressed!  I've just moved back to the area and was badly in need of a therapeutic massage. Laura was great.  Her space is nice and airy.  She did such a great job working out my kinks without hurting me.  And I loved that she paid attention to my notation that I carry stress in my face.  Most therapists ignore that though I always stress it.  But Laura spent a good 10-15 minutes on my face and jaw which felt so nice.  I enjoyed my visit and I will definitely be back!!!

Overall I would stay here again. 

Great  proximity to a wonderful running path right outside that either goes to Cyprus park or across the causeway depending which way you go. 

Wifi signal is weak and I had to stick to wireless. Using my laptop was touch and go however. Luckily was on LTE at this location and have unlimited data. 

Entering and leaving is kind of tricky at peak traffic times. Tests your patience waiting for the signal so you can turn left into the parking lot. Perhaps that is par for Florida. The bar and restaurant are nice although liquor prices seemed a bit high. 

Room is about what you'd expect...no microwave though and balcony isn't private as there's only a short fence between you and your neighbor. 

No breakfast available.

I asked the bartender how much bottled beer was, she said $2.50. At the end of the night when I received the bill they charged me $3.25 a beer. She said the manager just changed the price. So the bartender is ripping me off or the manager is ripping me off. That being said watch your tabs at this place. I will not be returning to this place.

I ordered the Lamb Shish Kabob Platter it was very good. I would definitely order it again. The only down fall was them not having the strawberry milkshake that I wanted. So I ordered a strawberry banana smoothie and it tasted and smelled horrible. It tasted like it was made with bad fruit I had to trash it. I'll never order that again but I will order the food. The customer service was great as well.

It's fine. Nothing special but not bad either. Good draft list. Got there Saturday about 6. Staff is nice but took over 20 minutes to get 2 beers after our beer order was taken. Had a pepperoni and sausage hand tossed pizza. Food came out hot. Pizza was also cooked well. I probably wouldn't choose this place for pizza if asked but would not decline if invited...

I really like Pet Play house. It is very clean and large. The dogs are allowed to run around inside and out. I have taken my dog there for both daycare and overnight stays. You have several options as to where your dog stays ( alone in a suite or a slumber party!) and if you have a small dog they can be separated. The dogs are supervised at all times. It's a great place to take your dog and feel safe. All the dogs there have to have proof of shots and that they have been fixed if they are over 6 months. I am always happy with our experience.

If I could give this place negative stars, I'd do it.  The owner?  Total dick.  Closest I've been to an honest-to-god *fistfight* in my entire adult life.  See, out behind the place, they offer pontoon boat rides up the river.  Sign says, "1PM."  My 8-year old, super excited to take a boat ride up the Delaware, buys tickets at about 12:45 and waits.  1:10PM, they come out & change the sign to 1:45.  1:45, the apparently drug-addled captain comes back to the waiting line, yells something about his dog, says he's not letting any of us on his boat.  Screams something about take your money and get off my property.  My daughter starts to cry, because she's never seen an adult tripping on what I assume was bad batch of meth, and this old bastard makes fun of her, does a pretend sob, says something like, "why don't you take your business to my competitor up the street, you little cry baby."

Shortly after, I participated in a loud, angry, verbal altercation in the front of his store.  Got my money back, took his suggestion and went up to the river tour that launches out behind The Landing restaurant (I highly recommend these folks, if for for no other reason, because they don't appear to be cooking meth).

But back to Gerenser...The owner is an ass.  I hope the place gets shut and/or burnt down; he deserves it, the way he treats paying customers.

Just went to get a car wash. The cash lane was blocked off with no explanation why. The lady was rude and not helpful at all. This place used to be good and has gone down hill. Mister car wash needs to think about hiring courteous employees. The customers are what give these people a job. I think people forget this. I will not be back to this place.

This place is always super busy and they only take cash, but they never disappoint. I myself prefer yogurtland but my spouse and friend swear by this place. The desert is always presented well and the employees are always professional. One of the places you must visit for dessert in New Orleans. Highly recommend.

I have passed this place for the past couple years and have never gone in but had a few minutes today and decided to give it a try.....

The restaurant is open with the cooking exposed on the right as you walk in and it smelled great.  The reason it doesnt get a higher rating started as I walked in.  I wasnt greeted by anyone and wasnt sure if I should seat myself or wait for assistance;  if I didnt seek someone out I would still be there.  I was told to grab a seat and someone would be right with me; I good at following instruction and did just that and then waited.....and waited.  In fairness they had a large party in the back that  was keeping them hopping but I had to go back to the counter and waited again for anyone to acknowledge me....and waited...I finally waved down a waitstaff member who told me they would be right with me, I had already grabbed a menu and been there by now for more than 15 minutes.  I told him I knew what I wanted and ordered it, he was pleasant and helpful and apologized for my having to wait.  I was appeased and went back to my table and waited.....and waited......it took another 25 minutes for my lunch to come and I was again apologized to.  

I can tell you it was worth the wait.  I ordered Chicken Rustica; a nice chicken breast in a bed of angel hair smothered with cherry peppers, capers, olive oil, olives and sausage.  It was a taste sensation and I think at 12.95 it was a really good value.  I would love to try it again for dinner and it is a BYOB; based on my meal today dinner could be excellent and as a BYOB would represent a nice meal out but my fear is that our trip here would be all we have time for.

I did talk to a friendly member of the waitstaff and asked them if they were understaffed today (to give them the benefit of the doubt) and was told "no"  I pointed out to them that I had counted 18 people who had come in and left because they werent greeted and didn't know what the protocol was here.  I was told that the person running the shift didnt leave the register area which is 50 feet from the front door and no one was approached as they entered.  

I HATE bad service, in this case it was caused by a lack of staff and a lack of a greeter or hostess and add a few more people on the floor so we can get in and out; especially at lunch.  No one has that much time for lunch.

I feel that if they became more attentive to the customer they would be deserving of another 1-1/2 stars.  It's your call Bacco!

Did the vip movie experience. It was fantastic!!!!  Seats are Uber comfy  with power reclining, power head rest adjustment and a heating function.  In theater food and beverage service with competitive reasonable pricing. The menu was fairly large and the food was excellent. Drinks came in a glass glass not plastic.  There is also a very nice lounge as you enter that is perfect for a before or after movie drink, appitizer, or dinner. It's a full dinner and movie night all in one place. This is an A+ movie experience and I can't wait to do it again!!!!!

Found this place after going to MO Botanical Gardens and what a find!! Great service , delicious food and there Baklava is perfection according to my taste buds! We both ordered chicken shwarma wraps..so tasty and an appetizer Mohammera..red pepper hummus with some tasty magic..
Go hungry and this place winner disappoint.. Also save room for the best tasting Baklava around!!

Don't bother going.   The NYC locations are 20x better.   Went on a Saturday night thinking it would be crowded and was excited for a good meal.   Ha...Food wasn't good and service was even worse.  For example, the best thing we ordered was my 10yr old kids pasta noodles with butter (not on the menu) for $12.45  NYC pricing in Philly.   Needless to say....I won't be going back there.

For airport food, this was really good and I enjoyed a big bowl of comforting ramen.  I was able to get a seat at the sushi bar which had placemats, chopsticks and actual silverware which made the dining experience even better.  The staff were friendly and worked efficiently to get your food out quickly and to keep the space clean and welcoming to more customers.  I would definitely come back here on a future trip through the airport.

I'm so happy my friend suggested Otto for a girls dinner. It was a warmish night, so the garage door was open to the sidewalk. We were initially seated near the front, but the staff was kind enough to move us to a bigger and more quiet table near the back. The beer list is fantastic, with tons of local beers and ciders to try. Prices are reasonable. But the star of the show was the food. Everything is served family style. We had the Otto sausage and the maple sausage with fries, the Mac and cheese and Brussels sprouts. Honestly, everything was so incredibly good. The sausage was juicy, and the Otto sausage had Gouda oozing out. I love how the sausages are served with hot mustard, sauerkraut and pickles. The Mac and cheese was particularly fantastic. It was smoky and creamy and everything l wanted. I can't wait to come back next time I'm home!
My campaign flyers were done beautifully and were also ready on time! Many people commented on the high quality of the materials. Also, the attention to detail and customer service were superb.

Meh. Didn't care for the vegetable ramen soup. This review is based on my vegan opinion of the soup. The soup consists of mostly just noodles. Where are the veggies?! Just some watercress and another vegetable. I'm not certain but I thought ramen noodles had the ingredients of egg in them but our server told us otherwise. Other than the soup it's a nice spot for a lunch  or a quick bite for dinner with a nice hip atmosphere.

WOW. Just wow. I went to Olive Garden tonight with a couple of friends and we are in awe of the service we received. Sean is hands down the best waiter I have ever had. He deserves a raise or a promotion!! Thanks Sean!

The name and location might throw you off, but I assure you this is a high quality spot. 

Skull's is a new establishment, though the place has been there for awhile. Apparently it's apart of the oldest building in Nashville. Whatever it is they redid the place and did it nice. 

The alley way it's in doesn't have the reputation of being a high class place, if that's what your looking for. Skull's is that though. A diamond in the rough. 

Cocktail bar, mixologists, burlesque, nice decor, lots of space. This is not some dive bar, not by a long shot. At the same time though, it's not pretentious at all. You don't have to dress nice to get in.

I'm sure this place is going to become a very popular destination.

I would have given the hotel a higher rating but my heat got stuck on 80 degrees, it was nauseating. I eventually got a different room, I had no choice. They told me it's a two heater system , heat in winter and AC in summer. I didn't get that because why can't you re adjust the heat or ac? I've never experienced a room so hot. 

Staff was very friendly. 
If the temperature wasn't an issue in my room they would have had a higher rating.
Did not see a "brick oven"? Ordered take out pizza and was to be a chicken cheesesteak only got no cheese. Pizza was ok, cheesesteak was dry. No mask worn by employees.

This is a pretty much perfect burger joint... with enough microbrews to satisfy any tap-room lover.
They are working to do things right, and you can see the sincerity and the hard work they put into this place. Today was their first day, so they are shaking things out.
SO, why is this NOT a 5 STAR review?
It would be, but first they need to get their seafood act together, and certify that they only serve sustainable fish and shrimp, and get their wait staff educated about what this means, and where and how their shrimp and fish is caught.  
This is a new restaurant in Santa Barbara, and it needs to step up and do the right thing for the oceans.
ONCE they do that (and I encourage you'all to ask them about this when you visit)... you can add 2 more stars to this review.
Now, I've just got to figure out which IPA to try first!.

I only shopped the fabric side. My sewing friends and I took a day trip from the Washington D.C. area to hit a couple of fabric stores in Philadelphia. This was my first time at Jomar. I hear there is another location but this was the better store for the amount of fabrics. The prices here are unheard of in D.C.! We're talking $1/$2/$3 yard. My first thought was how overwhelming this store is. It is large. It is like a warehouse with high ceilings and poor ventilation. Here are the bullet points:

Good:
* Prices are amazing.
* Good quality fabrics.
* Great place for lining fabrics.
* Huge selection.
* Plenty of parking.

Bad:
(But I qualify this by saying stock must change all the time so what I saw today is not what will be there tomorrow.)
* More solid colors of fabric than prints.
* Unless specifically stated, fabric content is not labeled. I would say 90% of the fabric there do not have a content label. You have to know your fabrics. With no label, you also don't know washing instructions. Experienced sewists will know.
* Place was stuffy with poor ventilation. It was a hot day in July and it was warm inside, even more so when one is wearing a face mask.

I do highly recommend this place for sewists even with the "bad" which really isn't that bad.

This is one of the best Thai food restaurants around. I love their Panang curry. Tonight I ordered the chicken pad Thai to go and gluten free. It was absolutely delicious and so much food I have leftovers for at least two meals if not three. I highly recommend you try this one.

I purchased a new Sienna XLE Premium today from Tyrone Harper.I live 200 miles east of Nashville and got my pricing from 3 dealers closer to my home.I called and spoke with Tyrone and told him what I wanted and what I would be trading and also told him where I would like to be on pricing O.T.D.everything inc.After about 3 calls back and forth he let me know they had agreed on my deal and I know this is hard to believe these days but they would also deliver my new van to me full tank of gas topped off when arriving to me,man yes I was impressed!! Best dealings ever for me and I have probably purchased 20 new vehicles in my lifetime.I saved money,had great dealings and got more than I could have imagined.Really Blessed to work with Tyrone and Thankful for our new Sienna.GO VOLS !

Wednesday buy 1 get 1 fajitas.  1 meal per person, is enough.  Bar staff, very friendly.  Place seems to have regulars at the bar, but still found a seat. Parking share with Fords, so a little tight  I will be back.

The food was excellent and the service was even better.  We tried the Island Trio and the Montgomery Bay Platter.  This was excellent but we both agree the Caribbean Gumbo we got for a starter stole the show!!!

We will definitely be back as the chef was especially nice and the Island Music was very enjoyable.

If your in NOLA and looking for something to do , this is a fun activity. It is a little bit away from the French quarter but it's totally worth it and fun. It's BYOB and you can bring food aboard to. 

There is a Bluetooth you can connect to on your boat or bring your own mini speaker. I didn't get the drivers name but he was very friendly and polite the whole time . The place even contacted cabs for all of us because there were no Uber's or Lyfts available 

When you pull up there is not much to see , and I would highly recommend using the restroom before you come but there are porter potty's available and there is also a bathroom on the boat that is super clean

Upon arriving I was greeted and welcomed into an amazing salon atmosphere. The decor was beautiful and the vibe was relaxing. So many talented artist. I saw Abbie Ray she was very professional and did an amazing job with copying what I wanted. She is super friendly and down to earth. I was impressed with how much I paid as I budgeted and expected to pay way more than I was charged! Can't wait to return!

This is a solid Sephora inside of JCPenney store. Today I went in to buy a primer I purchased there before. The one I wanted was out of stock but they offered up a strong replacement. What I really love about Sephora is their return policy and knowing that I can return the item if I don't like it. Also excellent service, I was helped right away.

When my wife and I were thinking of all the places we've been recently, she said "Booty's" and I said, "Oh yeah, the unicorn skull place." No seriously. Look at the wall behind the bar. It's pretty cool.

The dining area is quite small, as are most of the plates. Although the menu says you eat everything from a cone, a stick, or with your hands, that's not actually true. You are given silverware for anything that says "hands" on the menu. They're just trying to play up the "street food" vibe. 

Service is excellent. Very friendly and quite swift. The clientele is definitely "hipster" as some other reviewers have said. My wife (in the theater business) said that she goes to Booty's to escape the theater crowd. At which point I motioned across the room in the direction of one of her actors from about 1.5 yrs ago. Heh, theater people and local actors have to eat, too. Right? 

Food is remarkable. I predict it is not possible for the average person to be disappointed here. Imagine your favorite three food trucks, parked in the same place, which also has a full bar.    ....    you're welcome. lol.

Really, though, what sounds adventurous on the menu, is. What sounds typical and tasty, is. Everything is quite filling, although in relatively small portions (for New Orleans). You might get picky about the water (served in mason jars) or the decor or staff attire (think, 90s grunge but cleaner). But you're in the Marigny, folks, if you wanted white linen and white gloves, you should've stayed Uptown. This is where the hard-working, blue-collar, artistic New Orleanians live and eat.

Great location. Friendly staff. Very nice breakfast buffet including the usuals (scrambled eggs, bacon, fruit, cereal) and also local specialties like sausage gravy and biscuits. Lobby area very clean. Nice towels, which is typical of Hilton facilities. Only complaint is that my sink wasn't cleaned during my stay.

Beware of the fraxel! If you are considering doing it, think again! It undid my lift and I have way more wrinkles as an end result. The Dr wants to say I didn't use enough sunblock, but I bought 3 bottles from him. I was dripping in sunblock.

This place is amazing espicially the pulled pork sandwhich. Everyone who lives in the area must check out this place. The staff are friendly and the atmosphere is outstanding. Very clean and spacious place.

Great wood fire pizza. We tried a bunch but my favorite was the mushroom. Really good with a variety of mushrooms on it!!

Also love the fried chickpeas they put out in the table for you to munch on. So addicting.
CALI is absolutely wonderful. Went there twice this past weekend. The ambiance outdoors is great. The service could have been better on Sunday but the food was still great. I'll be back!
Best Mexican food I've found around here. The way they crisp up their meat without overcooking it is addicting.

Very good place to eat at, in this order: Greasy hot homemade chips with salsa, beer with limes, and daily Mexican food specials.  Urban Cantina is a great downtown spot to enjoy a heavy coma inducing meal.

Surprisingly, I didn't really like it. I've eaten here several times and it used to be really really good food. But the past 2 times I've eaten there the food is just so-so. The portions are really small and I haven't had the "WOW!" Factor with anything I have eaten the past few times. I do think the atmosphere, the wine, and the staff are awesome!! But for as expensive as it is, I either want something that taste amazing or at least fills me up. Just my opinion.

WOW what an experience, from the hostess, the server, the assistant, all-around great service what southern hospitality should be. The biscuits were unbelievable, the gumbo was good but the red snapper dish was one of the best dishes I've ever had, well rounded deliciousness! Were here for three days tempted to return before we leave it's that good!!

Awful.  The menu description of the Crown Candy BLT says "We pile bacon high and top with lettuce and tomato.  We could hardly see the bacon on the sandwich.  When we called out the waitress on this she said let me check with the cook.  She came back and said we can add extra bacon for an additional charge.  Why should we have to pay for something that is in the sandwich descriiption.  We had the Famous Barr original recipe french onion soup.  No where near the quality and taste of the soup that was served at Famous Barr.  The soup had very little cheese and the bread was a thin slice of I think french bread.  Would not recommend this restaurant to any of my friends or family.
Definitely a great place to get an haircut. I only paid $17 and I have to say it looks better than a $30 haircut. Definitely recommended

Always good pizza. Was able to piece together totally vegan pizza and at a totally reseanable price.  Staff always friendly.  I pay online so when I walk in they hand me my pizza I grab my drink and I'm out.

Great customer service from the manager Anthony. Even though they were busy with walk-ins, dine-ins, taking phone orders & bagging orders for the pickup station, he was still pleasant and professional. I think that's important because so many places are overwhelmed and understaffed which unfortunately leaves those working stressed and/or rude. I appreciate great customer service to go with great food. And if you want something great to eat and earm your belly; the white bean chilli soup is DELICIOUS!

9pm and turned away. The doors and search engines all say open until midnight, and we heard the same at the last place a city away so now, we're going to pay the 300.00 more for an ER many miles from home.

Amazingly, the food in the "Steak House" was actually better than the "White Orchid".  The Carpaccio of WAGYU Kobe Beef was absolutely delightful.  The Napa Valley Escargot was tasty with the butter garlic sauce.  The Lobster Bisque was just a tag on the lukewarm side, it will taste better if it is steaming hot.  The Almond crusted Jumbo Lump Crabcake was filled with lumps and lumps of crab meat.  The dinner was paired with a bottle of Groth 1999 Carbernet Sauvignon.  Other than the the service being a bit slow, overall it was a very enjoyable dinner!

The sandwiches are so delicious. I have had the cheese steak w American and the Cali chicken cheesesteak. Both were fantastic. The meat is so flavorful and not dry. The rolls are so soft and fresh. Our new favorite spot

Average overpriced Salads.  Its great to be able to create your own, but its just way too expensive for some lettuce and light toppings.  Items are usually fresh at this location.  Service could be picked up a bit.  Also think there is MSG in one of the ingrediants as I do get a decent sized headach being allergic to MSG after eating at any SaladWorks.

Overall, typical saladworks.  Overpriced but fresh.  Olive Garden all you can eat soup, salad and breadsticks is a much bettter deal than salad works at under $8 for lunch and under $10 for dinner which is less than what I spend at Salad Works.
Stopped by yesterday for lunch, but its now restaurant Viet Thai.
The service is super slow,everytime I come here I have to wait forever to get my food. Most of the times they mess up my order.

Honestly, if I could give fewer than one stars I would. Let me just say this. If you are a sample size girl who knows exactly what you want, needs no support or interaction, and loves to feel rushed.... than this is the place for you. Upon entering the store I was immediately treated as if no one there had any time in the day for me (despite the fact that I had made an appointment there 3 weeks earlier). I was handed 5 tags and told to go find gowns to put them on, and that I would only be able to try on those 5. 10 min. later after frankly unproductive wandering with my mom and family a consultant finally approached us. To say she was rude was an UNDERSTATEMENT. She briskly walked (way faster than I could keep up, I seriously felt like I was jogging) around for the next 10 minutes just pulling dresses that SHE though would look best on me. We then went back to the room where for the next 15 minutes she attempted to shove me into gowns that were about 8 sizes too small for me, some I couldn't even get on. The first gown I put on she said, "yeah that does not look good on you." I felt fat, ugly, and so defeated throughout the entire appointment. Luckily I did find my perfect gown that day (at Brides by Young right there on 82nd street btw... I can't way enough WONDERFUL things about them, so check them out), but EVERY other place I went I found a bride who had come from the Bridal Superstore that day with the same negative experience. The staff was rude, dismissive, and pushy. Don't waste your time here.

Bill's is consistent in 1 thing...surly service. Not sure if they train their employees  to behave that way or if they hire them because they have bad attitudes. I go out of my way to shop local but I like myself enough to never again subject myself to such rudeness and indifference.

One of the best steakhouses I've been to in Edmonton. Our server was on point. We started our meal with a dozen fresh oysters. They came with a bunch of different sauces and finishes, but I like mine with just a little bit of hot sauce or horse radish, so they were perfect. A great selection and very very fresh.
I ordered the rib eye Cooked blue rare, the inside was cooked perfectly.... could have been seared more on the outside, but still a great steak. The sides it came with were bone marrow that was amazing and mixed with herb butter and mashed potatoes plus we ordered a side of Brussel sprouts. The Brussel sprouts got sent back to be cooked more and when they came out again they were really good, just needed a bit more salt. I also ordered a side of Oscar topper because they don't have Bernaise sauce and I wanted it, it didn't come with very much sauce, but what it did come with was delicious.
For dessert I got the cheesecake without the berries or other stuff they usually serve on it and it was amazing... I highly recommend the cheesecake.
Overall we had an amazing experience at Van's.  There were a few small hiccups, but they overcame them and we will definitely be going back.

Love this place. Great respite from the city, peaceful and enchanting, like a DisneyWorld for nature lovers. The cafeteria and fine dining restaurants are both wonderful, but the cafeteria wins, in my opinion.

Have to valet.  Food was overpriced but delicious.  Server was outstanding.  Venue was empty and music was not what I expected in downtown Nashville.  Lots of museum-type stuff to look at but needs something to create atmosphere.

The best Greek food that we've had in a long time. My wife and I ordered the combination plate to share just in case we didn't like the food. we both were full and had left overs next time we visit the area we will be back.

This place is on 10 almost every single time we've been. Sometimes table service is a bit slow, but the dining room has never not been full, so I'm not mad about it. One of my top three brunch spots in STL for sure. Will not stop recommending to everyone who asks. I might sell my soul for the last ever plate of Cornbread Benedict.

Perfect addition to Wesley Chapel. Not a cookie cutter pub. Beautiful outdoor seating. Lots of big screen TVs. Best of all our server was excellent. Drinks are well made and good selection of beers of course. We will return for dinner soon. Entertainment on weekends. Love this place.

This place lacks variety, but what they do they do very well. Particularly, the buns here are quite excellent and so that makes the burger all the better. Oddly there's no tomato or lettuce to be had here on the burgers, but that's a small thing. 

There's a mayo bar with various types of mayo you can spread on your burger. Some are good, some are less so. I like the special sauce.
