Sides and drinks don't come with the burgers here. You can order on a side a number of things like sweet potato fries, tater tots, or onion rings.

Overall, a great burger with fast service. Definitely preferable to a fast food place like McDonald's if you have the time to dine in.

Not sure what people are expecting from this place.  It's literally a shack next to the railroad tracks.  C'mon now.

I don't really eat at chain restaurants - I try to support local business that sell food made with y'know, FOOD - not something created in a McLab.  

Karin's is simple comfort food.  Burgers (of the steak n'shake type variety), chili, grilled cheese sandwiches, fried stuff and ice cream type treats.

The chili portion is enormous and very hearty.

Kids always enjoy the various options - again, portions are not small.  Burgers are not huge, but are really intended to be eaten as double/triple type deals.

If you're in Smyrna for a sports event or something like that, give Karin's a try.

Martin Mainline Honda sold me a 2019 Civic Si not detailed, damaged in several part on the exterior yet charged full KBB price. Missing Honda's tire fix kit even though it was listed on Martin's product detail sheet. I asked to inquire about financing, they ran 11 hard inquiries until running the one I suggested (my regular bank) and getting approved. I would not suggest going there for a vehicle.

I used to like coming to this place but the work ethics are horrible staff is extremely rude I don't know if it because of staff shortage but who will want to work for such a disgusting place bathroom are filthy the windows are dirty & things like that show how careless they are about hygiene. The food is good not the best. Save your time & go some where else.

Love the flaming DR. Peppers! The owner Dave is amazing as well. I left my phone at the bar and I contacted them through Facebook and he got back to me within hours of my contact and arranged for my phone to be picked up and sent back to me. Most places don't put enough time or concern into their customers, especially in a place as crazy as New Orleans, and while this was definitely my fault, Dave went above and beyond for me and I really appreciate that! Beyond Dave, the bar is great and we had an amazing time (or 4) visiting.

Despite eating here several times and enjoying the food, my party of 2 arrived around 9:30 pm last Saturday after already eating dinner for a second round as we were still hungry. There was no line up and an empty table that was not cleared. The waitress told us upfront it will be a while as she was tied up but did not specify the amount of time. Sure-it's no problem waiting for a bit. However, as we watched, several employees walked past the dirty table countless times, either serving food, taking an order, or getting the bill. Some even went back to the kitchen with an empty tray, walking past the dirty table. Why couldn't someone help out a fellow coworker even if it's not their table to just clear it quickly to seat a waiting customer down? It was a small table with just a few dishes-one tray would clearly be sufficient to clear it. I understand if the restaurant was short staffed but it clearly did not look that way. There were at least 4 employees that weren't in the kitchen but huddled by the cash register. One boy was even just sorting through receipts. Even if it wasn't within your job description, a restaurant should function as a team and everyone can help for the sake of the business. All in all, we left because clearly it seemed as if they did not care for our business so why would we choose to dine at such an establishment?
Nothing Bundt Cakes. More Nothing Bundt Delicious! Make sure you give this place a look. Wonderful owners, hospitable employees. I'm never dissatisfied.
A great Mexican place with tons of options! The best thing about this place is how late they're open on the weekends!

Last week I had SB Pest Control take care of an ant invasion outside my house. I was impressed how easy and effective it was! They were on time, very courteous, reasonably priced, knowledgeable and best of all, at least so far the problem is solved. Couldn't have asked for better service. Thank you

I'm somewhat conflicted about Ants Pants Café.  The food is good and the place itself is quaint and bustling.  But due to some problems with the kitchen and the price point on a few of the items keeps me from giving a full endorsement.

The menu has a good variety, and the food is quite tasty.  The egg, ham, and brie sandwich was delicious, the fried potato chips are great to start with, and the pancakes were cooked well.   While the food is good, there are a few problems.  The staff always seems to be somewhat frantic, which may be due to the fact that the place is rather cramped, and to wait for a table you have to line up in the narrow walkway by the front counter/kitchen.  The kitchen makes quite a few mistakes, which is apparent while standing by the kitchen while you wait.  The kitchen unfortunately made a mistake on our food.  Finally, the price point seems to be a little off for some of the items.  The pancakes, while good, were completely overpriced.  Two really meager looking chocolate chip pancakes (that almost looked like they would be a side) was 9 dollars...and they actually charge for syrup on top of that!      

Overall, the food is good, but I never leave excited to come back soon.   I do think that other places in the city get me more excited about going out for brunch, such as Sabrina's or Honey's.

I've had practically everything on the menu: juices, meals, snacks, and I even buy from their little market: pao de queso mix and coffee.  

The capoeira classes are a BLAST!  They have music classes, dance classes, live music on the weekends...they have also thrown some fun parties: Samba parties, Brazil soccer game events, and Capoeira parties.  So much fun!  And, this place is guaranteed to go off when it comes to the World Cup!!!  

Great food, good atmosphere, friendly people = fun, fitness, and good authentic Brazilian food.  This is the only place of it's kind in Santa Barbara!  Why wouldn't more restaurants/establishments cater to peoples' general need for fun, food, entertainment, AND EXERCISE!?

We came here for the mini golf and petting zoo. While the commute may have been rough, the end result was so fun! My daughter ran around the mini golf course as if it were an obstacle course, yes she's still a bit too young for this, but it was so much fun buying locla produce and feeding it to the goats, sheep and geese and ducks in the back of the farm! Definitely worth a visit!
This place has the meanest employees I've ever met they curse at you they sit down and do nothing

Goodbye Sam's! As a westbank resident I gladly make the drive to Costco. The difference in customer service alone is worth the extra drive time. The employees here actually smile and greet you and don't seem completely miserable at their jobs. The checkout process is extremely organized, there are usually 2 people at each register- one to ring you up and one to unload and load your cart back up. This helps the line to move very quickly. I also love how you can't bring a cart full of items to the customer service desk to check out. The last time we went to the CS desk at Sam's we waited 17 minutes because they kept checking people out who should have gone to the register like everyone else. Costco's customer service is actually blocked off from shoppers, you must go through the registers to get there or go through a separate entrance outside. 

There are plenty of affordable organic options and the Kirkland products (Costco's store brand) are generally great quality and help you save even more.
My boyfriend and I stopped here one day for milkshakes- ADDICTING!!! I had the Captain Crunch Milkshake and I crave it every day. It was so good and the service was quick. Try the milkshakes!!!

Service and food were both cold. The brisket tasted like it was made from a commercial electrical smoker and made without love. The meat was cut too thick, wasn't tender, needed seasoning, and barely had smoke flavor.  The sauces were amateur, slaw was one sided, mashed potatoes were dry, and biscuits were cold. When we ordered 3oz. draft pours the server didn't know which beer was which. We were rushed and had to go to another table to get ketchup we ask for but was never delivered. We asked for the kids sliders with just meat and cheese and just got meat no cheese. The check was dropped without asking if we wanted anything else (which we wouldn't ordered anything else anyway). It was just not good. I'm really shocked I was recommended to go there from a local. Never again.

After the long anticipated wait for the new Panda Express to open, disappointment soon followed. They are seriously under staffed and what staff they do have are SO slow.  The understaffing causes the lines to be very long and by the time it is your turn most of the entrees are gone, so you are forced to again while they cook another batch. The drive thru line can take up to 30 mins during the supper rush. The food is pretty good but not always worth the wait. Hire more people Panda Express,

I love this place. I lived in Pville for a year and it was one of my fav places to go. The prices are better then in Exton or West Chester ( and trust me i have been to all of them).  The food is amazing. I was there again last night and had 3 rolls and a tuna avocado salad. The salad was out of this world. I would recommend this place to anyone!

Try the specials.

STAY AWAY!!!!!

Took them my AR15 SBR to remove a flash hider. 

First the "gunsmith" thought it was a 14.5 barrel with a pinned flash hider. Once i told him it was a 11.5 with a screwed on AAC mount he then DEMANDED to see my NFA paperwork. While trying to remove the mount, he couldn't get it and the barrel was turning. He stated he would have to "log it" and it would take a week and "at least" $40.00. After a week i got a call saying it was done. When i asked how much he said $120.00!!!!! And never even called to let me know.

When i arrived to pick it up i was greeted with the excuses of:

"most difficult flash hider removal i've ever had"
"yeah i talked to him (gunsmith) and he was under the impression you wanted it done right"

and I told him he should have called to let me know and his response was as he took it apart he kept finding more and more wrong with it and didn't want to keep calling me to get approval. I said why didn't he just take it completely apart to figure out the total damage and then call, he shrugged his shoulders.   

Then to top it off, they completely DESTROYED the AAC ($100.00) mount that was on it!!!

STAY FAR AWAY!!!

Just ate here tonight for the first time. Was very impressed with the burger with sautéed onions, mushrooms, blue cheese crumbles that melted into a sauce that was out of this world. And go for the tater tots!!!  Who doesn't love tater tots???  
The waitress was great. Very cheerful and does her job well. I would eat here again for sure.

Great place except the snack bar was closed as noted in previous reviews. Chips and cookies were not substantial lunch and we purchased 90 minute jump time which was too much time. 60 min.would have been plenty. There are other activities here which cost additional money.

I'd like to take this time to update my review although our previous experience was a bit rough and we left a very skewed rant of the review. The owner James himself directly reached out to me about my concerns it's one of the reasons why we prefer  local business versus going to Dunkin' Donuts I have recently revisit it and everything was spot on a bagels where great cream cheese was perfectly spread. I believe I had FKS also was piping hot cooked to perfection.   Also just recently found out they deliver which is great for the local surrounding area

Tiger sugar is the hype per usual, its funny when you hear people walking by whispering to their friends why there's a line for this place if they've never heard of it (esp the aunties and grandparents). But pretty awesome it's in Philly now (the city which I believe has probably every boba shop available in Chinatown, philly is basically Boba town). I've had tiger sugar in Taiwan and NYC primarily so the concept wasn't new to me. Their boba was very fresh and subtle sweet, they also have varying sizes of boba that gives it better texture 

But their concept which is a downer is that you can't adjust the sugar or ice content, which leaves the tea drinks rather too sweet for my taste. I do appreciate what they advertise as fresh milk for their tiger milk drinks, but its basically only horizon milk that can be purchased from the supermarket store. The girl who took my order also steered me toward the wrong path and told me the wrong ingredients that were in my drink, but hopefully that'll improve since it's just their grand opening week. 

Overall tiger sugar is a safe choice and worth the experience if you've never had before. Personally for me as a regular boba addict, $6 post tax for a cup of a boba without additional toppings isn't as worth as other boba shops in Chinatown (that's like getting a whole bahn mi).

Decent hotel in a terrific location -- just a short stroll across the Harbour Island bridge from the Tampa Convention Center, Riverwalk, and Amalie Arena. Our room was comfortable and clean, with two cozy Queen beds and an outstanding waterfront view. The hotel doesn't have much to get excited about in the way of amenities: there's a basic fitness center, a spacious lobby bar, and a small restaurant with a decent (if overpriced) breakfast buffet. But if you're just looking for a place to crash while exploring Tampa and its surroundings, the Westin does the trick. 

There's a Starbucks just off the main entrance and a couple of upscale restaurants directly adjacent. My one complaint was the expensive -- and mandatory -- valet parking, which seems like a bit of a swindle for a hotel with a huge underground deck. But overall, a nice place to stay for anyone visiting downtown Tampa.
If you love sushi and have high expectations then this place isn't for you! I will never order from here again!

When I think support local business, I automatically think about viRo's.  I'm not going to lie - my brain thinks Vito, but it's really Viro.  Why is it so hard?  Sometimes I get jealous of these little hometown non-diner favorites on the Food Channel and wish I could go to more of them.  Then, I realize I can - no excuses!

So many butter cookies, tiramisu, and fresh breads displayed.  There are plenty of pizzas and pasta dishes ready for you to take home and bake.  You can get some gelato.  You can get a Boar's Head sandwich.  Even if the combinations were bad, the quality of ingredients at Viro's are excellent.

I love the sandwiches, especially the kind with pesto, marinated onions, vinaigrette, or marinated meat -- or all of the above.  $6 for most sandwiches on fresh bread, a cookie, and chips.  Eating a sandwich reminds me how processed Subway tastes in comparison to a real deli sandwich.  I like the gelato too.  I can't think of anything at Viro's that is not good.  I've only tried cold items though and that's a shame.  I think warm items would bring my reviews to 5 stars - the deli portion is definitely 4 stars though.

OMG... one of the best tasting tri-tip sandwiches I have had in a minute.  Okay having said that, wished they would have included just one more slice of meat and salsa for the sandwich.  Its a little dissapointing when 1/4 of the sandwich is all bread and bbq sauce.  Shalhoob dont be afraid to load the meat and salsa.  Its a $14.00 sandwich.  Make it feel like one.
But amazing flavor...

Krista had the Baha fish tacos.  In Shalhoob's defense, she forgot baja tacos are fried, so that is forgivable.    However she found it bland, the blue corn tortilla falls apart with the sauce they included.
Did I mention how good the tri-tip sandwich was?  The staff was super friendly and attentive.  The atmosphere is a patio feel, but it is the funk zone, it is up and coming.  Parking is okay.  I like the community seating.
If the sandwich had been loaded like one might expect it would have been a 4 star review.  
Overall i would come back to give this place a second chance...Make something good, make it available and make it memorable.

I rated this restaurant the way I did primarily because of the service.
The food was delicious! We got a variety of plates and shared it among the three of us. We had the special which was hush puppies filled with crabmeat and it was delicious! I highly recommend sharing so you can taste everything!
The room that we were in was very loud. I can't imagine what it would sound like at full capacity! They need to install foam to absorb the sound some.
Our night was going great until the very end when we were asked to leave. We ordered drinks, food and dessert. After dessert we ordered another round of drinks. As soon as we took our last sip, a waitress picked up our glass immediately (I'm not even sure the glass hit the table) and we were being asked by our waiter if we could leave because two tables were waiting on us to finish (we were only a table of 3). I totally get it because capacity is at a minimum, but the way they went about it was really rude especially since we were still enjoying ourselves. Also I feel like they should have signage or something saying dinner must be completed in 1.5 hours. When we left, we stood outside for a little while ending our convos and we didn't see anyone waiting for a table. It makes me wonder if they were just trying to close early. It was super disappointing and put a damper on our night!
Friendly, efficient and quick. Food was generally okay but nothing special. I guess you can't expect much more at a mall, though.

I've know Jennifer for 15 years and she always exceeded my expectations when doing my hair! She's so talented and experienced and I know I'm always in good hands when she's doing my hair! Excellent haircuts, coloring and styling!! Love her!!!

Rachael was through, polite and educated about her practice. I enjoyed it so much and was in serious need. 
I am planning on making another appointment next month. Keep making the effort, thank you. aK

We held a rehearsal dinner with 50 guests at Fratelli's on a Thursday night.  Our guests were heavy wine drinkers so finding a BYO establishment was important to our wallets.  The planning process with John was very easy and he let us drop off 24 bottles of wine the morning of the event.  While we did not have a dedicated "private dining room", our area was essentially separated from the remainder of the restaurant.  Our guests really enjoyed the food and the service was very good.  We are extremely happy that we chose Fratelli's to host our rehearsal dinner!

I have only tried their horchata milk tea and I hated it. Do not order it. It tastes like artificial something with sugar. In addition the charged me a lot for a tea without boba (almost $6). Maybe their other drinks are good.

Dr Levine is a really bad dermatologist.  I took my 2-year-old for eczema, and he almost told me just to take it, that she would outgrow and it was nothing to do.  I did had eczema as a child, and I know how bad it can be, also I was ashamed of walking and scratching all the time, so i did not wanted my child to go through the same embarrassing childhood

Tara is the best! She is kind, attentive, and does not judge your brows! She will make them
Look beautiful and give you tips and tricks! Truly a genuine girl with great talent at what she does.

Great restaurant- food was excellent and service was even better. Very reasonable priced. The restaurant seems to do a larger take out business than dine in. Would definitely recommend if in the South Street area of PHL.

I had reseached having this done for over a decade. I was nervous about going through with the procedure. My visit was over 1 1/2 hours long, but this is to mke sure you are a good candidate for Lasik. 
The staff is very friendly and helpful. They seem to genuinely nice. The doctors are both very personable and will answer any questions.

They are also very accommodating to find appointment times to fit your needs. The prepared to watch a couple of videos and sign a lot of paperwork. They have options for financing the procedure as well as split payments with your HSA card or Flexible Spending Account. 

Day of surgery arrive at least 10-15 minutes early. If you are not the 1st patient of the day, you will have option to watch others before you. I choice not to watch because I was nervous.
The process is quick and for me not as painfully as I thought. The after discomfort was not as bad as I prepared for but it was not a walk in the park. 
 I woke up from my nap able to see as if I skeep in contacts without the dryness I normally had. 
Day 1 is better than I excepted.
Very happy with my experience.
Store hours are wrong. Drove an hour to get here just to be told they are closed. Rude employees.
Yo I been to a lot of airports and this one sucks. No one wants to pay for wifi either that's not cool.

I visited on Sunday and had a bad experience with bad tea and cold bread.  I decidEd to revisit tonight and had another experience.  The tea was again bad and the pizza was the worst I have ever eaten.  There was hardly any red sauce on the pizza and the pepperoni was just placed on top and was cold.  
the manager Gary was rude to my husband.  
you need to check your tea throughout the day.  Sour/bad tea can make people sick.  
I was excited about this new restaurant but they need to be consistent with quality of food and drink.

Massonan curry was perfect. Service was excellent and everything was sparkling clean

Everyone in my party was happy with their meals
Great selection of guns employees are very helpful place is clean they don't pressure you to buy anything

This YMCA is close to where I live and it is the most convenient one to go to for me. However, I will not be going to this location again. 

I had forgotten my card at home. The lady scanning the cards gave me the nastiest look when I told her this. (It was not busy at all there was no one waiting for me or anything) She then said that if I remembered my headphones, that I should be able to remember a simple card. Maybe she is right, but I still forgot it. I don't think I should be guilted because I forgot it. She then looked up my name and threw a guest book at me. There was no pen, so I asked for one. She let out a huge sigh and angrily got a pen out and tossed it down. I signed it, she found my name and I went on to the gym.

This wasn't my first experience with her being nasty, but it definitely made it so I didn't want to come back. 

I will be driving the extra 15+ minutes to go to the Fishers YMCA. I have only had good experiences at the Fishers YMCA and don't feel like I'm a bad person if I forget my card.

Came here as their Mexican brunch looked good.  We arrived and the place was empty for prime time brunch hours but we decided to stay anyway.  On first glance inside the place looked kinda dirty like it had a rough Saturday night drinking party.  There was also creepy mannequins hanging in the stairwell. The worst thing was going downstairs to the bathroom.  The air was moist and 1 of the 2 toilets was broken. This wouldn't be that bad until I found a mouse in the corner..gross.  Told the bartender and she maybe handled it?? But this was not brought back up to us. 
3 stars as the food was pretty decent but the cleanings needs work

While the former poster is right, the bugers are great as well as their ice cream in all shapes and forms. I have to say that most of the food is good here on the menu. ... 

However, the only drawback is that it's been closed for renovations for more than a year and a half. So, i hope they re-open soon.

I had a fantastic meal here about a month ago. Hush puppie appetizer was divine! Main course of Seared scallops perfectly done on a bed of jalapeño cheddar grits etc. So good I got the shivers! Even though it was July we ate supper outside and it was a great atmosphere. I have to say the staff was great! Very accommodating, professional and extremely pleasant! I am looking forward to returning.
So cute on the outside! Great prices and options! Such a cute little place. Good for kids or couples.

We stumbled into this place on Frenchmen street and were very happy we did.  The food was great and so was the service. I especially recommend the roast chicken with sweet potato and butternut squash hash -- one of the best side dishes I've ever tasted. Definitely worth your while.

Big airport. VERY long walk to the baggage claim. The employees are very helpful, though. The baggage claim area can get overcrowded and it's very humid. But I know that's because of how muggy it was outside. The experience was fine for my first time. No complaints!

Quick is right! I cant believe how quickly they got me in and out. I would guess it was about 5 minutes. Other oil change places make you stand there and wait 5 minutes while they punch things in their computer system. Also, I like that this is a locally-owned business. I definitely recommend Insta-Quick.
The food and service is great, the salad , empanada and mushroom burger is good.I will recommend it to my friends

My favorite muffin and bagel cafe is a chain breakfast restaurant, that also serves sandwiches for lunch. 
For a chain restaurant they do a good job on there bagels and muffins.
Deb and I went there as fte r church and before a movie. We both did not want a full breakfast, so this fit the bill.
There is ample seating inside and two tables outside. The ambiance is w uite inviting for a small coffee,bagel shop.

They have tables, booths and if you ar r lucky , comfy chairs that you can totally get lost in the sunday paper.
The breakfast sandwiches are good, it would be nice if the cheese was melted and not slapped on cold.
The coffee is average, not great but not horrible.
For the purpose of our trip, it served its purpose. A light breakfast, a comfy place to read the newspaper without feeling like we needed to rush out.
If we are in the area we will stop for a muffin. Do not forget to check in your first time to get a free muffin.

I love to come here and visit when I'm sad. The staff is always so helpful and friendly. I was able to adopt 2 of my cats from here. Very simple and easy process of adopting. I can't speak however on the process of adopting the dogs but I don't see why there would be a difference. The only downside and the reason I didn't give 5 stars is because they are a kill shelter. At the same time that's why I decided to adopt from here, to save an animals life.
Brisket is ON TIME. sweet and honey badger sauces are legit! Baked beans were good and the krazy korn was just ok. For sure a good bbq spot.

My husband and I absolutely love this place. We drive more than 30 minutes to have their goat cheese dip and bread for an appetizer and pizza with pepperoni and banana peppers. We always order a medium so there's leftovers to enjoy it two times. Everyone is friendly and attentive and the place has a pretty cool ambiance.
Service was great! Mary was so polite and went over all the menu items with us and answered all questions! Food was disappointing. Expected more but a great experience overall!
Great music all night long!  Nice appetizers to go with your or drink minimum. Worth a trip downtown

Hi:

I have just had one of the worst customer service experiences of my life with Sears.  

I had scheduled a repair specialist to come and fix my washing machine between 8am and 12pm on Saturday October 29.  I received an automated call at 7:45am telling me the service representative would be here at 8am.  At 8:30am when they had not arrived I called and was told we were still on track.  When they had still not arrived at noon I called to check on progress.  The automated response told me I had been scheduled for Tuesday between 1pm and 5pm.  I asked to speak to a customer representative and told that I was scheduled for Tuesday and so I must be mistaken.  I asked to speak with a supervisor and was placed on hold for 15 minutes.  I hung up called back and went through the same process.  I asked not to be placed on hold for another 15 minutes.  However, after 5 minutes on hold I gave up and called the local store.  The sales person looked up my case online and discovered that my repair rep had gone home sick at 9:30am and that they had tried to reschedule me with another individual but they had been busy so they rescheduled me for Tuesday.  When I complained that I was not available on Tuesday and that no-one had contacted me for my opinion I was told that I would have to speak with the Escalated Customer Service department.  I was connected with a woman who told me that I should understand that someone going home sick was perfectly reasonable.  When I jumped in to say that I was perfectly understanding of the situation, I would just like to have been called I was told very testily not to interrupt.  At that point, the call was over. Going with someone else.
Lots of flavors, including a very good Taro flavor, that are focused on frozen yogurt (with some of the non fat varieties).

We used to love going here when we visited family.  We had gone to dinner in downtown and we're looking forward to having some sweet cece's.  When we walked in the two young ladies working were on there phones and dancing around with other teenagers that were hanging out.  NONE on the machines were working and only showed 4 flavors to begin with.  We had  to interrupt the young ladies, on their phones again, to ask them what was going on.  They said they all worked but would pop.  So we tried that and it blew Ice cream all over us.  On top of all this the restrooms were nasty!  I am so very disappointed.  What once was a highlight of our visits turned into a real bust.

They say it is "A place for Steaks" but it is also, unfortunately, a place with a consistent fruit-fly problem.  

Every visit-winter, spring, summer or fall, I am constantly bombarded with these pesky creatures.  I have had them stick in my butter pats or bathe in my beer.  Pretty hard to enjoy their delicious fare when these critters are buzzing your plate.  Get rid of the free air show and I'll promise to come back and write a full review!

Food and service are the best. Great price, friendly and accommodating owner. I always recommended Majeed when my group of friends ask my opinion on the best middle eastern food i've had in st.louis. Best falafel in town. baba ganoush was outstanding.

Oh this was a disappointment! In college we used to go to Sams place in Murfreesboro and was beyond excited to have one less than 5mins away from us. But this location is lacking in many areas. 

Service-- we waited an hour to be seated which we did give this a pass since it was a Friday night. However, once we sat down our server greeted us and as we ordered food/drinks we sensed an annoyed attitude. We did have questions regarding different drinks and what were the happy hour items and he didn't know the answers immediately. Again, we didn't mind because it is a new restaurant and it had only been opened for a couple weeks. The server simply said 'I don't know' and to the point where we had gotten 4 'I don't know' or 'I'm not sure' we requested if he could seek the information before we ordered. The server wasn't pleased with this and looked very frustrated to have to gather these answers to simple questions such as, what flavor margaritas do you have? 

Another example of poor service: One of my friends ordered a beer and another server brought the beer to our table and it was spilling over. I was sitting at the end of the booth and said 'oh it's spilling and dripping everywhere'. The server replied 'oh that's cause they filled to the rim'. She took no initiative to grab a napkin to pass the beer or to wipe the spilled beer on the table after she passed it. We just unraveled our silverware napkins and wiped up the mess ourselves. 

Food-- Our party was 10 people so we did order a variety of food. Sadly, some items were good and some were not. The bourbon pasta, chicken tenders and ribs, pizza were all good! However, the nachos were cold. Again, we had a party of 10 so I suspected the nachos were just made first and sat while the other food was being prepared. We told the server the nachos were cold, mainly the chicken and said he would order another. 

I'm assuming this was an manager that brought the nachos back out as he had a white shirt on. But the manager sat the nachos down and lingered for a couple more second but DID NOT speak at all. He didn't inquire if those were better? He didn't indicate anything. We said thank you after he sat the plate down and he left. I have never seen an manager come to a table and did not speak. I felt like this was the ultimate lack of customer service. It also was very concerning because if this is the service and lack of communication that a manager is displaying this would definitely be a reflection for the servers and hostess etc.

Overall, this was a poor experience. We do understand this is a new location and there are some inexperienced workers however, we expected more and would strongly recommend additional training.

The items are expensive compared to other regular grocery stores. Fruits and Vegetables are over priced. The foodcourt are priced higher than outside Korean restaurants. We lined up at Ham Ji Bak and it was not worth waiting for an hour for our order as the soup was only noodles and full of onions with a little shred of squid. We came back a few days after, ordered different soups and again waited for more than 30 minutes. Same thing, the noodles tasted good but it was loaded again with onions. AGoong Yi  on the other hand, serves soup that are priced at almost $18.  Cafe moon's small pastries are over $3 each and  strong bitter coffee. We were so excited for this place but i could find way better food at a lower price and excellent quality somewhere else.

he worst customer service I have ever had. Daryl is a complete nightmare and treats customers (especially women) like sh**.  We switched dealerships and purchased a second Mercedes during the first visit. My love affair with the Mercedes brand will continue but never again at plaza.
This is my first time coming here, it's absolutely a pleasant experience. The staffs are very friendly and helpful. Warm and welcoming atmosphere.

If you have read my past reviews you should know that Walgreens is not my favorite drug store.

Not a bad place to get your passport photo taken, it was quick, easy and around $10.00

This place was poppin' for a Saturday afternoon. My boyfriend and I came here around 2:30 and there were only 3 empty tables left. Staff was super friendly and helpful. 
This place is a bit pricey though. You get four flavors for $10 in a flight. The glasses are a bit bigger than other places I've been at though. Snacks are ranging between $1.50-$8 but you are allowed to bring your own food in to eat.
I'm more of a cider person but my favorite was Sweetum. It was a stout but tasted very light. Plus the name reminded me of parks and rec haha.

This review is long overdue and was meant to be written two months ago.  I cared that little about this place to bother writing anything.  Couldn't resist any longer allowing people to waste their money on inedible food.

About two months ago I was visiting two friends in Philadelphia and had my sites on a cheese steak place before leaving PA.  My first authentic cheese steak was at Pat's and I have to say that Pat's has great cheese steaks.

Jim's on the other hand, is a whole other story and disappointing one at best.   Lucky for us we waited no longer then ten minutes to get our food on a Sunday early evening.  That concerned me.  If they were so great like everyone claims, why wasn't anyone there?  I expected a line and was willing to wait for great food.  With 400 plus reviews on Yelp averaging 4 stars for the South St location, one would think it's a home run.  "WOULD THINK" are two key words.

Let me start with the staff.  Indifferent, almost had my order wrong when I specifically requested no onions and if I wasn't watching the cooks hands, those onions would surely end up on my sandwich.  
Before I ever take my first bite I could already tell it won't be anything I expected.  Hero bread was small, steak wasn't stead it was ground beef, and the odor was off.  

I bite and YES!  Exactly what I was afraid off, BLAND, DRY, and worst of all I couldn't even finish it and didn't want to.  Not because I was full, rather because it was that bad.

 Without trying to offend anyone on Yelp, the reviewers who gave Jim's 3, 4 and 5 star DO NOT and CANNOT know what they are talking about.  Not sure what they are comparing Jim's to, but it's definitely not the other steaks houses.  I can understand if they over cooked my meat, but they didn't, all of the meat looked the same.  Over cooked and chopped so much that it might as well be ground beef.

I really wish that I had gone to Tony Luke's instead.  Which by the way I tried at The Borgada in Atlantic City over the weekend.  It may not have been the best cheese steak, but it was very appetizing and blew Jim's out of the water.

Everything about Jim's is wrong, the location, the staff, the lack of atmosphere and most of all THEIR CHEESE STEAKS.  When you do one thing, how can you not do it right?  Three ingredients, bread, meat, cheese.  REALLY?

I'd never go back here, not even if someone was treating me.  Nor would I ever allow any of my friends to waste their time traveling here.

Cozy, gets busy fast-get there early when it opens on weekend mornings for brunch. Note: started my review a couple of months ago! Craving a return visit!

Gluten free bread made in-house. That's not common at all! All the breads are made fresh in house every morning!

Gravlax bennies? Oh yeah, and the salmon is cured in-house too! Maple syrup and salt/brown sugar cure. Mmmmm. 

They take huge pride that everything that CAN be made in-house IS. Full stop. 

Lots of menu selection, and they'll definitely cater to adapting a menu item for dietary needs ie substituting salsa for hollandase (theirs is not #glutenfree). The gravlax gluten free bennie was a hit. I ate it ALL and I am not ashamed. I never eat it all - really! I'm the take home queen. I really should start carrying my own containers everywhere.

Order from the front counter, and pay on your way out.

Bring your own mug for a discount on coffee, nice touch. Water is self-serve in the fridge by the door, glasses share the coffee station.

Parking, you might need to walk a bit, but that seems to stop no one. 

Update: had the pleasure to meet the chef and owner at an event last week. Did you know they do wine tastings too? They sell out every time, so if you want to go, get in on the action early!

Oh, and Cafe Haven in Sh. Pk... of course it's a sister restaurant!

GORGEOUS Salon with amazing owners and stylists. i just had feather hair extensions put in and they are incredible. Lucy Pop stays on top of all the recent trends and executes them quite well. Great location on 19th and Broadway. I will be telling everybody I know about Lucy Pop and I can not stand the idea of somebody missing out on such an awesome experience. LOVE LOVE LOVE everything about the business; from the immediate feeling of comfort and marvellous ambiance to the high skill set of employees. Seriously- go there- now :)

I can only comment on our one experience picking up food for our dog. The lady who checked us out was very friendly. The store was nice and clean and stocked with a great variety of foods, treats, clothing, etc.

Tip- call ahead and order so it will be ready when you walk in. Everything is made to order so it can take a while sometimes. We love the lemon pepper wings and fries. Also, their ranch makes everything better. I haven't tried the chicken tenders yet, but I look forward to it. Also, they will let you get some of the seasonings on the side if you want to put them on your fries or double up on sauces. I gave them 4 stars because I've been to other locations where their wings taste a little better.
I went to Larry's for years while attending St. Joe's. While the food quality is ok the customer service is atrocious.

If you've been to Penca in years passed then this review is for you - GIVE THEM ANOTHER TRY! Seriously!

We went on a recent weekend evening and there was a mere 10 minute wait for an outdoor table on the adorable sidewalk patio.

The menu is still tightly curated but there are dishes for every palate. The vegan Chile en Nogada was so good I'm still thinking about it days later and my husband said the pork chop was the best....he's ever had!
I really appreciated that our server mentioned specific ingredients in other dishes we ordered after she realized I ordered a vegan entrée.

I am such a sucker for an inspired cocktail and the Elote Infused Mezcal cocktail is pure genius.

Ended the meal with Tres Leches Cake and making mental notes of all the things we'll order on our next visit.

While this place was packed,  they were telling people that it would be up to 90 minutes when it appeared to be about 20. Kind of rude considering that it appeared that they didn't take reservations. 

The beer selection is great but they do not pour a Guinness properly. Bartenders didn't have that sense of urgency and, in my opinion, weren't serving people fast enough. 

I had the fish and chips. It's a healthy portion and not battered to death. Not a bad deal. 

Also, know when the TV's need to be changed. Don't make a customer ask. I don't know a lot of folks who want to go to a pub and watch Dateline on a Saturday night when there is NFL Playoff football on.

The food was great... the Mixed Platter gives you a good variety of food and is highly recommended. We tried the naan and some a couple different curries and both were pretty authentic. The menu is very beginner friendly for Indian food and I would recommend it for sure. It is a bit pricey for Indian food but the ambiance is worth it. Be careful tho the coupon on Yelp for $10 off does not work because it has a past due expectation and kinda seems like a ploy to pull you into the restaurant and management didn't seem to care about honoring the coupon.

Really really good, but not quite 5 stars for me.  The gravy is good, but just a tick spicy for my taste.  The waffles were good but the cheese in the batter made me wish I had gone with plain.  
I'll go back and maybe find something I can give five stars to

Little ninja is the #1 ninja in the kitchen bc it's been confirmed.

- el picante blanco

Always super tasty food a and pretty looking even...
Staff is always super sweet and attentive and the decor, music etc just gives it that extra touch. Highly recommend! Been here 5 times now - great every time

This is my place to go for Chinese. They have the best crab Rangoons I've ever had. I love their teriyaki chicken, the meat is so tender and flavorful. My wife loves the General Tso's chicken and the chicken pad thai on lo mein. The best egg rolls we've had to. They owners are so nice and welcoming. They make you feel like a part of the family. Go try it you won't regret it.

The Blind Onion Pizza and Pub gets an A. Had a tasty pepperoni, mushrooms and garlic pizza. Dough made in house. Ranch dip and honey for crust dipping. The prices are good. The staff is friendly. Seating at bar plus tables and booths.

Ordered chicken teriyaki. The server was not stingy with portions. Much appreciated! I hate people who puts food on your plate but then they take out a piece or 2. Been to many panda Express chains throughout the United states.. but this probably tasted the best and felt I got my money's worth. They didnt have a lot of food on display (a lot of food was sold out)... but I only get teriyaki so it didnt bother me. After I got there a flood of people came but the cooks got down to it and was dishing things out quickly. People didnt seem to mind waiting the 8-10 mins for fresh food. Manager was extra friendly and sweet which was nice to see.

Was visiting Tampa from Miami. This place was recommended by a friend. Was there Saturday night and the club was pretty cool. The only downfall they played Spanish rap and reggaeton the entire night. Probably heard 3 hip hop songs. Spanish rap is not my fave. But other then that VIP was nice and waitress were really nice.

I really enjoyed dining here. I didn't realize that so many restaurants in Philly don't have liquor licenses so you have to bring your own bottle of wine. The food was filling and tasty. The service and hustle of the staff is unmatched! The customer service made my experience all the more pleasant! They have 2 locations. The other is across the street so it's not uncommon to see servers and diners walking across the street!

I'll be back again!

Application process was very smooth and easy, plus the team was very nice and no hidden fees coming at you anywhere. My first impression was pretty good so far, I will definitely update this when I move in...

Great Hibachi grill experience.  Good food, big portions. Staff was friendly and restaurant was clean. We made reservations for 7. We were seated promptly. If you want Hibachi in Nashville this is the place to go.

I had to drop this down a star based on a recent visit.  The sushi rolls were subpar and not up to modern flavoring.  I don't mind a smaller portion as long as the flavor and quality can back it up.  In this instance, I was rather disappointed by their rolls.  Maybe I am used to more of the new age sushi rolls w/ the exotic combination and flavoring.

Great customer service and great food make Piccola Gelateria a great choice for coffee, lunch, dinner, AND dessert!

That's right, they have absolutely delicious gelato flavors and so much more! If you're looking for something different you can choose from 4 types of savory CREPES as well as a few sweet ones!

Looking for coffee or a latte? Piccola has you covered. 
Looking to COMBINE espresso AND gelato? Yeah, that's a beautiful thing called AFFOGATO, and with so many flavors to choose from, you could never get bored of choosing flavors to pair with a wonderful cup of espresso! I'm looking forward to trying the amarena cherry affogato!

I love the savory crepes here and they're always a great little meal if you want something great but not a full meal. Of course, for some it might just be the perfect lunch or dinner!

I'm more of a savory guy and not huge on sweets, but Piccola Gelateria has made me a fan and customer!

My husband & I had a fabulous dinner & had exceptional service from Payton & Lisa. The oven roasted artichoke with blue crab was to die for! The gumbo was steaming hot & the sausage delicious. They split our dinner for us on 2 plates, the garlic crusted drum fish and it was delicious as well. Will definitely be back, loved the casual & welcoming atmosphere.
A funky, fun, HIGH QUALITY, corner eatery in SB.  Best for a breakfast stop.  But the cakes should be tried cuz they are suberb

I normally call and place my order and they give me a ticket number . Their seafood is great I spent $25 on this order. This is the "Happy Seafood Combo " with Alaskan snow crabs, mussels, 6xl shrimps and . They are a little heavy on the garlic . It's a bar so they are opened til late. Perfect for your late night seafood cravings. Extensive menu but I specifically go there for the seafood. Would definitely recommend to seafood lovers. Cash only establishment but ATM is inside.

Cornbread queen of New Orleans! they only have it on certain days of the week,  and one of those days is Tuesday. You'll find the locals lining up, it really is the best cornbread in the city, and served with Country Crock- totally Southern.

The seafood is decadent even by New Orleans standards. Hard to pull yourself away from the Shrimp Magazine - that sauce is boss, if you can't handle the butter get out the restaurant. also heard through the grapevine that they have $2.50 margaritas on saturdays now...don't know how those will go with the super-trad New Orleans food, but I'm more than willing to find out.

This Public Storage facility has the worst customer service of any business I have ever dealt with. We have had a storage unit at this locations for nearly 20 years. We pay our bill a year in advance and keep a clean and organized unit that we rarely visit . That being said, the past two years, without ever receiving a bill, we have gotten a phone call saying that our lock has been cut and out unit will be up for auction with in a month. 

If it wasn't so inconvenient to move to another storage facility I would have been out of this place long ago. They have no concept of customer service or how to run a business only how to send threats.

If you go forward with this Public Storage location, good luck.

I have been getting my quaffing here for about a year now. Tony is my barber and does a great job. I really enjoy the vibe here, I feel like a mans man when I'm there. It's definitely not cost cutters. I recommend getting the works every so often, the shave and hair cut will relax you and make you feel like you can take on the world.

Both their fine dining and pizzeria were outstanding.  Fine dining restaurant had wonderful.  Started with the incredible eggplant soufflé which is not to miss.  The homemade pasta was fabulous.  Spinach and cheese tortellini was our favorite.  Loup de mer sea bass entree was cooked to perfection. The beef tenderloin was also amazing. The service was top notch and I highly recommend dining here on your next visit to Santa Barbara.  Don't leave without trying dessert.  
We dined a separate night at their pizzeria was more casual but the food was amazing. The pizza is a must and even try the gluten free crust.  Whatever you do, order the black squid ink fried calamari -some of the best you will ever have.  The salads were tasty and desserts again were amazing - gelato, panna cotta and carmeized peaches with white cocolate ice cream.  Amazing dinners.
I go here about every other weekend and absolutely love this place. The staff is just as incredible as the food.

Great Italian and comparable to other popular spots in St. Louis (ahem, Pastaria) but without the long wait every night. Spaghetti and meatballs is literally the best I've ever had, I order it every time. I've never tasted anything here I didn't like but the sauce on the spaghetti is just PERFECT. Makes great leftovers, too!
Great place to go with friends to grab a yummy bite and unique and tasty drinks! I loved the ambiance.

Be wary of Sherri, the racist and cruel lady. She treats her customers like trash and is extremely condescending. (Particularly of color) The way she spoke to me and my father is despicable. She spoke to us like we are beneath her. If it's the wrong label, instead of telling us nicely that the label is incorrect, she yells at you even when you walk away. My dad would say sorry, and walk away. In lieu of accepting the apology and moving on, she would lecture him about how he's slow to comprehend the basics of printing a shipping label. Even when he drops off small packages, she would give him nasty side eye looks like he should be on his knees begging for her to accept the packages. Overall, beware at all costs if you're asian or black, you'll 100% without a doubt, catch an attitude from this lady. Management needs to screen this racist person and hire someone else.

Hmmm, so ok, ya, Sushi GoGo is right on the harbor, which should render it awesome, I suppose.  Somehow you imagine fresh fish being carted off boats and taken directly to this place.  Wrong.  First of all, it's a very small hole in the wall, so be ready to order on a weekend when you'll have to fight people off to fit in front of the cash register.  Secondly, the food isn't very good.  To be fair, I got the "weekend special," which was some very overcooked chicken teriyaki, the tiniest 5 piece California and spicy tuna rolls ever, a salad and 3 piece veggie tempura all for $7.95.  So for the price, I suppose you get a good deal, but the quality is totally lacking.  The tempura was actually pretty good, as was the spicy tuna roll, but the rest was terrible.  A few people in my party got some special sushi rolls, which they seemed to like.  I however, couldn't get past the overcooked chicken part.

Anyway, I'd give the spicy tuna and tempura 4 stars, and the chicken etc. 1 star, so somehow that equals 3 stars... and that's a stretch.
Food was excellent, service was great, definitely going again.we got there later in the eve just before closing so didn't get to have the full experience but what we did have was fantastic.
These guys were great. They showed up on time. They took good care of all of my stuff. I recommend them to anyone in Tucson.

The inspection took place as agreed on a timely basis. The report was delivered by email within hours of the inspection being completed. The inspection seems all inclusive. The explanation of items of concern were clear.

I was going to the old man's house for dinner and wanted to pick something up for dessert for the 4 of us.  Read some reviews for this place and since it is close to the house I thought I would give it a shot...... l-e-t-d-o-w-n.....   The guests at dinner liked them but I thought I would have been better off with a stop at Isgro or Termini which I usually do.   

They just seemed dry to me... I felt like I had a  hairball in my mouth...I got four different kinds... PB and choc...carrot cake....lemon rum and just black and white... Dry, dry, Arizona summer dry and Mohave desert dry..  I love PB and choc and would mainline that combo into my arm if I could but that cupcake was so disappoitning, there was no way it would have got into a syringe...the lemon rum reminded me of my great aunt's fruitcake I was forced to eat as a kid...carrot cake was the best of the bunch and by the time I got to the black and white I was seeing mirage's of Milk at the end of the table...

I mean maybe it was just a bad day in the kitchen but when I eat a cupcake I want it to stick to my fingers, I want the icing  in my facial hair, I want it to be like a cupcake mom made for your birthday that you brought into school and gave you that sugar rush that had your second grade teacher going nuts...I want it be .......you know a cupcake.

This place deserves zero stars. Literally waited 15 minutes for my food. A worker in the back was rubbing his nose the entire time and didn't wash his hands after. The drive thru worker was arguing about the refrigerator guy not being able to fix refrigeration problems and the night workers are dumb for not stocking the creamer in the right spot. Poor customer service by the drive thru worker. He was all over the place and confused. Will not be coming back.

Willa Jean will undoubtedly become a hot spot for brunch in the CBD. Our group of 8 arrived on a Saturday morning (with reservations) to check out this new chic restaurant in New Orleans. Besides table service they also have a counter to order any of their delicious coffee drinks and freshly baked bread for those looking to get in and out quickly.

The quick and dirty is that the food and beverages were all fantastic. The iced coffee and lattes were clearly all made from quality beans. The 'Dirty Water' cocktail was delicious (I asked for less syrup in mine to lessen the sweetness and it was perfect).

I ordered the eggs hollandaise with crab and the gf got the steak and eggs. Both met our expectations based on how good the banana bread and corn bread were.  Cheesy grits were rich... I think everyone could have ordered another bowl. I believe everyone in our group was completely satisfied with their orders. 

The four star review was based on service, which had several hiccups (forgotten drink, slow service at times, and mistakes on the check). I know most brand new restaurants deal with similar issues so I'll give this a pass and instead focus on what we'll try the next time we visit!

This is hands down my favorite Chinese place in Jersey. Don't let the cash only rule turn you off. Everything is reasonably priced but you still get massive Americanesque sizes. Their duck is very good. It's not as fatty as Chinatown ducks as well. You can really tell if a chinese place will be based on items that arent found in american chinese food. They have a lot. Even their soup is a step above. The wonton noodle soup broth tastes like an entire chicken went into it. Try everything. Don't just get chicken broccoli. I'm sure you won't be let down!
As much as I love their wings and I love their beer selection, three out of the five times we've eaten there the waitstaff has been horrible to the point where we needed to contact the manager.

Love this place!
Best Tacos. Affordable. Local beers. Guac is delish! Awesome ambiance, great patio, customer service is casual but friendly.

Fun atmosphere that is dimly lit inside with candles at each table. Between 4 of us we split the Veal Sweetbreads, Country Fried Steak and Beef Cheeks. I think my favorite was the sweetbreads in buffalo sauce but each dish was very enjoyable! A little pricey (about $90 with the addition of 2 Sazerac's) but well worth it! Service was excellent.

Jacques-Imo's is my favorite go to restaurant especially when friends from out of town are visiting. Smothered Cabbage and Maque Choux are my favorite sides. Everything on menu is delicious. Can't wait to eat here again.

If you are a AZ Transplant, like many of us are, you prob. have no clue about In-n-Out Burger, besides it is a fast food restaurant.  

The menu only consists of french fries, hamburger, cheeseburger, double double, coke products and strawberry/vanillia/chocolate shakes.

However, they have a SECRET menu...and I thought I would help a Yelper out by giving you the run down.  And it prints out on the receipt...........Did this come about as a marketing ploy or by demand...curious.

A "24 is a burger with two beef patties and four slices of cheese. You can also order a "33," a "42 or any other meat/cheese combo, a 44 is as large as you can get. 

2x Meat or "Double Meat  Two beef patties, no cheese.  The rule of 4 applies.

You can get anything animal style and it is quite popular : a mustard-cooked beef patty with additional pickles, cheese, spread and grilled onions diced up and mixed together on the grill.  You can also get your fries animal style!

Cut in half= cut in half

Flying dutchman burger, cheesex2, burger- bunless

Grilled cheese with onion, tomoato, and spread aka thousand island

Protein style- wrapped in lettuce instead of a bun

Veggie or Wish Burger- Bun, lettuce, tomoato, onion, pickle, spread- no cheese

Extra toast- toasty bun

Cheese Fries
Animal Fries
Fries light- undercooked
Fries Well done
Nepolitan shake
Choco vanilla swirl
root beer float
Tea Aid- arnold palmer
Lemon up- half 7 up hald lemon aid

You can order Extra spread in a packet
peppers (peppercini)
Grilled onion whole or chopped
Extra veggies
On the sal- all the veggies with the spread on top

They have a mini list on their webpage...guess the cat's out of the bag!

Me and my girlfriend came to this place to give it a try. There was no one in the front to greet us, so we decided to take a seat. We were sitting down for at least 15  minutes no one ever came and acknowledge us so we left. That place was empty there was only 2 people in there so idk what was  the reason to that. We decided to give Thai Sweet Basil a try and their customer service was way better.

Best, most healthful meal we had during our 2.5 day stay in SB. We walked there from the Hyatt Centric for breakfast. My wife, who just wanted a nourishing healthy breakfast had the veggie sandwich with humus cucumber on house made bread. She was very happy. I had the salmon hash and equally pleased. Great for vegetarians, vegans, or someone who just doesn't want run of the mill resort food.

Really great food here! I'm not telling my favorite, only because they will run out fast! So give them a try, you won't be disappointed! Plus they give you a great portions, for the prices! The staffs are also very helpful in your selections and friendly
This place was filthy and I will never go back. The chairs were dingy and the carpet looked like it had never been swept. Atmosphere was terrible, non-inviting....don't waste your time or money.

Delicious food and always accommodating to my gluten allergy! Handle covid very well. Have tried many of their burgers with modifications (ie. no onion ring) and they're still just as delicious. Good prices for SB and quality food.

Loved the atmosphere and vibe! 50s diner updated. Open air seating to 12th South. Tator tot nachos were yummy. Poutine didn't have enough cheese curds, and wished the chicken was crispier, but flavors were great.

The customer service person was sleeping upon arrival (we arrived at 7PM). They acted like we were bothering them. She continued to stay on her personal phone call the whole time... very frustrating!!!
Another nice mall in Delaware, let me see how much deals I can get today.

Delicious and innovative cocktails crafted right before your eyes by an enthusiastic and attentive mixologist! 

We plan to return with kids and friends.

I left this restaurant hungry.  I hate this.  

This is a restaurant to go to if you are concerned about portion control and your weight.  You will eat out and not overeat, because the portions are so small.  

We ordered 3 half portions of pasta and 3 appetizers.  The half portions of pasta, while tasty, are what I would feed a small child who is only a little hungry. The appetizers were on the small size, although the cauliflower was what one would expect. 

I could have eaten all three half portions of pasta by myself.  

I am not asking for heaps of pasta, just a reasonable amount.  I know this is homemade and not dried stuff.  Nevertheless, you should double what is on the plate.

If you don't improve the pasta portions you should consider putting the disclaimer Caetera desunt under your pasta portion sizes.

Sorry to be so unkind.  Your waitstaff is hard working and nice.

My and my wife visit the place every time when we are in New Hope.  It is just a wonderful warm spot compared to overrun restaurants of the Main street.

The main thing is that you don't have to sit down for an hour.  You can pick your snack and coffee and then go eat it overlooking the Delaware.
Well the rice was good. The rest of it the beef tacos and refried beans was not that great. Super Burrito was much better

We weren't really impressed with Donut Wheel. Maybe we just caught them at a bad time, but the available selection was sparse. The store was really cluttered with boxes all over the counter and the restroom was out of order. The donuts themselves were okay but nothing special.

Really enjoyed the flagels they offer! They are basically flattened bagels without all the bread. I got the everything flagel with plain cream cheese, and it was very filling and yummy. The flagel was large, but didn't have all the extra bread in the middle! Highly recommend. 

Also, they do have kiosks in the shop to order your food, however you still have to pay at the counter. I wish they had a system where you could also pay at the kiosk. 

Overall, I would recommend the flagels if you are not into all the bread that usually comes with a bagel!

We have been to this location three times now but we will not be returning after today. At check-in, we were not greeted because the women were too distracted by a fellow employee coming in to see them. Once I had checked my two boys in (ages 6 and 4), we proceeded to wait on the stylists as they watched a previous customer do magic tricks. Once it was my four year old's turn, we were never greeted by our stylist. She was rude to my little boy and didn't even bother trying to speak with me. Another stylist worked with my six year old. She was not nearly as rude, but her skills were about as good as my own. If the past two times we stopped in were memorable then I may not let one visit skew my review, but I do believe we will be driving a little further for better service next time.
24 hour bowling!  Best done at 3am after you come down from your all you can eat sushi high!

Great food, fair prices and the slow service was worth it. We came in on a Cardinals baseball night (wild card game) and the staff was overwhelmed. It took them 25 minutes to get our drink order (though my friend who arrived before me did get a water).

That being said, the food was spectacular. Best steak quesadilla ever. Cooked to a T. I got the onions on the side which gave me a chance at really isolating them and discovering how perfectly cooked they were. The salsa was also the best I ever had. The style of the salsa was most interesting: not runny, but more the consistency of apple butter. The Waffle fries were delicious and the portion was just the right size for an already big meal. I like waffle fries well-done rather than under-done so that the ridges can really hold ketchup, mayo or sour cream in their crevices and you don't get any of the taste of mealy, underdone potato. 

The house salad, with a creamy italian, was chock-full of finely-graded cheese (I think it wasprovolonee), gloriously festooned with finely-diced shallots,  and a few pimentos and a couple of mushrooms on top (which I easily picked off).

Our beers, Michelob Golden Light on draft, were cold and perfect, served in frozen glasses. What more could you ask? Oh ... better service! Our first round of MGLs we had to go to the bar. But I would definitely recommend them. Hopefully once the Covid lack-of-workers phenomenon lifts, Weber's will be back at full staff.

Oh and the prices ... so reasonable! Probably 1/3 of what you pay at nine mile garden and you get greater quantities and sit-down food to boot.

If you are going here to smoke pot and get high, do not go here. This is a CBD place with a touch of THC. THC is not legal in NOLA, it is decriminalized. They are selling the legal amount authorized. Just drink alcohol.

I've been having my nails done professionally for 22 years and this shop, by far has been the best. I relocated to Philadelphia two years ago and found Dianna's shop not long afterwards. My nails were damaged from years of acrylic overlay. Dianna introduced me to SNS Dip Powder. It made all the difference in the world! My nails are strong, long and healthy now. There are an array of colors available and Dianna does the most creative designs! The other ladies at the shop - Linh, Amy and Kathy are also very talented. The salon is clean, the utensils are properly sanitized and stored and the customer service is top notch. This is a full service salon with waxing and eyelash application. Bring cash though, they accept cards but there is an 8% charge added when you use one. Trust me, if you come here once you will return.

Talk about a great addition to Indy! I went here for a work dinner and we ordered as much as we could from the menu! 

For starters, the ceviche..honestly I am not a huge fan of this dish typically, but was so impressed! Very fresh and light. 

We proceeded to order a few different plates of food to split, and I couldn't even tell you what my favorite was. They were ALL amazing. Friendly staff and delicious drinks on top of this. 

We sat downstairs near the bag, but I am looking forward to going back these warmer months to check out their outdoor patio upstairs!
The food is decent, but WAY overpriced. I have had pizza and salad here a few times. The pizza was greasy on my last visit but kids still enjoyed it.
Just had a tasty seafood lunch. Enjoyed the tuna melt sandwich with french fries. My friend ordered the seafood stew and I tried that as well. Will definitely be back for lunch or dinner
Cafe Milano is always consistent and food is always great. Large portions and their weekday specials are awesome too. Deserts are huge and amazing. I will not pass through Reno with stopping here.

I don't normally write reviews because it's a waste of time for me. I would just like to say that I went to return a headset at this best buy and I stood in line for over 20 minutes and there were workers around me just goofing off. The woman who was returning this man's little mini fridge seriously didn't acknowledge me and ignored the fact that I was standing there for over 20 minutes. How hard would it have been to get another worker to help me. I was not pleased. It shouldn't take 20 minutes just to return a headset. I would like someone to get in contact with me.

While I do realize that this is not a five star restaurant, one would think that you can expect some sort of standard when you go out to eat. Unfortunately, the standard at Curran's was very poor. Where do I start? We ordered our drinks, (which were the only things to come to the table in a timely fashion) and upon arrival, my glass was...wait for it...FURRY! It had hair or lint all around the rim!!! I brought it back to the bartender to replace. After we ordered (2 appetizers and three meals) we waited...and waited... and waited some more. By the time our apps came out, we had one foot out the door. Our fried Mozzarella app was good, our crab "Bites" (they were the size of crab cakes!) were ice cold in the middle. We told the server - she was great, BTW, that we would pay for what we had and to stop our dinner order. She stated that the manager was aware and that he'd take care of it. 
I give the place 2 stars for the good service, the fried mozzarella, and the fact that the Manager did the right thing...and this is a stretch. 
Currans, we wanted to like you. get it together!!

The name truly says it all. As many of the other reviews have mentioned - the service here is unbeatable. They are honest, good people that genuinely care about your biking (or boarding) experience, and they go above and beyond to do anything they can to help! 

On my first visit to the store, the guy that was working helped me evaluate what type of bike would be best for me, explained about different features, how it should fit, etc. and then let me test ride it. He was super friendly and knowledgeble, and like everyone else on here says - not pretentious!  just eager to help.  He even offered that I could customize the colors, swap out wheels or seat or other parts with other stuff they had in the store, so i could get the exact bike I wanted!

After that I was pretty much in love and came back a week or so later after I decided I was ready to pull the trigger. The owner was in that day, Rob, and I couldn't say enough nice things about this guy. He recently opened the shop, and while they may have a few little kinks they are working out, they are super professional and you can tell he pours his heart and soul into this place. My friend and I ended up staying for a while chatting with him, (he offered us coffees or water while we waited) and letting my dog (who I had with me) play with the store mascot, Lou dog.  Rob showed me some sweet new bikes they just got in stock (new colors of the same one I was looking at) and I found the one I wanted. He sized it up for me, tested the brakes, tires, chain, etc and made sure it fit me just right before sending me off on a test ride saying "don't be shy - take as long as you need- make sure you are totally comfortable with it!". That made me feel confortable to really do just that - all too often you zip around the block and end up having to make a slightly ill-informed decision. With Rob's encouragement I took a nice, long ~10 min ride around the neighborhood, and that gave me the confidence to know I would be making a smart decision with this not so little purchase. You can tell they take pride because they keep a photo album on facebook of all their happy customers!  Rob knows what matters most and runs his business the RIGHT way - with integrity, personal attention, and an honest smile.

I absolutely love the personal touch this shop puts on every single interaction and have already spread the word to all my friends and family that this is the best shop in town. THAT is what 'community' is all about. Thanks guys!

.I had a three month treatment plan here, but the doctors and professionals involved have since moved on to their different practices.
.I was given what might be described as Power Plate stretching exercises, traction therapy, movement coaching, as well as spinal adjustments.
.I was pleased with their multi-faceted approach to therapy, and referred two relatives to come here for treatments. My wife is a complicated case, yet she experienced some improvement in regards to her initial complaint.
.While I met a couple of the massage therapists, I never came here looking for a massage, nor was I offered a massage as part of the treatment plan. I noticed that one of the owners was trained in massage therapy, before entering chiropractic school.
.It seems that the massage aspect of this business is disconnected from the regular treatment programs, and the massage therapists aren't even advertised by name.
This mall is amazing!!! I could spend hours here. They have high end and low end stores! You will pretty much find any stores you want at this mall, just make sure you wear your comfy shoes!

Go here to get parts etc. The Mechanics know their stuff so I learned to go DIRECTLY to them, and you get taken care of.  But for the most part this is another one of those Mid City businesses that caters to the "new" cool crowd and therefore if you don't know someone there or don't look like a hipster NOLA cycler type you may get ignored, that's what happened to me at first until I started schmoozing with the "cool" mechanics and talking shop to get myself in the "in" with these dudes....lmfao.   I give them 3 stars....would be more...but because of their ultra pretentious attitude. and every time they ask me ..."you must not be from here".....even though I have been living in the neighborhood years before they moved in and tell them that EVERY time.  I just now know the routine and get my parts and pick their brains so I don't have them fix it charge RIDICULOUS prices.  The mechanics are actually more approachable than the huber-chill, cycling studs doing the floor and cash register....so don't feel shy to go up the mechanics instead and ask questions or get something you need. They are more helpful.

THIS PLACE IS AMAZING! Do yourself a favor and just go! Everything on the menu was fantastic and even though my boyfriend almost made us go elsewhere due to the wait, I'm glad we ate here. It was worth the 30 minute wait and I'm sure it gets much longer but it's worth it. The omelettes are sooo good and so are the pancake balls (sorry can't remember the name). The service is warm and on point. I enjoyed everything and will surely be back!!!!

Had high hopes for this new restaurant in the Old Pueblo Grille spot, but it didn't measure up.  Service was slow at first, then attentive.  Husband's order was misunderstood but quickly replaced.  Food was bland, margaritas were very good.  Needs lots of attention before we'll go back.
I love this place. Literally the most beautiful and positive and delightful interaction I've had with any business in months. Quick efficient helpful.

Am pleased as punch!

I took my Tesla Model 3 there after someone hit my car in a parking lot.  The repair was done quickly to my surprise.  I only had to have a rental car for five days.  I had heard that Tesla's are notorious for taking a while to repair.  The car looked great afterward and they gave me a post 30 day detailing of the car to say "Thanks for choosing us!".  Not every car can be repaired in five days but I got it back just in time to take it on a road trip.  I would easily recommend this place to my friends.

Ok, I am literally writing this in the parking lot of the salon. I bought a Living Social deal for a Keratin treatment and cut. Stopped in yesterday and was greeted by Stacy who was extremely welcoming and friendly. I liked the atmosphere of the salon immediately. To my surprise, I was even given an appointment for the very next day! Decided to also get my color done because let's face it, who cares how smooth your hair looks if it's fifty shades of gray. All I can say is AMAZING EXPERIENCE! Jen was my stylist and she was excellent with matching my color, and cutting my hair so that it no longer looks ridiculously thick at the top. Her personality and sense of humor made the time fly by and honestly, I had a blast talking with her. If anyone is looking for a great stylist, I highly recommend Jen.  If there is ever a Groupon or Living Social offer for ANY service here, grab it. They are well worth it and you will be so glad you did!! Best experience at a salon EVER! Thank you Jen and Stacey!

35 cent prawn night. Minimum order is 10. I ordered 15 and they brought me 10. I asked why and the waitress says it has to be ordered in blocks of 10. I said 20 is too much and 10 is not enough. She tried to explain it's a new rule because it "messes up the kitchen." WTF?! I guess it confirms my lifelong suspicion; cooks can't count. Legalized theft as far as I'm concerned.

This oxtail pho though!!! 10/10 The food came out piping hot and stayed hot all the way until the end. Oxtail meat was fall off the bone tender  never have I ever had so much oxtail leftover at the end of my bowl. Really great quality and quantity. Broth was rich and clean. Staff was super friendly too.

My bf got the chicken pho and that was really good as well. Fried onions are a nice touch! Will definitely come back to this place next time I'm in Reno!

My husband and I were visiting from Georgia. We had the shrimp & crab dip as an appetizer. I had the crab cake dinner & he got the fried trio. Everything was absolutely AMAZING. The food came out quickly, our waiter was friendly, and we left completely satisfied! If we ever visit again, we'll be sure to come again.

This place has changed quite a bit since I was in it a year ago.  It's clear they're trying to make the place a bit cleaner and have a bit more universal appeal.

Since my last visit a year ago, they've made it a non-smoking bar (tables and ashtrays are still provided outside), and have installed a new jukebox as well as re clothed their bar-size pool tables and updated their dartboards so they support credit cards.  They also do some poker games there, but I don't know any of the details. Throw in a few more TVs and an updated menu with a new look and a few new options, and this place ends up being different then I remember.  The servers tend to vary a bit on appearance but most of them are pretty average; the service they offer on the other hand is generally pretty good; often being friendly and chatting up patrons while topping off your beer.  That much hasn't changed.

For a place as small as Nickel City is, they dedicate a good chunk of their bar area to darts and promote this feature readily with dart tourneys on both Friday and Saturday nights.
