The turnout for these tournaments used to be very high, but I've been told that this was due to a local league that played here and has since moved onto a different place that used full tourney dartboards (Nickel City's dartboards are about 6" lower off the ground and the line is about 1' closer, which is still a good challenge for amateurs).  Prices are pretty average for a bar for food and drink, and they have a decent selection of beers both on tap and in bottles.

Nickel City has also started a late-night promotion on Saturday nights where they give free beer away after 11PM until they close or kill the keg.  While the most recent beer of choice was Budweiser Select, it's still a nice touch, and free beer is free beer.

I definitely intend on heading back there again in the future in hopes that the crowd gets a bit more lively, since that's really my only complaint at this point; it was amazingly quiet for a Saturday night, with my party being 4 out of maybe 15 customers I saw come in all night.  This place is too good of a bar to be empty all of the time.

Went to the mall because our car was being worked on. ChickFilA opened promply at 9 am and were cheerful and ready to serve. The area was quiet which was nice to just have a peaceful breakfast. Everything was made fresh :)

Terrible service. Last time we came, we said we would never come back. But since friends wanted to come tonight, we thought we would give it another chance. WRONG CHOICE. Service sucks. Food is mediocre. Don't recommend this place to anyone who actually enjoys the 'All You Can Eat' sushi experience.

A few key ingredients for making a good bank:
--good hours (this one is open on Saturdays!)
--awesome ATM (envelope free deposits!!)
--nice tellers (always get cheery people at this branch)
--down to earth personal bankers (I've seen 2 at this particular branch, and they were BOTH awesome)
--good interest rates (helllllooooo cheaper car payment!!)

We had Security inspect a home for us in November of 2014. Setting up the appointment was easy and appeared to be well done.  However, the inspector was rude.  He took his time and did a good job but was unprofessional.  He tracked items into our new house staining the carpeting.  He also did not put blinds down or other items that are simple to do.

When we revived the inspection report it was good but extremely nitpicky. This upset the sellers.  Some things like leaves in the gutter was reported as a "must fix item."  The seller told us that it took him 2 minutes to fix the problem.  Also the inspector told us the house mostlikely had mold but did not simply a water stain from 10 years ago.

Review of the lobby cafe only. 

Nicely appointed but appropriately casual for a bustling tourist hotel. Cafe offers terrific views of the Vieux Carre. I was killing time in the Canal Place Center when I decided that the Westin Cafe would a far superior choice for lunch than the food court in the mall area. 

I chose the Wedge Cobb Salad from the very limited menu and found it to be ample in proportion and just what I expected. All ingredients were of good quality and the salad dressing was made in house, or so it seemed to my taste. 

Service a bit slower than I thought it should have been, but very pleasant and attentive, and what was the rush anyway?

Do keep in mind if you need standard lunch fare in an upscale setting with fine view.

Even though this place is about a half an hour from our home, it is worth the drive to find those hard to find batteries that are sometimes difficult to replace yourself.

And when you need a specialty bulb, they are a good place to stop too!

Over the years, we have picked up batteries that have been for a wide variety of devices.  On our most recent trip, my wife wanted to replace a watch battery and wasn't sure of the type she needed.  She also didn't want to risk scratching the back to open it.  So she brought it here and let them do it.

She had an option:  Either simply buy a battery or, for a roughly 50% mark up, get a lifetime replacement battery.  They put a small insert into the back of the watch to let them know you've purchased the lifetime option and so you don't have to remember to bring in paperwork as proof the next time.  Nice option if you love your watch and plan on keeping it for a long time (no pun intended).

The guys who work here are customer oriented and though you may have a bit of a wait, they are trying to make sure each customer is satisfied before moving onto the next one.  Fortunately, the man who was doing repairs was able to assist in sales to keep the line moving.

Good service with hard to find items and replacement.  That's why we keep coming back.

Review #3485

Love this place!  I go there for both skin care and injections...Their aestheticians are the best! Ask for Terri or Angela.  I have been going to them for three years and they really understand your skin's needs.  Recently started getting Botox here, and am making an appt for Radiesse--this is the reason for only four stars. The doc I saw couldn't care less about me. (I am asking for someone else for my Radiesse).  Left me sitting in a room for half an hour to give me a 15 second Botox injection. Ugh.  But they have other docs, so I hope to see someone new next time.
Greasy, boiled meat of which there was little of in my cheesesteak. Culturally offensive signs in the windows. Annoying 2 tier ordering system.

Que rico! Best rice and beans hands down. We order the mushrooms enchiladas, very tasty, rice perfectly cooked, the beans were perfectly season and creamy. Honestly best Mexican food I had eaten in years. The vegetarian torta was good and perfect for us vegetarians. The lady who took our order was nice and very attentive. I recommend this place

This is our go to "let's have a good meal with friends and a great beer" place.  My husband and I have been coming here on and off for the last 6 years.  We have consistently been happy with the wide food selection, beer menu and overall atmosphere.   It's somewhere in between a good diner, friendly pub, and gastro-pub/bistro.  It's not 4 star gourmet quality food but it is tasty, fresh, and interesting.  

Their beers, though, deserve a major shout out.  There is a blackboard detailing the latest keg and you can have a tasting glass of pretty much anything.  Regularly they have 12-15 high quality micro-brews on hand.  For example this weekend when I took some family for a highly enjoyable lunch all around I had a snazzy cranberry kolsch, hubby had a super-hoppy Terrapin, and my cousin had a DC based IPA.  They do growlers, which we don't take enough advantage of but have in the past.  Overall, a great place to come for a low key, consistently tasty meal and good beer.

I have to say that since I went in there for the first time...OMG! Not only did I have fantastic service. The food is amazing. Best burrito in Santa Barbara by far. The salsa selection was awesome. I mixed a few together. Very unique taste. I even bought a sticker so I could remember the name

I contemplated not writing a review just so their page could stay at 69 reviews!

The Silver Ballroom is a staple bar for me in south city. It is a great atmosphere, with a full bar and pinball lounge. You'll find the best punk rock juke box and a friendly staff. The pinball machines are well taken care of, and I hear that the tournaments are a lot of fun!

They serve housemade meat pies that are really good. A hot dog joint moved into the kitchen as well so they recently increased food options.

The prices are very reasonable and they offer industry prices on Sunday nights.

Even though this is a punk bar, the bathrooms are clean. The women's restroom even has some cute mirrors and a lotion dispenser, which is a nice touch!
These guys are solid.  If you're looking for a reliable, friendly mechanic, this is a great place.  My fam has gone here for decades and they have never let us down.

Hayden from Zoom Drain arrived at my house within a few hours of my call. He was professional, courteous, explained everything he was doing in-depth, answered my questions. I believe the pricing for my service was fair also. I will call Zoom Drain again if needed.
ISO of a new best favorite Korean stop in STL. I think we have a Great Candidate here.
The service and the drink selection are awesome (try the Hawaiian mule next time you're there!) but I personally am not a huge fan of the menu. Definitely a fun spot!

visiting sb and in need of a tune up, i found mesa barbers on yelp.  as i get a buzz (number 1 1/2 on top, 1 on sides), the yelp review said this is the spot.  review was right on.  waited only a few minutes and got what i needed.  very professional.  nice interior with college pennants from around the country.  cut was $18--more than the $7-$11 i pay in oakland, but, hey, i'm on vacation!

We have been having our cars serviced at BrakeMax for over 15 years. Jamie was always great as a manager, but Henry, the new manager, just far exceeded my expectations. He talked me into purchasing four new Michelin tires from him in November. He was able to beat Costco's sale price by $94. Soon afterwards, we moved to Colorado and within less than 1500 miles, one of my tires had a screw in it on the tire shoulder, where it couldn't be repaired. Little did I know that BrakeMax doesn't exist in Colorado, so I had to deal directly with Michelin and one of their local dealers, Pep Boys. After waiting TWO WEEKS, during which Pep Boys and Michelin failed to find me a replacement tire, I reached out to Henry. He told me to have the tire installed at Costco and BAM! He reimbursed me for the cost of the tire. He is honest and stands by his products. I give him the highest recommendation and would definitely continue doing business with him and the BrakeMax he's managing ANY TIME. Thank you, Henry!

So I discovered this place by accident. I google-mapped something nearby and saw a big block labeled "Lotusland."  Curiosity got the best of me, and I planned a trip.  I called up to purchase a ticket for the upcoming weekend. No? I can't come this weekend? I can come on a Saturday four weeks from now?  Um. Okay. FIFTY dollars. Five Zero? Um. Okay.  And I have a window when I can arrive and leave? Gotcha. 

     Four weeks later, I was in my car racing towards the place. Google lied to me about how long it'd take me to get there.  I was due to arrive a half hour after my originally scheduled time. I called up to let them know, and when I arrived at the parking lot gate, I was surprised when they welcomed me by name.  

     I parked and rushed up to the entry gate.  I was warmly welcomed, and given a sticker to wear.  No. Put it on your shirt now.  Sticker said I had until 11:45 to meander the grounds.  Nearly 2 hours from now.  Seemed like a lot of time.  A nice guy showed me a large map and suggested my route.   I took a photo of the big map (I thought it'd be easier to pull up than open up the brochure and map they also handed me.)  

8/28/2021 NOTE - You don't have to wear a mask in the grounds, but you have to wear a mask in the lil entry building/gift shop. Thanks the covids.
  
    I set out onto the grounds. I explored. I photographed. I ooohed. I aaaaahed. It was amazing. It was beautiful. It was incredible. The plants were healthy, and amazing. Wonderful. Beautiful. The grounds were well labeled, signs clearly pointed to where things were.  Elder Docents were in every different area/biome, answering whatever questions you had, and snapping photographs of you in front of plants if you asked.
 
    Around 11:45 however. I noticed that the old docents started appearing out of nowhere like ninjas.  They'd politely comment that your sticker time was nearly up and direct you towards the exit.  
    
    "What's that over there?" 
    "Oh, that's the beautiful garden of amazing beautifulness...but it's getting close to 11:45, and the exit is that-a-way."
    "Ah, okay."  [Scott walks towards the exit]

     On my way out, I was approached by 3 or 4 more elder docents who gently and politely informed me that my time was nearly up and directed me towards the exit.   I made it to the exit, and even spent another $30 on a refrigerator magnet and an air plant.
   
  So yes. It's beautiful. Yes. It's amazing. Yes. It's wonderful. Yes. It's incredible. But. It's also pretty darned expensive. I'd think for a fifty dollar entry fee, I'd be allowed to roam as long as I'd like. Maybe they're crowd controlling?  I dunno. 

So. Pro tips. Doublecheck Google's travel times to get there. Get there on time. I'm sure an extra half hour would have let me see a bit more of the 25 acre garden.  Be prepared to be politely and nicely shooed away when your sticker says so.

Try Brasil Arts - you'll be pleased.     Wanting to use an Axxess card, it happened we dined at Brasil Arts tonight.   Had been there a few times before for breakfast and lunch, found it good, but tonight's dinner, served by Michael, was surprisingly flavorful.   We both had the shrimp covered sea bass, healthy salad, and a big mound of old-fashioned tasty mashed potatoes.    R e a l l y  good - reasonably priced, large portions, juices were very good , the sauce over the fish, as well as the tea.   Have walked by Brasil Arts Cafe over a hundred times, now I'll be stepping in and dining there more often.  Menu is varied, it was quiet, highly recommended, especially when you want dine on something different than the usual fare found in town.    Kudos.

Although this place is pricy and there is usually a wait, it is very much worth the wait and the price. The waiter we had, Gavin, could not have been any nicer. The food came out in about half an hour but was delicious. Their broccoli cheddar soup is better than Panera! Highly recommend.

I love Indian food. I began to love it as a teenager in Rhodesia, an ex-British colony with lots of Indian influence and food. I extended my appreciation eating with Indian friends in college at the special diet tables and in their homes. After being in the states so many years, I can no longer take it very hot but the basic richness of flavors is still a joy to me and I deepened that appreciation while hosting an Indian exchange student for a year. Curry and that archipelego of foods is not new to me. Imagine my delight when I was going out for lunch in Indianapolis and began to smell curry. It turned out there was an Indian buffet right nearby and I made a bee-line for it. We were here for lunch and that is the only time I can speak to so far.
Delhi Palace was a delicious extended buffet of Indian foods. The dishes were fresh and varied and not too slanted to vegetarians. There was a goat curry which all my group enjoyed. The  Mongolian vegetarian balls were a marvel, "Wow, you gotta try this." There was naan on the buffet. I love naan. My only concern in Delhi Palace was that I would have loved different naans but I can go back to check out the offerings or I can order a la carte. And I shall.
The carrot halwa was so fresh it still tasted like fresh carrots. The gulab jamun made every one I ever had before seem stale, they were so fresh. 
I have never eaten in a more clean and peaceful Indian restaurant. It is small and hidden in a wealth of good places to eat. But Delhi Palace is worth seeking out.
Weak/watered down margaritas. Small seating area. Food was mediocre, cheese sauce similar to a concession stand. Steak street taco was tasty. Wouldn't return

Tish is amazing at what she does!  She transformed my dry lifeless curls after one treatment.  She's also a master with the scissors.  I showed her a picture of the cut I wanted and she easily accomplished it to a T!  She's attentive, warm, talented and only uses top of the line quality products.  I'm now a regular customer!

This was dinner number two during my stay in New Orleans. I decided to give po-boys a second chance (and not to mention that this place looked more promising). I was not disappointed. The po-boy was pretty big (as it should be). I also had ordered a bowl of seafood gumbo - and it was also delicious. I got here before the dinner rush - so I was promptly seated and waited on. Would definitely swing by here again.

Awful. Disgusting. Soggy. Made me feel sick afterwards. Never going back again. The service was super slow and we were ordering it through the drive through. The drive thru lady was a bitch. Do not order anything chicken. It was just terrible! Everything was terrible.

BECHAMEL was prepared perfectly, as well as the savory ham & cheese crepes!
SUNNY counter cafe, cheery and relaxing space in a nook of the Quarter;
MODERATELY priced, 2 crepes, fruit & café au lait: $20 after tax, enough for two!

I have been looking for a quiet little out of the way spot to have a good breakfast and Conti Café is a great find.  I immediately liked the fact that it was walk-up counter service (which saves time) and food delivered to your table when ready.  The dining area is also an odd mix that somehow works.  The sunny space has tiled floors, bright colors and "French Provincial styled" chairs but with red laminate tabletops and a B&B kind of buffet area with serve-yourself coffee and flatware.  Imagine a Holiday Inn Express on the Champs D'Elysees. The two savory ham and swiss crepes were outstanding with just enough creamy bechamel to dip each bite into.  The side of fruit was much more generous and fresher than similar restaurants.  With a huge cafe au lait my meal after tax was $20 AND I took half home.

I just had the worst dining experience in a while here. It took 15 minutes just to get a water and 30 minutes for our server to take out order. It took 40 minutes for our food to come. I just ended up taking my food to go and eating it at home. It was truly bad service. I do not recommend to go to this Longhorn Steakhouse location at all.

When the manager asked how my crab cakes were and I said they were terrible-burnt on the outside and raw on the inside, I kind of expected that she would either give me a refund, bring out a new order, or at least offer coupons. 

No dice. 

She just said that they have had trouble with the grill and she would have to tell the day manager. 

Look, I know it's fast food. But even there if I get raw food. I expect a refund or Re-make or something. 

Better call the health department.

This continues to be out go to location for most things that are toys related.  

They offer air miles which is a bonus.

As a general rule I won't buy anything here unless it's on sale.  You can routinely find the same items elsewhere for 20-35% less even not on sale.

That being said, this is my preferred location.

Every month or so they do Saturday toy/craft activities for free that the kiddos can take home. (I'm currently standing in a 100 person long line for free Star Wars Lego Day!  The kids get to make and take home an X-Wing fighter!)

And while I'm standing in line for an hour, I'm sure Daddy and the kids are running amok and filling up the shopping cart!

Oh, to be a Toys R Us kid again!

Power Home Remodeling's approach is entirely too high-pressure and sexist. First, a representative came to the door and asked if there was a time they could come by and do an estimate. I was very busy, but said the following week would work. The rep insisted that the appointment needed to be same day as there were already people in the neighborhood. 

Then, I was put on the phone with a dispatcher to "send someone out", indicating people were actually not in the area at all.  

That raised some red flags for me. Then, both the female rep and the male dispatcher were insistent that my husband be there for the measurement of the windows. That told me that perhaps the measurement appointment was not going to be just measurement. (Something later confirmed when I looked at the BBB reviews of the company. "Window measurement" somehow turns into a multi-hour sales call, it seems.)  And that the company was too stuck in the 1950s to realize that 1.) Some women are completely able to handle appointments, 2.) Some women make the financial decisions for the household, and 3.) Some women are homeowners without husbands.

Well this was where I was led after the Elite event @ Mexican Post. We arrived after the main party and so ended up sitting at a table off to the side. Our Yelper crew was just too large for the place to handle. We chatted with our little group and ordered some food. Nothing very special, and a little over priced. They serve wine but seemed to have very limited selection. Since I am not a fish or seafood eater I will probably not wander back.

We ordered take out through Grub Hub. This food was fabulous. My only complaint is that they forgot the sweet and sour sauce for the sweet and sour chicken. If they can get their delivery game on, this will be 5 stars from me and my go to Chinese!

This was our first time there . The meal was great !
If you want good home cooking this is it. Hot , seasoned well and plentiful. Great atmosphere too ! 
I just wish that they made fresh ice tea and lemonade.
Not the package brand. Other than we will return!!!

Great food, grand staff! BYOB. My wife likes to eat gluten free and I was in the mood for something good but healthy so we happened upon this place. No waiter or waitress takes your order, you grab the menu clipboard and decide what you want then order at the cashier station, then they bring it to your table. We had the cheese plate as an app and my wife had the BLT sandwich while I had the eggplant meatballs. All of it was delicious and filling. We also tried a low caffeine tea and a charcoal lemonade since we didn't bring a bottle with us. If you keep an open mind about the food - the fact you're eating vegan - then you'll enjoy each bite knowing it's all fresh, quality ingredients. The staff was very nice, easy going, and knowledgeable. Looking forward to going again in the near future!

Had a great time with family and friends there last week. Ambiance and décor was the beauty we expected. The little bar happy hour was fun before we ate. All the food was great. Service was good. We weren't rushed or hovered over. Valet in the French quarter made it so easy. I'd recommend Marti's to any New Orleanian who knows a good restaurant from not.
Just a meh. Not overly impressed with the Ramen or tempura. Haven't tried the sushi or anything else on the menu.

Nice small casual Russian cafe and market. Great for a light touch of international cuisine and treats. We had the meat dumplings and s couple different crepes and a latte. All great. Good service with a moderate pace. The crepes came quickly but the soup took a little longer which was fine. We want to go back and try some more things. Took home some frozen pierogis and chocolate for later which were very good too. It just opened 4 months ago so they are still getting established. Definitely worth trying if you are up for something different/exotic.

They have great poke, sashimi, as well as all you can eat sushi. I believe there are drink specials during the week too. It's a great neighborhood restaurant. I enjoy that they are close to home and locally owned. They have great take out specials if you don't want to wait ( there's usually a line) or want to eat at home, with every roll you order you get a free appetizer!!

It's amazing that they think they deserve $15 an hour. When you can't follow the simplest of instructions and get an order right consistently, a raise is the last thing you deserve. I'd hate to say, this McDonald's is going downhill fast...

The sushi chef changed!!!
The improvement isn't great, but he's still  better than the last sushi chef who always scowled at everyone!
The hot food at Ruby's is the best around, bar none~
The servers are slightly bettter than other chinese buffets too~~

Richie was our guide for the River Tour. He shared good information when we stopped, but unless you were directly behind him his commentary given when riding couldn't be heard. Unfortunately, we had a rider in our group who was competitive and needed to be nearest him. She spoiled things for us. The bikes were great. I might do it again in a private tour, but I certainly wouldn't spend or recommend anyone spend almost $50 pp for a group tour.

Decor it up to date and comfortable. Cleanliness and COVID safety was definitely a priority for them. Bed was delightful and their breakfast was delicious. Service was friendly and helpful. Location was perfect. We walked to most of downtown and dinner places. It was a great stay and I would definitely recommend this hotel!

Might be biased as I won a "major" $1000 lighting link jackpot :) 

Plenty of slots and sport booking and they obviously gets busy as they have their own parking garage.  This is a place where locals hang out, gamble and eat. 

They have multiple promotions going on at the same time and I managed to get a free coffee mug with the number of points I racked up on their 5x points Sunday.  Yes, another promotional casino card for your wallet. 

Their restaurant (called The Coffee Shop) is open but you have to make a reservation. Staff was prompt and made sure we were all separated. 

Their food (at the Coffee Shop) was not 5 star rating. I'll give the restaurant food 3 to maybe 3.5 stars. My Chili was kind of watery and my lasagna was kind of salty. Generous portions for the cost. I was expecting a "cup" of chili but got a decent sized bowl instead. The staff I'll rate at a definite 5+ stars as they were prompt, made sure my soda and water was full and worked hard to make sure everyone was served and happy.

Edit: if I lived closer, this would be the casino I'll be feeding my slot addiction!

a very bad place...worst costco i have ever been to...
as of today
i will never set foot in their "crapy wharehouse" again...

save your money and pride...shop sams club..
.hate to admit my brother was right...but this store sucks big time..

yes i am very pissed off as i write this...

jerry
Nice place. My daughter and I got pedicures. The free water bottle was nice. Quiet place and the two techs never said one word to us from start to finish. Pedicure was great and professional.

This is a very handy place to have as I do ship many things out for both business and personal
Staff is pleasant 
They handle all types of shipping service and have a great lunch caboose outside 
The parking lot is gravel so just take it slow on the way out

Hattie B's is a must try while visiting Nashville. The chicken is juicy fresh with a delightful crunchy batter if you try the tenders. The long wait is well worth it, but I highly suggest you go wait before they open. However there was hair in my food which made me give them a 3 star

Pros:
-I like how you get chicken and two choices of sides
-The sauces are delicious
-I didnt finish everything so I saved it for dinner and left over Hattie's was just as great
-Their pickles are tasty
-Next to gigi cupcakes, so if you want delicious dessert grab it while you're waiting in line or finished ordering.

Cons:
-My mac and cheese had a piece of hair in it
-Crazy long line that goes all the way outside
-Their mac and cheese were too watery for my own personal preference. I didn't enjoy it as much.

Tips:
-Order online to avoid the crazy long lines. You can pick a time when you can pick up. However, I would suggest that you dont go at the exact time cause they are always backed up. Go 5mins after the pick up time
-Their spicy options are ridiculously hot!
Always on point when I go. Nearly once a month. Great beer selection, knowledgeable staff and super amazing atmosphere especially on the weekends.

Go see Laura at Legal Sea Food! She was absolutely amazing and the food was great. My dad highly recommends the Half Pound Maine Lobster Roll**
It was my first time here and I got the Colossal Shrimp Scampi which was super good! We will be back and requesting Laura.

great place away from the madness of the French quarter. Happy hour oysters $1 had a unique topping and were delicious. Octopus was fabulous but the crunchy potatoes served with them were incredible.  Highly recommended

First time at this location and loved everything about it! 

Our server Phil was fantastic. Recommended the Rockefeller oysters and that was the best decision ever! He was attentive and friendly :) 

Ben, the manager came by to check on us at the end of our meal and just sealed the deal for us! 

Will definitely come back (with a reservation) :)

I don't know where to begin.  All I can say is what a disappointment.  I brought my husband and two smalls kids here for father's day weekend.  I was very excited to visit this resort and surprise my husband. We stayed in a villa on the second floor and could hear everything from doors slamming to the people above us walking around to guests partying until all hours of the night.  Isn't this place suppose to be relaxing and quiet? When we finally all fell asleep we were all awoken at 4am to a dog barking.  Such a nightmare...but it doesn't stop there.  At 6:45am the next day housekeeping came by our room on their walkie talkie's and woke up both of my kids.  After the worst night of sleep I spoke to the front desk who offered me a one hour extended check out (are you kidding me?) and a complimentary breakfast or lunch (seriously?).  He told me that they are working on the sound issues and that they understand people come there to relax.  We have visited many luxury resorts with our children and have NEVER seen such horrible customer service. I almost didn't write this review, but I was reminded by how irritated I was while staying there when I checked my credit card statement to find that I had been overcharged!  I called Bacara and they reversed the charge, but no "I'm sorry".  Just like it happens all the time and it's completely acceptable.  Do yourself a favor and check out the Four Seasons!

Update: after reading all of the rave reviews here, I felt convinced my bad latte yesterday must have been a fluke.... So I decided to go back today (plus PJ's was closed). My husband got a hot mocha latte, I got the vanilla oat latte again, and again mine was just so bland and weak/ watery. I went back this time and asked to buy another shot of espresso to add to it. The man was very nice and gave it to me for no charge. Once added to my latte, it was fine enough to drink. My husband, however, took one sip of his latte and threw it away. 
----------
I ordered a vanilla oat milk latte from here yesterday, and it was the worst latte I've ever had. It was practically flavorless and watery. I almost considered walking back to ask if maybe she somehow forgot to put the espresso shots in it. I didn't, and I drank it anyway because after giving her a $2 tip, I paid over $8 for that crappy coffee. Never again.

This is my first negative review and I feel bad for the restaurant but I rely on honest reviews for a good meal. I created my own stir fry first with red curry. The flavor was so odd and after trying to eat a few bits it just became worse and inedible. They were very nice though and made me the same order with the sesame garlic sauce. Again weird taste but it was lighter so I tried to eat it, but it had a dirt taste. I couldn't do it. Down the trash it went. I'm from California and love dining on my travels. Tomorrow I'll be in downtown Philly with hopes of better food experiences there.
DISGUSTING,  ate one slice and threw it in the trash. Too many toppings, soggy, and I will say it again, DISGUSTING.

I regret so much buying furniture there. First time, seemed fine. Second time, I bought a couch, seemed fine. 6 months later a guest held the arm rest to get up and the frame broke. They said it was not covered under the 7 yrs warranty we bought. Right before this happened, we bought a dinning table at Ashley (owned by Sam Levitz). The table came defective and the leaf extension would not close. They sent a technician out and never followed up on the part ordered. My father in law came over and fixed the table for us. When I called them, they said they had issued a store credit for us to pick another table. Are you kidding me? Never again!!!!
Came here for pedicures with my husband. Everything was very clean and the service was great. Hands down the best pedicure I've ever had in Reno. Definitely will return.

I use yelp often to find businesses, but I rarely write a review as I usually do not have much to ad that I haven't already read. It really take a business to go above and beyond for me.  SK Heating and Air Conditioning does just that.  Saki, the owner, is very personable and his pricing is extremely reasonable.   He was honest with me about when he could come to my house and was very flexible around my schedule.  He explained everything to me step by step.  I will always call SK Heating and Air Conditioning for all of my Heating and Air Conditioning needs and can't stress enough that everyone should you will not be disappointed!!

How do you rate these clowns ZERO STARS? Asking for a friend...my stomach! Wings and fries 50 minutes? Okay, but 45 minutes AFTER THAT? Then the douche driver gave my diet Pepsi to someone else on top of it all? The guy comes back with 5 regular sodas. All well & good, but I'm diabetic, so why?? The driver is indignant as Hell about my soda, gives me $2 back. Give me my tip back! Semi-warm food, no beverage. You lost a loyal customer, and I call SHENANIGANS! I should dispute the credit card charge, but petty is as petty does. We both lost this day, Ed. Only non-pork option near my job, and you dropped the ball. Can't recommend you to guests of the hotel in which I'm employed.

I came here the other day since the other reviews were awesome. I ordered a chicken bowl with brown rice topped with grilled veggies, black beans, lettuce, pico de gallo, jalapeño sauce (my favorite); arepa with grilled steak and told the guy making it to top it however he thought would taste best - and he gave me one of those "I got you, fam" looks and he definitely didn't disappoint; and a side of the fried plantains with the special sauce (maybe aji?). 

Burrito bowl I think was better than chipotle - marinaded chicken was really good, tender, not too salty. Arepa was phenomenal - I loved the bread and the steak was delicious (a lil fatty but we're not going for health here). My mom preferred the bowl over the arepa and I preferred the arepa so it worked out. Fried plantains were a good dessert too but being fried I think it was too much at the end of the meal. 

Tl;dr - awesome establishment with great staff and delicious food and highly recommend

The inside is industrial fabulous, but that's the only good thing I can say about Mulherin's. 
Yes, the parking lot is free but as it only holds 12 cars and you are forced to circle around, parking is difficult.  We had to make our rezzo two months in advance due to their full booking but as we sat down at 7:30 on a Saturday, there were plenty of tables available.  What's that about?  As it got busier, we had to raise our voices to converse.  The din was unpleasant AND THEY LIKE IT LIKE THAT.  But here's what you should know about the value:  the wine is a very short pour of, maybe, five ounces.  We were four people, we got four thin slices of bread.  At the next table were three people.  They got three slices of bread.  MULHERIN'S COUNTS THE BREAD. Hello!!!!! People like bread!  Now this is important:  when an octopus appetizer costs $16, do you expect some octopus on that?  There were two small coated pieces, probably weighing a total of 3 ounces!  The raviolis were the size of a nickel.  Yes, you got eight.  But eight nickels looks like nothing on a plate.  And a golfball--sized scoop of ice cream is $5.  After spending $175 for four people, we left hungry.   If you want to be trendy and leave hungry, then Mulherin's is for you. If you don't want to be gypped, go elsewhere.

To start off with the website is very easy to schedule on. I scheduled the custom tan. I was very happy with the skill level of Anna. I have been serviced by her before and she is excellent. I was very happy with my tan, it was the perfect color for me- it looked like a natural tan. I also like to be able to tip by Venmo. 
I definitely will be going back.

First the customer service department it self has some good people working for them I can acknowledge that. 

Second we were going to only buy a coffee table and end tables but sales person went and asked the manager to talk us into buying an additional TV stand, that he would give us a nice price off of things, well when completing the order he disappeared and not much of a discount was given. 


Purchased four pieces of furniture, less than 12 hours later my wife finds all the furniture on Amazon (Ashley) same company for almost half the price off each piece. I brought this to the attention of the store, they first stated that they would price match because I stated that I would cancel so they came back thirty minutes later and said they would not price match Amazon so a manager got on the phone mind you again it has been less than twelve hours, and states that he would willing to give me somewhat of a discount, because I didn't need the TV stand any longer he stated that I would have to reimburse them in some way in order to restock that item and adjusting the prices well, I finally said of hearing enough ask if this was a problem then cancel the entire order, they did at a $205 charge for restocking something that they have to restock that ordered on a Sunday and I canceled on a holiday to follow again less than 12 hours later from when I ordered 

I will sell the furniture that I bought my son as soon as it is paid off. We are never shopping at another Ashley Home Ever! When we pay of out previous order we are canceling our card.

I have never been talked to that by a manager of a store where I just spent a large amount of money. I suggest you read everything they had you. The website that contains their policy does not match what they give you to sign at the store. 

Bad products!, Bad Sales force!

Updated : 1/30/18
It has been a three weeks now since the Ashley Furniture Business Manager contacted me  Yelp with the same generic message to all complaints. I sent the information asked and have heard nothing from the company.  I would have expected to at least get the $205 they stole from my family back but I see I have to move forward with my plan because there is no change only the empty promise of help on Yelp to make people think they are there to help, what a joke!

The food is always amazing and quality of taste is always their concern. I've never had anything from there that I didn't like from their steak salads to their cheeseburgers and of course their cheesesteaks. My favorite is the mushroom steak. Their hot dogs are foot long Nathan's and you can get them almost anyway you can think of. Their barbecue sauce is also delicious if you like it on your burgers. Only thing to consider is their portions are sometimes too big so hopefully they consider smaller potions (single patties instead of just doubles) as additional menu items, but how can you really complain about getting too much? The staff is awesome and very personable. They introduced themselves to me the first time I went there and they call me by name whenever I stop by. Love this place.
Enjoyed a very nice omelette here Sunday morning. Cibatta toast, home fries and a good cup of coffee all at a fair price. Definitely  will be back. Thanks
Great hoagies but the cheesesteaks are just mediocre.

I have been going to Vision Works at this location for 10 years for my son. Emily has always gone above and beyond for us. Her customer service is impeccable. It is so nice to go in and see a friendly face. She goes out of her way to help my son find exactly what he needs...and even helps with just simple adjustments. I really cannot say enough wonderful things about Emily and how she represent Vision Works. I recently came in with an emergency...my son broke his only pair of glasses. She took the lenses out and found an inexpensive pair I could use temporarily. I don't think anyone else would have had that kind of patience but she knew we had to fix it for my son!!! She is the best:) Thank you Emily! Jennifer (Tristan) Hall

While in town for St. Patty's Day shopping on Magazine St.,  we passed Charcoal's going one direction and after several blocks of shopping, my wife decided she really wanted to try you guys out so we made the long walk back.  I had read the article and saw the pic in the "Where Yat" paper and was intrigued.  We wanted to enjoy the weather so we went upstairs and were greeted and promptly seated at a table on the balcony.Our server was polite and friendly but not very competent as a server.  Our drink order was taken, menus handed out and the menu was explained.  Drinks came back and we ordered.  I had a "build your own Kobe burger" and my wife had a house burger (I think).  We waited for approximately 15 minutes and our waitress never came back for drink refills.  The food came approximately 20 minutes into our visit, only to find out that we had no napkins/silverware and drinks still hadn't been refilled.  The guys running the food brought drinks.  After our first few bites, we were all sitting there with grease stained hands when one of our party walked in to get napkins and order our next round of drinks.  Someone (a manager maybe) did come out and apologize and say to let her know if we need anything.  The food was ordinary at best.  The Kobe burger was a major disappointment for what I paid for it, while the sweet potato tots were good.  My wife's burger looked and tasted better than mine.  Our server did finally return and we got the check so we didn't have to wait another 20 minutes for her return.  She didn't seem at all concerned at the fact that we had to get out own napkins and refills, and seemed to be content hanging out with the hostess chatting.  I think my check was around $60ish dollars for a mediocre meal and sub-par service.  We come to New Orleans annually and after this experience, I won't return to Charcoal's if they are even there next year.

Was in for my second visit last night to redeem my Living Social gift certificate.  Even more impressed second time around.  We got there an hour before our reservation and hung out at the bar.  As usual, the gigantic book of all things rum captures your attention.  We spent about five minutes looking through before we were able to make a decision.  I went with a mojito and my fiance chose the Bahama mama, which came in the funky tiki mug.  Both were outstanding.  That was where we splurged for the evening, moving to the $3 rum & cokes and bay breezes for the remainder of our stay.  We also grabbed some chips and guacamole to snack on while we waited.  The guac was really nice.  Had a spice to it that was a nice break from the more mild guac I'm used to.  The chips were nice and hot too, which is always a plus.

After an hour we decided to take our seats for the dinner reservation.  Got the Cuban eggrolls with mustard aioli for our app.  The taste was ridiculous.  Definitely sad when it was all gone.  For dinner, I got the paella, which had of chorizo, chicken, mussels and calamari.  Now I was questioning my decision a little because last time I got paella with calamari I nearly lost my appetite.  Don't get me wrong, I love calamari, but there's a big difference between fried and not fried calamari.  Was ecstatic to see that this paella had small rings, not the gigantic ones I had elsewhere.  As for the overall taste, it was delicious.  There was a very noticeable taste of curry that I really enjoyed.  Wasn't the biggest meal but the flavor was through the roof.  Finished our meal with some tres leches cake that was the perfect way to wrap things up.   Great taste and texture.  

Overall, it was a very enjoyable experience.  Can't complain about any of the drinks or the food.  Everything was great.  This may quickly become one of my favorite places to go in the city.  Can't wait to get back here.
Best nachos in Philadelphia? Totally worth the hype, the Bards brings it! Ask for Michael!

One of my favorite small local restaurants I love to go to when I'm in lower Bucks County.  I make it a point to meet here with my cousins for breakfast or lunch because it's real ... really great old fashioned food, servers and atmosphere.

The omelets and pancakes are as good as any pricey restaurant and the lunch club sandwiches are excellent.

So glad places like this still exist!!

I love this trail for biking but there are a few tricks you should keep in mind. The trail is good for serious riders and casual riders. 

My favorite place to pick up the trail is Dunedin. I like the shaded areas of the trail, good options to stop for food and beer, and enjoy riding it through Palm Harbor to Tarpon Springs. You will pass the Primate Sanctuary and can see the adorable orangutans climbing about.  And then there's some awesome state parks too - like Wall Springs Park! A must see!

The trail in Pinellas Park / Tyrone has some nice areas but some are very shady/scary. Ladies, don't go alone or at night (trail closes at sundown anyway).  A section of the trail of Clearwater is closed which forces you to go on the sidewalk near a busy road.

Moved to Fla from NYC 8yrs ago and that is how long it has taken to find a Mexican restaurant that I would return to on a steady basis. Basically, a small "hole in the wall" kind of place in a strip mall. The food was FANTASTIC!!!! You can tell the food is  all homemade from the salsa to the refried beans, which btw, were the BEST I have ever eaten. Waitress very friendly and informative, made recommendations from the menu. Highly recommend.

Coincidentally, when I was trying to become a Priest, my nickname in Priest School was "St Loose." I have no idea why. OK I have an idea. Shh.

Did you know that Panera Bread is from St Louis and in the St Louis area they are known as St Louis Bread Company.They should have kept that name for the whole country because I like when a place is proud of its roots. However all it is now is just another corporate conglomerate with really really Bland products. The only thing I appreciate is the free wifi which only lasts 30 minutes at some locations but not this one I don't think. And the free water and friendly service. But I can't imagine less impressive food and salads. I mainly just drank free water when I was here, but I also tried the sweet tea and a crappy salad. The Sweet Tea isn't great either, but your Mom is.

Went here with a friend at about 2 pm on Sunday afternoon. (December 7, 2014 to be exact.) The interior is small, but quaint.  We were both excited to have waffles and since this eatery specialized in waffles, we were expecting them to be pretty good.

To cut to the chase, the food was terrible.  It is actually my new standard for really bad food.  I ordered the Strawberry Shortcake waffles. (See attached picture) My buddy ordered the Sunny in Holand.  There was nothing about my waffle order that was good or fresh.  The waffles themselves were undercooked, the strawberries were frozen, the whipped cream came from a can.  Seriously, how do you screw up waffles, especially if it's your specialty?  I have had better waffles at make-it-yourself cheap hotel breakfasts. I have never yearned for frozen waffles from a toaster before, as I did during this experience.

My friend's Sunny in Holand waffles were equally sad.  His waffles were brown as is if freshly made, but cold and rubbery.  We had to speculate as to how long they had been sitting there.  At least they could have heated it up.  The eggs and hollandaise were equally sad. The only way they could be passable were if I were an astronaut and this was the only they could have eggs and hollandaise for breakfast, versus powdered toast.

We stopped by for dessert after dinner at another restuarant. I give 4 stars for the food and decor but 3 stars for the service. The cappuccino was delicious; the best I have had. It is served with rock candy which is a nice touch. The hazelnut cake was delicious. It tasted like Nutnella. However, the service could be improved. It was not busy at all yet we had to wait for the hostess to seat us. We had specified that we only wanted dessert yet she gave us the dinner menu and no dessert menu. It took a while for the food to arrive. I would recommend the dessert but service could be improved.

Professional, meticulous and very nice! They cleaned everything to perfection (including the toaster oven).  I was very impressed with the overall service - both the owner (Raylene) returned my calls promptly when trying to schedule an appointment and the women arrived on time just as promised.

I can't wait to have them return!

I love this coffee shop, which is why I'm leaving this unfortunate review. I think B&B makes their own almond milk and it's really flaky...it made me cough uncontrollably. Again, I really like the coffee here, but ditch the home-made almond milk cause it's not great.

As far as uptown restaurants go, superior seafood is a must go to! The service is stellar. The kitchen accommodates changes to the menu, and servers always can help with a choice. Fresh seafood (especially oysters) ALWAYS are a must have!!! Fish options are many, and drinks are plentiful! Try the Frozen French 75.... It is spectacular!!!!

I just got back from and la flight.  I called and told them I arrived.  They told me to wait outside and someone would be there in a minute.  I waited 30 minutes in 22 degrees at midnight waiting for someone.  When driver came I told him maybe they should tell us it will be a half hour wait.  He was very rude and said we have been in business for 20 years and we know what we are doing.  I explained that parking spot buses came by 10 times in the time for them to send their one bus.  He said they are much closer.  I told him next time I will spend the $1 more per day and stay where I will be picked up and not left it the cold.

HECK YES !!!
Hattie B's is a MUST when in Nashville!
We arrived late (9:55), and they close at 10:00.
They were supper friendly, helpful, informative and just showed us a great time!
The chicken strip plate I chose , came with 3 fingers (1 HOT & 2 Mild).
My sides were, crinkled cut fries, slaw and baked beans. Excellent choices!! 
My meal was just as good as I thought it would be! I must say that the portions are very large! The strips are Huge!
While we were finishing up with our meal, the staff were busy cleaning everything up.
Usually, you might get rushed out, because everyone is tired. Not so at Hattie B's!!
Everyone was so friendly, telling us about the place, other locations and possibly new towns they are coming to.
One of the best things about Hattie B's is that they donate the remaining food they have to a homeless shelter!!!!
That is what every restaurant should do !
I wish I could give more than 5 Stars, they deserve it!!
When in Nashville, go give them a try.
Go get Full

Before we get into the review of Zia's, let's talk about the outside of Zia's. There are a few curbside tables where diners were eating, and a guitar-playing musician playing for them. And by playing for them, I mean directing a hurricane of sound away from ZIa's wall out onto the neighborhood. We had a wait of about 45 minutes for a table, during which time this ridiculously loud music continued. We actually crossed the street around the corner from the musician to try to get a bit away while we waited for our table because the music was so loud.

Now, on to the actual restaurant:

The service was perfunctory. Zia's was quite busy, but part of going out is the service aspect, and you want more than just your water to be refilled by the designated water-jug-guy. Our waitress was too busy to ask us how our food was, and forgot to bring by a sauce that we requested for our appetizer until we asked a second time.

The food was delicious. If I was breaking things down on serving size, the food would get a 5. Flavor is more like a 3 or 4. The chicken parmigiana looked like it came off the bird version of a Dolly Parton; seriously, this was the largest chicken breast I think I've ever seen. If I had had anything else to eat that day I would have had to take some home. That said, the red sauce was pretty much just tomato, and so it was an enormous, tomato-flavored chicken breast. If there was some garlic or other spices in the sauce, they were't evident. Maybe that's how it's supposed to be, but if so, it made it pretty plain tasting.

Best: Good bread, free appetizer of toasted ravioli because we checked in w/yelp, huge portions.

Worst: Food a little bland, server didn't seem that attentive, music while you wait pretty aggresive.

I was feeling something healthy after a hard workout. I was not disappointed. So many different combinations you could make with salad!  I got a basic Cobb salad with chicken. It was cool to see the process how they gather all the fresh ingredients then dump them in to a giant bowl to toss the dressing and then they give it to you in a Togo bowl with a lid. Which was nice because the salad was huge and I definitely took half home.

I really liked this place! Full bar, smoked meats, great sides, fried pies?? HELLO PEG LEG, nice to meet you!

I stuffed myself to the gills with a PBR Tall boy (only $3.50!), split some dry rub chicken wings with the bf (prob my fave part of the meal! they were huge), and a pulled pork platter (pork was aight), with some baked beans (delish with a bit of heat) and mac & cheese (pretty dang good but I prefer Edley's mac).

SO. What's the atmosphere? Laid back, not trying to be as "hip" as Edley's is, which I really like. I like the family-owned vibe and the while I haven't tried the Peg Leg Bourbon yet, I'm intrigued! There was also some sort of marbles (?) table for games that seemed cool. I'm coming back. 

Price? About 40 bucks for a 2-course meal with drinks for 2. PLENTY of food. 

I am definitely coming back for a relaxed hang-out. I love that I can walk here and find a seat easily (!) and have a great drink and eat GLORIOUS wings and great sides. Can't wait to try the ribs!

My friend's mother passed away recently. Rather than send him flowers, I decided an arrangement of fruit would be nice. I ordered the arrangement on a Wednesday and wanted it to be delivered by Saturday. The arrangement was delivered; however, my friend never received it. On top of that, no note was left. To this day, we still have no idea what happened.

I contacted the store about a week later and explained the situation. They offered to make the delivery again with no extra cost to me. This time, they asked for ID to make sure they delivered the arrangement to the right person. In my opinion, this should have been done the first time. While I'm happy my friend finally got his arrangement, I realized that it's probably better to pick up the arrangement yourself and deliver it yourself (if possible).
Loved this place!!! Best brunch ever! Food was amazing recommend the mimosa and the butterscotch sconces!

The Four Seasons Resort,The Biltmore, in SB is gorgeous!!  This review is for the breakfast buffet we enjoyed!!! 

We enjoyed our morning breakfasts here at the Four Seasons buffet on their patio overlooking the ocean each morning of our visit.  Spanish architecture, and interiors of this prime property on the beach can't be matched as the perfect place to spend a slow morning sipping coffee, enjoying the perfect SB weather, gorgeous views of the beach and architecture that transforms your short meal to a short vacation away from home.  The buffet features, fresh sweet fruit in season, yogurt, mixed assortments of seeds and health mixes such as flax seed, and various healthy additions to cereals, yogurts and oatmeal.  Fresh breads, pastries, and bagels are available as well as fresh preserves, and gourmet butter spreads. Aged meats ..salumi, and other Italian aged meats/olives are available along with lox and bagels (with diced red onions and capers) The hot foods table featured eggs benedict, waffles, breakfast burritos, pancakes, roasted potatoes, hash browns, chicken and pork sausage, crispy bacon, and made to order omelets. The open air breeze is right outside and the view of the beach within reach ..just a short walk away.   We will be back....

Have been to Bongos several times lunch, dinner and and appetizers the food is good the drinks are great and the service is very friendly.  Love the atmosphere overlooking the beach, and if your lucky you can witness a wedding like we did one evening.  Each visit we heard some really good entertainment also.  I recommend highly..

I stood in line for 10 minutes or so, and when I got to the front, they announced they weren't cooking anymore, but their grab-and-go sandwiches were available. Apparently their cooks were leaving or something. So I said "I guess I will take one of those(pre-made sandwiches)." I was also going to get a fountain drink. So I go to get my drink but there is no ice. I gave up and tossed the cup in the trash. I got the sandwich but it was rotten smelling and very soggy. Dropped that in the trash. So $18 gone, not going to wait in line to discuss it with them- my time is important to me. I would avoid this place for sure.

I really want to give this place the benefit of the doubt, but they did me dirty when all I wanted was a burger and a beer. 

I had about 2 hours to kill before my flight, so I hopped in the only bar I saw in terminal A. It was pretty packed which gave me some pretty high hopes. Trusting the ease of a burger and a beer, I ordered their standard burger (medium rare) and swapped out the fries with a salad. The meal came out on a plastic plate with some plastic cutlery to match, which I found kind of surprising. I guess it saves time with a high rotations of patrons. I took a bite of the burger and it wasn't very hot and rather undercooked. I continued on thinking it was just rare, which was the worst mistake I could have made. Before finishing the burger, I had a wicked knot in my stomach and made nervous about my age and inevitable inheritance of my fathers heartburn. I realized the pain went from my chest down to my stomach, and knew it had to be the burger. I'll leave out the details, but facilities were used before the issue was corrected.

Good service and a large glass of beer will not redeem this spot for me, but it's the only reason I didn't go with a one star. Tread with caution.

Had them in the winter of 2013 and they worked great because I was on the fixed rate. 

In the winter of 2014, however, I did not elect the fixed rate and choose to pay the market rate. What a big mistake. Despite the heating oil market dropping precipitously, my rate would only change ever so slightly. I would call and they would reiterate what their market rate was. As they were charging me almost $2.80/gallon, several local competitors were closer to $2.30-$2.40/gallon. I called again and they informed me that they were more because they are "full service," which turns out to be I was paying roughly $.50/gallon extra for the "privilege" to call them and pay for them to make any repairs. I even spoke to their management team to rectify the situation and they were no help either.

If you don't buy into the fixed rate, be prepared to overpay. If you choose the fixed rate, I have no problems with them.

Another great Mexican restaurant in NOLA's CBD.  Located on Poydras the decor is modern and has beautiful artwork inside.  The menu/dishes embody the warmth of traditional Mexican cooking.  The restaurant products were fresh, and the cooks selected only the best ingredients.  The restaurant has a good choice of beer and wines worth sampling.  The service was outstanding, and I recommend this location.

Ordered pizza's and wings for delivery, and everything showed up piping hot!  We had med wings which were very good, as well as an order of honey bbq wings, also very good.
We had 1 pizza with sausage and mushrooms, the sausage was very tasty and lots of toppings, and they dont skimp on the cheese.  My son had a plain cheese pizza which was very good as well.
I would recommend giving them a try, one of the best pizza places that deliver into our neighborhood, waters edge.

Honestly every beer here is delicious. It's a super cool vibe also.  Ive done  business meetings, privately worked, and done happy hour. My favorite is still homestyle.Monday nights are chills Friday night is packed! Great after work vibes. I highly recommend.

This was my first time ordering Jimmy John's to be delivered.  I was having some friends over last Saturday night and decided to do something a little different than just appetizers.

Originally, I was going to order a 2 foot party sandwich, but it was $30 and I didn't see that it would have any variety.  So instead, I ordered three 8 inch sandwiches (24 inches=2 feet).  I ordered Turkey with Avocado, Roast Beef, and Tuna with Avocado.  Excellent variety for $19!  Since I was slicing up the sandwiches so my friends could enjoy a variety, I was happy to save $11 by not going with a party sandwich.

The quality of Jimmy John's sandwiches is excellent.  Their delivery service is excellent.

What a cool old school place to visit! Its nice to see there are still places around that people get dressed up for. Waiters all in tuxedos, super cool vibe. Great place for Steak and Oysters! Steaks cooked to perfection, great service. Loved being in the oldest place in New Orleans!

All three people working here were nice and friendly & smiley. So rare! And it was clean! Not smelly of artificial flavoring and plastic. We got to taste the tea before they sealed it. They steep the tea to order. 

But..., it was $7 a pop !!! No prices posted. No bathroom. And the boba was way way too soft. The tea was way too sweet.

This place would be overpriced even if the fish wasn't mushy/fishy. We tried several classic maki--way too much rice, carelessly constructed, fishy, not sparkling fresh. I guess if you've never had good sushi--or if you order maki filled with cooked items/veggies-- you might think this was an OK place. But it's still mad $$ for what you get.

Oysters please! A dozen charbroiled didn't stand a chance with this lady! In my belly they went. So succulent(a dozen wasn't enough). The table also ordered bbq shrimp (so frigging yummy!), shrimp and grits, wedge salad and a crawfish/artichoke/spinach dip(skip this lacking in flavor). The pics don't do it justice but a visit here and you won't be disappointed.

Great little place to go. We go down every Saturday to eat and drink. Stop in on Saturday to see Hector and his great musical talent..... The food here is amazing and has wonderful customer service.....

Quite possibly one of the most inefficient operations we've ever patronized. Consistent bottlenecks, long lines, delays, difficult communications, etc. Instead of allowing payment via their website or over the phone, they require that you pay in person and stand in their endless lines. Because of this outdated practice, getting in and out of the facility is an exercise in the absurd. There are only two reasons we continue to take our dog to Webster Groves Animal Hospital--we like our previous vet, and it's close to us. If another veterinarian were excellent close by, we wouldn't hesitate to switch. I don't know if this facility is chronically understaffed or if they just choose to inconvenience their customers. If they retained me as their business consultant, I'd change all of this overnight. I believe their owners or managers choose inefficiency and inconveniencing their customers over efficiency and great service. And, it was this way well before COVID. They charge top dollar for everything but don't deliver service that commensurates. If you have nothing to do and like to waste hours, this is the place for you.

Impressive. I ordered the plate with oxtail, curry goat, and jerk chicken. I was not disappointed. Hands down their jerk chicken was the best jerk chicken I have ever had. Not only was the food wonderful, but the staff was as well. Janise (the owner) is a lady that can cook and came over to talk to me about my experience. Great person. I highly recommend Yah Mon, and will return the next time I'm in Tampa.

My experience was not good at all. I went for an updo and express makeup. I showed the stylist a picture of what I wanted. The updo I got looked nothing like the picture. I did not expect it to look exact but it didn't even resemble it. I felt the stylist had no clue what she was doing - she kept starting over. At $70 the stylist should know what she is doing. Even if she was a little green, then she should of asked one of the other girls for help. I would of been perfectly fine with that.  Plus the updo I was looking for was a classic style. when it was completed she snapped a pic of the back with her phone and said "this is what the back looks like, ok" I live 15mins from the salon, by the time I got home it was falling out, pins were sticking out at the bottom. Luckily my husband was able to help me fix it. I was told express make was full eye make up. What I got was some brown eye shadow that matched my brown eyes and a little mascara, no eyeliner, no brow filler. The makeup was NYX and ELF cosmetics!!  Which I refused to pay $35.00 for. It was ridiculous. The manager came over and said express makeup is just eye shadow - really who in their tight mind would pay $35.00 for eye shadow!!  The manager come over and offered to have someone else fix it but by then I was done, I had to leave to be at my party on time. She took off the makeup after I told her multiply times I was not paying for it bottom line. But she really didn't seem to care. I felt like she just wanted to get me out. It's a real shame because as I was sitting their I was watching some of the other stylist blow outs and they were really decent... I was considering their blow dry club but not after this... maybe a different t location!! Hi

Honestly don't know why this place has an average rating of 3 stars because it was great. Walking in, we were sure whether or not to eat here because of the fact that we were looking for a bread bowl. But we said f it right. And did not regret it. The service was amazing. The manager was very welcoming, our older server was amazing (totally forgot his name, it started with an E..he was bilingual..super helpful.) and the young servers were so kind as well. My bread bowl was amazing. The only thing I would recommend is that the black broad outside the restaurant doesn't specify that all the specials are to go. Although the prices weren't bad at all. There was 10 of us and paid 150 ish before tip of course. But "to go specials only" would be a very good idea. Definitely coming back.

What do I love more than a great burger? A burger that I can put whatever I want on it. The topping and sauce options are plentiful at this burger joint -- over 20 different topping and sauce options. I came in with my roommate and we both went with the regular Burger Monger -- one 6 oz 100% Kobe Beef patty. (Other burger options include veggie, chicken, and double beef patty.) On my burger I got pepper jack cheese, tomatoes, red leaf lettuce, grilled mushrooms, grilled sweet onions, jalapenos, and tried a couple of the available sauces on the side.

The burger was juicy and had great flavor on a nicely toasted bun. All of the toppings were incredibly fresh and everything complimented each other great. We also got a 1/2 pound order of great fries, which was a perfect amount for the 2 of us. They were your standard fries, nothing really unique about them, but fried well.

Fortuitously, we came on a Monday when the 6 oz Burger Monger is $5 as opposed to the regular price of $6.99. The girl taking my order was very patient while I looked through the long list of toppings and sauces while asking several questions about them. Great burgers and fries combined with the friendly service makes for an great burger joint.

I got one of those prepaid debit card "Exceptations" and it was the worst card I ever had. They let a 500.00 cash withdrawal without my pin or permission and then made me prove their mistake and after 2 weeks with no money they refunded my money!! No appologies. No compensation for my troubles nothing!!! Then the manager at pls starts acting like it's no big deal!!?? Must happen alot there!!! Not with my money ever again!!!!
This place was not too far from where I was working. Went 2 days in a row. Food is really good. Service is a bit slow and prices could be cheaper but it was definitely good.

I am a fan of local, organic and fresh food. The concept of Chipoltle is great and the food is very good.  The downside is that while the fat content is not great, the sodium is way over the top, containing, in many cases, a days worth of sodium in one meal.  It kind of contradicts their healthy fresh food message. Nevertheless, it's still good on a once in a while basis.

If you're in the St. Louis area, you're in for a treat.  My opinion of St. Louis itself is that it's a grey, dreary, very cold place, especially in October through February, but this zoo is truly a remarkable place.

First of all, it's FREE.  That's right.  You heard me!  :-)  

Even parking is free.  If it's not too busy you can find free parking just north of the parking lot that charges, along the street.  The staff at the parking lot will even direct you there if you ask.  It's not shady at all.

I was a little disappointed that a couple of the aspects of the zoo were under maintenance but really folks, can you complain when something this grand is free?  

The place is huge with animals from ALL OVER THE WORLD.  I walked for a good two hours and still didn't explore everything.  Awesome!
Great place for several flavors of soft serve ice cream unlimited topping choices friendly service and a kid friendly atmosphere

We've enjoyed Vivace in the past and had high hopes for the new space.  Beautiful and nice views, but cavernous and to me rather cold.  When we went they were understaffed, no additional hires for twice the room, and it showed.  The menus were also the same, and it never was too inventive.  THE WORST thing is their wine list - it needs some SERIOUS work.  The old wine list was ok for the space but very limited, and it is the same list here.  And Tavolino is just down the street...

Greens feed is the best. They always have my feed in stock. We have old, young, small and large animals. They can fill all their needs in one stop. With a smile they send me out the door where I  get more smiles in the yard.
Sloan has waited on us several times and she's absolutely the best!!!!!!  The edge has one of the best happy hours in town!

This is a *fantastic* USPS office.  Never a crowd, rarely more than a few people in line.  The staff is knowledgeable, friendly, courteous and helpful. Parking nearby is almost always a breeze. My only quibbles: (a) they close half an hour for lunch and I can never remember when that it is and it's hard to find online, and (b) their stock of mailing materials is sometimes a little short.

Tasty takeout or delivery Chinese food! We will order from here again. This is your basic, american-Chinese cuisine. It tastes good, it's sold at a decent price, and you get a ton of filling food. You can't go wrong ordering your classic favorites here.

Located in a great area, very close to alot of attractions! You can walk to Bourbon Street and as far as Jackson Square without feeling like it's too far! Great little bars right outside on the street. Very easy, fast check in. Hotel is very pretty on the inside and the room was extremely clean! The legacy kitchen has excellent food and great staff!

Another bar I wish was in my neighborhood. Not that there's any shortage of decent bars in my neighborhood, but they are sometimes filled with folks I don't feel much like mingling with (I notice a lot of white slacks and polo shirts and people with intentional tans). Hmmm, by now it sounds like I'm constantly wishing I lived in a different neighborhood. I like my neighborhood, just sayin'. But J & J's ain't in it. How ridiculous would it be to move to a new 'hood for a bar? Not so ridiculous at all, you're thinking. I know you are.

So, yeah. I'd come here on the regular if it were in my hood. Is it a destination spot? Well, no. But it's everything I want in a neighborhood bar and the few times I ventured in, I liked what I saw and who I talked to. 

There are dogs. There are friendly people of all ages that you can talk to. There are nice bartenders. There are cheap drinks.

I went in with long, chest length hair, and I was hoping for a shorter style. The entire cut barely lasted five minutes because the stylist was only concerned about going on her lunch break and wanted me out of the chair as soon as possible. She didn't double-check her work at all and brushed off my concerns when I mentioned the unevenness. Clearly, not a single thought or care was given to my hair.

I'm now left with a choppy haircut that I can't even pull up in a ponytail! When I went in to talk about a refund, my concerns where dismissed and I was told to come back five hours later to talk to the manager. 

Yes, my hair is very thick. That's no excuse for a sloppy cut. If the stylist didn't have the training to deal with my hair type, she should have declined to cut it. I'm posting this to warn other thick-haired girls out there!

The best Food, Service, Price! Some people might say that Blue Corn is expensive compared to most Mexican restaurants. Difference is Blue Corn has a bar and is not any more expensive than your favorite American food joint. If all you want is tacos and beer, they have that. If your into Mexican fine dining  and best margaritas, they have that too. The staff is friendly, helpful and professional.

The sandwiches here are so big you can barely bite them. The place is relatively small but has indoor and outdoor seating as well as two places to order from. I'd def try the deviled crab crabs. They are very good.

This place was so good! My husband and I were visiting this place from out of state and to was my job to find a breakfast place. There were so many reviews when I looked it up that I couldn't not pass this place up! Everything was absolutely good from the service to the the drinks to the food. I was pregnant on the trip so I didn't get to partake in an alcoholic beverages, but my husband did. He got the bacon infused Bloody Mary ( house favorite drink) and be loved it! He said it was really good, especially the smokiness of the bacon in the drink. From the looks of it, seemed like either the classic Bloody Mary or the bacon infused Bloody Mary were the most popular drinks as everyone in the place pretty much had one or more on their table. The food was very good ! So fresh and hot. The pancakes had a nice little crisp to it, but yet we're still fluffy and filling. My husband said the Benedicts had so much flavor to it and that all the flavors were matched perfectly. There was not one flavor that overpowered the other. Definitely recommend if you are in town and I was also recommend checking in via the waitlist app on yelp as the wait to sit here can easily increase. We used the waitlist app and were placed no longer than 5 minutes after being there which a lot of people didnt like who were there before us butttt technically we checked in before them .

Seems like the menu had changed since I was last here. There are speciality tacos which were really good and of course burritos and quesadillas, the classics. The new tacos are a nice variety with cool names like triple threat, two timer and drunken yardbird. If you buy 2 the taco of the day is free. Great deal for a quick lunch or dinner.
Star Joey provided a wonderful experience. The food, music, and customer service could not be improved upon. We went back two nights in a row which, says it all.

A local favorite and food staple and I can see why!! I will be honest and go ahead and say that I am not a big fish spread fan, but this place has some very good fish spread!! I also partook of their cheeseburger and it was an excellent choice!!! The open air environment was very nice and I will make sure to come back here when I am in the area!
It must just be me. Not a big fan. Only ever shop for prepared food here. It is average at best and way over priced. Think the store has a very strange smell.

I always call in my order and make special requests and they always prepare my meal correctly. They are priced reasonably and the portions are generous. They have good COVID precautions and I haven't had any issues with the other patrons here. I eat here about once a month and look forward to it every time!
This is a 5-star place. Owner is awesome. Really beautiful inside. And most importantly: the coffee was amazing. The will be my go-to spot whenever I'm out this way.

I ordered food from here recently via postmates and I was disappointed. From the pictures I expected my food to look at least similar as advertised and it looked completely different. The 1/2 pound was only two burnt chicken tenders and the macaroni was so sloppy. The only thing I liked was the cheesecake. Don't order from here unless you want dessert.

The Elevation crew are the best! They make GREAT drinks! The space is nice and calm. It's the perfect neighborhood spot!
The coffee itself is really good, personally I like that they use different blends throughout the week so you never quite know which of the two or  three beans they'll use, but its always good!!!!!

Having lived in Hawaii for 7 years or so, I ate at the 3 different CPK's on Oahu more times than I care to admit. My wife and I were ecstatic to find one here, and immediately decided on celebrating Valentine's Day here. 

We lucked into a Valentine's special which was 2 for $32. We got an appetizer (spinach artichoke dip), two pizzas which I snapped pictures of (halfway through eating them, sorry), and a dessert of our choice. We chose the butter cake which was fantastic. The food was as good as I could've asked it to be. CPK at its best. Our waiter Michael was awesome! Cheery, attentive, and understanding. 

The only reason I can't give this a 5 star review is due to the hostess. She asked us if we wanted a table or a booth, and added that the booth was in the back. We didn't oppose and chose the booth. What she failed to mention was that the booth was literally right next to the bathroom door. It smelled terrible as people opened and closed the bathroom doors repeatedly so we asked to move to an outside table. Once we sat down outside I quickly began to smell trash. Low and behold, the outside seating is relatively close to the dumpster. We just accepted it at that point and enjoyed our time. 

I know there isn't much they can do about the seating arrangement, but this was a bit ridiculous to seat customers in front of the bathroom and then by a dumpster (with a small white wall roughly 5ft high being the only thing between the table and trash). 

Great food paired with great service from Michael means we will certainly be back. We'll just make sure to sit in the front of the restaurant next time!

Sites are all large paved full hookups, landscaped and well-maintained. There is plenty to do, especially if you are traveling with children or large groups. There is a pool, recreation room, playground, tennis and pickle ball courts.
