There is a pub onsite with limited hours. Breakfast and lunch can be incorporated into the daily price of your stay, if desired. The Front Porch is the name of the cafe wich is in the main sales building of the vast area of the Lazy Days dealership.
Cracker Barrel restaurant is within the property as well.
The location is very convenient off I4, near I75 so tourist attractions are minutes away.
Pets are welcome with the usual rules of being a responsible pet owner. There is a very small fenced-in pet run.
The only complaint I would mention is the quality of the food at the Front Porch: institutional food. Coffee service area needs cream and/or milk, not powdered chemical creamer, blech!
We had to leave a day early due to an approaching storm and the staff politely credited my account with no notice.
We stayed in a motorhome and would definitely stay there again
Great place to go to get away from the tourist crowd. Most here are on business, with a good core of visitors.

We did the walking ghost tour. Our tour guide was Kristen (with a K). She was awesome. Very informative and knew so much about the architecture of the city, as well as ghost stories. Answered all questions and was a gracious guide. My family of 4 all agreed we felt like we made a friend in New Orleans. Enjoyed the tour and would return.

Got this on Hotwire where it is rated as a 3.5* property and has a 3.9/5* customer review rating. In reality this is a dicey 1* motel. We would have been WAYYYY better off at a motel 6. First room smelled of smoke and putrid air freshener trying to mask the smoke. Front desk gave us another room (They were very nice and the one good thing about this place). Creepy dudes smoking on balconies, wrecked cars in the parking lot, room is very tired and dingy, burned out light bulbs, dust and dirt, it just sucks. At $35 a night it would have been fine, at $80 a night its a ripoff. Upsides: TV is big and bed is reasonably comfortable.

I really enjoy going for a veggie burger at Red Robin. One things that stands out about their veggie burger is that they offer two options - one can either get a Boca burger which is soy based and more meat like or a Garden patty which is a more vegetable based patty and less meat-like. I prefer the Garden patty and I always get it subbed on the Whiskey River Cheddar Burger - gotta love those onion straws! (And I do!) One suggestion - if you don't absolutely have to have lettuce, have them leave it off...the burger is still awfully thick but at least you can almost get your mouth around it. Also, ask for an extra plate or little platic cups for sauce; there is no room in the basket they give you. The fries are always hot and, remember, you can always ask for more if you are still hungry. We've never personally had to do that...
The only real complaint I have is that its kind of aimed at kids and this makes for an awfully loud dining experience unless you go later at night.

I've been coming here since 2013, and it breaks my heart to write this. This review is based on service and social distancing measures. 

Before I made a reservation, I was told they would sit 50% capacity with 6 feet spacing between tables. When we arrived, almost every table was filled, and the 2 empty tables left were soon to be filled. 

When I brought this to the attention of the hostess, she was defensive, and proceeded to tell me it was at 50% capacity. I understand that a lot of restaurants are disobeying guidelines to survive. I'm more upset that the hostess proceeded to lie to me to my face instead of owning their lack of compliance with current recommendations. Needless to say, we left. 

I've had this happen to me at fogo de Chao in KOP. The manager there however, took ownership that they were busy and sat more tables then they should. He apologized, and gave me a $25 gift card. Like I said, the dissatisfaction with birchrunville was how the hostess handled the situation.

I showed them a picture and she did not do anything close to what I wanted.  It was a struggle.  Next I asked how long it would take.  They did not seem concerned about my time deadline. I paid extra money for a stone massage on my feet.  I would not go back.

Talk about the most charming bed and breakfast ever!  

Bed and Breakfasts are great for charm and a quaint bedroom, but this one goes above and beyond.  The building is a 1920s farmhouse type of structure -- beautifully restored with a great original layout.  The grounds are extremely well kept and lush.  It is also located just off of State Street, which is the main stomping grounds of Santa Barbara.  

Additionally, if you stay in the Mock Turtle (the name of our room) you will find several journals in the top drawer of the dresser.  It is filled with notes and messages from other people who have stayed in the room.  It is a wonderful touch!

Finally, the customer service is so great that it doesn't feel like customer service at all.  The people are real and extremely helpful.  

Oh, and stay for their breakfast. Um, um, good.  

Go to SB!

Mediocre coffee, doughnuts tasted on the stale side. Also CASH ONLY which isn't a problem but we were oblivious and the ATM on site was not working, so some courteous onlookers took care of us. It was our own fault but they should probably follow everyone else around town and get a nice app. Anyways, underwhelming to say the least. Good prices, I'd like to think we caught them on a bad day.
Left a request to order, never heard back. It was somewhat short notice, however, at least respond and tell me it can't be done. Just annoying.

Went on a trip to Santa Barbara, decided to stop by Pickles & Swiss for lunch on Saturday; me and my girlfriend got the Venetian panini and the pickles and swiss classic with white bean and chicken orzo soup respectively. The food was amazing so was the service, the dill popcorn is fantastic and we decided to come back the next day before we left s/o to Owen, Gabe, & Tome for the awesome food and the popcorn definitely will be back when in Santa Barbara!

Went back after my raw egg experience.  They comped me a sandwich and I got a Reuben.  Figured they can't really mess up one of those.   Corned beef is generally good so...  Sandwich was too salty.....kraut was not tangy at all...barely any thousand island dressing, which would have cut the saltiness of the meat and made the sandwich much better and I don't recall ever seeing any cheese.

We went there with my husband and another couple and we loved it. Its next to the marina. We took a table outside on the lanai. We are all coming from Europe and we are kind of difficult with food and it was super good. Octopus is delicious, everything is fresh. The desserts are also very good and traditional. We will go back for sure ! 

Nous avons testé Mama's le weekend dernier en cherchant un restaurant autre que les fastfood du coin et nous n'avons pas été déçu, pour ceux qui aime la cuisine grecque traditionnelle je trouve que c'est vraiment pas mal, la pieuvre était juste parfaitement cuite, les viande très bonnes et les desserts sucrés et succulents. Bien sur le but n'est pas d'y aller pour manger un steak frites !

Nice coffee shop located in Westchase. I happened to be there early Friday evening (9/5/2014) so they had activities for kids outside in the main plaza. I parked about a 1/2 block away behind the bank. They have parking spaces in the plaza and then a number of lots around it, as well as parking on side streets in Westchase communities. If it's a busy evening, don't expect to find parking within the actual plaza. 

Interior is clean and fresh. They have the chalkboard menu to allow for a bit of an old-school feel to this overall "modernized" coffee shop. 

Ordered an iced matcha latte. Initially, I ordered it with no sugar. For matcha lovers, we can handle it this way. But, it was Friday night and a little sweet kick every now and then never hurt anyone so I asked that they add just a pinch of sugar so that the matcha isn't too overpowering. Enjoyed the drink after that. They use 100% pure matcha here, by the way. It's not sweetened like at Starbucks. 

They had karaoke when I was there. I didn't judge, just tried not to laugh in some cases. I won't comment on their wi-fi since I connected to a network that didn't require a password, but I don't think it was theirs (connection was slow).

Certainly a family-friendly shop. If you're going solo and are looking to get some work done, you can, but give them a call in advance to make sure you avoid any distractions. 

Nice variety of pastries, breakfast and lunch items that I'll try in the future. It was busy when I was there so I didn't get the personalized attention that many yelpers mentioned they received (when staff members check on guests that are dining in). Next time!
Go here and get the lamb and beef gyro. I swear it will be one of the best you've ever had

The food itself is good and seems to be clean enough, but consistently have less than disappointing carry out service. Today was my 3rd visit where I called in an order, told it would be 10-15 minutes, I showed up about 20 minutes later. Then stood at the carry out line being ignored for another 15 minutes while every other person was helped. Then some lady walks up to the carry out line, then someone walks over and helps her out. Then they ask me and I give my name only to find they lost my ticket. Here's a thought, acknowledge the folks waiting in the carry out line.

There isn't much here as far as parks go. It's mostly concrete with very little greenery. 

Of course at one side you'll find the famous love statue with plenty of people taking pictures with it. 

I'm not sure that there is really any reason to come to this park other than to see the love statue. The love statue was actually a lot smaller than I expected and was kind of anti-climatic.

Customer service is a huge part of any business. My experience with Del Air was less than pleasent. Without going into specific details I'm going to say the leadership I worked with lacked follow up and follow through. I was also hung up on when I called the customer service line to inquire about two appointments that were scheduled were the crew failed to show or even call to notify us they were not coming. The installation took place close to 30 days ago and we are still not complete with the process.  This has been a very frustrating interaction. The only positive note I can leave this review on is the sells rep Scott and the installer did a great job. I would reconsider my options if I had to go through this process of selecting a company to install my unit again.

Absolutely fabulous food all around but my fave is the original Thai Pho soup: Beef with rice noodles! Haven't found this quality even in New York. Staff is very friendly, professional and attentive. For desert: Thai Donuts, very lightweight and soo tasty!

Went here for the first time today.  I would get my eyebrows threaded before at the booths in the Market Gallery, but they are no longer there.  Anyway,  it's pretty pricey but I will say Dimple did a fantastic job. She was friendly, quick & amazing.  I'll be coming back.

Overall impression: a mixed bag, more of a 3.5 than a 3. There were some things I really liked about Pepperfire and some things that I thought could have been handled better. 

Positives: 
- Tablets at the front to order. I LOVE places that do this b/c less human interaction and less chance for servers to mess up your order.
- Service is really friendly; I think they made both our orders medium spicy instead of one medium and one xxtra hot, so they brought out a large amount of xxtra hot chili powder for me. 
- Prices are cheap and portions are large! The chicken is really good too. 
- Pepper cheese sandwich (fried cheese sandwich) was super good!! 
- Good beer selections!

Negatives: 
- not all of the tablets worked, which is frustrating to find out after you input your entire order 
- It took SO LONG to get our food, almost 1 hour which is insane considering it's essentially fast food 
- Lots of flies flying around, especially near the trash cans which is pretty gross


Might come again, but only when I have a lot of time to kill.

My children and I absolutely love Dr. Casagrande. He is the best pediatric dentist in the area. I have been bringing my children here from the time my children were 6, 4, and 0. They are now 27, 24 and 17. During the time my children have been coming here, we have moved a few times. For the past 5 years we have been living in Downingtown, PA. I still chose to bring my children here despite the 1 hour drive. I do have to say however that today was quite a different experience than we are used to. Upon arriving I said "Hello, we are here for my sons appt." Instead of a hello, I was greeted by the dark haired receptionist with a dirty look and "We don't even have any more appointment on the schedule today!" So again , I give my sons name and remind her that I called to say we would be a few mi s late due to traffic. She says" here, fill this out." As she grudgingly hands me the clipboard to update my sons info. I don't care what kind of day you are having, that was horrible customer service. There is absolutely no excuse for her behavior! If it was a problem being late, then you should have told me so when I called. I am extremely dissatisfied with the level of customer service.

I've eaten here 3x in six months.  I would say the food is OK overall.  I've gotten two dishes that were bland, considering all the ingredients it was a bit mystifying - they seemed packed with flavorful stuff.

I did get some seafood soup (crab) that was very tasty.

Got the french fries, everyone raves about them.  I can't say I was pleased, oil drenched as though they had sat for an hour and not very hot.

Had the omakase at the bar. First sat in the corner, couldn't see the chef and had the asst chef in front of us. He assembled the sushi. He was imprecise with the wasabi which detracted from the pieces. Sake pairing was good I'd suggest it. Overall does not meet the bar for precision that you expect from a $160 pp omakase experience. If you want that experience it will satisfy the urge but needs work to meet the elite level.

If I could give this zero stars, I would.  First, I understand it's a bar/restaurant, but it's dirty.  My husband and I took our older boys there and were mortified.  I was excited to try it since I heard good things.  First, the pick and peel shrimp and wings were delicious.  The roast beef, that they're known for, tasted like a dish cloth.  The pizza fries were cold and not done correctly.  My son who ordered fried shrimp ate two.  They were hard as a rock. The waitress was rude and they give you these tiny little paper plates. I've been at dives that are delicious. This was not one of them. Save your money.

We wanted to like Elevation Burger. We really, really did. And even if you factored out the employee at the cash register who acted like a complete idiot, the food was soooo disappointing. If you're going to specialize in burgers and charge high-end prices for them and use supposedly good beef, then why on EARTH are you NOT going to season the patties? Unseasoned meat makes for dull flavor. And if you're going to use grass-fed beef which, generally, has a lower fat content than your average CAFO beef, then WHY are going to cook the bejeezus out of your burgers. Dry doesn't even begin to describe the $6 burger we ordered. And then, let's talk about the fries. The boy and I were amused to read that they fry the fries in olive oil. I know that there's the whole "olive oil" is healthy craze in America and everyone and the mother thinks that EVOO (with a Rachel Ray Ee-Voh pronunciation) is a god sent answer to all that ails your poor french-fry eating heart, but it's not. Olive oil, especially the cheapo (as in quality, not price)  Bertolli Olive Oil we saw stored under the counter is not designed to handle the high heat of frying fries. Olive oil has a pretty low smoke point and if you heat olive oil to the temp you need to reach to fry potatoes to crispy perfection, well then, you're creating carcinogens in that there oil. So here's your "healthy" fries with a side of cancer. But we might even be able to forgive that basic culinary flub IF our fries had not shown up with so many burned bits. Well, first, they didn't show up at all. I know the joint was hoppin' with all four us in there, but to forget the fries and then to check the ticket like I was lying when I said that we ordered fries, well, that's just annoying. But to have the fries arrive with acrid-tasting burned bits on all of them, well, that's just bad frying. And not even the overly-cinnamoned Orange-Cinnamon iced tea could get that taste out of our mouth. If I wanted a dry, underseasoned burger with uninspired toppings and burned fries, I'd just go to Champ's across the way. I don't generally want that and I had such high hopes for a burger place that calls itself Elevation. As a tip for the owners, mosey on down to Center City and see how 500 degrees does it and then mosey on back to Collegeville and look at implementing some of their methods. And season your burger patties for crying out loud. You're not doing yourselves, your beef or your customers any favors by playing hide-and-seek with the salt and pepper.

I'm a new customer at LBD and I have to say, the customer service here is outstanding. The person I spoke to on the phone earlier (I regret I cannot recall his name, though I know he said it) helped me track down the weird bulb I need for work. He then gave me an item number for it.
Once I arrived at the store, Rodney was who helped me. He was so personable and helpful! He was very happy to see I had the item number, he said "somebody here did me a solid!" 
The transaction was quick and easy! I'm a personal shopper, and I deal with many people every day. Rodney is by far the best at wonderful customer service!

I rarely write Yelp reviews but I'm staring at my dewy glowing skin in the mirror and felt compelled to! Got the Glow facial with Georgia and the experience was so majestic. The studio is cozy and beautiful and all of the girls were welcoming. Georgia was very sweet, knowledgeable and had the most relaxing, gentle techniques - the real deal! I've been a facial/skincare junkie for the past 10ish years and this was one of the best facials I've ever had. It may even top my favorite of all time at the Wynn/Encore spa in Vegas (which is 3x the price!) Seriously, I feel like my skin looks 10 years younger. Already can't wait to go back!
Just ok. Lunch bar wasn't the best but if you're looming for your quick Indian fix, it's ok. Great chairs and tables, dining staff very pleasant.

I only go here. But when I go I only use kiet. He is phenomenal. 10/10 recommend. I go here for my eyebrows as well and highly recommend Megan. These two take their time and they take care of me every time I go.
This restaurant definitely exceeded my expectations! The food was amazing and a hefty serving size. I ordered too much.  Chicken quesadillas were so good.  I highly recommend this place.
Nine thai just opening up!!!!!  Thai street food the pice was cheap the food was good you can't find it in town ..

The room smelled like burnt popcorn. The fridge didn't get very cold. Our drinks were cool but not cold. The television wouldn't connect to the signal. When we finally got it to connect there were black splotches in the screen, obscuring the picture. The walls are  paper thin. The room next door had a yapping dog that would not shut up. The heater/air conditioner was really loud when it kicked on, like it was getting ready to break down. About midnight someone out in the parking lot was working on their car and pounding with a hammer. When we were checking in two guests came down to complain about their rooms. One guest said his room stunk, and the sink was backed up. There was only two handicap parking spaces in the parking lot and one of them was taken up by an unpermitted car all night. (I'm handicapped so that makes a difference). Our one positive was that the bathroom was clean.  We will not be staying here again.

This spot is "slept on".  If you've ever driven past, that's probably all you've done.  A certain website that suggests restaurants in your area guided me to it a few months ago and I have been a fan since.
There are, certainly, drawbacks.  The staff is a bit rude and it takes them a while to get you your stuff and and and... Definitely loses this place a full star, I just don't get what is so friggen hard about pretending like you want me as a customer, let alone gold star service with a smile.
Having said that, absolute 5 star food.  They have a bunch of "American Food" (fries and what have you) and a few cool variations on those; Southern/cajun inspiration for days (get the Reese wrap; it's chicken, and shrimp, and amazing).
They have so many flavors of Nantuckets Nectars... I'm pretty sure that alone earns one full star.
If you don't mind terrible attitudes and somewhat nonchalant service, this place is a gem you don't want to miss out on.

After hearing so many visitors to Nashville rave about this tour, I decided to accompany some out-of-town guests on the two-and-a-half hour tour. There were several things about it that made it great -- even for a home-boy. 

The way the tour is laid out gives the guide an excellent way to provide an overview of Tennessee's history back to its founding, the role of music -- of all kinds, the importance of universities (like Vanderbilt and Fisk) in the development of the city and a look into the economic growth Nashville is experiencing now.

As someone who rides a bike a lot in Nashville, one of the most impressive things to me about the tour was how well it is thought in minimizing the need to ride "up" hills. (It skirts around them.) Also, the route makes excellent use of bike lanes and non-busy streets so while it's "downtown," you don't feel unsafe.

Advice: I would plan the tour as a "first thing" to do in Nashville as the guide (in our case, Austin, the owner of the company) will have plenty of suggestions of things to see and do while in the city and the 6 mile bike ride takes you past lots of things you'll want to go back and see.

Walked into Crystal (I keep wanting to call this place Crys-Tal... you know, like I'm drinkin' super ghetto champagne)
But alas, I walked into Crystal tonight and all the owners and their families were sitting around a couple of tables-- no customers in sight.
I was a little worried but I picked up a to-go menu and ordered, dutifully.
Hmmm....
The prices are a dollar (or more) less than the South Grand strip of Vietnamese places
Same choices...
I'll give it a try!
I ordered some veggie dishes and gave it a go--
Great.   Fresh herbs and not overly salted.  
I'm still on the look out for vegetarian Pho soup if anyone knows of any here in STL.  Had a sweet little joint in San Francisco that could make some veggie Pho equal parts savory and heaven.

When this place is not too busy it is really cool.  They have a lot of cool drink choices and draft beers as well. They do not serve food but there are plenty of places to go nearby, My only complaint is that there is only one restroom and there is sometimes a little line. I will definitely be going back to this place.

Scream-A-Geddon is a local haunt so really keep that in mind before you drive out to Dade. It's also in DADE. So keep that in mind...its a drive and there isn't much else out there.

Every year they typically have 2 new house themes and do something unique to the Haunted Hayride trail.

I will say we definitely preferred last years (2016/2017) houses and miss the Haunted Hayride with people on bungie cords swinging from the Tree Hoppers course. They revved chainsaws above the hayride and made the experience extra spooky. 

2018 Haunts:

Rage 3D - A new house theme but just as wacky as last year its a bit disorienting but lots of fun. If you hate clowns you won't find them here..come on in : )

Demon's Revenge - The second new house theme. Some cool design inside and out but none of the features inside make sense with the narrative. Its also always a LONNNNG wait but not really worth more than a 20 minute wait or less. The ending is pretty cool though. You'll also see the "villagers" travelling from the back of Rage 3D toward the house where they chant "Burn the witch" or some other thing. The houses are staggered openings so at 7:30pm only Rage 3D and Blackpool Prison are open so those are the employees marching to Demon's Revenge prepping to open it.

Blackpool Prison - Scream-A-Geddon's crown jewel as far as I and my friends are concerned. This is an interactive house and if you are 18 and choose to wear the glow necklace the actors can separate you from your group, tickle you, move you around, stop you, shove you around, put you in an enclosed space with other people, sit you down on a chair for an extended period of time, take your stuff (one prisoner took my popcorn every time I went in lol..gave it back at the end of course). This one NEEDS the glow necklace to truly get the most out of it..otherwise you're just walking straight through the prison and other people getting manhandled...personally not as fun.

Dead Woods - This is a basic inside, outside trail with some dim light on the ground lighting the path. You're group gets one glow stick (mostly so the actors can see you coming...you're not gonna see anything with that glow stick). It's nice spooky design and the actors are great but this is one that definitely needs some updating ...there isn't much that happens in between the stops or many scary elements. More use of sounds, fog, maybe horns (like a parked  truck...Papa Tate arriving for supper?). The sound of someone following close behind us. Just something extra...otherwise my friends and I just laughed the entire time and tripped on the uneven ground a few times. I don't ever remember encountering Papa Tate or any big scary actor.

Infected Ground Zero - This is another interactive haunt. Like Blackpool Prison, you need to go interactive to get the most out of the haunt if not you're just walking around folks getting pulled all over the place. Keep in mind there is one interactive element in the beginning where they may put you in a body bag. I've always gotten zipped all the way up (this year they have a section cut so there really isn't a chance of suffocation but if you get anxiety or claustrophobic keep that in mind. There are also several exits..make sure to go out the right exist so you don't miss the rest of the haunt.

Zombie Paintball - We didn't do this and really had no interest in shooting zombies that can't shoot back. We're hoping they bring back the haunted hayride or add another trail. 

Stuff they can improve: 
The staggering of guests has always been a plus in my book unlike Halloween Horror Nights where you're lead like cattle nonstop through a house. However, this year we saw 2 people go in every 2 minutes on average and at most saw 2 people go in after 4 minutes which is insane and that was at the final hour AND at Demon's Revenge (the longest wait). I know they are trying to get people to purchase VIP but at only 4hrs, people should be able to get through most of the houses without VIP. VIP should be to speed up waits and allow repeat house visits..not a requirement to actually visit each house at least once. It was crazy this year on the weekend.  The weekday was a bit better with doable waits and we were able to see a few of the houses twice..we skipped Demon's Revenge (long wait and not worth it).

Create a smoking section - People smoke in the parking lot, in the line to get in, in the midway seating area, and in the lines (we only saw one person stop people from smoking at Blackpool and Rage 3D).


Stuff they do GREAT:

The firepits are always awesome and give it an intimate feeling like friends getting together to camp and hang out.

The actors in the midway are also fun.

Oh my gosh this place is SO good! I've had the Kalua pork and the chicken and they're both very good but my favorite by far is the beef with the Korean sauce. Oh my gosh is it good! The savory and sweet of the beef and sauce with the creaminess of the macaroni salad is a combination you have to try.

Staff is super nice- especially the lady who works at lunch time- really great.

My biggest problem is that this place is so close to my house - I'm going to be here a lot.

Had the baby back ribs lunch at this place and was not impressed.   They advertise the ribs are smoked.  The dish consisted of 4 small ribs...you could tell the ribs were boiled before going on the grill and no seasoning.  The meat was little tough and tasteless and the barbecue sauce was no help. The only saving grace of this meal was the mac and cheese.

I just moved to the Indy area a few months ago, and I was looking around online for bars/restaurants. I saw Shallos and thought WOW 500 different kinds of beer! I'm a beer connoisseur! It was everything I was wanting it to be! A little on the pricey side, but I can deal with that! Haven't had the food there yet, but It looks good!
I ordered an Italian hoagie yesterday. There was literally one piece of Cheese,  and one piece of lunchmeat. The rest of the hoagie was  tomato and lettuce. So disappointing.

Stopped in at Mr. Bubbles after shopping at Hong Kong Supermarket. The boba is similar to that of Tapioca Express - all powder/syrup and not made with fresh tea. I don't think places like that exist on NOLA though, so this is probably the best you'll get. I actually thought the taro boba was pretty expensive ($4.75) for a basic drink, but based on other Yelpers' reviews, this seems to be the average price around here. I think I'm just used to California pricing. Cute shop that seems to be family run and the girls at the counter were super sweet! Really fast service, too.

BEST margaritas and queso in Nashville. Literally will ruin you from going to other Mexican restaurants. The margarita has no mix in it and is FRESH FRESH FRESH. I have had one as I type this and feel slightly tipsy. I think I'll cap at 3 or margs. Literally love this place. Get avocado fries and dip them in the queso. Thank me later.
My first cigar once purchased at this place 20 years ago. I'm back for a little while and it is a great place to just have a snack and a great cigar.

Cute little place, hubs and I stopped on a whim for some coffee one early Sunday morning- we were happy they were open. I got the Cuban espresso in a latte, it was tasty and creamy. Maybe this is just me, but if cups are filled it bothers me. It was under filled, however it was first brought to me in a "for here" mug and I wanted it to go. Hubs got a Cuban espresso. He wasn't impressed. He mentioned it was cold and flavorless. 

They had food, indoor and some outdoor seating. Baristas were friendly and helpful. Not sure if I'd go out of my way to come back, but would if I needed coffee and was near by.
They had their grand opening today at a new location (Keswick Ave.) The inside is cute and decorated nicely, the service is excellent, and the cupcakes are delicious!

Amazing. Take your friends, loved ones, or enjoy a cocktail after work by yourself. The cocktails are great, I ordered the Tequila Mockingbird. As an appetizer we had the shrimp ceviche - - the ceviche had a nice kick to it. For dinner I had the chicken penne pasta (pictured) - the pasta and chicken were cooked well  
and the flavor was delightful. Our server Sophie was wonderful, very attentive and pleasant! Do not skip this option!

Great atmosphere when you walk in the restaurant. Very friendly staff. Food is really delicious because you get to decide the spicy level for your meal. I came for dinner and I want to come back for the lunch buffet. Go here!

I would give this a 4 star except for the not so friendly blond who works there who is always extremely rude to customers. I've been told this is the owners wife/girlfriend. Our waitress on the other hand was very nice. We are locals and have been here several times but not sure we will be back anymore. You pay too much money for a nice meal to be treated rude or to listen to her treat others badly.

I am an often customer, and adore this place. But my time there was always disturbed by a young man with spiked hair. Swearing and say inappropriate things. I have two young kids, and for them to hear this while at dinner is upsetting. 

The food is fabulous. (Sushi to die) And the rest of the staff are pleasant.

Absolutely breathtaking.  It felt as though we were transported to Scotland. The landscaping and architecture are well preserved. The town surrounding the museum is quaint with boutique style shops and a strong New England air. The museum itself is tucked away amidst other well preserved historical landmarks.

I ordered a meatloaf from Boca using Uber eats. It cost $18.00. It was a tiny slice of meatloaf (the end piece) that was cold! I could have ate 10 of them and not been full!  It was extremely insulting that they would assume a customer would be happy with that! The service there is awful and the food is crazy over priced! Definitely do not order food from Boca using Uber eats! They have a shitty staff and the portion size is like I said insulting! Never again!

A GOOD italian restaurant!    Fresh bread, with oil, parmesan cheese, pepper....yum!   The calamari was tender and tasty.   The chicken and mushroom lassagna was delicious.  It was all so very good!   The atmosphere was cozy and shadowed.   I've been here before and I will be here again!

I ordered lunch delivery from this store today at 11:20am. I didn't receive the delivery until 1:10pm. I ordered a cup of french onion soup ( one of my favorites ) and an italian sub. With the delivery fee and 10% tip, it cost $23 for this average-sized lunch.

When the delivery ( finally ) got here, the soup was cold to the touch, but the container was soaked almost all the way through, and felt like it was about to fall apart. There were no croutons or cheese, so I'm not sure how this is french onion soup. The sandwich bread was extremely hard, almost rock-like. The dressing and mayo was a tiny dot in the middle of the sandwich, but there was almost 1/4 pound of arugala on the sandwich. Seriously, after picking some of it off, I had enough to make a small salad out of it. To top it all off, I found multiple long ( what I hope were ) head-hairs INSIDE the sandwich while taking it apart. I immediately threw it away. This was easily the worst sandwich I've ever had in my life.

With each of these items, I had ordered a piece of French Baguette. When it got here, these pieces of bread felt like they were at least a week old. I took one bite out of it and almost chipped a tooth. I threw this out also.

So, after paying $23 and waiting almost 2 hours for the food, I ended up throwing it all away because it was either cold, hard as a rock, or found disgusting contents in it. I'm seriously tempted to call the Better Business Bureau and the Health Department to investigate this store.

Understandably, I will never order from this store again, and based on the experience I don't know if I'll ever go to another Panera again.
This is an awesome place for sushi. I've been here a many times for lunch. Their lunch specials are a real bargain.  Great sushi at a great price .

This place is great! I work on the south side of town and was excited to hear about a new place to eat. I ordered food on Friday and will be ordering from them again. I got the chicken ramen and it's delicious!!! The chicken was tender, the veggies were delicious and the noodles and broth were perfect. I appreciated that the broth was packaged separately from the rest of the stuff, keeping everything fresh.

Great sushi. A little pricey, but good. They don't have enough seating when it gets busy. Tuesdays from 4-7PM They offer 40% off all sushi rolls for dine in. It's BYOB! The pickled cucumbers that come with the meal are amazing. I don't like the metal chopsticks so I always ask for the wooden to go ones. They also have wooden slick ones, but I can't grip my sushi with it! So...to-go chopsticks for me. There is nothing on this menu that I don't like and I have tried almost EVERYTHING! The fried tofu (I usually hate tofu) is amazing! 
The place is owned by Russians and it's mostly russian speaking staff, though most of the sushi chefs are spanish speakers. They are very nice, though some times the service can be slow, especially if you are sitting at the sushi bar (discovered this on my last visit)
All and all though, this place is great. The sushi is really yummy and I can't resist the dine in deal on Tuesdays.

My wife and I tried this place on Saturday afternoon October 21st. Disappointed to say the least. Young lady who checked us in and took our order was super pleasant but beyond that this place has nothing going for it. Not a lot of options. Very overpriced. They can't even cook a decent hamburger for Pete's sakes. I asked for medium rare and it came out medium well, it was dry and for the patties they were barely bigger than a silver dollar $11 for two patties. They ran out of their   revered avocado fries when I got my order. I got the last order at 1:00 in the afternoon. Ridiculous. Here's a suggestion... Instead of putting 4 slices of avocado on the 2 little Arepas sandwich plate, put 2 and save the other 2 to put towards the avo fries. 
The wait to get our food was also unnecessarily long. The place was maybe 20% full, yet it took us a half hour for 2 small chicken Arepas and a freaking cheeseburger. The final indignity was when we were leaving, we wanted to try the hyped vegan I've cream. They were out. 2 on a Saturday afternoon with no one in the place. 
First visit, last visit. Far better options in Germantown.
Hands down one of the best dinners I've had in a while! The service was perfection, pretty much t be expected when I'm at a Starr restaurant. Food was amazing... and a fun, eclectic wine list.
Great tour it's an audio so the kids really e joyed it and it kept them excited.There is a lot of history here.
I was introduced to  frozen yogurt. I never like frozen yogurt but this is great. The store is very clean and the Staff is very polite.

The first time I came here to sell clothes they took everything and gave me 60$. However, things have changed and now when I come to sell clothes they try and offer me 4$ for two pieces out of the 40 things I bring. I always bring in the clothes they are asking for (winter clothes, summer clothes etc...) They are always name brand too. The most recent visit I came in with a huge bag of clothes. I waited OVER an hour for them to give me 4$ for two shirts... The people in front of me was given 135$ for a ton of winter clothes... I didn't understand because they were asking for summer clothes and not winter. I swear I'm never going back. The 4$ I received didn't even cover the gas it took to drive there. I can't believe they wouldn't take my clothes, but they'll take extremely worn Target clothes...

This isn't just good vegan food--it's good food. Period.
(And I'm not a vegetarian)

Ordered the V-steak, mac n cheez, and the lasagna.  Surprisingly, our favorite was the mac n cheez--it had so much more flavor than even your typical dairy mac n cheez.  Al dente pasta and creamy, flavorful sauce.  The other items were also very good, but I notice no one's talking up the mac n cheez on Yelp!

The place is cool and chill with a funky eclectic vibe and beats.  I loved the bookshelf situated near the window seating--perfect for while waiting for your food.  
The staff was also very friendly.

I'll be back.
Had the best massage of my life there! My favorite resort and spa in SB. Plus they are dog friendly which we loved when traveling with our pooch.

Whitney and Josh were such a pleasure to work with! Whitney answered all of my (what seemed list endless) questions without delay. They organized everything from Celebration's Party rentals, which took one less thing off my plate. Now- on to the important stuff- the food!!! We traveled from Santa Cruz, CA one night to do our tasting in their kitchen in Sparks, NV and drove back right after. And it was worth it!!  Their food in incredible, and was the perfect "experience" we were looking for for our wedding. I literally was having dreams about their pasta and chicken and waffles appetizer. I mentioned to Whitney that I just wanted to be sure my husband and I were able to actually eat some of the apps (since we would be taking pictures during cocktail hour). She personally delivered them and guarded off our bridal party who had already been served their own special plate that was brought down. Thank you both for everything! We are already trying to plan our next trip to visit, even if it's just for dinner.

I had gone into the urgent care because I was sick now, remind you I am 65-year-old retiree from California. I came here to take care of symptoms of frequent laryngitis. This nimrod doctor walked in to the examination room and said it was illegal for them to treat laryngitis. Since when is it illegal to treat anybody for any symptoms they complain about. later I went to my PCP and was treated with what now had become bronchitis because they choose not to treat me. I should have kept my damn co-pay and choose not to pay them.  Do not come to this urgent care bunch of Morons.

This restaurant is our FAVORITE breakfast place. The food is consistent and delicious, the atmosphere is relaxing and super clean and the service is excellent. Our server Michelle, on our last visit, not only remembered us, but also remembered our last conversation and meals ordered! That extra touch made us feel like we were eating with friends.

This is about Walgreens, not this particular retail location. I've learned that before going to a pharmacy I MUST call to find out if they have enough to fill my two different prescriptions for controlled medications. It seems as if Congress decides what availability of controlled medications will be. therefor, pharmacies may not have enough to fill prescriptions.  When I the phone number for a specific Walgreens the call is not handled there, but rather a central location. I ask to check stock for me, have been told this certain store has enough, go to the location and am told they do NOT have enough!! Apparently, whomever answers the call does not bother to check with the location(and makes up an answer?). AND, one of the pharmacists at this location INSISTS that information cannot be given over the phone!!

Stopped in to have lunch at Jaker's and it was not a memorable experience.  Our waitress was excellent, but the flies buzzing around our heads was annoying.  The waffle fries were very greasy and limp.  The croutons on my salad were also greasy and very bland.  The seafood linguine was good as were the finger steaks.  But the atmosphere of flies and greasy food makes Jaker's fall off the list of our local places to go.

We enjoyed our stay. Clean, quiet, comfy rooms and very nice staff. Felt a little spoiled having people bring our luggage up to our room. Room was up to date and well decorated. It was a bargain too. The casino itself was pretty small but the onsite restaurant was pretty good. I would stay here again.
Fabulous ! Don't miss it .... Seriously great food and service outstanding and the fit out really cool.

We have tried over 20 pizza parlors since moving to Reno and this is the worst pizza we have had. The crust was burned, cooked too fast, the sauce was more spicy Mexican than italian ( I have been to Italy), it was luke warm at best, beer was expensive fot 3/4 of a glass and a $10 salad at a pizza place is ridiculous.

My wife and I agreed as we left, never again for this place!!!!

What a find in Treasure Island! Great food, friendly staff, and a fun atmosphere. We went here for lunch on a Wednesday & ordered the roast beef po boy with rice and beans - it was delicious! Definitely eat at Shrimpy's if you're in the area!

So I went there with my brother for lunch and we actually had a nice time we closed out our tab everything was good he had another beer so I paid for the beer they put a hold on my credit card so for a five dollar beer cost me $25. I called and they said oh it could take up to 48 hours to clear. Shouldn't someone tell me that before and I would have just paid cash. What if I could only afford that beer and all my bills were coming out and I would've went to the negative? That didn't happen, I'm just saying someone should say hey we're gonna put a hold on your credit card absolutely not cool

Ordered the Blueberry Bacon Burger.  It tasted amazing. The fries were good and salted perfectly.  The burger itself was fantastic.  And filling.  I recommend coming here for real for lunch or something.  Great atmosphere and nice servers.  Seems like they have a buffet on sundays as well.  Great place, great burger. Cool t shirts  for $15

I went through this office to have my knee evaluated and they sent me to get an MRI, the results weren't fully conclusive because the swelling was too severe (per the radiologist), but they had a rough idea what was going on. My report says possible tears to my Medial Meniscus and my ACL. 

So it's been 3 weeks and my swelling has gone down, so I figure I can call them and have them reschedule me an MRI since the swelling has lessened and the previous results weren't fully
conclusive.

NOW they are telling me that the results before were FULLY conclusive and that I definitely have a tear in both my ACL and Meniscus. 
So with that said they said I have to come back in and get re-evaluated again just for them to schedule me an MRI. (They just want my money).

My question is... how are they looking at the SAME MRI report as before and coming up with different results and how are they telling me that the radiologists opinion of being inconclusive is wrong?

Severely disappointed....

Visited this location around 2:30 in the afternoon. This is important for my review because they weren't very busy being between lunch and dinner. The floors were in horrible condition. Trash and food all over the restaurant. The hostess cleaned off a table but wiping the food crumbs to the floor then sat a customer at that table without sweeping the floor. The customer pointed it out and then they swept but just by that table. The restroom was the same way with paper towels all over the floor. My drink came out in a dirty glass with food stuck to the outside of the glass. 

I did give them 2 stars only because the food was pretty good and was warm when it came out. The draw back about the food was that two sandwiches fries and two sodas totaled $33.00 with tax. Too much for sandwiches. 

Over priced food, dirty store. Not worth going back.

Wrote this review then saw the other reviews. It looks like a recent history of this location being dirty. I'd be afraid of how dirty the kitchen is. Apparently upper management does nothing about this store.

We got a cheesesteak with their cheddar cheese sauce, fried onions, mushrooms, roasted peppers and Voodoo sauce. We took our Cheesesteak outside to eat at the tables on closed Gay St. We asked the nice young man from Kildare's if we could order a drink to eat with our Buddy's cheesesteak. He said absolutely but we had to sit at their tables. Our cheesesteak came on a nice seeded roll. I think the wonderfulness of everything else may have hid some deficiencies in the roll. Good amount of meat that did not get lost in the other very powerful flavors. I was afraid this Cheesesteak was going to be too wet with the cheese sauce, roasted peppers and Voodoo sauce. It was not. Everything played together wonderfully. There were a lot of strong flavors on this Cheesesteak that could have conflicted with each other but they were mixed together wonderfully and had a very good balance. I wish I remembered the name of my drink from Kildare's cuz it was pretty darn tasty. Between the nice young woman behind the counter at Buddy's and our waiter from Kildare's we met 2 very helpful and hard-working young people

Our group had reservations, but they wanted to spread us out at opposite ends of the diner.

I had to move a dirty table myself in order to eat with companions.  It took a long time for the table to be cleaned, but they never took away the old flatware or napkins.  I also had to find the salt and pepper, and chairs for us.

I ordered the biscuits and gravy which took a very long time to come, and they were not even warm, let alone hot.  They likely sat somewhere for quite a long time before being served piecemeal style.  There was no sausage in this canned gravy, only tiny bits of mystery meat.  I'm from Virginia, and I know how to cook real Southern sausage gravy.

So, the food was rather bad and the waitstaff was as slow and inefficient as they could be.  But, they were polite.  We will not be returning here.

I read the reviews on Soak and decided to pay them a visit. I got 2 pink drinks which I really enjoyed. The pedicure was so relaxing. The ambience is great and they give attention to their clients. I'll definitely be going to Soak every time am in Reno!!
Favorite nail salon, hands down!! Everyone here is so nice and accommodating. I always leave happy with the service I receive   The only salon I'll go to :)

I don't know what to say about this venue, except that I had one of the scariest experiences of my whole life here. I missed the play, "The Bone House," by Marry Chan at The Fringe, but luckily they were running it again on Halloween at the Transalta Arts Barns. The Play was set up in a room with a small stage. My friend and I had heard this play was so scary that people would start crying and have to leave. We knew not to sit in the aisles, so we sat near the back. 

The play finally started, and I was listening to the guy speaking at the front. He was telling us about a serial killer that he was tracking. All of the sudden, I saw something in the corner of my eye. I turned my head and saw a guy dressed up in a the "Scream" outfit holding a giant knife. He was staring right at me, and I was so freaked out. He ended up not being the serial killer, but rather the tracker's crazy assistant. After that happened, my friend and I were split up, and I was seated in an aisle seat.

The tracker continued to tell us stories about the gruesome serial killer killings. Eventually, he led us to believe that the killer was right here in Edmonton. He had all of us stare at a negative image of the killer's face, then the lights went, and we could see a reflection of the face. The lights came back on, but later they went out again, and I couldn't see a thing.  The side door opened, and the killer walked in. He walked up and down the aisles. Although I couldn't see at all, I could feel the wind from his cape and at one point, he touch my head. I almost had a heart attack. Even though I knew this was a play, I literally was so afraid that I though I was going to die. The killer then tore out the tracker's heart and squeezed it. It was funny, because they splashed water all over everyone to make them think it was blood. That eased my tension a little bit. 

So, I'd have to say that the Transalta puts on some good plays, and I would definitely check another one out here sometime.
Just go! So. Freaking. Good!  And be sure to eat the white bread at the bottom! Seriously, what did they do to plain white bread to make it so good!

Checked out this brewery while in town last night, the two employees behind the bar were friendly and the beer was decent. It was around 4:45 on a Saturday and there were plenty of seats at the bar. Overall a real cool atmosphere and we'll stop in again sometime.

TLDR: Get your ass to Burrito Feliz and order birria tacos. Get  2x whatever you think you'll probably want to eat. You're Welcome.


Long form:

If you're an average taco consumer, you know there are two kinds. There are the kind you've seen at TGI Fridays and Chilis and, perhaps, Taco Bell and you know all these suck and deserve a different name. The second type is usually found at a taco truck or a taqueria, which isn't authentic unless there are colorful, paper, birthday party-esque decorations, and some type of mariachi-esque sounding music in the background. These tacos are awesome, abundant in Philly, and easily searchable.

If you've not come across quality versions of these, here are ways to ensure you're getting the real deal:

if they come wrapped in two (2) corn tortillas, that's a good sign

if they are small enough that you could eat at least 4, that's a good sign

if they come garnished with ONLY cilantro, onion, and perhaps little radishes, that's a good sign

if you're offered a mild green salsa and an asshole burning red salsa, that's a good sign

if you're speaking into a truck, that's a good sign

if anything "hip" is offered, thai curry, soy protein, korean gojujang, beyond meat, cauliflower, etc. GET THE FUCK OUT OF THERE!

if nobody appears to be Mexican at all, GET THE FUCK OUT OF THERE!

if you're offered ketchup at any time in the meal, GET THE FUCK OUT OF THERE!

All of this said, there are a few other types of tacos worth trying before you die. Birria tacos are on that list, and, somewhat recently, easily accessible in Philadelphia!!  These have been around forever at South Philly Barbacoa and she is the OG. You MUST check them out if you have not already.

Birria, which usually translates to "goat" in English, refers to shredded and braised meat (typically beef, at least in my Philly taco experience). These tacos are also often fried (tortilla and all) in the birria meat's oil, so the resulting taco is crispy, flavorful (even the tortilla itself), and insanely delicious. Anyone who's gotten drunk and made the regrettable decision of visiting a taco bell knows a crunchy taco just crumbles (while tasing like old cardboard) turning a shitty taco into a taco salad. This is NOT the case with these tacos- they crunch in all the right ways, leaving the consumer satisfied, majestified, and enthralled.

As if this all weren't enough, each order of birria tacos also comes with a dipping cup of the braising broth (consome) to dip the tacos! And... (yes, there's more!)... the tacos come w/ melted cheese, fiery asshole burning salsa, and a bunch of napkins!

I am a taco expert, but not a tech expert, so I was surprised to initially find these tacos on Instagram. My foody wife informed me that IG is now a place to find food- I didn't know that before!

Anyway, we live on 7th and South and decided to walk to Burrito Feliz for these Birria tacos. If it's not evident by my 3000 word post, the tacos were phenomenal and we will be back! They were phenomenally better than Barbacoa's (sorry barbacoa). On the walk down, we passed two other trucks (one which had a 2 hour-ish line) and at least two restaurants offering birria. I'm sure those are worth a taste as well, but I cannot imagine them being any better than Burrito Feliz. 

BTW- we got the churros (great but not slap ya mama great, and the shrimp tacos (good, but we should have just gotten more birria instead!!!).

I would visit Nashville again solely to eat at Bar Taco. I have been following them on instagram for years and finally made it to Nashville and of course just HAD to try it! The wait really wasn't that bad- especially since we went on Cinco- de Mayo. Had a delicious (but not cheap) margarita and got to play with the photo boot, then we were seated and first devoured their guac- please please treat yourself and get it. I loved the huge round chips, they were so fun & tasty! All of the tacos are what I would say as mini so I got to try 4 different ones. They were all extremely flavorful and delicious but the one that I still dream about is the pork belly taco!!! Apparently it is their most popular one and I can happily agree with them on that. We passed on desert for once, which I was not upset about because I had come to fill my belly with tacos, so gosh darn it, I did. And so should you!
My 10 year old says that "coming to Chaucer's makes me feel excited and inspired!"  Excellent selection of books, knowledgeable employees, and everything for everyone from child to senior.

Hard pass. 

It was chaos from the beginning to the end. Each leg of my journey was delayed, all due to airline controlled issues. I understand delays happen, but the lack of communication is what irritated me the most. 

On top of the non-existent communication, when I actually went to seek out the reasons for the delay, no aspect of an apology was uttered. I don't think that is too much to expect. Air travel nowadays is a complete headache, which I'm sure all the employees are aware, so for no one to be remotely sympathetic is just bad form. 

Overall just terrible customer service leaving everything to be desired. I guess that's why it's a budget airline. Get what you pay for I guess....common courtesy is going to cost extra.

Best tamales in all the land! Found this place by accident. Sooo good! Huge tamales! I have yet to find tamales that are as good as these, and I've been searching! They have a great selection of tamales (even a bunch of vegetarian ones!), tacos and other dishes. They do have a limited supply though. They make bulk batches for the day in the morning and once they're out that's it. Go early if you're looking for specific tamales. I wish they were outside of Indianapolis. Any plans on opening in Seattle? I wish!
This place is awesome!   The burrito de la roqueta and the Enchiladas Rancheros are my favorites.  I highly recommend.
Very comfy rooms. But wish they would stock the mini bar on a regular basis

I always get the taco's when I visit Tequila's.  I tried their habanero salsa on a whim one late night.  Sweet baby Jesus, that salsa means business.  Great flavor but have 2 to 3 of their ice cold beers on standby to extinguish the flames.

Whoo! We called them and asked for the pizza. It was reasonably priced. The workers were kind, and it was delivered about 10 minutes faster than they said it would be. It's the perfect late night- too tired to get out of bed pizza!

I wonder how long that display muffuletta's been sitting there under that glass case? It still looks pristine and like it was made that same morning of the day I look at it. Maybe it IS made freshly every morning for display. Ah, the wonders of Reading Terminal Market.

Anyway, that glorious muffuletta was the one thing I wanted from Beck's... although the rest of the menu looks good, too, it'll have to wait until another RTM adventure. It was the first muffuletta I'd ever had, and it did not disappoint. OK, I had to smash it down a little to get it to fit inside my mouth... but for all that filling, it sure was worth it. Though the sandwich was packed with like three different types of cured meat, that salty, savory olive salad is what really shined for me, especially against the sharp provolone. And the soft, slightly crispy roll that it's served on... I just wanted to roll myself up in it and take a nap. Next time, I'll have to try some of their gator dishes.

I have gone here for the past three years, and the food continues to get worse. This summer I have tried two different chicken entrees that were both disgusting. I would recommend eating dinner at a nice restaurant then going to the movies, not eating a terrible meal while watching a movie. And although the food is horrible, the service is good!

PLEASE DO NOT LIVE HERE. I can't be more serious when I say this is one of the WORST places to live. We have experienced maintenance coming in and out of our apartment whenever they please at any given day or time without notice. We had a huge leak in our bathroom ceiling that was unresolved for days. When it finally did get fixed, the workers had finished the job and left. However, thirty minutes after they left, they walked right into my apartment without knocking to "check the final status" of the ceiling. We had a radon tester use the office's key to gain access to our apartment, without having the courtesy to knock, after business hours. We addressed this to the leasing office and they basically told us there's nothing they are doing about it. We recently have been out of hot water for three days now. The staff nor maintenance have notified us or our building of the problem as well as given us an estimated timely fix. Our newest problem is standing water in our heating vents. It's January, we have been out of heat and have had to find a new place to stay until fixed. We called emergency maintenance at 1:30am when it happened, they didn't respond until 8:30am. Therefore, we slept overnight with no heat while no one had a care in the world. And once again, we have no idea when this issue should be resolved. I'm absolutely disgusted by the way Veridian Castleton "handles" problems. Will definitely not be renewing our lease.

Meh, I don't run on Dunkin... coffees are not that great, too much cream and I don't get why they don't just have simple syrup (no I don't want granulated sugar in my iced coffee, it doesn't melt!)

Not a fan of cakey donuts, so Krispe Kreme here I come!

Our first time having Duck Donuts was about 8 months ago in Myrtle Beach, SC and we houses a half dozen in 5 minutes.

This experience wasn't quite like our first due to them having "batter issues" which caused a delay of 20+ minutes for a 6 pack.

The donuts are a tad smaller than the traditional donuts and are pretty dense. Cake like. Create your own or order one of their specialty donuts. Can't go wrong.

So I'm going to go ahead and say that this just might be my absolute favorite dinner spot in Center City. I've heard you have to make reservations WAY ahead of time here, but my friend and I got lucky I guess because we made Saturday evening reservations only 2 days beforehand... I guess there must have been a cancelation. 

The food is AMAZING!!!! Every single bite of all the dishes I ordered were like chewing on a piece of heaven (If we assume heaven tastes wonderful). Also, I am vegetarian, and I felt like I had so many options to choose from - I went with the spaghetti squash salad and the apple chutney toast as an app. My friend (who love meat and everything related) got lamb and he said it was probably the best lamb he's had. The drinks were pretty good too... although be careful what you order, some of the drinks are almost too flavorful.

The service was absolutely incredible. As a first timer at Vernick, all our questions were answered and all the food was explained perfectly. The manager came up to us at one point and had a friendly little chat with us - it was a great experience overall.

This bar is so me. I love places like this. Dark, moody, low-lighting, but in a classy way. Because this place is for sure classy. Velvet seats, deep pulsing music (but not too loud, you can easily speak to your drinking companions). Incredibly attentive wait staff. You just feel fancy as soon as you walk in the door.

We visited on Memorial Day, for a drink between our appetizers at V-Street and our main dinner at Vedge. It was only about 6pm, but the place was already crowded, no seats left at the bar. Fortunately there is spill-over bar seating in the foyer, and we pulled some tables together to accommodate our group. I ordered the Witches of East, which is a truly beguiling cocktail that I loved. My sister and her boyfriend both ordered the Charlie's Punch of the day, which tasted a little bit like sangria and after just one sip I also loved that. Our table shared a bowl of their Warm Spiced Peanuts, which were definitely the best bar peanuts I've ever tasted. Totally addicting, you won't be able to not finish a bowl. 

I haven't been to a lot of bars in Philadelphia, but this has got to be one of the coolest bars in Philadelphia.

I visit this Subway quite often as I live very close.  A typical Subway with all the trimmings.  The staff is attentive and service is always fast and great.  I have never had any issues at this location.  Only downside is that I wish the drive through was still working, but going in gives me a chance to interact with the staff in person.

Brandon is the king of latte art. Totally made my morning! They have Saratoga spring water here... Always a good choice. Open at 6:30am on weekdays. Nice and quiet atmosphere. You can get your coffee to go or they have ceramic mugs if you want to stick around. Definitely better than Starbucks!

Limited outdoor seating due to Covid restrictions.  Tables are set up on the sidewalk. The restaurant asks you for your contact information just in case they need to do contact tracing and I wondered if this is for my safety or theirs??
I called at 4:50 pm to see if I could get a reservation.  After a few minutes of checking and double checking, I was told that if we could come at 5 pm, and could be gone by 6:30, we could have a table. The husband and I walked down and were there by 5:10 pm. There's only one other table occupied. Not sure where the dinner rush is, but I'll play the game. We are seated, we wait a few, someone comes to take our drink order. Iced-tea, and ginger ale.  Menus show up, we go through the specials drill. I notice that there is one item on the menu with an asterisk, and no explanation as to what that means.  The menu items was steak, and a poblano pepper.  The wait person says the asterisk is to denote that the item is spicy. So noted. My husband order the 6 oz steak*. I have salad with mixed seafood (totally noom approved). Dinner arrives. Steak is teensy, there is a pepper stuffed with cheese kinda like a chile relleno and sme mushroms. My salad is mixed greens and mixed seafood.  Both are exactly as billed.  My seafood is salmon, bay scallops, and three shrimp.  The tomato on the salad was delicious. The husband said the steak* was great. The husband and I split a chocolate pecan tart. It was fine, but nothing special.
Here's what I didn't like and why two-stars: We are asked to wear masks at the table unless eating. Except that the local diners sitting next to us did not.  The wait staff were complaining to the local customers about how hard it was making a quick transition to outside dining only.  I'm not here to listen to your complaints. Suck it up like the rest of us. Overall service was a solid meh, and best described as "I wish I didn't have to work tonight because Covid is scary."  And the prices! $36 for a 6 oz and a stuffed pasilla. $32 is more like it. My salad, tasty and filling but should be $18, not $21. 
Bottom line:  Would I eat here again? No.

We were a party of four who ordered four entree meals, along with several glasses of wine. 
When it was time for dessert we mentioned to our server that we were celebrating a birthday. We were charged $12.00 for a single slice of cake that should have been on the house. Most quality & reputable restaurants bring a piece (slice) of cake for the birthday person w/o being charged. When we received our bill we brought the charge to the attention of the manager. John Kulikowski manager was rude and uncooperative. We were astonished that a local establishment would choose to lose business & their reputation over a $12.00 celebratory slice of cake! We are giving one star for very poor management. 
We are local residents who will not be returning!
Chicken pho is awesome.  Great location and the staff is very helpful.  They made sure we knew how to get our meal tasting good and did it!
Easily the worst bartenders in the city. Very slow and rude which is also rare in New Orleans. Basically go anywhere else on Frenchmen and you'll have a better time.

I would highly recommend Antique Limousine for your wedding transportation needs.  Jack was wonderful to work with!   He responds timely, clearly explains the vehicles and pricing they have available, and ensures the vehicles are where you need them to be and on time.  The vehicles were clean and nice looking for wedding photos and transportation. We rented a sprinter van to get our groomsmen from a downtown hotel to the church and we also rented the white trolley to transport the bridesmaids to the church and then the wedding party to the reception (after the wedding).  The drivers of the vehicles were polite,friendly, and accommodating to our party.  

We liked that the white trolley was clean and free of advertising for some of the wedding pictures.  

Jack and his team did a GREAT job for us!!!
Best place to have a cold one and rest your feet. Great for people watching. Bartenders are super sweet.

Good food at an inexpensive price. My boyfriend and I saw this place as we were driving, and I'm glad we stopped. For about $9, I got fried rice, duck, hot and sour soup, crab rangoon AND an egg roll. Plus, we ordered this as take out, and the waiting time was less than 10 minutes. Will come here again!

By far my favorite Mexican restaurant in the city. The blood orange margaritas are the best margaritas I've ever had. I've only had the "original" guacamole and it was amazing. The nacho mama nachos were amazing. I love how their nachos are laid flat instead of piled on top (every nacho has topping on it)!! For dessert I always have the churros with the chocolate dipping sauce so yummy!

To say we were disappointed is an understatement. My husband and I were so excited to see what looked like a nice, quality, non-chain restaurant come to Mt. Juliet. We loved the exterior and were hopeful this quality and attention to detail would translate into a fantastic meal. Sadly, it did not. Although we had a reservation they clearly did not have a table for us, and asked if we'd be willing to sit outside. It was pleasant out so this didn't seem like a big deal, and with the noise level inside it was a welcome escape. Our waiter did a good job, greeted us promptly, and gave a good description of that night's specials. He also apologized a number of times, saying they'd had a big event that day and were still trying to catch up. For our starter we ordered the meat and cheese plate that from the description sounded delicious, and like it incorporated local ingredients. The presentation was nice, with the items set upon a sawed-log type platter. We weren't given appetizer plates even though we asked, and it was a little difficult to maneuver all the ingredients without one. The cheese came as a stack of deli-type triangles (maybe provolone?) but were dried out and tasted like they'd been sitting uncovered in the refrigerator. The meat was a pretty good assortment of prosciutto, speck, etc. but there was way too much of it for the measly few crostini we were given. They also included two hunks of zucchini bread which really did not blend well with the savory flavors of the board. We asked for more crostini and were brought three of the thinnest, most over-crisp little pieces. We basically sent the bulk of the board back, and did verbalize our concerns with the waiter who apologized again. For dinner I ordered the salmon special, served over shrimp etouffee and my husband ordered the ribeye, which they were out of. He subbed the tenderloin with the same sides as the ribeye. Although his steak was very tender and obviously high quality, it had absolutely zero seasoning. Flavorless. What a waste of a good piece of meat. He sucked it up and ate it anyway. I would have sent it back. As for my meal, it was delicious. At least the three bites I could tolerate. Where my husband's meal completely lacked salt, whoever cooked mine was extremely heavy handed. And I like salt, but I just could not eat it it was so unpleasant. All told we spent more than $100 for one of the worst meals we've had in a long time. They've only been open 6 weeks and maybe need a little more time to work out the kinks. As for us, we won't be going back.

I'm driving home, and decided to order from this restaurant, I called ahead and ordered a large beef fried rice and large chicken lo mein, the person told me the price and it'll be 40 minutes. Let's be for real, 40 minutes for two simple items. So I arrive at the restaurant, and there is a long line and customers were not happy with their service. I waited and extra 15-20 mins and decided to walk out.  They had customers complain, and all they could say is that they are busy.  I'm sorry, if you own a restaurant, and cannot keep up with high demands, then you shouldn't be running it.  I didn't even bother trying the food after a long wait.  One customer said the food is good, but their service is lousy.  They responded with they are sorry, but the aren't really busy.  I definitely would not recommend this place, went to Panda instead, I ordered more food and it was done in 10 mins.
really cute little cafe, the service and outdoor seating was lovely. Got the cinnamon raisin bagel and an iced tea, couldnt be happier .

Shakespeare & Co is a great addition to the Rittenhouse area - it's cute and cozy and has a nicely curated selection of books. 

When you first walk in, there is a cafe area that serves coffee, baked goods, and premade sandwiches. 

Just beyond that, they have the espresso printing machine, where you can print books right there! Apparently, you can print your own work or other books that they don't have in store. I haven't used it but it's an interesting idea!

The rest of the ground floor and the upper mezzanine are filled with books. Given the small space, I thought there was a pretty good selection, especially for trendier books. 

There's some seating in the cafe area and in the upper level, but I've never seen an empty seat, even during what I thought would be a less busy time (10 am on a Tuesday).

This is a really good coffee shop that was recommended by our AirBnB host. It's really close to downtown Tucson and is located in a really nice district. Presta Coffee Roasters curates a nice selection of roasts and their iced lattes are perfectly made. They have a nice airy feel, complete with hanging plants and open glass windows. Definitely would recommend to any new or experienced coffee drinkers.
Very nice location downtown. Very friendly and helpful staff, and the food is superb! The Ceasar salad is the best I've ever had.

Setting an appointment was easy, everything else was bad. They are horribly understaffed, so prepare for an ordeal even if you have an appointment. Or go to labcorp across the street- way more painless.

I ordered from here twice while on vacation. Both orders were delivered before expected time. Second order was missing one of the two desserts ordered but when I called back they handled it great. Offered to refund or simply come back and deliver a slice of cake. Tacos sencillos and especiales were both delicious.

Twice I have gone here to buy cars and twice it took this place EIGHT HOURS to complete the sale. The staff is friendly but the dealership is incredibly slow. Compare to two recent car purchases of friends which took only 3 to 4 hours at other dealerships. They took our trade ins to the back very early in the process so we were stuck in this place with no food and even their cafe vending area closed. It was an endurance trial both times. At the end I wasnt especially impressed with the deal I got particularly on our purchases this weekend either though we lovd the cars we bought. Would not recommend.
Outstanding food and service, I recommend the shrimp, pork belly and lemon grass Banh mi

Though it isn't perfect... I'd go here before BW3 any day of the week.  The wings are bigger and quite tasty... Highly recommend tweener if you like a traditional flavor.  The tater tots are awesome and drink specials pretty good.  We've also had nachos which we liked.

It's Ok, sort of a run down spot, has dedicated customers from
 Nashville Shores Amusement Park. I had the brisket chopped sandwich and was more like a pot roast. Great owners and people but the food was lacking.

Really good food, but small portions. We ordered Papas Huancaina and they just give us ONE potato. I been in several perivuan restaurant and they are pretty generous with the food. I don't question the price since the quality is very good, but please don't charge $8 for ONE potato.

Really pretty location on the beach; bartenders were very nice and appropriately engaging for as busy as they were.  Drinks well priced.  Went for two apps instead of a single meal --- tuna avocado was fine, but I wish I'd started there, because I would have rather ended on the crab fries.  Holy cow that was good.
delicious home style bbq...i love how you pick your meats and they serve it on a slab...great sides too!!!!
If you like onions than this is the place to order steamed mixed vegetable. I had 29 large pieces of onion, about 75% of the meal. I went back and was told that I ordered this meal. Yuck!

The cheesesteaks are delicious!! Best in the area and I've had them all. Get the grandma pie. It's crispy and very cheesy!  Chef salad was huge and full of quality meat. Hope this place lasts. Please patronize!

So-So Place for Rich Students: 

You can get expensive sandwiches and expensive pizza at this market, which for the most part tastes bland but still can stuff your stomach. Service is haphazard, staffed by 'alternative' people who don't care much about your experience. Doesn't have much taste but at least its something in an interesting setting.  I don't go there often as it has been overrun by yuppie students who are annoying.  I used to shop here when it was a hole in the wall.

Sat down with a party of six around 3 pm and waited 15 minutes. Had to get up and get a menu myself and told the bartender we were there and still no one came to acknowledge us. We left. The atmosphere was nice.

This place was wonderful.  First off, they take reservations and there is a valet, so they make it as convenient as possible.  I came here on a Sunday night with my fiancé and another couple. The atmosphere here is really cool. The restaurant is beautiful. The staff seemed to be very attentive.  Our server did not seem to come around much, but he was probably just very busy. The drink menu is large. The food here is fantastic. I had the Beef Tenderloin Biscuits and Andouille chowder. My fiancé had the Rib eye steak, and it was amazing.  One of the best I have ever tried. The group ordered oysters to start off, which I am not a fan of. However, they raved about them! This is definitely a place you will want to come, the price tag can be a little hefty, but it is great for a special occasion dinner in Nashville.

What a jewel! I've lived in the area for almost 4 years now and I can't believe it took me this long to go the Hungry A!! We got the smokie...egg, cheese, and hot sausage and an egg, cheese, and pepper..both on long rolls. DELICIOUS! The owners are gracious and accommodating. I loved the community table inside. Had we more time I would have eaten there. Growing up in a small town, it's nice to know that the small town community feel is still alive and well in places like this. They greet everyone by name. Absolutely wonderful.

Picture this: your favorite brewery serves cold coffee that's nitro infused.  That pretty much sums this place up. In other words it's awesome.  I had the cubano which is a cold coffee that is creamy and delicious. I got to try a delicious orange flavored coffee as well.   I prefer strong coffee so the cubano was a natural choice for me but they have other offerings that's aren't as strong.  They serve food and craft beer too.
