Absolutely LOVED my ghost and vampire tour with guide--Justin. I had been looking forward to doing a ghost tour for months and was not disappointed. Starting the tour with buy one get one hurricanes and a bathroom break half way through was a game changer for me. I thought our host did an excellent job conveying the history and folklore around each story. 

The only downfall that I could say about this tour is that it wasn't as spooky as I had hoped for, but it's hard to set the mood when everyone is out and about in the French Quarter, can't fault them for that!

I stop in here when I'm in town on business on my own. The staff are friendly and the food is good. I think they may be a test kitchen as I've has a few meals here that weren't available in other Earl's until a few months later.

These folks are still trying to figure out how to run a restaurant. Phoned in a take out order and was told it would be ready in 30 minutes. When I arrived I was told it would be another 30 minutes. When I returned again, I was told it be another 30 minutes....1.5 hours later still not ready.  I won't return again.

We've worked with Maid Cleaning Nashville for one year now. The first time they cleaned our house we remarked, "Wow! We have never seen our place so clean!!" That was after already having worked with other cleaning crews. They are always timely, thorough and easy to work with. I love that I can update schedules and change reservations through their online portal. I can't recommend them highly enough!

Headed back to my hometown for a wedding and was looking forward to trying this place out for brunch. I had already checked out the menu online and planned on getting the biscuits and gravy. However, when I got there I seen that they had a tofu scramble, chorizo, with pico de gallo, served with two tortilla's and home fries.... needless to say I got that! I was NOT disappointed! This was DELISH! I am looking forward to going back and trying other things on the menu!
Absolutely delicious! Not only is the French toast a staple and a must but the service was superb. My waiter, Jake, was very nice and hospitable.

The beer was great.....the food not so much. We stopped here on a Saturday afternoon and we were seated immediately. Huge European and craft beer selection. Our server recommended the Oxbow Loretta and Farmhouse Pale Ale among others. Both were very good. We both opted for the mussels as we had many recommendations to do so. We ordered Ghent and Red Light. The server promptly brought them out and they were a large portion. Neither broth had ANY flavor at all. They tasted pretty much like mussels cooked almost in water. Reading all the reviews about the mussels , this was a HUGE disappointment. I have a feeling that maybe the cooking staff was having a bad day perhaps, because there is no way that the mussels that WE received are the same mussels that have gained favorable reviews. I will say that the restaurant was busy and we received great service. I hate to leave a bad review on a place that is probably normally very good , but we did not get that experience unfortunately.

STAY AWAY, FOUND RUBBER GLOVE IN FOOD!!!!!!

The food was good, good price. Nice drinks, however, when I brought my foood back with a latex glove in it (finger part), they tried to cover it up and acted funny when I wanted my money back. Whne I called , the person I spoke to already told me they used those gloves in the kitchen. 

When I got there I told the manager what happened and he turned to the workers and said, in Spanish "Who told her we had these gloves? Why did you do that? Do you know who had the gloves on today? Do Not say Anything else!"  

He turned to me and started to tell me in English That theyy didnt use those gloves and If I was sure it wasnt mine. I told him IN SPANISH (He didnt know I spoke the language,

"I am staying in a hotel, where would I get a glove. I heard what you said to the workers. Give me my money back before I call the Health Department, the News staion AND the Newspapers. Next time do not think that all Peopel who are not Hispanic do not speak the language. You never know!"

The whole staff jaws dropped when they heard me saying this is spanish and thier little cover-up was ruined. 

BTW....I called the health department anyway. If they tried to cover this up, I am sure their practices are very nasty in the back.

My friend and I came here for ice cream and wine after riding The Wheel. It is such a cute place, our waitress was so friendly and fun, and even though we didnt eat, the food looked great! Such a fun experience, I'll definitely be back!
Liars! I called and they told me no wait.  We were there in 10 minutes and there was a 30 minute wait. Will never eat here again! Liars!

I have to say that the overall food was quite good. My problem lies with the fact that I inquired and about the sauces and their cost I was told by the server to ignore that they hadn't got around to correcting in on the menu and that they don't charge until the 5th one. So I ordered my burger 1/3 pound hormone  free antibiotic-free yada yada yada burger. I add brie, bacon, onion confit and mushrooms. And I get the roasted garlic aiol house mustard and sweet sriracha. The burger was spot on juicy and flavorful the mushrooms and brie worked great as did the sauces. My problem lies with the fact that I was charged for 2 of the 3 sauces after being told that sauces aren't charged until the 5th one. If your waitstaff tells customers something this should be the case. I will come back but I will also note this event at the time and will dissect my bill when it comes.
We came here specifically to try the orange zest sweet roll and it did not disappoint. So good!

There's nothing "ohana" about this place. I don't think they know what Ohana means. Well, at least they spent $$$ on decorating the place since the food and service was lacking. The gyoyza was small. The miso soup lacked flavor. The rice in the sushi wasn't the best and definitely not sushi rice. The service was horrible. I asked for a Sapporo and she looked confused. We ordered the sushi combo for two that included "chef's special" but no one could tell us what that is. There are several chef's special listed in the menu but the combo comes with the cheapest one. We really wanted to try the dessert but didn't want to take the chance.
We did the AYCE sushi for lunch.  You cannot beat the price any where else.  The sushi was fantastic and the service was awesome.  I highly recommend.

This is a fun place to sit down and eat tons of calories especially on a weekend night. The music is on, the staff is friendly, and the food is good. I'll stop just shy of saying the food is great because I was a bit sad over the jambalaya - it was really mild, and had very little sausage flavor. It lacked the umami one looks for in good jambalaya.  The hourly singing was fun, but I think it would get old on subsequent visits. The dessert - bread pudding - was yummy and big enough for two.

Very fun and friendly atmosphere, moved a little quick for this first timer but overall I really enjoyed my experience! Oh and it was a little hard to find if you aren't familiar with the area...lets just say google maps was pretty much inaccurate.

My fiancé and I were in town visiting from Atlanta, saw pics of this place and had to stop by! Decor is amaze! The outdoor space was inviting. Perfect place to kick up our feet, relax with our drinks and play a round of ping pong. The pools were cute. Super tiny, but cool concept. Oh! And they have a Crema coffee bar! Life.complete. Bowling alley was really cute with the awesome neon, but too pricey for me. Wish we had a place like this in ATL! I would be here all the time if I lived in Nash.

We happened by and were so glad we stopped to get something to eat. One of the best salmon dishes I've ever had--cooked medium as was suggested. I paired the salmon with the side Asian noodle salad. Wow, what a delicious combo. Refreshing mint and lemon water. We'll be back!

Came here for brunch on a first date and had the Stuffed Challah French Toast, which was recommended.  Date and Sabrina's both scored points on that.  Toast was delicious and decadent!  Thankfully the French Toast was so huge that I brought the leftovers home and had the other half for brunch the next day.

Beautiful place to sit and talk for brunch.  Nice atmosphere.  Lots of windows.

Service was a bit odd.  I'd had about 2 bites of my Stuffed French Toast and got brought the bill.  Wasn't ready to leave, though, and we weren't explicitly asked to leave.  Just not sure if/why the waiter was trying to get us to leave before we'd eaten anything.  ah well.  odd.

Not sure how other food is here, but that toast is 5 stars.

Mays Alreem provides authentic Middle Eastern Food. This place never disappoints. I have been to Mays Alreenm 3-4 times and always we get the authentic food. The service is also great and we don't have to wait much for food. For dinner we ordered Chicken Platter, Shish Taouk platter, Side hummus and falafel. All the food we ordered was amazing, delicious and finger-licking. We love this place and we definitely eat at this place once in a month. Do try this place for authentic food.

Ducked in for a late lunch on a Saturday afternoon. Bartender was VERY generous with the pours and very friendly. Gumbo and jambalaya were good for dive bar food. Visited in early August and air conditioning was on full blast, very comfortable. Would visit again.

Staying in a Baymont's "smoking" room from Feb.17 to 20 was absolutely my worst hotel experience EVER !! 

I was extremely frustrated NOT be allowed to move to a non-smoking room even I confessed that I made a mistake in choosing a "smoking" room on priceline in the first place.   My family, including two young kids, never smoke, so we felt sick during the stay on the 2nd floor which is dedicated to smoking guys........  It was so bad that had ruined our 4-day ski trip we had long anticipated.   My mind boggled at why the front desk staff was so determined not allow us to move to a non-smoking room by chanting "according to the policy, you have to pay the difference between price on the front desk and the online price."  However, both smoking and non-smoking rooms were priced equally.  I don't understand why Baymont has this ridiculous policy to torture non-smoking customers in a "smoking" room.  Why wasn't Baymont kind enough to compromise my mistake in choosing the room type.......  It costs nothing at all for Baymont to salvage my vacation !!

In fact, as soon as I realized the mistake, just about 1 minutes after finishing the online payment, I called the front desk and was told a lie "All room are booked". I know that was definitely a lie because two friends of mine booked two non-smoking rooms in Baymont a couple days later separately.   I also asked for help from priceline, but priceline was really less than helpful, doing nothing but faxing a request for me.

I have been travelling a lot, staying in so many different hotels.  Staying in a Baymont's "smoking" room from Feb.17 to 20 was absolutely my worst hotel experience EVER !!  I will recommend people not to even consider Baymont, and hopefully no one will be tortured by Baymont again.

Had these guys do some custom exhaust work that I thought up for my Mercedes AMG. They did a great job for a super fair price and even corrected some mistakes that a different shop made. I'd have no problem trusting them with my luxury car again (in fact, I have more custom exhaust work scheduled) or any other fancy custom. I also saw some of the work they did on an old beat up Chevy truck and it was nice to see that the quality of the work they did on that truck was the same as the quality on my Mercedes. To me that means they take there work seriously, and will do a good job no matter what kind of vehicle you're in. 1000 dollar truck or 100,000 dollar car.

State St. is closed, Arigato and many other restaurants have opened their seating into the street. The ambience was just amazing. We sat under string lights surrounded by the warmth of patio heaters. Candlelight lit our dinner table. 

We ordered the Tiger Roll, Eskimo Roll, Garlic Beef, Salmon Carpaccio, Garlic Edamame and oh my goodness was this some of the best sushi we ever had. 

Leo took our order and Javier and David assisted. We literally had 3 waiters take care of us. Shout out to them for making our night. 

Little did they know it was our 4 year anniversary and we haven't seen each other in 4 months due to quarantine. 

The food came out within 10 minutes of placing our order and boy was every bite worth every penny. 

We will definitely be back soon!

Beautiful atmosphere and great location. Service was good. Maybe it was because it was a busy night, but I was astounded that they ran out of the steak by 7:30 pm (seems like a dish you should stack up on). My appetizer of Grilled Butternut Squash tasted great but probably sat out for a while because it was room temperature. Entree of Chicken and Pheasant consisted of a tiny piece of chicken (which was also room temperature). Pheasant was fine. Had high hopes, but I didn't leave very happy. Better luck next time.

My wife and I visited Pastries N Chaat for  the daily lunch thali. We chose the vegetarian option as we are vegetarian. The thali consisted of yellow dal, veg of the day(ridge gourd beerakaya on this day), salad, rice( which was amazing), fresh chapati, samosa and a gulab jamun. 
The yellow dal, though simple, was uniquely flavored and delicious. The ridge-gourd was hot and spicy and cooked with tomato. It was light and fantastic. You would think that rice is just a ubiquitous addition to a thali but the rice was a stand out. The rice was cooked with whole spices like cardamon pods and cloves and was flavorful and addictive. The samosa was spiced nicely and crisply fresh. The chapati was fresh and hot. The gulab jamun was an excellent end to the meal. My only mistake was not asking for chutney. 
The person working the counter was generous and took good care of his customers. 
We will most definitely return; next time for something off the chaat menu.

Easily one of the BEST burgers in Manayunk, if not the entire city. Served with a side of Parmesan seasoned fries that are to die for! Bartenders are friendly, service is quick and atmosphere is comfortable. Would definitely recommend a visit.

Always a wonderful meal and fun experience at Ichiban! I didn't know but they now have an early bird dinner that is essentially the same as the full show dinner, only you get one entree item instead of the combo's. It's so worth it since you get so much food. And you can not pass up the fried rice upgrade! It is the best fried rice I have ever had.

Went here based on a locals recommendation and we were not disappointed! Only waited for about 10 minutes to get a table and it was definitely worth it. Started out with great cocktails and some pork skins (which were amazing). Then had a salad and Brussels sprouts, followed by pork skin and arroz con pollo. Everything was fabulous and the portions were quite large. Skip the lines and come here!
Really good Moroccan Food!  Just make sure you have a lot of time, b/k with all the courses it can take them awhile to get through...At least there are Belly Dancers to pass the time;-)
Very nice, clean store. Great management. Great deals. Very competitive prices. Hospital clean. The floors are gorgeous. Highly recommended!
This del Taco was not cooperative and the drive through lady was incredibly rude. I will not be there ever again.

Cuban district?  A lot of cigar bars and clubs. A few gay bars?  Very interesting strip. Must visit.  Make sure you park at marked parking spots.  Otherwise, you'll get a ticket or citation. 
You

Really enjoyed our lunch yesterday here at Favazza's! Our server was so friendly, informative, and attentive! Our drinks never went empty and she was committed to making sure we had a nice experience! We ordered the sea scallop special, Sicilian chicken, tortellini, and penna Rosa. Everything was great! A tad on the expensive side, so it won't be a place we visit regularly. 

COVID precaution: masks required, separate entrance/exit, reduced capacity, accepting reservations
Great band! Great bar atmosphere. Drinks are the highest price we've paid in three days but being out of the CRAZY party crowds down on Broadway was worth it.

AWFUL. We had a 9:30 reservation, yes, reservation  and they over booked to the restaurant to the point where couldn't seat us until 10:30!!!! I've NEVER IN MY LIFE had that experience. At what is worse is they weren't apologetic. I will not go back. Ive eaten here before, the food is okay but not good enough to justify this level of inconsideration to their patrons.

Updating review.... this remains my favorite place to get pedicures. Love the ambiance, love the waterless method used, every technician I've had is wonderful. Well done Poppy & Monroe for keeping your service and staff excellent.

Its
Better
Than Bourbon
Street. Music is
Great but expect crowds in many small bars.

Perfect place to enjoy a night out in the town! I've been to comedy shows on Friday and Saturday night. The price is reasonable, there's a bar, and I'm always guaranteed laughter. I definitely recommend checking this place out! 

ENTRANCE: It has a cool entrance in front of the Pioneer Center, leading to the underground venue.

PARKING: Can be very dark at night due to lack of lighting, and usually the parking lot is full (occupies about 30 cars, give or take) so you might find yourself parking on the street. 

PRICE: A little above the price of buying a movie ticket.

Ordered #12 Beach Club as follows: 
Italian Vinaigrette (Extra)
add onions
add oregano
French Bread
Cut in half
mayo (Extra)
Receive a dessert dry sandwich. I called the store and the guys say I could come by and let him add it to the sandwich. If I wanted to come to the store I would not have chosen delivery.

My boyfriend and I were starving and craving for a poke bowl. We saw this place on yelp and wanted to give it a try. Yelp said it closes at 11pm and when we got there at 10:30pm, lights out. Even the hours on the door said it opened until 11pm on Saturdays. Super disappointed.

I am here sitting at this place. The service , the food and the reaction from the ownership , management and servers is terrible . I've been sitting here for over 2 hours and the food is coming in pieces. The orders are not complete. I have brought this to to the attention of everyone that works here. They really don't care . They have ruined my friends engagement dinner and the brides birthday . There are 22 people here and they are all not happy!!! Never again!!!

The amount of meat you can get for the price is exceptional. It was a fun exp for the family. I won't rush back, I'd like to see the sides menu expand. With maybe some fruits, more veggies. Very nice and clean location.
This place rocks! The BEST burger I've ever had...seriously. The fried chicken is awesome too! I'm excited to work my way through the menu!

Flying Saucer of Nashville gets one star for their extensive beer collection, their only positive attribute. The bothered waitresses in their tacky school girl uniforms have a MAJOR attitude problem. If you complain about the service and hostility, chances are you'll be even more disappointed to find that  the so-called manager is on the same level. When a band plays, they charge a cover, the music is too loud and the atmosphere is unwelcoming in general. This place is a very poor showcase of Nashville hospitality.

Haven't been here in a long time 
But it has everything I'm looking for 

Just stopping by for Mark West Pinot Noir wine  
Only two bottles so I bought both

This wasn't a terrible place to stay, but it wasn't the best place we've ever stayed either. The room was good, comfortable bed, had a nice kitchenette with everything we needed, clean enough (except for a giant bug on the ceiling one morning. Terrifying way to wake up). They don't do maid service on weekends? Which is weird. Also breakfast was awful. They were out of pretty much everything, but the staff were in the kitchen eating. One of the staff members came out to the breakfast area, grabbed a biscuit and a sausage link with his bare hands and began eating it right there. Overall, it wasn't a terrible place to stay but we probably won't go out of our way to come back here.

Female owned business. Not the reason why I gave a 5 star though. Staff was very knowledgeable. Politely explained my options. I was never rushed to decide. They did my Jeep in under 2 hours. I already told my husband to get his Jeep done there. He's calling soon. Appointment made same day as tinting.
